sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।कृतोदकं तु राजानं धृतराष्ट्रं युधिष्ठिरः ।पुरस्कृत्य महाबाहुरुत्तताराकुलेन्द्रियः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,kṛtodakaṁ tu rājānaṁ dhṛtarāṣṭraṁ yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,puraskṛtya mahābāhuruttatārākulendriyaḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: The mighty-armed (mahābāhu) Yudhiṣṭhira, with agitated senses (ākulendriya), having performed the water ritual and respectfully leading King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, then ascended (from the river).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: The mighty-armed (mahābāhu) Yudhiṣṭhira, with agitated senses (ākulendriya), having performed the water ritual and respectfully leading King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, then ascended (from the river).

sanskrit: उत्तीर्य च महीपालो बाष्पव्याकुललोचनः ।पपात तीरे गङ्गाया व्याधविद्ध इव द्विपः ॥२॥
transliteration: uttīrya ca mahīpālo bāṣpavyākulalocanaḥ ,papāta tīre gaṅgāyā vyādhaviddha iva dvipaḥ.
english: And having emerged (from the water), the protector of the earth (mahīpāla), his eyes distressed by tears, fell on the bank of the Gaṅgā, like an elephant (dvipa) wounded by a hunter.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 2. And having emerged (from the water), the protector of the earth (mahīpāla), his eyes distressed by tears, fell on the bank of the Gaṅgā, like an elephant (dvipa) wounded by a hunter.

sanskrit: तं सीदमानं जग्राह भीमः कृष्णेन चोदितः ।मैवमित्यब्रवीच्चैनं कृष्णः परबलार्दनः ॥३॥
transliteration: taṁ sīdamānaṁ jagrāha bhīmaḥ kṛṣṇena coditaḥ ,maivamityabravīccainaṁ kṛṣṇaḥ parabalārdanaḥ.
english: Bhīma, urged by Kṛṣṇa (kṛṣṇa), caught him (Yudhiṣṭhira) as he was collapsing. And Kṛṣṇa (kṛṣṇa), the destroyer of enemy forces, said to him, 'Do not (act) thus!'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 3. Bhīma, urged by Kṛṣṇa (kṛṣṇa), caught him (Yudhiṣṭhira) as he was collapsing. And Kṛṣṇa (kṛṣṇa), the destroyer of enemy forces, said to him, 'Do not (act) thus!'

sanskrit: तमार्तं पतितं भूमौ निश्वसन्तं पुनः पुनः ।ददृशुः पाण्डवा राजन्धर्मात्मानं युधिष्ठिरम् ॥४॥
transliteration: tamārtaṁ patitaṁ bhūmau niśvasantaṁ punaḥ punaḥ ,dadṛśuḥ pāṇḍavā rājandharmātmānaṁ yudhiṣṭhiram.
english: O king, the Pāṇḍavas saw him, Yudhiṣṭhira, whose intrinsic nature (dharma) was righteousness, fallen on the ground, distressed, and sighing again and again.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 4. O king, the Pāṇḍavas saw him, Yudhiṣṭhira, whose intrinsic nature (dharma) was righteousness, fallen on the ground, distressed, and sighing again and again.

sanskrit: तं दृष्ट्वा दीनमनसं गतसत्त्वं जनेश्वरम् ।भूयः शोकसमाविष्टाः पाण्डवाः समुपाविशन् ॥५॥
transliteration: taṁ dṛṣṭvā dīnamanasaṁ gatasattvaṁ janeśvaram ,bhūyaḥ śokasamāviṣṭāḥ pāṇḍavāḥ samupāviśan.
english: Having seen him, the lord of men (Yudhiṣṭhira), disheartened and devoid of vigor, the Pāṇḍavas, overcome by even greater grief, sat down.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 5. Having seen him, the lord of men (Yudhiṣṭhira), disheartened and devoid of vigor, the Pāṇḍavas, overcome by even greater grief, sat down.

sanskrit: राजा च धृतराष्ट्रस्तमुपासीनो महाभुजः ।वाक्यमाह महाप्राज्ञो महाशोकप्रपीडितम् ॥६॥
transliteration: rājā ca dhṛtarāṣṭrastamupāsīno mahābhujaḥ ,vākyamāha mahāprājño mahāśokaprapīḍitam.
english: And King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the great-armed, greatly wise one, sitting near him (Yudhiṣṭhira), who was greatly afflicted by grief, spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 6. And King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the great-armed, greatly wise one, sitting near him (Yudhiṣṭhira), who was greatly afflicted by grief, spoke these words.

sanskrit: उत्तिष्ठ कुरुशार्दूल कुरु कार्यमनन्तरम् ।क्षत्रधर्मेण कौरव्य जितेयमवनिस्त्वया ॥७॥
transliteration: uttiṣṭha kuruśārdūla kuru kāryamanantaram ,kṣatradharmeṇa kauravya jiteyamavanistvayā.
english: Rise up, O tiger among the Kurus (kuruśārdūla)! Perform your next duty immediately. O descendant of Kuru (kauravya), this earth has been conquered by you, in accordance with the warrior's natural law (kṣatradharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 7. Rise up, O tiger among the Kurus (kuruśārdūla)! Perform your next duty immediately. O descendant of Kuru (kauravya), this earth has been conquered by you, in accordance with the warrior's natural law (kṣatradharma).

sanskrit: तां भुङ्क्ष्व भ्रातृभिः सार्धं सुहृद्भिश्च जनेश्वर ।न शोचितव्यं पश्यामि त्वया धर्मभृतां वर ॥८॥
transliteration: tāṁ bhuṅkṣva bhrātṛbhiḥ sārdhaṁ suhṛdbhiśca janeśvara ,na śocitavyaṁ paśyāmi tvayā dharmabhṛtāṁ vara.
english: O lord of men, best among those who uphold natural law (dharma), enjoy this [earth/kingdom] with your brothers and friends. I see nothing for you to grieve over.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 8. O lord of men, best among those who uphold natural law (dharma), enjoy this [earth/kingdom] with your brothers and friends. I see nothing for you to grieve over.

sanskrit: शोचितव्यं मया चैव गान्धार्या च विशां पते ।पुत्रैर्विहीनो राज्येन स्वप्नलब्धधनो यथा ॥९॥
transliteration: śocitavyaṁ mayā caiva gāndhāryā ca viśāṁ pate ,putrairvihīno rājyena svapnalabdhadhano yathā.
english: O lord of subjects, I and Gandhari certainly have reason to grieve. [I am now] deprived of sons and kingdom, like one who has obtained wealth in a dream.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 9. O lord of subjects, I and Gandhari certainly have reason to grieve. [I am now] deprived of sons and kingdom, like one who has obtained wealth in a dream.

sanskrit: अश्रुत्वा हितकामस्य विदुरस्य महात्मनः ।वाक्यानि सुमहार्थानि परितप्यामि दुर्मतिः ॥१०॥
transliteration: aśrutvā hitakāmasya vidurasya mahātmanaḥ ,vākyāni sumahārthāni paritapyāmi durmatiḥ.
english: Having not listened to the very profound words of the great-souled Vidura, who was my well-wisher, I, being foolish, now deeply regret [it].
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 10. Having not listened to the very profound words of the great-souled Vidura, who was my well-wisher, I, being foolish, now deeply regret [it].

sanskrit: उक्तवानेष मां पूर्वं धर्मात्मा दिव्यदर्शनः ।दुर्योधनापराधेन कुलं ते विनशिष्यति ॥११॥
transliteration: uktavāneṣa māṁ pūrvaṁ dharmātmā divyadarśanaḥ ,duryodhanāparādhena kulaṁ te vinaśiṣyati.
english: That righteous-souled (dharmātman) and divinely-sighted [Vidura] spoke to me previously: 'Your lineage will be destroyed due to Duryodhana's offense.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 11. That righteous-souled (dharmātman) and divinely-sighted [Vidura] spoke to me previously: 'Your lineage will be destroyed due to Duryodhana's offense.'

sanskrit: स्वस्ति चेदिच्छसे राजन्कुलस्यात्मन एव च ।वध्यतामेष दुष्टात्मा मन्दो राजा सुयोधनः ॥१२॥
transliteration: svasti cedicchase rājankulasyātmana eva ca ,vadhyatāmeṣa duṣṭātmā mando rājā suyodhanaḥ.
english: O King, if you desire welfare for your family and for yourself (ātman), then this evil-minded, foolish King Suyodhana should be killed.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 12. O King, if you desire welfare for your family and for yourself (ātman), then this evil-minded, foolish King Suyodhana should be killed.

sanskrit: कर्णश्च शकुनिश्चैव मैनं पश्यतु कर्हिचित् ।द्यूतसंपातमप्येषामप्रमत्तो निवारय ॥१३॥
transliteration: karṇaśca śakuniścaiva mainaṁ paśyatu karhicit ,dyūtasaṁpātamapyeṣāmapramatto nivāraya.
english: Let Karna and Shakuni never see him. You should diligently prevent even their gambling assemblies.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 13. Let Karna and Shakuni never see him. You should diligently prevent even their gambling assemblies.

sanskrit: अभिषेचय राजानं धर्मात्मानं युधिष्ठिरम् ।स पालयिष्यति वशी धर्मेण पृथिवीमिमाम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: abhiṣecaya rājānaṁ dharmātmānaṁ yudhiṣṭhiram ,sa pālayiṣyati vaśī dharmeṇa pṛthivīmimām.
english: Anoint the righteous (dharma-ātman) Yudhishthira as king. Being self-controlled, he will protect this earth in accordance with natural law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 14. Anoint the righteous (dharma-ātman) Yudhishthira as king. Being self-controlled, he will protect this earth in accordance with natural law (dharma).

sanskrit: अथ नेच्छसि राजानं कुन्तीपुत्रं युधिष्ठिरम् ।मेढीभूतः स्वयं राज्यं प्रतिगृह्णीष्व पार्थिव ॥१५॥
transliteration: atha necchasi rājānaṁ kuntīputraṁ yudhiṣṭhiram ,meḍhībhūtaḥ svayaṁ rājyaṁ pratigṛhṇīṣva pārthiva.
english: But if you do not desire Yudhishthira, Kunti's son, as king, then, O ruler (pārthiva), you yourself become the central support and accept the kingdom.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 15. But if you do not desire Yudhishthira, Kunti's son, as king, then, O ruler (pārthiva), you yourself become the central support and accept the kingdom.

sanskrit: समं सर्वेषु भूतेषु वर्तमानं नराधिप ।अनुजीवन्तु सर्वे त्वां ज्ञातयो ज्ञातिवर्धन ॥१६॥
transliteration: samaṁ sarveṣu bhūteṣu vartamānaṁ narādhipa ,anujīvantu sarve tvāṁ jñātayo jñātivardhana.
english: O ruler of men (narādhipa), O enhancer of kinsmen, may all your relatives thrive under your support, as you are present equally among all beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 16. O ruler of men (narādhipa), O enhancer of kinsmen, may all your relatives thrive under your support, as you are present equally among all beings.

sanskrit: एवं ब्रुवति कौन्तेय विदुरे दीर्घदर्शिनि ।दुर्योधनमहं पापमन्ववर्तं वृथामतिः ॥१७॥
transliteration: evaṁ bruvati kaunteya vidure dīrghadarśini ,duryodhanamahaṁ pāpamanvavartaṁ vṛthāmatiḥ.
english: O son of Kuntī (kaunteya), while the far-sighted Vidura spoke thus, I, with a foolish mind, followed the wicked Duryodhana.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 17. O son of Kuntī (kaunteya), while the far-sighted Vidura spoke thus, I, with a foolish mind, followed the wicked Duryodhana.

sanskrit: अश्रुत्वा ह्यस्य वीरस्य वाक्यानि मधुराण्यहम् ।फलं प्राप्य महद्दुःखं निमग्नः शोकसागरे ॥१८॥
transliteration: aśrutvā hyasya vīrasya vākyāni madhurāṇyaham ,phalaṁ prāpya mahadduḥkhaṁ nimagnaḥ śokasāgare.
english: Indeed, without listening to the sweet words of this hero, I obtained the fruit of great sorrow and became submerged in the ocean of grief.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 18. Indeed, without listening to the sweet words of this hero, I obtained the fruit of great sorrow and became submerged in the ocean of grief.

sanskrit: वृद्धौ हि ते स्वः पितरौ पश्यावां दुःखितौ नृप ।न शोचितव्यं भवता पश्यामीह जनाधिप ॥१९॥
transliteration: vṛddhau hi te svaḥ pitarau paśyāvāṁ duḥkhitau nṛpa ,na śocitavyaṁ bhavatā paśyāmīha janādhipa.
english: Indeed, O king (nṛpa), your own two parents are old; we both see them distressed. You should not grieve, O lord of people (janādhipa), for I perceive no cause for it here.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 19. Indeed, O king (nṛpa), your own two parents are old; we both see them distressed. You should not grieve, O lord of people (janādhipa), for I perceive no cause for it here.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवमुक्तस्तु राज्ञा स धृतराष्ट्रेण धीमता ।तूष्णीं बभूव मेधावी तमुवाचाथ केशवः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evamuktastu rājñā sa dhṛtarāṣṭreṇa dhīmatā ,tūṣṇīṁ babhūva medhāvī tamuvācātha keśavaḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: When he, that intelligent one, was thus addressed by the wise king Dhṛtarāṣṭra, he remained silent. Then Keśava spoke to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: When he, that intelligent one, was thus addressed by the wise king Dhṛtarāṣṭra, he remained silent. Then Keśava spoke to him.

sanskrit: अतीव मनसा शोकः क्रियमाणो जनाधिप ।संतापयति वैतस्य पूर्वप्रेतान्पितामहान् ॥२॥
transliteration: atīva manasā śokaḥ kriyamāṇo janādhipa ,saṁtāpayati vaitasya pūrvapretānpitāmahān.
english: Oh King, excessive sorrow harbored in the mind indeed causes distress to his departed ancestors.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 2. Oh King, excessive sorrow harbored in the mind indeed causes distress to his departed ancestors.

sanskrit: यजस्व विविधैर्यज्ञैर्बहुभिः स्वाप्तदक्षिणैः ।देवांस्तर्पय सोमेन स्वधया च पितॄनपि ॥३॥
transliteration: yajasva vividhairyajñairbahubhiḥ svāptadakṣiṇaiḥ ,devāṁstarpaya somena svadhayā ca pitṝnapi.
english: Perform many different Vedic rituals (yajña) with appropriate and abundant sacrificial fees. Gratify the gods with Soma and also the ancestors (pitṛ) with ancestral oblations (svadhā).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 3. Perform many different Vedic rituals (yajña) with appropriate and abundant sacrificial fees. Gratify the gods with Soma and also the ancestors (pitṛ) with ancestral oblations (svadhā).

sanskrit: त्वद्विधस्य महाबुद्धे नैतदद्योपपद्यते ।विदितं वेदितव्यं ते कर्तव्यमपि ते कृतम् ॥४॥
transliteration: tvadvidhasya mahābuddhe naitadadyopapadyate ,viditaṁ veditavyaṁ te kartavyamapi te kṛtam.
english: For one like you, O highly intelligent one, this (grief) is not appropriate now. That which was to be known has been known by you, and that which was to be done has also been accomplished by you.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 4. For one like you, O highly intelligent one, this (grief) is not appropriate now. That which was to be known has been known by you, and that which was to be done has also been accomplished by you.

sanskrit: श्रुताश्च राजधर्मास्ते भीष्माद्भागीरथीसुतात् ।कृष्णद्वैपायनाच्चैव नारदाद्विदुरात्तथा ॥५॥
transliteration: śrutāśca rājadharmāste bhīṣmādbhāgīrathīsutāt ,kṛṣṇadvaipāyanāccaiva nāradādvidurāttathā.
english: You have heard those principles of righteous governance (dharma) from Bhishma, the son of Bhagirathi, and indeed from Krishna Dvaipayana, as well as from Narada and Vidura.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 5. You have heard those principles of righteous governance (dharma) from Bhishma, the son of Bhagirathi, and indeed from Krishna Dvaipayana, as well as from Narada and Vidura.

sanskrit: नेमामर्हसि मूढानां वृत्तिं त्वमनुवर्तितुम् ।पितृपैतामहीं वृत्तिमास्थाय धुरमुद्वह ॥६॥
transliteration: nemāmarhasi mūḍhānāṁ vṛttiṁ tvamanuvartitum ,pitṛpaitāmahīṁ vṛttimāsthāya dhuramudvaha.
english: You should not imitate this behavior of the foolish. Rather, upholding your ancestral way of life, carry on your responsibility.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 6. You should not imitate this behavior of the foolish. Rather, upholding your ancestral way of life, carry on your responsibility.

sanskrit: युक्तं हि यशसा क्षत्रं स्वर्गं प्राप्तुमसंशयम् ।न हि कश्चन शूराणां निहतोऽत्र पराङ्मुखः ॥७॥
transliteration: yuktaṁ hi yaśasā kṣatraṁ svargaṁ prāptumasaṁśayam ,na hi kaścana śūrāṇāṁ nihato'tra parāṅmukhaḥ.
english: Indeed, it is certainly fitting for a warrior (kṣatram) to attain heaven with glory. For no hero slain in battle here has ever turned his back.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 7. Indeed, it is certainly fitting for a warrior (kṣatram) to attain heaven with glory. For no hero slain in battle here has ever turned his back.

sanskrit: त्यज शोकं महाराज भवितव्यं हि तत्तथा ।न शक्यास्ते पुनर्द्रष्टुं त्वया ह्यस्मिन्रणे हताः ॥८॥
transliteration: tyaja śokaṁ mahārāja bhavitavyaṁ hi tattathā ,na śakyāste punardraṣṭuṁ tvayā hyasminraṇe hatāḥ.
english: O great king, relinquish your sorrow! For what is destined (bhavitavya) must indeed come to pass. Those killed by you in this battle cannot be seen again.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 8. O great king, relinquish your sorrow! For what is destined (bhavitavya) must indeed come to pass. Those killed by you in this battle cannot be seen again.

sanskrit: एतावदुक्त्वा गोविन्दो धर्मराजं युधिष्ठिरम् ।विरराम महातेजास्तमुवाच युधिष्ठिरः ॥९॥
transliteration: etāvaduktvā govindo dharmarājaṁ yudhiṣṭhiram ,virarāma mahātejāstamuvāca yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: Having spoken thus to Dharmaraja (Yudhiṣṭhira), Govinda, the greatly effulgent one, fell silent. Then Yudhiṣṭhira spoke to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 9. Having spoken thus to Dharmaraja (Yudhiṣṭhira), Govinda, the greatly effulgent one, fell silent. Then Yudhiṣṭhira spoke to him.

sanskrit: गोविन्द मयि या प्रीतिस्तव सा विदिता मम ।सौहृदेन तथा प्रेम्णा सदा मामनुकम्पसे ॥१०॥
transliteration: govinda mayi yā prītistava sā viditā mama ,sauhṛdena tathā premṇā sadā māmanukampase.
english: O Govinda, the affection you have for me is well known to me. You always show compassion towards me through friendship and love.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 10. O Govinda, the affection you have for me is well known to me. You always show compassion towards me through friendship and love.

sanskrit: प्रियं तु मे स्यात्सुमहत्कृतं चक्रगदाधर ।श्रीमन्प्रीतेन मनसा सर्वं यादवनन्दन ॥११॥
transliteration: priyaṁ tu me syātsumahatkṛtaṁ cakragadādhara ,śrīmanprītena manasā sarvaṁ yādavanandana.
english: O wielder of discus and mace (Cakragadādhara), O glorious one, O delight of the Yadus (Yādavanandana)! It would be a very great and pleasing act for me if all this were accomplished with a pleased mind.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 11. O wielder of discus and mace (Cakragadādhara), O glorious one, O delight of the Yadus (Yādavanandana)! It would be a very great and pleasing act for me if all this were accomplished with a pleased mind.

sanskrit: यदि मामनुजानीयाद्भवान्गन्तुं तपोवनम् ।न हि शान्तिं प्रपश्यामि घातयित्वा पितामहम् ।कर्णं च पुरुषव्याघ्रं संग्रामेष्वपलायिनम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: yadi māmanujānīyādbhavāngantuṁ tapovanam ,na hi śāntiṁ prapaśyāmi ghātayitvā pitāmaham ,karṇaṁ ca puruṣavyāghraṁ saṁgrāmeṣvapalāyinam.
english: If your honor would permit me to go to the penance-grove (tapovanam), for I truly find no peace after having caused the death of grandfather (Pitāmaha) and Karṇa, that tiger among men who never retreated in battles.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 12. If your honor would permit me to go to the penance-grove (tapovanam), for I truly find no peace after having caused the death of grandfather (Pitāmaha) and Karṇa, that tiger among men who never retreated in battles.

sanskrit: कर्मणा येन मुच्येयमस्मात्क्रूरादरिंदम ।कर्मणस्तद्विधत्स्वेह येन शुध्यति मे मनः ॥१३॥
transliteration: karmaṇā yena mucyeyamasmātkrūrādariṁdama ,karmaṇastadvidhatsveha yena śudhyati me manaḥ.
english: O subduer of enemies, by which action (karma) I may be freed from this cruel deed, perform that very action here, so that my mind may become pure.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 13. O subduer of enemies, by which action (karma) I may be freed from this cruel deed, perform that very action here, so that my mind may become pure.

sanskrit: तमेवंवादिनं व्यासस्ततः प्रोवाच धर्मवित् ।सान्त्वयन्सुमहातेजाः शुभं वचनमर्थवत् ॥१४॥
transliteration: tamevaṁvādinaṁ vyāsastataḥ provāca dharmavit ,sāntvayansumahātejāḥ śubhaṁ vacanamarthavat.
english: Then Vyasa, who knew the natural law (dharma), consoling him, and possessed of great splendor, spoke auspicious and meaningful words to him who was speaking thus.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 14. Then Vyasa, who knew the natural law (dharma), consoling him, and possessed of great splendor, spoke auspicious and meaningful words to him who was speaking thus.

sanskrit: अकृता ते मतिस्तात पुनर्बाल्येन मुह्यसे ।किमाकाशे वयं सर्वे प्रलपाम मुहुर्मुहुः ॥१५॥
transliteration: akṛtā te matistāta punarbālyena muhyase ,kimākāśe vayaṁ sarve pralapāma muhurmuhuḥ.
english: O dear child (tāta), your intellect is immature, and you are again bewildered by childishness. Are we all repeatedly babbling into the empty sky?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 15. O dear child (tāta), your intellect is immature, and you are again bewildered by childishness. Are we all repeatedly babbling into the empty sky?

sanskrit: विदिताः क्षत्रधर्मास्ते येषां युद्धेन जीविका ।यथा प्रवृत्तो नृपतिर्नाधिबन्धेन युज्यते ॥१६॥
transliteration: viditāḥ kṣatradharmāste yeṣāṁ yuddhena jīvikā ,yathā pravṛtto nṛpatirnādhibandhena yujyate.
english: The duties of a warrior (kṣatradharma) are well-known to those whose livelihood is by warfare. Just as a king engaged (in battle) is not bound by restrictions.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 16. The duties of a warrior (kṣatradharma) are well-known to those whose livelihood is by warfare. Just as a king engaged (in battle) is not bound by restrictions.

sanskrit: मोक्षधर्माश्च निखिला याथातथ्येन ते श्रुताः ।असकृच्चैव संदेहाश्छिन्नास्ते कामजा मया ॥१७॥
transliteration: mokṣadharmāśca nikhilā yāthātathyena te śrutāḥ ,asakṛccaiva saṁdehāśchinnāste kāmajā mayā.
english: You have accurately heard all the principles of liberation (mokṣa). Indeed, your desire-born doubts have been repeatedly dispelled by me.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 17. You have accurately heard all the principles of liberation (mokṣa). Indeed, your desire-born doubts have been repeatedly dispelled by me.

sanskrit: अश्रद्दधानो दुर्मेधा लुप्तस्मृतिरसि ध्रुवम् ।मैवं भव न ते युक्तमिदमज्ञानमीदृशम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: aśraddadhāno durmedhā luptasmṛtirasi dhruvam ,maivaṁ bhava na te yuktamidamajñānamīdṛśam.
english: You are certainly faithless, dull-witted, and your memory is lost. Do not be like this; such ignorance is not befitting for you.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 18. You are certainly faithless, dull-witted, and your memory is lost. Do not be like this; such ignorance is not befitting for you.

sanskrit: प्रायश्चित्तानि सर्वाणि विदितानि च तेऽनघ ।युद्धधर्माश्च ते सर्वे दानधर्माश्च ते श्रुताः ॥१९॥
transliteration: prāyaścittāni sarvāṇi viditāni ca te'nagha ,yuddhadharmāśca te sarve dānadharmāśca te śrutāḥ.
english: O sinless one, all expiations are known to you. And all the principles of combat (dharma) and the principles of generosity (dharma) have been heard by you.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 19. O sinless one, all expiations are known to you. And all the principles of combat (dharma) and the principles of generosity (dharma) have been heard by you.

sanskrit: स कथं सर्वधर्मज्ञः सर्वागमविशारदः ।परिमुह्यसि भूयस्त्वमज्ञानादिव भारत ॥२०॥
transliteration: sa kathaṁ sarvadharmajñaḥ sarvāgamaviśāradaḥ ,parimuhyasi bhūyastvamajñānādiva bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, how can you, who are a knower of all principles (dharma) and an expert in all traditional teachings (āgama), be bewildered again, as if from ignorance?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 20. O Bhārata, how can you, who are a knower of all principles (dharma) and an expert in all traditional teachings (āgama), be bewildered again, as if from ignorance?

sanskrit: व्यास उवाच ।युधिष्ठिर तव प्रज्ञा न सम्यगिति मे मतिः ।न हि कश्चित्स्वयं मर्त्यः स्ववशः कुरुते क्रियाः ॥१॥
transliteration: vyāsa uvāca ,yudhiṣṭhira tava prajñā na samyagiti me matiḥ ,na hi kaścitsvayaṁ martyaḥ svavaśaḥ kurute kriyāḥ.
english: Vyāsa said: "Yudhiṣṭhira, my opinion is that your intellect is not entirely correct. Indeed, no mortal performs actions (kriyāḥ) completely of their own accord."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 1. Vyāsa said: "Yudhiṣṭhira, my opinion is that your intellect is not entirely correct. Indeed, no mortal performs actions (kriyāḥ) completely of their own accord."

sanskrit: ईश्वरेण नियुक्तोऽयं साध्वसाधु च मानवः ।करोति पुरुषः कर्म तत्र का परिदेवना ॥२॥
transliteration: īśvareṇa niyukto'yaṁ sādhvasādhu ca mānavaḥ ,karoti puruṣaḥ karma tatra kā paridevanā.
english: A human being (puruṣa), appointed by the Lord, performs both good and bad actions (karma). What cause for lamentation is there in that?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 2. A human being (puruṣa), appointed by the Lord, performs both good and bad actions (karma). What cause for lamentation is there in that?

sanskrit: आत्मानं मन्यसे चाथ पापकर्माणमन्ततः ।शृणु तत्र यथा पापमपकृष्येत भारत ॥३॥
transliteration: ātmānaṁ manyase cātha pāpakarmāṇamantataḥ ,śṛṇu tatra yathā pāpamapakṛṣyeta bhārata.
english: If you finally consider yourself (ātman) to be one who commits sinful deeds, then listen, O Bhārata, how sin can be removed in that regard.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 3. If you finally consider yourself (ātman) to be one who commits sinful deeds, then listen, O Bhārata, how sin can be removed in that regard.

sanskrit: तपोभिः क्रतुभिश्चैव दानेन च युधिष्ठिर ।तरन्ति नित्यं पुरुषा ये स्म पापानि कुर्वते ॥४॥
transliteration: tapobhiḥ kratubhiścaiva dānena ca yudhiṣṭhira ,taranti nityaṁ puruṣā ye sma pāpāni kurvate.
english: Through austerities (tapas), Vedic rituals, and charity (dāna), O Yudhiṣṭhira, people (puruṣa) who commit sins always overcome them.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 4. Through austerities (tapas), Vedic rituals, and charity (dāna), O Yudhiṣṭhira, people (puruṣa) who commit sins always overcome them.

sanskrit: यज्ञेन तपसा चैव दानेन च नराधिप ।पूयन्ते राजशार्दूल नरा दुष्कृतकर्मिणः ॥५॥
transliteration: yajñena tapasā caiva dānena ca narādhipa ,pūyante rājaśārdūla narā duṣkṛtakarmiṇaḥ.
english: O ruler of men, O tiger among kings, persons who have committed evil deeds are purified by means of (Vedic ritual) yajña, by austerity (tapas), and by giving gifts (dāna).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 5. O ruler of men, O tiger among kings, persons who have committed evil deeds are purified by means of (Vedic ritual) yajña, by austerity (tapas), and by giving gifts (dāna).

sanskrit: असुराश्च सुराश्चैव पुण्यहेतोर्मखक्रियाम् ।प्रयतन्ते महात्मानस्तस्माद्यज्ञाः परायणम् ॥६॥
transliteration: asurāśca surāścaiva puṇyahetormakhakriyām ,prayatante mahātmānastasmādyajñāḥ parāyaṇam.
english: Both asuras (asura) and devas (sura)  -  those great souls  -  strive for the performance of (Vedic ritual) yajña for the sake of merit. Therefore, (Vedic rituals) yajñas are the supreme refuge.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 6. Both asuras (asura) and devas (sura)  -  those great souls  -  strive for the performance of (Vedic ritual) yajña for the sake of merit. Therefore, (Vedic rituals) yajñas are the supreme refuge.

sanskrit: यज्ञैरेव महात्मानो बभूवुरधिकाः सुराः ।ततो देवाः क्रियावन्तो दानवानभ्यधर्षयन् ॥७॥
transliteration: yajñaireva mahātmāno babhūvuradhikāḥ surāḥ ,tato devāḥ kriyāvanto dānavānabhyadharṣayan.
english: It was by (Vedic rituals) yajñas alone that the great-souled devas (sura) became superior. Subsequently, these devas (sura), being diligent performers of ritual actions, overcame the Dānavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 7. It was by (Vedic rituals) yajñas alone that the great-souled devas (sura) became superior. Subsequently, these devas (sura), being diligent performers of ritual actions, overcame the Dānavas.

sanskrit: राजसूयाश्वमेधौ च सर्वमेधं च भारत ।नरमेधं च नृपते त्वमाहर युधिष्ठिर ॥८॥
transliteration: rājasūyāśvamedhau ca sarvamedhaṁ ca bhārata ,naramedhaṁ ca nṛpate tvamāhara yudhiṣṭhira.
english: O Yudhishthira, O descendant of Bharata, O king, you should perform the rājasūya (Vedic ritual) and aśvamedha (Vedic ritual), as well as the sarvamedha (Vedic ritual) and naramedha (Vedic ritual).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 8. O Yudhishthira, O descendant of Bharata, O king, you should perform the rājasūya (Vedic ritual) and aśvamedha (Vedic ritual), as well as the sarvamedha (Vedic ritual) and naramedha (Vedic ritual).

sanskrit: यजस्व वाजिमेधेन विधिवद्दक्षिणावता ।बहुकामान्नवित्तेन रामो दाशरथिर्यथा ॥९॥
transliteration: yajasva vājimedhena vidhivaddakṣiṇāvatā ,bahukāmānnavittena rāmo dāśarathiryathā.
english: Perform the horse-Vedic ritual (yajña) according to the prescribed rites, with abundant gifts, and with much desired wealth, just as Rāma, the son of Daśaratha, did.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 9. Perform the horse-Vedic ritual (yajña) according to the prescribed rites, with abundant gifts, and with much desired wealth, just as Rāma, the son of Daśaratha, did.

sanskrit: यथा च भरतो राजा दौःषन्तिः पृथिवीपतिः ।शाकुन्तलो महावीर्यस्तव पूर्वपितामहः ॥१०॥
transliteration: yathā ca bharato rājā dauḥṣantiḥ pṛthivīpatiḥ ,śākuntalo mahāvīryastava pūrvapitāmahaḥ.
english: And just as King Bharata, the son of Duṣyanta and Śakuntalā, the very powerful lord of the earth, your great-grandfather, [did].
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 10. And just as King Bharata, the son of Duṣyanta and Śakuntalā, the very powerful lord of the earth, your great-grandfather, [did].

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।असंशयं वाजिमेधः पावयेत्पृथिवीमपि ।अभिप्रायस्तु मे कश्चित्तं त्वं श्रोतुमिहार्हसि ॥११॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,asaṁśayaṁ vājimedhaḥ pāvayetpṛthivīmapi ,abhiprāyastu me kaścittaṁ tvaṁ śrotumihārhasi.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: 'Undoubtedly, the horse-Vedic ritual (yajña) would purify even the earth. But I have a certain concern, which you should hear now.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 11. Yudhiṣṭhira said: 'Undoubtedly, the horse-Vedic ritual (yajña) would purify even the earth. But I have a certain concern, which you should hear now.'

sanskrit: इमं ज्ञातिवधं कृत्वा सुमहान्तं द्विजोत्तम ।दानमल्पं न शक्यामि दातुं वित्तं च नास्ति मे ॥१२॥
transliteration: imaṁ jñātivadhaṁ kṛtvā sumahāntaṁ dvijottama ,dānamalpaṁ na śakyāmi dātuṁ vittaṁ ca nāsti me.
english: O best of the twice-born (dvija), having caused this immense slaughter of kinsmen, I am not able to give even a small offering (dāna), and I have no wealth.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 12. O best of the twice-born (dvija), having caused this immense slaughter of kinsmen, I am not able to give even a small offering (dāna), and I have no wealth.

sanskrit: न च बालानिमान्दीनानुत्सहे वसु याचितुम् ।तथैवार्द्रव्रणान्कृच्छ्रे वर्तमानान्नृपात्मजान् ॥१३॥
transliteration: na ca bālānimāndīnānutsahe vasu yācitum ,tathaivārdravraṇānkṛcchre vartamānānnṛpātmajān.
english: And I cannot bring myself to ask for wealth from these miserable children. Similarly, I cannot ask from these princes who are suffering greatly and have fresh wounds.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 13. And I cannot bring myself to ask for wealth from these miserable children. Similarly, I cannot ask from these princes who are suffering greatly and have fresh wounds.

sanskrit: स्वयं विनाश्य पृथिवीं यज्ञार्थे द्विजसत्तम ।करमाहारयिष्यामि कथं शोकपरायणान् ॥१४॥
transliteration: svayaṁ vināśya pṛthivīṁ yajñārthe dvijasattama ,karamāhārayiṣyāmi kathaṁ śokaparāyaṇān.
english: O best of twice-born ones (dvija), after personally destroying the earth for the sake of the Vedic ritual (yajña), how shall I collect taxes from those who are overwhelmed by grief?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 14. O best of twice-born ones (dvija), after personally destroying the earth for the sake of the Vedic ritual (yajña), how shall I collect taxes from those who are overwhelmed by grief?

sanskrit: दुर्योधनापराधेन वसुधा वसुधाधिपाः ।प्रनष्टा योजयित्वास्मानकीर्त्या मुनिसत्तम ॥१५॥
transliteration: duryodhanāparādhena vasudhā vasudhādhipāḥ ,pranaṣṭā yojayitvāsmānakīrtyā munisattama.
english: O best of sages (muni), by Duryodhana's offense, the earth has been destroyed. The rulers of the earth have (also) perished, having involved us in disgrace.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 15. O best of sages (muni), by Duryodhana's offense, the earth has been destroyed. The rulers of the earth have (also) perished, having involved us in disgrace.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनेन पृथिवी क्षयिता वित्तकारणात् ।कोशश्चापि विशीर्णोऽसौ धार्तराष्ट्रस्य दुर्मतेः ॥१६॥
transliteration: duryodhanena pṛthivī kṣayitā vittakāraṇāt ,kośaścāpi viśīrṇo'sau dhārtarāṣṭrasya durmateḥ.
english: By Duryodhana, the earth was wasted for the sake of wealth. And indeed, that treasury of the evil-minded son of Dhritarashtra (Duryodhana) has been completely ruined.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 16. By Duryodhana, the earth was wasted for the sake of wealth. And indeed, that treasury of the evil-minded son of Dhritarashtra (Duryodhana) has been completely ruined.

sanskrit: पृथिवी दक्षिणा चात्र विधिः प्रथमकल्पिकः ।विद्वद्भिः परिदृष्टोऽयं शिष्टो विधिविपर्ययः ॥१७॥
transliteration: pṛthivī dakṣiṇā cātra vidhiḥ prathamakalpikaḥ ,vidvadbhiḥ paridṛṣṭo'yaṁ śiṣṭo vidhiviparyayaḥ.
english: Here, offering land as the primary ritual gift (dakṣiṇā) is the fundamental procedure (vidhi). However, this deviation from the rule is observed and approved by the learned (vidvadbhiḥ).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 17. Here, offering land as the primary ritual gift (dakṣiṇā) is the fundamental procedure (vidhi). However, this deviation from the rule is observed and approved by the learned (vidvadbhiḥ).

sanskrit: न च प्रतिनिधिं कर्तुं चिकीर्षामि तपोधन ।अत्र मे भगवन्सम्यक्साचिव्यं कर्तुमर्हसि ॥१८॥
transliteration: na ca pratinidhiṁ kartuṁ cikīrṣāmi tapodhana ,atra me bhagavansamyaksācivyaṁ kartumarhasi.
english: And, O ascetic, I do not wish to create a substitute. Therefore, O revered one, you ought to render proper assistance to me in this matter.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 18. And, O ascetic, I do not wish to create a substitute. Therefore, O revered one, you ought to render proper assistance to me in this matter.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवमुक्तस्तु पार्थेन कृष्णद्वैपायनस्तदा ।मुहूर्तमनुसंचिन्त्य धर्मराजानमब्रवीत् ॥१९॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evamuktastu pārthena kṛṣṇadvaipāyanastadā ,muhūrtamanusaṁcintya dharmarājānamabravīt.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Kṛṣṇa Dvaipāyana (Vyāsa), thus addressed by Pārtha (Yudhiṣṭhira), then pondered for a moment and spoke to the king of natural law (dharmarāja).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 19. Vaiśampāyana said: Kṛṣṇa Dvaipāyana (Vyāsa), thus addressed by Pārtha (Yudhiṣṭhira), then pondered for a moment and spoke to the king of natural law (dharmarāja).

sanskrit: विद्यते द्रविणं पार्थ गिरौ हिमवति स्थितम् ।उत्सृष्टं ब्राह्मणैर्यज्ञे मरुत्तस्य महीपतेः ।तदानयस्व कौन्तेय पर्याप्तं तद्भविष्यति ॥२०॥
transliteration: vidyate draviṇaṁ pārtha girau himavati sthitam ,utsṛṣṭaṁ brāhmaṇairyajñe maruttasya mahīpateḥ ,tadānayasva kaunteya paryāptaṁ tadbhaviṣyati.
english: O Pārtha (Yudhiṣṭhira), wealth exists, located on the Himavat mountain. It was offered by the Brahmins in the Vedic ritual (yajña) of King Marutta. O son of Kuntī (Kaunteya), bring that; it will be sufficient.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 20. O Pārtha (Yudhiṣṭhira), wealth exists, located on the Himavat mountain. It was offered by the Brahmins in the Vedic ritual (yajña) of King Marutta. O son of Kuntī (Kaunteya), bring that; it will be sufficient.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।कथं यज्ञे मरुत्तस्य द्रविणं तत्समाचितम् ।कस्मिंश्च काले स नृपो बभूव वदतां वर ॥२१॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,kathaṁ yajñe maruttasya draviṇaṁ tatsamācitam ,kasmiṁśca kāle sa nṛpo babhūva vadatāṁ vara.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: O best of speakers, how was that wealth accumulated in Marutta's Vedic ritual (yajña)? And in what era did that king live?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 21. Yudhiṣṭhira said: O best of speakers, how was that wealth accumulated in Marutta's Vedic ritual (yajña)? And in what era did that king live?

sanskrit: व्यास उवाच ।यदि शुश्रूषसे पार्थ शृणु कारंधमं नृपम् ।यस्मिन्काले महावीर्यः स राजासीन्महाधनः ॥२२॥
transliteration: vyāsa uvāca ,yadi śuśrūṣase pārtha śṛṇu kāraṁdhamaṁ nṛpam ,yasminkāle mahāvīryaḥ sa rājāsīnmahādhanaḥ.
english: Vyāsa said: O Pārtha, if you wish to hear, then listen about King Karandhama, during whose time that extremely mighty king was also very wealthy.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 22. Vyāsa said: O Pārtha, if you wish to hear, then listen about King Karandhama, during whose time that extremely mighty king was also very wealthy.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।शुश्रूषे तस्य धर्मज्ञ राजर्षेः परिकीर्तनम् ।द्वैपायन मरुत्तस्य कथां प्रब्रूहि मेऽनघ ॥१॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,śuśrūṣe tasya dharmajña rājarṣeḥ parikīrtanam ,dvaipāyana maruttasya kathāṁ prabrūhi me'nagha.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: O knower of natural law (dharma), O Dvaipāyana, O sinless one, I wish to hear the narration of that royal sage Marutta's story; please recount it to me.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 1. Yudhiṣṭhira said: O knower of natural law (dharma), O Dvaipāyana, O sinless one, I wish to hear the narration of that royal sage Marutta's story; please recount it to me.

sanskrit: व्यास उवाच ।आसीत्कृतयुगे पूर्वं मनुर्दण्डधरः प्रभुः ।तस्य पुत्रो महेष्वासः प्रजातिरिति विश्रुतः ॥२॥
transliteration: vyāsa uvāca ,āsītkṛtayuge pūrvaṁ manurdaṇḍadharaḥ prabhuḥ ,tasya putro maheṣvāsaḥ prajātiriti viśrutaḥ.
english: Vyāsa said: Formerly, in the Kṛtayuga, there was a powerful Manu, who held the scepter of justice. His son, a great archer, was renowned as Prajāti.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 2. Vyāsa said: Formerly, in the Kṛtayuga, there was a powerful Manu, who held the scepter of justice. His son, a great archer, was renowned as Prajāti.

sanskrit: प्रजातेरभवत्पुत्रः क्षुप इत्यभिविश्रुतः ।क्षुपस्य पुत्रस्त्विक्ष्वाकुर्महीपालोऽभवत्प्रभुः ॥३॥
transliteration: prajāterabhavatputraḥ kṣupa ityabhiviśrutaḥ ,kṣupasya putrastvikṣvākurmahīpālo'bhavatprabhuḥ.
english: From Prajāti was born a son, well-known as Kshupa. Kshupa's son, in turn, was the powerful king Ikṣvāku.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 3. From Prajāti was born a son, well-known as Kshupa. Kshupa's son, in turn, was the powerful king Ikṣvāku.

sanskrit: तस्य पुत्रशतं राजन्नासीत्परमधार्मिकम् ।तांस्तु सर्वान्महीपालानिक्ष्वाकुरकरोत्प्रभुः ॥४॥
transliteration: tasya putraśataṁ rājannāsītparamadhārmikam ,tāṁstu sarvānmahīpālānikṣvākurakarotprabhuḥ.
english: O King, he had a hundred extremely righteous sons. Ikṣvāku, the mighty ruler, appointed all of them as kings.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 4. O King, he had a hundred extremely righteous sons. Ikṣvāku, the mighty ruler, appointed all of them as kings.

sanskrit: तेषां ज्येष्ठस्तु विंशोऽभूत्प्रतिमानं धनुष्मताम् ।विंशस्य पुत्रः कल्याणो विविंशो नाम भारत ॥५॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ jyeṣṭhastu viṁśo'bhūtpratimānaṁ dhanuṣmatām ,viṁśasya putraḥ kalyāṇo viviṁśo nāma bhārata.
english: Indeed, among those sons, the eldest was Viṃśa, who was an ideal for archers. O Bhārata, Viṃśa's noble son was named Viviṃśa.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 5. Indeed, among those sons, the eldest was Viṃśa, who was an ideal for archers. O Bhārata, Viṃśa's noble son was named Viviṃśa.

sanskrit: विविंशस्य सुता राजन्बभूवुर्दश पञ्च च ।सर्वे धनुषि विक्रान्ता ब्रह्मण्याः सत्यवादिनः ॥६॥
transliteration: viviṁśasya sutā rājanbabhūvurdaśa pañca ca ,sarve dhanuṣi vikrāntā brahmaṇyāḥ satyavādinaḥ.
english: O King, Viviṃśa had fifteen sons. All of them were valiant in archery, devoted to spiritual principles, and truthful.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 6. O King, Viviṃśa had fifteen sons. All of them were valiant in archery, devoted to spiritual principles, and truthful.

sanskrit: दानधर्मरताः सन्तः सततं प्रियवादिनः ।तेषां ज्येष्ठः खनीनेत्रः स तान्सर्वानपीडयत् ॥७॥
transliteration: dānadharmaratāḥ santaḥ satataṁ priyavādinaḥ ,teṣāṁ jyeṣṭhaḥ khanīnetraḥ sa tānsarvānapīḍayat.
english: Virtuous people devoted to the practice of generosity (dāna dharma) and always speaking kindly – their elder, Khanīnetra, oppressed all of them.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 7. Virtuous people devoted to the practice of generosity (dāna dharma) and always speaking kindly – their elder, Khanīnetra, oppressed all of them.

sanskrit: खनीनेत्रस्तु विक्रान्तो जित्वा राज्यमकण्टकम् ।नाशक्नोद्रक्षितुं राज्यं नान्वरज्यन्त तं प्रजाः ॥८॥
transliteration: khanīnetrastu vikrānto jitvā rājyamakaṇṭakam ,nāśaknodrakṣituṁ rājyaṁ nānvarajyanta taṁ prajāḥ.
english: But Khanīnetra, though mighty and having conquered a kingdom free of obstacles, was unable to protect it, and his subjects were not devoted to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 8. But Khanīnetra, though mighty and having conquered a kingdom free of obstacles, was unable to protect it, and his subjects were not devoted to him.

sanskrit: तमपास्य च तद्राष्ट्रं तस्य पुत्रं सुवर्चसम् ।अभ्यषिञ्चत राजेन्द्र मुदितं चाभवत्तदा ॥९॥
transliteration: tamapāsya ca tadrāṣṭraṁ tasya putraṁ suvarcasam ,abhyaṣiñcata rājendra muditaṁ cābhavattadā.
english: And having removed him, O best of kings, that kingdom then consecrated his son Suvarcas, and it became joyful at that time.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 9. And having removed him, O best of kings, that kingdom then consecrated his son Suvarcas, and it became joyful at that time.

sanskrit: स पितुर्विक्रियां दृष्ट्वा राज्यान्निरसनं तथा ।नियतो वर्तयामास प्रजाहितचिकीर्षया ॥१०॥
transliteration: sa piturvikriyāṁ dṛṣṭvā rājyānnirasanaṁ tathā ,niyato vartayāmāsa prajāhitacikīrṣayā.
english: Having witnessed his father's misconduct and his removal from the kingdom, he (Suvarcas) then conducted himself with self-control, motivated by a desire for the welfare of his subjects.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 10. Having witnessed his father's misconduct and his removal from the kingdom, he (Suvarcas) then conducted himself with self-control, motivated by a desire for the welfare of his subjects.

sanskrit: ब्रह्मण्यः सत्यवादी च शुचिः शमदमान्वितः ।प्रजास्तं चान्वरज्यन्त धर्मनित्यं मनस्विनम् ॥११॥
transliteration: brahmaṇyaḥ satyavādī ca śuciḥ śamadamānvitaḥ ,prajāstaṁ cānvarajyanta dharmanityaṁ manasvinam.
english: He was devoted to those who uphold spiritual knowledge, truthful, pure, and endowed with mental tranquility and self-control. And the subjects became attached to that high-minded king who was steadfast in his intrinsic nature (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 11. He was devoted to those who uphold spiritual knowledge, truthful, pure, and endowed with mental tranquility and self-control. And the subjects became attached to that high-minded king who was steadfast in his intrinsic nature (dharma).

sanskrit: तस्य धर्मप्रवृत्तस्य व्यशीर्यत्कोशवाहनम् ।तं क्षीणकोशं सामन्ताः समन्तात्पर्यपीडयन् ॥१२॥
transliteration: tasya dharmapravṛttasya vyaśīryatkośavāhanam ,taṁ kṣīṇakośaṁ sāmantāḥ samantātparyapīḍayan.
english: While he was devoted to his natural law (dharma), his treasury and military strength dwindled. Consequently, neighboring kings, seeing his depleted treasury, harassed him from all sides.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 12. While he was devoted to his natural law (dharma), his treasury and military strength dwindled. Consequently, neighboring kings, seeing his depleted treasury, harassed him from all sides.

sanskrit: स पीड्यमानो बहुभिः क्षीणकोशस्त्ववाहनः ।आर्तिमार्छत्परां राजा सह भृत्यैः पुरेण च ॥१३॥
transliteration: sa pīḍyamāno bahubhiḥ kṣīṇakośastvavāhanaḥ ,ārtimārchatparāṁ rājā saha bhṛtyaiḥ pureṇa ca.
english: Oppressed by many enemies, and with his treasury depleted and no military strength, that king, along with his servants and the entire city, experienced extreme distress.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 13. Oppressed by many enemies, and with his treasury depleted and no military strength, that king, along with his servants and the entire city, experienced extreme distress.

sanskrit: न चैनं परिहर्तुं तेऽशक्नुवन्परिसंक्षये ।सम्यग्वृत्तो हि राजा स धर्मनित्यो युधिष्ठिर ॥१४॥
transliteration: na cainaṁ parihartuṁ te'śaknuvanparisaṁkṣaye ,samyagvṛtto hi rājā sa dharmanityo yudhiṣṭhira.
english: And they were unable to protect him amidst that complete ruin. For that king was indeed of righteous conduct and steadfast in his natural law (dharma), O Yudhiṣṭhira.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 14. And they were unable to protect him amidst that complete ruin. For that king was indeed of righteous conduct and steadfast in his natural law (dharma), O Yudhiṣṭhira.

sanskrit: यदा तु परमामार्तिं गतोऽसौ सपुरो नृपः ।ततः प्रदध्मौ स करं प्रादुरासीत्ततो बलम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: yadā tu paramāmārtiṁ gato'sau sapuro nṛpaḥ ,tataḥ pradadhmau sa karaṁ prādurāsīttato balam.
english: When that king, along with his city, reached extreme distress, he then levied a tax (kara), and thereupon, power (balam) manifested.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 15. When that king, along with his city, reached extreme distress, he then levied a tax (kara), and thereupon, power (balam) manifested.

sanskrit: ततस्तानजयत्सर्वान्प्रातिसीमान्नराधिपान् ।एतस्मात्कारणाद्राजन्विश्रुतः स करंधमः ॥१६॥
transliteration: tatastānajayatsarvānprātisīmānnarādhipān ,etasmātkāraṇādrājanviśrutaḥ sa karaṁdhamaḥ.
english: Then he conquered all the neighboring kings. For this reason, O King, he became renowned as Karaṃdhama (tax-levier).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 16. Then he conquered all the neighboring kings. For this reason, O King, he became renowned as Karaṃdhama (tax-levier).

sanskrit: तस्य कारंधमः पुत्रस्त्रेतायुगमुखेऽभवत् ।इन्द्रादनवरः श्रीमान्देवैरपि सुदुर्जयः ॥१७॥
transliteration: tasya kāraṁdhamaḥ putrastretāyugamukhe'bhavat ,indrādanavaraḥ śrīmāndevairapi sudurjayaḥ.
english: His son, Karaṃdhama, was born at the beginning of the Tretā age (yuga). He was glorious, not inferior to Indra, and very difficult to conquer even for the gods.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 17. His son, Karaṃdhama, was born at the beginning of the Tretā age (yuga). He was glorious, not inferior to Indra, and very difficult to conquer even for the gods.

sanskrit: तस्य सर्वे महीपाला वर्तन्ते स्म वशे तदा ।स हि सम्राडभूत्तेषां वृत्तेन च बलेन च ॥१८॥
transliteration: tasya sarve mahīpālā vartante sma vaśe tadā ,sa hi samrāḍabhūtteṣāṁ vṛttena ca balena ca.
english: At that time, all kings were under his control; for he indeed became their emperor by his excellent conduct and by his power.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 18. At that time, all kings were under his control; for he indeed became their emperor by his excellent conduct and by his power.

sanskrit: अविक्षिन्नाम धर्मात्मा शौर्येणेन्द्रसमोऽभवत् ।यज्ञशीलः कर्मरतिर्धृतिमान्संयतेन्द्रियः ॥१९॥
transliteration: avikṣinnāma dharmātmā śauryeṇendrasamo'bhavat ,yajñaśīlaḥ karmaratirdhṛtimānsaṁyatendriyaḥ.
english: Avikṣit by name, whose intrinsic nature (dharma) was righteousness, became equal to Indra in valor. He was fond of Vedic rituals (yajñaśīla), delighted in action (karma), possessed fortitude, and had controlled his senses.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 19. Avikṣit by name, whose intrinsic nature (dharma) was righteousness, became equal to Indra in valor. He was fond of Vedic rituals (yajñaśīla), delighted in action (karma), possessed fortitude, and had controlled his senses.

sanskrit: तेजसादित्यसदृशः क्षमया पृथिवीसमः ।बृहस्पतिसमो बुद्ध्या हिमवानिव सुस्थिरः ॥२०॥
transliteration: tejasādityasadṛśaḥ kṣamayā pṛthivīsamaḥ ,bṛhaspatisamo buddhyā himavāniva susthiraḥ.
english: He was like the sun in splendor, like the earth in patience, like Bṛhaspati in intellect, and exceedingly stable like the Himālaya mountain.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 20. He was like the sun in splendor, like the earth in patience, like Bṛhaspati in intellect, and exceedingly stable like the Himālaya mountain.

sanskrit: कर्मणा मनसा वाचा दमेन प्रशमेन च ।मनांस्याराधयामास प्रजानां स महीपतिः ॥२१॥
transliteration: karmaṇā manasā vācā damena praśamena ca ,manāṁsyārādhayāmāsa prajānāṁ sa mahīpatiḥ.
english: That king pleased the minds of his subjects by his actions (karma), by his mind, by his speech, by his self-control, and by his tranquility.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 21. That king pleased the minds of his subjects by his actions (karma), by his mind, by his speech, by his self-control, and by his tranquility.

sanskrit: य ईजे हयमेधानां शतेन विधिवत्प्रभुः ।याजयामास यं विद्वान्स्वयमेवाङ्गिराः प्रभुः ॥२२॥
transliteration: ya īje hayamedhānāṁ śatena vidhivatprabhuḥ ,yājayāmāsa yaṁ vidvānsvayamevāṅgirāḥ prabhuḥ.
english: He, the mighty one, who himself performed a hundred horse Vedic rituals (yajña) according to the prescribed rites, and whom the learned Aṅgirās, the powerful one, caused to perform them.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 22. He, the mighty one, who himself performed a hundred horse Vedic rituals (yajña) according to the prescribed rites, and whom the learned Aṅgirās, the powerful one, caused to perform them.

sanskrit: तस्य पुत्रोऽतिचक्राम पितरं गुणवत्तया ।मरुत्तो नाम धर्मज्ञश्चक्रवर्ती महायशाः ॥२३॥
transliteration: tasya putro'ticakrāma pitaraṁ guṇavattayā ,marutto nāma dharmajñaścakravartī mahāyaśāḥ.
english: His son, named Marutta, surpassed his father in virtue. He was a knower of natural law (dharma), an emperor of great fame.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 23. His son, named Marutta, surpassed his father in virtue. He was a knower of natural law (dharma), an emperor of great fame.

sanskrit: नागायुतसमप्राणः साक्षाद्विष्णुरिवापरः ।स यक्ष्यमाणो धर्मात्मा शातकुम्भमयान्युत ।कारयामास शुभ्राणि भाजनानि सहस्रशः ॥२४॥
transliteration: nāgāyutasamaprāṇaḥ sākṣādviṣṇurivāparaḥ ,sa yakṣyamāṇo dharmātmā śātakumbhamayānyuta ,kārayāmāsa śubhrāṇi bhājanāni sahasraśaḥ.
english: He possessed strength equal to ten thousand elephants, truly like another Viṣṇu. That righteous-souled (dharma) king, intending to perform a Vedic ritual (yajña), had thousands of bright, pure gold vessels made.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 24. He possessed strength equal to ten thousand elephants, truly like another Viṣṇu. That righteous-souled (dharma) king, intending to perform a Vedic ritual (yajña), had thousands of bright, pure gold vessels made.

sanskrit: मेरुं पर्वतमासाद्य हिमवत्पार्श्व उत्तरे ।काञ्चनः सुमहान्पादस्तत्र कर्म चकार सः ॥२५॥
transliteration: meruṁ parvatamāsādya himavatpārśva uttare ,kāñcanaḥ sumahānpādastatra karma cakāra saḥ.
english: Having reached Mount Meru, on the northern side of the Himalayas, he constructed there a very great golden base for his ritual (karma).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 25. Having reached Mount Meru, on the northern side of the Himalayas, he constructed there a very great golden base for his ritual (karma).

sanskrit: ततः कुण्डानि पात्रीश्च पिठराण्यासनानि च ।चक्रुः सुवर्णकर्तारो येषां संख्या न विद्यते ॥२६॥
transliteration: tataḥ kuṇḍāni pātrīśca piṭharāṇyāsanāni ca ,cakruḥ suvarṇakartāro yeṣāṁ saṁkhyā na vidyate.
english: Then, gold-smiths made fire-pits, vessels, large pots, and seats, whose number was uncountable.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 26. Then, gold-smiths made fire-pits, vessels, large pots, and seats, whose number was uncountable.

sanskrit: तस्यैव च समीपे स यज्ञवाटो बभूव ह ।ईजे तत्र स धर्मात्मा विधिवत्पृथिवीपतिः ।मरुत्तः सहितैः सर्वैः प्रजापालैर्नराधिपः ॥२७॥
transliteration: tasyaiva ca samīpe sa yajñavāṭo babhūva ha ,īje tatra sa dharmātmā vidhivatpṛthivīpatiḥ ,maruttaḥ sahitaiḥ sarvaiḥ prajāpālairnarādhipaḥ.
english: And indeed, near that very spot, that ground for the Vedic ritual (yajña) came into existence. There, that righteous king (dharma-ātman), the lord of the earth, King Marutta, along with all the protectors of the people, performed a Vedic ritual (yajña) according to the prescribed rites.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 4 Verse 27. And indeed, near that very spot, that ground for the Vedic ritual (yajña) came into existence. There, that righteous king (dharma-ātman), the lord of the earth, King Marutta, along with all the protectors of the people, performed a Vedic ritual (yajña) according to the prescribed rites.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।कथंवीर्यः समभवत्स राजा वदतां वरः ।कथं च जातरूपेण समयुज्यत स द्विज ॥१॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,kathaṁvīryaḥ samabhavatsa rājā vadatāṁ varaḥ ,kathaṁ ca jātarūpeṇa samayujyata sa dvija.
english: Yudhishthira said: 'O best of speakers, what kind of power did that king possess? And O twice-born (dvija), how did he become associated with gold?'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 1. Yudhishthira said: 'O best of speakers, what kind of power did that king possess? And O twice-born (dvija), how did he become associated with gold?'

sanskrit: क्व च तत्सांप्रतं द्रव्यं भगवन्नवतिष्ठते ।कथं च शक्यमस्माभिस्तदवाप्तुं तपोधन ॥२॥
transliteration: kva ca tatsāṁprataṁ dravyaṁ bhagavannavatiṣṭhate ,kathaṁ ca śakyamasmābhistadavāptuṁ tapodhana.
english: O revered one, where does that wealth currently exist? And O ascetic, how is it possible for us to obtain it?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 2. O revered one, where does that wealth currently exist? And O ascetic, how is it possible for us to obtain it?

sanskrit: व्यास उवाच ।असुराश्चैव देवाश्च दक्षस्यासन्प्रजापतेः ।अपत्यं बहुलं तात तेऽस्पर्धन्त परस्परम् ॥३॥
transliteration: vyāsa uvāca ,asurāścaiva devāśca dakṣasyāsanprajāpateḥ ,apatyaṁ bahulaṁ tāta te'spardhanta parasparam.
english: Vyasa said: 'O dear one, the Asuras and the Devas were indeed the numerous progeny of Daksha Prajapati. They contended with each other.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 3. Vyasa said: 'O dear one, the Asuras and the Devas were indeed the numerous progeny of Daksha Prajapati. They contended with each other.'

sanskrit: तथैवाङ्गिरसः पुत्रौ व्रततुल्यौ बभूवतुः ।बृहस्पतिर्बृहत्तेजाः संवर्तश्च तपोधनः ॥४॥
transliteration: tathaivāṅgirasaḥ putrau vratatulyau babhūvatuḥ ,bṛhaspatirbṛhattejāḥ saṁvartaśca tapodhanaḥ.
english: Similarly, two sons of Angiras were born, equal in their sacred vows (vrata): Bṛhaspati, of immense brilliance, and Saṃvarta, whose wealth was spiritual discipline (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 4. Similarly, two sons of Angiras were born, equal in their sacred vows (vrata): Bṛhaspati, of immense brilliance, and Saṃvarta, whose wealth was spiritual discipline (tapas).

sanskrit: तावपि स्पर्धिनौ राजन्पृथगास्तां परस्परम् ।बृहस्पतिश्च संवर्तं बाधते स्म पुनः पुनः ॥५॥
transliteration: tāvapi spardhinau rājanpṛthagāstāṁ parasparam ,bṛhaspatiśca saṁvartaṁ bādhate sma punaḥ punaḥ.
english: O King, those two, too, were rivals, living separately from each other. Bṛhaspati repeatedly harassed Saṃvarta.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 5. O King, those two, too, were rivals, living separately from each other. Bṛhaspati repeatedly harassed Saṃvarta.

sanskrit: स बाध्यमानः सततं भ्रात्रा ज्येष्ठेन भारत ।अर्थानुत्सृज्य दिग्वासा वनवासमरोचयत् ॥६॥
transliteration: sa bādhyamānaḥ satataṁ bhrātrā jyeṣṭhena bhārata ,arthānutsṛjya digvāsā vanavāsamarocayat.
english: O Bhārata, he (Saṃvarta), being constantly harassed by his elder brother, gave up all his worldly possessions (artha), became an ascetic with only the directions as his garments, and chose to live in the forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 6. O Bhārata, he (Saṃvarta), being constantly harassed by his elder brother, gave up all his worldly possessions (artha), became an ascetic with only the directions as his garments, and chose to live in the forest.

sanskrit: वासवोऽप्यसुरान्सर्वान्निर्जित्य च निहत्य च ।इन्द्रत्वं प्राप्य लोकेषु ततो वव्रे पुरोहितम् ।पुत्रमङ्गिरसो ज्येष्ठं विप्रश्रेष्ठं बृहस्पतिम् ॥७॥
transliteration: vāsavo'pyasurānsarvānnirjitya ca nihatya ca ,indratvaṁ prāpya lokeṣu tato vavre purohitam ,putramaṅgiraso jyeṣṭhaṁ vipraśreṣṭhaṁ bṛhaspatim.
english: Having conquered and slain all the asuras (demons), Vāsava (Indra) also attained the position of Indra in the worlds. Then he chose as his preceptor (purohita) the elder son of Angiras, Bṛhaspati, who was the foremost among brahmins.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 7. Having conquered and slain all the asuras (demons), Vāsava (Indra) also attained the position of Indra in the worlds. Then he chose as his preceptor (purohita) the elder son of Angiras, Bṛhaspati, who was the foremost among brahmins.

sanskrit: याज्यस्त्वङ्गिरसः पूर्वमासीद्राजा करंधमः ।वीर्येणाप्रतिमो लोके वृत्तेन च बलेन च ।शतक्रतुरिवौजस्वी धर्मात्मा संशितव्रतः ॥८॥
transliteration: yājyastvaṅgirasaḥ pūrvamāsīdrājā karaṁdhamaḥ ,vīryeṇāpratimo loke vṛttena ca balena ca ,śatakraturivaujasvī dharmātmā saṁśitavrataḥ.
english: Formerly, there was King Karandhama, who was a minister to the sage Angiras. He was unequaled in the world in valor, character, and strength. He was as powerful as Indra (Śatakratu), had a righteous disposition (dharma), and was resolute in his vows.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 8. Formerly, there was King Karandhama, who was a minister to the sage Angiras. He was unequaled in the world in valor, character, and strength. He was as powerful as Indra (Śatakratu), had a righteous disposition (dharma), and was resolute in his vows.

sanskrit: वाहनं यस्य योधाश्च द्रव्याणि विविधानि च ।ध्यानादेवाभवद्राजन्मुखवातेन सर्वशः ॥९॥
transliteration: vāhanaṁ yasya yodhāśca dravyāṇi vividhāni ca ,dhyānādevābhavadrājanmukhavātena sarvaśaḥ.
english: O King, his conveyances, warriors, and various provisions came into being entirely by his mere thought and the breath of his mouth.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 9. O King, his conveyances, warriors, and various provisions came into being entirely by his mere thought and the breath of his mouth.

sanskrit: स गुणैः पार्थिवान्सर्वान्वशे चक्रे नराधिपः ।संजीव्य कालमिष्टं च सशरीरो दिवं गतः ॥१०॥
transliteration: sa guṇaiḥ pārthivānsarvānvaśe cakre narādhipaḥ ,saṁjīvya kālamiṣṭaṁ ca saśarīro divaṁ gataḥ.
english: That lord of men brought all kings under his control by his virtues. After living his desired lifespan, he ascended to heaven with his physical body.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 10. That lord of men brought all kings under his control by his virtues. After living his desired lifespan, he ascended to heaven with his physical body.

sanskrit: बभूव तस्य पुत्रस्तु ययातिरिव धर्मवित् ।अविक्षिन्नाम शत्रुक्षित्स वशे कृतवान्महीम् ।विक्रमेण गुणैश्चैव पितेवासीत्स पार्थिवः ॥११॥
transliteration: babhūva tasya putrastu yayātiriva dharmavit ,avikṣinnāma śatrukṣitsa vaśe kṛtavānmahīm ,vikrameṇa guṇaiścaiva pitevāsītsa pārthivaḥ.
english: Indeed, his son, named Avikṣit, who was a destroyer of enemies and knowledgeable in (dharma) like Yayati, was born. He brought the earth under his control. That king was like his father in valor and also in his virtues.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 11. Indeed, his son, named Avikṣit, who was a destroyer of enemies and knowledgeable in (dharma) like Yayati, was born. He brought the earth under his control. That king was like his father in valor and also in his virtues.

sanskrit: तस्य वासवतुल्योऽभून्मरुत्तो नाम वीर्यवान् ।पुत्रस्तमनुरक्ताभूत्पृथिवी सागराम्बरा ॥१२॥
transliteration: tasya vāsavatulyo'bhūnmarutto nāma vīryavān ,putrastamanuraktābhūtpṛthivī sāgarāmbarā.
english: His son, valorous and comparable to Indra (Vāsava), was named Marutta. The earth, clad in oceans, became devoted to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 12. His son, valorous and comparable to Indra (Vāsava), was named Marutta. The earth, clad in oceans, became devoted to him.

sanskrit: स्पर्धते सततं स स्म देवराजेन पार्थिवः ।वासवोऽपि मरुत्तेन स्पर्धते पाण्डुनन्दन ॥१३॥
transliteration: spardhate satataṁ sa sma devarājena pārthivaḥ ,vāsavo'pi maruttena spardhate pāṇḍunandana.
english: That king constantly vied with the king of the gods, Indra (Vāsava). O son of Pāṇḍu, Vāsava himself also vied with Marutta.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 13. That king constantly vied with the king of the gods, Indra (Vāsava). O son of Pāṇḍu, Vāsava himself also vied with Marutta.

sanskrit: शुचिः स गुणवानासीन्मरुत्तः पृथिवीपतिः ।यतमानोऽपि यं शक्रो न विशेषयति स्म ह ॥१४॥
transliteration: śuciḥ sa guṇavānāsīnmaruttaḥ pṛthivīpatiḥ ,yatamāno'pi yaṁ śakro na viśeṣayati sma ha.
english: That lord of the earth, Marutta, was pure and virtuous. Even when striving, Śakra could not surpass him.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 14. That lord of the earth, Marutta, was pure and virtuous. Even when striving, Śakra could not surpass him.

sanskrit: सोऽशक्नुवन्विशेषाय समाहूय बृहस्पतिम् ।उवाचेदं वचो देवैः सहितो हरिवाहनः ॥१५॥
transliteration: so'śaknuvanviśeṣāya samāhūya bṛhaspatim ,uvācedaṁ vaco devaiḥ sahito harivāhanaḥ.
english: Unable to achieve superiority, Harivāhana (Indra), accompanied by the gods, summoned Bṛhaspati and spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 15. Unable to achieve superiority, Harivāhana (Indra), accompanied by the gods, summoned Bṛhaspati and spoke these words.

sanskrit: बृहस्पते मरुत्तस्य मा स्म कार्षीः कथंचन ।दैवं कर्माथ वा पित्र्यं कर्तासि मम चेत्प्रियम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: bṛhaspate maruttasya mā sma kārṣīḥ kathaṁcana ,daivaṁ karmātha vā pitryaṁ kartāsi mama cetpriyam.
english: O Bṛhaspati, by no means perform any divine or ancestral ritual (karma) for Marutta. If you consider me dear, you will be my ritual performer.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 16. O Bṛhaspati, by no means perform any divine or ancestral ritual (karma) for Marutta. If you consider me dear, you will be my ritual performer.

sanskrit: अहं हि त्रिषु लोकेषु सुराणां च बृहस्पते ।इन्द्रत्वं प्राप्तवानेको मरुत्तस्तु महीपतिः ॥१७॥
transliteration: ahaṁ hi triṣu lokeṣu surāṇāṁ ca bṛhaspate ,indratvaṁ prāptavāneko maruttastu mahīpatiḥ.
english: Indeed, O Bṛhaspati, I alone have attained the position of Indra in the three worlds and among the gods. Marutta, however, is merely a king.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 17. Indeed, O Bṛhaspati, I alone have attained the position of Indra in the three worlds and among the gods. Marutta, however, is merely a king.

sanskrit: कथं ह्यमर्त्यं ब्रह्मंस्त्वं याजयित्वा सुराधिपम् ।याजयेर्मृत्युसंयुक्तं मरुत्तमविशङ्कया ॥१८॥
transliteration: kathaṁ hyamartyaṁ brahmaṁstvaṁ yājayitvā surādhipam ,yājayermṛtyusaṁyuktaṁ maruttamaviśaṅkayā.
english: O Brahmin, how can you, after officiating for me, the immortal lord of the gods, then without any hesitation officiate for Marutta, who is bound to mortality?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 18. O Brahmin, how can you, after officiating for me, the immortal lord of the gods, then without any hesitation officiate for Marutta, who is bound to mortality?

sanskrit: मां वा वृणीष्व भद्रं ते मरुत्तं वा महीपतिम् ।परित्यज्य मरुत्तं वा यथाजोषं भजस्व माम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: māṁ vā vṛṇīṣva bhadraṁ te maruttaṁ vā mahīpatim ,parityajya maruttaṁ vā yathājoṣaṁ bhajasva mām.
english: Choose either me or King Marutta; may good fortune be yours. Alternatively, abandon Marutta and serve me as you deem fit.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 19. Choose either me or King Marutta; may good fortune be yours. Alternatively, abandon Marutta and serve me as you deem fit.

sanskrit: एवमुक्तः स कौरव्य देवराज्ञा बृहस्पतिः ।मुहूर्तमिव संचिन्त्य देवराजानमब्रवीत् ॥२०॥
transliteration: evamuktaḥ sa kauravya devarājñā bṛhaspatiḥ ,muhūrtamiva saṁcintya devarājānamabravīt.
english: O descendant of Kuru, thus addressed by the king of the gods, Bṛhaspati reflected for a moment and then spoke to Indra.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 20. O descendant of Kuru, thus addressed by the king of the gods, Bṛhaspati reflected for a moment and then spoke to Indra.

sanskrit: त्वं भूतानामधिपतिस्त्वयि लोकाः प्रतिष्ठिताः ।नमुचेर्विश्वरूपस्य निहन्ता त्वं बलस्य च ॥२१॥
transliteration: tvaṁ bhūtānāmadhipatistvayi lokāḥ pratiṣṭhitāḥ ,namucerviśvarūpasya nihantā tvaṁ balasya ca.
english: You are the lord of all beings; in you the worlds are established. You are the slayer of Namuci, Viśvarūpa, and Bala.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 21. You are the lord of all beings; in you the worlds are established. You are the slayer of Namuci, Viśvarūpa, and Bala.

sanskrit: त्वमाजहर्थ देवानामेको वीर श्रियं पराम् ।त्वं बिभर्षि भुवं द्यां च सदैव बलसूदन ॥२२॥
transliteration: tvamājahartha devānāmeko vīra śriyaṁ parām ,tvaṁ bibharṣi bhuvaṁ dyāṁ ca sadaiva balasūdana.
english: O hero, you alone retrieved the supreme glory for the gods. O slayer of Bala, you always sustain both the earth and heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 22. O hero, you alone retrieved the supreme glory for the gods. O slayer of Bala, you always sustain both the earth and heaven.

sanskrit: पौरोहित्यं कथं कृत्वा तव देवगणेश्वर ।याजयेयमहं मर्त्यं मरुत्तं पाकशासन ॥२३॥
transliteration: paurohityaṁ kathaṁ kṛtvā tava devagaṇeśvara ,yājayeyamahaṁ martyaṁ maruttaṁ pākaśāsana.
english: O lord of the host of gods, O Indra, how can I, having served as your priest, cause a mortal, King Marutta, to perform a "Vedic ritual (yajña)"?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 23. O lord of the host of gods, O Indra, how can I, having served as your priest, cause a mortal, King Marutta, to perform a "Vedic ritual (yajña)"?

sanskrit: समाश्वसिहि देवेश नाहं मर्त्याय कर्हिचित् ।ग्रहीष्यामि स्रुवं यज्ञे शृणु चेदं वचो मम ॥२४॥
transliteration: samāśvasihi deveśa nāhaṁ martyāya karhicit ,grahīṣyāmi sruvaṁ yajñe śṛṇu cedaṁ vaco mama.
english: Be at ease, O Lord of gods. I will never, at any time, take the sacrificial ladle for a mortal in a Vedic ritual (yajña). And listen to these words of mine.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 24. Be at ease, O Lord of gods. I will never, at any time, take the sacrificial ladle for a mortal in a Vedic ritual (yajña). And listen to these words of mine.

sanskrit: हिरण्यरेतसोऽम्भः स्यात्परिवर्तेत मेदिनी ।भासं च न रविः कुर्यान्मत्सत्यं विचलेद्यदि ॥२५॥
transliteration: hiraṇyaretaso'mbhaḥ syātparivarteta medinī ,bhāsaṁ ca na raviḥ kuryānmatsatyaṁ vicaledyadi.
english: If my truth were to waver, then the fiery essence might become water, the earth might be overturned, and the sun would not emit light.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 25. If my truth were to waver, then the fiery essence might become water, the earth might be overturned, and the sun would not emit light.

sanskrit: बृहस्पतिवचः श्रुत्वा शक्रो विगतमत्सरः ।प्रशस्यैनं विवेशाथ स्वमेव भवनं तदा ॥२६॥
transliteration: bṛhaspativacaḥ śrutvā śakro vigatamatsaraḥ ,praśasyainaṁ viveśātha svameva bhavanaṁ tadā.
english: Having heard the words of Bṛhaspati, Indra, who was then free from envy, praised him and entered his own palace.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 5 Verse 26. Having heard the words of Bṛhaspati, Indra, who was then free from envy, praised him and entered his own palace.

sanskrit: व्यास उवाच ।अत्राप्युदाहरन्तीममितिहासं पुरातनम् ।बृहस्पतेश्च संवादं मरुत्तस्य च भारत ॥१॥
transliteration: vyāsa uvāca ,atrāpyudāharantīmamitihāsaṁ purātanam ,bṛhaspateśca saṁvādaṁ maruttasya ca bhārata.
english: Vyāsa said: In this regard, they also narrate this ancient historical account (itihāsa): the dialogue between Bṛhaspati and Marutta, O Bhārata.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 1. Vyāsa said: In this regard, they also narrate this ancient historical account (itihāsa): the dialogue between Bṛhaspati and Marutta, O Bhārata.

sanskrit: देवराजस्य समयं कृतमाङ्गिरसेन ह ।श्रुत्वा मरुत्तो नृपतिर्मन्युमाहारयत्तदा ॥२॥
transliteration: devarājasya samayaṁ kṛtamāṅgirasena ha ,śrutvā marutto nṛpatirmanyumāhārayattadā.
english: Indeed, upon hearing that the agreement of the king of gods had been made by Angirasa (Bṛhaspati), King Marutta then became angry.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 2. Indeed, upon hearing that the agreement of the king of gods had been made by Angirasa (Bṛhaspati), King Marutta then became angry.

sanskrit: संकल्प्य मनसा यज्ञं करंधमसुतात्मजः ।बृहस्पतिमुपागम्य वाग्मी वचनमब्रवीत् ॥३॥
transliteration: saṁkalpya manasā yajñaṁ karaṁdhamasutātmajaḥ ,bṛhaspatimupāgamya vāgmī vacanamabravīt.
english: Having resolved in his mind to perform a (Vedic ritual) (yajña), the eloquent son of Karandhama's daughter approached Bṛhaspati and spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 3. Having resolved in his mind to perform a (Vedic ritual) (yajña), the eloquent son of Karandhama's daughter approached Bṛhaspati and spoke these words.

sanskrit: भगवन्यन्मया पूर्वमभिगम्य तपोधन ।कृतोऽभिसंधिर्यज्ञाय भवतो वचनाद्गुरो ॥४॥
transliteration: bhagavanyanmayā pūrvamabhigamya tapodhana ,kṛto'bhisaṁdhiryajñāya bhavato vacanādguro.
english: O revered (bhagavan) ascetic (tapodhana) teacher (guru), that resolution (abhisaṃdhi) for a (Vedic ritual) (yajña) which I made previously, after having approached you and on your advice.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 4. O revered (bhagavan) ascetic (tapodhana) teacher (guru), that resolution (abhisaṃdhi) for a (Vedic ritual) (yajña) which I made previously, after having approached you and on your advice.

sanskrit: तमहं यष्टुमिच्छामि संभाराः संभृताश्च मे ।याज्योऽस्मि भवतः साधो तत्प्राप्नुहि विधत्स्व च ॥५॥
transliteration: tamahaṁ yaṣṭumicchāmi saṁbhārāḥ saṁbhṛtāśca me ,yājyo'smi bhavataḥ sādho tatprāpnuhi vidhatsva ca.
english: I wish to perform that (Vedic ritual) (yajña), and my provisions have been collected. O virtuous one (sādhu), I am fit to be officiated by you. Therefore, accept (my offer) and carry it out.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 5. I wish to perform that (Vedic ritual) (yajña), and my provisions have been collected. O virtuous one (sādhu), I am fit to be officiated by you. Therefore, accept (my offer) and carry it out.

sanskrit: बृहस्पतिरुवाच ।न कामये याजयितुं त्वामहं पृथिवीपते ।वृतोऽस्मि देवराजेन प्रतिज्ञातं च तस्य मे ॥६॥
transliteration: bṛhaspatiruvāca ,na kāmaye yājayituṁ tvāmahaṁ pṛthivīpate ,vṛto'smi devarājena pratijñātaṁ ca tasya me.
english: Bṛhaspati said: 'O ruler of the earth, I do not desire to officiate your Vedic ritual (yajña). I have been chosen by the king of the gods, and I have made a promise to him.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 6. Bṛhaspati said: 'O ruler of the earth, I do not desire to officiate your Vedic ritual (yajña). I have been chosen by the king of the gods, and I have made a promise to him.'

sanskrit: मरुत्त उवाच ।पित्र्यमस्मि तव क्षेत्रं बहु मन्ये च ते भृशम् ।न चास्म्ययाज्यतां प्राप्तो भजमानं भजस्व माम् ॥७॥
transliteration: marutta uvāca ,pitryamasmi tava kṣetraṁ bahu manye ca te bhṛśam ,na cāsmyayājyatāṁ prāpto bhajamānaṁ bhajasva mām.
english: Marutta said: 'I am your family's hereditary patron (kṣetra), and I hold you in high regard. I have not become unworthy of having a Vedic ritual (yajña) performed for me. Therefore, serve me, your devoted follower.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 7. Marutta said: 'I am your family's hereditary patron (kṣetra), and I hold you in high regard. I have not become unworthy of having a Vedic ritual (yajña) performed for me. Therefore, serve me, your devoted follower.'

sanskrit: बृहस्पतिरुवाच ।अमर्त्यं याजयित्वाहं याजयिष्ये न मानुषम् ।मरुत्त गच्छ वा मा वा निवृत्तोऽस्म्यद्य याजनात् ॥८॥
transliteration: bṛhaspatiruvāca ,amartyaṁ yājayitvāhaṁ yājayiṣye na mānuṣam ,marutta gaccha vā mā vā nivṛtto'smyadya yājanāt.
english: Bṛhaspati said: 'After officiating for an immortal, I will not officiate for a mortal. O Marutta, go or stay; I have now withdrawn from officiating Vedic rituals.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 8. Bṛhaspati said: 'After officiating for an immortal, I will not officiate for a mortal. O Marutta, go or stay; I have now withdrawn from officiating Vedic rituals.'

sanskrit: न त्वां याजयितास्म्यद्य वृणु त्वं यमिहेच्छसि ।उपाध्यायं महाबाहो यस्ते यज्ञं करिष्यति ॥९॥
transliteration: na tvāṁ yājayitāsmyadya vṛṇu tvaṁ yamihecchasi ,upādhyāyaṁ mahābāho yaste yajñaṁ kariṣyati.
english: I will not officiate for you today. O mighty-armed one, you choose here the preceptor (upādhyāya) whom you desire, one who will perform the Vedic ritual (yajña) for you.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 9. I will not officiate for you today. O mighty-armed one, you choose here the preceptor (upādhyāya) whom you desire, one who will perform the Vedic ritual (yajña) for you.

sanskrit: व्यास उवाच ।एवमुक्तस्तु नृपतिर्मरुत्तो व्रीडितोऽभवत् ।प्रत्यागच्छच्च संविग्नो ददर्श पथि नारदम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: vyāsa uvāca ,evamuktastu nṛpatirmarutto vrīḍito'bhavat ,pratyāgacchacca saṁvigno dadarśa pathi nāradam.
english: Vyasa said: After being addressed in this manner, King Marutta became embarrassed. Distressed, he returned and on the path, he saw Narada.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 10. Vyasa said: After being addressed in this manner, King Marutta became embarrassed. Distressed, he returned and on the path, he saw Narada.

sanskrit: देवर्षिणा समागम्य नारदेन स पार्थिवः ।विधिवत्प्राञ्जलिस्तस्थावथैनं नारदोऽब्रवीत् ॥११॥
transliteration: devarṣiṇā samāgamya nāradena sa pārthivaḥ ,vidhivatprāñjalistasthāvathainaṁ nārado'bravīt.
english: Having met the divine sage Narada, that king stood with folded hands, as prescribed. Then Narada spoke to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 11. Having met the divine sage Narada, that king stood with folded hands, as prescribed. Then Narada spoke to him.

sanskrit: राजर्षे नातिहृष्टोऽसि कच्चित्क्षेमं तवानघ ।क्व गतोऽसि कुतो वेदमप्रीतिस्थानमागतम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: rājarṣe nātihṛṣṭo'si kaccitkṣemaṁ tavānagha ,kva gato'si kuto vedamaprītisthānamāgatam.
english: O royal sage, you do not seem very joyful. O sinless one, I hope you are well. Where have you been, or from where has this cause for unhappiness come?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 12. O royal sage, you do not seem very joyful. O sinless one, I hope you are well. Where have you been, or from where has this cause for unhappiness come?

sanskrit: श्रोतव्यं चेन्मया राजन्ब्रूहि मे पार्थिवर्षभ ।व्यपनेष्यामि ते मन्युं सर्वयत्नैर्नराधिप ॥१३॥
transliteration: śrotavyaṁ cenmayā rājanbrūhi me pārthivarṣabha ,vyapaneṣyāmi te manyuṁ sarvayatnairnarādhipa.
english: O King, best among rulers, if it ought to be heard by me, please tell it to me. O lord of men, I shall remove your sorrow (manyu) with all my efforts.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 13. O King, best among rulers, if it ought to be heard by me, please tell it to me. O lord of men, I shall remove your sorrow (manyu) with all my efforts.

sanskrit: एवमुक्तो मरुत्तस्तु नारदेन महर्षिणा ।विप्रलम्भमुपाध्यायात्सर्वमेव न्यवेदयत् ॥१४॥
transliteration: evamukto maruttastu nāradena maharṣiṇā ,vipralambhamupādhyāyātsarvameva nyavedayat.
english: Thus addressed by the great sage (maharṣi) Nārada, Marutta reported everything concerning the rejection he experienced from his preceptor.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 14. Thus addressed by the great sage (maharṣi) Nārada, Marutta reported everything concerning the rejection he experienced from his preceptor.

sanskrit: गतोऽस्म्यङ्गिरसः पुत्रं देवाचार्यं बृहस्पतिम् ।यज्ञार्थमृत्विजं द्रष्टुं स च मां नाभ्यनन्दत ॥१५॥
transliteration: gato'smyaṅgirasaḥ putraṁ devācāryaṁ bṛhaspatim ,yajñārthamṛtvijaṁ draṣṭuṁ sa ca māṁ nābhyanandata.
english: I went to Bṛhaspati, the son of Aṅgiras and preceptor of the gods, to seek him as a priest for a Vedic ritual (yajña), but he did not welcome me.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 15. I went to Bṛhaspati, the son of Aṅgiras and preceptor of the gods, to seek him as a priest for a Vedic ritual (yajña), but he did not welcome me.

sanskrit: प्रत्याख्यातश्च तेनाहं जीवितुं नाद्य कामये ।परित्यक्तश्च गुरुणा दूषितश्चास्मि नारद ॥१६॥
transliteration: pratyākhyātaśca tenāhaṁ jīvituṁ nādya kāmaye ,parityaktaśca guruṇā dūṣitaścāsmi nārada.
english: Rejected by him, I no longer wish to live. O Nārada, I have been abandoned by my preceptor and thus disgraced.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 16. Rejected by him, I no longer wish to live. O Nārada, I have been abandoned by my preceptor and thus disgraced.

sanskrit: एवमुक्तस्तु राज्ञा स नारदः प्रत्युवाच ह ।आविक्षितं महाराज वाचा संजीवयन्निव ॥१७॥
transliteration: evamuktastu rājñā sa nāradaḥ pratyuvāca ha ,āvikṣitaṁ mahārāja vācā saṁjīvayanniva.
english: Thus addressed by the king, Nārada replied to Āvikṣita, O great king, as if reviving him with his words.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 17. Thus addressed by the king, Nārada replied to Āvikṣita, O great king, as if reviving him with his words.

sanskrit: राजन्नङ्गिरसः पुत्रः संवर्तो नाम धार्मिकः ।चङ्क्रमीति दिशः सर्वा दिग्वासा मोहयन्प्रजाः ॥१८॥
transliteration: rājannaṅgirasaḥ putraḥ saṁvarto nāma dhārmikaḥ ,caṅkramīti diśaḥ sarvā digvāsā mohayanprajāḥ.
english: O King, the son of Aṅgiras named Saṃvarta, who adheres to (dharma), wanders about in all directions, naked, bewildering the people.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 18. O King, the son of Aṅgiras named Saṃvarta, who adheres to (dharma), wanders about in all directions, naked, bewildering the people.

sanskrit: तं गच्छ यदि याज्यं त्वां न वाञ्छति बृहस्पतिः ।प्रसन्नस्त्वां महाराज संवर्तो याजयिष्यति ॥१९॥
transliteration: taṁ gaccha yadi yājyaṁ tvāṁ na vāñchati bṛhaspatiḥ ,prasannastvāṁ mahārāja saṁvarto yājayiṣyati.
english: Go to him if Bṛhaspati does not want to perform the (yajña) for you. O great king, Saṃvarta, being pleased, will conduct the (yajña) for you.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 19. Go to him if Bṛhaspati does not want to perform the (yajña) for you. O great king, Saṃvarta, being pleased, will conduct the (yajña) for you.

sanskrit: मरुत्त उवाच ।संजीवितोऽहं भवता वाक्येनानेन नारद ।पश्येयं क्व नु संवर्तं शंस मे वदतां वर ॥२०॥
transliteration: marutta uvāca ,saṁjīvito'haṁ bhavatā vākyenānena nārada ,paśyeyaṁ kva nu saṁvartaṁ śaṁsa me vadatāṁ vara.
english: Marutta said: 'O Nārada, I have been revived by these words of yours. Where, indeed, might I see Saṃvarta? Tell me, O best of speakers!'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 20. Marutta said: 'O Nārada, I have been revived by these words of yours. Where, indeed, might I see Saṃvarta? Tell me, O best of speakers!'

sanskrit: कथं च तस्मै वर्तेयं कथं मां न परित्यजेत् ।प्रत्याख्यातश्च तेनापि नाहं जीवितुमुत्सहे ॥२१॥
transliteration: kathaṁ ca tasmai varteyaṁ kathaṁ māṁ na parityajet ,pratyākhyātaśca tenāpi nāhaṁ jīvitumutsahe.
english: And how should I act towards him? How can I ensure he does not abandon me? For if I am rejected by him too, I would not dare to live.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 21. And how should I act towards him? How can I ensure he does not abandon me? For if I am rejected by him too, I would not dare to live.

sanskrit: नारद उवाच ।उन्मत्तवेषं बिभ्रत्स चङ्क्रमीति यथासुखम् ।वाराणसीं तु नगरीमभीक्ष्णमुपसेवते ॥२२॥
