transliteration: nārada uvāca ,unmattaveṣaṁ bibhratsa caṅkramīti yathāsukham ,vārāṇasīṁ tu nagarīmabhīkṣṇamupasevate.
english: Nārada said: "Bearing the guise of a madman, he wanders about as he pleases, but he frequently resorts to the city of Vārāṇasī."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 22. Nārada said: "Bearing the guise of a madman, he wanders about as he pleases, but he frequently resorts to the city of Vārāṇasī."

sanskrit: तस्या द्वारं समासाद्य न्यसेथाः कुणपं क्वचित् ।तं दृष्ट्वा यो निवर्तेत स संवर्तो महीपते ॥२३॥
transliteration: tasyā dvāraṁ samāsādya nyasethāḥ kuṇapaṁ kvacit ,taṁ dṛṣṭvā yo nivarteta sa saṁvarto mahīpate.
english: Having reached the door of that (city), you should place a corpse somewhere. O king, whoever turns back after seeing that [corpse] – he is Saṃvarta.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 23. Having reached the door of that (city), you should place a corpse somewhere. O king, whoever turns back after seeing that [corpse] – he is Saṃvarta.

sanskrit: तं पृष्ठतोऽनुगच्छेथा यत्र गच्छेत्स वीर्यवान् ।तमेकान्ते समासाद्य प्राञ्जलिः शरणं व्रजेः ॥२४॥
transliteration: taṁ pṛṣṭhato'nugacchethā yatra gacchetsa vīryavān ,tamekānte samāsādya prāñjaliḥ śaraṇaṁ vrajeḥ.
english: You should follow him from behind wherever that mighty one goes. Having approached him in a secluded place, you should, with folded hands, seek refuge (śaraṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 24. You should follow him from behind wherever that mighty one goes. Having approached him in a secluded place, you should, with folded hands, seek refuge (śaraṇa).

sanskrit: पृच्छेत्त्वां यदि केनाहं तवाख्यात इति स्म ह ।ब्रूयास्त्वं नारदेनेति संतप्त इव शत्रुहन् ॥२५॥
transliteration: pṛcchettvāṁ yadi kenāhaṁ tavākhyāta iti sma ha ,brūyāstvaṁ nāradeneti saṁtapta iva śatruhan.
english: If he asks you, 'By whom have I been told to you?', you should say, 'By Nārada,' as if distressed, O destroyer of enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 25. If he asks you, 'By whom have I been told to you?', you should say, 'By Nārada,' as if distressed, O destroyer of enemies.

sanskrit: स चेत्त्वामनुयुञ्जीत ममाभिगमनेप्सया ।शंसेथा वह्निमारूढं मामपि त्वमशङ्कया ॥२६॥
transliteration: sa cettvāmanuyuñjīta mamābhigamanepsayā ,śaṁsethā vahnimārūḍhaṁ māmapi tvamaśaṅkayā.
english: If he questions you with the desire to approach me, you should tell him, without hesitation, that I have ascended the fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 26. If he questions you with the desire to approach me, you should tell him, without hesitation, that I have ascended the fire.

sanskrit: व्यास उवाच ।स तथेति प्रतिश्रुत्य पूजयित्वा च नारदम् ।अभ्यनुज्ञाय राजर्षिर्ययौ वाराणसीं पुरीम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: vyāsa uvāca ,sa tatheti pratiśrutya pūjayitvā ca nāradam ,abhyanujñāya rājarṣiryayau vārāṇasīṁ purīm.
english: Vyāsa said: That royal sage (rājārṣi), having promised, 'So be it,' and having honored Nārada and taken his leave, went to the city of Vārāṇasī.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 27. Vyāsa said: That royal sage (rājārṣi), having promised, 'So be it,' and having honored Nārada and taken his leave, went to the city of Vārāṇasī.

sanskrit: तत्र गत्वा यथोक्तं स पुर्या द्वारे महायशाः ।कुणपं स्थापयामास नारदस्य वचः स्मरन् ॥२८॥
transliteration: tatra gatvā yathoktaṁ sa puryā dvāre mahāyaśāḥ ,kuṇapaṁ sthāpayāmāsa nāradasya vacaḥ smaran.
english: Having gone there, that illustrious one (the royal sage), as instructed, placed a corpse at the city gate, remembering Nārada's words.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 28. Having gone there, that illustrious one (the royal sage), as instructed, placed a corpse at the city gate, remembering Nārada's words.

sanskrit: यौगपद्येन विप्रश्च स पुरीद्वारमाविशत् ।ततः स कुणपं दृष्ट्वा सहसा स न्यवर्तत ॥२९॥
transliteration: yaugapadyena vipraśca sa purīdvāramāviśat ,tataḥ sa kuṇapaṁ dṛṣṭvā sahasā sa nyavartata.
english: And simultaneously, that brahmin (vipra) entered the city gate. Then, upon seeing the corpse, he suddenly turned back.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 29. And simultaneously, that brahmin (vipra) entered the city gate. Then, upon seeing the corpse, he suddenly turned back.

sanskrit: स तं निवृत्तमालक्ष्य प्राञ्जलिः पृष्ठतोऽन्वगात् ।आविक्षितो महीपालः संवर्तमुपशिक्षितुम् ॥३०॥
transliteration: sa taṁ nivṛttamālakṣya prāñjaliḥ pṛṣṭhato'nvagāt ,āvikṣito mahīpālaḥ saṁvartamupaśikṣitum.
english: Seeing that he had turned back, the king Āvikṣita, with folded hands, followed him from behind, intending to receive instruction from Saṃvarta.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 30. Seeing that he had turned back, the king Āvikṣita, with folded hands, followed him from behind, intending to receive instruction from Saṃvarta.

sanskrit: स एनं विजने दृष्ट्वा पांसुभिः कर्दमेन च ।श्लेष्मणा चापि राजानं ष्ठीवनैश्च समाकिरत् ॥३१॥
transliteration: sa enaṁ vijane dṛṣṭvā pāṁsubhiḥ kardamena ca ,śleṣmaṇā cāpi rājānaṁ ṣṭhīvanaiśca samākirat.
english: When Saṃvarta saw the king in a solitary place, he pelted him with dust, mud, phlegm, and even spit.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 31. When Saṃvarta saw the king in a solitary place, he pelted him with dust, mud, phlegm, and even spit.

sanskrit: स तथा बाध्यमानोऽपि संवर्तेन महीपतिः ।अन्वगादेव तमृषिं प्राञ्जलिः संप्रसादयन् ॥३२॥
transliteration: sa tathā bādhyamāno'pi saṁvartena mahīpatiḥ ,anvagādeva tamṛṣiṁ prāñjaliḥ saṁprasādayan.
english: Even though the king was thus harassed by Saṃvarta, he continued to follow that sage, with folded hands, pleading with him.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 32. Even though the king was thus harassed by Saṃvarta, he continued to follow that sage, with folded hands, pleading with him.

sanskrit: ततो निवृत्य संवर्तः परिश्रान्त उपाविशत् ।शीतलच्छायमासाद्य न्यग्रोधं बहुशाखिनम् ॥३३॥
transliteration: tato nivṛtya saṁvartaḥ pariśrānta upāviśat ,śītalacchāyamāsādya nyagrodhaṁ bahuśākhinam.
english: Then, Saṃvarta, having turned back and being exhausted, sat down after reaching a Banyan tree with many branches and cool shade.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 6 Verse 33. Then, Saṃvarta, having turned back and being exhausted, sat down after reaching a Banyan tree with many branches and cool shade.

sanskrit: संवर्त उवाच ।कथमस्मि त्वया ज्ञातः केन वा कथितोऽस्मि ते ।एतदाचक्ष्व मे तत्त्वमिच्छसे चेत्प्रियं मम ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁvarta uvāca ,kathamasmi tvayā jñātaḥ kena vā kathito'smi te ,etadācakṣva me tattvamicchase cetpriyaṁ mama.
english: Saṃvarta said: 'How have you come to know me, or by whom was I described to you? Tell me this truth if you wish to please me.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 1. Saṃvarta said: 'How have you come to know me, or by whom was I described to you? Tell me this truth if you wish to please me.'

sanskrit: सत्यं ते ब्रुवतः सर्वे संपत्स्यन्ते मनोरथाः ।मिथ्या तु ब्रुवतो मूर्धा सप्तधा ते फलिष्यति ॥२॥
transliteration: satyaṁ te bruvataḥ sarve saṁpatsyante manorathāḥ ,mithyā tu bruvato mūrdhā saptadhā te phaliṣyati.
english: If you speak the truth, all your desires will be fulfilled. But if you speak falsely, your head will split into seven pieces.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 2. If you speak the truth, all your desires will be fulfilled. But if you speak falsely, your head will split into seven pieces.

sanskrit: मरुत्त उवाच ।नारदेन भवान्मह्यमाख्यातो ह्यटता पथि ।गुरुपुत्रो ममेति त्वं ततो मे प्रीतिरुत्तमा ॥३॥
transliteration: marutta uvāca ,nāradena bhavānmahyamākhyāto hyaṭatā pathi ,guruputro mameti tvaṁ tato me prītiruttamā.
english: Marutta said: 'Nārada, while wandering on the path, indeed told me about you, saying, "You are my preceptor's (guru's) son." Therefore, my affection for you is supreme.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 3. Marutta said: 'Nārada, while wandering on the path, indeed told me about you, saying, "You are my preceptor's (guru's) son." Therefore, my affection for you is supreme.'

sanskrit: संवर्त उवाच ।सत्यमेतद्भवानाह स मां जानाति सत्रिणम् ।कथयस्वैतदेकं मे क्व नु संप्रति नारदः ॥४॥
transliteration: saṁvarta uvāca ,satyametadbhavānāha sa māṁ jānāti satriṇam ,kathayasvaitadekaṁ me kva nu saṁprati nāradaḥ.
english: Saṃvarta said: 'You speak the truth. He (Nārada) knows me as a performer of (Vedic rituals). Now, tell me just one thing: where is Nārada at present?'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 4. Saṃvarta said: 'You speak the truth. He (Nārada) knows me as a performer of (Vedic rituals). Now, tell me just one thing: where is Nārada at present?'

sanskrit: मरुत्त उवाच ।भवन्तं कथयित्वा तु मम देवर्षिसत्तमः ।ततो मामभ्यनुज्ञाय प्रविष्टो हव्यवाहनम् ॥५॥
transliteration: marutta uvāca ,bhavantaṁ kathayitvā tu mama devarṣisattamaḥ ,tato māmabhyanujñāya praviṣṭo havyavāhanam.
english: Marutta said: "The best of divine sages, having indeed spoken to me about you, then, after granting me permission, entered the fire (havyavāhana)."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 5. Marutta said: "The best of divine sages, having indeed spoken to me about you, then, after granting me permission, entered the fire (havyavāhana)."

sanskrit: व्यास उवाच ।श्रुत्वा तु पार्थिवस्यैतत्संवर्तः परया मुदा ।एतावदहमप्येनं कुर्यामिति तदाब्रवीत् ॥६॥
transliteration: vyāsa uvāca ,śrutvā tu pārthivasyaitatsaṁvartaḥ parayā mudā ,etāvadahamapyenaṁ kuryāmiti tadābravīt.
english: Vyāsa said: "Having heard this from the king, Saṃvarta, with great delight, then said, 'I also shall do this much for him.'"
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 6. Vyāsa said: "Having heard this from the king, Saṃvarta, with great delight, then said, 'I also shall do this much for him.'"

sanskrit: ततो मरुत्तमुन्मत्तो वाचा निर्भर्त्सयन्निव ।रूक्षया ब्राह्मणो राजन्पुनः पुनरथाब्रवीत् ॥७॥
transliteration: tato maruttamunmatto vācā nirbhartsayanniva ,rūkṣayā brāhmaṇo rājanpunaḥ punarathābravīt.
english: Then, the brahmin, like a frenzied person, as if rebuking Marutta with harsh words, O king, spoke again and again.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 7. Then, the brahmin, like a frenzied person, as if rebuking Marutta with harsh words, O king, spoke again and again.

sanskrit: वातप्रधानेन मया स्वचित्तवशवर्तिना ।एवं विकृतरूपेण कथं याजितुमिच्छसि ॥८॥
transliteration: vātapradhānena mayā svacittavaśavartinā ,evaṁ vikṛtarūpeṇa kathaṁ yājitumicchasi.
english: How can you desire me, who am thus afflicted by Vāta (the wind-element), acting under the sway of my own mind, and disfigured in form, to officiate a Vedic ritual (yajña)?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 8. How can you desire me, who am thus afflicted by Vāta (the wind-element), acting under the sway of my own mind, and disfigured in form, to officiate a Vedic ritual (yajña)?

sanskrit: भ्राता मम समर्थश्च वासवेन च सत्कृतः ।वर्तते याजने चैव तेन कर्माणि कारय ॥९॥
transliteration: bhrātā mama samarthaśca vāsavena ca satkṛtaḥ ,vartate yājane caiva tena karmāṇi kāraya.
english: My brother is capable and honored by Indra. He is indeed engaged in performing Vedic rituals (yajña). Therefore, have the rituals (karma) performed by him.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 9. My brother is capable and honored by Indra. He is indeed engaged in performing Vedic rituals (yajña). Therefore, have the rituals (karma) performed by him.

sanskrit: गृहं स्वं चैव याज्याश्च सर्वा गृह्याश्च देवताः ।पूर्वजेन ममाक्षिप्तं शरीरं वर्जितं त्विदम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: gṛhaṁ svaṁ caiva yājyāśca sarvā gṛhyāśca devatāḥ ,pūrvajena mamākṣiptaṁ śarīraṁ varjitaṁ tvidam.
english: My own home, as well as all the objects of the Vedic ritual (yajña) and the household deities, have been seized by my elder brother, but this body has been abandoned.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 10. My own home, as well as all the objects of the Vedic ritual (yajña) and the household deities, have been seized by my elder brother, but this body has been abandoned.

sanskrit: नाहं तेनाननुज्ञातस्त्वामाविक्षित कर्हिचित् ।याजयेयं कथंचिद्वै स हि पूज्यतमो मम ॥११॥
transliteration: nāhaṁ tenānanujñātastvāmāvikṣita karhicit ,yājayeyaṁ kathaṁcidvai sa hi pūjyatamo mama.
english: I, not being authorized by him, would never, in any way, engage you (who have approached for this purpose) in a Vedic ritual (yajña). For he is indeed the most revered to me.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 11. I, not being authorized by him, would never, in any way, engage you (who have approached for this purpose) in a Vedic ritual (yajña). For he is indeed the most revered to me.

sanskrit: स त्वं बृहस्पतिं गच्छ तमनुज्ञाप्य चाव्रज ।ततोऽहं याजयिष्ये त्वां यदि यष्टुमिहेच्छसि ॥१२॥
transliteration: sa tvaṁ bṛhaspatiṁ gaccha tamanujñāpya cāvraja ,tato'haṁ yājayiṣye tvāṁ yadi yaṣṭumihecchasi.
english: Therefore, you go to Bṛhaspati, and having obtained his permission, return. Then I will perform the Vedic ritual (yajña) for you if you desire to perform a (Vedic) ritual here.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 12. Therefore, you go to Bṛhaspati, and having obtained his permission, return. Then I will perform the Vedic ritual (yajña) for you if you desire to perform a (Vedic) ritual here.

sanskrit: मरुत्त उवाच ।बृहस्पतिं गतः पूर्वमहं संवर्त तच्छृणु ।न मां कामयते याज्यमसौ वासववारितः ॥१३॥
transliteration: marutta uvāca ,bṛhaspatiṁ gataḥ pūrvamahaṁ saṁvarta tacchṛṇu ,na māṁ kāmayate yājyamasau vāsavavāritaḥ.
english: Marutta said: O Saṃvarta, listen to this. Earlier, I went to Bṛhaspati. He did not desire to act as priest for me (yājya), as he was forbidden by Vāsava (Indra).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 13. Marutta said: O Saṃvarta, listen to this. Earlier, I went to Bṛhaspati. He did not desire to act as priest for me (yājya), as he was forbidden by Vāsava (Indra).

sanskrit: अमरं याज्यमासाद्य मामृषे मा स्म मानुषम् ।याजयेथा मरुत्तं त्वं मर्त्यधर्माणमातुरम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: amaraṁ yājyamāsādya māmṛṣe mā sma mānuṣam ,yājayethā maruttaṁ tvaṁ martyadharmāṇamāturam.
english: O sage (Bṛhaspati), having obtained me, an immortal, as your patron (yājya), you must not officiate for Marutta, who is a human, possesses a mortal nature (dharma), and is distressed.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 14. O sage (Bṛhaspati), having obtained me, an immortal, as your patron (yājya), you must not officiate for Marutta, who is a human, possesses a mortal nature (dharma), and is distressed.

sanskrit: स्पर्धते च मया विप्र सदा वै स हि पार्थिवः ।एवमस्त्विति चाप्युक्तो भ्रात्रा ते बलवृत्रहा ॥१५॥
transliteration: spardhate ca mayā vipra sadā vai sa hi pārthivaḥ ,evamastviti cāpyukto bhrātrā te balavṛtrahā.
english: O Brahmin (Bṛhaspati), that king (Marutta) indeed always competes with me (Indra). And 'So be it' was also declared by your brother, the slayer of Bala and Vṛtra (Indra).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 15. O Brahmin (Bṛhaspati), that king (Marutta) indeed always competes with me (Indra). And 'So be it' was also declared by your brother, the slayer of Bala and Vṛtra (Indra).

sanskrit: स मामभिगतं प्रेम्णा याज्यवन्न बुभूषति ।देवराजमुपाश्रित्य तद्विद्धि मुनिपुंगव ॥१६॥
transliteration: sa māmabhigataṁ premṇā yājyavanna bubhūṣati ,devarājamupāśritya tadviddhi munipuṁgava.
english: He (Bṛhaspati) does not wish to accept me, who approached him with affection, as a patron (yājya). Understand this, O best of sages (munipuṅgava), as he relies on the king of gods (Indra).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 16. He (Bṛhaspati) does not wish to accept me, who approached him with affection, as a patron (yājya). Understand this, O best of sages (munipuṅgava), as he relies on the king of gods (Indra).

sanskrit: सोऽहमिच्छामि भवता सर्वस्वेनापि याजितुम् ।कामये समतिक्रान्तुं वासवं त्वत्कृतैर्गुणैः ॥१७॥
transliteration: so'hamicchāmi bhavatā sarvasvenāpi yājitum ,kāmaye samatikrāntuṁ vāsavaṁ tvatkṛtairguṇaiḥ.
english: Therefore, I wish to perform a Vedic ritual (yajña) with your assistance, offering even all my possessions. I desire to surpass Vāsava (Indra) through the merits you will help me accumulate.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 17. Therefore, I wish to perform a Vedic ritual (yajña) with your assistance, offering even all my possessions. I desire to surpass Vāsava (Indra) through the merits you will help me accumulate.

sanskrit: न हि मे वर्तते बुद्धिर्गन्तुं ब्रह्मन्बृहस्पतिम् ।प्रत्याख्यातो हि तेनास्मि तथानपकृते सति ॥१८॥
transliteration: na hi me vartate buddhirgantuṁ brahmanbṛhaspatim ,pratyākhyāto hi tenāsmi tathānapakṛte sati.
english: Indeed, O brahmin (brahman), my intellect does not incline me to go to Bṛhaspati. For I was rejected by him, even though I had committed no offense.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 18. Indeed, O brahmin (brahman), my intellect does not incline me to go to Bṛhaspati. For I was rejected by him, even though I had committed no offense.

sanskrit: संवर्त उवाच ।चिकीर्षसि यथाकामं सर्वमेतत्त्वयि ध्रुवम् ।यदि सर्वानभिप्रायान्कर्तासि मम पार्थिव ॥१९॥
transliteration: saṁvarta uvāca ,cikīrṣasi yathākāmaṁ sarvametattvayi dhruvam ,yadi sarvānabhiprāyānkartāsi mama pārthiva.
english: Saṃvarta said: 'Whatever you wish to accomplish, all this is indeed possible for you. (However), O king (pārthiva), this is only if you will fulfill all my intentions.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 19. Saṃvarta said: 'Whatever you wish to accomplish, all this is indeed possible for you. (However), O king (pārthiva), this is only if you will fulfill all my intentions.'

sanskrit: याज्यमानं मया हि त्वां बृहस्पतिपुरंदरौ ।द्विषेतां समभिक्रुद्धावेतदेकं समर्थय ॥२०॥
transliteration: yājyamānaṁ mayā hi tvāṁ bṛhaspatipuraṁdarau ,dviṣetāṁ samabhikruddhāvetadekaṁ samarthaya.
english: Indeed, Bṛhaspati and Purandara (Indra) will certainly resent you when you are performing a Vedic ritual (yajña) with my assistance, especially as they will be greatly enraged. Be certain of this one fact.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 20. Indeed, Bṛhaspati and Purandara (Indra) will certainly resent you when you are performing a Vedic ritual (yajña) with my assistance, especially as they will be greatly enraged. Be certain of this one fact.

sanskrit: स्थैर्यमत्र कथं ते स्यात्स त्वं निःसंशयं कुरु ।कुपितस्त्वां न हीदानीं भस्म कुर्यां सबान्धवम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: sthairyamatra kathaṁ te syātsa tvaṁ niḥsaṁśayaṁ kuru ,kupitastvāṁ na hīdānīṁ bhasma kuryāṁ sabāndhavam.
english: How can you maintain such firmness in this situation? You must certainly act without hesitation. For truly, if I were angered, I would now turn you and your relatives into ashes.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 21. How can you maintain such firmness in this situation? You must certainly act without hesitation. For truly, if I were angered, I would now turn you and your relatives into ashes.

sanskrit: मरुत्त उवाच ।यावत्तपेत्सहस्रांशुस्तिष्ठेरंश्चापि पर्वताः ।तावल्लोकान्न लभेयं त्यजेयं संगतं यदि ॥२२॥
transliteration: marutta uvāca ,yāvattapetsahasrāṁśustiṣṭheraṁścāpi parvatāḥ ,tāvallokānna labheyaṁ tyajeyaṁ saṁgataṁ yadi.
english: Marutta said: 'As long as the sun (sahasrāṃśu) shines and the mountains remain, so long would I not attain the heavenly worlds if I were to abandon this association.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 22. Marutta said: 'As long as the sun (sahasrāṃśu) shines and the mountains remain, so long would I not attain the heavenly worlds if I were to abandon this association.'

sanskrit: मा चापि शुभबुद्धित्वं लभेयमिह कर्हिचित् ।सम्यग्ज्ञाने वैषये वा त्यजेयं संगतं यदि ॥२३॥
transliteration: mā cāpi śubhabuddhitvaṁ labheyamiha karhicit ,samyagjñāne vaiṣaye vā tyajeyaṁ saṁgataṁ yadi.
english: And may I never again attain good understanding (buddhi) here, whether in right knowledge or in worldly matters, if I were to abandon my association.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 23. And may I never again attain good understanding (buddhi) here, whether in right knowledge or in worldly matters, if I were to abandon my association.

sanskrit: संवर्त उवाच ।आविक्षित शुभा बुद्धिर्धीयतां तव कर्मसु ।याजनं हि ममाप्येवं वर्तते त्वयि पार्थिव ॥२४॥
transliteration: saṁvarta uvāca ,āvikṣita śubhā buddhirdhīyatāṁ tava karmasu ,yājanaṁ hi mamāpyevaṁ vartate tvayi pārthiva.
english: Saṃvarta said: 'O Avikṣit, let auspicious discernment (buddhi) be directed towards your deeds (karma). For truly, my priestly service (yājanam) also depends thus upon you, O King.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 24. Saṃvarta said: 'O Avikṣit, let auspicious discernment (buddhi) be directed towards your deeds (karma). For truly, my priestly service (yājanam) also depends thus upon you, O King.'

sanskrit: संविधास्ये च ते राजन्नक्षयं द्रव्यमुत्तमम् ।येन देवान्सगन्धर्वाञ्शक्रं चाभिभविष्यसि ॥२५॥
transliteration: saṁvidhāsye ca te rājannakṣayaṁ dravyamuttamam ,yena devānsagandharvāñśakraṁ cābhibhaviṣyasi.
english: O king, I shall certainly provide you with excellent, inexhaustible wealth, by which you will surely conquer the gods, including the gandharvas, and even Indra.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 25. O king, I shall certainly provide you with excellent, inexhaustible wealth, by which you will surely conquer the gods, including the gandharvas, and even Indra.

sanskrit: न तु मे वर्तते बुद्धिर्धने याज्येषु वा पुनः ।विप्रियं तु चिकीर्षामि भ्रातुश्चेन्द्रस्य चोभयोः ॥२६॥
transliteration: na tu me vartate buddhirdhane yājyeṣu vā punaḥ ,vipriyaṁ tu cikīrṣāmi bhrātuścendrasya cobhayoḥ.
english: My intention is not directed towards wealth or towards those to whom Vedic rituals are offered. Rather, I wish to cause displeasure to both my brother and Indra.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 26. My intention is not directed towards wealth or towards those to whom Vedic rituals are offered. Rather, I wish to cause displeasure to both my brother and Indra.

sanskrit: गमयिष्यामि चेन्द्रेण समतामपि ते ध्रुवम् ।प्रियं च ते करिष्यामि सत्यमेतद्ब्रवीमि ते ॥२७॥
transliteration: gamayiṣyāmi cendreṇa samatāmapi te dhruvam ,priyaṁ ca te kariṣyāmi satyametadbravīmi te.
english: And I shall certainly make you equal with Indra. I shall also do what is pleasing to you; I tell you this truth.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 7 Verse 27. And I shall certainly make you equal with Indra. I shall also do what is pleasing to you; I tell you this truth.

sanskrit: संवर्त उवाच ।गिरेर्हिमवतः पृष्ठे मुञ्जवान्नाम पर्वतः ।तप्यते यत्र भगवांस्तपो नित्यमुमापतिः ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁvarta uvāca ,girerhimavataḥ pṛṣṭhe muñjavānnāma parvataḥ ,tapyate yatra bhagavāṁstapo nityamumāpatiḥ.
english: Saṃvarta said: On the ridge of the Himālayan mountain (Himavat) there is a mountain named Muñjavān, where the glorious Lord Shiva (Umāpati) constantly performs austerity (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 1. Saṃvarta said: On the ridge of the Himālayan mountain (Himavat) there is a mountain named Muñjavān, where the glorious Lord Shiva (Umāpati) constantly performs austerity (tapas).

sanskrit: वनस्पतीनां मूलेषु टङ्केषु शिखरेषु च ।गुहासु शैलराजस्य यथाकामं यथासुखम् ॥२॥
transliteration: vanaspatīnāṁ mūleṣu ṭaṅkeṣu śikhareṣu ca ,guhāsu śailarājasya yathākāmaṁ yathāsukham.
english: Among the roots of trees, on their crags and peaks, and in the caves of the king of mountains, one may live according to one's desire and comfort.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 2. Among the roots of trees, on their crags and peaks, and in the caves of the king of mountains, one may live according to one's desire and comfort.

sanskrit: उमासहायो भगवान्यत्र नित्यं महेश्वरः ।आस्ते शूली महातेजा नानाभूतगणावृतः ॥३॥
transliteration: umāsahāyo bhagavānyatra nityaṁ maheśvaraḥ ,āste śūlī mahātejā nānābhūtagaṇāvṛtaḥ.
english: Where the divine (Bhagavān) Maheśvara, accompanied by Umā, always resides, bearing a trident, of immense splendor, surrounded by hosts of various spirits (bhūtas).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 3. Where the divine (Bhagavān) Maheśvara, accompanied by Umā, always resides, bearing a trident, of immense splendor, surrounded by hosts of various spirits (bhūtas).

sanskrit: तत्र रुद्राश्च साध्याश्च विश्वेऽथ वसवस्तथा ।यमश्च वरुणश्चैव कुबेरश्च सहानुगः ॥४॥
transliteration: tatra rudrāśca sādhyāśca viśve'tha vasavastathā ,yamaśca varuṇaścaiva kuberaśca sahānugaḥ.
english: There reside the Rudras and the Sādhyas, and also the Viśvedevas and the Vasus; similarly, Yama and Varuṇa, and Kubera with his retinue.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 4. There reside the Rudras and the Sādhyas, and also the Viśvedevas and the Vasus; similarly, Yama and Varuṇa, and Kubera with his retinue.

sanskrit: भूतानि च पिशाचाश्च नासत्यावश्विनावपि ।गन्धर्वाप्सरसश्चैव यक्षा देवर्षयस्तथा ॥५॥
transliteration: bhūtāni ca piśācāśca nāsatyāvaśvināvapi ,gandharvāpsarasaścaiva yakṣā devarṣayastathā.
english: And the spirits (bhūtas) and ghastly beings (piśācas), and the two Nāsatyas (Aśvins) as well; similarly, the Gandharvas and Apsarases, and the Yakṣas, and the divine sages (devarṣis).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 5. And the spirits (bhūtas) and ghastly beings (piśācas), and the two Nāsatyas (Aśvins) as well; similarly, the Gandharvas and Apsarases, and the Yakṣas, and the divine sages (devarṣis).

sanskrit: आदित्या मरुतश्चैव यातुधानाश्च सर्वशः ।उपासन्ते महात्मानं बहुरूपमुमापतिम् ॥६॥
transliteration: ādityā marutaścaiva yātudhānāśca sarvaśaḥ ,upāsante mahātmānaṁ bahurūpamumāpatim.
english: The Adityas, the Maruts, and indeed all the demons, worship the great-souled, multi-formed lord of Umā (Śiva).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 6. The Adityas, the Maruts, and indeed all the demons, worship the great-souled, multi-formed lord of Umā (Śiva).

sanskrit: रमते भगवांस्तत्र कुबेरानुचरैः सह ।विकृतैर्विकृताकारैः क्रीडद्भिः पृथिवीपते ।श्रिया ज्वलन्दृश्यते वै बालादित्यसमद्युतिः ॥७॥
transliteration: ramate bhagavāṁstatra kuberānucaraiḥ saha ,vikṛtairvikṛtākāraiḥ krīḍadbhiḥ pṛthivīpate ,śriyā jvalandṛśyate vai bālādityasamadyutiḥ.
english: O lord of the earth, the glorious one (bhagavān) delights there with Kubera's disfigured and monstrous-looking attendants, who are all playing. Indeed, he is seen blazing with splendor, possessing the brilliance of the rising sun.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 7. O lord of the earth, the glorious one (bhagavān) delights there with Kubera's disfigured and monstrous-looking attendants, who are all playing. Indeed, he is seen blazing with splendor, possessing the brilliance of the rising sun.

sanskrit: न रूपं दृश्यते तस्य संस्थानं वा कथंचन ।निर्देष्टुं प्राणिभिः कैश्चित्प्राकृतैर्मांसलोचनैः ॥८॥
transliteration: na rūpaṁ dṛśyate tasya saṁsthānaṁ vā kathaṁcana ,nirdeṣṭuṁ prāṇibhiḥ kaiścitprākṛtairmāṁsalocanaiḥ.
english: His form or structure is not seen by any means, nor can he be described by any ordinary beings (prāṇin) with fleshly eyes.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 8. His form or structure is not seen by any means, nor can he be described by any ordinary beings (prāṇin) with fleshly eyes.

sanskrit: नोष्णं न शिशिरं तत्र न वायुर्न च भास्करः ।न जरा क्षुत्पिपासे वा न मृत्युर्न भयं नृप ॥९॥
transliteration: noṣṇaṁ na śiśiraṁ tatra na vāyurna ca bhāskaraḥ ,na jarā kṣutpipāse vā na mṛtyurna bhayaṁ nṛpa.
english: O King, there is no heat nor cold, no wind nor sun (bhāskara) there. Neither old age, hunger and thirst, nor death or fear exist.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 9. O King, there is no heat nor cold, no wind nor sun (bhāskara) there. Neither old age, hunger and thirst, nor death or fear exist.

sanskrit: तस्य शैलस्य पार्श्वेषु सर्वेषु जयतां वर ।धातवो जातरूपस्य रश्मयः सवितुर्यथा ॥१०॥
transliteration: tasya śailasya pārśveṣu sarveṣu jayatāṁ vara ,dhātavo jātarūpasya raśmayaḥ savituryathā.
english: O best among the victorious, on all sides of that mountain, there are veins of gold, shining like the rays of the sun.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 10. O best among the victorious, on all sides of that mountain, there are veins of gold, shining like the rays of the sun.

sanskrit: रक्ष्यन्ते ते कुबेरस्य सहायैरुद्यतायुधैः ।चिकीर्षद्भिः प्रियं राजन्कुबेरस्य महात्मनः ॥११॥
transliteration: rakṣyante te kuberasya sahāyairudyatāyudhaiḥ ,cikīrṣadbhiḥ priyaṁ rājankuberasya mahātmanaḥ.
english: O King, those [gold veins] are guarded by the assistants of Kubera, who bear uplifted weapons and desire to do what is pleasing to the great-souled Kubera.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 11. O King, those [gold veins] are guarded by the assistants of Kubera, who bear uplifted weapons and desire to do what is pleasing to the great-souled Kubera.

sanskrit: तस्मै भगवते कृत्वा नमः शर्वाय वेधसे ।रुद्राय शितिकण्ठाय सुरूपाय सुवर्चसे ॥१२॥
transliteration: tasmai bhagavate kṛtvā namaḥ śarvāya vedhase ,rudrāya śitikaṇṭhāya surūpāya suvarcase.
english: Having offered obeisance to that venerable Lord [Śiva], to Śarva, the ordainer (Vedhas), to Rudra, the blue-necked (śitikaṇṭha) one, the beautiful-formed, and the radiant one.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 12. Having offered obeisance to that venerable Lord [Śiva], to Śarva, the ordainer (Vedhas), to Rudra, the blue-necked (śitikaṇṭha) one, the beautiful-formed, and the radiant one.

sanskrit: कपर्दिने करालाय हर्यक्ष्णे वरदाय च ।त्र्यक्ष्णे पूष्णो दन्तभिदे वामनाय शिवाय च ॥१३॥
transliteration: kapardine karālāya haryakṣṇe varadāya ca ,tryakṣṇe pūṣṇo dantabhide vāmanāya śivāya ca.
english: And to Kapardin (the one with matted hair), to the formidable, to the lion-eyed, and to the bestower of boons; to the three-eyed, to the one who broke Pūṣan's teeth, to the dwarf (Vāmana), and to the auspicious (śiva) one.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 13. And to Kapardin (the one with matted hair), to the formidable, to the lion-eyed, and to the bestower of boons; to the three-eyed, to the one who broke Pūṣan's teeth, to the dwarf (Vāmana), and to the auspicious (śiva) one.

sanskrit: याम्यायाव्यक्तकेशाय सद्वृत्ते शंकराय च ।क्षेम्याय हरिनेत्राय स्थाणवे पुरुषाय च ॥१४॥
transliteration: yāmyāyāvyaktakeśāya sadvṛtte śaṁkarāya ca ,kṣemyāya harinetrāya sthāṇave puruṣāya ca.
english: Salutations to the controller, to the one with unmanifested hair, to the one of good conduct, and to Śaṃkara. To the one who brings well-being, to the one whose eye is Hari (Viṣṇu), to the immovable one (sthāṇu), and to the cosmic person (puruṣa).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 14. Salutations to the controller, to the one with unmanifested hair, to the one of good conduct, and to Śaṃkara. To the one who brings well-being, to the one whose eye is Hari (Viṣṇu), to the immovable one (sthāṇu), and to the cosmic person (puruṣa).

sanskrit: हरिकेशाय मुण्डाय कृशायोत्तारणाय च ।भास्कराय सुतीर्थाय देवदेवाय रंहसे ॥१५॥
transliteration: harikeśāya muṇḍāya kṛśāyottāraṇāya ca ,bhāskarāya sutīrthāya devadevāya raṁhase.
english: Salutations to the one with tawny hair, to the ascetic, to the emaciated one, and to the deliverer. To the radiant one, to the one with sacred fords, to the God of gods, and to the powerful one.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 15. Salutations to the one with tawny hair, to the ascetic, to the emaciated one, and to the deliverer. To the radiant one, to the one with sacred fords, to the God of gods, and to the powerful one.

sanskrit: उष्णीषिणे सुवक्त्राय सहस्राक्षाय मीढुषे ।गिरिशाय प्रशान्ताय यतये चीरवाससे ॥१६॥
transliteration: uṣṇīṣiṇe suvaktrāya sahasrākṣāya mīḍhuṣe ,giriśāya praśāntāya yataye cīravāsase.
english: Salutations to the one wearing a turban, to the one with a beautiful face, to the thousand-eyed one, and to the bountiful one. To the lord of mountains, to the utterly peaceful one, to the ascetic (yati), and to the one wearing bark garments.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 16. Salutations to the one wearing a turban, to the one with a beautiful face, to the thousand-eyed one, and to the bountiful one. To the lord of mountains, to the utterly peaceful one, to the ascetic (yati), and to the one wearing bark garments.

sanskrit: बिल्वदण्डाय सिद्धाय सर्वदण्डधराय च ।मृगव्याधाय महते धन्विनेऽथ भवाय च ॥१७॥
transliteration: bilvadaṇḍāya siddhāya sarvadaṇḍadharāya ca ,mṛgavyādhāya mahate dhanvine'tha bhavāya ca.
english: Salutations to the one holding a Bilva staff, to the perfected one, to the bearer of all authority, and to the hunter of deer. To the great one, to the archer, and to the source of existence (bhava).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 17. Salutations to the one holding a Bilva staff, to the perfected one, to the bearer of all authority, and to the hunter of deer. To the great one, to the archer, and to the source of existence (bhava).

sanskrit: वराय सौम्यवक्त्राय पशुहस्ताय वर्षिणे ।हिरण्यबाहवे राजन्नुग्राय पतये दिशाम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: varāya saumyavaktrāya paśuhastāya varṣiṇe ,hiraṇyabāhave rājannugrāya pataye diśām.
english: To the excellent one, whose face is gentle, who holds an animal in his hand, who showers blessings, whose arms are golden, O King, to the fierce one, the lord of the directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 18. To the excellent one, whose face is gentle, who holds an animal in his hand, who showers blessings, whose arms are golden, O King, to the fierce one, the lord of the directions.

sanskrit: पशूनां पतये चैव भूतानां पतये तथा ।वृषाय मातृभक्ताय सेनान्ये मध्यमाय च ॥१९॥
transliteration: paśūnāṁ pataye caiva bhūtānāṁ pataye tathā ,vṛṣāya mātṛbhaktāya senānye madhyamāya ca.
english: To the lord of animals (paśu), and indeed, to the lord of all beings; also, to the bull-like one, to the one devoted to his mother, to the commander of the army, and to the one who is the middle (or impartial).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 19. To the lord of animals (paśu), and indeed, to the lord of all beings; also, to the bull-like one, to the one devoted to his mother, to the commander of the army, and to the one who is the middle (or impartial).

sanskrit: स्रुवहस्ताय पतये धन्विने भार्गवाय च ।अजाय कृष्णनेत्राय विरूपाक्षाय चैव ह ॥२०॥
transliteration: sruvahastāya pataye dhanvine bhārgavāya ca ,ajāya kṛṣṇanetrāya virūpākṣāya caiva ha.
english: To the one holding a sacrificial ladle (sruva) in his hand, to the lord, to the archer, and to the descendant of Bhṛgu; to the unborn (aja), to the one with dark eyes, and indeed, to the one with distorted or unusual eyes.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 20. To the one holding a sacrificial ladle (sruva) in his hand, to the lord, to the archer, and to the descendant of Bhṛgu; to the unborn (aja), to the one with dark eyes, and indeed, to the one with distorted or unusual eyes.

sanskrit: तीक्ष्णदंष्ट्राय तीक्ष्णाय वैश्वानरमुखाय च ।महाद्युतयेऽनङ्गाय सर्वाङ्गाय प्रजावते ॥२१॥
transliteration: tīkṣṇadaṁṣṭrāya tīkṣṇāya vaiśvānaramukhāya ca ,mahādyutaye'naṅgāya sarvāṅgāya prajāvate.
english: To the one with sharp fangs, to the fierce one, and to the one whose mouth is the universal fire (Vaiśvānara); to the one of great splendor, to the bodiless one, to the one with a universal body, and to the progenitor of offspring.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 21. To the one with sharp fangs, to the fierce one, and to the one whose mouth is the universal fire (Vaiśvānara); to the one of great splendor, to the bodiless one, to the one with a universal body, and to the progenitor of offspring.

sanskrit: तथा शुक्राधिपतये पृथवे कृत्तिवाससे ।कपालमालिने नित्यं सुवर्णमुकुटाय च ॥२२॥
transliteration: tathā śukrādhipataye pṛthave kṛttivāsase ,kapālamāline nityaṁ suvarṇamukuṭāya ca.
english: And similarly, to the lord of Śukra, to the expansive one, to him who wears a hide as garment, to him adorned with a garland of skulls, and always to him with a golden crown.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 22. And similarly, to the lord of Śukra, to the expansive one, to him who wears a hide as garment, to him adorned with a garland of skulls, and always to him with a golden crown.

sanskrit: महादेवाय कृष्णाय त्र्यम्बकायानघाय च ।क्रोधनाय नृशंसाय मृदवे बाहुशालिने ॥२३॥
transliteration: mahādevāya kṛṣṇāya tryambakāyānaghāya ca ,krodhanāya nṛśaṁsāya mṛdave bāhuśāline.
english: To the great god (mahādeva), to the dark one, to the three-eyed one, and to the sinless one; to the wrathful, to the cruel, to the gentle, and to the mighty-armed one.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 23. To the great god (mahādeva), to the dark one, to the three-eyed one, and to the sinless one; to the wrathful, to the cruel, to the gentle, and to the mighty-armed one.

sanskrit: दण्डिने तप्ततपसे तथैव क्रूरकर्मणे ।सहस्रशिरसे चैव सहस्रचरणाय च ।नमः स्वधास्वरूपाय बहुरूपाय दंष्ट्रिणे ॥२४॥
transliteration: daṇḍine taptatapase tathaiva krūrakarmaṇe ,sahasraśirase caiva sahasracaraṇāya ca ,namaḥ svadhāsvarūpāya bahurūpāya daṁṣṭriṇe.
english: To the wielder of the staff, to him of intense asceticism (tapas), and similarly to him of fierce deeds; and also to the thousand-headed one and the thousand-footed one. Salutations to him whose essential nature (svarūpa) is svadhā, to the multi-formed one, and to the tusked one.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 24. To the wielder of the staff, to him of intense asceticism (tapas), and similarly to him of fierce deeds; and also to the thousand-headed one and the thousand-footed one. Salutations to him whose essential nature (svarūpa) is svadhā, to the multi-formed one, and to the tusked one.

sanskrit: पिनाकिनं महादेवं महायोगिनमव्ययम् ।त्रिशूलपाणिं वरदं त्र्यम्बकं भुवनेश्वरम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: pinākinaṁ mahādevaṁ mahāyoginamavyayam ,triśūlapāṇiṁ varadaṁ tryambakaṁ bhuvaneśvaram.
english: The wielder of the Pināka bow, the great god (mahādeva), the great practitioner of (yoga), the immutable one, the one with a trident in hand, the bestower of boons, the three-eyed one, and the lord of the world.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 25. The wielder of the Pināka bow, the great god (mahādeva), the great practitioner of (yoga), the immutable one, the one with a trident in hand, the bestower of boons, the three-eyed one, and the lord of the world.

sanskrit: त्रिपुरघ्नं त्रिनयनं त्रिलोकेशं महौजसम् ।प्रभवं सर्वभूतानां धारणं धरणीधरम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: tripuraghnaṁ trinayanaṁ trilokeśaṁ mahaujasam ,prabhavaṁ sarvabhūtānāṁ dhāraṇaṁ dharaṇīdharam.
english: The destroyer of the three cities, the three-eyed one, the lord of the three worlds, the immensely powerful one, the source of all beings, the sustainer, and the upholder of the earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 26. The destroyer of the three cities, the three-eyed one, the lord of the three worlds, the immensely powerful one, the source of all beings, the sustainer, and the upholder of the earth.

sanskrit: ईशानं शंकरं सर्वं शिवं विश्वेश्वरं भवम् ।उमापतिं पशुपतिं विश्वरूपं महेश्वरम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: īśānaṁ śaṁkaraṁ sarvaṁ śivaṁ viśveśvaraṁ bhavam ,umāpatiṁ paśupatiṁ viśvarūpaṁ maheśvaram.
english: The ruler, the benevolent one, the all-pervading, the auspicious (śivam), the lord of the universe, the existent one (bhavam), the husband of Umā, the lord of creatures (paśupati), the one with a universal form, and the great lord.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 27. The ruler, the benevolent one, the all-pervading, the auspicious (śivam), the lord of the universe, the existent one (bhavam), the husband of Umā, the lord of creatures (paśupati), the one with a universal form, and the great lord.

sanskrit: विरूपाक्षं दशभुजं तिष्यगोवृषभध्वजम् ।उग्रं स्थाणुं शिवं घोरं शर्वं गौरीशमीश्वरम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: virūpākṣaṁ daśabhujaṁ tiṣyagovṛṣabhadhvajam ,ugraṁ sthāṇuṁ śivaṁ ghoraṁ śarvaṁ gaurīśamīśvaram.
english: The odd-eyed (virūpākṣam), the ten-armed, the one whose banner is the bull, the fierce, the immobile (sthāṇu), the auspicious one, the dreadful, the destroyer (śarva), and the lord of Gaurī, the supreme ruler.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 28. The odd-eyed (virūpākṣam), the ten-armed, the one whose banner is the bull, the fierce, the immobile (sthāṇu), the auspicious one, the dreadful, the destroyer (śarva), and the lord of Gaurī, the supreme ruler.

sanskrit: शितिकण्ठमजं शुक्रं पृथुं पृथुहरं हरम् ।विश्वरूपं विरूपाक्षं बहुरूपमुमापतिम् ॥२९॥
transliteration: śitikaṇṭhamajaṁ śukraṁ pṛthuṁ pṛthuharaṁ haram ,viśvarūpaṁ virūpākṣaṁ bahurūpamumāpatim.
english: The blue-necked (śitikaṇṭha), the unborn, the radiant, the expansive, the great destroyer, the remover, the universal-formed, the odd-eyed (virūpākṣam), the multiformed, and the husband of Umā (umāpati).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 29. The blue-necked (śitikaṇṭha), the unborn, the radiant, the expansive, the great destroyer, the remover, the universal-formed, the odd-eyed (virūpākṣam), the multiformed, and the husband of Umā (umāpati).

sanskrit: प्रणम्य शिरसा देवमनङ्गाङ्गहरं हरम् ।शरण्यं शरणं याहि महादेवं चतुर्मुखम् ॥३०॥
transliteration: praṇamya śirasā devamanaṅgāṅgaharaṁ haram ,śaraṇyaṁ śaraṇaṁ yāhi mahādevaṁ caturmukham.
english: Having bowed your head to the god Hara, who destroyed the body of Anaṅga (Kāma), approach for refuge the great god, the four-faced one, who is worthy of protection.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 30. Having bowed your head to the god Hara, who destroyed the body of Anaṅga (Kāma), approach for refuge the great god, the four-faced one, who is worthy of protection.

sanskrit: एवं कृत्वा नमस्तस्मै महादेवाय रंहसे ।महात्मने क्षितिपते तत्सुवर्णमवाप्स्यसि ।सुवर्णमाहरिष्यन्तस्तत्र गच्छन्तु ते नराः ॥३१॥
transliteration: evaṁ kṛtvā namastasmai mahādevāya raṁhase ,mahātmane kṣitipate tatsuvarṇamavāpsyasi ,suvarṇamāhariṣyantastatra gacchantu te narāḥ.
english: Having thus offered obeisance to that Mahādeva, the powerful and great-souled (mahātman) one, O king, you will obtain that gold. Let those men who are to collect the gold go there.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 31. Having thus offered obeisance to that Mahādeva, the powerful and great-souled (mahātman) one, O king, you will obtain that gold. Let those men who are to collect the gold go there.

sanskrit: व्यास उवाच ।इत्युक्तः स वचस्तस्य चक्रे कारंधमात्मजः ।ततोऽतिमानुषं सर्वं चक्रे यज्ञस्य संविधिम् ।सौवर्णानि च भाण्डानि संचक्रुस्तत्र शिल्पिनः ॥३२॥
transliteration: vyāsa uvāca ,ityuktaḥ sa vacastasya cakre kāraṁdhamātmajaḥ ,tato'timānuṣaṁ sarvaṁ cakre yajñasya saṁvidhim ,sauvarṇāni ca bhāṇḍāni saṁcakrustatra śilpinaḥ.
english: Vyāsa said: Thus addressed, the son of Kāraṃdhama acted upon his words. Thereafter, he made all the arrangements for the (yajña) Vedic ritual extraordinary. And there, the artisans made golden vessels.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 32. Vyāsa said: Thus addressed, the son of Kāraṃdhama acted upon his words. Thereafter, he made all the arrangements for the (yajña) Vedic ritual extraordinary. And there, the artisans made golden vessels.

sanskrit: बृहस्पतिस्तु तां श्रुत्वा मरुत्तस्य महीपतेः ।समृद्धिमति देवेभ्यः संतापमकरोद्भृशम् ॥३३॥
transliteration: bṛhaspatistu tāṁ śrutvā maruttasya mahīpateḥ ,samṛddhimati devebhyaḥ saṁtāpamakarodbhṛśam.
english: But Bṛhaspati, having heard of that exceedingly great prosperity of King Marutta, truly felt deep distress for the gods.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 33. But Bṛhaspati, having heard of that exceedingly great prosperity of King Marutta, truly felt deep distress for the gods.

sanskrit: स तप्यमानो वैवर्ण्यं कृशत्वं चागमत्परम् ।भविष्यति हि मे शत्रुः संवर्तो वसुमानिति ॥३४॥
transliteration: sa tapyamāno vaivarṇyaṁ kṛśatvaṁ cāgamatparam ,bhaviṣyati hi me śatruḥ saṁvarto vasumāniti.
english: As he suffered intensely, he became extremely discolored and emaciated. [He thought/feared], 'Indeed, Samvarta, the wealthy one, will become my enemy.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 34. As he suffered intensely, he became extremely discolored and emaciated. [He thought/feared], 'Indeed, Samvarta, the wealthy one, will become my enemy.'

sanskrit: तं श्रुत्वा भृशसंतप्तं देवराजो बृहस्पतिम् ।अभिगम्यामरवृतः प्रोवाचेदं वचस्तदा ॥३५॥
transliteration: taṁ śrutvā bhṛśasaṁtaptaṁ devarājo bṛhaspatim ,abhigamyāmaravṛtaḥ provācedaṁ vacastadā.
english: Having heard that he was intensely distressed, the king of gods, surrounded by the immortals, then approached Bṛhaspati and spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 8 Verse 35. Having heard that he was intensely distressed, the king of gods, surrounded by the immortals, then approached Bṛhaspati and spoke these words.

sanskrit: इन्द्र उवाच ।कच्चित्सुखं स्वपिषि त्वं बृहस्पते कच्चिन्मनोज्ञाः परिचारकास्ते ।कच्चिद्देवानां सुखकामोऽसि विप्र कच्चिद्देवास्त्वां परिपालयन्ति ॥१॥
transliteration: indra uvāca ,kaccitsukhaṁ svapiṣi tvaṁ bṛhaspate; kaccinmanojñāḥ paricārakāste ,kacciddevānāṁ sukhakāmo'si vipra; kacciddevāstvāṁ paripālayanti.
english: Indra said, 'O Bṛhaspati, do you sleep peacefully? Are your attendants agreeable? O sage, do you wish for the well-being of the gods? And do the gods protect you?'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 1. Indra said, 'O Bṛhaspati, do you sleep peacefully? Are your attendants agreeable? O sage, do you wish for the well-being of the gods? And do the gods protect you?'

sanskrit: बृहस्पतिरुवाच ।सुखं शयेऽहं शयने महेन्द्र तथा मनोज्ञाः परिचारका मे ।तथा देवानां सुखकामोऽस्मि शक्र देवाश्च मां सुभृशं पालयन्ति ॥२॥
transliteration: bṛhaspatiruvāca ,sukhaṁ śaye'haṁ śayane mahendra; tathā manojñāḥ paricārakā me ,tathā devānāṁ sukhakāmo'smi śakra; devāśca māṁ subhṛśaṁ pālayanti.
english: Bṛhaspati said, 'O great Indra, I sleep peacefully in bed. And my attendants are likewise pleasing. O Śakra, I am also desirous of the welfare of the gods, and the gods protect me very greatly.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 2. Bṛhaspati said, 'O great Indra, I sleep peacefully in bed. And my attendants are likewise pleasing. O Śakra, I am also desirous of the welfare of the gods, and the gods protect me very greatly.'

sanskrit: इन्द्र उवाच ।कुतो दुःखं मानसं देहजं वा पाण्डुर्विवर्णश्च कुतस्त्वमद्य ।आचक्ष्व मे तद्द्विज यावदेतान्निहन्मि सर्वांस्तव दुःखकर्तॄन् ॥३॥
transliteration: indra uvāca ,kuto duḥkhaṁ mānasaṁ dehajaṁ vā; pāṇḍurvivarṇaśca kutastvamadya ,ācakṣva me taddvija yāvadetā;nnihanmi sarvāṁstava duḥkhakartṝn.
english: Indra said: "From where does this mental or bodily suffering arise? And why are you pale and discolored today? Tell me that, O twice-born (dvija), so that I may slay all those who cause your distress."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 3. Indra said: "From where does this mental or bodily suffering arise? And why are you pale and discolored today? Tell me that, O twice-born (dvija), so that I may slay all those who cause your distress."

sanskrit: बृहस्पतिरुवाच ।मरुत्तमाहुर्मघवन्यक्ष्यमाणं महायज्ञेनोत्तमदक्षिणेन ।तं संवर्तो याजयितेति मे श्रुतं तदिच्छामि न स तं याजयेत ॥४॥
transliteration: bṛhaspatiruvāca ,maruttamāhurmaghavanyakṣyamāṇaṁ; mahāyajñenottamadakṣiṇena ,taṁ saṁvarto yājayiteti me śrutaṁ; tadicchāmi na sa taṁ yājayeta.
english: Bṛhaspati said: "O Maghavan (Indra), they say that Marutta is about to perform a great Vedic ritual (mahāyajña) with excellent priestly fees. I have heard that Saṃvarta will officiate for him. Therefore, I do not wish that he should officiate for him."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 4. Bṛhaspati said: "O Maghavan (Indra), they say that Marutta is about to perform a great Vedic ritual (mahāyajña) with excellent priestly fees. I have heard that Saṃvarta will officiate for him. Therefore, I do not wish that he should officiate for him."

sanskrit: इन्द्र उवाच ।सर्वान्कामाननुजातोऽसि विप्र यस्त्वं देवानां मन्त्रयसे पुरोधाः ।उभौ च ते जन्ममृत्यू व्यतीतौ किं संवर्तस्तव कर्ताद्य विप्र ॥५॥
transliteration: indra uvāca ,sarvānkāmānanujāto'si vipra; yastvaṁ devānāṁ mantrayase purodhāḥ ,ubhau ca te janmamṛtyū vyatītau; kiṁ saṁvartastava kartādya vipra.
english: Indra said: "O brahmin (vipra), you are born with all desires fulfilled, and you counsel the gods as their chief priest (purodhā). Both birth and death have passed for you. Why should Saṃvarta be your concern today, O brahmin (vipra)?"
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 5. Indra said: "O brahmin (vipra), you are born with all desires fulfilled, and you counsel the gods as their chief priest (purodhā). Both birth and death have passed for you. Why should Saṃvarta be your concern today, O brahmin (vipra)?"

sanskrit: बृहस्पतिरुवाच ।देवैः सह त्वमसुरान्संप्रणुद्य जिघांससेऽद्याप्युत सानुबन्धान् ।यं यं समृद्धं पश्यसि तत्र तत्र दुःखं सपत्नेषु समृद्धभावः ॥६॥
transliteration: bṛhaspatiruvāca ,devaiḥ saha tvamasurānsaṁpraṇudya; jighāṁsase'dyāpyuta sānubandhān ,yaṁ yaṁ samṛddhaṁ paśyasi tatra tatra; duḥkhaṁ sapatneṣu samṛddhabhāvaḥ.
english: Bṛhaspati said: "You, along with the gods, having completely driven away the asuras, still wish to kill them, even their followers. Whomever you see prosperous, that prosperity of your rivals invariably causes you distress."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 6. Bṛhaspati said: "You, along with the gods, having completely driven away the asuras, still wish to kill them, even their followers. Whomever you see prosperous, that prosperity of your rivals invariably causes you distress."

sanskrit: अतोऽस्मि देवेन्द्र विवर्णरूपः सपत्नो मे वर्धते तन्निशम्य ।सर्वोपायैर्मघवन्संनियच्छ संवर्तं वा पार्थिवं वा मरुत्तम् ॥७॥
transliteration: ato'smi devendra vivarṇarūpaḥ; sapatno me vardhate tanniśamya ,sarvopāyairmaghavansaṁniyaccha; saṁvartaṁ vā pārthivaṁ vā maruttam.
english: Hearing that my rival prospers, O king of gods, I am therefore discolored (pale-faced). O Maghavan, by all means, restrain either Saṃvarta or Marutta the king.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 7. Hearing that my rival prospers, O king of gods, I am therefore discolored (pale-faced). O Maghavan, by all means, restrain either Saṃvarta or Marutta the king.

sanskrit: इन्द्र उवाच ।एहि गच्छ प्रहितो जातवेदो बृहस्पतिं परिदातुं मरुत्ते ।अयं वै त्वा याजयिता बृहस्पतिस्तथामरं चैव करिष्यतीति ॥८॥
transliteration: indra uvāca ,ehi gaccha prahito jātavedo; bṛhaspatiṁ paridātuṁ marutte ,ayaṁ vai tvā yājayitā bṛhaspati;stathāmaraṁ caiva kariṣyatīti.
english: Indra spoke: 'Come, go forth, O Jātavedas (Agni), sent to entrust Bṛhaspati to Marutta. Indeed, this Bṛhaspati will cause him (Marutta) to perform a Vedic ritual (yajña) and will also render him immortal.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 8. Indra spoke: 'Come, go forth, O Jātavedas (Agni), sent to entrust Bṛhaspati to Marutta. Indeed, this Bṛhaspati will cause him (Marutta) to perform a Vedic ritual (yajña) and will also render him immortal.'

sanskrit: अग्निरुवाच ।अयं गच्छामि तव शक्राद्य दूतो बृहस्पतिं परिदातुं मरुत्ते ।वाचं सत्यां पुरुहूतस्य कर्तुं बृहस्पतेश्चापचितिं चिकीर्षुः ॥९॥
transliteration: agniruvāca ,ayaṁ gacchāmi tava śakrādya dūto; bṛhaspatiṁ paridātuṁ marutte ,vācaṁ satyāṁ puruhūtasya kartuṁ; bṛhaspateścāpacitiṁ cikīrṣuḥ.
english: Agni spoke: 'I, as your messenger, O Śakra (Indra), go today to entrust Bṛhaspati to Marutta, intending to make the word of Puruhūta (Indra) true and desiring to show respect to Bṛhaspati.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 9. Agni spoke: 'I, as your messenger, O Śakra (Indra), go today to entrust Bṛhaspati to Marutta, intending to make the word of Puruhūta (Indra) true and desiring to show respect to Bṛhaspati.'

sanskrit: व्यास उवाच ।ततः प्रायाद्धूमकेतुर्महात्मा वनस्पतीन्वीरुधश्चावमृद्नन् ।कामाद्धिमान्ते परिवर्तमानः काष्ठातिगो मातरिश्वेव नर्दन् ॥१०॥
transliteration: vyāsa uvāca ,tataḥ prāyāddhūmaketurmahātmā; vanaspatīnvīrudhaścāvamṛdnan ,kāmāddhimānte parivartamānaḥ; kāṣṭhātigo mātariśveva nardan.
english: Vyāsa spoke: 'Then the great-souled Dhūmaketū (Agni) departed, crushing both trees and creepers. He moved about at the end of winter according to his will, roaring like the wind (mātariśvan) that transcends all directions.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 10. Vyāsa spoke: 'Then the great-souled Dhūmaketū (Agni) departed, crushing both trees and creepers. He moved about at the end of winter according to his will, roaring like the wind (mātariśvan) that transcends all directions.'

sanskrit: मरुत्त उवाच ।आश्चर्यमद्य पश्यामि रूपिणं वह्निमागतम् ।आसनं सलिलं पाद्यं गां चोपानय वै मुने ॥११॥
transliteration: marutta uvāca ,āścaryamadya paśyāmi rūpiṇaṁ vahnimāgatam ,āsanaṁ salilaṁ pādyaṁ gāṁ copānaya vai mune.
english: Marutta said: 'Today I see a marvel: fire (vahni) has arrived in embodied form. O wise one (muni), please bring a seat, water, water for the feet, and a cow.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 11. Marutta said: 'Today I see a marvel: fire (vahni) has arrived in embodied form. O wise one (muni), please bring a seat, water, water for the feet, and a cow.'

sanskrit: अग्निरुवाच ।आसनं सलिलं पाद्यं प्रतिनन्दामि तेऽनघ ।इन्द्रेण तु समादिष्टं विद्धि मां दूतमागतम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: agniruvāca ,āsanaṁ salilaṁ pādyaṁ pratinandāmi te'nagha ,indreṇa tu samādiṣṭaṁ viddhi māṁ dūtamāgatam.
english: Agni said: 'O sinless one, I gratefully accept your seat, water, and water for the feet. But know that I have come as a messenger, specifically instructed by Indra.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 12. Agni said: 'O sinless one, I gratefully accept your seat, water, and water for the feet. But know that I have come as a messenger, specifically instructed by Indra.'

sanskrit: मरुत्त उवाच ।कच्चिच्छ्रीमान्देवराजः सुखी च कच्चिच्चास्मान्प्रीयते धूमकेतो ।कच्चिद्देवाश्चास्य वशे यथावत्तद्ब्रूहि त्वं मम कार्त्स्न्येन देव ॥१३॥
transliteration: marutta uvāca ,kaccicchrīmāndevarājaḥ sukhī ca; kacciccāsmānprīyate dhūmaketo ,kacciddevāścāsya vaśe yathāva;ttadbrūhi tvaṁ mama kārtsnyena deva.
english: Marutta said: 'O smoke-bannered one (dhumaketu), is the glorious king of gods (devarāja) well and happy? Is he also pleased with us? Are the gods truly under his control? Please tell me all of this completely, O god.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 13. Marutta said: 'O smoke-bannered one (dhumaketu), is the glorious king of gods (devarāja) well and happy? Is he also pleased with us? Are the gods truly under his control? Please tell me all of this completely, O god.'

sanskrit: अग्निरुवाच ।शक्रो भृशं सुसुखी पार्थिवेन्द्र प्रीतिं चेच्छत्यजरां वै त्वया सः ।देवाश्च सर्वे वशगास्तस्य राजन्संदेशं त्वं शृणु मे देवराज्ञः ॥१४॥
transliteration: agniruvāca ,śakro bhṛśaṁ susukhī pārthivendra; prītiṁ cecchatyajarāṁ vai tvayā saḥ ,devāśca sarve vaśagāstasya rāja;nsaṁdeśaṁ tvaṁ śṛṇu me devarājñaḥ.
english: Agni said: 'O king (pārthivendra), Shakra (Indra) is exceedingly happy, and indeed he desires an unfading affection with you. All the gods are obedient to him, O king. Now, listen to my message from the king of gods (devarāja).'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 14. Agni said: 'O king (pārthivendra), Shakra (Indra) is exceedingly happy, and indeed he desires an unfading affection with you. All the gods are obedient to him, O king. Now, listen to my message from the king of gods (devarāja).'

sanskrit: यदर्थं मां प्राहिणोत्त्वत्सकाशं बृहस्पतिं परिदातुं मरुत्ते ।अयं गुरुर्याजयिता नृप त्वां मर्त्यं सन्तममरं त्वां करोतु ॥१५॥
transliteration: yadarthaṁ māṁ prāhiṇottvatsakāśaṁ; bṛhaspatiṁ paridātuṁ marutte ,ayaṁ gururyājayitā nṛpa tvāṁ; martyaṁ santamamaraṁ tvāṁ karotu.
english: O Marutta, the purpose for which he sent me to your presence was to hand over Bṛhaspati. O king, may this preceptor, the officiant for your Vedic ritual (yajña), make you, though mortal, immortal.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 15. O Marutta, the purpose for which he sent me to your presence was to hand over Bṛhaspati. O king, may this preceptor, the officiant for your Vedic ritual (yajña), make you, though mortal, immortal.

sanskrit: मरुत्त उवाच ।संवर्तोऽयं याजयिता द्विजो मे बृहस्पतेरञ्जलिरेष तस्य ।नासौ देवं याजयित्वा महेन्द्रं मर्त्यं सन्तं याजयन्नद्य शोभेत् ॥१६॥
transliteration: marutta uvāca ,saṁvarto'yaṁ yājayitā dvijo me; bṛhaspaterañjalireṣa tasya ,nāsau devaṁ yājayitvā mahendraṁ; martyaṁ santaṁ yājayannadya śobhet.
english: Marutta said: 'This Saṃvarta is my Brahmin ritual (yajña) officiant. His (Saṃvarta's) reverence (añjali) is directed towards Bṛhaspati. That one (Bṛhaspati), having officiated a Vedic ritual (yajña) for the great Indra, would not be suitable today to officiate for a mortal being (martya).'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 16. Marutta said: 'This Saṃvarta is my Brahmin ritual (yajña) officiant. His (Saṃvarta's) reverence (añjali) is directed towards Bṛhaspati. That one (Bṛhaspati), having officiated a Vedic ritual (yajña) for the great Indra, would not be suitable today to officiate for a mortal being (martya).'

sanskrit: अग्निरुवाच ।ये वै लोका देवलोके महान्तः संप्राप्स्यसे तान्देवराजप्रसादात् ।त्वां चेदसौ याजयेद्वै बृहस्पतिर्नूनं स्वर्गं त्वं जयेः कीर्तियुक्तः ॥१७॥
transliteration: agniruvāca ,ye vai lokā devaloke mahāntaḥ; saṁprāpsyase tāndevarājaprasādāt ,tvāṁ cedasau yājayedvai bṛhaspati;rnūnaṁ svargaṁ tvaṁ jayeḥ kīrtiyuktaḥ.
english: Agni said: 'Those great worlds that exist in the realm of the gods, you will certainly attain them by the grace of the king of gods. If that Bṛhaspati indeed officiates a Vedic ritual (yajña) for you, then surely you, endowed with fame, would conquer heaven.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 17. Agni said: 'Those great worlds that exist in the realm of the gods, you will certainly attain them by the grace of the king of gods. If that Bṛhaspati indeed officiates a Vedic ritual (yajña) for you, then surely you, endowed with fame, would conquer heaven.'

sanskrit: तथा लोका मानुषा ये च दिव्याः प्रजापतेश्चापि ये वै महान्तः ।ते ते जिता देवराज्यं च कृत्स्नं बृहस्पतिश्चेद्याजयेत्त्वां नरेन्द्र ॥१८॥
transliteration: tathā lokā mānuṣā ye ca divyāḥ; prajāpateścāpi ye vai mahāntaḥ ,te te jitā devarājyaṁ ca kṛtsnaṁ; bṛhaspatiścedyājayettvāṁ narendra.
english: O king (narendra), similarly, those human and divine worlds, and also those great worlds of Prajāpati  -  all of those will be conquered for you, and the entire kingdom of the gods (will be yours), if Bṛhaspati officiates a Vedic ritual (yajña) for you.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 18. O king (narendra), similarly, those human and divine worlds, and also those great worlds of Prajāpati  -  all of those will be conquered for you, and the entire kingdom of the gods (will be yours), if Bṛhaspati officiates a Vedic ritual (yajña) for you.

sanskrit: संवर्त उवाच ।मास्मानेवं त्वं पुनरागाः कथंचिद्बृहस्पतिं परिदातुं मरुत्ते ।मा त्वां धक्ष्ये चक्षुषा दारुणेन संक्रुद्धोऽहं पावक तन्निबोध ॥१९॥
transliteration: saṁvarta uvāca ,māsmānevaṁ tvaṁ punarāgāḥ kathaṁci;dbṛhaspatiṁ paridātuṁ marutte ,mā tvāṁ dhakṣye cakṣuṣā dāruṇena; saṁkruddho'haṁ pāvaka tannibodha.
english: Sāṃvarta said: "Do not ever approach us again in this manner to hand over Bṛhaspati to Marutta. O Pāvaka (fire god), know that I, being greatly enraged, will burn you with my fierce eye."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 19. Sāṃvarta said: "Do not ever approach us again in this manner to hand over Bṛhaspati to Marutta. O Pāvaka (fire god), know that I, being greatly enraged, will burn you with my fierce eye."

sanskrit: व्यास उवाच ।ततो देवानगमद्धूमकेतुर्दाहाद्भीतो व्यथितोऽश्वत्थपर्णवत् ।तं वै दृष्ट्वा प्राह शक्रो महात्मा बृहस्पतेः संनिधौ हव्यवाहम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: vyāsa uvāca ,tato devānagamaddhūmaketu;rdāhādbhīto vyathito'śvatthaparṇavat ,taṁ vai dṛṣṭvā prāha śakro mahātmā; bṛhaspateḥ saṁnidhau havyavāham.
english: Vyāsa said: "Then Dhūmaketu (Agni), terrified by the prospect of being burnt and agitated like an Aśvattha leaf, went to the gods. Seeing him, the great-souled Śakra (Indra) addressed Havyavāha (Agni) in the presence of Bṛhaspati."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 20. Vyāsa said: "Then Dhūmaketu (Agni), terrified by the prospect of being burnt and agitated like an Aśvattha leaf, went to the gods. Seeing him, the great-souled Śakra (Indra) addressed Havyavāha (Agni) in the presence of Bṛhaspati."

sanskrit: यत्त्वं गतः प्रहितो जातवेदो बृहस्पतिं परिदातुं मरुत्ते ।तत्किं प्राह स नृपो यक्ष्यमाणः कच्चिद्वचः प्रतिगृह्णाति तच्च ॥२१॥
transliteration: yattvaṁ gataḥ prahito jātavedo; bṛhaspatiṁ paridātuṁ marutte ,tatkiṁ prāha sa nṛpo yakṣyamāṇaḥ; kaccidvacaḥ pratigṛhṇāti tacca.
english: "Since you, O Jātavedas (Agni), were dispatched to hand over Bṛhaspati to Marutta, what did that king, who is desirous of performing a (Vedic ritual/yajña), say? And does he indeed accept our proposal?"
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 21. "Since you, O Jātavedas (Agni), were dispatched to hand over Bṛhaspati to Marutta, what did that king, who is desirous of performing a (Vedic ritual/yajña), say? And does he indeed accept our proposal?"

sanskrit: अग्निरुवाच ।न ते वाचं रोचयते मरुत्तो बृहस्पतेरञ्जलिं प्राहिणोत्सः ।संवर्तो मां याजयितेत्यभीक्ष्णं पुनः पुनः स मया प्रोच्यमानः ॥२२॥
transliteration: agniruvāca ,na te vācaṁ rocayate marutto; bṛhaspaterañjaliṁ prāhiṇotsaḥ ,saṁvarto māṁ yājayitetyabhīkṣṇaṁ; punaḥ punaḥ sa mayā procyamānaḥ.
english: Agni said: "Marutta does not agree with your words; instead, he has extended an oblation (añjali) to Bṛhaspati. While I was being repeatedly urged by him, he kept asserting: 'Sāṃvarta will officiate my (Vedic ritual/yajña)."'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 22. Agni said: "Marutta does not agree with your words; instead, he has extended an oblation (añjali) to Bṛhaspati. While I was being repeatedly urged by him, he kept asserting: 'Sāṃvarta will officiate my (Vedic ritual/yajña)."'

sanskrit: उवाचेदं मानुषा ये च दिव्याः प्रजापतेर्ये च लोका महान्तः ।तांश्चेल्लभेयं संविदं तेन कृत्वा तथापि नेच्छेयमिति प्रतीतः ॥२३॥
transliteration: uvācedaṁ mānuṣā ye ca divyāḥ; prajāpaterye ca lokā mahāntaḥ ,tāṁścellabheyaṁ saṁvidaṁ tena kṛtvā; tathāpi neccheyamiti pratītaḥ.
english: He declared this, convinced: "Even if I were to attain knowledge (saṃvid) regarding these great realms - those of humans, those of divine beings, and those of Prajāpati - even then, I would not desire it."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 23. He declared this, convinced: "Even if I were to attain knowledge (saṃvid) regarding these great realms - those of humans, those of divine beings, and those of Prajāpati - even then, I would not desire it."

sanskrit: इन्द्र उवाच ।पुनर्भवान्पार्थिवं तं समेत्य वाक्यं मदीयं प्रापय स्वार्थयुक्तम् ।पुनर्यद्युक्तो न करिष्यते वचस्ततो वज्रं संप्रहर्तास्मि तस्मै ॥२४॥
transliteration: indra uvāca ,punarbhavānpārthivaṁ taṁ sametya; vākyaṁ madīyaṁ prāpaya svārthayuktam ,punaryadyukto na kariṣyate vaca;stato vajraṁ saṁprahartāsmi tasmai.
english: Indra said: "You (honored one) must go again, approach that earthly king, and convey my message, which is for your own benefit. If, again, he does not follow what is said, then I will strike him with the thunderbolt."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 24. Indra said: "You (honored one) must go again, approach that earthly king, and convey my message, which is for your own benefit. If, again, he does not follow what is said, then I will strike him with the thunderbolt."

sanskrit: अग्निरुवाच ।गन्धर्वराड्यात्वयं तत्र दूतो बिभेम्यहं वासव तत्र गन्तुम् ।संरब्धो मामब्रवीत्तीक्ष्णरोषः संवर्तो वाक्यं चरितब्रह्मचर्यः ॥२५॥
transliteration: agniruvāca ,gandharvarāḍyātvayaṁ tatra dūto; bibhemyahaṁ vāsava tatra gantum ,saṁrabdho māmabravīttīkṣṇaroṣaḥ; saṁvarto vākyaṁ caritabrahmacaryaḥ.
english: Agni said: "Let this Gandharva king go there as a messenger. O Vāsava (Indra), I am afraid to go there. Saṃvarta, intensely enraged and one who has practiced celibacy (brahmacarya), spoke these words to me."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 25. Agni said: "Let this Gandharva king go there as a messenger. O Vāsava (Indra), I am afraid to go there. Saṃvarta, intensely enraged and one who has practiced celibacy (brahmacarya), spoke these words to me."

sanskrit: यद्यागच्छेः पुनरेवं कथंचिद्बृहस्पतिं परिदातुं मरुत्ते ।दहेयं त्वां चक्षुषा दारुणेन संक्रुद्ध इत्येतदवैहि शक्र ॥२६॥
transliteration: yadyāgaccheḥ punarevaṁ kathaṁci;dbṛhaspatiṁ paridātuṁ marutte ,daheyaṁ tvāṁ cakṣuṣā dāruṇena; saṁkruddha ityetadavaihi śakra.
english: "If you ever come again in this manner, somehow to deliver Bṛhaspati to Marut, I will burn you with my terrible eye, being greatly enraged." Know this as what he said, O Śakra (Indra).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 26. "If you ever come again in this manner, somehow to deliver Bṛhaspati to Marut, I will burn you with my terrible eye, being greatly enraged." Know this as what he said, O Śakra (Indra).

sanskrit: इन्द्र उवाच ।त्वमेवान्यान्दहसे जातवेदो न हि त्वदन्यो विद्यते भस्मकर्ता ।त्वत्संस्पर्शात्सर्वलोको बिभेत्यश्रद्धेयं वदसे हव्यवाह ॥२७॥
transliteration: indra uvāca ,tvamevānyāndahase jātavedo; na hi tvadanyo vidyate bhasmakartā ,tvatsaṁsparśātsarvaloko bibhe;tyaśraddheyaṁ vadase havyavāha.
english: Indra said: "You alone consume others, O Fire (jātavedas); indeed, there is no one else apart from you who can reduce things to ashes. The entire world trembles at your touch. Yet, O carrier of oblations (havyavāh), you speak something unbelievable."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 27. Indra said: "You alone consume others, O Fire (jātavedas); indeed, there is no one else apart from you who can reduce things to ashes. The entire world trembles at your touch. Yet, O carrier of oblations (havyavāh), you speak something unbelievable."

sanskrit: अग्निरुवाच ।दिवं देवेन्द्र पृथिवीं चैव सर्वां संवेष्टयेस्त्वं स्वबलेनैव शक्र ।एवंविधस्येह सतस्तवासौ कथं वृत्रस्त्रिदिवं प्राग्जहार ॥२८॥
transliteration: agniruvāca ,divaṁ devendra pṛthivīṁ caiva sarvāṁ; saṁveṣṭayestvaṁ svabalenaiva śakra ,evaṁvidhasyeha satastavāsau; kathaṁ vṛtrastridivaṁ prāgjahāra.
english: Agni said: "O Indra, chief of the gods (devendra), O mighty one (śakra), you could indeed envelop the entire heaven and earth with your own strength. How then did Vṛtra previously seize the three worlds (tridivam) from you, who are so powerful and present here?"
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 28. Agni said: "O Indra, chief of the gods (devendra), O mighty one (śakra), you could indeed envelop the entire heaven and earth with your own strength. How then did Vṛtra previously seize the three worlds (tridivam) from you, who are so powerful and present here?"

sanskrit: इन्द्र उवाच ।न चण्डिका जङ्गमा नो करेणुर्न वारिसोमं प्रपिबामि वह्ने ।न दुर्बले वै विसृजामि वज्रं को मेऽसुखाय प्रहरेन्मनुष्यः ॥२९॥
transliteration: indra uvāca ,na caṇḍikā jaṅgamā no kareṇu;rna vārisomaṁ prapibāmi vahne ,na durbale vai visṛjāmi vajraṁ; ko me'sukhāya praharenmanuṣyaḥ.
english: Indra said: "O Fire (vahne), I am not a roving goddess Caṇḍikā, nor am I an elephant cow, nor do I drink water-soma. Indeed, I do not hurl my thunderbolt (vajra) at the weak. What mortal would dare strike me to cause me misery?"
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 29. Indra said: "O Fire (vahne), I am not a roving goddess Caṇḍikā, nor am I an elephant cow, nor do I drink water-soma. Indeed, I do not hurl my thunderbolt (vajra) at the weak. What mortal would dare strike me to cause me misery?"

sanskrit: प्रव्राजयेयं कालकेयान्पृथिव्यामपाकर्षं दानवानन्तरिक्षात् ।दिवः प्रह्रादमवसानमानयं को मेऽसुखाय प्रहरेत मर्त्यः ॥३०॥
transliteration: pravrājayeyaṁ kālakeyānpṛthivyā;mapākarṣaṁ dānavānantarikṣāt ,divaḥ prahrādamavasānamānayaṁ; ko me'sukhāya prahareta martyaḥ.
english: "I would banish the Kālakeyas from the earth and drag the Dānavas down from the atmosphere. I would bring Prahlāda down from heaven to his demise. What mortal (martya) would dare strike me to cause me misery?"
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 30. "I would banish the Kālakeyas from the earth and drag the Dānavas down from the atmosphere. I would bring Prahlāda down from heaven to his demise. What mortal (martya) would dare strike me to cause me misery?"

sanskrit: अग्निरुवाच ।यत्र शर्यातिं च्यवनो याजयिष्यन्सहाश्विभ्यां सोममगृह्णदेकः ।तं त्वं क्रुद्धः प्रत्यषेधीः पुरस्ताच्छर्यातियज्ञं स्मर तं महेन्द्र ॥३१॥
transliteration: agniruvāca ,yatra śaryātiṁ cyavano yājayiṣya;nsahāśvibhyāṁ somamagṛhṇadekaḥ ,taṁ tvaṁ kruddhaḥ pratyaṣedhīḥ purastā;ccharyātiyajñaṁ smara taṁ mahendra.
english: Agni spoke: "O great Indra, remember that occasion when Cyavana, while officiating for King Śaryāti in a Vedic ritual (yajña), was about to accept the soma offering for the Aśvins, uniquely. You, becoming enraged, previously obstructed him at that very Vedic ritual (yajña) of Śaryāti."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 31. Agni spoke: "O great Indra, remember that occasion when Cyavana, while officiating for King Śaryāti in a Vedic ritual (yajña), was about to accept the soma offering for the Aśvins, uniquely. You, becoming enraged, previously obstructed him at that very Vedic ritual (yajña) of Śaryāti."

sanskrit: वज्रं गृहीत्वा च पुरंदर त्वं संप्राहार्षीश्च्यवनस्यातिघोरम् ।स ते विप्रः सह वज्रेण बाहुमपागृह्णात्तपसा जातमन्युः ॥३२॥
transliteration: vajraṁ gṛhītvā ca puraṁdara tvaṁ; saṁprāhārṣīścyavanasyātighoram ,sa te vipraḥ saha vajreṇa bāhu;mapāgṛhṇāttapasā jātamanyuḥ.
english: And you, O Purandara, having grasped the thunderbolt, struck Cyavana a most terrible blow. But that sage (vipra), his anger aroused, by means of his ascetic power (tapas), seized your arm along with the thunderbolt.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 32. And you, O Purandara, having grasped the thunderbolt, struck Cyavana a most terrible blow. But that sage (vipra), his anger aroused, by means of his ascetic power (tapas), seized your arm along with the thunderbolt.

sanskrit: ततो रोषात्सर्वतो घोररूपं सपत्नं ते जनयामास भूयः ।मदं नामासुरं विश्वरूपं यं त्वं दृष्ट्वा चक्षुषी संन्यमीलः ॥३३॥
transliteration: tato roṣātsarvato ghorarūpaṁ; sapatnaṁ te janayāmāsa bhūyaḥ ,madaṁ nāmāsuraṁ viśvarūpaṁ; yaṁ tvaṁ dṛṣṭvā cakṣuṣī saṁnyamīlaḥ.
english: Then, out of anger, he (Cyavana) again generated for you a dreadful-looking enemy, by name Mada, a demon (asura) of cosmic form, at the sight of whom you closed both your eyes.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 33. Then, out of anger, he (Cyavana) again generated for you a dreadful-looking enemy, by name Mada, a demon (asura) of cosmic form, at the sight of whom you closed both your eyes.

sanskrit: हनुरेका जगतीस्था तथैका दिवं गता महतो दानवस्य ।सहस्रं दन्तानां शतयोजनानां सुतीक्ष्णानां घोररूपं बभूव ॥३४॥
transliteration: hanurekā jagatīsthā tathaikā; divaṁ gatā mahato dānavasya ,sahasraṁ dantānāṁ śatayojanānāṁ; sutīkṣṇānāṁ ghorarūpaṁ babhūva.
english: One jaw of that mighty demon (dānava) was situated on the earth, and the other had reached the sky. A thousand teeth, each a hundred yojanas long and exceedingly sharp, became a dreadful sight.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 34. One jaw of that mighty demon (dānava) was situated on the earth, and the other had reached the sky. A thousand teeth, each a hundred yojanas long and exceedingly sharp, became a dreadful sight.

sanskrit: वृत्ताः स्थूला रजतस्तम्भवर्णा दंष्ट्राश्चतस्रो द्वे शते योजनानाम् ।स त्वां दन्तान्विदशन्नभ्यधावज्जिघांसया शूलमुद्यम्य घोरम् ॥३५॥
transliteration: vṛttāḥ sthūlā rajatastambhavarṇā; daṁṣṭrāścatasro dve śate yojanānām ,sa tvāṁ dantānvidaśannabhyadhāva;jjighāṁsayā śūlamudyamya ghoram.
english: Four tusks, round, thick, and the color of silver pillars, each two hundred *yojanas* long - he rushed towards you, gnashing his teeth, raising a terrible spear with the intention to kill.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 35. Four tusks, round, thick, and the color of silver pillars, each two hundred *yojanas* long - he rushed towards you, gnashing his teeth, raising a terrible spear with the intention to kill.

sanskrit: अपश्यस्त्वं तं तदा घोररूपं सर्वे त्वन्ये ददृशुर्दर्शनीयम् ।यस्माद्भीतः प्राञ्जलिस्त्वं महर्षिमागच्छेथाः शरणं दानवघ्न ॥३६॥
transliteration: apaśyastvaṁ taṁ tadā ghorarūpaṁ; sarve tvanye dadṛśurdarśanīyam ,yasmādbhītaḥ prāñjalistvaṁ maharṣi;māgacchethāḥ śaraṇaṁ dānavaghna.
english: You then saw him in a terrible form, while all others perceived him as beautiful. Frightened by him, you, with folded hands, approached the great sage for refuge, O slayer of Dānavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 36. You then saw him in a terrible form, while all others perceived him as beautiful. Frightened by him, you, with folded hands, approached the great sage for refuge, O slayer of Dānavas.

sanskrit: क्षत्रादेवं ब्रह्मबलं गरीयो न ब्रह्मतः किंचिदन्यद्गरीयः ।सोऽहं जानन्ब्रह्मतेजो यथावन्न संवर्तं गन्तुमिच्छामि शक्र ॥३७॥
transliteration: kṣatrādevaṁ brahmabalaṁ garīyo; na brahmataḥ kiṁcidanyadgarīyaḥ ,so'haṁ jānanbrahmatejo yathāva;nna saṁvartaṁ gantumicchāmi śakra.
english: Thus, the power of (brahman) is superior to that of the warrior (kṣatra); nothing else is superior to (brahman). Therefore, O Indra, knowing the spiritual power (brahma-tejas) as it truly is, I do not wish to face destruction.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 9 Verse 37. Thus, the power of (brahman) is superior to that of the warrior (kṣatra); nothing else is superior to (brahman). Therefore, O Indra, knowing the spiritual power (brahma-tejas) as it truly is, I do not wish to face destruction.

sanskrit: इन्द्र उवाच ।एवमेतद्ब्रह्मबलं गरीयो न ब्रह्मतः किंचिदन्यद्गरीयः ।आविक्षितस्य तु बलं न मृष्ये वज्रमस्मै प्रहरिष्यामि घोरम् ॥१॥
transliteration: indra uvāca ,evametadbrahmabalaṁ garīyo; na brahmataḥ kiṁcidanyadgarīyaḥ ,āvikṣitasya tu balaṁ na mṛṣye; vajramasmai prahariṣyāmi ghoram.
english: Indra said: 'It is indeed true that the power of (brahman) is superior, and nothing else is superior to (brahman). However, I will not tolerate the strength of Āvīkṣita; I will strike him with my terrible thunderbolt.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 1. Indra said: 'It is indeed true that the power of (brahman) is superior, and nothing else is superior to (brahman). However, I will not tolerate the strength of Āvīkṣita; I will strike him with my terrible thunderbolt.'

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र प्रहितो गच्छ मरुत्तं संवर्तेन सहितं तं वदस्व ।बृहस्पतिं त्वमुपशिक्षस्व राजन्वज्रं वा ते प्रहरिष्यामि घोरम् ॥२॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra prahito gaccha maruttaṁ; saṁvartena sahitaṁ taṁ vadasva ,bṛhaspatiṁ tvamupaśikṣasva rāja;nvajraṁ vā te prahariṣyāmi ghoram.
english: Dhṛtarāṣṭra, having been sent, go to Marutta, who is accompanied by Saṃvarta, and convey this message to him. O king, either instruct yourself under Bṛhaspati, or I will strike you with a terrible thunderbolt.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 2. Dhṛtarāṣṭra, having been sent, go to Marutta, who is accompanied by Saṃvarta, and convey this message to him. O king, either instruct yourself under Bṛhaspati, or I will strike you with a terrible thunderbolt.

sanskrit: व्यास उवाच ।ततो गत्वा धृतराष्ट्रो नरेन्द्रं प्रोवाचेदं वचनं वासवस्य ।गन्धर्वं मां धृतराष्ट्रं निबोध त्वामागतं वक्तुकामं नरेन्द्र ॥३॥
transliteration: vyāsa uvāca ,tato gatvā dhṛtarāṣṭro narendraṁ; provācedaṁ vacanaṁ vāsavasya ,gandharvaṁ māṁ dhṛtarāṣṭraṁ nibodha; tvāmāgataṁ vaktukāmaṁ narendra.
english: Vyāsa said: Then, having gone, Dhṛtarāṣṭra spoke these words of Vāsava (Indra) to the king (Marutta): 'O king, know that I am the gandharva Dhṛtarāṣṭra, who has come to you, desiring to speak.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 3. Vyāsa said: Then, having gone, Dhṛtarāṣṭra spoke these words of Vāsava (Indra) to the king (Marutta): 'O king, know that I am the gandharva Dhṛtarāṣṭra, who has come to you, desiring to speak.'

sanskrit: ऐन्द्रं वाक्यं शृणु मे राजसिंह यत्प्राह लोकाधिपतिर्महात्मा ।बृहस्पतिं याजकं त्वं वृणीष्व वज्रं वा ते प्रहरिष्यामि घोरम् ।वचश्चेदेतन्न करिष्यसे मे प्राहैतदेतावदचिन्त्यकर्मा ॥४॥
transliteration: aindraṁ vākyaṁ śṛṇu me rājasiṁha; yatprāha lokādhipatirmahātmā ,bṛhaspatiṁ yājakaṁ tvaṁ vṛṇīṣva; vajraṁ vā te prahariṣyāmi ghoram ,vacaścedetanna kariṣyase me; prāhaitadetāvadacintyakarmā.
english: O lion among kings, listen to these words of Indra that the great-souled lord of the worlds (Indra) has spoken through me. Either choose Bṛhaspati as your officiating priest, or I will strike you with a terrible thunderbolt. If you do not heed these words of mine, then this (consequence) is what he, whose actions are beyond comprehension, has declared.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 4. O lion among kings, listen to these words of Indra that the great-souled lord of the worlds (Indra) has spoken through me. Either choose Bṛhaspati as your officiating priest, or I will strike you with a terrible thunderbolt. If you do not heed these words of mine, then this (consequence) is what he, whose actions are beyond comprehension, has declared.

sanskrit: मरुत्त उवाच ।त्वं चैवैतद्वेत्थ पुरंदरश्च विश्वेदेवा वसवश्चाश्विनौ च ।मित्रद्रोहे निष्कृतिर्वै यथैव नास्तीति लोकेषु सदैव वादः ॥५॥
transliteration: marutta uvāca ,tvaṁ caivaitadvettha puraṁdaraśca; viśvedevā vasavaścāśvinau ca ,mitradrohe niṣkṛtirvai yathaiva; nāstīti lokeṣu sadaiva vādaḥ.
english: Marutta said: 'You (the messenger Dhṛtarāṣṭra) and Purandara (Indra), along with the Viśvedevas, Vasus, and the two Aśvins, all know this truth: there is indeed no atonement for treachery against a friend. This is always the established principle among people.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 5. Marutta said: 'You (the messenger Dhṛtarāṣṭra) and Purandara (Indra), along with the Viśvedevas, Vasus, and the two Aśvins, all know this truth: there is indeed no atonement for treachery against a friend. This is always the established principle among people.'

sanskrit: बृहस्पतिर्याजयिता महेन्द्रं देवश्रेष्ठं वज्रभृतां वरिष्ठम् ।संवर्तो मां याजयिताद्य राजन्न ते वाक्यं तस्य वा रोचयामि ॥६॥
transliteration: bṛhaspatiryājayitā mahendraṁ; devaśreṣṭhaṁ vajrabhṛtāṁ variṣṭham ,saṁvarto māṁ yājayitādya rāja;nna te vākyaṁ tasya vā rocayāmi.
english: Bṛhaspati performs the Vedic ritual for Mahendra, the chief of the gods, the foremost among those who wield the thunderbolt. Today, Saṃvarta will conduct the Vedic ritual for me, O King. I do not approve of your statement, nor his.
