search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 6. Bṛhaspati performs the Vedic ritual for Mahendra, the chief of the gods, the foremost among those who wield the thunderbolt. Today, Saṃvarta will conduct the Vedic ritual for me, O King. I do not approve of your statement, nor his.

sanskrit: गन्धर्व उवाच ।घोरो नादः श्रूयते वासवस्य नभस्तले गर्जतो राजसिंह ।व्यक्तं वज्रं मोक्ष्यते ते महेन्द्रः क्षेमं राजंश्चिन्त्यतामेष कालः ॥७॥
transliteration: gandharva uvāca ,ghoro nādaḥ śrūyate vāsavasya; nabhastale garjato rājasiṁha ,vyaktaṁ vajraṁ mokṣyate te mahendraḥ; kṣemaṁ rājaṁścintyatāmeṣa kālaḥ.
english: The Gandharva said: 'O lion among kings, a terrible roar of Vāsava (Indra) is heard thundering in the sky! Clearly, Mahendra will release his thunderbolt upon you. O King, this is the time, safety must be considered!'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 7. The Gandharva said: 'O lion among kings, a terrible roar of Vāsava (Indra) is heard thundering in the sky! Clearly, Mahendra will release his thunderbolt upon you. O King, this is the time, safety must be considered!'

sanskrit: व्यास उवाच ।इत्येवमुक्तो धृतराष्ट्रेण राजा श्रुत्वा नादं नदतो वासवस्य ।तपोनित्यं धर्मविदां वरिष्ठं संवर्तं तं ज्ञापयामास कार्यम् ॥८॥
transliteration: vyāsa uvāca ,ityevamukto dhṛtarāṣṭreṇa rājā; śrutvā nādaṁ nadato vāsavasya ,taponityaṁ dharmavidāṁ variṣṭhaṁ; saṁvartaṁ taṁ jñāpayāmāsa kāryam.
english: Vyāsa said: 'Thus addressed by Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the king, having heard the roar of the thundering Vāsava (Indra), informed that Saṃvarta - who was constantly devoted to asceticism (tapas) and foremost among those who understand (natural) law (dharma) - of the task.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 8. Vyāsa said: 'Thus addressed by Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the king, having heard the roar of the thundering Vāsava (Indra), informed that Saṃvarta - who was constantly devoted to asceticism (tapas) and foremost among those who understand (natural) law (dharma) - of the task.'

sanskrit: मरुत्त उवाच ।इममश्मानं प्लवमानमारादध्वा दूरं तेन न दृश्यतेऽद्य ।प्रपद्येऽहं शर्म विप्रेन्द्र त्वत्तः प्रयच्छ तस्मादभयं विप्रमुख्य ॥९॥
transliteration: marutta uvāca ,imamaśmānaṁ plavamānamārā;dadhvā dūraṁ tena na dṛśyate'dya ,prapadye'haṁ śarma viprendra tvattaḥ; prayaccha tasmādabhayaṁ vipramukhya.
english: Marutta said: 'This floating stone is not seen by him (Indra) today, as the distance is great. I seek refuge from you, O best of brahmins. Therefore, grant me fearlessness, O foremost brahmin!'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 9. Marutta said: 'This floating stone is not seen by him (Indra) today, as the distance is great. I seek refuge from you, O best of brahmins. Therefore, grant me fearlessness, O foremost brahmin!'

sanskrit: अयमायाति वै वज्री दिशो विद्योतयन्दश ।अमानुषेण घोरेण सदस्यास्त्रासिता हि नः ॥१०॥
transliteration: ayamāyāti vai vajrī diśo vidyotayandaśa ,amānuṣeṇa ghoreṇa sadasyāstrāsitā hi naḥ.
english: Indeed, the wielder of the thunderbolt (Vajrī) approaches, illuminating all ten directions. Our assembly members have been terrified by a dreadful, superhuman presence.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 10. Indeed, the wielder of the thunderbolt (Vajrī) approaches, illuminating all ten directions. Our assembly members have been terrified by a dreadful, superhuman presence.

sanskrit: संवर्त उवाच ।भयं शक्राद्व्येतु ते राजसिंह प्रणोत्स्येऽहं भयमेतत्सुघोरम् ।संस्तम्भिन्या विद्यया क्षिप्रमेव मा भैस्त्वमस्माद्भव चापि प्रतीतः ॥११॥
transliteration: saṁvarta uvāca ,bhayaṁ śakrādvyetu te rājasiṁha; praṇotsye'haṁ bhayametatsughoram ,saṁstambhinyā vidyayā kṣiprameva; mā bhaistvamasmādbhava cāpi pratītaḥ.
english: Saṃvarta said: 'O lion among kings, let your fear of Śakra (Indra) vanish. I shall indeed dispel this extremely dreadful fear. With the paralyzing knowledge (saṃstambhinī vidyā), I will swiftly ensure it. Do not be afraid of this, and also be confident!'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 11. Saṃvarta said: 'O lion among kings, let your fear of Śakra (Indra) vanish. I shall indeed dispel this extremely dreadful fear. With the paralyzing knowledge (saṃstambhinī vidyā), I will swiftly ensure it. Do not be afraid of this, and also be confident!'

sanskrit: अहं संस्तम्भयिष्यामि मा भैस्त्वं शक्रतो नृप ।सर्वेषामेव देवानां क्षपितान्यायुधानि मे ॥१२॥
transliteration: ahaṁ saṁstambhayiṣyāmi mā bhaistvaṁ śakrato nṛpa ,sarveṣāmeva devānāṁ kṣapitānyāyudhāni me.
english: O king, I shall paralyze (Śakra); do not be afraid of Śakra (Indra). Indeed, all the weapons of the gods have been rendered ineffective by me.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 12. O king, I shall paralyze (Śakra); do not be afraid of Śakra (Indra). Indeed, all the weapons of the gods have been rendered ineffective by me.

sanskrit: दिशो वज्रं व्रजतां वायुरेतु वर्षं भूत्वा निपततु काननेषु ।आपः प्लवन्त्वन्तरिक्षे वृथा च सौदामिनी दृश्यतां मा बिभस्त्वम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: diśo vajraṁ vrajatāṁ vāyuretu; varṣaṁ bhūtvā nipatatu kānaneṣu ,āpaḥ plavantvantarikṣe vṛthā ca; saudāminī dṛśyatāṁ mā bibhastvam.
english: Let the thunderbolt go towards other directions. Let the wind come, and let it become rain and fall upon the forests. Let the waters float uselessly in the sky, and let the lightning (saudāminī) be seen in vain. Do not be afraid.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 13. Let the thunderbolt go towards other directions. Let the wind come, and let it become rain and fall upon the forests. Let the waters float uselessly in the sky, and let the lightning (saudāminī) be seen in vain. Do not be afraid.

sanskrit: अथो वह्निस्त्रातु वा सर्वतस्ते कामं वर्षं वर्षतु वासवो वा ।वज्रं तथा स्थापयतां च वायुर्महाघोरं प्लवमानं जलौघैः ॥१४॥
transliteration: atho vahnistrātu vā sarvataste; kāmaṁ varṣaṁ varṣatu vāsavo vā ,vajraṁ tathā sthāpayatāṁ ca vāyu;rmahāghoraṁ plavamānaṁ jalaughaiḥ.
english: And now, let Agni protect you from all sides, or let Vasava (Indra) bring desired rain. And likewise, let [the deities including] Vayu stabilize the exceedingly dreadful thunderbolt, which is floating amidst torrents of water.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 14. And now, let Agni protect you from all sides, or let Vasava (Indra) bring desired rain. And likewise, let [the deities including] Vayu stabilize the exceedingly dreadful thunderbolt, which is floating amidst torrents of water.

sanskrit: मरुत्त उवाच ।घोरः शब्दः श्रूयते वै महास्वनो वज्रस्यैष सहितो मारुतेन ।आत्मा हि मे प्रव्यथते मुहुर्मुहुर्न मे स्वास्थ्यं जायते चाद्य विप्र ॥१५॥
transliteration: marutta uvāca ,ghoraḥ śabdaḥ śrūyate vai mahāsvano; vajrasyaiṣa sahito mārutena ,ātmā hi me pravyathate muhurmuhu;rna me svāsthyaṁ jāyate cādya vipra.
english: Marutta said: 'A dreadful sound is heard, indeed, a great roar of the thunderbolt, accompanied by the wind. My inner self (ātman) is greatly distressed repeatedly; I do not experience peace of mind today, O brahmin.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 15. Marutta said: 'A dreadful sound is heard, indeed, a great roar of the thunderbolt, accompanied by the wind. My inner self (ātman) is greatly distressed repeatedly; I do not experience peace of mind today, O brahmin.'

sanskrit: संवर्त उवाच ।वज्रादुग्राद्व्येतु भयं तवाद्य वातो भूत्वा हन्मि नरेन्द्र वज्रम् ।भयं त्यक्त्वा वरमन्यं वृणीष्व कं ते कामं तपसा साधयामि ॥१६॥
transliteration: saṁvarta uvāca ,vajrādugrādvyetu bhayaṁ tavādya; vāto bhūtvā hanmi narendra vajram ,bhayaṁ tyaktvā varamanyaṁ vṛṇīṣva; kaṁ te kāmaṁ tapasā sādhayāmi.
english: Saṃvarta said: 'Let your fear of the dreadful thunderbolt vanish today. Becoming the wind, O king, I will strike down the thunderbolt. Abandoning fear, choose another boon. What desire of yours shall I accomplish through ascetic practice (tapas)?'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 16. Saṃvarta said: 'Let your fear of the dreadful thunderbolt vanish today. Becoming the wind, O king, I will strike down the thunderbolt. Abandoning fear, choose another boon. What desire of yours shall I accomplish through ascetic practice (tapas)?'

sanskrit: मरुत्त उवाच ।इन्द्रः साक्षात्सहसाभ्येतु विप्र हविर्यज्ञे प्रतिगृह्णातु चैव ।स्वं स्वं धिष्ण्यं चैव जुषन्तु देवाः सुतं सोमं प्रतिगृह्णन्तु चैव ॥१७॥
transliteration: marutta uvāca ,indraḥ sākṣātsahasābhyetu vipra; haviryajñe pratigṛhṇātu caiva ,svaṁ svaṁ dhiṣṇyaṁ caiva juṣantu devāḥ; sutaṁ somaṁ pratigṛhṇantu caiva.
english: Marutta said: 'Let Indra himself come swiftly, O brahmin, and let him accept the oblation in this Vedic ritual (yajña). And let the gods indeed enjoy their respective dwelling places, and also accept the pressed soma (soma juice).'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 17. Marutta said: 'Let Indra himself come swiftly, O brahmin, and let him accept the oblation in this Vedic ritual (yajña). And let the gods indeed enjoy their respective dwelling places, and also accept the pressed soma (soma juice).'

sanskrit: संवर्त उवाच ।अयमिन्द्रो हरिभिरायाति राजन्देवैः सर्वैः सहितः सोमपीथी ।मन्त्राहूतो यज्ञमिमं मयाद्य पश्यस्वैनं मन्त्रविस्रस्तकायम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: saṁvarta uvāca ,ayamindro haribhirāyāti rāja;ndevaiḥ sarvaiḥ sahitaḥ somapīthī ,mantrāhūto yajñamimaṁ mayādya; paśyasvainaṁ mantravisrastakāyam.
english: Samvarta said: "O King, here comes Indra with his tawny horses, accompanied by all the gods, a drinker of soma. He has been invoked by my mantras to this Vedic ritual (yajña) today. Behold him, his body softened by the power of the mantras."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 18. Samvarta said: "O King, here comes Indra with his tawny horses, accompanied by all the gods, a drinker of soma. He has been invoked by my mantras to this Vedic ritual (yajña) today. Behold him, his body softened by the power of the mantras."

sanskrit: व्यास उवाच ।ततो देवैः सहितो देवराजो रथे युक्त्वा तान्हरीन्वाजिमुख्यान् ।आयाद्यज्ञमधि राज्ञः पिपासुराविक्षितस्याप्रमेयस्य सोमम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: vyāsa uvāca ,tato devaiḥ sahito devarājo; rathe yuktvā tānharīnvājimukhyān ,āyādyajñamadhi rājñaḥ pipāsu;rāvikṣitasyāprameyasya somam.
english: Vyasa said: Then the king of the gods, Indra, accompanied by the other gods, yoking those excellent tawny horses to his chariot, came to the (Vedic ritual) yajña of the immeasurable king Avikṣita, desirous of drinking the soma.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 19. Vyasa said: Then the king of the gods, Indra, accompanied by the other gods, yoking those excellent tawny horses to his chariot, came to the (Vedic ritual) yajña of the immeasurable king Avikṣita, desirous of drinking the soma.

sanskrit: तमायान्तं सहितं देवसंघैः प्रत्युद्ययौ सपुरोधा मरुत्तः ।चक्रे पूजां देवराजाय चाग्र्यां यथाशास्त्रं विधिवत्प्रीयमाणः ॥२०॥
transliteration: tamāyāntaṁ sahitaṁ devasaṁghaiḥ; pratyudyayau sapurodhā maruttaḥ ,cakre pūjāṁ devarājāya cāgryāṁ; yathāśāstraṁ vidhivatprīyamāṇaḥ.
english: Marutta, accompanied by his priest, went forth to meet him as he was approaching, together with the hosts of gods. Delighted, he performed an excellent worship for the king of gods, according to the scriptures and proper rites.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 20. Marutta, accompanied by his priest, went forth to meet him as he was approaching, together with the hosts of gods. Delighted, he performed an excellent worship for the king of gods, according to the scriptures and proper rites.

sanskrit: संवर्त उवाच ।स्वागतं ते पुरुहूतेह विद्वन्यज्ञोऽद्यायं संनिहिते त्वयीन्द्र ।शोशुभ्यते बलवृत्रघ्न भूयः पिबस्व सोमं सुतमुद्यतं मया ॥२१॥
transliteration: saṁvarta uvāca ,svāgataṁ te puruhūteha vidva;nyajño'dyāyaṁ saṁnihite tvayīndra ,śośubhyate balavṛtraghna bhūyaḥ; pibasva somaṁ sutamudyataṁ mayā.
english: Samvarta said: "Welcome to you here, O learned one, O widely invoked Indra! This Vedic ritual (yajña) shines brightly today, now that you are present. O Slayer of Vṛtra and mighty one, drink again the soma juice prepared and offered by me."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 21. Samvarta said: "Welcome to you here, O learned one, O widely invoked Indra! This Vedic ritual (yajña) shines brightly today, now that you are present. O Slayer of Vṛtra and mighty one, drink again the soma juice prepared and offered by me."

sanskrit: मरुत्त उवाच ।शिवेन मां पश्य नमश्च तेऽस्तु प्राप्तो यज्ञः सफलं जीवितं मे ।अयं यज्ञं कुरुते मे सुरेन्द्र बृहस्पतेरवरो जन्मना यः ॥२२॥
transliteration: marutta uvāca ,śivena māṁ paśya namaśca te'stu; prāpto yajñaḥ saphalaṁ jīvitaṁ me ,ayaṁ yajñaṁ kurute me surendra; bṛhaspateravaro janmanā yaḥ.
english: Marutta said: Look upon me auspiciously, and salutations to you! My Vedic ritual (yajña) has been attained, and my life is fruitful. O king of gods, this one performs my Vedic ritual (yajña) for me, he who is younger than Brihaspati by birth.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 22. Marutta said: Look upon me auspiciously, and salutations to you! My Vedic ritual (yajña) has been attained, and my life is fruitful. O king of gods, this one performs my Vedic ritual (yajña) for me, he who is younger than Brihaspati by birth.

sanskrit: इन्द्र उवाच ।जानामि ते गुरुमेनं तपोधनं बृहस्पतेरनुजं तिग्मतेजसम् ।यस्याह्वानादागतोऽहं नरेन्द्र प्रीतिर्मेऽद्य त्वयि मन्युः प्रनष्टः ॥२३॥
transliteration: indra uvāca ,jānāmi te gurumenaṁ tapodhanaṁ; bṛhaspateranujaṁ tigmatejasam ,yasyāhvānādāgato'haṁ narendra; prītirme'dya tvayi manyuḥ pranaṣṭaḥ.
english: Indra said: "I know this preceptor (guru) of yours, rich in austerity (tapas), the younger brother of Brihaspati, who possesses sharp brilliance. O king (narendra), I have come here at his invitation. My affection for you is now present, and my displeasure has vanished."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 23. Indra said: "I know this preceptor (guru) of yours, rich in austerity (tapas), the younger brother of Brihaspati, who possesses sharp brilliance. O king (narendra), I have come here at his invitation. My affection for you is now present, and my displeasure has vanished."

sanskrit: संवर्त उवाच ।यदि प्रीतस्त्वमसि वै देवराज तस्मात्स्वयं शाधि यज्ञे विधानम् ।स्वयं सर्वान्कुरु मार्गान्सुरेन्द्र जानात्वयं सर्वलोकश्च देव ॥२४॥
transliteration: saṁvarta uvāca ,yadi prītastvamasi vai devarāja; tasmātsvayaṁ śādhi yajñe vidhānam ,svayaṁ sarvānkuru mārgānsurendra; jānātvayaṁ sarvalokaśca deva.
english: Saṃvarta said: "O king of gods (devarāja), if you are indeed pleased, then you yourself ordain the procedure for this Vedic ritual (yajña). O king of gods (surendra), make all the arrangements yourself! And let all these worlds, O god, know this!"
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 24. Saṃvarta said: "O king of gods (devarāja), if you are indeed pleased, then you yourself ordain the procedure for this Vedic ritual (yajña). O king of gods (surendra), make all the arrangements yourself! And let all these worlds, O god, know this!"

sanskrit: व्यास उवाच ।एवमुक्तस्त्वाङ्गिरसेन शक्रः समादिदेश स्वयमेव देवान् ।सभाः क्रियन्तामावसथाश्च मुख्याः सहस्रशश्चित्रभौमाः समृद्धाः ॥२५॥
transliteration: vyāsa uvāca ,evamuktastvāṅgirasena śakraḥ; samādideśa svayameva devān ,sabhāḥ kriyantāmāvasathāśca mukhyāḥ; sahasraśaścitrabhaumāḥ samṛddhāḥ.
english: Vyāsa said: "Thus addressed by Aṅgirasa (Saṃvarta), Indra himself immediately commanded the gods: 'Let thousands of chief halls and dwellings be constructed, having colorful grounds and being richly equipped!'"
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 25. Vyāsa said: "Thus addressed by Aṅgirasa (Saṃvarta), Indra himself immediately commanded the gods: 'Let thousands of chief halls and dwellings be constructed, having colorful grounds and being richly equipped!'"

sanskrit: कॢप्तस्थूणाः कुरुतारोहणानि गन्धर्वाणामप्सरसां च शीघ्रम् ।येषु नृत्येरन्नप्सरसः सहस्रशः स्वर्गोद्देशः क्रियतां यज्ञवाटः ॥२६॥
transliteration: kḷptasthūṇāḥ kurutārohaṇāni; gandharvāṇāmapsarasāṁ ca śīghram ,yeṣu nṛtyerannapsarasaḥ sahasraśaḥ; svargoddeśaḥ kriyatāṁ yajñavāṭaḥ.
english: Quickly construct raised platforms with fixed pillars for the Gandharvas and Apsaras, upon which thousands of Apsaras may dance. Let the enclosure for the Vedic ritual (yajña) be made like a heavenly region.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 26. Quickly construct raised platforms with fixed pillars for the Gandharvas and Apsaras, upon which thousands of Apsaras may dance. Let the enclosure for the Vedic ritual (yajña) be made like a heavenly region.

sanskrit: इत्युक्तास्ते चक्रुराशु प्रतीता दिवौकसः शक्रवाक्यान्नरेन्द्र ।ततो वाक्यं प्राह राजानमिन्द्रः प्रीतो राजन्पूजयानो मरुत्तम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: ityuktāste cakrurāśu pratītā; divaukasaḥ śakravākyānnarendra ,tato vākyaṁ prāha rājānamindraḥ; prīto rājanpūjayāno maruttam.
english: O King, thus addressed, the confident gods, convinced by Indra's words, quickly proceeded. Then, pleased, Indra spoke to King Marutta, honoring him, saying, "O King..."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 27. O King, thus addressed, the confident gods, convinced by Indra's words, quickly proceeded. Then, pleased, Indra spoke to King Marutta, honoring him, saying, "O King..."

sanskrit: एष त्वयाहमिह राजन्समेत्य ये चाप्यन्ये तव पूर्वे नरेन्द्राः ।सर्वाश्चान्या देवताः प्रीयमाणा हविस्तुभ्यं प्रतिगृह्णन्तु राजन् ॥२८॥
transliteration: eṣa tvayāhamiha rājansametya; ye cāpyanye tava pūrve narendrāḥ ,sarvāścānyā devatāḥ prīyamāṇā; havistubhyaṁ pratigṛhṇantu rājan.
english: "O King, I myself, having come here with you, and also all your other former kings, along with all other pleased deities, O King, let them accept this offering (havis) from you."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 28. "O King, I myself, having come here with you, and also all your other former kings, along with all other pleased deities, O King, let them accept this offering (havis) from you."

sanskrit: आग्नेयं वै लोहितमालभन्तां वैश्वदेवं बहुरूपं विराजन् ।नीलं चोक्षाणं मेध्यमभ्यालभन्तां चलच्छिश्नं मत्प्रदिष्टं द्विजेन्द्राः ॥२९॥
transliteration: āgneyaṁ vai lohitamālabhantāṁ; vaiśvadevaṁ bahurūpaṁ virājan ,nīlaṁ cokṣāṇaṁ medhyamabhyālabhantāṁ; calacchiśnaṁ matpradiṣṭaṁ dvijendrāḥ.
english: "O glorious one, O chief of Brahmins (dvija), let them indeed offer a red animal for Agni and a multi-colored one for the Viśvedevas. And let them offer a blue bull, which is pure and virile (calacchiśnam), as commanded by me."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 29. "O glorious one, O chief of Brahmins (dvija), let them indeed offer a red animal for Agni and a multi-colored one for the Viśvedevas. And let them offer a blue bull, which is pure and virile (calacchiśnam), as commanded by me."

sanskrit: ततो यज्ञो ववृधे तस्य राज्ञो यत्र देवाः स्वयमन्नानि जह्रुः ।यस्मिञ्शक्रो ब्राह्मणैः पूज्यमानः सदस्योऽभूद्धरिमान्देवराजः ॥३०॥
transliteration: tato yajño vavṛdhe tasya rājño; yatra devāḥ svayamannāni jahruḥ ,yasmiñśakro brāhmaṇaiḥ pūjyamānaḥ; sadasyo'bhūddharimāndevarājaḥ.
english: Then that king's Vedic ritual (yajña) flourished, in which the gods themselves accepted the offerings. Within that assembly, Indra (śakra), the glorious king of the gods, being honored by the brahmins, became a participant.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 30. Then that king's Vedic ritual (yajña) flourished, in which the gods themselves accepted the offerings. Within that assembly, Indra (śakra), the glorious king of the gods, being honored by the brahmins, became a participant.

sanskrit: ततः संवर्तश्चित्यगतो महात्मा यथा वह्निः प्रज्वलितो द्वितीयः ।हवींष्युच्चैराह्वयन्देवसंघाञ्जुहावाग्नौ मन्त्रवत्सुप्रतीतः ॥३१॥
transliteration: tataḥ saṁvartaścityagato mahātmā; yathā vahniḥ prajvalito dvitīyaḥ ,havīṁṣyuccairāhvayandevasaṁghā;ñjuhāvāgnau mantravatsupratītaḥ.
english: Then the great (mahātman) Saṃvarta, having approached the fire-altar, like a second blazing fire, loudly invoked the hosts of gods and, utterly pleased, offered oblations into the fire with sacred formulae (mantra).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 31. Then the great (mahātman) Saṃvarta, having approached the fire-altar, like a second blazing fire, loudly invoked the hosts of gods and, utterly pleased, offered oblations into the fire with sacred formulae (mantra).

sanskrit: ततः पीत्वा बलभित्सोममग्र्यं ये चाप्यन्ये सोमपा वै दिवौकसः ।सर्वेऽनुज्ञाताः प्रययुः पार्थिवेन यथाजोषं तर्पिताः प्रीतिमन्तः ॥३२॥
transliteration: tataḥ pītvā balabhitsomamagryaṁ; ye cāpyanye somapā vai divaukasaḥ ,sarve'nujñātāḥ prayayuḥ pārthivena; yathājoṣaṁ tarpitāḥ prītimantaḥ.
english: Then, after Indra (balabhit) and the other soma-drinking dwellers of heaven had consumed the excellent soma, all of them, permitted by the king, departed to their hearts' content, satisfied and filled with joy.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 32. Then, after Indra (balabhit) and the other soma-drinking dwellers of heaven had consumed the excellent soma, all of them, permitted by the king, departed to their hearts' content, satisfied and filled with joy.

sanskrit: ततो राजा जातरूपस्य राशीन्पदे पदे कारयामास हृष्टः ।द्विजातिभ्यो विसृजन्भूरि वित्तं रराज वित्तेश इवारिहन्ता ॥३३॥
transliteration: tato rājā jātarūpasya rāśī;npade pade kārayāmāsa hṛṣṭaḥ ,dvijātibhyo visṛjanbhūri vittaṁ; rarāja vitteśa ivārihantā.
english: Then, the joyful king, the destroyer of enemies, caused heaps of gold to be made at every step, and distributing abundant wealth to the twice-born (dvija), he shone like Kubera, the lord of riches.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 33. Then, the joyful king, the destroyer of enemies, caused heaps of gold to be made at every step, and distributing abundant wealth to the twice-born (dvija), he shone like Kubera, the lord of riches.

sanskrit: ततो वित्तं विविधं संनिधाय यथोत्साहं कारयित्वा च कोशम् ।अनुज्ञातो गुरुणा संनिवृत्य शशास गामखिलां सागरान्ताम् ॥३४॥
transliteration: tato vittaṁ vividhaṁ saṁnidhāya; yathotsāhaṁ kārayitvā ca kośam ,anujñāto guruṇā saṁnivṛtya; śaśāsa gāmakhilāṁ sāgarāntām.
english: Then, having collected various types of wealth and having managed the treasury according to his capacity, the king, after being permitted by his preceptor (guru), returned and ruled the entire earth extending to the oceans.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 34. Then, having collected various types of wealth and having managed the treasury according to his capacity, the king, after being permitted by his preceptor (guru), returned and ruled the entire earth extending to the oceans.

sanskrit: एवंगुणः संबभूवेह राजा यस्य क्रतौ तत्सुवर्णं प्रभूतम् ।तत्त्वं समादाय नरेन्द्र वित्तं यजस्व देवांस्तर्पयानो विधानैः ॥३५॥
transliteration: evaṁguṇaḥ saṁbabhūveha rājā; yasya kratau tatsuvarṇaṁ prabhūtam ,tattvaṁ samādāya narendra vittaṁ; yajasva devāṁstarpayāno vidhānaiḥ.
english: Such a virtuous king indeed existed here, in whose great Vedic ritual (kratu) there was abundant gold. Therefore, O king, you should gather that wealth and perform a Vedic ritual, satisfying the gods with the prescribed procedures.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 35. Such a virtuous king indeed existed here, in whose great Vedic ritual (kratu) there was abundant gold. Therefore, O king, you should gather that wealth and perform a Vedic ritual, satisfying the gods with the prescribed procedures.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततो राजा पाण्डवो हृष्टरूपः श्रुत्वा वाक्यं सत्यवत्याः सुतस्य ।मनश्चक्रे तेन वित्तेन यष्टुं ततोऽमात्यैर्मन्त्रयामास भूयः ॥३६॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tato rājā pāṇḍavo hṛṣṭarūpaḥ; śrutvā vākyaṁ satyavatyāḥ sutasya ,manaścakre tena vittena yaṣṭuṁ; tato'mātyairmantrayāmāsa bhūyaḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then the Pāṇḍava king (Yudhiṣṭhira), appearing joyous after hearing the words of the son of Satyavatī (Vyāsa), resolved to perform a Vedic ritual (yajña) with that wealth. Thereafter, he consulted with his ministers again.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 10 Verse 36. Vaiśampāyana said: Then the Pāṇḍava king (Yudhiṣṭhira), appearing joyous after hearing the words of the son of Satyavatī (Vyāsa), resolved to perform a Vedic ritual (yajña) with that wealth. Thereafter, he consulted with his ministers again.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।इत्युक्ते नृपतौ तस्मिन्व्यासेनाद्भुतकर्मणा ।वासुदेवो महातेजास्ततो वचनमाददे ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,ityukte nṛpatau tasminvyāsenādbhutakarmaṇā ,vāsudevo mahātejāstato vacanamādade.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: When that was thus spoken to the king by Vyāsa, who performed wondrous deeds, then the greatly effulgent Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa) began to speak.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: When that was thus spoken to the king by Vyāsa, who performed wondrous deeds, then the greatly effulgent Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa) began to speak.

sanskrit: तं नृपं दीनमनसं निहतज्ञातिबान्धवम् ।उपप्लुतमिवादित्यं सधूममिव पावकम् ॥२॥
transliteration: taṁ nṛpaṁ dīnamanasaṁ nihatajñātibāndhavam ,upaplutamivādityaṁ sadhūmamiva pāvakam.
english: -  that king whose mind was dejected, whose kinsmen and relatives had been slain, who was like the sun obscured (by an eclipse) and like a fire enveloped in smoke.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 2. -  that king whose mind was dejected, whose kinsmen and relatives had been slain, who was like the sun obscured (by an eclipse) and like a fire enveloped in smoke.

sanskrit: निर्विण्णमनसं पार्थं ज्ञात्वा वृष्णिकुलोद्वहः ।आश्वासयन्धर्मसुतं प्रवक्तुमुपचक्रमे ॥३॥
transliteration: nirviṇṇamanasaṁ pārthaṁ jñātvā vṛṣṇikulodvahaḥ ,āśvāsayandharmasutaṁ pravaktumupacakrame.
english: Understanding Arjuna to be dejected, the upholder of the Vṛṣṇi clan (Kṛṣṇa) began to console the son of dharma (Yudhiṣṭhira) and to speak.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 3. Understanding Arjuna to be dejected, the upholder of the Vṛṣṇi clan (Kṛṣṇa) began to console the son of dharma (Yudhiṣṭhira) and to speak.

sanskrit: वासुदेव उवाच ।सर्वं जिह्मं मृत्युपदमार्जवं ब्रह्मणः पदम् ।एतावाञ्ज्ञानविषयः किं प्रलापः करिष्यति ॥४॥
transliteration: vāsudeva uvāca ,sarvaṁ jihmaṁ mṛtyupadamārjavaṁ brahmaṇaḥ padam ,etāvāñjñānaviṣayaḥ kiṁ pralāpaḥ kariṣyati.
english: Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa) said: All crookedness is the path to death, while straightforwardness is the state of brahman. This much is the scope of true knowledge; what will useless talk accomplish?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 4. Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa) said: All crookedness is the path to death, while straightforwardness is the state of brahman. This much is the scope of true knowledge; what will useless talk accomplish?

sanskrit: नैव ते निष्ठितं कर्म नैव ते शत्रवो जिताः ।कथं शत्रुं शरीरस्थमात्मानं नावबुध्यसे ॥५॥
transliteration: naiva te niṣṭhitaṁ karma naiva te śatravo jitāḥ ,kathaṁ śatruṁ śarīrasthamātmānaṁ nāvabudhyase.
english: Neither is your action (karma) completed, nor are your enemies vanquished. How is it that you do not recognize the enemy residing within your body as your own self (ātman)?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 5. Neither is your action (karma) completed, nor are your enemies vanquished. How is it that you do not recognize the enemy residing within your body as your own self (ātman)?

sanskrit: अत्र ते वर्तयिष्यामि यथाधर्मं यथाश्रुतम् ।इन्द्रस्य सह वृत्रेण यथा युद्धमवर्तत ॥६॥
transliteration: atra te vartayiṣyāmi yathādharmaṁ yathāśrutam ,indrasya saha vṛtreṇa yathā yuddhamavartata.
english: In this regard, I will relate to you, in accordance with the constitution (dharma) and as it has been heard (from tradition), how the battle between Indra and Vṛtra transpired.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 6. In this regard, I will relate to you, in accordance with the constitution (dharma) and as it has been heard (from tradition), how the battle between Indra and Vṛtra transpired.

sanskrit: वृत्रेण पृथिवी व्याप्ता पुरा किल नराधिप ।दृष्ट्वा स पृथिवीं व्याप्तां गन्धस्य विषये हृते ।धराहरणदुर्गन्धो विषयः समपद्यत ॥७॥
transliteration: vṛtreṇa pṛthivī vyāptā purā kila narādhipa ,dṛṣṭvā sa pṛthivīṁ vyāptāṁ gandhasya viṣaye hṛte ,dharāharaṇadurgandho viṣayaḥ samapadyata.
english: O King, in ancient times, Vṛtra had completely enveloped the earth, it is said. When he saw the earth thus pervaded, and with the domain of fragrance taken away, that sphere (of earth) became filled with the foul odor resulting from its seizure.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 7. O King, in ancient times, Vṛtra had completely enveloped the earth, it is said. When he saw the earth thus pervaded, and with the domain of fragrance taken away, that sphere (of earth) became filled with the foul odor resulting from its seizure.

sanskrit: शतक्रतुश्चुकोपाथ गन्धस्य विषये हृते ।वृत्रस्य स ततः क्रुद्धो वज्रं घोरमवासृजत् ॥८॥
transliteration: śatakratuścukopātha gandhasya viṣaye hṛte ,vṛtrasya sa tataḥ kruddho vajraṁ ghoramavāsṛjat.
english: Then, when the domain of fragrance had been seized, Indra (śatakratu) became enraged. Infuriated at Vṛtra, he then discharged his terrible thunderbolt.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 8. Then, when the domain of fragrance had been seized, Indra (śatakratu) became enraged. Infuriated at Vṛtra, he then discharged his terrible thunderbolt.

sanskrit: स वध्यमानो वज्रेण पृथिव्यां भूरितेजसा ।विवेश सहसैवापो जग्राह विषयं ततः ॥९॥
transliteration: sa vadhyamāno vajreṇa pṛthivyāṁ bhūritejasā ,viveśa sahasaivāpo jagrāha viṣayaṁ tataḥ.
english: While being struck by the extremely powerful thunderbolt on earth, Vṛtra suddenly entered the waters. He then seized their domain.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 9. While being struck by the extremely powerful thunderbolt on earth, Vṛtra suddenly entered the waters. He then seized their domain.

sanskrit: व्याप्तास्वथाप्सु वृत्रेण रसे च विषये हृते ।शतक्रतुरभिक्रुद्धस्तासु वज्रमवासृजत् ॥१०॥
transliteration: vyāptāsvathāpsu vṛtreṇa rase ca viṣaye hṛte ,śatakraturabhikruddhastāsu vajramavāsṛjat.
english: Then, when the waters were also pervaded by Vṛtra, and the domain of taste had been seized, Indra (śatakratu) became greatly enraged and hurled the thunderbolt into those waters.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 10. Then, when the waters were also pervaded by Vṛtra, and the domain of taste had been seized, Indra (śatakratu) became greatly enraged and hurled the thunderbolt into those waters.

sanskrit: स वध्यमानो वज्रेण सलिले भूरितेजसा ।विवेश सहसा ज्योतिर्जग्राह विषयं ततः ॥११॥
transliteration: sa vadhyamāno vajreṇa salile bhūritejasā ,viveśa sahasā jyotirjagrāha viṣayaṁ tataḥ.
english: As he was being struck in the water by the immensely powerful thunderbolt, he suddenly entered the element of light (jyotis) and then took possession of its sphere.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 11. As he was being struck in the water by the immensely powerful thunderbolt, he suddenly entered the element of light (jyotis) and then took possession of its sphere.

sanskrit: व्याप्ते ज्योतिषि वृत्रेण रूपेऽथ विषये हृते ।शतक्रतुरभिक्रुद्धस्तत्र वज्रमवासृजत् ॥१२॥
transliteration: vyāpte jyotiṣi vṛtreṇa rūpe'tha viṣaye hṛte ,śatakraturabhikruddhastatra vajramavāsṛjat.
english: When the element of light (jyotis), along with its inherent nature and its sphere, had been pervaded and seized by Vṛtra, Indra (śatakratuḥ), becoming extremely enraged, then discharged the thunderbolt there.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 12. When the element of light (jyotis), along with its inherent nature and its sphere, had been pervaded and seized by Vṛtra, Indra (śatakratuḥ), becoming extremely enraged, then discharged the thunderbolt there.

sanskrit: स वध्यमानो वज्रेण सुभृशं भूरितेजसा ।विवेश सहसा वायुं जग्राह विषयं ततः ॥१३॥
transliteration: sa vadhyamāno vajreṇa subhṛśaṁ bhūritejasā ,viveśa sahasā vāyuṁ jagrāha viṣayaṁ tataḥ.
english: As he was being exceedingly struck by the immensely powerful thunderbolt, he suddenly entered the element of air (vāyu) and then took possession of its sphere.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 13. As he was being exceedingly struck by the immensely powerful thunderbolt, he suddenly entered the element of air (vāyu) and then took possession of its sphere.

sanskrit: व्याप्ते वायौ तु वृत्रेण स्पर्शेऽथ विषये हृते ।शतक्रतुरभिक्रुद्धस्तत्र वज्रमवासृजत् ॥१४॥
transliteration: vyāpte vāyau tu vṛtreṇa sparśe'tha viṣaye hṛte ,śatakraturabhikruddhastatra vajramavāsṛjat.
english: When the element of air (vāyu), along with its quality of touch (sparśa) and its sphere, had been pervaded and seized by Vṛtra, Indra (śatakratuḥ), becoming extremely enraged, then discharged the thunderbolt there.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 14. When the element of air (vāyu), along with its quality of touch (sparśa) and its sphere, had been pervaded and seized by Vṛtra, Indra (śatakratuḥ), becoming extremely enraged, then discharged the thunderbolt there.

sanskrit: स वध्यमानो वज्रेण तस्मिन्नमिततेजसा ।आकाशमभिदुद्राव जग्राह विषयं ततः ॥१५॥
transliteration: sa vadhyamāno vajreṇa tasminnamitatejasā ,ākāśamabhidudrāva jagrāha viṣayaṁ tataḥ.
english: As he (Vṛtra) was being struck by that thunderbolt, wielded by the immensely powerful (Indra), he then fled to the sky and seized its domain.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 15. As he (Vṛtra) was being struck by that thunderbolt, wielded by the immensely powerful (Indra), he then fled to the sky and seized its domain.

sanskrit: आकाशे वृत्रभूते च शब्दे च विषये हृते ।शतक्रतुरभिक्रुद्धस्तत्र वज्रमवासृजत् ॥१६॥
transliteration: ākāśe vṛtrabhūte ca śabde ca viṣaye hṛte ,śatakraturabhikruddhastatra vajramavāsṛjat.
english: When the sky itself became Vṛtra and the domain of sound was absorbed, Indra (śatakratuḥ), becoming greatly enraged, then hurled his thunderbolt.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 16. When the sky itself became Vṛtra and the domain of sound was absorbed, Indra (śatakratuḥ), becoming greatly enraged, then hurled his thunderbolt.

sanskrit: स वध्यमानो वज्रेण तस्मिन्नमिततेजसा ।विवेश सहसा शक्रं जग्राह विषयं ततः ॥१७॥
transliteration: sa vadhyamāno vajreṇa tasminnamitatejasā ,viveśa sahasā śakraṁ jagrāha viṣayaṁ tataḥ.
english: As he (Vṛtra) was being struck by that thunderbolt, wielded by the immensely powerful (Indra), he suddenly entered into Indra (śakra) and then seized his domain.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 17. As he (Vṛtra) was being struck by that thunderbolt, wielded by the immensely powerful (Indra), he suddenly entered into Indra (śakra) and then seized his domain.

sanskrit: तस्य वृत्रगृहीतस्य मोहः समभवन्महान् ।रथंतरेण तं तात वसिष्ठः प्रत्यबोधयत् ॥१८॥
transliteration: tasya vṛtragṛhītasya mohaḥ samabhavanmahān ,rathaṁtareṇa taṁ tāta vasiṣṭhaḥ pratyabodhayat.
english: A great delusion overtook him (Indra) who had been seized by Vṛtra. Then, O dear one, Vasiṣṭha awakened him by means of the Rathantara chant.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 18. A great delusion overtook him (Indra) who had been seized by Vṛtra. Then, O dear one, Vasiṣṭha awakened him by means of the Rathantara chant.

sanskrit: ततो वृत्रं शरीरस्थं जघान भरतर्षभ ।शतक्रतुरदृश्येन वज्रेणेतीह नः श्रुतम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: tato vṛtraṁ śarīrasthaṁ jaghāna bharatarṣabha ,śatakraturadṛśyena vajreṇetīha naḥ śrutam.
english: O best of Bharatas, it is heard by us here that Indra (śatakratu) then killed Vṛtra, who was dwelling in his body, with an invisible thunderbolt.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 19. O best of Bharatas, it is heard by us here that Indra (śatakratu) then killed Vṛtra, who was dwelling in his body, with an invisible thunderbolt.

sanskrit: इदं धर्मरहस्यं च शक्रेणोक्तं महर्षिषु ।ऋषिभिश्च मम प्रोक्तं तन्निबोध नराधिप ॥२०॥
transliteration: idaṁ dharmarahasyaṁ ca śakreṇoktaṁ maharṣiṣu ,ṛṣibhiśca mama proktaṁ tannibodha narādhipa.
english: O lord of men (narādhipa), this secret of the natural law (dharma) was spoken by Indra (śakra) among the great sages, and it was also told to me by the sages. Learn that now.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 11 Verse 20. O lord of men (narādhipa), this secret of the natural law (dharma) was spoken by Indra (śakra) among the great sages, and it was also told to me by the sages. Learn that now.

sanskrit: वासुदेव उवाच ।द्विविधो जायते व्याधिः शारीरो मानसस्तथा ।परस्परं तयोर्जन्म निर्द्वंद्वं नोपलभ्यते ॥१॥
transliteration: vāsudeva uvāca ,dvividho jāyate vyādhiḥ śārīro mānasastathā ,parasparaṁ tayorjanma nirdvaṁdvaṁ nopalabhyate.
english: Vāsudeva said: Affliction (vyādhi) arises in two forms: physical and mental. The origin of these two is not found to be independent of each other (nirdvandva).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 1. Vāsudeva said: Affliction (vyādhi) arises in two forms: physical and mental. The origin of these two is not found to be independent of each other (nirdvandva).

sanskrit: शरीरे जायते व्याधिः शारीरो नात्र संशयः ।मानसो जायते व्याधिर्मनस्येवेति निश्चयः ॥२॥
transliteration: śarīre jāyate vyādhiḥ śārīro nātra saṁśayaḥ ,mānaso jāyate vyādhirmanasyeveti niścayaḥ.
english: A physical affliction (vyādhi) arises in the body; there is no doubt about this. A mental affliction (vyādhi) arises solely in the mind; this is the definite conclusion.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 2. A physical affliction (vyādhi) arises in the body; there is no doubt about this. A mental affliction (vyādhi) arises solely in the mind; this is the definite conclusion.

sanskrit: शीतोष्णे चैव वायुश्च गुणा राजञ्शरीरजाः ।तेषां गुणानां साम्यं चेत्तदाहुः स्वस्थलक्षणम् ।उष्णेन बाध्यते शीतं शीतेनोष्णं च बाध्यते ॥३॥
transliteration: śītoṣṇe caiva vāyuśca guṇā rājañśarīrajāḥ ,teṣāṁ guṇānāṁ sāmyaṁ cettadāhuḥ svasthalakṣaṇam ,uṣṇena bādhyate śītaṁ śītenoṣṇaṁ ca bādhyate.
english: O king, cold, heat, and also wind are qualities that originate in the body. If there is a balance of these qualities, then that is called the characteristic of a healthy state. Cold is overcome by heat, and heat is overcome by cold.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 3. O king, cold, heat, and also wind are qualities that originate in the body. If there is a balance of these qualities, then that is called the characteristic of a healthy state. Cold is overcome by heat, and heat is overcome by cold.

sanskrit: सत्त्वं रजस्तमश्चेति त्रयस्त्वात्मगुणाः स्मृताः ।तेषां गुणानां साम्यं चेत्तदाहुः स्वस्थलक्षणम् ।तेषामन्यतमोत्सेके विधानमुपदिश्यते ॥४॥
transliteration: sattvaṁ rajastamaśceti trayastvātmaguṇāḥ smṛtāḥ ,teṣāṁ guṇānāṁ sāmyaṁ cettadāhuḥ svasthalakṣaṇam ,teṣāmanyatamotseke vidhānamupadiśyate.
english: Sattva (sattva), rajas (rajas), and tamas (tamas) – these three are indeed known as qualities of the self (ātman). If there is an equilibrium of these qualities, then that is declared to be the characteristic of a healthy state. When any one of them is in excess, a remedy is prescribed.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 4. Sattva (sattva), rajas (rajas), and tamas (tamas) – these three are indeed known as qualities of the self (ātman). If there is an equilibrium of these qualities, then that is declared to be the characteristic of a healthy state. When any one of them is in excess, a remedy is prescribed.

sanskrit: हर्षेण बाध्यते शोको हर्षः शोकेन बाध्यते ।कश्चिद्दुःखे वर्तमानः सुखस्य स्मर्तुमिच्छति ।कश्चित्सुखे वर्तमानो दुःखस्य स्मर्तुमिच्छति ॥५॥
transliteration: harṣeṇa bādhyate śoko harṣaḥ śokena bādhyate ,kaścidduḥkhe vartamānaḥ sukhasya smartumicchati ,kaścitsukhe vartamāno duḥkhasya smartumicchati.
english: Sorrow is overcome by joy, and joy is overcome by sorrow. Someone who is experiencing sorrow wishes to remember happiness, and someone who is experiencing happiness wishes to remember sorrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 5. Sorrow is overcome by joy, and joy is overcome by sorrow. Someone who is experiencing sorrow wishes to remember happiness, and someone who is experiencing happiness wishes to remember sorrow.

sanskrit: स त्वं न दुःखी दुःखस्य न सुखी सुसुखस्य वा ।स्मर्तुमिच्छसि कौन्तेय दिष्टं हि बलवत्तरम् ॥६॥
transliteration: sa tvaṁ na duḥkhī duḥkhasya na sukhī susukhasya vā ,smartumicchasi kaunteya diṣṭaṁ hi balavattaram.
english: O son of Kunti (Kaunteya), you are not sorrowful on account of sorrow, nor happy on account of great happiness. Do you wish to recall these? Indeed, destiny (diṣṭam) is more powerful.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 6. O son of Kunti (Kaunteya), you are not sorrowful on account of sorrow, nor happy on account of great happiness. Do you wish to recall these? Indeed, destiny (diṣṭam) is more powerful.

sanskrit: अथ वा ते स्वभावोऽयं येन पार्थावकृष्यसे ।दृष्ट्वा सभागतां कृष्णामेकवस्त्रां रजस्वलाम् ।मिषतां पाण्डवेयानां न तत्संस्मर्तुमिच्छसि ॥७॥
transliteration: atha vā te svabhāvo'yaṁ yena pārthāvakṛṣyase ,dṛṣṭvā sabhāgatāṁ kṛṣṇāmekavastrāṁ rajasvalām ,miṣatāṁ pāṇḍaveyānāṁ na tatsaṁsmartumicchasi.
english: Or perhaps, O Pārtha, this is your intrinsic nature (svabhāva) by which you are so distracted, that you do not wish to remember the time when Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī) was brought into the assembly, wearing a single garment and menstruating, while the Pāṇḍavas watched.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 7. Or perhaps, O Pārtha, this is your intrinsic nature (svabhāva) by which you are so distracted, that you do not wish to remember the time when Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī) was brought into the assembly, wearing a single garment and menstruating, while the Pāṇḍavas watched.

sanskrit: प्रव्राजनं च नगरादजिनैश्च विवासनम् ।महारण्यनिवासश्च न तस्य स्मर्तुमिच्छसि ॥८॥
transliteration: pravrājanaṁ ca nagarādajinaiśca vivāsanam ,mahāraṇyanivāsaśca na tasya smartumicchasi.
english: You do not wish to remember the banishment from the city, the expulsion where deer skins (served as garments), and the dwelling in the great forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 8. You do not wish to remember the banishment from the city, the expulsion where deer skins (served as garments), and the dwelling in the great forest.

sanskrit: जटासुरात्परिक्लेशश्चित्रसेनेन चाहवः ।सैन्धवाच्च परिक्लेशो न तस्य स्मर्तुमिच्छसि ॥९॥
transliteration: jaṭāsurātparikleśaścitrasenena cāhavaḥ ,saindhavācca parikleśo na tasya smartumicchasi.
english: You do not wish to remember the harassment from Jaṭāsura, the conflict with Citrasena, and the harassment from Saindhava.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 9. You do not wish to remember the harassment from Jaṭāsura, the conflict with Citrasena, and the harassment from Saindhava.

sanskrit: पुनरज्ञातचर्यायां कीचकेन पदा वधः ।याज्ञसेन्यास्तदा पार्थ न तस्य स्मर्तुमिच्छसि ॥१०॥
transliteration: punarajñātacaryāyāṁ kīcakena padā vadhaḥ ,yājñasenyāstadā pārtha na tasya smartumicchasi.
english: O Pārtha, you do not wish to remember that, during the period of living incognito, Kīcaka struck Yājñasenī (Draupadī) with his foot.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 10. O Pārtha, you do not wish to remember that, during the period of living incognito, Kīcaka struck Yājñasenī (Draupadī) with his foot.

sanskrit: यच्च ते द्रोणभीष्माभ्यां युद्धमासीदरिंदम ।मनसैकेन योद्धव्यं तत्ते युद्धमुपस्थितम् ।तस्मादभ्युपगन्तव्यं युद्धाय भरतर्षभ ॥११॥
transliteration: yacca te droṇabhīṣmābhyāṁ yuddhamāsīdariṁdama ,manasaikena yoddhavyaṁ tatte yuddhamupasthitam ,tasmādabhyupagantavyaṁ yuddhāya bharatarṣabha.
english: O subjugator of foes, that battle which you had with Droṇa and Bhīṣma is past. Now, this battle, which must be fought by the mind alone, has presented itself to you. Therefore, O best of Bhāratas, this battle must be undertaken.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 11. O subjugator of foes, that battle which you had with Droṇa and Bhīṣma is past. Now, this battle, which must be fought by the mind alone, has presented itself to you. Therefore, O best of Bhāratas, this battle must be undertaken.

sanskrit: परमव्यक्तरूपस्य परं मुक्त्वा स्वकर्मभिः ।यत्र नैव शरैः कार्यं न भृत्यैर्न च बन्धुभिः ।आत्मनैकेन योद्धव्यं तत्ते युद्धमुपस्थितम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: paramavyaktarūpasya paraṁ muktvā svakarmabhiḥ ,yatra naiva śaraiḥ kāryaṁ na bhṛtyairna ca bandhubhiḥ ,ātmanaikena yoddhavyaṁ tatte yuddhamupasthitam.
english: For the supreme unmanifest nature (prakṛti) – having abandoned all other (worldly) pursuits through one's own actions (karma) – where no work is to be done by arrows, nor by servants, nor by relatives. That battle, which must be fought by the self (ātman) alone, has now come upon you.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 12. For the supreme unmanifest nature (prakṛti) – having abandoned all other (worldly) pursuits through one's own actions (karma) – where no work is to be done by arrows, nor by servants, nor by relatives. That battle, which must be fought by the self (ātman) alone, has now come upon you.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्ननिर्जिते युद्धे कामवस्थां गमिष्यसि ।एतज्ज्ञात्वा तु कौन्तेय कृतकृत्यो भविष्यसि ॥१३॥
transliteration: tasminnanirjite yuddhe kāmavasthāṁ gamiṣyasi ,etajjñātvā tu kaunteya kṛtakṛtyo bhaviṣyasi.
english: If that battle is not conquered, what state will you attain? But, O son of Kuntī, having understood this, you will have accomplished all that needs to be done.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 13. If that battle is not conquered, what state will you attain? But, O son of Kuntī, having understood this, you will have accomplished all that needs to be done.

sanskrit: एतां बुद्धिं विनिश्चित्य भूतानामागतिं गतिम् ।पितृपैतामहे वृत्ते शाधि राज्यं यथोचितम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: etāṁ buddhiṁ viniścitya bhūtānāmāgatiṁ gatim ,pitṛpaitāmahe vṛtte śādhi rājyaṁ yathocitam.
english: Having firmly ascertained this understanding – the origin and dissolution of all beings – and maintaining the ancestral tradition, rule the kingdom as is proper.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 12 Verse 14. Having firmly ascertained this understanding – the origin and dissolution of all beings – and maintaining the ancestral tradition, rule the kingdom as is proper.

sanskrit: वासुदेव उवाच ।न बाह्यं द्रव्यमुत्सृज्य सिद्धिर्भवति भारत ।शारीरं द्रव्यमुत्सृज्य सिद्धिर्भवति वा न वा ॥१॥
transliteration: vāsudeva uvāca ,na bāhyaṁ dravyamutsṛjya siddhirbhavati bhārata ,śārīraṁ dravyamutsṛjya siddhirbhavati vā na vā.
english: Vāsudeva said: "O Bhārata, success is not achieved merely by abandoning external possessions. And even by giving up possessions related to the body, it is uncertain whether success will be achieved."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 1. Vāsudeva said: "O Bhārata, success is not achieved merely by abandoning external possessions. And even by giving up possessions related to the body, it is uncertain whether success will be achieved."

sanskrit: बाह्यद्रव्यविमुक्तस्य शारीरेषु च गृध्यतः ।यो धर्मो यत्सुखं चैव द्विषतामस्तु तत्तथा ॥२॥
transliteration: bāhyadravyavimuktasya śārīreṣu ca gṛdhyataḥ ,yo dharmo yatsukhaṁ caiva dviṣatāmastu tattathā.
english: To one who is free from external possessions, yet still grasps at things related to the body, whatever intrinsic nature (dharma) or happiness that person experiences, let that very same fate befall their enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 2. To one who is free from external possessions, yet still grasps at things related to the body, whatever intrinsic nature (dharma) or happiness that person experiences, let that very same fate befall their enemies.

sanskrit: द्व्यक्षरस्तु भवेन्मृत्युस्त्र्यक्षरं ब्रह्म शाश्वतम् ।ममेति द्व्यक्षरो मृत्युर्न ममेति च शाश्वतम् ॥३॥
transliteration: dvyakṣarastu bhavenmṛtyustryakṣaraṁ brahma śāśvatam ,mameti dvyakṣaro mṛtyurna mameti ca śāśvatam.
english: Indeed, death (mṛtyu) is considered two-syllabled, while the eternal (śāśvatam) ultimate reality (brahman) is three-syllabled. The thought 'mine' (mama) is two-syllabled death (mṛtyu); whereas 'not mine' (na mama) leads to the eternal (śāśvatam) (ultimate reality, brahman).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 3. Indeed, death (mṛtyu) is considered two-syllabled, while the eternal (śāśvatam) ultimate reality (brahman) is three-syllabled. The thought 'mine' (mama) is two-syllabled death (mṛtyu); whereas 'not mine' (na mama) leads to the eternal (śāśvatam) (ultimate reality, brahman).

sanskrit: ब्रह्म मृत्युश्च तौ राजन्नात्मन्येव व्यवस्थितौ ।अदृश्यमानौ भूतानि योधयेतामसंशयम् ॥४॥
transliteration: brahma mṛtyuśca tau rājannātmanyeva vyavasthitau ,adṛśyamānau bhūtāni yodhayetāmasaṁśayam.
english: O King, both the ultimate reality (brahman) and death (mṛtyu) are established within the self (ātman). Though unseen, these two undoubtedly cause living beings to contend (or fight).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 4. O King, both the ultimate reality (brahman) and death (mṛtyu) are established within the self (ātman). Though unseen, these two undoubtedly cause living beings to contend (or fight).

sanskrit: अविनाशोऽस्य सत्त्वस्य नियतो यदि भारत ।भित्त्वा शरीरं भूतानामहिंसां प्रतिपद्यते ॥५॥
transliteration: avināśo'sya sattvasya niyato yadi bhārata ,bhittvā śarīraṁ bhūtānāmahiṁsāṁ pratipadyate.
english: O Bhārata, if the intrinsic nature (sattva) of this being is certainly imperishable, then by destroying the bodies of creatures, one indeed attains non-harming (ahiṃsā).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 5. O Bhārata, if the intrinsic nature (sattva) of this being is certainly imperishable, then by destroying the bodies of creatures, one indeed attains non-harming (ahiṃsā).

sanskrit: लब्ध्वापि पृथिवीं सर्वां सहस्थावरजङ्गमाम् ।ममत्वं यस्य नैव स्यात्किं तया स करिष्यति ॥६॥
transliteration: labdhvāpi pṛthivīṁ sarvāṁ sahasthāvarajaṅgamām ,mamatvaṁ yasya naiva syātkiṁ tayā sa kariṣyati.
english: Even after obtaining the entire earth, including all its stationary and moving creatures, if a person has no possessiveness (mamatva) towards it, what will he do with it?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 6. Even after obtaining the entire earth, including all its stationary and moving creatures, if a person has no possessiveness (mamatva) towards it, what will he do with it?

sanskrit: अथ वा वसतः पार्थ वने वन्येन जीवतः ।ममता यस्य द्रव्येषु मृत्योरास्ये स वर्तते ॥७॥
transliteration: atha vā vasataḥ pārtha vane vanyena jīvataḥ ,mamatā yasya dravyeṣu mṛtyorāsye sa vartate.
english: Or, O Pārtha, even if one lives in the forest, subsisting on wild food, but still harbors possessiveness (mamatva) towards possessions, he dwells in the jaws of death.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 7. Or, O Pārtha, even if one lives in the forest, subsisting on wild food, but still harbors possessiveness (mamatva) towards possessions, he dwells in the jaws of death.

sanskrit: बाह्यान्तराणां शत्रूणां स्वभावं पश्य भारत ।यन्न पश्यति तद्भूतं मुच्यते स महाभयात् ॥८॥
transliteration: bāhyāntarāṇāṁ śatrūṇāṁ svabhāvaṁ paśya bhārata ,yanna paśyati tadbhūtaṁ mucyate sa mahābhayāt.
english: O Bhārata, observe the intrinsic nature (svabhāva) of both external and internal enemies. That being (bhūta) who fails to perceive this is liberated from great fear.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 8. O Bhārata, observe the intrinsic nature (svabhāva) of both external and internal enemies. That being (bhūta) who fails to perceive this is liberated from great fear.

sanskrit: कामात्मानं न प्रशंसन्ति लोके न चाकामात्काचिदस्ति प्रवृत्तिः ।दानं हि वेदाध्ययनं तपश्च कामेन कर्माणि च वैदिकानि ॥९॥
transliteration: kāmātmānaṁ na praśaṁsanti loke; na cākāmātkācidasti pravṛttiḥ ,dānaṁ hi vedādhyayanaṁ tapaśca; kāmena karmāṇi ca vaidikāni.
english: People in the world do not praise individuals whose inner self (ātman) is consumed by desire, nor is there any activity (pravṛtti) that arises without some desire. Indeed, charity, the study of the Vedas, austerity, and even Vedic actions (karma) are all performed due to desire.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 9. People in the world do not praise individuals whose inner self (ātman) is consumed by desire, nor is there any activity (pravṛtti) that arises without some desire. Indeed, charity, the study of the Vedas, austerity, and even Vedic actions (karma) are all performed due to desire.

sanskrit: व्रतं यज्ञान्नियमान्ध्यानयोगान्कामेन यो नारभते विदित्वा ।यद्यद्ध्ययं कामयते स धर्मो न यो धर्मो नियमस्तस्य मूलम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: vrataṁ yajñānniyamāndhyānayogā;nkāmena yo nārabhate viditvā ,yadyaddhyayaṁ kāmayate sa dharmo; na yo dharmo niyamastasya mūlam.
english: One who, knowing that they are motivated by desire, does not undertake vows, Vedic rituals (yajña), disciplines, or meditative practices – for such a person, whatever they desire becomes their natural law (dharma), and that is not a natural law (dharma) whose origin is merely a rule.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 10. One who, knowing that they are motivated by desire, does not undertake vows, Vedic rituals (yajña), disciplines, or meditative practices – for such a person, whatever they desire becomes their natural law (dharma), and that is not a natural law (dharma) whose origin is merely a rule.

sanskrit: अत्र गाथाः कामगीताः कीर्तयन्ति पुराविदः ।शृणु संकीर्त्यमानास्ता निखिलेन युधिष्ठिर ॥११॥
transliteration: atra gāthāḥ kāmagītāḥ kīrtayanti purāvidaḥ ,śṛṇu saṁkīrtyamānāstā nikhilena yudhiṣṭhira.
english: Here, experts in ancient lore recount verses inspired by Kāma (the deity of desire). Listen to them being fully proclaimed, Yudhiṣṭhira.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 11. Here, experts in ancient lore recount verses inspired by Kāma (the deity of desire). Listen to them being fully proclaimed, Yudhiṣṭhira.

sanskrit: नाहं शक्योऽनुपायेन हन्तुं भूतेन केनचित् ।यो मां प्रयतते हन्तुं ज्ञात्वा प्रहरणे बलम् ।तस्य तस्मिन्प्रहरणे पुनः प्रादुर्भवाम्यहम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: nāhaṁ śakyo'nupāyena hantuṁ bhūtena kenacit ,yo māṁ prayatate hantuṁ jñātvā praharaṇe balam ,tasya tasminpraharaṇe punaḥ prādurbhavāmyaham.
english: I cannot be killed by any being through inappropriate means. Whoever attempts to kill me, relying on the strength of their weapon, I reappear again within that very weapon.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 12. I cannot be killed by any being through inappropriate means. Whoever attempts to kill me, relying on the strength of their weapon, I reappear again within that very weapon.

sanskrit: यो मां प्रयतते हन्तुं यज्ञैर्विविधदक्षिणैः ।जङ्गमेष्विव कर्मात्मा पुनः प्रादुर्भवाम्यहम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: yo māṁ prayatate hantuṁ yajñairvividhadakṣiṇaiḥ ,jaṅgameṣviva karmātmā punaḥ prādurbhavāmyaham.
english: Whoever strives to grasp me through Vedic rituals (yajña) accompanied by diverse offerings, I manifest again, just as the individual soul (ātman) manifests in mobile beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 13. Whoever strives to grasp me through Vedic rituals (yajña) accompanied by diverse offerings, I manifest again, just as the individual soul (ātman) manifests in mobile beings.

sanskrit: यो मां प्रयतते हन्तुं वेदैर्वेदान्तसाधनैः ।स्थावरेष्विव शान्तात्मा तस्य प्रादुर्भवाम्यहम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: yo māṁ prayatate hantuṁ vedairvedāntasādhanaiḥ ,sthāvareṣviva śāntātmā tasya prādurbhavāmyaham.
english: Whoever strives to grasp me through the Vedas and the methods of Vedānta, I manifest for him, just like the tranquil soul (ātman) in immovable beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 14. Whoever strives to grasp me through the Vedas and the methods of Vedānta, I manifest for him, just like the tranquil soul (ātman) in immovable beings.

sanskrit: यो मां प्रयतते हन्तुं धृत्या सत्यपराक्रमः ।भावो भवामि तस्याहं स च मां नावबुध्यते ॥१५॥
transliteration: yo māṁ prayatate hantuṁ dhṛtyā satyaparākramaḥ ,bhāvo bhavāmi tasyāhaṁ sa ca māṁ nāvabudhyate.
english: Whoever, whose strength is truth, strives to grasp me through unwavering resolve (dhṛti), I become his essential nature (bhāva). Yet, he does not comprehend me.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 15. Whoever, whose strength is truth, strives to grasp me through unwavering resolve (dhṛti), I become his essential nature (bhāva). Yet, he does not comprehend me.

sanskrit: यो मां प्रयतते हन्तुं तपसा संशितव्रतः ।ततस्तपसि तस्याथ पुनः प्रादुर्भवाम्यहम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: yo māṁ prayatate hantuṁ tapasā saṁśitavrataḥ ,tatastapasi tasyātha punaḥ prādurbhavāmyaham.
english: Whoever, whose vow is firm, strives to grasp me through spiritual austerity (tapas), I then manifest again within that very austerity (tapas) of his.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 16. Whoever, whose vow is firm, strives to grasp me through spiritual austerity (tapas), I then manifest again within that very austerity (tapas) of his.

sanskrit: यो मां प्रयतते हन्तुं मोक्षमास्थाय पण्डितः ।तस्य मोक्षरतिस्थस्य नृत्यामि च हसामि च ।अवध्यः सर्वभूतानामहमेकः सनातनः ॥१७॥
transliteration: yo māṁ prayatate hantuṁ mokṣamāsthāya paṇḍitaḥ ,tasya mokṣaratisthasya nṛtyāmi ca hasāmi ca ,avadhyaḥ sarvabhūtānāmahamekaḥ sanātanaḥ.
english: The wise one who endeavors to transcend me, by resorting to liberation (mokṣa), for that one, who abides in the delight of liberation, I both dance and laugh. I am the one eternal, invulnerable to all beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 17. The wise one who endeavors to transcend me, by resorting to liberation (mokṣa), for that one, who abides in the delight of liberation, I both dance and laugh. I am the one eternal, invulnerable to all beings.

sanskrit: तस्मात्त्वमपि तं कामं यज्ञैर्विविधदक्षिणैः ।धर्मं कुरु महाराज तत्र ते स भविष्यति ॥१८॥
transliteration: tasmāttvamapi taṁ kāmaṁ yajñairvividhadakṣiṇaiḥ ,dharmaṁ kuru mahārāja tatra te sa bhaviṣyati.
english: Therefore, O great king, you too should uphold your natural law (dharma) by performing Vedic rituals (yajña) accompanied by various fees, with that desire in mind. Then, that (desired outcome) will certainly be yours.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 18. Therefore, O great king, you too should uphold your natural law (dharma) by performing Vedic rituals (yajña) accompanied by various fees, with that desire in mind. Then, that (desired outcome) will certainly be yours.

sanskrit: यजस्व वाजिमेधेन विधिवद्दक्षिणावता ।अन्यैश्च विविधैर्यज्ञैः समृद्धैराप्तदक्षिणैः ॥१९॥
transliteration: yajasva vājimedhena vidhivaddakṣiṇāvatā ,anyaiśca vividhairyajñaiḥ samṛddhairāptadakṣiṇaiḥ.
english: Perform the Vajimedha Vedic ritual (yajña) according to the prescribed rules and with proper fees. Also, perform other various abundant Vedic rituals (yajña) that are accompanied by sufficient fees.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 19. Perform the Vajimedha Vedic ritual (yajña) according to the prescribed rules and with proper fees. Also, perform other various abundant Vedic rituals (yajña) that are accompanied by sufficient fees.

sanskrit: मा ते व्यथास्तु निहतान्बन्धून्वीक्ष्य पुनः पुनः ।न शक्यास्ते पुनर्द्रष्टुं ये हतास्मिन्रणाजिरे ॥२०॥
transliteration: mā te vyathāstu nihatānbandhūnvīkṣya punaḥ punaḥ ,na śakyāste punardraṣṭuṁ ye hatāsminraṇājire.
english: Let there be no distress for you, repeatedly seeing your slain relatives. Those who have been killed in this battlefield cannot be seen again.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 20. Let there be no distress for you, repeatedly seeing your slain relatives. Those who have been killed in this battlefield cannot be seen again.

sanskrit: स त्वमिष्ट्वा महायज्ञैः समृद्धैराप्तदक्षिणैः ।लोके कीर्तिं परां प्राप्य गतिमग्र्यां गमिष्यसि ॥२१॥
transliteration: sa tvamiṣṭvā mahāyajñaiḥ samṛddhairāptadakṣiṇaiḥ ,loke kīrtiṁ parāṁ prāpya gatimagryāṁ gamiṣyasi.
english: Having performed great and successful (Vedic rituals) (yajña) for which the appropriate fees have been given, you will attain supreme fame in the world and reach the highest destination.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 13 Verse 21. Having performed great and successful (Vedic rituals) (yajña) for which the appropriate fees have been given, you will attain supreme fame in the world and reach the highest destination.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवं बहुविधैर्वाक्यैर्मुनिभिस्तैस्तपोधनैः ।समाश्वस्यत राजर्षिर्हतबन्धुर्युधिष्ठिरः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evaṁ bahuvidhairvākyairmunibhistaistapodhanaiḥ ,samāśvasyata rājarṣirhatabandhuryudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: In this manner, King Yudhiṣṭhira, who had lost his kinsmen, was consoled by those ascetics (muni) with various kinds of words.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 1. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: In this manner, King Yudhiṣṭhira, who had lost his kinsmen, was consoled by those ascetics (muni) with various kinds of words.

sanskrit: सोऽनुनीतो भगवता विष्टरश्रवसा स्वयम् ।द्वैपायनेन कृष्णेन देवस्थानेन चाभिभूः ॥२॥
transliteration: so'nunīto bhagavatā viṣṭaraśravasā svayam ,dvaipāyanena kṛṣṇena devasthānena cābhibhūḥ.
english: He, the conqueror, was personally consoled by the revered Viṣṭaraśravas (Lord Kṛṣṇa), by Dvaipāyana (Vyāsa), and by Devasthāna.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 2. He, the conqueror, was personally consoled by the revered Viṣṭaraśravas (Lord Kṛṣṇa), by Dvaipāyana (Vyāsa), and by Devasthāna.

sanskrit: नारदेनाथ भीमेन नकुलेन च पार्थिवः ।कृष्णया सहदेवेन विजयेन च धीमता ॥३॥
transliteration: nāradenātha bhīmena nakulena ca pārthivaḥ ,kṛṣṇayā sahadevena vijayena ca dhīmatā.
english: The king (Yudhiṣṭhira) was consoled by Nārada, by Bhīma, by Nakula, by Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī), by Sahadeva, and by the intelligent Vijaya (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 3. The king (Yudhiṣṭhira) was consoled by Nārada, by Bhīma, by Nakula, by Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī), by Sahadeva, and by the intelligent Vijaya (Arjuna).

sanskrit: अन्यैश्च पुरुषव्याघ्रैर्ब्राह्मणैः शास्त्रदृष्टिभिः ।व्यजहाच्छोकजं दुःखं संतापं चैव मानसम् ॥४॥
transliteration: anyaiśca puruṣavyāghrairbrāhmaṇaiḥ śāstradṛṣṭibhiḥ ,vyajahācchokajaṁ duḥkhaṁ saṁtāpaṁ caiva mānasam.
english: With the assistance of other tiger-like men (puruṣavyāghra) and brahmins possessing scriptural insight (śāstradṛṣṭi), he gave up the pain born of sorrow and mental anguish.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 4. With the assistance of other tiger-like men (puruṣavyāghra) and brahmins possessing scriptural insight (śāstradṛṣṭi), he gave up the pain born of sorrow and mental anguish.

sanskrit: अर्चयामास देवांश्च ब्राह्मणांश्च युधिष्ठिरः ।कृत्वाथ प्रेतकार्याणि बन्धूनां स पुनर्नृपः ।अन्वशासत धर्मात्मा पृथिवीं सागराम्बराम् ॥५॥
transliteration: arcayāmāsa devāṁśca brāhmaṇāṁśca yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,kṛtvātha pretakāryāṇi bandhūnāṁ sa punarnṛpaḥ ,anvaśāsata dharmātmā pṛthivīṁ sāgarāmbarām.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira worshipped the gods and the brahmins. Then, having performed the funeral rites of his relatives, that king, a man of virtuous nature (dharmātmā), again ruled the earth, which is girdled by the oceans.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 5. Yudhiṣṭhira worshipped the gods and the brahmins. Then, having performed the funeral rites of his relatives, that king, a man of virtuous nature (dharmātmā), again ruled the earth, which is girdled by the oceans.

sanskrit: प्रशान्तचेताः कौरव्यः स्वराज्यं प्राप्य केवलम् ।व्यासं च नारदं चैव तांश्चान्यानब्रवीन्नृपः ॥६॥
transliteration: praśāntacetāḥ kauravyaḥ svarājyaṁ prāpya kevalam ,vyāsaṁ ca nāradaṁ caiva tāṁścānyānabravīnnṛpaḥ.
english: With a tranquil mind, the Kaurava, having obtained his entire kingdom, then spoke to Vyāsa, Nārada, and other such individuals.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 6. With a tranquil mind, the Kaurava, having obtained his entire kingdom, then spoke to Vyāsa, Nārada, and other such individuals.

sanskrit: आश्वासितोऽहं प्राग्वृद्धैर्भवद्भिर्मुनिपुंगवैः ।न सूक्ष्ममपि मे किंचिद्व्यलीकमिह विद्यते ॥७॥
transliteration: āśvāsito'haṁ prāgvṛddhairbhavadbhirmunipuṁgavaiḥ ,na sūkṣmamapi me kiṁcidvyalīkamiha vidyate.
english: I have been comforted by you, venerable chief sages (munipuṃgava). Not even a slight cause for unhappiness (vyalīka) remains for me here.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 7. I have been comforted by you, venerable chief sages (munipuṃgava). Not even a slight cause for unhappiness (vyalīka) remains for me here.

sanskrit: अर्थश्च सुमहान्प्राप्तो येन यक्ष्यामि देवताः ।पुरस्कृत्येह भवतः समानेष्यामहे मखम् ॥८॥
transliteration: arthaśca sumahānprāpto yena yakṣyāmi devatāḥ ,puraskṛtyeha bhavataḥ samāneṣyāmahe makham.
english: A very great wealth has been acquired, by which I shall perform (Vedic rituals) for the gods. Having honored you here, we shall conduct the (Vedic ritual) (makham).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 8. A very great wealth has been acquired, by which I shall perform (Vedic rituals) for the gods. Having honored you here, we shall conduct the (Vedic ritual) (makham).

sanskrit: हिमवन्तं त्वया गुप्ता गमिष्यामः पितामह ।बह्वाश्चर्यो हि देशः स श्रूयते द्विजसत्तम ॥९॥
transliteration: himavantaṁ tvayā guptā gamiṣyāmaḥ pitāmaha ,bahvāścaryo hi deśaḥ sa śrūyate dvijasattama.
english: Protected by you, O Grandfather, we will go to the Himalayas. Indeed, that region, O best among the twice-born (dvija), is known for its many wonders.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 9. Protected by you, O Grandfather, we will go to the Himalayas. Indeed, that region, O best among the twice-born (dvija), is known for its many wonders.

sanskrit: तथा भगवता चित्रं कल्याणं बहु भाषितम् ।देवर्षिणा नारदेन देवस्थानेन चैव ह ॥१०॥
transliteration: tathā bhagavatā citraṁ kalyāṇaṁ bahu bhāṣitam ,devarṣiṇā nāradena devasthānena caiva ha.
english: Thus, much varied welfare was spoken of by the revered ones, by the divine sage Nārada, and indeed by Devasthāna.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 10. Thus, much varied welfare was spoken of by the revered ones, by the divine sage Nārada, and indeed by Devasthāna.

sanskrit: नाभागधेयः पुरुषः कश्चिदेवंविधान्गुरून् ।लभते व्यसनं प्राप्य सुहृदः साधुसंमतान् ॥११॥
transliteration: nābhāgadheyaḥ puruṣaḥ kaścidevaṁvidhāngurūn ,labhate vyasanaṁ prāpya suhṛdaḥ sādhusaṁmatān.
english: No person who is without a share of good fortune (abhāgadheya) obtains such preceptors (guru) or virtuous friends, even after encountering distress.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 11. No person who is without a share of good fortune (abhāgadheya) obtains such preceptors (guru) or virtuous friends, even after encountering distress.

sanskrit: एवमुक्तास्तु ते राज्ञा सर्व एव महर्षयः ।अभ्यनुज्ञाप्य राजानं तथोभौ कृष्णफल्गुनौ ।पश्यतामेव सर्वेषां तत्रैवादर्शनं ययुः ॥१२॥
transliteration: evamuktāstu te rājñā sarva eva maharṣayaḥ ,abhyanujñāpya rājānaṁ tathobhau kṛṣṇaphalgunau ,paśyatāmeva sarveṣāṁ tatraivādarśanaṁ yayuḥ.
english: Thus addressed by the king, all those great sages, having taken leave of the king and also of both Krishna and Arjuna, disappeared from that very spot, even as everyone watched.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 12. Thus addressed by the king, all those great sages, having taken leave of the king and also of both Krishna and Arjuna, disappeared from that very spot, even as everyone watched.

sanskrit: ततो धर्मसुतो राजा तत्रैवोपाविशत्प्रभुः ।एवं नातिमहान्कालः स तेषामभ्यवर्तत ॥१३॥
transliteration: tato dharmasuto rājā tatraivopāviśatprabhuḥ ,evaṁ nātimahānkālaḥ sa teṣāmabhyavartata.
english: Then the king, the son of natural law (dharma) and a great lord, sat down there. In this way, not a very long time passed for them.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 13. Then the king, the son of natural law (dharma) and a great lord, sat down there. In this way, not a very long time passed for them.

sanskrit: कुर्वतां शौचकर्माणि भीष्मस्य निधने तदा ।महादानानि विप्रेभ्यो ददतामौर्ध्वदैहिकम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: kurvatāṁ śaucakarmāṇi bhīṣmasya nidhane tadā ,mahādānāni viprebhyo dadatāmaurdhvadaihikam.
english: While they were performing purification rites at the time of Bhishma's demise, and giving great gifts to the brahmins for his post-mortem ceremonies.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 14. While they were performing purification rites at the time of Bhishma's demise, and giving great gifts to the brahmins for his post-mortem ceremonies.

sanskrit: भीष्मकर्णपुरोगाणां कुरूणां कुरुनन्दन ।सहितो धृतराष्ट्रेण प्रददावौर्ध्वदैहिकम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: bhīṣmakarṇapurogāṇāṁ kurūṇāṁ kurunandana ,sahito dhṛtarāṣṭreṇa pradadāvaurdhvadaihikam.
english: O delight of the Kurus, accompanied by Dhṛtarāṣṭra, he performed the post-mortem rites for the Kurus, including those led by Bhishma and Karna.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 15. O delight of the Kurus, accompanied by Dhṛtarāṣṭra, he performed the post-mortem rites for the Kurus, including those led by Bhishma and Karna.

sanskrit: ततो दत्त्वा बहु धनं विप्रेभ्यः पाण्डवर्षभः ।धृतराष्ट्रं पुरस्कृत्य विवेश गजसाह्वयम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: tato dattvā bahu dhanaṁ viprebhyaḥ pāṇḍavarṣabhaḥ ,dhṛtarāṣṭraṁ puraskṛtya viveśa gajasāhvayam.
english: Then, the best of the Pāṇḍavas, after distributing much wealth to the brahmins and honoring Dhṛtarāṣṭra by placing him at the forefront, entered Hastinapura.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 16. Then, the best of the Pāṇḍavas, after distributing much wealth to the brahmins and honoring Dhṛtarāṣṭra by placing him at the forefront, entered Hastinapura.

sanskrit: स समाश्वास्य पितरं प्रज्ञाचक्षुषमीश्वरम् ।अन्वशाद्वै स धर्मात्मा पृथिवीं भ्रातृभिः सह ॥१७॥
transliteration: sa samāśvāsya pitaraṁ prajñācakṣuṣamīśvaram ,anvaśādvai sa dharmātmā pṛthivīṁ bhrātṛbhiḥ saha.
english: He, that righteous one (dharmātmā), having consoled his father – the blind (prajñācakṣus) lord – truly ruled the earth along with his brothers.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 14 Verse 17. He, that righteous one (dharmātmā), having consoled his father – the blind (prajñācakṣus) lord – truly ruled the earth along with his brothers.

sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।विजिते पाण्डवेयैस्तु प्रशान्ते च द्विजोत्तम ।राष्ट्रे किं चक्रतुर्वीरौ वासुदेवधनंजयौ ॥१॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,vijite pāṇḍaveyaistu praśānte ca dvijottama ,rāṣṭre kiṁ cakraturvīrau vāsudevadhanaṁjayau.
english: Janamejaya said: "O best of brahmins, when the kingdom had been conquered by the Pāṇḍavas and peace was established, what did the two heroes, Vāsudeva and Dhanañjaya, do?"
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 1. Janamejaya said: "O best of brahmins, when the kingdom had been conquered by the Pāṇḍavas and peace was established, what did the two heroes, Vāsudeva and Dhanañjaya, do?"

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।विजिते पाण्डवेयैस्तु प्रशान्ते च विशां पते ।राष्ट्रे बभूवतुर्हृष्टौ वासुदेवधनंजयौ ॥२॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,vijite pāṇḍaveyaistu praśānte ca viśāṁ pate ,rāṣṭre babhūvaturhṛṣṭau vāsudevadhanaṁjayau.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: "O ruler (viśāṁ pate), when the kingdom had been conquered by the Pāṇḍavas and peace was established, Vāsudeva and Dhanañjaya were indeed greatly delighted."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 2. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: "O ruler (viśāṁ pate), when the kingdom had been conquered by the Pāṇḍavas and peace was established, Vāsudeva and Dhanañjaya were indeed greatly delighted."

sanskrit: विजह्राते मुदा युक्तौ दिवि देवेश्वराविव ।तौ वनेषु विचित्रेषु पर्वतानां च सानुषु ॥३॥
transliteration: vijahrāte mudā yuktau divi deveśvarāviva ,tau vaneṣu vicitreṣu parvatānāṁ ca sānuṣu.
english: The two, endowed with joy, sported like the two lords of gods in heaven. They wandered through diverse forests and on the slopes of mountains.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 3. The two, endowed with joy, sported like the two lords of gods in heaven. They wandered through diverse forests and on the slopes of mountains.

sanskrit: शैलेषु रमणीयेषु पल्वलेषु नदीषु च ।चङ्क्रम्यमाणौ संहृष्टावश्विनाविव नन्दने ॥४॥
transliteration: śaileṣu ramaṇīyeṣu palvaleṣu nadīṣu ca ,caṅkramyamāṇau saṁhṛṣṭāvaśvināviva nandane.
english: They wandered, greatly delighted, on charming mountains, by ponds, and along rivers, just like the two Aśvins in the Nandana garden.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 4. They wandered, greatly delighted, on charming mountains, by ponds, and along rivers, just like the two Aśvins in the Nandana garden.

sanskrit: इन्द्रप्रस्थे महात्मानौ रेमाते कृष्णपाण्डवौ ।प्रविश्य तां सभां रम्यां विजह्राते च भारत ॥५॥
transliteration: indraprasthe mahātmānau remāte kṛṣṇapāṇḍavau ,praviśya tāṁ sabhāṁ ramyāṁ vijahrāte ca bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, the two great-souled ones, Krishna and the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna), enjoyed themselves in Indraprastha. Having entered that beautiful assembly hall, they also sported.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 5. O Bhārata, the two great-souled ones, Krishna and the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna), enjoyed themselves in Indraprastha. Having entered that beautiful assembly hall, they also sported.

sanskrit: तत्र युद्धकथाश्चित्राः परिक्लेशांश्च पार्थिव ।कथायोगे कथायोगे कथयामासतुस्तदा ॥६॥
transliteration: tatra yuddhakathāścitrāḥ parikleśāṁśca pārthiva ,kathāyoge kathāyoge kathayāmāsatustadā.
english: O king (pārthiva), there, during their conversations and discussions, they narrated various tales of battles and their afflictions at that time.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 6. O king (pārthiva), there, during their conversations and discussions, they narrated various tales of battles and their afflictions at that time.

sanskrit: ऋषीणां देवतानां च वंशांस्तावाहतुस्तदा ।प्रीयमाणौ महात्मानौ पुराणावृषिसत्तमौ ॥७॥
transliteration: ṛṣīṇāṁ devatānāṁ ca vaṁśāṁstāvāhatustadā ,prīyamāṇau mahātmānau purāṇāvṛṣisattamau.
english: Then, those two ancient, great-souled, and most excellent sages, delighting, recounted the lineages of sages and deities.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 7. Then, those two ancient, great-souled, and most excellent sages, delighting, recounted the lineages of sages and deities.

sanskrit: मधुरास्तु कथाश्चित्राश्चित्रार्थपदनिश्चयाः ।निश्चयज्ञः स पार्थाय कथयामास केशवः ॥८॥
transliteration: madhurāstu kathāścitrāścitrārthapadaniścayāḥ ,niścayajñaḥ sa pārthāya kathayāmāsa keśavaḥ.
english: Keshava, who possessed certain knowledge, narrated to Partha (Arjuna) delightful and varied stories, whose meanings and terms were precise.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 8. Keshava, who possessed certain knowledge, narrated to Partha (Arjuna) delightful and varied stories, whose meanings and terms were precise.

sanskrit: पुत्रशोकाभिसंतप्तं ज्ञातीनां च सहस्रशः ।कथाभिः शमयामास पार्थं शौरिर्जनार्दनः ॥९॥
transliteration: putraśokābhisaṁtaptaṁ jñātīnāṁ ca sahasraśaḥ ,kathābhiḥ śamayāmāsa pārthaṁ śaurirjanārdanaḥ.
english: Shauri (Krishna), Janardana, pacified Partha (Arjuna) - who was intensely distressed by grief for his sons and for thousands of kinsmen - by means of these stories.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 9. Shauri (Krishna), Janardana, pacified Partha (Arjuna) - who was intensely distressed by grief for his sons and for thousands of kinsmen - by means of these stories.

sanskrit: स तमाश्वास्य विधिवद्विधानज्ञो महातपाः ।अपहृत्यात्मनो भारं विशश्रामेव सात्वतः ॥१०॥
transliteration: sa tamāśvāsya vidhivadvidhānajño mahātapāḥ ,apahṛtyātmano bhāraṁ viśaśrāmeva sātvataḥ.
english: Having properly consoled him, that expert in sacred rules and one of great spiritual power (Krishna, the Sātvata), felt as if he had removed his own burden and then rested.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 10. Having properly consoled him, that expert in sacred rules and one of great spiritual power (Krishna, the Sātvata), felt as if he had removed his own burden and then rested.

sanskrit: ततः कथान्ते गोविन्दो गुडाकेशमुवाच ह ।सान्त्वयञ्श्लक्ष्णया वाचा हेतुयुक्तमिदं वचः ॥११॥
transliteration: tataḥ kathānte govindo guḍākeśamuvāca ha ,sāntvayañślakṣṇayā vācā hetuyuktamidaṁ vacaḥ.
english: Then, at the conclusion of the discourse, Govinda addressed Guḍākeśa (Arjuna) with these gentle and reasonable words, comforting him.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 11. Then, at the conclusion of the discourse, Govinda addressed Guḍākeśa (Arjuna) with these gentle and reasonable words, comforting him.

sanskrit: विजितेयं धरा कृत्स्ना सव्यसाचिन्परंतप ।त्वद्बाहुबलमाश्रित्य राज्ञा धर्मसुतेन ह ॥१२॥
transliteration: vijiteyaṁ dharā kṛtsnā savyasācinparaṁtapa ,tvadbāhubalamāśritya rājñā dharmasutena ha.
english: O Savyasācin (Arjuna), O tormentor of foes, this entire earth has indeed been conquered by King Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of (dharma), relying on the might of your arms.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 12. O Savyasācin (Arjuna), O tormentor of foes, this entire earth has indeed been conquered by King Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of (dharma), relying on the might of your arms.

sanskrit: असपत्नां महीं भुङ्क्ते धर्मराजो युधिष्ठिरः ।भीमसेनप्रभावेन यमयोश्च नरोत्तम ॥१३॥
transliteration: asapatnāṁ mahīṁ bhuṅkte dharmarājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,bhīmasenaprabhāvena yamayośca narottama.
english: O best of men (Arjuna), King Yudhiṣṭhira, the ruler of (dharma), enjoys the earth without rivals, by the power of Bhīmasena and the two Yamas (Nākula and Sahadeva).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 13. O best of men (Arjuna), King Yudhiṣṭhira, the ruler of (dharma), enjoys the earth without rivals, by the power of Bhīmasena and the two Yamas (Nākula and Sahadeva).

sanskrit: धर्मेण राज्ञा धर्मज्ञ प्राप्तं राज्यमकण्टकम् ।धर्मेण निहतः संख्ये स च राजा सुयोधनः ॥१४॥
transliteration: dharmeṇa rājñā dharmajña prāptaṁ rājyamakaṇṭakam ,dharmeṇa nihataḥ saṁkhye sa ca rājā suyodhanaḥ.
english: O knower of (dharma) (Arjuna), the kingdom without rivals was obtained by the King through righteous means (dharma). And by righteous means (dharma), that King Suyodhana (Duryodhana) was killed in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 14. O knower of (dharma) (Arjuna), the kingdom without rivals was obtained by the King through righteous means (dharma). And by righteous means (dharma), that King Suyodhana (Duryodhana) was killed in battle.

sanskrit: अधर्मरुचयो लुब्धाः सदा चाप्रियवादिनः ।धार्तराष्ट्रा दुरात्मानः सानुबन्धा निपातिताः ॥१५॥
transliteration: adharmarucayo lubdhāḥ sadā cāpriyavādinaḥ ,dhārtarāṣṭrā durātmānaḥ sānubandhā nipātitāḥ.
english: The sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, who were inclined towards unrighteousness (adharma), greedy, always spoke unpleasant words, and were wicked, have been struck down along with their relatives.
