search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 15. The sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, who were inclined towards unrighteousness (adharma), greedy, always spoke unpleasant words, and were wicked, have been struck down along with their relatives.

sanskrit: प्रशान्तामखिलां पार्थ पृथिवीं पृथिवीपतिः ।भुङ्क्ते धर्मसुतो राजा त्वया गुप्तः कुरूद्वह ॥१६॥
transliteration: praśāntāmakhilāṁ pārtha pṛthivīṁ pṛthivīpatiḥ ,bhuṅkte dharmasuto rājā tvayā guptaḥ kurūdvaha.
english: O Pārtha, O foremost of the Kurus, King Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of (dharma), rules the entire peaceful earth, protected by you.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 16. O Pārtha, O foremost of the Kurus, King Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of (dharma), rules the entire peaceful earth, protected by you.

sanskrit: रमे चाहं त्वया सार्धमरण्येष्वपि पाण्डव ।किमु यत्र जनोऽयं वै पृथा चामित्रकर्शन ॥१७॥
transliteration: rame cāhaṁ tvayā sārdhamaraṇyeṣvapi pāṇḍava ,kimu yatra jano'yaṁ vai pṛthā cāmitrakarśana.
english: O Pāṇḍava, I delight even in the forests with you. How much more so where these people and Pṛthā (Kuntī) are, O tormentor of enemies?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 17. O Pāṇḍava, I delight even in the forests with you. How much more so where these people and Pṛthā (Kuntī) are, O tormentor of enemies?

sanskrit: यत्र धर्मसुतो राजा यत्र भीमो महाबलः ।यत्र माद्रवतीपुत्रौ रतिस्तत्र परा मम ॥१८॥
transliteration: yatra dharmasuto rājā yatra bhīmo mahābalaḥ ,yatra mādravatīputrau ratistatra parā mama.
english: Where the king is the son of (dharma), where Bhīma of great strength is, and where the two sons of Mādrī are, there is my supreme delight.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 18. Where the king is the son of (dharma), where Bhīma of great strength is, and where the two sons of Mādrī are, there is my supreme delight.

sanskrit: तथैव स्वर्गकल्पेषु सभोद्देशेषु भारत ।रमणीयेषु पुण्येषु सहितस्य त्वयानघ ॥१९॥
transliteration: tathaiva svargakalpeṣu sabhoddeśeṣu bhārata ,ramaṇīyeṣu puṇyeṣu sahitasya tvayānagha.
english: And likewise, O Bhārata, O sinless one, I recall the time spent with you in delightful and sacred assembly halls that were like heavenly realms.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 19. And likewise, O Bhārata, O sinless one, I recall the time spent with you in delightful and sacred assembly halls that were like heavenly realms.

sanskrit: कालो महांस्त्वतीतो मे शूरपुत्रमपश्यतः ।बलदेवं च कौरव्य तथान्यान्वृष्णिपुंगवान् ॥२०॥
transliteration: kālo mahāṁstvatīto me śūraputramapaśyataḥ ,baladevaṁ ca kauravya tathānyānvṛṣṇipuṁgavān.
english: Indeed, a long time has passed for me, O descendant of Kuru, without seeing the son of Śūra, and Baladeva, as well as other excellent Vṛṣṇi heroes.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 20. Indeed, a long time has passed for me, O descendant of Kuru, without seeing the son of Śūra, and Baladeva, as well as other excellent Vṛṣṇi heroes.

sanskrit: सोऽहं गन्तुमभीप्सामि पुरीं द्वारवतीं प्रति ।रोचतां गमनं मह्यं तवापि पुरुषर्षभ ॥२१॥
transliteration: so'haṁ gantumabhīpsāmi purīṁ dvāravatīṁ prati ,rocatāṁ gamanaṁ mahyaṁ tavāpi puruṣarṣabha.
english: Therefore, I desire to go towards the city of Dvāravatī. May my departure be agreeable to you also, O best among men.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 21. Therefore, I desire to go towards the city of Dvāravatī. May my departure be agreeable to you also, O best among men.

sanskrit: उक्तो बहुविधं राजा तत्र तत्र युधिष्ठिरः ।स ह भीष्मेण यद्युक्तमस्माभिः शोककारिते ॥२२॥
transliteration: ukto bahuvidhaṁ rājā tatra tatra yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,sa ha bhīṣmeṇa yadyuktamasmābhiḥ śokakārite.
english: King Yudhiṣṭhira was advised extensively on various occasions. Indeed, what was appropriate concerning the sorrowful situation was told to him by Bhīṣma and also by us.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 22. King Yudhiṣṭhira was advised extensively on various occasions. Indeed, what was appropriate concerning the sorrowful situation was told to him by Bhīṣma and also by us.

sanskrit: शिष्टो युधिष्ठिरोऽस्माभिः शास्ता सन्नपि पाण्डवः ।तेन तच्च वचः सम्यग्गृहीतं सुमहात्मना ॥२३॥
transliteration: śiṣṭo yudhiṣṭhiro'smābhiḥ śāstā sannapi pāṇḍavaḥ ,tena tacca vacaḥ samyaggṛhītaṁ sumahātmanā.
english: Even though Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Pāṇḍu, is our teacher, he was instructed by us. That excellent advice was properly accepted by that great soul (mahātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 23. Even though Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Pāṇḍu, is our teacher, he was instructed by us. That excellent advice was properly accepted by that great soul (mahātman).

sanskrit: धर्मपुत्रे हि धर्मज्ञे कृतज्ञे सत्यवादिनि ।सत्यं धर्मो मतिश्चाग्र्या स्थितिश्च सततं स्थिरा ॥२४॥
transliteration: dharmaputre hi dharmajñe kṛtajñe satyavādini ,satyaṁ dharmo matiścāgryā sthitiśca satataṁ sthirā.
english: Indeed, in the son of Dharma (dharma), Yudhiṣṭhira, who understands (dharma), is grateful, and speaks the truth, truth, the natural law (dharma), excellent intelligence, and constant steadfastness are ever present.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 24. Indeed, in the son of Dharma (dharma), Yudhiṣṭhira, who understands (dharma), is grateful, and speaks the truth, truth, the natural law (dharma), excellent intelligence, and constant steadfastness are ever present.

sanskrit: तद्गत्वा तं महात्मानं यदि ते रोचतेऽर्जुन ।अस्मद्गमनसंयुक्तं वचो ब्रूहि जनाधिपम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: tadgatvā taṁ mahātmānaṁ yadi te rocate'rjuna ,asmadgamanasaṁyuktaṁ vaco brūhi janādhipam.
english: Therefore, Arjuna, if it pleases you, go to that great soul (mahātman) and speak to the king (janādhipa) a message connected with our departure.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 25. Therefore, Arjuna, if it pleases you, go to that great soul (mahātman) and speak to the king (janādhipa) a message connected with our departure.

sanskrit: न हि तस्याप्रियं कुर्यां प्राणत्यागेऽप्युपस्थिते ।कुतो गन्तुं महाबाहो पुरीं द्वारवतीं प्रति ॥२६॥
transliteration: na hi tasyāpriyaṁ kuryāṁ prāṇatyāge'pyupasthite ,kuto gantuṁ mahābāho purīṁ dvāravatīṁ prati.
english: Indeed, I would not do anything disagreeable to him, even if my life were at stake. How then, O mighty-armed one, can I go to the city of Dvāravatī?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 26. Indeed, I would not do anything disagreeable to him, even if my life were at stake. How then, O mighty-armed one, can I go to the city of Dvāravatī?

sanskrit: सर्वं त्विदमहं पार्थ त्वत्प्रीतिहितकाम्यया ।ब्रवीमि सत्यं कौरव्य न मिथ्यैतत्कथंचन ॥२७॥
transliteration: sarvaṁ tvidamahaṁ pārtha tvatprītihitakāmyayā ,bravīmi satyaṁ kauravya na mithyaitatkathaṁcana.
english: O Pārtha, I declare all this to you truly, O descendant of Kuru, desiring your well-being and satisfaction. This is in no way false.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 27. O Pārtha, I declare all this to you truly, O descendant of Kuru, desiring your well-being and satisfaction. This is in no way false.

sanskrit: प्रयोजनं च निर्वृत्तमिह वासे ममार्जुन ।धार्तराष्ट्रो हतो राजा सबलः सपदानुगः ॥२८॥
transliteration: prayojanaṁ ca nirvṛttamiha vāse mamārjuna ,dhārtarāṣṭro hato rājā sabalaḥ sapadānugaḥ.
english: And my purpose for residing here, O Arjuna, has been fulfilled. Dhṛtarāṣṭra's son, the king, has been slain along with his army and followers.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 28. And my purpose for residing here, O Arjuna, has been fulfilled. Dhṛtarāṣṭra's son, the king, has been slain along with his army and followers.

sanskrit: पृथिवी च वशे तात धर्मपुत्रस्य धीमतः ।स्थिता समुद्रवसना सशैलवनकानना ।चिता रत्नैर्बहुविधैः कुरुराजस्य पाण्डव ॥२९॥
transliteration: pṛthivī ca vaśe tāta dharmaputrasya dhīmataḥ ,sthitā samudravasanā saśailavanakānanā ,citā ratnairbahuvidhaiḥ kururājasya pāṇḍava.
english: And the earth, O dear one, is now under the control of the wise son of natural law (dharma), Yudhiṣṭhira. Adorned with oceans as her garment, encompassing mountains, forests, and groves, and filled with diverse jewels, she belongs to the king of the Kurus, O Pāṇḍava.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 29. And the earth, O dear one, is now under the control of the wise son of natural law (dharma), Yudhiṣṭhira. Adorned with oceans as her garment, encompassing mountains, forests, and groves, and filled with diverse jewels, she belongs to the king of the Kurus, O Pāṇḍava.

sanskrit: धर्मेण राजा धर्मज्ञः पातु सर्वां वसुंधराम् ।उपास्यमानो बहुभिः सिद्धैश्चापि महात्मभिः ।स्तूयमानश्च सततं बन्दिभिर्भरतर्षभ ॥३०॥
transliteration: dharmeṇa rājā dharmajñaḥ pātu sarvāṁ vasuṁdharām ,upāsyamāno bahubhiḥ siddhaiścāpi mahātmabhiḥ ,stūyamānaśca satataṁ bandibhirbharatarṣabha.
english: May the king, who understands natural law (dharma), protect the entire earth through natural law (dharma). May he be served by many perfected beings (siddhas) and great souls (mahātman-s), and constantly praised by bards, O best of the Bhāratas.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 30. May the king, who understands natural law (dharma), protect the entire earth through natural law (dharma). May he be served by many perfected beings (siddhas) and great souls (mahātman-s), and constantly praised by bards, O best of the Bhāratas.

sanskrit: तन्मया सह गत्वाद्य राजानं कुरुवर्धनम् ।आपृच्छ कुरुशार्दूल गमनं द्वारकां प्रति ॥३१॥
transliteration: tanmayā saha gatvādya rājānaṁ kuruvardhanam ,āpṛccha kuruśārdūla gamanaṁ dvārakāṁ prati.
english: Therefore, O tiger among Kurus, go with me today to King Kuruvardhana and ask his permission for our journey to Dvārakā.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 31. Therefore, O tiger among Kurus, go with me today to King Kuruvardhana and ask his permission for our journey to Dvārakā.

sanskrit: इदं शरीरं वसु यच्च मे गृहे निवेदितं पार्थ सदा युधिष्ठिरे ।प्रियश्च मान्यश्च हि मे युधिष्ठिरः सदा कुरूणामधिपो महामतिः ॥३२॥
transliteration: idaṁ śarīraṁ vasu yacca me gṛhe; niveditaṁ pārtha sadā yudhiṣṭhire ,priyaśca mānyaśca hi me yudhiṣṭhiraḥ; sadā kurūṇāmadhipo mahāmatiḥ.
english: O Pārtha, this body of mine and all the wealth in my house have always been dedicated to Yudhiṣṭhira. Indeed, Yudhiṣṭhira is always dear and respected by me; he is the great-minded chief of the Kurus.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 32. O Pārtha, this body of mine and all the wealth in my house have always been dedicated to Yudhiṣṭhira. Indeed, Yudhiṣṭhira is always dear and respected by me; he is the great-minded chief of the Kurus.

sanskrit: प्रयोजनं चापि निवासकारणे न विद्यते मे त्वदृते महाभुज ।स्थिता हि पृथ्वी तव पार्थ शासने गुरोः सुवृत्तस्य युधिष्ठिरस्य ह ॥३३॥
transliteration: prayojanaṁ cāpi nivāsakāraṇe; na vidyate me tvadṛte mahābhuja ,sthitā hi pṛthvī tava pārtha śāsane; guroḥ suvṛttasya yudhiṣṭhirasya ha.
english: And indeed, O mighty-armed one, I have no need for a dwelling place without you. For this earth, O Pārtha, stands under your rule and that of the virtuous teacher (guru) Yudhiṣṭhira.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 33. And indeed, O mighty-armed one, I have no need for a dwelling place without you. For this earth, O Pārtha, stands under your rule and that of the virtuous teacher (guru) Yudhiṣṭhira.

sanskrit: इतीदमुक्तं स तदा महात्मना जनार्दनेनामितविक्रमोऽर्जुनः ।तथेति कृच्छ्रादिव वाचमीरयज्जनार्दनं संप्रतिपूज्य पार्थिव ॥३४॥
transliteration: itīdamuktaṁ sa tadā mahātmanā; janārdanenāmitavikramo'rjunaḥ ,tatheti kṛcchrādiva vācamīraya;jjanārdanaṁ saṁpratipūjya pārthiva.
english: Thus spoke the great-souled (mahātman) Janārdana (Krishna). Then Arjuna, whose valor was immeasurable, having properly honored Janārdana, uttered the words, 'So be it,' as if with great difficulty, O king.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 15 Verse 34. Thus spoke the great-souled (mahātman) Janārdana (Krishna). Then Arjuna, whose valor was immeasurable, having properly honored Janārdana, uttered the words, 'So be it,' as if with great difficulty, O king.

sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।सभायां वसतोस्तस्यां निहत्यारीन्महात्मनोः ।केशवार्जुनयोः का नु कथा समभवद्द्विज ॥१॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,sabhāyāṁ vasatostasyāṁ nihatyārīnmahātmanoḥ ,keśavārjunayoḥ kā nu kathā samabhavaddvija.
english: Janamejaya said: "O twice-born (dvija), what kind of conversation took place between Keśava (Krishna) and Arjuna, those magnanimous heroes, while they resided in that assembly hall after having vanquished their enemies?"
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 1. Janamejaya said: "O twice-born (dvija), what kind of conversation took place between Keśava (Krishna) and Arjuna, those magnanimous heroes, while they resided in that assembly hall after having vanquished their enemies?"

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।कृष्णेन सहितः पार्थः स्वराज्यं प्राप्य केवलम् ।तस्यां सभायां रम्यायां विजहार मुदा युतः ॥२॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,kṛṣṇena sahitaḥ pārthaḥ svarājyaṁ prāpya kevalam ,tasyāṁ sabhāyāṁ ramyāyāṁ vijahāra mudā yutaḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana replied: "Arjuna, accompanied by Krishna, having fully regained his kingdom, enjoyed himself in that beautiful assembly hall, filled with joy."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 2. Vaiśampāyana replied: "Arjuna, accompanied by Krishna, having fully regained his kingdom, enjoyed himself in that beautiful assembly hall, filled with joy."

sanskrit: ततः कंचित्सभोद्देशं स्वर्गोद्देशसमं नृप ।यदृच्छया तौ मुदितौ जग्मतुः स्वजनावृतौ ॥३॥
transliteration: tataḥ kaṁcitsabhoddeśaṁ svargoddeśasamaṁ nṛpa ,yadṛcchayā tau muditau jagmatuḥ svajanāvṛtau.
english: Then, O king, those two joyful ones, surrounded by their own people, casually went to a certain part of the assembly hall, which was like a part of heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 3. Then, O king, those two joyful ones, surrounded by their own people, casually went to a certain part of the assembly hall, which was like a part of heaven.

sanskrit: ततः प्रतीतः कृष्णेन सहितः पाण्डवोऽर्जुनः ।निरीक्ष्य तां सभां रम्यामिदं वचनमब्रवीत् ॥४॥
transliteration: tataḥ pratītaḥ kṛṣṇena sahitaḥ pāṇḍavo'rjunaḥ ,nirīkṣya tāṁ sabhāṁ ramyāmidaṁ vacanamabravīt.
english: Then, the pleased Pāṇḍava Arjuna, accompanied by Krishna, having observed that charming assembly hall, spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 4. Then, the pleased Pāṇḍava Arjuna, accompanied by Krishna, having observed that charming assembly hall, spoke these words.

sanskrit: विदितं ते महाबाहो संग्रामे समुपस्थिते ।माहात्म्यं देवकीमातस्तच्च ते रूपमैश्वरम् ॥५॥
transliteration: viditaṁ te mahābāho saṁgrāme samupasthite ,māhātmyaṁ devakīmātastacca te rūpamaiśvaram.
english: O mighty-armed one, your greatness and that divine form of yours were known to you when the battle was at hand, O mother of Devaki.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 5. O mighty-armed one, your greatness and that divine form of yours were known to you when the battle was at hand, O mother of Devaki.

sanskrit: यत्तु तद्भवता प्रोक्तं तदा केशव सौहृदात् ।तत्सर्वं पुरुषव्याघ्र नष्टं मे नष्टचेतसः ॥६॥
transliteration: yattu tadbhavatā proktaṁ tadā keśava sauhṛdāt ,tatsarvaṁ puruṣavyāghra naṣṭaṁ me naṣṭacetasaḥ.
english: But whatever was said by you then, O Keśava, out of friendship, all that, O tiger among men, is lost to me, whose mind is bewildered.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 6. But whatever was said by you then, O Keśava, out of friendship, all that, O tiger among men, is lost to me, whose mind is bewildered.

sanskrit: मम कौतूहलं त्वस्ति तेष्वर्थेषु पुनः प्रभो ।भवांश्च द्वारकां गन्ता नचिरादिव माधव ॥७॥
transliteration: mama kautūhalaṁ tvasti teṣvartheṣu punaḥ prabho ,bhavāṁśca dvārakāṁ gantā nacirādiva mādhava.
english: However, O Lord, my curiosity concerning those subjects is revived. And you, O Mādhava, will soon depart for Dvārakā.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 7. However, O Lord, my curiosity concerning those subjects is revived. And you, O Mādhava, will soon depart for Dvārakā.

sanskrit: एवमुक्तस्ततः कृष्णः फल्गुनं प्रत्यभाषत ।परिष्वज्य महातेजा वचनं वदतां वरः ॥८॥
transliteration: evamuktastataḥ kṛṣṇaḥ phalgunaṁ pratyabhāṣata ,pariṣvajya mahātejā vacanaṁ vadatāṁ varaḥ.
english: Thus addressed, Kṛṣṇa, the greatly effulgent one and best among speakers, then embraced Phālguna and replied to him with a word.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 8. Thus addressed, Kṛṣṇa, the greatly effulgent one and best among speakers, then embraced Phālguna and replied to him with a word.

sanskrit: श्रावितस्त्वं मया गुह्यं ज्ञापितश्च सनातनम् ।धर्मं स्वरूपिणं पार्थ सर्वलोकांश्च शाश्वतान् ॥९॥
transliteration: śrāvitastvaṁ mayā guhyaṁ jñāpitaśca sanātanam ,dharmaṁ svarūpiṇaṁ pārtha sarvalokāṁśca śāśvatān.
english: O Pārtha, I have made you hear the profound (teaching) and revealed to you the eternal natural law (dharma) which embodies its own inherent nature, as well as all the everlasting worlds.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 9. O Pārtha, I have made you hear the profound (teaching) and revealed to you the eternal natural law (dharma) which embodies its own inherent nature, as well as all the everlasting worlds.

sanskrit: अबुद्ध्वा यन्न गृह्णीथास्तन्मे सुमहदप्रियम् ।नूनमश्रद्दधानोऽसि दुर्मेधाश्चासि पाण्डव ॥१०॥
transliteration: abuddhvā yanna gṛhṇīthāstanme sumahadapriyam ,nūnamaśraddadhāno'si durmedhāścāsi pāṇḍava.
english: That which you do not grasp without understanding it is a source of great displeasure to me. Surely, O Pāṇḍava, you are without spiritual conviction (śraddhā) and are dull-witted.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 10. That which you do not grasp without understanding it is a source of great displeasure to me. Surely, O Pāṇḍava, you are without spiritual conviction (śraddhā) and are dull-witted.

sanskrit: स हि धर्मः सुपर्याप्तो ब्रह्मणः पदवेदने ।न शक्यं तन्मया भूयस्तथा वक्तुमशेषतः ॥११॥
transliteration: sa hi dharmaḥ suparyāpto brahmaṇaḥ padavedane ,na śakyaṁ tanmayā bhūyastathā vaktumaśeṣataḥ.
english: That teaching (dharma) is indeed perfectly sufficient for understanding the state of the ultimate reality (brahman). It is not possible for me to explain that again in the same way, exhaustively.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 11. That teaching (dharma) is indeed perfectly sufficient for understanding the state of the ultimate reality (brahman). It is not possible for me to explain that again in the same way, exhaustively.

sanskrit: परं हि ब्रह्म कथितं योगयुक्तेन तन्मया ।इतिहासं तु वक्ष्यामि तस्मिन्नर्थे पुरातनम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: paraṁ hi brahma kathitaṁ yogayuktena tanmayā ,itihāsaṁ tu vakṣyāmi tasminnarthe purātanam.
english: Indeed, the supreme (brahman) has been declared by me, who am engaged in the spiritual discipline (yoga). But concerning that subject, I will now relate an ancient narrative (itihāsa).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 12. Indeed, the supreme (brahman) has been declared by me, who am engaged in the spiritual discipline (yoga). But concerning that subject, I will now relate an ancient narrative (itihāsa).

sanskrit: यथा तां बुद्धिमास्थाय गतिमग्र्यां गमिष्यसि ।शृणु धर्मभृतां श्रेष्ठ गदतः सर्वमेव मे ॥१३॥
transliteration: yathā tāṁ buddhimāsthāya gatimagryāṁ gamiṣyasi ,śṛṇu dharmabhṛtāṁ śreṣṭha gadataḥ sarvameva me.
english: Listen, O best among those who uphold the natural law (dharma), to everything I will tell you, regarding how, by cultivating that understanding, you will attain the supreme state.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 13. Listen, O best among those who uphold the natural law (dharma), to everything I will tell you, regarding how, by cultivating that understanding, you will attain the supreme state.

sanskrit: आगच्छद्ब्राह्मणः कश्चित्स्वर्गलोकादरिंदम ।ब्रह्मलोकाच्च दुर्धर्षः सोऽस्माभिः पूजितोऽभवत् ॥१४॥
transliteration: āgacchadbrāhmaṇaḥ kaścitsvargalokādariṁdama ,brahmalokācca durdharṣaḥ so'smābhiḥ pūjito'bhavat.
english: O subduer of foes, a formidable brahmin came from the heavenly realm and also from the (brahma) world; he was honored by us.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 14. O subduer of foes, a formidable brahmin came from the heavenly realm and also from the (brahma) world; he was honored by us.

sanskrit: अस्माभिः परिपृष्टश्च यदाह भरतर्षभ ।दिव्येन विधिना पार्थ तच्छृणुष्वाविचारयन् ॥१५॥
transliteration: asmābhiḥ paripṛṣṭaśca yadāha bharatarṣabha ,divyena vidhinā pārtha tacchṛṇuṣvāvicārayan.
english: O bull among the Bharatas, O son of Pṛthā, listen without doubting to what he said to us when questioned, in a divine manner.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 15. O bull among the Bharatas, O son of Pṛthā, listen without doubting to what he said to us when questioned, in a divine manner.

sanskrit: ब्राह्मण उवाच ।मोक्षधर्मं समाश्रित्य कृष्ण यन्मानुपृच्छसि ।भूतानामनुकम्पार्थं यन्मोहच्छेदनं प्रभो ॥१६॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇa uvāca ,mokṣadharmaṁ samāśritya kṛṣṇa yanmānupṛcchasi ,bhūtānāmanukampārthaṁ yanmohacchedanaṁ prabho.
english: The Brahmin said: 'O Kṛṣṇa, O Lord, what you are asking me about concerning the intrinsic nature (dharma) of liberation (mokṣa)  -  that which is for the sake of compassion for all beings and which dispels delusion...'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 16. The Brahmin said: 'O Kṛṣṇa, O Lord, what you are asking me about concerning the intrinsic nature (dharma) of liberation (mokṣa)  -  that which is for the sake of compassion for all beings and which dispels delusion...'

sanskrit: तत्तेऽहं संप्रवक्ष्यामि यथावन्मधुसूदन ।शृणुष्वावहितो भूत्वा गदतो मम माधव ॥१७॥
transliteration: tatte'haṁ saṁpravakṣyāmi yathāvanmadhusūdana ,śṛṇuṣvāvahito bhūtvā gadato mama mādhava.
english: O Madhusūdana, I will now explain that to you precisely. O Mādhava, listen attentively while I speak.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 17. O Madhusūdana, I will now explain that to you precisely. O Mādhava, listen attentively while I speak.

sanskrit: कश्चिद्विप्रस्तपोयुक्तः काश्यपो धर्मवित्तमः ।आससाद द्विजं कंचिद्धर्माणामागतागमम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: kaścidviprastapoyuktaḥ kāśyapo dharmavittamaḥ ,āsasāda dvijaṁ kaṁciddharmāṇāmāgatāgamam.
english: A certain Brahmin (vipra) named Kāśyapa, who was devoted to austerity (tapas) and foremost among those who understood natural law (dharma), approached another Brahmin (dvija) who had perfectly mastered the sacred texts concerning all principles of natural law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 18. A certain Brahmin (vipra) named Kāśyapa, who was devoted to austerity (tapas) and foremost among those who understood natural law (dharma), approached another Brahmin (dvija) who had perfectly mastered the sacred texts concerning all principles of natural law (dharma).

sanskrit: गतागते सुबहुशो ज्ञानविज्ञानपारगम् ।लोकतत्त्वार्थकुशलं ज्ञातारं सुखदुःखयोः ॥१९॥
transliteration: gatāgate subahuśo jñānavijñānapāragam ,lokatattvārthakuśalaṁ jñātāraṁ sukhaduḥkhayoḥ.
english: Who had repeatedly traversed the cycles of birth and death (saṃsāra), was deeply proficient in both theoretical (jñāna) and practical (vijñāna) knowledge, skilled in understanding the true meaning of the principles of the world, and a knower of both pleasure and pain.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 19. Who had repeatedly traversed the cycles of birth and death (saṃsāra), was deeply proficient in both theoretical (jñāna) and practical (vijñāna) knowledge, skilled in understanding the true meaning of the principles of the world, and a knower of both pleasure and pain.

sanskrit: जातीमरणतत्त्वज्ञं कोविदं पुण्यपापयोः ।द्रष्टारमुच्चनीचानां कर्मभिर्देहिनां गतिम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: jātīmaraṇatattvajñaṁ kovidaṁ puṇyapāpayoḥ ,draṣṭāramuccanīcānāṁ karmabhirdehināṁ gatim.
english: One who understood the true nature of birth and death, an expert concerning merit (puṇya) and demerit (pāpa), and who perceived the high and low states (gati) of embodied beings (dehin) according to their actions (karma).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 20. One who understood the true nature of birth and death, an expert concerning merit (puṇya) and demerit (pāpa), and who perceived the high and low states (gati) of embodied beings (dehin) according to their actions (karma).

sanskrit: चरन्तं मुक्तवत्सिद्धं प्रशान्तं संयतेन्द्रियम् ।दीप्यमानं श्रिया ब्राह्म्या क्रममाणं च सर्वशः ॥२१॥
transliteration: carantaṁ muktavatsiddhaṁ praśāntaṁ saṁyatendriyam ,dīpyamānaṁ śriyā brāhmyā kramamāṇaṁ ca sarvaśaḥ.
english: Wandering like a liberated and perfected being, utterly tranquil, with controlled senses, radiant with spiritual splendor, and moving about everywhere.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 21. Wandering like a liberated and perfected being, utterly tranquil, with controlled senses, radiant with spiritual splendor, and moving about everywhere.

sanskrit: अन्तर्धानगतिज्ञं च श्रुत्वा तत्त्वेन काश्यपः ।तथैवान्तर्हितैः सिद्धैर्यान्तं चक्रधरैः सह ॥२२॥
transliteration: antardhānagatijñaṁ ca śrutvā tattvena kāśyapaḥ ,tathaivāntarhitaiḥ siddhairyāntaṁ cakradharaiḥ saha.
english: And Kaśyapa, having truly heard about him  -  who knew the art of disappearing, and who was journeying with perfected beings (siddhas) who had likewise become invisible, accompanied by discus-bearers.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 22. And Kaśyapa, having truly heard about him  -  who knew the art of disappearing, and who was journeying with perfected beings (siddhas) who had likewise become invisible, accompanied by discus-bearers.

sanskrit: संभाषमाणमेकान्ते समासीनं च तैः सह ।यदृच्छया च गच्छन्तमसक्तं पवनं यथा ॥२३॥
transliteration: saṁbhāṣamāṇamekānte samāsīnaṁ ca taiḥ saha ,yadṛcchayā ca gacchantamasaktaṁ pavanaṁ yathā.
english: Also, conversing in private and seated with them (the perfected beings), and moving freely, unattached like the wind.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 23. Also, conversing in private and seated with them (the perfected beings), and moving freely, unattached like the wind.

sanskrit: तं समासाद्य मेधावी स तदा द्विजसत्तमः ।चरणौ धर्मकामो वै तपस्वी सुसमाहितः ।प्रतिपेदे यथान्यायं भक्त्या परमया युतः ॥२४॥
transliteration: taṁ samāsādya medhāvī sa tadā dvijasattamaḥ ,caraṇau dharmakāmo vai tapasvī susamāhitaḥ ,pratipede yathānyāyaṁ bhaktyā paramayā yutaḥ.
english: Having approached him, then that wise Kaśyapa, the foremost among brahmins, being desirous of righteous conduct (dharma), an ascetic (tapasvī) and very well-composed, properly venerated his feet, endowed with supreme devotion (bhakti).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 24. Having approached him, then that wise Kaśyapa, the foremost among brahmins, being desirous of righteous conduct (dharma), an ascetic (tapasvī) and very well-composed, properly venerated his feet, endowed with supreme devotion (bhakti).

sanskrit: विस्मितश्चाद्भुतं दृष्ट्वा काश्यपस्तं द्विजोत्तमम् ।परिचारेण महता गुरुं वैद्यमतोषयत् ॥२५॥
transliteration: vismitaścādbhutaṁ dṛṣṭvā kāśyapastaṁ dvijottamam ,paricāreṇa mahatā guruṁ vaidyamatoṣayat.
english: Kashyapa, astonished upon seeing that amazing best of the twice-born, who was his teacher (guru) and physician, pleased him with great service.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 25. Kashyapa, astonished upon seeing that amazing best of the twice-born, who was his teacher (guru) and physician, pleased him with great service.

sanskrit: प्रीतात्मा चोपपन्नश्च श्रुतचारित्रसंयुतः ।भावेन तोषयच्चैनं गुरुवृत्त्या परंतपः ॥२६॥
transliteration: prītātmā copapannaśca śrutacāritrasaṁyutaḥ ,bhāvena toṣayaccainaṁ guruvṛttyā paraṁtapaḥ.
english: With a pleased spirit (ātman), and endowed with learning and good conduct, he (Kashyapa), O tormentor of foes, pleased him (the teacher, guru) with sincere devotion and proper student-like behavior.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 26. With a pleased spirit (ātman), and endowed with learning and good conduct, he (Kashyapa), O tormentor of foes, pleased him (the teacher, guru) with sincere devotion and proper student-like behavior.

sanskrit: तस्मै तुष्टः स शिष्याय प्रसन्नोऽथाब्रवीद्गुरुः ।सिद्धिं परामभिप्रेक्ष्य शृणु तन्मे जनार्दन ॥२७॥
transliteration: tasmai tuṣṭaḥ sa śiṣyāya prasanno'thābravīdguruḥ ,siddhiṁ parāmabhiprekṣya śṛṇu tanme janārdana.
english: Pleased and gracious towards that disciple, the teacher (guru) then said to him: "O Janardana, intending supreme perfection (siddhi), listen to this from me."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 27. Pleased and gracious towards that disciple, the teacher (guru) then said to him: "O Janardana, intending supreme perfection (siddhi), listen to this from me."

sanskrit: विविधैः कर्मभिस्तात पुण्ययोगैश्च केवलैः ।गच्छन्तीह गतिं मर्त्या देवलोकेऽपि च स्थितिम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: vividhaiḥ karmabhistāta puṇyayogaiśca kevalaiḥ ,gacchantīha gatiṁ martyā devaloke'pi ca sthitim.
english: O dear one, by various actions (karma) and by pure meritorious connections (yoga), mortals attain a certain state in this world, and also a position in the divine realm.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 28. O dear one, by various actions (karma) and by pure meritorious connections (yoga), mortals attain a certain state in this world, and also a position in the divine realm.

sanskrit: न क्वचित्सुखमत्यन्तं न क्वचिच्छाश्वती स्थितिः ।स्थानाच्च महतो भ्रंशो दुःखलब्धात्पुनः पुनः ॥२९॥
transliteration: na kvacitsukhamatyantaṁ na kvacicchāśvatī sthitiḥ ,sthānācca mahato bhraṁśo duḥkhalabdhātpunaḥ punaḥ.
english: There is never absolute happiness anywhere, nor a permanent state of being. And there is a repeated falling from a great position, even one attained with difficulty.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 29. There is never absolute happiness anywhere, nor a permanent state of being. And there is a repeated falling from a great position, even one attained with difficulty.

sanskrit: अशुभा गतयः प्राप्ताः कष्टा मे पापसेवनात् ।काममन्युपरीतेन तृष्णया मोहितेन च ॥३०॥
transliteration: aśubhā gatayaḥ prāptāḥ kaṣṭā me pāpasevanāt ,kāmamanyuparītena tṛṣṇayā mohitena ca.
english: Because of my indulgence in sinful acts, I have experienced inauspicious and painful states of existence (gati), driven by desire, anger, and delusion (moha) due to craving.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 30. Because of my indulgence in sinful acts, I have experienced inauspicious and painful states of existence (gati), driven by desire, anger, and delusion (moha) due to craving.

sanskrit: पुनः पुनश्च मरणं जन्म चैव पुनः पुनः ।आहारा विविधा भुक्ताः पीता नानाविधाः स्तनाः ॥३१॥
transliteration: punaḥ punaśca maraṇaṁ janma caiva punaḥ punaḥ ,āhārā vividhā bhuktāḥ pītā nānāvidhāḥ stanāḥ.
english: Repeatedly there has been death and repeatedly rebirth (saṃsāra). Various kinds of foods have been eaten, and breasts of many kinds have been suckled.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 31. Repeatedly there has been death and repeatedly rebirth (saṃsāra). Various kinds of foods have been eaten, and breasts of many kinds have been suckled.

sanskrit: मातरो विविधा दृष्टाः पितरश्च पृथग्विधाः ।सुखानि च विचित्राणि दुःखानि च मयानघ ॥३२॥
transliteration: mātaro vividhā dṛṣṭāḥ pitaraśca pṛthagvidhāḥ ,sukhāni ca vicitrāṇi duḥkhāni ca mayānagha.
english: O blameless one (anagha), various mothers have been seen, and fathers of different kinds. And by me, diverse joys and sorrows have been experienced.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 32. O blameless one (anagha), various mothers have been seen, and fathers of different kinds. And by me, diverse joys and sorrows have been experienced.

sanskrit: प्रियैर्विवासो बहुशः संवासश्चाप्रियैः सह ।धननाशश्च संप्राप्तो लब्ध्वा दुःखेन तद्धनम् ॥३३॥
transliteration: priyairvivāso bahuśaḥ saṁvāsaścāpriyaiḥ saha ,dhananāśaśca saṁprāpto labdhvā duḥkhena taddhanam.
english: Frequent separation from dear ones and association with the unpleasant, as well as the loss of wealth that was acquired with great difficulty, have been experienced.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 33. Frequent separation from dear ones and association with the unpleasant, as well as the loss of wealth that was acquired with great difficulty, have been experienced.

sanskrit: अवमानाः सुकष्टाश्च परतः स्वजनात्तथा ।शारीरा मानसाश्चापि वेदना भृशदारुणाः ॥३४॥
transliteration: avamānāḥ sukaṣṭāśca parataḥ svajanāttathā ,śārīrā mānasāścāpi vedanā bhṛśadāruṇāḥ.
english: Furthermore, very painful insults from others and likewise from one's own relatives, and also extremely dreadful physical and mental sufferings (vedanā) [have been encountered].
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 34. Furthermore, very painful insults from others and likewise from one's own relatives, and also extremely dreadful physical and mental sufferings (vedanā) [have been encountered].

sanskrit: प्राप्ता विमाननाश्चोग्रा वधबन्धाश्च दारुणाः ।पतनं निरये चैव यातनाश्च यमक्षये ॥३५॥
transliteration: prāptā vimānanāścogrā vadhabandhāśca dāruṇāḥ ,patanaṁ niraye caiva yātanāśca yamakṣaye.
english: Fierce humiliations and dreadful death sentences and imprisonment have been encountered, and also a fall into hell (niraya) and torments in Yama's abode (yamakṣaya).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 35. Fierce humiliations and dreadful death sentences and imprisonment have been encountered, and also a fall into hell (niraya) and torments in Yama's abode (yamakṣaya).

sanskrit: जरा रोगाश्च सततं वासनानि च भूरिशः ।लोकेऽस्मिन्ननुभूतानि द्वंद्वजानि भृशं मया ॥३६॥
transliteration: jarā rogāśca satataṁ vāsanāni ca bhūriśaḥ ,loke'sminnanubhūtāni dvaṁdvajāni bhṛśaṁ mayā.
english: By me, in this world, old age, diseases, and countless latent tendencies (vāsanā), all born of the pairs of opposites (dvandvaja), have been constantly and intensely experienced.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 36. By me, in this world, old age, diseases, and countless latent tendencies (vāsanā), all born of the pairs of opposites (dvandvaja), have been constantly and intensely experienced.

sanskrit: ततः कदाचिन्निर्वेदान्निकारान्निकृतेन च ।लोकतन्त्रं परित्यक्तं दुःखार्तेन भृशं मया ।ततः सिद्धिरियं प्राप्ता प्रसादादात्मनो मया ॥३७॥
transliteration: tataḥ kadācinnirvedānnikārānnikṛtena ca ,lokatantraṁ parityaktaṁ duḥkhārtena bhṛśaṁ mayā ,tataḥ siddhiriyaṁ prāptā prasādādātmano mayā.
english: Then, at some point, due to dejection and various humiliations, and being greatly afflicted by sorrow, I abandoned the worldly system. Consequently, this spiritual accomplishment (siddhi) was attained by me through the grace of the self (ātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 37. Then, at some point, due to dejection and various humiliations, and being greatly afflicted by sorrow, I abandoned the worldly system. Consequently, this spiritual accomplishment (siddhi) was attained by me through the grace of the self (ātman).

sanskrit: नाहं पुनरिहागन्ता लोकानालोकयाम्यहम् ।आ सिद्धेरा प्रजासर्गादात्मनो मे गतिः शुभा ॥३८॥
transliteration: nāhaṁ punarihāgantā lokānālokayāmyaham ,ā siddherā prajāsargādātmano me gatiḥ śubhā.
english: I will not return here again. I observe the worlds. From the creation of beings (prajāsarga) until final spiritual attainment (siddhi), my auspicious destiny concerning the self (ātman) is assured.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 38. I will not return here again. I observe the worlds. From the creation of beings (prajāsarga) until final spiritual attainment (siddhi), my auspicious destiny concerning the self (ātman) is assured.

sanskrit: उपलब्धा द्विजश्रेष्ठ तथेयं सिद्धिरुत्तमा ।इतः परं गमिष्यामि ततः परतरं पुनः ।ब्रह्मणः पदमव्यग्रं मा ते भूदत्र संशयः ॥३९॥
transliteration: upalabdhā dvijaśreṣṭha tatheyaṁ siddhiruttamā ,itaḥ paraṁ gamiṣyāmi tataḥ parataraṁ punaḥ ,brahmaṇaḥ padamavyagraṁ mā te bhūdatra saṁśayaḥ.
english: O best among Brahmins, this excellent spiritual attainment (siddhi) has thus been achieved. From here I shall go further, and then even beyond that, to the unwavering state of Brahman (brahman). May you have no doubt about this.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 39. O best among Brahmins, this excellent spiritual attainment (siddhi) has thus been achieved. From here I shall go further, and then even beyond that, to the unwavering state of Brahman (brahman). May you have no doubt about this.

sanskrit: नाहं पुनरिहागन्ता मर्त्यलोकं परंतप ।प्रीतोऽस्मि ते महाप्राज्ञ ब्रूहि किं करवाणि ते ॥४०॥
transliteration: nāhaṁ punarihāgantā martyalokaṁ paraṁtapa ,prīto'smi te mahāprājña brūhi kiṁ karavāṇi te.
english: I will not return to this mortal world (martyaloka) again, O scorcher of foes (paraṃtapa). I am pleased with you, O greatly wise one (mahāprājña). Tell me, what may I do for you?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 40. I will not return to this mortal world (martyaloka) again, O scorcher of foes (paraṃtapa). I am pleased with you, O greatly wise one (mahāprājña). Tell me, what may I do for you?

sanskrit: यदीप्सुरुपपन्नस्त्वं तस्य कालोऽयमागतः ।अभिजाने च तदहं यदर्थं मा त्वमागतः ।अचिरात्तु गमिष्यामि येनाहं त्वामचूचुदम् ॥४१॥
transliteration: yadīpsurupapannastvaṁ tasya kālo'yamāgataḥ ,abhijāne ca tadahaṁ yadarthaṁ mā tvamāgataḥ ,acirāttu gamiṣyāmi yenāhaṁ tvāmacūcudam.
english: Whatever you seek, for that you have approached me, and its time has now arrived. I know well the purpose for which you have come to me. Indeed, I shall soon depart, and that is precisely why I have urged you (to approach me).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 41. Whatever you seek, for that you have approached me, and its time has now arrived. I know well the purpose for which you have come to me. Indeed, I shall soon depart, and that is precisely why I have urged you (to approach me).

sanskrit: भृशं प्रीतोऽस्मि भवतश्चारित्रेण विचक्षण ।परिपृच्छ यावद्भवते भाषेयं यत्तवेप्सितम् ॥४२॥
transliteration: bhṛśaṁ prīto'smi bhavataścāritreṇa vicakṣaṇa ,paripṛccha yāvadbhavate bhāṣeyaṁ yattavepsitam.
english: O wise one, I am greatly pleased by your character. Ask whatever you wish, and I will explain to you that which you desire.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 42. O wise one, I am greatly pleased by your character. Ask whatever you wish, and I will explain to you that which you desire.

sanskrit: बहु मन्ये च ते बुद्धिं भृशं संपूजयामि च ।येनाहं भवता बुद्धो मेधावी ह्यसि काश्यप ॥४३॥
transliteration: bahu manye ca te buddhiṁ bhṛśaṁ saṁpūjayāmi ca ,yenāhaṁ bhavatā buddho medhāvī hyasi kāśyapa.
english: And I greatly esteem your intellect, and I honor you immensely. For I have been understood by you; indeed, you are intelligent, O Kaśyapa.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 16 Verse 43. And I greatly esteem your intellect, and I honor you immensely. For I have been understood by you; indeed, you are intelligent, O Kaśyapa.

sanskrit: वासुदेव उवाच ।ततस्तस्योपसंगृह्य पादौ प्रश्नान्सुदुर्वचान् ।पप्रच्छ तांश्च सर्वान्स प्राह धर्मभृतां वरः ॥१॥
transliteration: vāsudeva uvāca ,tatastasyopasaṁgṛhya pādau praśnānsudurvacān ,papraccha tāṁśca sarvānsa prāha dharmabhṛtāṁ varaḥ.
english: Vāsudeva spoke: Then, reverently touching his feet, he (Vāsudeva) asked him very difficult questions. And to all of them, the supreme upholder of natural law (dharma) responded.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 1. Vāsudeva spoke: Then, reverently touching his feet, he (Vāsudeva) asked him very difficult questions. And to all of them, the supreme upholder of natural law (dharma) responded.

sanskrit: काश्यप उवाच ।कथं शरीरं च्यवते कथं चैवोपपद्यते ।कथं कष्टाच्च संसारात्संसरन्परिमुच्यते ॥२॥
transliteration: kāśyapa uvāca ,kathaṁ śarīraṁ cyavate kathaṁ caivopapadyate ,kathaṁ kaṣṭācca saṁsārātsaṁsaranparimucyate.
english: Kaśyapa said, "How does the body perish, and how does it come into being again? How does a person become liberated while transmigrating through the painful cycle of repeated births and deaths (saṃsāra)?"
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 2. Kaśyapa said, "How does the body perish, and how does it come into being again? How does a person become liberated while transmigrating through the painful cycle of repeated births and deaths (saṃsāra)?"

sanskrit: आत्मानं वा कथं युक्त्वा तच्छरीरं विमुञ्चति ।शरीरतश्च निर्मुक्तः कथमन्यत्प्रपद्यते ॥३॥
transliteration: ātmānaṁ vā kathaṁ yuktvā taccharīraṁ vimuñcati ,śarīrataśca nirmuktaḥ kathamanyatprapadyate.
english: Or how, by focusing the self (ātman), does one relinquish that body? And how, when freed from the body, does one obtain another?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 3. Or how, by focusing the self (ātman), does one relinquish that body? And how, when freed from the body, does one obtain another?

sanskrit: कथं शुभाशुभे चायं कर्मणी स्वकृते नरः ।उपभुङ्क्ते क्व वा कर्म विदेहस्योपतिष्ठति ॥४॥
transliteration: kathaṁ śubhāśubhe cāyaṁ karmaṇī svakṛte naraḥ ,upabhuṅkte kva vā karma videhasyopatiṣṭhati.
english: How does this person (nara) experience the good and bad actions (karma) that he himself has performed? Or where does the (effect of) karma remain for a disembodied being?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 4. How does this person (nara) experience the good and bad actions (karma) that he himself has performed? Or where does the (effect of) karma remain for a disembodied being?

sanskrit: ब्राह्मण उवाच ।एवं संचोदितः सिद्धः प्रश्नांस्तान्प्रत्यभाषत ।आनुपूर्व्येण वार्ष्णेय यथा तन्मे वचः शृणु ॥५॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇa uvāca ,evaṁ saṁcoditaḥ siddhaḥ praśnāṁstānpratyabhāṣata ,ānupūrvyeṇa vārṣṇeya yathā tanme vacaḥ śṛṇu.
english: The Brahmin said, "Thus questioned, the accomplished sage (siddha) answered those questions. O Vārṣṇeya, listen to my words as I relate them, in due order."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 5. The Brahmin said, "Thus questioned, the accomplished sage (siddha) answered those questions. O Vārṣṇeya, listen to my words as I relate them, in due order."

sanskrit: सिद्ध उवाच ।आयुःकीर्तिकराणीह यानि कर्माणि सेवते ।शरीरग्रहणेऽन्यस्मिंस्तेषु क्षीणेषु सर्वशः ॥६॥
transliteration: siddha uvāca ,āyuḥkīrtikarāṇīha yāni karmāṇi sevate ,śarīragrahaṇe'nyasmiṁsteṣu kṣīṇeṣu sarvaśaḥ.
english: The perfected being said: "When those actions (karma) that bring longevity and fame in this world are completely exhausted in another embodiment..."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 6. The perfected being said: "When those actions (karma) that bring longevity and fame in this world are completely exhausted in another embodiment..."

sanskrit: आयुःक्षयपरीतात्मा विपरीतानि सेवते ।बुद्धिर्व्यावर्तते चास्य विनाशे प्रत्युपस्थिते ॥७॥
transliteration: āyuḥkṣayaparītātmā viparītāni sevate ,buddhirvyāvartate cāsya vināśe pratyupasthite.
english: When one's self (ātman) is afflicted by the diminution of life, he engages in adverse actions. And his intellect (buddhi) deviates when destruction is imminent.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 7. When one's self (ātman) is afflicted by the diminution of life, he engages in adverse actions. And his intellect (buddhi) deviates when destruction is imminent.

sanskrit: सत्त्वं बलं च कालं चाप्यविदित्वात्मनस्तथा ।अतिवेलमुपाश्नाति तैर्विरुद्धान्यनात्मवान् ॥८॥
transliteration: sattvaṁ balaṁ ca kālaṁ cāpyaviditvātmanastathā ,ativelamupāśnāti tairviruddhānyanātmavān.
english: An unwise person (anātmavān), without knowing his own vitality (sattva), strength, or even the appropriate time, excessively consumes things adverse to them.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 8. An unwise person (anātmavān), without knowing his own vitality (sattva), strength, or even the appropriate time, excessively consumes things adverse to them.

sanskrit: यदायमतिकष्टानि सर्वाण्युपनिषेवते ।अत्यर्थमपि वा भुङ्क्ते न वा भुङ्क्ते कदाचन ॥९॥
transliteration: yadāyamatikaṣṭāni sarvāṇyupaniṣevate ,atyarthamapi vā bhuṅkte na vā bhuṅkte kadācana.
english: When this person continually engages in all extremely distressing actions, or eats excessively, or does not eat at all.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 9. When this person continually engages in all extremely distressing actions, or eats excessively, or does not eat at all.

sanskrit: दुष्टान्नं विषमान्नं च सोऽन्योन्येन विरोधि च ।गुरु वापि समं भुङ्क्ते नातिजीर्णेऽपि वा पुनः ॥१०॥
transliteration: duṣṭānnaṁ viṣamānnaṁ ca so'nyonyena virodhi ca ,guru vāpi samaṁ bhuṅkte nātijīrṇe'pi vā punaḥ.
english: One consumes spoiled food, or incompatible food that is mutually contradictory. They also eat heavy food, or even proper food, without the previous meal being fully digested, or they eat again too soon.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 10. One consumes spoiled food, or incompatible food that is mutually contradictory. They also eat heavy food, or even proper food, without the previous meal being fully digested, or they eat again too soon.

sanskrit: व्यायाममतिमात्रं वा व्यवायं चोपसेवते ।सततं कर्मलोभाद्वा प्राप्तं वेगविधारणम् ॥११॥
transliteration: vyāyāmamatimātraṁ vā vyavāyaṁ copasevate ,satataṁ karmalobhādvā prāptaṁ vegavidhāraṇam.
english: One indulges excessively in physical exercise or sexual activity. Or, constantly driven by an excessive desire for work (karma), one suppresses natural urges that have arisen.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 11. One indulges excessively in physical exercise or sexual activity. Or, constantly driven by an excessive desire for work (karma), one suppresses natural urges that have arisen.

sanskrit: रसातियुक्तमन्नं वा दिवास्वप्नं निषेवते ।अपक्वानागते काले स्वयं दोषान्प्रकोपयन् ॥१२॥
transliteration: rasātiyuktamannaṁ vā divāsvapnaṁ niṣevate ,apakvānāgate kāle svayaṁ doṣānprakopayan.
english: One consumes food excessively rich in tastes (rasa) or indulges in daytime sleep. By doing so, when there is an undigested state and an untimely moment, they themselves aggravate the bodily humors (doṣa).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 12. One consumes food excessively rich in tastes (rasa) or indulges in daytime sleep. By doing so, when there is an undigested state and an untimely moment, they themselves aggravate the bodily humors (doṣa).

sanskrit: स्वदोषकोपनाद्रोगं लभते मरणान्तिकम् ।अथ चोद्बन्धनादीनि परीतानि व्यवस्यति ॥१३॥
transliteration: svadoṣakopanādrogaṁ labhate maraṇāntikam ,atha codbandhanādīni parītāni vyavasyati.
english: From the aggravation of one's own bodily humors (doṣa), one contracts a fatal disease. And then, overwhelmed by such suffering, one undertakes suicidal actions like hanging (udbandhana) and the like.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 13. From the aggravation of one's own bodily humors (doṣa), one contracts a fatal disease. And then, overwhelmed by such suffering, one undertakes suicidal actions like hanging (udbandhana) and the like.

sanskrit: तस्य तैः कारणैर्जन्तोः शरीराच्च्यवते यथा ।जीवितं प्रोच्यमानं तद्यथावदुपधारय ॥१४॥
transliteration: tasya taiḥ kāraṇairjantoḥ śarīrāccyavate yathā ,jīvitaṁ procyamānaṁ tadyathāvadupadhāraya.
english: Understand properly, as it is being explained, how the life of a being departs from the body due to those reasons.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 14. Understand properly, as it is being explained, how the life of a being departs from the body due to those reasons.

sanskrit: ऊष्मा प्रकुपितः काये तीव्रवायुसमीरितः ।शरीरमनुपर्येति सर्वान्प्राणान्रुणद्धि वै ॥१५॥
transliteration: ūṣmā prakupitaḥ kāye tīvravāyusamīritaḥ ,śarīramanuparyeti sarvānprāṇānruṇaddhi vai.
english: The vital heat, greatly agitated within the body and impelled by strong air (vāyu), pervades the entire body and indeed obstructs all the vital breaths (prāṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 15. The vital heat, greatly agitated within the body and impelled by strong air (vāyu), pervades the entire body and indeed obstructs all the vital breaths (prāṇa).

sanskrit: अत्यर्थं बलवानूष्मा शरीरे परिकोपितः ।भिनत्ति जीवस्थानानि तानि मर्माणि विद्धि च ॥१६॥
transliteration: atyarthaṁ balavānūṣmā śarīre parikopitaḥ ,bhinatti jīvasthānāni tāni marmāṇi viddhi ca.
english: The exceedingly powerful vital heat, intensely inflamed in the body, breaks the vital centers. And know those to be the vulnerable points (marmāṇi).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 16. The exceedingly powerful vital heat, intensely inflamed in the body, breaks the vital centers. And know those to be the vulnerable points (marmāṇi).

sanskrit: ततः सवेदनः सद्यो जीवः प्रच्यवते क्षरन् ।शरीरं त्यजते जन्तुश्छिद्यमानेषु मर्मसु ।वेदनाभिः परीतात्मा तद्विद्धि द्विजसत्तम ॥१७॥
transliteration: tataḥ savedanaḥ sadyo jīvaḥ pracyavate kṣaran ,śarīraṁ tyajate jantuśchidyamāneṣu marmasu ,vedanābhiḥ parītātmā tadviddhi dvijasattama.
english: Then, immediately, the living principle (jīva) departs with pain and decays. When the vital points (marmasu) are being severed, the creature abandons the body, its individual self (ātman) overwhelmed by pains. Know this, O best of the twice-born.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 17. Then, immediately, the living principle (jīva) departs with pain and decays. When the vital points (marmasu) are being severed, the creature abandons the body, its individual self (ātman) overwhelmed by pains. Know this, O best of the twice-born.

sanskrit: जातीमरणसंविग्नाः सततं सर्वजन्तवः ।दृश्यन्ते संत्यजन्तश्च शरीराणि द्विजर्षभ ॥१८॥
transliteration: jātīmaraṇasaṁvignāḥ satataṁ sarvajantavaḥ ,dṛśyante saṁtyajantaśca śarīrāṇi dvijarṣabha.
english: O best of the Brahmins, all living beings are constantly seen to be distressed by birth and death, and to be abandoning their bodies.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 18. O best of the Brahmins, all living beings are constantly seen to be distressed by birth and death, and to be abandoning their bodies.

sanskrit: गर्भसंक्रमणे चापि मर्मणामतिसर्पणे ।तादृशीमेव लभते वेदनां मानवः पुनः ॥१९॥
transliteration: garbhasaṁkramaṇe cāpi marmaṇāmatisarpaṇe ,tādṛśīmeva labhate vedanāṁ mānavaḥ punaḥ.
english: And again, a human being experiences precisely such intense pain during the transition through the womb and the tearing of vital points in the body.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 19. And again, a human being experiences precisely such intense pain during the transition through the womb and the tearing of vital points in the body.

sanskrit: भिन्नसंधिरथ क्लेदमद्भिः स लभते नरः ।यथा पञ्चसु भूतेषु संश्रितत्वं निगच्छति ।शैत्यात्प्रकुपितः काये तीव्रवायुसमीरितः ॥२०॥
transliteration: bhinnasaṁdhiratha kledamadbhiḥ sa labhate naraḥ ,yathā pañcasu bhūteṣu saṁśritatvaṁ nigacchati ,śaityātprakupitaḥ kāye tīvravāyusamīritaḥ.
english: Then, a person (nara) with dislocated joints experiences moisture from water as he attains a state of dissolution into the five great elements (pañca bhūta). Within his body, a fierce wind (vāyu) becomes agitated due to cold.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 20. Then, a person (nara) with dislocated joints experiences moisture from water as he attains a state of dissolution into the five great elements (pañca bhūta). Within his body, a fierce wind (vāyu) becomes agitated due to cold.

sanskrit: यः स पञ्चसु भूतेषु प्राणापाने व्यवस्थितः ।स गच्छत्यूर्ध्वगो वायुः कृच्छ्रान्मुक्त्वा शरीरिणम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: yaḥ sa pañcasu bhūteṣu prāṇāpāne vyavasthitaḥ ,sa gacchatyūrdhvago vāyuḥ kṛcchrānmuktvā śarīriṇam.
english: That very wind (vāyu) which is established as "prāṇa" and "apāna" within the five great elements (pañca bhūta), and which moves upwards, then departs, having painfully left the embodied being (śarīrin).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 21. That very wind (vāyu) which is established as "prāṇa" and "apāna" within the five great elements (pañca bhūta), and which moves upwards, then departs, having painfully left the embodied being (śarīrin).

sanskrit: शरीरं च जहात्येव निरुच्छ्वासश्च दृश्यते ।निरूष्मा स निरुच्छ्वासो निःश्रीको गतचेतनः ॥२२॥
transliteration: śarīraṁ ca jahātyeva nirucchvāsaśca dṛśyate ,nirūṣmā sa nirucchvāso niḥśrīko gatacetanaḥ.
english: The body is indeed abandoned, and one is seen to be without breath. That individual is without warmth, breathless, devoid of vitality, and has lost consciousness.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 22. The body is indeed abandoned, and one is seen to be without breath. That individual is without warmth, breathless, devoid of vitality, and has lost consciousness.

sanskrit: ब्रह्मणा संपरित्यक्तो मृत इत्युच्यते नरः ।स्रोतोभिर्यैर्विजानाति इन्द्रियार्थाञ्शरीरभृत् ।तैरेव न विजानाति प्राणमाहारसंभवम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: brahmaṇā saṁparityakto mṛta ityucyate naraḥ ,srotobhiryairvijānāti indriyārthāñśarīrabhṛt ,taireva na vijānāti prāṇamāhārasaṁbhavam.
english: A person, when entirely forsaken by the ultimate reality (brahman), is declared to be dead. The embodied being (śarīrabhṛt), through the very channels by which it perceives the objects of the senses, does not perceive the vital breath (prāṇa) that originates from food.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 23. A person, when entirely forsaken by the ultimate reality (brahman), is declared to be dead. The embodied being (śarīrabhṛt), through the very channels by which it perceives the objects of the senses, does not perceive the vital breath (prāṇa) that originates from food.

sanskrit: तत्रैव कुरुते काये यः स जीवः सनातनः ।तेषां यद्यद्भवेद्युक्तं संनिपाते क्वचित्क्वचित् ।तत्तन्मर्म विजानीहि शास्त्रदृष्टं हि तत्तथा ॥२४॥
transliteration: tatraiva kurute kāye yaḥ sa jīvaḥ sanātanaḥ ,teṣāṁ yadyadbhavedyuktaṁ saṁnipāte kvacitkvacit ,tattanmarma vijānīhi śāstradṛṣṭaṁ hi tattathā.
english: That which performs actions within the body is indeed the eternal living entity (jīva). Whatever of these (channels/senses) may be connected at various junctures here and there, know that particular point to be a vital point (marma), for it is indeed declared as such in the scriptures.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 24. That which performs actions within the body is indeed the eternal living entity (jīva). Whatever of these (channels/senses) may be connected at various junctures here and there, know that particular point to be a vital point (marma), for it is indeed declared as such in the scriptures.

sanskrit: तेषु मर्मसु भिन्नेषु ततः स समुदीरयन् ।आविश्य हृदयं जन्तोः सत्त्वं चाशु रुणद्धि वै ।ततः स चेतनो जन्तुर्नाभिजानाति किंचन ॥२५॥
transliteration: teṣu marmasu bhinneṣu tataḥ sa samudīrayan ,āviśya hṛdayaṁ jantoḥ sattvaṁ cāśu ruṇaddhi vai ,tataḥ sa cetano janturnābhijānāti kiṁcana.
english: When those vital points (marma) are injured, then that (force), stirring itself up, enters the heart of the living being and quickly obstructs its vital essence. Consequently, that conscious living being perceives nothing at all.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 25. When those vital points (marma) are injured, then that (force), stirring itself up, enters the heart of the living being and quickly obstructs its vital essence. Consequently, that conscious living being perceives nothing at all.

sanskrit: तमसा संवृतज्ञानः संवृतेष्वथ मर्मसु ।स जीवो निरधिष्ठानश्चाव्यते मातरिश्वना ॥२६॥
transliteration: tamasā saṁvṛtajñānaḥ saṁvṛteṣvatha marmasu ,sa jīvo niradhiṣṭhānaścāvyate mātariśvanā.
english: When the individual soul (jīva) has its knowledge obscured by ignorance (tamas) and its vital points are similarly enveloped, it becomes unsupported and is then agitated by the vital breath.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 26. When the individual soul (jīva) has its knowledge obscured by ignorance (tamas) and its vital points are similarly enveloped, it becomes unsupported and is then agitated by the vital breath.

sanskrit: ततः स तं महोच्छ्वासं भृशमुच्छ्वस्य दारुणम् ।निष्क्रामन्कम्पयत्याशु तच्छरीरमचेतनम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa taṁ mahocchvāsaṁ bhṛśamucchvasya dāruṇam ,niṣkrāmankampayatyāśu taccharīramacetanam.
english: Thereupon, having exhaled that terrible, great gasp of breath, the individual soul quickly shakes that unconscious body as it departs.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 27. Thereupon, having exhaled that terrible, great gasp of breath, the individual soul quickly shakes that unconscious body as it departs.

sanskrit: स जीवः प्रच्युतः कायात्कर्मभिः स्वैः समावृतः ।अङ्कितः स्वैः शुभैः पुण्यैः पापैर्वाप्युपपद्यते ॥२८॥
transliteration: sa jīvaḥ pracyutaḥ kāyātkarmabhiḥ svaiḥ samāvṛtaḥ ,aṅkitaḥ svaiḥ śubhaiḥ puṇyaiḥ pāpairvāpyupapadyate.
english: That individual soul (jīva), having departed from the body, enveloped by its own actions (karma) and marked by its own good deeds (puṇya) or sins, then proceeds (to its next existence).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 28. That individual soul (jīva), having departed from the body, enveloped by its own actions (karma) and marked by its own good deeds (puṇya) or sins, then proceeds (to its next existence).

sanskrit: ब्राह्मणा ज्ञानसंपन्ना यथावच्छ्रुतनिश्चयाः ।इतरं कृतपुण्यं वा तं विजानन्ति लक्षणैः ॥२९॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇā jñānasaṁpannā yathāvacchrutaniścayāḥ ,itaraṁ kṛtapuṇyaṁ vā taṁ vijānanti lakṣaṇaiḥ.
english: Brahmins, who are endowed with knowledge and whose convictions are firmly established by scriptural revelation, recognize that soul (jīva) by its characteristics, whether it is one that has accumulated merit (puṇya) or is of another kind.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 29. Brahmins, who are endowed with knowledge and whose convictions are firmly established by scriptural revelation, recognize that soul (jīva) by its characteristics, whether it is one that has accumulated merit (puṇya) or is of another kind.

sanskrit: यथान्धकारे खद्योतं लीयमानं ततस्ततः ।चक्षुष्मन्तः प्रपश्यन्ति तथा तं ज्ञानचक्षुषः ॥३०॥
transliteration: yathāndhakāre khadyotaṁ līyamānaṁ tatastataḥ ,cakṣuṣmantaḥ prapaśyanti tathā taṁ jñānacakṣuṣaḥ.
english: Just as those with physical eyes clearly perceive a firefly disappearing here and there in the darkness, similarly, those who possess the eye of wisdom (jñānacakṣuṣaḥ) perceive that subtle reality.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 30. Just as those with physical eyes clearly perceive a firefly disappearing here and there in the darkness, similarly, those who possess the eye of wisdom (jñānacakṣuṣaḥ) perceive that subtle reality.

sanskrit: पश्यन्त्येवंविधाः सिद्धा जीवं दिव्येन चक्षुषा ।च्यवन्तं जायमानं च योनिं चानुप्रवेशितम् ॥३१॥
transliteration: paśyantyevaṁvidhāḥ siddhā jīvaṁ divyena cakṣuṣā ,cyavantaṁ jāyamānaṁ ca yoniṁ cānupraveśitam.
english: Such perfected beings (siddhāḥ) perceive the individual soul (jīva) with their divine vision, observing it as it departs from one existence, is born again, and enters into various wombs.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 31. Such perfected beings (siddhāḥ) perceive the individual soul (jīva) with their divine vision, observing it as it departs from one existence, is born again, and enters into various wombs.

sanskrit: तस्य स्थानानि दृष्टानि त्रिविधानीह शास्त्रतः ।कर्मभूमिरियं भूमिर्यत्र तिष्ठन्ति जन्तवः ॥३२॥
transliteration: tasya sthānāni dṛṣṭāni trividhānīha śāstrataḥ ,karmabhūmiriyaṁ bhūmiryatra tiṣṭhanti jantavaḥ.
english: According to the scriptures (śāstra), its three types of abodes (sthānāni) have been observed here. This earth is the realm of action (karma), where all living beings reside.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 32. According to the scriptures (śāstra), its three types of abodes (sthānāni) have been observed here. This earth is the realm of action (karma), where all living beings reside.

sanskrit: ततः शुभाशुभं कृत्वा लभन्ते सर्वदेहिनः ।इहैवोच्चावचान्भोगान्प्राप्नुवन्ति स्वकर्मभिः ॥३३॥
transliteration: tataḥ śubhāśubhaṁ kṛtvā labhante sarvadehinaḥ ,ihaivoccāvacānbhogānprāpnuvanti svakarmabhiḥ.
english: Consequently, having performed both meritorious and demeritorious actions (karma), all embodied beings (sarvadehinaḥ) attain various (high and low) experiences (bhogān) right here in this world through their own deeds.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 33. Consequently, having performed both meritorious and demeritorious actions (karma), all embodied beings (sarvadehinaḥ) attain various (high and low) experiences (bhogān) right here in this world through their own deeds.

sanskrit: इहैवाशुभकर्मा तु कर्मभिर्निरयं गतः ।अवाक्स निरये पापो मानवः पच्यते भृशम् ।तस्मात्सुदुर्लभो मोक्ष आत्मा रक्ष्यो भृशं ततः ॥३४॥
transliteration: ihaivāśubhakarmā tu karmabhirnirayaṁ gataḥ ,avāksa niraye pāpo mānavaḥ pacyate bhṛśam ,tasmātsudurlabho mokṣa ātmā rakṣyo bhṛśaṁ tataḥ.
english: Indeed, a person whose deeds are unrighteous (aśubhakarma) goes to hell through their actions right here. That sinful human is intensely tormented, head downward, in hell. Therefore, liberation (mokṣa) is extremely difficult to achieve, and thus one's self (ātman) must be intensely protected.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 34. Indeed, a person whose deeds are unrighteous (aśubhakarma) goes to hell through their actions right here. That sinful human is intensely tormented, head downward, in hell. Therefore, liberation (mokṣa) is extremely difficult to achieve, and thus one's self (ātman) must be intensely protected.

sanskrit: ऊर्ध्वं तु जन्तवो गत्वा येषु स्थानेष्ववस्थिताः ।कीर्त्यमानानि तानीह तत्त्वतः संनिबोध मे ।तच्छ्रुत्वा नैष्ठिकीं बुद्धिं बुध्येथाः कर्मनिश्चयात् ॥३५॥
transliteration: ūrdhvaṁ tu jantavo gatvā yeṣu sthāneṣvavasthitāḥ ,kīrtyamānāni tānīha tattvataḥ saṁnibodha me ,tacchrutvā naiṣṭhikīṁ buddhiṁ budhyethāḥ karmaniścayāt.
english: Now, understand from me truly these states into which living beings go and are situated in higher realms, as they are being described here. Having heard that, you should develop a resolute understanding (buddhi) concerning the firm determination of actions (karma).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 35. Now, understand from me truly these states into which living beings go and are situated in higher realms, as they are being described here. Having heard that, you should develop a resolute understanding (buddhi) concerning the firm determination of actions (karma).

sanskrit: तारारूपाणि सर्वाणि यच्चैतच्चन्द्रमण्डलम् ।यच्च विभ्राजते लोके स्वभासा सूर्यमण्डलम् ।स्थानान्येतानि जानीहि नराणां पुण्यकर्मणाम् ॥३६॥
transliteration: tārārūpāṇi sarvāṇi yaccaitaccandramaṇḍalam ,yacca vibhrājate loke svabhāsā sūryamaṇḍalam ,sthānānyetāni jānīhi narāṇāṁ puṇyakarmaṇām.
english: Know that all the stellar forms, this lunar sphere (candramaṇḍala), and the solar sphere (sūryamaṇḍala) which shines brightly in the world by its own radiance, these are the realms of humans who perform meritorious (puṇyakarma) deeds.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 36. Know that all the stellar forms, this lunar sphere (candramaṇḍala), and the solar sphere (sūryamaṇḍala) which shines brightly in the world by its own radiance, these are the realms of humans who perform meritorious (puṇyakarma) deeds.

sanskrit: कर्मक्षयाच्च ते सर्वे च्यवन्ते वै पुनः पुनः ।तत्रापि च विशेषोऽस्ति दिवि नीचोच्चमध्यमः ॥३७॥
transliteration: karmakṣayācca te sarve cyavante vai punaḥ punaḥ ,tatrāpi ca viśeṣo'sti divi nīcoccamadhyamaḥ.
english: And from the exhaustion of their meritorious actions (karma), all of them certainly fall down again and again. Moreover, even in heaven, there exists a distinction of low, middle, and high positions.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 37. And from the exhaustion of their meritorious actions (karma), all of them certainly fall down again and again. Moreover, even in heaven, there exists a distinction of low, middle, and high positions.

sanskrit: न तत्राप्यस्ति संतोषो दृष्ट्वा दीप्ततरां श्रियम् ।इत्येता गतयः सर्वाः पृथक्त्वे समुदीरिताः ॥३८॥
transliteration: na tatrāpyasti saṁtoṣo dṛṣṭvā dīptatarāṁ śriyam ,ityetā gatayaḥ sarvāḥ pṛthaktve samudīritāḥ.
english: Even upon seeing greater splendor, there is no satisfaction. Thus, all these paths are described as distinct.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 38. Even upon seeing greater splendor, there is no satisfaction. Thus, all these paths are described as distinct.

sanskrit: उपपत्तिं तु गर्भस्य वक्ष्याम्यहमतः परम् ।यथावत्तां निगदतः शृणुष्वावहितो द्विज ॥३९॥
transliteration: upapattiṁ tu garbhasya vakṣyāmyahamataḥ param ,yathāvattāṁ nigadataḥ śṛṇuṣvāvahito dvija.
english: Now, I will explain the origin of the embryo hereafter. Listen attentively, O twice-born (dvija), as I narrate it exactly as it is.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 17 Verse 39. Now, I will explain the origin of the embryo hereafter. Listen attentively, O twice-born (dvija), as I narrate it exactly as it is.

sanskrit: ब्राह्मण उवाच ।शुभानामशुभानां च नेह नाशोऽस्ति कर्मणाम् ।प्राप्य प्राप्य तु पच्यन्ते क्षेत्रं क्षेत्रं तथा तथा ॥१॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇa uvāca ,śubhānāmaśubhānāṁ ca neha nāśo'sti karmaṇām ,prāpya prāpya tu pacyante kṣetraṁ kṣetraṁ tathā tathā.
english: The Brahmin said: In this world, there is no destruction of good or bad actions (karma). Instead, by repeatedly entering various 'fields' (bodies or circumstances), they ripen accordingly.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 1. The Brahmin said: In this world, there is no destruction of good or bad actions (karma). Instead, by repeatedly entering various 'fields' (bodies or circumstances), they ripen accordingly.

sanskrit: यथा प्रसूयमानस्तु फली दद्यात्फलं बहु ।तथा स्याद्विपुलं पुण्यं शुद्धेन मनसा कृतम् ॥२॥
transliteration: yathā prasūyamānastu phalī dadyātphalaṁ bahu ,tathā syādvipulaṁ puṇyaṁ śuddhena manasā kṛtam.
english: Just as a fruit-bearing tree yields abundant fruit when it produces, similarly, great merit (puṇya) results from actions performed with a pure mind.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 2. Just as a fruit-bearing tree yields abundant fruit when it produces, similarly, great merit (puṇya) results from actions performed with a pure mind.

sanskrit: पापं चापि तथैव स्यात्पापेन मनसा कृतम् ।पुरोधाय मनो हीह कर्मण्यात्मा प्रवर्तते ॥३॥
transliteration: pāpaṁ cāpi tathaiva syātpāpena manasā kṛtam ,purodhāya mano hīha karmaṇyātmā pravartate.
english: Sin also occurs similarly when an action is performed with a sinful mind, for it is by focusing the mind that the self (ātman) engages in actions here.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 3. Sin also occurs similarly when an action is performed with a sinful mind, for it is by focusing the mind that the self (ātman) engages in actions here.

sanskrit: यथा कर्मसमादिष्टं काममन्युसमावृतः ।नरो गर्भं प्रविशति तच्चापि शृणु चोत्तरम् ॥४॥
transliteration: yathā karmasamādiṣṭaṁ kāmamanyusamāvṛtaḥ ,naro garbhaṁ praviśati taccāpi śṛṇu cottaram.
english: And hear this explanation of how a person, overwhelmed by desire and anger, enters a womb according to actions (karma) designated by past deeds.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 4. And hear this explanation of how a person, overwhelmed by desire and anger, enters a womb according to actions (karma) designated by past deeds.

sanskrit: शुक्रं शोणितसंसृष्टं स्त्रिया गर्भाशयं गतम् ।क्षेत्रं कर्मजमाप्नोति शुभं वा यदि वाशुभम् ॥५॥
transliteration: śukraṁ śoṇitasaṁsṛṣṭaṁ striyā garbhāśayaṁ gatam ,kṣetraṁ karmajamāpnoti śubhaṁ vā yadi vāśubham.
english: Sperm, mixed with blood and having entered a woman's womb, obtains a body (field) determined by (karma), which may be auspicious or inauspicious.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 5. Sperm, mixed with blood and having entered a woman's womb, obtains a body (field) determined by (karma), which may be auspicious or inauspicious.

sanskrit: सौक्ष्म्यादव्यक्तभावाच्च न स क्वचन सज्जते ।संप्राप्य ब्रह्मणः कायं तस्मात्तद्ब्रह्म शाश्वतम् ।तद्बीजं सर्वभूतानां तेन जीवन्ति जन्तवः ॥६॥
transliteration: saukṣmyādavyaktabhāvācca na sa kvacana sajjate ,saṁprāpya brahmaṇaḥ kāyaṁ tasmāttadbrahma śāśvatam ,tadbījaṁ sarvabhūtānāṁ tena jīvanti jantavaḥ.
english: Because of its subtlety and unmanifest nature, it does not cling to anything. Therefore, having attained the body of brahman (the Absolute), that brahman (the Absolute) is eternal. That is the seed of all beings; through it, all creatures exist.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 6. Because of its subtlety and unmanifest nature, it does not cling to anything. Therefore, having attained the body of brahman (the Absolute), that brahman (the Absolute) is eternal. That is the seed of all beings; through it, all creatures exist.

sanskrit: स जीवः सर्वगात्राणि गर्भस्याविश्य भागशः ।दधाति चेतसा सद्यः प्राणस्थानेष्ववस्थितः ।ततः स्पन्दयतेऽङ्गानि स गर्भश्चेतनान्वितः ॥७॥
transliteration: sa jīvaḥ sarvagātrāṇi garbhasyāviśya bhāgaśaḥ ,dadhāti cetasā sadyaḥ prāṇasthāneṣvavasthitaḥ ,tataḥ spandayate'ṅgāni sa garbhaścetanānvitaḥ.
english: The individual soul (jīva), having entered all the limbs of the embryo in parts, immediately sustains them with consciousness, being established in the vital (prāṇa) centers. Then, that embryo, endowed with consciousness (cetanā), begins to pulsate.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 7. The individual soul (jīva), having entered all the limbs of the embryo in parts, immediately sustains them with consciousness, being established in the vital (prāṇa) centers. Then, that embryo, endowed with consciousness (cetanā), begins to pulsate.

sanskrit: यथा हि लोहनिष्यन्दो निषिक्तो बिम्बविग्रहम् ।उपैति तद्वज्जानीहि गर्भे जीवप्रवेशनम् ॥८॥
transliteration: yathā hi lohaniṣyando niṣikto bimbavigraham ,upaiti tadvajjānīhi garbhe jīvapraveśanam.
english: Just as molten iron, when poured, assumes the form of the mold, similarly understand the entry of the individual soul (jīva) into the embryo.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 8. Just as molten iron, when poured, assumes the form of the mold, similarly understand the entry of the individual soul (jīva) into the embryo.

sanskrit: लोहपिण्डं यथा वह्निः प्रविशत्यभितापयन् ।तथा त्वमपि जानीहि गर्भे जीवोपपादनम् ॥९॥
transliteration: lohapiṇḍaṁ yathā vahniḥ praviśatyabhitāpayan ,tathā tvamapi jānīhi garbhe jīvopapādanam.
english: Just as fire enters a lump of iron, making it intensely hot, similarly you should understand the manifestation of the individual soul (jīva) in the embryo.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 9. Just as fire enters a lump of iron, making it intensely hot, similarly you should understand the manifestation of the individual soul (jīva) in the embryo.

sanskrit: यथा च दीपः शरणं दीप्यमानः प्रकाशयेत् ।एवमेव शरीराणि प्रकाशयति चेतना ॥१०॥
transliteration: yathā ca dīpaḥ śaraṇaṁ dīpyamānaḥ prakāśayet ,evameva śarīrāṇi prakāśayati cetanā.
english: Just as a lamp, shining, illuminates a dwelling, so too does consciousness (cetanā) illuminate the various bodies.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 10. Just as a lamp, shining, illuminates a dwelling, so too does consciousness (cetanā) illuminate the various bodies.

sanskrit: यद्यच्च कुरुते कर्म शुभं वा यदि वाशुभम् ।पूर्वदेहकृतं सर्वमवश्यमुपभुज्यते ॥११॥
transliteration: yadyacca kurute karma śubhaṁ vā yadi vāśubham ,pūrvadehakṛtaṁ sarvamavaśyamupabhujyate.
english: Whatever action (karma) one performs, whether good or bad, all that was done in a previous body is inevitably experienced.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 11. Whatever action (karma) one performs, whether good or bad, all that was done in a previous body is inevitably experienced.

sanskrit: ततस्तत्क्षीयते चैव पुनश्चान्यत्प्रचीयते ।यावत्तन्मोक्षयोगस्थं धर्मं नैवावबुध्यते ॥१२॥
transliteration: tatastatkṣīyate caiva punaścānyatpracīyate ,yāvattanmokṣayogasthaṁ dharmaṁ naivāvabudhyate.
english: After that (karma) is exhausted, other (karma) again accumulates. This cycle continues as long as one does not truly comprehend the natural law (dharma) that leads to the state of liberation (mokṣa).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 12. After that (karma) is exhausted, other (karma) again accumulates. This cycle continues as long as one does not truly comprehend the natural law (dharma) that leads to the state of liberation (mokṣa).

sanskrit: तत्र धर्मं प्रवक्ष्यामि सुखी भवति येन वै ।आवर्तमानो जातीषु तथान्योन्यासु सत्तम ॥१३॥
transliteration: tatra dharmaṁ pravakṣyāmi sukhī bhavati yena vai ,āvartamāno jātīṣu tathānyonyāsu sattama.
english: O best among the virtuous (sattama), in this context, I will declare that natural law (dharma) by which one becomes happy, even while revolving through different existences.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 13. O best among the virtuous (sattama), in this context, I will declare that natural law (dharma) by which one becomes happy, even while revolving through different existences.

sanskrit: दानं व्रतं ब्रह्मचर्यं यथोक्तव्रतधारणम् ।दमः प्रशान्तता चैव भूतानां चानुकम्पनम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: dānaṁ vrataṁ brahmacaryaṁ yathoktavratadhāraṇam ,damaḥ praśāntatā caiva bhūtānāṁ cānukampanam.
english: (This natural law (dharma) includes) charity (dāna), observing vows, celibacy (brahmacarya), undertaking prescribed observances, self-control (dama), and also tranquility, and compassion for all living beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 14. (This natural law (dharma) includes) charity (dāna), observing vows, celibacy (brahmacarya), undertaking prescribed observances, self-control (dama), and also tranquility, and compassion for all living beings.

sanskrit: संयमश्चानृशंस्यं च परस्वादानवर्जनम् ।व्यलीकानामकरणं भूतानां यत्र सा भुवि ॥१५॥
transliteration: saṁyamaścānṛśaṁsyaṁ ca parasvādānavarjanam ,vyalīkānāmakaraṇaṁ bhūtānāṁ yatra sā bhuvi.
english: Where there is self-control, compassion, abstaining from seizing others' property, and refraining from harming any beings - that (virtue) exists on earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 15. Where there is self-control, compassion, abstaining from seizing others' property, and refraining from harming any beings - that (virtue) exists on earth.

sanskrit: मातापित्रोश्च शुश्रूषा देवतातिथिपूजनम् ।गुरुपूजा घृणा शौचं नित्यमिन्द्रियसंयमः ॥१६॥
transliteration: mātāpitrośca śuśrūṣā devatātithipūjanam ,gurupūjā ghṛṇā śaucaṁ nityamindriyasaṁyamaḥ.
english: (Also included are) service to one's mother and father, the worship of deities and guests, reverence for spiritual teachers (guru), compassion, purity, and constant self-control of the senses.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 16. (Also included are) service to one's mother and father, the worship of deities and guests, reverence for spiritual teachers (guru), compassion, purity, and constant self-control of the senses.

sanskrit: प्रवर्तनं शुभानां च तत्सतां वृत्तमुच्यते ।ततो धर्मः प्रभवति यः प्रजाः पाति शाश्वतीः ॥१७॥
transliteration: pravartanaṁ śubhānāṁ ca tatsatāṁ vṛttamucyate ,tato dharmaḥ prabhavati yaḥ prajāḥ pāti śāśvatīḥ.
english: The performance of auspicious actions - this is declared to be the conduct of the virtuous. From this (conduct) arises natural law (dharma), which eternally protects beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 17. The performance of auspicious actions - this is declared to be the conduct of the virtuous. From this (conduct) arises natural law (dharma), which eternally protects beings.

sanskrit: एवं सत्सु सदा पश्येत्तत्र ह्येषा ध्रुवा स्थितिः ।आचारो धर्ममाचष्टे यस्मिन्सन्तो व्यवस्थिताः ॥१८॥
transliteration: evaṁ satsu sadā paśyettatra hyeṣā dhruvā sthitiḥ ,ācāro dharmamācaṣṭe yasminsanto vyavasthitāḥ.
english: Thus, one should always observe (these qualities) among the virtuous; for indeed, in them lies this firm and constant state. Good conduct (ācāra) defines the natural law (dharma) in which the wise (santaḥ) are established.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 18. Thus, one should always observe (these qualities) among the virtuous; for indeed, in them lies this firm and constant state. Good conduct (ācāra) defines the natural law (dharma) in which the wise (santaḥ) are established.

sanskrit: तेषु तद्धर्मनिक्षिप्तं यः स धर्मः सनातनः ।यस्तं समभिपद्येत न स दुर्गतिमाप्नुयात् ॥१९॥
transliteration: teṣu taddharmanikṣiptaṁ yaḥ sa dharmaḥ sanātanaḥ ,yastaṁ samabhipadyeta na sa durgatimāpnuyāt.
english: That eternal natural law (dharma), which is inherent within beings – whoever realizes it will not encounter misfortune.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 19. That eternal natural law (dharma), which is inherent within beings – whoever realizes it will not encounter misfortune.

sanskrit: अतो नियम्यते लोकः प्रमुह्य धर्मवर्त्मसु ।यस्तु योगी च मुक्तश्च स एतेभ्यो विशिष्यते ॥२०॥
transliteration: ato niyamyate lokaḥ pramuhya dharmavartmasu ,yastu yogī ca muktaśca sa etebhyo viśiṣyate.
english: Therefore, humanity is constrained, being bewildered on the paths of natural law (dharma). But the one who is both a practitioner of yoga (yogī) and liberated (mukta), he is superior to these.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 20. Therefore, humanity is constrained, being bewildered on the paths of natural law (dharma). But the one who is both a practitioner of yoga (yogī) and liberated (mukta), he is superior to these.

sanskrit: वर्तमानस्य धर्मेण पुरुषस्य यथा तथा ।संसारतारणं ह्यस्य कालेन महता भवेत् ॥२१॥
transliteration: vartamānasya dharmeṇa puruṣasya yathā tathā ,saṁsāratāraṇaṁ hyasya kālena mahatā bhavet.
english: For a person (puruṣa) living according to natural law (dharma), his liberation (saṃsāra-tāraṇa) from the cycle of rebirth (saṃsāra), in whatever way it may happen, will indeed occur over a long period of time.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 21. For a person (puruṣa) living according to natural law (dharma), his liberation (saṃsāra-tāraṇa) from the cycle of rebirth (saṃsāra), in whatever way it may happen, will indeed occur over a long period of time.

sanskrit: एवं पूर्वकृतं कर्म सर्वो जन्तुर्निषेवते ।सर्वं तत्कारणं येन निकृतोऽयमिहागतः ॥२२॥
transliteration: evaṁ pūrvakṛtaṁ karma sarvo janturniṣevate ,sarvaṁ tatkāraṇaṁ yena nikṛto'yamihāgataḥ.
english: Thus, every living being (jantu) experiences the results of actions (karma) done in the past. All of that is the cause by which this being, having been diminished, has arrived here.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 22. Thus, every living being (jantu) experiences the results of actions (karma) done in the past. All of that is the cause by which this being, having been diminished, has arrived here.

sanskrit: शरीरग्रहणं चास्य केन पूर्वं प्रकल्पितम् ।इत्येवं संशयो लोके तच्च वक्ष्याम्यतः परम् ॥२३॥
