transliteration: śarīragrahaṇaṁ cāsya kena pūrvaṁ prakalpitam ,ityevaṁ saṁśayo loke tacca vakṣyāmyataḥ param.
english: And by whom was the assumption of a body (śarīragrahaṇam) by the individual (ātman) previously ordained? Such a doubt exists among people, and I will explain that hereafter.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 23. And by whom was the assumption of a body (śarīragrahaṇam) by the individual (ātman) previously ordained? Such a doubt exists among people, and I will explain that hereafter.

sanskrit: शरीरमात्मनः कृत्वा सर्वभूतपितामहः ।त्रैलोक्यमसृजद्ब्रह्मा कृत्स्नं स्थावरजङ्गमम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: śarīramātmanaḥ kṛtvā sarvabhūtapitāmahaḥ ,trailokyamasṛjadbrahmā kṛtsnaṁ sthāvarajaṅgamam.
english: Brahmā, the grandfather of all beings, having created a body (śarīra) for himself (ātman), then brought forth the entire three worlds, including all that is stationary and moving.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 24. Brahmā, the grandfather of all beings, having created a body (śarīra) for himself (ātman), then brought forth the entire three worlds, including all that is stationary and moving.

sanskrit: ततः प्रधानमसृजच्चेतना सा शरीरिणाम् ।यया सर्वमिदं व्याप्तं यां लोके परमां विदुः ॥२५॥
transliteration: tataḥ pradhānamasṛjaccetanā sā śarīriṇām ,yayā sarvamidaṁ vyāptaṁ yāṁ loke paramāṁ viduḥ.
english: Then he created the primordial matter (pradhāna) and the sentient principle (cetanā). That (cetanā) belongs to embodied beings, and it is by this (cetanā) that all this (world) is pervaded, and which people consider supreme in the world.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 25. Then he created the primordial matter (pradhāna) and the sentient principle (cetanā). sanskrit: इह तत्क्षरमित्युक्तं परं त्वमृतमक्षरम् ।त्रयाणां मिथुनं सर्वमेकैकस्य पृथक्पृथक् ॥२६॥
transliteration: iha tatkṣaramityuktaṁ paraṁ tvamṛtamakṣaram ,trayāṇāṁ mithunaṁ sarvamekaikasya pṛthakpṛthak.
english: Here, that which is mutable is declared to be "kṣaram" (perishable). But the supreme (principle) is indeed immortal and "akṣaram" (imperishable). The entire aggregate of phenomena is a combination of these three, each existing distinctly.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 26. Here, that which is mutable is declared to be "kṣaram" (perishable). sanskrit: असृजत्सर्वभूतानि पूर्वसृष्टः प्रजापतिः ।स्थावराणि च भूतानि इत्येषा पौर्विकी श्रुतिः ॥२७॥
transliteration: asṛjatsarvabhūtāni pūrvasṛṣṭaḥ prajāpatiḥ ,sthāvarāṇi ca bhūtāni ityeṣā paurvikī śrutiḥ.
english: Prajapati, who was himself previously created, brought forth all beings, including the immobile ones. This is indeed an ancient revealed text (śruti).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 27. Prajapati, who was himself previously created, brought forth all beings, including the immobile ones. This is indeed an ancient revealed text (śruti).

sanskrit: तस्य कालपरीमाणमकरोत्स पितामहः ।भूतेषु परिवृत्तिं च पुनरावृत्तिमेव च ॥२८॥
transliteration: tasya kālaparīmāṇamakarotsa pitāmahaḥ ,bhūteṣu parivṛttiṁ ca punarāvṛttimeva ca.
english: That grandfather (Brahma) determined the measure of its time [the creation's duration] and also the cycles of change and repeated return (saṃsāra) for all beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 28. That grandfather (Brahma) determined the measure of its time [the creation's duration] and also the cycles of change and repeated return (saṃsāra) for all beings.

sanskrit: यथात्र कश्चिन्मेधावी दृष्टात्मा पूर्वजन्मनि ।यत्प्रवक्ष्यामि तत्सर्वं यथावदुपपद्यते ॥२९॥
transliteration: yathātra kaścinmedhāvī dṛṣṭātmā pūrvajanmani ,yatpravakṣyāmi tatsarvaṁ yathāvadupapadyate.
english: Just as a wise individual who has realized the self (ātman) in a previous life (pūrvajanman), so too, whatever I shall declare here, all of it will be truly comprehended.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 29. Just as a wise individual who has realized the self (ātman) in a previous life (pūrvajanman), so too, whatever I shall declare here, all of it will be truly comprehended.

sanskrit: सुखदुःखे सदा सम्यगनित्ये यः प्रपश्यति ।कायं चामेध्यसंघातं विनाशं कर्मसंहितम् ॥३०॥
transliteration: sukhaduḥkhe sadā samyaganitye yaḥ prapaśyati ,kāyaṁ cāmedhyasaṁghātaṁ vināśaṁ karmasaṁhitam.
english: He who always rightly perceives pleasure and pain as impermanent, and the body as an impure aggregate that is destined for destruction and bound by past actions (karma).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 30. He who always rightly perceives pleasure and pain as impermanent, and the body as an impure aggregate that is destined for destruction and bound by past actions (karma).

sanskrit: यच्च किंचित्सुखं तच्च सर्वं दुःखमिति स्मरन् ।संसारसागरं घोरं तरिष्यति सुदुस्तरम् ॥३१॥
transliteration: yacca kiṁcitsukhaṁ tacca sarvaṁ duḥkhamiti smaran ,saṁsārasāgaraṁ ghoraṁ tariṣyati sudustaram.
english: Remembering that whatever little happiness exists, that too is entirely sorrow, one will cross the terrible ocean of cyclic existence (saṃsāra), which is exceedingly difficult to traverse.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 31. Remembering that whatever little happiness exists, that too is entirely sorrow, one will cross the terrible ocean of cyclic existence (saṃsāra), which is exceedingly difficult to traverse.

sanskrit: जातीमरणरोगैश्च समाविष्टः प्रधानवित् ।चेतनावत्सु चैतन्यं समं भूतेषु पश्यति ॥३२॥
transliteration: jātīmaraṇarogaiśca samāviṣṭaḥ pradhānavit ,cetanāvatsu caitanyaṁ samaṁ bhūteṣu paśyati.
english: Even though affected by births, deaths, and diseases, the knower of the fundamental principle (prakṛti) perceives equal consciousness in all sentient beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 32. Even though affected by births, deaths, and diseases, the knower of the fundamental principle (prakṛti) perceives equal consciousness in all sentient beings.

sanskrit: निर्विद्यते ततः कृत्स्नं मार्गमाणः परं पदम् ।तस्योपदेशं वक्ष्यामि याथातथ्येन सत्तम ॥३३॥
transliteration: nirvidyate tataḥ kṛtsnaṁ mārgamāṇaḥ paraṁ padam ,tasyopadeśaṁ vakṣyāmi yāthātathyena sattama.
english: Then, seeking the supreme state, he becomes detached from all of worldly existence. I will now impart his teaching to you, O most excellent one, precisely as it is.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 33. Then, seeking the supreme state, he becomes detached from all of worldly existence. I will now impart his teaching to you, O most excellent one, precisely as it is.

sanskrit: शाश्वतस्याव्ययस्याथ पदस्य ज्ञानमुत्तमम् ।प्रोच्यमानं मया विप्र निबोधेदमशेषतः ॥३४॥
transliteration: śāśvatasyāvyayasyātha padasya jñānamuttamam ,procyamānaṁ mayā vipra nibodhedamaśeṣataḥ.
english: Now, O Brahmin, comprehend this excellent knowledge of the eternal and immutable state, which is being expounded by me completely and without reservation.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 18 Verse 34. Now, O Brahmin, comprehend this excellent knowledge of the eternal and immutable state, which is being expounded by me completely and without reservation.

sanskrit: ब्राह्मण उवाच ।यः स्यादेकायने लीनस्तूष्णीं किंचिदचिन्तयन् ।पूर्वं पूर्वं परित्यज्य स निरारम्भको भवेत् ॥१॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇa uvāca ,yaḥ syādekāyane līnastūṣṇīṁ kiṁcidacintayan ,pūrvaṁ pūrvaṁ parityajya sa nirārambhako bhavet.
english: The Brahmin said: One who remains absorbed in a single path, silently contemplating nothing, abandoning each former thing, such a person becomes free from undertakings (nirārambhaka).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 1. The Brahmin said: One who remains absorbed in a single path, silently contemplating nothing, abandoning each former thing, such a person becomes free from undertakings (nirārambhaka).

sanskrit: सर्वमित्रः सर्वसहः समरक्तो जितेन्द्रियः ।व्यपेतभयमन्युश्च कामहा मुच्यते नरः ॥२॥
transliteration: sarvamitraḥ sarvasahaḥ samarakto jitendriyaḥ ,vyapetabhayamanyuśca kāmahā mucyate naraḥ.
english: A person who is a friend to all, tolerant of all, equally affectionate to all, has conquered their senses, is free from fear and anger, and has overcome desires, such a person is liberated (mokṣa).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 2. A person who is a friend to all, tolerant of all, equally affectionate to all, has conquered their senses, is free from fear and anger, and has overcome desires, such a person is liberated (mokṣa).

sanskrit: आत्मवत्सर्वभूतेषु यश्चरेन्नियतः शुचिः ।अमानी निरभीमानः सर्वतो मुक्त एव सः ॥३॥
transliteration: ātmavatsarvabhūteṣu yaścarenniyataḥ śuciḥ ,amānī nirabhīmānaḥ sarvato mukta eva saḥ.
english: The one who, pure and self-controlled, behaves towards all beings as they would towards their own self (ātman), such a person is devoid of pride, free from ego (abhimāna), and truly liberated from all bonds.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 3. The one who, pure and self-controlled, behaves towards all beings as they would towards their own self (ātman), such a person is devoid of pride, free from ego (abhimāna), and truly liberated from all bonds.

sanskrit: जीवितं मरणं चोभे सुखदुःखे तथैव च ।लाभालाभे प्रियद्वेष्ये यः समः स च मुच्यते ॥४॥
transliteration: jīvitaṁ maraṇaṁ cobhe sukhaduḥkhe tathaiva ca ,lābhālābhe priyadveṣye yaḥ samaḥ sa ca mucyate.
english: One who remains balanced (sama) regarding both life and death, happiness and sorrow, gain and loss, and beloved and hateful things, such a person is liberated.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 4. One who remains balanced (sama) regarding both life and death, happiness and sorrow, gain and loss, and beloved and hateful things, such a person is liberated.

sanskrit: न कस्यचित्स्पृहयते नावजानाति किंचन ।निर्द्वंद्वो वीतरागात्मा सर्वतो मुक्त एव सः ॥५॥
transliteration: na kasyacitspṛhayate nāvajānāti kiṁcana ,nirdvaṁdvo vītarāgātmā sarvato mukta eva saḥ.
english: He desires no one and despises nothing. Free from dualities, with a self devoid of passion, he is indeed completely liberated.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 5. He desires no one and despises nothing. Free from dualities, with a self devoid of passion, he is indeed completely liberated.

sanskrit: अनमित्रोऽथ निर्बन्धुरनपत्यश्च यः क्वचित् ।त्यक्तधर्मार्थकामश्च निराकाङ्क्षी स मुच्यते ॥६॥
transliteration: anamitro'tha nirbandhuranapatyaśca yaḥ kvacit ,tyaktadharmārthakāmaśca nirākāṅkṣī sa mucyate.
english: If one is ever without enemies, relatives, or offspring, and has abandoned the pursuits of natural law (dharma), material wealth (artha), and sensual desires (kāma), and is free from all longing, he is liberated.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 6. If one is ever without enemies, relatives, or offspring, and has abandoned the pursuits of natural law (dharma), material wealth (artha), and sensual desires (kāma), and is free from all longing, he is liberated.

sanskrit: नैव धर्मी न चाधर्मी पूर्वोपचितहा च यः ।धातुक्षयप्रशान्तात्मा निर्द्वंद्वः स विमुच्यते ॥७॥
transliteration: naiva dharmī na cādharmī pūrvopacitahā ca yaḥ ,dhātukṣayapraśāntātmā nirdvaṁdvaḥ sa vimucyate.
english: He who is neither righteous nor unrighteous, and has relinquished the accumulated effects of past actions (karma), whose self is profoundly peaceful due to the waning of the constituent elements (of the body and mind), and who is free from dualities, he is completely liberated.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 7. He who is neither righteous nor unrighteous, and has relinquished the accumulated effects of past actions (karma), whose self is profoundly peaceful due to the waning of the constituent elements (of the body and mind), and who is free from dualities, he is completely liberated.

sanskrit: अकर्मा चाविकाङ्क्षश्च पश्यञ्जगदशाश्वतम् ।अस्वस्थमवशं नित्यं जन्मसंसारमोहितम् ॥८॥
transliteration: akarmā cāvikāṅkṣaśca paśyañjagadaśāśvatam ,asvasthamavaśaṁ nityaṁ janmasaṁsāramohitam.
english: And who is free from action (karma) and desire, seeing the world as impermanent, unhealthy, helpless, and constantly deluded by birth and the cycle of transmigration (saṃsāra).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 8. And who is free from action (karma) and desire, seeing the world as impermanent, unhealthy, helpless, and constantly deluded by birth and the cycle of transmigration (saṃsāra).

sanskrit: वैराग्यबुद्धिः सततं तापदोषव्यपेक्षकः ।आत्मबन्धविनिर्मोक्षं स करोत्यचिरादिव ॥९॥
transliteration: vairāgyabuddhiḥ satataṁ tāpadoṣavyapekṣakaḥ ,ātmabandhavinirmokṣaṁ sa karotyacirādiva.
english: He who constantly possesses an intellect of dispassion and perceives the flaws inherent in worldly afflictions quickly achieves liberation (mokṣa) from the bondage of the self (ātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 9. He who constantly possesses an intellect of dispassion and perceives the flaws inherent in worldly afflictions quickly achieves liberation (mokṣa) from the bondage of the self (ātman).

sanskrit: अगन्धरसमस्पर्शमशब्दमपरिग्रहम् ।अरूपमनभिज्ञेयं दृष्ट्वात्मानं विमुच्यते ॥१०॥
transliteration: agandharasamasparśamaśabdamaparigraham ,arūpamanabhijñeyaṁ dṛṣṭvātmānaṁ vimucyate.
english: Having realized the self (ātman) as devoid of smell, taste, or touch, without sound, without possessions, formless, and ultimately unknowable, one becomes liberated (mokṣa).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 10. Having realized the self (ātman) as devoid of smell, taste, or touch, without sound, without possessions, formless, and ultimately unknowable, one becomes liberated (mokṣa).

sanskrit: पञ्चभूतगुणैर्हीनममूर्तिमदलेपकम् ।अगुणं गुणभोक्तारं यः पश्यति स मुच्यते ॥११॥
transliteration: pañcabhūtaguṇairhīnamamūrtimadalepakam ,aguṇaṁ guṇabhoktāraṁ yaḥ paśyati sa mucyate.
english: He who perceives the self (ātman) as devoid of the qualities of the five great elements, formless, untainted, without its own intrinsic material qualities (guṇas), yet the experiencer of all qualities, he becomes liberated (mokṣa).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 11. He who perceives the self (ātman) as devoid of the qualities of the five great elements, formless, untainted, without its own intrinsic material qualities (guṇas), yet the experiencer of all qualities, he becomes liberated (mokṣa).

sanskrit: विहाय सर्वसंकल्पान्बुद्ध्या शारीरमानसान् ।शनैर्निर्वाणमाप्नोति निरिन्धन इवानलः ॥१२॥
transliteration: vihāya sarvasaṁkalpānbuddhyā śārīramānasān ,śanairnirvāṇamāpnoti nirindhana ivānalaḥ.
english: Having abandoned all bodily and mental intentions through the intellect, one gradually attains complete tranquility (nirvāṇa), just like a fire that has run out of fuel.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 12. Having abandoned all bodily and mental intentions through the intellect, one gradually attains complete tranquility (nirvāṇa), just like a fire that has run out of fuel.

sanskrit: विमुक्तः सर्वसंस्कारैस्ततो ब्रह्म सनातनम् ।परमाप्नोति संशान्तमचलं दिव्यमक्षरम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: vimuktaḥ sarvasaṁskāraistato brahma sanātanam ,paramāpnoti saṁśāntamacalaṁ divyamakṣaram.
english: Freed from all conditionings (saṃskāra), one then attains the eternal, utterly peaceful, unwavering, divine, imperishable absolute (brahman).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 13. Freed from all conditionings (saṃskāra), one then attains the eternal, utterly peaceful, unwavering, divine, imperishable absolute (brahman).

sanskrit: अतः परं प्रवक्ष्यामि योगशास्त्रमनुत्तमम् ।यज्ज्ञात्वा सिद्धमात्मानं लोके पश्यन्ति योगिनः ॥१४॥
transliteration: ataḥ paraṁ pravakṣyāmi yogaśāstramanuttamam ,yajjñātvā siddhamātmānaṁ loke paśyanti yoginaḥ.
english: Therefore, I will now declare the supreme (yoga) scripture, knowing which practitioners of yoga (yogin) perceive the perfected true self (ātman) in the world.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 14. Therefore, I will now declare the supreme (yoga) scripture, knowing which practitioners of yoga (yogin) perceive the perfected true self (ātman) in the world.

sanskrit: तस्योपदेशं पश्यामि यथावत्तन्निबोध मे ।यैर्द्वारैश्चारयन्नित्यं पश्यत्यात्मानमात्मनि ॥१५॥
transliteration: tasyopadeśaṁ paśyāmi yathāvattannibodha me ,yairdvāraiścārayannityaṁ paśyatyātmānamātmani.
english: I comprehend his instruction; therefore, understand it from me precisely. Through which gateways, constantly directing (the mind), one perceives the self (ātman) within the self (ātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 15. I comprehend his instruction; therefore, understand it from me precisely. Through which gateways, constantly directing (the mind), one perceives the self (ātman) within the self (ātman).

sanskrit: इन्द्रियाणि तु संहृत्य मन आत्मनि धारयेत् ।तीव्रं तप्त्वा तपः पूर्वं ततो योक्तुमुपक्रमेत् ॥१६॥
transliteration: indriyāṇi tu saṁhṛtya mana ātmani dhārayet ,tīvraṁ taptvā tapaḥ pūrvaṁ tato yoktumupakramet.
english: Indeed, having withdrawn the senses, one should fix the mind on the self (ātman). First, having intensely practiced austerity (tapas), one should then begin to engage in (yoga).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 16. Indeed, having withdrawn the senses, one should fix the mind on the self (ātman). First, having intensely practiced austerity (tapas), one should then begin to engage in (yoga).

sanskrit: तपस्वी त्यक्तसंकल्पो दम्भाहंकारवर्जितः ।मनीषी मनसा विप्रः पश्यत्यात्मानमात्मनि ॥१७॥
transliteration: tapasvī tyaktasaṁkalpo dambhāhaṁkāravarjitaḥ ,manīṣī manasā vipraḥ paśyatyātmānamātmani.
english: An intelligent and discerning brahmin (vipra), an ascetic (tapasvī) who has abandoned intentions and is free from hypocrisy and ego (ahaṅkāra), sees the self (ātman) within their own self through the mind.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 17. An intelligent and discerning brahmin (vipra), an ascetic (tapasvī) who has abandoned intentions and is free from hypocrisy and ego (ahaṅkāra), sees the self (ātman) within their own self through the mind.

sanskrit: स चेच्छक्नोत्ययं साधुर्योक्तुमात्मानमात्मनि ।तत एकान्तशीलः स पश्यत्यात्मानमात्मनि ॥१८॥
transliteration: sa cecchaknotyayaṁ sādhuryoktumātmānamātmani ,tata ekāntaśīlaḥ sa paśyatyātmānamātmani.
english: If this virtuous person (sādhu) is able to fix the self (ātman) within the self (ātman), then, being devoted to solitude, he perceives the self (ātman) within his own self.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 18. If this virtuous person (sādhu) is able to fix the self (ātman) within the self (ātman), then, being devoted to solitude, he perceives the self (ātman) within his own self.

sanskrit: संयतः सततं युक्त आत्मवान्विजितेन्द्रियः ।तथायमात्मनात्मानं साधु युक्तः प्रपश्यति ॥१९॥
transliteration: saṁyataḥ satataṁ yukta ātmavānvijitendriyaḥ ,tathāyamātmanātmānaṁ sādhu yuktaḥ prapaśyati.
english: This person, constantly restrained and disciplined, self-possessed and having conquered the senses (vijitendriyaḥ), similarly perceives the self (ātman) by means of the self (ātman), being properly concentrated.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 19. This person, constantly restrained and disciplined, self-possessed and having conquered the senses (vijitendriyaḥ), similarly perceives the self (ātman) by means of the self (ātman), being properly concentrated.

sanskrit: यथा हि पुरुषः स्वप्ने दृष्ट्वा पश्यत्यसाविति ।तथारूपमिवात्मानं साधु युक्तः प्रपश्यति ॥२०॥
transliteration: yathā hi puruṣaḥ svapne dṛṣṭvā paśyatyasāviti ,tathārūpamivātmānaṁ sādhu yuktaḥ prapaśyati.
english: Just as a person (puruṣa) in a dream, having seen something, perceives 'that is it,' similarly, the properly concentrated person perceives the self (ātman) as if it were a form (rūpa).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 20. Just as a person (puruṣa) in a dream, having seen something, perceives 'that is it,' similarly, the properly concentrated person perceives the self (ātman) as if it were a form (rūpa).

sanskrit: इषीकां वा यथा मुञ्जात्कश्चिन्निर्हृत्य दर्शयेत् ।योगी निष्कृष्टमात्मानं तथा संपश्यते तनौ ॥२१॥
transliteration: iṣīkāṁ vā yathā muñjātkaścinnirhṛtya darśayet ,yogī niṣkṛṣṭamātmānaṁ tathā saṁpaśyate tanau.
english: Just as someone might extract a reed from muñja grass and display it, so too does a practitioner of yoga (yogī) perceive the true self (ātman) as distinct and separated from the body.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 21. Just as someone might extract a reed from muñja grass and display it, so too does a practitioner of yoga (yogī) perceive the true self (ātman) as distinct and separated from the body.

sanskrit: मुञ्जं शरीरं तस्याहुरिषीकामात्मनि श्रिताम् ।एतन्निदर्शनं प्रोक्तं योगविद्भिरनुत्तमम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: muñjaṁ śarīraṁ tasyāhuriṣīkāmātmani śritām ,etannidarśanaṁ proktaṁ yogavidbhiranuttamam.
english: The muñja grass is said to be its body, and the reed is referred to as the self (ātman) residing within. This is proclaimed as an excellent analogy by those who understand (yoga).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 22. The muñja grass is said to be its body, and the reed is referred to as the self (ātman) residing within. This is proclaimed as an excellent analogy by those who understand (yoga).

sanskrit: यदा हि युक्तमात्मानं सम्यक्पश्यति देहभृत् ।तदास्य नेशते कश्चित्त्रैलोक्यस्यापि यः प्रभुः ॥२३॥
transliteration: yadā hi yuktamātmānaṁ samyakpaśyati dehabhṛt ,tadāsya neśate kaścittrailokyasyāpi yaḥ prabhuḥ.
english: When indeed the embodied being correctly perceives the self (ātman) as truly connected (or united), then no one, not even he who is the lord of the three worlds, can dominate him.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 23. When indeed the embodied being correctly perceives the self (ātman) as truly connected (or united), then no one, not even he who is the lord of the three worlds, can dominate him.

sanskrit: अन्योन्याश्चैव तनवो यथेष्टं प्रतिपद्यते ।विनिवृत्य जरामृत्यू न हृष्यति न शोचति ॥२४॥
transliteration: anyonyāścaiva tanavo yatheṣṭaṁ pratipadyate ,vinivṛtya jarāmṛtyū na hṛṣyati na śocati.
english: And indeed, one attains various bodies according to one's desire. Having turned away from old age and death, one neither rejoices nor grieves.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 24. And indeed, one attains various bodies according to one's desire. Having turned away from old age and death, one neither rejoices nor grieves.

sanskrit: देवानामपि देवत्वं युक्तः कारयते वशी ।ब्रह्म चाव्ययमाप्नोति हित्वा देहमशाश्वतम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: devānāmapi devatvaṁ yuktaḥ kārayate vaśī ,brahma cāvyayamāpnoti hitvā dehamaśāśvatam.
english: The self-controlled (vaśī) individual, who is disciplined (yukta), bestows divinity even upon the gods. And, abandoning the impermanent body, such a one attains the imperishable supreme reality (brahman).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 25. The self-controlled (vaśī) individual, who is disciplined (yukta), bestows divinity even upon the gods. And, abandoning the impermanent body, such a one attains the imperishable supreme reality (brahman).

sanskrit: विनश्यत्स्वपि लोकेषु न भयं तस्य जायते ।क्लिश्यमानेषु भूतेषु न स क्लिश्यति केनचित् ॥२६॥
transliteration: vinaśyatsvapi lokeṣu na bhayaṁ tasya jāyate ,kliśyamāneṣu bhūteṣu na sa kliśyati kenacit.
english: Even when the worlds are perishing, no fear arises for him. When beings (bhūta) are tormented, he himself is not tormented by anything.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 26. Even when the worlds are perishing, no fear arises for him. When beings (bhūta) are tormented, he himself is not tormented by anything.

sanskrit: दुःखशोकमयैर्घोरैः सङ्गस्नेहसमुद्भवैः ।न विचाल्येत युक्तात्मा निःस्पृहः शान्तमानसः ॥२७॥
transliteration: duḥkhaśokamayairghoraiḥ saṅgasnehasamudbhavaiḥ ,na vicālyeta yuktātmā niḥspṛhaḥ śāntamānasaḥ.
english: The disciplined (yukta) soul, free from longing, whose mind is peaceful, is not shaken by the terrible things full of sorrow and grief that arise from attachment and affection.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 27. The disciplined (yukta) soul, free from longing, whose mind is peaceful, is not shaken by the terrible things full of sorrow and grief that arise from attachment and affection.

sanskrit: नैनं शस्त्राणि विध्यन्ते न मृत्युश्चास्य विद्यते ।नातः सुखतरं किंचिल्लोके क्वचन विद्यते ॥२८॥
transliteration: nainaṁ śastrāṇi vidhyante na mṛtyuścāsya vidyate ,nātaḥ sukhataraṁ kiṁcilloke kvacana vidyate.
english: Weapons do not pierce him, nor does death (mṛtyu) exist for him. Therefore, nothing more pleasant than this (state) exists anywhere in the world (loka).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 28. Weapons do not pierce him, nor does death (mṛtyu) exist for him. Therefore, nothing more pleasant than this (state) exists anywhere in the world (loka).

sanskrit: सम्यग्युक्त्वा यदात्मानमात्मन्येव प्रपश्यति ।तदैव न स्पृहयते साक्षादपि शतक्रतोः ॥२९॥
transliteration: samyagyuktvā yadātmānamātmanyeva prapaśyati ,tadaiva na spṛhayate sākṣādapi śatakratoḥ.
english: When one, having properly disciplined the individual self (ātman), perceives the self (ātman) within oneself alone, then one does not long for even the direct presence of Indra, the performer of a hundred (Vedic rituals).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 29. When one, having properly disciplined the individual self (ātman), perceives the self (ātman) within oneself alone, then one does not long for even the direct presence of Indra, the performer of a hundred (Vedic rituals).

sanskrit: निर्वेदस्तु न गन्तव्यो युञ्जानेन कथंचन ।योगमेकान्तशीलस्तु यथा युञ्जीत तच्छृणु ॥३०॥
transliteration: nirvedastu na gantavyo yuñjānena kathaṁcana ,yogamekāntaśīlastu yathā yuñjīta tacchṛṇu.
english: However, one who is engaged in spiritual discipline (yoga) should never fall into despondency. Now, listen to how one who is dedicated to solitary practice (yoga) should apply it.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 30. However, one who is engaged in spiritual discipline (yoga) should never fall into despondency. Now, listen to how one who is dedicated to solitary practice (yoga) should apply it.

sanskrit: दृष्टपूर्वां दिशं चिन्त्य यस्मिन्संनिवसेत्पुरे ।पुरस्याभ्यन्तरे तस्य मनश्चार्यं न बाह्यतः ॥३१॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭapūrvāṁ diśaṁ cintya yasminsaṁnivasetpure ,purasyābhyantare tasya manaścāryaṁ na bāhyataḥ.
english: Having considered a previously seen direction, in whichever town one might dwell, one's mind should be focused within that town, not externally.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 31. Having considered a previously seen direction, in whichever town one might dwell, one's mind should be focused within that town, not externally.

sanskrit: पुरस्याभ्यन्तरे तिष्ठन्यस्मिन्नावसथे वसेत् ।तस्मिन्नावसथे धार्यं सबाह्याभ्यन्तरं मनः ॥३२॥
transliteration: purasyābhyantare tiṣṭhanyasminnāvasathe vaset ,tasminnāvasathe dhāryaṁ sabāhyābhyantaraṁ manaḥ.
english: Remaining within the city, in whichever dwelling one resides, one's mind, along with its external and internal aspects, should be kept fixed within that very dwelling.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 32. Remaining within the city, in whichever dwelling one resides, one's mind, along with its external and internal aspects, should be kept fixed within that very dwelling.

sanskrit: प्रचिन्त्यावसथं कृत्स्नं यस्मिन्कायेऽवतिष्ठते ।तस्मिन्काये मनश्चार्यं न कथंचन बाह्यतः ॥३३॥
transliteration: pracintyāvasathaṁ kṛtsnaṁ yasminkāye'vatiṣṭhate ,tasminkāye manaścāryaṁ na kathaṁcana bāhyataḥ.
english: Deeply contemplating this entire body, which serves as one's dwelling, one should direct the mind solely within it, never allowing it to wander outside.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 33. Deeply contemplating this entire body, which serves as one's dwelling, one should direct the mind solely within it, never allowing it to wander outside.

sanskrit: संनियम्येन्द्रियग्रामं निर्घोषे निर्जने वने ।कायमभ्यन्तरं कृत्स्नमेकाग्रः परिचिन्तयेत् ॥३४॥
transliteration: saṁniyamyendriyagrāmaṁ nirghoṣe nirjane vane ,kāyamabhyantaraṁ kṛtsnamekāgraḥ paricintayet.
english: Completely restraining the senses in a quiet, solitary forest, one should, with single-pointed concentration, contemplate the entire inner body.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 34. Completely restraining the senses in a quiet, solitary forest, one should, with single-pointed concentration, contemplate the entire inner body.

sanskrit: दन्तांस्तालु च जिह्वां च गलं ग्रीवां तथैव च ।हृदयं चिन्तयेच्चापि तथा हृदयबन्धनम् ॥३५॥
transliteration: dantāṁstālu ca jihvāṁ ca galaṁ grīvāṁ tathaiva ca ,hṛdayaṁ cintayeccāpi tathā hṛdayabandhanam.
english: One should contemplate the teeth, the palate, the tongue, the throat, and the neck. Likewise, one should reflect on the heart and also that which binds the heart (its attachments or surrounding structures).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 35. One should contemplate the teeth, the palate, the tongue, the throat, and the neck. Likewise, one should reflect on the heart and also that which binds the heart (its attachments or surrounding structures).

sanskrit: इत्युक्तः स मया शिष्यो मेधावी मधुसूदन ।पप्रच्छ पुनरेवेमं मोक्षधर्मं सुदुर्वचम् ॥३६॥
transliteration: ityuktaḥ sa mayā śiṣyo medhāvī madhusūdana ,papraccha punarevemaṁ mokṣadharmaṁ sudurvacam.
english: O Madhusūdana, when I had thus instructed that intelligent disciple, he again asked about this natural law (dharma) of liberation (mokṣa), which is very difficult to explain.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 36. O Madhusūdana, when I had thus instructed that intelligent disciple, he again asked about this natural law (dharma) of liberation (mokṣa), which is very difficult to explain.

sanskrit: भुक्तं भुक्तं कथमिदमन्नं कोष्ठे विपच्यते ।कथं रसत्वं व्रजति शोणितं जायते कथम् ।तथा मांसं च मेदश्च स्नाय्वस्थीनि च पोषति ॥३७॥
transliteration: bhuktaṁ bhuktaṁ kathamidamannaṁ koṣṭhe vipacyate ,kathaṁ rasatvaṁ vrajati śoṇitaṁ jāyate katham ,tathā māṁsaṁ ca medaśca snāyvasthīni ca poṣati.
english: How is the food that is eaten repeatedly digested within the abdominal cavity? How does it transform into nutrient fluid, and how does it become blood? Furthermore, how does it nourish the flesh, fat, sinews, and bones?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 37. How is the food that is eaten repeatedly digested within the abdominal cavity? How does it transform into nutrient fluid, and how does it become blood? Furthermore, how does it nourish the flesh, fat, sinews, and bones?

sanskrit: कथमेतानि सर्वाणि शरीराणि शरीरिणाम् ।वर्धन्ते वर्धमानस्य वर्धते च कथं बलम् ।निरोजसां निष्क्रमणं मलानां च पृथक्पृथक् ॥३८॥
transliteration: kathametāni sarvāṇi śarīrāṇi śarīriṇām ,vardhante vardhamānasya vardhate ca kathaṁ balam ,nirojasāṁ niṣkramaṇaṁ malānāṁ ca pṛthakpṛthak.
english: How do all these bodies of embodied beings grow, and how does strength increase for the growing individual? Furthermore, how are inert substances removed, and how are waste products eliminated separately?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 38. How do all these bodies of embodied beings grow, and how does strength increase for the growing individual? Furthermore, how are inert substances removed, and how are waste products eliminated separately?

sanskrit: कुतो वायं प्रश्वसिति उच्छ्वसित्यपि वा पुनः ।कं च देशमधिष्ठाय तिष्ठत्यात्मायमात्मनि ॥३९॥
transliteration: kuto vāyaṁ praśvasiti ucchvasityapi vā punaḥ ,kaṁ ca deśamadhiṣṭhāya tiṣṭhatyātmāyamātmani.
english: From where does this (being) inhale, and where does it exhale? And which region does this individual self (ātman) occupy when it dwells within the body (ātmani)?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 39. From where does this (being) inhale, and where does it exhale? And which region does this individual self (ātman) occupy when it dwells within the body (ātmani)?

sanskrit: जीवः कायं वहति चेच्चेष्टयानः कलेवरम् ।किंवर्णं कीदृशं चैव निवेशयति वै मनः ।याथातथ्येन भगवन्वक्तुमर्हसि मेऽनघ ॥४०॥
transliteration: jīvaḥ kāyaṁ vahati cecceṣṭayānaḥ kalevaram ,kiṁvarṇaṁ kīdṛśaṁ caiva niveśayati vai manaḥ ,yāthātathyena bhagavanvaktumarhasi me'nagha.
english: If the living being (jīva) sustains and animates the body, then what is the nature and color with which the mind (manas) establishes itself (in that body)? O venerable Lord, O sinless one, you ought to explain this to me in its true nature.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 40. If the living being (jīva) sustains and animates the body, then what is the nature and color with which the mind (manas) establishes itself (in that body)? O venerable Lord, O sinless one, you ought to explain this to me in its true nature.

sanskrit: इति संपरिपृष्टोऽहं तेन विप्रेण माधव ।प्रत्यब्रुवं महाबाहो यथाश्रुतमरिंदम ॥४१॥
transliteration: iti saṁparipṛṣṭo'haṁ tena vipreṇa mādhava ,pratyabruvaṁ mahābāho yathāśrutamariṁdama.
english: O Mādhava, O mighty-armed one, O subduer of enemies, when I was thus thoroughly questioned by that brahmin, I replied exactly as I had heard.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 41. O Mādhava, O mighty-armed one, O subduer of enemies, when I was thus thoroughly questioned by that brahmin, I replied exactly as I had heard.

sanskrit: यथा स्वकोष्ठे प्रक्षिप्य कोष्ठं भाण्डमना भवेत् ।तथा स्वकाये प्रक्षिप्य मनो द्वारैरनिश्चलैः ।आत्मानं तत्र मार्गेत प्रमादं परिवर्जयेत् ॥४२॥
transliteration: yathā svakoṣṭhe prakṣipya koṣṭhaṁ bhāṇḍamanā bhavet ,tathā svakāye prakṣipya mano dvārairaniścalaiḥ ,ātmānaṁ tatra mārgeta pramādaṁ parivarjayet.
english: Just as one should be intent on the goods after placing them in one's own storeroom, so too, after fixing the mind within one's own body through unwavering senses, one should seek the individual self (ātman) therein, and completely avoid negligence.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 42. Just as one should be intent on the goods after placing them in one's own storeroom, so too, after fixing the mind within one's own body through unwavering senses, one should seek the individual self (ātman) therein, and completely avoid negligence.

sanskrit: एवं सततमुद्युक्तः प्रीतात्मा नचिरादिव ।आसादयति तद्ब्रह्म यद्दृष्ट्वा स्यात्प्रधानवित् ॥४३॥
transliteration: evaṁ satatamudyuktaḥ prītātmā nacirādiva ,āsādayati tadbrahma yaddṛṣṭvā syātpradhānavit.
english: Thus, one who is constantly diligent and whose individual self (ātman) is pleased, soon attains that ultimate reality (brahman). Having realized which, one becomes a knower of the fundamental nature (pradhāna).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 43. Thus, one who is constantly diligent and whose individual self (ātman) is pleased, soon attains that ultimate reality (brahman). Having realized which, one becomes a knower of the fundamental nature (pradhāna).

sanskrit: न त्वसौ चक्षुषा ग्राह्यो न च सर्वैरपीन्द्रियैः ।मनसैव प्रदीपेन महानात्मनि दृश्यते ॥४४॥
transliteration: na tvasau cakṣuṣā grāhyo na ca sarvairapīndriyaiḥ ,manasaiva pradīpena mahānātmani dṛśyate.
english: But that (ātman) is not to be grasped by the eye, nor by any of the senses. The great (ātman) is seen within the self (ātmani) only by the mind (manas), which serves as a lamp.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 44. But that (ātman) is not to be grasped by the eye, nor by any of the senses. The great (ātman) is seen within the self (ātmani) only by the mind (manas), which serves as a lamp.

sanskrit: सर्वतःपाणिपादं तं सर्वतोक्षिशिरोमुखम् ।जीवो निष्क्रान्तमात्मानं शरीरात्संप्रपश्यति ॥४५॥
transliteration: sarvataḥpāṇipādaṁ taṁ sarvatokṣiśiromukham ,jīvo niṣkrāntamātmānaṁ śarīrātsaṁprapaśyati.
english: The individual soul (jīva), having departed from the body, perceives the supreme Self (ātman) as possessing hands and feet everywhere, and eyes, heads, and mouths everywhere.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 45. The individual soul (jīva), having departed from the body, perceives the supreme Self (ātman) as possessing hands and feet everywhere, and eyes, heads, and mouths everywhere.

sanskrit: स तदुत्सृज्य देहं स्वं धारयन्ब्रह्म केवलम् ।आत्मानमालोकयति मनसा प्रहसन्निव ॥४६॥
transliteration: sa tadutsṛjya dehaṁ svaṁ dhārayanbrahma kevalam ,ātmānamālokayati manasā prahasanniva.
english: That (jīva), having abandoned his own body and sustaining only (brahman), perceives the (ātman) with his mind, as if smiling.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 46. That (jīva), having abandoned his own body and sustaining only (brahman), perceives the (ātman) with his mind, as if smiling.

sanskrit: इदं सर्वरहस्यं ते मयोक्तं द्विजसत्तम ।आपृच्छे साधयिष्यामि गच्छ शिष्य यथासुखम् ॥४७॥
transliteration: idaṁ sarvarahasyaṁ te mayoktaṁ dvijasattama ,āpṛcche sādhayiṣyāmi gaccha śiṣya yathāsukham.
english: “O best of brahmins, this entire secret has been revealed to you by me. I take my leave, I shall proceed (on my task). Go, disciple, as you please.”
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 47. “O best of brahmins, this entire secret has been revealed to you by me. I take my leave, I shall proceed (on my task). Go, disciple, as you please.”

sanskrit: इत्युक्तः स तदा कृष्ण मया शिष्यो महातपाः ।अगच्छत यथाकामं ब्राह्मणश्छिन्नसंशयः ॥४८॥
transliteration: ityuktaḥ sa tadā kṛṣṇa mayā śiṣyo mahātapāḥ ,agacchata yathākāmaṁ brāhmaṇaśchinnasaṁśayaḥ.
english: “O Kṛṣṇa, that great ascetic disciple, thus addressed by me, then departed as he wished, that brahmin whose doubts were dispelled.”
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 48. “O Kṛṣṇa, that great ascetic disciple, thus addressed by me, then departed as he wished, that brahmin whose doubts were dispelled.”

sanskrit: वासुदेव उवाच ।इत्युक्त्वा स तदा वाक्यं मां पार्थ द्विजपुंगवः ।मोक्षधर्माश्रितः सम्यक्तत्रैवान्तरधीयत ॥४९॥
transliteration: vāsudeva uvāca ,ityuktvā sa tadā vākyaṁ māṁ pārtha dvijapuṁgavaḥ ,mokṣadharmāśritaḥ samyaktatraivāntaradhīyata.
english: Vāsudeva said: 'O Pārtha, having thus spoken those words to me, that foremost among the twice-born, who was thoroughly devoted to the principles of liberation (mokṣa-dharma), vanished right there.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 49. Vāsudeva said: 'O Pārtha, having thus spoken those words to me, that foremost among the twice-born, who was thoroughly devoted to the principles of liberation (mokṣa-dharma), vanished right there.'

sanskrit: कच्चिदेतत्त्वया पार्थ श्रुतमेकाग्रचेतसा ।तदापि हि रथस्थस्त्वं श्रुतवानेतदेव हि ॥५०॥
transliteration: kaccidetattvayā pārtha śrutamekāgracetasā ,tadāpi hi rathasthastvaṁ śrutavānetadeva hi.
english: O Pārtha, I hope you heard this with a concentrated mind. For indeed, even then, you, though seated in the chariot, had heard this very thing.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 50. O Pārtha, I hope you heard this with a concentrated mind. For indeed, even then, you, though seated in the chariot, had heard this very thing.

sanskrit: नैतत्पार्थ सुविज्ञेयं व्यामिश्रेणेति मे मतिः ।नरेणाकृतसंज्ञेन विदग्धेनाकृतात्मना ॥५१॥
transliteration: naitatpārtha suvijñeyaṁ vyāmiśreṇeti me matiḥ ,nareṇākṛtasaṁjñena vidagdhenākṛtātmanā.
english: O Pārtha, this is not easily understood by a person with a confused mind – such is my conviction. Nor by one whose understanding is undeveloped, by a merely shrewd individual, or by one whose inner self (ātman) is uncultivated.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 51. O Pārtha, this is not easily understood by a person with a confused mind – such is my conviction. Nor by one whose understanding is undeveloped, by a merely shrewd individual, or by one whose inner self (ātman) is uncultivated.

sanskrit: सुरहस्यमिदं प्रोक्तं देवानां भरतर्षभ ।कच्चिन्नेदं श्रुतं पार्थ मर्त्येनान्येन केनचित् ॥५२॥
transliteration: surahasyamidaṁ proktaṁ devānāṁ bharatarṣabha ,kaccinnedaṁ śrutaṁ pārtha martyenānyena kenacit.
english: This has been declared a profound secret of the gods, O best among the Bhāratas. O Pārtha, I hope this has not been heard by any other mortal.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 52. This has been declared a profound secret of the gods, O best among the Bhāratas. O Pārtha, I hope this has not been heard by any other mortal.

sanskrit: न ह्येतच्छ्रोतुमर्होऽन्यो मनुष्यस्त्वामृतेऽनघ ।नैतदद्य सुविज्ञेयं व्यामिश्रेणान्तरात्मना ॥५३॥
transliteration: na hyetacchrotumarho'nyo manuṣyastvāmṛte'nagha ,naitadadya suvijñeyaṁ vyāmiśreṇāntarātmanā.
english: Indeed, no other human being is worthy to hear this, O sinless one, except you. Nor can this be easily understood today by a confused inner being (antarātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 53. Indeed, no other human being is worthy to hear this, O sinless one, except you. Nor can this be easily understood today by a confused inner being (antarātman).

sanskrit: क्रियावद्भिर्हि कौन्तेय देवलोकः समावृतः ।न चैतदिष्टं देवानां मर्त्यै रूपनिवर्तनम् ॥५४॥
transliteration: kriyāvadbhirhi kaunteya devalokaḥ samāvṛtaḥ ,na caitadiṣṭaṁ devānāṁ martyai rūpanivartanam.
english: Indeed, O son of Kunti, the realm of the gods (devaloka) is filled with those who perform auspicious actions. And this transformation of form by mortals is not desired by the gods.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 54. Indeed, O son of Kunti, the realm of the gods (devaloka) is filled with those who perform auspicious actions. And this transformation of form by mortals is not desired by the gods.

sanskrit: परा हि सा गतिः पार्थ यत्तद्ब्रह्म सनातनम् ।यत्रामृतत्वं प्राप्नोति त्यक्त्वा दुःखं सदा सुखी ॥५५॥
transliteration: parā hi sā gatiḥ pārtha yattadbrahma sanātanam ,yatrāmṛtatvaṁ prāpnoti tyaktvā duḥkhaṁ sadā sukhī.
english: Indeed, O son of Pṛthā (Pārtha), that supreme state is the eternal reality (sanātana brahman), where, having abandoned sorrow, one attains immortality (amṛtatva) and is forever happy.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 55. Indeed, O son of Pṛthā (Pārtha), that supreme state is the eternal reality (sanātana brahman), where, having abandoned sorrow, one attains immortality (amṛtatva) and is forever happy.

sanskrit: एवं हि धर्ममास्थाय येऽपि स्युः पापयोनयः ।स्त्रियो वैश्यास्तथा शूद्रास्तेऽपि यान्ति परां गतिम् ॥५६॥
transliteration: evaṁ hi dharmamāsthāya ye'pi syuḥ pāpayonayaḥ ,striyo vaiśyāstathā śūdrāste'pi yānti parāṁ gatim.
english: Indeed, even those who may be of sinful birth, having adopted this natural law (dharma) - women, vaiśyas, and śūdras - they too attain the supreme state.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 56. Indeed, even those who may be of sinful birth, having adopted this natural law (dharma) - women, vaiśyas, and śūdras - they too attain the supreme state.

sanskrit: किं पुनर्ब्राह्मणाः पार्थ क्षत्रिया वा बहुश्रुताः ।स्वधर्मरतयो नित्यं ब्रह्मलोकपरायणाः ॥५७॥
transliteration: kiṁ punarbrāhmaṇāḥ pārtha kṣatriyā vā bahuśrutāḥ ,svadharmaratayo nityaṁ brahmalokaparāyaṇāḥ.
english: O Pārtha, what then of Brahmins or Kṣatriyas who are highly learned, always devoted to their own natural law (dharma), and whose ultimate aim is the realm of Brahmā?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 57. O Pārtha, what then of Brahmins or Kṣatriyas who are highly learned, always devoted to their own natural law (dharma), and whose ultimate aim is the realm of Brahmā?

sanskrit: हेतुमच्चैतदुद्दिष्टमुपायाश्चास्य साधने ।सिद्धेः फलं च मोक्षश्च दुःखस्य च विनिर्णयः ।अतः परं सुखं त्वन्यत्किं नु स्याद्भरतर्षभ ॥५८॥
transliteration: hetumaccaitaduddiṣṭamupāyāścāsya sādhane ,siddheḥ phalaṁ ca mokṣaśca duḥkhasya ca vinirṇayaḥ ,ataḥ paraṁ sukhaṁ tvanyatkiṁ nu syādbharatarṣabha.
english: This teaching is expounded with its rationale, along with the methods for its accomplishment. The fruit of perfection is liberation (mokṣa) and the decisive resolution of suffering. Therefore, O best of the Bhāratas, what other happiness could there possibly be beyond this?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 58. This teaching is expounded with its rationale, along with the methods for its accomplishment. The fruit of perfection is liberation (mokṣa) and the decisive resolution of suffering. Therefore, O best of the Bhāratas, what other happiness could there possibly be beyond this?

sanskrit: श्रुतवाञ्श्रद्दधानश्च पराक्रान्तश्च पाण्डव ।यः परित्यजते मर्त्यो लोकतन्त्रमसारवत् ।एतैरुपायैः स क्षिप्रं परां गतिमवाप्नुयात् ॥५९॥
transliteration: śrutavāñśraddadhānaśca parākrāntaśca pāṇḍava ,yaḥ parityajate martyo lokatantramasāravat ,etairupāyaiḥ sa kṣipraṁ parāṁ gatimavāpnuyāt.
english: O Pāṇḍava, any mortal who is learned, possesses faith (śraddhā), and is courageous, and who abandons the insubstantial worldly system, quickly attains the supreme state by these means.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 59. O Pāṇḍava, any mortal who is learned, possesses faith (śraddhā), and is courageous, and who abandons the insubstantial worldly system, quickly attains the supreme state by these means.

sanskrit: एतावदेव वक्तव्यं नातो भूयोऽस्ति किंचन ।षण्मासान्नित्ययुक्तस्य योगः पार्थ प्रवर्तते ॥६०॥
transliteration: etāvadeva vaktavyaṁ nāto bhūyo'sti kiṁcana ,ṣaṇmāsānnityayuktasya yogaḥ pārtha pravartate.
english: This much alone is to be stated; there is nothing more than this. O Pārtha, for one who is constantly engaged in spiritual discipline (yoga), the discipline (yoga) becomes effective within six months.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 19 Verse 60. This much alone is to be stated; there is nothing more than this. O Pārtha, for one who is constantly engaged in spiritual discipline (yoga), the discipline (yoga) becomes effective within six months.

sanskrit: वासुदेव उवाच ।अत्राप्युदाहरन्तीममितिहासं पुरातनम् ।दंपत्योः पार्थ संवादमभयं नाम नामतः ॥१॥
transliteration: vāsudeva uvāca ,atrāpyudāharantīmamitihāsaṁ purātanam ,daṁpatyoḥ pārtha saṁvādamabhayaṁ nāma nāmataḥ.
english: Vāsudeva said: Here, they also recount this ancient story, O Pārtha, a dialogue between a husband and wife named Abhaya.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 20 Verse 1. Vāsudeva said: Here, they also recount this ancient story, O Pārtha, a dialogue between a husband and wife named Abhaya.

sanskrit: ब्राह्मणी ब्राह्मणं कंचिज्ज्ञानविज्ञानपारगम् ।दृष्ट्वा विविक्त आसीनं भार्या भर्तारमब्रवीत् ॥२॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇī brāhmaṇaṁ kaṁcijjñānavijñānapāragam ,dṛṣṭvā vivikta āsīnaṁ bhāryā bhartāramabravīt.
english: Having seen her husband, a certain brahmin who was a master of both theoretical knowledge (jñāna) and practical wisdom (vijñāna), seated in a secluded spot, the wife spoke to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 20 Verse 2. Having seen her husband, a certain brahmin who was a master of both theoretical knowledge (jñāna) and practical wisdom (vijñāna), seated in a secluded spot, the wife spoke to him.

sanskrit: कं नु लोकं गमिष्यामि त्वामहं पतिमाश्रिता ।न्यस्तकर्माणमासीनं कीनाशमविचक्षणम् ॥३॥
transliteration: kaṁ nu lokaṁ gamiṣyāmi tvāmahaṁ patimāśritā ,nyastakarmāṇamāsīnaṁ kīnāśamavicakṣaṇam.
english: To which world will I go, I who am dependent on you as my husband - you who are seated, having abandoned your actions (karma), like a miserly and undiscerning person?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 20 Verse 3. To which world will I go, I who am dependent on you as my husband - you who are seated, having abandoned your actions (karma), like a miserly and undiscerning person?

sanskrit: भार्याः पतिकृताँल्लोकानाप्नुवन्तीति नः श्रुतम् ।त्वामहं पतिमासाद्य कां गमिष्यामि वै गतिम् ॥४॥
transliteration: bhāryāḥ patikṛtāँllokānāpnuvantīti naḥ śrutam ,tvāmahaṁ patimāsādya kāṁ gamiṣyāmi vai gatim.
english: It has been heard by us that wives attain the worlds (loka) earned by their husbands. What destination, then, shall I, having obtained you as my husband, truly reach?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 20 Verse 4. It has been heard by us that wives attain the worlds (loka) earned by their husbands. What destination, then, shall I, having obtained you as my husband, truly reach?

sanskrit: एवमुक्तः स शान्तात्मा तामुवाच हसन्निव ।सुभगे नाभ्यसूयामि वाक्यस्यास्य तवानघे ॥५॥
transliteration: evamuktaḥ sa śāntātmā tāmuvāca hasanniva ,subhage nābhyasūyāmi vākyasyāsya tavānaghe.
english: When addressed thus, he, serene in spirit (ātman), spoke to her as if smiling: 'O fortunate one, O sinless one, I do not bear ill will towards this statement of yours.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 20 Verse 5. When addressed thus, he, serene in spirit (ātman), spoke to her as if smiling: 'O fortunate one, O sinless one, I do not bear ill will towards this statement of yours.'

sanskrit: ग्राह्यं दृश्यं च श्राव्यं च यदिदं कर्म विद्यते ।एतदेव व्यवस्यन्ति कर्म कर्मेति कर्मिणः ॥६॥
transliteration: grāhyaṁ dṛśyaṁ ca śrāvyaṁ ca yadidaṁ karma vidyate ,etadeva vyavasyanti karma karmeti karmiṇaḥ.
english: Whatever is perceptible, visible, and audible – this is what action (karma) is known to be. Those who perform actions (karma) consider this very thing to be 'action'.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 20 Verse 6. Whatever is perceptible, visible, and audible – this is what action (karma) is known to be. Those who perform actions (karma) consider this very thing to be 'action'.

sanskrit: मोहमेव नियच्छन्ति कर्मणा ज्ञानवर्जिताः ।नैष्कर्म्यं न च लोकेऽस्मिन्मौर्तमित्युपलभ्यते ॥७॥
transliteration: mohameva niyacchanti karmaṇā jñānavarjitāḥ ,naiṣkarmyaṁ na ca loke'sminmaurtamityupalabhyate.
english: Those devoid of knowledge only succumb to delusion through their action (karma). And in this world, freedom from action (naiṣkarmya) is not perceived as something embodied or material.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 20 Verse 7. Those devoid of knowledge only succumb to delusion through their action (karma). And in this world, freedom from action (naiṣkarmya) is not perceived as something embodied or material.

sanskrit: कर्मणा मनसा वाचा शुभं वा यदि वाशुभम् ।जन्मादिमूर्तिभेदानां कर्म भूतेषु वर्तते ॥८॥
transliteration: karmaṇā manasā vācā śubhaṁ vā yadi vāśubham ,janmādimūrtibhedānāṁ karma bhūteṣu vartate.
english: Action (karma), whether good or bad, performed through body, mind, or speech, operates among all beings, resulting in diverse forms and states of existence, beginning with birth.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 20 Verse 8. Action (karma), whether good or bad, performed through body, mind, or speech, operates among all beings, resulting in diverse forms and states of existence, beginning with birth.

sanskrit: रक्षोभिर्वध्यमानेषु दृश्यद्रव्येषु कर्मसु ।आत्मस्थमात्मना तेन दृष्टमायतनं मया ॥९॥
transliteration: rakṣobhirvadhyamāneṣu dṛśyadravyeṣu karmasu ,ātmasthamātmanā tena dṛṣṭamāyatanaṁ mayā.
english: While Vedic rituals (karma) involving visible offerings were being obstructed by rākṣasas, the abode situated in the self (ātman) was seen by me through the self (ātman) itself.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 20 Verse 9. While Vedic rituals (karma) involving visible offerings were being obstructed by rākṣasas, the abode situated in the self (ātman) was seen by me through the self (ātman) itself.

sanskrit: यत्र तद्ब्रह्म निर्द्वंद्वं यत्र सोमः सहाग्निना ।व्यवायं कुरुते नित्यं धीरो भूतानि धारयन् ॥१०॥
transliteration: yatra tadbrahma nirdvaṁdvaṁ yatra somaḥ sahāgninā ,vyavāyaṁ kurute nityaṁ dhīro bhūtāni dhārayan.
english: Where that supreme reality (brahman) is free from dualities, and where Soma constantly unites with Agni, there abides the steadfast one, sustaining all existences (bhūtāni).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 20 Verse 10. Where that supreme reality (brahman) is free from dualities, and where Soma constantly unites with Agni, there abides the steadfast one, sustaining all existences (bhūtāni).

sanskrit: यत्र ब्रह्मादयो युक्तास्तदक्षरमुपासते ।विद्वांसः सुव्रता यत्र शान्तात्मानो जितेन्द्रियाः ॥११॥
transliteration: yatra brahmādayo yuktāstadakṣaramupāsate ,vidvāṁsaḥ suvratā yatra śāntātmāno jitendriyāḥ.
english: Where Brahmā and other exalted beings, being focused, worship that imperishable (akṣaram) reality, and where there are wise ones, steadfast in their vows, with tranquil selves (ātman), and with senses subdued.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 20 Verse 11. Where Brahmā and other exalted beings, being focused, worship that imperishable (akṣaram) reality, and where there are wise ones, steadfast in their vows, with tranquil selves (ātman), and with senses subdued.

sanskrit: घ्राणेन न तदाघ्रेयं न तदाद्यं च जिह्वया ।स्पर्शेन च न तत्स्पृश्यं मनसा त्वेव गम्यते ॥१२॥
transliteration: ghrāṇena na tadāghreyaṁ na tadādyaṁ ca jihvayā ,sparśena ca na tatspṛśyaṁ manasā tveva gamyate.
english: That (reality) cannot be smelt by the nose, nor can it be tasted by the tongue, and it cannot be touched by physical contact; rather, it is known only through the mind.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 20 Verse 12. That (reality) cannot be smelt by the nose, nor can it be tasted by the tongue, and it cannot be touched by physical contact; rather, it is known only through the mind.

sanskrit: चक्षुषा न विषह्यं च यत्किंचिच्छ्रवणात्परम् ।अगन्धमरसस्पर्शमरूपाशब्दमव्ययम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: cakṣuṣā na viṣahyaṁ ca yatkiṁcicchravaṇātparam ,agandhamarasasparśamarūpāśabdamavyayam.
english: And whatever is beyond the range of hearing, not perceivable by the eye; it is without smell, without taste, without touch, without form, without sound, and imperishable.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 20 Verse 13. And whatever is beyond the range of hearing, not perceivable by the eye; it is without smell, without taste, without touch, without form, without sound, and imperishable.

sanskrit: यतः प्रवर्तते तन्त्रं यत्र च प्रतितिष्ठति ।प्राणोऽपानः समानश्च व्यानश्चोदान एव च ॥१४॥
transliteration: yataḥ pravartate tantraṁ yatra ca pratitiṣṭhati ,prāṇo'pānaḥ samānaśca vyānaścodāna eva ca.
english: From which the system of vital forces originates and in which it rests: the outgoing breath (prāṇa), the downward-moving breath (apāna), the equilibrating breath (samāna), the pervading breath (vyāna), and also the upward-moving breath (udāna).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 20 Verse 14. From which the system of vital forces originates and in which it rests: the outgoing breath (prāṇa), the downward-moving breath (apāna), the equilibrating breath (samāna), the pervading breath (vyāna), and also the upward-moving breath (udāna).

sanskrit: तत एव प्रवर्तन्ते तमेव प्रविशन्ति च ।समानव्यानयोर्मध्ये प्राणापानौ विचेरतुः ॥१५॥
transliteration: tata eva pravartante tameva praviśanti ca ,samānavyānayormadhye prāṇāpānau viceratuḥ.
english: From that (source) alone they (the vital energies) originate, and into that alone they enter. Between the equilibrating breath (samāna) and the pervading breath (vyāna), the outgoing breath (prāṇa) and the downward-moving breath (apāna) move.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 20 Verse 15. From that (source) alone they (the vital energies) originate, and into that alone they enter. Between the equilibrating breath (samāna) and the pervading breath (vyāna), the outgoing breath (prāṇa) and the downward-moving breath (apāna) move.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्सुप्ते प्रलीयेते समानो व्यान एव च ।अपानप्राणयोर्मध्ये उदानो व्याप्य तिष्ठति ।तस्माच्छयानं पुरुषं प्राणापानौ न मुञ्चतः ॥१६॥
transliteration: tasminsupte pralīyete samāno vyāna eva ca ,apānaprāṇayormadhye udāno vyāpya tiṣṭhati ,tasmācchayānaṁ puruṣaṁ prāṇāpānau na muñcataḥ.
english: When that (being) is asleep, the equilibrating breath (samāna) and the pervading breath (vyāna) alone merge (into the source). Between the downward-moving breath (apāna) and the outgoing breath (prāṇa), the upward-moving breath (udāna) remains, pervading. Therefore, the outgoing breath (prāṇa) and the downward-moving breath (apāna) do not abandon the sleeping person (puruṣa).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 20 Verse 16. When that (being) is asleep, the equilibrating breath (samāna) and the pervading breath (vyāna) alone merge (into the source). Between the downward-moving breath (apāna) and the outgoing breath (prāṇa), the upward-moving breath (udāna) remains, pervading. Therefore, the outgoing breath (prāṇa) and the downward-moving breath (apāna) do not abandon the sleeping person (puruṣa).

sanskrit: प्राणानायम्यते येन तमुदानं प्रचक्षते ।तस्मात्तपो व्यवस्यन्ति तद्भवं ब्रह्मवादिनः ॥१७॥
transliteration: prāṇānāyamyate yena tamudānaṁ pracakṣate ,tasmāttapo vyavasyanti tadbhavaṁ brahmavādinaḥ.
english: That by which the vital breaths (prāṇa) are controlled is called udāna. For this reason, the expounders of brahman (brahmavādin) undertake austerity (tapas) which originates from it.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 20 Verse 17. That by which the vital breaths (prāṇa) are controlled is called udāna. For this reason, the expounders of brahman (brahmavādin) undertake austerity (tapas) which originates from it.

sanskrit: तेषामन्योन्यभक्षाणां सर्वेषां देहचारिणाम् ।अग्निर्वैश्वानरो मध्ये सप्तधा विहितोऽन्तरा ॥१८॥
transliteration: teṣāmanyonyabhakṣāṇāṁ sarveṣāṁ dehacāriṇām ,agnirvaiśvānaro madhye saptadhā vihito'ntarā.
english: Among all these embodied beings (dehacārin) who feed on one another, the Vaiśvānara fire is arranged internally in seven ways.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 20 Verse 18. Among all these embodied beings (dehacārin) who feed on one another, the Vaiśvānara fire is arranged internally in seven ways.

sanskrit: घ्राणं जिह्वा च चक्षुश्च त्वक्च श्रोत्रं च पञ्चमम् ।मनो बुद्धिश्च सप्तैता जिह्वा वैश्वानरार्चिषः ॥१९॥
transliteration: ghrāṇaṁ jihvā ca cakṣuśca tvakca śrotraṁ ca pañcamam ,mano buddhiśca saptaitā jihvā vaiśvānarārciṣaḥ.
english: The nose, tongue, eye, skin, and ear as the fifth, along with the mind and intellect (buddhi) – these seven are the 'tongues' (flames) of the Vaiśvānara fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 20 Verse 19. The nose, tongue, eye, skin, and ear as the fifth, along with the mind and intellect (buddhi) – these seven are the 'tongues' (flames) of the Vaiśvānara fire.

sanskrit: घ्रेयं पेयं च दृश्यं च स्पृश्यं श्रव्यं तथैव च ।मन्तव्यमथ बोद्धव्यं ताः सप्त समिधो मम ॥२०॥
transliteration: ghreyaṁ peyaṁ ca dṛśyaṁ ca spṛśyaṁ śravyaṁ tathaiva ca ,mantavyamatha boddhavyaṁ tāḥ sapta samidho mama.
english: That which is to be smelled, that which is to be tasted, that which is to be seen, that which is to be touched, and that which is to be heard; likewise, that which is to be contemplated and that which is to be understood – these seven are my fuels (samidh).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 20 Verse 20. That which is to be smelled, that which is to be tasted, that which is to be seen, that which is to be touched, and that which is to be heard; likewise, that which is to be contemplated and that which is to be understood – these seven are my fuels (samidh).

sanskrit: घ्राता भक्षयिता द्रष्टा स्प्रष्टा श्रोता च पञ्चमः ।मन्ता बोद्धा च सप्तैते भवन्ति परमर्त्विजः ॥२१॥
transliteration: ghrātā bhakṣayitā draṣṭā spraṣṭā śrotā ca pañcamaḥ ,mantā boddhā ca saptaite bhavanti paramartvijaḥ.
english: The smeller, the eater, the seer, the toucher, and fifth, the listener; the thinker and the understander – these seven become the supreme priests.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 20 Verse 21. The smeller, the eater, the seer, the toucher, and fifth, the listener; the thinker and the understander – these seven become the supreme priests.

sanskrit: घ्रेये पेये च दृश्ये च स्पृश्ये श्रव्ये तथैव च ।हवींष्यग्निषु होतारः सप्तधा सप्त सप्तसु ।सम्यक्प्रक्षिप्य विद्वांसो जनयन्ति स्वयोनिषु ॥२२॥
transliteration: ghreye peye ca dṛśye ca spṛśye śravye tathaiva ca ,havīṁṣyagniṣu hotāraḥ saptadhā sapta saptasu ,samyakprakṣipya vidvāṁso janayanti svayoniṣu.
english: Into what is smelled, drunk, seen, touched, and similarly heard – into these seven "fires" (agniṣu), the seven wise priests (hotāraḥ) in seven ways properly offer oblations (havīṃṣi), and thereby generate them in their own origins (svayoniṣu).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 20 Verse 22. Into what is smelled, drunk, seen, touched, and similarly heard – into these seven "fires" (agniṣu), the seven wise priests (hotāraḥ) in seven ways properly offer oblations (havīṃṣi), and thereby generate them in their own origins (svayoniṣu).

sanskrit: पृथिवी वायुराकाशमापो ज्योतिश्च पञ्चमम् ।मनो बुद्धिश्च सप्तैते योनिरित्येव शब्दिताः ॥२३॥
transliteration: pṛthivī vāyurākāśamāpo jyotiśca pañcamam ,mano buddhiśca saptaite yonirityeva śabditāḥ.
english: Earth, air, space, water, and fifth, light (jyotiḥ); mind (manas) and intellect (buddhi) – these seven are indeed declared to be the origin (yoni).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 20 Verse 23. Earth, air, space, water, and fifth, light (jyotiḥ); mind (manas) and intellect (buddhi) – these seven are indeed declared to be the origin (yoni).

sanskrit: हविर्भूता गुणाः सर्वे प्रविशन्त्यग्निजं मुखम् ।अन्तर्वासमुषित्वा च जायन्ते स्वासु योनिषु ।तत्रैव च निरुध्यन्ते प्रलये भूतभावने ॥२४॥
transliteration: havirbhūtā guṇāḥ sarve praviśantyagnijaṁ mukham ,antarvāsamuṣitvā ca jāyante svāsu yoniṣu ,tatraiva ca nirudhyante pralaye bhūtabhāvane.
english: All qualities (guṇāḥ), having become oblations (havirbhūtāḥ), enter the mouth born of fire (agnijam mukham). And having resided within temporarily, they are born in their own origins (svayoniṣu). And in that very cosmic dissolution (pralaye), the source of creation (bhūtabhāvana), they are absorbed (nirudhyante).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 20 Verse 24. All qualities (guṇāḥ), having become oblations (havirbhūtāḥ), enter the mouth born of fire (agnijam mukham). And having resided within temporarily, they are born in their own origins (svayoniṣu). And in that very cosmic dissolution (pralaye), the source of creation (bhūtabhāvana), they are absorbed (nirudhyante).

sanskrit: ततः संजायते गन्धस्ततः संजायते रसः ।ततः संजायते रूपं ततः स्पर्शोऽभिजायते ॥२५॥
transliteration: tataḥ saṁjāyate gandhastataḥ saṁjāyate rasaḥ ,tataḥ saṁjāyate rūpaṁ tataḥ sparśo'bhijāyate.
english: From that, smell comes into being; from that, taste comes into being. From that, form comes into being; from that, touch arises.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 20 Verse 25. From that, smell comes into being; from that, taste comes into being. From that, form comes into being; from that, touch arises.

sanskrit: ततः संजायते शब्दः संशयस्तत्र जायते ।ततः संजायते निष्ठा जन्मैतत्सप्तधा विदुः ॥२६॥
transliteration: tataḥ saṁjāyate śabdaḥ saṁśayastatra jāyate ,tataḥ saṁjāyate niṣṭhā janmaitatsaptadhā viduḥ.
english: From that, sound arises; then doubt (saṃśaya) comes into being there. From that, firm conviction (niṣṭhā) arises. They understand this birth as sevenfold.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 20 Verse 26. From that, sound arises; then doubt (saṃśaya) comes into being there. From that, firm conviction (niṣṭhā) arises. They understand this birth as sevenfold.

sanskrit: अनेनैव प्रकारेण प्रगृहीतं पुरातनैः ।पूर्णाहुतिभिरापूर्णास्तेऽभिपूर्यन्ति तेजसा ॥२७॥
transliteration: anenaiva prakāreṇa pragṛhītaṁ purātanaiḥ ,pūrṇāhutibhirāpūrṇāste'bhipūryanti tejasā.
english: Through this very method, it was grasped by the ancients. Those who are filled by full oblations become complete with divine radiance (tejas).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 20 Verse 27. Through this very method, it was grasped by the ancients. Those who are filled by full oblations become complete with divine radiance (tejas).

sanskrit: ब्राह्मण उवाच ।अत्राप्युदाहरन्तीममितिहासं पुरातनम् ।निबोध दशहोतॄणां विधानमिह यादृशम् ॥१॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇa uvāca ,atrāpyudāharantīmamitihāsaṁ purātanam ,nibodha daśahotṝṇāṁ vidhānamiha yādṛśam.
english: The Brahmin said: Here, they also relate this ancient traditional narrative (itihāsa). Understand the specific arrangement (vidhāna) of the ten Hotṛ priests as it is explained here.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 21 Verse 1. The Brahmin said: Here, they also relate this ancient traditional narrative (itihāsa). Understand the specific arrangement (vidhāna) of the ten Hotṛ priests as it is explained here.

sanskrit: सर्वमेवात्र विज्ञेयं चित्तं ज्ञानमवेक्षते ।रेतः शरीरभृत्काये विज्ञाता तु शरीरभृत् ॥२॥
transliteration: sarvamevātra vijñeyaṁ cittaṁ jñānamavekṣate ,retaḥ śarīrabhṛtkāye vijñātā tu śarīrabhṛt.
english: All this must be understood here: The mind (citta) perceives knowledge. Semen (retas) is present in the physical body of the living entity, but the knower (vijñātā) is indeed the true living entity.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 21 Verse 2. All this must be understood here: The mind (citta) perceives knowledge. Semen (retas) is present in the physical body of the living entity, but the knower (vijñātā) is indeed the true living entity.

sanskrit: शरीरभृद्गार्हपत्यस्तस्मादन्यः प्रणीयते ।ततश्चाहवनीयस्तु तस्मिन्संक्षिप्यते हविः ॥३॥
transliteration: śarīrabhṛdgārhapatyastasmādanyaḥ praṇīyate ,tataścāhavanīyastu tasminsaṁkṣipyate haviḥ.
english: The living entity (śarīrabhṛt) is likened to the Gārhapatya (ritual) fire. From that, another (fire) is brought forth, and that, indeed, is the Āhavanīya (ritual) fire, in which the oblation (haviḥ) is offered.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 21 Verse 3. The living entity (śarīrabhṛt) is likened to the Gārhapatya (ritual) fire. From that, another (fire) is brought forth, and that, indeed, is the Āhavanīya (ritual) fire, in which the oblation (haviḥ) is offered.

sanskrit: ततो वाचस्पतिर्जज्ञे समानः पर्यवेक्षते ।रूपं भवति वै व्यक्तं तदनुद्रवते मनः ॥४॥
transliteration: tato vācaspatirjajñe samānaḥ paryavekṣate ,rūpaṁ bhavati vai vyaktaṁ tadanudravate manaḥ.
english: From that (ritual), Vācaspati (Lord of Speech) was born. A universal (principle) observes. Form (rūpa) indeed becomes manifest, and the mind (manas) then follows after it.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 21 Verse 4. From that (ritual), Vācaspati (Lord of Speech) was born. A universal (principle) observes. Form (rūpa) indeed becomes manifest, and the mind (manas) then follows after it.

sanskrit: ब्राह्मण्युवाच ।कस्माद्वागभवत्पूर्वं कस्मात्पश्चान्मनोऽभवत् ।मनसा चिन्तितं वाक्यं यदा समभिपद्यते ॥५॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇyuvāca ,kasmādvāgabhavatpūrvaṁ kasmātpaścānmano'bhavat ,manasā cintitaṁ vākyaṁ yadā samabhipadyate.
english: The Brāhmaṇī asked: 'From what did speech (vāc) arise first, and from what did the mind (manas) arise later? (I ask this because I observe) when a statement conceived by the mind (manas) fully manifests.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 21 Verse 5. The Brāhmaṇī asked: 'From what did speech (vāc) arise first, and from what did the mind (manas) arise later? (I ask this because I observe) when a statement conceived by the mind (manas) fully manifests.'

sanskrit: केन विज्ञानयोगेन मतिश्चित्तं समास्थिता ।समुन्नीता नाध्यगच्छत्को वैनां प्रतिषेधति ॥६॥
transliteration: kena vijñānayogena matiścittaṁ samāsthitā ,samunnītā nādhyagacchatko vaināṁ pratiṣedhati.
english: By what application of spiritual discipline (vijñāna-yoga) does the intellect become established in the mind? Though elevated, it does not comprehend. Who, indeed, obstructs it?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 21 Verse 6. By what application of spiritual discipline (vijñāna-yoga) does the intellect become established in the mind? Though elevated, it does not comprehend. Who, indeed, obstructs it?

sanskrit: ब्राह्मण उवाच ।तामपानः पतिर्भूत्वा तस्मात्प्रेष्यत्यपानताम् ।तां मतिं मनसः प्राहुर्मनस्तस्मादवेक्षते ॥७॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇa uvāca ,tāmapānaḥ patirbhūtvā tasmātpreṣyatyapānatām ,tāṁ matiṁ manasaḥ prāhurmanastasmādavekṣate.
english: The Brahmin said: Having become its master, the downward vital air (apāna) directs it (the intellect) towards the state of apāna. They declare that intellect (mati) to be of the mind (manas); therefore, the mind oversees it.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 21 Verse 7. The Brahmin said: Having become its master, the downward vital air (apāna) directs it (the intellect) towards the state of apāna. They declare that intellect (mati) to be of the mind (manas); therefore, the mind oversees it.

sanskrit: प्रश्नं तु वाङ्मनसोर्मां यस्मात्त्वमनुपृच्छसि ।तस्मात्ते वर्तयिष्यामि तयोरेव समाह्वयम् ॥८॥
transliteration: praśnaṁ tu vāṅmanasormāṁ yasmāttvamanupṛcchasi ,tasmātte vartayiṣyāmi tayoreva samāhvayam.
english: But since you ask me this question concerning speech and mind, I will therefore explain to you their mutual interaction.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 21 Verse 8. But since you ask me this question concerning speech and mind, I will therefore explain to you their mutual interaction.

sanskrit: उभे वाङ्मनसी गत्वा भूतात्मानमपृच्छताम् ।आवयोः श्रेष्ठमाचक्ष्व छिन्धि नौ संशयं विभो ॥९॥
transliteration: ubhe vāṅmanasī gatvā bhūtātmānamapṛcchatām ,āvayoḥ śreṣṭhamācakṣva chindhi nau saṁśayaṁ vibho.
english: Both speech and mind approached the universal self (bhūtātman) and asked: 'Declare who is superior between us two, O Lord! Resolve our doubt.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 21 Verse 9. Both speech and mind approached the universal self (bhūtātman) and asked: 'Declare who is superior between us two, O Lord! Resolve our doubt.'

sanskrit: मन इत्येव भगवांस्तदा प्राह सरस्वतीम् ।अहं वै कामधुक्तुभ्यमिति तं प्राह वागथ ॥१०॥
transliteration: mana ityeva bhagavāṁstadā prāha sarasvatīm ,ahaṁ vai kāmadhuktubhyamiti taṁ prāha vāgatha.
english: Thus, the venerable Lord then spoke to Sarasvati, saying, 'You are indeed the mind (manas).' Then, Speech (Vāc) said to him, 'I am indeed the fulfiller of desires for you.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 21 Verse 10. Thus, the venerable Lord then spoke to Sarasvati, saying, 'You are indeed the mind (manas).' Then, Speech (Vāc) said to him, 'I am indeed the fulfiller of desires for you.'

sanskrit: स्थावरं जङ्गमं चैव विद्ध्युभे मनसी मम ।स्थावरं मत्सकाशे वै जङ्गमं विषये तव ॥११॥
transliteration: sthāvaraṁ jaṅgamaṁ caiva viddhyubhe manasī mama ,sthāvaraṁ matsakāśe vai jaṅgamaṁ viṣaye tava.
english: Know both the immovable and the movable aspects of my mind (manas). The immovable aspect is indeed with me, while the movable is within your domain.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 21 Verse 11. Know both the immovable and the movable aspects of my mind (manas). The immovable aspect is indeed with me, while the movable is within your domain.

sanskrit: यस्तु ते विषयं गच्छेन्मन्त्रो वर्णः स्वरोऽपि वा ।तन्मनो जङ्गमं नाम तस्मादसि गरीयसी ॥१२॥
transliteration: yastu te viṣayaṁ gacchenmantro varṇaḥ svaro'pi vā ,tanmano jaṅgamaṁ nāma tasmādasi garīyasī.
english: Whatever mantra (mantra), syllable, or even tone enters your domain, that is indeed called the movable mind (manas). Therefore, you are superior.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 21 Verse 12. Whatever mantra (mantra), syllable, or even tone enters your domain, that is indeed called the movable mind (manas). Therefore, you are superior.

sanskrit: यस्मादसि च मा वोचः स्वयमभ्येत्य शोभने ।तस्मादुच्छ्वासमासाद्य न वक्ष्यसि सरस्वति ॥१३॥
transliteration: yasmādasi ca mā vocaḥ svayamabhyetya śobhane ,tasmāducchvāsamāsādya na vakṣyasi sarasvati.
english: And since you, O beautiful one, having come [to me], do not speak on your own, therefore, O Sarasvati, you will not speak after taking a breath (or after a pause).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 21 Verse 13. And since you, O beautiful one, having come [to me], do not speak on your own, therefore, O Sarasvati, you will not speak after taking a breath (or after a pause).

sanskrit: प्राणापानान्तरे देवी वाग्वै नित्यं स्म तिष्ठति ।प्रेर्यमाणा महाभागे विना प्राणमपानती ।प्रजापतिमुपाधावत्प्रसीद भगवन्निति ॥१४॥
transliteration: prāṇāpānāntare devī vāgvai nityaṁ sma tiṣṭhati ,preryamāṇā mahābhāge vinā prāṇamapānatī ,prajāpatimupādhāvatprasīda bhagavanniti.
english: O most fortunate one, the divine speech (vāc) always resides between the life-breath (prāṇa) and downward breath (apāna). Being impelled, yet exhaling without proper inhalation (prāṇa), she approached Prajapati, saying, "Be gracious, O Lord!"
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 21 Verse 14. O most fortunate one, the divine speech (vāc) always resides between the life-breath (prāṇa) and downward breath (apāna). Being impelled, yet exhaling without proper inhalation (prāṇa), she approached Prajapati, saying, "Be gracious, O Lord!"

sanskrit: ततः प्राणः प्रादुरभूद्वाचमाप्याययन्पुनः ।तस्मादुच्छ्वासमासाद्य न वाग्वदति कर्हिचित् ॥१५॥
transliteration: tataḥ prāṇaḥ prādurabhūdvācamāpyāyayanpunaḥ ,tasmāducchvāsamāsādya na vāgvadati karhicit.
english: Then the life-breath (prāṇa) appeared, nourishing speech (vāc) again. Therefore, it is precisely when mere exhalation (ucchvāsa) is attained that speech (vāc) never speaks (on its own).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 21 Verse 15. Then the life-breath (prāṇa) appeared, nourishing speech (vāc) again. Therefore, it is precisely when mere exhalation (ucchvāsa) is attained that speech (vāc) never speaks (on its own).

sanskrit: घोषिणी जातनिर्घोषा नित्यमेव प्रवर्तते ।तयोरपि च घोषिण्योर्निर्घोषैव गरीयसी ॥१६॥
transliteration: ghoṣiṇī jātanirghoṣā nityameva pravartate ,tayorapi ca ghoṣiṇyornirghoṣaiva garīyasī.
english: Speech (Vāc), in its form with sound and having become soundless, always functions. And even among these two aspects, the soundless one is indeed more significant.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 21 Verse 16. Speech (Vāc), in its form with sound and having become soundless, always functions. And even among these two aspects, the soundless one is indeed more significant.

sanskrit: गौरिव प्रस्रवत्येषा रसमुत्तमशालिनी ।सततं स्यन्दते ह्येषा शाश्वतं ब्रह्मवादिनी ॥१७॥
transliteration: gauriva prasravatyeṣā rasamuttamaśālinī ,satataṁ syandate hyeṣā śāśvataṁ brahmavādinī.
english: This (speech), endowed with supreme excellence, flows forth essence like a cow. Indeed, she constantly streams, articulating the eternal (brahman).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 21 Verse 17. This (speech), endowed with supreme excellence, flows forth essence like a cow. Indeed, she constantly streams, articulating the eternal (brahman).

sanskrit: दिव्यादिव्यप्रभावेन भारती गौः शुचिस्मिते ।एतयोरन्तरं पश्य सूक्ष्मयोः स्यन्दमानयोः ॥१८॥
transliteration: divyādivyaprabhāvena bhāratī gauḥ śucismite ,etayorantaraṁ paśya sūkṣmayoḥ syandamānayoḥ.
english: O pure-smiling one! Observe the subtle distinction between these two, Bhāratī (Speech) and Gauḥ (Sound), as they manifest, endowed with both divine and non-divine power.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 21 Verse 18. O pure-smiling one! Observe the subtle distinction between these two, Bhāratī (Speech) and Gauḥ (Sound), as they manifest, endowed with both divine and non-divine power.

sanskrit: अनुत्पन्नेषु वाक्येषु चोद्यमाना सिसृक्षया ।किं नु पूर्वं ततो देवी व्याजहार सरस्वती ॥१९॥
transliteration: anutpanneṣu vākyeṣu codyamānā sisṛkṣayā ,kiṁ nu pūrvaṁ tato devī vyājahāra sarasvatī.
english: Then, impelled by the desire to create even in regard to words yet unproduced, the goddess (devī) Sarasvatī declared: 'What indeed was prior?'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 21 Verse 19. Then, impelled by the desire to create even in regard to words yet unproduced, the goddess (devī) Sarasvatī declared: 'What indeed was prior?'

sanskrit: प्राणेन या संभवते शरीरे प्राणादपानं प्रतिपद्यते च ।उदानभूता च विसृज्य देहं व्यानेन सर्वं दिवमावृणोति ॥२०॥
transliteration: prāṇena yā saṁbhavate śarīre; prāṇādapānaṁ pratipadyate ca ,udānabhūtā ca visṛjya dehaṁ; vyānena sarvaṁ divamāvṛṇoti.
english: That (speech) which originates in the body through the life-breath (prāṇa), and from (prāṇa) reaches the downward breath (apāna). And having become the upward breath (udāna), relinquishing the body, it pervades the entire heavens through the circulating breath (vyāna).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 21 Verse 20. That (speech) which originates in the body through the life-breath (prāṇa), and from (prāṇa) reaches the downward breath (apāna). And having become the upward breath (udāna), relinquishing the body, it pervades the entire heavens through the circulating breath (vyāna).

sanskrit: ततः समाने प्रतितिष्ठतीह इत्येव पूर्वं प्रजजल्प चापि ।तस्मान्मनः स्थावरत्वाद्विशिष्टं तथा देवी जङ्गमत्वाद्विशिष्टा ॥२१॥
transliteration: tataḥ samāne pratitiṣṭhatīha; ityeva pūrvaṁ prajajalpa cāpi ,tasmānmanaḥ sthāvaratvādviśiṣṭaṁ; tathā devī jaṅgamatvādviśiṣṭā.
english: Then it (speech) abides here in the equalizing breath (samāna). Thus indeed she spoke earlier. Therefore, the mind (manas) is distinguished by its stillness, and similarly, the goddess (devī) is distinguished by her mobility.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 21 Verse 21. Then it (speech) abides here in the equalizing breath (samāna). Thus indeed she spoke earlier. Therefore, the mind (manas) is distinguished by its stillness, and similarly, the goddess (devī) is distinguished by her mobility.

sanskrit: ब्राह्मण उवाच ।अत्राप्युदाहरन्तीममितिहासं पुरातनम् ।सुभगे सप्तहोतॄणां विधानमिह यादृशम् ॥१॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇa uvāca ,atrāpyudāharantīmamitihāsaṁ purātanam ,subhage saptahotṝṇāṁ vidhānamiha yādṛśam.
english: The Brahmin said: In this context, they also relate this ancient narrative, O beautiful one, concerning the nature of the seven offerers (hotṛs) here.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 1. The Brahmin said: In this context, they also relate this ancient narrative, O beautiful one, concerning the nature of the seven offerers (hotṛs) here.

sanskrit: घ्राणं चक्षुश्च जिह्वा च त्वक्श्रोत्रं चैव पञ्चमम् ।मनो बुद्धिश्च सप्तैते होतारः पृथगाश्रिताः ॥२॥
transliteration: ghrāṇaṁ cakṣuśca jihvā ca tvakśrotraṁ caiva pañcamam ,mano buddhiśca saptaite hotāraḥ pṛthagāśritāḥ.
english: The sense of smell, the eye, the tongue, the skin, and the ear as the fifth; the mind (manas) and the intellect (buddhi) - these seven are the offerers (hotṛs), each independently situated.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 2. The sense of smell, the eye, the tongue, the skin, and the ear as the fifth; the mind (manas) and the intellect (buddhi) - these seven are the offerers (hotṛs), each independently situated.

sanskrit: सूक्ष्मेऽवकाशे सन्तस्ते न पश्यन्तीतरेतरम् ।एतान्वै सप्तहोतॄंस्त्वं स्वभावाद्विद्धि शोभने ॥३॥
transliteration: sūkṣme'vakāśe santaste na paśyantītaretaram ,etānvai saptahotṝṁstvaṁ svabhāvādviddhi śobhane.
english: Existing in a subtle space, they do not perceive each other. Therefore, O beautiful one, know these seven offerers (hotṛs) according to their intrinsic nature (svabhāva).
