search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 3. Existing in a subtle space, they do not perceive each other. Therefore, O beautiful one, know these seven offerers (hotṛs) according to their intrinsic nature (svabhāva).

sanskrit: ब्राह्मण्युवाच ।सूक्ष्मेऽवकाशे सन्तस्ते कथं नान्योन्यदर्शिनः ।कथंस्वभावा भगवन्नेतदाचक्ष्व मे विभो ॥४॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇyuvāca ,sūkṣme'vakāśe santaste kathaṁ nānyonyadarśinaḥ ,kathaṁsvabhāvā bhagavannetadācakṣva me vibho.
english: The Brahmini said: Existing in a subtle space, how is it that they do not perceive each other? What is their intrinsic nature (svabhāva), O venerable one? Explain this to me, O lord (vibhu).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 4. The Brahmini said: Existing in a subtle space, how is it that they do not perceive each other? What is their intrinsic nature (svabhāva), O venerable one? Explain this to me, O lord (vibhu).

sanskrit: ब्राह्मण उवाच ।गुणाज्ञानमविज्ञानं गुणिज्ञानमभिज्ञता ।परस्परगुणानेते न विजानन्ति कर्हिचित् ॥५॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇa uvāca ,guṇājñānamavijñānaṁ guṇijñānamabhijñatā ,parasparaguṇānete na vijānanti karhicit.
english: The Brahmin said: Not knowing attributes is a lack of understanding; knowing the possessor of attributes is true insight. These attributes and their possessors, however, never truly understand each other.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 5. The Brahmin said: Not knowing attributes is a lack of understanding; knowing the possessor of attributes is true insight. These attributes and their possessors, however, never truly understand each other.

sanskrit: जिह्वा चक्षुस्तथा श्रोत्रं त्वङ्मनो बुद्धिरेव च ।न गन्धानधिगच्छन्ति घ्राणस्तानधिगच्छति ॥६॥
transliteration: jihvā cakṣustathā śrotraṁ tvaṅmano buddhireva ca ,na gandhānadhigacchanti ghrāṇastānadhigacchati.
english: The tongue, eye, ear, skin, mind, and intellect - these do not perceive smells. Only the nose perceives them.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 6. The tongue, eye, ear, skin, mind, and intellect - these do not perceive smells. Only the nose perceives them.

sanskrit: घ्राणं चक्षुस्तथा श्रोत्रं त्वङ्मनो बुद्धिरेव च ।न रसानधिगच्छन्ति जिह्वा तानधिगच्छति ॥७॥
transliteration: ghrāṇaṁ cakṣustathā śrotraṁ tvaṅmano buddhireva ca ,na rasānadhigacchanti jihvā tānadhigacchati.
english: The nose, eye, ear, skin, mind, and intellect - these do not perceive tastes. Only the tongue perceives them.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 7. The nose, eye, ear, skin, mind, and intellect - these do not perceive tastes. Only the tongue perceives them.

sanskrit: घ्राणं जिह्वा तथा श्रोत्रं त्वङ्मनो बुद्धिरेव च ।न रूपाण्यधिगच्छन्ति चक्षुस्तान्यधिगच्छति ॥८॥
transliteration: ghrāṇaṁ jihvā tathā śrotraṁ tvaṅmano buddhireva ca ,na rūpāṇyadhigacchanti cakṣustānyadhigacchati.
english: The nose, tongue, ear, skin, mind, and intellect - these do not perceive forms. Only the eye perceives them.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 8. The nose, tongue, ear, skin, mind, and intellect - these do not perceive forms. Only the eye perceives them.

sanskrit: घ्राणं जिह्वा च चक्षुश्च श्रोत्रं बुद्धिर्मनस्तथा ।न स्पर्शानधिगच्छन्ति त्वक्च तानधिगच्छति ॥९॥
transliteration: ghrāṇaṁ jihvā ca cakṣuśca śrotraṁ buddhirmanastathā ,na sparśānadhigacchanti tvakca tānadhigacchati.
english: The nose, tongue, eye, ear, intellect, and mind do not perceive tactile sensations. The skin, however, perceives them.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 9. The nose, tongue, eye, ear, intellect, and mind do not perceive tactile sensations. The skin, however, perceives them.

sanskrit: घ्राणं जिह्वा च चक्षुश्च त्वङ्मनो बुद्धिरेव च ।न शब्दानधिगच्छन्ति श्रोत्रं तानधिगच्छति ॥१०॥
transliteration: ghrāṇaṁ jihvā ca cakṣuśca tvaṅmano buddhireva ca ,na śabdānadhigacchanti śrotraṁ tānadhigacchati.
english: The nose, tongue, eye, skin, mind, and intellect do not perceive sounds. The ear, however, perceives them.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 10. The nose, tongue, eye, skin, mind, and intellect do not perceive sounds. The ear, however, perceives them.

sanskrit: घ्राणं जिह्वा च चक्षुश्च त्वक्श्रोत्रं बुद्धिरेव च ।संशयान्नाधिगच्छन्ति मनस्तानधिगच्छति ॥११॥
transliteration: ghrāṇaṁ jihvā ca cakṣuśca tvakśrotraṁ buddhireva ca ,saṁśayānnādhigacchanti manastānadhigacchati.
english: The nose, tongue, eye, skin, ear, and intellect do not perceive doubts. The mind, however, perceives them.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 11. The nose, tongue, eye, skin, ear, and intellect do not perceive doubts. The mind, however, perceives them.

sanskrit: घ्राणं जिह्वा च चक्षुश्च त्वक्श्रोत्रं मन एव च ।न निष्ठामधिगच्छन्ति बुद्धिस्तामधिगच्छति ॥१२॥
transliteration: ghrāṇaṁ jihvā ca cakṣuśca tvakśrotraṁ mana eva ca ,na niṣṭhāmadhigacchanti buddhistāmadhigacchati.
english: The nose, tongue, eye, skin, ear, and mind do not perceive ultimate conclusions or resolute conviction. The intellect, however, perceives it.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 12. The nose, tongue, eye, skin, ear, and mind do not perceive ultimate conclusions or resolute conviction. The intellect, however, perceives it.

sanskrit: अत्राप्युदाहरन्तीममितिहासं पुरातनम् ।इन्द्रियाणां च संवादं मनसश्चैव भामिनि ॥१३॥
transliteration: atrāpyudāharantīmamitihāsaṁ purātanam ,indriyāṇāṁ ca saṁvādaṁ manasaścaiva bhāmini.
english: O radiant one, here they also narrate this ancient story, a dialogue between the senses (indriya) and the mind (manas).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 13. O radiant one, here they also narrate this ancient story, a dialogue between the senses (indriya) and the mind (manas).

sanskrit: मन उवाच ।न घ्राति मामृते घ्राणं रसं जिह्वा न बुध्यते ।रूपं चक्षुर्न गृह्णाति त्वक्स्पर्शं नावबुध्यते ॥१४॥
transliteration: mana uvāca ,na ghrāti māmṛte ghrāṇaṁ rasaṁ jihvā na budhyate ,rūpaṁ cakṣurna gṛhṇāti tvaksparśaṁ nāvabudhyate.
english: The mind (manas) spoke: 'Without me, the nose (ghrāṇa) does not smell, the tongue (jihvā) does not perceive taste, the eye (cakṣus) does not grasp form, and the skin (tvak) does not comprehend touch.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 14. The mind (manas) spoke: 'Without me, the nose (ghrāṇa) does not smell, the tongue (jihvā) does not perceive taste, the eye (cakṣus) does not grasp form, and the skin (tvak) does not comprehend touch.'

sanskrit: न श्रोत्रं बुध्यते शब्दं मया हीनं कथंचन ।प्रवरं सर्वभूतानामहमस्मि सनातनम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: na śrotraṁ budhyate śabdaṁ mayā hīnaṁ kathaṁcana ,pravaraṁ sarvabhūtānāmahamasmi sanātanam.
english: The ear (śrotra) does not perceive sound in any way without me. I am the foremost and eternal among all beings (sarvabhūta).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 15. The ear (śrotra) does not perceive sound in any way without me. I am the foremost and eternal among all beings (sarvabhūta).

sanskrit: अगाराणीव शून्यानि शान्तार्चिष इवाग्नयः ।इन्द्रियाणि न भासन्ते मया हीनानि नित्यशः ॥१६॥
transliteration: agārāṇīva śūnyāni śāntārciṣa ivāgnayaḥ ,indriyāṇi na bhāsante mayā hīnāni nityaśaḥ.
english: Without me, the senses (indriya) do not shine, always remaining like empty houses or like fires with extinguished flames.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 16. Without me, the senses (indriya) do not shine, always remaining like empty houses or like fires with extinguished flames.

sanskrit: काष्ठानीवार्द्रशुष्काणि यतमानैरपीन्द्रियैः ।गुणार्थान्नाधिगच्छन्ति मामृते सर्वजन्तवः ॥१७॥
transliteration: kāṣṭhānīvārdraśuṣkāṇi yatamānairapīndriyaiḥ ,guṇārthānnādhigacchanti māmṛte sarvajantavaḥ.
english: Just as wet and dry pieces of wood cannot perceive objects, so too all beings, even while striving with their senses, do not grasp the objects of the senses without Me.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 17. Just as wet and dry pieces of wood cannot perceive objects, so too all beings, even while striving with their senses, do not grasp the objects of the senses without Me.

sanskrit: इन्द्रियाण्यूचुः ।एवमेतद्भवेत्सत्यं यथैतन्मन्यते भवान् ।ऋतेऽस्मानस्मदर्थांस्तु भोगान्भुङ्क्ते भवान्यदि ॥१८॥
transliteration: indriyāṇyūcuḥ ,evametadbhavetsatyaṁ yathaitanmanyate bhavān ,ṛte'smānasmadarthāṁstu bhogānbhuṅkte bhavānyadi.
english: The senses said: "This would indeed be true, just as You believe, if You were to enjoy objects of gratification intended for us, without us."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 18. The senses said: "This would indeed be true, just as You believe, if You were to enjoy objects of gratification intended for us, without us."

sanskrit: यद्यस्मासु प्रलीनेषु तर्पणं प्राणधारणम् ।भोगान्भुङ्क्षे रसान्भुङ्क्षे यथैतन्मन्यते तथा ॥१९॥
transliteration: yadyasmāsu pralīneṣu tarpaṇaṁ prāṇadhāraṇam ,bhogānbhuṅkṣe rasānbhuṅkṣe yathaitanmanyate tathā.
english: If, when we (the senses) are absorbed and inactive, You can sustain life by enjoying nourishment, and experience gratifications and tastes, then it is just as You believe.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 19. If, when we (the senses) are absorbed and inactive, You can sustain life by enjoying nourishment, and experience gratifications and tastes, then it is just as You believe.

sanskrit: अथ वास्मासु लीनेषु तिष्ठत्सु विषयेषु च ।यदि संकल्पमात्रेण भुङ्क्ते भोगान्यथार्थवत् ॥२०॥
transliteration: atha vāsmāsu līneṣu tiṣṭhatsu viṣayeṣu ca ,yadi saṁkalpamātreṇa bhuṅkte bhogānyathārthavat.
english: Or else, if, when we (the senses) are withdrawn but the sense objects remain present, You could still truly enjoy objects of gratification merely by an act of will, then Your assertion would be correct.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 20. Or else, if, when we (the senses) are withdrawn but the sense objects remain present, You could still truly enjoy objects of gratification merely by an act of will, then Your assertion would be correct.

sanskrit: अथ चेन्मन्यसे सिद्धिमस्मदर्थेषु नित्यदा ।घ्राणेन रूपमादत्स्व रसमादत्स्व चक्षुषा ॥२१॥
transliteration: atha cenmanyase siddhimasmadartheṣu nityadā ,ghrāṇena rūpamādatsva rasamādatsva cakṣuṣā.
english: Now, if you always assume success in our endeavors, then apprehend form with the nose, and apprehend taste with the eye.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 21. Now, if you always assume success in our endeavors, then apprehend form with the nose, and apprehend taste with the eye.

sanskrit: श्रोत्रेण गन्धमादत्स्व निष्ठामादत्स्व जिह्वया ।त्वचा च शब्दमादत्स्व बुद्ध्या स्पर्शमथापि च ॥२२॥
transliteration: śrotreṇa gandhamādatsva niṣṭhāmādatsva jihvayā ,tvacā ca śabdamādatsva buddhyā sparśamathāpi ca.
english: Apprehend smell with the ear, and apprehend resolution with the tongue. And with the skin, apprehend sound; and furthermore, apprehend touch with the intellect.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 22. Apprehend smell with the ear, and apprehend resolution with the tongue. And with the skin, apprehend sound; and furthermore, apprehend touch with the intellect.

sanskrit: बलवन्तो ह्यनियमा नियमा दुर्बलीयसाम् ।भोगानपूर्वानादत्स्व नोच्छिष्टं भोक्तुमर्हसि ॥२३॥
transliteration: balavanto hyaniyamā niyamā durbalīyasām ,bhogānapūrvānādatsva nocchiṣṭaṁ bhoktumarhasi.
english: Indeed, there are no restrictions for the strong; restrictions are for the weaker ones. Therefore, acquire fresh enjoyments; you should not partake of leftovers.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 23. Indeed, there are no restrictions for the strong; restrictions are for the weaker ones. Therefore, acquire fresh enjoyments; you should not partake of leftovers.

sanskrit: यथा हि शिष्यः शास्तारं श्रुत्यर्थमभिधावति ।ततः श्रुतमुपादाय श्रुतार्थमुपतिष्ठति ॥२४॥
transliteration: yathā hi śiṣyaḥ śāstāraṁ śrutyarthamabhidhāvati ,tataḥ śrutamupādāya śrutārthamupatiṣṭhati.
english: Indeed, just as a student approaches the teacher for the meaning of the teaching, so too, having taken up what was heard, he then comprehends the intended meaning.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 24. Indeed, just as a student approaches the teacher for the meaning of the teaching, so too, having taken up what was heard, he then comprehends the intended meaning.

sanskrit: विषयानेवमस्माभिर्दर्शितानभिमन्यसे ।अनागतानतीतांश्च स्वप्ने जागरणे तथा ॥२५॥
transliteration: viṣayānevamasmābhirdarśitānabhimanyase ,anāgatānatītāṁśca svapne jāgaraṇe tathā.
english: You acknowledge these objects of experience, as we have thus presented them, just as you do with past and future objects of experience, whether in a dream or in the waking state.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 25. You acknowledge these objects of experience, as we have thus presented them, just as you do with past and future objects of experience, whether in a dream or in the waking state.

sanskrit: वैमनस्यं गतानां च जन्तूनामल्पचेतसाम् ।अस्मदर्थे कृते कार्ये दृश्यते प्राणधारणम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: vaimanasyaṁ gatānāṁ ca jantūnāmalpacetasām ,asmadarthe kṛte kārye dṛśyate prāṇadhāraṇam.
english: And for beings, even those dejected and of small intellect, the very maintenance of life (prāṇa) is observed through actions performed for our benefit.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 26. And for beings, even those dejected and of small intellect, the very maintenance of life (prāṇa) is observed through actions performed for our benefit.

sanskrit: बहूनपि हि संकल्पान्मत्वा स्वप्नानुपास्य च ।बुभुक्षया पीड्यमानो विषयानेव धावसि ॥२७॥
transliteration: bahūnapi hi saṁkalpānmatvā svapnānupāsya ca ,bubhukṣayā pīḍyamāno viṣayāneva dhāvasi.
english: Indeed, having considered many resolutions and having resorted to dreams, being tormented by hunger, you run only to sense-objects.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 27. Indeed, having considered many resolutions and having resorted to dreams, being tormented by hunger, you run only to sense-objects.

sanskrit: अगारमद्वारमिव प्रविश्य संकल्पभोगो विषयानविन्दन् ।प्राणक्षये शान्तिमुपैति नित्यं दारुक्षयेऽग्निर्ज्वलितो यथैव ॥२८॥
transliteration: agāramadvāramiva praviśya; saṁkalpabhogo viṣayānavindan ,prāṇakṣaye śāntimupaiti nityaṁ; dārukṣaye'gnirjvalito yathaiva.
english: One whose enjoyment is (derived solely from) mental resolutions, having entered (existence) as if into a house without a door, and not finding (actual) sense-objects, attains cessation (śānti) permanently upon the exhaustion of life (prāṇa), just as a burning fire (is extinguished) upon the depletion of its wood (fuel).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 28. One whose enjoyment is (derived solely from) mental resolutions, having entered (existence) as if into a house without a door, and not finding (actual) sense-objects, attains cessation (śānti) permanently upon the exhaustion of life (prāṇa), just as a burning fire (is extinguished) upon the depletion of its wood (fuel).

sanskrit: कामं तु नः स्वेषु गुणेषु सङ्गः कामं च नान्योन्यगुणोपलब्धिः ।अस्मानृते नास्ति तवोपलब्धिस्त्वामप्यृतेऽस्मान्न भजेत हर्षः ॥२९॥
transliteration: kāmaṁ tu naḥ sveṣu guṇeṣu saṅgaḥ; kāmaṁ ca nānyonyaguṇopalabdhiḥ ,asmānṛte nāsti tavopalabdhi;stvāmapyṛte'smānna bhajeta harṣaḥ.
english: Indeed, we are attached to our own intrinsic qualities (guṇa), and there is no mutual perception of qualities. Without us, there is no perception of you, and without you, joy would not come to us.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 22 Verse 29. Indeed, we are attached to our own intrinsic qualities (guṇa), and there is no mutual perception of qualities. Without us, there is no perception of you, and without you, joy would not come to us.

sanskrit: ब्राह्मण उवाच ।अत्राप्युदाहरन्तीममितिहासं पुरातनम् ।सुभगे पञ्चहोतॄणां विधानमिह यादृशम् ॥१॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇa uvāca ,atrāpyudāharantīmamitihāsaṁ purātanam ,subhage pañcahotṝṇāṁ vidhānamiha yādṛśam.
english: The brahmin said: "In this regard, they also cite this ancient narrative (itihāsa), O beautiful one, explaining how the procedure (vidhāna) of the five ritualists (pañcahotṛ) operates here."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 23 Verse 1. The brahmin said: "In this regard, they also cite this ancient narrative (itihāsa), O beautiful one, explaining how the procedure (vidhāna) of the five ritualists (pañcahotṛ) operates here."

sanskrit: प्राणापानावुदानश्च समानो व्यान एव च ।पञ्चहोतॄनथैतान्वै परं भावं विदुर्बुधाः ॥२॥
transliteration: prāṇāpānāvudānaśca samāno vyāna eva ca ,pañcahotṝnathaitānvai paraṁ bhāvaṁ vidurbudhāḥ.
english: Prāṇa, Apāna, Udāna, Samāna, and Vyāna - these (five vital breaths) are indeed known by the wise as the five ritualists (pañcahotṛ), and as the supreme state (parā bhāva).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 23 Verse 2. Prāṇa, Apāna, Udāna, Samāna, and Vyāna - these (five vital breaths) are indeed known by the wise as the five ritualists (pañcahotṛ), and as the supreme state (parā bhāva).

sanskrit: ब्राह्मण्युवाच ।स्वभावात्सप्त होतार इति ते पूर्विका मतिः ।यथा वै पञ्च होतारः परो भावस्तथोच्यताम् ॥३॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇyuvāca ,svabhāvātsapta hotāra iti te pūrvikā matiḥ ,yathā vai pañca hotāraḥ paro bhāvastathocyatām.
english: The brahmin woman said: "Your previous understanding (mati) was, 'There are seven ritualists (hotṛ) by their intrinsic nature (svabhāva).' Therefore, please explain how the five ritualists are indeed the supreme state (parā bhāva)."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 23 Verse 3. The brahmin woman said: "Your previous understanding (mati) was, 'There are seven ritualists (hotṛ) by their intrinsic nature (svabhāva).' Therefore, please explain how the five ritualists are indeed the supreme state (parā bhāva)."

sanskrit: ब्राह्मण उवाच ।प्राणेन संभृतो वायुरपानो जायते ततः ।अपाने संभृतो वायुस्ततो व्यानः प्रवर्तते ॥४॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇa uvāca ,prāṇena saṁbhṛto vāyurapāno jāyate tataḥ ,apāne saṁbhṛto vāyustato vyānaḥ pravartate.
english: The Brahmin said: "The air, sustained by the upward moving vital breath (prāṇa), then becomes the downward moving breath (apāna). The air, sustained within the downward moving breath (apāna), then operates as the pervasive breath (vyāna)."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 23 Verse 4. The Brahmin said: "The air, sustained by the upward moving vital breath (prāṇa), then becomes the downward moving breath (apāna). The air, sustained within the downward moving breath (apāna), then operates as the pervasive breath (vyāna)."

sanskrit: व्यानेन संभृतो वायुस्ततोदानः प्रवर्तते ।उदाने संभृतो वायुः समानः संप्रवर्तते ॥५॥
transliteration: vyānena saṁbhṛto vāyustatodānaḥ pravartate ,udāne saṁbhṛto vāyuḥ samānaḥ saṁpravartate.
english: The air, sustained by the pervasive breath (vyāna), then operates as the upward moving breath (udāna). The air, sustained within the upward moving breath (udāna), then functions as the balancing breath (samāna).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 23 Verse 5. The air, sustained by the pervasive breath (vyāna), then operates as the upward moving breath (udāna). The air, sustained within the upward moving breath (udāna), then functions as the balancing breath (samāna).

sanskrit: तेऽपृच्छन्त पुरा गत्वा पूर्वजातं प्रजापतिम् ।यो नो ज्येष्ठस्तमाचक्ष्व स नः श्रेष्ठो भविष्यति ॥६॥
transliteration: te'pṛcchanta purā gatvā pūrvajātaṁ prajāpatim ,yo no jyeṣṭhastamācakṣva sa naḥ śreṣṭho bhaviṣyati.
english: Having previously gone, they asked the primeval (prajāpati), the Lord of creatures: 'Declare to us who among us is the eldest, for he shall be the best among us.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 23 Verse 6. Having previously gone, they asked the primeval (prajāpati), the Lord of creatures: 'Declare to us who among us is the eldest, for he shall be the best among us.'

sanskrit: ब्रह्मोवाच ।यस्मिन्प्रलीने प्रलयं व्रजन्ति सर्वे प्राणाः प्राणभृतां शरीरे ।यस्मिन्प्रचीर्णे च पुनश्चरन्ति स वै श्रेष्ठो गच्छत यत्र कामः ॥७॥
transliteration: brahmovāca ,yasminpralīne pralayaṁ vrajanti; sarve prāṇāḥ prāṇabhṛtāṁ śarīre ,yasminpracīrṇe ca punaścaranti; sa vai śreṣṭho gacchata yatra kāmaḥ.
english: Brahma said: 'The one in whom all the vital breaths (prāṇa) within the bodies of living beings experience dissolution (pralaya) when he (the chief life force) ceases to function, and in whom they again become active when he functions – he is indeed the best. Go, wherever your inclination leads you.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 23 Verse 7. Brahma said: 'The one in whom all the vital breaths (prāṇa) within the bodies of living beings experience dissolution (pralaya) when he (the chief life force) ceases to function, and in whom they again become active when he functions – he is indeed the best. Go, wherever your inclination leads you.'

sanskrit: प्राण उवाच ।मयि प्रलीने प्रलयं व्रजन्ति सर्वे प्राणाः प्राणभृतां शरीरे ।मयि प्रचीर्णे च पुनश्चरन्ति श्रेष्ठो ह्यहं पश्यत मां प्रलीनम् ॥८॥
transliteration: prāṇa uvāca ,mayi pralīne pralayaṁ vrajanti; sarve prāṇāḥ prāṇabhṛtāṁ śarīre ,mayi pracīrṇe ca punaścaranti; śreṣṭho hyahaṁ paśyata māṁ pralīnam.
english: The vital breath (prāṇa) said: 'When I am absorbed, all the vital functions (prāṇas) in the bodies of living beings cease. And when I resume my activity, they become active again. Indeed, I am the foremost; behold me when I am absorbed.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 23 Verse 8. The vital breath (prāṇa) said: 'When I am absorbed, all the vital functions (prāṇas) in the bodies of living beings cease. And when I resume my activity, they become active again. Indeed, I am the foremost; behold me when I am absorbed.'

sanskrit: ब्राह्मण उवाच ।प्राणः प्रलीयत ततः पुनश्च प्रचचार ह ।समानश्चाप्युदानश्च वचोऽब्रूतां ततः शुभे ॥९॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇa uvāca ,prāṇaḥ pralīyata tataḥ punaśca pracacāra ha ,samānaścāpyudānaśca vaco'brūtāṁ tataḥ śubhe.
english: The brahmin said: 'The vital breath (prāṇa) was absorbed, and then it became active again. Thereupon, O auspicious one, Samāna and Udāna also spoke these words.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 23 Verse 9. The brahmin said: 'The vital breath (prāṇa) was absorbed, and then it became active again. Thereupon, O auspicious one, Samāna and Udāna also spoke these words.'

sanskrit: न त्वं सर्वमिदं व्याप्य तिष्ठसीह यथा वयम् ।न त्वं श्रेष्ठोऽसि नः प्राण अपानो हि वशे तव ।प्रचचार पुनः प्राणस्तमपानोऽभ्यभाषत ॥१०॥
transliteration: na tvaṁ sarvamidaṁ vyāpya tiṣṭhasīha yathā vayam ,na tvaṁ śreṣṭho'si naḥ prāṇa apāno hi vaśe tava ,pracacāra punaḥ prāṇastamapāno'bhyabhāṣata.
english: You do not pervade all this and exist here as we do. You are not the foremost among us, O vital breath (prāṇa). Indeed, Apāna is under your control. The vital breath (prāṇa) moved again, and Apāna addressed him.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 23 Verse 10. You do not pervade all this and exist here as we do. You are not the foremost among us, O vital breath (prāṇa). Indeed, Apāna is under your control. The vital breath (prāṇa) moved again, and Apāna addressed him.

sanskrit: मयि प्रलीने प्रलयं व्रजन्ति सर्वे प्राणाः प्राणभृतां शरीरे ।मयि प्रचीर्णे च पुनश्चरन्ति श्रेष्ठो ह्यहं पश्यत मां प्रलीनम् ॥११॥
transliteration: mayi pralīne pralayaṁ vrajanti; sarve prāṇāḥ prāṇabhṛtāṁ śarīre ,mayi pracīrṇe ca punaścaranti; śreṣṭho hyahaṁ paśyata māṁ pralīnam.
english: When I am absorbed, all the vital functions (prāṇas) in the bodies of living beings cease. And when I resume my activity, they become active again. Indeed, I am the foremost; behold me when I am absorbed.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 23 Verse 11. When I am absorbed, all the vital functions (prāṇas) in the bodies of living beings cease. And when I resume my activity, they become active again. Indeed, I am the foremost; behold me when I am absorbed.

sanskrit: व्यानश्च तमुदानश्च भाषमाणमथोचतुः ।अपान न त्वं श्रेष्ठोऽसि प्राणो हि वशगस्तव ॥१२॥
transliteration: vyānaśca tamudānaśca bhāṣamāṇamathocatuḥ ,apāna na tvaṁ śreṣṭho'si prāṇo hi vaśagastava.
english: Vyana and Udana then said to him (Apana), while he was speaking, 'O Apana, you are not the best, for the vital breath (prāṇa) is indeed under your control.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 23 Verse 12. Vyana and Udana then said to him (Apana), while he was speaking, 'O Apana, you are not the best, for the vital breath (prāṇa) is indeed under your control.'

sanskrit: अपानः प्रचचाराथ व्यानस्तं पुनरब्रवीत् ।श्रेष्ठोऽहमस्मि सर्वेषां श्रूयतां येन हेतुना ॥१३॥
transliteration: apānaḥ pracacārātha vyānastaṁ punarabravīt ,śreṣṭho'hamasmi sarveṣāṁ śrūyatāṁ yena hetunā.
english: Apana then departed. Vyana, in turn, said to him, 'I am the best of all; listen to the reason why.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 23 Verse 13. Apana then departed. Vyana, in turn, said to him, 'I am the best of all; listen to the reason why.'

sanskrit: मयि प्रलीने प्रलयं व्रजन्ति सर्वे प्राणाः प्राणभृतां शरीरे ।मयि प्रचीर्णे च पुनश्चरन्ति श्रेष्ठो ह्यहं पश्यत मां प्रलीनम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: mayi pralīne pralayaṁ vrajanti; sarve prāṇāḥ prāṇabhṛtāṁ śarīre ,mayi pracīrṇe ca punaścaranti; śreṣṭho hyahaṁ paśyata māṁ pralīnam.
english: When I (Vyana) cease, all vital airs (prāṇas) in the body of living beings attain cessation. And when I circulate again, they function again. I am indeed the best! Behold me when I have ceased!
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 23 Verse 14. When I (Vyana) cease, all vital airs (prāṇas) in the body of living beings attain cessation. And when I circulate again, they function again. I am indeed the best! Behold me when I have ceased!

sanskrit: प्रालीयत ततो व्यानः पुनश्च प्रचचार ह ।प्राणापानावुदानश्च समानश्च तमब्रुवन् ।न त्वं श्रेष्ठोऽसि नो व्यान समानो हि वशे तव ॥१५॥
transliteration: prālīyata tato vyānaḥ punaśca pracacāra ha ,prāṇāpānāvudānaśca samānaśca tamabruvan ,na tvaṁ śreṣṭho'si no vyāna samāno hi vaśe tava.
english: Then Vyana ceased, and indeed, he functioned again. Prana, Apana, Udana, and Samana then said to him, 'O Vyana, you are not the best of us, for Samana is indeed under your control.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 23 Verse 15. Then Vyana ceased, and indeed, he functioned again. Prana, Apana, Udana, and Samana then said to him, 'O Vyana, you are not the best of us, for Samana is indeed under your control.'

sanskrit: प्रचचार पुनर्व्यानः समानः पुनरब्रवीत् ।श्रेष्ठोऽहमस्मि सर्वेषां श्रूयतां येन हेतुना ॥१६॥
transliteration: pracacāra punarvyānaḥ samānaḥ punarabravīt ,śreṣṭho'hamasmi sarveṣāṁ śrūyatāṁ yena hetunā.
english: Vyāna circulated again, and Samāna spoke again: 'I am the most superior among all; let the reason for this be heard.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 23 Verse 16. Vyāna circulated again, and Samāna spoke again: 'I am the most superior among all; let the reason for this be heard.'

sanskrit: मयि प्रलीने प्रलयं व्रजन्ति सर्वे प्राणाः प्राणभृतां शरीरे ।मयि प्रचीर्णे च पुनश्चरन्ति श्रेष्ठो ह्यहं पश्यत मां प्रलीनम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: mayi pralīne pralayaṁ vrajanti; sarve prāṇāḥ prāṇabhṛtāṁ śarīre ,mayi pracīrṇe ca punaścaranti; śreṣṭho hyahaṁ paśyata māṁ pralīnam.
english: When I am absorbed, all the vital airs (prāṇa) in the bodies of living beings go to dissolution. And when I am again active, they too become active again. For I am the most superior; behold me absorbed!
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 23 Verse 17. When I am absorbed, all the vital airs (prāṇa) in the bodies of living beings go to dissolution. And when I am again active, they too become active again. For I am the most superior; behold me absorbed!

sanskrit: ततः समानः प्रालिल्ये पुनश्च प्रचचार ह ।प्राणापानावुदानश्च व्यानश्चैव तमब्रुवन् ।समान न त्वं श्रेष्ठोऽसि व्यान एव वशे तव ॥१८॥
transliteration: tataḥ samānaḥ prālilye punaśca pracacāra ha ,prāṇāpānāvudānaśca vyānaścaiva tamabruvan ,samāna na tvaṁ śreṣṭho'si vyāna eva vaśe tava.
english: Then samāna (prāṇa) dissolved and indeed circulated again. Prāṇa, apāna, udāna, and vyāna then said to him: 'O Samāna, you are not the most superior; only vyāna is under your control.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 23 Verse 18. Then samāna (prāṇa) dissolved and indeed circulated again. Prāṇa, apāna, udāna, and vyāna then said to him: 'O Samāna, you are not the most superior; only vyāna is under your control.'

sanskrit: समानः प्रचचाराथ उदानस्तमुवाच ह ।श्रेष्ठोऽहमस्मि सर्वेषां श्रूयतां येन हेतुना ॥१९॥

english: Samāna then circulated. Thereupon, udāna (prāṇa) said to him: 'I am the most superior among all; let the reason for this be heard.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 23 Verse 19. Samāna then circulated. Thereupon, udāna (prāṇa) said to him: 'I am the most superior among all; let the reason for this be heard.'

sanskrit: मयि प्रलीने प्रलयं व्रजन्ति सर्वे प्राणाः प्राणभृतां शरीरे ।मयि प्रचीर्णे च पुनश्चरन्ति श्रेष्ठो ह्यहं पश्यत मां प्रलीनम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: mayi pralīne pralayaṁ vrajanti; sarve prāṇāḥ prāṇabhṛtāṁ śarīre ,mayi pracīrṇe ca punaścaranti; śreṣṭho hyahaṁ paśyata māṁ pralīnam.
english: When I (the chief vital breath) am absorbed, all vital breaths (prāṇa) in the bodies of living beings go to dissolution. And when I am again active, they move once more. Indeed, I am the most excellent; behold me even when absorbed.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 23 Verse 20. When I (the chief vital breath) am absorbed, all vital breaths (prāṇa) in the bodies of living beings go to dissolution. And when I am again active, they move once more. Indeed, I am the most excellent; behold me even when absorbed.

sanskrit: ततः प्रालीयतोदानः पुनश्च प्रचचार ह ।प्राणापानौ समानश्च व्यानश्चैव तमब्रुवन् ।उदान न त्वं श्रेष्ठोऽसि व्यान एव वशे तव ॥२१॥
transliteration: tataḥ prālīyatodānaḥ punaśca pracacāra ha ,prāṇāpānau samānaśca vyānaścaiva tamabruvan ,udāna na tvaṁ śreṣṭho'si vyāna eva vaśe tava.
english: Then Udāna became absorbed and indeed moved forth again. Prāṇa, Apāna, Samāna, and Vyāna said to him: "O Udāna, you are not the chief; Vyāna alone is under your control."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 23 Verse 21. Then Udāna became absorbed and indeed moved forth again. Prāṇa, Apāna, Samāna, and Vyāna said to him: "O Udāna, you are not the chief; Vyāna alone is under your control."

sanskrit: ततस्तानब्रवीद्ब्रह्मा समवेतान्प्रजापतिः ।सर्वे श्रेष्ठा न वा श्रेष्ठाः सर्वे चान्योन्यधर्मिणः ।सर्वे स्वविषये श्रेष्ठाः सर्वे चान्योन्यरक्षिणः ॥२२॥
transliteration: tatastānabravīdbrahmā samavetānprajāpatiḥ ,sarve śreṣṭhā na vā śreṣṭhāḥ sarve cānyonyadharmiṇaḥ ,sarve svaviṣaye śreṣṭhāḥ sarve cānyonyarakṣiṇaḥ.
english: Then Brahmā, the lord of creatures (prajāpati), said to them, who were assembled: "Are all of you superior, or are none of you superior? All of you share each other's intrinsic nature (dharma). All are superior in their own sphere, and all protect one another."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 23 Verse 22. Then Brahmā, the lord of creatures (prajāpati), said to them, who were assembled: "Are all of you superior, or are none of you superior? All of you share each other's intrinsic nature (dharma). All are superior in their own sphere, and all protect one another."

sanskrit: एकः स्थिरश्चास्थिरश्च विशेषात्पञ्च वायवः ।एक एव ममैवात्मा बहुधाप्युपचीयते ॥२३॥
transliteration: ekaḥ sthiraścāsthiraśca viśeṣātpañca vāyavaḥ ,eka eva mamaivātmā bahudhāpyupacīyate.
english: One (principle) is both stable and unstable, manifested specifically as the five vital airs. My own (ātman) is indeed one, yet it is augmented and manifests in many ways.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 23 Verse 23. One (principle) is both stable and unstable, manifested specifically as the five vital airs. My own (ātman) is indeed one, yet it is augmented and manifests in many ways.

sanskrit: परस्परस्य सुहृदो भावयन्तः परस्परम् ।स्वस्ति व्रजत भद्रं वो धारयध्वं परस्परम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: parasparasya suhṛdo bhāvayantaḥ parasparam ,svasti vrajata bhadraṁ vo dhārayadhvaṁ parasparam.
english: Cultivating mutual friendship, proceed with well-being; may auspiciousness be with you, and uphold each other.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 23 Verse 24. Cultivating mutual friendship, proceed with well-being; may auspiciousness be with you, and uphold each other.

sanskrit: ब्राह्मण उवाच ।अत्राप्युदाहरन्तीममितिहासं पुरातनम् ।नारदस्य च संवादमृषेर्देवमतस्य च ॥१॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇa uvāca ,atrāpyudāharantīmamitihāsaṁ purātanam ,nāradasya ca saṁvādamṛṣerdevamatasya ca.
english: The Brahmin said: Here they also recount this ancient narrative, the dialogue between Nārada and the sage Devamata.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 24 Verse 1. The Brahmin said: Here they also recount this ancient narrative, the dialogue between Nārada and the sage Devamata.

sanskrit: देवमत उवाच ।जन्तोः संजायमानस्य किं नु पूर्वं प्रवर्तते ।प्राणोऽपानः समानो वा व्यानो वोदान एव च ॥२॥
transliteration: devamata uvāca ,jantoḥ saṁjāyamānasya kiṁ nu pūrvaṁ pravartate ,prāṇo'pānaḥ samāno vā vyāno vodāna eva ca.
english: Devamata said: For a living being (jantu) coming into existence, what indeed operates first? Is it the upward-moving vital air (prāṇa), the downward-moving vital air (apāna), the equalizing vital air (samāna), the pervasive vital air (vyāna), or even the ascending vital air (udāna)?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 24 Verse 2. Devamata said: For a living being (jantu) coming into existence, what indeed operates first? Is it the upward-moving vital air (prāṇa), the downward-moving vital air (apāna), the equalizing vital air (samāna), the pervasive vital air (vyāna), or even the ascending vital air (udāna)?

sanskrit: नारद उवाच ।येनायं सृज्यते जन्तुस्ततोऽन्यः पूर्वमेति तम् ।प्राणद्वंद्वं च विज्ञेयं तिर्यगं चोर्ध्वगं च यत् ॥३॥
transliteration: nārada uvāca ,yenāyaṁ sṛjyate jantustato'nyaḥ pūrvameti tam ,prāṇadvaṁdvaṁ ca vijñeyaṁ tiryagaṁ cordhvagaṁ ca yat.
english: Nārada said: That by which this living being (jantu) is created, separate from that (creational cause), a vital force (prāṇa) first enters it. And the dual vital principle (prāṇa-dvandvam), which moves both horizontally (tiryagam) and upwards (ūrdhvagām), should be known.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 24 Verse 3. Nārada said: That by which this living being (jantu) is created, separate from that (creational cause), a vital force (prāṇa) first enters it. And the dual vital principle (prāṇa-dvandvam), which moves both horizontally (tiryagam) and upwards (ūrdhvagām), should be known.

sanskrit: देवमत उवाच ।केनायं सृज्यते जन्तुः कश्चान्यः पूर्वमेति तम् ।प्राणद्वंद्वं च मे ब्रूहि तिर्यगूर्ध्वं च निश्चयात् ॥४॥
transliteration: devamata uvāca ,kenāyaṁ sṛjyate jantuḥ kaścānyaḥ pūrvameti tam ,prāṇadvaṁdvaṁ ca me brūhi tiryagūrdhvaṁ ca niścayāt.
english: Devamata said: "By whom is this living being created? And who else precedes it? Also, tell me with certainty about the pair of vital breaths (prāṇa) that move sideways and upwards."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 24 Verse 4. Devamata said: "By whom is this living being created? And who else precedes it? Also, tell me with certainty about the pair of vital breaths (prāṇa) that move sideways and upwards."

sanskrit: नारद उवाच ।संकल्पाज्जायते हर्षः शब्दादपि च जायते ।रसात्संजायते चापि रूपादपि च जायते ॥५॥
transliteration: nārada uvāca ,saṁkalpājjāyate harṣaḥ śabdādapi ca jāyate ,rasātsaṁjāyate cāpi rūpādapi ca jāyate.
english: Narada said: "Joy (harṣa) arises from intention (saṃkalpa), and it also arises from sound (śabda). Moreover, it arises from taste (rasa) and also from form (rūpa)."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 24 Verse 5. Narada said: "Joy (harṣa) arises from intention (saṃkalpa), and it also arises from sound (śabda). Moreover, it arises from taste (rasa) and also from form (rūpa)."

sanskrit: स्पर्शात्संजायते चापि गन्धादपि च जायते ।एतद्रूपमुदानस्य हर्षो मिथुनसंभवः ॥६॥
transliteration: sparśātsaṁjāyate cāpi gandhādapi ca jāyate ,etadrūpamudānasya harṣo mithunasaṁbhavaḥ.
english: And it also arises from touch (sparśa), and from smell (gandha). This is the nature of the upward-moving vital breath (udāna). Joy (harṣa) is born from physical union (mithuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 24 Verse 6. And it also arises from touch (sparśa), and from smell (gandha). This is the nature of the upward-moving vital breath (udāna). Joy (harṣa) is born from physical union (mithuna).

sanskrit: कामात्संजायते शुक्रं कामात्संजायते रसः ।समानव्यानजनिते सामान्ये शुक्रशोणिते ॥७॥
transliteration: kāmātsaṁjāyate śukraṁ kāmātsaṁjāyate rasaḥ ,samānavyānajanite sāmānye śukraśoṇite.
english: Semen (śukra) arises from desire (kāma), and vital fluid (rasa) also arises from desire (kāma). Semen (śukra) and blood (śoṇita) are generated in common by the vital breaths (prāṇa) samāna and vyāna.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 24 Verse 7. Semen (śukra) arises from desire (kāma), and vital fluid (rasa) also arises from desire (kāma). Semen (śukra) and blood (śoṇita) are generated in common by the vital breaths (prāṇa) samāna and vyāna.

sanskrit: शुक्राच्छोणितसंसृष्टात्पूर्वं प्राणः प्रवर्तते ।प्राणेन विकृते शुक्रे ततोऽपानः प्रवर्तते ॥८॥
transliteration: śukrācchoṇitasaṁsṛṣṭātpūrvaṁ prāṇaḥ pravartate ,prāṇena vikṛte śukre tato'pānaḥ pravartate.
english: The upward vital breath (prāṇa) first begins to function from semen mixed with blood. When the semen is transformed by the upward vital breath (prāṇa), then the downward vital breath (apāna) begins to function.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 24 Verse 8. The upward vital breath (prāṇa) first begins to function from semen mixed with blood. When the semen is transformed by the upward vital breath (prāṇa), then the downward vital breath (apāna) begins to function.

sanskrit: प्राणापानाविदं द्वंद्वमवाक्चोर्ध्वं च गच्छतः ।व्यानः समानश्चैवोभौ तिर्यग्द्वंद्वत्वमुच्यते ॥९॥
transliteration: prāṇāpānāvidaṁ dvaṁdvamavākcordhvaṁ ca gacchataḥ ,vyānaḥ samānaścaivobhau tiryagdvaṁdvatvamucyate.
english: This pair, consisting of the upward vital breath (prāṇa) and the downward vital breath (apāna), moves both downwards and upwards. The vital breaths (vyāna) and (samāna), both, are spoken of as the transverse pair.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 24 Verse 9. This pair, consisting of the upward vital breath (prāṇa) and the downward vital breath (apāna), moves both downwards and upwards. The vital breaths (vyāna) and (samāna), both, are spoken of as the transverse pair.

sanskrit: अग्निर्वै देवताः सर्वा इति वेदस्य शासनम् ।संजायते ब्राह्मणेषु ज्ञानं बुद्धिसमन्वितम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: agnirvai devatāḥ sarvā iti vedasya śāsanam ,saṁjāyate brāhmaṇeṣu jñānaṁ buddhisamanvitam.
english: Indeed, Agni is all the deities; this is the authoritative teaching of the Veda. Knowledge, endowed with wisdom, arises in brahmins.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 24 Verse 10. Indeed, Agni is all the deities; this is the authoritative teaching of the Veda. Knowledge, endowed with wisdom, arises in brahmins.

sanskrit: तस्य धूमस्तमोरूपं रजो भस्म सुरेतसः ।सत्त्वं संजायते तस्य यत्र प्रक्षिप्यते हविः ॥११॥
transliteration: tasya dhūmastamorūpaṁ rajo bhasma suretasaḥ ,sattvaṁ saṁjāyate tasya yatra prakṣipyate haviḥ.
english: The smoke of that (fire) represents darkness (tamas); its ash, from the well-kindled fire (suretas), represents passion (rajas). Goodness (sattva) arises from that (fire) where the oblation (havis) is offered.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 24 Verse 11. The smoke of that (fire) represents darkness (tamas); its ash, from the well-kindled fire (suretas), represents passion (rajas). Goodness (sattva) arises from that (fire) where the oblation (havis) is offered.

sanskrit: आघारौ समानो व्यानश्च इति यज्ञविदो विदुः ।प्राणापानावाज्यभागौ तयोर्मध्ये हुताशनः ।एतद्रूपमुदानस्य परमं ब्राह्मणा विदुः ॥१२॥
transliteration: āghārau samāno vyānaśca iti yajñavido viduḥ ,prāṇāpānāvājyabhāgau tayormadhye hutāśanaḥ ,etadrūpamudānasya paramaṁ brāhmaṇā viduḥ.
english: The experts in (Vedic ritual) (yajña) know samāna and vyāna to be the two āghāra oblations. Prāṇa and apāna are considered the two portions of ghee, and in between them resides the sacrificial fire (hutāśana). The brahmins know this to be the supreme form of udāna.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 24 Verse 12. The experts in (Vedic ritual) (yajña) know samāna and vyāna to be the two āghāra oblations. Prāṇa and apāna are considered the two portions of ghee, and in between them resides the sacrificial fire (hutāśana). The brahmins know this to be the supreme form of udāna.

sanskrit: निर्द्वंद्वमितियत्त्वेतत्तन्मे निगदतः शृणु ॥१३॥
transliteration: nirdvaṁdvamiti yattvetattanmenigadataḥ śṛṇu.
english: Now, listen to me as I explain what is meant by 'free from duality' (nirdvandva).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 24 Verse 13. Now, listen to me as I explain what is meant by 'free from duality' (nirdvandva).

sanskrit: अहोरात्रमिदं द्वंद्वं तयोर्मध्ये हुताशनः ।एतद्रूपमुदानस्य परमं ब्राह्मणा विदुः ॥१४॥
transliteration: ahorātramidaṁ dvaṁdvaṁ tayormadhye hutāśanaḥ ,etadrūpamudānasya paramaṁ brāhmaṇā viduḥ.
english: Day and night form a duality, and in between them is the sacrificial fire (hutāśana). The brahmins know this to be the supreme form of udāna.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 24 Verse 14. Day and night form a duality, and in between them is the sacrificial fire (hutāśana). The brahmins know this to be the supreme form of udāna.

sanskrit: उभे चैवायने द्वंद्वं तयोर्मध्ये हुताशनः ।एतद्रूपमुदानस्य परमं ब्राह्मणा विदुः ॥१५॥
transliteration: ubhe caivāyane dvaṁdvaṁ tayormadhye hutāśanaḥ ,etadrūpamudānasya paramaṁ brāhmaṇā viduḥ.
english: Both the solstices are indeed a duality, and in between them is the sacrificial fire (hutāśana). The brahmins know this to be the supreme form of udāna.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 24 Verse 15. Both the solstices are indeed a duality, and in between them is the sacrificial fire (hutāśana). The brahmins know this to be the supreme form of udāna.

sanskrit: उभे सत्यानृते द्वंद्वं तयोर्मध्ये हुताशनः ।एतद्रूपमुदानस्य परमं ब्राह्मणा विदुः ॥१६॥
transliteration: ubhe satyānṛte dvaṁdvaṁ tayormadhye hutāśanaḥ ,etadrūpamudānasya paramaṁ brāhmaṇā viduḥ.
english: Truth and untruth constitute a duality. In the middle of these two is the fire. Brahmins understand this to be the supreme form of the udāna (vital air).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 24 Verse 16. Truth and untruth constitute a duality. In the middle of these two is the fire. Brahmins understand this to be the supreme form of the udāna (vital air).

sanskrit: उभे शुभाशुभे द्वंद्वं तयोर्मध्ये हुताशनः ।एतद्रूपमुदानस्य परमं ब्राह्मणा विदुः ॥१७॥
transliteration: ubhe śubhāśubhe dvaṁdvaṁ tayormadhye hutāśanaḥ ,etadrūpamudānasya paramaṁ brāhmaṇā viduḥ.
english: Good and evil constitute a duality. In the middle of these two is the fire. Brahmins understand this to be the supreme form of the udāna (vital air).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 24 Verse 17. Good and evil constitute a duality. In the middle of these two is the fire. Brahmins understand this to be the supreme form of the udāna (vital air).

sanskrit: सच्चासच्चैव तद्द्वंद्वं तयोर्मध्ये हुताशनः ।एतद्रूपमुदानस्य परमं ब्राह्मणा विदुः ॥१८॥
transliteration: saccāsaccaiva taddvaṁdvaṁ tayormadhye hutāśanaḥ ,etadrūpamudānasya paramaṁ brāhmaṇā viduḥ.
english: Existence and non-existence, this indeed is a duality. In the middle of these two is the fire. Brahmins understand this to be the supreme form of the udāna (vital air).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 24 Verse 18. Existence and non-existence, this indeed is a duality. In the middle of these two is the fire. Brahmins understand this to be the supreme form of the udāna (vital air).

sanskrit: प्रथमं समानो व्यानो व्यस्यते कर्म तेन तत् ।तृतीयं तु समानेन पुनरेव व्यवस्यते ॥१९॥
transliteration: prathamaṁ samāno vyāno vyasyate karma tena tat ,tṛtīyaṁ tu samānena punareva vyavasyate.
english: First, the samāna (vital air) and vyāna (vital air) are mentioned. That particular function (karma) is distributed by the former (samāna). But the third function is again regulated solely by samāna.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 24 Verse 19. First, the samāna (vital air) and vyāna (vital air) are mentioned. That particular function (karma) is distributed by the former (samāna). But the third function is again regulated solely by samāna.

sanskrit: शान्त्यर्थं वामदेवं च शान्तिर्ब्रह्म सनातनम् ।एतद्रूपमुदानस्य परमं ब्राह्मणा विदुः ॥२०॥
transliteration: śāntyarthaṁ vāmadevaṁ ca śāntirbrahma sanātanam ,etadrūpamudānasya paramaṁ brāhmaṇā viduḥ.
english: For the purpose of tranquility, Vāmadeva (is revered), and peace (śānti) itself is the eternal (brahman). Learned ones understand this form of udāna to be the supreme (brahman).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 24 Verse 20. For the purpose of tranquility, Vāmadeva (is revered), and peace (śānti) itself is the eternal (brahman). Learned ones understand this form of udāna to be the supreme (brahman).

sanskrit: ब्राह्मण उवाच ।अत्राप्युदाहरन्तीममितिहासं पुरातनम् ।चातुर्होत्रविधानस्य विधानमिह यादृशम् ॥१॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇa uvāca ,atrāpyudāharantīmamitihāsaṁ purātanam ,cāturhotravidhānasya vidhānamiha yādṛśam.
english: The Brahmin (brāhmaṇaḥ) said: In this context, they also relate this ancient narrative. (It describes) the nature of the procedure for the four chief priests (cāturhotra) as it is established here.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 25 Verse 1. The Brahmin (brāhmaṇaḥ) said: In this context, they also relate this ancient narrative. (It describes) the nature of the procedure for the four chief priests (cāturhotra) as it is established here.

sanskrit: तस्य सर्वस्य विधिवद्विधानमुपदेक्ष्यते ।शृणु मे गदतो भद्रे रहस्यमिदमुत्तमम् ॥२॥
transliteration: tasya sarvasya vidhivadvidhānamupadekṣyate ,śṛṇu me gadato bhadre rahasyamidamuttamam.
english: The procedure for all that will be properly expounded. O graceful lady, listen to me as I explain this supreme secret.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 25 Verse 2. The procedure for all that will be properly expounded. O graceful lady, listen to me as I explain this supreme secret.

sanskrit: करणं कर्म कर्ता च मोक्ष इत्येव भामिनि ।चत्वार एते होतारो यैरिदं जगदावृतम् ॥३॥
transliteration: karaṇaṁ karma kartā ca mokṣa ityeva bhāmini ,catvāra ete hotāro yairidaṁ jagadāvṛtam.
english: The instrument, the action (karma), the agent, and liberation (mokṣa) – these four, O charming lady, are indeed the 'ritual priests' (hotāraḥ) by whom this universe is encompassed.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 25 Verse 3. The instrument, the action (karma), the agent, and liberation (mokṣa) – these four, O charming lady, are indeed the 'ritual priests' (hotāraḥ) by whom this universe is encompassed.

sanskrit: होतॄणां साधनं चैव शृणु सर्वमशेषतः ।घ्राणं जिह्वा च चक्षुश्च त्वक्च श्रोत्रं च पञ्चमम् ।मनो बुद्धिश्च सप्तैते विज्ञेया गुणहेतवः ॥४॥
transliteration: hotṝṇāṁ sādhanaṁ caiva śṛṇu sarvamaśeṣataḥ ,ghrāṇaṁ jihvā ca cakṣuśca tvakca śrotraṁ ca pañcamam ,mano buddhiśca saptaite vijñeyā guṇahetavaḥ.
english: Listen fully and completely to all the instruments of the experiencers. The nose, tongue, eye, skin, and the ear as the fifth; the mind and the intellect - these seven should be known as the causes for experiencing qualities.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 25 Verse 4. Listen fully and completely to all the instruments of the experiencers. The nose, tongue, eye, skin, and the ear as the fifth; the mind and the intellect - these seven should be known as the causes for experiencing qualities.

sanskrit: गन्धो रसश्च रूपं च शब्दः स्पर्शश्च पञ्चमः ।मन्तव्यमथ बोद्धव्यं सप्तैते कर्महेतवः ॥५॥
transliteration: gandho rasaśca rūpaṁ ca śabdaḥ sparśaśca pañcamaḥ ,mantavyamatha boddhavyaṁ saptaite karmahetavaḥ.
english: Smell, taste, form, sound, and touch as the fifth; and what is to be thought (by the mind) and what is to be understood (by the intellect) - these seven are known as the causes of action (karma).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 25 Verse 5. Smell, taste, form, sound, and touch as the fifth; and what is to be thought (by the mind) and what is to be understood (by the intellect) - these seven are known as the causes of action (karma).

sanskrit: घ्राता भक्षयिता द्रष्टा स्प्रष्टा श्रोता च पञ्चमः ।मन्ता बोद्धा च सप्तैते विज्ञेयाः कर्तृहेतवः ॥६॥
transliteration: ghrātā bhakṣayitā draṣṭā spraṣṭā śrotā ca pañcamaḥ ,mantā boddhā ca saptaite vijñeyāḥ kartṛhetavaḥ.
english: The smeller, the taster, the seer, the toucher, and the hearer as the fifth; and the thinker and the understander - these seven should be known as the causes of agency.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 25 Verse 6. The smeller, the taster, the seer, the toucher, and the hearer as the fifth; and the thinker and the understander - these seven should be known as the causes of agency.

sanskrit: स्वगुणं भक्षयन्त्येते गुणवन्तः शुभाशुभम् ।अहं च निर्गुणोऽत्रेति सप्तैते मोक्षहेतवः ॥७॥
transliteration: svaguṇaṁ bhakṣayantyete guṇavantaḥ śubhāśubham ,ahaṁ ca nirguṇo'treti saptaite mokṣahetavaḥ.
english: These beings, endowed with qualities, experience their own inherent qualities, both good and bad. (The realization) "And I, here, am without qualities" - these seven (principles) are the causes of liberation (mokṣa).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 25 Verse 7. These beings, endowed with qualities, experience their own inherent qualities, both good and bad. (The realization) "And I, here, am without qualities" - these seven (principles) are the causes of liberation (mokṣa).

sanskrit: विदुषां बुध्यमानानां स्वं स्वं स्थानं यथाविधि ।गुणास्ते देवताभूताः सततं भुञ्जते हविः ॥८॥
transliteration: viduṣāṁ budhyamānānāṁ svaṁ svaṁ sthānaṁ yathāvidhi ,guṇāste devatābhūtāḥ satataṁ bhuñjate haviḥ.
english: For the discerning individuals who understand their own respective positions according to the proper rules, those very qualities (guṇāḥ), having assumed the forms of deities, constantly partake of the ritual offerings.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 25 Verse 8. For the discerning individuals who understand their own respective positions according to the proper rules, those very qualities (guṇāḥ), having assumed the forms of deities, constantly partake of the ritual offerings.

sanskrit: अदन्ह्यविद्वानन्नानि ममत्वेनोपपद्यते ।आत्मार्थं पाचयन्नित्यं ममत्वेनोपहन्यते ॥९॥
transliteration: adanhyavidvānannāni mamatvenopapadyate ,ātmārthaṁ pācayannityaṁ mamatvenopahanyate.
english: Indeed, an ignorant person who eats food with a sense of possessiveness (mamatva) incurs negative consequences. One who constantly cooks food for their own self (ātman) is ruined by possessiveness (mamatva).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 25 Verse 9. Indeed, an ignorant person who eats food with a sense of possessiveness (mamatva) incurs negative consequences. One who constantly cooks food for their own self (ātman) is ruined by possessiveness (mamatva).

sanskrit: अभक्ष्यभक्षणं चैव मद्यपानं च हन्ति तम् ।स चान्नं हन्ति तच्चान्नं स हत्वा हन्यते बुधः ॥१०॥
transliteration: abhakṣyabhakṣaṇaṁ caiva madyapānaṁ ca hanti tam ,sa cānnaṁ hanti taccānnaṁ sa hatvā hanyate budhaḥ.
english: Eating forbidden food and drinking liquor harm a person. And that person harms the food; and that food in turn harms him. Thus, even a discerning person (budha), having caused harm, is himself harmed.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 25 Verse 10. Eating forbidden food and drinking liquor harm a person. And that person harms the food; and that food in turn harms him. Thus, even a discerning person (budha), having caused harm, is himself harmed.

sanskrit: अत्ता ह्यन्नमिदं विद्वान्पुनर्जनयतीश्वरः ।स चान्नाज्जायते तस्मिन्सूक्ष्मो नाम व्यतिक्रमः ॥११॥
transliteration: attā hyannamidaṁ vidvānpunarjanayatīśvaraḥ ,sa cānnājjāyate tasminsūkṣmo nāma vyatikramaḥ.
english: Indeed, the wise controller (īśvara), who is also the eater, regenerates this food. And he (the controller) himself is born from food. In this process, there exists a subtle irregularity, known as a transgression (vyatikrama).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 25 Verse 11. Indeed, the wise controller (īśvara), who is also the eater, regenerates this food. And he (the controller) himself is born from food. In this process, there exists a subtle irregularity, known as a transgression (vyatikrama).

sanskrit: मनसा गम्यते यच्च यच्च वाचा निरुद्यते ।श्रोत्रेण श्रूयते यच्च चक्षुषा यच्च दृश्यते ॥१२॥
transliteration: manasā gamyate yacca yacca vācā nirudyate ,śrotreṇa śrūyate yacca cakṣuṣā yacca dṛśyate.
english: And that which is perceived by the mind, and that which is expressed by speech, and that which is heard by the ear, and that which is seen by the eye.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 25 Verse 12. And that which is perceived by the mind, and that which is expressed by speech, and that which is heard by the ear, and that which is seen by the eye.

sanskrit: स्पर्शेन स्पृश्यते यच्च घ्राणेन घ्रायते च यत् ।मनःषष्ठानि संयम्य हवींष्येतानि सर्वशः ॥१३॥
transliteration: sparśena spṛśyate yacca ghrāṇena ghrāyate ca yat ,manaḥṣaṣṭhāni saṁyamya havīṁṣyetāni sarvaśaḥ.
english: And that which is perceived by touch, and that which is perceived by smell; having completely restrained all these oblations, which are guided by the mind as the sixth (sense).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 25 Verse 13. And that which is perceived by touch, and that which is perceived by smell; having completely restrained all these oblations, which are guided by the mind as the sixth (sense).

sanskrit: गुणवत्पावको मह्यं दीप्यते हव्यवाहनः ।योगयज्ञः प्रवृत्तो मे ज्ञानब्रह्ममनोद्भवः ।प्राणस्तोत्रोऽपानशस्त्रः सर्वत्यागसुदक्षिणः ॥१४॥
transliteration: guṇavatpāvako mahyaṁ dīpyate havyavāhanaḥ ,yogayajñaḥ pravṛtto me jñānabrahmamanodbhavaḥ ,prāṇastotro'pānaśastraḥ sarvatyāgasudakṣiṇaḥ.
english: For me, the excellent purifier, the carrier of oblations, blazes forth. My (yoga) (yajña) has commenced, originating from the mind of the knowledge of (brahman). It has the vital breath (prāṇa) as its hymn, the downward breath (apāna) as its ritual implement, and complete renunciation as its auspicious fee (dakṣiṇā).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 25 Verse 14. For me, the excellent purifier, the carrier of oblations, blazes forth. My (yoga) (yajña) has commenced, originating from the mind of the knowledge of (brahman). It has the vital breath (prāṇa) as its hymn, the downward breath (apāna) as its ritual implement, and complete renunciation as its auspicious fee (dakṣiṇā).

sanskrit: कर्मानुमन्ता ब्रह्मा मे कर्ताध्वर्युः कृतस्तुतिः ।कृतप्रशास्ता तच्छास्त्रमपवर्गोऽस्य दक्षिणा ॥१५॥
transliteration: karmānumantā brahmā me kartādhvaryuḥ kṛtastutiḥ ,kṛtapraśāstā tacchāstramapavargo'sya dakṣiṇā.
english: My Brahmā priest is the approver of actions (karma). The Adhvaryu is the performer, whose praises are sung. The Praśāstā is the instructor, whose precepts are followed. That teaching (śāstra) is its guiding principle, and final liberation (apavarga) is its fee (dakṣiṇā).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 25 Verse 15. My Brahmā priest is the approver of actions (karma). The Adhvaryu is the performer, whose praises are sung. The Praśāstā is the instructor, whose precepts are followed. That teaching (śāstra) is its guiding principle, and final liberation (apavarga) is its fee (dakṣiṇā).

sanskrit: ऋचश्चाप्यत्र शंसन्ति नारायणविदो जनाः ।नारायणाय देवाय यदबध्नन्पशून्पुरा ॥१६॥
transliteration: ṛcaścāpyatra śaṁsanti nārāyaṇavido janāḥ ,nārāyaṇāya devāya yadabadhnanpaśūnpurā.
english: And indeed, people who understand Narayana also recite Rigvedic verses concerning this, (recalling) how in ancient times they bound animals for the deity Narayana.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 25 Verse 16. And indeed, people who understand Narayana also recite Rigvedic verses concerning this, (recalling) how in ancient times they bound animals for the deity Narayana.

sanskrit: तत्र सामानि गायन्ति तानि चाहुर्निदर्शनम् ।देवं नारायणं भीरु सर्वात्मानं निबोध मे ॥१७॥
transliteration: tatra sāmāni gāyanti tāni cāhurnidarśanam ,devaṁ nārāyaṇaṁ bhīru sarvātmānaṁ nibodha me.
english: There, they chant the Samavedic hymns, and they declare these (hymns) to be an illustration. O timid one, understand from me that Narayana is the supreme deity, the self of all (ātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 25 Verse 17. There, they chant the Samavedic hymns, and they declare these (hymns) to be an illustration. O timid one, understand from me that Narayana is the supreme deity, the self of all (ātman).

sanskrit: ब्राह्मण उवाच ।एकः शास्ता न द्वितीयोऽस्ति शास्ता यथा नियुक्तोऽस्मि तथा चरामि ।हृद्येष तिष्ठन्पुरुषः शास्ति शास्ता तेनैव युक्तः प्रवणादिवोदकम् ॥१॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇa uvāca ,ekaḥ śāstā na dvitīyo'sti śāstā; yathā niyukto'smi tathā carāmi ,hṛdyeṣa tiṣṭhanpuruṣaḥ śāsti śāstā; tenaiva yuktaḥ pravaṇādivodakam.
english: The Brahmin said: "There is but one master; there is no second master. As I am directed, so I act. This master, the supreme cosmic person (puruṣa), residing in the heart, governs; one is guided by that very one, just as water (flows) down a slope."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 26 Verse 1. The Brahmin said: "There is but one master; there is no second master. As I am directed, so I act. This master, the supreme cosmic person (puruṣa), residing in the heart, governs; one is guided by that very one, just as water (flows) down a slope."

sanskrit: एको गुरुर्नास्ति ततो द्वितीयो यो हृच्छयस्तमहमनुब्रवीमि ।तेनानुशिष्टा गुरुणा सदैव पराभूता दानवाः सर्व एव ॥२॥
transliteration: eko gururnāsti tato dvitīyo; yo hṛcchayastamahamanubravīmi ,tenānuśiṣṭā guruṇā sadaiva; parābhūtā dānavāḥ sarva eva.
english: There is only one teacher (guru); there is no second beyond him. I follow the one who resides in the heart. All the Dānavas (demons), even though instructed by that (same) teacher (guru), were always defeated.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 26 Verse 2. There is only one teacher (guru); there is no second beyond him. I follow the one who resides in the heart. All the Dānavas (demons), even though instructed by that (same) teacher (guru), were always defeated.

sanskrit: एको बन्धुर्नास्ति ततो द्वितीयो यो हृच्छयस्तमहमनुब्रवीमि ।तेनानुशिष्टा बान्धवा बन्धुमन्तः सप्तर्षयः सप्त दिवि प्रभान्ति ॥३॥
transliteration: eko bandhurnāsti tato dvitīyo; yo hṛcchayastamahamanubravīmi ,tenānuśiṣṭā bāndhavā bandhumantaḥ; saptarṣayaḥ sapta divi prabhānti.
english: There is no other friend like the one who resides within the heart (hṛcchaya), and him I declare. Instructed by that indwelling friend, those relatives who possess such a friend, like the seven sages, shine brightly as seven stars in the heavens.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 26 Verse 3. There is no other friend like the one who resides within the heart (hṛcchaya), and him I declare. Instructed by that indwelling friend, those relatives who possess such a friend, like the seven sages, shine brightly as seven stars in the heavens.

sanskrit: एकः श्रोता नास्ति ततो द्वितीयो यो हृच्छयस्तमहमनुब्रवीमि ।तस्मिन्गुरौ गुरुवासं निरुष्य शक्रो गतः सर्वलोकामरत्वम् ॥४॥
transliteration: ekaḥ śrotā nāsti tato dvitīyo; yo hṛcchayastamahamanubravīmi ,tasmingurau guruvāsaṁ niruṣya; śakro gataḥ sarvalokāmaratvam.
english: There is no other listener like the one who resides within the heart (hṛcchaya), and him I declare. Having dwelt with that spiritual teacher (guru) and completed his instructions, Indra (Śakra) attained immortality in all worlds.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 26 Verse 4. There is no other listener like the one who resides within the heart (hṛcchaya), and him I declare. Having dwelt with that spiritual teacher (guru) and completed his instructions, Indra (Śakra) attained immortality in all worlds.

sanskrit: एको द्वेष्टा नास्ति ततो द्वितीयो यो हृच्छयस्तमहमनुब्रवीमि ।तेनानुशिष्टा गुरुणा सदैव लोकद्विष्टाः पन्नगाः सर्व एव ॥५॥
transliteration: eko dveṣṭā nāsti tato dvitīyo; yo hṛcchayastamahamanubravīmi ,tenānuśiṣṭā guruṇā sadaiva; lokadviṣṭāḥ pannagāḥ sarva eva.
english: There is no other hater like the one who resides within the heart (hṛcchaya), and him I declare. All those serpents, who are hated by the world, were always instructed by that (inner) teacher (guru).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 26 Verse 5. There is no other hater like the one who resides within the heart (hṛcchaya), and him I declare. All those serpents, who are hated by the world, were always instructed by that (inner) teacher (guru).

sanskrit: अत्राप्युदाहरन्तीममितिहासं पुरातनम् ।प्रजापतौ पन्नगानां देवर्षीणां च संविदम् ॥६॥
transliteration: atrāpyudāharantīmamitihāsaṁ purātanam ,prajāpatau pannagānāṁ devarṣīṇāṁ ca saṁvidam.
english: Here, they also narrate this ancient narrative concerning the assembly of serpents and divine sages before Prajāpati.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 26 Verse 6. Here, they also narrate this ancient narrative concerning the assembly of serpents and divine sages before Prajāpati.

sanskrit: देवर्षयश्च नागाश्च असुराश्च प्रजापतिम् ।पर्यपृच्छन्नुपासीनाः श्रेयो नः प्रोच्यतामिति ॥७॥
transliteration: devarṣayaśca nāgāśca asurāśca prajāpatim ,paryapṛcchannupāsīnāḥ śreyo naḥ procyatāmiti.
english: The divine sages, nāgas, and asuras, having approached Prajapati, asked him, "Please declare what is ultimately beneficial for us."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 26 Verse 7. The divine sages, nāgas, and asuras, having approached Prajapati, asked him, "Please declare what is ultimately beneficial for us."

sanskrit: तेषां प्रोवाच भगवाञ्श्रेयः समनुपृच्छताम् ।ओमित्येकाक्षरं ब्रह्म ते श्रुत्वा प्राद्रवन्दिशः ॥८॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ provāca bhagavāñśreyaḥ samanupṛcchatām ,omityekākṣaraṁ brahma te śrutvā prādravandiśaḥ.
english: To those who were diligently inquiring about ultimate good (śreyaḥ), the venerable one spoke: "The single syllable 'Om' is the ultimate reality (brahman)." Having heard this, they ran away in various directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 26 Verse 8. To those who were diligently inquiring about ultimate good (śreyaḥ), the venerable one spoke: "The single syllable 'Om' is the ultimate reality (brahman)." Having heard this, they ran away in various directions.

sanskrit: तेषां प्राद्रवमाणानामुपदेशार्थमात्मनः ।सर्पाणां दशने भावः प्रवृत्तः पूर्वमेव तु ॥९॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ prādravamāṇānāmupadeśārthamātmanaḥ ,sarpāṇāṁ daśane bhāvaḥ pravṛttaḥ pūrvameva tu.
english: For those who were running away, an instruction for their own (spiritual) benefit was given; however, the intrinsic nature of serpents, an inclination towards biting, had already manifested previously.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 26 Verse 9. For those who were running away, an instruction for their own (spiritual) benefit was given; however, the intrinsic nature of serpents, an inclination towards biting, had already manifested previously.

sanskrit: असुराणां प्रवृत्तस्तु दम्भभावः स्वभावजः ।दानं देवा व्यवसिता दममेव महर्षयः ॥१०॥
transliteration: asurāṇāṁ pravṛttastu dambhabhāvaḥ svabhāvajaḥ ,dānaṁ devā vyavasitā damameva maharṣayaḥ.
english: But for the asuras, the nature of arrogance (dambhabhāva), born of their intrinsic nature (svabhāva), had manifested. The devas (gods) resolved upon giving (dāna), and the great sages indeed resolved upon self-control.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 26 Verse 10. But for the asuras, the nature of arrogance (dambhabhāva), born of their intrinsic nature (svabhāva), had manifested. The devas (gods) resolved upon giving (dāna), and the great sages indeed resolved upon self-control.

sanskrit: एकं शास्तारमासाद्य शब्देनैकेन संस्कृताः ।नाना व्यवसिताः सर्वे सर्पदेवर्षिदानवाः ॥११॥
transliteration: ekaṁ śāstāramāsādya śabdenaikena saṁskṛtāḥ ,nānā vyavasitāḥ sarve sarpadevarṣidānavāḥ.
english: Having approached a single teacher and been purified by a single instruction, all of them - serpents, divine sages, and demons - became variously resolute.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 26 Verse 11. Having approached a single teacher and been purified by a single instruction, all of them - serpents, divine sages, and demons - became variously resolute.

sanskrit: शृणोत्ययं प्रोच्यमानं गृह्णाति च यथातथम् ।पृच्छतस्तावतो भूयो गुरुरन्योऽनुमन्यते ॥१२॥
transliteration: śṛṇotyayaṁ procyamānaṁ gṛhṇāti ca yathātatham ,pṛcchatastāvato bhūyo gururanyo'numanyate.
english: This student listens to what is being taught and comprehends it accurately. Another teacher (guru) then approves of the one who asks more questions.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 26 Verse 12. This student listens to what is being taught and comprehends it accurately. Another teacher (guru) then approves of the one who asks more questions.

sanskrit: तस्य चानुमते कर्म ततः पश्चात्प्रवर्तते ।गुरुर्बोद्धा च शत्रुश्च द्वेष्टा च हृदि संश्रितः ॥१३॥
transliteration: tasya cānumate karma tataḥ paścātpravartate ,gururboddhā ca śatruśca dveṣṭā ca hṛdi saṁśritaḥ.
english: And with his (the guru's) approval, action (karma) then proceeds afterwards. The teacher (guru) is also the knower, the enemy, and the hater, residing in the heart.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 26 Verse 13. And with his (the guru's) approval, action (karma) then proceeds afterwards. The teacher (guru) is also the knower, the enemy, and the hater, residing in the heart.

sanskrit: पापेन विचरँल्लोके पापचारी भवत्ययम् ।शुभेन विचरँल्लोके शुभचारी भवत्युत ॥१४॥
transliteration: pāpena vicaraँlloke pāpacārī bhavatyayam ,śubhena vicaraँlloke śubhacārī bhavatyuta.
english: Wandering in the world with evil, this person becomes an evil-doer. Alternatively, wandering in the world with good, he becomes a good-doer.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 26 Verse 14. Wandering in the world with evil, this person becomes an evil-doer. Alternatively, wandering in the world with good, he becomes a good-doer.

sanskrit: कामचारी तु कामेन य इन्द्रियसुखे रतः ।व्रतवारी सदैवैष य इन्द्रियजये रतः ॥१५॥
transliteration: kāmacārī tu kāmena ya indriyasukhe rataḥ ,vratavārī sadaivaiṣa ya indriyajaye rataḥ.
english: One who acts as they please, delighting in sensory pleasures, is driven by desire. But this person, who delights in the conquest of the senses, is always an observer of spiritual disciplines (vrata).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 26 Verse 15. One who acts as they please, delighting in sensory pleasures, is driven by desire. But this person, who delights in the conquest of the senses, is always an observer of spiritual disciplines (vrata).

sanskrit: अपेतव्रतकर्मा तु केवलं ब्रह्मणि श्रितः ।ब्रह्मभूतश्चरँल्लोके ब्रह्मचारी भवत्ययम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: apetavratakarmā tu kevalaṁ brahmaṇi śritaḥ ,brahmabhūtaścaraँlloke brahmacārī bhavatyayam.
english: But this person, who has abandoned external vows and ritual actions, relying solely on the ultimate reality (brahman), and who, having become one with (brahman), moves in the world, truly becomes a student of the absolute (brahmacārī).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 26 Verse 16. But this person, who has abandoned external vows and ritual actions, relying solely on the ultimate reality (brahman), and who, having become one with (brahman), moves in the world, truly becomes a student of the absolute (brahmacārī).

sanskrit: ब्रह्मैव समिधस्तस्य ब्रह्माग्निर्ब्रह्मसंस्तरः ।आपो ब्रह्म गुरुर्ब्रह्म स ब्रह्मणि समाहितः ॥१७॥
transliteration: brahmaiva samidhastasya brahmāgnirbrahmasaṁstaraḥ ,āpo brahma gururbrahma sa brahmaṇi samāhitaḥ.
english: For such a person, the ultimate reality (brahman) itself is the sacrificial fuel, (brahman) is the fire, and (brahman) is the sacred grass for the ritual. The waters are (brahman), and the teacher (guru) is (brahman); he is completely absorbed in (brahman).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 26 Verse 17. For such a person, the ultimate reality (brahman) itself is the sacrificial fuel, (brahman) is the fire, and (brahman) is the sacred grass for the ritual. The waters are (brahman), and the teacher (guru) is (brahman); he is completely absorbed in (brahman).

sanskrit: एतदेतादृशं सूक्ष्मं ब्रह्मचर्यं विदुर्बुधाः ।विदित्वा चान्वपद्यन्त क्षेत्रज्ञेनानुदर्शिनः ॥१८॥
transliteration: etadetādṛśaṁ sūkṣmaṁ brahmacaryaṁ vidurbudhāḥ ,viditvā cānvapadyanta kṣetrajñenānudarśinaḥ.
english: The wise (budhāḥ) know this subtle and unique form of spiritual discipline (brahmacarya). And having understood it, those who perceive through the knower of the field (kṣetrajña) subsequently attained it.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 26 Verse 18. The wise (budhāḥ) know this subtle and unique form of spiritual discipline (brahmacarya). And having understood it, those who perceive through the knower of the field (kṣetrajña) subsequently attained it.

sanskrit: ब्राह्मण उवाच ।संकल्पदंशमशकं शोकहर्षहिमातपम् ।मोहान्धकारतिमिरं लोभव्यालसरीसृपम् ॥१॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇa uvāca ,saṁkalpadaṁśamaśakaṁ śokaharṣahimātapam ,mohāndhakāratimiraṁ lobhavyālasarīsṛpam.
english: The Brahmin said: (I have traversed a path characterized by) mosquitoes that sting with mental resolves, the cold and heat caused by sorrow and joy, the dense darkness of delusion (moha), and crawling reptiles of greed.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 27 Verse 1. The Brahmin said: (I have traversed a path characterized by) mosquitoes that sting with mental resolves, the cold and heat caused by sorrow and joy, the dense darkness of delusion (moha), and crawling reptiles of greed.

sanskrit: विषयैकात्ययाध्वानं कामक्रोधविरोधकम् ।तदतीत्य महादुर्गं प्रविष्टोऽस्मि महद्वनम् ॥२॥
transliteration: viṣayaikātyayādhvānaṁ kāmakrodhavirodhakam ,tadatītya mahādurgaṁ praviṣṭo'smi mahadvanam.
english: (This path is also) a journey where sense objects (viṣaya) are the sole passage, and which creates conflict arising from desire (kāma) and anger (krodha). Having crossed over that great fortress (mahādurga), I have entered a vast forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 27 Verse 2. (This path is also) a journey where sense objects (viṣaya) are the sole passage, and which creates conflict arising from desire (kāma) and anger (krodha). Having crossed over that great fortress (mahādurga), I have entered a vast forest.

sanskrit: ब्राह्मण्युवाच ।क्व तद्वनं महाप्राज्ञ के वृक्षाः सरितश्च काः ।गिरयः पर्वताश्चैव कियत्यध्वनि तद्वनम् ॥३॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇyuvāca ,kva tadvanaṁ mahāprājña ke vṛkṣāḥ saritaśca kāḥ ,girayaḥ parvatāścaiva kiyatyadhvani tadvanam.
english: The Brahmin's wife said: "Where is that forest, O greatly wise one? What trees are there? And what rivers? And what mountains and peaks? At what distance is that forest?"
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 27 Verse 3. The Brahmin's wife said: "Where is that forest, O greatly wise one? What trees are there? And what rivers? And what mountains and peaks? At what distance is that forest?"

sanskrit: ब्राह्मण उवाच ।न तदस्ति पृथग्भावे किंचिदन्यत्ततः समम् ।न तदस्त्यपृथग्भावे किंचिद्दूरतरं ततः ॥४॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇa uvāca ,na tadasti pṛthagbhāve kiṁcidanyattataḥ samam ,na tadastyapṛthagbhāve kiṁciddūrataraṁ tataḥ.
english: The Brahmin said: "That (forest) does not exist as a separate entity, nor is anything else equal to it. Nor does it exist as a non-separate entity, nor is anything more distant than it."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 27 Verse 4. The Brahmin said: "That (forest) does not exist as a separate entity, nor is anything else equal to it. Nor does it exist as a non-separate entity, nor is anything more distant than it."

sanskrit: तस्माद्ध्रस्वतरं नास्ति न ततोऽस्ति बृहत्तरम् ।नास्ति तस्माद्दुःखतरं नास्त्यन्यत्तत्समं सुखम् ॥५॥
transliteration: tasmāddhrasvataraṁ nāsti na tato'sti bṛhattaram ,nāsti tasmādduḥkhataraṁ nāstyanyattatsamaṁ sukham.
english: There is nothing shorter than it, nor anything greater than it. There is nothing more sorrowful than it, nor any other happiness equal to it.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 27 Verse 5. There is nothing shorter than it, nor anything greater than it. There is nothing more sorrowful than it, nor any other happiness equal to it.

sanskrit: न तत्प्रविश्य शोचन्ति न प्रहृष्यन्ति च द्विजाः ।न च बिभ्यति केषांचित्तेभ्यो बिभ्यति के च न ॥६॥
transliteration: na tatpraviśya śocanti na prahṛṣyanti ca dvijāḥ ,na ca bibhyati keṣāṁcittebhyo bibhyati ke ca na.
english: Having entered that (state or realm), the twice-born (dvijas) neither grieve nor rejoice. They do not fear anyone, nor do some others fear them.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 27 Verse 6. Having entered that (state or realm), the twice-born (dvijas) neither grieve nor rejoice. They do not fear anyone, nor do some others fear them.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्वने सप्त महाद्रुमाश्च फलानि सप्तातिथयश्च सप्त ।सप्ताश्रमाः सप्त समाधयश्च दीक्षाश्च सप्तैतदरण्यरूपम् ॥७॥
transliteration: tasminvane sapta mahādrumāśca; phalāni saptātithayaśca sapta ,saptāśramāḥ sapta samādhayaśca; dīkṣāśca saptaitadaraṇyarūpam.
english: In that forest, there are seven great trees, seven fruits, and seven guests. There are seven stages of life (āśramas), seven states of meditative absorption (samādhis), and seven initiations; this is the true nature of that forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 27 Verse 7. In that forest, there are seven great trees, seven fruits, and seven guests. There are seven stages of life (āśramas), seven states of meditative absorption (samādhis), and seven initiations; this is the true nature of that forest.

sanskrit: पञ्चवर्णानि दिव्यानि पुष्पाणि च फलानि च ।सृजन्तः पादपास्तत्र व्याप्य तिष्ठन्ति तद्वनम् ॥८॥
transliteration: pañcavarṇāni divyāni puṣpāṇi ca phalāni ca ,sṛjantaḥ pādapāstatra vyāpya tiṣṭhanti tadvanam.
english: There, trees stand, pervading that forest, producing divine, five-colored flowers and fruits.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 27 Verse 8. There, trees stand, pervading that forest, producing divine, five-colored flowers and fruits.

sanskrit: सुवर्णानि द्विवर्णानि पुष्पाणि च फलानि च ।सृजन्तः पादपास्तत्र व्याप्य तिष्ठन्ति तद्वनम् ॥९॥
transliteration: suvarṇāni dvivarṇāni puṣpāṇi ca phalāni ca ,sṛjantaḥ pādapāstatra vyāpya tiṣṭhanti tadvanam.
english: There, trees stand covering that forest, producing golden and two-colored flowers and fruits.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 27 Verse 9. There, trees stand covering that forest, producing golden and two-colored flowers and fruits.

sanskrit: चतुर्वर्णानि दिव्यानि पुष्पाणि च फलानि च ।सृजन्तः पादपास्तत्र व्याप्य तिष्ठन्ति तद्वनम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: caturvarṇāni divyāni puṣpāṇi ca phalāni ca ,sṛjantaḥ pādapāstatra vyāpya tiṣṭhanti tadvanam.
english: There, trees stand covering that forest, producing divine and four-colored flowers and fruits.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 27 Verse 10. There, trees stand covering that forest, producing divine and four-colored flowers and fruits.

sanskrit: शंकराणि त्रिवर्णानि पुष्पाणि च फलानि च ।सृजन्तः पादपास्तत्र व्याप्य तिष्ठन्ति तद्वनम् ॥११॥
transliteration: śaṁkarāṇi trivarṇāni puṣpāṇi ca phalāni ca ,sṛjantaḥ pādapāstatra vyāpya tiṣṭhanti tadvanam.
english: There, trees stand covering that forest, producing auspicious and three-colored flowers and fruits.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 27 Verse 11. There, trees stand covering that forest, producing auspicious and three-colored flowers and fruits.

sanskrit: सुरभीण्येकवर्णानि पुष्पाणि च फलानि च ।सृजन्तः पादपास्तत्र व्याप्य तिष्ठन्ति तद्वनम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: surabhīṇyekavarṇāni puṣpāṇi ca phalāni ca ,sṛjantaḥ pādapāstatra vyāpya tiṣṭhanti tadvanam.
english: There, trees stand covering that forest, producing fragrant and single-colored flowers and fruits.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 27 Verse 12. There, trees stand covering that forest, producing fragrant and single-colored flowers and fruits.

sanskrit: बहून्यव्यक्तवर्णानि पुष्पाणि च फलानि च ।विसृजन्तौ महावृक्षौ तद्वनं व्याप्य तिष्ठतः ॥१३॥
transliteration: bahūnyavyaktavarṇāni puṣpāṇi ca phalāni ca ,visṛjantau mahāvṛkṣau tadvanaṁ vyāpya tiṣṭhataḥ.
english: Two mighty trees, producing numerous flowers and fruits of indistinct colors, stand there, having spread throughout that forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 27 Verse 13. Two mighty trees, producing numerous flowers and fruits of indistinct colors, stand there, having spread throughout that forest.

sanskrit: एको ह्यग्निः सुमना ब्राह्मणोऽत्र पञ्चेन्द्रियाणि समिधश्चात्र सन्ति ।तेभ्यो मोक्षाः सप्त भवन्ति दीक्षा गुणाः फलान्यतिथयः फलाशाः ॥१४॥
transliteration: eko hyagniḥ sumanā brāhmaṇo'tra; pañcendriyāṇi samidhaścātra santi ,tebhyo mokṣāḥ sapta bhavanti dīkṣā; guṇāḥ phalānyatithayaḥ phalāśāḥ.
english: Indeed, here the single fire is a pure-minded person (brāhmaṇa), and the five sense organs are the sacrificial fuels. From these, seven liberations (mokṣa) arise. Also present are initiations (dīkṣā), the intrinsic qualities (guṇa), results, guests, and the desires for results.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 27 Verse 14. Indeed, here the single fire is a pure-minded person (brāhmaṇa), and the five sense organs are the sacrificial fuels. From these, seven liberations (mokṣa) arise. Also present are initiations (dīkṣā), the intrinsic qualities (guṇa), results, guests, and the desires for results.

sanskrit: आतिथ्यं प्रतिगृह्णन्ति तत्र सप्त महर्षयः ।अर्चितेषु प्रलीनेषु तेष्वन्यद्रोचते वनम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: ātithyaṁ pratigṛhṇanti tatra sapta maharṣayaḥ ,arciteṣu pralīneṣu teṣvanyadrocate vanam.
english: There, the seven great sages accept hospitality. When they are honored and then absorbed (into the divine), another forest becomes pleasing.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 27 Verse 15. There, the seven great sages accept hospitality. When they are honored and then absorbed (into the divine), another forest becomes pleasing.

sanskrit: प्रतिज्ञावृक्षमफलं शान्तिच्छायासमन्वितम् ।ज्ञानाश्रयं तृप्तितोयमन्तःक्षेत्रज्ञभास्करम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: pratijñāvṛkṣamaphalaṁ śānticchāyāsamanvitam ,jñānāśrayaṁ tṛptitoyamantaḥkṣetrajñabhāskaram.
english: (It is) a forest like a tree of vows that yields no fruit, endowed with the shade of peace, a refuge of knowledge, having the water of contentment, and the inner knower (kṣetrajña) as its sun.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 27 Verse 16. (It is) a forest like a tree of vows that yields no fruit, endowed with the shade of peace, a refuge of knowledge, having the water of contentment, and the inner knower (kṣetrajña) as its sun.

sanskrit: येऽधिगच्छन्ति तत्सन्तस्तेषां नास्ति भयं पुनः ।ऊर्ध्वं चावाक्च तिर्यक्च तस्य नान्तोऽधिगम्यते ॥१७॥
transliteration: ye'dhigacchanti tatsantasteṣāṁ nāsti bhayaṁ punaḥ ,ūrdhvaṁ cāvākca tiryakca tasya nānto'dhigamyate.
