english: Those truly devoted people who attain that ultimate reality (brahman) have no fear thereafter. Its end is not perceived, neither upwards, nor downwards, nor sideways.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 27 Verse 17. Those truly devoted people who attain that ultimate reality (brahman) have no fear thereafter. Its end is not perceived, neither upwards, nor downwards, nor sideways.

sanskrit: सप्त स्त्रियस्तत्र वसन्ति सद्यो अवाङ्मुखा भानुमत्यो जनित्र्यः ।ऊर्ध्वं रसानां ददते प्रजाभ्यः सर्वान्यथा सर्वमनित्यतां च ॥१८॥
transliteration: sapta striyastatra vasanti sadyo; avāṅmukhā bhānumatyo janitryaḥ ,ūrdhvaṁ rasānāṁ dadate prajābhyaḥ; sarvānyathā sarvamanityatāṁ ca.
english: Seven radiant, downward-facing mothers (or producing principles) reside there. They immediately offer the upward essences to all creatures, bestowing all things, and indeed all impermanence.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 27 Verse 18. Seven radiant, downward-facing mothers (or producing principles) reside there. They immediately offer the upward essences to all creatures, bestowing all things, and indeed all impermanence.

sanskrit: तत्रैव प्रतितिष्ठन्ति पुनस्तत्रोदयन्ति च ।सप्त सप्तर्षयः सिद्धा वसिष्ठप्रमुखाः सह ॥१९॥
transliteration: tatraiva pratitiṣṭhanti punastatrodayanti ca ,sapta saptarṣayaḥ siddhā vasiṣṭhapramukhāḥ saha.
english: The seven perfected sages (saptaṛṣi), led by Vasiṣṭha, together abide there and again emerge there (in cycles).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 27 Verse 19. The seven perfected sages (saptaṛṣi), led by Vasiṣṭha, together abide there and again emerge there (in cycles).

sanskrit: यशो वर्चो भगश्चैव विजयः सिद्धितेजसी ।एवमेवानुवर्तन्ते सप्त ज्योतींषि भास्करम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: yaśo varco bhagaścaiva vijayaḥ siddhitejasī ,evamevānuvartante sapta jyotīṁṣi bhāskaram.
english: Glory, brilliance, fortune, and indeed victory, success, and splendor – in this very manner, the seven luminaries follow the sun (Bhāskara).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 27 Verse 20. Glory, brilliance, fortune, and indeed victory, success, and splendor – in this very manner, the seven luminaries follow the sun (Bhāskara).

sanskrit: गिरयः पर्वताश्चैव सन्ति तत्र समासतः ।नद्यश्च सरितो वारि वहन्त्यो ब्रह्मसंभवम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: girayaḥ parvatāścaiva santi tatra samāsataḥ ,nadyaśca sarito vāri vahantyo brahmasaṁbhavam.
english: Mountains and hills are indeed found there in abundance. Rivers and streams carry water that originates from the Absolute (brahman).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 27 Verse 21. Mountains and hills are indeed found there in abundance. Rivers and streams carry water that originates from the Absolute (brahman).

sanskrit: नदीनां संगमस्तत्र वैतानः समुपह्वरे ।स्वात्मतृप्ता यतो यान्ति साक्षाद्दान्ताः पितामहम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: nadīnāṁ saṁgamastatra vaitānaḥ samupahvare ,svātmatṛptā yato yānti sākṣāddāntāḥ pitāmaham.
english: There, at a confluence of rivers, in a nearby spot suitable for Vedic ritual (yajña), those who are content in their true self (ātman) and are self-controlled directly proceed to the Grandfather (Brahmā).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 27 Verse 22. There, at a confluence of rivers, in a nearby spot suitable for Vedic ritual (yajña), those who are content in their true self (ātman) and are self-controlled directly proceed to the Grandfather (Brahmā).

sanskrit: कृशाशाः सुव्रताशाश्च तपसा दग्धकिल्बिषाः ।आत्मन्यात्मानमावेश्य ब्रह्माणं समुपासते ॥२३॥
transliteration: kṛśāśāḥ suvratāśāśca tapasā dagdhakilbiṣāḥ ,ātmanyātmānamāveśya brahmāṇaṁ samupāsate.
english: Those whose worldly desires are meager, whose hopes are fixed on righteous observances, and whose impurities are consumed by spiritual discipline (tapas), they merge the individual self (ātman) into the supreme Self (ātman) and worship the Absolute (brahman).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 27 Verse 23. Those whose worldly desires are meager, whose hopes are fixed on righteous observances, and whose impurities are consumed by spiritual discipline (tapas), they merge the individual self (ātman) into the supreme Self (ātman) and worship the Absolute (brahman).

sanskrit: ऋचमप्यत्र शंसन्ति विद्यारण्यविदो जनाः ।तदरण्यमभिप्रेत्य यथाधीरमजायत ॥२४॥
transliteration: ṛcamapyatra śaṁsanti vidyāraṇyavido janāḥ ,tadaraṇyamabhipretya yathādhīramajāyata.
english: Knowledgeable individuals (vidyāraṇyavidaḥ) here also chant the Ṛgveda verse. With reference to that forest (araṇya), it came into being in accordance with their wisdom.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 27 Verse 24. Knowledgeable individuals (vidyāraṇyavidaḥ) here also chant the Ṛgveda verse. With reference to that forest (araṇya), it came into being in accordance with their wisdom.

sanskrit: एतदेतादृशं दिव्यमरण्यं ब्राह्मणा विदुः ।विदित्वा चान्वतिष्ठन्त क्षेत्रज्ञेनानुदर्शितम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: etadetādṛśaṁ divyamaraṇyaṁ brāhmaṇā viduḥ ,viditvā cānvatiṣṭhanta kṣetrajñenānudarśitam.
english: Brahmins understand this kind of divine experience (araṇya), and having understood it, they remained established in it, as demonstrated by the knower of the field (kṣetrajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 27 Verse 25. Brahmins understand this kind of divine experience (araṇya), and having understood it, they remained established in it, as demonstrated by the knower of the field (kṣetrajña).

sanskrit: ब्राह्मण उवाच ।गन्धान्न जिघ्रामि रसान्न वेद्मि रूपं न पश्यामि न च स्पृशामि ।न चापि शब्दान्विविधाञ्शृणोमि न चापि संकल्पमुपैमि किंचित् ॥१॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇa uvāca ,gandhānna jighrāmi rasānna vedmi; rūpaṁ na paśyāmi na ca spṛśāmi ,na cāpi śabdānvividhāñśṛṇomi; na cāpi saṁkalpamupaimi kiṁcit.
english: The brahmin said: I do not smell odors, I do not perceive tastes, I do not see forms, nor do I touch. Nor do I hear various sounds, nor do I entertain any specific resolve (saṃkalpa).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 28 Verse 1. The brahmin said: I do not smell odors, I do not perceive tastes, I do not see forms, nor do I touch. Nor do I hear various sounds, nor do I entertain any specific resolve (saṃkalpa).

sanskrit: अर्थानिष्टान्कामयते स्वभावः सर्वान्द्वेष्यान्प्रद्विषते स्वभावः ।कामद्वेषावुद्भवतः स्वभावात्प्राणापानौ जन्तुदेहान्निवेश्य ॥२॥
transliteration: arthāniṣṭānkāmayate svabhāvaḥ; sarvāndveṣyānpradviṣate svabhāvaḥ ,kāmadveṣāvudbhavataḥ svabhāvā;tprāṇāpānau jantudehānniveśya.
english: One's intrinsic nature (svabhāva) desires agreeable objects, and one's intrinsic nature dislikes all hateful things. Desire (kāma) and aversion (dveṣa) arise from intrinsic nature (svabhāva) after settling the vital breaths (prāṇa and apāna) into the bodies of living beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 28 Verse 2. One's intrinsic nature (svabhāva) desires agreeable objects, and one's intrinsic nature dislikes all hateful things. Desire (kāma) and aversion (dveṣa) arise from intrinsic nature (svabhāva) after settling the vital breaths (prāṇa and apāna) into the bodies of living beings.

sanskrit: तेभ्यश्चान्यांस्तेष्वनित्यांश्च भावान्भूतात्मानं लक्षयेयं शरीरे ।तस्मिंस्तिष्ठन्नास्मि शक्यः कथंचित्कामक्रोधाभ्यां जरया मृत्युना च ॥३॥
transliteration: tebhyaścānyāṁsteṣvanityāṁśca bhāvā;nbhūtātmānaṁ lakṣayeyaṁ śarīre ,tasmiṁstiṣṭhannāsmi śakyaḥ kathaṁci;tkāmakrodhābhyāṁ jarayā mṛtyunā ca.
english: From those (sensory objects) and in those impermanent entities, I should discern the elemental self (bhūtātman) within the body. Remaining in that (state), I am somehow not susceptible to desire (kāma) and anger (krodha), old age, or death.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 28 Verse 3. From those (sensory objects) and in those impermanent entities, I should discern the elemental self (bhūtātman) within the body. Remaining in that (state), I am somehow not susceptible to desire (kāma) and anger (krodha), old age, or death.

sanskrit: अकामयानस्य च सर्वकामानविद्विषाणस्य च सर्वदोषान् ।न मे स्वभावेषु भवन्ति लेपास्तोयस्य बिन्दोरिव पुष्करेषु ॥४॥
transliteration: akāmayānasya ca sarvakāmā;navidviṣāṇasya ca sarvadoṣān ,na me svabhāveṣu bhavanti lepā;stoyasya bindoriva puṣkareṣu.
english: For one who neither craves all desires nor dislikes all faults, no impurities cling to my intrinsic natures (svabhāva), just as a drop of water does not adhere to lotus leaves.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 28 Verse 4. For one who neither craves all desires nor dislikes all faults, no impurities cling to my intrinsic natures (svabhāva), just as a drop of water does not adhere to lotus leaves.

sanskrit: नित्यस्य चैतस्य भवन्ति नित्या निरीक्षमाणस्य बहून्स्वभावान् ।न सज्जते कर्मसु भोगजालं दिवीव सूर्यस्य मयूखजालम् ॥५॥
transliteration: nityasya caitasya bhavanti nityā; nirīkṣamāṇasya bahūnsvabhāvān ,na sajjate karmasu bhogajālaṁ; divīva sūryasya mayūkhajālam.
english: And for this eternal (being), who observes many intrinsic natures (svabhāva), no lasting impurities arise. The web of enjoyments (bhoga) does not adhere to actions (karma), just as the network of sunbeams does not cling in the sky.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 28 Verse 5. And for this eternal (being), who observes many intrinsic natures (svabhāva), no lasting impurities arise. The web of enjoyments (bhoga) does not adhere to actions (karma), just as the network of sunbeams does not cling in the sky.

sanskrit: अत्राप्युदाहरन्तीममितिहासं पुरातनम् ।अध्वर्युयतिसंवादं तं निबोध यशस्विनि ॥६॥
transliteration: atrāpyudāharantīmamitihāsaṁ purātanam ,adhvaryuyatisaṁvādaṁ taṁ nibodha yaśasvini.
english: Here, they also recount this ancient narrative: the dialogue between an adhvaryu priest and a renunciant (yati). O glorious one, listen to it.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 28 Verse 6. Here, they also recount this ancient narrative: the dialogue between an adhvaryu priest and a renunciant (yati). O glorious one, listen to it.

sanskrit: प्रोक्ष्यमाणं पशुं दृष्ट्वा यज्ञकर्मण्यथाब्रवीत् ।यतिरध्वर्युमासीनो हिंसेयमिति कुत्सयन् ॥७॥
transliteration: prokṣyamāṇaṁ paśuṁ dṛṣṭvā yajñakarmaṇyathābravīt ,yatiradhvaryumāsīno hiṁseyamiti kutsayan.
english: Having seen an animal being consecrated in the context of a Vedic ritual (yajña), the renunciant (yati), while seated, then spoke to the adhvaryu priest, criticizing, "This is violence!"
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 28 Verse 7. Having seen an animal being consecrated in the context of a Vedic ritual (yajña), the renunciant (yati), while seated, then spoke to the adhvaryu priest, criticizing, "This is violence!"

sanskrit: तमध्वर्युः प्रत्युवाच नायं छागो विनश्यति ।श्रेयसा योक्ष्यते जन्तुर्यदि श्रुतिरियं तथा ॥८॥
transliteration: tamadhvaryuḥ pratyuvāca nāyaṁ chāgo vinaśyati ,śreyasā yokṣyate janturyadi śrutiriyaṁ tathā.
english: The priest (adhvaryu) replied, "This goat will not perish. If this Vedic declaration (śruti) is indeed so, then this being will be endowed with auspiciousness."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 28 Verse 8. The priest (adhvaryu) replied, "This goat will not perish. If this Vedic declaration (śruti) is indeed so, then this being will be endowed with auspiciousness."

sanskrit: यो ह्यस्य पार्थिवो भागः पृथिवीं स गमिष्यति ।यदस्य वारिजं किंचिदपस्तत्प्रतिपद्यते ॥९॥
transliteration: yo hyasya pārthivo bhāgaḥ pṛthivīṁ sa gamiṣyati ,yadasya vārijaṁ kiṁcidapastatpratipadyate.
english: Whatever earthly part of it exists, that will go to the earth. Whatever watery part of it exists, that attains the waters.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 28 Verse 9. Whatever earthly part of it exists, that will go to the earth. Whatever watery part of it exists, that attains the waters.

sanskrit: सूर्यं चक्षुर्दिशः श्रोत्रे प्राणोऽस्य दिवमेव च ।आगमे वर्तमानस्य न मे दोषोऽस्ति कश्चन ॥१०॥
transliteration: sūryaṁ cakṣurdiśaḥ śrotre prāṇo'sya divameva ca ,āgame vartamānasya na me doṣo'sti kaścana.
english: Its eyes go to the sun, its ears to the directions, and its breath (prāṇa) to the sky itself. No fault whatsoever exists for me, who am acting according to tradition (āgama).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 28 Verse 10. Its eyes go to the sun, its ears to the directions, and its breath (prāṇa) to the sky itself. No fault whatsoever exists for me, who am acting according to tradition (āgama).

sanskrit: यतिरुवाच ।प्राणैर्वियोगे छागस्य यदि श्रेयः प्रपश्यसि ।छागार्थे वर्तते यज्ञो भवतः किं प्रयोजनम् ॥११॥
transliteration: yatiruvāca ,prāṇairviyoge chāgasya yadi śreyaḥ prapaśyasi ,chāgārthe vartate yajño bhavataḥ kiṁ prayojanam.
english: The ascetic (yati) said: "If you clearly see auspiciousness in the separation from the goat's life-breaths (prāṇa), then what purpose does your Vedic ritual (yajña) serve, which is performed for the sake of the goat?"
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 28 Verse 11. The ascetic (yati) said: "If you clearly see auspiciousness in the separation from the goat's life-breaths (prāṇa), then what purpose does your Vedic ritual (yajña) serve, which is performed for the sake of the goat?"

sanskrit: अनु त्वा मन्यतां माता पिता भ्राता सखापि च ।मन्त्रयस्वैनमुन्नीय परवन्तं विशेषतः ॥१२॥
transliteration: anu tvā manyatāṁ mātā pitā bhrātā sakhāpi ca ,mantrayasvainamunnīya paravantaṁ viśeṣataḥ.
english: Let your mother, father, brother, and friend approve of you. Elevate him and consult him, especially one who is important or has others dependent on him.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 28 Verse 12. Let your mother, father, brother, and friend approve of you. Elevate him and consult him, especially one who is important or has others dependent on him.

sanskrit: य एवमनुमन्येरंस्तान्भवान्प्रष्टुमर्हति ।तेषामनुमतं श्रुत्वा शक्या कर्तुं विचारणा ॥१३॥
transliteration: ya evamanumanyeraṁstānbhavānpraṣṭumarhati ,teṣāmanumataṁ śrutvā śakyā kartuṁ vicāraṇā.
english: You (sir) should question those who would thus give their approval. Having heard their opinion, it is then possible to conduct a proper investigation.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 28 Verse 13. You (sir) should question those who would thus give their approval. Having heard their opinion, it is then possible to conduct a proper investigation.

sanskrit: प्राणा अप्यस्य छागस्य प्रापितास्ते स्वयोनिषु ।शरीरं केवलं शिष्टं निश्चेष्टमिति मे मतिः ॥१४॥
transliteration: prāṇā apyasya chāgasya prāpitāste svayoniṣu ,śarīraṁ kevalaṁ śiṣṭaṁ niśceṣṭamiti me matiḥ.
english: The vital breaths of this goat have also been returned to their respective sources. Only the body remains, motionless; this is my opinion.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 28 Verse 14. The vital breaths of this goat have also been returned to their respective sources. Only the body remains, motionless; this is my opinion.

sanskrit: इन्धनस्य तु तुल्येन शरीरेण विचेतसा ।हिंसा निर्वेष्टुकामानामिन्धनं पशुसंज्ञितम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: indhanasya tu tulyena śarīreṇa vicetasā ,hiṁsā nirveṣṭukāmānāmindhanaṁ paśusaṁjñitam.
english: But with a body that is merely like fuel and devoid of consciousness, for those who wish to perform injury, the animal is designated as fuel.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 28 Verse 15. But with a body that is merely like fuel and devoid of consciousness, for those who wish to perform injury, the animal is designated as fuel.

sanskrit: अहिंसा सर्वधर्माणामिति वृद्धानुशासनम् ।यदहिंस्रं भवेत्कर्म तत्कार्यमिति विद्महे ॥१६॥
transliteration: ahiṁsā sarvadharmāṇāmiti vṛddhānuśāsanam ,yadahiṁsraṁ bhavetkarma tatkāryamiti vidmahe.
english: Non-violence is the intrinsic nature (dharma) for all, such is the instruction of the elders. We know that whatever action (karma) is non-violent, that should be undertaken.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 28 Verse 16. Non-violence is the intrinsic nature (dharma) for all, such is the instruction of the elders. We know that whatever action (karma) is non-violent, that should be undertaken.

sanskrit: अहिंसेति प्रतिज्ञेयं यदि वक्ष्याम्यतः परम् ।शक्यं बहुविधं वक्तुं भवतः कार्यदूषणम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: ahiṁseti pratijñeyaṁ yadi vakṣyāmyataḥ param ,śakyaṁ bahuvidhaṁ vaktuṁ bhavataḥ kāryadūṣaṇam.
english: If I make this assertion about non-violence and speak further on the subject, it is possible to demonstrate the many defects in your conduct (karma).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 28 Verse 17. If I make this assertion about non-violence and speak further on the subject, it is possible to demonstrate the many defects in your conduct (karma).

sanskrit: अहिंसा सर्वभूतानां नित्यमस्मासु रोचते ।प्रत्यक्षतः साधयामो न परोक्षमुपास्महे ॥१८॥
transliteration: ahiṁsā sarvabhūtānāṁ nityamasmāsu rocate ,pratyakṣataḥ sādhayāmo na parokṣamupāsmahe.
english: Non-violence towards all living beings (bhūtas) is always cherished by us. We achieve (our goals) directly; we do not resort to indirect (means).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 28 Verse 18. Non-violence towards all living beings (bhūtas) is always cherished by us. We achieve (our goals) directly; we do not resort to indirect (means).

sanskrit: अध्वर्युरुवाच ।भूमेर्गन्धगुणान्भुङ्क्षे पिबस्यापोमयान्रसान् ।ज्योतिषां पश्यसे रूपं स्पृशस्यनिलजान्गुणान् ॥१९॥
transliteration: adhvaryuruvāca ,bhūmergandhaguṇānbhuṅkṣe pibasyāpomayānrasān ,jyotiṣāṁ paśyase rūpaṁ spṛśasyanilajānguṇān.
english: The Adhvaryu priest said: 'You experience the qualities of scent from the earth, you drink the liquid essences, you perceive the forms of light, and you touch the qualities born of the wind.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 28 Verse 19. The Adhvaryu priest said: 'You experience the qualities of scent from the earth, you drink the liquid essences, you perceive the forms of light, and you touch the qualities born of the wind.'

sanskrit: शृणोष्याकाशजं शब्दं मनसा मन्यसे मतिम् ।सर्वाण्येतानि भूतानि प्राणा इति च मन्यसे ॥२०॥
transliteration: śṛṇoṣyākāśajaṁ śabdaṁ manasā manyase matim ,sarvāṇyetāni bhūtāni prāṇā iti ca manyase.
english: You hear the sound born of space and perceive thought with your mind. And you consider all these beings to be life forces (prāṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 28 Verse 20. You hear the sound born of space and perceive thought with your mind. And you consider all these beings to be life forces (prāṇa).

sanskrit: प्राणादाने च नित्योऽसि हिंसायां वर्तते भवान् ।नास्ति चेष्टा विना हिंसां किं वा त्वं मन्यसे द्विज ॥२१॥
transliteration: prāṇādāne ca nityo'si hiṁsāyāṁ vartate bhavān ,nāsti ceṣṭā vinā hiṁsāṁ kiṁ vā tvaṁ manyase dvija.
english: You are constantly involved in taking life, and you engage in violence (hiṃsā). There is no activity (ceṣṭā) without violence (hiṃsā). What then do you believe, O twice-born (dvija)?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 28 Verse 21. You are constantly involved in taking life, and you engage in violence (hiṃsā). There is no activity (ceṣṭā) without violence (hiṃsā). What then do you believe, O twice-born (dvija)?

sanskrit: यतिरुवाच ।अक्षरं च क्षरं चैव द्वैधीभावोऽयमात्मनः ।अक्षरं तत्र सद्भावः स्वभावः क्षर उच्यते ॥२२॥
transliteration: yatiruvāca ,akṣaraṁ ca kṣaraṁ caiva dvaidhībhāvo'yamātmanaḥ ,akṣaraṁ tatra sadbhāvaḥ svabhāvaḥ kṣara ucyate.
english: The ascetic said: The imperishable (akṣara) and the perishable (kṣara) constitute this twofold nature of the self (ātman). Among them, the imperishable is the state of true existence, while the perishable is termed the intrinsic nature (svabhāva).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 28 Verse 22. The ascetic said: The imperishable (akṣara) and the perishable (kṣara) constitute this twofold nature of the self (ātman). Among them, the imperishable is the state of true existence, while the perishable is termed the intrinsic nature (svabhāva).

sanskrit: प्राणो जिह्वा मनः सत्त्वं स्वभावो रजसा सह ।भावैरेतैर्विमुक्तस्य निर्द्वंद्वस्य निराशिषः ॥२३॥
transliteration: prāṇo jihvā manaḥ sattvaṁ svabhāvo rajasā saha ,bhāvairetairvimuktasya nirdvaṁdvasya nirāśiṣaḥ.
english: Life force (prāṇa), tongue, mind, the quality of goodness (sattva), and intrinsic nature (svabhāva) together with the quality of passion (rajas) – these are the states (bhāva) from which one is liberated, one who is free from dualities, and devoid of desires.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 28 Verse 23. Life force (prāṇa), tongue, mind, the quality of goodness (sattva), and intrinsic nature (svabhāva) together with the quality of passion (rajas) – these are the states (bhāva) from which one is liberated, one who is free from dualities, and devoid of desires.

sanskrit: समस्य सर्वभूतेषु निर्ममस्य जितात्मनः ।समन्तात्परिमुक्तस्य न भयं विद्यते क्वचित् ॥२४॥
transliteration: samasya sarvabhūteṣu nirmamasya jitātmanaḥ ,samantātparimuktasya na bhayaṁ vidyate kvacit.
english: For one who is impartial towards all beings, free from possessiveness, with a controlled self (ātman), and completely liberated, there is no fear whatsoever.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 28 Verse 24. For one who is impartial towards all beings, free from possessiveness, with a controlled self (ātman), and completely liberated, there is no fear whatsoever.

sanskrit: अध्वर्युरुवाच ।सद्भिरेवेह संवासः कार्यो मतिमतां वर ।भवतो हि मतं श्रुत्वा प्रतिभाति मतिर्मम ॥२५॥
transliteration: adhvaryuruvāca ,sadbhireveha saṁvāsaḥ kāryo matimatāṁ vara ,bhavato hi mataṁ śrutvā pratibhāti matirmama.
english: The Adhvaryu said: "Indeed, O best among the wise, one should associate with good people (sadbhiḥ) here. For, having heard your opinion, my understanding (mati) becomes clear."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 28 Verse 25. The Adhvaryu said: "Indeed, O best among the wise, one should associate with good people (sadbhiḥ) here. For, having heard your opinion, my understanding (mati) becomes clear."

sanskrit: भगवन्भगवद्बुद्ध्या प्रतिबुद्धो ब्रवीम्यहम् ।मतं मन्तुं क्रतुं कर्तुं नापराधोऽस्ति मे द्विज ॥२६॥
transliteration: bhagavanbhagavadbuddhyā pratibuddho bravīmyaham ,mataṁ mantuṁ kratuṁ kartuṁ nāparādho'sti me dvija.
english: O revered one, I speak, enlightened by your divine intellect. O twice-born (dvija), there is no offense for me in considering your opinion (matam) as a sacred ritual (kratu) to be performed.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 28 Verse 26. O revered one, I speak, enlightened by your divine intellect. O twice-born (dvija), there is no offense for me in considering your opinion (matam) as a sacred ritual (kratu) to be performed.

sanskrit: ब्राह्मण उवाच ।उपपत्त्या यतिस्तूष्णीं वर्तमानस्ततः परम् ।अध्वर्युरपि निर्मोहः प्रचचार महामखे ॥२७॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇa uvāca ,upapattyā yatistūṣṇīṁ vartamānastataḥ param ,adhvaryurapi nirmohaḥ pracacāra mahāmakhe.
english: The Brahmin said: "Thereafter, the ascetic, remaining silent through proper reasoning, and the Adhvaryu, too, free from delusion, proceeded with the great sacred rite (makha)."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 28 Verse 27. The Brahmin said: "Thereafter, the ascetic, remaining silent through proper reasoning, and the Adhvaryu, too, free from delusion, proceeded with the great sacred rite (makha)."

sanskrit: एवमेतादृशं मोक्षं सुसूक्ष्मं ब्राह्मणा विदुः ।विदित्वा चानुतिष्ठन्ति क्षेत्रज्ञेनानुदर्शिना ॥२८॥
transliteration: evametādṛśaṁ mokṣaṁ susūkṣmaṁ brāhmaṇā viduḥ ,viditvā cānutiṣṭhanti kṣetrajñenānudarśinā.
english: Thus, Brahmins understand such a very subtle liberation (mokṣa). Having understood it, they practice it, guided by the discerning knower of the field (kṣetrajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 28 Verse 28. Thus, Brahmins understand such a very subtle liberation (mokṣa). Having understood it, they practice it, guided by the discerning knower of the field (kṣetrajña).

sanskrit: ब्राह्मण उवाच ।अत्राप्युदाहरन्तीममितिहासं पुरातनम् ।कार्तवीर्यस्य संवादं समुद्रस्य च भामिनि ॥१॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇa uvāca ,atrāpyudāharantīmamitihāsaṁ purātanam ,kārtavīryasya saṁvādaṁ samudrasya ca bhāmini.
english: The Brahmin said: 'In this context, they also recount this ancient story (itihāsa): the conversation between Kārtavīrya and the ocean, O beautiful lady!'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 1. The Brahmin said: 'In this context, they also recount this ancient story (itihāsa): the conversation between Kārtavīrya and the ocean, O beautiful lady!'

sanskrit: कार्तवीर्यार्जुनो नाम राजा बाहुसहस्रवान् ।येन सागरपर्यन्ता धनुषा निर्जिता मही ॥२॥
transliteration: kārtavīryārjuno nāma rājā bāhusahasravān ,yena sāgaraparyantā dhanuṣā nirjitā mahī.
english: There was a king named Kārtavīryārjuna, who possessed a thousand arms. He conquered the entire earth, up to the ocean, with his bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 2. There was a king named Kārtavīryārjuna, who possessed a thousand arms. He conquered the entire earth, up to the ocean, with his bow.

sanskrit: स कदाचित्समुद्रान्ते विचरन्बलदर्पितः ।अवाकिरच्छरशतैः समुद्रमिति नः श्रुतम् ॥३॥
transliteration: sa kadācitsamudrānte vicaranbaladarpitaḥ ,avākiraccharaśataiḥ samudramiti naḥ śrutam.
english: Once, while he (Kārtavīryārjuna) was wandering by the ocean, proud of his immense strength, he showered the ocean with hundreds of arrows. This is how it has been narrated to us.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 3. Once, while he (Kārtavīryārjuna) was wandering by the ocean, proud of his immense strength, he showered the ocean with hundreds of arrows. This is how it has been narrated to us.

sanskrit: तं समुद्रो नमस्कृत्य कृताञ्जलिरुवाच ह ।मा मुञ्च वीर नाराचान्ब्रूहि किं करवाणि ते ॥४॥
transliteration: taṁ samudro namaskṛtya kṛtāñjaliruvāca ha ,mā muñca vīra nārācānbrūhi kiṁ karavāṇi te.
english: The ocean, having bowed to him with folded hands, indeed said: "O hero, do not release your arrows! Tell me, what should I do for you?"
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 4. The ocean, having bowed to him with folded hands, indeed said: "O hero, do not release your arrows! Tell me, what should I do for you?"

sanskrit: मदाश्रयाणि भूतानि त्वद्विसृष्टैर्महेषुभिः ।वध्यन्ते राजशार्दूल तेभ्यो देह्यभयं विभो ॥५॥
transliteration: madāśrayāṇi bhūtāni tvadvisṛṣṭairmaheṣubhiḥ ,vadhyante rājaśārdūla tebhyo dehyabhayaṁ vibho.
english: “O tiger among kings, O powerful one, the beings relying on me are being killed by the great arrows released by you. Grant them safety!”,
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 5. “O tiger among kings, O powerful one, the beings relying on me are being killed by the great arrows released by you. Grant them safety!”,

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच ।मत्समो यदि संग्रामे शरासनधरः क्वचित् ।विद्यते तं ममाचक्ष्व यः समासीत मां मृधे ॥६॥
transliteration: arjuna uvāca ,matsamo yadi saṁgrāme śarāsanadharaḥ kvacit ,vidyate taṁ mamācakṣva yaḥ samāsīta māṁ mṛdhe.
english: Arjuna said: "If anywhere in battle there exists someone equal to me, holding a bow, who would confront me in combat, then tell me about him."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 6. Arjuna said: "If anywhere in battle there exists someone equal to me, holding a bow, who would confront me in combat, then tell me about him."

sanskrit: समुद्र उवाच ।महर्षिर्जमदग्निस्ते यदि राजन्परिश्रुतः ।तस्य पुत्रस्तवातिथ्यं यथावत्कर्तुमर्हति ॥७॥
transliteration: samudra uvāca ,maharṣirjamadagniste yadi rājanpariśrutaḥ ,tasya putrastavātithyaṁ yathāvatkartumarhati.
english: The ocean said: "O king, if the great sage Jamadagni is well-known to you, then his son is able to properly offer hospitality to you."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 7. The ocean said: "O king, if the great sage Jamadagni is well-known to you, then his son is able to properly offer hospitality to you."

sanskrit: ततः स राजा प्रययौ क्रोधेन महता वृतः ।स तमाश्रममागम्य राममेवान्वपद्यत ॥८॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa rājā prayayau krodhena mahatā vṛtaḥ ,sa tamāśramamāgamya rāmamevānvapadyata.
english: Then that king, overcome by great anger, set out. Upon reaching that hermitage, he indeed found Rama.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 8. Then that king, overcome by great anger, set out. Upon reaching that hermitage, he indeed found Rama.

sanskrit: स रामप्रतिकूलानि चकार सह बन्धुभिः ।आयासं जनयामास रामस्य च महात्मनः ॥९॥
transliteration: sa rāmapratikūlāni cakāra saha bandhubhiḥ ,āyāsaṁ janayāmāsa rāmasya ca mahātmanaḥ.
english: He, along with his kinsmen, committed hostile acts against Rama and caused distress to the great-souled (mahātman) Rama.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 9. He, along with his kinsmen, committed hostile acts against Rama and caused distress to the great-souled (mahātman) Rama.

sanskrit: ततस्तेजः प्रजज्वाल रामस्यामिततेजसः ।प्रदहद्रिपुसैन्यानि तदा कमललोचने ॥१०॥
transliteration: tatastejaḥ prajajvāla rāmasyāmitatejasaḥ ,pradahadripusainyāni tadā kamalalocane.
english: Then, O lotus-eyed one (kamalalocana), the immeasurable (amitatejas) radiance of Rama blazed forth, consuming the enemy armies at that time.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 10. Then, O lotus-eyed one (kamalalocana), the immeasurable (amitatejas) radiance of Rama blazed forth, consuming the enemy armies at that time.

sanskrit: ततः परशुमादाय स तं बाहुसहस्रिणम् ।चिच्छेद सहसा रामो बाहुशाखमिव द्रुमम् ॥११॥
transliteration: tataḥ paraśumādāya sa taṁ bāhusahasriṇam ,ciccheda sahasā rāmo bāhuśākhamiva drumam.
english: Then Rama, taking up his axe, suddenly severed that thousand-armed one (bāhusahasrin), just as one would cut down a tree with many branches.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 11. Then Rama, taking up his axe, suddenly severed that thousand-armed one (bāhusahasrin), just as one would cut down a tree with many branches.

sanskrit: तं हतं पतितं दृष्ट्वा समेताः सर्वबान्धवाः ।असीनादाय शक्तीश्च भार्गवं पर्यवारयन् ॥१२॥
transliteration: taṁ hataṁ patitaṁ dṛṣṭvā sametāḥ sarvabāndhavāḥ ,asīnādāya śaktīśca bhārgavaṁ paryavārayan.
english: Having seen him slain and fallen, all his relatives assembled, taking up their swords and spears, and surrounded Bhargava.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 12. Having seen him slain and fallen, all his relatives assembled, taking up their swords and spears, and surrounded Bhargava.

sanskrit: रामोऽपि धनुरादाय रथमारुह्य सत्वरः ।विसृजञ्शरवर्षाणि व्यधमत्पार्थिवं बलम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: rāmo'pi dhanurādāya rathamāruhya satvaraḥ ,visṛjañśaravarṣāṇi vyadhamatpārthivaṁ balam.
english: Rama also, swiftly taking his bow and mounting his chariot, discharged showers of arrows and destroyed the royal army.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 13. Rama also, swiftly taking his bow and mounting his chariot, discharged showers of arrows and destroyed the royal army.

sanskrit: ततस्तु क्षत्रियाः केचिज्जमदग्निं निहत्य च ।विविशुर्गिरिदुर्गाणि मृगाः सिंहार्दिता इव ॥१४॥
transliteration: tatastu kṣatriyāḥ kecijjamadagniṁ nihatya ca ,viviśurgiridurgāṇi mṛgāḥ siṁhārditā iva.
english: Then some kshatriyas, having killed Jamadagni, entered mountain strongholds, just like deer harassed by lions.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 14. Then some kshatriyas, having killed Jamadagni, entered mountain strongholds, just like deer harassed by lions.

sanskrit: तेषां स्वविहितं कर्म तद्भयान्नानुतिष्ठताम् ।प्रजा वृषलतां प्राप्ता ब्राह्मणानामदर्शनात् ॥१५॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ svavihitaṁ karma tadbhayānnānutiṣṭhatām ,prajā vṛṣalatāṁ prāptā brāhmaṇānāmadarśanāt.
english: From that fear, as they did not perform their prescribed actions (karma), and due to the absence of brahmins, the subjects (praja) attained a degraded state (vṛṣalatā).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 15. From that fear, as they did not perform their prescribed actions (karma), and due to the absence of brahmins, the subjects (praja) attained a degraded state (vṛṣalatā).

sanskrit: त एते द्रमिडाः काशाः पुण्ड्राश्च शबरैः सह ।वृषलत्वं परिगता व्युत्थानात्क्षत्रधर्मतः ॥१६॥
transliteration: ta ete dramiḍāḥ kāśāḥ puṇḍrāśca śabaraiḥ saha ,vṛṣalatvaṁ parigatā vyutthānātkṣatradharmataḥ.
english: Those Draviḍas, Kāśas, and Puṇḍras, along with the Śabaras, fell to the status of outcasts due to their deviation from the intrinsic nature (dharma) of a kṣatriya.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 16. Those Draviḍas, Kāśas, and Puṇḍras, along with the Śabaras, fell to the status of outcasts due to their deviation from the intrinsic nature (dharma) of a kṣatriya.

sanskrit: ततस्तु हतवीरासु क्षत्रियासु पुनः पुनः ।द्विजैरुत्पादितं क्षत्रं जामदग्न्यो न्यकृन्तत ॥१७॥
transliteration: tatastu hatavīrāsu kṣatriyāsu punaḥ punaḥ ,dvijairutpāditaṁ kṣatraṁ jāmadagnyo nyakṛntata.
english: Then, when the kṣatriya women had repeatedly lost their warriors, Jamadagni's son (Paraśurāma) destroyed the kṣatriya class that had been regenerated by the twice-born.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 17. Then, when the kṣatriya women had repeatedly lost their warriors, Jamadagni's son (Paraśurāma) destroyed the kṣatriya class that had been regenerated by the twice-born.

sanskrit: एकविंशतिमेधान्ते रामं वागशरीरिणी ।दिव्या प्रोवाच मधुरा सर्वलोकपरिश्रुता ॥१८॥
transliteration: ekaviṁśatimedhānte rāmaṁ vāgaśarīriṇī ,divyā provāca madhurā sarvalokapariśrutā.
english: At the conclusion of twenty-one Vedic rituals (yajña), a divine, sweet, bodiless voice, known throughout all worlds, spoke to Rāma.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 18. At the conclusion of twenty-one Vedic rituals (yajña), a divine, sweet, bodiless voice, known throughout all worlds, spoke to Rāma.

sanskrit: राम राम निवर्तस्व कं गुणं तात पश्यसि ।क्षत्रबन्धूनिमान्प्राणैर्विप्रयोज्य पुनः पुनः ॥१९॥
transliteration: rāma rāma nivartasva kaṁ guṇaṁ tāta paśyasi ,kṣatrabandhūnimānprāṇairviprayojya punaḥ punaḥ.
english: O Rāma, O Rāma, turn back! My dear one, what merit (guṇa) do you see in repeatedly depriving these kṣatriya-kinsmen of their lives?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 19. O Rāma, O Rāma, turn back! My dear one, what merit (guṇa) do you see in repeatedly depriving these kṣatriya-kinsmen of their lives?

sanskrit: तथैव तं महात्मानमृचीकप्रमुखास्तदा ।पितामहा महाभाग निवर्तस्वेत्यथाब्रुवन् ॥२०॥
transliteration: tathaiva taṁ mahātmānamṛcīkapramukhāstadā ,pitāmahā mahābhāga nivartasvetyathābruvan.
english: In the same manner, then, the venerable ancestors, headed by Ṛcīka, spoke to that great-souled person, saying, 'O greatly fortunate one, turn back!'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 20. In the same manner, then, the venerable ancestors, headed by Ṛcīka, spoke to that great-souled person, saying, 'O greatly fortunate one, turn back!'

sanskrit: पितुर्वधममृष्यंस्तु रामः प्रोवाच तानृषीन् ।नार्हन्तीह भवन्तो मां निवारयितुमित्युत ॥२१॥
transliteration: piturvadhamamṛṣyaṁstu rāmaḥ provāca tānṛṣīn ,nārhantīha bhavanto māṁ nivārayitumityuta.
english: But Rāma, unable to tolerate his father's murder, replied to those sages: 'Indeed, you, venerable ones, are not capable of restraining me in this matter.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 21. But Rāma, unable to tolerate his father's murder, replied to those sages: 'Indeed, you, venerable ones, are not capable of restraining me in this matter.'

sanskrit: पितर ऊचुः ।नार्हसे क्षत्रबन्धूंस्त्वं निहन्तुं जयतां वर ।न हि युक्तं त्वया हन्तुं ब्राह्मणेन सता नृपान् ॥२२॥
transliteration: pitara ūcuḥ ,nārhase kṣatrabandhūṁstvaṁ nihantuṁ jayatāṁ vara ,na hi yuktaṁ tvayā hantuṁ brāhmaṇena satā nṛpān.
english: The ancestors said: 'O best among victors, you should not kill these so-called kṣatriyas. For indeed, it is not appropriate for you, a brahmin (brāhmaṇa), to slay kings.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 29 Verse 22. The ancestors said: 'O best among victors, you should not kill these so-called kṣatriyas. For indeed, it is not appropriate for you, a brahmin (brāhmaṇa), to slay kings.'

sanskrit: पितर ऊचुः ।अत्राप्युदाहरन्तीममितिहासं पुरातनम् ।श्रुत्वा च तत्तथा कार्यं भवता द्विजसत्तम ॥१॥
transliteration: pitara ūcuḥ ,atrāpyudāharantīmamitihāsaṁ purātanam ,śrutvā ca tattathā kāryaṁ bhavatā dvijasattama.
english: The ancestors said: 'Here, they also cite this ancient narrative (itihāsa). And having heard that, O best among brahmins (dvija), it should be done by you accordingly.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 30 Verse 1. The ancestors said: 'Here, they also cite this ancient narrative (itihāsa). And having heard that, O best among brahmins (dvija), it should be done by you accordingly.'

sanskrit: अलर्को नाम राजर्षिरभवत्सुमहातपाः ।धर्मज्ञः सत्यसंधश्च महात्मा सुमहाव्रतः ॥२॥
transliteration: alarko nāma rājarṣirabhavatsumahātapāḥ ,dharmajñaḥ satyasaṁdhaśca mahātmā sumahāvrataḥ.
english: There was a royal sage named Alarka, who possessed great asceticism, was knowledgeable in natural law (dharma), truthful to his commitments, a great soul, and of very great vows.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 30 Verse 2. There was a royal sage named Alarka, who possessed great asceticism, was knowledgeable in natural law (dharma), truthful to his commitments, a great soul, and of very great vows.

sanskrit: स सागरान्तां धनुषा विनिर्जित्य महीमिमाम् ।कृत्वा सुदुष्करं कर्म मनः सूक्ष्मे समादधे ॥३॥
transliteration: sa sāgarāntāṁ dhanuṣā vinirjitya mahīmimām ,kṛtvā suduṣkaraṁ karma manaḥ sūkṣme samādadhe.
english: Having completely conquered this earth, extending to the oceans, with his bow, and having performed a very difficult action (karma), he then focused his mind on the subtle.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 30 Verse 3. Having completely conquered this earth, extending to the oceans, with his bow, and having performed a very difficult action (karma), he then focused his mind on the subtle.

sanskrit: स्थितस्य वृक्षमूलेऽथ तस्य चिन्ता बभूव ह ।उत्सृज्य सुमहद्राज्यं सूक्ष्मं प्रति महामते ॥४॥
transliteration: sthitasya vṛkṣamūle'tha tasya cintā babhūva ha ,utsṛjya sumahadrājyaṁ sūkṣmaṁ prati mahāmate.
english: Then, as that great-minded one was situated at the foot of a tree, a thought indeed arose in him: 'Having abandoned a very great kingdom, [my focus is now] on the subtle.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 30 Verse 4. Then, as that great-minded one was situated at the foot of a tree, a thought indeed arose in him: 'Having abandoned a very great kingdom, [my focus is now] on the subtle.'

sanskrit: अलर्क उवाच ।मनसो मे बलं जातं मनो जित्वा ध्रुवो जयः ।अन्यत्र बाणानस्यामि शत्रुभिः परिवारितः ॥५॥
transliteration: alarka uvāca ,manaso me balaṁ jātaṁ mano jitvā dhruvo jayaḥ ,anyatra bāṇānasyāmi śatrubhiḥ parivāritaḥ.
english: Alarka said: 'Strength has arisen in my mind. By conquering the mind (manas), victory is certain. Otherwise, if surrounded by enemies, I would be discharging arrows.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 30 Verse 5. Alarka said: 'Strength has arisen in my mind. By conquering the mind (manas), victory is certain. Otherwise, if surrounded by enemies, I would be discharging arrows.'

sanskrit: यदिदं चापलान्मूर्तेः सर्वमेतच्चिकीर्षति ।मनः प्रति सुतीक्ष्णाग्रानहं मोक्ष्यामि सायकान् ॥६॥
transliteration: yadidaṁ cāpalānmūrteḥ sarvametaccikīrṣati ,manaḥ prati sutīkṣṇāgrānahaṁ mokṣyāmi sāyakān.
english: Since this mind desires to do all these things due to the body's impulsiveness, I will unleash my very sharp-pointed arrows against the mind.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 30 Verse 6. Since this mind desires to do all these things due to the body's impulsiveness, I will unleash my very sharp-pointed arrows against the mind.

sanskrit: मन उवाच ।नेमे बाणास्तरिष्यन्ति मामलर्क कथंचन ।तवैव मर्म भेत्स्यन्ति भिन्नमर्मा मरिष्यसि ॥७॥
transliteration: mana uvāca ,neme bāṇāstariṣyanti māmalarka kathaṁcana ,tavaiva marma bhetsyanti bhinnamarmā mariṣyasi.
english: The mind said: 'O Alarka, these arrows will certainly not overcome me in any way. Rather, they will pierce your own vital parts (marman), and with your vital parts pierced, you will die.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 30 Verse 7. The mind said: 'O Alarka, these arrows will certainly not overcome me in any way. Rather, they will pierce your own vital parts (marman), and with your vital parts pierced, you will die.'

sanskrit: अन्यान्बाणान्समीक्षस्व यैस्त्वं मां सूदयिष्यसि ।तच्छ्रुत्वा स विचिन्त्याथ ततो वचनमब्रवीत् ॥८॥
transliteration: anyānbāṇānsamīkṣasva yaistvaṁ māṁ sūdayiṣyasi ,tacchrutvā sa vicintyātha tato vacanamabravīt.
english: Find other arrows by which you intend to defeat me. Having heard that, he (Alarka) pondered and then spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 30 Verse 8. Find other arrows by which you intend to defeat me. Having heard that, he (Alarka) pondered and then spoke these words.

sanskrit: अलर्क उवाच ।आघ्राय सुबहून्गन्धांस्तानेव प्रतिगृध्यति ।तस्माद्घ्राणं प्रति शरान्प्रतिमोक्ष्याम्यहं शितान् ॥९॥
transliteration: alarka uvāca ,āghrāya subahūngandhāṁstāneva pratigṛdhyati ,tasmādghrāṇaṁ prati śarānpratimokṣyāmyahaṁ śitān.
english: Alarka said: 'Having smelled many fragrances, (the mind) greedily clings to those very same ones. Therefore, I will release my sharpened arrows against the sense of smell (ghrāṇa).'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 30 Verse 9. Alarka said: 'Having smelled many fragrances, (the mind) greedily clings to those very same ones. Therefore, I will release my sharpened arrows against the sense of smell (ghrāṇa).'

sanskrit: स विचिन्त्य चिरं कालमलर्को द्विजसत्तम ।नाध्यगच्छत्परं श्रेयो योगान्मतिमतां वरः ॥२७॥

english: O best among the twice-born (dvijas), having pondered for a long time, Alarka, the foremost among the intelligent, did not attain the supreme well-being (śreyas) through (yoga).

sanskrit: स एकाग्रं मनः कृत्वा निश्चलो योगमास्थितः ।इन्द्रियाणि जघानाशु बाणेनैकेन वीर्यवान् ।योगेनात्मानमाविश्य संसिद्धिं परमां ययौ ॥२८॥
transliteration: sa ekāgraṁ manaḥ kṛtvā niścalo yogamāsthitaḥ ,indriyāṇi jaghānāśu bāṇenaikena vīryavān ,yogenātmānamāviśya saṁsiddhiṁ paramāṁ yayau.
english: He, having made his mind one-pointed and unwavering, resorted to (yoga). The powerful one swiftly conquered his senses (indriyas) with a single arrow. Through (yoga), having entered into his inner self (ātman), he attained the highest perfection.

sanskrit: विस्मितश्चापि राजर्षिरिमां गाथां जगाद ह ।अहो कष्टं यदस्माभिः पूर्वं राज्यमनुष्ठितम् ।इति पश्चान्मया ज्ञातं योगान्नास्ति परं सुखम् ॥२९॥
transliteration: vismitaścāpi rājarṣirimāṁ gāthāṁ jagāda ha ,aho kaṣṭaṁ yadasmābhiḥ pūrvaṁ rājyamanuṣṭhitam ,iti paścānmayā jñātaṁ yogānnāsti paraṁ sukham.
english: The astonished royal sage (rājarṣi) indeed spoke this verse: "Alas, how lamentable that kingship was formerly practiced by us! Thus, it was known by me later that there is no supreme happiness (sukha) beyond (yoga)."

sanskrit: इति त्वमपि जानीहि राम मा क्षत्रियाञ्जहि ।तपो घोरमुपातिष्ठ ततः श्रेयोऽभिपत्स्यसे ॥३०॥
transliteration: iti tvamapi jānīhi rāma mā kṣatriyāñjahi ,tapo ghoramupātiṣṭha tataḥ śreyo'bhipatsyase.
english: Therefore, O Rāma, understand this as well: do not kill the kṣatriyas. Undertake severe asceticism (tapas); then you will attain prosperity.

sanskrit: ब्राह्मण उवाच ।इत्युक्तः स तपो घोरं जामदग्न्यः पितामहैः ।आस्थितः सुमहाभागो ययौ सिद्धिं च दुर्गमाम् ॥३१॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇa uvāca ,ityuktaḥ sa tapo ghoraṁ jāmadagnyaḥ pitāmahaiḥ ,āsthitaḥ sumahābhāgo yayau siddhiṁ ca durgamām.
english: The Brahmin said: Thus addressed by his grandfathers, Jāmadagnya, the greatly fortunate one, undertook severe asceticism (tapas) and attained a hard-to-reach accomplishment (siddhi).

sanskrit: ब्राह्मण उवाच ।त्रयो वै रिपवो लोके नव वै गुणतः स्मृताः ।हर्षः स्तम्भोऽभिमानश्च त्रयस्ते सात्त्विका गुणाः ॥१॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇa uvāca ,trayo vai ripavo loke nava vai guṇataḥ smṛtāḥ ,harṣaḥ stambho'bhimānaśca trayaste sāttvikā guṇāḥ.
english: The Brahmin said: Indeed, there are three enemies in the world; nine are remembered according to their inherent qualities (guṇas). Elation, stubbornness, and ego (ahaṅkāra) – these three are considered the sattvic qualities.

sanskrit: शोकः क्रोधोऽतिसंरम्भो राजसास्ते गुणाः स्मृताः ।स्वप्नस्तन्द्री च मोहश्च त्रयस्ते तामसा गुणाः ॥२॥
transliteration: śokaḥ krodho'tisaṁrambho rājasāste guṇāḥ smṛtāḥ ,svapnastandrī ca mohaśca trayaste tāmasā guṇāḥ.
english: Sorrow, anger, and excessive agitation – these are considered the rajasic qualities. Sleep, lethargy, and delusion (moha) – these three are the tamasic qualities.
