sanskrit: एतान्निकृत्य धृतिमान्बाणसंघैरतन्द्रितः ।जेतुं परानुत्सहते प्रशान्तात्मा जितेन्द्रियः ॥३॥
transliteration: etānnikṛtya dhṛtimānbāṇasaṁghairatandritaḥ ,jetuṁ parānutsahate praśāntātmā jitendriyaḥ.
english: A resolute and diligent person, whose self (ātman) is tranquil and senses (indriya) are controlled, endeavors to defeat his enemies by striking them down with volleys of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 31 Verse 3. A resolute and diligent person, whose self (ātman) is tranquil and senses (indriya) are controlled, endeavors to defeat his enemies by striking them down with volleys of arrows.

sanskrit: अत्र गाथाः कीर्तयन्ति पुराकल्पविदो जनाः ।अम्बरीषेण या गीता राज्ञा राज्यं प्रशासता ॥४॥
transliteration: atra gāthāḥ kīrtayanti purākalpavido janāḥ ,ambarīṣeṇa yā gītā rājñā rājyaṁ praśāsatā.
english: Regarding this, those who are knowledgeable in ancient traditions recount verses that were sung by King Ambariṣa while he was governing the kingdom.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 31 Verse 4. Regarding this, those who are knowledgeable in ancient traditions recount verses that were sung by King Ambariṣa while he was governing the kingdom.

sanskrit: समुदीर्णेषु दोषेषु वध्यमानेषु साधुषु ।जग्राह तरसा राज्यमम्बरीष इति श्रुतिः ॥५॥
transliteration: samudīrṇeṣu doṣeṣu vadhyamāneṣu sādhuṣu ,jagrāha tarasā rājyamambarīṣa iti śrutiḥ.
english: Tradition (śruti) says that Ambariṣa forcefully seized the kingdom when faults (doṣa) had emerged and good people were being oppressed.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 31 Verse 5. Tradition (śruti) says that Ambariṣa forcefully seized the kingdom when faults (doṣa) had emerged and good people were being oppressed.

sanskrit: स निगृह्य महादोषान्साधून्समभिपूज्य च ।जगाम महतीं सिद्धिं गाथां चेमां जगाद ह ॥६॥
transliteration: sa nigṛhya mahādoṣānsādhūnsamabhipūjya ca ,jagāma mahatīṁ siddhiṁ gāthāṁ cemāṁ jagāda ha.
english: Having subdued great evils (doṣa) and honored virtuous people, he attained great accomplishment (siddhi), and indeed, he spoke this verse (gāthā).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 31 Verse 6. Having subdued great evils (doṣa) and honored virtuous people, he attained great accomplishment (siddhi), and indeed, he spoke this verse (gāthā).

sanskrit: भूयिष्ठं मे जिता दोषा निहताः सर्वशत्रवः ।एको दोषोऽवशिष्टस्तु वध्यः स न हतो मया ॥७॥
transliteration: bhūyiṣṭhaṁ me jitā doṣā nihatāḥ sarvaśatravaḥ ,eko doṣo'vaśiṣṭastu vadhyaḥ sa na hato mayā.
english: Mostly, my faults have been overcome, and all enemies have been destroyed. But one remaining fault, which ought to be killed, has not been killed by me.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 31 Verse 7. Mostly, my faults have been overcome, and all enemies have been destroyed. But one remaining fault, which ought to be killed, has not been killed by me.

sanskrit: येन युक्तो जन्तुरयं वैतृष्ण्यं नाधिगच्छति ।तृष्णार्त इव निम्नानि धावमानो न बुध्यते ॥८॥
transliteration: yena yukto janturayaṁ vaitṛṣṇyaṁ nādhigacchati ,tṛṣṇārta iva nimnāni dhāvamāno na budhyate.
english: By which (greed), this creature, being joined (with it), does not attain freedom from desire (vaitṛṣṇya). Like one tormented by thirst (tṛṣṇā) running to low places, he does not comprehend (the nature of his endless pursuit).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 31 Verse 8. By which (greed), this creature, being joined (with it), does not attain freedom from desire (vaitṛṣṇya). Like one tormented by thirst (tṛṣṇā) running to low places, he does not comprehend (the nature of his endless pursuit).

sanskrit: अकार्यमपि येनेह प्रयुक्तः सेवते नरः ।तं लोभमसिभिस्तीक्ष्णैर्निकृन्तन्तं निकृन्तत ॥९॥
transliteration: akāryamapi yeneha prayuktaḥ sevate naraḥ ,taṁ lobhamasibhistīkṣṇairnikṛntantaṁ nikṛntata.
english: Even an improper act, a person, impelled by which (greed), commits in this world. That greed (lobha), which is cutting (them) down, you all should cut down with sharp swords.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 31 Verse 9. Even an improper act, a person, impelled by which (greed), commits in this world. That greed (lobha), which is cutting (them) down, you all should cut down with sharp swords.

sanskrit: लोभाद्धि जायते तृष्णा ततश्चिन्ता प्रसज्यते ।स लिप्समानो लभते भूयिष्ठं राजसान्गुणान् ॥१०॥
transliteration: lobhāddhi jāyate tṛṣṇā tataścintā prasajyate ,sa lipsamāno labhate bhūyiṣṭhaṁ rājasānguṇān.
english: Indeed, from greed (lobha) arises desire (tṛṣṇā); from that, worry (cintā) results. He, desiring to obtain (more), mostly acquires the qualities of passion (rajas guṇas).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 31 Verse 10. Indeed, from greed (lobha) arises desire (tṛṣṇā); from that, worry (cintā) results. He, desiring to obtain (more), mostly acquires the qualities of passion (rajas guṇas).

sanskrit: स तैर्गुणैः संहतदेहबन्धनः पुनः पुनर्जायति कर्म चेहते ।जन्मक्षये भिन्नविकीर्णदेहः पुनर्मृत्युं गच्छति जन्मनि स्वे ॥११॥
transliteration: sa tairguṇaiḥ saṁhatadehabandhanaḥ; punaḥ punarjāyati karma cehate ,janmakṣaye bhinnavikīrṇadehaḥ; punarmṛtyuṁ gacchati janmani sve.
english: Bound by those qualities, he is born repeatedly and performs actions (karma). Upon the cessation of a birth, with his body broken and scattered, he again encounters death in his subsequent birth (saṃsāra).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 31 Verse 11. Bound by those qualities, he is born repeatedly and performs actions (karma). Upon the cessation of a birth, with his body broken and scattered, he again encounters death in his subsequent birth (saṃsāra).

sanskrit: तस्मादेनं सम्यगवेक्ष्य लोभं निगृह्य धृत्यात्मनि राज्यमिच्छेत् ।एतद्राज्यं नान्यदस्तीति विद्याद्यस्त्वत्र राजा विजितो ममैकः ॥१२॥
transliteration: tasmādenaṁ samyagavekṣya lobhaṁ; nigṛhya dhṛtyātmani rājyamicchet ,etadrājyaṁ nānyadastīti vidyā;dyastvatra rājā vijito mamaikaḥ.
english: Therefore, having properly observed this greed, one should restrain it with fortitude and desire dominion over one's own self (ātman). One should know that this is true dominion (rājya) and there is no other, for in this realm, the one who has conquered himself is my only true king.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 31 Verse 12. Therefore, having properly observed this greed, one should restrain it with fortitude and desire dominion over one's own self (ātman). One should know that this is true dominion (rājya) and there is no other, for in this realm, the one who has conquered himself is my only true king.

sanskrit: इति राज्ञाम्बरीषेण गाथा गीता यशस्विना ।आधिराज्यं पुरस्कृत्य लोभमेकं निकृन्तता ॥१३॥
transliteration: iti rājñāmbarīṣeṇa gāthā gītā yaśasvinā ,ādhirājyaṁ puraskṛtya lobhamekaṁ nikṛntatā.
english: Thus, this verse was sung by the glorious King Ambarīṣa, who, by prioritizing supreme dominion, annihilated his sole enemy, greed.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 31 Verse 13. Thus, this verse was sung by the glorious King Ambarīṣa, who, by prioritizing supreme dominion, annihilated his sole enemy, greed.

sanskrit: ब्राह्मण उवाच ।अत्राप्युदाहरन्तीममितिहासं पुरातनम् ।ब्राह्मणस्य च संवादं जनकस्य च भामिनि ॥१॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇa uvāca ,atrāpyudāharantīmamitihāsaṁ purātanam ,brāhmaṇasya ca saṁvādaṁ janakasya ca bhāmini.
english: The brāhmaṇa said: 'In this regard, they also narrate this ancient historical account (itihāsa): the dialogue between a brāhmaṇa and Janaka, O noble lady.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 32 Verse 1. The brāhmaṇa said: 'In this regard, they also narrate this ancient historical account (itihāsa): the dialogue between a brāhmaṇa and Janaka, O noble lady.'

sanskrit: ब्राह्मणं जनको राजा सन्नं कस्मिंश्चिदागमे ।विषये मे न वस्तव्यमिति शिष्ट्यर्थमब्रवीत् ॥२॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇaṁ janako rājā sannaṁ kasmiṁścidāgame ,viṣaye me na vastavyamiti śiṣṭyarthamabravīt.
english: King Janaka, for the purpose of instruction, said to a brahmin who was perplexed about a certain teaching: "One should not persist in this matter in my domain [of knowledge]."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 32 Verse 2. King Janaka, for the purpose of instruction, said to a brahmin who was perplexed about a certain teaching: "One should not persist in this matter in my domain [of knowledge]."

sanskrit: इत्युक्तः प्रत्युवाचाथ ब्राह्मणो राजसत्तमम् ।आचक्ष्व विषयं राजन्यावांस्तव वशे स्थितः ॥३॥
transliteration: ityuktaḥ pratyuvācātha brāhmaṇo rājasattamam ,ācakṣva viṣayaṁ rājanyāvāṁstava vaśe sthitaḥ.
english: Then, thus addressed, the brahmin replied to the best of kings: "O King, declare the subject in which we two are situated under your control."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 32 Verse 3. Then, thus addressed, the brahmin replied to the best of kings: "O King, declare the subject in which we two are situated under your control."

sanskrit: सोऽन्यस्य विषये राज्ञो वस्तुमिच्छाम्यहं विभो ।वचस्ते कर्तुमिच्छामि यथाशास्त्रं महीपते ॥४॥
transliteration: so'nyasya viṣaye rājño vastumicchāmyahaṁ vibho ,vacaste kartumicchāmi yathāśāstraṁ mahīpate.
english: "I wish to dwell in the domain of another king, O Lord. I wish to carry out your command according to scripture, O King."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 32 Verse 4. "I wish to dwell in the domain of another king, O Lord. I wish to carry out your command according to scripture, O King."

sanskrit: इत्युक्तः स तदा राजा ब्राह्मणेन यशस्विना ।मुहुरुष्णं च निःश्वस्य न स तं प्रत्यभाषत ॥५॥
transliteration: ityuktaḥ sa tadā rājā brāhmaṇena yaśasvinā ,muhuruṣṇaṁ ca niḥśvasya na sa taṁ pratyabhāṣata.
english: Thus addressed then by the glorious brahmin, that king, having sighed hotly again and again, did not reply to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 32 Verse 5. Thus addressed then by the glorious brahmin, that king, having sighed hotly again and again, did not reply to him.

sanskrit: तमासीनं ध्यायमानं राजानममितौजसम् ।कश्मलं सहसागच्छद्भानुमन्तमिव ग्रहः ॥६॥
transliteration: tamāsīnaṁ dhyāyamānaṁ rājānamamitaujasam ,kaśmalaṁ sahasāgacchadbhānumantamiva grahaḥ.
english: Suddenly, confusion (kaśmalam) afflicted that king of immense radiance, who was seated and absorbed in meditation (dhyāna), just as a planetary seizure (graha) afflicts the sun.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 32 Verse 6. Suddenly, confusion (kaśmalam) afflicted that king of immense radiance, who was seated and absorbed in meditation (dhyāna), just as a planetary seizure (graha) afflicts the sun.

sanskrit: समाश्वास्य ततो राजा व्यपेते कश्मले तदा ।ततो मुहूर्तादिव तं ब्राह्मणं वाक्यमब्रवीत् ॥७॥
transliteration: samāśvāsya tato rājā vyapete kaśmale tadā ,tato muhūrtādiva taṁ brāhmaṇaṁ vākyamabravīt.
english: After reassuring him, when that confusion (kaśmalam) had then vanished, the king, a moment later, spoke these words to the brahmin.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 32 Verse 7. After reassuring him, when that confusion (kaśmalam) had then vanished, the king, a moment later, spoke these words to the brahmin.

sanskrit: पितृपैतामहे राज्ये वश्ये जनपदे सति ।विषयं नाधिगच्छामि विचिन्वन्पृथिवीमिमाम् ॥८॥
transliteration: pitṛpaitāmahe rājye vaśye janapade sati ,viṣayaṁ nādhigacchāmi vicinvanpṛthivīmimām.
english: Even though my paternal and ancestral kingdom (rājya) exists, with its obedient populace (janapada), I find no purpose (viṣayam) while searching throughout this earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 32 Verse 8. Even though my paternal and ancestral kingdom (rājya) exists, with its obedient populace (janapada), I find no purpose (viṣayam) while searching throughout this earth.

sanskrit: नाध्यगच्छं यदा पृथ्व्यां मिथिला मार्गिता मया ।नाध्यगच्छं यदा तस्यां स्वप्रजा मार्गिता मया ॥९॥
transliteration: nādhyagacchaṁ yadā pṛthvyāṁ mithilā mārgitā mayā ,nādhyagacchaṁ yadā tasyāṁ svaprajā mārgitā mayā.
english: When I could not find Mithila on the earth, even though I searched for it, and when I could not find my own subjects (praja) within Mithila, even though I searched for them.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 32 Verse 9. When I could not find Mithila on the earth, even though I searched for it, and when I could not find my own subjects (praja) within Mithila, even though I searched for them.

sanskrit: नाध्यगच्छं यदा तासु तदा मे कश्मलोऽभवत् ।ततो मे कश्मलस्यान्ते मतिः पुनरुपस्थिता ॥१०॥
transliteration: nādhyagacchaṁ yadā tāsu tadā me kaśmalo'bhavat ,tato me kaśmalasyānte matiḥ punarupasthitā.
english: When I did not find satisfaction in those (things), then bewilderment arose in me. Then, at the end of my bewilderment, understanding (mati) again presented itself.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 32 Verse 10. When I did not find satisfaction in those (things), then bewilderment arose in me. Then, at the end of my bewilderment, understanding (mati) again presented itself.

sanskrit: तया न विषयं मन्ये सर्वो वा विषयो मम ।आत्मापि चायं न मम सर्वा वा पृथिवी मम ।उष्यतां यावदुत्साहो भुज्यतां यावदिष्यते ॥११॥
transliteration: tayā na viṣayaṁ manye sarvo vā viṣayo mama ,ātmāpi cāyaṁ na mama sarvā vā pṛthivī mama ,uṣyatāṁ yāvadutsāho bhujyatāṁ yāvadiṣyate.
english: Through that (understanding), I do not consider anything as a mere object (viṣaya); or rather, every object (viṣaya) belongs to me. Even this true self (ātman) is not mine, or perhaps, the entire earth is mine. Let one dwell (here) as long as there is enthusiasm, and let things be enjoyed as long as they are desired.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 32 Verse 11. Through that (understanding), I do not consider anything as a mere object (viṣaya); or rather, every object (viṣaya) belongs to me. Even this true self (ātman) is not mine, or perhaps, the entire earth is mine. Let one dwell (here) as long as there is enthusiasm, and let things be enjoyed as long as they are desired.

sanskrit: पितृपैतामहे राज्ये वश्ये जनपदे सति ।ब्रूहि कां बुद्धिमास्थाय ममत्वं वर्जितं त्वया ॥१२॥
transliteration: pitṛpaitāmahe rājye vaśye janapade sati ,brūhi kāṁ buddhimāsthāya mamatvaṁ varjitaṁ tvayā.
english: With an ancestral kingdom, and the populace under your control, tell me, by adopting what kind of understanding (buddhi) have you renounced possessiveness?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 32 Verse 12. With an ancestral kingdom, and the populace under your control, tell me, by adopting what kind of understanding (buddhi) have you renounced possessiveness?

sanskrit: कां वा बुद्धिं विनिश्चित्य सर्वो वै विषयस्तव ।नावैषि विषयं येन सर्वो वा विषयस्तव ॥१३॥
transliteration: kāṁ vā buddhiṁ viniścitya sarvo vai viṣayastava ,nāvaiṣi viṣayaṁ yena sarvo vā viṣayastava.
english: By having ascertained what understanding (buddhi), is it that every object (viṣaya) truly belongs to you? Or is it that through it, you do not perceive (anything as a separate) object (viṣaya), and yet every object (viṣaya) is indeed yours?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 32 Verse 13. By having ascertained what understanding (buddhi), is it that every object (viṣaya) truly belongs to you? Or is it that through it, you do not perceive (anything as a separate) object (viṣaya), and yet every object (viṣaya) is indeed yours?

sanskrit: जनक उवाच ।अन्तवन्त इहारम्भा विदिताः सर्वकर्मसु ।नाध्यगच्छमहं यस्मान्ममेदमिति यद्भवेत् ॥१४॥
transliteration: janaka uvāca ,antavanta ihārambhā viditāḥ sarvakarmasu ,nādhyagacchamahaṁ yasmānmamedamiti yadbhavet.
english: Janaka said: "All undertakings in this world are known to be finite in all actions. Therefore, I have never found anything that I could claim as 'This is mine'."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 32 Verse 14. Janaka said: "All undertakings in this world are known to be finite in all actions. Therefore, I have never found anything that I could claim as 'This is mine'."

sanskrit: कस्येदमिति कस्य स्वमिति वेदवचस्तथा ।नाध्यगच्छमहं बुद्ध्या ममेदमिति यद्भवेत् ॥१५॥
transliteration: kasyedamiti kasya svamiti vedavacastathā ,nādhyagacchamahaṁ buddhyā mamedamiti yadbhavet.
english: Thus, considering the Vedic statements 'Whose is this?' and 'Whose is one's own property?', I have not understood with my intellect what could truly be 'mine'.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 32 Verse 15. Thus, considering the Vedic statements 'Whose is this?' and 'Whose is one's own property?', I have not understood with my intellect what could truly be 'mine'.

sanskrit: एतां बुद्धिं विनिश्चित्य ममत्वं वर्जितं मया ।शृणु बुद्धिं तु यां ज्ञात्वा सर्वत्र विषयो मम ॥१६॥
transliteration: etāṁ buddhiṁ viniścitya mamatvaṁ varjitaṁ mayā ,śṛṇu buddhiṁ tu yāṁ jñātvā sarvatra viṣayo mama.
english: Having firmly ascertained this understanding, all possessiveness (mamatva) has been abandoned by me. Now, listen to the wisdom by which, once known, everything becomes my domain.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 32 Verse 16. Having firmly ascertained this understanding, all possessiveness (mamatva) has been abandoned by me. Now, listen to the wisdom by which, once known, everything becomes my domain.

sanskrit: नाहमात्मार्थमिच्छामि गन्धान्घ्राणगतानपि ।तस्मान्मे निर्जिता भूमिर्वशे तिष्ठति नित्यदा ॥१७॥
transliteration: nāhamātmārthamicchāmi gandhānghrāṇagatānapi ,tasmānme nirjitā bhūmirvaśe tiṣṭhati nityadā.
english: I do not desire even fragrances that have reached my nose for my own self (ātman). Therefore, the entire earth has been conquered by me and remains under my control always.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 32 Verse 17. I do not desire even fragrances that have reached my nose for my own self (ātman). Therefore, the entire earth has been conquered by me and remains under my control always.

sanskrit: नाहमात्मार्थमिच्छामि रसानास्येऽपि वर्ततः ।आपो मे निर्जितास्तस्माद्वशे तिष्ठन्ति नित्यदा ॥१८॥
transliteration: nāhamātmārthamicchāmi rasānāsye'pi vartataḥ ,āpo me nirjitāstasmādvaśe tiṣṭhanti nityadā.
english: I do not desire flavors, even those present in the mouth, for my own (ātman) sake. Therefore, the element of water (āpaḥ) has been subdued by me and perpetually remains under my control.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 32 Verse 18. I do not desire flavors, even those present in the mouth, for my own (ātman) sake. Therefore, the element of water (āpaḥ) has been subdued by me and perpetually remains under my control.

sanskrit: नाहमात्मार्थमिच्छामि रूपं ज्योतिश्च चक्षुषा ।तस्मान्मे निर्जितं ज्योतिर्वशे तिष्ठति नित्यदा ॥१९॥
transliteration: nāhamātmārthamicchāmi rūpaṁ jyotiśca cakṣuṣā ,tasmānme nirjitaṁ jyotirvaśe tiṣṭhati nityadā.
english: I do not desire forms and light, perceived by the eye, for my own (ātman) sake. Therefore, the element of fire/light (jyotis) is conquered by me and perpetually remains under my control.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 32 Verse 19. I do not desire forms and light, perceived by the eye, for my own (ātman) sake. Therefore, the element of fire/light (jyotis) is conquered by me and perpetually remains under my control.

sanskrit: नाहमात्मार्थमिच्छामि स्पर्शांस्त्वचि गताश्च ये ।तस्मान्मे निर्जितो वायुर्वशे तिष्ठति नित्यदा ॥२०॥
transliteration: nāhamātmārthamicchāmi sparśāṁstvaci gatāśca ye ,tasmānme nirjito vāyurvaśe tiṣṭhati nityadā.
english: I do not desire for my own (ātman) sake touches which are present in the skin. Therefore, the element of air (vāyu) is conquered by me and perpetually remains under my control.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 32 Verse 20. I do not desire for my own (ātman) sake touches which are present in the skin. Therefore, the element of air (vāyu) is conquered by me and perpetually remains under my control.

sanskrit: नाहमात्मार्थमिच्छामि शब्दाञ्श्रोत्रगतानपि ।तस्मान्मे निर्जिताः शब्दा वशे तिष्ठन्ति नित्यदा ॥२१॥
transliteration: nāhamātmārthamicchāmi śabdāñśrotragatānapi ,tasmānme nirjitāḥ śabdā vaśe tiṣṭhanti nityadā.
english: I do not desire for my own (ātman) sake sounds, even those received by the ear. Therefore, the element of ether/space (śabda) is conquered by me and perpetually remains under my control.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 32 Verse 21. I do not desire for my own (ātman) sake sounds, even those received by the ear. Therefore, the element of ether/space (śabda) is conquered by me and perpetually remains under my control.

sanskrit: नाहमात्मार्थमिच्छामि मनो नित्यं मनोन्तरे ।मनो मे निर्जितं तस्माद्वशे तिष्ठति नित्यदा ॥२२॥
transliteration: nāhamātmārthamicchāmi mano nityaṁ manontare ,mano me nirjitaṁ tasmādvaśe tiṣṭhati nityadā.
english: I do not desire anything for my own personal (ātman) benefit. The mind is constantly moving from one object to another. However, my mind has been completely conquered; therefore, it always remains under my control.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 32 Verse 22. I do not desire anything for my own personal (ātman) benefit. The mind is constantly moving from one object to another. However, my mind has been completely conquered; therefore, it always remains under my control.

sanskrit: देवेभ्यश्च पितृभ्यश्च भूतेभ्योऽतिथिभिः सह ।इत्यर्थं सर्व एवेमे समारम्भा भवन्ति वै ॥२३॥
transliteration: devebhyaśca pitṛbhyaśca bhūtebhyo'tithibhiḥ saha ,ityarthaṁ sarva eveme samārambhā bhavanti vai.
english: All these undertakings (sāmarambhāḥ) are indeed for the sake of the gods, the ancestors, (all) beings, and together with the guests.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 32 Verse 23. All these undertakings (sāmarambhāḥ) are indeed for the sake of the gods, the ancestors, (all) beings, and together with the guests.

sanskrit: ततः प्रहस्य जनकं ब्राह्मणः पुनरब्रवीत् ।त्वज्जिज्ञासार्थमद्येह विद्धि मां धर्ममागतम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: tataḥ prahasya janakaṁ brāhmaṇaḥ punarabravīt ,tvajjijñāsārthamadyeha viddhi māṁ dharmamāgatam.
english: Then, laughing, the Brahmin spoke again to Janaka: "Know me, who has come here today for the purpose of your inquiry, to be the very principle of natural law (dharma)."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 32 Verse 24. Then, laughing, the Brahmin spoke again to Janaka: "Know me, who has come here today for the purpose of your inquiry, to be the very principle of natural law (dharma)."

sanskrit: त्वमस्य ब्रह्मनाभस्य बुद्ध्यारस्यानिवर्तिनः ।सत्त्वनेमिनिरुद्धस्य चक्रस्यैकः प्रवर्तकः ॥२५॥
transliteration: tvamasya brahmanābhasya buddhyārasyānivartinaḥ ,sattvaneminiruddhasya cakrasyaikaḥ pravartakaḥ.
english: You are the sole impeller of this unceasing wheel, whose hub is the ultimate reality (brahman), whose spokes are wisdom, and whose rim is restrained by the principle of goodness (sattva).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 32 Verse 25. You are the sole impeller of this unceasing wheel, whose hub is the ultimate reality (brahman), whose spokes are wisdom, and whose rim is restrained by the principle of goodness (sattva).

sanskrit: ब्राह्मण उवाच ।नाहं तथा भीरु चरामि लोके तथा त्वं मां तर्कयसे स्वबुद्ध्या ।विप्रोऽस्मि मुक्तोऽस्मि वनेचरोऽस्मि गृहस्थधर्मा ब्रह्मचारी तथास्मि ॥१॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇa uvāca ,nāhaṁ tathā bhīru carāmi loke; tathā tvaṁ māṁ tarkayase svabuddhyā ,vipro'smi mukto'smi vanecaro'smi; gṛhasthadharmā brahmacārī tathāsmi.
english: The brahmin said: "I do not move about the world in such a fearful manner as you surmise me to be, based on your own understanding. I am a brahmin (vipra), I am liberated (mukta), I am a forest-dweller, and I also follow the natural law (dharma) of a householder, and I am a celibate student (brahmacārī)."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 33 Verse 1. The brahmin said: "I do not move about the world in such a fearful manner as you surmise me to be, based on your own understanding. I am a brahmin (vipra), I am liberated (mukta), I am a forest-dweller, and I also follow the natural law (dharma) of a householder, and I am a celibate student (brahmacārī)."

sanskrit: नाहमस्मि यथा मां त्वं पश्यसे चक्षुषा शुभे ।मया व्याप्तमिदं सर्वं यत्किंचिज्जगतीगतम् ॥२॥
transliteration: nāhamasmi yathā māṁ tvaṁ paśyase cakṣuṣā śubhe ,mayā vyāptamidaṁ sarvaṁ yatkiṁcijjagatīgatam.
english: O beautiful one, I am not as you perceive me with your eye. Everything in this world, whatever exists, is pervaded by me.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 33 Verse 2. O beautiful one, I am not as you perceive me with your eye. Everything in this world, whatever exists, is pervaded by me.

sanskrit: ये केचिज्जन्तवो लोके जङ्गमाः स्थावराश्च ह ।तेषां मामन्तकं विद्धि दारूणामिव पावकम् ॥३॥
transliteration: ye kecijjantavo loke jaṅgamāḥ sthāvarāśca ha ,teṣāṁ māmantakaṁ viddhi dārūṇāmiva pāvakam.
english: Know me to be the destroyer of all beings in the world, whether moving or stationary, just as fire consumes wood.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 33 Verse 3. Know me to be the destroyer of all beings in the world, whether moving or stationary, just as fire consumes wood.

sanskrit: राज्यं पृथिव्यां सर्वस्यामथ वापि त्रिविष्टपे ।तथा बुद्धिरियं वेत्ति बुद्धिरेव धनं मम ॥४॥
transliteration: rājyaṁ pṛthivyāṁ sarvasyāmatha vāpi triviṣṭape ,tathā buddhiriyaṁ vetti buddhireva dhanaṁ mama.
english: My intellect perceives dominion over the entire earth, or even in the three heavens (triviṣṭapa). Thus, intellect alone is my wealth.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 33 Verse 4. My intellect perceives dominion over the entire earth, or even in the three heavens (triviṣṭapa). Thus, intellect alone is my wealth.

sanskrit: एकः पन्था ब्राह्मणानां येन गच्छन्ति तद्विदः ।गृहेषु वनवासेषु गुरुवासेषु भिक्षुषु ।लिङ्गैर्बहुभिरव्यग्रैरेका बुद्धिरुपास्यते ॥५॥
transliteration: ekaḥ panthā brāhmaṇānāṁ yena gacchanti tadvidaḥ ,gṛheṣu vanavāseṣu guruvāseṣu bhikṣuṣu ,liṅgairbahubhiravyagrairekā buddhirupāsyate.
english: There is a single path for those who know that ultimate reality (brahman), by which they proceed. This one resolute understanding is pursued through many diverse, unwavering outward forms – whether in households, forest hermitages, with a teacher (guru), or among mendicants.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 33 Verse 5. There is a single path for those who know that ultimate reality (brahman), by which they proceed. This one resolute understanding is pursued through many diverse, unwavering outward forms – whether in households, forest hermitages, with a teacher (guru), or among mendicants.

sanskrit: नानालिङ्गाश्रमस्थानां येषां बुद्धिः शमात्मिका ।ते भावमेकमायान्ति सरितः सागरं यथा ॥६॥
transliteration: nānāliṅgāśramasthānāṁ yeṣāṁ buddhiḥ śamātmikā ,te bhāvamekamāyānti saritaḥ sāgaraṁ yathā.
english: Those whose understanding is characterized by tranquility, despite being situated in various outward forms and stages of life (āśrama), achieve a single state of being, just as rivers merge into the ocean.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 33 Verse 6. Those whose understanding is characterized by tranquility, despite being situated in various outward forms and stages of life (āśrama), achieve a single state of being, just as rivers merge into the ocean.

sanskrit: बुद्ध्यायं गम्यते मार्गः शरीरेण न गम्यते ।आद्यन्तवन्ति कर्माणि शरीरं कर्मबन्धनम् ॥७॥
transliteration: buddhyāyaṁ gamyate mārgaḥ śarīreṇa na gamyate ,ādyantavanti karmāṇi śarīraṁ karmabandhanam.
english: This path is approached through understanding, not through the physical body. All actions (karma) have a beginning and an end; the body itself is a bond of action (karma).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 33 Verse 7. This path is approached through understanding, not through the physical body. All actions (karma) have a beginning and an end; the body itself is a bond of action (karma).

sanskrit: तस्मात्ते सुभगे नास्ति परलोककृतं भयम् ।मद्भावभावनिरता ममैवात्मानमेष्यसि ॥८॥
transliteration: tasmātte subhage nāsti paralokakṛtaṁ bhayam ,madbhāvabhāvaniratā mamaivātmānameṣyasi.
english: Therefore, O blessed one, you have no fear concerning the afterlife. Absorbed in the contemplation of My divine nature, you will surely attain My very own self (ātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 33 Verse 8. Therefore, O blessed one, you have no fear concerning the afterlife. Absorbed in the contemplation of My divine nature, you will surely attain My very own self (ātman).

sanskrit: ब्राह्मण्युवाच ।नेदमल्पात्मना शक्यं वेदितुं नाकृतात्मना ।बहु चाल्पं च संक्षिप्तं विप्लुतं च मतं मम ॥१॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇyuvāca ,nedamalpātmanā śakyaṁ vedituṁ nākṛtātmanā ,bahu cālpaṁ ca saṁkṣiptaṁ viplutaṁ ca mataṁ mama.
english: The brahmin lady said: "This cannot be understood by a person with a small mind or by one whose inner self (ātman) is not cultivated. My opinion is that it is both vast and concise, both condensed and extensively diffused."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 34 Verse 1. The brahmin lady said: "This cannot be understood by a person with a small mind or by one whose inner self (ātman) is not cultivated. My opinion is that it is both vast and concise, both condensed and extensively diffused."

sanskrit: उपायं तु मम ब्रूहि येनैषा लभ्यते मतिः ।तन्मन्ये कारणतमं यत एषा प्रवर्तते ॥२॥
transliteration: upāyaṁ tu mama brūhi yenaiṣā labhyate matiḥ ,tanmanye kāraṇatamaṁ yata eṣā pravartate.
english: Therefore, tell me the method by which this understanding (mati) can be attained. I consider that method to be the primary cause from which this knowledge originates.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 34 Verse 2. Therefore, tell me the method by which this understanding (mati) can be attained. I consider that method to be the primary cause from which this knowledge originates.

sanskrit: ब्राह्मण उवाच ।अरणीं ब्राह्मणीं विद्धि गुरुरस्योत्तरारणिः ।तपःश्रुतेऽभिमथ्नीतो ज्ञानाग्निर्जायते ततः ॥३॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇa uvāca ,araṇīṁ brāhmaṇīṁ viddhi gururasyottarāraṇiḥ ,tapaḥśrute'bhimathnīto jñānāgnirjāyate tataḥ.
english: The brahmin said: "Understand the brahmin lady to be the lower kindling stick (araṇī), and the guru to be its upper kindling stick. When austerity (tapas) and sacred learning (śruti) are churned together, then the fire of knowledge (jñānāgni) is born from that."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 34 Verse 3. The brahmin said: "Understand the brahmin lady to be the lower kindling stick (araṇī), and the guru to be its upper kindling stick. When austerity (tapas) and sacred learning (śruti) are churned together, then the fire of knowledge (jñānāgni) is born from that."

sanskrit: ब्राह्मण्युवाच ।यदिदं ब्रह्मणो लिङ्गं क्षेत्रज्ञमिति संज्ञितम् ।ग्रहीतुं येन तच्छक्यं लक्षणं तस्य तत्क्व नु ॥४॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇyuvāca ,yadidaṁ brahmaṇo liṅgaṁ kṣetrajñamiti saṁjñitam ,grahītuṁ yena tacchakyaṁ lakṣaṇaṁ tasya tatkva nu.
english: The brahmin lady said: "This characteristic of the absolute (brahman), which is called the 'knower of the field' (kṣetrajña), by what means can it be grasped? Where, then, is its definition (lakṣaṇa)?"
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 34 Verse 4. The brahmin lady said: "This characteristic of the absolute (brahman), which is called the 'knower of the field' (kṣetrajña), by what means can it be grasped? Where, then, is its definition (lakṣaṇa)?"

sanskrit: ब्राह्मण उवाच ।अलिङ्गो निर्गुणश्चैव कारणं नास्य विद्यते ।उपायमेव वक्ष्यामि येन गृह्येत वा न वा ॥५॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇa uvāca ,aliṅgo nirguṇaścaiva kāraṇaṁ nāsya vidyate ,upāyameva vakṣyāmi yena gṛhyeta vā na vā.
english: The brahmin said: That which is without distinguishing characteristics and without qualities (nirguṇa) indeed has no cause. I shall explain the very means by which it may or may not be grasped.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 34 Verse 5. The brahmin said: That which is without distinguishing characteristics and without qualities (nirguṇa) indeed has no cause. I shall explain the very means by which it may or may not be grasped.

sanskrit: सम्यगप्युपदिष्टश्च भ्रमरैरिव लक्ष्यते ।कर्मबुद्धिरबुद्धित्वाज्ज्ञानलिङ्गैरिवाश्रितम् ॥६॥
transliteration: samyagapyupadiṣṭaśca bhramarairiva lakṣyate ,karmabuddhirabuddhitvājjñānaliṅgairivāśritam.
english: Even when properly taught, it is merely perceived superficially, as if by bees. An intellect focused on ritual action, due to its lack of true understanding, apprehends it as though it were dependent on mere signs of knowledge.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 34 Verse 6. Even when properly taught, it is merely perceived superficially, as if by bees. An intellect focused on ritual action, due to its lack of true understanding, apprehends it as though it were dependent on mere signs of knowledge.

sanskrit: इदं कार्यमिदं नेति न मोक्षेषूपदिश्यते ।पश्यतः शृण्वतो बुद्धिरात्मनो येषु जायते ॥७॥
transliteration: idaṁ kāryamidaṁ neti na mokṣeṣūpadiśyate ,paśyataḥ śṛṇvato buddhirātmano yeṣu jāyate.
english: The distinction 'this is to be done, this is not' is not taught in matters concerning final liberation (mokṣa). It is in these (matters or states) that the understanding of the self (ātman) arises for one who truly observes and apprehends.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 34 Verse 7. The distinction 'this is to be done, this is not' is not taught in matters concerning final liberation (mokṣa). It is in these (matters or states) that the understanding of the self (ātman) arises for one who truly observes and apprehends.

sanskrit: यावन्त इह शक्येरंस्तावतोंऽशान्प्रकल्पयेत् ।व्यक्तानव्यक्तरूपांश्च शतशोऽथ सहस्रशः ॥८॥
transliteration: yāvanta iha śakyeraṁstāvatoṁ'śānprakalpayet ,vyaktānavyaktarūpāṁśca śataśo'tha sahasraśaḥ.
english: One should contemplate as many parts and aspects as are possible here, conceiving of them as both manifest and unmanifest forms, by hundreds and by thousands.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 34 Verse 8. One should contemplate as many parts and aspects as are possible here, conceiving of them as both manifest and unmanifest forms, by hundreds and by thousands.

sanskrit: सर्वान्नानात्वयुक्तांश्च सर्वान्प्रत्यक्षहेतुकान् ।यतः परं न विद्येत ततोऽभ्यासे भविष्यति ॥९॥
transliteration: sarvānnānātvayuktāṁśca sarvānpratyakṣahetukān ,yataḥ paraṁ na vidyeta tato'bhyāse bhaviṣyati.
english: All things that are characterized by diversity and all things that have empirical causes - that ultimate reality, beyond which nothing exists, will be realized through spiritual practice (abhyāsa).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 34 Verse 9. All things that are characterized by diversity and all things that have empirical causes - that ultimate reality, beyond which nothing exists, will be realized through spiritual practice (abhyāsa).

sanskrit: वासुदेव उवाच ।ततस्तु तस्या ब्राह्मण्या मतिः क्षेत्रज्ञसंक्षये ।क्षेत्रज्ञादेव परतः क्षेत्रज्ञोऽन्यः प्रवर्तते ॥१०॥
transliteration: vāsudeva uvāca ,tatastu tasyā brāhmaṇyā matiḥ kṣetrajñasaṁkṣaye ,kṣetrajñādeva parataḥ kṣetrajño'nyaḥ pravartate.
english: Vāsudeva said: Then, however, that brāhmaṇī's understanding was concerning the cessation of the knower of the field (kṣetrajña). Indeed, from the knower of the field (kṣetrajña) itself, another knower of the field (kṣetrajña) subsequently manifests.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 34 Verse 10. Vāsudeva said: Then, however, that brāhmaṇī's understanding was concerning the cessation of the knower of the field (kṣetrajña). Indeed, from the knower of the field (kṣetrajña) itself, another knower of the field (kṣetrajña) subsequently manifests.

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच ।क्व नु सा ब्राह्मणी कृष्ण क्व चासौ ब्राह्मणर्षभः ।याभ्यां सिद्धिरियं प्राप्ता तावुभौ वद मेऽच्युत ॥११॥
transliteration: arjuna uvāca ,kva nu sā brāhmaṇī kṛṣṇa kva cāsau brāhmaṇarṣabhaḥ ,yābhyāṁ siddhiriyaṁ prāptā tāvubhau vada me'cyuta.
english: Arjuna said: Where, pray tell, O Krishna, is that brāhmaṇī? And where is that best among brahmins (brāhmaṇarṣabha)? O Acyuta, tell me about both of them, by whom this spiritual perfection (siddhi) was attained.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 34 Verse 11. Arjuna said: Where, pray tell, O Krishna, is that brāhmaṇī? And where is that best among brahmins (brāhmaṇarṣabha)? O Acyuta, tell me about both of them, by whom this spiritual perfection (siddhi) was attained.

sanskrit: वासुदेव उवाच ।मनो मे ब्राह्मणं विद्धि बुद्धिं मे विद्धि ब्राह्मणीम् ।क्षेत्रज्ञ इति यश्चोक्तः सोऽहमेव धनंजय ॥१२॥
transliteration: vāsudeva uvāca ,mano me brāhmaṇaṁ viddhi buddhiṁ me viddhi brāhmaṇīm ,kṣetrajña iti yaścoktaḥ so'hameva dhanaṁjaya.
english: Vāsudeva said: Know my mind to be the brahmin, and know my intellect (buddhi) to be the brāhmaṇī. And he who is spoken of as the knower of the field (kṣetrajña) - that indeed is I myself, O Dhanañjaya.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 34 Verse 12. Vāsudeva said: Know my mind to be the brahmin, and know my intellect (buddhi) to be the brāhmaṇī. And he who is spoken of as the knower of the field (kṣetrajña) - that indeed is I myself, O Dhanañjaya.

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच ।ब्रह्म यत्परमं वेद्यं तन्मे व्याख्यातुमर्हसि ।भवतो हि प्रसादेन सूक्ष्मे मे रमते मतिः ॥१॥
transliteration: arjuna uvāca ,brahma yatparamaṁ vedyaṁ tanme vyākhyātumarhasi ,bhavato hi prasādena sūkṣme me ramate matiḥ.
english: Arjuna said: You are indeed worthy to explain to me that supreme reality (brahman) which is to be known. For by your grace, my intellect now delights in subtle subjects.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 1. Arjuna said: You are indeed worthy to explain to me that supreme reality (brahman) which is to be known. For by your grace, my intellect now delights in subtle subjects.

sanskrit: वासुदेव उवाच ।अत्राप्युदाहरन्तीममितिहासं पुरातनम् ।संवादं मोक्षसंयुक्तं शिष्यस्य गुरुणा सह ॥२॥
transliteration: vāsudeva uvāca ,atrāpyudāharantīmamitihāsaṁ purātanam ,saṁvādaṁ mokṣasaṁyuktaṁ śiṣyasya guruṇā saha.
english: Vasudeva said: In this context, they also relate this ancient narrative (itihāsa), a dialogue connected with liberation (mokṣa) between a disciple and his teacher (guru).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 2. Vasudeva said: In this context, they also relate this ancient narrative (itihāsa), a dialogue connected with liberation (mokṣa) between a disciple and his teacher (guru).

sanskrit: कश्चिद्ब्राह्मणमासीनमाचार्यं संशितव्रतम् ।शिष्यः पप्रच्छ मेधावी किं स्विच्छ्रेयः परंतप ॥३॥
transliteration: kaścidbrāhmaṇamāsīnamācāryaṁ saṁśitavratam ,śiṣyaḥ papraccha medhāvī kiṁ svicchreyaḥ paraṁtapa.
english: A certain intelligent disciple approached a Brahmin who was seated, a teacher (ācārya) of strict vows, and asked: 'O vanquisher of foes (paraṃtapa), what truly is the supreme good?'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 3. A certain intelligent disciple approached a Brahmin who was seated, a teacher (ācārya) of strict vows, and asked: 'O vanquisher of foes (paraṃtapa), what truly is the supreme good?'

sanskrit: भगवन्तं प्रपन्नोऽहं निःश्रेयसपरायणः ।याचे त्वां शिरसा विप्र यद्ब्रूयां तद्विचक्ष्व मे ॥४॥
transliteration: bhagavantaṁ prapanno'haṁ niḥśreyasaparāyaṇaḥ ,yāce tvāṁ śirasā vipra yadbrūyāṁ tadvicakṣva me.
english: I, who am entirely devoted to the supreme good, have taken refuge in you, venerable sir. O Brahmin (vipra), I implore you with my head bowed: please explain to me whatever I may speak [regarding this matter].
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 4. I, who am entirely devoted to the supreme good, have taken refuge in you, venerable sir. O Brahmin (vipra), I implore you with my head bowed: please explain to me whatever I may speak [regarding this matter].

sanskrit: तमेवंवादिनं पार्थ शिष्यं गुरुरुवाच ह ।कथयस्व प्रवक्ष्यामि यत्र ते संशयो द्विज ॥५॥
transliteration: tamevaṁvādinaṁ pārtha śiṣyaṁ gururuvāca ha ,kathayasva pravakṣyāmi yatra te saṁśayo dvija.
english: Indeed, to that disciple who spoke thus, O Pārtha, the guru said: 'O twice-born (dvija), state your doubt, and I will explain it.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 5. Indeed, to that disciple who spoke thus, O Pārtha, the guru said: 'O twice-born (dvija), state your doubt, and I will explain it.'

sanskrit: इत्युक्तः स कुरुश्रेष्ठ गुरुणा गुरुवत्सलः ।प्राञ्जलिः परिपप्रच्छ यत्तच्छृणु महामते ॥६॥
transliteration: ityuktaḥ sa kuruśreṣṭha guruṇā guruvatsalaḥ ,prāñjaliḥ paripapraccha yattacchṛṇu mahāmate.
english: O best of Kurus, the disciple, devoted to his guru, being thus addressed by the guru, asked with folded hands. O great-minded one, listen to what he inquired.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 6. O best of Kurus, the disciple, devoted to his guru, being thus addressed by the guru, asked with folded hands. O great-minded one, listen to what he inquired.

sanskrit: शिष्य उवाच ।कुतश्चाहं कुतश्च त्वं तत्सत्यं ब्रूहि यत्परम् ।कुतो जातानि भूतानि स्थावराणि चराणि च ॥७॥
transliteration: śiṣya uvāca ,kutaścāhaṁ kutaśca tvaṁ tatsatyaṁ brūhi yatparam ,kuto jātāni bhūtāni sthāvarāṇi carāṇi ca.
english: The disciple said: 'From where do I originate, and from where do you originate? Please tell me that ultimate truth (satya). From where are all beings, both stationary and moving, born?'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 7. The disciple said: 'From where do I originate, and from where do you originate? Please tell me that ultimate truth (satya). From where are all beings, both stationary and moving, born?'

sanskrit: केन जीवन्ति भूतानि तेषामायुः किमात्मकम् ।किं सत्यं किं तपो विप्र के गुणाः सद्भिरीरिताः ।के पन्थानः शिवाः सन्ति किं सुखं किं च दुष्कृतम् ॥८॥
transliteration: kena jīvanti bhūtāni teṣāmāyuḥ kimātmakam ,kiṁ satyaṁ kiṁ tapo vipra ke guṇāḥ sadbhirīritāḥ ,ke panthānaḥ śivāḥ santi kiṁ sukhaṁ kiṁ ca duṣkṛtam.
english: By what do beings live? What is the nature (ātman) of their life? O Brahmin, what is truth, and what is austerity (tapas)? What are the virtues declared by the good? Which paths are auspicious? What is happiness, and what is misfortune?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 8. By what do beings live? What is the nature (ātman) of their life? O Brahmin, what is truth, and what is austerity (tapas)? What are the virtues declared by the good? Which paths are auspicious? What is happiness, and what is misfortune?

sanskrit: एतान्मे भगवन्प्रश्नान्याथातथ्येन सत्तम ।वक्तुमर्हसि विप्रर्षे यथावदिह तत्त्वतः ॥९॥
transliteration: etānme bhagavanpraśnānyāthātathyena sattama ,vaktumarhasi viprarṣe yathāvadiha tattvataḥ.
english: O Lord, O best among the righteous, O sage among Brahmins, you should explain these questions to me precisely and truthfully, revealing their fundamental nature.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 9. O Lord, O best among the righteous, O sage among Brahmins, you should explain these questions to me precisely and truthfully, revealing their fundamental nature.

sanskrit: वासुदेव उवाच ।तस्मै संप्रतिपन्नाय यथावत्परिपृच्छते ।शिष्याय गुणयुक्ताय शान्ताय गुरुवर्तिने ।छायाभूताय दान्ताय यतये ब्रह्मचारिणे ॥१०॥
transliteration: vāsudeva uvāca ,tasmai saṁpratipannāya yathāvatparipṛcchate ,śiṣyāya guṇayuktāya śāntāya guruvartine ,chāyābhūtāya dāntāya yataye brahmacāriṇe.
english: Vāsudeva said: To that agreeable disciple, who questioned appropriately, who was endowed with virtues, tranquil, devoted to his teacher, ever-present like a shadow, self-controlled, a spiritual striver (yati), and a student of sacred knowledge (brahmacārin).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 10. Vāsudeva said: To that agreeable disciple, who questioned appropriately, who was endowed with virtues, tranquil, devoted to his teacher, ever-present like a shadow, self-controlled, a spiritual striver (yati), and a student of sacred knowledge (brahmacārin).

sanskrit: तान्प्रश्नानब्रवीत्पार्थ मेधावी स धृतव्रतः ।गुरुः कुरुकुलश्रेष्ठ सम्यक्सर्वानरिंदम ॥११॥
transliteration: tānpraśnānabravītpārtha medhāvī sa dhṛtavrataḥ ,guruḥ kurukulaśreṣṭha samyaksarvānariṁdama.
english: O Pārtha, O best of the Kuru dynasty, O subduer of enemies, that wise teacher, steadfast in his vows, properly answered all those questions.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 11. O Pārtha, O best of the Kuru dynasty, O subduer of enemies, that wise teacher, steadfast in his vows, properly answered all those questions.

sanskrit: ब्रह्मप्रोक्तमिदं धर्ममृषिप्रवरसेवितम् ।वेदविद्यासमावाप्यं तत्त्वभूतार्थभावनम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: brahmaproktamidaṁ dharmamṛṣipravarasevitam ,vedavidyāsamāvāpyaṁ tattvabhūtārthabhāvanam.
english: This is the natural law (dharma), proclaimed by brahman, practiced by the foremost sages, attainable through Vedic knowledge, and characterized by the contemplation of the essential truth of reality.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 12. This is the natural law (dharma), proclaimed by brahman, practiced by the foremost sages, attainable through Vedic knowledge, and characterized by the contemplation of the essential truth of reality.

sanskrit: भूतभव्यभविष्यादिधर्मकामार्थनिश्चयम् ।सिद्धसंघपरिज्ञातं पुराकल्पं सनातनम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: bhūtabhavyabhaviṣyādidharmakāmārthaniścayam ,siddhasaṁghaparijñātaṁ purākalpaṁ sanātanam.
english: The ancient, eternal tradition (purākalpam), which ascertains the constitution (dharma), legitimate desires, and prosperity for the past, present, and future, and which is fully known by the assemblies of perfected beings (siddhas).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 13. The ancient, eternal tradition (purākalpam), which ascertains the constitution (dharma), legitimate desires, and prosperity for the past, present, and future, and which is fully known by the assemblies of perfected beings (siddhas).

sanskrit: प्रवक्ष्येऽहं महाप्राज्ञ पदमुत्तममद्य ते ।बुद्ध्वा यदिह संसिद्धा भवन्तीह मनीषिणः ॥१४॥
transliteration: pravakṣye'haṁ mahāprājña padamuttamamadya te ,buddhvā yadiha saṁsiddhā bhavantīha manīṣiṇaḥ.
english: O greatly wise one (mahāprājña), I will now declare to you that supreme state by understanding which the wise (manīṣiṇaḥ) here become perfected (saṃsiddhā).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 14. O greatly wise one (mahāprājña), I will now declare to you that supreme state by understanding which the wise (manīṣiṇaḥ) here become perfected (saṃsiddhā).

sanskrit: उपगम्यर्षयः पूर्वं जिज्ञासन्तः परस्परम् ।बृहस्पतिभरद्वाजौ गौतमो भार्गवस्तथा ॥१५॥
transliteration: upagamyarṣayaḥ pūrvaṁ jijñāsantaḥ parasparam ,bṛhaspatibharadvājau gautamo bhārgavastathā.
english: Formerly, sages (ṛṣayaḥ), including Brihaspati, Bharadvaja, Gautama, and Bhargava, having come together, questioned each other.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 15. Formerly, sages (ṛṣayaḥ), including Brihaspati, Bharadvaja, Gautama, and Bhargava, having come together, questioned each other.

sanskrit: वसिष्ठः काश्यपश्चैव विश्वामित्रोऽत्रिरेव च ।मार्गान्सर्वान्परिक्रम्य परिश्रान्ताः स्वकर्मभिः ॥१६॥
transliteration: vasiṣṭhaḥ kāśyapaścaiva viśvāmitro'trireva ca ,mārgānsarvānparikramya pariśrāntāḥ svakarmabhiḥ.
english: Vasiṣṭha, Kāśyapa, Viśvāmitra, and Atri also, having traversed all paths, became exhausted by their own undertakings.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 16. Vasiṣṭha, Kāśyapa, Viśvāmitra, and Atri also, having traversed all paths, became exhausted by their own undertakings.

sanskrit: ऋषिमाङ्गिरसं वृद्धं पुरस्कृत्य तु ते द्विजाः ।ददृशुर्ब्रह्मभवने ब्रह्माणं वीतकल्मषम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: ṛṣimāṅgirasaṁ vṛddhaṁ puraskṛtya tu te dvijāḥ ,dadṛśurbrahmabhavane brahmāṇaṁ vītakalmaṣam.
english: Placing the aged sage Aṅgirasa at their forefront, those twice-born ones (dvija) beheld Brahmā, who was free from all impurities, in Brahmā's abode.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 17. Placing the aged sage Aṅgirasa at their forefront, those twice-born ones (dvija) beheld Brahmā, who was free from all impurities, in Brahmā's abode.

sanskrit: तं प्रणम्य महात्मानं सुखासीनं महर्षयः ।पप्रच्छुर्विनयोपेता निःश्रेयसमिदं परम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: taṁ praṇamya mahātmānaṁ sukhāsīnaṁ maharṣayaḥ ,papracchurvinayopetā niḥśreyasamidaṁ param.
english: The great sages, humbly approaching, bowed to that comfortably seated, great-souled one and questioned him regarding this supreme good (niḥśreyasa).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 18. The great sages, humbly approaching, bowed to that comfortably seated, great-souled one and questioned him regarding this supreme good (niḥśreyasa).

sanskrit: कथं कर्म क्रियात्साधु कथं मुच्येत किल्बिषात् ।के नो मार्गाः शिवाश्च स्युः किं सत्यं किं च दुष्कृतम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: kathaṁ karma kriyātsādhu kathaṁ mucyeta kilbiṣāt ,ke no mārgāḥ śivāśca syuḥ kiṁ satyaṁ kiṁ ca duṣkṛtam.
english: How should action (karma) be performed correctly? How can one be freed from faults? What paths would be auspicious for us? What is truth, and what constitutes wrongdoing?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 19. How should action (karma) be performed correctly? How can one be freed from faults? What paths would be auspicious for us? What is truth, and what constitutes wrongdoing?

sanskrit: केनोभौ कर्मपन्थानौ महत्त्वं केन विन्दति ।प्रलयं चापवर्गं च भूतानां प्रभवाप्ययौ ॥२०॥
transliteration: kenobhau karmapanthānau mahattvaṁ kena vindati ,pralayaṁ cāpavargaṁ ca bhūtānāṁ prabhavāpyayau.
english: By what means are both paths of action (karma) understood? How does one attain greatness? [And they also asked about] the dissolution and ultimate liberation (apavarga) of beings, as well as their origin and end.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 20. By what means are both paths of action (karma) understood? How does one attain greatness? [And they also asked about] the dissolution and ultimate liberation (apavarga) of beings, as well as their origin and end.

sanskrit: इत्युक्तः स मुनिश्रेष्ठैर्यदाह प्रपितामहः ।तत्तेऽहं संप्रवक्ष्यामि शृणु शिष्य यथागमम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: ityuktaḥ sa muniśreṣṭhairyadāha prapitāmahaḥ ,tatte'haṁ saṁpravakṣyāmi śṛṇu śiṣya yathāgamam.
english: Thus addressed by the best of sages, I will now fully explain to you what the great-grandfather (Brahmā) said. Listen, disciple, as it is presented in the sacred tradition.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 21. Thus addressed by the best of sages, I will now fully explain to you what the great-grandfather (Brahmā) said. Listen, disciple, as it is presented in the sacred tradition.

sanskrit: ब्रह्मोवाच ।सत्याद्भूतानि जातानि स्थावराणि चराणि च ।तपसा तानि जीवन्ति इति तद्वित्त सुव्रताः ॥२२॥
transliteration: brahmovāca ,satyādbhūtāni jātāni sthāvarāṇi carāṇi ca ,tapasā tāni jīvanti iti tadvitta suvratāḥ.
english: Brahmā said: From truth (satya), all beings, both immobile and mobile, are born. They sustain themselves through spiritual discipline (tapas). Know this, O those of good vows!
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 22. Brahmā said: From truth (satya), all beings, both immobile and mobile, are born. They sustain themselves through spiritual discipline (tapas). Know this, O those of good vows!

sanskrit: स्वां योनिं पुनरागम्य वर्तन्ते स्वेन कर्मणा ।सत्यं हि गुणसंयुक्तं नियतं पञ्चलक्षणम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: svāṁ yoniṁ punarāgamya vartante svena karmaṇā ,satyaṁ hi guṇasaṁyuktaṁ niyataṁ pañcalakṣaṇam.
english: Having returned to their own origin, they continue to exist by their own actions (karma). For truth (satya) is indeed endowed with qualities, constant, and characterized by five features.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 23. Having returned to their own origin, they continue to exist by their own actions (karma). For truth (satya) is indeed endowed with qualities, constant, and characterized by five features.

sanskrit: ब्रह्म सत्यं तपः सत्यं सत्यं चैव प्रजापतिः ।सत्याद्भूतानि जातानि भूतं सत्यमयं महत् ॥२४॥
transliteration: brahma satyaṁ tapaḥ satyaṁ satyaṁ caiva prajāpatiḥ ,satyādbhūtāni jātāni bhūtaṁ satyamayaṁ mahat.
english: The supreme reality (brahman) is truth, spiritual discipline (tapas) is truth, and indeed, the Lord of Creation (prajāpati) is truth. From truth, all beings are born, and the great existence itself is full of truth.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 24. The supreme reality (brahman) is truth, spiritual discipline (tapas) is truth, and indeed, the Lord of Creation (prajāpati) is truth. From truth, all beings are born, and the great existence itself is full of truth.

sanskrit: तस्मात्सत्याश्रया विप्रा नित्यं योगपरायणाः ।अतीतक्रोधसंतापा नियता धर्मसेतवः ॥२५॥
transliteration: tasmātsatyāśrayā viprā nityaṁ yogaparāyaṇāḥ ,atītakrodhasaṁtāpā niyatā dharmasetavaḥ.
english: Therefore, brahmins (vipra) who rely on truth, are always devoted to spiritual discipline (yoga), have overcome anger and distress, and are self-controlled, serve as the very foundations of natural law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 25. Therefore, brahmins (vipra) who rely on truth, are always devoted to spiritual discipline (yoga), have overcome anger and distress, and are self-controlled, serve as the very foundations of natural law (dharma).

sanskrit: अन्योन्यनियतान्वैद्यान्धर्मसेतुप्रवर्तकान् ।तानहं संप्रवक्ष्यामि शाश्वतान्लोकभावनान् ॥२६॥
transliteration: anyonyaniyatānvaidyāndharmasetupravartakān ,tānahaṁ saṁpravakṣyāmi śāśvatānlokabhāvanān.
english: I shall fully declare those eternal, world-sustaining learned ones (vaidya) who are mutually disciplined and act as promulgators of natural law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 26. I shall fully declare those eternal, world-sustaining learned ones (vaidya) who are mutually disciplined and act as promulgators of natural law (dharma).

sanskrit: चातुर्विद्यं तथा वर्णांश्चतुरश्चाश्रमान्पृथक् ।धर्ममेकं चतुष्पादं नित्यमाहुर्मनीषिणः ॥२७॥
transliteration: cāturvidyaṁ tathā varṇāṁścaturaścāśramānpṛthak ,dharmamekaṁ catuṣpādaṁ nityamāhurmanīṣiṇaḥ.
english: The wise (manīṣin) declare that the four Vedas, as well as the four social classes (varṇa) and the four stages of life (āśrama) – each distinctly – represent the one eternal natural law (dharma), which stands firm on four pillars.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 27. The wise (manīṣin) declare that the four Vedas, as well as the four social classes (varṇa) and the four stages of life (āśrama) – each distinctly – represent the one eternal natural law (dharma), which stands firm on four pillars.

sanskrit: पन्थानं वः प्रवक्ष्यामि शिवं क्षेमकरं द्विजाः ।नियतं ब्रह्मभावाय यातं पूर्वं मनीषिभिः ॥२८॥
transliteration: panthānaṁ vaḥ pravakṣyāmi śivaṁ kṣemakaraṁ dvijāḥ ,niyataṁ brahmabhāvāya yātaṁ pūrvaṁ manīṣibhiḥ.
english: O twice-born ones (dvija)! I will declare to you the auspicious and welfare-bestowing path which leads unerringly to the state of (brahman), and which was traversed previously by the wise (manīṣin).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 28. O twice-born ones (dvija)! I will declare to you the auspicious and welfare-bestowing path which leads unerringly to the state of (brahman), and which was traversed previously by the wise (manīṣin).

sanskrit: गदतस्तं ममाद्येह पन्थानं दुर्विदं परम् ।निबोधत महाभागा निखिलेन परं पदम् ॥२९॥
transliteration: gadatastaṁ mamādyeha panthānaṁ durvidaṁ param ,nibodhata mahābhāgā nikhilena paraṁ padam.
english: O greatly fortunate ones, as I speak to you here today about that supreme (parama) and difficult-to-know path, understand completely the ultimate state.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 29. O greatly fortunate ones, as I speak to you here today about that supreme (parama) and difficult-to-know path, understand completely the ultimate state.

sanskrit: ब्रह्मचारिकमेवाहुराश्रमं प्रथमं पदम् ।गार्हस्थ्यं तु द्वितीयं स्याद्वानप्रस्थमतः परम् ।ततः परं तु विज्ञेयमध्यात्मं परमं पदम् ॥३०॥
transliteration: brahmacārikamevāhurāśramaṁ prathamaṁ padam ,gārhasthyaṁ tu dvitīyaṁ syādvānaprasthamataḥ param ,tataḥ paraṁ tu vijñeyamadhyātmaṁ paramaṁ padam.
english: They declare the celibate student (brahmacarya) stage as the first stage of life (āśrama). The householder state is the second, followed by the forest-dweller (vānaprastha) stage. Beyond that, the spiritual (adhyātma) state should be known as the ultimate state.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 30. They declare the celibate student (brahmacarya) stage as the first stage of life (āśrama). The householder state is the second, followed by the forest-dweller (vānaprastha) stage. Beyond that, the spiritual (adhyātma) state should be known as the ultimate state.

sanskrit: ज्योतिराकाशमादित्यो वायुरिन्द्रः प्रजापतिः ।नोपैति यावदध्यात्मं तावदेतान्न पश्यति ।तस्योपायं प्रवक्ष्यामि पुरस्तात्तं निबोधत ॥३१॥
transliteration: jyotirākāśamādityo vāyurindraḥ prajāpatiḥ ,nopaiti yāvadadhyātmaṁ tāvadetānna paśyati ,tasyopāyaṁ pravakṣyāmi purastāttaṁ nibodhata.
english: Light, space (ākāśa), the sun, wind, Indra, Prajāpati  -  as long as one does not approach the spiritual (adhyātma) state, one does not perceive these (deities/elements). I will explain the means to attain that; understand it fully beforehand.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 31. Light, space (ākāśa), the sun, wind, Indra, Prajāpati  -  as long as one does not approach the spiritual (adhyātma) state, one does not perceive these (deities/elements). sanskrit: फलमूलानिलभुजां मुनीनां वसतां वने ।वानप्रस्थं द्विजातीनां त्रयाणामुपदिश्यते ॥३२॥
transliteration: phalamūlānilabhujāṁ munīnāṁ vasatāṁ vane ,vānaprasthaṁ dvijātīnāṁ trayāṇāmupadiśyate.
english: The forest-dweller (vānaprastha) stage is prescribed for the three twice-born (dvijāti) classes, who are sages (muni) living in the forest, subsisting on fruits, roots, and air.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 32. The forest-dweller (vānaprastha) stage is prescribed for the three twice-born (dvijāti) classes, who are sages (muni) living in the forest, subsisting on fruits, roots, and air.

sanskrit: सर्वेषामेव वर्णानां गार्हस्थ्यं तद्विधीयते ।श्रद्धालक्षणमित्येवं धर्मं धीराः प्रचक्षते ॥३३॥
transliteration: sarveṣāmeva varṇānāṁ gārhasthyaṁ tadvidhīyate ,śraddhālakṣaṇamityevaṁ dharmaṁ dhīrāḥ pracakṣate.
english: The householder's life (gārhasthya) is indeed prescribed for all social classes (varṇas). The wise (dhīra) thus declare this natural law (dharma) to be characterized by faith (śraddhā).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 33. The householder's life (gārhasthya) is indeed prescribed for all social classes (varṇas). The wise (dhīra) thus declare this natural law (dharma) to be characterized by faith (śraddhā).

sanskrit: इत्येते देवयाना वः पन्थानः परिकीर्तिताः ।सद्भिरध्यासिता धीरैः कर्मभिर्धर्मसेतवः ॥३४॥
transliteration: ityete devayānā vaḥ panthānaḥ parikīrtitāḥ ,sadbhiradhyāsitā dhīraiḥ karmabhirdharmasetavaḥ.
english: These are the divine paths (devayāna) that have been proclaimed for you. They are the bridges of natural law (dharma), frequented by the virtuous and the wise (dhīra) through their (righteous) actions (karma).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 34. These are the divine paths (devayāna) that have been proclaimed for you. They are the bridges of natural law (dharma), frequented by the virtuous and the wise (dhīra) through their (righteous) actions (karma).

sanskrit: एतेषां पृथगध्यास्ते यो धर्मं संशितव्रतः ।कालात्पश्यति भूतानां सदैव प्रभवाप्ययौ ॥३५॥
transliteration: eteṣāṁ pṛthagadhyāste yo dharmaṁ saṁśitavrataḥ ,kālātpaśyati bhūtānāṁ sadaiva prabhavāpyayau.
english: He who, with firm vows, individually practices the natural law (dharma) for each of these, always perceives, through time, the origin and dissolution (prabhavāpyaya) of all beings (bhūtas).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 35. He who, with firm vows, individually practices the natural law (dharma) for each of these, always perceives, through time, the origin and dissolution (prabhavāpyaya) of all beings (bhūtas).

sanskrit: अतस्तत्त्वानि वक्ष्यामि याथातथ्येन हेतुना ।विषयस्थानि सर्वाणि वर्तमानानि भागशः ॥३६॥
transliteration: atastattvāni vakṣyāmi yāthātathyena hetunā ,viṣayasthāni sarvāṇi vartamānāni bhāgaśaḥ.
english: Therefore, I will truly explain all these realities (tattva), with proper reasoning, presenting them in detail, as they exist (vartamāna) in their respective domains (viṣaya), section by section.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 36. Therefore, I will truly explain all these realities (tattva), with proper reasoning, presenting them in detail, as they exist (vartamāna) in their respective domains (viṣaya), section by section.

sanskrit: महानात्मा तथाव्यक्तमहंकारस्तथैव च ।इन्द्रियाणि दशैकं च महाभूतानि पञ्च च ॥३७॥
transliteration: mahānātmā tathāvyaktamahaṁkārastathaiva ca ,indriyāṇi daśaikaṁ ca mahābhūtāni pañca ca.
english: The great self (ātman), and the unmanifest (avyakta), and the ego (ahaṅkāra) as well; also the eleven senses, and the five great elements.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 37. The great self (ātman), and the unmanifest (avyakta), and the ego (ahaṅkāra) as well; also the eleven senses, and the five great elements.

sanskrit: विशेषाः पञ्चभूतानामित्येषा वैदिकी श्रुतिः ।चतुर्विंशतिरेषा वस्तत्त्वानां संप्रकीर्तिता ॥३८॥
transliteration: viśeṣāḥ pañcabhūtānāmityeṣā vaidikī śrutiḥ ,caturviṁśatireṣā vastattvānāṁ saṁprakīrtitā.
english: The specific qualities of the five great elements  -  this is the Vedic revelation (śruti). These twenty-four principles (tattvas) are thus declared to you.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 38. The specific qualities of the five great elements  -  this is the Vedic revelation (śruti). These twenty-four principles (tattvas) are thus declared to you.

sanskrit: तत्त्वानामथ यो वेद सर्वेषां प्रभवाप्ययौ ।स धीरः सर्वभूतेषु न मोहमधिगच्छति ॥३९॥
transliteration: tattvānāmatha yo veda sarveṣāṁ prabhavāpyayau ,sa dhīraḥ sarvabhūteṣu na mohamadhigacchati.
english: And whoever knows the origin and dissolution of all these principles (tattvas); that wise person (dhīra) does not fall into delusion concerning all beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 39. And whoever knows the origin and dissolution of all these principles (tattvas); that wise person (dhīra) does not fall into delusion concerning all beings.

sanskrit: तत्त्वानि यो वेदयते यथातथं गुणांश्च सर्वानखिलाश्च देवताः ।विधूतपाप्मा प्रविमुच्य बन्धनं स सर्वलोकानमलान्समश्नुते ॥४०॥
transliteration: tattvāni yo vedayate yathātathaṁ; guṇāṁśca sarvānakhilāśca devatāḥ ,vidhūtapāpmā pravimucya bandhanaṁ; sa sarvalokānamalānsamaśnute.
english: Whoever truly knows the principles (tattvas), and all qualities (guṇas) completely, and the deities (devatās); that person, having shaken off all wrongdoing, and having been completely freed from bondage (bandhanam), attains all the pure realms.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 35 Verse 40. Whoever truly knows the principles (tattvas), and all qualities (guṇas) completely, and the deities (devatās); that person, having shaken off all wrongdoing, and having been completely freed from bondage (bandhanam), attains all the pure realms.

sanskrit: ब्रह्मोवाच ।तदव्यक्तमनुद्रिक्तं सर्वव्यापि ध्रुवं स्थिरम् ।नवद्वारं पुरं विद्यात्त्रिगुणं पञ्चधातुकम् ॥१॥
transliteration: brahmovāca ,tadavyaktamanudriktaṁ sarvavyāpi dhruvaṁ sthiram ,navadvāraṁ puraṁ vidyāttriguṇaṁ pañcadhātukam.
english: Brahmā said: One should understand that unmanifest (brahman) as immutable, all-pervading, constant, and stable. The body is to be known as a city with nine gates, composed of the three qualities (guṇas), and consisting of five constituents.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 1. Brahmā said: One should understand that unmanifest (brahman) as immutable, all-pervading, constant, and stable. The body is to be known as a city with nine gates, composed of the three qualities (guṇas), and consisting of five constituents.

sanskrit: एकादशपरिक्षेपं मनो व्याकरणात्मकम् ।बुद्धिस्वामिकमित्येतत्परमेकादशं भवेत् ॥२॥
transliteration: ekādaśaparikṣepaṁ mano vyākaraṇātmakam ,buddhisvāmikamityetatparamekādaśaṁ bhavet.
english: The mind (manas), analytical in nature and having eleven coverings, is ruled by the intellect (buddhi); this (mind) should be regarded as the primary eleventh (faculty).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 2. The mind (manas), analytical in nature and having eleven coverings, is ruled by the intellect (buddhi); this (mind) should be regarded as the primary eleventh (faculty).

sanskrit: त्रीणि स्रोतांसि यान्यस्मिन्नाप्यायन्ते पुनः पुनः ।प्रणाड्यस्तिस्र एवैताः प्रवर्तन्ते गुणात्मिकाः ॥३॥
transliteration: trīṇi srotāṁsi yānyasminnāpyāyante punaḥ punaḥ ,praṇāḍyastisra evaitāḥ pravartante guṇātmikāḥ.
english: There are three streams which are repeatedly nourished in this (body). Indeed, these three channels, which are by nature the qualities (guṇas), continuously operate.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 3. There are three streams which are repeatedly nourished in this (body). Indeed, these three channels, which are by nature the qualities (guṇas), continuously operate.

sanskrit: तमो रजस्तथा सत्त्वं गुणानेतान्प्रचक्षते ।अन्योन्यमिथुनाः सर्वे तथान्योन्यानुजीविनः ॥४॥
transliteration: tamo rajastathā sattvaṁ guṇānetānpracakṣate ,anyonyamithunāḥ sarve tathānyonyānujīvinaḥ.
english: They declare these attributes to be inertia (tamas), activity (rajas), and clarity (sattva). All these qualities (guṇas) are mutually conjoined and likewise interdependent for their existence.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 4. They declare these attributes to be inertia (tamas), activity (rajas), and clarity (sattva). All these qualities (guṇas) are mutually conjoined and likewise interdependent for their existence.

sanskrit: अन्योन्यापाश्रयाश्चैव तथान्योन्यानुवर्तिनः ।अन्योन्यव्यतिषक्ताश्च त्रिगुणाः पञ्च धातवः ॥५॥
transliteration: anyonyāpāśrayāścaiva tathānyonyānuvartinaḥ ,anyonyavyatiṣaktāśca triguṇāḥ pañca dhātavaḥ.
english: The three constituents (triguṇāḥ) and the five fundamental elements (pañca dhātavaḥ) are mutually dependent, mutually supportive, and mutually intertwined.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 5. The three constituents (triguṇāḥ) and the five fundamental elements (pañca dhātavaḥ) are mutually dependent, mutually supportive, and mutually intertwined.

sanskrit: तमसो मिथुनं सत्त्वं सत्त्वस्य मिथुनं रजः ।रजसश्चापि सत्त्वं स्यात्सत्त्वस्य मिथुनं तमः ॥६॥
transliteration: tamaso mithunaṁ sattvaṁ sattvasya mithunaṁ rajaḥ ,rajasaścāpi sattvaṁ syātsattvasya mithunaṁ tamaḥ.
english: Sattva is the counterpart (mithunam) of tamas; rajas is the counterpart of sattva. Similarly, sattva is also the counterpart of rajas, and tamas is the counterpart of sattva.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 6. Sattva is the counterpart (mithunam) of tamas; rajas is the counterpart of sattva. Similarly, sattva is also the counterpart of rajas, and tamas is the counterpart of sattva.

sanskrit: नियम्यते तमो यत्र रजस्तत्र प्रवर्तते ।नियम्यते रजो यत्र सत्त्वं तत्र प्रवर्तते ॥७॥
transliteration: niyamyate tamo yatra rajastatra pravartate ,niyamyate rajo yatra sattvaṁ tatra pravartate.
english: Where tamas is controlled, there rajas becomes active. Where rajas is controlled, there sattva becomes active.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 7. Where tamas is controlled, there rajas becomes active. Where rajas is controlled, there sattva becomes active.

sanskrit: नैशात्मकं तमो विद्यात्त्रिगुणं मोहसंज्ञितम् ।अधर्मलक्षणं चैव नियतं पापकर्मसु ॥८॥
transliteration: naiśātmakaṁ tamo vidyāttriguṇaṁ mohasaṁjñitam ,adharmalakṣaṇaṁ caiva niyataṁ pāpakarmasu.
english: One should know tamas as inherently dark, as one of the three constituents (guṇas), called delusion (mohasaṃjñitam), marked by unrighteousness (adharma), and consistently associated with sinful actions.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 8. One should know tamas as inherently dark, as one of the three constituents (guṇas), called delusion (mohasaṃjñitam), marked by unrighteousness (adharma), and consistently associated with sinful actions.

sanskrit: प्रवृत्त्यात्मकमेवाहू रजः पर्यायकारकम् ।प्रवृत्तं सर्वभूतेषु दृश्यतोत्पत्तिलक्षणम् ॥९॥
transliteration: pravṛttyātmakamevāhū rajaḥ paryāyakārakam ,pravṛttaṁ sarvabhūteṣu dṛśyatotpattilakṣaṇam.
english: They declare that "rajas" (the quality of passion) is characterized by activity and causes transformation. It manifests in all beings, observable through the characteristic of generation.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 9. They declare that "rajas" (the quality of passion) is characterized by activity and causes transformation. It manifests in all beings, observable through the characteristic of generation.

sanskrit: प्रकाशं सर्वभूतेषु लाघवं श्रद्दधानता ।सात्त्विकं रूपमेवं तु लाघवं साधुसंमितम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: prakāśaṁ sarvabhūteṣu lāghavaṁ śraddadhānatā ,sāttvikaṁ rūpamevaṁ tu lāghavaṁ sādhusaṁmitam.
english: Clarity in all beings, lightness, and faith are the characteristics of "sattva" (the quality of goodness). Likewise, lightness is considered good and proper.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 10. Clarity in all beings, lightness, and faith are the characteristics of "sattva" (the quality of goodness). Likewise, lightness is considered good and proper.

sanskrit: एतेषां गुणतत्त्वं हि वक्ष्यते हेत्वहेतुभिः ।समासव्यासयुक्तानि तत्त्वतस्तानि वित्त मे ॥११॥
transliteration: eteṣāṁ guṇatattvaṁ hi vakṣyate hetvahetubhiḥ ,samāsavyāsayuktāni tattvatastāni vitta me.
english: Indeed, the true nature of these qualities ("guṇa"s) will be explained with their causes and non-causes. Understand these, which are presented concisely and expansively, from me as they truly are.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 11. Indeed, the true nature of these qualities ("guṇa"s) will be explained with their causes and non-causes. Understand these, which are presented concisely and expansively, from me as they truly are.

sanskrit: संमोहोऽज्ञानमत्यागः कर्मणामविनिर्णयः ।स्वप्नः स्तम्भो भयं लोभः शोकः सुकृतदूषणम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: saṁmoho'jñānamatyāgaḥ karmaṇāmavinirṇayaḥ ,svapnaḥ stambho bhayaṁ lobhaḥ śokaḥ sukṛtadūṣaṇam.
english: Delusion, ignorance, non-renunciation of actions, indecision regarding actions, sleep, stupor, fear, greed, sorrow, and the defilement of good deeds are among the characteristics of "tamas" (the quality of darkness/inertia).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 12. Delusion, ignorance, non-renunciation of actions, indecision regarding actions, sleep, stupor, fear, greed, sorrow, and the defilement of good deeds are among the characteristics of "tamas" (the quality of darkness/inertia).

sanskrit: अस्मृतिश्चाविपाकश्च नास्तिक्यं भिन्नवृत्तिता ।निर्विशेषत्वमन्धत्वं जघन्यगुणवृत्तिता ॥१३॥
transliteration: asmṛtiścāvipākaśca nāstikyaṁ bhinnavṛttitā ,nirviśeṣatvamandhatvaṁ jaghanyaguṇavṛttitā.
english: Forgetfulness and lack of discrimination, atheism and inconsistent behavior; lack of distinction (of qualities), blindness, and indulgence in base qualities.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 13. Forgetfulness and lack of discrimination, atheism and inconsistent behavior; lack of distinction (of qualities), blindness, and indulgence in base qualities.

sanskrit: अकृते कृतमानित्वमज्ञाने ज्ञानमानिता ।अमैत्री विकृतो भावो अश्रद्धा मूढभावना ॥१४॥
transliteration: akṛte kṛtamānitvamajñāne jñānamānitā ,amaitrī vikṛto bhāvo aśraddhā mūḍhabhāvanā.
english: Regarding what is not done as done, considering oneself knowledgeable in ignorance; unfriendliness, a distorted disposition, lack of faith (śraddhā), and foolish notions.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 14. Regarding what is not done as done, considering oneself knowledgeable in ignorance; unfriendliness, a distorted disposition, lack of faith (śraddhā), and foolish notions.

sanskrit: अनार्जवमसंज्ञत्वं कर्म पापमचेतना ।गुरुत्वं सन्नभावत्वमसितत्वमवाग्गतिः ॥१५॥
transliteration: anārjavamasaṁjñatvaṁ karma pāpamacetanā ,gurutvaṁ sannabhāvatvamasitatvamavāggatiḥ.
english: Dishonesty, lack of discernment, sinful action (karma), and unconsciousness; dullness, the state of non-existence, darkness, and downward motion (degradation).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 15. Dishonesty, lack of discernment, sinful action (karma), and unconsciousness; dullness, the state of non-existence, darkness, and downward motion (degradation).

sanskrit: सर्व एते गुणा विप्रास्तामसाः संप्रकीर्तिताः ।ये चान्ये नियता भावा लोकेऽस्मिन्मोहसंज्ञिताः ॥१६॥
transliteration: sarva ete guṇā viprāstāmasāḥ saṁprakīrtitāḥ ,ye cānye niyatā bhāvā loke'sminmohasaṁjñitāḥ.
english: O brahmins (viprāḥ), all these qualities have been declared to be rooted in tamas. And whatever other fixed dispositions exist in this world are known as those born of delusion.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 16. O brahmins (viprāḥ), all these qualities have been declared to be rooted in tamas. And whatever other fixed dispositions exist in this world are known as those born of delusion.

sanskrit: तत्र तत्र नियम्यन्ते सर्वे ते तामसा गुणाः ।परिवादकथा नित्यं देवब्राह्मणवैदिकाः ॥१७॥
transliteration: tatra tatra niyamyante sarve te tāmasā guṇāḥ ,parivādakathā nityaṁ devabrāhmaṇavaidikāḥ.
english: In various situations, all those qualities pertaining to "tamas" (tamas) are observed. These include constant slanderous talk concerning deities, Brahmins, and Vedic subjects.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 17. In various situations, all those qualities pertaining to "tamas" (tamas) are observed. These include constant slanderous talk concerning deities, Brahmins, and Vedic subjects.

sanskrit: अत्यागश्चाभिमानश्च मोहो मन्युस्तथाक्षमा ।मत्सरश्चैव भूतेषु तामसं वृत्तमिष्यते ॥१८॥
transliteration: atyāgaścābhimānaśca moho manyustathākṣamā ,matsaraścaiva bhūteṣu tāmasaṁ vṛttamiṣyate.
english: Non-renunciation, pride, delusion, anger, and similarly intolerance, and indeed envy towards all beings – this is considered to be conduct pertaining to "tamas" (tamas).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 18. Non-renunciation, pride, delusion, anger, and similarly intolerance, and indeed envy towards all beings – this is considered to be conduct pertaining to "tamas" (tamas).

sanskrit: वृथारम्भाश्च ये केचिद्वृथादानानि यानि च ।वृथाभक्षणमित्येतत्तामसं वृत्तमिष्यते ॥१९॥
transliteration: vṛthārambhāśca ye kecidvṛthādānāni yāni ca ,vṛthābhakṣaṇamityetattāmasaṁ vṛttamiṣyate.
english: Whatever useless undertakings, and whichever useless gifts (dāna), and useless eating – this is indeed considered conduct pertaining to "tamas" (tamas).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 19. Whatever useless undertakings, and whichever useless gifts (dāna), and useless eating – this is indeed considered conduct pertaining to "tamas" (tamas).

sanskrit: अतिवादोऽतितिक्षा च मात्सर्यमतिमानिता ।अश्रद्दधानता चैव तामसं वृत्तमिष्यते ॥२०॥
transliteration: ativādo'titikṣā ca mātsaryamatimānitā ,aśraddadhānatā caiva tāmasaṁ vṛttamiṣyate.
english: Excessive speech, excessive patience (leading to apathy or inaction), and envy, excessive self-importance, and indeed lack of faith (śraddhā) – this is considered conduct pertaining to "tamas" (tamas).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 20. Excessive speech, excessive patience (leading to apathy or inaction), and envy, excessive self-importance, and indeed lack of faith (śraddhā) – this is considered conduct pertaining to "tamas" (tamas).

sanskrit: एवंविधास्तु ये केचिल्लोकेऽस्मिन्पापकर्मिणः ।मनुष्या भिन्नमर्यादाः सर्वे ते तामसा जनाः ॥२१॥
transliteration: evaṁvidhāstu ye kecilloke'sminpāpakarmiṇaḥ ,manuṣyā bhinnamaryādāḥ sarve te tāmasā janāḥ.
english: Indeed, whoever in this world are human beings performing sinful deeds and transgressing moral boundaries – all those individuals are of a dull, ignorant nature (tāmasāḥ).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 21. Indeed, whoever in this world are human beings performing sinful deeds and transgressing moral boundaries – all those individuals are of a dull, ignorant nature (tāmasāḥ).

sanskrit: तेषां योनिं प्रवक्ष्यामि नियतां पापकर्मणाम् ।अवाङ्निरयभावाय तिर्यङ्निरयगामिनः ॥२२॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ yoniṁ pravakṣyāmi niyatāṁ pāpakarmaṇām ,avāṅnirayabhāvāya tiryaṅnirayagāminaḥ.
english: I will declare their fixed realms of birth (yoni) for those who commit sinful deeds (pāpakarmaṇām): they are destined for a downward descent into hell, leading to animal existences and other hellish states.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 22. I will declare their fixed realms of birth (yoni) for those who commit sinful deeds (pāpakarmaṇām): they are destined for a downward descent into hell, leading to animal existences and other hellish states.

sanskrit: स्थावराणि च भूतानि पशवो वाहनानि च ।क्रव्यादा दन्दशूकाश्च कृमिकीटविहंगमाः ॥२३॥
transliteration: sthāvarāṇi ca bhūtāni paśavo vāhanāni ca ,kravyādā dandaśūkāśca kṛmikīṭavihaṁgamāḥ.
english: Immobile beings and other creatures, animals and beasts of burden, flesh-eaters and venomous serpents, as well as worms, insects, and birds.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 23. Immobile beings and other creatures, animals and beasts of burden, flesh-eaters and venomous serpents, as well as worms, insects, and birds.

sanskrit: अण्डजा जन्तवो ये च सर्वे चापि चतुष्पदाः ।उन्मत्ता बधिरा मूका ये चान्ये पापरोगिणः ॥२४॥
transliteration: aṇḍajā jantavo ye ca sarve cāpi catuṣpadāḥ ,unmattā badhirā mūkā ye cānye pāparogiṇaḥ.
english: Egg-born creatures and other beings, and also all quadrupeds; the insane, the deaf, the mute, and any others afflicted with dreadful diseases.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 24. Egg-born creatures and other beings, and also all quadrupeds; the insane, the deaf, the mute, and any others afflicted with dreadful diseases.

sanskrit: मग्नास्तमसि दुर्वृत्ताः स्वकर्मकृतलक्षणाः ।अवाक्स्रोतस इत्येते मग्नास्तमसि तामसाः ॥२५॥
transliteration: magnāstamasi durvṛttāḥ svakarmakṛtalakṣaṇāḥ ,avāksrotasa ityete magnāstamasi tāmasāḥ.
english: Those of wicked conduct, whose characteristics are shaped by their own actions (karma), are plunged into darkness. Indeed, these beings, whose course of existence flows downwards, are of the nature of ignorance (tamas) and are submerged in darkness.
