sanskrit: तेषामुत्कर्षमुद्रेकं वक्ष्याम्यहमतः परम् ।यथा ते सुकृताँल्लोकाँल्लभन्ते पुण्यकर्मिणः ॥२६॥
transliteration: teṣāmutkarṣamudrekaṁ vakṣyāmyahamataḥ param ,yathā te sukṛtāँllokāँllabhante puṇyakarmiṇaḥ.
english: Hereafter, I shall describe their upliftment and prosperity, and how they, the performers of meritorious actions (karma), attain the worlds (loka) earned by their virtuous deeds.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 26. Hereafter, I shall describe their upliftment and prosperity, and how they, the performers of meritorious actions (karma), attain the worlds (loka) earned by their virtuous deeds.

sanskrit: अन्यथा प्रतिपन्नास्तु विवृद्धा ये च कर्मसु ।स्वकर्मनिरतानां च ब्राह्मणानां शुभैषिणाम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: anyathā pratipannāstu vivṛddhā ye ca karmasu ,svakarmaniratānāṁ ca brāhmaṇānāṁ śubhaiṣiṇām.
english: But those who have adopted a different path and have greatly flourished in their actions (karma), and those Brahmins who are dedicated to their own duties and desire prosperity...
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 27. But those who have adopted a different path and have greatly flourished in their actions (karma), and those Brahmins who are dedicated to their own duties and desire prosperity...

sanskrit: संस्कारेणोर्ध्वमायान्ति यतमानाः सलोकताम् ।स्वर्गं गच्छन्ति देवानामित्येषा वैदिकी श्रुतिः ॥२८॥
transliteration: saṁskāreṇordhvamāyānti yatamānāḥ salokatām ,svargaṁ gacchanti devānāmityeṣā vaidikī śrutiḥ.
english: By means of ritual purification (saṃskāra), striving individuals ascend upwards to co-existence with the divine (salokatā). They go to the heaven of the gods; this is indeed the Vedic revelation (śruti).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 28. By means of ritual purification (saṃskāra), striving individuals ascend upwards to co-existence with the divine (salokatā). They go to the heaven of the gods; this is indeed the Vedic revelation (śruti).

sanskrit: अन्यथा प्रतिपन्नास्तु विवृद्धाः स्वेषु कर्मसु ।पुनरावृत्तिधर्माणस्ते भवन्तीह मानुषाः ॥२९॥
transliteration: anyathā pratipannāstu vivṛddhāḥ sveṣu karmasu ,punarāvṛttidharmāṇaste bhavantīha mānuṣāḥ.
english: However, those individuals who have adopted a mistaken understanding or course of action, and who are excessively attached to their own deeds (karma), are reborn here as humans whose intrinsic nature (dharma) is repeated rebirth.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 29. However, those individuals who have adopted a mistaken understanding or course of action, and who are excessively attached to their own deeds (karma), are reborn here as humans whose intrinsic nature (dharma) is repeated rebirth.

sanskrit: पापयोनिं समापन्नाश्चण्डाला मूकचूचुकाः ।वर्णान्पर्यायशश्चापि प्राप्नुवन्त्युत्तरोत्तरम् ॥३०॥
transliteration: pāpayoniṁ samāpannāścaṇḍālā mūkacūcukāḥ ,varṇānparyāyaśaścāpi prāpnuvantyuttarottaram.
english: Having entered into sinful birth-states (pāpayoni), they become outcastes (caṇḍāla) and those who are mute and stuttering; and they successively obtain lower and lower social classes (varṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 30. Having entered into sinful birth-states (pāpayoni), they become outcastes (caṇḍāla) and those who are mute and stuttering; and they successively obtain lower and lower social classes (varṇa).

sanskrit: शूद्रयोनिमतिक्रम्य ये चान्ये तामसा गुणाः ।स्रोतोमध्ये समागम्य वर्तन्ते तामसे गुणे ॥३१॥
transliteration: śūdrayonimatikramya ye cānye tāmasā guṇāḥ ,srotomadhye samāgamya vartante tāmase guṇe.
english: And those other individuals who, having transcended the birth-state of a śūdra, and whose qualities (guṇa) are primarily those of dullness (tamas), having converged in the flow (of transmigration), remain absorbed in the tamasic quality (guṇa).

sanskrit: अभिषङ्गस्तु कामेषु महामोह इति स्मृतः ।ऋषयो मुनयो देवा मुह्यन्त्यत्र सुखेप्सवः ॥३२॥
transliteration: abhiṣaṅgastu kāmeṣu mahāmoha iti smṛtaḥ ,ṛṣayo munayo devā muhyantyatra sukhepsavaḥ.
english: Indeed, deep attachment to worldly desires (kāma) is declared to be great delusion (mahāmoha). Even sages (ṛṣi), hermits, and gods become bewildered in this situation, being solely desirous of happiness.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 32. Indeed, deep attachment to worldly desires (kāma) is declared to be great delusion (mahāmoha). Even sages (ṛṣi), hermits, and gods become bewildered in this situation, being solely desirous of happiness.

sanskrit: तमो मोहो महामोहस्तामिस्रः क्रोधसंज्ञितः ।मरणं त्वन्धतामिस्रं तामिस्रं क्रोध उच्यते ॥३३॥
transliteration: tamo moho mahāmohastāmisraḥ krodhasaṁjñitaḥ ,maraṇaṁ tvandhatāmisraṁ tāmisraṁ krodha ucyate.
english: The state of ignorance (tamas) includes delusion and great delusion. Gloom (tāmisra) is designated as anger. Death, however, is blinding darkness (andhatāmisra); this gloom is specifically referred to as anger.

sanskrit: भावतो गुणतश्चैव योनितश्चैव तत्त्वतः ।सर्वमेतत्तमो विप्राः कीर्तितं वो यथाविधि ॥३४॥
transliteration: bhāvato guṇataścaiva yonitaścaiva tattvataḥ ,sarvametattamo viprāḥ kīrtitaṁ vo yathāvidhi.
english: O wise ones, all this ignorance (tamas) has been explained to you properly, considering its nature, its qualities, its origin, and its fundamental essence.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 34. O wise ones, all this ignorance (tamas) has been explained to you properly, considering its nature, its qualities, its origin, and its fundamental essence.

sanskrit: को न्वेतद्बुध्यते साधु को न्वेतत्साधु पश्यति ।अतत्त्वे तत्त्वदर्शी यस्तमसस्तत्त्वलक्षणम् ॥३५॥
transliteration: ko nvetadbudhyate sādhu ko nvetatsādhu paśyati ,atattve tattvadarśī yastamasastattvalakṣaṇam.
english: Who indeed truly comprehends this? Who indeed truly perceives this? The fundamental characteristic of ignorance (tamas) is to see the true essence in that which lacks it.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 35. Who indeed truly comprehends this? Who indeed truly perceives this? The fundamental characteristic of ignorance (tamas) is to see the true essence in that which lacks it.

sanskrit: तमोगुणा वो बहुधा प्रकीर्तिता यथावदुक्तं च तमः परावरम् ।नरो हि यो वेद गुणानिमान्सदा स तामसैः सर्वगुणैः प्रमुच्यते ॥३६॥
transliteration: tamoguṇā vo bahudhā prakīrtitā; yathāvaduktaṁ ca tamaḥ parāvaram ,naro hi yo veda guṇānimānsadā; sa tāmasaiḥ sarvaguṇaiḥ pramucyate.
english: The qualities influenced by ignorance have been manifoldly proclaimed to you, and ignorance (tamas) itself, encompassing both higher and lower aspects, has been duly explained. Indeed, the person who always understands these qualities is completely liberated from all attributes arising from ignorance (tāmasa).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 36 Verse 36. The qualities influenced by ignorance have been manifoldly proclaimed to you, and ignorance (tamas) itself, encompassing both higher and lower aspects, has been duly explained. sanskrit: ब्रह्मोवाच ।रजोऽहं वः प्रवक्ष्यामि याथातथ्येन सत्तमाः ।निबोधत महाभागा गुणवृत्तं च सर्वशः ॥१॥
transliteration: brahmovāca ,rajo'haṁ vaḥ pravakṣyāmi yāthātathyena sattamāḥ ,nibodhata mahābhāgā guṇavṛttaṁ ca sarvaśaḥ.
english: Brahmā said: "O most excellent ones, O greatly fortunate ones, I will now truthfully explain to you the quality of rajas. Completely understand the activities of the qualities (guṇas)."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 37 Verse 1. Brahmā said: "O most excellent ones, O greatly fortunate ones, I will now truthfully explain to you the quality of rajas. Completely understand the activities of the qualities (guṇas)."

sanskrit: संघातो रूपमायासः सुखदुःखे हिमातपौ ।ऐश्वर्यं विग्रहः संधिर्हेतुवादोऽरतिः क्षमा ॥२॥
transliteration: saṁghāto rūpamāyāsaḥ sukhaduḥkhe himātapau ,aiśvaryaṁ vigrahaḥ saṁdhirhetuvādo'ratiḥ kṣamā.
english: Combination, form, strenuous effort, pleasure and pain, cold and heat, dominion, conflict, reconciliation, reasoned argument, discontent, and forbearance.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 37 Verse 2. Combination, form, strenuous effort, pleasure and pain, cold and heat, dominion, conflict, reconciliation, reasoned argument, discontent, and forbearance.

sanskrit: बलं शौर्यं मदो रोषो व्यायामकलहावपि ।ईर्ष्येप्सा पैशुनं युद्धं ममत्वं परिपालनम् ॥३॥
transliteration: balaṁ śauryaṁ mado roṣo vyāyāmakalahāvapi ,īrṣyepsā paiśunaṁ yuddhaṁ mamatvaṁ paripālanam.
english: Strength, valor, arrogance, anger, and also physical exercise and quarrels, envy, fervent desire, malicious gossip, war, possessiveness, and diligent protection.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 37 Verse 3. Strength, valor, arrogance, anger, and also physical exercise and quarrels, envy, fervent desire, malicious gossip, war, possessiveness, and diligent protection.

sanskrit: वधबन्धपरिक्लेशाः क्रयो विक्रय एव च ।निकृन्त छिन्धि भिन्धीति परमर्मावकर्तनम् ॥४॥
transliteration: vadhabandhaparikleśāḥ krayo vikraya eva ca ,nikṛnta chindhi bhindhīti paramarmāvakartanam.
english: Slaughter, imprisonment, and severe suffering, buying and selling, and indeed the cries of 'Cut down!', 'Cut off!', 'Break!', and the deep wounding of others' vital points.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 37 Verse 4. Slaughter, imprisonment, and severe suffering, buying and selling, and indeed the cries of 'Cut down!', 'Cut off!', 'Break!', and the deep wounding of others' vital points.

sanskrit: उग्रं दारुणमाक्रोशः परवित्तानुशासनम् ।लोकचिन्ता विचिन्ता च मत्सरः परिभाषणम् ॥५॥
transliteration: ugraṁ dāruṇamākrośaḥ paravittānuśāsanam ,lokacintā vicintā ca matsaraḥ paribhāṣaṇam.
english: Fierce anger, cruelty, insult, the control over others' possessions, concern for the world, deep anxiety, envy, and hostile speech.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 37 Verse 5. Fierce anger, cruelty, insult, the control over others' possessions, concern for the world, deep anxiety, envy, and hostile speech.

sanskrit: मृषावादो मृषादानं विकल्पः परिभाषणम् ।निन्दा स्तुतिः प्रशंसा च प्रतापः परितर्पणम् ॥६॥
transliteration: mṛṣāvādo mṛṣādānaṁ vikalpaḥ paribhāṣaṇam ,nindā stutiḥ praśaṁsā ca pratāpaḥ paritarpaṇam.
english: False speech, improper giving, indecision, censure, blame, praise, commendation, valor, and gratification.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 37 Verse 6. False speech, improper giving, indecision, censure, blame, praise, commendation, valor, and gratification.

sanskrit: परिचर्या च शुश्रूषा सेवा तृष्णा व्यपाश्रयः ।व्यूहोऽनयः प्रमादश्च परितापः परिग्रहः ॥७॥
transliteration: paricaryā ca śuśrūṣā sevā tṛṣṇā vyapāśrayaḥ ,vyūho'nayaḥ pramādaśca paritāpaḥ parigrahaḥ.
english: Attendance, service, devotion, craving, seeking refuge, strategic arrangement, bad policy, carelessness, suffering, and acquisition.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 37 Verse 7. Attendance, service, devotion, craving, seeking refuge, strategic arrangement, bad policy, carelessness, suffering, and acquisition.

sanskrit: संस्कारा ये च लोकेऽस्मिन्प्रवर्तन्ते पृथक्पृथक् ।नृषु नारीषु भूतेषु द्रव्येषु शरणेषु च ॥८॥
transliteration: saṁskārā ye ca loke'sminpravartante pṛthakpṛthak ,nṛṣu nārīṣu bhūteṣu dravyeṣu śaraṇeṣu ca.
english: And those conditioning impressions (saṃskāra) that distinctly operate in this world, among men, women, all beings, material objects, and places of refuge.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 37 Verse 8. And those conditioning impressions (saṃskāra) that distinctly operate in this world, among men, women, all beings, material objects, and places of refuge.

sanskrit: संतापोऽप्रत्ययश्चैव व्रतानि नियमाश्च ये ।प्रदानमाशीर्युक्तं च सततं मे भवत्विति ॥९॥
transliteration: saṁtāpo'pratyayaścaiva vratāni niyamāśca ye ,pradānamāśīryuktaṁ ca satataṁ me bhavatviti.
english: May intense spiritual ardor, and disbelief, as well as the vows and prescribed observances, and giving accompanied by blessings, always be for me.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 37 Verse 9. May intense spiritual ardor, and disbelief, as well as the vows and prescribed observances, and giving accompanied by blessings, always be for me.

sanskrit: स्वधाकारो नमस्कारः स्वाहाकारो वषट्क्रिया ।याजनाध्यापने चोभे तथैवाहुः परिग्रहम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: svadhākāro namaskāraḥ svāhākāro vaṣaṭkriyā ,yājanādhyāpane cobhe tathaivāhuḥ parigraham.
english: The utterance of "svadhā" for ancestors, salutation, the utterance of "svāhā" for deities, and the ritual cry of "vaṣaṭ"; likewise, both officiating at a (Vedic ritual) [yajña] and teaching - these, they say, constitute a means of acceptance (parigraha).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 37 Verse 10. The utterance of "svadhā" for ancestors, salutation, the utterance of "svāhā" for deities, and the ritual cry of "vaṣaṭ"; likewise, both officiating at a (Vedic ritual) [yajña] and teaching - these, they say, constitute a means of acceptance (parigraha).

sanskrit: इदं मे स्यादिदं मे स्यात्स्नेहो गुणसमुद्भवः ।अभिद्रोहस्तथा माया निकृतिर्मान एव च ॥११॥
transliteration: idaṁ me syādidaṁ me syātsneho guṇasamudbhavaḥ ,abhidrohastathā māyā nikṛtirmāna eva ca.
english: 'This should be mine, this should be mine' - such is the attachment that arises from (perceived) qualities. Likewise, malice, and also illusion (māyā), deceit, and pride (māna) [are present].
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 37 Verse 11. 'This should be mine, this should be mine' - such is the attachment that arises from (perceived) qualities. Likewise, malice, and also illusion (māyā), deceit, and pride (māna) [are present].

sanskrit: स्तैन्यं हिंसा परीवादः परितापः प्रजागरः ।स्तम्भो दम्भोऽथ रागश्च भक्तिः प्रीतिः प्रमोदनम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: stainyaṁ hiṁsā parīvādaḥ paritāpaḥ prajāgaraḥ ,stambho dambho'tha rāgaśca bhaktiḥ prītiḥ pramodanam.
english: Theft, violence, slander, anguish, and sleeplessness; furthermore, rigidity, hypocrisy, and also attachment, devotion (bhakti), joy, and delight.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 37 Verse 12. Theft, violence, slander, anguish, and sleeplessness; furthermore, rigidity, hypocrisy, and also attachment, devotion (bhakti), joy, and delight.

sanskrit: द्यूतं च जनवादश्च संबन्धाः स्त्रीकृताश्च ये ।नृत्तवादित्रगीतानि प्रसङ्गा ये च केचन ।सर्व एते गुणा विप्रा राजसाः संप्रकीर्तिताः ॥१३॥
transliteration: dyūtaṁ ca janavādaśca saṁbandhāḥ strīkṛtāśca ye ,nṛttavāditragītāni prasaṅgā ye ca kecana ,sarva ete guṇā viprā rājasāḥ saṁprakīrtitāḥ.
english: Gambling, gossip, and illicit relationships with women, along with dancing, instrumental music, singing, and any other such attachments – all these qualities, O brahmins, are declared to be born of passion (rajas).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 37 Verse 13. Gambling, gossip, and illicit relationships with women, along with dancing, instrumental music, singing, and any other such attachments – all these qualities, O brahmins, are declared to be born of passion (rajas).

sanskrit: भूतभव्यभविष्याणां भावानां भुवि भावनाः ।त्रिवर्गनिरता नित्यं धर्मोऽर्थः काम इत्यपि ॥१४॥
transliteration: bhūtabhavyabhaviṣyāṇāṁ bhāvānāṁ bhuvi bhāvanāḥ ,trivarganiratā nityaṁ dharmo'rthaḥ kāma ityapi.
english: On earth, the sentiments (bhāvanāḥ) of beings (bhāvānām) regarding the past, present, and future are constantly focused on the three aims of human life (trivarga): righteous conduct (dharma), material prosperity (artha), and desire (kāma).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 37 Verse 14. On earth, the sentiments (bhāvanāḥ) of beings (bhāvānām) regarding the past, present, and future are constantly focused on the three aims of human life (trivarga): righteous conduct (dharma), material prosperity (artha), and desire (kāma).

sanskrit: कामवृत्ताः प्रमोदन्ते सर्वकामसमृद्धिभिः ।अर्वाक्स्रोतस इत्येते तैजसा रजसावृताः ॥१५॥
transliteration: kāmavṛttāḥ pramodante sarvakāmasamṛddhibhiḥ ,arvāksrotasa ityete taijasā rajasāvṛtāḥ.
english: Those whose conduct is driven by desire (kāma) rejoice with the complete fulfillment of all desires. Such individuals, known as "अर्वाक्स्रोतसः" (those with a downward flow of energy), are fiery (taijasa) and enveloped by the quality of passion (rajas).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 37 Verse 15. Those whose conduct is driven by desire (kāma) rejoice with the complete fulfillment of all desires. Such individuals, known as "अर्वाक्स्रोतसः" (those with a downward flow of energy), are fiery (taijasa) and enveloped by the quality of passion (rajas).

sanskrit: अस्मिँल्लोके प्रमोदन्ते जायमानाः पुनः पुनः ।प्रेत्यभाविकमीहन्त इह लौकिकमेव च ।ददति प्रतिगृह्णन्ति जपन्त्यथ च जुह्वति ॥१६॥
transliteration: asmiँlloke pramodante jāyamānāḥ punaḥ punaḥ ,pretyabhāvikamīhanta iha laukikameva ca ,dadati pratigṛhṇanti japantyatha ca juhvati.
english: In this world, those who are born repeatedly rejoice. They strive for both things related to the afterlife and for worldly matters here. They give, they receive, they recite (mantras), and they perform Vedic fire rituals (juhvati).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 37 Verse 16. In this world, those who are born repeatedly rejoice. They strive for both things related to the afterlife and for worldly matters here. They give, they receive, they recite (mantras), and they perform Vedic fire rituals (juhvati).

sanskrit: रजोगुणा वो बहुधानुकीर्तिता यथावदुक्तं गुणवृत्तमेव च ।नरो हि यो वेद गुणानिमान्सदा स राजसैः सर्वगुणैर्विमुच्यते ॥१७॥
transliteration: rajoguṇā vo bahudhānukīrtitā; yathāvaduktaṁ guṇavṛttameva ca ,naro hi yo veda guṇānimānsadā; sa rājasaiḥ sarvaguṇairvimucyate.
english: The attributes of rajas (rajoguṇa) have been extensively described to you, and their characteristic behaviors have also been accurately stated. Indeed, a person who always understands these qualities is freed from all the attributes associated with rajas.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 37 Verse 17. The attributes of rajas (rajoguṇa) have been extensively described to you, and their characteristic behaviors have also been accurately stated. Indeed, a person who always understands these qualities is freed from all the attributes associated with rajas.

sanskrit: ब्रह्मोवाच ।अतः परं प्रवक्ष्यामि तृतीयं गुणमुत्तमम् ।सर्वभूतहितं लोके सतां धर्ममनिन्दितम् ॥१॥
transliteration: brahmovāca ,ataḥ paraṁ pravakṣyāmi tṛtīyaṁ guṇamuttamam ,sarvabhūtahitaṁ loke satāṁ dharmamaninditam.
english: Brahmā said: 'After this, I will declare the third and excellent quality, which is beneficial to all beings in the world, and is the irreproachable natural law (dharma) of the virtuous.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 38 Verse 1. Brahmā said: 'After this, I will declare the third and excellent quality, which is beneficial to all beings in the world, and is the irreproachable natural law (dharma) of the virtuous.'

sanskrit: आनन्दः प्रीतिरुद्रेकः प्राकाश्यं सुखमेव च ।अकार्पण्यमसंरम्भः संतोषः श्रद्दधानता ॥२॥
transliteration: ānandaḥ prītirudrekaḥ prākāśyaṁ sukhameva ca ,akārpaṇyamasaṁrambhaḥ saṁtoṣaḥ śraddadhānatā.
english: Joy, delight, exuberance, clarity, and also happiness; generosity, calmness, contentment, and faithful earnestness (śraddhā).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 38 Verse 2. Joy, delight, exuberance, clarity, and also happiness; generosity, calmness, contentment, and faithful earnestness (śraddhā).

sanskrit: क्षमा धृतिरहिंसा च समता सत्यमार्जवम् ।अक्रोधश्चानसूया च शौचं दाक्ष्यं पराक्रमः ॥३॥
transliteration: kṣamā dhṛtirahiṁsā ca samatā satyamārjavam ,akrodhaścānasūyā ca śaucaṁ dākṣyaṁ parākramaḥ.
english: Forgiveness, steadfast fortitude, and non-violence; impartiality, truthfulness, and integrity; the absence of anger, and the absence of envy; purity, dexterity, and valor.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 38 Verse 3. Forgiveness, steadfast fortitude, and non-violence; impartiality, truthfulness, and integrity; the absence of anger, and the absence of envy; purity, dexterity, and valor.

sanskrit: मुधा ज्ञानं मुधा वृत्तं मुधा सेवा मुधा श्रमः ।एवं यो युक्तधर्मः स्यात्सोऽमुत्रानन्त्यमश्नुते ॥४॥
transliteration: mudhā jñānaṁ mudhā vṛttaṁ mudhā sevā mudhā śramaḥ ,evaṁ yo yuktadharmaḥ syātso'mutrānantyamaśnute.
english: Knowledge, conduct, service, and effort are all in vain. He who is thus steadfast in natural law (dharma) attains endlessness in the hereafter.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 38 Verse 4. Knowledge, conduct, service, and effort are all in vain. He who is thus steadfast in natural law (dharma) attains endlessness in the hereafter.

sanskrit: निर्ममो निरहंकारो निराशीः सर्वतः समः ।अकामहत इत्येष सतां धर्मः सनातनः ॥५॥
transliteration: nirmamo nirahaṁkāro nirāśīḥ sarvataḥ samaḥ ,akāmahata ityeṣa satāṁ dharmaḥ sanātanaḥ.
english: Free from possessiveness, egoless, without expectations, impartial in all respects, and unaffected by desire – such is the eternal intrinsic nature (dharma) of the virtuous.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 38 Verse 5. Free from possessiveness, egoless, without expectations, impartial in all respects, and unaffected by desire – such is the eternal intrinsic nature (dharma) of the virtuous.

sanskrit: विश्रम्भो ह्रीस्तितिक्षा च त्यागः शौचमतन्द्रिता ।आनृशंस्यमसंमोहो दया भूतेष्वपैशुनम् ॥६॥
transliteration: viśrambho hrīstitikṣā ca tyāgaḥ śaucamatandritā ,ānṛśaṁsyamasaṁmoho dayā bhūteṣvapaiśunam.
english: Trust, modesty, forbearance, and renunciation; purity, vigilance, non-cruelty, freedom from delusion, compassion towards all beings, and absence of malice.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 38 Verse 6. Trust, modesty, forbearance, and renunciation; purity, vigilance, non-cruelty, freedom from delusion, compassion towards all beings, and absence of malice.

sanskrit: हर्षस्तुष्टिर्विस्मयश्च विनयः साधुवृत्तता ।शान्तिकर्म विशुद्धिश्च शुभा बुद्धिर्विमोचनम् ॥७॥
transliteration: harṣastuṣṭirvismayaśca vinayaḥ sādhuvṛttatā ,śāntikarma viśuddhiśca śubhā buddhirvimocanam.
english: Joy, contentment, wonder, and humility; virtuous conduct, peaceful action, complete purity, auspicious intellect, and liberation.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 38 Verse 7. Joy, contentment, wonder, and humility; virtuous conduct, peaceful action, complete purity, auspicious intellect, and liberation.

sanskrit: उपेक्षा ब्रह्मचर्यं च परित्यागश्च सर्वशः ।निर्ममत्वमनाशीस्त्वमपरिक्रीतधर्मता ॥८॥
transliteration: upekṣā brahmacaryaṁ ca parityāgaśca sarvaśaḥ ,nirmamatvamanāśīstvamaparikrītadharmatā.
english: Disregard for worldly matters, spiritual studenthood (brahmacarya), and complete renunciation of everything; freedom from possessiveness, imperishableness, and the state of having one's intrinsic nature (dharma) uncompromised.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 38 Verse 8. Disregard for worldly matters, spiritual studenthood (brahmacarya), and complete renunciation of everything; freedom from possessiveness, imperishableness, and the state of having one's intrinsic nature (dharma) uncompromised.

sanskrit: मुधा दानं मुधा यज्ञो मुधाधीतं मुधा व्रतम् ।मुधा प्रतिग्रहश्चैव मुधा धर्मो मुधा तपः ॥९॥
transliteration: mudhā dānaṁ mudhā yajño mudhādhītaṁ mudhā vratam ,mudhā pratigrahaścaiva mudhā dharmo mudhā tapaḥ.
english: Giving is fruitless, a Vedic ritual (yajña) is fruitless, study is fruitless, a vow is fruitless. Likewise, accepting gifts is fruitless, one's natural law (dharma) is fruitless, and austerity (tapas) is fruitless.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 38 Verse 9. Giving is fruitless, a Vedic ritual (yajña) is fruitless, study is fruitless, a vow is fruitless. Likewise, accepting gifts is fruitless, one's natural law (dharma) is fruitless, and austerity (tapas) is fruitless.

sanskrit: एवंवृत्तास्तु ये केचिल्लोकेऽस्मिन्सत्त्वसंश्रयाः ।ब्राह्मणा ब्रह्मयोनिस्थास्ते धीराः साधुदर्शिनः ॥१०॥
transliteration: evaṁvṛttāstu ye kecilloke'sminsattvasaṁśrayāḥ ,brāhmaṇā brahmayonisthāste dhīrāḥ sādhudarśinaḥ.
english: Those in this world who act in such a way, who rely on the quality of goodness (sattva), and who are Brahmins established in the source of the Absolute (brahman) - they are truly wise and discern what is right.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 38 Verse 10. Those in this world who act in such a way, who rely on the quality of goodness (sattva), and who are Brahmins established in the source of the Absolute (brahman) - they are truly wise and discern what is right.

sanskrit: हित्वा सर्वाणि पापानि निःशोका ह्यजरामराः ।दिवं प्राप्य तु ते धीराः कुर्वते वै ततस्ततः ॥११॥
transliteration: hitvā sarvāṇi pāpāni niḥśokā hyajarāmarāḥ ,divaṁ prāpya tu te dhīrāḥ kurvate vai tatastataḥ.
english: Having abandoned all sins, they are indeed free from sorrow and are immortal. And having attained heaven, those resolute ones certainly move about everywhere.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 38 Verse 11. Having abandoned all sins, they are indeed free from sorrow and are immortal. And having attained heaven, those resolute ones certainly move about everywhere.

sanskrit: ईशित्वं च वशित्वं च लघुत्वं मनसश्च ते ।विकुर्वते महात्मानो देवास्त्रिदिवगा इव ॥१२॥
transliteration: īśitvaṁ ca vaśitvaṁ ca laghutvaṁ manasaśca te ,vikurvate mahātmāno devāstridivagā iva.
english: Great souls (mahātmā) manifest qualities like mastery, control, and the subtlety of mind, just as the gods who dwell in the celestial spheres do.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 38 Verse 12. Great souls (mahātmā) manifest qualities like mastery, control, and the subtlety of mind, just as the gods who dwell in the celestial spheres do.

sanskrit: ऊर्ध्वस्रोतस इत्येते देवा वैकारिकाः स्मृताः ।विकुर्वते प्रकृत्या वै दिवं प्राप्तास्ततस्ततः ।यद्यदिच्छन्ति तत्सर्वं भजन्ते विभजन्ति च ॥१३॥
transliteration: ūrdhvasrotasa ityete devā vaikārikāḥ smṛtāḥ ,vikurvate prakṛtyā vai divaṁ prāptāstatastataḥ ,yadyadicchanti tatsarvaṁ bhajante vibhajanti ca.
english: These gods, whose nourishment flows upwards, are considered to be modifications (vikāra). Having reached the celestial realms, they indeed transform by their inherent nature (prakṛti). Whatever they desire, they enjoy all of it and also distribute it.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 38 Verse 13. These gods, whose nourishment flows upwards, are considered to be modifications (vikāra). Having reached the celestial realms, they indeed transform by their inherent nature (prakṛti). Whatever they desire, they enjoy all of it and also distribute it.

sanskrit: इत्येतत्सात्त्विकं वृत्तं कथितं वो द्विजर्षभाः ।एतद्विज्ञाय विधिवल्लभते यद्यदिच्छति ॥१४॥
transliteration: ityetatsāttvikaṁ vṛttaṁ kathitaṁ vo dvijarṣabhāḥ ,etadvijñāya vidhivallabhate yadyadicchati.
english: Thus, this sattvic (sattva-guṇa) characteristic has been described to you, O foremost among the twice-born (dvija). Having properly understood this, one obtains whatever one desires.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 38 Verse 14. Thus, this sattvic (sattva-guṇa) characteristic has been described to you, O foremost among the twice-born (dvija). Having properly understood this, one obtains whatever one desires.

sanskrit: प्रकीर्तिताः सत्त्वगुणा विशेषतो यथावदुक्तं गुणवृत्तमेव च ।नरस्तु यो वेद गुणानिमान्सदा गुणान्स भुङ्क्ते न गुणैः स भुज्यते ॥१५॥
transliteration: prakīrtitāḥ sattvaguṇā viśeṣato; yathāvaduktaṁ guṇavṛttameva ca ,narastu yo veda guṇānimānsadā; guṇānsa bhuṅkte na guṇaiḥ sa bhujyate.
english: The sattva-qualities (sattva-guṇa) have been specifically recounted, and indeed the nature of all such qualities (guṇa) has been accurately stated. However, the person who always understands these qualities (guṇa) experiences them; he is not experienced by them (i.e., he is not bound by them).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 38 Verse 15. The sattva-qualities (sattva-guṇa) have been specifically recounted, and indeed the nature of all such qualities (guṇa) has been accurately stated. However, the person who always understands these qualities (guṇa) experiences them; he is not experienced by them (i.e., he is not bound by them).

sanskrit: ब्रह्मोवाच ।नैव शक्या गुणा वक्तुं पृथक्त्वेनेह सर्वशः ।अविच्छिन्नानि दृश्यन्ते रजः सत्त्वं तमस्तथा ॥१॥
transliteration: brahmovāca ,naiva śakyā guṇā vaktuṁ pṛthaktveneha sarvaśaḥ ,avicchinnāni dṛśyante rajaḥ sattvaṁ tamastathā.
english: Brahmā said: Indeed, it is not possible to describe these qualities (guṇas) entirely separately here, for "rajas", "sattva", and "tamas" are always perceived as indivisible.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 39 Verse 1. Brahmā said: Indeed, it is not possible to describe these qualities (guṇas) entirely separately here, for "rajas", "sattva", and "tamas" are always perceived as indivisible.

sanskrit: अन्योन्यमनुषज्जन्ते अन्योन्यं चानुजीविनः ।अन्योन्यापाश्रयाः सर्वे तथान्योन्यानुवर्तिनः ॥२॥
transliteration: anyonyamanuṣajjante anyonyaṁ cānujīvinaḥ ,anyonyāpāśrayāḥ sarve tathānyonyānuvartinaḥ.
english: They cling to each other and are mutually sustained; all of them are interdependent and likewise conform to one another.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 39 Verse 2. They cling to each other and are mutually sustained; all of them are interdependent and likewise conform to one another.

sanskrit: यावत्सत्त्वं तमस्तावद्वर्तते नात्र संशयः ।यावत्तमश्च सत्त्वं च रजस्तावदिहोच्यते ॥३॥
transliteration: yāvatsattvaṁ tamastāvadvartate nātra saṁśayaḥ ,yāvattamaśca sattvaṁ ca rajastāvadihocyate.
english: As long as "sattva" exists, so long does "tamas" also prevail; there is no doubt about this. And as long as both "tamas" and "sattva" are present, "rajas" is said to exist here.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 39 Verse 3. As long as "sattva" exists, so long does "tamas" also prevail; there is no doubt about this. And as long as both "tamas" and "sattva" are present, "rajas" is said to exist here.

sanskrit: संहत्य कुर्वते यात्रां सहिताः संघचारिणः ।संघातवृत्तयो ह्येते वर्तन्ते हेत्वहेतुभिः ॥४॥
transliteration: saṁhatya kurvate yātrāṁ sahitāḥ saṁghacāriṇaḥ ,saṁghātavṛttayo hyete vartante hetvahetubhiḥ.
english: Having united, they (the guṇas) proceed in their course, being companions who act collectively. Indeed, these (guṇas), whose modes of action are interdependent, operate both with and without specific causes.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 39 Verse 4. Having united, they (the guṇas) proceed in their course, being companions who act collectively. Indeed, these (guṇas), whose modes of action are interdependent, operate both with and without specific causes.

sanskrit: उद्रेकव्यतिरेकाणां तेषामन्योन्यवर्तिनाम् ।वर्तते तद्यथान्यूनं व्यतिरिक्तं च सर्वशः ॥५॥
transliteration: udrekavyatirekāṇāṁ teṣāmanyonyavartinām ,vartate tadyathānyūnaṁ vyatiriktaṁ ca sarvaśaḥ.
english: Of these (qualities), which exist mutually and are characterized by predominance and subordination, their interaction operates in such a way that it is always complete (not deficient) and yet also marked by excess in every respect.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 39 Verse 5. Of these (qualities), which exist mutually and are characterized by predominance and subordination, their interaction operates in such a way that it is always complete (not deficient) and yet also marked by excess in every respect.

sanskrit: व्यतिरिक्तं तमो यत्र तिर्यग्भावगतं भवेत् ।अल्पं तत्र रजो ज्ञेयं सत्त्वं चाल्पतरं ततः ॥६॥
transliteration: vyatiriktaṁ tamo yatra tiryagbhāvagataṁ bhavet ,alpaṁ tatra rajo jñeyaṁ sattvaṁ cālpataraṁ tataḥ.
english: Where inertia (tamas) is predominant and has entered the transverse state (tiryag-bhāva), there activity (rajas) should be understood as little, and purity (sattva) as even less than that.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 39 Verse 6. Where inertia (tamas) is predominant and has entered the transverse state (tiryag-bhāva), there activity (rajas) should be understood as little, and purity (sattva) as even less than that.

sanskrit: उद्रिक्तं च रजो यत्र मध्यस्रोतोगतं भवेत् ।अल्पं तत्र तमो ज्ञेयं सत्त्वं चाल्पतरं ततः ॥७॥
transliteration: udriktaṁ ca rajo yatra madhyasrotogataṁ bhavet ,alpaṁ tatra tamo jñeyaṁ sattvaṁ cālpataraṁ tataḥ.
english: And where activity (rajas) is predominant and has entered the middle stream, there inertia (tamas) should be understood as little, and purity (sattva) as even less than that.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 39 Verse 7. And where activity (rajas) is predominant and has entered the middle stream, there inertia (tamas) should be understood as little, and purity (sattva) as even less than that.

sanskrit: उद्रिक्तं च यदा सत्त्वमूर्ध्वस्रोतोगतं भवेत् ।अल्पं तत्र रजो ज्ञेयं तमश्चाल्पतरं ततः ॥८॥
transliteration: udriktaṁ ca yadā sattvamūrdhvasrotogataṁ bhavet ,alpaṁ tatra rajo jñeyaṁ tamaścālpataraṁ tataḥ.
english: And when purity (sattva) is predominant and has entered the upward stream, there activity (rajas) should be understood as little, and inertia (tamas) as even less than that.

sanskrit: सत्त्वं वैकारिकं योनिरिन्द्रियाणां प्रकाशिका ।न हि सत्त्वात्परो भावः कश्चिदन्यो विधीयते ॥९॥
transliteration: sattvaṁ vaikārikaṁ yonirindriyāṇāṁ prakāśikā ,na hi sattvātparo bhāvaḥ kaścidanyo vidhīyate.
english: The quality of purity (sattva) is a modification, the source of the senses, and the illuminating principle. Indeed, no other state of being is declared to be superior to sattva.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 39 Verse 9. The quality of purity (sattva) is a modification, the source of the senses, and the illuminating principle. Indeed, no other state of being is declared to be superior to sattva.

sanskrit: ऊर्ध्वं गच्छन्ति सत्त्वस्था मध्ये तिष्ठन्ति राजसाः ।जघन्यगुणसंयुक्ता यान्त्यधस्तामसा जनाः ॥१०॥
transliteration: ūrdhvaṁ gacchanti sattvasthā madhye tiṣṭhanti rājasāḥ ,jaghanyaguṇasaṁyuktā yāntyadhastāmasā janāḥ.
english: Those established in the quality of purity (sattva) ascend upwards; those dominated by the quality of passion (rajas) remain in the middle. People endowed with the lowest quality (tamas) descend downwards.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 39 Verse 10. Those established in the quality of purity (sattva) ascend upwards; those dominated by the quality of passion (rajas) remain in the middle. People endowed with the lowest quality (tamas) descend downwards.

sanskrit: तमः शूद्रे रजः क्षत्रे ब्राह्मणे सत्त्वमुत्तमम् ।इत्येवं त्रिषु वर्णेषु विवर्तन्ते गुणास्त्रयः ॥११॥
transliteration: tamaḥ śūdre rajaḥ kṣatre brāhmaṇe sattvamuttamam ,ityevaṁ triṣu varṇeṣu vivartante guṇāstrayaḥ.
english: The quality of ignorance (tamas) is dominant in the śūdra (laborer), passion (rajas) in the kṣatriya (warrior), and purity (sattva) is supreme in the brāhmaṇa (priest). Thus, these three qualities (guṇas) manifest themselves in the three social classes (varṇas).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 39 Verse 11. sanskrit: दूरादपि हि दृश्यन्ते सहिताः संघचारिणः ।तमः सत्त्वं रजश्चैव पृथक्त्वं नानुशुश्रुम ॥१२॥
transliteration: dūrādapi hi dṛśyante sahitāḥ saṁghacāriṇaḥ ,tamaḥ sattvaṁ rajaścaiva pṛthaktvaṁ nānuśuśruma.
english: Indeed, even from afar, those who move in groups appear united. However, we have not heard of the qualities of ignorance (tamas), purity (sattva), and passion (rajas) existing separately.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 39 Verse 12. Indeed, even from afar, those who move in groups appear united. However, we have not heard of the qualities of ignorance (tamas), purity (sattva), and passion (rajas) existing separately.

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा चादित्यमुद्यन्तं कुचोराणां भयं भवेत् ।अध्वगाः परितप्येरंस्तृष्णार्ता दुःखभागिनः ॥१३॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvā cādityamudyantaṁ kucorāṇāṁ bhayaṁ bhavet ,adhvagāḥ paritapyeraṁstṛṣṇārtā duḥkhabhāginaḥ.
english: Upon seeing the rising sun, bad thieves experience fear. Wayfarers tormented by thirst would greatly suffer, becoming sharers in misery.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 39 Verse 13. Upon seeing the rising sun, bad thieves experience fear. Wayfarers tormented by thirst would greatly suffer, becoming sharers in misery.

sanskrit: आदित्यः सत्त्वमुद्दिष्टं कुचोरास्तु यथा तमः ।परितापोऽध्वगानां च राजसो गुण उच्यते ॥१४॥
transliteration: ādityaḥ sattvamuddiṣṭaṁ kucorāstu yathā tamaḥ ,paritāpo'dhvagānāṁ ca rājaso guṇa ucyate.
english: The sun is designated as purity (sattva), but bad thieves are like darkness (tamas). And the suffering of travelers is declared to be the quality of passion (rajas).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 39 Verse 14. The sun is designated as purity (sattva), but bad thieves are like darkness (tamas). And the suffering of travelers is declared to be the quality of passion (rajas).

sanskrit: प्राकाश्यं सत्त्वमादित्ये संतापो राजसो गुणः ।उपप्लवस्तु विज्ञेयस्तामसस्तस्य पर्वसु ॥१५॥
transliteration: prākāśyaṁ sattvamāditye saṁtāpo rājaso guṇaḥ ,upaplavastu vijñeyastāmasastasya parvasu.
english: The luminosity in the sun is purity (sattva), and its heat is the quality of passion (rajas). However, a disturbance (upaplava) at its junctions (parvasu) should be known as ignorance (tamas).

sanskrit: एवं ज्योतिःषु सर्वेषु विवर्तन्ते गुणास्त्रयः ।पर्यायेण च वर्तन्ते तत्र तत्र तथा तथा ॥१६॥
transliteration: evaṁ jyotiḥṣu sarveṣu vivartante guṇāstrayaḥ ,paryāyeṇa ca vartante tatra tatra tathā tathā.
english: In this manner, the three qualities (guṇa) manifest in all luminous objects. And they operate, each in its turn, in various places and in various ways.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 39 Verse 16. In this manner, the three qualities (guṇa) manifest in all luminous objects. And they operate, each in its turn, in various places and in various ways.

sanskrit: स्थावरेषु च भूतेषु तिर्यग्भावगतं तमः ।राजसास्तु विवर्तन्ते स्नेहभावस्तु सात्त्विकः ॥१७॥
transliteration: sthāvareṣu ca bhūteṣu tiryagbhāvagataṁ tamaḥ ,rājasāstu vivartante snehabhāvastu sāttvikaḥ.
english: Among stationary beings and creatures, the quality of inertia (tamas) manifests as a dull, lower state of existence. Those dominated by the quality of passion (rajas) engage in various activities, while the sentiment of affection is characteristic of the quality of goodness (sattva).

sanskrit: अहस्त्रिधा तु विज्ञेयं त्रिधा रात्रिर्विधीयते ।मासार्धमासवर्षाणि ऋतवः संधयस्तथा ॥१८॥
transliteration: ahastridhā tu vijñeyaṁ tridhā rātrirvidhīyate ,māsārdhamāsavarṣāṇi ṛtavaḥ saṁdhayastathā.
english: A day should indeed be understood as having three divisions, and a night is similarly ordained with three parts. Months, half-months, years, seasons, and their junctures are likewise structured.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 39 Verse 18. A day should indeed be understood as having three divisions, and a night is similarly ordained with three parts. Months, half-months, years, seasons, and their junctures are likewise structured.

sanskrit: त्रिधा दानानि दीयन्ते त्रिधा यज्ञः प्रवर्तते ।त्रिधा लोकास्त्रिधा वेदास्त्रिधा विद्यास्त्रिधा गतिः ॥१९॥

english: Gifts (dāna) are offered in three ways, and the Vedic ritual (yajña) proceeds in three ways. The worlds are three-fold, the Vedas are three-fold, knowledge is three-fold, and destinies are three-fold.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 39 Verse 19. Gifts (dāna) are offered in three ways, and the Vedic ritual (yajña) proceeds in three ways. The worlds are three-fold, the Vedas are three-fold, knowledge is three-fold, and destinies are three-fold.

sanskrit: भूतं भव्यं भविष्यच्च धर्मोऽर्थः काम इत्यपि ।प्राणापानावुदानश्चाप्येत एव त्रयो गुणाः ॥२०॥
transliteration: bhūtaṁ bhavyaṁ bhaviṣyacca dharmo'rthaḥ kāma ityapi ,prāṇāpānāvudānaścāpyeta eva trayo guṇāḥ.
english: The past, the present, and the future, as well as natural law (dharma), purpose, and desire, along with the vital breaths prana, apana, and udana - these, indeed, are the three qualities (guṇas).

sanskrit: यत्किंचिदिह वै लोके सर्वमेष्वेव तत्त्रिषु ।त्रयो गुणाः प्रवर्तन्ते अव्यक्ता नित्यमेव तु ।सत्त्वं रजस्तमश्चैव गुणसर्गः सनातनः ॥२१॥
transliteration: yatkiṁcidiha vai loke sarvameṣveva tattriṣu ,trayo guṇāḥ pravartante avyaktā nityameva tu ,sattvaṁ rajastamaścaiva guṇasargaḥ sanātanaḥ.
english: Whatever exists here in the world, all of that is indeed contained within these three. The three qualities (guṇas) constantly operate, and the unmanifest (prakṛti) itself is always present. Sattva, rajas, and tamas – this creation of qualities is eternal.

sanskrit: तमोऽव्यक्तं शिवं नित्यमजं योनिः सनातनः ।प्रकृतिर्विकारः प्रलयः प्रधानं प्रभवाप्ययौ ॥२२॥
transliteration: tamo'vyaktaṁ śivaṁ nityamajaṁ yoniḥ sanātanaḥ ,prakṛtirvikāraḥ pralayaḥ pradhānaṁ prabhavāpyayau.
english: The unmanifest (prakṛti), characterized by tamas (quality), is auspicious, eternal, unborn, and the eternal source. It is primal nature (prakṛti), modification, dissolution, the primary cause (pradhāna), and both origin and dissolution.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 39 Verse 22. The unmanifest (prakṛti), characterized by tamas (quality), is auspicious, eternal, unborn, and the eternal source. sanskrit: अनुद्रिक्तमनूनं च ह्यकम्पमचलं ध्रुवम् ।सदसच्चैव तत्सर्वमव्यक्तं त्रिगुणं स्मृतम् ।ज्ञेयानि नामधेयानि नरैरध्यात्मचिन्तकैः ॥२३॥
transliteration: anudriktamanūnaṁ ca hyakampamacalaṁ dhruvam ,sadasaccaiva tatsarvamavyaktaṁ triguṇaṁ smṛtam ,jñeyāni nāmadheyāni narairadhyātmacintakaiḥ.
english: (The unmanifest is) indeed not excessive, not deficient, unshaken, unmoving, and firm. That entire unmanifest (prakṛti), which is both existent and non-existent and composed of three qualities (guṇas), is declared (as such). These names should be known by spiritual thinkers.

sanskrit: अव्यक्तनामानि गुणांश्च तत्त्वतो यो वेद सर्वाणि गतीश्च केवलाः ।विमुक्तदेहः प्रविभागतत्त्ववित्स मुच्यते सर्वगुणैर्निरामयः ॥२४॥
transliteration: avyaktanāmāni guṇāṁśca tattvato; yo veda sarvāṇi gatīśca kevalāḥ ,vimuktadehaḥ pravibhāgatattvavi;tsa mucyate sarvaguṇairnirāmayaḥ.
english: Whoever truly knows the names of the unmanifest (prakṛti), the qualities (guṇas), and all the distinct states (tattvas), that person, freed from the body and having discerned the principles, is liberated (mokṣa), free from all qualities (guṇas), and becomes undisturbed.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 39 Verse 24. Whoever truly knows the names of the unmanifest (prakṛti), the qualities (guṇas), and all the distinct states (tattvas), that person, freed from the body and having discerned the principles, is liberated (mokṣa), free from all qualities (guṇas), and becomes undisturbed.

sanskrit: ब्रह्मोवाच ।अव्यक्तात्पूर्वमुत्पन्नो महानात्मा महामतिः ।आदिर्गुणानां सर्वेषां प्रथमः सर्ग उच्यते ॥१॥
transliteration: brahmovāca ,avyaktātpūrvamutpanno mahānātmā mahāmatiḥ ,ādirguṇānāṁ sarveṣāṁ prathamaḥ sarga ucyate.
english: Brahmā said: The great (ātman), possessing a profound intellect, which first originated from the unmanifest (prakṛti), is declared to be the primal creation and the source of all the qualities (guṇas).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 40 Verse 1. Brahmā said: The great (ātman), possessing a profound intellect, which first originated from the unmanifest (prakṛti), is declared to be the primal creation and the source of all the qualities (guṇas).

sanskrit: महानात्मा मतिर्विष्णुर्विश्वः शंभुश्च वीर्यवान् ।बुद्धिः प्रज्ञोपलब्धिश्च तथा ख्यातिर्धृतिः स्मृतिः ॥२॥
transliteration: mahānātmā matirviṣṇurviśvaḥ śaṁbhuśca vīryavān ,buddhiḥ prajñopalabdhiśca tathā khyātirdhṛtiḥ smṛtiḥ.
english: The great (ātman) is identified as intellect, Viṣṇu, the all-pervading (viśva), and the powerful Śambhu. It is also understanding (buddhi), wisdom, perception, knowledge, resolve, and memory.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 40 Verse 2. The great (ātman) is identified as intellect, Viṣṇu, the all-pervading (viśva), and the powerful Śambhu. It is also understanding (buddhi), wisdom, perception, knowledge, resolve, and memory.

sanskrit: पर्यायवाचकैः शब्दैर्महानात्मा विभाव्यते ।तं जानन्ब्राह्मणो विद्वान्न प्रमोहं निगच्छति ॥३॥
transliteration: paryāyavācakaiḥ śabdairmahānātmā vibhāvyate ,taṁ jānanbrāhmaṇo vidvānna pramohaṁ nigacchati.
english: The great (ātman) is understood through various synonymous terms. A learned Brahmin, knowing that (ātman), does not fall into delusion.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 40 Verse 3. The great (ātman) is understood through various synonymous terms. A learned Brahmin, knowing that (ātman), does not fall into delusion.

sanskrit: सर्वतःपाणिपादश्च सर्वतोक्षिशिरोमुखः ।सर्वतःश्रुतिमाँल्लोके सर्वं व्याप्य स तिष्ठति ॥४॥
transliteration: sarvataḥpāṇipādaśca sarvatokṣiśiromukhaḥ ,sarvataḥśrutimāँlloke sarvaṁ vyāpya sa tiṣṭhati.
english: He has hands and feet everywhere, and eyes, heads, and mouths everywhere. He has ears everywhere in the world; pervading all, he remains.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 40 Verse 4. He has hands and feet everywhere, and eyes, heads, and mouths everywhere. He has ears everywhere in the world; pervading all, he remains.

sanskrit: महाप्रभार्चिः पुरुषः सर्वस्य हृदि निश्रितः ।अणिमा लघिमा प्राप्तिरीशानो ज्योतिरव्ययः ॥५॥
transliteration: mahāprabhārciḥ puruṣaḥ sarvasya hṛdi niśritaḥ ,aṇimā laghimā prāptirīśāno jyotiravyayaḥ.
english: The supreme cosmic person (puruṣa), resplendent with great light, dwells within the heart of all beings. He embodies the powers of minuteness (aṇimā), lightness (laghimā), and attainment (prāpti), and is the ruler, the imperishable light.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 40 Verse 5. The supreme cosmic person (puruṣa), resplendent with great light, dwells within the heart of all beings. He embodies the powers of minuteness (aṇimā), lightness (laghimā), and attainment (prāpti), and is the ruler, the imperishable light.

sanskrit: तत्र बुद्धिमतां लोकाः संन्यासनिरताश्च ये ।ध्यानिनो नित्ययोगाश्च सत्यसंधा जितेन्द्रियाः ॥६॥
transliteration: tatra buddhimatāṁ lokāḥ saṁnyāsaniratāśca ye ,dhyānino nityayogāśca satyasaṁdhā jitendriyāḥ.
english: To that supreme state attain those intelligent individuals, those dedicated to renunciation (saṃnyāsa), constant in their meditative practice (dhyāna), ever-united in (yoga), committed to truth, and those who have controlled their senses.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 40 Verse 6. To that supreme state attain those intelligent individuals, those dedicated to renunciation (saṃnyāsa), constant in their meditative practice (dhyāna), ever-united in (yoga), committed to truth, and those who have controlled their senses.

sanskrit: ज्ञानवन्तश्च ये केचिदलुब्धा जितमन्यवः ।प्रसन्नमनसो धीरा निर्ममा निरहंकृताः ।विमुक्ताः सर्व एवैते महत्त्वमुपयान्ति वै ॥७॥
transliteration: jñānavantaśca ye kecidalubdhā jitamanyavaḥ ,prasannamanaso dhīrā nirmamā nirahaṁkṛtāḥ ,vimuktāḥ sarva evaite mahattvamupayānti vai.
english: And furthermore, those who possess true knowledge, who are free from greed and have conquered their anger, who have serene minds and are steadfast, free from possessiveness and from ego (ahaṃkāra) - all these liberated ones indeed attain the supreme state.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 40 Verse 7. And furthermore, those who possess true knowledge, who are free from greed and have conquered their anger, who have serene minds and are steadfast, free from possessiveness and from ego (ahaṃkāra) - all these liberated ones indeed attain the supreme state.

sanskrit: आत्मनो महतो वेद यः पुण्यां गतिमुत्तमाम् ।स धीरः सर्वलोकेषु न मोहमधिगच्छति ।विष्णुरेवादिसर्गेषु स्वयंभूर्भवति प्रभुः ॥८॥
transliteration: ātmano mahato veda yaḥ puṇyāṁ gatimuttamām ,sa dhīraḥ sarvalokeṣu na mohamadhigacchati ,viṣṇurevādisargeṣu svayaṁbhūrbhavati prabhuḥ.
english: That steadfast person who truly knows the sacred and supreme state of the great self (ātman) never experiences delusion in any world. Indeed, it is Viṣṇu Himself, the self-existent Lord, who manifests in all primal creations.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 40 Verse 8. That steadfast person who truly knows the sacred and supreme state of the great self (ātman) never experiences delusion in any world. Indeed, it is Viṣṇu Himself, the self-existent Lord, who manifests in all primal creations.

sanskrit: एवं हि यो वेद गुहाशयं प्रभुं नरः पुराणं पुरुषं विश्वरूपम् ।हिरण्मयं बुद्धिमतां परां गतिं स बुद्धिमान्बुद्धिमतीत्य तिष्ठति ॥९॥
transliteration: evaṁ hi yo veda guhāśayaṁ prabhuṁ; naraḥ purāṇaṁ puruṣaṁ viśvarūpam ,hiraṇmayaṁ buddhimatāṁ parāṁ gatiṁ; sa buddhimānbuddhimatītya tiṣṭhati.
english: Indeed, a person who thus knows the Lord residing in the cave (of the heart) - the ancient cosmic person (puruṣa) of universal form, who is luminous and the supreme destination for the intelligent - that wise person, transcending (his own) intellect, abides.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 40 Verse 9. Indeed, a person who thus knows the Lord residing in the cave (of the heart) - the ancient cosmic person (puruṣa) of universal form, who is luminous and the supreme destination for the intelligent - that wise person, transcending (his own) intellect, abides.

sanskrit: ब्रह्मोवाच ।य उत्पन्नो महान्पूर्वमहंकारः स उच्यते ।अहमित्येव संभूतो द्वितीयः सर्ग उच्यते ॥१॥
transliteration: brahmovāca ,ya utpanno mahānpūrvamahaṁkāraḥ sa ucyate ,ahamityeva saṁbhūto dvitīyaḥ sarga ucyate.
english: Brahmā said: That which was first produced as the great one is called ego (ahaṅkāra). That which originated merely as 'I' is called the second creation.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 41 Verse 1. Brahmā said: That which was first produced as the great one is called ego (ahaṅkāra). That which originated merely as 'I' is called the second creation.

sanskrit: अहंकारश्च भूतादिर्वैकारिक इति स्मृतः ।तेजसश्चेतना धातुः प्रजासर्गः प्रजापतिः ॥२॥
transliteration: ahaṁkāraśca bhūtādirvaikārika iti smṛtaḥ ,tejasaścetanā dhātuḥ prajāsargaḥ prajāpatiḥ.
english: And ego (ahaṅkāra) is considered the origin of the elements and is known as 'transformative' (vaikārika). From the luminous principle (tejas) comes the element of consciousness. Prajāpati is the creator of beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 41 Verse 2. And ego (ahaṅkāra) is considered the origin of the elements and is known as 'transformative' (vaikārika). From the luminous principle (tejas) comes the element of consciousness. Prajāpati is the creator of beings.

sanskrit: देवानां प्रभवो देवो मनसश्च त्रिलोककृत् ।अहमित्येव तत्सर्वमभिमन्ता स उच्यते ॥३॥
transliteration: devānāṁ prabhavo devo manasaśca trilokakṛt ,ahamityeva tatsarvamabhimantā sa ucyate.
english: The ego (ahaṅkāra) is the source of the gods, the divine (principle), and of the mind; it is the creator of the three worlds. All of that, expressing itself merely as 'I,' is called the conceiver (abhimantā).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 41 Verse 3. The ego (ahaṅkāra) is the source of the gods, the divine (principle), and of the mind; it is the creator of the three worlds. All of that, expressing itself merely as 'I,' is called the conceiver (abhimantā).

sanskrit: अध्यात्मज्ञाननित्यानां मुनीनां भावितात्मनाम् ।स्वाध्यायक्रतुसिद्धानामेष लोकः सनातनः ॥४॥
transliteration: adhyātmajñānanityānāṁ munīnāṁ bhāvitātmanām ,svādhyāyakratusiddhānāmeṣa lokaḥ sanātanaḥ.
english: This eternal realm belongs to the sages (muni) who are constantly devoted to spiritual knowledge, whose selves (ātman) are purified, and who have achieved perfection through self-study and Vedic rituals (kratu).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 41 Verse 4. This eternal realm belongs to the sages (muni) who are constantly devoted to spiritual knowledge, whose selves (ātman) are purified, and who have achieved perfection through self-study and Vedic rituals (kratu).

sanskrit: अहंकारेणाहरतो गुणानिमान्भूतादिरेवं सृजते स भूतकृत् ।वैकारिकः सर्वमिदं विचेष्टते स्वतेजसा रञ्जयते जगत्तथा ॥५॥
transliteration: ahaṁkāreṇāharato guṇānimā;nbhūtādirevaṁ sṛjate sa bhūtakṛt ,vaikārikaḥ sarvamidaṁ viceṣṭate; svatejasā rañjayate jagattathā.
english: The creator of beings (bhūtakṛt), seizing these qualities (guṇa) through the ego (ahaṅkāra), thus creates the primeval elements (bhūtādi). This ego (vaikārika), being subject to modification, causes all this to function and, by its own inherent power, illuminates the world.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 41 Verse 5. The creator of beings (bhūtakṛt), seizing these qualities (guṇa) through the ego (ahaṅkāra), thus creates the primeval elements (bhūtādi). This ego (vaikārika), being subject to modification, causes all this to function and, by its own inherent power, illuminates the world.

sanskrit: ब्रह्मोवाच ।अहंकारात्प्रसूतानि महाभूतानि पञ्च वै ।पृथिवी वायुराकाशमापो ज्योतिश्च पञ्चमम् ॥१॥
transliteration: brahmovāca ,ahaṁkārātprasūtāni mahābhūtāni pañca vai ,pṛthivī vāyurākāśamāpo jyotiśca pañcamam.
english: Brahmā (brahman) said: Indeed, the five great elements (mahābhūta) are born from ego (ahaṅkāra): earth, air, ether, water, and fire as the fifth.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 1. Brahmā (brahman) said: Indeed, the five great elements (mahābhūta) are born from ego (ahaṅkāra): earth, air, ether, water, and fire as the fifth.

sanskrit: तेषु भूतानि मुह्यन्ते महाभूतेषु पञ्चसु ।शब्दस्पर्शनरूपेषु रसगन्धक्रियासु च ॥२॥
transliteration: teṣu bhūtāni muhyante mahābhūteṣu pañcasu ,śabdasparśanarūpeṣu rasagandhakriyāsu ca.
english: Beings become deluded by these five great elements (mahābhūta), and by the qualities of sound, touch, form, taste, and smell, as well as by their inherent functions.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 2. Beings become deluded by these five great elements (mahābhūta), and by the qualities of sound, touch, form, taste, and smell, as well as by their inherent functions.

sanskrit: महाभूतविनाशान्ते प्रलये प्रत्युपस्थिते ।सर्वप्राणभृतां धीरा महदुत्पद्यते भयम् ॥३॥
transliteration: mahābhūtavināśānte pralaye pratyupasthite ,sarvaprāṇabhṛtāṁ dhīrā mahadutpadyate bhayam.
english: O wise ones, at the end of the destruction of the great elements (mahābhūta), when the cosmic dissolution (pralaya) is imminent, a great fear arises in all living beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 3. O wise ones, at the end of the destruction of the great elements (mahābhūta), when the cosmic dissolution (pralaya) is imminent, a great fear arises in all living beings.

sanskrit: यद्यस्माज्जायते भूतं तत्र तत्प्रविलीयते ।लीयन्ते प्रतिलोमानि जायन्ते चोत्तरोत्तरम् ॥४॥
transliteration: yadyasmājjāyate bhūtaṁ tatra tatpravilīyate ,līyante pratilomāni jāyante cottarottaram.
english: Whatever being (bhūta) is born from a particular source, into that very (source) it dissolves. The elements dissolve in a reverse order (pratiloma), and progressively new ones are born (uttarottara).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 4. Whatever being (bhūta) is born from a particular source, into that very (source) it dissolves. The elements dissolve in a reverse order (pratiloma), and progressively new ones are born (uttarottara).

sanskrit: ततः प्रलीने सर्वस्मिन्भूते स्थावरजङ्गमे ।स्मृतिमन्तस्तदा धीरा न लीयन्ते कदाचन ॥५॥
transliteration: tataḥ pralīne sarvasminbhūte sthāvarajaṅgame ,smṛtimantastadā dhīrā na līyante kadācana.
english: Then, when all beings (bhūta), both moving and stationary, have dissolved, the wise ones (dhīra) who possess (spiritual) memory (smṛti) never dissolve at any time.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 5. Then, when all beings (bhūta), both moving and stationary, have dissolved, the wise ones (dhīra) who possess (spiritual) memory (smṛti) never dissolve at any time.

sanskrit: शब्दः स्पर्शस्तथा रूपं रसो गन्धश्च पञ्चमः ।क्रियाकारणयुक्ताः स्युरनित्या मोहसंज्ञिताः ॥६॥
transliteration: śabdaḥ sparśastathā rūpaṁ raso gandhaśca pañcamaḥ ,kriyākāraṇayuktāḥ syuranityā mohasaṁjñitāḥ.
english: Sound (śabda), touch (sparśa), form (rūpa), taste (rasa), and fifth, smell (gandha) – these are endowed with action and cause. They are impermanent (anitya) and are designated as 'delusion' (mohasaṃjñita).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 6. Sound (śabda), touch (sparśa), form (rūpa), taste (rasa), and fifth, smell (gandha) – these are endowed with action and cause. They are impermanent (anitya) and are designated as 'delusion' (mohasaṃjñita).

sanskrit: लोभप्रजनसंयुक्ता निर्विशेषा ह्यकिंचनाः ।मांसशोणितसंघाता अन्योन्यस्योपजीविनः ॥७॥
transliteration: lobhaprajanasaṁyuktā nirviśeṣā hyakiṁcanāḥ ,māṁsaśoṇitasaṁghātā anyonyasyopajīvinaḥ.
english: They are marked by greed and the urge to procreate, without distinction, and indeed possessing nothing. They are mere aggregates of flesh and blood, dependent on one another for sustenance.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 7. They are marked by greed and the urge to procreate, without distinction, and indeed possessing nothing. They are mere aggregates of flesh and blood, dependent on one another for sustenance.

sanskrit: बहिरात्मान इत्येते दीनाः कृपणवृत्तयः ।प्राणापानावुदानश्च समानो व्यान एव च ॥८॥
transliteration: bahirātmāna ityete dīnāḥ kṛpaṇavṛttayaḥ ,prāṇāpānāvudānaśca samāno vyāna eva ca.
english: These are considered the outer selves (ātman), miserable and of wretched behavior. Prāṇa, apāna, udāna, samāna, and vyāna are also (the vital airs).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 8. These are considered the outer selves (ātman), miserable and of wretched behavior. Prāṇa, apāna, udāna, samāna, and vyāna are also (the vital airs).

sanskrit: अन्तरात्मेति चाप्येते नियताः पञ्च वायवः ।वाङ्मनोबुद्धिरित्येभिः सार्धमष्टात्मकं जगत् ॥९॥
transliteration: antarātmeti cāpyete niyatāḥ pañca vāyavaḥ ,vāṅmanobuddhirityebhiḥ sārdhamaṣṭātmakaṁ jagat.
english: And these five regulated vital airs are also called the inner self (ātman). Along with these (five vital airs) and speech, mind, and intellect, the world is eight-fold in nature.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 9. And these five regulated vital airs are also called the inner self (ātman). Along with these (five vital airs) and speech, mind, and intellect, the world is eight-fold in nature.

sanskrit: त्वग्घ्राणश्रोत्रचक्षूंषि रसनं वाक्च संयता ।विशुद्धं च मनो यस्य बुद्धिश्चाव्यभिचारिणी ॥१०॥
transliteration: tvagghrāṇaśrotracakṣūṁṣi rasanaṁ vākca saṁyatā ,viśuddhaṁ ca mano yasya buddhiścāvyabhicāriṇī.
english: Whose senses of touch (skin), smell (nose), hearing (ears), sight (eyes), taste (tongue), and speech are restrained; and whose mind is pure, and intellect unwavering.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 10. Whose senses of touch (skin), smell (nose), hearing (ears), sight (eyes), taste (tongue), and speech are restrained; and whose mind is pure, and intellect unwavering.

sanskrit: अष्टौ यस्याग्नयो ह्येते न दहन्ते मनः सदा ।स तद्ब्रह्म शुभं याति यस्माद्भूयो न विद्यते ॥११॥
transliteration: aṣṭau yasyāgnayo hyete na dahante manaḥ sadā ,sa tadbrahma śubhaṁ yāti yasmādbhūyo na vidyate.
english: He whose mind is not perpetually afflicted by these eight fires attains that auspicious absolute (brahman), beyond which nothing further exists.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 11. He whose mind is not perpetually afflicted by these eight fires attains that auspicious absolute (brahman), beyond which nothing further exists.

sanskrit: एकादश च यान्याहुरिन्द्रियाणि विशेषतः ।अहंकारप्रसूतानि तानि वक्ष्याम्यहं द्विजाः ॥१२॥
transliteration: ekādaśa ca yānyāhurindriyāṇi viśeṣataḥ ,ahaṁkāraprasūtāni tāni vakṣyāmyahaṁ dvijāḥ.
english: O twice-born ones, I shall now explain those eleven sense organs which are especially said to be born from ego (ahaṃkāra).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 12. O twice-born ones, I shall now explain those eleven sense organs which are especially said to be born from ego (ahaṃkāra).

sanskrit: श्रोत्रं त्वक्चक्षुषी जिह्वा नासिका चैव पञ्चमी ।पादौ पायुरुपस्थं च हस्तौ वाग्दशमी भवेत् ॥१३॥
transliteration: śrotraṁ tvakcakṣuṣī jihvā nāsikā caiva pañcamī ,pādau pāyurupasthaṁ ca hastau vāgdaśamī bhavet.
english: The ear, skin, eyes, tongue, and the nose as the fifth; the feet, anus, genitals, and hands, with speech being the tenth.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 13. The ear, skin, eyes, tongue, and the nose as the fifth; the feet, anus, genitals, and hands, with speech being the tenth.

sanskrit: इन्द्रियग्राम इत्येष मन एकादशं भवेत् ।एतं ग्रामं जयेत्पूर्वं ततो ब्रह्म प्रकाशते ॥१४॥
transliteration: indriyagrāma ityeṣa mana ekādaśaṁ bhavet ,etaṁ grāmaṁ jayetpūrvaṁ tato brahma prakāśate.
english: This is the group of senses; the mind would be the eleventh. One should first conquer this group, then the absolute (brahman) shines forth.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 14. This is the group of senses; the mind would be the eleventh. One should first conquer this group, then the absolute (brahman) shines forth.

sanskrit: बुद्धीन्द्रियाणि पञ्चाहुः पञ्च कर्मेन्द्रियाणि च ।श्रोत्रादीन्यपि पञ्चाहुर्बुद्धियुक्तानि तत्त्वतः ॥१५॥
transliteration: buddhīndriyāṇi pañcāhuḥ pañca karmendriyāṇi ca ,śrotrādīnyapi pañcāhurbuddhiyuktāni tattvataḥ.
english: They declare that there are five cognitive organs and five organs of action. Likewise, they state that the five (sense organs) beginning with the ear are essentially endowed with intelligence.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 15. They declare that there are five cognitive organs and five organs of action. Likewise, they state that the five (sense organs) beginning with the ear are essentially endowed with intelligence.

sanskrit: अविशेषाणि चान्यानि कर्मयुक्तानि तानि तु ।उभयत्र मनो ज्ञेयं बुद्धिर्द्वादशमी भवेत् ॥१६॥
transliteration: aviśeṣāṇi cānyāni karmayuktāni tāni tu ,ubhayatra mano jñeyaṁ buddhirdvādaśamī bhavet.
english: And other things, the undifferentiated (subtle elements), are indeed associated with action. The mind (manas) should be known as belonging to both (cognitive and action organs), and the intellect (buddhi) would be the twelfth.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 16. And other things, the undifferentiated (subtle elements), are indeed associated with action. The mind (manas) should be known as belonging to both (cognitive and action organs), and the intellect (buddhi) would be the twelfth.

sanskrit: इत्युक्तानीन्द्रियाणीमान्येकादश मया क्रमात् ।मन्यन्ते कृतमित्येव विदित्वैतानि पण्डिताः ॥१७॥
transliteration: ityuktānīndriyāṇīmānyekādaśa mayā kramāt ,manyante kṛtamityeva viditvaitāni paṇḍitāḥ.
english: Thus, these eleven organs have been enumerated by me in order. The learned, having understood these, truly consider (their purpose) fulfilled.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 17. Thus, these eleven organs have been enumerated by me in order. The learned, having understood these, truly consider (their purpose) fulfilled.

sanskrit: त्रीणि स्थानानि भूतानां चतुर्थं नोपपद्यते ।स्थलमापस्तथाकाशं जन्म चापि चतुर्विधम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: trīṇi sthānāni bhūtānāṁ caturthaṁ nopapadyate ,sthalamāpastathākāśaṁ janma cāpi caturvidham.
english: There are three abodes for living beings; a fourth is not possible: (those of) land, water, and similarly, air (or sky). And birth is also of four kinds.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 18. There are three abodes for living beings; a fourth is not possible: (those of) land, water, and similarly, air (or sky). And birth is also of four kinds.

sanskrit: अण्डजोद्भिज्जसंस्वेदजरायुजमथापि च ।चतुर्धा जन्म इत्येतद्भूतग्रामस्य लक्ष्यते ॥१९॥
transliteration: aṇḍajodbhijjasaṁsvedajarāyujamathāpi ca ,caturdhā janma ityetadbhūtagrāmasya lakṣyate.
english: Furthermore, these four types of birth are observed for the multitude of beings: those born from eggs, from sprouts, from moisture, and from the womb.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 19. Furthermore, these four types of birth are observed for the multitude of beings: those born from eggs, from sprouts, from moisture, and from the womb.

sanskrit: अचराण्यपि भूतानि खेचराणि तथैव च ।अण्डजानि विजानीयात्सर्वांश्चैव सरीसृपान् ॥२०॥
transliteration: acarāṇyapi bhūtāni khecarāṇi tathaiva ca ,aṇḍajāni vijānīyātsarvāṁścaiva sarīsṛpān.
english: One should understand that immobile beings, as well as those that move in the sky, and indeed all reptiles, are considered to be born from eggs (aṇḍaja).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 20. One should understand that immobile beings, as well as those that move in the sky, and indeed all reptiles, are considered to be born from eggs (aṇḍaja).

sanskrit: संस्वेदाः कृमयः प्रोक्ता जन्तवश्च तथाविधाः ।जन्म द्वितीयमित्येतज्जघन्यतरमुच्यते ॥२१॥
transliteration: saṁsvedāḥ kṛmayaḥ proktā jantavaśca tathāvidhāḥ ,janma dvitīyamityetajjaghanyataramucyate.
english: The moisture-born (saṃsveda) are declared to be worms and similar creatures. This is said to be the second, more inferior birth.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 21. The moisture-born (saṃsveda) are declared to be worms and similar creatures. This is said to be the second, more inferior birth.

sanskrit: भित्त्वा तु पृथिवीं यानि जायन्ते कालपर्ययात् ।उद्भिज्जानीति तान्याहुर्भूतानि द्विजसत्तमाः ॥२२॥
transliteration: bhittvā tu pṛthivīṁ yāni jāyante kālaparyayāt ,udbhijjānīti tānyāhurbhūtāni dvijasattamāḥ.
english: O best among the twice-born (dvija)! Those beings which emerge by splitting the earth over time are called sprout-born (udbhijja).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 22. O best among the twice-born (dvija)! Those beings which emerge by splitting the earth over time are called sprout-born (udbhijja).

sanskrit: द्विपादबहुपादानि तिर्यग्गतिमतीनि च ।जरायुजानि भूतानि वित्त तान्यपि सत्तमाः ॥२३॥
transliteration: dvipādabahupādāni tiryaggatimatīni ca ,jarāyujāni bhūtāni vitta tānyapi sattamāḥ.
english: O excellent people, know also these beings: the two-footed, the many-footed, the terrestrial creatures, and those born from a womb.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 23. O excellent people, know also these beings: the two-footed, the many-footed, the terrestrial creatures, and those born from a womb.

sanskrit: द्विविधापीह विज्ञेया ब्रह्मयोनिः सनातना ।तपः कर्म च यत्पुण्यमित्येष विदुषां नयः ॥२४॥
transliteration: dvividhāpīha vijñeyā brahmayoniḥ sanātanā ,tapaḥ karma ca yatpuṇyamityeṣa viduṣāṁ nayaḥ.
english: Here, the eternal source of (brahman) is indeed to be known as twofold: namely, austerity (tapas) and meritorious action (karma). This is the principle of the wise.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 24. Here, the eternal source of (brahman) is indeed to be known as twofold: namely, austerity (tapas) and meritorious action (karma). This is the principle of the wise.

sanskrit: द्विविधं कर्म विज्ञेयमिज्या दानं च यन्मखे ।जातस्याध्ययनं पुण्यमिति वृद्धानुशासनम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: dvividhaṁ karma vijñeyamijyā dānaṁ ca yanmakhe ,jātasyādhyayanaṁ puṇyamiti vṛddhānuśāsanam.
english: Action (karma) is to be understood as twofold: first, the (Vedic ritual) and giving that occurs in a (Vedic ritual); and second, study, which is meritorious for the twice-born. This is the teaching of the elders.

sanskrit: एतद्यो वेद विधिवत्स मुक्तः स्याद्द्विजर्षभाः ।विमुक्तः सर्वपापेभ्य इति चैव निबोधत ॥२६॥
transliteration: etadyo veda vidhivatsa muktaḥ syāddvijarṣabhāḥ ,vimuktaḥ sarvapāpebhya iti caiva nibodhata.
english: O best among the twice-born (dvija)s, whoever knows this properly would attain liberation (mokṣa). And indeed, understand that he is completely freed from all sins.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 26. O best among the twice-born (dvija)s, whoever knows this properly would attain liberation (mokṣa). And indeed, understand that he is completely freed from all sins.

sanskrit: आकाशं प्रथमं भूतं श्रोत्रमध्यात्ममुच्यते ।अधिभूतं तथा शब्दो दिशस्तत्राधिदैवतम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: ākāśaṁ prathamaṁ bhūtaṁ śrotramadhyātmamucyate ,adhibhūtaṁ tathā śabdo diśastatrādhidaivatam.
english: Space (ākāśa) is declared to be the first element. The ear is called its inner faculty (adhyātma); likewise, sound is its gross object (adhibhūta), and the directions are its presiding divinity (adhidaivata).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 27. Space (ākāśa) is declared to be the first element. The ear is called its inner faculty (adhyātma); likewise, sound is its gross object (adhibhūta), and the directions are its presiding divinity (adhidaivata).

sanskrit: द्वितीयं मारुतो भूतं त्वगध्यात्मं च विश्रुतम् ।स्प्रष्टव्यमधिभूतं च विद्युत्तत्राधिदैवतम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: dvitīyaṁ māruto bhūtaṁ tvagadhyātmaṁ ca viśrutam ,spraṣṭavyamadhibhūtaṁ ca vidyuttatrādhidaivatam.
english: Air (maruta) is known as the second element. The skin is celebrated as its inner faculty (adhyātma), and the tangible object (spraṣṭavya) is its gross object (adhibhūta). Lightning (vidyut) is its presiding deity (adhidaivata).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 28. Air (maruta) is known as the second element. The skin is celebrated as its inner faculty (adhyātma), and the tangible object (spraṣṭavya) is its gross object (adhibhūta). Lightning (vidyut) is its presiding deity (adhidaivata).

sanskrit: तृतीयं ज्योतिरित्याहुश्चक्षुरध्यात्ममुच्यते ।अधिभूतं ततो रूपं सूर्यस्तत्राधिदैवतम् ॥२९॥
transliteration: tṛtīyaṁ jyotirityāhuścakṣuradhyātmamucyate ,adhibhūtaṁ tato rūpaṁ sūryastatrādhidaivatam.
english: They call light (jyotiḥ) the third (element). The eye is said to be its inner faculty (adhyātma); then form (rūpa) is its gross object (adhibhūta), and the sun (sūrya) is its presiding deity (adhidaivata).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 29. They call light (jyotiḥ) the third (element). The eye is said to be its inner faculty (adhyātma); then form (rūpa) is its gross object (adhibhūta), and the sun (sūrya) is its presiding deity (adhidaivata).

sanskrit: चतुर्थमापो विज्ञेयं जिह्वा चाध्यात्ममिष्यते ।अधिभूतं रसश्चात्र सोमस्तत्राधिदैवतम् ॥३०॥
transliteration: caturthamāpo vijñeyaṁ jihvā cādhyātmamiṣyate ,adhibhūtaṁ rasaścātra somastatrādhidaivatam.
english: Water (āpaḥ) should be known as the fourth (element). The tongue (jihvā) is considered its inner faculty (adhyātma), and taste (rasa) is its gross object (adhibhūta) here. Soma (the moon) is its presiding deity (adhidaivata).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 30. Water (āpaḥ) should be known as the fourth (element). The tongue (jihvā) is considered its inner faculty (adhyātma), and taste (rasa) is its gross object (adhibhūta) here. Soma (the moon) is its presiding deity (adhidaivata).

sanskrit: पृथिवी पञ्चमं भूतं घ्राणश्चाध्यात्ममिष्यते ।अधिभूतं तथा गन्धो वायुस्तत्राधिदैवतम् ॥३१॥
transliteration: pṛthivī pañcamaṁ bhūtaṁ ghrāṇaścādhyātmamiṣyate ,adhibhūtaṁ tathā gandho vāyustatrādhidaivatam.
english: The earth is considered the fifth element. The nose is regarded as its individual (adhyātma) aspect. Similarly, odor is its elemental (adhibhūta) aspect, and Vāyu is its divine (adhidaivata) aspect.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 31. The earth is considered the fifth element. The nose is regarded as its individual (adhyātma) aspect. Similarly, odor is its elemental (adhibhūta) aspect, and Vāyu is its divine (adhidaivata) aspect.

sanskrit: एष पञ्चसु भूतेषु चतुष्टयविधिः स्मृतः ।अतः परं प्रवक्ष्यामि सर्वं त्रिविधमिन्द्रियम् ॥३२॥
transliteration: eṣa pañcasu bhūteṣu catuṣṭayavidhiḥ smṛtaḥ ,ataḥ paraṁ pravakṣyāmi sarvaṁ trividhamindriyam.
english: This fourfold principle is declared concerning the five elements. Henceforth, I will explain all the threefold sense organs.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 32. This fourfold principle is declared concerning the five elements. Henceforth, I will explain all the threefold sense organs.

sanskrit: पादावध्यात्ममित्याहुर्ब्राह्मणास्तत्त्वदर्शिनः ।अधिभूतं तु गन्तव्यं विष्णुस्तत्राधिदैवतम् ॥३३॥
transliteration: pādāvadhyātmamityāhurbrāhmaṇāstattvadarśinaḥ ,adhibhūtaṁ tu gantavyaṁ viṣṇustatrādhidaivatam.
english: The brahmins, who are seers of reality, declare the feet to be the individual (adhyātma) aspect. The destination is indeed the elemental (adhibhūta) aspect, and Vishnu is the divine (adhidaivata) aspect in this context.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 33. The brahmins, who are seers of reality, declare the feet to be the individual (adhyātma) aspect. The destination is indeed the elemental (adhibhūta) aspect, and Vishnu is the divine (adhidaivata) aspect in this context.

sanskrit: अवाग्गतिरपानश्च पायुरध्यात्ममिष्यते ।अधिभूतं विसर्गश्च मित्रस्तत्राधिदैवतम् ॥३४॥
transliteration: avāggatirapānaśca pāyuradhyātmamiṣyate ,adhibhūtaṁ visargaśca mitrastatrādhidaivatam.
english: The anus is regarded as the individual (adhyātma) aspect, associated with downward movement and the vital air (apāna) that governs elimination. Excretion is its elemental (adhibhūta) aspect, and Mitra is its divine (adhidaivata) aspect in this context.

sanskrit: प्रजनः सर्वभूतानामुपस्थोऽध्यात्ममुच्यते ।अधिभूतं तथा शुक्रं दैवतं च प्रजापतिः ॥३५॥
transliteration: prajanaḥ sarvabhūtānāmupastho'dhyātmamucyate ,adhibhūtaṁ tathā śukraṁ daivataṁ ca prajāpatiḥ.
english: The generative organ, which facilitates the procreation of all beings, is called the internal faculty (adhyātma). Similarly, semen is its material aspect (adhibhūtam), and Prajāpati (prajāpati) is its presiding deity.

sanskrit: हस्तावध्यात्ममित्याहुरध्यात्मविदुषो जनाः ।अधिभूतं तु कर्माणि शक्रस्तत्राधिदैवतम् ॥३६॥
transliteration: hastāvadhyātmamityāhuradhyātmaviduṣo janāḥ ,adhibhūtaṁ tu karmāṇi śakrastatrādhidaivatam.
english: Those who understand the spiritual realm say that the two hands are the internal faculty (adhyātma). Its material aspect (adhibhūtam), however, consists of actions, and Śakra is its presiding deity.

sanskrit: वैश्वदेवी मनःपूर्वा वागध्यात्ममिहोच्यते ।वक्तव्यमधिभूतं च वह्निस्तत्राधिदैवतम् ॥३७॥
transliteration: vaiśvadevī manaḥpūrvā vāgadhyātmamihocyate ,vaktavyamadhibhūtaṁ ca vahnistatrādhidaivatam.
english: Here, speech (vāk), which is universal and mind-preceded, is called the internal faculty (adhyātma). That which is to be spoken is its material aspect (adhibhūtam), and Vahni is its presiding deity.

sanskrit: अध्यात्मं मन इत्याहुः पञ्चभूतानुचारकम् ।अधिभूतं च मन्तव्यं चन्द्रमाश्चाधिदैवतम् ॥३८॥
transliteration: adhyātmaṁ mana ityāhuḥ pañcabhūtānucārakam ,adhibhūtaṁ ca mantavyaṁ candramāścādhidaivatam.
english: They declare that the mind, which operates among the five elements, is the internal faculty (adhyātma). Furthermore, the object of thought is considered its material aspect (adhibhūtam), and Candramās is its presiding deity.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 38. They declare that the mind, which operates among the five elements, is the internal faculty (adhyātma). Furthermore, the object of thought is considered its material aspect (adhibhūtam), and Candramās is its presiding deity.

sanskrit: अध्यात्मं बुद्धिरित्याहुः षडिन्द्रियविचारिणी ।अधिभूतं तु विज्ञेयं ब्रह्मा तत्राधिदैवतम् ॥३९॥
transliteration: adhyātmaṁ buddhirityāhuḥ ṣaḍindriyavicāriṇī ,adhibhūtaṁ tu vijñeyaṁ brahmā tatrādhidaivatam.
english: They say that the intellect (buddhi), which contemplates the six senses, is considered the spiritual (adhyātmam) aspect. Material existence (adhibhūtam), on the other hand, should be understood, with Brahmā as its presiding deity (adhidaivatam).

sanskrit: यथावदध्यात्मविधिरेष वः कीर्तितो मया ।ज्ञानमस्य हि धर्मज्ञाः प्राप्तं बुद्धिमतामिह ॥४०॥
transliteration: yathāvadadhyātmavidhireṣa vaḥ kīrtito mayā ,jñānamasya hi dharmajñāḥ prāptaṁ buddhimatāmiha.
english: This spiritual method (adhyātma-vidhi) has been declared by me to you as it should be. Indeed, O knowers of natural law (dharma), the knowledge of this is attained here by the intelligent.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 40. This spiritual method (adhyātma-vidhi) has been declared by me to you as it should be. Indeed, O knowers of natural law (dharma), the knowledge of this is attained here by the intelligent.

sanskrit: इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थाश्च महाभूतानि पञ्च च ।सर्वाण्येतानि संधाय मनसा संप्रधारयेत् ॥४१॥
transliteration: indriyāṇīndriyārthāśca mahābhūtāni pañca ca ,sarvāṇyetāni saṁdhāya manasā saṁpradhārayet.
english: One should firmly grasp all these - the sense organs, their objects, and the five great elements - by means of the mind, having brought them together.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 41. One should firmly grasp all these - the sense organs, their objects, and the five great elements - by means of the mind, having brought them together.

sanskrit: क्षीणे मनसि सर्वस्मिन्न जन्मसुखमिष्यते ।ज्ञानसंपन्नसत्त्वानां तत्सुखं विदुषां मतम् ॥४२॥
transliteration: kṣīṇe manasi sarvasminna janmasukhamiṣyate ,jñānasaṁpannasattvānāṁ tatsukhaṁ viduṣāṁ matam.
english: When the entire mind is completely attenuated, the happiness of repeated existence is no longer desired. That happiness is regarded as the view of the wise, for beings endowed with knowledge.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 42. When the entire mind is completely attenuated, the happiness of repeated existence is no longer desired. That happiness is regarded as the view of the wise, for beings endowed with knowledge.

sanskrit: अतः परं प्रवक्ष्यामि सूक्ष्मभावकरीं शिवाम् ।निवृत्तिं सर्वभूतेषु मृदुना दारुणेन वा ॥४३॥
transliteration: ataḥ paraṁ pravakṣyāmi sūkṣmabhāvakarīṁ śivām ,nivṛttiṁ sarvabhūteṣu mṛdunā dāruṇena vā.
english: Hereafter, I will explain the auspicious (śivā) cessation (nivṛtti) that brings about subtle states, which applies to all beings, whether attained through gentle or harsh means.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 43. Hereafter, I will explain the auspicious (śivā) cessation (nivṛtti) that brings about subtle states, which applies to all beings, whether attained through gentle or harsh means.

sanskrit: गुणागुणमनासङ्गमेकचर्यमनन्तरम् ।एतद्ब्राह्मणतो वृत्तमाहुरेकपदं सुखम् ॥४४॥
transliteration: guṇāguṇamanāsaṅgamekacaryamanantaram ,etadbrāhmaṇato vṛttamāhurekapadaṁ sukham.
english: They declare that this conduct (vṛtta) - which is indifferent to merits and faults, free from attachment, solitary in its practice, and unceasing - is the path of a brahmin (brāhmaṇataḥ) and the sole source of happiness.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 44. They declare that this conduct (vṛtta) - which is indifferent to merits and faults, free from attachment, solitary in its practice, and unceasing - is the path of a brahmin (brāhmaṇataḥ) and the sole source of happiness.

sanskrit: विद्वान्कूर्म इवाङ्गानि कामान्संहृत्य सर्वशः ।विरजाः सर्वतो मुक्तो यो नरः स सुखी सदा ॥४५॥
transliteration: vidvānkūrma ivāṅgāni kāmānsaṁhṛtya sarvaśaḥ ,virajāḥ sarvato mukto yo naraḥ sa sukhī sadā.
english: That person who, like a tortoise withdrawing its limbs, completely retracts all desires, and who is pure (viraja) and entirely liberated, that person is always truly happy.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 45. That person who, like a tortoise withdrawing its limbs, completely retracts all desires, and who is pure (viraja) and entirely liberated, that person is always truly happy.

sanskrit: कामानात्मनि संयम्य क्षीणतृष्णः समाहितः ।सर्वभूतसुहृन्मैत्रो ब्रह्मभूयं स गच्छति ॥४६॥
