transliteration: kāmānātmani saṁyamya kṣīṇatṛṣṇaḥ samāhitaḥ ,sarvabhūtasuhṛnmaitro brahmabhūyaṁ sa gacchati.
english: He who, having restrained desires (kāma) within his self (ātman), whose craving is extinguished, who is perfectly composed, and who is a friend and well-wisher to all beings, that person attains the state of (brahman).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 46. He who, having restrained desires (kāma) within his self (ātman), whose craving is extinguished, who is perfectly composed, and who is a friend and well-wisher to all beings, that person attains the state of (brahman).

sanskrit: इन्द्रियाणां निरोधेन सर्वेषां विषयैषिणाम् ।मुनेर्जनपदत्यागादध्यात्माग्निः समिध्यते ॥४७॥
transliteration: indriyāṇāṁ nirodhena sarveṣāṁ viṣayaiṣiṇām ,munerjanapadatyāgādadhyātmāgniḥ samidhyate.
english: The spiritual fire (adhyātmāgni) of a sage (muni) is kindled by the restraint of all senses that crave sense objects, and by the abandonment of inhabited places.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 47. The spiritual fire (adhyātmāgni) of a sage (muni) is kindled by the restraint of all senses that crave sense objects, and by the abandonment of inhabited places.

sanskrit: यथाग्निरिन्धनैरिद्धो महाज्योतिः प्रकाशते ।तथेन्द्रियनिरोधेन महानात्मा प्रकाशते ॥४८॥
transliteration: yathāgnirindhanairiddho mahājyotiḥ prakāśate ,tathendriyanirodhena mahānātmā prakāśate.
english: Just as fire, when kindled by fuel, shines forth as a great light, so too, by the restraint of the senses, the great self (ātman) shines forth.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 48. Just as fire, when kindled by fuel, shines forth as a great light, so too, by the restraint of the senses, the great self (ātman) shines forth.

sanskrit: यदा पश्यति भूतानि प्रसन्नात्मात्मनो हृदि ।स्वयंयोनिस्तदा सूक्ष्मात्सूक्ष्ममाप्नोत्यनुत्तमम् ॥४९॥
transliteration: yadā paśyati bhūtāni prasannātmātmano hṛdi ,svayaṁyonistadā sūkṣmātsūkṣmamāpnotyanuttamam.
english: When one, whose self (ātman) is serene, perceives all beings within one's own heart, then that self-existent (svayaṃyoni) being attains the subtle, the unsurpassed.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 49. When one, whose self (ātman) is serene, perceives all beings within one's own heart, then that self-existent (svayaṃyoni) being attains the subtle, the unsurpassed.

sanskrit: अग्नी रूपं पयः स्रोतो वायुः स्पर्शनमेव च ।मही पङ्कधरं घोरमाकाशं श्रवणं तथा ॥५०॥
transliteration: agnī rūpaṁ payaḥ sroto vāyuḥ sparśanameva ca ,mahī paṅkadharaṁ ghoramākāśaṁ śravaṇaṁ tathā.
english: For fire, there is form; for water, there is flow; for air, there is touch; and indeed, for earth, there is the dreadful mud-bearing quality; and similarly, for space, there is hearing.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 50. For fire, there is form; for water, there is flow; for air, there is touch; and indeed, for earth, there is the dreadful mud-bearing quality; and similarly, for space, there is hearing.

sanskrit: रागशोकसमाविष्टं पञ्चस्रोतःसमावृतम् ।पञ्चभूतसमायुक्तं नवद्वारं द्विदैवतम् ॥५१॥
transliteration: rāgaśokasamāviṣṭaṁ pañcasrotaḥsamāvṛtam ,pañcabhūtasamāyuktaṁ navadvāraṁ dvidaivatam.
english: It (the body) is pervaded by attachment and sorrow, encompassed by the five streams (of sensory perception), composed of the five great elements, possessing nine gates, and governed by two principles.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 51. It (the body) is pervaded by attachment and sorrow, encompassed by the five streams (of sensory perception), composed of the five great elements, possessing nine gates, and governed by two principles.

sanskrit: रजस्वलमथादृश्यं त्रिगुणं च त्रिधातुकम् ।संसर्गाभिरतं मूढं शरीरमिति धारणा ॥५२॥
transliteration: rajasvalamathādṛśyaṁ triguṇaṁ ca tridhātukam ,saṁsargābhirataṁ mūḍhaṁ śarīramiti dhāraṇā.
english: This is the conception (dhāraṇā) of the body (śarīra): it is characterized by activity (rajas), imperceptible (in its subtle form), composed of the three qualities (guṇas), having three humors (doṣas), delighting in worldly contact, and deluded.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 52. This is the conception (dhāraṇā) of the body (śarīra): it is characterized by activity (rajas), imperceptible (in its subtle form), composed of the three qualities (guṇas), having three humors (doṣas), delighting in worldly contact, and deluded.

sanskrit: दुश्चरं जीवलोकेऽस्मिन्सत्त्वं प्रति समाश्रितम् ।एतदेव हि लोकेऽस्मिन्कालचक्रं प्रवर्तते ॥५३॥
transliteration: duścaraṁ jīvaloke'sminsattvaṁ prati samāśritam ,etadeva hi loke'sminkālacakraṁ pravartate.
english: This (embodied existence) is difficult to navigate in this world of living beings; it relies on individual beings (sattva). Indeed, it is this very (existence) in this world that causes the wheel of time (kālacakra) to revolve.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 53. This (embodied existence) is difficult to navigate in this world of living beings; it relies on individual beings (sattva). Indeed, it is this very (existence) in this world that causes the wheel of time (kālacakra) to revolve.

sanskrit: एतन्महार्णवं घोरमगाधं मोहसंज्ञितम् ।विसृजेत्संक्षिपेच्चैव बोधयेत्सामरं जगत् ॥५४॥
transliteration: etanmahārṇavaṁ ghoramagādhaṁ mohasaṁjñitam ,visṛjetsaṁkṣipeccaiva bodhayetsāmaraṁ jagat.
english: This (existence) is a vast, terrifying, unfathomable ocean, known as delusion (moha). Therefore, one should relinquish (attachment to it) and indeed, one should withdraw (from its illusions), and one should awaken the entire world (jagat), including its divine beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 54. This (existence) is a vast, terrifying, unfathomable ocean, known as delusion (moha). Therefore, one should relinquish (attachment to it) and indeed, one should withdraw (from its illusions), and one should awaken the entire world (jagat), including its divine beings.

sanskrit: कामक्रोधौ भयं मोहमभिद्रोहमथानृतम् ।इन्द्रियाणां निरोधेन स तांस्त्यजति दुस्त्यजान् ॥५५॥
transliteration: kāmakrodhau bhayaṁ mohamabhidrohamathānṛtam ,indriyāṇāṁ nirodhena sa tāṁstyajati dustyajān.
english: By restraining the senses, such a person abandons these difficult-to-relinquish qualities: desire (kāma), anger (krodha), fear, delusion, malice, and untruth.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 55. By restraining the senses, such a person abandons these difficult-to-relinquish qualities: desire (kāma), anger (krodha), fear, delusion, malice, and untruth.

sanskrit: यस्यैते निर्जिता लोके त्रिगुणाः पञ्च धातवः ।व्योम्नि तस्य परं स्थानमनन्तमथ लक्ष्यते ॥५६॥
transliteration: yasyaite nirjitā loke triguṇāḥ pañca dhātavaḥ ,vyomni tasya paraṁ sthānamanantamatha lakṣyate.
english: For one by whom these three qualities (guṇas) and five basic elements (dhātus) are conquered in the world, his supreme, infinite abode is then perceived in the ether (vyoma).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 56. For one by whom these three qualities (guṇas) and five basic elements (dhātus) are conquered in the world, his supreme, infinite abode is then perceived in the ether (vyoma).

sanskrit: कामकूलामपारान्तां मनःस्रोतोभयावहाम् ।नदीं दुर्गह्रदां तीर्णः कामक्रोधावुभौ जयेत् ॥५७॥
transliteration: kāmakūlāmapārāntāṁ manaḥsrotobhayāvahām ,nadīṁ durgahradāṁ tīrṇaḥ kāmakrodhāvubhau jayet.
english: Having successfully crossed this river - one characterized by desire (kāma) as its banks, being boundless, generating fear through its mental currents, and possessing dangerous whirlpools - one should then conquer both desire (kāma) and anger (krodha).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 57. Having successfully crossed this river - one characterized by desire (kāma) as its banks, being boundless, generating fear through its mental currents, and possessing dangerous whirlpools - one should then conquer both desire (kāma) and anger (krodha).

sanskrit: स सर्वदोषनिर्मुक्तस्ततः पश्यति यत्परम् ।मनो मनसि संधाय पश्यत्यात्मानमात्मनि ॥५८॥
transliteration: sa sarvadoṣanirmuktastataḥ paśyati yatparam ,mano manasi saṁdhāya paśyatyātmānamātmani.
english: Liberated from all faults, he then perceives that which is supreme. Having concentrated his mind (manas) within the self, he perceives the true self (ātman) within the supreme Self (ātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 58. Liberated from all faults, he then perceives that which is supreme. Having concentrated his mind (manas) within the self, he perceives the true self (ātman) within the supreme Self (ātman).

sanskrit: सर्ववित्सर्वभूतेषु वीक्षत्यात्मानमात्मनि ।एकधा बहुधा चैव विकुर्वाणस्ततस्ततः ॥५९॥
transliteration: sarvavitsarvabhūteṣu vīkṣatyātmānamātmani ,ekadhā bahudhā caiva vikurvāṇastatastataḥ.
english: The all-knower perceives the self (ātman) within the self (ātman) in all beings, manifesting both as one and in many forms, everywhere.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 59. The all-knower perceives the self (ātman) within the self (ātman) in all beings, manifesting both as one and in many forms, everywhere.

sanskrit: ध्रुवं पश्यति रूपाणि दीपाद्दीपशतं यथा ।स वै विष्णुश्च मित्रश्च वरुणोऽग्निः प्रजापतिः ॥६०॥
transliteration: dhruvaṁ paśyati rūpāṇi dīpāddīpaśataṁ yathā ,sa vai viṣṇuśca mitraśca varuṇo'gniḥ prajāpatiḥ.
english: He constantly perceives forms, just as a hundred lamps might be lit from a single lamp. Indeed, he is Viṣṇu, Mitra, Varuṇa, Agni, and Prajāpati.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 60. He constantly perceives forms, just as a hundred lamps might be lit from a single lamp. Indeed, he is Viṣṇu, Mitra, Varuṇa, Agni, and Prajāpati.

sanskrit: स हि धाता विधाता च स प्रभुः सर्वतोमुखः ।हृदयं सर्वभूतानां महानात्मा प्रकाशते ॥६१॥
transliteration: sa hi dhātā vidhātā ca sa prabhuḥ sarvatomukhaḥ ,hṛdayaṁ sarvabhūtānāṁ mahānātmā prakāśate.
english: Indeed, he is the ordainer and the dispenser; he is the Lord, present everywhere. The great self (ātman) shines forth in the heart of all beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 61. Indeed, he is the ordainer and the dispenser; he is the Lord, present everywhere. The great self (ātman) shines forth in the heart of all beings.

sanskrit: तं विप्रसंघाश्च सुरासुराश्च यक्षाः पिशाचाः पितरो वयांसि ।रक्षोगणा भूतगणाश्च सर्वे महर्षयश्चैव सदा स्तुवन्ति ॥६२॥
transliteration: taṁ viprasaṁghāśca surāsurāśca; yakṣāḥ piśācāḥ pitaro vayāṁsi ,rakṣogaṇā bhūtagaṇāśca sarve; maharṣayaścaiva sadā stuvanti.
english: Groups of Brahmins, gods and demons, Yakṣas, Piśācas, ancestors (pitṛs), birds, hosts of Rākṣasas, and all hosts of spirits (bhūtas), as well as the great sages (ṛṣis)  -  all constantly praise him.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 42 Verse 62. Groups of Brahmins, gods and demons, Yakṣas, Piśācas, ancestors (pitṛs), birds, hosts of Rākṣasas, and all hosts of spirits (bhūtas), as well as the great sages (ṛṣis)  -  all constantly praise him.

sanskrit: ब्रह्मोवाच ।मनुष्याणां तु राजन्यः क्षत्रियो मध्यमो गुणः ।कुञ्जरो वाहनानां च सिंहश्चारण्यवासिनाम् ॥१॥
transliteration: brahmovāca ,manuṣyāṇāṁ tu rājanyaḥ kṣatriyo madhyamo guṇaḥ ,kuñjaro vāhanānāṁ ca siṁhaścāraṇyavāsinām.
english: Brahmā said: "Among humans, the kṣatriya (rājanya) class is indeed preeminent; an elephant among mounts, and a lion among forest dwellers."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 1. Brahmā said: "Among humans, the kṣatriya (rājanya) class is indeed preeminent; an elephant among mounts, and a lion among forest dwellers."

sanskrit: अविः पशूनां सर्वेषामाखुश्च बिलवासिनाम् ।गवां गोवृषभश्चैव स्त्रीणां पुरुष एव च ॥२॥
transliteration: aviḥ paśūnāṁ sarveṣāmākhuśca bilavāsinām ,gavāṁ govṛṣabhaścaiva strīṇāṁ puruṣa eva ca.
english: A ram among all animals, and a mouse among those living in holes; a bull among cattle, and a man among women.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 2. A ram among all animals, and a mouse among those living in holes; a bull among cattle, and a man among women.

sanskrit: न्यग्रोधो जम्बुवृक्षश्च पिप्पलः शाल्मलिस्तथा ।शिंशपा मेषशृङ्गश्च तथा कीचकवेणवः ।एते द्रुमाणां राजानो लोकेऽस्मिन्नात्र संशयः ॥३॥
transliteration: nyagrodho jambuvṛkṣaśca pippalaḥ śālmalistathā ,śiṁśapā meṣaśṛṅgaśca tathā kīcakaveṇavaḥ ,ete drumāṇāṁ rājāno loke'sminnātra saṁśayaḥ.
english: The banyan tree, the rose-apple tree, the pipal, and similarly the silk-cotton tree; the śiṃśapā tree, the meṣaśṛṅga plant, and also the kīcaka bamboos  -  these are the kings among trees in this world; there is no doubt about this.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 3. The banyan tree, the rose-apple tree, the pipal, and similarly the silk-cotton tree; the śiṃśapā tree, the meṣaśṛṅga plant, and also the kīcaka bamboos  -  these are the kings among trees in this world; there is no doubt about this.

sanskrit: हिमवान्पारियात्रश्च सह्यो विन्ध्यस्त्रिकूटवान् ।श्वेतो नीलश्च भासश्च काष्ठवांश्चैव पर्वतः ॥४॥
transliteration: himavānpāriyātraśca sahyo vindhyastrikūṭavān ,śveto nīlaśca bhāsaśca kāṣṭhavāṁścaiva parvataḥ.
english: Himavān, Pāriyātra, Sahya, Vindhya, Trikūṭavat, Śveta, Nīla, Bhāsa, and indeed Kāṣṭhavān are mountains.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 4. Himavān, Pāriyātra, Sahya, Vindhya, Trikūṭavat, Śveta, Nīla, Bhāsa, and indeed Kāṣṭhavān are mountains.

sanskrit: शुभस्कन्धो महेन्द्रश्च माल्यवान्पर्वतस्तथा ।एते पर्वतराजानो गणानां मरुतस्तथा ॥५॥
transliteration: śubhaskandho mahendraśca mālyavānparvatastathā ,ete parvatarājāno gaṇānāṁ marutastathā.
english: Śubhaskandha, Mahendra, and also Mount Mālyavān – these are the foremost among mountains. Similarly, the Maruts are foremost among their hosts (gaṇas).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 5. Śubhaskandha, Mahendra, and also Mount Mālyavān – these are the foremost among mountains. Similarly, the Maruts are foremost among their hosts (gaṇas).

sanskrit: सूर्यो ग्रहाणामधिपो नक्षत्राणां च चन्द्रमाः ।यमः पितॄणामधिपः सरितामथ सागरः ॥६॥
transliteration: sūryo grahāṇāmadhipo nakṣatrāṇāṁ ca candramāḥ ,yamaḥ pitṝṇāmadhipaḥ saritāmatha sāgaraḥ.
english: The Sun is the lord of the planets, and the Moon is of the constellations. Yama is the lord of the ancestors (pitṛs), and the ocean is the lord of the rivers.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 6. The Sun is the lord of the planets, and the Moon is of the constellations. Yama is the lord of the ancestors (pitṛs), and the ocean is the lord of the rivers.

sanskrit: अम्भसां वरुणो राजा सत्त्वानां मित्र उच्यते ।अर्कोऽधिपतिरुष्णानां ज्योतिषामिन्दुरुच्यते ॥७॥
transliteration: ambhasāṁ varuṇo rājā sattvānāṁ mitra ucyate ,arko'dhipatiruṣṇānāṁ jyotiṣāmindurucyate.
english: Varuṇa is the king of the waters. Mitra is said to be (the lord) of living beings. The Sun (Arka) is the lord of hot things, and the Moon (Indu) is said to be (the lord) of luminaries.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 7. Varuṇa is the king of the waters. Mitra is said to be (the lord) of living beings. The Sun (Arka) is the lord of hot things, and the Moon (Indu) is said to be (the lord) of luminaries.

sanskrit: अग्निर्भूतपतिर्नित्यं ब्राह्मणानां बृहस्पतिः ।ओषधीनां पतिः सोमो विष्णुर्बलवतां वरः ॥८॥
transliteration: agnirbhūtapatirnityaṁ brāhmaṇānāṁ bṛhaspatiḥ ,oṣadhīnāṁ patiḥ somo viṣṇurbalavatāṁ varaḥ.
english: Agni is always the lord of beings. Bṛhaspati is (the lord) of Brahmins. Soma is the lord of herbs. Viṣṇu is the best of the strong.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 8. Agni is always the lord of beings. Bṛhaspati is (the lord) of Brahmins. Soma is the lord of herbs. Viṣṇu is the best of the strong.

sanskrit: त्वष्टाधिराजो रूपाणां पशूनामीश्वरः शिवः ।दक्षिणानां तथा यज्ञो वेदानामृषयस्तथा ॥९॥
transliteration: tvaṣṭādhirājo rūpāṇāṁ paśūnāmīśvaraḥ śivaḥ ,dakṣiṇānāṁ tathā yajño vedānāmṛṣayastathā.
english: Tvaṣṭā is the paramount ruler of forms; Śiva is the lord of animals. Likewise, the (Vedic ritual) yajña is the dispenser of ritual gifts, and the sages are the authorities of the Vedas.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 9. Tvaṣṭā is the paramount ruler of forms; Śiva is the lord of animals. Likewise, the (Vedic ritual) yajña is the dispenser of ritual gifts, and the sages are the authorities of the Vedas.

sanskrit: दिशामुदीची विप्राणां सोमो राजा प्रतापवान् ।कुबेरः सर्वयक्षाणां देवतानां पुरंदरः ।एष भूतादिकः सर्गः प्रजानां च प्रजापतिः ॥१०॥
transliteration: diśāmudīcī viprāṇāṁ somo rājā pratāpavān ,kuberaḥ sarvayakṣāṇāṁ devatānāṁ puraṁdaraḥ ,eṣa bhūtādikaḥ sargaḥ prajānāṁ ca prajāpatiḥ.
english: Among directions, the North (is supreme); among Brahmins, Soma is the glorious king. Kubera (is supreme) among all Yakṣas, and Purandara (Indra) among the deities. This (supreme being) is the primordial origin of all beings and the lord of creation (prajāpati) of all creatures.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 10. Among directions, the North (is supreme); among Brahmins, Soma is the glorious king. Kubera (is supreme) among all Yakṣas, and Purandara (Indra) among the deities. This (supreme being) is the primordial origin of all beings and the lord of creation (prajāpati) of all creatures.

sanskrit: सर्वेषामेव भूतानामहं ब्रह्ममयो महान् ।भूतं परतरं मत्तो विष्णोर्वापि न विद्यते ॥११॥
transliteration: sarveṣāmeva bhūtānāmahaṁ brahmamayo mahān ,bhūtaṁ parataraṁ matto viṣṇorvāpi na vidyate.
english: Among all beings, I am indeed the great one, pervaded by (brahman). No being superior to me, nor, indeed, to Viṣṇu, exists.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 11. Among all beings, I am indeed the great one, pervaded by (brahman). No being superior to me, nor, indeed, to Viṣṇu, exists.

sanskrit: राजाधिराजः सर्वासां विष्णुर्ब्रह्ममयो महान् ।ईश्वरं तं विजानीमः स विभुः स प्रजापतिः ॥१२॥
transliteration: rājādhirājaḥ sarvāsāṁ viṣṇurbrahmamayo mahān ,īśvaraṁ taṁ vijānīmaḥ sa vibhuḥ sa prajāpatiḥ.
english: Viṣṇu, the great one pervaded by (brahman), is the king of kings of all (beings). We understand Him to be the supreme Lord; He is the all-pervading, and He is the lord of creation (prajāpati).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 12. Viṣṇu, the great one pervaded by (brahman), is the king of kings of all (beings). We understand Him to be the supreme Lord; He is the all-pervading, and He is the lord of creation (prajāpati).

sanskrit: नरकिंनरयक्षाणां गन्धर्वोरगरक्षसाम् ।देवदानवनागानां सर्वेषामीश्वरो हि सः ॥१३॥
transliteration: narakiṁnarayakṣāṇāṁ gandharvoragarakṣasām ,devadānavanāgānāṁ sarveṣāmīśvaro hi saḥ.
english: He is indeed the lord of all - men, kinnaras, yakṣas, gandharvas, uragas, rakṣasas, devas, dānavas, and nāgas.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 13. He is indeed the lord of all - men, kinnaras, yakṣas, gandharvas, uragas, rakṣasas, devas, dānavas, and nāgas.

sanskrit: भगदेवानुयातानां सर्वासां वामलोचना ।माहेश्वरी महादेवी प्रोच्यते पार्वतीति या ॥१४॥
transliteration: bhagadevānuyātānāṁ sarvāsāṁ vāmalocanā ,māheśvarī mahādevī procyate pārvatīti yā.
english: She who is called Pārvatī is Maheśvarī, the great goddess, among all beautiful-eyed women who are attended by Bhaga and the deities.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 14. She who is called Pārvatī is Maheśvarī, the great goddess, among all beautiful-eyed women who are attended by Bhaga and the deities.

sanskrit: उमां देवीं विजानीत नारीणामुत्तमां शुभाम् ।रतीनां वसुमत्यस्तु स्त्रीणामप्सरसस्तथा ॥१५॥
transliteration: umāṁ devīṁ vijānīta nārīṇāmuttamāṁ śubhām ,ratīnāṁ vasumatyastu strīṇāmapsarasastathā.
english: Understand the goddess Umā to be the supreme and auspicious among women. Similarly, among the Ratis, (know) Vasumatī, and among (other) women, the Apsarases.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 15. Understand the goddess Umā to be the supreme and auspicious among women. Similarly, among the Ratis, (know) Vasumatī, and among (other) women, the Apsarases.

sanskrit: धर्मकामाश्च राजानो ब्राह्मणा धर्मलक्षणाः ।तस्माद्राजा द्विजातीनां प्रयतेतेह रक्षणे ॥१६॥
transliteration: dharmakāmāśca rājāno brāhmaṇā dharmalakṣaṇāḥ ,tasmādrājā dvijātīnāṁ prayateteha rakṣaṇe.
english: Kings are desirous of righteousness (dharma) and Brahmins are marked by righteousness (dharma). Therefore, the king should strive for the protection of the twice-born (dvija) here in this world.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 16. Kings are desirous of righteousness (dharma) and Brahmins are marked by righteousness (dharma). Therefore, the king should strive for the protection of the twice-born (dvija) here in this world.

sanskrit: राज्ञां हि विषये येषामवसीदन्ति साधवः ।हीनास्ते स्वगुणैः सर्वैः प्रेत्यावाङ्मार्गगामिनः ॥१७॥
transliteration: rājñāṁ hi viṣaye yeṣāmavasīdanti sādhavaḥ ,hīnāste svaguṇaiḥ sarvaiḥ pretyāvāṅmārgagāminaḥ.
english: Kings in whose dominion virtuous people suffer are indeed deprived of all their own merits. After death, they travel a downward path.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 17. Kings in whose dominion virtuous people suffer are indeed deprived of all their own merits. After death, they travel a downward path.

sanskrit: राज्ञां तु विषये येषां साधवः परिरक्षिताः ।तेऽस्मिँल्लोके प्रमोदन्ते प्रेत्य चानन्त्यमेव च ।प्राप्नुवन्ति महात्मान इति वित्त द्विजर्षभाः ॥१८॥
transliteration: rājñāṁ tu viṣaye yeṣāṁ sādhavaḥ parirakṣitāḥ ,te'smiँlloke pramodante pretya cānantyameva ca ,prāpnuvanti mahātmāna iti vitta dvijarṣabhāḥ.
english: However, those kings in whose dominion virtuous people are well-protected rejoice in this world. And after death, these noble souls (mahātmānaḥ) certainly attain endlessness. Understand this, O best among the twice-born.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 18. However, those kings in whose dominion virtuous people are well-protected rejoice in this world. And after death, these noble souls (mahātmānaḥ) certainly attain endlessness. Understand this, O best among the twice-born.

sanskrit: अत ऊर्ध्वं प्रवक्ष्यामि नियतं धर्मलक्षणम् ।अहिंसालक्षणो धर्मो हिंसा चाधर्मलक्षणा ॥१९॥
transliteration: ata ūrdhvaṁ pravakṣyāmi niyataṁ dharmalakṣaṇam ,ahiṁsālakṣaṇo dharmo hiṁsā cādharmalakṣaṇā.
english: From this point forward, I will explain the fixed characteristic of natural law (dharma). Natural law (dharma) is defined by non-violence, and violence is characterized by unrighteousness (adharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 19. From this point forward, I will explain the fixed characteristic of natural law (dharma). Natural law (dharma) is defined by non-violence, and violence is characterized by unrighteousness (adharma).

sanskrit: प्रकाशलक्षणा देवा मनुष्याः कर्मलक्षणाः ।शब्दलक्षणमाकाशं वायुस्तु स्पर्शलक्षणः ॥२०॥
transliteration: prakāśalakṣaṇā devā manuṣyāḥ karmalakṣaṇāḥ ,śabdalakṣaṇamākāśaṁ vāyustu sparśalakṣaṇaḥ.
english: Gods are characterized by light; human beings are defined by action (karma). Space (ākāśa) is characterized by sound, whereas wind is characterized by touch.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 20. Gods are characterized by light; human beings are defined by action (karma). Space (ākāśa) is characterized by sound, whereas wind is characterized by touch.

sanskrit: ज्योतिषां लक्षणं रूपमापश्च रसलक्षणाः ।धरणी सर्वभूतानां पृथिवी गन्धलक्षणा ॥२१॥
transliteration: jyotiṣāṁ lakṣaṇaṁ rūpamāpaśca rasalakṣaṇāḥ ,dharaṇī sarvabhūtānāṁ pṛthivī gandhalakṣaṇā.
english: The characteristic of luminous bodies is form, and waters are characterized by taste. The earth (dharaṇī) is the supporter of all beings, and (this same) earth (pṛthivī) is characterized by smell.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 21. The characteristic of luminous bodies is form, and waters are characterized by taste. The earth (dharaṇī) is the supporter of all beings, and (this same) earth (pṛthivī) is characterized by smell.

sanskrit: स्वरव्यञ्जनसंस्कारा भारती सत्यलक्षणा ।मनसो लक्षणं चिन्ता तथोक्ता बुद्धिरन्वयात् ॥२२॥
transliteration: svaravyañjanasaṁskārā bhāratī satyalakṣaṇā ,manaso lakṣaṇaṁ cintā tathoktā buddhiranvayāt.
english: Speech (bhāratī) is characterized by the refinement of vowels and consonants, and by truth. The characteristic of the mind (manas) is reflection, and similarly, the intellect (buddhi) is declared to be known through logical inference.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 22. Speech (bhāratī) is characterized by the refinement of vowels and consonants, and by truth. The characteristic of the mind (manas) is reflection, and similarly, the intellect (buddhi) is declared to be known through logical inference.

sanskrit: मनसा चिन्तयानोऽर्थान्बुद्ध्या चैव व्यवस्यति ।बुद्धिर्हि व्यवसायेन लक्ष्यते नात्र संशयः ॥२३॥
transliteration: manasā cintayāno'rthānbuddhyā caiva vyavasyati ,buddhirhi vyavasāyena lakṣyate nātra saṁśayaḥ.
english: Reflecting upon objects with the mind (manas), one indeed decides with the intellect (buddhi). For the intellect (buddhi) is characterized by resolute determination; there is no doubt in this matter.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 23. Reflecting upon objects with the mind (manas), one indeed decides with the intellect (buddhi). For the intellect (buddhi) is characterized by resolute determination; there is no doubt in this matter.

sanskrit: लक्षणं महतो ध्यानमव्यक्तं साधुलक्षणम् ।प्रवृत्तिलक्षणो योगो ज्ञानं संन्यासलक्षणम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: lakṣaṇaṁ mahato dhyānamavyaktaṁ sādhulakṣaṇam ,pravṛttilakṣaṇo yogo jñānaṁ saṁnyāsalakṣaṇam.
english: The characteristic of the Great Principle (mahat) is meditation (dhyāna); the unmanifest (avyakta) is characterized by the virtuous. The practice of (yoga) is characterized by active engagement, and knowledge is characterized by renunciation (saṃnyāsa).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 24. The characteristic of the Great Principle (mahat) is meditation (dhyāna); the unmanifest (avyakta) is characterized by the virtuous. The practice of (yoga) is characterized by active engagement, and knowledge is characterized by renunciation (saṃnyāsa).

sanskrit: तस्माज्ज्ञानं पुरस्कृत्य संन्यसेदिह बुद्धिमान् ।संन्यासी ज्ञानसंयुक्तः प्राप्नोति परमां गतिम् ।अतीतोऽद्वंद्वमभ्येति तमोमृत्युजरातिगम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: tasmājjñānaṁ puraskṛtya saṁnyasediha buddhimān ,saṁnyāsī jñānasaṁyuktaḥ prāpnoti paramāṁ gatim ,atīto'dvaṁdvamabhyeti tamomṛtyujarātigam.
english: Therefore, an intelligent person should embrace the path of renunciation (saṃnyāsa) in this world, prioritizing knowledge (jñāna). A renunciant (saṃnyāsī) who is endowed with knowledge attains the supreme destination. Such a one, having transcended all dualities, reaches a state beyond darkness (ignorance), death, and old age.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 25. Therefore, an intelligent person should embrace the path of renunciation (saṃnyāsa) in this world, prioritizing knowledge (jñāna). A renunciant (saṃnyāsī) who is endowed with knowledge attains the supreme destination. Such a one, having transcended all dualities, reaches a state beyond darkness (ignorance), death, and old age.

sanskrit: धर्मलक्षणसंयुक्तमुक्तं वो विधिवन्मया ।गुणानां ग्रहणं सम्यग्वक्ष्याम्यहमतः परम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: dharmalakṣaṇasaṁyuktamuktaṁ vo vidhivanmayā ,guṇānāṁ grahaṇaṁ samyagvakṣyāmyahamataḥ param.
english: The definition of (dharma), endowed with its specific characteristics, has been duly explained to you by me. Hereafter, I will thoroughly describe the understanding of the [three fundamental qualities of nature] (guṇas).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 26. The definition of (dharma), endowed with its specific characteristics, has been duly explained to you by me. Hereafter, I will thoroughly describe the understanding of the [three fundamental qualities of nature] (guṇas).

sanskrit: पार्थिवो यस्तु गन्धो वै घ्राणेनेह स गृह्यते ।घ्राणस्थश्च तथा वायुर्गन्धज्ञाने विधीयते ॥२७॥
transliteration: pārthivo yastu gandho vai ghrāṇeneha sa gṛhyate ,ghrāṇasthaśca tathā vāyurgandhajñāne vidhīyate.
english: The earthly odor, which is indeed apprehended by the nose in this context, and similarly, the air (vāyu) located within the nose, serves a purpose in the perception of scent.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 27. The earthly odor, which is indeed apprehended by the nose in this context, and similarly, the air (vāyu) located within the nose, serves a purpose in the perception of scent.

sanskrit: अपां धातुरसो नित्यं जिह्वया स तु गृह्यते ।जिह्वास्थश्च तथा सोमो रसज्ञाने विधीयते ॥२८॥
transliteration: apāṁ dhāturaso nityaṁ jihvayā sa tu gṛhyate ,jihvāsthaśca tathā somo rasajñāne vidhīyate.
english: The elemental taste (rasa) of water is always apprehended by the tongue. And similarly, Soma, which is located in the tongue, functions in the perception of taste.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 28. The elemental taste (rasa) of water is always apprehended by the tongue. And similarly, Soma, which is located in the tongue, functions in the perception of taste.

sanskrit: ज्योतिषश्च गुणो रूपं चक्षुषा तच्च गृह्यते ।चक्षुःस्थश्च तथादित्यो रूपज्ञाने विधीयते ॥२९॥
transliteration: jyotiṣaśca guṇo rūpaṁ cakṣuṣā tacca gṛhyate ,cakṣuḥsthaśca tathādityo rūpajñāne vidhīyate.
english: Form is the essential quality of light, and that is perceived by the eye. Similarly, the sun, residing in the eye, is constituted for the apprehension of form.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 29. Form is the essential quality of light, and that is perceived by the eye. Similarly, the sun, residing in the eye, is constituted for the apprehension of form.

sanskrit: वायव्यस्तु तथा स्पर्शस्त्वचा प्रज्ञायते च सः ।त्वक्स्थश्चैव तथा वायुः स्पर्शज्ञाने विधीयते ॥३०॥
transliteration: vāyavyastu tathā sparśastvacā prajñāyate ca saḥ ,tvaksthaścaiva tathā vāyuḥ sparśajñāne vidhīyate.
english: Similarly, touch is the quality associated with the air element, and it is perceived by the skin. Likewise, air, residing in the skin, is constituted for the apprehension of touch.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 30. Similarly, touch is the quality associated with the air element, and it is perceived by the skin. Likewise, air, residing in the skin, is constituted for the apprehension of touch.

sanskrit: आकाशस्य गुणो घोषः श्रोत्रेण स तु गृह्यते ।श्रोत्रस्थाश्च दिशः सर्वाः शब्दज्ञाने प्रकीर्तिताः ॥३१॥
transliteration: ākāśasya guṇo ghoṣaḥ śrotreṇa sa tu gṛhyate ,śrotrasthāśca diśaḥ sarvāḥ śabdajñāne prakīrtitāḥ.
english: Sound is the essential quality of space, and it is perceived by the ear. All directions, situated within the ear, are declared to be for the apprehension of sound.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 31. Sound is the essential quality of space, and it is perceived by the ear. All directions, situated within the ear, are declared to be for the apprehension of sound.

sanskrit: मनसस्तु गुणश्चिन्ता प्रज्ञया स तु गृह्यते ।हृदिस्थचेतनाधातुर्मनोज्ञाने विधीयते ॥३२॥
transliteration: manasastu guṇaścintā prajñayā sa tu gṛhyate ,hṛdisthacetanādhāturmanojñāne vidhīyate.
english: Thought is the essential quality of the mind, and it is perceived by intellect. The conscious element, residing in the heart, is constituted for the apprehension of mental processes.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 32. Thought is the essential quality of the mind, and it is perceived by intellect. The conscious element, residing in the heart, is constituted for the apprehension of mental processes.

sanskrit: बुद्धिरध्यवसायेन ध्यानेन च महांस्तथा ।निश्चित्य ग्रहणं नित्यमव्यक्तं नात्र संशयः ॥३३॥
transliteration: buddhiradhyavasāyena dhyānena ca mahāṁstathā ,niścitya grahaṇaṁ nityamavyaktaṁ nātra saṁśayaḥ.
english: Thus, the great principle (mahat), through intellect, determination, and meditation (dhyāna), definitively ascertains the eternal unmanifest (prakṛti) as an object of apprehension. There is no doubt in this.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 33. Thus, the great principle (mahat), through intellect, determination, and meditation (dhyāna), definitively ascertains the eternal unmanifest (prakṛti) as an object of apprehension. There is no doubt in this.

sanskrit: अलिङ्गग्रहणो नित्यः क्षेत्रज्ञो निर्गुणात्मकः ।तस्मादलिङ्गः क्षेत्रज्ञः केवलं ज्ञानलक्षणः ॥३४॥
transliteration: aliṅgagrahaṇo nityaḥ kṣetrajño nirguṇātmakaḥ ,tasmādaliṅgaḥ kṣetrajñaḥ kevalaṁ jñānalakṣaṇaḥ.
english: The knower of the field (kṣetrajña) is eternal, not apprehended by distinguishing marks, and is by nature devoid of qualities (guṇas). Therefore, the knower of the field (kṣetrajña) is without distinguishing marks and is solely characterized by knowledge.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 34. The knower of the field (kṣetrajña) is eternal, not apprehended by distinguishing marks, and is by nature devoid of qualities (guṇas). Therefore, the knower of the field (kṣetrajña) is without distinguishing marks and is solely characterized by knowledge.

sanskrit: अव्यक्तं क्षेत्रमुद्दिष्टं गुणानां प्रभवाप्ययम् ।सदा पश्याम्यहं लीनं विजानामि शृणोमि च ॥३५॥
transliteration: avyaktaṁ kṣetramuddiṣṭaṁ guṇānāṁ prabhavāpyayam ,sadā paśyāmyahaṁ līnaṁ vijānāmi śṛṇomi ca.
english: The unmanifest (prakṛti) is designated as the field, the origin and dissolution of the qualities (guṇas). I always perceive it as latent, I understand, and I hear (about it).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 35. The unmanifest (prakṛti) is designated as the field, the origin and dissolution of the qualities (guṇas). I always perceive it as latent, I understand, and I hear (about it).

sanskrit: पुरुषस्तद्विजानीते तस्मात्क्षेत्रज्ञ उच्यते ।गुणवृत्तं तथा कृत्स्नं क्षेत्रज्ञः परिपश्यति ॥३६॥
transliteration: puruṣastadvijānīte tasmātkṣetrajña ucyate ,guṇavṛttaṁ tathā kṛtsnaṁ kṣetrajñaḥ paripaśyati.
english: The cosmic person (puruṣa) knows that (field), and therefore he is called the knower of the field (kṣetrajña). Thus, the knower of the field (kṣetrajña) fully perceives the entire activity of the qualities (guṇas).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 36. The cosmic person (puruṣa) knows that (field), and therefore he is called the knower of the field (kṣetrajña). Thus, the knower of the field (kṣetrajña) fully perceives the entire activity of the qualities (guṇas).

sanskrit: आदिमध्यावसानान्तं सृज्यमानमचेतनम् ।न गुणा विदुरात्मानं सृज्यमानं पुनः पुनः ॥३७॥
transliteration: ādimadhyāvasānāntaṁ sṛjyamānamacetanam ,na guṇā vidurātmānaṁ sṛjyamānaṁ punaḥ punaḥ.
english: The constituents of nature (guṇas) do not know themselves (ātman), being unconscious, repeatedly manifested, and possessing a beginning, a middle, and an end.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 37. The constituents of nature (guṇas) do not know themselves (ātman), being unconscious, repeatedly manifested, and possessing a beginning, a middle, and an end.

sanskrit: न सत्यं वेद वै कश्चित्क्षेत्रज्ञस्त्वेव विन्दति ।गुणानां गुणभूतानां यत्परं परतो महत् ॥३८॥
transliteration: na satyaṁ veda vai kaścitkṣetrajñastveva vindati ,guṇānāṁ guṇabhūtānāṁ yatparaṁ parato mahat.
english: Indeed, no one knows the reality, but the knower of the field (kṣetrajña) alone understands that which is supreme, higher than the great (mahat) principle, and pertains to the constituents of nature and their transformations.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 38. Indeed, no one knows the reality, but the knower of the field (kṣetrajña) alone understands that which is supreme, higher than the great (mahat) principle, and pertains to the constituents of nature and their transformations.

sanskrit: तस्माद्गुणांश्च तत्त्वं च परित्यज्येह तत्त्ववित् ।क्षीणदोषो गुणान्हित्वा क्षेत्रज्ञं प्रविशत्यथ ॥३९॥
transliteration: tasmādguṇāṁśca tattvaṁ ca parityajyeha tattvavit ,kṣīṇadoṣo guṇānhitvā kṣetrajñaṁ praviśatyatha.
english: Therefore, the knower of reality, having abandoned the constituents of nature and (all other) fundamental principles in this world, with all imperfections diminished, then truly gives up the constituents of nature and merges into the knower of the field (kṣetrajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 39. Therefore, the knower of reality, having abandoned the constituents of nature and (all other) fundamental principles in this world, with all imperfections diminished, then truly gives up the constituents of nature and merges into the knower of the field (kṣetrajña).

sanskrit: निर्द्वंद्वो निर्नमस्कारो निःस्वधाकार एव च ।अचलश्चानिकेतश्च क्षेत्रज्ञः स परो विभुः ॥४०॥
transliteration: nirdvaṁdvo nirnamaskāro niḥsvadhākāra eva ca ,acalaścāniketaśca kṣetrajñaḥ sa paro vibhuḥ.
english: That knower of the field (kṣetrajña) is supreme and all-pervading. He is free from dualities, free from the need for salutations, and from ancestral offerings (svadhā); he is unmoving and without a fixed abode.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 43 Verse 40. That knower of the field (kṣetrajña) is supreme and all-pervading. He is free from dualities, free from the need for salutations, and from ancestral offerings (svadhā); he is unmoving and without a fixed abode.

sanskrit: ब्रह्मोवाच ।यदादिमध्यपर्यन्तं ग्रहणोपायमेव च ।नामलक्षणसंयुक्तं सर्वं वक्ष्यामि तत्त्वतः ॥१॥
transliteration: brahmovāca ,yadādimadhyaparyantaṁ grahaṇopāyameva ca ,nāmalakṣaṇasaṁyuktaṁ sarvaṁ vakṣyāmi tattvataḥ.
english: Brahmā said: I will fundamentally explain everything that has a beginning, middle, and end, serves as a means of comprehension, and is associated with names and characteristics.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 1. Brahmā said: I will fundamentally explain everything that has a beginning, middle, and end, serves as a means of comprehension, and is associated with names and characteristics.

sanskrit: अहः पूर्वं ततो रात्रिर्मासाः शुक्लादयः स्मृताः ।श्रविष्ठादीनि ऋक्षाणि ऋतवः शिशिरादयः ॥२॥
transliteration: ahaḥ pūrvaṁ tato rātrirmāsāḥ śuklādayaḥ smṛtāḥ ,śraviṣṭhādīni ṛkṣāṇi ṛtavaḥ śiśirādayaḥ.
english: Day is first, then night. The months are reckoned from the bright fortnight (śukla-pakṣa). The constellations (nakṣatras) begin with Śraviṣṭhā, and the seasons begin with Śiśira.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 2. Day is first, then night. The months are reckoned from the bright fortnight (śukla-pakṣa). The constellations (nakṣatras) begin with Śraviṣṭhā, and the seasons begin with Śiśira.

sanskrit: भूमिरादिस्तु गन्धानां रसानामाप एव च ।रूपाणां ज्योतिरादिस्तु स्पर्शादिर्वायुरुच्यते ।शब्दस्यादिस्तथाकाशमेष भूतकृतो गुणः ॥३॥
transliteration: bhūmirādistu gandhānāṁ rasānāmāpa eva ca ,rūpāṇāṁ jyotirādistu sparśādirvāyurucyate ,śabdasyādistathākāśameṣa bhūtakṛto guṇaḥ.
english: Earth is the primary source of odors, and water is indeed the source of tastes. Light (or fire) is the primary source of forms, and air is said to be the source of touch. Similarly, space (ākāśa) is the source of sound. This describes the fundamental qualities produced by the elements (bhūtas).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 3. Earth is the primary source of odors, and water is indeed the source of tastes. Light (or fire) is the primary source of forms, and air is said to be the source of touch. Similarly, space (ākāśa) is the source of sound. This describes the fundamental qualities produced by the elements (bhūtas).

sanskrit: अतः परं प्रवक्ष्यामि भूतानामादिमुत्तमम् ।आदित्यो ज्योतिषामादिरग्निर्भूतादिरिष्यते ॥४॥
transliteration: ataḥ paraṁ pravakṣyāmi bhūtānāmādimuttamam ,ādityo jyotiṣāmādiragnirbhūtādiriṣyate.
english: Next, I will declare the supreme origin of the elements (bhūtas). The sun is the origin of all luminaries, and fire is regarded as the fundamental source of all beings (bhūtas).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 4. Next, I will declare the supreme origin of the elements (bhūtas). The sun is the origin of all luminaries, and fire is regarded as the fundamental source of all beings (bhūtas).

sanskrit: सावित्री सर्वविद्यानां देवतानां प्रजापतिः ।ओंकारः सर्ववेदानां वचसां प्राण एव च ।यद्यस्मिन्नियतं लोके सर्वं सावित्रमुच्यते ॥५॥
transliteration: sāvitrī sarvavidyānāṁ devatānāṁ prajāpatiḥ ,oṁkāraḥ sarvavedānāṁ vacasāṁ prāṇa eva ca ,yadyasminniyataṁ loke sarvaṁ sāvitramucyate.
english: Sāvitrī is the essence of all knowledge. The lord of creation (prajāpati) is the foremost among the deities. The sacred syllable Om (oṃkāra) is the essence of all the Vedas, and indeed the very life-breath of all words. Whatever is firmly established in this world, all that is declared to be Sāvitra.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 5. Sāvitrī is the essence of all knowledge. The lord of creation (prajāpati) is the foremost among the deities. The sacred syllable Om (oṃkāra) is the essence of all the Vedas, and indeed the very life-breath of all words. Whatever is firmly established in this world, all that is declared to be Sāvitra.

sanskrit: गायत्री छन्दसामादिः पशूनामज उच्यते ।गावश्चतुष्पदामादिर्मनुष्याणां द्विजातयः ॥६॥
transliteration: gāyatrī chandasāmādiḥ paśūnāmaja ucyate ,gāvaścatuṣpadāmādirmanuṣyāṇāṁ dvijātayaḥ.
english: The Gāyatrī (mantra) is the foremost among meters. The goat is said to be the first among animals. Cows are considered the foremost among quadrupeds, and among human beings, it is the twice-born (dvijāti).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 6. The Gāyatrī (mantra) is the foremost among meters. The goat is said to be the first among animals. Cows are considered the foremost among quadrupeds, and among human beings, it is the twice-born (dvijāti).

sanskrit: श्येनः पतत्रिणामादिर्यज्ञानां हुतमुत्तमम् ।परिसर्पिणां तु सर्वेषां ज्येष्ठः सर्पो द्विजोत्तमाः ॥७॥
transliteration: śyenaḥ patatriṇāmādiryajñānāṁ hutamuttamam ,parisarpiṇāṁ tu sarveṣāṁ jyeṣṭhaḥ sarpo dvijottamāḥ.
english: The hawk is the foremost among birds. Among Vedic rituals (yajña), the fire offering (huta) is considered the most excellent. And among all creeping creatures, the serpent is the eldest, O best among the twice-born (dvijottama).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 7. The hawk is the foremost among birds. Among Vedic rituals (yajña), the fire offering (huta) is considered the most excellent. And among all creeping creatures, the serpent is the eldest, O best among the twice-born (dvijottama).

sanskrit: कृतमादिर्युगानां च सर्वेषां नात्र संशयः ।हिरण्यं सर्वरत्नानामोषधीनां यवास्तथा ॥८॥
transliteration: kṛtamādiryugānāṁ ca sarveṣāṁ nātra saṁśayaḥ ,hiraṇyaṁ sarvaratnānāmoṣadhīnāṁ yavāstathā.
english: The Kṛta (Satya) yuga is the first among all ages, of this there is no doubt. Gold is the foremost among all jewels, and similarly, among herbs, barley (yava) is supreme.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 8. The Kṛta (Satya) yuga is the first among all ages, of this there is no doubt. Gold is the foremost among all jewels, and similarly, among herbs, barley (yava) is supreme.

sanskrit: सर्वेषां भक्ष्यभोज्यानामन्नं परममुच्यते ।द्रवाणां चैव सर्वेषां पेयानामाप उत्तमाः ॥९॥
transliteration: sarveṣāṁ bhakṣyabhojyānāmannaṁ paramamucyate ,dravāṇāṁ caiva sarveṣāṁ peyānāmāpa uttamāḥ.
english: Among all things that can be eaten and consumed, food is declared to be supreme. And among all liquids and drinks, water is the best.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 9. Among all things that can be eaten and consumed, food is declared to be supreme. And among all liquids and drinks, water is the best.

sanskrit: स्थावराणां च भूतानां सर्वेषामविशेषतः ।ब्रह्मक्षेत्रं सदा पुण्यं प्लक्षः प्रथमजः स्मृतः ॥१०॥
transliteration: sthāvarāṇāṁ ca bhūtānāṁ sarveṣāmaviśeṣataḥ ,brahmakṣetraṁ sadā puṇyaṁ plakṣaḥ prathamajaḥ smṛtaḥ.
english: And among all immobile beings, without exception, the sacred land (brahmakṣetra) is always pure; the plakṣa tree is considered the first-born.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 10. And among all immobile beings, without exception, the sacred land (brahmakṣetra) is always pure; the plakṣa tree is considered the first-born.

sanskrit: अहं प्रजापतीनां च सर्वेषां नात्र संशयः ।मम विष्णुरचिन्त्यात्मा स्वयंभूरिति स स्मृतः ॥११॥
transliteration: ahaṁ prajāpatīnāṁ ca sarveṣāṁ nātra saṁśayaḥ ,mama viṣṇuracintyātmā svayaṁbhūriti sa smṛtaḥ.
english: Among all the lords of creation (prajāpati), I am indeed the greatest; there is no doubt about this. By me, Viṣṇu, whose essence (ātman) is inconceivable, is known as the self-born (svayambhū).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 11. Among all the lords of creation (prajāpati), I am indeed the greatest; there is no doubt about this. By me, Viṣṇu, whose essence (ātman) is inconceivable, is known as the self-born (svayambhū).

sanskrit: पर्वतानां महामेरुः सर्वेषामग्रजः स्मृतः ।दिशां च प्रदिशां चोर्ध्वा दिग्जाता प्रथमं तथा ॥१२॥
transliteration: parvatānāṁ mahāmeruḥ sarveṣāmagrajaḥ smṛtaḥ ,diśāṁ ca pradiśāṁ cordhvā digjātā prathamaṁ tathā.
english: Among all mountains, Mahāmeru is considered the foremost. And among the primary directions and secondary directions, the upper direction (ūrdhva dik) is thus considered the first-born.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 12. Among all mountains, Mahāmeru is considered the foremost. And among the primary directions and secondary directions, the upper direction (ūrdhva dik) is thus considered the first-born.

sanskrit: तथा त्रिपथगा गङ्गा नदीनामग्रजा स्मृता ।तथा सरोदपानानां सर्वेषां सागरोऽग्रजः ॥१३॥
transliteration: tathā tripathagā gaṅgā nadīnāmagrajā smṛtā ,tathā sarodapānānāṁ sarveṣāṁ sāgaro'grajaḥ.
english: The Gaṅgā, which flows in three paths, is considered the foremost among rivers. Similarly, the ocean is the most prominent among all lakes and reservoirs.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 13. The Gaṅgā, which flows in three paths, is considered the foremost among rivers. Similarly, the ocean is the most prominent among all lakes and reservoirs.

sanskrit: देवदानवभूतानां पिशाचोरगरक्षसाम् ।नरकिंनरयक्षाणां सर्वेषामीश्वरः प्रभुः ॥१४॥
transliteration: devadānavabhūtānāṁ piśācoragarakṣasām ,narakiṁnarayakṣāṇāṁ sarveṣāmīśvaraḥ prabhuḥ.
english: He is the lord and master (īśvara prabhu) of all the gods, demons, various beings, goblins, serpents, rākṣasas, humans, kinnaras, and yakṣas.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 14. He is the lord and master (īśvara prabhu) of all the gods, demons, various beings, goblins, serpents, rākṣasas, humans, kinnaras, and yakṣas.

sanskrit: आदिर्विश्वस्य जगतो विष्णुर्ब्रह्ममयो महान् ।भूतं परतरं तस्मात्त्रैलोक्ये नेह विद्यते ॥१५॥
transliteration: ādirviśvasya jagato viṣṇurbrahmamayo mahān ,bhūtaṁ parataraṁ tasmāttrailokye neha vidyate.
english: Viṣṇu is the great origin of the entire universe (viśva jagat) and is comprised of (brahman). No being superior to him exists here in the three worlds (trailokya).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 15. Viṣṇu is the great origin of the entire universe (viśva jagat) and is comprised of (brahman). No being superior to him exists here in the three worlds (trailokya).

sanskrit: आश्रमाणां च गार्हस्थ्यं सर्वेषां नात्र संशयः ।लोकानामादिरव्यक्तं सर्वस्यान्तस्तदेव च ॥१६॥
transliteration: āśramāṇāṁ ca gārhasthyaṁ sarveṣāṁ nātra saṁśayaḥ ,lokānāmādiravyaktaṁ sarvasyāntastadeva ca.
english: Among all the stages of life (āśramas), the householder (gārhasthya) stage is (considered superior); there is no doubt about this. The unmanifest (avyakta) is the origin of all worlds, and that same (unmanifest) is also the end of everything.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 16. Among all the stages of life (āśramas), the householder (gārhasthya) stage is (considered superior); there is no doubt about this. The unmanifest (avyakta) is the origin of all worlds, and that same (unmanifest) is also the end of everything.

sanskrit: अहान्यस्तमयान्तानि उदयान्ता च शर्वरी ।सुखस्यान्तः सदा दुःखं दुःखस्यान्तः सदा सुखम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: ahānyastamayāntāni udayāntā ca śarvarī ,sukhasyāntaḥ sadā duḥkhaṁ duḥkhasyāntaḥ sadā sukham.
english: Days conclude with sunset, and nights conclude with sunrise. The end of happiness is always sorrow, and the end of sorrow is always happiness.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 17. Days conclude with sunset, and nights conclude with sunrise. The end of happiness is always sorrow, and the end of sorrow is always happiness.

sanskrit: सर्वे क्षयान्ता निचयाः पतनान्ताः समुच्छ्रयाः ।संयोगा विप्रयोगान्ता मरणान्तं हि जीवितम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: sarve kṣayāntā nicayāḥ patanāntāḥ samucchrayāḥ ,saṁyogā viprayogāntā maraṇāntaṁ hi jīvitam.
english: All accumulations are destined to decay; all ascents are destined to fall. All unions end in separation, and indeed, life itself ends in death.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 18. All accumulations are destined to decay; all ascents are destined to fall. All unions end in separation, and indeed, life itself ends in death.

sanskrit: सर्वं कृतं विनाशान्तं जातस्य मरणं ध्रुवम् ।अशाश्वतं हि लोकेऽस्मिन्सर्वं स्थावरजङ्गमम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: sarvaṁ kṛtaṁ vināśāntaṁ jātasya maraṇaṁ dhruvam ,aśāśvataṁ hi loke'sminsarvaṁ sthāvarajaṅgamam.
english: Everything that is created ends in destruction. Death is certain for that which has been born. Indeed, in this world, everything, whether stationary or moving, is impermanent.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 19. Everything that is created ends in destruction. Death is certain for that which has been born. Indeed, in this world, everything, whether stationary or moving, is impermanent.

sanskrit: इष्टं दत्तं तपोऽधीतं व्रतानि नियमाश्च ये ।सर्वमेतद्विनाशान्तं ज्ञानस्यान्तो न विद्यते ॥२०॥
transliteration: iṣṭaṁ dattaṁ tapo'dhītaṁ vratāni niyamāśca ye ,sarvametadvināśāntaṁ jñānasyānto na vidyate.
english: Whatever has been offered in Vedic ritual (iṣṭam), given as charity (dattam), austerity (tapas) practiced, sacred texts studied, vows taken, and observances (niyamāḥ) followed - all of this eventually ends in destruction. However, there is no end to true knowledge (jñāna).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 20. Whatever has been offered in Vedic ritual (iṣṭam), given as charity (dattam), austerity (tapas) practiced, sacred texts studied, vows taken, and observances (niyamāḥ) followed - all of this eventually ends in destruction. However, there is no end to true knowledge (jñāna).

sanskrit: तस्माज्ज्ञानेन शुद्धेन प्रसन्नात्मा समाहितः ।निर्ममो निरहंकारो मुच्यते सर्वपाप्मभिः ॥२१॥
transliteration: tasmājjñānena śuddhena prasannātmā samāhitaḥ ,nirmamo nirahaṁkāro mucyate sarvapāpmabhiḥ.
english: Therefore, one whose inner being (ātman) is serene and composed through pure knowledge, and who is free from possessiveness and ego (ahaṅkāra), is liberated from all evils.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 44 Verse 21. Therefore, one whose inner being (ātman) is serene and composed through pure knowledge, and who is free from possessiveness and ego (ahaṅkāra), is liberated from all evils.

sanskrit: ब्रह्मोवाच ।बुद्धिसारं मनस्तम्भमिन्द्रियग्रामबन्धनम् ।महाभूतारविष्कम्भं निमेषपरिवेष्टनम् ॥१॥
transliteration: brahmovāca ,buddhisāraṁ manastambhamindriyagrāmabandhanam ,mahābhūtāraviṣkambhaṁ nimeṣapariveṣṭanam.
english: Brahmā said: (This body is) the essence of intelligence (buddhi), a pillar for the mind (manas), a bondage for the group of senses, an obstruction to the wheel of the great elements, and enveloped by the blinking of an eye.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 1. Brahmā said: (This body is) the essence of intelligence (buddhi), a pillar for the mind (manas), a bondage for the group of senses, an obstruction to the wheel of the great elements, and enveloped by the blinking of an eye.

sanskrit: जराशोकसमाविष्टं व्याधिव्यसनसंचरम् ।देशकालविचारीदं श्रमव्यायामनिस्वनम् ॥२॥
transliteration: jarāśokasamāviṣṭaṁ vyādhivyasanasaṁcaram ,deśakālavicārīdaṁ śramavyāyāmanisvanam.
english: It is permeated by old age and sorrow, traversed by sickness and misfortune, concerned with specific place and time, and produces sounds of exertion and weariness.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 2. It is permeated by old age and sorrow, traversed by sickness and misfortune, concerned with specific place and time, and produces sounds of exertion and weariness.

sanskrit: अहोरात्रपरिक्षेपं शीतोष्णपरिमण्डलम् ।सुखदुःखान्तसंक्लेशं क्षुत्पिपासावकीलनम् ॥३॥
transliteration: ahorātraparikṣepaṁ śītoṣṇaparimaṇḍalam ,sukhaduḥkhāntasaṁkleśaṁ kṣutpipāsāvakīlanam.
english: It is bound by the span of day and night, subject to the extremes of cold and heat, distressed by the culmination of pleasure and pain, and firmly secured by hunger and thirst.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 3. It is bound by the span of day and night, subject to the extremes of cold and heat, distressed by the culmination of pleasure and pain, and firmly secured by hunger and thirst.

sanskrit: छायातपविलेखं च निमेषोन्मेषविह्वलम् ।घोरमोहजनाकीर्णं वर्तमानमचेतनम् ॥४॥
transliteration: chāyātapavilekhaṁ ca nimeṣonmeṣavihvalam ,ghoramohajanākīrṇaṁ vartamānamacetanam.
english: It is marked by the alternation of light and shadow, agitated by the constant closing and opening (of eyes or cycles), crowded with people steeped in dreadful delusion (moha), ever-present, and insentient.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 4. It is marked by the alternation of light and shadow, agitated by the constant closing and opening (of eyes or cycles), crowded with people steeped in dreadful delusion (moha), ever-present, and insentient.

sanskrit: मासार्धमासगणितं विषमं लोकसंचरम् ।तमोनिचयपङ्कं च रजोवेगप्रवर्तकम् ॥५॥
transliteration: māsārdhamāsagaṇitaṁ viṣamaṁ lokasaṁcaram ,tamonicayapaṅkaṁ ca rajovegapravartakam.
english: It is reckoned by months and half-months, difficult, the movement of the world (saṃsāra), a mire of accumulated darkness (tamas), and that which generates the impulse of passion (rajas).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 5. It is reckoned by months and half-months, difficult, the movement of the world (saṃsāra), a mire of accumulated darkness (tamas), and that which generates the impulse of passion (rajas).

sanskrit: सत्त्वालंकारदीप्तं च गुणसंघातमण्डलम् ।स्वरविग्रहनाभीकं शोकसंघातवर्तनम् ॥६॥
transliteration: sattvālaṁkāradīptaṁ ca guṇasaṁghātamaṇḍalam ,svaravigrahanābhīkaṁ śokasaṁghātavartanam.
english: It shines with the ornament of goodness (sattva), and is an assembly of aggregated qualities (guṇas); its center is in the manifestation of sound, and it causes the occurrence of accumulated sorrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 6. It shines with the ornament of goodness (sattva), and is an assembly of aggregated qualities (guṇas); its center is in the manifestation of sound, and it causes the occurrence of accumulated sorrow.

sanskrit: क्रियाकारणसंयुक्तं रागविस्तारमायतम् ।लोभेप्सापरिसंख्यातं विविक्तज्ञानसंभवम् ॥७॥
transliteration: kriyākāraṇasaṁyuktaṁ rāgavistāramāyatam ,lobhepsāparisaṁkhyātaṁ viviktajñānasaṁbhavam.
english: It is connected with action (kriyā) and cause, a vast expansion of attachment (rāga) and desire; it is entirely characterized by greed and longing, and is the potential source of discriminative knowledge (jñāna).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 7. It is connected with action (kriyā) and cause, a vast expansion of attachment (rāga) and desire; it is entirely characterized by greed and longing, and is the potential source of discriminative knowledge (jñāna).

sanskrit: भयमोहपरीवारं भूतसंमोहकारकम् ।आनन्दप्रीतिधारं च कामक्रोधपरिग्रहम् ॥८॥
transliteration: bhayamohaparīvāraṁ bhūtasaṁmohakārakam ,ānandaprītidhāraṁ ca kāmakrodhaparigraham.
english: It is encompassed by fear and delusion, causing the complete bewilderment of beings. It also upholds joy and affection, and is characterized by desire and anger.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 8. It is encompassed by fear and delusion, causing the complete bewilderment of beings. It also upholds joy and affection, and is characterized by desire and anger.

sanskrit: महदादिविशेषान्तमसक्तप्रभवाव्ययम् ।मनोजवनमश्रान्तं कालचक्रं प्रवर्तते ॥९॥
transliteration: mahadādiviśeṣāntamasaktaprabhavāvyayam ,manojavanamaśrāntaṁ kālacakraṁ pravartate.
english: The wheel of time (kālacakra) revolves; it extends from the great principle to the specific elements, is unattached, has an imperishable origin, is swift as thought, and is tireless.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 9. The wheel of time (kālacakra) revolves; it extends from the great principle to the specific elements, is unattached, has an imperishable origin, is swift as thought, and is tireless.

sanskrit: एतद्द्वंद्वसमायुक्तं कालचक्रमचेतनम् ।विसृजेत्संक्षिपेच्चापि बोधयेत्सामरं जगत् ॥१०॥
transliteration: etaddvaṁdvasamāyuktaṁ kālacakramacetanam ,visṛjetsaṁkṣipeccāpi bodhayetsāmaraṁ jagat.
english: This insentient wheel of time (kālacakra), united with pairs of opposites, generates, dissolves, and also illuminates the world along with its divine beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 10. This insentient wheel of time (kālacakra), united with pairs of opposites, generates, dissolves, and also illuminates the world along with its divine beings.

sanskrit: कालचक्रप्रवृत्तिं च निवृत्तिं चैव तत्त्वतः ।यस्तु वेद नरो नित्यं न स भूतेषु मुह्यति ॥११॥
transliteration: kālacakrapravṛttiṁ ca nivṛttiṁ caiva tattvataḥ ,yastu veda naro nityaṁ na sa bhūteṣu muhyati.
english: That person who truly and perpetually understands the operation and the cessation of the wheel of time (kālacakra), he is not deluded among beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 11. That person who truly and perpetually understands the operation and the cessation of the wheel of time (kālacakra), he is not deluded among beings.

sanskrit: विमुक्तः सर्वसंक्लेशैः सर्वद्वंद्वातिगो मुनिः ।विमुक्तः सर्वपापेभ्यः प्राप्नोति परमां गतिम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: vimuktaḥ sarvasaṁkleśaiḥ sarvadvaṁdvātigo muniḥ ,vimuktaḥ sarvapāpebhyaḥ prāpnoti paramāṁ gatim.
english: A contemplative person (muni), freed from all distresses and transcending all dualities, becomes liberated from all sins and thus attains the supreme destination.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 12. A contemplative person (muni), freed from all distresses and transcending all dualities, becomes liberated from all sins and thus attains the supreme destination.

sanskrit: गृहस्थो ब्रह्मचारी च वानप्रस्थोऽथ भिक्षुकः ।चत्वार आश्रमाः प्रोक्ताः सर्वे गार्हस्थ्यमूलकाः ॥१३॥
transliteration: gṛhastho brahmacārī ca vānaprastho'tha bhikṣukaḥ ,catvāra āśramāḥ proktāḥ sarve gārhasthyamūlakāḥ.
english: The householder, the celibate student (brahmacārī), the forest dweller (vānaprastha), and the mendicant – these four stages of life (āśrama) are declared, and all of them are rooted in the householder stage.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 13. The householder, the celibate student (brahmacārī), the forest dweller (vānaprastha), and the mendicant – these four stages of life (āśrama) are declared, and all of them are rooted in the householder stage.

sanskrit: यः कश्चिदिह लोके च ह्यागमः संप्रकीर्तितः ।तस्यान्तगमनं श्रेयः कीर्तिरेषा सनातनी ॥१४॥
transliteration: yaḥ kaścidiha loke ca hyāgamaḥ saṁprakīrtitaḥ ,tasyāntagamanaṁ śreyaḥ kīrtireṣā sanātanī.
english: Whatever tradition or sacred text (āgama) is extensively proclaimed in this world, reaching its culmination is beneficial. This principle is considered eternal.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 14. Whatever tradition or sacred text (āgama) is extensively proclaimed in this world, reaching its culmination is beneficial. This principle is considered eternal.

sanskrit: संस्कारैः संस्कृतः पूर्वं यथावच्चरितव्रतः ।जातौ गुणविशिष्टायां समावर्तेत वेदवित् ॥१५॥
transliteration: saṁskāraiḥ saṁskṛtaḥ pūrvaṁ yathāvaccaritavrataḥ ,jātau guṇaviśiṣṭāyāṁ samāvarteta vedavit.
english: One who has been purified by the prescribed rituals (saṃskāra), who has properly observed his vows earlier, and who is a knower of the Vedas, should return (from his student life) after being born into a family distinguished by virtuous qualities.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 15. One who has been purified by the prescribed rituals (saṃskāra), who has properly observed his vows earlier, and who is a knower of the Vedas, should return (from his student life) after being born into a family distinguished by virtuous qualities.

sanskrit: स्वदारनिरतो दान्तः शिष्टाचारो जितेन्द्रियः ।पञ्चभिश्च महायज्ञैः श्रद्दधानो यजेत ह ॥१६॥
transliteration: svadāranirato dāntaḥ śiṣṭācāro jitendriyaḥ ,pañcabhiśca mahāyajñaiḥ śraddadhāno yajeta ha.
english: A man devoted to his own wife, self-controlled, following noble customs, and having conquered his senses, should devoutly perform the five great (Vedic rituals).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 16. A man devoted to his own wife, self-controlled, following noble customs, and having conquered his senses, should devoutly perform the five great (Vedic rituals).

sanskrit: देवतातिथिशिष्टाशी निरतो वेदकर्मसु ।इज्याप्रदानयुक्तश्च यथाशक्ति यथाविधि ॥१७॥
transliteration: devatātithiśiṣṭāśī nirato vedakarmasu ,ijyāpradānayuktaśca yathāśakti yathāvidhi.
english: One should be devoted to Vedic rites, eating only after serving the deities, guests, and respected individuals. He should also be engaged in performing (Vedic rituals) and making donations (dāna), doing so according to his capacity and in accordance with the prescribed rules.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 17. One should be devoted to Vedic rites, eating only after serving the deities, guests, and respected individuals. He should also be engaged in performing (Vedic rituals) and making donations (dāna), doing so according to his capacity and in accordance with the prescribed rules.

sanskrit: न पाणिपादचपलो न नेत्रचपलो मुनिः ।न च वागङ्गचपल इति शिष्टस्य गोचरः ॥१८॥
transliteration: na pāṇipādacapalo na netracapalo muniḥ ,na ca vāgaṅgacapala iti śiṣṭasya gocaraḥ.
english: A sage (muni) is not restless with their hands and feet, nor with their eyes, nor with their speech and other limbs. This indeed is the mark of a disciplined individual.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 18. A sage (muni) is not restless with their hands and feet, nor with their eyes, nor with their speech and other limbs. This indeed is the mark of a disciplined individual.

sanskrit: नित्ययज्ञोपवीती स्याच्छुक्लवासाः शुचिव्रतः ।नियतो दमदानाभ्यां सदा शिष्टैश्च संविशेत् ॥१९॥
transliteration: nityayajñopavītī syācchuklavāsāḥ śucivrataḥ ,niyato damadānābhyāṁ sadā śiṣṭaiśca saṁviśet.
english: One should always wear the sacred thread, be clad in white garments, and maintain pure vows. He should be self-controlled through restraint and generosity (dāna), and always associate with cultured individuals.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 19. One should always wear the sacred thread, be clad in white garments, and maintain pure vows. He should be self-controlled through restraint and generosity (dāna), and always associate with cultured individuals.

sanskrit: जितशिश्नोदरो मैत्रः शिष्टाचारसमाहितः ।वैणवीं धारयेद्यष्टिं सोदकं च कमण्डलुम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: jitaśiśnodaro maitraḥ śiṣṭācārasamāhitaḥ ,vaiṇavīṁ dhārayedyaṣṭiṁ sodakaṁ ca kamaṇḍalum.
english: One who has disciplined their senses and appetites, is friendly, and is established in proper conduct, should carry a bamboo staff and a water pot filled with water.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 20. One who has disciplined their senses and appetites, is friendly, and is established in proper conduct, should carry a bamboo staff and a water pot filled with water.

sanskrit: अधीत्याध्यापनं कुर्यात्तथा यजनयाजने ।दानं प्रतिग्रहं चैव षड्गुणां वृत्तिमाचरेत् ॥२१॥
transliteration: adhītyādhyāpanaṁ kuryāttathā yajanayājane ,dānaṁ pratigrahaṁ caiva ṣaḍguṇāṁ vṛttimācaret.
english: Having completed one's studies, one should engage in teaching. Similarly, one should perform Vedic rituals (yajña) for oneself and officiate them (yājana) for others. One should also practice the six-fold livelihood, which includes giving (dāna) and receiving donations (pratigraha).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 21. Having completed one's studies, one should engage in teaching. Similarly, one should perform Vedic rituals (yajña) for oneself and officiate them (yājana) for others. One should also practice the six-fold livelihood, which includes giving (dāna) and receiving donations (pratigraha).

sanskrit: त्रीणि कर्माणि यानीह ब्राह्मणानां तु जीविका ।याजनाध्यापने चोभे शुद्धाच्चापि प्रतिग्रहः ॥२२॥
transliteration: trīṇi karmāṇi yānīha brāhmaṇānāṁ tu jīvikā ,yājanādhyāpane cobhe śuddhāccāpi pratigrahaḥ.
english: Indeed, the three duties that constitute the livelihood for brāhmaṇas in this world are both officiating Vedic rituals (yājana) and teaching (adhyāpana), and also receiving donations (pratigraha) from a pure source.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 22. Indeed, the three duties that constitute the livelihood for brāhmaṇas in this world are both officiating Vedic rituals (yājana) and teaching (adhyāpana), and also receiving donations (pratigraha) from a pure source.

sanskrit: अवशेषाणि चान्यानि त्रीणि कर्माणि यानि तु ।दानमध्ययनं यज्ञो धर्मयुक्तानि तानि तु ॥२३॥
transliteration: avaśeṣāṇi cānyāni trīṇi karmāṇi yāni tu ,dānamadhyayanaṁ yajño dharmayuktāni tāni tu.
english: And the other three remaining duties, which are giving (dāna), studying (adhyayana), and performing Vedic rituals (yajña), these indeed are imbued with natural law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 23. And the other three remaining duties, which are giving (dāna), studying (adhyayana), and performing Vedic rituals (yajña), these indeed are imbued with natural law (dharma).

sanskrit: तेष्वप्रमादं कुर्वीत त्रिषु कर्मसु धर्मवित् ।दान्तो मैत्रः क्षमायुक्तः सर्वभूतसमो मुनिः ॥२४॥
transliteration: teṣvapramādaṁ kurvīta triṣu karmasu dharmavit ,dānto maitraḥ kṣamāyuktaḥ sarvabhūtasamo muniḥ.
english: The sage (muni) who understands natural law (dharma), is self-controlled, friendly, endowed with patience, and views all beings equally, should be attentive in these three actions.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 24. The sage (muni) who understands natural law (dharma), is self-controlled, friendly, endowed with patience, and views all beings equally, should be attentive in these three actions.

sanskrit: सर्वमेतद्यथाशक्ति विप्रो निर्वर्तयञ्शुचिः ।एवं युक्तो जयेत्स्वर्गं गृहस्थः संशितव्रतः ॥२५॥
transliteration: sarvametadyathāśakti vipro nirvartayañśuciḥ ,evaṁ yukto jayetsvargaṁ gṛhasthaḥ saṁśitavrataḥ.
english: A pure Brahmin (vipra) who, as a householder with resolute vows, performs all this according to his capacity and is thus engaged, should attain heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 45 Verse 25. A pure Brahmin (vipra) who, as a householder with resolute vows, performs all this according to his capacity and is thus engaged, should attain heaven.

sanskrit: ब्रह्मोवाच ।एवमेतेन मार्गेण पूर्वोक्तेन यथाविधि ।अधीतवान्यथाशक्ति तथैव ब्रह्मचर्यवान् ॥१॥
transliteration: brahmovāca ,evametena mārgeṇa pūrvoktena yathāvidhi ,adhītavānyathāśakti tathaiva brahmacaryavān.
english: Brahmā said: "Thus, by this previously mentioned path, according to the prescribed rules, the one who has studied according to their capacity, and likewise maintained (brahmacarya)."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 1. Brahmā said: "Thus, by this previously mentioned path, according to the prescribed rules, the one who has studied according to their capacity, and likewise maintained (brahmacarya)."

sanskrit: स्वधर्मनिरतो विद्वान्सर्वेन्द्रिययतो मुनिः ।गुरोः प्रियहिते युक्तः सत्यधर्मपरः शुचिः ॥२॥
transliteration: svadharmanirato vidvānsarvendriyayato muniḥ ,guroḥ priyahite yuktaḥ satyadharmaparaḥ śuciḥ.
english: A wise sage (muni), devoted to his prescribed duty (svadharma), with all senses restrained, engaged in actions for the welfare and benefit of his teacher (guru), dedicated to truth and natural law (dharma), and pure.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 2. A wise sage (muni), devoted to his prescribed duty (svadharma), with all senses restrained, engaged in actions for the welfare and benefit of his teacher (guru), dedicated to truth and natural law (dharma), and pure.

sanskrit: गुरुणा समनुज्ञातो भुञ्जीतान्नमकुत्सयन् ।हविष्यभैक्ष्यभुक्चापि स्थानासनविहारवान् ॥३॥
transliteration: guruṇā samanujñāto bhuñjītānnamakutsayan ,haviṣyabhaikṣyabhukcāpi sthānāsanavihāravān.
english: Having been permitted by his teacher (guru), he should eat food without finding fault, consuming only sacred food or alms, and maintaining moderation in his posture, seating, and movements.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 3. Having been permitted by his teacher (guru), he should eat food without finding fault, consuming only sacred food or alms, and maintaining moderation in his posture, seating, and movements.

sanskrit: द्विकालमग्निं जुह्वानः शुचिर्भूत्वा समाहितः ।धारयीत सदा दण्डं बैल्वं पालाशमेव वा ॥४॥
transliteration: dvikālamagniṁ juhvānaḥ śucirbhūtvā samāhitaḥ ,dhārayīta sadā daṇḍaṁ bailvaṁ pālāśameva vā.
english: Having become pure and composed, he should perform the Vedic fire ritual (homa) twice daily, and always carry a staff made of bilva or palāśa wood.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 4. Having become pure and composed, he should perform the Vedic fire ritual (homa) twice daily, and always carry a staff made of bilva or palāśa wood.

sanskrit: क्षौमं कार्पासिकं वापि मृगाजिनमथापि वा ।सर्वं काषायरक्तं स्याद्वासो वापि द्विजस्य ह ॥५॥
transliteration: kṣaumaṁ kārpāsikaṁ vāpi mṛgājinamathāpi vā ,sarvaṁ kāṣāyaraktaṁ syādvāso vāpi dvijasya ha.
english: All garments of the twice-born (dvija), whether linen or cotton, or even a deerskin, should be dyed reddish-brown.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 5. All garments of the twice-born (dvija), whether linen or cotton, or even a deerskin, should be dyed reddish-brown.

sanskrit: मेखला च भवेन्मौञ्जी जटी नित्योदकस्तथा ।यज्ञोपवीती स्वाध्यायी अलुप्तनियतव्रतः ॥६॥
transliteration: mekhalā ca bhavenmauñjī jaṭī nityodakastathā ,yajñopavītī svādhyāyī aluptaniyatavrataḥ.
english: And his girdle should be made of muñja grass; he should wear matted hair, always possess water (for purity), similarly wear the sacred thread, be devoted to self-study (svādhyāya), and strictly adhere to his prescribed vows.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 6. And his girdle should be made of muñja grass; he should wear matted hair, always possess water (for purity), similarly wear the sacred thread, be devoted to self-study (svādhyāya), and strictly adhere to his prescribed vows.

sanskrit: पूताभिश्च तथैवाद्भिः सदा दैवततर्पणम् ।भावेन नियतः कुर्वन्ब्रह्मचारी प्रशस्यते ॥७॥
transliteration: pūtābhiśca tathaivādbhiḥ sadā daivatatarpaṇam ,bhāvena niyataḥ kurvanbrahmacārī praśasyate.
english: A student (brahmacārī) who is self-controlled and always performs the ritual libation to the deities with devotion using pure waters is praised.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 7. A student (brahmacārī) who is self-controlled and always performs the ritual libation to the deities with devotion using pure waters is praised.

sanskrit: एवं युक्तो जयेत्स्वर्गमूर्ध्वरेताः समाहितः ।न संसरति जातीषु परमं स्थानमाश्रितः ॥८॥
transliteration: evaṁ yukto jayetsvargamūrdhvaretāḥ samāhitaḥ ,na saṁsarati jātīṣu paramaṁ sthānamāśritaḥ.
english: One who is thus disciplined, celibate (ūrdhvaretāḥ), and concentrated will conquer heaven. Having attained the supreme state, he does not transmigrate through various births (saṃsāra).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 8. One who is thus disciplined, celibate (ūrdhvaretāḥ), and concentrated will conquer heaven. Having attained the supreme state, he does not transmigrate through various births (saṃsāra).

sanskrit: संस्कृतः सर्वसंस्कारैस्तथैव ब्रह्मचर्यवान् ।ग्रामान्निष्क्रम्य चारण्यं मुनिः प्रव्रजितो वसेत् ॥९॥
transliteration: saṁskṛtaḥ sarvasaṁskāraistathaiva brahmacaryavān ,grāmānniṣkramya cāraṇyaṁ muniḥ pravrajito vaset.
english: A sage (muni) who has been perfected by all the purificatory rites (saṃskāras) and likewise observes celibacy, should, having renounced the world, leave the village and dwell in the forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 9. A sage (muni) who has been perfected by all the purificatory rites (saṃskāras) and likewise observes celibacy, should, having renounced the world, leave the village and dwell in the forest.

sanskrit: चर्मवल्कलसंवीतः स्वयं प्रातरुपस्पृशेत् ।अरण्यगोचरो नित्यं न ग्रामं प्रविशेत्पुनः ॥१०॥
transliteration: carmavalkalasaṁvītaḥ svayaṁ prātarupaspṛśet ,araṇyagocaro nityaṁ na grāmaṁ praviśetpunaḥ.
english: Dressed in skin and bark garments, he should perform his morning ablutions himself. Always dwelling in the forest, he should not enter the village again.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 10. Dressed in skin and bark garments, he should perform his morning ablutions himself. Always dwelling in the forest, he should not enter the village again.

sanskrit: अर्चयन्नतिथीन्काले दद्याच्चापि प्रतिश्रयम् ।फलपत्रावरैर्मूलैः श्यामाकेन च वर्तयन् ॥११॥
transliteration: arcayannatithīnkāle dadyāccāpi pratiśrayam ,phalapatrāvarairmūlaiḥ śyāmākena ca vartayan.
english: Honoring guests at the proper time, one should also offer them shelter. One should sustain oneself with fruits, leaves, inferior roots, and śyāmāka millet.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 11. Honoring guests at the proper time, one should also offer them shelter. One should sustain oneself with fruits, leaves, inferior roots, and śyāmāka millet.

sanskrit: प्रवृत्तमुदकं वायुं सर्वं वानेयमा तृणात् ।प्राश्नीयादानुपूर्व्येण यथादीक्षमतन्द्रितः ॥१२॥
transliteration: pravṛttamudakaṁ vāyuṁ sarvaṁ vāneyamā tṛṇāt ,prāśnīyādānupūrvyeṇa yathādīkṣamatandritaḥ.
english: One should diligently consume flowing water, air, and all things grown in the forest, even down to grass, in the prescribed order and according to one's spiritual vow (dīkṣā).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 12. One should diligently consume flowing water, air, and all things grown in the forest, even down to grass, in the prescribed order and according to one's spiritual vow (dīkṣā).

sanskrit: आमूलफलभिक्षाभिरर्चेदतिथिमागतम् ।यद्भक्षः स्यात्ततो दद्याद्भिक्षां नित्यमतन्द्रितः ॥१३॥
transliteration: āmūlaphalabhikṣābhirarcedatithimāgatam ,yadbhakṣaḥ syāttato dadyādbhikṣāṁ nityamatandritaḥ.
english: One should honor an arrived guest with alms consisting of roots and fruits. Whatever food one (the host) may consume, from that one should always diligently offer alms.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 13. One should honor an arrived guest with alms consisting of roots and fruits. Whatever food one (the host) may consume, from that one should always diligently offer alms.

sanskrit: देवतातिथिपूर्वं च सदा भुञ्जीत वाग्यतः ।अस्कन्दितमनाश्चैव लघ्वाशी देवताश्रयः ॥१४॥
transliteration: devatātithipūrvaṁ ca sadā bhuñjīta vāgyataḥ ,askanditamanāścaiva laghvāśī devatāśrayaḥ.
english: And always, with restrained speech, one should eat only after serving the deities and guests first. (One should be) of unperturbed mind, eating lightly, and devoted to the deities.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 14. And always, with restrained speech, one should eat only after serving the deities and guests first. (One should be) of unperturbed mind, eating lightly, and devoted to the deities.

sanskrit: दान्तो मैत्रः क्षमायुक्तः केशश्मश्रु च धारयन् ।जुह्वन्स्वाध्यायशीलश्च सत्यधर्मपरायणः ॥१५॥
transliteration: dānto maitraḥ kṣamāyuktaḥ keśaśmaśru ca dhārayan ,juhvansvādhyāyaśīlaśca satyadharmaparāyaṇaḥ.
english: One who is self-controlled, benevolent, endowed with patience, allows his hair and beard to grow, performs Vedic fire rituals, is devoted to sacred study, and is dedicated to truth and natural law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 15. One who is self-controlled, benevolent, endowed with patience, allows his hair and beard to grow, performs Vedic fire rituals, is devoted to sacred study, and is dedicated to truth and natural law (dharma).

sanskrit: त्यक्तदेहः सदा दक्षो वननित्यः समाहितः ।एवं युक्तो जयेत्स्वर्गं वानप्रस्थो जितेन्द्रियः ॥१६॥
transliteration: tyaktadehaḥ sadā dakṣo vananityaḥ samāhitaḥ ,evaṁ yukto jayetsvargaṁ vānaprastho jitendriyaḥ.
english: A forest-dweller (vānaprastha) who has renounced attachment to the body, is always diligent, constantly dwells in the forest, and is composed; such a one, with senses subdued, would thus conquer heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 16. A forest-dweller (vānaprastha) who has renounced attachment to the body, is always diligent, constantly dwells in the forest, and is composed; such a one, with senses subdued, would thus conquer heaven.

sanskrit: गृहस्थो ब्रह्मचारी च वानप्रस्थोऽथ वा पुनः ।य इच्छेन्मोक्षमास्थातुमुत्तमां वृत्तिमाश्रयेत् ॥१७॥
transliteration: gṛhastho brahmacārī ca vānaprastho'tha vā punaḥ ,ya icchenmokṣamāsthātumuttamāṁ vṛttimāśrayet.
english: Whether one is a householder, a student (brahmacārī), or a forest-dweller (vānaprastha), whoever desires to attain final liberation (mokṣa) should adopt the highest mode of conduct.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 17. Whether one is a householder, a student (brahmacārī), or a forest-dweller (vānaprastha), whoever desires to attain final liberation (mokṣa) should adopt the highest mode of conduct.

sanskrit: अभयं सर्वभूतेभ्यो दत्त्वा नैष्कर्म्यमाचरेत् ।सर्वभूतहितो मैत्रः सर्वेन्द्रिययतो मुनिः ॥१८॥
transliteration: abhayaṁ sarvabhūtebhyo dattvā naiṣkarmyamācaret ,sarvabhūtahito maitraḥ sarvendriyayato muniḥ.
english: Having granted freedom from fear to all beings, one should practice non-action (naiṣkarmya). A sage (muni) of this kind is benevolent to all creatures, friendly, and has restrained all his senses.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 18. Having granted freedom from fear to all beings, one should practice non-action (naiṣkarmya). A sage (muni) of this kind is benevolent to all creatures, friendly, and has restrained all his senses.

sanskrit: अयाचितमसंकॢप्तमुपपन्नं यदृच्छया ।जोषयेत सदा भोज्यं ग्रासमागतमस्पृहः ॥१९॥
transliteration: ayācitamasaṁkḷptamupapannaṁ yadṛcchayā ,joṣayeta sadā bhojyaṁ grāsamāgatamaspṛhaḥ.
english: A desireless person should always partake, without longing, of food (a morsel) that arrives spontaneously, unasked for, and unprepared.
