search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 19. A desireless person should always partake, without longing, of food (a morsel) that arrives spontaneously, unasked for, and unprepared.

sanskrit: यात्रामात्रं च भुञ्जीत केवलं प्राणयात्रिकम् ।धर्मलब्धं तथाश्नीयान्न काममनुवर्तयेत् ॥२०॥
transliteration: yātrāmātraṁ ca bhuñjīta kevalaṁ prāṇayātrikam ,dharmalabdhaṁ tathāśnīyānna kāmamanuvartayet.
english: One should eat only for bodily maintenance and solely for the sustenance of life. Similarly, one should consume what is righteously acquired (dharma-labdham), and not yield to desire (kāma).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 20. One should eat only for bodily maintenance and solely for the sustenance of life. Similarly, one should consume what is righteously acquired (dharma-labdham), and not yield to desire (kāma).

sanskrit: ग्रासादाच्छादनाच्चान्यन्न गृह्णीयात्कथंचन ।यावदाहारयेत्तावत्प्रतिगृह्णीत नान्यथा ॥२१॥
transliteration: grāsādācchādanāccānyanna gṛhṇīyātkathaṁcana ,yāvadāhārayettāvatpratigṛhṇīta nānyathā.
english: One should not accept anything beyond a mere morsel of food and covering (clothing), under any circumstances. One should accept only as much as is necessary for sustenance, and no more.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 21. One should not accept anything beyond a mere morsel of food and covering (clothing), under any circumstances. One should accept only as much as is necessary for sustenance, and no more.

sanskrit: परेभ्यो न प्रतिग्राह्यं न च देयं कदाचन ।दैन्यभावाच्च भूतानां संविभज्य सदा बुधः ॥२२॥
transliteration: parebhyo na pratigrāhyaṁ na ca deyaṁ kadācana ,dainyabhāvācca bhūtānāṁ saṁvibhajya sadā budhaḥ.
english: A wise person (budha) should never accept anything from others, nor should anything (beyond what is essential) be given at any time. Yet, always considering the pitiable state of living beings, the wise one should distribute (what is fitting).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 22. A wise person (budha) should never accept anything from others, nor should anything (beyond what is essential) be given at any time. Yet, always considering the pitiable state of living beings, the wise one should distribute (what is fitting).

sanskrit: नाददीत परस्वानि न गृह्णीयादयाचितम् ।न किंचिद्विषयं भुक्त्वा स्पृहयेत्तस्य वै पुनः ॥२३॥
transliteration: nādadīta parasvāni na gṛhṇīyādayācitam ,na kiṁcidviṣayaṁ bhuktvā spṛhayettasya vai punaḥ.
english: One should not take what belongs to others, nor should one accept what is not offered. Having once experienced any sense object, one should not long for it again.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 23. One should not take what belongs to others, nor should one accept what is not offered. Having once experienced any sense object, one should not long for it again.

sanskrit: मृदमापस्तथाश्मानं पत्रपुष्पफलानि च ।असंवृतानि गृह्णीयात्प्रवृत्तानीह कार्यवान् ॥२४॥
transliteration: mṛdamāpastathāśmānaṁ patrapuṣpaphalāni ca ,asaṁvṛtāni gṛhṇīyātpravṛttānīha kāryavān.
english: A person engaged in their duties may take earth, water, stones, and leaves, flowers, and fruits, even if not explicitly presented, provided they are readily available.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 24. A person engaged in their duties may take earth, water, stones, and leaves, flowers, and fruits, even if not explicitly presented, provided they are readily available.

sanskrit: न शिल्पजीविकां जीवेद्द्विरन्नं नोत कामयेत् ।न द्वेष्टा नोपदेष्टा च भवेत निरुपस्कृतः ।श्रद्धापूतानि भुञ्जीत निमित्तानि विवर्जयेत् ॥२५॥
transliteration: na śilpajīvikāṁ jīveddvirannaṁ nota kāmayet ,na dveṣṭā nopadeṣṭā ca bhaveta nirupaskṛtaḥ ,śraddhāpūtāni bhuñjīta nimittāni vivarjayet.
english: One should not live by a craft, nor should one desire double food. One should not be an enemy or an instructor, but rather remain free from external help or embellishment. One should consume food purified by faith (śraddhā) and avoid ominous signs.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 25. One should not live by a craft, nor should one desire double food. One should not be an enemy or an instructor, but rather remain free from external help or embellishment. One should consume food purified by faith (śraddhā) and avoid ominous signs.

sanskrit: मुधावृत्तिरसक्तश्च सर्वभूतैरसंविदम् ।कृत्वा वह्निं चरेद्भैक्ष्यं विधूमे भुक्तवज्जने ॥२६॥
transliteration: mudhāvṛttirasaktaśca sarvabhūtairasaṁvidam ,kṛtvā vahniṁ caredbhaikṣyaṁ vidhūme bhuktavajjane.
english: Having performed the fire (vahni) ritual, one should wander for alms when the smoke has ceased and people have finished their meals, living without a definite occupation, detached, and without specific social interaction with all beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 26. Having performed the fire (vahni) ritual, one should wander for alms when the smoke has ceased and people have finished their meals, living without a definite occupation, detached, and without specific social interaction with all beings.

sanskrit: वृत्ते शरावसंपाते भैक्ष्यं लिप्सेत मोक्षवित् ।लाभे न च प्रहृष्येत नालाभे विमना भवेत् ॥२७॥
transliteration: vṛtte śarāvasaṁpāte bhaikṣyaṁ lipseta mokṣavit ,lābhe na ca prahṛṣyeta nālābhe vimanā bhavet.
english: After the alms have been collected in the bowl, the knower of liberation (mokṣa) should accept them. One should neither rejoice upon receiving alms nor become dejected upon not receiving them.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 27. After the alms have been collected in the bowl, the knower of liberation (mokṣa) should accept them. One should neither rejoice upon receiving alms nor become dejected upon not receiving them.

sanskrit: मात्राशी कालमाकाङ्क्षंश्चरेद्भैक्ष्यं समाहितः ।लाभं साधारणं नेच्छेन्न भुञ्जीताभिपूजितः ।अभिपूजितलाभाद्धि विजुगुप्सेत भिक्षुकः ॥२८॥
transliteration: mātrāśī kālamākāṅkṣaṁścaredbhaikṣyaṁ samāhitaḥ ,lābhaṁ sādhāraṇaṁ necchenna bhuñjītābhipūjitaḥ ,abhipūjitalābhāddhi vijugupseta bhikṣukaḥ.
english: Eating in moderation and awaiting the opportune moment, one should seek alms with a concentrated mind. One should not desire ordinary worldly gain, nor should one eat when highly honored. Indeed, a mendicant should feel aversion towards any gain obtained through being honored.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 28. Eating in moderation and awaiting the opportune moment, one should seek alms with a concentrated mind. One should not desire ordinary worldly gain, nor should one eat when highly honored. Indeed, a mendicant should feel aversion towards any gain obtained through being honored.

sanskrit: शुक्तान्यम्लानि तिक्तानि कषायकटुकानि च ।नास्वादयीत भुञ्जानो रसांश्च मधुरांस्तथा ।यात्रामात्रं च भुञ्जीत केवलं प्राणयात्रिकम् ॥२९॥
transliteration: śuktānyamlāni tiktāni kaṣāyakaṭukāni ca ,nāsvādayīta bhuñjāno rasāṁśca madhurāṁstathā ,yātrāmātraṁ ca bhuñjīta kevalaṁ prāṇayātrikam.
english: While eating, one should not savor fermented, sour, bitter, astringent, or pungent tastes, nor sweet tastes. One should eat only for sustenance, solely for the maintenance of life.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 29. While eating, one should not savor fermented, sour, bitter, astringent, or pungent tastes, nor sweet tastes. One should eat only for sustenance, solely for the maintenance of life.

sanskrit: असंरोधेन भूतानां वृत्तिं लिप्सेत मोक्षवित् ।न चान्यमनुभिक्षेत भिक्षमाणः कथंचन ॥३०॥
transliteration: asaṁrodhena bhūtānāṁ vṛttiṁ lipseta mokṣavit ,na cānyamanubhikṣeta bhikṣamāṇaḥ kathaṁcana.
english: The knower of liberation (mokṣa) should seek sustenance without causing harm to any beings. And while engaged in begging, one should never beg again from another person.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 30. The knower of liberation (mokṣa) should seek sustenance without causing harm to any beings. And while engaged in begging, one should never beg again from another person.

sanskrit: न संनिकाशयेद्धर्मं विविक्ते विरजाश्चरेत् ।शून्यागारमरण्यं वा वृक्षमूलं नदीं तथा ।प्रतिश्रयार्थं सेवेत पार्वतीं वा पुनर्गुहाम् ॥३१॥
transliteration: na saṁnikāśayeddharmaṁ vivikte virajāścaret ,śūnyāgāramaraṇyaṁ vā vṛkṣamūlaṁ nadīṁ tathā ,pratiśrayārthaṁ seveta pārvatīṁ vā punarguhām.
english: One should not publicize their spiritual practice (dharma). A person free from worldly attachments should wander in a solitary place, taking shelter in an empty house, a forest, at the foot of a tree, by a river, or a mountain (region), or again, a cave.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 31. One should not publicize their spiritual practice (dharma). A person free from worldly attachments should wander in a solitary place, taking shelter in an empty house, a forest, at the foot of a tree, by a river, or a mountain (region), or again, a cave.

sanskrit: ग्रामैकरात्रिको ग्रीष्मे वर्षास्वेकत्र वा वसेत् ।अध्वा सूर्येण निर्दिष्टः कीटवच्च चरेन्महीम् ॥३२॥
transliteration: grāmaikarātriko grīṣme varṣāsvekatra vā vaset ,adhvā sūryeṇa nirdiṣṭaḥ kīṭavacca carenmahīm.
english: During summer, one should stay for only one night in a village, or during the rainy season, one should reside in a single place. Their travel path should be guided by the sun, and they should wander the earth like an insect.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 32. During summer, one should stay for only one night in a village, or during the rainy season, one should reside in a single place. Their travel path should be guided by the sun, and they should wander the earth like an insect.

sanskrit: दयार्थं चैव भूतानां समीक्ष्य पृथिवीं चरेत् ।संचयांश्च न कुर्वीत स्नेहवासं च वर्जयेत् ॥३३॥
transliteration: dayārthaṁ caiva bhūtānāṁ samīkṣya pṛthivīṁ caret ,saṁcayāṁśca na kurvīta snehavāsaṁ ca varjayet.
english: Indeed, out of compassion for all living beings, one should wander the earth carefully, having first surveyed the ground. One should not accumulate possessions, and one should avoid dwelling in attachment.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 33. Indeed, out of compassion for all living beings, one should wander the earth carefully, having first surveyed the ground. One should not accumulate possessions, and one should avoid dwelling in attachment.

sanskrit: पूतेन चाम्भसा नित्यं कार्यं कुर्वीत मोक्षवित् ।उपस्पृशेदुद्धृताभिरद्भिश्च पुरुषः सदा ॥३४॥
transliteration: pūtena cāmbhasā nityaṁ kāryaṁ kurvīta mokṣavit ,upaspṛśeduddhṛtābhiradbhiśca puruṣaḥ sadā.
english: One who understands liberation (mokṣa) should always perform their duties with pure water. A person (puruṣa) should always perform ritual purification by touching or sipping water that has been drawn (from a source).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 34. One who understands liberation (mokṣa) should always perform their duties with pure water. A person (puruṣa) should always perform ritual purification by touching or sipping water that has been drawn (from a source).

sanskrit: अहिंसा ब्रह्मचर्यं च सत्यमार्जवमेव च ।अक्रोधश्चानसूया च दमो नित्यमपैशुनम् ॥३५॥
transliteration: ahiṁsā brahmacaryaṁ ca satyamārjavameva ca ,akrodhaścānasūyā ca damo nityamapaiśunam.
english: Non-violence (ahiṃsā), celibacy (brahmacarya), truthfulness, straightforwardness, freedom from anger, absence of envy, self-control, and constant absence of malice.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 35. Non-violence (ahiṃsā), celibacy (brahmacarya), truthfulness, straightforwardness, freedom from anger, absence of envy, self-control, and constant absence of malice.

sanskrit: अष्टास्वेतेषु युक्तः स्याद्व्रतेषु नियतेन्द्रियः ।अपापमशठं वृत्तमजिह्मं नित्यमाचरेत् ॥३६॥
transliteration: aṣṭāsveteṣu yuktaḥ syādvrateṣu niyatendriyaḥ ,apāpamaśaṭhaṁ vṛttamajihmaṁ nityamācaret.
english: With senses controlled, one should be committed to these eight vows. One should always engage in conduct that is sinless, free from deceit, and honest.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 36. With senses controlled, one should be committed to these eight vows. One should always engage in conduct that is sinless, free from deceit, and honest.

sanskrit: आशीर्युक्तानि कर्माणि हिंसायुक्तानि यानि च ।लोकसंग्रहधर्मं च नैव कुर्यान्न कारयेत् ॥३७॥
transliteration: āśīryuktāni karmāṇi hiṁsāyuktāni yāni ca ,lokasaṁgrahadharmaṁ ca naiva kuryānna kārayet.
english: One should neither perform nor cause to be performed actions (karma) that are motivated by wishes or involve violence, nor the intrinsic nature (dharma) of maintaining social cohesion.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 37. One should neither perform nor cause to be performed actions (karma) that are motivated by wishes or involve violence, nor the intrinsic nature (dharma) of maintaining social cohesion.

sanskrit: सर्वभावानतिक्रम्य लघुमात्रः परिव्रजेत् ।समः सर्वेषु भूतेषु स्थावरेषु चरेषु च ॥३८॥
transliteration: sarvabhāvānatikramya laghumātraḥ parivrajet ,samaḥ sarveṣu bhūteṣu sthāvareṣu careṣu ca.
english: Having transcended all states of being, one should wander (as an ascetic, parivrājaka) with very few possessions, treating all beings equally, whether they are stationary or moving.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 38. Having transcended all states of being, one should wander (as an ascetic, parivrājaka) with very few possessions, treating all beings equally, whether they are stationary or moving.

sanskrit: परं नोद्वेजयेत्कंचिन्न च कस्यचिदुद्विजेत् ।विश्वास्यः सर्वभूतानामग्र्यो मोक्षविदुच्यते ॥३९॥
transliteration: paraṁ nodvejayetkaṁcinna ca kasyacidudvijet ,viśvāsyaḥ sarvabhūtānāmagryo mokṣaviducyate.
english: One should not distress anyone, nor should one be distressed by anyone. Such a person, trustworthy to all beings and foremost, is called a knower of liberation (mokṣa).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 39. One should not distress anyone, nor should one be distressed by anyone. Such a person, trustworthy to all beings and foremost, is called a knower of liberation (mokṣa).

sanskrit: अनागतं च न ध्यायेन्नातीतमनुचिन्तयेत् ।वर्तमानमुपेक्षेत कालाकाङ्क्षी समाहितः ॥४०॥
transliteration: anāgataṁ ca na dhyāyennātītamanucintayet ,vartamānamupekṣeta kālākāṅkṣī samāhitaḥ.
english: One should not contemplate what is yet to come, nor dwell on what has passed. Composed and awaiting the opportune moment, one should remain detached from the present.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 40. One should not contemplate what is yet to come, nor dwell on what has passed. Composed and awaiting the opportune moment, one should remain detached from the present.

sanskrit: न चक्षुषा न मनसा न वाचा दूषयेत्क्वचित् ।न प्रत्यक्षं परोक्षं वा किंचिद्दुष्टं समाचरेत् ॥४१॥
transliteration: na cakṣuṣā na manasā na vācā dūṣayetkvacit ,na pratyakṣaṁ parokṣaṁ vā kiṁcidduṣṭaṁ samācaret.
english: One should never find fault with anyone by sight, by mind, or by speech. Nor should one commit any wrongful act, whether overtly or covertly.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 41. One should never find fault with anyone by sight, by mind, or by speech. Nor should one commit any wrongful act, whether overtly or covertly.

sanskrit: इन्द्रियाण्युपसंहृत्य कूर्मोऽङ्गानीव सर्वशः ।क्षीणेन्द्रियमनोबुद्धिर्निरीक्षेत निरिन्द्रियः ॥४२॥
transliteration: indriyāṇyupasaṁhṛtya kūrmo'ṅgānīva sarvaśaḥ ,kṣīṇendriyamanobuddhirnirīkṣeta nirindriyaḥ.
english: Just as a tortoise withdraws all its limbs, so too, having completely withdrawn the senses, one should observe, with senses, mind, and intellect subdued, as if free from the senses.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 42. Just as a tortoise withdraws all its limbs, so too, having completely withdrawn the senses, one should observe, with senses, mind, and intellect subdued, as if free from the senses.

sanskrit: निर्द्वंद्वो निर्नमस्कारो निःस्वाहाकार एव च ।निर्ममो निरहंकारो निर्योगक्षेम एव च ॥४३॥
transliteration: nirdvaṁdvo nirnamaskāro niḥsvāhākāra eva ca ,nirmamo nirahaṁkāro niryogakṣema eva ca.
english: One who is free from pairs of opposites, free from formal salutations, and also free from the utterance of "svāhā" during Vedic rituals; one who is free from possessiveness, free from ego (ahaṅkāra), and also free from worries about acquisition and preservation.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 43. One who is free from pairs of opposites, free from formal salutations, and also free from the utterance of "svāhā" during Vedic rituals; one who is free from possessiveness, free from ego (ahaṅkāra), and also free from worries about acquisition and preservation.

sanskrit: निराशीः सर्वभूतेषु निरासङ्गो निराश्रयः ।सर्वज्ञः सर्वतो मुक्तो मुच्यते नात्र संशयः ॥४४॥
transliteration: nirāśīḥ sarvabhūteṣu nirāsaṅgo nirāśrayaḥ ,sarvajñaḥ sarvato mukto mucyate nātra saṁśayaḥ.
english: One who is without expectations from all beings, unattached, and without (external) support; such an all-knowing, completely liberated one is indeed freed - there is no doubt about this.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 44. One who is without expectations from all beings, unattached, and without (external) support; such an all-knowing, completely liberated one is indeed freed - there is no doubt about this.

sanskrit: अपाणिपादपृष्ठं तमशिरस्कमनूदरम् ।प्रहीणगुणकर्माणं केवलं विमलं स्थिरम् ॥४५॥
transliteration: apāṇipādapṛṣṭhaṁ tamaśiraskamanūdaram ,prahīṇaguṇakarmāṇaṁ kevalaṁ vimalaṁ sthiram.
english: That which is without hands, feet, or back, without a head, and without a belly; that which is devoid of (material) qualities (guṇa) and actions (karma), which is absolute, pure, and stable.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 45. That which is without hands, feet, or back, without a head, and without a belly; that which is devoid of (material) qualities (guṇa) and actions (karma), which is absolute, pure, and stable.

sanskrit: अगन्धरसमस्पर्शमरूपाशब्दमेव च ।अत्वगस्थ्यथ वामज्जममांसमपि चैव ह ॥४६॥
transliteration: agandharasamasparśamarūpāśabdameva ca ,atvagasthyatha vāmajjamamāṁsamapi caiva ha.
english: And also that which is without smell, taste, or touch; without form or sound; without skin, bones, or marrow; and indeed, it is also without flesh.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 46. And also that which is without smell, taste, or touch; without form or sound; without skin, bones, or marrow; and indeed, it is also without flesh.

sanskrit: निश्चिन्तमव्ययं नित्यं हृदिस्थमपि नित्यदा ।सर्वभूतस्थमात्मानं ये पश्यन्ति न ते मृताः ॥४७॥
transliteration: niścintamavyayaṁ nityaṁ hṛdisthamapi nityadā ,sarvabhūtasthamātmānaṁ ye paśyanti na te mṛtāḥ.
english: Those who perceive the self (ātman) that is free from worry, imperishable, and eternal, dwelling constantly in the heart and in all beings - they are not subject to death.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 47. Those who perceive the self (ātman) that is free from worry, imperishable, and eternal, dwelling constantly in the heart and in all beings - they are not subject to death.

sanskrit: न तत्र क्रमते बुद्धिर्नेन्द्रियाणि न देवताः ।वेदा यज्ञाश्च लोकाश्च न तपो न पराक्रमः ।यत्र ज्ञानवतां प्राप्तिरलिङ्गग्रहणा स्मृता ॥४८॥
transliteration: na tatra kramate buddhirnendriyāṇi na devatāḥ ,vedā yajñāśca lokāśca na tapo na parākramaḥ ,yatra jñānavatāṁ prāptiraliṅgagrahaṇā smṛtā.
english: To that [supreme reality], neither the intellect (buddhi) nor the senses nor the deities can approach. Neither the Vedas, nor Vedic rituals (yajña), nor the [heavenly] worlds, nor asceticism (tapas), nor prowess can attain it. That is remembered as the attainment of the wise, characterized by the apprehension of the unmanifest.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 48. To that [supreme reality], neither the intellect (buddhi) nor the senses nor the deities can approach. That is remembered as the attainment of the wise, characterized by the apprehension of the unmanifest.

sanskrit: तस्मादलिङ्गो धर्मज्ञो धर्मव्रतमनुव्रतः ।गूढधर्माश्रितो विद्वानज्ञातचरितं चरेत् ॥४९॥
transliteration: tasmādaliṅgo dharmajño dharmavratamanuvrataḥ ,gūḍhadharmāśrito vidvānajñātacaritaṁ caret.
english: Therefore, a wise person (vidvān) who is beyond manifest qualities, a knower of natural law (dharma), dedicated to the observance of natural law (dharma), and one who has resorted to profound natural law (dharma), should lead a life (carita) of unknown conduct.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 49. sanskrit: अमूढो मूढरूपेण चरेद्धर्ममदूषयन् ।यथैनमवमन्येरन्परे सततमेव हि ॥५०॥
transliteration: amūḍho mūḍharūpeṇa careddharmamadūṣayan ,yathainamavamanyeranpare satatameva hi.
english: Though undeluded, one should live in the guise of a fool, without violating natural law (dharma), so that others may indeed constantly disrespect him.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 50. Though undeluded, one should live in the guise of a fool, without violating natural law (dharma), so that others may indeed constantly disrespect him.

sanskrit: तथावृत्तश्चरेद्धर्मं सतां वर्त्माविदूषयन् ।यो ह्येवं वृत्तसंपन्नः स मुनिः श्रेष्ठ उच्यते ॥५१॥
transliteration: tathāvṛttaścareddharmaṁ satāṁ vartmāvidūṣayan ,yo hyevaṁ vṛttasaṁpannaḥ sa muniḥ śreṣṭha ucyate.
english: One should practice the natural law (dharma), conducting oneself in a way that does not corrupt the path of the virtuous. Indeed, whoever is thus endowed with excellent character is declared to be the most excellent sage (muni).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 51. One should practice the natural law (dharma), conducting oneself in a way that does not corrupt the path of the virtuous. Indeed, whoever is thus endowed with excellent character is declared to be the most excellent sage (muni).

sanskrit: इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थांश्च महाभूतानि पञ्च च ।मनोबुद्धिरथात्मानमव्यक्तं पुरुषं तथा ॥५२॥
transliteration: indriyāṇīndriyārthāṁśca mahābhūtāni pañca ca ,manobuddhirathātmānamavyaktaṁ puruṣaṁ tathā.
english: The senses, and the objects of the senses, as well as the five great elements, then the mind, the intellect, the individual consciousness (ātman), the unmanifest (avyakta), and the supreme cosmic person (puruṣa).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 52. The senses, and the objects of the senses, as well as the five great elements, then the mind, the intellect, the individual consciousness (ātman), the unmanifest (avyakta), and the supreme cosmic person (puruṣa).

sanskrit: सर्वमेतत्प्रसंख्याय सम्यक्संत्यज्य निर्मलः ।ततः स्वर्गमवाप्नोति विमुक्तः सर्वबन्धनैः ॥५३॥
transliteration: sarvametatprasaṁkhyāya samyaksaṁtyajya nirmalaḥ ,tataḥ svargamavāpnoti vimuktaḥ sarvabandhanaiḥ.
english: Having thoroughly discerned all these (elements from the previous verse) and completely abandoned them, a person becomes pure. Thereafter, liberated from all bonds, they attain heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 53. Having thoroughly discerned all these (elements from the previous verse) and completely abandoned them, a person becomes pure. Thereafter, liberated from all bonds, they attain heaven.

sanskrit: एतदेवान्तवेलायां परिसंख्याय तत्त्ववित् ।ध्यायेदेकान्तमास्थाय मुच्यतेऽथ निराश्रयः ॥५४॥
transliteration: etadevāntavelāyāṁ parisaṁkhyāya tattvavit ,dhyāyedekāntamāsthāya mucyate'tha nirāśrayaḥ.
english: A knower of truth (tattvavit), having indeed fully discerned this (reality) at the final hour, should meditate by resorting to a secluded place. Then, becoming unsupported and independent, such a person achieves liberation (mokṣa).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 54. A knower of truth (tattvavit), having indeed fully discerned this (reality) at the final hour, should meditate by resorting to a secluded place. Then, becoming unsupported and independent, such a person achieves liberation (mokṣa).

sanskrit: निर्मुक्तः सर्वसङ्गेभ्यो वायुराकाशगो यथा ।क्षीणकोशो निरातङ्कः प्राप्नोति परमं पदम् ॥५५॥
transliteration: nirmuktaḥ sarvasaṅgebhyo vāyurākāśago yathā ,kṣīṇakośo nirātaṅkaḥ prāpnoti paramaṁ padam.
english: Just as the wind moves freely in the sky, so too one who is completely freed from all attachments, whose ego-sense (kośa) is diminished, and who is free from fear, attains the supreme state.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 46 Verse 55. Just as the wind moves freely in the sky, so too one who is completely freed from all attachments, whose ego-sense (kośa) is diminished, and who is free from fear, attains the supreme state.

sanskrit: ब्रह्मोवाच ।संन्यासं तप इत्याहुर्वृद्धा निश्चितदर्शिनः ।ब्राह्मणा ब्रह्मयोनिस्था ज्ञानं ब्रह्म परं विदुः ॥१॥
transliteration: brahmovāca ,saṁnyāsaṁ tapa ityāhurvṛddhā niścitadarśinaḥ ,brāhmaṇā brahmayonisthā jñānaṁ brahma paraṁ viduḥ.
english: Brahmā said: 'The elders, those with unerring vision, call renunciation (saṃnyāsa) a form of austerity (tapas). The brahmins, who are established in the origin of (brahman), recognize that knowledge (jñāna) is the supreme (brahman).'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 47 Verse 1. Brahmā said: 'The elders, those with unerring vision, call renunciation (saṃnyāsa) a form of austerity (tapas). The brahmins, who are established in the origin of (brahman), recognize that knowledge (jñāna) is the supreme (brahman).'

sanskrit: अविदूरात्परं ब्रह्म वेदविद्याव्यपाश्रयम् ।निर्द्वंद्वं निर्गुणं नित्यमचिन्त्यं गुह्यमुत्तमम् ॥२॥
transliteration: avidūrātparaṁ brahma vedavidyāvyapāśrayam ,nirdvaṁdvaṁ nirguṇaṁ nityamacintyaṁ guhyamuttamam.
english: The supreme (brahman) is not distant; it is the refuge of Vedic knowledge, free from dualities, without attributes (guṇa), eternal, inconceivable, a profound mystery (guhya), and utterly supreme.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 47 Verse 2. The supreme (brahman) is not distant; it is the refuge of Vedic knowledge, free from dualities, without attributes (guṇa), eternal, inconceivable, a profound mystery (guhya), and utterly supreme.

sanskrit: ज्ञानेन तपसा चैव धीराः पश्यन्ति तत्पदम् ।निर्णिक्ततमसः पूता व्युत्क्रान्तरजसोऽमलाः ॥३॥
transliteration: jñānena tapasā caiva dhīrāḥ paśyanti tatpadam ,nirṇiktatamasaḥ pūtā vyutkrāntarajaso'malāḥ.
english: Indeed, the resolute ones perceive that supreme state through knowledge (jñāna) and austerity (tapas). These are the ones whose darkness (tamas) has been cleansed away, who are purified, who have transcended the quality of passion (rajas), and who are free from blemish.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 47 Verse 3. Indeed, the resolute ones perceive that supreme state through knowledge (jñāna) and austerity (tapas). These are the ones whose darkness (tamas) has been cleansed away, who are purified, who have transcended the quality of passion (rajas), and who are free from blemish.

sanskrit: तपसा क्षेममध्वानं गच्छन्ति परमैषिणः ।संन्यासनिरता नित्यं ये ब्रह्मविदुषो जनाः ॥४॥
transliteration: tapasā kṣemamadhvānaṁ gacchanti paramaiṣiṇaḥ ,saṁnyāsaniratā nityaṁ ye brahmaviduṣo janāḥ.
english: Those people who are constantly devoted to renunciation (saṃnyāsa) and who are knowers of brahman, seeking the supreme, attain the path to ultimate well-being through spiritual discipline (tapas).

sanskrit: तपः प्रदीप इत्याहुराचारो धर्मसाधकः ।ज्ञानं त्वेव परं विद्म संन्यासस्तप उत्तमम् ॥५॥
transliteration: tapaḥ pradīpa ityāhurācāro dharmasādhakaḥ ,jñānaṁ tveva paraṁ vidma saṁnyāsastapa uttamam.
english: They declare that spiritual discipline (tapas) is like a lamp, and good conduct facilitates the establishment of natural law (dharma). But we know knowledge itself to be supreme, and renunciation (saṃnyāsa) is the highest form of spiritual discipline (tapas).

sanskrit: यस्तु वेद निराबाधं ज्ञानं तत्त्वविनिश्चयात् ।सर्वभूतस्थमात्मानं स सर्वगतिरिष्यते ॥६॥
transliteration: yastu veda nirābādhaṁ jñānaṁ tattvaviniścayāt ,sarvabhūtasthamātmānaṁ sa sarvagatiriṣyate.
english: But he who, through the firm ascertainment of reality, knows the self (ātman) to be unobstructed knowledge abiding in all beings, he is regarded as one who comprehends all paths.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 47 Verse 6. But he who, through the firm ascertainment of reality, knows the self (ātman) to be unobstructed knowledge abiding in all beings, he is regarded as one who comprehends all paths.

sanskrit: यो विद्वान्सहवासं च विवासं चैव पश्यति ।तथैवैकत्वनानात्वे स दुःखात्परिमुच्यते ॥७॥
transliteration: yo vidvānsahavāsaṁ ca vivāsaṁ caiva paśyati ,tathaivaikatvanānātve sa duḥkhātparimucyate.
english: He who, being a discerning person, perceives both association and dissociation, and similarly unity and diversity, he becomes completely liberated from sorrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 47 Verse 7. He who, being a discerning person, perceives both association and dissociation, and similarly unity and diversity, he becomes completely liberated from sorrow.

sanskrit: यो न कामयते किंचिन्न किंचिदवमन्यते ।इहलोकस्थ एवैष ब्रह्मभूयाय कल्पते ॥८॥
transliteration: yo na kāmayate kiṁcinna kiṁcidavamanyate ,ihalokastha evaiṣa brahmabhūyāya kalpate.
english: The one who desires nothing and despises nothing, such a person, even while living in this world, becomes qualified for achieving the state of (brahman).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 47 Verse 8. The one who desires nothing and despises nothing, such a person, even while living in this world, becomes qualified for achieving the state of (brahman).

sanskrit: प्रधानगुणतत्त्वज्ञः सर्वभूतविधानवित् ।निर्ममो निरहंकारो मुच्यते नात्र संशयः ॥९॥
transliteration: pradhānaguṇatattvajñaḥ sarvabhūtavidhānavit ,nirmamo nirahaṁkāro mucyate nātra saṁśayaḥ.
english: The one who understands the fundamental principles of nature (prakṛti) and its constituents (guṇas), who knows the laws governing all beings, who is free from possessiveness and ego (ahaṅkāra), is certainly liberated. There is no doubt about this.

sanskrit: निर्द्वंद्वो निर्नमस्कारो निःस्वधाकार एव च ।निर्गुणं नित्यमद्वंद्वं प्रशमेनैव गच्छति ॥१०॥
transliteration: nirdvaṁdvo nirnamaskāro niḥsvadhākāra eva ca ,nirguṇaṁ nityamadvaṁdvaṁ praśamenaiva gacchati.
english: One who is free from dualities, free from ritualistic salutations, and free from ancestral offerings, attains the attributeless, eternal, and non-dual state solely through profound tranquility.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 47 Verse 10. One who is free from dualities, free from ritualistic salutations, and free from ancestral offerings, attains the attributeless, eternal, and non-dual state solely through profound tranquility.

sanskrit: हित्वा गुणमयं सर्वं कर्म जन्तुः शुभाशुभम् ।उभे सत्यानृते हित्वा मुच्यते नात्र संशयः ॥११॥
transliteration: hitvā guṇamayaṁ sarvaṁ karma jantuḥ śubhāśubham ,ubhe satyānṛte hitvā mucyate nātra saṁśayaḥ.
english: A living being, having abandoned all actions (karma), both auspicious and inauspicious, which are rooted in the constituents of nature (guṇas), and having given up both truth and untruth, is liberated. There is no doubt about this.

sanskrit: अव्यक्तबीजप्रभवो बुद्धिस्कन्धमयो महान् ।महाहंकारविटप इन्द्रियान्तरकोटरः ॥१२॥
transliteration: avyaktabījaprabhavo buddhiskandhamayo mahān ,mahāhaṁkāraviṭapa indriyāntarakoṭaraḥ.
english: This great (tree) originates from the unmanifest (avyakta) as its seed, has the intellect (buddhi) as its trunk, the great ego (ahaṃkāra) as its branches, and the senses as its inner hollows.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 47 Verse 12. This great (tree) originates from the unmanifest (avyakta) as its seed, has the intellect (buddhi) as its trunk, the great ego (ahaṃkāra) as its branches, and the senses as its inner hollows.

sanskrit: महाभूतविशाखश्च विशेषप्रतिशाखवान् ।सदापर्णः सदापुष्पः शुभाशुभफलोदयः ।आजीवः सर्वभूतानां ब्रह्मवृक्षः सनातनः ॥१३॥
transliteration: mahābhūtaviśākhaśca viśeṣapratiśākhavān ,sadāparṇaḥ sadāpuṣpaḥ śubhāśubhaphalodayaḥ ,ājīvaḥ sarvabhūtānāṁ brahmavṛkṣaḥ sanātanaḥ.
english: And it has the great elements as its main branches, with subtle objects as its sub-branches. Always verdant with leaves and flowers, it produces both auspicious and inauspicious fruits. This eternal Brahma-tree is the sustenance of all beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 47 Verse 13. And it has the great elements as its main branches, with subtle objects as its sub-branches. sanskrit: एतच्छित्त्वा च भित्त्वा च ज्ञानेन परमासिना ।हित्वा चामरतां प्राप्य जह्याद्वै मृत्युजन्मनी ।निर्ममो निरहंकारो मुच्यते नात्र संशयः ॥१४॥
transliteration: etacchittvā ca bhittvā ca jñānena paramāsinā ,hitvā cāmaratāṁ prāpya jahyādvai mṛtyujanmanī ,nirmamo nirahaṁkāro mucyate nātra saṁśayaḥ.
english: Having cut and pierced this (tree) with the supreme sword of knowledge (jñāna), and having abandoned (attachment to it), one should attain immortality and indeed relinquish birth and death. Free from possessiveness and ego (ahaṃkāra), one is liberated; there is no doubt about this.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 47 Verse 14. sanskrit: द्वावेतौ पक्षिणौ नित्यौ सखायौ चाप्यचेतनौ ।एताभ्यां तु परो यस्य चेतनावानिति स्मृतः ॥१५॥
transliteration: dvāvetau pakṣiṇau nityau sakhāyau cāpyacetanau ,etābhyāṁ tu paro yasya cetanāvāniti smṛtaḥ.
english: These two, the intellect (buddhi) and ego (ahaṃkāra), are eternal friends, and they are also unconscious. But beyond these two, the supreme consciousness (puruṣa) is indeed remembered.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 47 Verse 15. These two, the intellect (buddhi) and ego (ahaṃkāra), are eternal friends, and they are also unconscious. But beyond these two, the supreme consciousness (puruṣa) is indeed remembered.

sanskrit: अचेतनः सत्त्वसंघातयुक्तः सत्त्वात्परं चेतयतेऽन्तरात्मा ।स क्षेत्रज्ञः सत्त्वसंघातबुद्धिर्गुणातिगो मुच्यते मृत्युपाशात् ॥१६॥
transliteration: acetanaḥ sattvasaṁghātayuktaḥ; sattvātparaṁ cetayate'ntarātmā ,sa kṣetrajñaḥ sattvasaṁghātabuddhi;rguṇātigo mucyate mṛtyupāśāt.
english: The inner self (antarātman), though united with an unconscious aggregate of elements, nevertheless perceives that which is beyond these elements. That knower of the field (kṣetrajña), whose intellect is linked to the aggregate of elements, transcends the qualities (guṇa) and is liberated from the snare of death.

sanskrit: ब्रह्मोवाच ।केचिद्ब्रह्ममयं वृक्षं केचिद्ब्रह्ममयं महत् ।केचित्पुरुषमव्यक्तं केचित्परमनामयम् ।मन्यन्ते सर्वमप्येतदव्यक्तप्रभवाव्ययम् ॥१॥
transliteration: brahmovāca ,kecidbrahmamayaṁ vṛkṣaṁ kecidbrahmamayaṁ mahat ,kecitpuruṣamavyaktaṁ kecitparamanāmayam ,manyante sarvamapyetadavyaktaprabhavāvyayam.
english: Brahmā said: Some consider (brahman) to be a tree whose nature is (brahman); others consider it to be the great (brahman) itself. They all believe that this entire (universe) originates from the unmanifest and is imperishable.

sanskrit: उच्छ्वासमात्रमपि चेद्योऽन्तकाले समो भवेत् ।आत्मानमुपसंगम्य सोऽमृतत्वाय कल्पते ॥२॥
transliteration: ucchvāsamātramapi cedyo'ntakāle samo bhavet ,ātmānamupasaṁgamya so'mṛtatvāya kalpate.
english: If anyone, even for the duration of a single breath, remains composed at the final moment (antakāle), that person, by approaching the self (ātman), becomes eligible for immortality (amṛtatva).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 48 Verse 2. If anyone, even for the duration of a single breath, remains composed at the final moment (antakāle), that person, by approaching the self (ātman), becomes eligible for immortality (amṛtatva).

sanskrit: निमेषमात्रमपि चेत्संयम्यात्मानमात्मनि ।गच्छत्यात्मप्रसादेन विदुषां प्राप्तिमव्ययाम् ॥३॥
transliteration: nimeṣamātramapi cetsaṁyamyātmānamātmani ,gacchatyātmaprasādena viduṣāṁ prāptimavyayām.
english: If one controls the self (ātman) within the self (ātman), even for merely the duration of a blink, that person, by the grace of the self (ātman), attains the imperishable state achieved by the wise.

sanskrit: प्राणायामैरथ प्राणान्संयम्य स पुनः पुनः ।दशद्वादशभिर्वापि चतुर्विंशात्परं ततः ॥४॥
transliteration: prāṇāyāmairatha prāṇānsaṁyamya sa punaḥ punaḥ ,daśadvādaśabhirvāpi caturviṁśātparaṁ tataḥ.
english: Then, repeatedly restraining the vital energies (prāṇa) through breath control (prāṇāyāma), he performs this ten or twelve times, or even beyond twenty-four.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 48 Verse 4. Then, repeatedly restraining the vital energies (prāṇa) through breath control (prāṇāyāma), he performs this ten or twelve times, or even beyond twenty-four.

sanskrit: एवं पूर्वं प्रसन्नात्मा लभते यद्यदिच्छति ।अव्यक्तात्सत्त्वमुद्रिक्तममृतत्वाय कल्पते ॥५॥
transliteration: evaṁ pūrvaṁ prasannātmā labhate yadyadicchati ,avyaktātsattvamudriktamamṛtatvāya kalpate.
english: Thus, a person with a serene inner self (ātman) first obtains whatever he desires. The eminent clarity (sattva) that arises from the unmanifest (avyakta) becomes suitable for achieving immortality (amṛtatva).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 48 Verse 5. Thus, a person with a serene inner self (ātman) first obtains whatever he desires. The eminent clarity (sattva) that arises from the unmanifest (avyakta) becomes suitable for achieving immortality (amṛtatva).

sanskrit: सत्त्वात्परतरं नान्यत्प्रशंसन्तीह तद्विदः ।अनुमानाद्विजानीमः पुरुषं सत्त्वसंश्रयम् ।न शक्यमन्यथा गन्तुं पुरुषं तमथो द्विजाः ॥६॥
transliteration: sattvātparataraṁ nānyatpraśaṁsantīha tadvidaḥ ,anumānādvijānīmaḥ puruṣaṁ sattvasaṁśrayam ,na śakyamanyathā gantuṁ puruṣaṁ tamatho dvijāḥ.
english: Here, those who know praise nothing superior to purity (sattva). Through inference, we understand the cosmic person (puruṣa) as being supported by (sattva). O twice-born ones, it is not possible to comprehend that (puruṣa) otherwise.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 48 Verse 6. Here, those who know praise nothing superior to purity (sattva). Through inference, we understand the cosmic person (puruṣa) as being supported by (sattva). O twice-born ones, it is not possible to comprehend that (puruṣa) otherwise.

sanskrit: क्षमा धृतिरहिंसा च समता सत्यमार्जवम् ।ज्ञानं त्यागोऽथ संन्यासः सात्त्विकं वृत्तमिष्यते ॥७॥
transliteration: kṣamā dhṛtirahiṁsā ca samatā satyamārjavam ,jñānaṁ tyāgo'tha saṁnyāsaḥ sāttvikaṁ vṛttamiṣyate.
english: Forgiveness, fortitude, non-violence (ahiṃsā), impartiality, truthfulness, straightforwardness, knowledge, renunciation, and also complete renunciation (saṃnyāsa)  -  this kind of conduct is considered to be characteristic of purity (sāttvika).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 48 Verse 7. sanskrit: एतेनैवानुमानेन मन्यन्तेऽथ मनीषिणः ।सत्त्वं च पुरुषश्चैकस्तत्र नास्ति विचारणा ॥८॥
transliteration: etenaivānumānena manyante'tha manīṣiṇaḥ ,sattvaṁ ca puruṣaścaikastatra nāsti vicāraṇā.
english: By this very inference, the wise ones now believe that the mind (sattva) and the conscious entity (puruṣa) are one. There is no room for further discussion on that matter.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 48 Verse 8. By this very inference, the wise ones now believe that the mind (sattva) and the conscious entity (puruṣa) are one. There is no room for further discussion on that matter.

sanskrit: आहुरेके च विद्वांसो ये ज्ञाने सुप्रतिष्ठिताः ।क्षेत्रज्ञसत्त्वयोरैक्यमित्येतन्नोपपद्यते ॥९॥
transliteration: āhureke ca vidvāṁso ye jñāne supratiṣṭhitāḥ ,kṣetrajñasattvayoraikyamityetannopapadyate.
english: And some scholars, who are well-established in true knowledge, declare that the oneness of the knower of the field (kṣetrajña) and the mind (sattva) is not tenable.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 48 Verse 9. And some scholars, who are well-established in true knowledge, declare that the oneness of the knower of the field (kṣetrajña) and the mind (sattva) is not tenable.

sanskrit: पृथग्भूतस्ततो नित्यमित्येतदविचारितम् ।पृथग्भावश्च विज्ञेयः सहजश्चापि तत्त्वतः ॥१०॥
transliteration: pṛthagbhūtastato nityamityetadavicāritam ,pṛthagbhāvaśca vijñeyaḥ sahajaścāpi tattvataḥ.
english: The assertion that it (the self) is eternally separate from that (sattva/mind) is not properly considered. In reality, this distinctness (pṛthagbhāva) should be understood as inherent and natural (sahaja).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 48 Verse 10. The assertion that it (the self) is eternally separate from that (sattva/mind) is not properly considered. In reality, this distinctness (pṛthagbhāva) should be understood as inherent and natural (sahaja).

sanskrit: तथैवैकत्वनानात्वमिष्यते विदुषां नयः ।मशकोदुम्बरे त्वैक्यं पृथक्त्वमपि दृश्यते ॥११॥
transliteration: tathaivaikatvanānātvamiṣyate viduṣāṁ nayaḥ ,maśakodumbare tvaikyaṁ pṛthaktvamapi dṛśyate.
english: Similarly, the view of the wise accepts both unity and diversity. For instance, in the case of a mosquito and a fig, both identity and distinctness are observed.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 48 Verse 11. Similarly, the view of the wise accepts both unity and diversity. For instance, in the case of a mosquito and a fig, both identity and distinctness are observed.

sanskrit: मत्स्यो यथान्यः स्यादप्सु संप्रयोगस्तथानयोः ।संबन्धस्तोयबिन्दूनां पर्णे कोकनदस्य च ॥१२॥
transliteration: matsyo yathānyaḥ syādapsu saṁprayogastathānayoḥ ,saṁbandhastoyabindūnāṁ parṇe kokanadasya ca.
english: Just as a fish, though in water, remains distinct, so too is the close association of these two. And similarly, it is like the connection of water droplets on the leaf of a red lotus.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 48 Verse 12. Just as a fish, though in water, remains distinct, so too is the close association of these two. And similarly, it is like the connection of water droplets on the leaf of a red lotus.

sanskrit: गुरुरुवाच ।इत्युक्तवन्तं ते विप्रास्तदा लोकपितामहम् ।पुनः संशयमापन्नाः पप्रच्छुर्द्विजसत्तमाः ॥१३॥
transliteration: gururuvāca ,ityuktavantaṁ te viprāstadā lokapitāmaham ,punaḥ saṁśayamāpannāḥ papracchurdvijasattamāḥ.
english: The Guru said: "Those eminent Brahmins, having again become assailed by doubt (saṃśaya), then asked the grandfather of the world (lokapitāmaha), who had just spoken thus."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 48 Verse 13. The Guru said: "Those eminent Brahmins, having again become assailed by doubt (saṃśaya), then asked the grandfather of the world (lokapitāmaha), who had just spoken thus."

sanskrit: ऋषय ऊचुः ।किं स्विदेवेह धर्माणामनुष्ठेयतमं स्मृतम् ।व्याहतामिव पश्यामो धर्मस्य विविधां गतिम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: ṛṣaya ūcuḥ ,kiṁ svideveha dharmāṇāmanuṣṭheyatamaṁ smṛtam ,vyāhatāmiva paśyāmo dharmasya vividhāṁ gatim.
english: The Rishis said: "What, indeed, among the (dharma)s here, is considered the most to be practiced? We perceive the diverse courses of (dharma) as if contradictory."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 48 Verse 14. The Rishis said: "What, indeed, among the (dharma)s here, is considered the most to be practiced? We perceive the diverse courses of (dharma) as if contradictory."

sanskrit: ऊर्ध्वं देहाद्वदन्त्येके नैतदस्तीति चापरे ।केचित्संशयितं सर्वं निःसंशयमथापरे ॥१५॥
transliteration: ūrdhvaṁ dehādvadantyeke naitadastīti cāpare ,kecitsaṁśayitaṁ sarvaṁ niḥsaṁśayamathāpare.
english: Some assert that there is existence after the physical body, while others say that this is not so. Some consider everything to be doubtful, while still others hold it to be entirely without doubt.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 48 Verse 15. Some assert that there is existence after the physical body, while others say that this is not so. Some consider everything to be doubtful, while still others hold it to be entirely without doubt.

sanskrit: अनित्यं नित्यमित्येके नास्त्यस्तीत्यपि चापरे ।एकरूपं द्विधेत्येके व्यामिश्रमिति चापरे ।एकमेके पृथक्चान्ये बहुत्वमिति चापरे ॥१६॥
transliteration: anityaṁ nityamityeke nāstyastītyapi cāpare ,ekarūpaṁ dvidhetyeke vyāmiśramiti cāpare ,ekameke pṛthakcānye bahutvamiti cāpare.
english: Some assert reality to be impermanent, while others consider it eternal. Similarly, some claim it does not exist, while others maintain that it does. Some believe it has a single form, others that it is twofold, and still others that it is a complex mixture. Moreover, some consider it to be one, others distinct, and yet others perceive it as a multitude.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 48 Verse 16. Some assert reality to be impermanent, while others consider it eternal. Similarly, some claim it does not exist, while others maintain that it does. Some believe it has a single form, others that it is twofold, and still others that it is a complex mixture. Moreover, some consider it to be one, others distinct, and yet others perceive it as a multitude.

sanskrit: मन्यन्ते ब्राह्मणा एवं प्राज्ञास्तत्त्वार्थदर्शिनः ।जटाजिनधराश्चान्ये मुण्डाः केचिदसंवृताः ॥१७॥
transliteration: manyante brāhmaṇā evaṁ prājñāstattvārthadarśinaḥ ,jaṭājinadharāścānye muṇḍāḥ kecidasaṁvṛtāḥ.
english: Thus, wise brahmins (brāhmaṇa), who are seers of the true meaning of reality, conceive of things. While others wear matted hair and deerskins, some are shaven-headed, and some remain completely uncovered.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 48 Verse 17. Thus, wise brahmins (brāhmaṇa), who are seers of the true meaning of reality, conceive of things. While others wear matted hair and deerskins, some are shaven-headed, and some remain completely uncovered.

sanskrit: अस्नानं केचिदिच्छन्ति स्नानमित्यपि चापरे ।आहारं केचिदिच्छन्ति केचिच्चानशने रताः ॥१८॥
transliteration: asnānaṁ kecidicchanti snānamityapi cāpare ,āhāraṁ kecidicchanti keciccānaśane ratāḥ.
english: Some prefer not bathing, while others favor bathing. Similarly, some prefer consuming food, while others are devoted to fasting.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 48 Verse 18. Some prefer not bathing, while others favor bathing. Similarly, some prefer consuming food, while others are devoted to fasting.

sanskrit: कर्म केचित्प्रशंसन्ति प्रशान्तिमपि चापरे ।देशकालावुभौ केचिन्नैतदस्तीति चापरे ।केचिन्मोक्षं प्रशंसन्ति केचिद्भोगान्पृथग्विधान् ॥१९॥
transliteration: karma kecitpraśaṁsanti praśāntimapi cāpare ,deśakālāvubhau kecinnaitadastīti cāpare ,kecinmokṣaṁ praśaṁsanti kecidbhogānpṛthagvidhān.
english: Some commend action (karma), while others praise tranquility. Some consider both place and time (important), while others assert that such things do not exist. Some extol final liberation (mokṣa), while others praise various kinds of worldly enjoyments.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 48 Verse 19. Some commend action (karma), while others praise tranquility. Some consider both place and time (important), while others assert that such things do not exist. Some extol final liberation (mokṣa), while others praise various kinds of worldly enjoyments.

sanskrit: धनानि केचिदिच्छन्ति निर्धनत्वं तथापरे ।उपास्यसाधनं त्वेके नैतदस्तीति चापरे ॥२०॥
transliteration: dhanāni kecidicchanti nirdhanatvaṁ tathāpare ,upāsyasādhanaṁ tveke naitadastīti cāpare.
english: Some people desire wealth, while others desire poverty. Still, some aspire to the means for meditation or worship. And yet others believe, "This (means) does not exist."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 48 Verse 20. Some people desire wealth, while others desire poverty. Still, some aspire to the means for meditation or worship. And yet others believe, "This (means) does not exist."

sanskrit: अहिंसानिरताश्चान्ये केचिद्धिंसापरायणाः ।पुण्येन यशसेत्येके नैतदस्तीति चापरे ॥२१॥
transliteration: ahiṁsāniratāścānye keciddhiṁsāparāyaṇāḥ ,puṇyena yaśasetyeke naitadastīti cāpare.
english: And others are devoted to non-violence, while some are intent on violence. Some believe it is for merit (puṇya) or fame, and yet others think, "This (purpose or result) does not exist."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 48 Verse 21. And others are devoted to non-violence, while some are intent on violence. Some believe it is for merit (puṇya) or fame, and yet others think, "This (purpose or result) does not exist."

sanskrit: सद्भावनिरताश्चान्ये केचित्संशयिते स्थिताः ।दुःखादन्ये सुखादन्ये ध्यानमित्यपरे स्थिताः ॥२२॥
transliteration: sadbhāvaniratāścānye kecitsaṁśayite sthitāḥ ,duḥkhādanye sukhādanye dhyānamityapare sthitāḥ.
english: And others are devoted to good intentions or reality, while some remain in doubt. Some people engage in (meditation) due to suffering, others due to happiness. Still others remain thinking, "This is (dhyāna)."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 48 Verse 22. And others are devoted to good intentions or reality, while some remain in doubt. Some people engage in (meditation) due to suffering, others due to happiness. Still others remain thinking, "This is (dhyāna)."

sanskrit: यज्ञमित्यपरे धीराः प्रदानमिति चापरे ।सर्वमेके प्रशंसन्ति न सर्वमिति चापरे ॥२३॥
transliteration: yajñamityapare dhīrāḥ pradānamiti cāpare ,sarvameke praśaṁsanti na sarvamiti cāpare.
english: Other wise individuals consider it a Vedic ritual (yajña), and still others consider it an offering. Some praise everything, while others believe, "Not everything (is worthy of praise)."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 48 Verse 23. Other wise individuals consider it a Vedic ritual (yajña), and still others consider it an offering. Some praise everything, while others believe, "Not everything (is worthy of praise)."

sanskrit: तपस्त्वन्ये प्रशंसन्ति स्वाध्यायमपरे जनाः ।ज्ञानं संन्यासमित्येके स्वभावं भूतचिन्तकाः ॥२४॥
transliteration: tapastvanye praśaṁsanti svādhyāyamapare janāḥ ,jñānaṁ saṁnyāsamityeke svabhāvaṁ bhūtacintakāḥ.
english: Some people praise austerity (tapas), others praise self-study. Still others consider knowledge and renunciation to be supreme, while those who contemplate the nature of beings speak of intrinsic nature (svabhāva).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 48 Verse 24. Some people praise austerity (tapas), others praise self-study. Still others consider knowledge and renunciation to be supreme, while those who contemplate the nature of beings speak of intrinsic nature (svabhāva).

sanskrit: एवं व्युत्थापिते धर्मे बहुधा विप्रधावति ।निश्चयं नाधिगच्छामः संमूढाः सुरसत्तम ॥२५॥
transliteration: evaṁ vyutthāpite dharme bahudhā vipradhāvati ,niścayaṁ nādhigacchāmaḥ saṁmūḍhāḥ surasattama.
english: When the natural law (dharma) is thus proclaimed in many ways, it becomes confused and widespread. We, being completely bewildered, do not attain a clear conclusion, O best among the gods.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 48 Verse 25. When the natural law (dharma) is thus proclaimed in many ways, it becomes confused and widespread. We, being completely bewildered, do not attain a clear conclusion, O best among the gods.

sanskrit: इदं श्रेय इदं श्रेय इत्येवं प्रस्थितो जनः ।यो हि यस्मिन्रतो धर्मे स तं पूजयते सदा ॥२६॥
transliteration: idaṁ śreya idaṁ śreya ityevaṁ prasthito janaḥ ,yo hi yasminrato dharme sa taṁ pūjayate sadā.
english: People proceed in this manner, saying 'This is superior, this is superior!' Indeed, whichever natural law (dharma) a person is devoted to, he always reveres that.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 48 Verse 26. People proceed in this manner, saying 'This is superior, this is superior!' Indeed, whichever natural law (dharma) a person is devoted to, he always reveres that.

sanskrit: तत्र नो विहता प्रज्ञा मनश्च बहुलीकृतम् ।एतदाख्यातुमिच्छामः श्रेयः किमिति सत्तम ॥२७॥
transliteration: tatra no vihatā prajñā manaśca bahulīkṛtam ,etadākhyātumicchāmaḥ śreyaḥ kimiti sattama.
english: Because of this, our wisdom is impaired, and our minds are greatly confused. We wish to know this: What, O best one, is truly superior?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 48 Verse 27. Because of this, our wisdom is impaired, and our minds are greatly confused. We wish to know this: What, O best one, is truly superior?

sanskrit: अतः परं च यद्गुह्यं तद्भवान्वक्तुमर्हति ।सत्त्वक्षेत्रज्ञयोश्चैव संबन्धः केन हेतुना ॥२८॥
transliteration: ataḥ paraṁ ca yadguhyaṁ tadbhavānvaktumarhati ,sattvakṣetrajñayoścaiva saṁbandhaḥ kena hetunā.
english: And furthermore, you are capable of explaining whatever profound secret remains. What is the relationship between the mind (sattva) and the knower of the field (kṣetrajña), and by what cause does it arise?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 48 Verse 28. And furthermore, you are capable of explaining whatever profound secret remains. What is the relationship between the mind (sattva) and the knower of the field (kṣetrajña), and by what cause does it arise?

sanskrit: एवमुक्तः स तैर्विप्रैर्भगवाँल्लोकभावनः ।तेभ्यः शशंस धर्मात्मा याथातथ्येन बुद्धिमान् ॥२९॥
transliteration: evamuktaḥ sa tairviprairbhagavāँllokabhāvanaḥ ,tebhyaḥ śaśaṁsa dharmātmā yāthātathyena buddhimān.
english: Thus addressed by those Brahmins, the glorious Lord, sustainer of the worlds, whose nature was righteousness (dharma), and who was wise, explained to them truly, in accordance with reality.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 48 Verse 29. Thus addressed by those Brahmins, the glorious Lord, sustainer of the worlds, whose nature was righteousness (dharma), and who was wise, explained to them truly, in accordance with reality.

sanskrit: ब्रह्मोवाच ।हन्त वः संप्रवक्ष्यामि यन्मां पृच्छथ सत्तमाः ।समस्तमिह तच्छ्रुत्वा सम्यगेवावधार्यताम् ॥१॥
transliteration: brahmovāca ,hanta vaḥ saṁpravakṣyāmi yanmāṁ pṛcchatha sattamāḥ ,samastamiha tacchrutvā samyagevāvadhāryatām.
english: Brahmā spoke: "Indeed, O excellent ones, I will fully explain to you what you ask me. Having heard all of it here, let it be properly grasped."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 1. Brahmā spoke: "Indeed, O excellent ones, I will fully explain to you what you ask me. Having heard all of it here, let it be properly grasped."

sanskrit: अहिंसा सर्वभूतानामेतत्कृत्यतमं मतम् ।एतत्पदमनुद्विग्नं वरिष्ठं धर्मलक्षणम् ॥२॥
transliteration: ahiṁsā sarvabhūtānāmetatkṛtyatamaṁ matam ,etatpadamanudvignaṁ variṣṭhaṁ dharmalakṣaṇam.
english: Non-violence towards all beings is considered the most important duty. This undisturbed state is the supreme characteristic of natural law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 2. Non-violence towards all beings is considered the most important duty. This undisturbed state is the supreme characteristic of natural law (dharma).

sanskrit: ज्ञानं निःश्रेय इत्याहुर्वृद्धा निश्चयदर्शिनः ।तस्माज्ज्ञानेन शुद्धेन मुच्यते सर्वपातकैः ॥३॥
transliteration: jñānaṁ niḥśreya ityāhurvṛddhā niścayadarśinaḥ ,tasmājjñānena śuddhena mucyate sarvapātakaiḥ.
english: The elders, who possess firm discernment, declare that knowledge is the ultimate liberation (mokṣa). Therefore, by means of pure knowledge, one is freed from all transgressions.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 3. The elders, who possess firm discernment, declare that knowledge is the ultimate liberation (mokṣa). Therefore, by means of pure knowledge, one is freed from all transgressions.

sanskrit: हिंसापराश्च ये लोके ये च नास्तिकवृत्तयः ।लोभमोहसमायुक्तास्ते वै निरयगामिनः ॥४॥
transliteration: hiṁsāparāśca ye loke ye ca nāstikavṛttayaḥ ,lobhamohasamāyuktāste vai nirayagāminaḥ.
english: Those in the world who are inclined towards violence and who hold atheistic dispositions, being overwhelmed by greed and delusion, they are indeed destined for hell.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 4. Those in the world who are inclined towards violence and who hold atheistic dispositions, being overwhelmed by greed and delusion, they are indeed destined for hell.

sanskrit: आशीर्युक्तानि कर्माणि कुर्वते ये त्वतन्द्रिताः ।तेऽस्मिँल्लोके प्रमोदन्ते जायमानाः पुनः पुनः ॥५॥
transliteration: āśīryuktāni karmāṇi kurvate ye tvatandritāḥ ,te'smiँlloke pramodante jāyamānāḥ punaḥ punaḥ.
english: But those who diligently perform actions (karma) driven by desires, they rejoice in this world, taking repeated births.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 5. But those who diligently perform actions (karma) driven by desires, they rejoice in this world, taking repeated births.

sanskrit: कुर्वते ये तु कर्माणि श्रद्दधाना विपश्चितः ।अनाशीर्योगसंयुक्तास्ते धीराः साधुदर्शिनः ॥६॥
transliteration: kurvate ye tu karmāṇi śraddadhānā vipaścitaḥ ,anāśīryogasaṁyuktāste dhīrāḥ sādhudarśinaḥ.
english: But those discerning and wise individuals who perform actions (karma) with faith (śraddhā), and are endowed with spiritual discipline (yoga), they are truly the steadfast ones, possessing proper insight.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 6. But those discerning and wise individuals who perform actions (karma) with faith (śraddhā), and are endowed with spiritual discipline (yoga), they are truly the steadfast ones, possessing proper insight.

sanskrit: अतः परं प्रवक्ष्यामि सत्त्वक्षेत्रज्ञयोर्यथा ।संयोगो विप्रयोगश्च तन्निबोधत सत्तमाः ॥७॥
transliteration: ataḥ paraṁ pravakṣyāmi sattvakṣetrajñayoryathā ,saṁyogo viprayogaśca tannibodhata sattamāḥ.
english: Hereafter, O excellent ones, I will explain the nature of the conjunction and separation of nature (sattva) and the knower of the field (kṣetrajña). Please understand this.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 7. Hereafter, O excellent ones, I will explain the nature of the conjunction and separation of nature (sattva) and the knower of the field (kṣetrajña). Please understand this.

sanskrit: विषयो विषयित्वं च संबन्धोऽयमिहोच्यते ।विषयी पुरुषो नित्यं सत्त्वं च विषयः स्मृतः ॥८॥
transliteration: viṣayo viṣayitvaṁ ca saṁbandho'yamihocyate ,viṣayī puruṣo nityaṁ sattvaṁ ca viṣayaḥ smṛtaḥ.
english: Here, this relationship is described as consisting of an object (viṣaya) and subjectivity (viṣayitvam). The supreme cosmic person (puruṣa) is always the subject, and nature (sattva) is considered the object.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 8. Here, this relationship is described as consisting of an object (viṣaya) and subjectivity (viṣayitvam). The supreme cosmic person (puruṣa) is always the subject, and nature (sattva) is considered the object.

sanskrit: व्याख्यातं पूर्वकल्पेन मशकोदुम्बरं यथा ।भुज्यमानं न जानीते नित्यं सत्त्वमचेतनम् ।यस्त्वेव तु विजानीते यो भुङ्क्ते यश्च भुज्यते ॥९॥
transliteration: vyākhyātaṁ pūrvakalpena maśakodumbaraṁ yathā ,bhujyamānaṁ na jānīte nityaṁ sattvamacetanam ,yastveva tu vijānīte yo bhuṅkte yaśca bhujyate.
english: This has been explained by the previous analogy, just as a mosquito inside an udumbara fig. The unconscious nature (sattva), though always being experienced, does not understand. But the one who truly comprehends both the experiencer and the experienced, that is the knower of the field (kṣetrajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 9. This has been explained by the previous analogy, just as a mosquito inside an udumbara fig. The unconscious nature (sattva), though always being experienced, does not understand. But the one who truly comprehends both the experiencer and the experienced, that is the knower of the field (kṣetrajña).

sanskrit: अनित्यं द्वंद्वसंयुक्तं सत्त्वमाहुर्गुणात्मकम् ।निर्द्वंद्वो निष्कलो नित्यः क्षेत्रज्ञो निर्गुणात्मकः ॥१०॥
transliteration: anityaṁ dvaṁdvasaṁyuktaṁ sattvamāhurguṇātmakam ,nirdvaṁdvo niṣkalo nityaḥ kṣetrajño nirguṇātmakaḥ.
english: They declare nature (sattva) to be impermanent, connected with dualities, and inherently consisting of the guṇas. The knower of the field (kṣetrajña) is, however, free from dualities, without parts, eternal, and intrinsically free from the guṇas.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 10. They declare nature (sattva) to be impermanent, connected with dualities, and inherently consisting of the guṇas. The knower of the field (kṣetrajña) is, however, free from dualities, without parts, eternal, and intrinsically free from the guṇas.

sanskrit: समः संज्ञागतस्त्वेवं यदा सर्वत्र दृश्यते ।उपभुङ्क्ते सदा सत्त्वमापः पुष्करपर्णवत् ॥११॥
transliteration: samaḥ saṁjñāgatastvevaṁ yadā sarvatra dṛśyate ,upabhuṅkte sadā sattvamāpaḥ puṣkaraparṇavat.
english: When one, having attained an equal perception, thus sees equality everywhere, one always experiences the quality of goodness (sattva), yet remains unaffected, just like water on a lotus leaf.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 11. When one, having attained an equal perception, thus sees equality everywhere, one always experiences the quality of goodness (sattva), yet remains unaffected, just like water on a lotus leaf.

sanskrit: सर्वैरपि गुणैर्विद्वान्व्यतिषक्तो न लिप्यते ।जलबिन्दुर्यथा लोलः पद्मिनीपत्रसंस्थितः ।एवमेवाप्यसंसक्तः पुरुषः स्यान्न संशयः ॥१२॥
transliteration: sarvairapi guṇairvidvānvyatiṣakto na lipyate ,jalabinduryathā lolaḥ padminīpatrasaṁsthitaḥ ,evamevāpyasaṁsaktaḥ puruṣaḥ syānna saṁśayaḥ.
english: Even though a wise person is associated with all the qualities (guṇas), they are not stained. Just as a free-moving drop of water rests on a lotus leaf, so too, without a doubt, the individual consciousness (puruṣa) remains unattached.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 12. Even though a wise person is associated with all the qualities (guṇas), they are not stained. Just as a free-moving drop of water rests on a lotus leaf, so too, without a doubt, the individual consciousness (puruṣa) remains unattached.

sanskrit: द्रव्यमात्रमभूत्सत्त्वं पुरुषस्येति निश्चयः ।यथा द्रव्यं च कर्ता च संयोगोऽप्यनयोस्तथा ॥१३॥
transliteration: dravyamātramabhūtsattvaṁ puruṣasyeti niścayaḥ ,yathā dravyaṁ ca kartā ca saṁyogo'pyanayostathā.
english: It is the firm conclusion that the principle of goodness (sattva) was merely a constituent for the individual consciousness (puruṣa). Just as there is a substance and an agent, so too is the conjunction between them.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 13. It is the firm conclusion that the principle of goodness (sattva) was merely a constituent for the individual consciousness (puruṣa). Just as there is a substance and an agent, so too is the conjunction between them.

sanskrit: यथा प्रदीपमादाय कश्चित्तमसि गच्छति ।तथा सत्त्वप्रदीपेन गच्छन्ति परमैषिणः ॥१४॥
transliteration: yathā pradīpamādāya kaścittamasi gacchati ,tathā sattvapradīpena gacchanti paramaiṣiṇaḥ.
english: Just as someone takes a lamp and proceeds through darkness, so too, those who strive for the supreme goal advance by means of the lamp of clarity (sattva).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 14. Just as someone takes a lamp and proceeds through darkness, so too, those who strive for the supreme goal advance by means of the lamp of clarity (sattva).

sanskrit: यावद्द्रव्यगुणस्तावत्प्रदीपः संप्रकाशते ।क्षीणद्रव्यगुणं ज्योतिरन्तर्धानाय गच्छति ॥१५॥
transliteration: yāvaddravyaguṇastāvatpradīpaḥ saṁprakāśate ,kṣīṇadravyaguṇaṁ jyotirantardhānāya gacchati.
english: As long as the qualities of its substance (like oil and wick) are present, a lamp shines brightly. When the qualities of its substance are exhausted, the light proceeds towards extinction.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 15. As long as the qualities of its substance (like oil and wick) are present, a lamp shines brightly. When the qualities of its substance are exhausted, the light proceeds towards extinction.

sanskrit: व्यक्तः सत्त्वगुणस्त्वेवं पुरुषोऽव्यक्त इष्यते ।एतद्विप्रा विजानीत हन्त भूयो ब्रवीमि वः ॥१६॥
transliteration: vyaktaḥ sattvaguṇastvevaṁ puruṣo'vyakta iṣyate ,etadviprā vijānīta hanta bhūyo bravīmi vaḥ.
english: Thus, the quality of goodness (sattva guṇa) is considered manifest, while the conscious principle (puruṣa) is understood as unmanifest. O wise ones, understand this; indeed, I will speak to you further.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 16. Thus, the quality of goodness (sattva guṇa) is considered manifest, while the conscious principle (puruṣa) is understood as unmanifest. O wise ones, understand this; indeed, I will speak to you further.

sanskrit: सहस्रेणापि दुर्मेधा न वृद्धिमधिगच्छति ।चतुर्थेनाप्यथांशेन बुद्धिमान्सुखमेधते ॥१७॥
transliteration: sahasreṇāpi durmedhā na vṛddhimadhigacchati ,caturthenāpyathāṁśena buddhimānsukhamedhate.
english: Even with a thousand (means), a dull-witted person does not achieve prosperity. Yet, an intelligent person thrives happily even with just a fourth portion (of means).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 17. Even with a thousand (means), a dull-witted person does not achieve prosperity. Yet, an intelligent person thrives happily even with just a fourth portion (of means).

sanskrit: एवं धर्मस्य विज्ञेयं संसाधनमुपायतः ।उपायज्ञो हि मेधावी सुखमत्यन्तमश्नुते ॥१८॥
transliteration: evaṁ dharmasya vijñeyaṁ saṁsādhanamupāyataḥ ,upāyajño hi medhāvī sukhamatyantamaśnute.
english: In this way, the accomplishment of one's intrinsic nature (dharma) must be understood through appropriate methods. Indeed, a wise person who knows these methods attains ultimate happiness.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 18. In this way, the accomplishment of one's intrinsic nature (dharma) must be understood through appropriate methods. Indeed, a wise person who knows these methods attains ultimate happiness.

sanskrit: यथाध्वानमपाथेयः प्रपन्नो मानवः क्वचित् ।क्लेशेन याति महता विनश्यत्यन्तरापि वा ॥१९॥
transliteration: yathādhvānamapātheyaḥ prapanno mānavaḥ kvacit ,kleśena yāti mahatā vinaśyatyantarāpi vā.
english: Just as a person who sets out on a journey somewhere without provisions for the trip proceeds with great difficulty, or even perishes midway.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 19. Just as a person who sets out on a journey somewhere without provisions for the trip proceeds with great difficulty, or even perishes midway.

sanskrit: तथा कर्मसु विज्ञेयं फलं भवति वा न वा ।पुरुषस्यात्मनिःश्रेयः शुभाशुभनिदर्शनम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: tathā karmasu vijñeyaṁ phalaṁ bhavati vā na vā ,puruṣasyātmaniḥśreyaḥ śubhāśubhanidarśanam.
english: Similarly, regarding actions (karma), it should be understood that the result may or may not occur; it serves as an indicator of what is beneficial or detrimental to a person's (puruṣa) ultimate welfare (ātmaniḥśreyas).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 20. Similarly, regarding actions (karma), it should be understood that the result may or may not occur; it serves as an indicator of what is beneficial or detrimental to a person's (puruṣa) ultimate welfare (ātmaniḥśreyas).

sanskrit: यथा च दीर्घमध्वानं पद्भ्यामेव प्रपद्यते ।अदृष्टपूर्वं सहसा तत्त्वदर्शनवर्जितः ॥२१॥
transliteration: yathā ca dīrghamadhvānaṁ padbhyāmeva prapadyate ,adṛṣṭapūrvaṁ sahasā tattvadarśanavarjitaḥ.
english: And just as someone lacking true insight (tattvadarśana) suddenly undertakes a long, unprecedented journey only by foot.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 21. And just as someone lacking true insight (tattvadarśana) suddenly undertakes a long, unprecedented journey only by foot.

sanskrit: तमेव च यथाध्वानं रथेनेहाशुगामिना ।यायादश्वप्रयुक्तेन तथा बुद्धिमतां गतिः ॥२२॥
transliteration: tameva ca yathādhvānaṁ rathenehāśugāminā ,yāyādaśvaprayuktena tathā buddhimatāṁ gatiḥ.
english: And just as one might swiftly traverse that very same path by chariot, pulled by horses, so too is the progress of the intelligent.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 22. And just as one might swiftly traverse that very same path by chariot, pulled by horses, so too is the progress of the intelligent.

sanskrit: उच्चं पर्वतमारुह्य नान्ववेक्षेत भूगतम् ।रथेन रथिनं पश्येत्क्लिश्यमानमचेतनम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: uccaṁ parvatamāruhya nānvavekṣeta bhūgatam ,rathena rathinaṁ paśyetkliśyamānamacetanam.
english: Having ascended a high mountain, one should not look back at things on the ground. Instead, one should observe a person in a chariot, who is suffering and unconscious.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 23. Having ascended a high mountain, one should not look back at things on the ground. Instead, one should observe a person in a chariot, who is suffering and unconscious.

sanskrit: यावद्रथपथस्तावद्रथेन स तु गच्छति ।क्षीणे रथपथे प्राज्ञो रथमुत्सृज्य गच्छति ॥२४॥
transliteration: yāvadrathapathastāvadrathena sa tu gacchati ,kṣīṇe rathapathe prājño rathamutsṛjya gacchati.
english: As long as a chariot-path exists, one travels by chariot. When the chariot-path is exhausted, the wise person abandons the chariot and proceeds.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 24. As long as a chariot-path exists, one travels by chariot. When the chariot-path is exhausted, the wise person abandons the chariot and proceeds.

sanskrit: एवं गच्छति मेधावी तत्त्वयोगविधानवित् ।समाज्ञाय महाबुद्धिरुत्तरादुत्तरोत्तरम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: evaṁ gacchati medhāvī tattvayogavidhānavit ,samājñāya mahābuddhiruttarāduttarottaram.
english: Thus proceeds the intelligent one, who understands the methods of (yoga) union with reality. The greatly wise person, having thoroughly comprehended, advances from one higher state to increasingly higher states.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 25. Thus proceeds the intelligent one, who understands the methods of (yoga) union with reality. The greatly wise person, having thoroughly comprehended, advances from one higher state to increasingly higher states.

sanskrit: यथा महार्णवं घोरमप्लवः संप्रगाहते ।बाहुभ्यामेव संमोहाद्वधं चर्च्छत्यसंशयम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: yathā mahārṇavaṁ ghoramaplavaḥ saṁpragāhate ,bāhubhyāmeva saṁmohādvadhaṁ carcchatyasaṁśayam.
english: Just as one without a boat plunges into a terrible great ocean relying only on their two arms, so too, out of great delusion, they surely meet death, without a doubt.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 26. Just as one without a boat plunges into a terrible great ocean relying only on their two arms, so too, out of great delusion, they surely meet death, without a doubt.

sanskrit: नावा चापि यथा प्राज्ञो विभागज्ञस्तरित्रया ।अक्लान्तः सलिलं गाहेत्क्षिप्रं संतरति ध्रुवम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: nāvā cāpi yathā prājño vibhāgajñastaritrayā ,aklāntaḥ salilaṁ gāhetkṣipraṁ saṁtarati dhruvam.
english: Just as a wise person, who understands the proper function and handling of a boat, would enter the water without weariness and surely cross it swiftly.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 27. Just as a wise person, who understands the proper function and handling of a boat, would enter the water without weariness and surely cross it swiftly.

sanskrit: तीर्णो गच्छेत्परं पारं नावमुत्सृज्य निर्ममः ।व्याख्यातं पूर्वकल्पेन यथा रथिपदातिनौ ॥२८॥
transliteration: tīrṇo gacchetparaṁ pāraṁ nāvamutsṛjya nirmamaḥ ,vyākhyātaṁ pūrvakalpena yathā rathipadātinau.
english: Having crossed, one who is free from possessiveness (mama) should reach the other shore by abandoning the boat. This has been explained by the previous analogy, just like the case of the charioteer and the foot-soldier.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 28. Having crossed, one who is free from possessiveness (mama) should reach the other shore by abandoning the boat. This has been explained by the previous analogy, just like the case of the charioteer and the foot-soldier.

sanskrit: स्नेहात्संमोहमापन्नो नावि दाशो यथा तथा ।ममत्वेनाभिभूतः स तत्रैव परिवर्तते ॥२९॥
transliteration: snehātsaṁmohamāpanno nāvi dāśo yathā tathā ,mamatvenābhibhūtaḥ sa tatraiva parivartate.
english: Just as a boatman, out of attachment, becomes bewildered concerning the boat, so too, a person overcome by the sense of 'mine' or possessiveness (mama) revolves in that very state.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 29. Just as a boatman, out of attachment, becomes bewildered concerning the boat, so too, a person overcome by the sense of 'mine' or possessiveness (mama) revolves in that very state.

sanskrit: नावं न शक्यमारुह्य स्थले विपरिवर्तितुम् ।तथैव रथमारुह्य नाप्सु चर्या विधीयते ॥३०॥
transliteration: nāvaṁ na śakyamāruhya sthale viparivartitum ,tathaiva rathamāruhya nāpsu caryā vidhīyate.
english: It is not possible to move about on dry land by mounting a boat. Likewise, having mounted a chariot, one cannot perform movement in the waters.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 30. It is not possible to move about on dry land by mounting a boat. Likewise, having mounted a chariot, one cannot perform movement in the waters.

sanskrit: एवं कर्म कृतं चित्रं विषयस्थं पृथक्पृथक् ।यथा कर्म कृतं लोके तथा तदुपपद्यते ॥३१॥
transliteration: evaṁ karma kṛtaṁ citraṁ viṣayasthaṁ pṛthakpṛthak ,yathā karma kṛtaṁ loke tathā tadupapadyate.
english: Thus, actions (karma) are performed in various ways, each related to its specific objects. Just as an action (karma) is performed in the world, so too does its consequence manifest accordingly.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 31. Thus, actions (karma) are performed in various ways, each related to its specific objects. Just as an action (karma) is performed in the world, so too does its consequence manifest accordingly.

sanskrit: यन्नैव गन्धिनो रस्यं न रूपस्पर्शशब्दवत् ।मन्यन्ते मुनयो बुद्ध्या तत्प्रधानं प्रचक्षते ॥३२॥
transliteration: yannaiva gandhino rasyaṁ na rūpasparśaśabdavat ,manyante munayo buddhyā tatpradhānaṁ pracakṣate.
english: That which indeed possesses neither fragrance nor taste, nor form, touch, or sound – that, the sages (muni), by their intellect, declare to be the primal cause (pradhāna).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 32. That which indeed possesses neither fragrance nor taste, nor form, touch, or sound – that, the sages (muni), by their intellect, declare to be the primal cause (pradhāna).

sanskrit: तत्र प्रधानमव्यक्तमव्यक्तस्य गुणो महान् ।महतः प्रधानभूतस्य गुणोऽहंकार एव च ॥३३॥
transliteration: tatra pradhānamavyaktamavyaktasya guṇo mahān ,mahataḥ pradhānabhūtasya guṇo'haṁkāra eva ca.
english: In this context, the primal cause (pradhāna) is the unmanifest (avyakta). The great cosmic intellect (mahat) is an evolute of the unmanifest (avyakta). And indeed, ego (ahaṅkāra) is an evolute of that primal great cosmic intellect (mahat).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 33. In this context, the primal cause (pradhāna) is the unmanifest (avyakta). sanskrit: अहंकारप्रधानस्य महाभूतकृतो गुणः ।पृथक्त्वेन हि भूतानां विषया वै गुणाः स्मृताः ॥३४॥
transliteration: ahaṁkārapradhānasya mahābhūtakṛto guṇaḥ ,pṛthaktvena hi bhūtānāṁ viṣayā vai guṇāḥ smṛtāḥ.
english: For that which has ego (ahaṅkāra) as its primary principle, the subtle qualities (guṇa) that generate the great elements (mahābhūta) arise. Indeed, the sense-objects (viṣaya) of these elements (bhūta) are, by their distinctness, declared to be their qualities.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 34. sanskrit: बीजधर्मं यथाव्यक्तं तथैव प्रसवात्मकम् ।बीजधर्मा महानात्मा प्रसवश्चेति नः श्रुतम् ॥३५॥
transliteration: bījadharmaṁ yathāvyaktaṁ tathaiva prasavātmakam ,bījadharmā mahānātmā prasavaśceti naḥ śrutam.
english: Just as the manifested (vyakta) world is generative in nature, so too is the intrinsic nature (dharma) of the seed. It has been heard by us that the great self (mahān ātman) is both the seed-principle and the product of generation (prasava).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 35. Just as the manifested (vyakta) world is generative in nature, so too is the intrinsic nature (dharma) of the seed. sanskrit: बीजधर्मा त्वहंकारः प्रसवश्च पुनः पुनः ।बीजप्रसवधर्माणि महाभूतानि पञ्च वै ॥३६॥
transliteration: bījadharmā tvahaṁkāraḥ prasavaśca punaḥ punaḥ ,bījaprasavadharmāṇi mahābhūtāni pañca vai.
english: However, ego (ahaṅkāra) possesses the intrinsic nature (dharma) of a seed, and its production occurs repeatedly. Indeed, the five great elements (mahābhūtas) have the intrinsic nature (dharma) of both seed and production (prasava).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 36. However, ego (ahaṅkāra) possesses the intrinsic nature (dharma) of a seed, and its production occurs repeatedly. Indeed, the five great elements (mahābhūtas) have the intrinsic nature (dharma) of both seed and production (prasava).

sanskrit: बीजधर्मिण इत्याहुः प्रसवं च न कुर्वते ।विशेषाः पञ्चभूतानां तेषां वित्तं विशेषणम् ॥३७॥
transliteration: bījadharmiṇa ityāhuḥ prasavaṁ ca na kurvate ,viśeṣāḥ pañcabhūtānāṁ teṣāṁ vittaṁ viśeṣaṇam.
english: They declare that those possessing a seed-like intrinsic nature (dharma) do not, however, generate further evolution (prasava). The specific qualities (viśeṣas) of these five elements (mahābhūtas) are known to be their distinguishing characteristic (viśeṣaṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 37. They declare that those possessing a seed-like intrinsic nature (dharma) do not, however, generate further evolution (prasava). sanskrit: तत्रैकगुणमाकाशं द्विगुणो वायुरुच्यते ।त्रिगुणं ज्योतिरित्याहुरापश्चापि चतुर्गुणाः ॥३८॥
transliteration: tatraikaguṇamākāśaṁ dviguṇo vāyurucyate ,triguṇaṁ jyotirityāhurāpaścāpi caturguṇāḥ.
english: In this regard, space (ākāśa) is said to possess one quality. Air (vāyu) is called two-qualified. They declare that light (jyotiḥ) has three qualities, and waters (āpaḥ) are also four-qualified.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 38. In this regard, space (ākāśa) is said to possess one quality. Air (vāyu) is called two-qualified. They declare that light (jyotiḥ) has three qualities, and waters (āpaḥ) are also four-qualified.

sanskrit: पृथ्वी पञ्चगुणा ज्ञेया त्रसस्थावरसंकुला ।सर्वभूतकरी देवी शुभाशुभनिदर्शना ॥३९॥
transliteration: pṛthvī pañcaguṇā jñeyā trasasthāvarasaṁkulā ,sarvabhūtakarī devī śubhāśubhanidarśanā.
english: The Earth (pṛthvī) should be understood as possessing five qualities. It is densely populated by both moving and stationary beings, is the creator of all creatures, a divine entity (devī), and manifests both auspicious and inauspicious results.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 39. The Earth (pṛthvī) should be understood as possessing five qualities.
