sanskrit: शब्दः स्पर्शस्तथा रूपं रसो गन्धश्च पञ्चमः ।एते पञ्च गुणा भूमेर्विज्ञेया द्विजसत्तमाः ॥४०॥
transliteration: śabdaḥ sparśastathā rūpaṁ raso gandhaśca pañcamaḥ ,ete pañca guṇā bhūmervijñeyā dvijasattamāḥ.
english: Sound, touch, form, taste, and fifthly, smell - these are the five qualities of the Earth (pṛthvī) that should be known, O best among the twice-born (dvijasattamāḥ)!
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 40. Sound, touch, form, taste, and fifthly, smell - these are the five qualities of the Earth (pṛthvī) that should be known, O best among the twice-born (dvijasattamāḥ)!

sanskrit: पार्थिवश्च सदा गन्धो गन्धश्च बहुधा स्मृतः ।तस्य गन्धस्य वक्ष्यामि विस्तरेण बहून्गुणान् ॥४१॥
transliteration: pārthivaśca sadā gandho gandhaśca bahudhā smṛtaḥ ,tasya gandhasya vakṣyāmi vistareṇa bahūnguṇān.
english: Terrestrial smell (gandha) is always present, and this smell is considered to be of many varieties. I will now explain in detail its numerous qualities.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 41. Terrestrial smell (gandha) is always present, and this smell is considered to be of many varieties. I will now explain in detail its numerous qualities.

sanskrit: इष्टश्चानिष्टगन्धश्च मधुरोऽम्लः कटुस्तथा ।निर्हारी संहतः स्निग्धो रूक्षो विशद एव च ।एवं दशविधो ज्ञेयः पार्थिवो गन्ध इत्युत ॥४२॥
transliteration: iṣṭaścāniṣṭagandhaśca madhuro'mlaḥ kaṭustathā ,nirhārī saṁhataḥ snigdho rūkṣo viśada eva ca ,evaṁ daśavidho jñeyaḥ pārthivo gandha ityuta.
english: Agreeable and disagreeable, sweet, sour, pungent, and also diffusive, compact, oily, rough, and clear - thus, the terrestrial smell (gandha) is indeed to be known as ten-fold.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 42. Agreeable and disagreeable, sweet, sour, pungent, and also diffusive, compact, oily, rough, and clear - thus, the terrestrial smell (gandha) is indeed to be known as ten-fold.

sanskrit: शब्दः स्पर्शस्तथा रूपं रसश्चापां गुणाः स्मृताः ।रसज्ञानं तु वक्ष्यामि रसस्तु बहुधा स्मृतः ॥४३॥
transliteration: śabdaḥ sparśastathā rūpaṁ rasaścāpāṁ guṇāḥ smṛtāḥ ,rasajñānaṁ tu vakṣyāmi rasastu bahudhā smṛtaḥ.
english: Sound, touch, form, and taste are considered the qualities of water. Now, I will explain the knowledge of taste, for taste is remembered to be of many kinds.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 43. Sound, touch, form, and taste are considered the qualities of water. Now, I will explain the knowledge of taste, for taste is remembered to be of many kinds.

sanskrit: मधुरोऽम्लः कटुस्तिक्तः कषायो लवणस्तथा ।एवं षड्विधविस्तारो रसो वारिमयः स्मृतः ॥४४॥
transliteration: madhuro'mlaḥ kaṭustiktaḥ kaṣāyo lavaṇastathā ,evaṁ ṣaḍvidhavistāro raso vārimayaḥ smṛtaḥ.
english: Sweet, sour, pungent, bitter, astringent, and salty - thus, taste is considered to be of six extensive kinds, being composed of water.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 44. Sweet, sour, pungent, bitter, astringent, and salty - thus, taste is considered to be of six extensive kinds, being composed of water.

sanskrit: शब्दः स्पर्शस्तथा रूपं त्रिगुणं ज्योतिरुच्यते ।ज्योतिषश्च गुणो रूपं रूपं च बहुधा स्मृतम् ॥४५॥
transliteration: śabdaḥ sparśastathā rūpaṁ triguṇaṁ jyotirucyate ,jyotiṣaśca guṇo rūpaṁ rūpaṁ ca bahudhā smṛtam.
english: Sound, touch, and form - these three are declared to be the qualities of light (jyotiḥ). And the quality of light (jyotiḥ) itself is form; this form is considered to be of many kinds.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 45. Sound, touch, and form - these three are declared to be the qualities of light (jyotiḥ). And the quality of light (jyotiḥ) itself is form; this form is considered to be of many kinds.

sanskrit: शुक्लं कृष्णं तथा रक्तं नीलं पीतारुणं तथा ।ह्रस्वं दीर्घं तथा स्थूलं चतुरस्राणु वृत्तकम् ॥४६॥
transliteration: śuklaṁ kṛṣṇaṁ tathā raktaṁ nīlaṁ pītāruṇaṁ tathā ,hrasvaṁ dīrghaṁ tathā sthūlaṁ caturasrāṇu vṛttakam.
english: White, black, and also red, blue, and orange (yellow-red); similarly, short, long, and also thick, square, minute, and circular.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 46. White, black, and also red, blue, and orange (yellow-red); similarly, short, long, and also thick, square, minute, and circular.

sanskrit: एवं द्वादशविस्तारं तेजसो रूपमुच्यते ।विज्ञेयं ब्राह्मणैर्नित्यं धर्मज्ञैः सत्यवादिभिः ॥४७॥
transliteration: evaṁ dvādaśavistāraṁ tejaso rūpamucyate ,vijñeyaṁ brāhmaṇairnityaṁ dharmajñaiḥ satyavādibhiḥ.
english: Thus, the twelve-fold manifestation of radiant energy (tejas) is described. It should always be understood by those brahmins (brāhmaṇa) who are knowledgeable in natural law (dharma) and speak the truth.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 47. Thus, the twelve-fold manifestation of radiant energy (tejas) is described. It should always be understood by those brahmins (brāhmaṇa) who are knowledgeable in natural law (dharma) and speak the truth.

sanskrit: शब्दस्पर्शौ च विज्ञेयौ द्विगुणो वायुरुच्यते ।वायोश्चापि गुणः स्पर्शः स्पर्शश्च बहुधा स्मृतः ॥४८॥
transliteration: śabdasparśau ca vijñeyau dviguṇo vāyurucyate ,vāyoścāpi guṇaḥ sparśaḥ sparśaśca bahudhā smṛtaḥ.
english: Sound and touch are to be understood; air (vāyu) is said to possess two qualities. Indeed, touch is a quality of air (vāyu), and this touch is remembered to manifest in many forms.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 48. Sound and touch are to be understood; air (vāyu) is said to possess two qualities. Indeed, touch is a quality of air (vāyu), and this touch is remembered to manifest in many forms.

sanskrit: उष्णः शीतः सुखो दुःखः स्निग्धो विशद एव च ।कठिनश्चिक्कणः श्लक्ष्णः पिच्छिलो दारुणो मृदुः ॥४९॥
transliteration: uṣṇaḥ śītaḥ sukho duḥkhaḥ snigdho viśada eva ca ,kaṭhinaścikkaṇaḥ ślakṣṇaḥ picchilo dāruṇo mṛduḥ.
english: Hot, cold, pleasant, unpleasant, oily, clear, and indeed hard, smooth, soft, slimy, harsh, and gentle.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 49. Hot, cold, pleasant, unpleasant, oily, clear, and indeed hard, smooth, soft, slimy, harsh, and gentle.

sanskrit: एवं द्वादशविस्तारो वायव्यो गुण उच्यते ।विधिवद्ब्रह्मणैः सिद्धैर्धर्मज्ञैस्तत्त्वदर्शिभिः ॥५०॥
transliteration: evaṁ dvādaśavistāro vāyavyo guṇa ucyate ,vidhivadbrahmaṇaiḥ siddhairdharmajñaistattvadarśibhiḥ.
english: Thus, this twelve-fold manifestation, which is a quality of air (vāyu), is described. It is understood by duly accomplished brahmins (brāhmaṇa) who are knowledgeable in natural law (dharma) and are seers of ultimate reality (tattva).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 50. Thus, this twelve-fold manifestation, which is a quality of air (vāyu), is described. It is understood by duly accomplished brahmins (brāhmaṇa) who are knowledgeable in natural law (dharma) and are seers of ultimate reality (tattva).

sanskrit: तत्रैकगुणमाकाशं शब्द इत्येव च स्मृतः ।तस्य शब्दस्य वक्ष्यामि विस्तरेण बहून्गुणान् ॥५१॥
transliteration: tatraikaguṇamākāśaṁ śabda ityeva ca smṛtaḥ ,tasya śabdasya vakṣyāmi vistareṇa bahūnguṇān.

search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 51. Space is considered to possess a single quality, sound. I will now explain in detail the many attributes of this sound.

sanskrit: षड्जर्षभौ च गान्धारो मध्यमः पञ्चमस्तथा ।अतः परं तु विज्ञेयो निषादो धैवतस्तथा ॥५२॥
transliteration: ṣaḍjarṣabhau ca gāndhāro madhyamaḥ pañcamastathā ,ataḥ paraṁ tu vijñeyo niṣādo dhaivatastathā.
english: The musical notes are ṣaḍja, ṛṣabha, gāndhāra, madhyama, and pañcama. Furthermore, niṣāda and dhaivata are also to be understood.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 52. The musical notes are ṣaḍja, ṛṣabha, gāndhāra, madhyama, and pañcama. Furthermore, niṣāda and dhaivata are also to be understood.

sanskrit: इष्टोऽनिष्टश्च शब्दस्तु संहतः प्रविभागवान् ।एवं बहुविधो ज्ञेयः शब्द आकाशसंभवः ॥५३॥
transliteration: iṣṭo'niṣṭaśca śabdastu saṁhataḥ pravibhāgavān ,evaṁ bahuvidho jñeyaḥ śabda ākāśasaṁbhavaḥ.
english: Sound can be pleasant or unpleasant, combined and differentiated. Therefore, sound, which originates from space (ākāśa), should be understood as having many varieties.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 53. Sound can be pleasant or unpleasant, combined and differentiated. Therefore, sound, which originates from space (ākāśa), should be understood as having many varieties.

sanskrit: आकाशमुत्तमं भूतमहंकारस्ततः परम् ।अहंकारात्परा बुद्धिर्बुद्धेरात्मा ततः परः ॥५४॥
transliteration: ākāśamuttamaṁ bhūtamahaṁkārastataḥ param ,ahaṁkārātparā buddhirbuddherātmā tataḥ paraḥ.
english: Space is the supreme element; beyond that is the ego (ahaṅkāra). Superior to the ego (ahaṅkāra) is the intellect, and beyond the intellect is the self (ātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 54. Space is the supreme element; beyond that is the ego (ahaṅkāra). Superior to the ego (ahaṅkāra) is the intellect, and beyond the intellect is the self (ātman).

sanskrit: तस्मात्तु परमव्यक्तमव्यक्तात्पुरुषः परः ।परावरज्ञो भूतानां यं प्राप्यानन्त्यमश्नुते ॥५५॥
transliteration: tasmāttu paramavyaktamavyaktātpuruṣaḥ paraḥ ,parāvarajño bhūtānāṁ yaṁ prāpyānantyamaśnute.
english: Indeed, the supreme unmanifest is superior to that (subtle unmanifest). Superior to the unmanifest is the supreme cosmic person (puruṣa). Having attained this (puruṣa), who knows both the higher and the lower aspects of all beings, one achieves infinity.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 49 Verse 55. Indeed, the supreme unmanifest is superior to that (subtle unmanifest). sanskrit: ब्रह्मोवाच ।भूतानामथ पञ्चानां यथैषामीश्वरं मनः ।नियमे च विसर्गे च भूतात्मा मन एव च ॥१॥
transliteration: brahmovāca ,bhūtānāmatha pañcānāṁ yathaiṣāmīśvaraṁ manaḥ ,niyame ca visarge ca bhūtātmā mana eva ca.
english: Brahmā said: Now, concerning the five elements, how the mind (manas) is their lord, both in their regulation and their emanation. Indeed, the mind (manas) itself is the inner essence (ātman) of beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 1. Brahmā said: Now, concerning the five elements, how the mind (manas) is their lord, both in their regulation and their emanation. Indeed, the mind (manas) itself is the inner essence (ātman) of beings.

sanskrit: अधिष्ठाता मनो नित्यं भूतानां महतां तथा ।बुद्धिरैश्वर्यमाचष्टे क्षेत्रज्ञः सर्व उच्यते ॥२॥
transliteration: adhiṣṭhātā mano nityaṁ bhūtānāṁ mahatāṁ tathā ,buddhiraiśvaryamācaṣṭe kṣetrajñaḥ sarva ucyate.
english: The mind (manas) is always the presiding controller, and similarly, it is the controller of the great elements. The intellect (buddhi) manifests supremacy, and the knower of the field (kṣetrajña) is called the All.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 2. The mind (manas) is always the presiding controller, and similarly, it is the controller of the great elements. The intellect (buddhi) manifests supremacy, and the knower of the field (kṣetrajña) is called the All.

sanskrit: इन्द्रियाणि मनो युङ्क्ते सदश्वानिव सारथिः ।इन्द्रियाणि मनो बुद्धिं क्षेत्रज्ञो युञ्जते सदा ॥३॥
transliteration: indriyāṇi mano yuṅkte sadaśvāniva sārathiḥ ,indriyāṇi mano buddhiṁ kṣetrajño yuñjate sadā.
english: Just as a charioteer harnesses good horses, the mind (manas) controls the senses. Similarly, the knower of the field (kṣetrajña) always controls the senses, the mind (manas), and the intellect (buddhi).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 3. Just as a charioteer harnesses good horses, the mind (manas) controls the senses. Similarly, the knower of the field (kṣetrajña) always controls the senses, the mind (manas), and the intellect (buddhi).

sanskrit: महाभूतसमायुक्तं बुद्धिसंयमनं रथम् ।तमारुह्य स भूतात्मा समन्तात्परिधावति ॥४॥
transliteration: mahābhūtasamāyuktaṁ buddhisaṁyamanaṁ ratham ,tamāruhya sa bhūtātmā samantātparidhāvati.
english: The individual soul (ātman) mounts that chariot, which is composed of the great elements and controlled by the intellect, and then roams about everywhere.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 4. The individual soul (ātman) mounts that chariot, which is composed of the great elements and controlled by the intellect, and then roams about everywhere.

sanskrit: इन्द्रियग्रामसंयुक्तो मनःसारथिरेव च ।बुद्धिसंयमनो नित्यं महान्ब्रह्ममयो रथः ॥५॥
transliteration: indriyagrāmasaṁyukto manaḥsārathireva ca ,buddhisaṁyamano nityaṁ mahānbrahmamayo rathaḥ.
english: Indeed, this great chariot, which is endowed with the multitude of senses, has the mind as its charioteer, is always controlled by the intellect, and consists of (brahman).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 5. Indeed, this great chariot, which is endowed with the multitude of senses, has the mind as its charioteer, is always controlled by the intellect, and consists of (brahman).

sanskrit: एवं यो वेत्ति विद्वान्वै सदा ब्रह्ममयं रथम् ।स धीरः सर्वलोकेषु न मोहमधिगच्छति ॥६॥
transliteration: evaṁ yo vetti vidvānvai sadā brahmamayaṁ ratham ,sa dhīraḥ sarvalokeṣu na mohamadhigacchati.
english: Indeed, that wise person who always knows this chariot to be consisting of (brahman) in this manner, that steadfast one does not fall into delusion in any of the worlds.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 6. Indeed, that wise person who always knows this chariot to be consisting of (brahman) in this manner, that steadfast one does not fall into delusion in any of the worlds.

sanskrit: अव्यक्तादि विशेषान्तं त्रसस्थावरसंकुलम् ।चन्द्रसूर्यप्रभालोकं ग्रहनक्षत्रमण्डितम् ॥७॥
transliteration: avyaktādi viśeṣāntaṁ trasasthāvarasaṁkulam ,candrasūryaprabhālokaṁ grahanakṣatramaṇḍitam.
english: It is from the unmanifest (avyakta) beginning up to the manifest end; it is crowded with moving and unmoving beings; it is illuminated by the radiance of the moon and the sun; and it is adorned with planets and constellations.

sanskrit: नदीपर्वतजालैश्च सर्वतः परिभूषितम् ।विविधाभिस्तथाद्भिश्च सततं समलंकृतम् ॥८॥
transliteration: nadīparvatajālaiśca sarvataḥ paribhūṣitam ,vividhābhistathādbhiśca satataṁ samalaṁkṛtam.
english: It is adorned everywhere with networks of rivers and mountains, and constantly embellished by various waters.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 8. It is adorned everywhere with networks of rivers and mountains, and constantly embellished by various waters.

sanskrit: आजीवः सर्वभूतानां सर्वप्राणभृतां गतिः ।एतद्ब्रह्मवनं नित्यं यस्मिंश्चरति क्षेत्रवित् ॥९॥
transliteration: ājīvaḥ sarvabhūtānāṁ sarvaprāṇabhṛtāṁ gatiḥ ,etadbrahmavanaṁ nityaṁ yasmiṁścarati kṣetravit.
english: This is the livelihood of all beings and the resort of all living creatures. This is the eternal Brahman-forest, in which the knower of the field (kṣetravit) dwells.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 9. This is the livelihood of all beings and the resort of all living creatures. This is the eternal Brahman-forest, in which the knower of the field (kṣetravit) dwells.

sanskrit: लोकेऽस्मिन्यानि भूतानि स्थावराणि चराणि च ।तान्येवाग्रे प्रलीयन्ते पश्चाद्भूतकृता गुणाः ।गुणेभ्यः पञ्चभूतानि एष भूतसमुच्छ्रयः ॥१०॥
transliteration: loke'sminyāni bhūtāni sthāvarāṇi carāṇi ca ,tānyevāgre pralīyante paścādbhūtakṛtā guṇāḥ ,guṇebhyaḥ pañcabhūtāni eṣa bhūtasamucchrayaḥ.
english: In this world, all beings, both stationary and mobile, first dissolve. Afterwards, the elemental qualities (guṇa) [also dissolve]. From these qualities (guṇa) arise the five great elements (pañcabhūta). This is the aggregate evolution of existence.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 10. In this world, all beings, both stationary and mobile, first dissolve. Afterwards, the elemental qualities (guṇa) [also dissolve]. sanskrit: देवा मनुष्या गन्धर्वाः पिशाचासुरराक्षसाः ।सर्वे स्वभावतः सृष्टा न क्रियाभ्यो न कारणात् ॥११॥
transliteration: devā manuṣyā gandharvāḥ piśācāsurarākṣasāḥ ,sarve svabhāvataḥ sṛṣṭā na kriyābhyo na kāraṇāt.
english: Gods, humans, gandharvas, piśācas, asuras, and rākṣasas – all are created by their intrinsic nature (svabhāva), not by actions, nor by any specific cause.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 11. Gods, humans, gandharvas, piśācas, asuras, and rākṣasas – all are created by their intrinsic nature (svabhāva), not by actions, nor by any specific cause.

sanskrit: एते विश्वकृतो विप्रा जायन्ते ह पुनः पुनः ।तेभ्यः प्रसूतास्तेष्वेव महाभूतेषु पञ्चसु ।प्रलीयन्ते यथाकालमूर्मयः सागरे यथा ॥१२॥
transliteration: ete viśvakṛto viprā jāyante ha punaḥ punaḥ ,tebhyaḥ prasūtāsteṣveva mahābhūteṣu pañcasu ,pralīyante yathākālamūrmayaḥ sāgare yathā.
english: These wise (vipra) creators of the universe are indeed born again and again. What is born from them dissolves into those very five great elements (mahābhūta) in due course, just as waves merge into the ocean.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 12. These wise (vipra) creators of the universe are indeed born again and again. What is born from them dissolves into those very five great elements (mahābhūta) in due course, just as waves merge into the ocean.

sanskrit: विश्वसृग्भ्यस्तु भूतेभ्यो महाभूतानि गच्छति ।भूतेभ्यश्चापि पञ्चभ्यो मुक्तो गच्छेत्प्रजापतिम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: viśvasṛgbhyastu bhūtebhyo mahābhūtāni gacchati ,bhūtebhyaścāpi pañcabhyo mukto gacchetprajāpatim.
english: Indeed, from the universal creators (viśvasṛj) and from beings (bhūta), one proceeds towards the great elements (mahābhūta). And from these five elements, a liberated one (mukta) attains Prajāpati.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 13. Indeed, from the universal creators (viśvasṛj) and from beings (bhūta), one proceeds towards the great elements (mahābhūta). And from these five elements, a liberated one (mukta) attains Prajāpati.

sanskrit: प्रजापतिरिदं सर्वं तपसैवासृजत्प्रभुः ।तथैव वेदानृषयस्तपसा प्रतिपेदिरे ॥१४॥
transliteration: prajāpatiridaṁ sarvaṁ tapasaivāsṛjatprabhuḥ ,tathaiva vedānṛṣayastapasā pratipedire.
english: Lord Prajāpati indeed created all this by means of austerity (tapas). Similarly, the sages (ṛṣi) realized the Vedas (veda) through austerity (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 14. Lord Prajāpati indeed created all this by means of austerity (tapas). Similarly, the sages (ṛṣi) realized the Vedas (veda) through austerity (tapas).

sanskrit: तपसश्चानुपूर्व्येण फलमूलाशिनस्तथा ।त्रैलोक्यं तपसा सिद्धाः पश्यन्तीह समाहिताः ॥१५॥
transliteration: tapasaścānupūrvyeṇa phalamūlāśinastathā ,trailokyaṁ tapasā siddhāḥ paśyantīha samāhitāḥ.
english: And through the successive practice of austerity (tapas), those perfected ones (siddha) who subsist on fruits and roots, being concentrated (samāhita), perceive the three worlds (trailokya) right here through their austerity (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 15. And through the successive practice of austerity (tapas), those perfected ones (siddha) who subsist on fruits and roots, being concentrated (samāhita), perceive the three worlds (trailokya) right here through their austerity (tapas).

sanskrit: ओषधान्यगदादीनी नानाविद्याश्च सर्वशः ।तपसैव प्रसिध्यन्ति तपोमूलं हि साधनम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: oṣadhānyagadādīnī nānāvidyāśca sarvaśaḥ ,tapasaiva prasidhyanti tapomūlaṁ hi sādhanam.
english: Herbs, remedies, and various sciences are all perfected solely through ascetic practice (tapas). Indeed, ascetic practice (tapas) is the very foundation of all accomplishment.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 16. Herbs, remedies, and various sciences are all perfected solely through ascetic practice (tapas). Indeed, ascetic practice (tapas) is the very foundation of all accomplishment.

sanskrit: यद्दुरापं दुराम्नायं दुराधर्षं दुरन्वयम् ।तत्सर्वं तपसा साध्यं तपो हि दुरतिक्रमम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: yaddurāpaṁ durāmnāyaṁ durādharṣaṁ duranvayam ,tatsarvaṁ tapasā sādhyaṁ tapo hi duratikramam.
english: Whatever is difficult to obtain, difficult to comprehend, difficult to overpower, or difficult to explain - all that can be achieved through ascetic practice (tapas). Indeed, ascetic practice (tapas) is insurmountable.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 17. Whatever is difficult to obtain, difficult to comprehend, difficult to overpower, or difficult to explain - all that can be achieved through ascetic practice (tapas). Indeed, ascetic practice (tapas) is insurmountable.

sanskrit: सुरापो ब्रह्महा स्तेयी भ्रूणहा गुरुतल्पगः ।तपसैव सुतप्तेन मुच्यन्ते किल्बिषात्ततः ॥१८॥
transliteration: surāpo brahmahā steyī bhrūṇahā gurutalpagaḥ ,tapasaiva sutaptena mucyante kilbiṣāttataḥ.
english: A drinker of wine, a slayer of a brahmin, a thief, a destroyer of an embryo (bhrūṇahā), and one who violates the teacher's wife - they are all liberated from their misdeeds solely through intensely performed ascetic practice (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 18. A drinker of wine, a slayer of a brahmin, a thief, a destroyer of an embryo (bhrūṇahā), and one who violates the teacher's wife - they are all liberated from their misdeeds solely through intensely performed ascetic practice (tapas).

sanskrit: मनुष्याः पितरो देवाः पशवो मृगपक्षिणः ।यानि चान्यानि भूतानि त्रसानि स्थावराणि च ॥१९॥
transliteration: manuṣyāḥ pitaro devāḥ paśavo mṛgapakṣiṇaḥ ,yāni cānyāni bhūtāni trasāni sthāvarāṇi ca.
english: Humans, ancestors, gods, domesticated animals, wild animals and birds - and whatever other beings exist, both mobile and stationary.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 19. Humans, ancestors, gods, domesticated animals, wild animals and birds - and whatever other beings exist, both mobile and stationary.

sanskrit: तपःपरायणा नित्यं सिध्यन्ते तपसा सदा ।तथैव तपसा देवा महाभागा दिवं गताः ॥२०॥
transliteration: tapaḥparāyaṇā nityaṁ sidhyante tapasā sadā ,tathaiva tapasā devā mahābhāgā divaṁ gatāḥ.
english: Those who are constantly devoted to ascetic practice (tapas) always achieve success through such practice. Similarly, the highly fortunate gods attained heaven through their ascetic practice (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 20. Those who are constantly devoted to ascetic practice (tapas) always achieve success through such practice. Similarly, the highly fortunate gods attained heaven through their ascetic practice (tapas).

sanskrit: आशीर्युक्तानि कर्माणि कुर्वते ये त्वतन्द्रिताः ।अहंकारसमायुक्तास्ते सकाशे प्रजापतेः ॥२१॥
transliteration: āśīryuktāni karmāṇi kurvate ye tvatandritāḥ ,ahaṁkārasamāyuktāste sakāśe prajāpateḥ.
english: But those who diligently perform actions (karma) motivated by desires for blessings, being possessed of ego (ahaṃkāra), they remain in the presence of Prajāpati.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 21. But those who diligently perform actions (karma) motivated by desires for blessings, being possessed of ego (ahaṃkāra), they remain in the presence of Prajāpati.

sanskrit: ध्यानयोगेन शुद्धेन निर्ममा निरहंकृताः ।प्राप्नुवन्ति महात्मानो महान्तं लोकमुत्तमम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: dhyānayogena śuddhena nirmamā nirahaṁkṛtāḥ ,prāpnuvanti mahātmāno mahāntaṁ lokamuttamam.
english: Great souls, who are pure, free from possessiveness, and free from ego (ahaṃkāra), attain a great and supreme realm through the pure practice of meditative discipline (dhyāna-yoga).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 22. Great souls, who are pure, free from possessiveness, and free from ego (ahaṃkāra), attain a great and supreme realm through the pure practice of meditative discipline (dhyāna-yoga).

sanskrit: ध्यानयोगादुपागम्य प्रसन्नमतयः सदा ।सुखोपचयमव्यक्तं प्रविशन्त्यात्मवत्तया ॥२३॥
transliteration: dhyānayogādupāgamya prasannamatayaḥ sadā ,sukhopacayamavyaktaṁ praviśantyātmavattayā.
english: Always having attained serenity through meditative discipline (dhyāna-yoga), those with tranquil minds enter the unmanifest state of increasing happiness by virtue of their self-mastery (ātmavattayā).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 23. Always having attained serenity through meditative discipline (dhyāna-yoga), those with tranquil minds enter the unmanifest state of increasing happiness by virtue of their self-mastery (ātmavattayā).

sanskrit: ध्यानयोगादुपागम्य निर्ममा निरहंकृताः ।अव्यक्तं प्रविशन्तीह महान्तं लोकमुत्तमम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: dhyānayogādupāgamya nirmamā nirahaṁkṛtāḥ ,avyaktaṁ praviśantīha mahāntaṁ lokamuttamam.
english: Through the spiritual discipline (yoga) of meditation (dhyāna), having become free from possessiveness and ego (ahaṅkāra), they enter here the great, supreme, unmanifest (avyakta) realm.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 24. Through the spiritual discipline (yoga) of meditation (dhyāna), having become free from possessiveness and ego (ahaṅkāra), they enter here the great, supreme, unmanifest (avyakta) realm.

sanskrit: अव्यक्तादेव संभूतः समयज्ञो गतः पुनः ।तमोरजोभ्यां निर्मुक्तः सत्त्वमास्थाय केवलम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: avyaktādeva saṁbhūtaḥ samayajño gataḥ punaḥ ,tamorajobhyāṁ nirmuktaḥ sattvamāsthāya kevalam.
english: One who has indeed arisen from the unmanifest (avyakta) and departed again (from the cycle of rebirth), having understood the true nature of things, such a one, liberated from the qualities of darkness (tamas) and passion (rajas), becomes established solely in purity (sattva).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 25. One who has indeed arisen from the unmanifest (avyakta) and departed again (from the cycle of rebirth), having understood the true nature of things, such a one, liberated from the qualities of darkness (tamas) and passion (rajas), becomes established solely in purity (sattva).

sanskrit: विमुक्तः सर्वपापेभ्यः सर्वं त्यजति निष्कलः ।क्षेत्रज्ञ इति तं विद्याद्यस्तं वेद स वेदवित् ॥२६॥
transliteration: vimuktaḥ sarvapāpebhyaḥ sarvaṁ tyajati niṣkalaḥ ,kṣetrajña iti taṁ vidyādyastaṁ veda sa vedavit.
english: Liberated from all sins, he abandons everything and is unblemished. One should know him as the knower of the field (kṣetrajña). He who knows that [knower of the field] is truly a knower of the Vedas.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 26. Liberated from all sins, he abandons everything and is unblemished. One should know him as the knower of the field (kṣetrajña). He who knows that [knower of the field] is truly a knower of the Vedas.

sanskrit: चित्तं चित्तादुपागम्य मुनिरासीत संयतः ।यच्चित्तस्तन्मना भूत्वा गुह्यमेतत्सनातनम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: cittaṁ cittādupāgamya munirāsīta saṁyataḥ ,yaccittastanmanā bhūtvā guhyametatsanātanam.
english: A self-controlled sage (muni), having withdrawn the mind (citta) from its [external] objects, should sit. Having become one whose mind and thoughts are fixed on this eternal secret, [one realizes it].
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 27. A self-controlled sage (muni), having withdrawn the mind (citta) from its [external] objects, should sit. Having become one whose mind and thoughts are fixed on this eternal secret, [one realizes it].

sanskrit: अव्यक्तादि विशेषान्तमविद्यालक्षणं स्मृतम् ।निबोधत यथा हीदं गुणैर्लक्षणमित्युत ॥२८॥
transliteration: avyaktādi viśeṣāntamavidyālakṣaṇaṁ smṛtam ,nibodhata yathā hīdaṁ guṇairlakṣaṇamityuta.
english: That which extends from the unmanifest to the specific is traditionally understood to be characterized by ignorance (avidyā). Therefore, understand how this, too, is defined by the [three] qualities (guṇas).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 28. That which extends from the unmanifest to the specific is traditionally understood to be characterized by ignorance (avidyā). Therefore, understand how this, too, is defined by the [three] qualities (guṇas).

sanskrit: द्व्यक्षरस्तु भवेन्मृत्युस्त्र्यक्षरं ब्रह्म शाश्वतम् ।ममेति च भवेन्मृत्युर्न ममेति च शाश्वतम् ॥२९॥
transliteration: dvyakṣarastu bhavenmṛtyustryakṣaraṁ brahma śāśvatam ,mameti ca bhavenmṛtyurna mameti ca śāśvatam.
english: Death is said to be two syllables, while the eternal (brahman) is three syllables. The assertion 'this is mine' (mama) verily becomes death, and the assertion 'this is not mine' (na mama) verily leads to the eternal (brahman).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 29. Death is said to be two syllables, while the eternal (brahman) is three syllables. The assertion 'this is mine' (mama) verily becomes death, and the assertion 'this is not mine' (na mama) verily leads to the eternal (brahman).

sanskrit: कर्म केचित्प्रशंसन्ति मन्दबुद्धितरा नराः ।ये तु बुद्धा महात्मानो न प्रशंसन्ति कर्म ते ॥३०॥
transliteration: karma kecitpraśaṁsanti mandabuddhitarā narāḥ ,ye tu buddhā mahātmāno na praśaṁsanti karma te.
english: Some dull-witted men praise ritual action (karma). But those who are enlightened, the great souls (mahātman), they do not praise action (karma).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 30. Some dull-witted men praise ritual action (karma). But those who are enlightened, the great souls (mahātman), they do not praise action (karma).

sanskrit: कर्मणा जायते जन्तुर्मूर्तिमान्षोडशात्मकः ।पुरुषं सृजतेऽविद्या अग्राह्यममृताशिनम् ॥३१॥
transliteration: karmaṇā jāyate janturmūrtimānṣoḍaśātmakaḥ ,puruṣaṁ sṛjate'vidyā agrāhyamamṛtāśinam.
english: By means of past action (karma), a living being is born, endowed with a form and consisting of sixteen constituents. Ignorance (avidyā) creates this individual person (puruṣa), who is indeed imperceptible and partakes of immortality.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 31. By means of past action (karma), a living being is born, endowed with a form and consisting of sixteen constituents. Ignorance (avidyā) creates this individual person (puruṣa), who is indeed imperceptible and partakes of immortality.

sanskrit: तस्मात्कर्मसु निःस्नेहा ये केचित्पारदर्शिनः ।विद्यामयोऽयं पुरुषो न तु कर्ममयः स्मृतः ॥३२॥
transliteration: tasmātkarmasu niḥsnehā ye kecitpāradarśinaḥ ,vidyāmayo'yaṁ puruṣo na tu karmamayaḥ smṛtaḥ.
english: Therefore, those discerning ones who are free from attachment in actions (karma) understand this: this person (puruṣa) is considered to be constituted by knowledge, not by actions (karma) alone.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 32. Therefore, those discerning ones who are free from attachment in actions (karma) understand this: this person (puruṣa) is considered to be constituted by knowledge, not by actions (karma) alone.

sanskrit: अपूर्वममृतं नित्यं य एनमविचारिणम् ।य एनं विन्दतेऽऽत्मानमग्राह्यममृताशिनम् ।अग्राह्योऽमृतो भवति य एभिः कारणैर्ध्रुवः ॥३३॥
transliteration: apūrvamamṛtaṁ nityaṁ ya enamavicāriṇam ,ya enaṁ vindate''tmānamagrāhyamamṛtāśinam ,agrāhyo'mṛto bhavati ya ebhiḥ kāraṇairdhruvaḥ.
english: That which is unprecedented, immortal, and eternal, and that (essence) which is beyond conceptual thought; he who realizes this self (ātman), which is both incomprehensible and partaking of immortality, himself becomes incomprehensible and immortal, being firmly established by these reasons.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 33. That which is unprecedented, immortal, and eternal, and that (essence) which is beyond conceptual thought; he who realizes this self (ātman), which is both incomprehensible and partaking of immortality, himself becomes incomprehensible and immortal, being firmly established by these reasons.

sanskrit: अपोह्य सर्वसंकल्पान्संयम्यात्मानमात्मनि ।स तद्ब्रह्म शुभं वेत्ति यस्माद्भूयो न विद्यते ॥३४॥
transliteration: apohya sarvasaṁkalpānsaṁyamyātmānamātmani ,sa tadbrahma śubhaṁ vetti yasmādbhūyo na vidyate.
english: Having renounced all mental constructs (saṅkalpa) and restrained the self (ātman) within the (higher) self, one truly knows that auspicious (brahman), beyond which nothing further exists.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 34. Having renounced all mental constructs (saṅkalpa) and restrained the self (ātman) within the (higher) self, one truly knows that auspicious (brahman), beyond which nothing further exists.

sanskrit: प्रसादेनैव सत्त्वस्य प्रसादं समवाप्नुयात् ।लक्षणं हि प्रसादस्य यथा स्यात्स्वप्नदर्शनम् ॥३५॥
transliteration: prasādenaiva sattvasya prasādaṁ samavāpnuyāt ,lakṣaṇaṁ hi prasādasya yathā syātsvapnadarśanam.
english: One should attain grace (prasāda) through the grace (prasāda) of the pure essence (sattva) alone. For, the characteristic of this grace is indeed like a dream vision.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 35. One should attain grace (prasāda) through the grace (prasāda) of the pure essence (sattva) alone. For, the characteristic of this grace is indeed like a dream vision.

sanskrit: गतिरेषा तु मुक्तानां ये ज्ञानपरिनिष्ठिताः ।प्रवृत्तयश्च याः सर्वाः पश्यन्ति परिणामजाः ॥३६॥
transliteration: gatireṣā tu muktānāṁ ye jñānapariniṣṭhitāḥ ,pravṛttayaśca yāḥ sarvāḥ paśyanti pariṇāmajāḥ.
english: This indeed is the state for the liberated, who are firmly established in knowledge. They perceive all activities as arising from consequences.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 36. This indeed is the state for the liberated, who are firmly established in knowledge. They perceive all activities as arising from consequences.

sanskrit: एषा गतिरसक्तानामेष धर्मः सनातनः ।एषा ज्ञानवतां प्राप्तिरेतद्वृत्तमनिन्दितम् ॥३७॥
transliteration: eṣā gatirasaktānāmeṣa dharmaḥ sanātanaḥ ,eṣā jñānavatāṁ prāptiretadvṛttamaninditam.
english: This is the state of the unattached; this is the eternal natural law (dharma). This is the attainment of the wise; this is blameless conduct.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 37. This is the state of the unattached; this is the eternal natural law (dharma). This is the attainment of the wise; this is blameless conduct.

sanskrit: समेन सर्वभूतेषु निःस्पृहेण निराशिषा ।शक्या गतिरियं गन्तुं सर्वत्र समदर्शिना ॥३८॥
transliteration: samena sarvabhūteṣu niḥspṛheṇa nirāśiṣā ,śakyā gatiriyaṁ gantuṁ sarvatra samadarśinā.
english: This state can be attained by one who is equal toward all beings, free from desires, devoid of expectations, and who perceives equality everywhere.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 38. This state can be attained by one who is equal toward all beings, free from desires, devoid of expectations, and who perceives equality everywhere.

sanskrit: एतद्वः सर्वमाख्यातं मया विप्रर्षिसत्तमाः ।एवमाचरत क्षिप्रं ततः सिद्धिमवाप्स्यथ ॥३९॥
transliteration: etadvaḥ sarvamākhyātaṁ mayā viprarṣisattamāḥ ,evamācarata kṣipraṁ tataḥ siddhimavāpsyatha.
english: All this has been explained by me to you, O foremost Brahmin sages. Act thus quickly; then you will attain spiritual perfection (siddhi).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 39. All this has been explained by me to you, O foremost Brahmin sages. Act thus quickly; then you will attain spiritual perfection (siddhi).

sanskrit: गुरुरुवाच ।इत्युक्तास्ते तु मुनयो ब्रह्मणा गुरुणा तथा ।कृतवन्तो महात्मानस्ततो लोकानवाप्नुवन् ॥४०॥
transliteration: gururuvāca ,ityuktāste tu munayo brahmaṇā guruṇā tathā ,kṛtavanto mahātmānastato lokānavāpnuvan.
english: The preceptor (guru) said: Thus addressed by Brahmā, the revered teacher, those great-souled sages (muni) performed their duties and subsequently attained the higher realms.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 40. The preceptor (guru) said: Thus addressed by Brahmā, the revered teacher, those great-souled sages (muni) performed their duties and subsequently attained the higher realms.

sanskrit: त्वमप्येतन्महाभाग यथोक्तं ब्रह्मणो वचः ।सम्यगाचर शुद्धात्मंस्ततः सिद्धिमवाप्स्यसि ॥४१॥
transliteration: tvamapyetanmahābhāga yathoktaṁ brahmaṇo vacaḥ ,samyagācara śuddhātmaṁstataḥ siddhimavāpsyasi.
english: "You too, O highly fortunate one, pure-souled one, should properly observe the word of Brahmā as it was spoken; thereafter, you will attain perfection (siddhi)."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 41. "You too, O highly fortunate one, pure-souled one, should properly observe the word of Brahmā as it was spoken; thereafter, you will attain perfection (siddhi)."

sanskrit: वासुदेव उवाच ।इत्युक्तः स तदा शिष्यो गुरुणा धर्ममुत्तमम् ।चकार सर्वं कौन्तेय ततो मोक्षमवाप्तवान् ॥४२॥
transliteration: vāsudeva uvāca ,ityuktaḥ sa tadā śiṣyo guruṇā dharmamuttamam ,cakāra sarvaṁ kaunteya tato mokṣamavāptavān.
english: Vāsudeva said: O son of Kuntī, that disciple, thus instructed by his preceptor (guru), then perfectly performed all his excellent duties (dharma) and thereafter attained final liberation (mokṣa).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 42. Vāsudeva said: O son of Kuntī, that disciple, thus instructed by his preceptor (guru), then perfectly performed all his excellent duties (dharma) and thereafter attained final liberation (mokṣa).

sanskrit: कृतकृत्यश्च स तदा शिष्यः कुरुकुलोद्वह ।तत्पदं समनुप्राप्तो यत्र गत्वा न शोचति ॥४३॥
transliteration: kṛtakṛtyaśca sa tadā śiṣyaḥ kurukulodvaha ,tatpadaṁ samanuprāpto yatra gatvā na śocati.
english: And that disciple, O upholder of the Kuru lineage, having fulfilled his purpose, then attained that supreme state (tat padam) where, having gone, one does not grieve.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 43. And that disciple, O upholder of the Kuru lineage, having fulfilled his purpose, then attained that supreme state (tat padam) where, having gone, one does not grieve.

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच ।को न्वसौ ब्राह्मणः कृष्ण कश्च शिष्यो जनार्दन ।श्रोतव्यं चेन्मयैतद्वै तत्त्वमाचक्ष्व मे विभो ॥४४॥
transliteration: arjuna uvāca ,ko nvasau brāhmaṇaḥ kṛṣṇa kaśca śiṣyo janārdana ,śrotavyaṁ cenmayaitadvai tattvamācakṣva me vibho.
english: Arjuna said: "Who then is this Brahmin, Krishna? And who is the disciple, Janardana? If this truth is to be heard by me, O mighty one, then explain that truth to me."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 44. Arjuna said: "Who then is this Brahmin, Krishna? And who is the disciple, Janardana? If this truth is to be heard by me, O mighty one, then explain that truth to me."

sanskrit: वासुदेव उवाच ।अहं गुरुर्महाबाहो मनः शिष्यं च विद्धि मे ।त्वत्प्रीत्या गुह्यमेतच्च कथितं मे धनंजय ॥४५॥
transliteration: vāsudeva uvāca ,ahaṁ gururmahābāho manaḥ śiṣyaṁ ca viddhi me ,tvatprītyā guhyametacca kathitaṁ me dhanaṁjaya.
english: Vasudeva said: "O mighty-armed one, know that I am the preceptor (guru) and the mind is my disciple. This secret, told by me to you, is due to your affection, O Dhananjaya."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 45. Vasudeva said: "O mighty-armed one, know that I am the preceptor (guru) and the mind is my disciple. This secret, told by me to you, is due to your affection, O Dhananjaya."

sanskrit: मयि चेदस्ति ते प्रीतिर्नित्यं कुरुकुलोद्वह ।अध्यात्ममेतच्छ्रुत्वा त्वं सम्यगाचर सुव्रत ॥४६॥
transliteration: mayi cedasti te prītirnityaṁ kurukulodvaha ,adhyātmametacchrutvā tvaṁ samyagācara suvrata.
english: If you have constant affection for me, O upholder of the Kuru lineage, then having heard this concerning the supreme self (adhyātma), practice it properly, O one of good vows.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 46. If you have constant affection for me, O upholder of the Kuru lineage, then having heard this concerning the supreme self (adhyātma), practice it properly, O one of good vows.

sanskrit: ततस्त्वं सम्यगाचीर्णे धर्मेऽस्मिन्कुरुनन्दन ।सर्वपापविशुद्धात्मा मोक्षं प्राप्स्यसि केवलम् ॥४७॥
transliteration: tatastvaṁ samyagācīrṇe dharme'sminkurunandana ,sarvapāpaviśuddhātmā mokṣaṁ prāpsyasi kevalam.
english: Then, O delight of the Kurus, when this natural law (dharma) has been properly practiced by you, with your self (ātman) purified of all sins, you will attain absolute liberation (mokṣa).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 47. Then, O delight of the Kurus, when this natural law (dharma) has been properly practiced by you, with your self (ātman) purified of all sins, you will attain absolute liberation (mokṣa).

sanskrit: पूर्वमप्येतदेवोक्तं युद्धकाल उपस्थिते ।मया तव महाबाहो तस्मादत्र मनः कुरु ॥४८॥
transliteration: pūrvamapyetadevoktaṁ yuddhakāla upasthite ,mayā tava mahābāho tasmādatra manaḥ kuru.
english: O mighty-armed one, this very thing was previously told to you by me when the time for battle was imminent. Therefore, focus your mind on this now.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 48. O mighty-armed one, this very thing was previously told to you by me when the time for battle was imminent. Therefore, focus your mind on this now.

sanskrit: मया तु भरतश्रेष्ठ चिरदृष्टः पिता विभो ।तमहं द्रष्टुमिच्छामि संमते तव फल्गुन ॥४९॥
transliteration: mayā tu bharataśreṣṭha ciradṛṣṭaḥ pitā vibho ,tamahaṁ draṣṭumicchāmi saṁmate tava phalguna.
english: But, O best of the Bharatas, O lord, my father has been seen by me for a long time. O Phalguna, I wish to see him if you agree.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 49. But, O best of the Bharatas, O lord, my father has been seen by me for a long time. O Phalguna, I wish to see him if you agree.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।इत्युक्तवचनं कृष्णं प्रत्युवाच धनंजयः ।गच्छावो नगरं कृष्ण गजसाह्वयमद्य वै ॥५०॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,ityuktavacanaṁ kṛṣṇaṁ pratyuvāca dhanaṁjayaḥ ,gacchāvo nagaraṁ kṛṣṇa gajasāhvayamadya vai.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Dhananjaya replied to Krishna, who had spoken these words: 'O Krishna, let us both go to the city named Gajasa today itself.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 50. Vaiśampāyana said: Dhananjaya replied to Krishna, who had spoken these words: 'O Krishna, let us both go to the city named Gajasa today itself.'

sanskrit: समेत्य तत्र राजानं धर्मात्मानं युधिष्ठिरम् ।समनुज्ञाप्य दुर्धर्षं स्वां पुरीं यातुमर्हसि ॥५१॥
transliteration: sametya tatra rājānaṁ dharmātmānaṁ yudhiṣṭhiram ,samanujñāpya durdharṣaṁ svāṁ purīṁ yātumarhasi.
english: Having met there King Yudhiṣṭhira, the virtuous-souled (dharma) and invincible one, you should seek his permission and then go to your own city.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 50 Verse 51. Having met there King Yudhiṣṭhira, the virtuous-souled (dharma) and invincible one, you should seek his permission and then go to your own city.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततोऽभ्यचोदयत्कृष्णो युज्यतामिति दारुकम् ।मुहूर्तादिव चाचष्ट युक्तमित्येव दारुकः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tato'bhyacodayatkṛṣṇo yujyatāmiti dārukam ,muhūrtādiva cācaṣṭa yuktamityeva dārukaḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then Kṛṣṇa urged Dāruka, 'Let it be yoked!' Within a moment, Dāruka replied, 'It is indeed yoked.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Then Kṛṣṇa urged Dāruka, 'Let it be yoked!' Within a moment, Dāruka replied, 'It is indeed yoked.'

sanskrit: तथैव चानुयात्राणि चोदयामास पाण्डवः ।सज्जयध्वं प्रयास्यामो नगरं गजसाह्वयम् ॥२॥
transliteration: tathaiva cānuyātrāṇi codayāmāsa pāṇḍavaḥ ,sajjayadhvaṁ prayāsyāmo nagaraṁ gajasāhvayam.
english: And in the same manner, the Pāṇḍava (pāṇḍava) urged his retinue: 'Prepare yourselves! We shall proceed to the city of Hastināpura.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 2. And in the same manner, the Pāṇḍava (pāṇḍava) urged his retinue: 'Prepare yourselves! We shall proceed to the city of Hastināpura.'

sanskrit: इत्युक्ताः सैनिकास्ते तु सज्जीभूता विशां पते ।आचख्युः सज्जमित्येव पार्थायामिततेजसे ॥३॥
transliteration: ityuktāḥ sainikāste tu sajjībhūtā viśāṁ pate ,ācakhyuḥ sajjamityeva pārthāyāmitatejase.
english: O lord of men (viśām pate), those soldiers, thus addressed, became prepared. They then informed Pārtha, who possessed immeasurable splendor, 'It is ready.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 3. O lord of men (viśām pate), those soldiers, thus addressed, became prepared. They then informed Pārtha, who possessed immeasurable splendor, 'It is ready.'

sanskrit: ततस्तौ रथमास्थाय प्रयातौ कृष्णपाण्डवौ ।विकुर्वाणौ कथाश्चित्राः प्रीयमाणौ विशां पते ॥४॥
transliteration: tatastau rathamāsthāya prayātau kṛṣṇapāṇḍavau ,vikurvāṇau kathāścitrāḥ prīyamāṇau viśāṁ pate.
english: O lord of men (viśām pate), then those two, Kṛṣṇa and the Pāṇḍava (pāṇḍava), mounting the chariot, set forth. They exchanged varied and delightful stories, finding joy in their journey.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 4. O lord of men (viśām pate), then those two, Kṛṣṇa and the Pāṇḍava (pāṇḍava), mounting the chariot, set forth. They exchanged varied and delightful stories, finding joy in their journey.

sanskrit: रथस्थं तु महातेजा वासुदेवं धनंजयः ।पुनरेवाब्रवीद्वाक्यमिदं भरतसत्तम ॥५॥
transliteration: rathasthaṁ tu mahātejā vāsudevaṁ dhanaṁjayaḥ ,punarevābravīdvākyamidaṁ bharatasattama.
english: Then, O best of Bharatas, the greatly effulgent Dhanañjaya, seated on the chariot, spoke these words again to Vāsudeva.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 5. Then, O best of Bharatas, the greatly effulgent Dhanañjaya, seated on the chariot, spoke these words again to Vāsudeva.

sanskrit: त्वत्प्रसादाज्जयः प्राप्तो राज्ञा वृष्णिकुलोद्वह ।निहताः शत्रवश्चापि प्राप्तं राज्यमकण्टकम् ॥६॥
transliteration: tvatprasādājjayaḥ prāpto rājñā vṛṣṇikulodvaha ,nihatāḥ śatravaścāpi prāptaṁ rājyamakaṇṭakam.
english: By your grace, O upholder of the Vṛṣṇi lineage, victory has been achieved by the king; the enemies have been slain, and an undisputed kingdom has been secured.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 6. By your grace, O upholder of the Vṛṣṇi lineage, victory has been achieved by the king; the enemies have been slain, and an undisputed kingdom has been secured.

sanskrit: नाथवन्तश्च भवता पाण्डवा मधुसूदन ।भवन्तं प्लवमासाद्य तीर्णाः स्म कुरुसागरम् ॥७॥
transliteration: nāthavantaśca bhavatā pāṇḍavā madhusūdana ,bhavantaṁ plavamāsādya tīrṇāḥ sma kurusāgaram.
english: And, O slayer of Madhu (Madhusūdana), the Pāṇḍavas, having you as their protector, and having found you as a boat, we have crossed the ocean of Kurus.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 7. And, O slayer of Madhu (Madhusūdana), the Pāṇḍavas, having you as their protector, and having found you as a boat, we have crossed the ocean of Kurus.

sanskrit: विश्वकर्मन्नमस्तेऽस्तु विश्वात्मन्विश्वसंभव ।यथाहं त्वा विजानामि यथा चाहं भवन्मनाः ॥८॥
transliteration: viśvakarmannamaste'stu viśvātmanviśvasaṁbhava ,yathāhaṁ tvā vijānāmi yathā cāhaṁ bhavanmanāḥ.
english: O architect of the universe (viśvakarman), salutations to you! O universal self (viśvātman), O origin of the universe (viśvasambhava)! Just as I know you, so too am I completely devoted to you.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 8. O architect of the universe (viśvakarman), salutations to you! O universal self (viśvātman), O origin of the universe (viśvasambhava)! Just as I know you, so too am I completely devoted to you.

sanskrit: त्वत्तेजःसंभवो नित्यं हुताशो मधुसूदन ।रतिः क्रीडामयी तुभ्यं माया ते रोदसी विभो ॥९॥
transliteration: tvattejaḥsaṁbhavo nityaṁ hutāśo madhusūdana ,ratiḥ krīḍāmayī tubhyaṁ māyā te rodasī vibho.
english: O Madhusūdana, fire is eternally born from your splendor. O mighty Lord, playful delight is for you, and heaven and earth constitute your divine creative power (māyā).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 9. O Madhusūdana, fire is eternally born from your splendor. O mighty Lord, playful delight is for you, and heaven and earth constitute your divine creative power (māyā).

sanskrit: त्वयि सर्वमिदं विश्वं यदिदं स्थाणुजङ्गमम् ।त्वं हि सर्वं विकुरुषे भूतग्रामं सनातनम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: tvayi sarvamidaṁ viśvaṁ yadidaṁ sthāṇujaṅgamam ,tvaṁ hi sarvaṁ vikuruṣe bhūtagrāmaṁ sanātanam.
english: This entire universe, comprising all that is unmoving and moving, resides within you. Indeed, you eternally manifest all aggregates of beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 10. This entire universe, comprising all that is unmoving and moving, resides within you. Indeed, you eternally manifest all aggregates of beings.

sanskrit: पृथिवीं चान्तरिक्षं च तथा स्थावरजङ्गमम् ।हसितं तेऽमला ज्योत्स्ना ऋतवश्चेन्द्रियान्वयाः ॥११॥
transliteration: pṛthivīṁ cāntarikṣaṁ ca tathā sthāvarajaṅgamam ,hasitaṁ te'malā jyotsnā ṛtavaścendriyānvayāḥ.
english: Earth and the atmosphere, as well as all that is stationary and mobile, are your manifestations. Your laughter is pure moonlight, and the seasons are the connections of the senses.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 11. Earth and the atmosphere, as well as all that is stationary and mobile, are your manifestations. Your laughter is pure moonlight, and the seasons are the connections of the senses.

sanskrit: प्राणो वायुः सततगः क्रोधो मृत्युः सनातनः ।प्रसादे चापि पद्मा श्रीर्नित्यं त्वयि महामते ॥१२॥
transliteration: prāṇo vāyuḥ satatagaḥ krodho mṛtyuḥ sanātanaḥ ,prasāde cāpi padmā śrīrnityaṁ tvayi mahāmate.
english: The vital breath (prāṇa) is the ever-moving air. Anger is eternal death. And indeed, O great-minded one, the lotus-born goddess Śrī eternally dwells within your grace (prasāda).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 12. The vital breath (prāṇa) is the ever-moving air. Anger is eternal death. And indeed, O great-minded one, the lotus-born goddess Śrī eternally dwells within your grace (prasāda).

sanskrit: रतिस्तुष्टिर्धृतिः क्षान्तिस्त्वयि चेदं चराचरम् ।त्वमेवेह युगान्तेषु निधनं प्रोच्यसेऽनघ ॥१३॥
transliteration: ratistuṣṭirdhṛtiḥ kṣāntistvayi cedaṁ carācaram ,tvameveha yugānteṣu nidhanaṁ procyase'nagha.
english: Delight, satisfaction, steadfastness, and forbearance are all found in you, and indeed this entire animate and inanimate world. You alone, O faultless one, are declared to be the dissolution at the end of all cosmic ages (yugas).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 13. Delight, satisfaction, steadfastness, and forbearance are all found in you, and indeed this entire animate and inanimate world. You alone, O faultless one, are declared to be the dissolution at the end of all cosmic ages (yugas).

sanskrit: सुदीर्घेणापि कालेन न ते शक्या गुणा मया ।आत्मा च परमो वक्तुं नमस्ते नलिनेक्षण ॥१४॥
transliteration: sudīrgheṇāpi kālena na te śakyā guṇā mayā ,ātmā ca paramo vaktuṁ namaste nalinekṣaṇa.
english: Even with a very long time, your qualities cannot be described by me, nor can your supreme self (ātman). Salutations to you, O lotus-eyed one!
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 14. Even with a very long time, your qualities cannot be described by me, nor can your supreme self (ātman). Salutations to you, O lotus-eyed one!

sanskrit: विदितो मेऽसि दुर्धर्ष नारदाद्देवलात्तथा ।कृष्णद्वैपायनाच्चैव तथा कुरुपितामहात् ॥१५॥
transliteration: vidito me'si durdharṣa nāradāddevalāttathā ,kṛṣṇadvaipāyanāccaiva tathā kurupitāmahāt.
english: O unassailable one, you are known to me from Nārada, from Devala, and also from Kṛṣṇa Dvaipāyana (Vyāsa), and similarly from the grandfather of the Kurus (Bhīṣma).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 15. O unassailable one, you are known to me from Nārada, from Devala, and also from Kṛṣṇa Dvaipāyana (Vyāsa), and similarly from the grandfather of the Kurus (Bhīṣma).

sanskrit: त्वयि सर्वं समासक्तं त्वमेवैको जनेश्वरः ।यच्चानुग्रहसंयुक्तमेतदुक्तं त्वयानघ ॥१६॥
transliteration: tvayi sarvaṁ samāsaktaṁ tvamevaiko janeśvaraḥ ,yaccānugrahasaṁyuktametaduktaṁ tvayānagha.
english: All things are dependent upon you; you alone are the ruler of all beings. And this (teaching) full of grace has been spoken by you, O faultless one.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 16. All things are dependent upon you; you alone are the ruler of all beings. And this (teaching) full of grace has been spoken by you, O faultless one.

sanskrit: एतत्सर्वमहं सम्यगाचरिष्ये जनार्दन ।इदं चाद्भुतमत्यर्थं कृतमस्मत्प्रियेप्सया ॥१७॥
transliteration: etatsarvamahaṁ samyagācariṣye janārdana ,idaṁ cādbhutamatyarthaṁ kṛtamasmatpriyepsayā.
english: O Janardana, I shall duly accomplish all this. And this exceedingly wonderful feat was performed out of a desire for what is dear to us.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 17. O Janardana, I shall duly accomplish all this. And this exceedingly wonderful feat was performed out of a desire for what is dear to us.

sanskrit: यत्पापो निहतः संख्ये कौरव्यो धृतराष्ट्रजः ।त्वया दग्धं हि तत्सैन्यं मया विजितमाहवे ॥१८॥
transliteration: yatpāpo nihataḥ saṁkhye kauravyo dhṛtarāṣṭrajaḥ ,tvayā dagdhaṁ hi tatsainyaṁ mayā vijitamāhave.
english: That sinful Kaurava, son of Dhritarashtra, was struck down in battle. Indeed, that army was burnt by you, and it was conquered by me in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 18. That sinful Kaurava, son of Dhritarashtra, was struck down in battle. Indeed, that army was burnt by you, and it was conquered by me in battle.

sanskrit: भवता तत्कृतं कर्म येनावाप्तो जयो मया ।दुर्योधनस्य संग्रामे तव बुद्धिपराक्रमैः ॥१९॥
transliteration: bhavatā tatkṛtaṁ karma yenāvāpto jayo mayā ,duryodhanasya saṁgrāme tava buddhiparākramaiḥ.
english: That deed (karma) was performed by you, by means of which I achieved victory in the battle against Duryodhana, through your intelligence (buddhi) and valor.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 19. That deed (karma) was performed by you, by means of which I achieved victory in the battle against Duryodhana, through your intelligence (buddhi) and valor.

sanskrit: कर्णस्य च वधोपायो यथावत्संप्रदर्शितः ।सैन्धवस्य च पापस्य भूरिश्रवस एव च ॥२०॥
transliteration: karṇasya ca vadhopāyo yathāvatsaṁpradarśitaḥ ,saindhavasya ca pāpasya bhūriśravasa eva ca.
english: And the method for slaying Karṇa was precisely demonstrated. Also, regarding the wicked Saindhava and indeed Bhūrīśravas (the method of their slaying).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 20. And the method for slaying Karṇa was precisely demonstrated. Also, regarding the wicked Saindhava and indeed Bhūrīśravas (the method of their slaying).

sanskrit: अहं च प्रीयमाणेन त्वया देवकिनन्दन ।यदुक्तस्तत्करिष्यामि न हि मेऽत्र विचारणा ॥२१॥
transliteration: ahaṁ ca prīyamāṇena tvayā devakinandana ,yaduktastatkariṣyāmi na hi me'tra vicāraṇā.
english: And I, O son of Devaki, will do what was said by you, who are pleased. Indeed, there is no hesitation in me regarding this.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 21. And I, O son of Devaki, will do what was said by you, who are pleased. Indeed, there is no hesitation in me regarding this.

sanskrit: राजानं च समासाद्य धर्मात्मानं युधिष्ठिरम् ।चोदयिष्यामि धर्मज्ञ गमनार्थं तवानघ ॥२२॥
transliteration: rājānaṁ ca samāsādya dharmātmānaṁ yudhiṣṭhiram ,codayiṣyāmi dharmajña gamanārthaṁ tavānagha.
english: And having approached King Yudhiṣṭhira, who is righteous (dharma) in spirit, O knower of righteousness (dharma), O sinless one, I will urge him concerning your departure.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 22. And having approached King Yudhiṣṭhira, who is righteous (dharma) in spirit, O knower of righteousness (dharma), O sinless one, I will urge him concerning your departure.

sanskrit: रुचितं हि ममैतत्ते द्वारकागमनं प्रभो ।अचिराच्चैव दृष्टा त्वं मातुलं मधुसूदन ।बलदेवं च दुर्धर्षं तथान्यान्वृष्णिपुंगवान् ॥२३॥
transliteration: rucitaṁ hi mamaitatte dvārakāgamanaṁ prabho ,acirāccaiva dṛṣṭā tvaṁ mātulaṁ madhusūdana ,baladevaṁ ca durdharṣaṁ tathānyānvṛṣṇipuṁgavān.
english: Indeed, this journey of yours to Dvārakā is pleasing to me, O Lord. And very soon, having seen your maternal uncle Madhusūdana, the unconquerable Baladeva, and likewise other eminent Vṛṣṇi heroes, you will be content.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 23. Indeed, this journey of yours to Dvārakā is pleasing to me, O Lord. And very soon, having seen your maternal uncle Madhusūdana, the unconquerable Baladeva, and likewise other eminent Vṛṣṇi heroes, you will be content.

sanskrit: एवं संभाषमाणौ तौ प्राप्तौ वारणसाह्वयम् ।तथा विविशतुश्चोभौ संप्रहृष्टनराकुलम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: evaṁ saṁbhāṣamāṇau tau prāptau vāraṇasāhvayam ,tathā viviśatuścobhau saṁprahṛṣṭanarākulam.
english: Thus, while conversing, both of them reached the city named Vāraṇā. And both of them entered that place, which was crowded with extremely joyful people.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 24. Thus, while conversing, both of them reached the city named Vāraṇā. And both of them entered that place, which was crowded with extremely joyful people.

sanskrit: तौ गत्वा धृतराष्ट्रस्य गृहं शक्रगृहोपमम् ।ददृशाते महाराज धृतराष्ट्रं जनेश्वरम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: tau gatvā dhṛtarāṣṭrasya gṛhaṁ śakragṛhopamam ,dadṛśāte mahārāja dhṛtarāṣṭraṁ janeśvaram.
english: Having gone to Dhṛtarāṣṭra's house, which was comparable to Indra's abode, they saw the great king Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the lord of men.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 25. Having gone to Dhṛtarāṣṭra's house, which was comparable to Indra's abode, they saw the great king Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the lord of men.

sanskrit: विदुरं च महाबुद्धिं राजानं च युधिष्ठिरम् ।भीमसेनं च दुर्धर्षं माद्रीपुत्रौ च पाण्डवौ ।धृतराष्ट्रमुपासीनं युयुत्सुं चापराजितम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: viduraṁ ca mahābuddhiṁ rājānaṁ ca yudhiṣṭhiram ,bhīmasenaṁ ca durdharṣaṁ mādrīputrau ca pāṇḍavau ,dhṛtarāṣṭramupāsīnaṁ yuyutsuṁ cāparājitam.
english: And they saw Vidura, the greatly intelligent, King Yudhiṣṭhira, Bhīmasena the formidable, the two Pāṇḍava sons of Mādrī, and Yuyutsu, the unconquered, all seated near Dhṛtarāṣṭra.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 26. And they saw Vidura, the greatly intelligent, King Yudhiṣṭhira, Bhīmasena the formidable, the two Pāṇḍava sons of Mādrī, and Yuyutsu, the unconquered, all seated near Dhṛtarāṣṭra.

sanskrit: गान्धारीं च महाप्राज्ञां पृथां कृष्णां च भामिनीम् ।सुभद्राद्याश्च ताः सर्वा भरतानां स्त्रियस्तथा ।ददृशाते स्थिताः सर्वा गान्धारीं परिवार्य वै ॥२७॥
transliteration: gāndhārīṁ ca mahāprājñāṁ pṛthāṁ kṛṣṇāṁ ca bhāminīm ,subhadrādyāśca tāḥ sarvā bharatānāṁ striyastathā ,dadṛśāte sthitāḥ sarvā gāndhārīṁ parivārya vai.
english: And they saw Gāndhārī, the greatly wise, Pṛthā (Kuntī), and Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī) the spirited one, and all those women including Subhadrā, and also the wives of the Bhāratas, all standing, surrounding Gāndhārī.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 27. And they saw Gāndhārī, the greatly wise, Pṛthā (Kuntī), and Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī) the spirited one, and all those women including Subhadrā, and also the wives of the Bhāratas, all standing, surrounding Gāndhārī.

sanskrit: ततः समेत्य राजानं धृतराष्ट्रमरिंदमौ ।निवेद्य नामधेये स्वे तस्य पादावगृह्णताम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: tataḥ sametya rājānaṁ dhṛtarāṣṭramariṁdamau ,nivedya nāmadheye sve tasya pādāvagṛhṇatām.
english: Then, having approached King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, those two destroyers of foes, after announcing their own names, touched his feet.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 28. Then, having approached King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, those two destroyers of foes, after announcing their own names, touched his feet.

sanskrit: गान्धार्याश्च पृथायाश्च धर्मराज्ञस्तथैव च ।भीमस्य च महात्मानौ तथा पादावगृह्णताम् ॥२९॥
transliteration: gāndhāryāśca pṛthāyāśca dharmarājñastathaiva ca ,bhīmasya ca mahātmānau tathā pādāvagṛhṇatām.
english: And the two great souls (mahātmanau) likewise clasped the feet of Gandhari, Pritha, the king of righteousness (dharma), and Bhima.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 29. And the two great souls (mahātmanau) likewise clasped the feet of Gandhari, Pritha, the king of righteousness (dharma), and Bhima.

sanskrit: क्षत्तारं चापि संपूज्य पृष्ट्वा कुशलमव्ययम् ।तैः सार्धं नृपतिं वृद्धं ततस्तं पर्युपासताम् ॥३०॥
transliteration: kṣattāraṁ cāpi saṁpūjya pṛṣṭvā kuśalamavyayam ,taiḥ sārdhaṁ nṛpatiṁ vṛddhaṁ tatastaṁ paryupāsatām.
english: Having also honored the charioteer (Kṣattā) and inquired about his unfailing well-being, they then, along with those others, attended upon that aged king.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 30. Having also honored the charioteer (Kṣattā) and inquired about his unfailing well-being, they then, along with those others, attended upon that aged king.

sanskrit: ततो निशि महाराज धृतराष्ट्रः कुरूद्वहान् ।जनार्दनं च मेधावी व्यसर्जयत वै गृहान् ॥३१॥
transliteration: tato niśi mahārāja dhṛtarāṣṭraḥ kurūdvahān ,janārdanaṁ ca medhāvī vyasarjayata vai gṛhān.
english: Thereafter, O great king, the intelligent Dhritarashtra, during the night, sent away the leaders of the Kurus and Janardana (Kṛṣṇa) to their respective homes.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 31. Thereafter, O great king, the intelligent Dhritarashtra, during the night, sent away the leaders of the Kurus and Janardana (Kṛṣṇa) to their respective homes.

sanskrit: तेऽनुज्ञाता नृपतिना ययुः स्वं स्वं निवेशनम् ।धनंजयगृहानेव ययौ कृष्णस्तु वीर्यवान् ॥३२॥
transliteration: te'nujñātā nṛpatinā yayuḥ svaṁ svaṁ niveśanam ,dhanaṁjayagṛhāneva yayau kṛṣṇastu vīryavān.
english: Having been permitted by the king, they went to their respective dwellings. But the valiant Kṛṣṇa went directly to Dhananjaya's (Arjuna's) house.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 32. Having been permitted by the king, they went to their respective dwellings. But the valiant Kṛṣṇa went directly to Dhananjaya's (Arjuna's) house.

sanskrit: तत्रार्चितो यथान्यायं सर्वकामैरुपस्थितः ।कृष्णः सुष्वाप मेधावी धनंजयसहायवान् ॥३३॥
transliteration: tatrārcito yathānyāyaṁ sarvakāmairupasthitaḥ ,kṛṣṇaḥ suṣvāpa medhāvī dhanaṁjayasahāyavān.
english: Having been duly honored and provided with all necessities there, the intelligent Krishna, accompanied by Dhananjaya (Arjuna), slept.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 33. Having been duly honored and provided with all necessities there, the intelligent Krishna, accompanied by Dhananjaya (Arjuna), slept.

sanskrit: प्रभातायां तु शर्वर्यां कृतपूर्वाह्णिकक्रियौ ।धर्मराजस्य भवनं जग्मतुः परमार्चितौ ।यत्रास्ते स सहामात्यो धर्मराजो महामनाः ॥३४॥
transliteration: prabhātāyāṁ tu śarvaryāṁ kṛtapūrvāhṇikakriyau ,dharmarājasya bhavanaṁ jagmatuḥ paramārcitau ,yatrāste sa sahāmātyo dharmarājo mahāmanāḥ.
english: When the night had dawned, and having performed their morning rituals, the two (Krishna and Arjuna), highly honored, went to the palace of Dharmaraja Yudhishthira, where that high-minded king, accompanied by his ministers, resides.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 34. When the night had dawned, and having performed their morning rituals, the two (Krishna and Arjuna), highly honored, went to the palace of Dharmaraja Yudhishthira, where that high-minded king, accompanied by his ministers, resides.

sanskrit: ततस्तौ तत्प्रविश्याथ ददृशाते महाबलौ ।धर्मराजानमासीनं देवराजमिवाश्विनौ ॥३५॥
transliteration: tatastau tatpraviśyātha dadṛśāte mahābalau ,dharmarājānamāsīnaṁ devarājamivāśvinau.
english: Then, having entered that (palace), those two mighty ones saw Dharmaraja seated, just as the two Ashvins (divine twins) would see the king of gods (Indra).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 35. Then, having entered that (palace), those two mighty ones saw Dharmaraja seated, just as the two Ashvins (divine twins) would see the king of gods (Indra).

sanskrit: तौ समासाद्य राजानं वार्ष्णेयकुरुपुंगवौ ।निषीदतुरनुज्ञातौ प्रीयमाणेन तेन वै ॥३६॥
transliteration: tau samāsādya rājānaṁ vārṣṇeyakurupuṁgavau ,niṣīdaturanujñātau prīyamāṇena tena vai.
english: Those two, the best among the Vrishnis and Kurus, having approached the king, sat down, indeed permitted by him who was pleased.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 36. Those two, the best among the Vrishnis and Kurus, having approached the king, sat down, indeed permitted by him who was pleased.

sanskrit: ततः स राजा मेधावी विवक्षू प्रेक्ष्य तावुभौ ।प्रोवाच वदतां श्रेष्ठो वचनं राजसत्तमः ॥३७॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa rājā medhāvī vivakṣū prekṣya tāvubhau ,provāca vadatāṁ śreṣṭho vacanaṁ rājasattamaḥ.
english: Then, that wise king, the best among speakers and the best of rulers, seeing those two desirous of speaking, uttered these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 37. Then, that wise king, the best among speakers and the best of rulers, seeing those two desirous of speaking, uttered these words.

sanskrit: विवक्षू हि युवां मन्ये वीरौ यदुकुरूद्वहौ ।ब्रूत कर्तास्मि सर्वं वां न चिरान्मा विचार्यताम् ॥३८॥
transliteration: vivakṣū hi yuvāṁ manye vīrau yadukurūdvahau ,brūta kartāsmi sarvaṁ vāṁ na cirānmā vicāryatām.
english: Indeed, I perceive that you two, valiant heroes who uphold both the Yadus and the Kurus, wish to speak. Speak! I will accomplish everything for you without delay; do not hesitate or deliberate.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 38. Indeed, I perceive that you two, valiant heroes who uphold both the Yadus and the Kurus, wish to speak. Speak! I will accomplish everything for you without delay; do not hesitate or deliberate.

sanskrit: इत्युक्ते फल्गुनस्तत्र धर्मराजानमब्रवीत् ।विनीतवदुपागम्य वाक्यं वाक्यविशारदः ॥३९॥
transliteration: ityukte phalgunastatra dharmarājānamabravīt ,vinītavadupāgamya vākyaṁ vākyaviśāradaḥ.
english: When this was said, Arjuna (Phalguna), an expert in speech, then respectfully approached the king (dharma rāja Yudhiṣṭhira) and spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 39. When this was said, Arjuna (Phalguna), an expert in speech, then respectfully approached the king (dharma rāja Yudhiṣṭhira) and spoke these words.

sanskrit: अयं चिरोषितो राजन्वासुदेवः प्रतापवान् ।भवन्तं समनुज्ञाप्य पितरं द्रष्टुमिच्छति ॥४०॥
transliteration: ayaṁ ciroṣito rājanvāsudevaḥ pratāpavān ,bhavantaṁ samanujñāpya pitaraṁ draṣṭumicchati.
english: O King, this glorious (pratāpavān) Vāsudeva, who has resided here for a long time, desires to see his father after obtaining your permission.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 40. O King, this glorious (pratāpavān) Vāsudeva, who has resided here for a long time, desires to see his father after obtaining your permission.

sanskrit: स गच्छेदभ्यनुज्ञातो भवता यदि मन्यसे ।आनर्तनगरीं वीरस्तदनुज्ञातुमर्हसि ॥४१॥
transliteration: sa gacchedabhyanujñāto bhavatā yadi manyase ,ānartanagarīṁ vīrastadanujñātumarhasi.
english: If you (Kṛṣṇa) deem it appropriate and consider yourself permitted, then you may depart. O hero, you should indeed allow yourself to go to the city of Ānarta (Dvārakā).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 41. If you (Kṛṣṇa) deem it appropriate and consider yourself permitted, then you may depart. O hero, you should indeed allow yourself to go to the city of Ānarta (Dvārakā).

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।पुण्डरीकाक्ष भद्रं ते गच्छ त्वं मधुसूदन ।पुरीं द्वारवतीमद्य द्रष्टुं शूरसुतं प्रभुम् ॥४२॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,puṇḍarīkākṣa bhadraṁ te gaccha tvaṁ madhusūdana ,purīṁ dvāravatīmadya draṣṭuṁ śūrasutaṁ prabhum.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: O lotus-eyed (Kṛṣṇa), O Madhusūdana (Kṛṣṇa), may well-being be with you! You should go today to the city of Dvāravatī, to see the lord, the son of Śūra (Vasudeva).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 42. Yudhiṣṭhira said: O lotus-eyed (Kṛṣṇa), O Madhusūdana (Kṛṣṇa), may well-being be with you! You should go today to the city of Dvāravatī, to see the lord, the son of Śūra (Vasudeva).

sanskrit: रोचते मे महाबाहो गमनं तव केशव ।मातुलश्चिरदृष्टो मे त्वया देवी च देवकी ॥४३॥
transliteration: rocate me mahābāho gamanaṁ tava keśava ,mātulaściradṛṣṭo me tvayā devī ca devakī.
english: O mighty-armed (Kṛṣṇa), O Keśava, your departure is agreeable to me. My uncle (Vasudeva) has not been seen by me for a long time, and neither has the divine Devakī (owing to your long stay here with us).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 43. O mighty-armed (Kṛṣṇa), O Keśava, your departure is agreeable to me. My uncle (Vasudeva) has not been seen by me for a long time, and neither has the divine Devakī (owing to your long stay here with us).

sanskrit: मातुलं वसुदेवं त्वं बलदेवं च माधव ।पूजयेथा महाप्राज्ञ मद्वाक्येन यथार्हतः ॥४४॥
transliteration: mātulaṁ vasudevaṁ tvaṁ baladevaṁ ca mādhava ,pūjayethā mahāprājña madvākyena yathārhataḥ.
english: O Mādhava (Kṛṣṇa), O greatly wise one, you should honor uncle Vasudeva and Baladeva on my behalf, as is appropriate.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 44. O Mādhava (Kṛṣṇa), O greatly wise one, you should honor uncle Vasudeva and Baladeva on my behalf, as is appropriate.

sanskrit: स्मरेथाश्चापि मां नित्यं भीमं च बलिनां वरम् ।फल्गुनं नकुलं चैव सहदेवं च माधव ॥४५॥
transliteration: smarethāścāpi māṁ nityaṁ bhīmaṁ ca balināṁ varam ,phalgunaṁ nakulaṁ caiva sahadevaṁ ca mādhava.
english: O Madhava, you should always remember me, and Bhima, the best among the strong, as well as Arjuna, Nakula, and Sahadeva.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 45. O Madhava, you should always remember me, and Bhima, the best among the strong, as well as Arjuna, Nakula, and Sahadeva.

sanskrit: आनर्तानवलोक्य त्वं पितरं च महाभुज ।वृष्णींश्च पुनरागच्छेर्हयमेधे ममानघ ॥४६॥
transliteration: ānartānavalokya tvaṁ pitaraṁ ca mahābhuja ,vṛṣṇīṁśca punarāgaccherhayamedhe mamānagha.
english: O great-armed one, O sinless one, after seeing the Anartas, your father, and the Vrishnis, you should return again for my horse ritual (hayamedha).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 46. O great-armed one, O sinless one, after seeing the Anartas, your father, and the Vrishnis, you should return again for my horse ritual (hayamedha).

sanskrit: स गच्छ रत्नान्यादाय विविधानि वसूनि च ।यच्चाप्यन्यन्मनोज्ञं ते तदप्यादत्स्व सात्वत ॥४७॥
transliteration: sa gaccha ratnānyādāya vividhāni vasūni ca ,yaccāpyanyanmanojñaṁ te tadapyādatsva sātvata.
english: O Sātvata, go and take various jewels and riches. And whatever else is agreeable to you, that also take for yourself.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 47. O Sātvata, go and take various jewels and riches. And whatever else is agreeable to you, that also take for yourself.

sanskrit: इयं हि वसुधा सर्वा प्रसादात्तव माधव ।अस्मानुपगता वीर निहताश्चापि शत्रवः ॥४८॥
transliteration: iyaṁ hi vasudhā sarvā prasādāttava mādhava ,asmānupagatā vīra nihatāścāpi śatravaḥ.
english: O Madhava, O hero, indeed this entire earth has come to us by your grace, and our enemies have also been slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 48. O Madhava, O hero, indeed this entire earth has come to us by your grace, and our enemies have also been slain.

sanskrit: एवं ब्रुवति कौरव्ये धर्मराजे युधिष्ठिरे ।वासुदेवो वरः पुंसामिदं वचनमब्रवीत् ॥४९॥
transliteration: evaṁ bruvati kauravye dharmarāje yudhiṣṭhire ,vāsudevo varaḥ puṁsāmidaṁ vacanamabravīt.
english: While the Kuru prince, Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of natural law (dharma), was speaking thus, Vāsudeva, the best among men, uttered these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 49. While the Kuru prince, Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of natural law (dharma), was speaking thus, Vāsudeva, the best among men, uttered these words.

sanskrit: तवैव रत्नानि धनं च केवलम्धरा च कृत्स्ना तु महाभुजाद्य वै ।यदस्ति चान्यद्द्रविणं गृहेषु मे त्वमेव तस्येश्वर नित्यमीश्वरः ॥५०॥
transliteration: tavaiva ratnāni dhanaṁ ca kevala;mdharā ca kṛtsnā tu mahābhujādya vai ,yadasti cānyaddraviṇaṁ gṛheṣu me; tvameva tasyeśvara nityamīśvaraḥ.
english: O mighty-armed one, your own jewels, wealth, and indeed the entire earth are exclusively yours today. Furthermore, whatever other property is in my homes, you alone are eternally its master.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 50. O mighty-armed one, your own jewels, wealth, and indeed the entire earth are exclusively yours today. Furthermore, whatever other property is in my homes, you alone are eternally its master.

sanskrit: तथेत्यथोक्तः प्रतिपूजितस्तदा गदाग्रजो धर्मसुतेन वीर्यवान् ।पितृष्वसामभ्यवदद्यथाविधि संपूजितश्चाप्यगमत्प्रदक्षिणम् ॥५१॥
transliteration: tathetyathoktaḥ pratipūjitastadā; gadāgrajo dharmasutena vīryavān ,pitṛṣvasāmabhyavadadyathāvidhi; saṁpūjitaścāpyagamatpradakṣiṇam.
english: Then, having been told "So be it" and duly honored by Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of natural law (dharma), the powerful Kṛṣṇa (gadāgraja) addressed his paternal aunt appropriately. Well-honored, he then departed, circumambulating her.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 51. Then, having been told "So be it" and duly honored by Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of natural law (dharma), the powerful Kṛṣṇa (gadāgraja) addressed his paternal aunt appropriately. Well-honored, he then departed, circumambulating her.

sanskrit: तया स सम्यक्प्रतिनन्दितस्तदा तथैव सर्वैर्विदुरादिभिस्ततः ।विनिर्ययौ नागपुराद्गदाग्रजो रथेन दिव्येन चतुर्युजा हरिः ॥५२॥
transliteration: tayā sa samyakpratinanditastadā; tathaiva sarvairvidurādibhistataḥ ,viniryayau nāgapurādgadāgrajo; rathena divyena caturyujā hariḥ.
english: Then, having been properly welcomed by her (his aunt), and similarly by all others led by Vidura, he, Hari (Kṛṣṇa), the elder brother of Gadā, departed from Nāgapura in his divine chariot drawn by four steeds.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 52. Then, having been properly welcomed by her (his aunt), and similarly by all others led by Vidura, he, Hari (Kṛṣṇa), the elder brother of Gadā, departed from Nāgapura in his divine chariot drawn by four steeds.

sanskrit: रथं सुभद्रामधिरोप्य भामिनीं युधिष्ठिरस्यानुमते जनार्दनः ।पितृष्वसायाश्च तथा महाभुजो विनिर्ययौ पौरजनाभिसंवृतः ॥५३॥
transliteration: rathaṁ subhadrāmadhiropya bhāminīṁ; yudhiṣṭhirasyānumate janārdanaḥ ,pitṛṣvasāyāśca tathā mahābhujo; viniryayau paurajanābhisaṁvṛtaḥ.
english: The mighty-armed Janardana, having seated the beautiful Subhadra in the chariot, with the permission of Yudhishthira and also of his paternal aunt, set out surrounded by the citizens.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 53. The mighty-armed Janardana, having seated the beautiful Subhadra in the chariot, with the permission of Yudhishthira and also of his paternal aunt, set out surrounded by the citizens.

sanskrit: तमन्वगाद्वानरवर्यकेतनः ससात्यकिर्माद्रवतीसुतावपि ।अगाधबुद्धिर्विदुरश्च माधवं स्वयं च भीमो गजराजविक्रमः ॥५४॥
transliteration: tamanvagādvānaravaryaketanaḥ; sasātyakirmādravatīsutāvapi ,agādhabuddhirviduraśca mādhavaṁ; svayaṁ ca bhīmo gajarājavikramaḥ.
english: Arjuna, whose banner bore the best of monkeys, followed him (Madhava), as did Satyaki, the two sons of Madri (Nakula and Sahadeva), the deep-minded Vidura, and Bhima himself, valorous as a lordly elephant.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 54. Arjuna, whose banner bore the best of monkeys, followed him (Madhava), as did Satyaki, the two sons of Madri (Nakula and Sahadeva), the deep-minded Vidura, and Bhima himself, valorous as a lordly elephant.

sanskrit: निवर्तयित्वा कुरुराष्ट्रवर्धनांस्ततः स सर्वान्विदुरं च वीर्यवान् ।जनार्दनो दारुकमाह सत्वरः प्रचोदयाश्वानिति सात्यकिस्तदा ॥५५॥
transliteration: nivartayitvā kururāṣṭravardhanāṁ;stataḥ sa sarvānviduraṁ ca vīryavān ,janārdano dārukamāha satvaraḥ; pracodayāśvāniti sātyakistadā.
english: Having dismissed all the enhancers of the Kuru kingdom and also Vidura, the powerful Janardana then swiftly commanded Daruka, "Drive the horses!" Satyaki too was there at that time.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 55. Having dismissed all the enhancers of the Kuru kingdom and also Vidura, the powerful Janardana then swiftly commanded Daruka, "Drive the horses!" Satyaki too was there at that time.

sanskrit: ततो ययौ शत्रुगणप्रमर्दनः शिनिप्रवीरानुगतो जनार्दनः ।यथा निहत्यारिगणाञ्शतक्रतुर्दिवं तथानर्तपुरीं प्रतापवान् ॥५६॥
transliteration: tato yayau śatrugaṇapramardanaḥ; śinipravīrānugato janārdanaḥ ,yathā nihatyārigaṇāñśatakratu;rdivaṁ tathānartapurīṁ pratāpavān.
english: Then the glorious Janardana, the destroyer of hosts of enemies, followed by the hero of the Shinis (Satyaki), departed for the city of Anarta, just as Shatakratu (Indra), having slain his hosts of enemies, goes to heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 51 Verse 56. Then the glorious Janardana, the destroyer of hosts of enemies, followed by the hero of the Shinis (Satyaki), departed for the city of Anarta, just as Shatakratu (Indra), having slain his hosts of enemies, goes to heaven.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तथा प्रयान्तं वार्ष्णेयं द्वारकां भरतर्षभाः ।परिष्वज्य न्यवर्तन्त सानुयात्राः परंतपाः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tathā prayāntaṁ vārṣṇeyaṁ dvārakāṁ bharatarṣabhāḥ ,pariṣvajya nyavartanta sānuyātrāḥ paraṁtapāḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: As the Vārṣṇeya (Kṛṣṇa) was thus departing for Dvārakā, the foremost of the Bhāratas, those tormentors of foes, embraced him and then returned along with their entourage.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 52 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: As the Vārṣṇeya (Kṛṣṇa) was thus departing for Dvārakā, the foremost of the Bhāratas, those tormentors of foes, embraced him and then returned along with their entourage.

sanskrit: पुनः पुनश्च वार्ष्णेयं पर्यष्वजत फल्गुनः ।आ चक्षुर्विषयाच्चैनं ददर्श च पुनः पुनः ॥२॥
transliteration: punaḥ punaśca vārṣṇeyaṁ paryaṣvajata phalgunaḥ ,ā cakṣurviṣayāccainaṁ dadarśa ca punaḥ punaḥ.
english: Phalguna (Arjuna) embraced the Vārṣṇeya (Kṛṣṇa) again and again. And he looked at him repeatedly until Kṛṣṇa was beyond the range of sight.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 52 Verse 2. Phalguna (Arjuna) embraced the Vārṣṇeya (Kṛṣṇa) again and again. And he looked at him repeatedly until Kṛṣṇa was beyond the range of sight.

sanskrit: कृच्छ्रेणैव च तां पार्थो गोविन्दे विनिवेशिताम् ।संजहार तदा दृष्टिं कृष्णश्चाप्यपराजितः ॥३॥
transliteration: kṛcchreṇaiva ca tāṁ pārtho govinde viniveśitām ,saṁjahāra tadā dṛṣṭiṁ kṛṣṇaścāpyaparājitaḥ.
english: With great difficulty, Pārtha (Arjuna) then withdrew that gaze which was fixed upon Govinda (Kṛṣṇa). And Kṛṣṇa, the unconquered one, also (withdrew his gaze).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 52 Verse 3. With great difficulty, Pārtha (Arjuna) then withdrew that gaze which was fixed upon Govinda (Kṛṣṇa). And Kṛṣṇa, the unconquered one, also (withdrew his gaze).

sanskrit: तस्य प्रयाणे यान्यासन्निमित्तानि महात्मनः ।बहून्यद्भुतरूपाणि तानि मे गदतः शृणु ॥४॥
transliteration: tasya prayāṇe yānyāsannimittāni mahātmanaḥ ,bahūnyadbhutarūpāṇi tāni me gadataḥ śṛṇu.
english: Listen to me as I narrate the many wondrous omens that appeared at the departure of that great soul (Kṛṣṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 52 Verse 4. Listen to me as I narrate the many wondrous omens that appeared at the departure of that great soul (Kṛṣṇa).

sanskrit: वायुर्वेगेन महता रथस्य पुरतो ववौ ।कुर्वन्निःशर्करं मार्गं विरजस्कमकण्टकम् ॥५॥
transliteration: vāyurvegena mahatā rathasya purato vavau ,kurvanniḥśarkaraṁ mārgaṁ virajaskamakaṇṭakam.
english: The wind blew with great speed in front of the chariot, making the path free of gravel, dust, and thorns.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 52 Verse 5. The wind blew with great speed in front of the chariot, making the path free of gravel, dust, and thorns.

sanskrit: ववर्ष वासवश्चापि तोयं शुचि सुगन्धि च ।दिव्यानि चैव पुष्पाणि पुरतः शार्ङ्गधन्वनः ॥६॥
