transliteration: vavarṣa vāsavaścāpi toyaṁ śuci sugandhi ca ,divyāni caiva puṣpāṇi purataḥ śārṅgadhanvanaḥ.
english: And Indra also rained pure and fragrant water, as well as divine flowers, in front of the wielder of the Śārṅga bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 52 Verse 6. And Indra also rained pure and fragrant water, as well as divine flowers, in front of the wielder of the Śārṅga bow.

sanskrit: स प्रयातो महाबाहुः समेषु मरुधन्वसु ।ददर्शाथ मुनिश्रेष्ठमुत्तङ्कममितौजसम् ॥७॥
transliteration: sa prayāto mahābāhuḥ sameṣu marudhanvasu ,dadarśātha muniśreṣṭhamuttaṅkamamitaujasam.
english: He, the mighty-armed one, journeyed through plains and deserts. Then he saw Uttanka, the best among sages (muni), possessing immeasurable energy.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 52 Verse 7. He, the mighty-armed one, journeyed through plains and deserts. Then he saw Uttanka, the best among sages (muni), possessing immeasurable energy.

sanskrit: स तं संपूज्य तेजस्वी मुनिं पृथुललोचनः ।पूजितस्तेन च तदा पर्यपृच्छदनामयम् ॥८॥
transliteration: sa taṁ saṁpūjya tejasvī muniṁ pṛthulalocanaḥ ,pūjitastena ca tadā paryapṛcchadanāmayam.
english: He, the brilliant one with broad eyes, having honored that sage (muni), and being honored by him in return, then inquired about his well-being.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 52 Verse 8. He, the brilliant one with broad eyes, having honored that sage (muni), and being honored by him in return, then inquired about his well-being.

sanskrit: स पृष्टः कुशलं तेन संपूज्य मधुसूदनम् ।उत्तङ्को ब्राह्मणश्रेष्ठस्ततः पप्रच्छ माधवम् ॥९॥
transliteration: sa pṛṣṭaḥ kuśalaṁ tena saṁpūjya madhusūdanam ,uttaṅko brāhmaṇaśreṣṭhastataḥ papraccha mādhavam.
english: The great Brahmin Uttanka, having been asked about his welfare by Madhusudana (Krishna) and having duly honored him, then questioned Madhava.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 52 Verse 9. The great Brahmin Uttanka, having been asked about his welfare by Madhusudana (Krishna) and having duly honored him, then questioned Madhava.

sanskrit: कच्चिच्छौरे त्वया गत्वा कुरुपाण्डवसद्म तत् ।कृतं सौभ्रात्रमचलं तन्मे व्याख्यातुमर्हसि ॥१०॥
transliteration: kaccicchaure tvayā gatvā kurupāṇḍavasadma tat ,kṛtaṁ saubhrātramacalaṁ tanme vyākhyātumarhasi.
english: O Shauri, having gone to the abode of the Kurus and Pandavas, I hope you have established an unwavering bond of brotherhood. You ought to explain that to me.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 52 Verse 10. O Shauri, having gone to the abode of the Kurus and Pandavas, I hope you have established an unwavering bond of brotherhood. You ought to explain that to me.

sanskrit: अभिसंधाय तान्वीरानुपावृत्तोऽसि केशव ।संबन्धिनः सुदयितान्सततं वृष्णिपुंगव ॥११॥
transliteration: abhisaṁdhāya tānvīrānupāvṛtto'si keśava ,saṁbandhinaḥ sudayitānsatataṁ vṛṣṇipuṁgava.
english: O Keshava, O bull among the Vrishnis, have you returned after conciliating those heroes, your constantly beloved relatives?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 52 Verse 11. O Keshava, O bull among the Vrishnis, have you returned after conciliating those heroes, your constantly beloved relatives?

sanskrit: कच्चित्पाण्डुसुताः पञ्च धृतराष्ट्रस्य चात्मजाः ।लोकेषु विहरिष्यन्ति त्वया सह परंतप ॥१२॥
transliteration: kaccitpāṇḍusutāḥ pañca dhṛtarāṣṭrasya cātmajāḥ ,lokeṣu vihariṣyanti tvayā saha paraṁtapa.
english: O tormentor of foes, I hope that the five sons of Pandu and the sons of Dhritarashtra will live peacefully in the world together with you.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 52 Verse 12. O tormentor of foes, I hope that the five sons of Pandu and the sons of Dhritarashtra will live peacefully in the world together with you.

sanskrit: स्वराष्ट्रेषु च राजानः कच्चित्प्राप्स्यन्ति वै सुखम् ।कौरवेषु प्रशान्तेषु त्वया नाथेन माधव ॥१३॥
transliteration: svarāṣṭreṣu ca rājānaḥ kaccitprāpsyanti vai sukham ,kauraveṣu praśānteṣu tvayā nāthena mādhava.
english: O Madhava, I hope that the kings in their own kingdoms will indeed attain well-being now that the Kauravas have been completely subdued by you, their protector.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 52 Verse 13. O Madhava, I hope that the kings in their own kingdoms will indeed attain well-being now that the Kauravas have been completely subdued by you, their protector.

sanskrit: या मे संभावना तात त्वयि नित्यमवर्तत ।अपि सा सफला कृष्ण कृता ते भरतान्प्रति ॥१४॥
transliteration: yā me saṁbhāvanā tāta tvayi nityamavartata ,api sā saphalā kṛṣṇa kṛtā te bharatānprati.
english: O dear one, that high regard which I always held for you, O Krishna, has it now been made fruitful by you concerning the Bharatas?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 52 Verse 14. O dear one, that high regard which I always held for you, O Krishna, has it now been made fruitful by you concerning the Bharatas?

sanskrit: वासुदेव उवाच ।कृतो यत्नो मया ब्रह्मन्सौभ्रात्रे कौरवान्प्रति ।न चाशक्यन्त संधातुं तेऽधर्मरुचयो मया ॥१५॥
transliteration: vāsudeva uvāca ,kṛto yatno mayā brahmansaubhrātre kauravānprati ,na cāśakyanta saṁdhātuṁ te'dharmarucayo mayā.
english: Vasudeva said: 'O Brahmin, I made an effort for reconciliation with the Kauravas. But they, being inclined towards unrighteousness (adharma), could not be brought to an agreement by me.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 52 Verse 15. Vasudeva said: 'O Brahmin, I made an effort for reconciliation with the Kauravas. But they, being inclined towards unrighteousness (adharma), could not be brought to an agreement by me.'

sanskrit: ततस्ते निधनं प्राप्ताः सर्वे ससुतबान्धवाः ।न दिष्टमभ्यतिक्रान्तुं शक्यं बुद्ध्या बलेन वा ।महर्षे विदितं नूनं सर्वमेतत्तवानघ ॥१६॥
transliteration: tataste nidhanaṁ prāptāḥ sarve sasutabāndhavāḥ ,na diṣṭamabhyatikrāntuṁ śakyaṁ buddhyā balena vā ,maharṣe viditaṁ nūnaṁ sarvametattavānagha.
english: Consequently, all of them, along with their sons and relatives, met their demise. Destiny (diṣṭam) cannot be overcome by intellect or by force. O great sage, O sinless one, surely all of this is already known to you.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 52 Verse 16. Consequently, all of them, along with their sons and relatives, met their demise. Destiny (diṣṭam) cannot be overcome by intellect or by force. O great sage, O sinless one, surely all of this is already known to you.

sanskrit: तेऽत्यक्रामन्मतिं मह्यं भीष्मस्य विदुरस्य च ।ततो यमक्षयं जग्मुः समासाद्येतरेतरम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: te'tyakrāmanmatiṁ mahyaṁ bhīṣmasya vidurasya ca ,tato yamakṣayaṁ jagmuḥ samāsādyetaretaram.
english: They ignored my advice, as well as that of Bhishma and Vidura. Consequently, after confronting each other, they all went to the abode of death.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 52 Verse 17. They ignored my advice, as well as that of Bhishma and Vidura. Consequently, after confronting each other, they all went to the abode of death.

sanskrit: पञ्च वै पाण्डवाः शिष्टा हतमित्रा हतात्मजाः ।धार्तराष्ट्राश्च निहताः सर्वे ससुतबान्धवाः ॥१८॥
transliteration: pañca vai pāṇḍavāḥ śiṣṭā hatamitrā hatātmajāḥ ,dhārtarāṣṭrāśca nihatāḥ sarve sasutabāndhavāḥ.
english: Indeed, only five Pandavas remained, their friends and sons having been killed. And all the Dhartarashtras, along with their sons and relatives, were slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 52 Verse 18. Indeed, only five Pandavas remained, their friends and sons having been killed. And all the Dhartarashtras, along with their sons and relatives, were slain.

sanskrit: इत्युक्तवचने कृष्णे भृशं क्रोधसमन्वितः ।उत्तङ्कः प्रत्युवाचैनं रोषादुत्फाल्य लोचने ॥१९॥
transliteration: ityuktavacane kṛṣṇe bhṛśaṁ krodhasamanvitaḥ ,uttaṅkaḥ pratyuvācainaṁ roṣādutphālya locane.
english: When Krishna had spoken these words, Uttanka, greatly enraged, replied to him, his eyes bulging with fury.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 52 Verse 19. When Krishna had spoken these words, Uttanka, greatly enraged, replied to him, his eyes bulging with fury.

sanskrit: यस्माच्छक्तेन ते कृष्ण न त्राताः कुरुपाण्डवाः ।संबन्धिनः प्रियास्तस्माच्छप्स्येऽहं त्वामसंशयम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: yasmācchaktena te kṛṣṇa na trātāḥ kurupāṇḍavāḥ ,saṁbandhinaḥ priyāstasmācchapsye'haṁ tvāmasaṁśayam.
english: Because you, O Krishna, although capable, did not protect the Kurus and Pandavas, who were your dear relatives, I will therefore curse you, without a doubt.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 52 Verse 20. Because you, O Krishna, although capable, did not protect the Kurus and Pandavas, who were your dear relatives, I will therefore curse you, without a doubt.

sanskrit: न च ते प्रसभं यस्मात्ते निगृह्य निवर्तिताः ।तस्मान्मन्युपरीतस्त्वां शप्स्यामि मधुसूदन ॥२१॥
transliteration: na ca te prasabhaṁ yasmātte nigṛhya nivartitāḥ ,tasmānmanyuparītastvāṁ śapsyāmi madhusūdana.
english: And because they were not forcibly restrained and turned back by you, therefore, filled with anger, I will curse you, O Madhusūdana.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 52 Verse 21. And because they were not forcibly restrained and turned back by you, therefore, filled with anger, I will curse you, O Madhusūdana.

sanskrit: त्वया हि शक्तेन सता मिथ्याचारेण माधव ।उपचीर्णाः कुरुश्रेष्ठा यस्त्वेतान्समुपेक्षथाः ॥२२॥
transliteration: tvayā hi śaktena satā mithyācāreṇa mādhava ,upacīrṇāḥ kuruśreṣṭhā yastvetānsamupekṣathāḥ.
english: O Mādhava, although you are capable, you have acted wrongly, treating these foremost Kuru warriors poorly, for you entirely neglected them.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 52 Verse 22. O Mādhava, although you are capable, you have acted wrongly, treating these foremost Kuru warriors poorly, for you entirely neglected them.

sanskrit: वासुदेव उवाच ।शृणु मे विस्तरेणेदं यद्वक्ष्ये भृगुनन्दन ।गृहाणानुनयं चापि तपस्वी ह्यसि भार्गव ॥२३॥
transliteration: vāsudeva uvāca ,śṛṇu me vistareṇedaṁ yadvakṣye bhṛgunandana ,gṛhāṇānunayaṁ cāpi tapasvī hyasi bhārgava.
english: Vāsudeva said: 'Listen to me in detail regarding what I shall tell you, O Bhṛgunandana. And please accept this entreaty, for indeed you are an ascetic (tapasvī), O Bhārgava.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 52 Verse 23. Vāsudeva said: 'Listen to me in detail regarding what I shall tell you, O Bhṛgunandana. And please accept this entreaty, for indeed you are an ascetic (tapasvī), O Bhārgava.'

sanskrit: श्रुत्वा त्वमेतदध्यात्मं मुञ्चेथाः शापमद्य वै ।न च मां तपसाल्पेन शक्तोऽभिभवितुं पुमान् ॥२४॥
transliteration: śrutvā tvametadadhyātmaṁ muñcethāḥ śāpamadya vai ,na ca māṁ tapasālpena śakto'bhibhavituṁ pumān.
english: Having heard this spiritual (adhyātma) teaching, you should certainly withdraw your curse today. For no man is capable of overcoming me with meager austerity (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 52 Verse 24. Having heard this spiritual (adhyātma) teaching, you should certainly withdraw your curse today. For no man is capable of overcoming me with meager austerity (tapas).

sanskrit: न च ते तपसो नाशमिच्छामि जपतां वर ।तपस्ते सुमहद्दीप्तं गुरवश्चापि तोषिताः ॥२५॥
transliteration: na ca te tapaso nāśamicchāmi japatāṁ vara ,tapaste sumahaddīptaṁ guravaścāpi toṣitāḥ.
english: O best among those who recite, I do not wish for your asceticism (tapas) to be lost. Your asceticism (tapas) is very great and radiant, and your preceptors (guru) are also pleased.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 52 Verse 25. O best among those who recite, I do not wish for your asceticism (tapas) to be lost. Your asceticism (tapas) is very great and radiant, and your preceptors (guru) are also pleased.

sanskrit: कौमारं ब्रह्मचर्यं ते जानामि द्विजसत्तम ।दुःखार्जितस्य तपसस्तस्मान्नेच्छामि ते व्ययम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: kaumāraṁ brahmacaryaṁ te jānāmi dvijasattama ,duḥkhārjitasya tapasastasmānnecchāmi te vyayam.
english: O best among the twice-born (dvija), I know your celibacy (brahmacarya) from childhood. Therefore, I do not wish for the loss of your asceticism (tapas), which was acquired with such difficulty.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 52 Verse 26. O best among the twice-born (dvija), I know your celibacy (brahmacarya) from childhood. Therefore, I do not wish for the loss of your asceticism (tapas), which was acquired with such difficulty.

sanskrit: उत्तङ्क उवाच ।ब्रूहि केशव तत्त्वेन त्वमध्यात्ममनिन्दितम् ।श्रुत्वा श्रेयोऽभिधास्यामि शापं वा ते जनार्दन ॥१॥
transliteration: uttaṅka uvāca ,brūhi keśava tattvena tvamadhyātmamaninditam ,śrutvā śreyo'bhidhāsyāmi śāpaṁ vā te janārdana.
english: Uttanka said: O Keśava, truly explain to me the blameless spiritual knowledge (adhyātma). Having heard it, O Janārdana, I will pronounce either a blessing or a curse upon you.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 53 Verse 1. Uttanka said: O Keśava, truly explain to me the blameless spiritual knowledge (adhyātma). Having heard it, O Janārdana, I will pronounce either a blessing or a curse upon you.

sanskrit: वासुदेव उवाच ।तमो रजश्च सत्त्वं च विद्धि भावान्मदाश्रयान् ।तथा रुद्रान्वसूंश्चापि विद्धि मत्प्रभवान्द्विज ॥२॥
transliteration: vāsudeva uvāca ,tamo rajaśca sattvaṁ ca viddhi bhāvānmadāśrayān ,tathā rudrānvasūṁścāpi viddhi matprabhavāndvija.
english: Vāsudeva said: Know that inertia (tamas), passion (rajas), and goodness (sattva) are states of being dependent on Me. Similarly, O twice-born (dvija), know that the Rudras and Vasus also originate from Me.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 53 Verse 2. Vāsudeva said: Know that inertia (tamas), passion (rajas), and goodness (sattva) are states of being dependent on Me. Similarly, O twice-born (dvija), know that the Rudras and Vasus also originate from Me.

sanskrit: मयि सर्वाणि भूतानि सर्वभूतेषु चाप्यहम् ।स्थित इत्यभिजानीहि मा तेऽभूदत्र संशयः ॥३॥
transliteration: mayi sarvāṇi bhūtāni sarvabhūteṣu cāpyaham ,sthita ityabhijānīhi mā te'bhūdatra saṁśayaḥ.
english: All beings exist within me, and I too exist within all beings. Understand this clearly; let there be no doubt for you in this.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 53 Verse 3. All beings exist within me, and I too exist within all beings. Understand this clearly; let there be no doubt for you in this.

sanskrit: तथा दैत्यगणान्सर्वान्यक्षराक्षसपन्नगान् ।गन्धर्वाप्सरसश्चैव विद्धि मत्प्रभवान्द्विज ॥४॥
transliteration: tathā daityagaṇānsarvānyakṣarākṣasapannagān ,gandharvāpsarasaścaiva viddhi matprabhavāndvija.
english: Similarly, O twice-born (dvija), understand that all the multitudes of daityas, yakshas, rakshasas, pannagas (serpents), gandharvas, and apsaras originate from me.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 53 Verse 4. Similarly, O twice-born (dvija), understand that all the multitudes of daityas, yakshas, rakshasas, pannagas (serpents), gandharvas, and apsaras originate from me.

sanskrit: सदसच्चैव यत्प्राहुरव्यक्तं व्यक्तमेव च ।अक्षरं च क्षरं चैव सर्वमेतन्मदात्मकम् ॥५॥
transliteration: sadasaccaiva yatprāhuravyaktaṁ vyaktameva ca ,akṣaraṁ ca kṣaraṁ caiva sarvametanmadātmakam.
english: Whatever they declare to be both existent and non-existent, and also the unmanifest and the manifest, and likewise the imperishable (akṣara) and the perishable (kṣara) - all of this is permeated by my essential nature (ātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 53 Verse 5. Whatever they declare to be both existent and non-existent, and also the unmanifest and the manifest, and likewise the imperishable (akṣara) and the perishable (kṣara) - all of this is permeated by my essential nature (ātman).

sanskrit: ये चाश्रमेषु वै धर्माश्चतुर्षु विहिता मुने ।दैवानि चैव कर्माणि विद्धि सर्वं मदात्मकम् ॥६॥
transliteration: ye cāśrameṣu vai dharmāścaturṣu vihitā mune ,daivāni caiva karmāṇi viddhi sarvaṁ madātmakam.
english: And whatever principles (dharma) are prescribed in the four stages of life (āśrama), O sage (muni), and whatever divine actions (karma) exist, understand that all of this is permeated by my essential nature (ātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 53 Verse 6. And whatever principles (dharma) are prescribed in the four stages of life (āśrama), O sage (muni), and whatever divine actions (karma) exist, understand that all of this is permeated by my essential nature (ātman).

sanskrit: असच्च सदसच्चैव यद्विश्वं सदसतः परम् ।ततः परं नास्ति चैव देवदेवात्सनातनात् ॥७॥
transliteration: asacca sadasaccaiva yadviśvaṁ sadasataḥ param ,tataḥ paraṁ nāsti caiva devadevātsanātanāt.
english: The universe is indeed both the non-existent and the existent-non-existent, and truly, it is also beyond the existent and non-existent. Beyond that eternal God of gods, there is truly nothing else.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 53 Verse 7. The universe is indeed both the non-existent and the existent-non-existent, and truly, it is also beyond the existent and non-existent. Beyond that eternal God of gods, there is truly nothing else.

sanskrit: ओंकारप्रभवान्वेदान्विद्धि मां त्वं भृगूद्वह ।यूपं सोमं तथैवेह त्रिदशाप्यायनं मखे ॥८॥
transliteration: oṁkāraprabhavānvedānviddhi māṁ tvaṁ bhṛgūdvaha ,yūpaṁ somaṁ tathaiveha tridaśāpyāyanaṁ makhe.
english: O descendant of Bhrigu, know that I am the Vedas, originating from the sacred syllable Om (oṃkāra). I am also the sacrificial post, the soma, and the nourishment for the gods in the Vedic ritual (yajña) performed here.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 53 Verse 8. O descendant of Bhrigu, know that I am the Vedas, originating from the sacred syllable Om (oṃkāra). I am also the sacrificial post, the soma, and the nourishment for the gods in the Vedic ritual (yajña) performed here.

sanskrit: होतारमपि हव्यं च विद्धि मां भृगुनन्दन ।अध्वर्युः कल्पकश्चापि हविः परमसंस्कृतम् ॥९॥
transliteration: hotāramapi havyaṁ ca viddhi māṁ bhṛgunandana ,adhvaryuḥ kalpakaścāpi haviḥ paramasaṁskṛtam.
english: O son of Bhrigu, know that I am the Hotar priest and also the offering (havyam). I am also the Adhvaryu priest, the arranger, and the supremely prepared oblation (havis).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 53 Verse 9. O son of Bhrigu, know that I am the Hotar priest and also the offering (havyam). I am also the Adhvaryu priest, the arranger, and the supremely prepared oblation (havis).

sanskrit: उद्गाता चापि मां स्तौति गीतघोषैर्महाध्वरे ।प्रायश्चित्तेषु मां ब्रह्मञ्शान्तिमङ्गलवाचकाः ।स्तुवन्ति विश्वकर्माणं सततं द्विजसत्तमाः ॥१०॥
transliteration: udgātā cāpi māṁ stauti gītaghoṣairmahādhvare ,prāyaścitteṣu māṁ brahmañśāntimaṅgalavācakāḥ ,stuvanti viśvakarmāṇaṁ satataṁ dvijasattamāḥ.
english: And also, the Udgatar priest praises me with chants in the great Vedic ritual (yajña). O Brahmin, in acts of atonement, the reciters of peace and auspiciousness, along with the best among the twice-born (dvija), constantly praise me, the creator of the universe (Viśvakarman).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 53 Verse 10. And also, the Udgatar priest praises me with chants in the great Vedic ritual (yajña). O Brahmin, in acts of atonement, the reciters of peace and auspiciousness, along with the best among the twice-born (dvija), constantly praise me, the creator of the universe (Viśvakarman).

sanskrit: विद्धि मह्यं सुतं धर्ममग्रजं द्विजसत्तम ।मानसं दयितं विप्र सर्वभूतदयात्मकम् ॥११॥
transliteration: viddhi mahyaṁ sutaṁ dharmamagrajaṁ dvijasattama ,mānasaṁ dayitaṁ vipra sarvabhūtadayātmakam.
english: O best among the twice-born (dvijasattama), O brahmin, know my son, the elder, mind-born, and beloved, whose intrinsic nature (dharma) is compassion for all beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 53 Verse 11. O best among the twice-born (dvijasattama), O brahmin, know my son, the elder, mind-born, and beloved, whose intrinsic nature (dharma) is compassion for all beings.

sanskrit: तत्राहं वर्तमानैश्च निवृत्तैश्चैव मानवैः ।बह्वीः संसरमाणो वै योनीर्हि द्विजसत्तम ॥१२॥
transliteration: tatrāhaṁ vartamānaiśca nivṛttaiścaiva mānavaiḥ ,bahvīḥ saṁsaramāṇo vai yonīrhi dvijasattama.
english: O best among the twice-born (dvijasattama), there I, undergoing transmigration (saṃsāra), indeed pass through many births, alongside both living and departed human beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 53 Verse 12. O best among the twice-born (dvijasattama), there I, undergoing transmigration (saṃsāra), indeed pass through many births, alongside both living and departed human beings.

sanskrit: धर्मसंरक्षणार्थाय धर्मसंस्थापनाय च ।तैस्तैर्वेषैश्च रूपैश्च त्रिषु लोकेषु भार्गव ॥१३॥
transliteration: dharmasaṁrakṣaṇārthāya dharmasaṁsthāpanāya ca ,taistairveṣaiśca rūpaiśca triṣu lokeṣu bhārgava.
english: O Bhargava, for the protection of the natural law (dharma) and for its establishment, I appear in the three worlds with various guises and forms.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 53 Verse 13. O Bhargava, for the protection of the natural law (dharma) and for its establishment, I appear in the three worlds with various guises and forms.

sanskrit: अहं विष्णुरहं ब्रह्मा शक्रोऽथ प्रभवाप्ययः ।भूतग्रामस्य सर्वस्य स्रष्टा संहार एव च ॥१४॥
transliteration: ahaṁ viṣṇurahaṁ brahmā śakro'tha prabhavāpyayaḥ ,bhūtagrāmasya sarvasya sraṣṭā saṁhāra eva ca.
english: I am Vishnu, I am Brahma, and also Indra. I am the origin and dissolution of all beings, the creator and indeed the destroyer.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 53 Verse 14. I am Vishnu, I am Brahma, and also Indra. I am the origin and dissolution of all beings, the creator and indeed the destroyer.

sanskrit: अधर्मे वर्तमानानां सर्वेषामहमप्युत ।धर्मस्य सेतुं बध्नामि चलिते चलिते युगे ।तास्ता योनीः प्रविश्याहं प्रजानां हितकाम्यया ॥१५॥
transliteration: adharme vartamānānāṁ sarveṣāmahamapyuta ,dharmasya setuṁ badhnāmi calite calite yuge ,tāstā yonīḥ praviśyāhaṁ prajānāṁ hitakāmyayā.
english: Indeed, for all those who are engaged in unrighteousness (adharma), I establish the boundary of natural law (dharma) in every passing age. Entering into those various species, I do this out of a desire for the welfare of all beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 53 Verse 15. Indeed, for all those who are engaged in unrighteousness (adharma), I establish the boundary of natural law (dharma) in every passing age. Entering into those various species, I do this out of a desire for the welfare of all beings.

sanskrit: यदा त्वहं देवयोनौ वर्तामि भृगुनन्दन ।तदाहं देववत्सर्वमाचरामि न संशयः ॥१६॥
transliteration: yadā tvahaṁ devayonau vartāmi bhṛgunandana ,tadāhaṁ devavatsarvamācarāmi na saṁśayaḥ.
english: Indeed, when I exist in a divine species, O descendant of Bhrigu, then I act entirely like a god; there is no doubt about it.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 53 Verse 16. Indeed, when I exist in a divine species, O descendant of Bhrigu, then I act entirely like a god; there is no doubt about it.

sanskrit: यदा गन्धर्वयोनौ तु वर्तामि भृगुनन्दन ।तदा गन्धर्ववच्चेष्टाः सर्वाश्चेष्टामि भार्गव ॥१७॥
transliteration: yadā gandharvayonau tu vartāmi bhṛgunandana ,tadā gandharvavacceṣṭāḥ sarvāśceṣṭāmi bhārgava.
english: And when I exist in a Gandharva species, O descendant of Bhrigu, then I perform all actions like a Gandharva, O Bhargava.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 53 Verse 17. And when I exist in a Gandharva species, O descendant of Bhrigu, then I perform all actions like a Gandharva, O Bhargava.

sanskrit: नागयोनौ यदा चैव तदा वर्तामि नागवत् ।यक्षराक्षसयोनीश्च यथावद्विचराम्यहम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: nāgayonau yadā caiva tadā vartāmi nāgavat ,yakṣarākṣasayonīśca yathāvadvicarāmyaham.
english: When I am indeed in a Naga species, then I exist like a Naga. And similarly, I move about appropriately in the Yaksha and Rakshasa species.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 53 Verse 18. When I am indeed in a Naga species, then I exist like a Naga. And similarly, I move about appropriately in the Yaksha and Rakshasa species.

sanskrit: मानुष्ये वर्तमाने तु कृपणं याचिता मया ।न च ते जातसंमोहा वचो गृह्णन्ति मे हितम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: mānuṣye vartamāne tu kṛpaṇaṁ yācitā mayā ,na ca te jātasaṁmohā vaco gṛhṇanti me hitam.
english: Indeed, while they were in their human state, I pitifully implored them. But they, having become utterly deluded, do not accept my beneficial counsel.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 53 Verse 19. Indeed, while they were in their human state, I pitifully implored them. But they, having become utterly deluded, do not accept my beneficial counsel.

sanskrit: भयं च महदुद्दिश्य त्रासिताः कुरवो मया ।क्रुद्धेव भूत्वा च पुनर्यथावदनुदर्शिताः ॥२०॥
transliteration: bhayaṁ ca mahaduddiśya trāsitāḥ kuravo mayā ,kruddheva bhūtvā ca punaryathāvadanudarśitāḥ.
english: And I frightened the Kurus by pointing out a great danger. Then, as if having become enraged, I again instructed them properly.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 53 Verse 20. And I frightened the Kurus by pointing out a great danger. Then, as if having become enraged, I again instructed them properly.

sanskrit: तेऽधर्मेणेह संयुक्ताः परीताः कालधर्मणा ।धर्मेण निहता युद्धे गताः स्वर्गं न संशयः ॥२१॥
transliteration: te'dharmeṇeha saṁyuktāḥ parītāḥ kāladharmaṇā ,dharmeṇa nihatā yuddhe gatāḥ svargaṁ na saṁśayaḥ.
english: They (the Kauravas) were, in this world, associated with unrighteousness and overcome by the law of time (kāla). Yet, having been struck down in battle in accordance with (dharma), they undoubtedly attained heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 53 Verse 21. They (the Kauravas) were, in this world, associated with unrighteousness and overcome by the law of time (kāla). Yet, having been struck down in battle in accordance with (dharma), they undoubtedly attained heaven.

sanskrit: लोकेषु पाण्डवाश्चैव गताः ख्यातिं द्विजोत्तम ।एतत्ते सर्वमाख्यातं यन्मां त्वं परिपृच्छसि ॥२२॥
transliteration: lokeṣu pāṇḍavāścaiva gatāḥ khyātiṁ dvijottama ,etatte sarvamākhyātaṁ yanmāṁ tvaṁ paripṛcchasi.
english: And the Pāṇḍavas, indeed, attained great fame among all people, O best of the twice-born (dvija)! All of this that you inquire of me has been narrated to you.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 53 Verse 22. And the Pāṇḍavas, indeed, attained great fame among all people, O best of the twice-born (dvija)! All of this that you inquire of me has been narrated to you.

sanskrit: उत्तङ्क उवाच ।अभिजानामि जगतः कर्तारं त्वां जनार्दन ।नूनं भवत्प्रसादोऽयमिति मे नास्ति संशयः ॥१॥
transliteration: uttaṅka uvāca ,abhijānāmi jagataḥ kartāraṁ tvāṁ janārdana ,nūnaṁ bhavatprasādo'yamiti me nāsti saṁśayaḥ.
english: Uttanka said: "O Janardana, I recognize you as the creator of the universe. Surely, this is due to your grace, and I have no doubt about it."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 54 Verse 1. Uttanka said: "O Janardana, I recognize you as the creator of the universe. Surely, this is due to your grace, and I have no doubt about it."

sanskrit: चित्तं च सुप्रसन्नं मे त्वद्भावगतमच्युत ।विनिवृत्तश्च मे कोप इति विद्धि परंतप ॥२॥
transliteration: cittaṁ ca suprasannaṁ me tvadbhāvagatamacyuta ,vinivṛttaśca me kopa iti viddhi paraṁtapa.
english: "O Acyuta, my mind is now very serene, immersed in your true being (bhāva). O scorcher of foes (parantapa), know that my anger has entirely subsided."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 54 Verse 2. "O Acyuta, my mind is now very serene, immersed in your true being (bhāva). O scorcher of foes (parantapa), know that my anger has entirely subsided."

sanskrit: यदि त्वनुग्रहं कंचित्त्वत्तोऽर्होऽहं जनार्दन ।द्रष्टुमिच्छामि ते रूपमैश्वरं तन्निदर्शय ॥३॥
transliteration: yadi tvanugrahaṁ kaṁcittvatto'rho'haṁ janārdana ,draṣṭumicchāmi te rūpamaiśvaraṁ tannidarśaya.
english: "O Janardana, if I am truly worthy of any favor from you, I wish to see your divine form. Please reveal that to me."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 54 Verse 3. "O Janardana, if I am truly worthy of any favor from you, I wish to see your divine form. Please reveal that to me."

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततः स तस्मै प्रीतात्मा दर्शयामास तद्वपुः ।शाश्वतं वैष्णवं धीमान्ददृशे यद्धनंजयः ॥४॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tataḥ sa tasmai prītātmā darśayāmāsa tadvapuḥ ,śāśvataṁ vaiṣṇavaṁ dhīmāndadṛśe yaddhanaṁjayaḥ.
english: Vaishampayana said: "Then, that wise one (dhīmān), with a delighted inner self (ātman), showed him that eternal form of Vishnu (vaiṣṇava) which Dhananjaya had seen."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 54 Verse 4. Vaishampayana said: "Then, that wise one (dhīmān), with a delighted inner self (ātman), showed him that eternal form of Vishnu (vaiṣṇava) which Dhananjaya had seen."

sanskrit: स ददर्श महात्मानं विश्वरूपं महाभुजम् ।विस्मयं च ययौ विप्रस्तद्दृष्ट्वा रूपमैश्वरम् ॥५॥
transliteration: sa dadarśa mahātmānaṁ viśvarūpaṁ mahābhujam ,vismayaṁ ca yayau viprastaddṛṣṭvā rūpamaiśvaram.
english: He beheld the great-souled one, who possessed a universal form and mighty arms. The Brahmin was filled with astonishment upon seeing that divine form.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 54 Verse 5. He beheld the great-souled one, who possessed a universal form and mighty arms. The Brahmin was filled with astonishment upon seeing that divine form.

sanskrit: उत्तङ्क उवाच ।विश्वकर्मन्नमस्तेऽस्तु यस्य ते रूपमीदृशम् ।पद्भ्यां ते पृथिवी व्याप्ता शिरसा चावृतं नभः ॥६॥
transliteration: uttaṅka uvāca ,viśvakarmannamaste'stu yasya te rūpamīdṛśam ,padbhyāṁ te pṛthivī vyāptā śirasā cāvṛtaṁ nabhaḥ.
english: Uttanka said: "O Viśvakarman, homage to you, whose form is so vast! The earth is encompassed by your feet, and the sky is covered by your head."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 54 Verse 6. Uttanka said: "O Viśvakarman, homage to you, whose form is so vast! The earth is encompassed by your feet, and the sky is covered by your head."

sanskrit: द्यावापृथिव्योर्यन्मध्यं जठरेण तदावृतम् ।भुजाभ्यामावृताश्चाशास्त्वमिदं सर्वमच्युत ॥७॥
transliteration: dyāvāpṛthivyoryanmadhyaṁ jaṭhareṇa tadāvṛtam ,bhujābhyāmāvṛtāścāśāstvamidaṁ sarvamacyuta.
english: Whatever lies between heaven and earth is enveloped by your belly; and the directions are covered by your two arms. O Acyuta, you are all of this.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 54 Verse 7. Whatever lies between heaven and earth is enveloped by your belly; and the directions are covered by your two arms. O Acyuta, you are all of this.

sanskrit: संहरस्व पुनर्देव रूपमक्षय्यमुत्तमम् ।पुनस्त्वां स्वेन रूपेण द्रष्टुमिच्छामि शाश्वतम् ॥८॥
transliteration: saṁharasva punardeva rūpamakṣayyamuttamam ,punastvāṁ svena rūpeṇa draṣṭumicchāmi śāśvatam.
english: O God, please withdraw this indestructible and supreme form. I wish to see you again in your own eternal form.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 54 Verse 8. O God, please withdraw this indestructible and supreme form. I wish to see you again in your own eternal form.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तमुवाच प्रसन्नात्मा गोविन्दो जनमेजय ।वरं वृणीष्वेति तदा तमुत्तङ्कोऽब्रवीदिदम् ॥९॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tamuvāca prasannātmā govindo janamejaya ,varaṁ vṛṇīṣveti tadā tamuttaṅko'bravīdidam.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then Govinda, with a pleased mind (ātman), said to him, 'Choose a boon!' At that, Uttanka said this to him, O Janamejaya.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 54 Verse 9. Vaiśampāyana said: Then Govinda, with a pleased mind (ātman), said to him, 'Choose a boon!' At that, Uttanka said this to him, O Janamejaya.

sanskrit: पर्याप्त एष एवाद्य वरस्त्वत्तो महाद्युते ।यत्ते रूपमिदं कृष्ण पश्यामि प्रभवाप्ययम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: paryāpta eṣa evādya varastvatto mahādyute ,yatte rūpamidaṁ kṛṣṇa paśyāmi prabhavāpyayam.
english: O greatly effulgent one, this boon from you is indeed sufficient today: that I behold this form of yours, O Krishna, which is the origin and dissolution (prabhavāpyayam) of all.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 54 Verse 10. O greatly effulgent one, this boon from you is indeed sufficient today: that I behold this form of yours, O Krishna, which is the origin and dissolution (prabhavāpyayam) of all.

sanskrit: तमब्रवीत्पुनः कृष्णो मा त्वमत्र विचारय ।अवश्यमेतत्कर्तव्यममोघं दर्शनं मम ॥११॥
transliteration: tamabravītpunaḥ kṛṣṇo mā tvamatra vicāraya ,avaśyametatkartavyamamoghaṁ darśanaṁ mama.
english: Krishna again said to him, 'Do not deliberate on this matter. This must certainly be done, for my vision (darśana) is never in vain.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 54 Verse 11. Krishna again said to him, 'Do not deliberate on this matter. This must certainly be done, for my vision (darśana) is never in vain.'

sanskrit: उत्तङ्क उवाच ।अवश्यकरणीयं वै यद्येतन्मन्यसे विभो ।तोयमिच्छामि यत्रेष्टं मरुष्वेतद्धि दुर्लभम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: uttaṅka uvāca ,avaśyakaraṇīyaṁ vai yadyetanmanyase vibho ,toyamicchāmi yatreṣṭaṁ maruṣvetaddhi durlabham.
english: Uttanka said: 'If you consider this to be certainly accomplishable, O All-pervading Lord (vibho), then I desire water wherever it is wished, for this (water) is indeed difficult to obtain in deserts.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 54 Verse 12. Uttanka said: 'If you consider this to be certainly accomplishable, O All-pervading Lord (vibho), then I desire water wherever it is wished, for this (water) is indeed difficult to obtain in deserts.'

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततः संहृत्य तत्तेजः प्रोवाचोत्तङ्कमीश्वरः ।एष्टव्ये सति चिन्त्योऽहमित्युक्त्वा द्वारकां ययौ ॥१३॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tataḥ saṁhṛtya tattejaḥ provācottaṅkamīśvaraḥ ,eṣṭavye sati cintyo'hamityuktvā dvārakāṁ yayau.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then, having withdrawn that energy, the Lord spoke to Uttanka, "When something is to be desired or obtained, I should be remembered." Having said this, he went to Dvārakā.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 54 Verse 13. Vaiśampāyana said: Then, having withdrawn that energy, the Lord spoke to Uttanka, "When something is to be desired or obtained, I should be remembered." Having said this, he went to Dvārakā.

sanskrit: ततः कदाचिद्भगवानुत्तङ्कस्तोयकाङ्क्षया ।तृषितः परिचक्राम मरौ सस्मार चाच्युतम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: tataḥ kadācidbhagavānuttaṅkastoyakāṅkṣayā ,tṛṣitaḥ paricakrāma marau sasmāra cācyutam.
english: Then, one day, the revered Uttanka, thirsty and desiring water, wandered through the desert and remembered Acyuta.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 54 Verse 14. Then, one day, the revered Uttanka, thirsty and desiring water, wandered through the desert and remembered Acyuta.

sanskrit: ततो दिग्वाससं धीमान्मातङ्गं मलपङ्किनम् ।अपश्यत मरौ तस्मिञ्श्वयूथपरिवारितम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: tato digvāsasaṁ dhīmānmātaṅgaṁ malapaṅkinam ,apaśyata marau tasmiñśvayūthaparivāritam.
english: Then, the intelligent one (Uttanka) saw in that desert a naked outcaste (mātaṅga) covered in dirt and mud, surrounded by a pack of dogs.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 54 Verse 15. Then, the intelligent one (Uttanka) saw in that desert a naked outcaste (mātaṅga) covered in dirt and mud, surrounded by a pack of dogs.

sanskrit: भीषणं बद्धनिस्त्रिंशं बाणकार्मुकधारिणम् ।तस्याधः स्रोतसोऽपश्यद्वारि भूरि द्विजोत्तमः ॥१६॥
transliteration: bhīṣaṇaṁ baddhanistriṁśaṁ bāṇakārmukadhāriṇam ,tasyādhaḥ srotaso'paśyadvāri bhūri dvijottamaḥ.
english: The best of the twice-born (Uttanka) saw [him] terrifying, with a sword fastened, and holding a bow and arrow. Below him, he saw abundant water from a spring.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 54 Verse 16. The best of the twice-born (Uttanka) saw [him] terrifying, with a sword fastened, and holding a bow and arrow. Below him, he saw abundant water from a spring.

sanskrit: स्मरन्नेव च तं प्राह मातङ्गः प्रहसन्निव ।एह्युत्तङ्क प्रतीच्छस्व मत्तो वारि भृगूद्वह ।कृपा हि मे सुमहती त्वां दृष्ट्वा तृट्समाहतम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: smaranneva ca taṁ prāha mātaṅgaḥ prahasanniva ,ehyuttaṅka pratīcchasva matto vāri bhṛgūdvaha ,kṛpā hi me sumahatī tvāṁ dṛṣṭvā tṛṭsamāhatam.
english: Remembering him, Matanga, the Caṇḍāla, then spoke to Uttanka as if laughing: 'Come, O descendant of Bhrigu, accept this water from me. Indeed, I feel great compassion for you, seeing you afflicted by thirst.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 54 Verse 17. Remembering him, Matanga, the Caṇḍāla, then spoke to Uttanka as if laughing: 'Come, O descendant of Bhrigu, accept this water from me. Indeed, I feel great compassion for you, seeing you afflicted by thirst.'

sanskrit: इत्युक्तस्तेन स मुनिस्तत्तोयं नाभ्यनन्दत ।चिक्षेप च स तं धीमान्वाग्भिरुग्राभिरच्युतम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: ityuktastena sa munistattoyaṁ nābhyanandata ,cikṣepa ca sa taṁ dhīmānvāgbhirugrābhiracyutam.
english: Addressed thus by him, that sage (Uttanka) did not approve of that water. And that intelligent one (Uttanka) cursed him (Matanga), who remained firm (acyuta), with fierce words.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 54 Verse 18. Addressed thus by him, that sage (Uttanka) did not approve of that water. And that intelligent one (Uttanka) cursed him (Matanga), who remained firm (acyuta), with fierce words.

sanskrit: पुनः पुनश्च मातङ्गः पिबस्वेति तमब्रवीत् ।न चापिबत्स सक्रोधः क्षुभितेनान्तरात्मना ॥१९॥
transliteration: punaḥ punaśca mātaṅgaḥ pibasveti tamabravīt ,na cāpibatsa sakrodhaḥ kṣubhitenāntarātmanā.
english: And again and again, Matanga said to him, 'Drink!' But he, full of anger and with a disturbed inner self (ātman), did not drink.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 54 Verse 19. And again and again, Matanga said to him, 'Drink!' But he, full of anger and with a disturbed inner self (ātman), did not drink.

sanskrit: स तथा निश्चयात्तेन प्रत्याख्यातो महात्मना ।श्वभिः सह महाराज तत्रैवान्तरधीयत ॥२०॥
transliteration: sa tathā niścayāttena pratyākhyāto mahātmanā ,śvabhiḥ saha mahārāja tatraivāntaradhīyata.
english: O great king, thus resolutely rejected by that great-souled one (mahātman) (Uttanka), he (Matanga) then disappeared right there, along with his dogs.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 54 Verse 20. O great king, thus resolutely rejected by that great-souled one (mahātman) (Uttanka), he (Matanga) then disappeared right there, along with his dogs.

sanskrit: उत्तङ्कस्तं तथा दृष्ट्वा ततो व्रीडितमानसः ।मेने प्रलब्धमात्मानं कृष्णेनामित्रघातिना ॥२१॥
transliteration: uttaṅkastaṁ tathā dṛṣṭvā tato vrīḍitamānasaḥ ,mene pralabdhamātmānaṁ kṛṣṇenāmitraghātinā.
english: Seeing him in that state, Uttaṅka then became greatly embarrassed. He considered himself (ātman) deceived by Kṛṣṇa, the destroyer of enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 54 Verse 21. Seeing him in that state, Uttaṅka then became greatly embarrassed. He considered himself (ātman) deceived by Kṛṣṇa, the destroyer of enemies.

sanskrit: अथ तेनैव मार्गेण शङ्खचक्रगदाधरः ।आजगाम महाबाहुरुत्तङ्कश्चैनमब्रवीत् ॥२२॥
transliteration: atha tenaiva mārgeṇa śaṅkhacakragadādharaḥ ,ājagāma mahābāhuruttaṅkaścainamabravīt.
english: Then, along that very path, the mighty-armed one, bearing the conch, discus, and mace, arrived. And Uttaṅka spoke to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 54 Verse 22. Then, along that very path, the mighty-armed one, bearing the conch, discus, and mace, arrived. And Uttaṅka spoke to him.

sanskrit: न युक्तं तादृशं दातुं त्वया पुरुषसत्तम ।सलिलं विप्रमुख्येभ्यो मातङ्गस्रोतसा विभो ॥२३॥
transliteration: na yuktaṁ tādṛśaṁ dātuṁ tvayā puruṣasattama ,salilaṁ vipramukhyebhyo mātaṅgasrotasā vibho.
english: O best among men, O Lord, it was not proper for you to offer such water - water from an outcaste's (mātaṅga) source - to the chief Brahmins.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 54 Verse 23. O best among men, O Lord, it was not proper for you to offer such water - water from an outcaste's (mātaṅga) source - to the chief Brahmins.

sanskrit: इत्युक्तवचनं धीमान्महाबुद्धिर्जनार्दनः ।उत्तङ्कं श्लक्ष्णया वाचा सान्त्वयन्निदमब्रवीत् ॥२४॥
transliteration: ityuktavacanaṁ dhīmānmahābuddhirjanārdanaḥ ,uttaṅkaṁ ślakṣṇayā vācā sāntvayannidamabravīt.
english: After Uttaṅka had spoken thus, the wise and greatly intelligent Janārdana (Kṛṣṇa), comforting Uttaṅka with a gentle voice, said this.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 54 Verse 24. After Uttaṅka had spoken thus, the wise and greatly intelligent Janārdana (Kṛṣṇa), comforting Uttaṅka with a gentle voice, said this.

sanskrit: यादृशेनेह रूपेण योग्यं दातुं वृतेन वै ।तादृशं खलु मे दत्तं त्वं तु तन्नावबुध्यसे ॥२५॥
transliteration: yādṛśeneha rūpeṇa yogyaṁ dātuṁ vṛtena vai ,tādṛśaṁ khalu me dattaṁ tvaṁ tu tannāvabudhyase.
english: Indeed, whatever form was suitable to be given, as requested, exactly that has been bestowed upon me. However, you do not comprehend this.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 54 Verse 25. Indeed, whatever form was suitable to be given, as requested, exactly that has been bestowed upon me. However, you do not comprehend this.

sanskrit: मया त्वदर्थमुक्तो हि वज्रपाणिः पुरंदरः ।उत्तङ्कायामृतं देहि तोयरूपमिति प्रभुः ॥२६॥
transliteration: mayā tvadarthamukto hi vajrapāṇiḥ puraṁdaraḥ ,uttaṅkāyāmṛtaṁ dehi toyarūpamiti prabhuḥ.
english: Indeed, I addressed the lord Vajrapāṇi (Indra), Purandara, for your sake, saying: 'Give immortality (amṛta) to Uttaṅka in the form of water!'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 54 Verse 26. Indeed, I addressed the lord Vajrapāṇi (Indra), Purandara, for your sake, saying: 'Give immortality (amṛta) to Uttaṅka in the form of water!'

sanskrit: स मामुवाच देवेन्द्रो न मर्त्योऽमर्त्यतां व्रजेत् ।अन्यमस्मै वरं देहीत्यसकृद्भृगुनन्दन ॥२७॥
transliteration: sa māmuvāca devendro na martyo'martyatāṁ vrajet ,anyamasmai varaṁ dehītyasakṛdbhṛgunandana.
english: The lord of the gods (Indra) repeatedly said to me, 'A mortal being should not attain immortality (amṛtatā). Give him another boon, O son of Bhṛgu!'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 54 Verse 27. The lord of the gods (Indra) repeatedly said to me, 'A mortal being should not attain immortality (amṛtatā). Give him another boon, O son of Bhṛgu!'

sanskrit: अमृतं देयमित्येव मयोक्तः स शचीपतिः ।स मां प्रसाद्य देवेन्द्रः पुनरेवेदमब्रवीत् ॥२८॥
transliteration: amṛtaṁ deyamityeva mayoktaḥ sa śacīpatiḥ ,sa māṁ prasādya devendraḥ punarevedamabravīt.
english: When I insisted, 'Immortality (amṛta) must be given,' that lord of the gods (Indra), husband of Śacī, then appeased me and spoke these words again.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 54 Verse 28. When I insisted, 'Immortality (amṛta) must be given,' that lord of the gods (Indra), husband of Śacī, then appeased me and spoke these words again.

sanskrit: यदि देयमवश्यं वै मातङ्गोऽहं महाद्युते ।भूत्वामृतं प्रदास्यामि भार्गवाय महात्मने ॥२९॥
transliteration: yadi deyamavaśyaṁ vai mātaṅgo'haṁ mahādyute ,bhūtvāmṛtaṁ pradāsyāmi bhārgavāya mahātmane.
english: If it must certainly be given, O greatly resplendent one, then I shall become an outcaste (Mātanga) and give the nectar (amṛta) to the great-souled Bhārgava.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 54 Verse 29. If it must certainly be given, O greatly resplendent one, then I shall become an outcaste (Mātanga) and give the nectar (amṛta) to the great-souled Bhārgava.

sanskrit: यद्येवं प्रतिगृह्णाति भार्गवोऽमृतमद्य वै ।प्रदातुमेष गच्छामि भार्गवायामृतं प्रभो ।प्रत्याख्यातस्त्वहं तेन न दद्यामिति भार्गव ॥३०॥
transliteration: yadyevaṁ pratigṛhṇāti bhārgavo'mṛtamadya vai ,pradātumeṣa gacchāmi bhārgavāyāmṛtaṁ prabho ,pratyākhyātastvahaṁ tena na dadyāmiti bhārgava.
english: If Bhārgava indeed accepts the nectar (amṛta) today, then I shall go to give this nectar (amṛta) to Bhārgava, O lord. But I was rejected by him (Bhārgava), O Bhārgava, [who declared,] 'I should not give!'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 54 Verse 30. If Bhārgava indeed accepts the nectar (amṛta) today, then I shall go to give this nectar (amṛta) to Bhārgava, O lord. But I was rejected by him (Bhārgava), O Bhārgava, [who declared,] 'I should not give!'

sanskrit: स तथा समयं कृत्वा तेन रूपेण वासवः ।उपस्थितस्त्वया चापि प्रत्याख्यातोऽमृतं ददत् ।चण्डालरूपी भगवान्सुमहांस्ते व्यतिक्रमः ॥३१॥
transliteration: sa tathā samayaṁ kṛtvā tena rūpeṇa vāsavaḥ ,upasthitastvayā cāpi pratyākhyāto'mṛtaṁ dadat ,caṇḍālarūpī bhagavānsumahāṁste vyatikramaḥ.
english: Having thus made a resolve, Vasava (Indra) was present in that form (of a Mātanga). He, the divine one, giving nectar (amṛta) in the form of an outcaste (caṇḍāla), was rejected even by you. Great indeed is your transgression.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 54 Verse 31. Having thus made a resolve, Vasava (Indra) was present in that form (of a Mātanga). He, the divine one, giving nectar (amṛta) in the form of an outcaste (caṇḍāla), was rejected even by you. Great indeed is your transgression.

sanskrit: यत्तु शक्यं मया कर्तुं भूय एव तवेप्सितम् ।तोयेप्सां तव दुर्धर्ष करिष्ये सफलामहम् ॥३२॥
transliteration: yattu śakyaṁ mayā kartuṁ bhūya eva tavepsitam ,toyepsāṁ tava durdharṣa kariṣye saphalāmaham.
english: But whatever else you desire, which can be accomplished by me, O unconquerable one, I shall certainly make your longing for water fruitful.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 54 Verse 32. But whatever else you desire, which can be accomplished by me, O unconquerable one, I shall certainly make your longing for water fruitful.

sanskrit: येष्वहःसु तव ब्रह्मन्सलिलेच्छा भविष्यति ।तदा मरौ भविष्यन्ति जलपूर्णाः पयोधराः ॥३३॥
transliteration: yeṣvahaḥsu tava brahmansalilecchā bhaviṣyati ,tadā marau bhaviṣyanti jalapūrṇāḥ payodharāḥ.
english: O brahmin, on whatever days you desire water, then in the desert, water-filled clouds will appear.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 54 Verse 33. O brahmin, on whatever days you desire water, then in the desert, water-filled clouds will appear.

sanskrit: रसवच्च प्रदास्यन्ति ते तोयं भृगुनन्दन ।उत्तङ्कमेघा इत्युक्ताः ख्यातिं यास्यन्ति चापि ते ॥३४॥
transliteration: rasavacca pradāsyanti te toyaṁ bhṛgunandana ,uttaṅkameghā ityuktāḥ khyātiṁ yāsyanti cāpi te.
english: And they will give sweet water, O descendant of Bhṛgu. They will also attain fame, being known as 'Utṭanka clouds'.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 54 Verse 34. And they will give sweet water, O descendant of Bhṛgu. They will also attain fame, being known as 'Utṭanka clouds'.

sanskrit: इत्युक्तः प्रीतिमान्विप्रः कृष्णेन स बभूव ह ।अद्याप्युत्तङ्कमेघाश्च मरौ वर्षन्ति भारत ॥३५॥
transliteration: ityuktaḥ prītimānvipraḥ kṛṣṇena sa babhūva ha ,adyāpyuttaṅkameghāśca marau varṣanti bhārata.
english: Thus addressed by Kṛṣṇa, that brahmin indeed became pleased. And even today, O Bhārata, the Utṭanka clouds rain in the desert.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 54 Verse 35. Thus addressed by Kṛṣṇa, that brahmin indeed became pleased. And even today, O Bhārata, the Utṭanka clouds rain in the desert.

sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।उत्तङ्कः केन तपसा संयुक्तः सुमहातपाः ।यः शापं दातुकामोऽभूद्विष्णवे प्रभविष्णवे ॥१॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,uttaṅkaḥ kena tapasā saṁyuktaḥ sumahātapāḥ ,yaḥ śāpaṁ dātukāmo'bhūdviṣṇave prabhaviṣṇave.
english: Janamejaya said: "By what (kind of) spiritual discipline (tapas) was Utṭanka, the practitioner of great austerities (tapas), endowed, such that he became desirous of giving a curse to the powerful Viṣṇu?"
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 55 Verse 1. Janamejaya said: "By what (kind of) spiritual discipline (tapas) was Utṭanka, the practitioner of great austerities (tapas), endowed, such that he became desirous of giving a curse to the powerful Viṣṇu?"

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।उत्तङ्को महता युक्तस्तपसा जनमेजय ।गुरुभक्तः स तेजस्वी नान्यं कंचिदपूजयत् ॥२॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,uttaṅko mahatā yuktastapasā janamejaya ,gurubhaktaḥ sa tejasvī nānyaṁ kaṁcidapūjayat.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: O Janamejaya, Uttanka, endowed with great ascetic discipline (tapas), was devoted to his guru. That radiant one worshipped no one else.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 55 Verse 2. Vaiśampāyana said: O Janamejaya, Uttanka, endowed with great ascetic discipline (tapas), was devoted to his guru. That radiant one worshipped no one else.

sanskrit: सर्वेषामृषिपुत्राणामेष चासीन्मनोरथः ।औत्तङ्कीं गुरुवृत्तिं वै प्राप्नुयामिति भारत ॥३॥
transliteration: sarveṣāmṛṣiputrāṇāmeṣa cāsīnmanorathaḥ ,auttaṅkīṁ guruvṛttiṁ vai prāpnuyāmiti bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, this was indeed the desire of all the sons of sages: 'May I achieve a devotion to my guru (guruvṛtti) like Uttanka's!'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 55 Verse 3. O Bhārata, this was indeed the desire of all the sons of sages: 'May I achieve a devotion to my guru (guruvṛtti) like Uttanka's!'

sanskrit: गौतमस्य तु शिष्याणां बहूनां जनमेजय ।उत्तङ्केऽभ्यधिका प्रीतिः स्नेहश्चैवाभवत्तदा ॥४॥
transliteration: gautamasya tu śiṣyāṇāṁ bahūnāṁ janamejaya ,uttaṅke'bhyadhikā prītiḥ snehaścaivābhavattadā.
english: O Janamejaya, among many of Gautama's disciples, a particularly great affection and fondness indeed developed towards Uttanka at that time.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 55 Verse 4. O Janamejaya, among many of Gautama's disciples, a particularly great affection and fondness indeed developed towards Uttanka at that time.

sanskrit: स तस्य दमशौचाभ्यां विक्रान्तेन च कर्मणा ।सम्यक्चैवोपचारेण गौतमः प्रीतिमानभूत् ॥५॥
transliteration: sa tasya damaśaucābhyāṁ vikrāntena ca karmaṇā ,samyakcaivopacāreṇa gautamaḥ prītimānabhūt.
english: Gautama became delighted by his (Uttanka's) self-control (dama) and purity, by his powerful actions, and indeed by his excellent service.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 55 Verse 5. Gautama became delighted by his (Uttanka's) self-control (dama) and purity, by his powerful actions, and indeed by his excellent service.

sanskrit: अथ शिष्यसहस्राणि समनुज्ञाय गौतमः ।उत्तङ्कं परया प्रीत्या नाभ्यनुज्ञातुमैच्छत ॥६॥
transliteration: atha śiṣyasahasrāṇi samanujñāya gautamaḥ ,uttaṅkaṁ parayā prītyā nābhyanujñātumaicchata.
english: Then, after dismissing thousands of his other disciples, Gautama, with great affection, did not wish to dismiss Utṭaṅka.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 55 Verse 6. Then, after dismissing thousands of his other disciples, Gautama, with great affection, did not wish to dismiss Utṭaṅka.

sanskrit: तं क्रमेण जरा तात प्रतिपेदे महामुनिम् ।न चान्वबुध्यत तदा स मुनिर्गुरुवत्सलः ॥७॥
transliteration: taṁ krameṇa jarā tāta pratipede mahāmunim ,na cānvabudhyata tadā sa munirguruvatsalaḥ.
english: Dear son, gradually old age befell that great sage (muni). Yet, at that time, that sage, so devoted to his (guru), did not perceive it.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 55 Verse 7. Dear son, gradually old age befell that great sage (muni). Yet, at that time, that sage, so devoted to his (guru), did not perceive it.

sanskrit: ततः कदाचिद्राजेन्द्र काष्ठान्यानयितुं ययौ ।उत्तङ्कः काष्ठभारं च महान्तं समुपानयत् ॥८॥
transliteration: tataḥ kadācidrājendra kāṣṭhānyānayituṁ yayau ,uttaṅkaḥ kāṣṭhabhāraṁ ca mahāntaṁ samupānayat.
english: Then one day, O king of kings (rājendra), Utṭaṅka went to bring firewood. And he brought back a large load of wood.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 55 Verse 8. Then one day, O king of kings (rājendra), Utṭaṅka went to bring firewood. And he brought back a large load of wood.

sanskrit: स तु भाराभिभूतात्मा काष्ठभारमरिंदम ।निष्पिपेष क्षितौ राजन्परिश्रान्तो बुभुक्षितः ॥९॥
transliteration: sa tu bhārābhibhūtātmā kāṣṭhabhāramariṁdama ,niṣpipeṣa kṣitau rājanpariśrānto bubhukṣitaḥ.
english: But he, whose inner self (ātman) was overwhelmed by the heavy load, O subduer of enemies, being completely exhausted and hungry, crushed that bundle of wood on the ground, O king.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 55 Verse 9. But he, whose inner self (ātman) was overwhelmed by the heavy load, O subduer of enemies, being completely exhausted and hungry, crushed that bundle of wood on the ground, O king.

sanskrit: तस्य काष्ठे विलग्नाभूज्जटा रूप्यसमप्रभा ।ततः काष्ठैः सह तदा पपात धरणीतले ॥१०॥
transliteration: tasya kāṣṭhe vilagnābhūjjaṭā rūpyasamaprabhā ,tataḥ kāṣṭhaiḥ saha tadā papāta dharaṇītale.
english: His matted lock, shining like silver, became entangled in the wood. Then, along with the pieces of wood, he fell to the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 55 Verse 10. His matted lock, shining like silver, became entangled in the wood. Then, along with the pieces of wood, he fell to the ground.

sanskrit: ततः स भारनिष्पिष्टः क्षुधाविष्टश्च भार्गवः ।दृष्ट्वा तां वयसोऽवस्थां रुरोदार्तस्वरं तदा ॥११॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa bhāraniṣpiṣṭaḥ kṣudhāviṣṭaśca bhārgavaḥ ,dṛṣṭvā tāṁ vayaso'vasthāṁ rurodārtasvaraṁ tadā.
english: Then that Bhargava, crushed by the burden and afflicted by hunger, seeing his advanced age, cried out in a pained voice.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 55 Verse 11. Then that Bhargava, crushed by the burden and afflicted by hunger, seeing his advanced age, cried out in a pained voice.

sanskrit: ततो गुरुसुता तस्य पद्मपत्रनिभेक्षणा ।जग्राहाश्रूणि सुश्रोणी करेण पृथुलोचना ।पितुर्नियोगाद्धर्मज्ञा शिरसावनता तदा ॥१२॥
transliteration: tato gurusutā tasya padmapatranibhekṣaṇā ,jagrāhāśrūṇi suśroṇī kareṇa pṛthulocanā ,piturniyogāddharmajñā śirasāvanatā tadā.
english: Then, his guru's daughter, whose eyes resembled lotus petals, the beautiful-hipped and broad-eyed woman, knowing her sacred duty (dharma) and bowing her head at her father's command, collected his tears with her hand.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 55 Verse 12. Then, his guru's daughter, whose eyes resembled lotus petals, the beautiful-hipped and broad-eyed woman, knowing her sacred duty (dharma) and bowing her head at her father's command, collected his tears with her hand.

sanskrit: तस्या निपेततुर्दग्धौ करौ तैरश्रुबिन्दुभिः ।न हि तानश्रुपातान्वै शक्ता धारयितुं मही ॥१३॥
transliteration: tasyā nipetaturdagdhau karau tairaśrubindubhiḥ ,na hi tānaśrupātānvai śaktā dhārayituṁ mahī.
english: Her two burnt hands fell because of those tear-drops. Indeed, the earth (mahī) was not capable of containing those tear-falls.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 55 Verse 13. Her two burnt hands fell because of those tear-drops. Indeed, the earth (mahī) was not capable of containing those tear-falls.

sanskrit: गौतमस्त्वब्रवीद्विप्रमुत्तङ्कं प्रीतमानसः ।कस्मात्तात तवाद्येह शोकोत्तरमिदं मनः ।स स्वैरं ब्रूहि विप्रर्षे श्रोतुमिच्छामि ते वचः ॥१४॥
transliteration: gautamastvabravīdvipramuttaṅkaṁ prītamānasaḥ ,kasmāttāta tavādyeha śokottaramidaṁ manaḥ ,sa svairaṁ brūhi viprarṣe śrotumicchāmi te vacaḥ.
english: Gautama, with a pleased mind, spoke to the brahmin Utanka: "My dear son, why is your mind so overwhelmed with sorrow here today? O brahmin sage, please speak freely; I wish to hear your words."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 55 Verse 14. Gautama, with a pleased mind, spoke to the brahmin Utanka: "My dear son, why is your mind so overwhelmed with sorrow here today? O brahmin sage, please speak freely; I wish to hear your words."

sanskrit: उत्तङ्क उवाच ।भवद्गतेन मनसा भवत्प्रियचिकीर्षया ।भवद्भक्तिगतेनेह भवद्भावानुगेन च ॥१५॥
transliteration: uttaṅka uvāca ,bhavadgatena manasā bhavatpriyacikīrṣayā ,bhavadbhaktigateneha bhavadbhāvānugena ca.
english: Utanka said: "With a mind wholly devoted to you, out of a desire to do what is pleasing to you, with my mind immersed in devotion (bhakti) to you, and always following your disposition..."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 55 Verse 15. Utanka said: "With a mind wholly devoted to you, out of a desire to do what is pleasing to you, with my mind immersed in devotion (bhakti) to you, and always following your disposition..."

sanskrit: जरेयं नावबुद्धा मे नाभिज्ञातं सुखं च मे ।शतवर्षोषितं हि त्वं न मामभ्यनुजानथाः ॥१६॥
transliteration: jareyaṁ nāvabuddhā me nābhijñātaṁ sukhaṁ ca me ,śatavarṣoṣitaṁ hi tvaṁ na māmabhyanujānathāḥ.
english: “I did not perceive this old age upon myself, nor did I experience any happiness. For you, though having resided here for a hundred years, have not yet permitted me (to depart)."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 55 Verse 16. “I did not perceive this old age upon myself, nor did I experience any happiness. For you, though having resided here for a hundred years, have not yet permitted me (to depart)."

sanskrit: भवता ह्यभ्यनुज्ञाताः शिष्याः प्रत्यवरा मया ।उपपन्ना द्विजश्रेष्ठ शतशोऽथ सहस्रशः ॥१७॥
transliteration: bhavatā hyabhyanujñātāḥ śiṣyāḥ pratyavarā mayā ,upapannā dvijaśreṣṭha śataśo'tha sahasraśaḥ.
english: “Indeed, O best of brahmins, disciples junior to me have been permitted by you, and hundreds, even thousands, have come (to you and completed their studies)."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 55 Verse 17. “Indeed, O best of brahmins, disciples junior to me have been permitted by you, and hundreds, even thousands, have come (to you and completed their studies)."

sanskrit: गौतम उवाच ।त्वत्प्रीतियुक्तेन मया गुरुशुश्रूषया तव ।व्यतिक्रामन्महान्कालो नावबुद्धो द्विजर्षभ ॥१८॥
transliteration: gautama uvāca ,tvatprītiyuktena mayā guruśuśrūṣayā tava ,vyatikrāmanmahānkālo nāvabuddho dvijarṣabha.
english: Gautama said: "O best among the twice-born, a long period of time, spent by me in devotion and service to you, has passed unnoticed."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 55 Verse 18. Gautama said: "O best among the twice-born, a long period of time, spent by me in devotion and service to you, has passed unnoticed."

sanskrit: किं त्वद्य यदि ते श्रद्धा गमनं प्रति भार्गव ।अनुज्ञां गृह्य मत्तस्त्वं गृहान्गच्छस्व मा चिरम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: kiṁ tvadya yadi te śraddhā gamanaṁ prati bhārgava ,anujñāṁ gṛhya mattastvaṁ gṛhāngacchasva mā ciram.
english: But now, O Bhargava, if you wish to depart, then take my permission and go home without delay.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 55 Verse 19. But now, O Bhargava, if you wish to depart, then take my permission and go home without delay.

sanskrit: उत्तङ्क उवाच ।गुर्वर्थं कं प्रयच्छामि ब्रूहि त्वं द्विजसत्तम ।तमुपाकृत्य गच्छेयमनुज्ञातस्त्वया विभो ॥२०॥
transliteration: uttaṅka uvāca ,gurvarthaṁ kaṁ prayacchāmi brūhi tvaṁ dvijasattama ,tamupākṛtya gaccheyamanujñātastvayā vibho.
english: Uttanka said: "O best among Brahmins, O mighty one, tell me what I should offer as a teacher's fee. Having presented that, I shall depart, having been permitted by you."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 55 Verse 20. Uttanka said: "O best among Brahmins, O mighty one, tell me what I should offer as a teacher's fee. Having presented that, I shall depart, having been permitted by you."

sanskrit: गौतम उवाच ।दक्षिणा परितोषो वै गुरूणां सद्भिरुच्यते ।तव ह्याचरतो ब्रह्मंस्तुष्टोऽहं वै न संशयः ॥२१॥
transliteration: gautama uvāca ,dakṣiṇā paritoṣo vai gurūṇāṁ sadbhirucyate ,tava hyācarato brahmaṁstuṣṭo'haṁ vai na saṁśayaḥ.
english: Gautama said: "Indeed, the satisfaction of teachers is declared by the virtuous to be the proper fee (dakṣiṇā). I am certainly pleased with your conduct, O Brahmin; there is no doubt about it."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 55 Verse 21. Gautama said: "Indeed, the satisfaction of teachers is declared by the virtuous to be the proper fee (dakṣiṇā). I am certainly pleased with your conduct, O Brahmin; there is no doubt about it."

sanskrit: इत्थं च परितुष्टं मां विजानीहि भृगूद्वह ।युवा षोडशवर्षो हि यदद्य भविता भवान् ॥२२॥
transliteration: itthaṁ ca parituṣṭaṁ māṁ vijānīhi bhṛgūdvaha ,yuvā ṣoḍaśavarṣo hi yadadya bhavitā bhavān.
english: O descendant of Bhṛgu, know that I am thus completely satisfied, for today you shall become a youth of sixteen years.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 55 Verse 22. O descendant of Bhṛgu, know that I am thus completely satisfied, for today you shall become a youth of sixteen years.

sanskrit: ददामि पत्नीं कन्यां च स्वां ते दुहितरं द्विज ।एतामृते हि नान्या वै त्वत्तेजोऽर्हति सेवितुम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: dadāmi patnīṁ kanyāṁ ca svāṁ te duhitaraṁ dvija ,etāmṛte hi nānyā vai tvattejo'rhati sevitum.
english: O twice-born (dvija), I give you my own daughter, a maiden, to be your wife. For truly, no other woman but her is worthy to attend to your splendor.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 55 Verse 23. O twice-born (dvija), I give you my own daughter, a maiden, to be your wife. For truly, no other woman but her is worthy to attend to your splendor.

sanskrit: ततस्तां प्रतिजग्राह युवा भूत्वा यशस्विनीम् ।गुरुणा चाभ्यनुज्ञातो गुरुपत्नीमथाब्रवीत् ॥२४॥
transliteration: tatastāṁ pratijagrāha yuvā bhūtvā yaśasvinīm ,guruṇā cābhyanujñāto gurupatnīmathābravīt.
english: Then, having transformed into a youth, he accepted that renowned maiden. And having been granted permission by his spiritual teacher (guru), he then spoke to the spiritual teacher's (guru) wife.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 55 Verse 24. Then, having transformed into a youth, he accepted that renowned maiden. And having been granted permission by his spiritual teacher (guru), he then spoke to the spiritual teacher's (guru) wife.

sanskrit: किं भवत्यै प्रयच्छामि गुर्वर्थं विनियुङ्क्ष्व माम् ।प्रियं हि तव काङ्क्षामि प्राणैरपि धनैरपि ॥२५॥
transliteration: kiṁ bhavatyai prayacchāmi gurvarthaṁ viniyuṅkṣva mām ,priyaṁ hi tava kāṅkṣāmi prāṇairapi dhanairapi.
english: What shall I offer to you for the sake of the spiritual teacher (guru)? Please employ me. For truly, I desire what is pleasing to you, even at the cost of my life and wealth.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 55 Verse 25. What shall I offer to you for the sake of the spiritual teacher (guru)? Please employ me. For truly, I desire what is pleasing to you, even at the cost of my life and wealth.

sanskrit: यद्दुर्लभं हि लोकेऽस्मिन्रत्नमत्यद्भुतं भवेत् ।तदानयेयं तपसा न हि मेऽत्रास्ति संशयः ॥२६॥
transliteration: yaddurlabhaṁ hi loke'sminratnamatyadbhutaṁ bhavet ,tadānayeyaṁ tapasā na hi me'trāsti saṁśayaḥ.
english: Whatever rare and truly extraordinary jewel may exist in this world, I can surely bring that through my spiritual discipline (tapas). There is no doubt about this for me.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 55 Verse 26. Whatever rare and truly extraordinary jewel may exist in this world, I can surely bring that through my spiritual discipline (tapas). There is no doubt about this for me.

sanskrit: अहल्योवाच ।परितुष्टास्मि ते पुत्र नित्यं भगवता सह ।पर्याप्तये तद्भद्रं ते गच्छ तात यथेच्छकम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: ahalyovāca ,parituṣṭāsmi te putra nityaṁ bhagavatā saha ,paryāptaye tadbhadraṁ te gaccha tāta yathecchakam.
english: Ahalya said: "O son, I am fully pleased with you, who are ever associated with the divine (Bhagavat). May good fortune be yours. Go, my dear child, as you wish."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 55 Verse 27. Ahalya said: "O son, I am fully pleased with you, who are ever associated with the divine (Bhagavat). May good fortune be yours. Go, my dear child, as you wish."

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।उत्तङ्कस्तु महाराज पुनरेवाब्रवीद्वचः ।आज्ञापयस्व मां मातः कर्तव्यं हि प्रियं तव ॥२८॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,uttaṅkastu mahārāja punarevābravīdvacaḥ ,ājñāpayasva māṁ mātaḥ kartavyaṁ hi priyaṁ tava.
english: Vaiśampayana said: "O great king, Utanka then spoke again, saying: 'Command me, O mother. Indeed, I must fulfill your cherished wish.'"
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 55 Verse 28. Vaiśampayana said: "O great king, Utanka then spoke again, saying: 'Command me, O mother. Indeed, I must fulfill your cherished wish.'"

sanskrit: अहल्योवाच ।सौदासपत्न्या विदिते दिव्ये वै मणिकुण्डले ।ते समानय भद्रं ते गुर्वर्थः सुकृतो भवेत् ॥२९॥
transliteration: ahalyovāca ,saudāsapatnyā vidite divye vai maṇikuṇḍale ,te samānaya bhadraṁ te gurvarthaḥ sukṛto bhavet.
english: Ahalya said: "Bring those two divine jeweled earrings that are known to belong to Saudasa's wife. May good fortune be with you. In this way, your guru's purpose (gurvartha) will be well fulfilled."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 55 Verse 29. Ahalya said: "Bring those two divine jeweled earrings that are known to belong to Saudasa's wife. May good fortune be with you. In this way, your guru's purpose (gurvartha) will be well fulfilled."

sanskrit: स तथेति प्रतिश्रुत्य जगाम जनमेजय ।गुरुपत्नीप्रियार्थं वै ते समानयितुं तदा ॥३०॥
transliteration: sa tatheti pratiśrutya jagāma janamejaya ,gurupatnīpriyārthaṁ vai te samānayituṁ tadā.
english: Saying, 'So be it,' and having made that promise, he went forth, O Janamejaya, to fetch them then for the pleasure of his guru's wife.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 55 Verse 30. Saying, 'So be it,' and having made that promise, he went forth, O Janamejaya, to fetch them then for the pleasure of his guru's wife.

sanskrit: स जगाम ततः शीघ्रमुत्तङ्को ब्राह्मणर्षभः ।सौदासं पुरुषादं वै भिक्षितुं मणिकुण्डले ॥३१॥
transliteration: sa jagāma tataḥ śīghramuttaṅko brāhmaṇarṣabhaḥ ,saudāsaṁ puruṣādaṁ vai bhikṣituṁ maṇikuṇḍale.
english: That excellent Brahmin (brāhmaṇarṣabha) Uttanka then quickly went to Soudasa, who was a man-eater, to beg for the jeweled earrings.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 55 Verse 31. That excellent Brahmin (brāhmaṇarṣabha) Uttanka then quickly went to Soudasa, who was a man-eater, to beg for the jeweled earrings.

sanskrit: गौतमस्त्वब्रवीत्पत्नीमुत्तङ्को नाद्य दृश्यते ।इति पृष्टा तमाचष्ट कुण्डलार्थं गतं तु वै ॥३२॥
transliteration: gautamastvabravītpatnīmuttaṅko nādya dṛśyate ,iti pṛṣṭā tamācaṣṭa kuṇḍalārthaṁ gataṁ tu vai.
english: Gautama then said to his wife, 'Uttanka is not seen today.' When questioned this way, she indeed informed him that he had gone for the sake of the earrings.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 55 Verse 32. Gautama then said to his wife, 'Uttanka is not seen today.' When questioned this way, she indeed informed him that he had gone for the sake of the earrings.

sanskrit: ततः प्रोवाच पत्नीं स न ते सम्यगिदं कृतम् ।शप्तः स पार्थिवो नूनं ब्राह्मणं तं वधिष्यति ॥३३॥
transliteration: tataḥ provāca patnīṁ sa na te samyagidaṁ kṛtam ,śaptaḥ sa pārthivo nūnaṁ brāhmaṇaṁ taṁ vadhiṣyati.
english: Then he (Gautama) said to his wife, 'This was not done properly by you. That king, being cursed, will certainly kill that Brahmin.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 55 Verse 33. Then he (Gautama) said to his wife, 'This was not done properly by you. That king, being cursed, will certainly kill that Brahmin.'

sanskrit: अहल्योवाच ।अजानन्त्या नियुक्तः स भगवन्ब्राह्मणोऽद्य मे ।भवत्प्रसादान्न भयं किंचित्तस्य भविष्यति ॥३४॥
transliteration: ahalyovāca ,ajānantyā niyuktaḥ sa bhagavanbrāhmaṇo'dya me ,bhavatprasādānna bhayaṁ kiṁcittasya bhaviṣyati.
english: Ahalya said: "O revered one (Gautama), that brahmin was engaged by me today unknowingly. By your grace, no fear whatsoever will come to him."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 55 Verse 34. Ahalya said: "O revered one (Gautama), that brahmin was engaged by me today unknowingly. By your grace, no fear whatsoever will come to him."

sanskrit: इत्युक्तः प्राह तां पत्नीमेवमस्त्विति गौतमः ।उत्तङ्कोऽपि वने शून्ये राजानं तं ददर्श ह ॥३५॥
transliteration: ityuktaḥ prāha tāṁ patnīmevamastviti gautamaḥ ,uttaṅko'pi vane śūnye rājānaṁ taṁ dadarśa ha.
english: Gautama, thus addressed, said to his wife, "So be it." Uttanka also saw that king in a deserted forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 55 Verse 35. Gautama, thus addressed, said to his wife, "So be it." Uttanka also saw that king in a deserted forest.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।स तं दृष्ट्वा तथाभूतं राजानं घोरदर्शनम् ।दीर्घश्मश्रुधरं नॄणां शोणितेन समुक्षितम् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,sa taṁ dṛṣṭvā tathābhūtaṁ rājānaṁ ghoradarśanam ,dīrghaśmaśrudharaṁ nṝṇāṁ śoṇitena samukṣitam.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having seen that king - who was in such a state, of terrible appearance, wearing long beards, and smeared with the blood of men - (Uttanka...).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 56 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Having seen that king - who was in such a state, of terrible appearance, wearing long beards, and smeared with the blood of men - (Uttanka...).

sanskrit: चकार न व्यथां विप्रो राजा त्वेनमथाब्रवीत् ।प्रत्युत्थाय महातेजा भयकर्ता यमोपमः ॥२॥
transliteration: cakāra na vyathāṁ vipro rājā tvenamathābravīt ,pratyutthāya mahātejā bhayakartā yamopamaḥ.
english: ...the brahmin (Uttanka) did not feel any distress. But the king, who was exceedingly powerful, fear-inducing, and like Yama, rose up and then spoke to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 56 Verse 2. ...the brahmin (Uttanka) did not feel any distress. But the king, who was exceedingly powerful, fear-inducing, and like Yama, rose up and then spoke to him.

sanskrit: दिष्ट्या त्वमसि कल्याण षष्ठे काले ममान्तिकम् ।भक्षं मृगयमाणस्य संप्राप्तो द्विजसत्तम ॥३॥
transliteration: diṣṭyā tvamasi kalyāṇa ṣaṣṭhe kāle mamāntikam ,bhakṣaṁ mṛgayamāṇasya saṁprāpto dvijasattama.
english: Fortunately, O blessed one, you have arrived before me at my sixth mealtime, as I was searching for food, O best among the twice-born.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 56 Verse 3. Fortunately, O blessed one, you have arrived before me at my sixth mealtime, as I was searching for food, O best among the twice-born.

sanskrit: उत्तङ्क उवाच ।राजन्गुर्वर्थिनं विद्धि चरन्तं मामिहागतम् ।न च गुर्वर्थमुद्युक्तं हिंस्यमाहुर्मनीषिणः ॥४॥
transliteration: uttaṅka uvāca ,rājangurvarthinaṁ viddhi carantaṁ māmihāgatam ,na ca gurvarthamudyuktaṁ hiṁsyamāhurmanīṣiṇaḥ.
english: Uttaṅka said: 'O King, know that I have arrived here, having wandered for my guru's (guru) purpose. Furthermore, the wise declare that one engaged in the guru's (guru) mission should not be harmed.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 56 Verse 4. Uttaṅka said: 'O King, know that I have arrived here, having wandered for my guru's (guru) purpose. Furthermore, the wise declare that one engaged in the guru's (guru) mission should not be harmed.'

sanskrit: राजोवाच ।षष्ठे काले ममाहारो विहितो द्विजसत्तम ।न च शक्यः समुत्स्रष्टुं क्षुधितेन मयाद्य वै ॥५॥
transliteration: rājovāca ,ṣaṣṭhe kāle mamāhāro vihito dvijasattama ,na ca śakyaḥ samutsraṣṭuṁ kṣudhitena mayādya vai.
english: The King said: 'O best among the twice-born, my meal is prescribed for the sixth hour. It is indeed impossible for me, being hungry, to give it up today.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 56 Verse 5. The King said: 'O best among the twice-born, my meal is prescribed for the sixth hour. It is indeed impossible for me, being hungry, to give it up today.'

sanskrit: उत्तङ्क उवाच ।एवमस्तु महाराज समयः क्रियतां तु मे ।गुर्वर्थमभिनिर्वर्त्य पुनरेष्यामि ते वशम् ॥६॥
transliteration: uttaṅka uvāca ,evamastu mahārāja samayaḥ kriyatāṁ tu me ,gurvarthamabhinirvartya punareṣyāmi te vaśam.
english: Uttaṅka said: 'So be it, O great King. But let an agreement be made for me: having accomplished my guru's (guru) purpose, I will return under your authority.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 56 Verse 6. Uttaṅka said: 'So be it, O great King. But let an agreement be made for me: having accomplished my guru's (guru) purpose, I will return under your authority.'

sanskrit: संश्रुतश्च मया योऽर्थो गुरवे राजसत्तम ।त्वदधीनः स राजेन्द्र तं त्वा भिक्षे नरेश्वर ॥७॥
transliteration: saṁśrutaśca mayā yo'rtho gurave rājasattama ,tvadadhīnaḥ sa rājendra taṁ tvā bhikṣe nareśvara.
english: O best of kings, the task that I promised to my teacher, that very task, O king of kings, is dependent on you. O lord of men, I beg you for that.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 56 Verse 7. O best of kings, the task that I promised to my teacher, that very task, O king of kings, is dependent on you. O lord of men, I beg you for that.

sanskrit: ददासि विप्रमुख्येभ्यस्त्वं हि रत्नानि सर्वशः ।दाता त्वं च नरव्याघ्र पात्रभूतः क्षिताविह ।पात्रं प्रतिग्रहे चापि विद्धि मां नृपसत्तम ॥८॥
transliteration: dadāsi vipramukhyebhyastvaṁ hi ratnāni sarvaśaḥ ,dātā tvaṁ ca naravyāghra pātrabhūtaḥ kṣitāviha ,pātraṁ pratigrahe cāpi viddhi māṁ nṛpasattama.
english: Indeed, you always bestow jewels upon leading Brahmins. You, O tiger among men, are a great giver, and here on earth, you yourself are worthy of receiving. Know me too, O best of kings, as a fitting recipient for this acceptance.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 56 Verse 8. Indeed, you always bestow jewels upon leading Brahmins. You, O tiger among men, are a great giver, and here on earth, you yourself are worthy of receiving. Know me too, O best of kings, as a fitting recipient for this acceptance.

sanskrit: उपाकृत्य गुरोरर्थं त्वदायत्तमरिंदम ।समयेनेह राजेन्द्र पुनरेष्यामि ते वशम् ॥९॥
transliteration: upākṛtya gurorarthaṁ tvadāyattamariṁdama ,samayeneha rājendra punareṣyāmi te vaśam.
english: O subduer of enemies, having fulfilled the teacher's task - which is dependent on you - I will return here, O king of kings, again to your authority at the proper time.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 56 Verse 9. O subduer of enemies, having fulfilled the teacher's task - which is dependent on you - I will return here, O king of kings, again to your authority at the proper time.

sanskrit: सत्यं ते प्रतिजानामि नात्र मिथ्यास्ति किंचन ।अनृतं नोक्तपूर्वं मे स्वैरेष्वपि कुतोऽन्यथा ॥१०॥
transliteration: satyaṁ te pratijānāmi nātra mithyāsti kiṁcana ,anṛtaṁ noktapūrvaṁ me svaireṣvapi kuto'nyathā.
english: I truly promise this to you; there is absolutely no falsehood here. No untruth has ever been spoken by me, not even in jest; how then could it be otherwise (in a serious matter)?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 56 Verse 10. I truly promise this to you; there is absolutely no falsehood here. No untruth has ever been spoken by me, not even in jest; how then could it be otherwise (in a serious matter)?

sanskrit: सौदास उवाच ।यदि मत्तस्त्वदायत्तो गुर्वर्थः कृत एव सः ।यदि चास्मि प्रतिग्राह्यः सांप्रतं तद्ब्रवीहि मे ॥११॥
transliteration: saudāsa uvāca ,yadi mattastvadāyatto gurvarthaḥ kṛta eva saḥ ,yadi cāsmi pratigrāhyaḥ sāṁprataṁ tadbravīhi me.
english: Saudāsa said: "If the guru's (guru) purpose, which depends on you, has indeed been accomplished by me, and if I am now deemed worthy of your acceptance, then tell me that."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 56 Verse 11. Saudāsa said: "If the guru's (guru) purpose, which depends on you, has indeed been accomplished by me, and if I am now deemed worthy of your acceptance, then tell me that."

sanskrit: उत्तङ्क उवाच ।प्रतिग्राह्यो मतो मे त्वं सदैव पुरुषर्षभ ।सोऽहं त्वामनुसंप्राप्तो भिक्षितुं मणिकुण्डले ॥१२॥
transliteration: uttaṅka uvāca ,pratigrāhyo mato me tvaṁ sadaiva puruṣarṣabha ,so'haṁ tvāmanusaṁprāpto bhikṣituṁ maṇikuṇḍale.
english: Uttanka said: "O best among men, you are always considered worthy of my acceptance. Therefore, I have approached you to beg for the jeweled earrings."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 56 Verse 12. Uttanka said: "O best among men, you are always considered worthy of my acceptance. Therefore, I have approached you to beg for the jeweled earrings."

sanskrit: सौदास उवाच ।पत्न्यास्ते मम विप्रर्षे रुचिरे मणिकुण्डले ।वरयार्थं त्वमन्यं वै तं ते दास्यामि सुव्रत ॥१३॥
transliteration: saudāsa uvāca ,patnyāste mama viprarṣe rucire maṇikuṇḍale ,varayārthaṁ tvamanyaṁ vai taṁ te dāsyāmi suvrata.
english: Saudāsa said: "O sage (viprarṣi), those beautiful jeweled earrings belong to my wife. Ask for something else, O man of excellent vows (suvrata); I will certainly give that to you."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 56 Verse 13. Saudāsa said: "O sage (viprarṣi), those beautiful jeweled earrings belong to my wife. Ask for something else, O man of excellent vows (suvrata); I will certainly give that to you."

sanskrit: उत्तङ्क उवाच ।अलं ते व्यपदेशेन प्रमाणं यदि ते वयम् ।प्रयच्छ कुण्डले मे त्वं सत्यवाग्भव पार्थिव ॥१४॥
transliteration: uttaṅka uvāca ,alaṁ te vyapadeśena pramāṇaṁ yadi te vayam ,prayaccha kuṇḍale me tvaṁ satyavāgbhava pārthiva.
english: Uttanka said: "Enough with your excuses! If we (my guru's authority via me) are a recognized authority to you, then give me the earrings. Be truthful, O king (pārthiva)."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 56 Verse 14. Uttanka said: "Enough with your excuses! If we (my guru's authority via me) are a recognized authority to you, then give me the earrings. Be truthful, O king (pārthiva)."

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।इत्युक्तस्त्वब्रवीद्राजा तमुत्तङ्कं पुनर्वचः ।गच्छ मद्वचनाद्देवीं ब्रूहि देहीति सत्तम ॥१५॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,ityuktastvabravīdrājā tamuttaṅkaṁ punarvacaḥ ,gaccha madvacanāddevīṁ brūhi dehīti sattama.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Thus addressed, the king then spoke again to Uttanka, saying, "Go, at my word, and tell the goddess, 'Give them (the earrings)!' O best among men!"
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 56 Verse 15. Vaiśampāyana said: Thus addressed, the king then spoke again to Uttanka, saying, "Go, at my word, and tell the goddess, 'Give them (the earrings)!' O best among men!"

sanskrit: सैवमुक्ता त्वया नूनं मद्वाक्येन शुचिस्मिता ।प्रदास्यति द्विजश्रेष्ठ कुण्डले ते न संशयः ॥१६॥
transliteration: saivamuktā tvayā nūnaṁ madvākyena śucismitā ,pradāsyati dvijaśreṣṭha kuṇḍale te na saṁśayaḥ.
english: When she is thus addressed by you, by my word, that pure-smiling one will certainly give you the two earrings, O best among the twice-born (dvija)! There is no doubt about it.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 56 Verse 16. When she is thus addressed by you, by my word, that pure-smiling one will certainly give you the two earrings, O best among the twice-born (dvija)! There is no doubt about it.

sanskrit: उत्तङ्क उवाच ।क्व पत्नी भवतः शक्या मया द्रष्टुं नरेश्वर ।स्वयं वापि भवान्पत्नीं किमर्थं नोपसर्पति ॥१७॥
transliteration: uttaṅka uvāca ,kva patnī bhavataḥ śakyā mayā draṣṭuṁ nareśvara ,svayaṁ vāpi bhavānpatnīṁ kimarthaṁ nopasarpati.
english: Uttanka said: "O lord of men (nareśvara), where can your wife be seen by me? Or why do you yourself not approach your wife?"
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 56 Verse 17. Uttanka said: "O lord of men (nareśvara), where can your wife be seen by me? Or why do you yourself not approach your wife?"

sanskrit: सौदास उवाच ।द्रक्ष्यते तां भवानद्य कस्मिंश्चिद्वननिर्झरे ।षष्ठे काले न हि मया सा शक्या द्रष्टुमद्य वै ॥१८॥
transliteration: saudāsa uvāca ,drakṣyate tāṁ bhavānadya kasmiṁścidvananirjhare ,ṣaṣṭhe kāle na hi mayā sā śakyā draṣṭumadya vai.
english: Saudāsa said: "You will see her today in some forest waterfall. Indeed, she cannot be seen by me now during the sixth period (of the day/night)."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 56 Verse 18. Saudāsa said: "You will see her today in some forest waterfall. Indeed, she cannot be seen by me now during the sixth period (of the day/night)."

sanskrit: उत्तङ्कस्तु तथोक्तः स जगाम भरतर्षभ ।मदयन्तीं च दृष्ट्वा सोऽज्ञापयत्स्वं प्रयोजनम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: uttaṅkastu tathoktaḥ sa jagāma bharatarṣabha ,madayantīṁ ca dṛṣṭvā so'jñāpayatsvaṁ prayojanam.
english: O best among the Bharatas, Uttanka, thus spoken to, went and, having seen Madayanti, conveyed his purpose to her.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 56 Verse 19. O best among the Bharatas, Uttanka, thus spoken to, went and, having seen Madayanti, conveyed his purpose to her.
