sanskrit: सौदासवचनं श्रुत्वा ततः सा पृथुलोचना ।प्रत्युवाच महाबुद्धिमुत्तङ्कं जनमेजय ॥२०॥
transliteration: saudāsavacanaṁ śrutvā tataḥ sā pṛthulocanā ,pratyuvāca mahābuddhimuttaṅkaṁ janamejaya.
english: O Janamejaya, having heard Saudasa's words, that wide-eyed lady then replied to the highly intelligent Uttanka.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 56 Verse 20. O Janamejaya, having heard Saudasa's words, that wide-eyed lady then replied to the highly intelligent Uttanka.

sanskrit: एवमेतन्महाब्रह्मन्नानृतं वदसेऽनघ ।अभिज्ञानं तु किंचित्त्वं समानेतुमिहार्हसि ॥२१॥
transliteration: evametanmahābrahmannānṛtaṁ vadase'nagha ,abhijñānaṁ tu kiṁcittvaṁ samānetumihārhasi.
english: O great brahmin (brahman), O sinless one, what you say is not untrue. However, you should bring some token of recognition here.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 56 Verse 21. O great brahmin (brahman), O sinless one, what you say is not untrue. However, you should bring some token of recognition here.

sanskrit: इमे हि दिव्ये मणिकुण्डले मे देवाश्च यक्षाश्च महोरगाश्च ।तैस्तैरुपायैः परिहर्तुकामाश्छिद्रेषु नित्यं परितर्कयन्ति ॥२२॥
transliteration: ime hi divye maṇikuṇḍale me; devāśca yakṣāśca mahoragāśca ,taistairupāyaiḥ parihartukāmā;śchidreṣu nityaṁ paritarkayanti.
english: Indeed, these divine jeweled earrings belong to me, and gods, Yakshas, and great serpents, desiring to carry them away by various means, are constantly watching for vulnerabilities.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 56 Verse 22. Indeed, these divine jeweled earrings belong to me, and gods, Yakshas, and great serpents, desiring to carry them away by various means, are constantly watching for vulnerabilities.

sanskrit: निक्षिप्तमेतद्भुवि पन्नगास्तु रत्नं समासाद्य परामृषेयुः ।यक्षास्तथोच्छिष्टधृतं सुराश्च निद्रावशं त्वा परिधर्षयेयुः ॥२३॥
transliteration: nikṣiptametadbhuvi pannagāstu; ratnaṁ samāsādya parāmṛṣeyuḥ ,yakṣāstathocchiṣṭadhṛtaṁ surāśca; nidrāvaśaṁ tvā paridharṣayeyuḥ.
english: If this were cast upon the earth, snakes would surely seize the jewel and claim it. Similarly, Yakṣas and gods, deeming it as a mere remnant, would harass you if you were overcome by sleep.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 56 Verse 23. If this were cast upon the earth, snakes would surely seize the jewel and claim it. Similarly, Yakṣas and gods, deeming it as a mere remnant, would harass you if you were overcome by sleep.

sanskrit: छिद्रेष्वेतेषु हि सदा ह्यधृष्येषु द्विजर्षभ ।देवराक्षसनागानामप्रमत्तेन धार्यते ॥२४॥
transliteration: chidreṣveteṣu hi sadā hyadhṛṣyeṣu dvijarṣabha ,devarākṣasanāgānāmapramattena dhāryate.
english: O best among the twice-born (dvija)! In these hidden and always unassailable places, it must be vigilantly guarded against gods, Rākṣasas, and Nāgas.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 56 Verse 24. O best among the twice-born (dvija)! In these hidden and always unassailable places, it must be vigilantly guarded against gods, Rākṣasas, and Nāgas.

sanskrit: स्यन्देते हि दिवा रुक्मं रात्रौ च द्विजसत्तम ।नक्तं नक्षत्रताराणां प्रभामाक्षिप्य वर्तते ॥२५॥
transliteration: syandete hi divā rukmaṁ rātrau ca dvijasattama ,naktaṁ nakṣatratārāṇāṁ prabhāmākṣipya vartate.
english: Indeed, O best among the twice-born (dvija)! The luster flows forth both by day and by night. At night, it shines, eclipsing the radiance of the constellations and stars.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 56 Verse 25. Indeed, O best among the twice-born (dvija)! The luster flows forth both by day and by night. At night, it shines, eclipsing the radiance of the constellations and stars.

sanskrit: एते ह्यामुच्य भगवन्क्षुत्पिपासाभयं कुतः ।विषाग्निश्वापदेभ्यश्च भयं जातु न विद्यते ॥२६॥
transliteration: ete hyāmucya bhagavankṣutpipāsābhayaṁ kutaḥ ,viṣāgniśvāpadebhyaśca bhayaṁ jātu na vidyate.
english: O venerable one! Having worn these, from where could there be fear of hunger and thirst? And from poison, fire, and wild animals, fear never arises.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 56 Verse 26. O venerable one! Having worn these, from where could there be fear of hunger and thirst? And from poison, fire, and wild animals, fear never arises.

sanskrit: ह्रस्वेन चैते आमुक्ते भवतो ह्रस्वके तदा ।अनुरूपेण चामुक्ते तत्प्रमाणे हि जायतः ॥२७॥
transliteration: hrasvena caite āmukte bhavato hrasvake tadā ,anurūpeṇa cāmukte tatpramāṇe hi jāyataḥ.
english: And these (earrings), when worn by a short person, become short then. And when worn by a person of suitable stature, they indeed become of that original size.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 56 Verse 27. And these (earrings), when worn by a short person, become short then. And when worn by a person of suitable stature, they indeed become of that original size.

sanskrit: एवंविधे ममैते वै कुण्डले परमार्चिते ।त्रिषु लोकेषु विख्याते तदभिज्ञानमानय ॥२८॥
transliteration: evaṁvidhe mamaite vai kuṇḍale paramārcite ,triṣu lokeṣu vikhyāte tadabhijñānamānaya.
english: Such are these two highly adorned earrings of mine, indeed, renowned in the three worlds. Bring me that token of recognition (abhijñāna).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 56 Verse 28. Such are these two highly adorned earrings of mine, indeed, renowned in the three worlds. Bring me that token of recognition (abhijñāna).

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।स मित्रसहमासाद्य त्वभिज्ञानमयाचत ।तस्मै ददावभिज्ञानं स चेक्ष्वाकुवरस्तदा ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,sa mitrasahamāsādya tvabhijñānamayācata ,tasmai dadāvabhijñānaṁ sa cekṣvākuvarastadā.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having approached Mitrasaha, he asked for the token of recognition (abhijñāna). And that best of Ikṣvākus then gave the token of recognition to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Having approached Mitrasaha, he asked for the token of recognition (abhijñāna). And that best of Ikṣvākus then gave the token of recognition to him.

sanskrit: सौदास उवाच ।न चैवैषा गतिः क्षेम्या न चान्या विद्यते गतिः ।एतन्मे मतमाज्ञाय प्रयच्छ मणिकुण्डले ॥२॥
transliteration: saudāsa uvāca ,na caivaiṣā gatiḥ kṣemyā na cānyā vidyate gatiḥ ,etanme matamājñāya prayaccha maṇikuṇḍale.
english: Saudāsa said: This course of action is certainly not safe, and no other course is available. Therefore, having understood this opinion of mine, give me the jewel earrings.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 2. Saudāsa said: This course of action is certainly not safe, and no other course is available. Therefore, having understood this opinion of mine, give me the jewel earrings.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।इत्युक्तस्तामुत्तङ्कस्तु भर्तुर्वाक्यमथाब्रवीत् ।श्रुत्वा च सा ततः प्रादात्तस्मै ते मणिकुण्डले ॥३॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,ityuktastāmuttaṅkastu bharturvākyamathābravīt ,śrutvā ca sā tataḥ prādāttasmai te maṇikuṇḍale.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Thus addressed, Uttanka then conveyed his master's command to her. And she, having heard, then gave those jeweled earrings to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 3. Vaiśampāyana said: Thus addressed, Uttanka then conveyed his master's command to her. And she, having heard, then gave those jeweled earrings to him.

sanskrit: अवाप्य कुण्डले ते तु राजानं पुनरब्रवीत् ।किमेतद्गुह्यवचनं श्रोतुमिच्छामि पार्थिव ॥४॥
transliteration: avāpya kuṇḍale te tu rājānaṁ punarabravīt ,kimetadguhyavacanaṁ śrotumicchāmi pārthiva.
english: But having obtained those two earrings, he again spoke to the king: "O King, what is this secret statement? I wish to hear it."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 4. But having obtained those two earrings, he again spoke to the king: "O King, what is this secret statement? I wish to hear it."

sanskrit: सौदास उवाच ।प्रजा निसर्गाद्विप्रान्वै क्षत्रियाः पूजयन्ति ह ।विप्रेभ्यश्चापि बहवो दोषाः प्रादुर्भवन्ति नः ॥५॥
transliteration: saudāsa uvāca ,prajā nisargādviprānvai kṣatriyāḥ pūjayanti ha ,viprebhyaścāpi bahavo doṣāḥ prādurbhavanti naḥ.
english: Saudāsa said: "By nature, the people, particularly kshatriyas, indeed revere the brahmins (vipra). Yet, many faults also arise for us from these very brahmins (vipra)."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 5. Saudāsa said: "By nature, the people, particularly kshatriyas, indeed revere the brahmins (vipra). Yet, many faults also arise for us from these very brahmins (vipra)."

sanskrit: सोऽहं द्विजेभ्यः प्रणतो विप्राद्दोषमवाप्तवान् ।गतिमन्यां न पश्यामि मदयन्तीसहायवान् ।स्वर्गद्वारस्य गमने स्थाने चेह द्विजोत्तम ॥६॥
transliteration: so'haṁ dvijebhyaḥ praṇato viprāddoṣamavāptavān ,gatimanyāṁ na paśyāmi madayantīsahāyavān ,svargadvārasya gamane sthāne ceha dvijottama.
english: "I, who bowed to the brahmins (dvija), have nevertheless incurred a fault from a brahmin (vipra). Having Madayantī as my companion, I see no other recourse, O best of brahmins (dvija), here in this place, on the way to the gate of heaven."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 6. "I, who bowed to the brahmins (dvija), have nevertheless incurred a fault from a brahmin (vipra). Having Madayantī as my companion, I see no other recourse, O best of brahmins (dvija), here in this place, on the way to the gate of heaven."

sanskrit: न हि राज्ञा विशेषेण विरुद्धेन द्विजातिभिः ।शक्यं नृलोके संस्थातुं प्रेत्य वा सुखमेधितुम् ॥७॥
transliteration: na hi rājñā viśeṣeṇa viruddhena dvijātibhiḥ ,śakyaṁ nṛloke saṁsthātuṁ pretya vā sukhamedhitum.
english: Indeed, it is not possible for a king, especially one who is opposed by the twice-born (dvijas), to remain established in the human world or to attain happiness in the afterlife.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 7. Indeed, it is not possible for a king, especially one who is opposed by the twice-born (dvijas), to remain established in the human world or to attain happiness in the afterlife.

sanskrit: तदिष्टे ते मयैवैते दत्ते स्वे मणिकुण्डले ।यः कृतस्तेऽद्य समयः सफलं तं कुरुष्व मे ॥८॥
transliteration: tadiṣṭe te mayaivaite datte sve maṇikuṇḍale ,yaḥ kṛtaste'dya samayaḥ saphalaṁ taṁ kuruṣva me.
english: Therefore, these two jeweled earrings, my own valued possessions, are given by me to you. The agreement (samaya) you made today, make it fruitful for me.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 8. Therefore, these two jeweled earrings, my own valued possessions, are given by me to you. The agreement (samaya) you made today, make it fruitful for me.

sanskrit: उत्तङ्क उवाच ।राजंस्तथेह कर्तास्मि पुनरेष्यामि ते वशम् ।प्रश्नं तु कंचित्प्रष्टुं त्वां व्यवसिष्ये परंतप ॥९॥
transliteration: uttaṅka uvāca ,rājaṁstatheha kartāsmi punareṣyāmi te vaśam ,praśnaṁ tu kaṁcitpraṣṭuṁ tvāṁ vyavasiṣye paraṁtapa.
english: Uttanka said: 'O King, I shall act accordingly here, and I will return to your command. But, O scorcher of foes, I intend to ask you a certain question.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 9. Uttanka said: 'O King, I shall act accordingly here, and I will return to your command. But, O scorcher of foes, I intend to ask you a certain question.'

sanskrit: सौदास उवाच ।ब्रूहि विप्र यथाकामं प्रतिवक्तास्मि ते वचः ।छेत्तास्मि संशयं तेऽद्य न मेऽत्रास्ति विचारणा ॥१०॥
transliteration: saudāsa uvāca ,brūhi vipra yathākāmaṁ prativaktāsmi te vacaḥ ,chettāsmi saṁśayaṁ te'dya na me'trāsti vicāraṇā.
english: Saudasa said: 'Speak, O Brahmin, as you desire! I will answer your question and remove your doubt today. There is no hesitation for me in this matter.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 10. Saudasa said: 'Speak, O Brahmin, as you desire! I will answer your question and remove your doubt today. There is no hesitation for me in this matter.'

sanskrit: उत्तङ्क उवाच ।प्राहुर्वाक्संगतं मित्रं धर्मनैपुण्यदर्शिनः ।मित्रेषु यश्च विषमः स्तेन इत्येव तं विदुः ॥११॥
transliteration: uttaṅka uvāca ,prāhurvāksaṁgataṁ mitraṁ dharmanaipuṇyadarśinaḥ ,mitreṣu yaśca viṣamaḥ stena ityeva taṁ viduḥ.
english: Uttanka said: Those who discern the subtleties of righteousness (dharma) declare that a friend is one whose speech is consistent (with their actions). And whoever behaves unevenly or deceitfully among friends, they indeed know him to be a thief.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 11. Uttanka said: Those who discern the subtleties of righteousness (dharma) declare that a friend is one whose speech is consistent (with their actions). And whoever behaves unevenly or deceitfully among friends, they indeed know him to be a thief.

sanskrit: स भवान्मित्रतामद्य संप्राप्तो मम पार्थिव ।स मे बुद्धिं प्रयच्छस्व समां बुद्धिमतां वर ॥१२॥
transliteration: sa bhavānmitratāmadya saṁprāpto mama pārthiva ,sa me buddhiṁ prayacchasva samāṁ buddhimatāṁ vara.
english: O King (pārthiva), you have now attained my friendship. Therefore, O best among the wise, please grant me an impartial (samām) understanding (buddhi).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 12. O King (pārthiva), you have now attained my friendship. Therefore, O best among the wise, please grant me an impartial (samām) understanding (buddhi).

sanskrit: अवाप्तार्थोऽहमद्येह भवांश्च पुरुषादकः ।भवत्सकाशमागन्तुं क्षमं मम न वेति वा ॥१३॥
transliteration: avāptārtho'hamadyeha bhavāṁśca puruṣādakaḥ ,bhavatsakāśamāgantuṁ kṣamaṁ mama na veti vā.
english: I have accomplished my objective here today, and you are a man-eater. Is it appropriate for me to approach your presence, or not?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 13. I have accomplished my objective here today, and you are a man-eater. Is it appropriate for me to approach your presence, or not?

sanskrit: सौदास उवाच ।क्षमं चेदिह वक्तव्यं मया द्विजवरोत्तम ।मत्समीपं द्विजश्रेष्ठ नागन्तव्यं कथंचन ॥१४॥
transliteration: saudāsa uvāca ,kṣamaṁ cediha vaktavyaṁ mayā dvijavarottama ,matsamīpaṁ dvijaśreṣṭha nāgantavyaṁ kathaṁcana.
english: Saudasa said: If it is appropriate for me to speak here, O most excellent among brahmins, then, O best of brahmins, you must not come near me under any circumstances.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 14. Saudasa said: If it is appropriate for me to speak here, O most excellent among brahmins, then, O best of brahmins, you must not come near me under any circumstances.

sanskrit: एवं तव प्रपश्यामि श्रेयो भृगुकुलोद्वह ।आगच्छतो हि ते विप्र भवेन्मृत्युरसंशयम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: evaṁ tava prapaśyāmi śreyo bhṛgukulodvaha ,āgacchato hi te vipra bhavenmṛtyurasaṁśayam.
english: O scion of the Bhrigu family, I clearly see what is best for you in this situation. Indeed, O Brahmin, if you proceed, death will undoubtedly befall you.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 15. O scion of the Bhrigu family, I clearly see what is best for you in this situation. Indeed, O Brahmin, if you proceed, death will undoubtedly befall you.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।इत्युक्तः स तदा राज्ञा क्षमं बुद्धिमता हितम् ।समनुज्ञाप्य राजानमहल्यां प्रति जग्मिवान् ॥१६॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,ityuktaḥ sa tadā rājñā kṣamaṁ buddhimatā hitam ,samanujñāpya rājānamahalyāṁ prati jagmivān.
english: Vaiśampayana said: Thus addressed then by the wise king with fitting and beneficial words, he took leave of the king and departed towards Ahalya.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 16. Vaiśampayana said: Thus addressed then by the wise king with fitting and beneficial words, he took leave of the king and departed towards Ahalya.

sanskrit: गृहीत्वा कुण्डले दिव्ये गुरुपत्न्याः प्रियंकरः ।जवेन महता प्रायाद्गौतमस्याश्रमं प्रति ॥१७॥
transliteration: gṛhītvā kuṇḍale divye gurupatnyāḥ priyaṁkaraḥ ,javena mahatā prāyādgautamasyāśramaṁ prati.
english: Pleasing to the guru's wife, he took her two divine earrings and departed with great speed towards Gautama's hermitage (āśrama).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 17. Pleasing to the guru's wife, he took her two divine earrings and departed with great speed towards Gautama's hermitage (āśrama).

sanskrit: यथा तयो रक्षणं च मदयन्त्याभिभाषितम् ।तथा ते कुण्डले बद्ध्वा तथा कृष्णाजिनेऽनयत् ॥१८॥
transliteration: yathā tayo rakṣaṇaṁ ca madayantyābhibhāṣitam ,tathā te kuṇḍale baddhvā tathā kṛṣṇājine'nayat.
english: Following Madayantī's instructions regarding the protection of those two, he bound the earrings to a black antelope skin and carried them.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 18. Following Madayantī's instructions regarding the protection of those two, he bound the earrings to a black antelope skin and carried them.

sanskrit: स कस्मिंश्चित्क्षुधाविष्टः फलभारसमन्वितम् ।बिल्वं ददर्श कस्मिंश्चिदारुरोह क्षुधान्वितः ॥१९॥
transliteration: sa kasmiṁścitkṣudhāviṣṭaḥ phalabhārasamanvitam ,bilvaṁ dadarśa kasmiṁścidāruroha kṣudhānvitaḥ.
english: Being afflicted by hunger, he saw a bilva tree somewhere, laden with fruit. Driven by hunger, he then climbed it.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 19. Being afflicted by hunger, he saw a bilva tree somewhere, laden with fruit. Driven by hunger, he then climbed it.

sanskrit: शाखास्वासज्य तस्यैव कृष्णाजिनमरिंदम ।यस्मिंस्ते कुण्डले बद्धे तदा द्विजवरेण वै ॥२०॥
transliteration: śākhāsvāsajya tasyaiva kṛṣṇājinamariṁdama ,yasmiṁste kuṇḍale baddhe tadā dvijavareṇa vai.
english: O subduer of enemies (arim-dama), he hung his black deerskin (kṛṣṇājina) on the branches of that very tree. Indeed, in that deerskin (kṛṣṇājina), those two earrings had been tied by the revered brahmin (dvija).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 20. O subduer of enemies (arim-dama), he hung his black deerskin (kṛṣṇājina) on the branches of that very tree. Indeed, in that deerskin (kṛṣṇājina), those two earrings had been tied by the revered brahmin (dvija).

sanskrit: विशीर्णबन्धने तस्मिन्गते कृष्णाजिने महीम् ।अपश्यद्भुजगः कश्चित्ते तत्र मणिकुण्डले ॥२१॥
transliteration: viśīrṇabandhane tasmingate kṛṣṇājine mahīm ,apaśyadbhujagaḥ kaścitte tatra maṇikuṇḍale.
english: When that black deerskin (kṛṣṇājina), its fastening having become loose, fell to the ground, a certain snake saw those two gem-studded earrings there.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 21. When that black deerskin (kṛṣṇājina), its fastening having become loose, fell to the ground, a certain snake saw those two gem-studded earrings there.

sanskrit: ऐरावतकुलोत्पन्नः शीघ्रो भूत्वा तदा स वै ।विदश्यास्येन वल्मीकं विवेशाथ स कुण्डले ॥२२॥
transliteration: airāvatakulotpannaḥ śīghro bhūtvā tadā sa vai ,vidaśyāsyena valmīkaṁ viveśātha sa kuṇḍale.
english: That snake, born in the lineage of Airāvata, then swiftly, indeed, tore open an anthill with its mouth. Then it entered (the anthill) with those two earrings.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 22. That snake, born in the lineage of Airāvata, then swiftly, indeed, tore open an anthill with its mouth. Then it entered (the anthill) with those two earrings.

sanskrit: ह्रियमाणे तु दृष्ट्वा स कुण्डले भुजगेन ह ।पपात वृक्षात्सोद्वेगो दुःखात्परमकोपनः ॥२३॥
transliteration: hriyamāṇe tu dṛṣṭvā sa kuṇḍale bhujagena ha ,papāta vṛkṣātsodvego duḥkhātparamakopanaḥ.
english: When he saw the two earrings being carried away by the serpent, he fell from the tree, agitated by grief and extremely furious.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 23. When he saw the two earrings being carried away by the serpent, he fell from the tree, agitated by grief and extremely furious.

sanskrit: स दण्डकाष्ठमादाय वल्मीकमखनत्तदा ।क्रोधामर्षाभितप्ताङ्गस्ततो वै द्विजपुंगवः ॥२४॥
transliteration: sa daṇḍakāṣṭhamādāya valmīkamakhanattadā ,krodhāmarṣābhitaptāṅgastato vai dvijapuṁgavaḥ.
english: Then, that excellent brahmin (dvijapuṅgava), his body scorched by anger and indignation, took a stick and began to dig into the anthill.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 24. Then, that excellent brahmin (dvijapuṅgava), his body scorched by anger and indignation, took a stick and began to dig into the anthill.

sanskrit: तस्य वेगमसह्यं तमसहन्ती वसुंधरा ।दण्डकाष्ठाभिनुन्नाङ्गी चचाल भृशमातुरा ॥२५॥
transliteration: tasya vegamasahyaṁ tamasahantī vasuṁdharā ,daṇḍakāṣṭhābhinunnāṅgī cacāla bhṛśamāturā.
english: The Earth (vasundharā), unable to endure that unbearable force of his, her body deeply affected by the stick, trembled greatly, in great distress.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 25. The Earth (vasundharā), unable to endure that unbearable force of his, her body deeply affected by the stick, trembled greatly, in great distress.

sanskrit: ततः खनत एवाथ विप्रर्षेर्धरणीतलम् ।नागलोकस्य पन्थानं कर्तुकामस्य निश्चयात् ॥२६॥
transliteration: tataḥ khanata evātha viprarṣerdharaṇītalam ,nāgalokasya panthānaṁ kartukāmasya niścayāt.
english: Then, as that brahmin-sage (viprarṣi) continued to dig into the surface of the earth, he was resolved to create a path to the serpent realm (nāgaloka).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 26. Then, as that brahmin-sage (viprarṣi) continued to dig into the surface of the earth, he was resolved to create a path to the serpent realm (nāgaloka).

sanskrit: रथेन हरियुक्तेन तं देशमुपजग्मिवान् ।वज्रपाणिर्महातेजा ददर्श च द्विजोत्तमम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: rathena hariyuktena taṁ deśamupajagmivān ,vajrapāṇirmahātejā dadarśa ca dvijottamam.
english: The greatly effulgent, thunderbolt-handed Indra (Vajrapāṇi) arrived at that place in a chariot drawn by his horses and saw the best of brahmins.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 27. The greatly effulgent, thunderbolt-handed Indra (Vajrapāṇi) arrived at that place in a chariot drawn by his horses and saw the best of brahmins.

sanskrit: स तु तं ब्राह्मणो भूत्वा तस्य दुःखेन दुःखितः ।उत्तङ्कमब्रवीत्तात नैतच्छक्यं त्वयेति वै ॥२८॥
transliteration: sa tu taṁ brāhmaṇo bhūtvā tasya duḥkhena duḥkhitaḥ ,uttaṅkamabravīttāta naitacchakyaṁ tvayeti vai.
english: But he (Indra), having assumed the form of a brahmin and being distressed by his (Uttanka's) sorrow, said to Uttanka, 'My dear son, this cannot be achieved by you,' indeed.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 28. But he (Indra), having assumed the form of a brahmin and being distressed by his (Uttanka's) sorrow, said to Uttanka, 'My dear son, this cannot be achieved by you,' indeed.

sanskrit: इतो हि नागलोको वै योजनानि सहस्रशः ।न दण्डकाष्ठसाध्यं च मन्ये कार्यमिदं तव ॥२९॥
transliteration: ito hi nāgaloko vai yojanāni sahasraśaḥ ,na daṇḍakāṣṭhasādhyaṁ ca manye kāryamidaṁ tava.
english: Indeed, from here the serpent-world (nāgaloka) is thousands of yojanas away. And I believe this task of yours cannot be accomplished by simple means.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 29. Indeed, from here the serpent-world (nāgaloka) is thousands of yojanas away. And I believe this task of yours cannot be accomplished by simple means.

sanskrit: उत्तङ्क उवाच ।नागलोके यदि ब्रह्मन्न शक्ये कुण्डले मया ।प्राप्तुं प्राणान्विमोक्ष्यामि पश्यतस्ते द्विजोत्तम ॥३०॥
transliteration: uttaṅka uvāca ,nāgaloke yadi brahmanna śakye kuṇḍale mayā ,prāptuṁ prāṇānvimokṣyāmi paśyataste dvijottama.
english: Uttanka said: 'O (brahmin), if those earrings cannot be obtained by me in the serpent-world (nāgaloka), then, O best of brahmins, I will give up my life while you watch.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 30. Uttanka said: 'O (brahmin), if those earrings cannot be obtained by me in the serpent-world (nāgaloka), then, O best of brahmins, I will give up my life while you watch.'

sanskrit: यदा स नाशकत्तस्य निश्चयं कर्तुमन्यथा ।वज्रपाणिस्तदा दण्डं वज्रास्त्रेण युयोज ह ॥३१॥
transliteration: yadā sa nāśakattasya niścayaṁ kartumanyathā ,vajrapāṇistadā daṇḍaṁ vajrāstreṇa yuyoja ha.
english: When he (Daṇḍa) could not be made to alter his firm resolve, then Vajrapāṇi (Indra) engaged that staff with his thunderbolt-weapon.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 31. When he (Daṇḍa) could not be made to alter his firm resolve, then Vajrapāṇi (Indra) engaged that staff with his thunderbolt-weapon.

sanskrit: ततो वज्रप्रहारैस्तैर्दार्यमाणा वसुंधरा ।नागलोकस्य पन्थानमकरोज्जनमेजय ॥३२॥
transliteration: tato vajraprahāraistairdāryamāṇā vasuṁdharā ,nāgalokasya panthānamakarojjanamejaya.
english: Then, O Janamejaya, the earth, being split by those thunderbolt strikes, created a path to the nāga world.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 32. Then, O Janamejaya, the earth, being split by those thunderbolt strikes, created a path to the nāga world.

sanskrit: स तेन मार्गेण तदा नागलोकं विवेश ह ।ददर्श नागलोकं च योजनानि सहस्रशः ॥३३॥
transliteration: sa tena mārgeṇa tadā nāgalokaṁ viveśa ha ,dadarśa nāgalokaṁ ca yojanāni sahasraśaḥ.
english: He then entered the nāga world by that path. And he saw the nāga world, vast for thousands of yojanas.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 33. He then entered the nāga world by that path. And he saw the nāga world, vast for thousands of yojanas.

sanskrit: प्राकारनिचयैर्दिव्यैर्मणिमुक्ताभ्यलंकृतैः ।उपपन्नं महाभाग शातकुम्भमयैस्तथा ॥३४॥
transliteration: prākāranicayairdivyairmaṇimuktābhyalaṁkṛtaiḥ ,upapannaṁ mahābhāga śātakumbhamayaistathā.
english: O great-souled one, it was endowed with divine rows of ramparts, adorned with jewels and pearls, and likewise, with those made of pure gold.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 34. O great-souled one, it was endowed with divine rows of ramparts, adorned with jewels and pearls, and likewise, with those made of pure gold.

sanskrit: वापीः स्फटिकसोपाना नदीश्च विमलोदकाः ।ददर्श वृक्षांश्च बहून्नानाद्विजगणायुतान् ॥३५॥
transliteration: vāpīḥ sphaṭikasopānā nadīśca vimalodakāḥ ,dadarśa vṛkṣāṁśca bahūnnānādvijagaṇāyutān.
english: He saw ponds with crystal steps, and rivers with clear water, and many trees frequented by various flocks of birds.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 35. He saw ponds with crystal steps, and rivers with clear water, and many trees frequented by various flocks of birds.

sanskrit: तस्य लोकस्य च द्वारं ददर्श स भृगूद्वहः ।पञ्चयोजनविस्तारमायतं शतयोजनम् ॥३६॥
transliteration: tasya lokasya ca dvāraṁ dadarśa sa bhṛgūdvahaḥ ,pañcayojanavistāramāyataṁ śatayojanam.
english: And he, the descendant of Bhrigu, saw the gate of that world, which was five yojanas wide and a hundred yojanas long.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 36. And he, the descendant of Bhrigu, saw the gate of that world, which was five yojanas wide and a hundred yojanas long.

sanskrit: नागलोकमुत्तङ्कस्तु प्रेक्ष्य दीनोऽभवत्तदा ।निराशश्चाभवत्तात कुण्डलाहरणे पुनः ॥३७॥
transliteration: nāgalokamuttaṅkastu prekṣya dīno'bhavattadā ,nirāśaścābhavattāta kuṇḍalāharaṇe punaḥ.
english: But after seeing the world of serpents (nāgaloka), Uttanka then became distressed, and, O dear one, he again became hopeless about obtaining the earrings.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 37. But after seeing the world of serpents (nāgaloka), Uttanka then became distressed, and, O dear one, he again became hopeless about obtaining the earrings.

sanskrit: तत्र प्रोवाच तुरगस्तं कृष्णश्वेतवालधिः ।ताम्रास्यनेत्रः कौरव्य प्रज्वलन्निव तेजसा ॥३८॥
transliteration: tatra provāca turagastaṁ kṛṣṇaśvetavāladhiḥ ,tāmrāsyanetraḥ kauravya prajvalanniva tejasā.
english: Thereupon, the horse with a black and white tail, and with copper-red face and eyes, spoke to him as if blazing with splendor, O descendant of Kuru.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 38. Thereupon, the horse with a black and white tail, and with copper-red face and eyes, spoke to him as if blazing with splendor, O descendant of Kuru.

sanskrit: धमस्वापानमेतन्मे ततस्त्वं विप्र लप्स्यसे ।ऐरावतसुतेनेह तवानीते हि कुण्डले ॥३९॥
transliteration: dhamasvāpānametanme tatastvaṁ vipra lapsyase ,airāvatasuteneha tavānīte hi kuṇḍale.
english: O brahmin, this is the 'drinking of smoke' ritual (dhamasvāpānam) for you, offered by me; perform it, and thereafter you will obtain (the earrings). Indeed, your earrings have been brought here by Airāvata's son.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 39. O brahmin, this is the 'drinking of smoke' ritual (dhamasvāpānam) for you, offered by me; perform it, and thereafter you will obtain (the earrings). Indeed, your earrings have been brought here by Airāvata's son.

sanskrit: मा जुगुप्सां कृथाः पुत्र त्वमत्रार्थे कथंचन ।त्वयैतद्धि समाचीर्णं गौतमस्याश्रमे तदा ॥४०॥
transliteration: mā jugupsāṁ kṛthāḥ putra tvamatrārthe kathaṁcana ,tvayaitaddhi samācīrṇaṁ gautamasyāśrame tadā.
english: Son, do not feel any aversion (jugupsā) at all in this matter. Indeed, this very act was performed by you then in Gautama's hermitage (āśrama).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 40. Son, do not feel any aversion (jugupsā) at all in this matter. Indeed, this very act was performed by you then in Gautama's hermitage (āśrama).

sanskrit: उत्तङ्क उवाच ।कथं भवन्तं जानीयामुपाध्यायाश्रमं प्रति ।यन्मया चीर्णपूर्वं च श्रोतुमिच्छामि तद्ध्यहम् ॥४१॥
transliteration: uttaṅka uvāca ,kathaṁ bhavantaṁ jānīyāmupādhyāyāśramaṁ prati ,yanmayā cīrṇapūrvaṁ ca śrotumicchāmi taddhyaham.
english: Uttanka said: 'How am I to identify you to my preceptor's (guru) wife upon returning to the hermitage (āśrama)? And what was previously done by me, that indeed I wish to hear.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 41. Uttanka said: 'How am I to identify you to my preceptor's (guru) wife upon returning to the hermitage (āśrama)? And what was previously done by me, that indeed I wish to hear.'

sanskrit: अश्व उवाच ।गुरोर्गुरुं मां जानीहि ज्वलितं जातवेदसम् ।त्वया ह्यहं सदा वत्स गुरोरर्थेऽभिपूजितः ॥४२॥
transliteration: aśva uvāca ,gurorguruṁ māṁ jānīhi jvalitaṁ jātavedasam ,tvayā hyahaṁ sadā vatsa gurorarthe'bhipūjitaḥ.
english: The horse said: 'Know me as the supreme preceptor (guru), the blazing fire (jātavedas). Indeed, my dear child, you have always worshipped me for your preceptor's (guru) sake.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 42. The horse said: 'Know me as the supreme preceptor (guru), the blazing fire (jātavedas). Indeed, my dear child, you have always worshipped me for your preceptor's (guru) sake.'

sanskrit: सततं पूजितो विप्र शुचिना भृगुनन्दन ।तस्माच्छ्रेयो विधास्यामि तवैवं कुरु मा चिरम् ॥४३॥
transliteration: satataṁ pūjito vipra śucinā bhṛgunandana ,tasmācchreyo vidhāsyāmi tavaivaṁ kuru mā ciram.
english: O brahmin, O son of Bhṛgu, I am constantly honored by the pure one. Therefore, I will grant you prosperity. Do this right away, without delay.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 43. O brahmin, O son of Bhṛgu, I am constantly honored by the pure one. Therefore, I will grant you prosperity. Do this right away, without delay.

sanskrit: इत्युक्तः स तथाकार्षीदुत्तङ्कश्चित्रभानुना ।घृतार्चिः प्रीतिमांश्चापि प्रजज्वाल दिधक्षया ॥४४॥
transliteration: ityuktaḥ sa tathākārṣīduttaṅkaścitrabhānunā ,ghṛtārciḥ prītimāṁścāpi prajajvāla didhakṣayā.
english: Thus spoken to, Uttanka acted accordingly with Citrabhānu (Agni). And Citrabhānu, whose flames were fed with ghee and who was full of satisfaction, blazed forth, desiring to consume.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 44. Thus spoken to, Uttanka acted accordingly with Citrabhānu (Agni). And Citrabhānu, whose flames were fed with ghee and who was full of satisfaction, blazed forth, desiring to consume.

sanskrit: ततोऽस्य रोमकूपेभ्यो ध्मायमानस्य भारत ।घनः प्रादुरभूद्धूमो नागलोकभयावहः ॥४५॥
transliteration: tato'sya romakūpebhyo dhmāyamānasya bhārata ,ghanaḥ prādurabhūddhūmo nāgalokabhayāvahaḥ.
english: Then, O descendant of Bharata, from the pores of his (Agni's) skin, as he was being fanned, a thick smoke manifested, terrifying to the serpent world (nāgaloka).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 45. Then, O descendant of Bharata, from the pores of his (Agni's) skin, as he was being fanned, a thick smoke manifested, terrifying to the serpent world (nāgaloka).

sanskrit: तेन धूमेन सहसा वर्धमानेन भारत ।नागलोके महाराज न प्रज्ञायत किंचन ॥४६॥
transliteration: tena dhūmena sahasā vardhamānena bhārata ,nāgaloke mahārāja na prajñāyata kiṁcana.
english: O descendant of Bharata, O great king, by that smoke which suddenly and rapidly increased, nothing at all could be perceived in the serpent world (nāgaloka).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 46. O descendant of Bharata, O great king, by that smoke which suddenly and rapidly increased, nothing at all could be perceived in the serpent world (nāgaloka).

sanskrit: हाहाकृतमभूत्सर्वमैरावतनिवेशनम् ।वासुकिप्रमुखानां च नागानां जनमेजय ॥४७॥
transliteration: hāhākṛtamabhūtsarvamairāvataniveśanam ,vāsukipramukhānāṁ ca nāgānāṁ janamejaya.
english: O Janamejaya, the entire abode of the Nāgas, including those led by Vāsuki, became filled with cries of distress.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 47. O Janamejaya, the entire abode of the Nāgas, including those led by Vāsuki, became filled with cries of distress.

sanskrit: न प्रकाशन्त वेश्मानि धूमरुद्धानि भारत ।नीहारसंवृतानीव वनानि गिरयस्तथा ॥४८॥
transliteration: na prakāśanta veśmāni dhūmaruddhāni bhārata ,nīhārasaṁvṛtānīva vanāni girayastathā.
english: O Bhārata, the houses, obstructed by smoke, were not visible, just like forests and mountains are enveloped by mist.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 48. O Bhārata, the houses, obstructed by smoke, were not visible, just like forests and mountains are enveloped by mist.

sanskrit: ते धूमरक्तनयना वह्नितेजोभितापिताः ।आजग्मुर्निश्चयं ज्ञातुं भार्गवस्यातितेजसः ॥४९॥
transliteration: te dhūmaraktanayanā vahnitejobhitāpitāḥ ,ājagmurniścayaṁ jñātuṁ bhārgavasyātitejasaḥ.
english: With their eyes reddened by smoke and scorched by the intense heat of the fire, they came to ascertain the resolve of the supremely powerful Bhārgava.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 49. With their eyes reddened by smoke and scorched by the intense heat of the fire, they came to ascertain the resolve of the supremely powerful Bhārgava.

sanskrit: श्रुत्वा च निश्चयं तस्य महर्षेस्तिग्मतेजसः ।संभ्रान्तमनसः सर्वे पूजां चक्रुर्यथाविधि ॥५०॥
transliteration: śrutvā ca niścayaṁ tasya maharṣestigmatejasaḥ ,saṁbhrāntamanasaḥ sarve pūjāṁ cakruryathāvidhi.
english: And having heard the resolve of that great sage (maharṣi) of fierce energy, all of them, with agitated minds, performed worship according to the prescribed rites.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 50. And having heard the resolve of that great sage (maharṣi) of fierce energy, all of them, with agitated minds, performed worship according to the prescribed rites.

sanskrit: सर्वे प्राञ्जलयो नागा वृद्धबालपुरोगमाः ।शिरोभिः प्रणिपत्योचुः प्रसीद भगवन्निति ॥५१॥
transliteration: sarve prāñjalayo nāgā vṛddhabālapurogamāḥ ,śirobhiḥ praṇipatyocuḥ prasīda bhagavanniti.
english: All the Nāgas, with the old and young at the forefront, stood with folded hands, bowed down with their heads, and said, "O revered one, be pleased!"
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 51. All the Nāgas, with the old and young at the forefront, stood with folded hands, bowed down with their heads, and said, "O revered one, be pleased!"

sanskrit: प्रसाद्य ब्राह्मणं ते तु पाद्यमर्घ्यं निवेद्य च ।प्रायच्छन्कुण्डले दिव्ये पन्नगाः परमार्चिते ॥५२॥
transliteration: prasādya brāhmaṇaṁ te tu pādyamarghyaṁ nivedya ca ,prāyacchankuṇḍale divye pannagāḥ paramārcite.
english: Having pleased the brahmin, those Nāgas, moreover, offered water for the feet and a respectful offering, and then gave him the two divine and exquisitely adorned earrings.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 52. Having pleased the brahmin, those Nāgas, moreover, offered water for the feet and a respectful offering, and then gave him the two divine and exquisitely adorned earrings.

sanskrit: ततः संपूजितो नागैस्तत्रोत्तङ्कः प्रतापवान् ।अग्निं प्रदक्षिणं कृत्वा जगाम गुरुसद्म तत् ॥५३॥
transliteration: tataḥ saṁpūjito nāgaistatrottaṅkaḥ pratāpavān ,agniṁ pradakṣiṇaṁ kṛtvā jagāma gurusadma tat.
english: Thereafter, the mighty Uttanka, having been honored by the Nāgas there, circumambulated the fire and went to that abode of his teacher (guru).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 53. Thereafter, the mighty Uttanka, having been honored by the Nāgas there, circumambulated the fire and went to that abode of his teacher (guru).

sanskrit: स गत्वा त्वरितो राजन्गौतमस्य निवेशनम् ।प्रायच्छत्कुण्डले दिव्ये गुरुपत्न्यै तदानघ ॥५४॥
transliteration: sa gatvā tvarito rājangautamasya niveśanam ,prāyacchatkuṇḍale divye gurupatnyai tadānagha.
english: O king, O sinless one, he, having gone quickly to the abode of Gautama, then gave the two divine earrings to the teacher's wife (guru-patnī).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 54. O king, O sinless one, he, having gone quickly to the abode of Gautama, then gave the two divine earrings to the teacher's wife (guru-patnī).

sanskrit: एवं महात्मना तेन त्रीँल्लोकाञ्जनमेजय ।परिक्रम्याहृते दिव्ये ततस्ते मणिकुण्डले ॥५५॥
transliteration: evaṁ mahātmanā tena trīँllokāñjanamejaya ,parikramyāhṛte divye tataste maṇikuṇḍale.
english: O Janamejaya, thus, after that great-souled person had traversed the three worlds, those two divine gem earrings were then brought back.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 55. O Janamejaya, thus, after that great-souled person had traversed the three worlds, those two divine gem earrings were then brought back.

sanskrit: एवंप्रभावः स मुनिरुत्तङ्को भरतर्षभ ।परेण तपसा युक्तो यन्मां त्वं परिपृच्छसि ॥५६॥
transliteration: evaṁprabhāvaḥ sa muniruttaṅko bharatarṣabha ,pareṇa tapasā yukto yanmāṁ tvaṁ paripṛcchasi.
english: O best of Bharatas, that sage (muni) Uttaṅka was endowed with such great power and supreme austerity (tapas), about whom you are now asking me.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 57 Verse 56. O best of Bharatas, that sage (muni) Uttaṅka was endowed with such great power and supreme austerity (tapas), about whom you are now asking me.

sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।उत्तङ्काय वरं दत्त्वा गोविन्दो द्विजसत्तम ।अत ऊर्ध्वं महाबाहुः किं चकार महायशाः ॥१॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,uttaṅkāya varaṁ dattvā govindo dvijasattama ,ata ūrdhvaṁ mahābāhuḥ kiṁ cakāra mahāyaśāḥ.
english: Janamejaya said: 'O best of brahmins, what did Govinda, the mighty-armed and greatly glorious one, do after giving a boon to Uttaṅka?'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 58 Verse 1. Janamejaya said: 'O best of brahmins, what did Govinda, the mighty-armed and greatly glorious one, do after giving a boon to Uttaṅka?'

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।दत्त्वा वरमुत्तङ्काय प्रायात्सात्यकिना सह ।द्वारकामेव गोविन्दः शीघ्रवेगैर्महाहयैः ॥२॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,dattvā varamuttaṅkāya prāyātsātyakinā saha ,dvārakāmeva govindaḥ śīghravegairmahāhayaiḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: 'Having granted a boon to Uttaṅka, Govinda departed for Dvārakā with Sātyaki, accompanied by swift-paced, great horses.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 58 Verse 2. Vaiśampāyana said: 'Having granted a boon to Uttaṅka, Govinda departed for Dvārakā with Sātyaki, accompanied by swift-paced, great horses.'

sanskrit: सरांसि च नदीश्चैव वनानि विविधानि च ।अतिक्रम्य ससादाथ रम्यां द्वारवतीं पुरीम् ॥३॥
transliteration: sarāṁsi ca nadīścaiva vanāni vividhāni ca ,atikramya sasādātha ramyāṁ dvāravatīṁ purīm.
english: Having crossed lakes, rivers, and various forests, he then reached the beautiful city of Dvāravatī.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 58 Verse 3. Having crossed lakes, rivers, and various forests, he then reached the beautiful city of Dvāravatī.

sanskrit: वर्तमाने महाराज महे रैवतकस्य च ।उपायात्पुण्डरीकाक्षो युयुधानानुगस्तदा ॥४॥
transliteration: vartamāne mahārāja mahe raivatakasya ca ,upāyātpuṇḍarīkākṣo yuyudhānānugastadā.
english: While the great festival (mahotsava) of Raivataka was taking place, O great king, the lotus-eyed one (Kṛṣṇa), followed by Yuyudhāna, then arrived.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 58 Verse 4. While the great festival (mahotsava) of Raivataka was taking place, O great king, the lotus-eyed one (Kṛṣṇa), followed by Yuyudhāna, then arrived.

sanskrit: अलंकृतस्तु स गिरिर्नानारूपविचित्रितैः ।बभौ रुक्ममयैः काशैः सर्वतः पुरुषर्षभ ॥५॥
transliteration: alaṁkṛtastu sa girirnānārūpavicitritaiḥ ,babhau rukmamayaiḥ kāśaiḥ sarvataḥ puruṣarṣabha.
english: That mountain, however, O best among men (puruṣarṣabha), adorned with various splendid forms and shining everywhere with golden kāśa grass, looked magnificent.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 58 Verse 5. That mountain, however, O best among men (puruṣarṣabha), adorned with various splendid forms and shining everywhere with golden kāśa grass, looked magnificent.

sanskrit: काञ्चनस्रग्भिरग्र्याभिः सुमनोभिस्तथैव च ।वासोभिश्च महाशैलः कल्पवृक्षैश्च सर्वशः ॥६॥
transliteration: kāñcanasragbhiragryābhiḥ sumanobhistathaiva ca ,vāsobhiśca mahāśailaḥ kalpavṛkṣaiśca sarvaśaḥ.
english: The great mountain (mahāśaila) was, moreover, everywhere (adorned) with excellent golden garlands, and with beautiful flowers, and with garments, and with wish-fulfilling trees (kalpavṛkṣa).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 58 Verse 6. The great mountain (mahāśaila) was, moreover, everywhere (adorned) with excellent golden garlands, and with beautiful flowers, and with garments, and with wish-fulfilling trees (kalpavṛkṣa).

sanskrit: दीपवृक्षैश्च सौवर्णैरभीक्ष्णमुपशोभितः ।गुहानिर्झरदेशेषु दिवाभूतो बभूव ह ॥७॥
transliteration: dīpavṛkṣaiśca sauvarṇairabhīkṣṇamupaśobhitaḥ ,guhānirjharadeśeṣu divābhūto babhūva ha.
english: Constantly adorned with golden lamp-trees, it (the mountain) appeared as if it were daylight in its regions of caves and waterfalls.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 58 Verse 7. Constantly adorned with golden lamp-trees, it (the mountain) appeared as if it were daylight in its regions of caves and waterfalls.

sanskrit: पताकाभिर्विचित्राभिः सघण्टाभिः समन्ततः ।पुंभिः स्त्रीभिश्च संघुष्टः प्रगीत इव चाभवत् ।अतीव प्रेक्षणीयोऽभून्मेरुर्मुनिगणैरिव ॥८॥
transliteration: patākābhirvicitrābhiḥ saghaṇṭābhiḥ samantataḥ ,puṁbhiḥ strībhiśca saṁghuṣṭaḥ pragīta iva cābhavat ,atīva prekṣaṇīyo'bhūnmerurmunigaṇairiva.
english: Adorned on all sides with various, colorful banners and bells, it (the mountain) resounded with the voices of men and women, as if being sung itself. Mount Meru became exceedingly beautiful to behold, just like (a mountain graced by) multitudes of sages (muni).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 58 Verse 8. Adorned on all sides with various, colorful banners and bells, it (the mountain) resounded with the voices of men and women, as if being sung itself. Mount Meru became exceedingly beautiful to behold, just like (a mountain graced by) multitudes of sages (muni).

sanskrit: मत्तानां हृष्टरूपाणां स्त्रीणां पुंसां च भारत ।गायतां पर्वतेन्द्रस्य दिवस्पृगिव निस्वनः ॥९॥
transliteration: mattānāṁ hṛṣṭarūpāṇāṁ strīṇāṁ puṁsāṁ ca bhārata ,gāyatāṁ parvatendrasya divaspṛgiva nisvanaḥ.
english: O Bhārata, the joyous sounds of intoxicated men and women singing were like the sky-reaching roar of the king of mountains.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 58 Verse 9. O Bhārata, the joyous sounds of intoxicated men and women singing were like the sky-reaching roar of the king of mountains.

sanskrit: प्रमत्तमत्तसंमत्तक्ष्वेडितोत्कृष्टसंकुला ।तथा किलकिलाशब्दैर्भूरभूत्सुमनोहरा ॥१०॥
transliteration: pramattamattasaṁmattakṣveḍitotkṛṣṭasaṁkulā ,tathā kilakilāśabdairbhūrabhūtsumanoharā.
english: The place became very charming, filled with the cries, loud shouts, and joyful noises of the extremely intoxicated, maddened, and utterly intoxicated (people).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 58 Verse 10. The place became very charming, filled with the cries, loud shouts, and joyful noises of the extremely intoxicated, maddened, and utterly intoxicated (people).

sanskrit: विपणापणवान्रम्यो भक्ष्यभोज्यविहारवान् ।वस्त्रमाल्योत्करयुतो वीणावेणुमृदङ्गवान् ॥११॥
transliteration: vipaṇāpaṇavānramyo bhakṣyabhojyavihāravān ,vastramālyotkarayuto vīṇāveṇumṛdaṅgavān.
english: It was charming, bustling with markets and shops, and filled with places for various foods and entertainment. It abounded in clothes and garlands, and resounded with the sounds of lutes, flutes, and drums.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 58 Verse 11. It was charming, bustling with markets and shops, and filled with places for various foods and entertainment. It abounded in clothes and garlands, and resounded with the sounds of lutes, flutes, and drums.

sanskrit: सुरामैरेयमिश्रेण भक्ष्यभोज्येन चैव ह ।दीनान्धकृपणादिभ्यो दीयमानेन चानिशम् ।बभौ परमकल्याणो महस्तस्य महागिरेः ॥१२॥
transliteration: surāmaireyamiśreṇa bhakṣyabhojyena caiva ha ,dīnāndhakṛpaṇādibhyo dīyamānena cāniśam ,babhau paramakalyāṇo mahastasya mahāgireḥ.
english: Its great splendor (mahas) shone with supreme auspiciousness, as sura and maireya liquors, along with various foods, were constantly given to the poor, blind, and other needy people.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 58 Verse 12. Its great splendor (mahas) shone with supreme auspiciousness, as sura and maireya liquors, along with various foods, were constantly given to the poor, blind, and other needy people.

sanskrit: पुण्यावसथवान्वीर पुण्यकृद्भिर्निषेवितः ।विहारो वृष्णिवीराणां महे रैवतकस्य ह ।स नगो वेश्मसंकीर्णो देवलोक इवाबभौ ॥१३॥
transliteration: puṇyāvasathavānvīra puṇyakṛdbhirniṣevitaḥ ,vihāro vṛṣṇivīrāṇāṁ mahe raivatakasya ha ,sa nago veśmasaṁkīrṇo devaloka ivābabhau.
english: O hero, it possessed sacred dwellings and was frequented by those who performed meritorious deeds. It was a pleasure ground for the Vṛṣṇi heroes, especially during the great festival (mahas) of Raivataka. That mountain, crowded with houses, truly shone like the world of gods (devaloka).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 58 Verse 13. O hero, it possessed sacred dwellings and was frequented by those who performed meritorious deeds. It was a pleasure ground for the Vṛṣṇi heroes, especially during the great festival (mahas) of Raivataka. That mountain, crowded with houses, truly shone like the world of gods (devaloka).

sanskrit: तदा च कृष्णसांनिध्यमासाद्य भरतर्षभ ।शक्रसद्मप्रतीकाशो बभूव स हि शैलराट् ॥१४॥
transliteration: tadā ca kṛṣṇasāṁnidhyamāsādya bharatarṣabha ,śakrasadmapratīkāśo babhūva sa hi śailarāṭ.
english: Then, having obtained the presence of (Kṛṣṇa), O best of the Bhāratas, that king of mountains (śailarāṭ) indeed became like Indra's abode.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 58 Verse 14. Then, having obtained the presence of (Kṛṣṇa), O best of the Bhāratas, that king of mountains (śailarāṭ) indeed became like Indra's abode.

sanskrit: ततः संपूज्यमानः स विवेश भवनं शुभम् ।गोविन्दः सात्यकिश्चैव जगाम भवनं स्वकम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: tataḥ saṁpūjyamānaḥ sa viveśa bhavanaṁ śubham ,govindaḥ sātyakiścaiva jagāma bhavanaṁ svakam.
english: Then, being honored, he entered an auspicious dwelling. Govinda and Satyaki also went to their own homes.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 58 Verse 15. Then, being honored, he entered an auspicious dwelling. Govinda and Satyaki also went to their own homes.

sanskrit: विवेश च स हृष्टात्मा चिरकालप्रवासकः ।कृत्वा नसुकरं कर्म दानवेष्विव वासवः ॥१६॥
transliteration: viveśa ca sa hṛṣṭātmā cirakālapravāsakaḥ ,kṛtvā nasukaraṁ karma dānaveṣviva vāsavaḥ.
english: And he, joyful in spirit, who had been absent for a long time, entered, having accomplished a difficult deed (karma), like Indra among the Danavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 58 Verse 16. And he, joyful in spirit, who had been absent for a long time, entered, having accomplished a difficult deed (karma), like Indra among the Danavas.

sanskrit: उपयातं तु वार्ष्णेयं भोजवृष्ण्यन्धकास्तदा ।अभ्यगच्छन्महात्मानं देवा इव शतक्रतुम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: upayātaṁ tu vārṣṇeyaṁ bhojavṛṣṇyandhakāstadā ,abhyagacchanmahātmānaṁ devā iva śatakratum.
english: Then, the Bhojas, Vṛṣṇis, and Andhakas approached the great-souled one (mahā-ātman), Vārṣṇeya, who had arrived, just as the gods approach Śatakratu (Indra).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 58 Verse 17. Then, the Bhojas, Vṛṣṇis, and Andhakas approached the great-souled one (mahā-ātman), Vārṣṇeya, who had arrived, just as the gods approach Śatakratu (Indra).

sanskrit: स तानभ्यर्च्य मेधावी पृष्ट्वा च कुशलं तदा ।अभ्यवादयत प्रीतः पितरं मातरं तथा ॥१८॥
transliteration: sa tānabhyarcya medhāvī pṛṣṭvā ca kuśalaṁ tadā ,abhyavādayata prītaḥ pitaraṁ mātaraṁ tathā.
english: Then, that sagacious one, having honored them and inquired about their well-being, joyfully saluted his father and also his mother.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 58 Verse 18. Then, that sagacious one, having honored them and inquired about their well-being, joyfully saluted his father and also his mother.

sanskrit: ताभ्यां च संपरिष्वक्तः सान्त्वितश्च महाभुजः ।उपोपविष्टस्तैः सर्वैर्वृष्णिभिः परिवारितः ॥१९॥
transliteration: tābhyāṁ ca saṁpariṣvaktaḥ sāntvitaśca mahābhujaḥ ,upopaviṣṭastaiḥ sarvairvṛṣṇibhiḥ parivāritaḥ.
english: O mighty-armed one, embraced and comforted by those two, he sat down, surrounded by all the Vrishnis.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 58 Verse 19. O mighty-armed one, embraced and comforted by those two, he sat down, surrounded by all the Vrishnis.

sanskrit: स विश्रान्तो महातेजाः कृतपादावसेचनः ।कथयामास तं कृष्णः पृष्टः पित्रा महाहवम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: sa viśrānto mahātejāḥ kṛtapādāvasecanaḥ ,kathayāmāsa taṁ kṛṣṇaḥ pṛṣṭaḥ pitrā mahāhavam.
english: Having rested and had his feet washed, the greatly energetic Krishna, when asked by his father, narrated the great battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 58 Verse 20. Having rested and had his feet washed, the greatly energetic Krishna, when asked by his father, narrated the great battle.

sanskrit: वसुदेव उवाच ।श्रुतवानस्मि वार्ष्णेय संग्रामं परमाद्भुतम् ।नराणां वदतां पुत्र कथोद्घातेषु नित्यशः ॥१॥
transliteration: vasudeva uvāca ,śrutavānasmi vārṣṇeya saṁgrāmaṁ paramādbhutam ,narāṇāṁ vadatāṁ putra kathodghāteṣu nityaśaḥ.
english: Vasudeva said: 'O Vrishni (Vārṣṇeya), my son, I have constantly heard people speak of the exceedingly wonderful battle in their discussions.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 59 Verse 1. Vasudeva said: 'O Vrishni (Vārṣṇeya), my son, I have constantly heard people speak of the exceedingly wonderful battle in their discussions.'

sanskrit: त्वं तु प्रत्यक्षदर्शी च कार्यज्ञश्च महाभुज ।तस्मात्प्रब्रूहि संग्रामं याथातथ्येन मेऽनघ ॥२॥
transliteration: tvaṁ tu pratyakṣadarśī ca kāryajñaśca mahābhuja ,tasmātprabrūhi saṁgrāmaṁ yāthātathyena me'nagha.
english: But you, O mighty-armed one, are a direct witness and understander of affairs. Therefore, O sinless one, describe the battle to me exactly as it happened.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 59 Verse 2. But you, O mighty-armed one, are a direct witness and understander of affairs. Therefore, O sinless one, describe the battle to me exactly as it happened.

sanskrit: यथा तदभवद्युद्धं पाण्डवानां महात्मनाम् ।भीष्मकर्णकृपद्रोणशल्यादिभिरनुत्तमम् ॥३॥
transliteration: yathā tadabhavadyuddhaṁ pāṇḍavānāṁ mahātmanām ,bhīṣmakarṇakṛpadroṇaśalyādibhiranuttamam.
english: This describes how that unsurpassed battle of the noble Pāṇḍavas unfolded, involving Bhīṣma, Karṇa, Kṛpa, Droṇa, Śalya, and other prominent warriors.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 59 Verse 3. This describes how that unsurpassed battle of the noble Pāṇḍavas unfolded, involving Bhīṣma, Karṇa, Kṛpa, Droṇa, Śalya, and other prominent warriors.

sanskrit: अन्येषां क्षत्रियाणां च कृतास्त्राणामनेकशः ।नानावेषाकृतिमतां नानादेशनिवासिनाम् ॥४॥
transliteration: anyeṣāṁ kṣatriyāṇāṁ ca kṛtāstrāṇāmanekaśaḥ ,nānāveṣākṛtimatāṁ nānādeśanivāsinām.
english: And also of many other warriors, those skilled in weaponry, appearing in diverse attires and forms, and residing in various lands.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 59 Verse 4. And also of many other warriors, those skilled in weaponry, appearing in diverse attires and forms, and residing in various lands.

sanskrit: इत्युक्तः पुण्डरीकाक्षः पित्रा मातुस्तदन्तिके ।शशंस कुरुवीराणां संग्रामे निधनं यथा ॥५॥
transliteration: ityuktaḥ puṇḍarīkākṣaḥ pitrā mātustadantike ,śaśaṁsa kuruvīrāṇāṁ saṁgrāme nidhanaṁ yathā.
english: Thus addressed by his father in the presence of his mother, the lotus-eyed (puṇḍarīkākṣaḥ) [Kṛṣṇa] then narrated how the Kuru heroes met their demise in the war.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 59 Verse 5. Thus addressed by his father in the presence of his mother, the lotus-eyed (puṇḍarīkākṣaḥ) [Kṛṣṇa] then narrated how the Kuru heroes met their demise in the war.

sanskrit: वासुदेव उवाच ।अत्यद्भुतानि कर्माणि क्षत्रियाणां महात्मनाम् ।बहुलत्वान्न संख्यातुं शक्यान्यब्दशतैरपि ॥६॥
transliteration: vāsudeva uvāca ,atyadbhutāni karmāṇi kṣatriyāṇāṁ mahātmanām ,bahulatvānna saṁkhyātuṁ śakyānyabdaśatairapi.
english: Vāsudeva said: "The truly astonishing deeds (karma) of the great-souled warriors are so numerous that it would not be possible to count them even over hundreds of years."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 59 Verse 6. Vāsudeva said: "The truly astonishing deeds (karma) of the great-souled warriors are so numerous that it would not be possible to count them even over hundreds of years."

sanskrit: प्राधान्यतस्तु गदतः समासेनैव मे शृणु ।कर्माणि पृथिवीशानां यथावदमरद्युते ॥७॥
transliteration: prādhānyatastu gadataḥ samāsenaiva me śṛṇu ,karmāṇi pṛthivīśānāṁ yathāvadamaradyute.
english: O one with divine splendor (amaradyute), listen to me as I speak concisely and primarily about the proper actions (karma) of kings.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 59 Verse 7. O one with divine splendor (amaradyute), listen to me as I speak concisely and primarily about the proper actions (karma) of kings.

sanskrit: भीष्मः सेनापतिरभूदेकादशचमूपतिः ।कौरव्यः कौरवेयाणां देवानामिव वासवः ॥८॥
transliteration: bhīṣmaḥ senāpatirabhūdekādaśacamūpatiḥ ,kauravyaḥ kauraveyāṇāṁ devānāmiva vāsavaḥ.
english: Bhishma, a Kuru descendant, became the commander-in-chief of eleven divisions for the Kauravas, just as Vāsava (Indra) is for the gods.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 59 Verse 8. Bhishma, a Kuru descendant, became the commander-in-chief of eleven divisions for the Kauravas, just as Vāsava (Indra) is for the gods.

sanskrit: शिखण्डी पाण्डुपुत्राणां नेता सप्तचमूपतिः ।बभूव रक्षितो धीमान्धीमता सव्यसाचिना ॥९॥
transliteration: śikhaṇḍī pāṇḍuputrāṇāṁ netā saptacamūpatiḥ ,babhūva rakṣito dhīmāndhīmatā savyasācinā.
english: Shikhandi, the intelligent leader and commander of seven divisions for the Pandavas, was protected by the wise Savyasaci (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 59 Verse 9. Shikhandi, the intelligent leader and commander of seven divisions for the Pandavas, was protected by the wise Savyasaci (Arjuna).

sanskrit: तेषां तदभवद्युद्धं दशाहानि महात्मनाम् ।कुरूणां पाण्डवानां च सुमहद्रोमहर्षणम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ tadabhavadyuddhaṁ daśāhāni mahātmanām ,kurūṇāṁ pāṇḍavānāṁ ca sumahadromaharṣaṇam.
english: That very great, hair-raising battle occurred for ten days between those great-souled (mahātmanām) Kurus and Pandavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 59 Verse 10. That very great, hair-raising battle occurred for ten days between those great-souled (mahātmanām) Kurus and Pandavas.

sanskrit: ततः शिखण्डी गाङ्गेयमयुध्यन्तं महाहवे ।जघान बहुभिर्बाणैः सह गाण्डीवधन्वना ॥११॥
transliteration: tataḥ śikhaṇḍī gāṅgeyamayudhyantaṁ mahāhave ,jaghāna bahubhirbāṇaiḥ saha gāṇḍīvadhanvanā.
english: Then, in the great battle, Shikhandi, along with Arjuna who wielded the Gāṇḍīva bow, struck Bhishma, who was not engaged in combat, with many arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 59 Verse 11. Then, in the great battle, Shikhandi, along with Arjuna who wielded the Gāṇḍīva bow, struck Bhishma, who was not engaged in combat, with many arrows.

sanskrit: अकरोत्स ततः कालं शरतल्पगतो मुनिः ।अयनं दक्षिणं हित्वा संप्राप्ते चोत्तरायणे ॥१२॥
transliteration: akarotsa tataḥ kālaṁ śaratalpagato muniḥ ,ayanaṁ dakṣiṇaṁ hitvā saṁprāpte cottarāyaṇe.
english: Then, that sage (muni), lying on a bed of arrows, departed, having forsaken the southern solstice and awaiting the arrival of the northern solstice (uttarāyaṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 59 Verse 12. Then, that sage (muni), lying on a bed of arrows, departed, having forsaken the southern solstice and awaiting the arrival of the northern solstice (uttarāyaṇa).

sanskrit: ततः सेनापतिरभूद्द्रोणोऽस्त्रविदुषां वरः ।प्रवीरः कौरवेन्द्रस्य काव्यो दैत्यपतेरिव ॥१३॥
transliteration: tataḥ senāpatirabhūddroṇo'straviduṣāṁ varaḥ ,pravīraḥ kauravendrasya kāvyo daityapateriva.
english: Then Droṇa, the foremost among those skilled in weaponry and a great hero to the king of the Kauravas, became the commander-in-chief, just as Kavya (Śukra) was for the lord of the Daityas.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 59 Verse 13. Then Droṇa, the foremost among those skilled in weaponry and a great hero to the king of the Kauravas, became the commander-in-chief, just as Kavya (Śukra) was for the lord of the Daityas.

sanskrit: अक्षौहिणीभिः शिष्टाभिर्नवभिर्द्विजसत्तमः ।संवृतः समरश्लाघी गुप्तः कृपवृषादिभिः ॥१४॥
transliteration: akṣauhiṇībhiḥ śiṣṭābhirnavabhirdvijasattamaḥ ,saṁvṛtaḥ samaraślāghī guptaḥ kṛpavṛṣādibhiḥ.
english: That best of Brahmins (dvijasattama), Droṇa, who prided himself on his skill in battle, was surrounded by the nine remaining akṣauhiṇīs and protected by Kṛpa, Vṛṣa (Vṛṣasena), and others.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 59 Verse 14. That best of Brahmins (dvijasattama), Droṇa, who prided himself on his skill in battle, was surrounded by the nine remaining akṣauhiṇīs and protected by Kṛpa, Vṛṣa (Vṛṣasena), and others.

sanskrit: धृष्टद्युम्नस्त्वभून्नेता पाण्डवानां महास्त्रवित् ।गुप्तो भीमेन तेजस्वी मित्रेण वरुणो यथा ॥१५॥
transliteration: dhṛṣṭadyumnastvabhūnnetā pāṇḍavānāṁ mahāstravit ,gupto bhīmena tejasvī mitreṇa varuṇo yathā.
english: Dhrishtadyumna, a master of mighty weapons, became the leader of the Pandavas. He was powerful, protected by Bhima, just as Varuna (the god of cosmic order) is protected by Mitra (the sun god).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 59 Verse 15. Dhrishtadyumna, a master of mighty weapons, became the leader of the Pandavas. He was powerful, protected by Bhima, just as Varuna (the god of cosmic order) is protected by Mitra (the sun god).

sanskrit: पञ्चसेनापरिवृतो द्रोणप्रेप्सुर्महामनाः ।पितुर्निकारान्संस्मृत्य रणे कर्माकरोन्महत् ॥१६॥
transliteration: pañcasenāparivṛto droṇaprepsurmahāmanāḥ ,piturnikārānsaṁsmṛtya raṇe karmākaronmahat.
english: Surrounded by five divisions of troops, the high-minded Dhrishtadyumna, seeking Drona and remembering his father's insults, performed a mighty action (karma) in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 59 Verse 16. Surrounded by five divisions of troops, the high-minded Dhrishtadyumna, seeking Drona and remembering his father's insults, performed a mighty action (karma) in battle.

sanskrit: तस्मिंस्ते पृथिवीपाला द्रोणपार्षतसंगरे ।नानादिगागता वीराः प्रायशो निधनं गताः ॥१७॥
transliteration: tasmiṁste pṛthivīpālā droṇapārṣatasaṁgare ,nānādigāgatā vīrāḥ prāyaśo nidhanaṁ gatāḥ.
english: In that battle between Drona and Prishata's son (Dhrishtadyumna), those kings, rulers of the earth, brave warriors who had come from various directions, mostly met their death.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 59 Verse 17. In that battle between Drona and Prishata's son (Dhrishtadyumna), those kings, rulers of the earth, brave warriors who had come from various directions, mostly met their death.

sanskrit: दिनानि पञ्च तद्युद्धमभूत्परमदारुणम् ।ततो द्रोणः परिश्रान्तो धृष्टद्युम्नवशं गतः ॥१८॥
transliteration: dināni pañca tadyuddhamabhūtparamadāruṇam ,tato droṇaḥ pariśrānto dhṛṣṭadyumnavaśaṁ gataḥ.
english: That battle lasted five days and was extremely dreadful. Then, Drona, utterly exhausted, fell under Dhrishtadyumna's control.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 59 Verse 18. That battle lasted five days and was extremely dreadful. Then, Drona, utterly exhausted, fell under Dhrishtadyumna's control.

sanskrit: ततः सेनापतिरभूत्कर्णो दौर्योधने बले ।अक्षौहिणीभिः शिष्टाभिर्वृतः पञ्चभिराहवे ॥१९॥
transliteration: tataḥ senāpatirabhūtkarṇo dauryodhane bale ,akṣauhiṇībhiḥ śiṣṭābhirvṛtaḥ pañcabhirāhave.
english: Then Karṇa became the commander-in-chief of Duryodhana's army, accompanied in battle by the five remaining akṣauhiṇīs.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 59 Verse 19. Then Karṇa became the commander-in-chief of Duryodhana's army, accompanied in battle by the five remaining akṣauhiṇīs.

sanskrit: तिस्रस्तु पाण्डुपुत्राणां चम्वो बीभत्सुपालिताः ।हतप्रवीरभूयिष्ठा बभूवुः समवस्थिताः ॥२०॥
transliteration: tisrastu pāṇḍuputrāṇāṁ camvo bībhatsupālitāḥ ,hatapravīrabhūyiṣṭhā babhūvuḥ samavasthitāḥ.
english: However, the three divisions of the sons of Pāṇḍu (Pāṇḍavas), led by Bibhatsu (Arjuna), remained arrayed, though most of their chief heroes had been slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 59 Verse 20. However, the three divisions of the sons of Pāṇḍu (Pāṇḍavas), led by Bibhatsu (Arjuna), remained arrayed, though most of their chief heroes had been slain.

sanskrit: ततः पार्थं समासाद्य पतंग इव पावकम् ।पञ्चत्वमगमत्सौतिर्द्वितीयेऽहनि दारुणे ॥२१॥
transliteration: tataḥ pārthaṁ samāsādya pataṁga iva pāvakam ,pañcatvamagamatsautirdvitīye'hani dāruṇe.
english: Then, having approached Pārtha (Arjuna) like a moth rushes into a fire, Sauti met his death on the terrible second day.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 59 Verse 21. Then, having approached Pārtha (Arjuna) like a moth rushes into a fire, Sauti met his death on the terrible second day.

sanskrit: हते कर्णे तु कौरव्या निरुत्साहा हतौजसः ।अक्षौहिणीभिस्तिसृभिर्मद्रेशं पर्यवारयन् ॥२२॥
transliteration: hate karṇe tu kauravyā nirutsāhā hataujasaḥ ,akṣauhiṇībhistisṛbhirmadreśaṁ paryavārayan.
english: However, when Karṇa was slain, the Kauravas, disheartened and devoid of vigor, rallied around the King of Madras (Śalya) with the three remaining akṣauhiṇīs.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 59 Verse 22. However, when Karṇa was slain, the Kauravas, disheartened and devoid of vigor, rallied around the King of Madras (Śalya) with the three remaining akṣauhiṇīs.

sanskrit: हतवाहनभूयिष्ठाः पाण्डवास्तु युधिष्ठिरम् ।अक्षौहिण्या निरुत्साहाः शिष्टया पर्यवारयन् ॥२३॥
transliteration: hatavāhanabhūyiṣṭhāḥ pāṇḍavāstu yudhiṣṭhiram ,akṣauhiṇyā nirutsāhāḥ śiṣṭayā paryavārayan.
english: The Pāṇḍavas, with most of their mounts destroyed and feeling disheartened, surrounded Yudhiṣṭhira with their remaining division of the army (akṣauhiṇī).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 59 Verse 23. The Pāṇḍavas, with most of their mounts destroyed and feeling disheartened, surrounded Yudhiṣṭhira with their remaining division of the army (akṣauhiṇī).

sanskrit: अवधीन्मद्रराजानं कुरुराजो युधिष्ठिरः ।तस्मिंस्तथार्धदिवसे कर्म कृत्वा सुदुष्करम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: avadhīnmadrarājānaṁ kururājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,tasmiṁstathārdhadivase karma kṛtvā suduṣkaram.
english: King Yudhiṣṭhira, the ruler of the Kurus, killed Śalya, the king of Madra, during that same half-day, after having performed an exceedingly difficult deed (karma).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 59 Verse 24. King Yudhiṣṭhira, the ruler of the Kurus, killed Śalya, the king of Madra, during that same half-day, after having performed an exceedingly difficult deed (karma).

sanskrit: हते शल्ये तु शकुनिं सहदेवो महामनाः ।आहर्तारं कलेस्तस्य जघानामितविक्रमः ॥२५॥
transliteration: hate śalye tu śakuniṁ sahadevo mahāmanāḥ ,āhartāraṁ kalestasya jaghānāmitavikramaḥ.
english: After Śalya was killed, the noble-minded Sahadeva, possessing immeasurable valor, slew Śakuni, who was the instigator of that strife (kali).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 59 Verse 25. After Śalya was killed, the noble-minded Sahadeva, possessing immeasurable valor, slew Śakuni, who was the instigator of that strife (kali).

sanskrit: निहते शकुनौ राजा धार्तराष्ट्रः सुदुर्मनाः ।अपाक्रामद्गदापाणिर्हतभूयिष्ठसैनिकः ॥२६॥
transliteration: nihate śakunau rājā dhārtarāṣṭraḥ sudurmanāḥ ,apākrāmadgadāpāṇirhatabhūyiṣṭhasainikaḥ.
english: After Śakuni was killed, King Duryodhana, son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, greatly disheartened and with most of his soldiers slain, retreated, holding his mace.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 59 Verse 26. After Śakuni was killed, King Duryodhana, son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, greatly disheartened and with most of his soldiers slain, retreated, holding his mace.

sanskrit: तमन्वधावत्संक्रुद्धो भीमसेनः प्रतापवान् ।ह्रदे द्वैपायने चापि सलिलस्थं ददर्श तम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: tamanvadhāvatsaṁkruddho bhīmasenaḥ pratāpavān ,hrade dvaipāyane cāpi salilasthaṁ dadarśa tam.
english: The powerful Bhimasena, greatly enraged, pursued him. And he also saw him standing in the water of Dvaipayana Lake.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 59 Verse 27. The powerful Bhimasena, greatly enraged, pursued him. And he also saw him standing in the water of Dvaipayana Lake.

sanskrit: ततः शिष्टेन सैन्येन समन्तात्परिवार्य तम् ।उपोपविविशुर्हृष्टा ह्रदस्थं पञ्च पाण्डवाः ॥२८॥
transliteration: tataḥ śiṣṭena sainyena samantātparivārya tam ,upopaviviśurhṛṣṭā hradasthaṁ pañca pāṇḍavāḥ.
english: Then, with the remaining army, the five delighted Pāṇḍavas surrounded him from all sides and sat down near him, who was in the lake.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 59 Verse 28. Then, with the remaining army, the five delighted Pāṇḍavas surrounded him from all sides and sat down near him, who was in the lake.

sanskrit: विगाह्य सलिलं त्वाशु वाग्बाणैर्भृशविक्षतः ।उत्थाय स गदापाणिर्युद्धाय समुपस्थितः ॥२९॥
transliteration: vigāhya salilaṁ tvāśu vāgbāṇairbhṛśavikṣataḥ ,utthāya sa gadāpāṇiryuddhāya samupasthitaḥ.
english: But he, severely wounded by the verbal arrows, quickly rose, emerging from the water with his mace in hand, and stood ready for battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 59 Verse 29. But he, severely wounded by the verbal arrows, quickly rose, emerging from the water with his mace in hand, and stood ready for battle.

sanskrit: ततः स निहतो राजा धार्तराष्ट्रो महामृधे ।भीमसेनेन विक्रम्य पश्यतां पृथिवीक्षिताम् ॥३०॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa nihato rājā dhārtarāṣṭro mahāmṛdhe ,bhīmasenena vikramya paśyatāṁ pṛthivīkṣitām.
english: Then, that king, Dhārtarāṣṭra (Duryodhana), was valiantly slain by Bhimasena in the great battle, in the sight of the earth-rulers (pṛthivīkṣit).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 59 Verse 30. Then, that king, Dhārtarāṣṭra (Duryodhana), was valiantly slain by Bhimasena in the great battle, in the sight of the earth-rulers (pṛthivīkṣit).

sanskrit: ततस्तत्पाण्डवं सैन्यं संसुप्तं शिबिरे निशि ।निहतं द्रोणपुत्रेण पितुर्वधममृष्यता ॥३१॥
transliteration: tatastatpāṇḍavaṁ sainyaṁ saṁsuptaṁ śibire niśi ,nihataṁ droṇaputreṇa piturvadhamamṛṣyatā.
english: Then, that Pandava army, fast asleep in the camp at night, was slain by Droṇa's son, who was unable to endure his father's death.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 59 Verse 31. Then, that Pandava army, fast asleep in the camp at night, was slain by Droṇa's son, who was unable to endure his father's death.

sanskrit: हतपुत्रा हतबला हतमित्रा मया सह ।युयुधानद्वितीयेन पञ्च शिष्टाः स्म पाण्डवाः ॥३२॥
transliteration: hataputrā hatabalā hatamitrā mayā saha ,yuyudhānadvitīyena pañca śiṣṭāḥ sma pāṇḍavāḥ.
english: With their sons, forces, and friends killed, only five Pandavas remained with me, accompanied by Yuyudhana.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 59 Verse 32. With their sons, forces, and friends killed, only five Pandavas remained with me, accompanied by Yuyudhana.

sanskrit: सहैव कृपभोजाभ्यां द्रौणिर्युद्धादमुच्यत ।युयुत्सुश्चापि कौरव्यो मुक्तः पाण्डवसंश्रयात् ॥३३॥
transliteration: sahaiva kṛpabhojābhyāṁ drauṇiryuddhādamucyata ,yuyutsuścāpi kauravyo muktaḥ pāṇḍavasaṁśrayāt.
english: Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāmā), accompanied by Kṛpa and Bhoja, escaped from the battle. Yuyutsu, the Kuru prince, was also freed from his association with the Pandavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 59 Verse 33. Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāmā), accompanied by Kṛpa and Bhoja, escaped from the battle. Yuyutsu, the Kuru prince, was also freed from his association with the Pandavas.

sanskrit: निहते कौरवेन्द्रे च सानुबन्धे सुयोधने ।विदुरः संजयश्चैव धर्मराजमुपस्थितौ ॥३४॥
transliteration: nihate kauravendre ca sānubandhe suyodhane ,viduraḥ saṁjayaścaiva dharmarājamupasthitau.
english: When Suyodhana, the chief of the Kauravas, was slain along with his entire retinue, Vidura and Sañjaya approached Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of righteousness (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 59 Verse 34. When Suyodhana, the chief of the Kauravas, was slain along with his entire retinue, Vidura and Sañjaya approached Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of righteousness (dharma).

sanskrit: एवं तदभवद्युद्धमहान्यष्टादश प्रभो ।यत्र ते पृथिवीपाला निहताः स्वर्गमावसन् ॥३५॥
transliteration: evaṁ tadabhavadyuddhamahānyaṣṭādaśa prabho ,yatra te pṛthivīpālā nihatāḥ svargamāvasan.
english: Thus, O lord, those eighteen great battles occurred, in which those kings, having been slain, went to heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 59 Verse 35. Thus, O lord, those eighteen great battles occurred, in which those kings, having been slain, went to heaven.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।शृण्वतां तु महाराज कथां तां रोमहर्षणीम् ।दुःखहर्षपरिक्लेशा वृष्णीनामभवंस्तदा ॥३६॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,śṛṇvatāṁ tu mahārāja kathāṁ tāṁ romaharṣaṇīm ,duḥkhaharṣaparikleśā vṛṣṇīnāmabhavaṁstadā.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: 'O great king, as they listened to that thrilling story, the Vṛṣṇis then experienced sorrow, joy, and distress.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 59 Verse 36. Vaiśampāyana said: 'O great king, as they listened to that thrilling story, the Vṛṣṇis then experienced sorrow, joy, and distress.'

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।कथयन्नेव तु तदा वासुदेवः प्रतापवान् ।महाभारतयुद्धं तत्कथान्ते पितुरग्रतः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,kathayanneva tu tadā vāsudevaḥ pratāpavān ,mahābhāratayuddhaṁ tatkathānte pituragrataḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: 'Then, the powerful (Vāsudeva) Krishna, while narrating the (Mahābhārata) war, was indeed at the conclusion of that story before his father.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 60 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: 'Then, the powerful (Vāsudeva) Krishna, while narrating the (Mahābhārata) war, was indeed at the conclusion of that story before his father.'

sanskrit: अभिमन्योर्वधं वीरः सोऽत्यक्रामत भारत ।अप्रियं वसुदेवस्य मा भूदिति महामनाः ॥२॥
transliteration: abhimanyorvadhaṁ vīraḥ so'tyakrāmata bhārata ,apriyaṁ vasudevasya mā bhūditi mahāmanāḥ.
english: O Bhārata, that great-minded hero (Krishna) omitted the narration of Abhimanyu's slaying, thinking, 'May this not be disagreeable to (Vasudeva).'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 60 Verse 2. O Bhārata, that great-minded hero (Krishna) omitted the narration of Abhimanyu's slaying, thinking, 'May this not be disagreeable to (Vasudeva).'

sanskrit: मा दौहित्रवधं श्रुत्वा वसुदेवो महात्ययम् ।दुःखशोकाभिसंतप्तो भवेदिति महामतिः ॥३॥
transliteration: mā dauhitravadhaṁ śrutvā vasudevo mahātyayam ,duḥkhaśokābhisaṁtapto bhavediti mahāmatiḥ.
english: Lest Vasudeva, the sagacious one, upon hearing of the great calamity of his grandson's death, should become overcome with intense sorrow and grief.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 60 Verse 3. Lest Vasudeva, the sagacious one, upon hearing of the great calamity of his grandson's death, should become overcome with intense sorrow and grief.

sanskrit: सुभद्रा तु तमुत्क्रान्तमात्मजस्य वधं रणे ।आचक्ष्व कृष्ण सौभद्रवधमित्यपतद्भुवि ॥४॥
transliteration: subhadrā tu tamutkrāntamātmajasya vadhaṁ raṇe ,ācakṣva kṛṣṇa saubhadravadhamityapatadbhuvi.
english: But Subhadra, referring to the death of her son (ātmaja) who had departed in battle, exclaimed, 'Tell me, Krishna, about Saubhadra's death!'  -  and with these words, she collapsed to the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 60 Verse 4. But Subhadra, referring to the death of her son (ātmaja) who had departed in battle, exclaimed, 'Tell me, Krishna, about Saubhadra's death!'  -  and with these words, she collapsed to the ground.

sanskrit: तामपश्यन्निपतितां वसुदेवः क्षितौ तदा ।दृष्ट्वैव च पपातोर्व्यां सोऽपि दुःखेन मूर्छितः ॥५॥
transliteration: tāmapaśyannipatitāṁ vasudevaḥ kṣitau tadā ,dṛṣṭvaiva ca papātorvyāṁ so'pi duḥkhena mūrchitaḥ.
english: Then Vasudeva, seeing her (Subhadra) fallen to the ground, himself, just upon seeing (her), also fell to the earth, unconscious with sorrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 60 Verse 5. Then Vasudeva, seeing her (Subhadra) fallen to the ground, himself, just upon seeing (her), also fell to the earth, unconscious with sorrow.

sanskrit: ततः स दौहित्रवधाद्दुःखशोकसमन्वितः ।वसुदेवो महाराज कृष्णं वाक्यमथाब्रवीत् ॥६॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa dauhitravadhādduḥkhaśokasamanvitaḥ ,vasudevo mahārāja kṛṣṇaṁ vākyamathābravīt.
english: Thereafter, Vasudeva, filled with sorrow and grief due to his grandson's death, then spoke these words to Krishna, O great king.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 60 Verse 6. Thereafter, Vasudeva, filled with sorrow and grief due to his grandson's death, then spoke these words to Krishna, O great king.

sanskrit: ननु त्वं पुण्डरीकाक्ष सत्यवाग्भुवि विश्रुतः ।यद्दौहित्रवधं मेऽद्य न ख्यापयसि शत्रुहन् ॥७॥
transliteration: nanu tvaṁ puṇḍarīkākṣa satyavāgbhuvi viśrutaḥ ,yaddauhitravadhaṁ me'dya na khyāpayasi śatruhan.
english: O lotus-eyed one, surely you are renowned on earth as a speaker of truth. O slayer of enemies, why do you not inform me today about the death of my grandson?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 60 Verse 7. O lotus-eyed one, surely you are renowned on earth as a speaker of truth. O slayer of enemies, why do you not inform me today about the death of my grandson?

sanskrit: तद्भागिनेयनिधनं तत्त्वेनाचक्ष्व मे विभो ।सदृशाक्षस्तव कथं शत्रुभिर्निहतो रणे ॥८॥
transliteration: tadbhāgineyanidhanaṁ tattvenācakṣva me vibho ,sadṛśākṣastava kathaṁ śatrubhirnihato raṇe.
english: Therefore, tell me the truth, O all-pervading one, about the death of your nephew. How was one like him, your equal, slain by enemies in battle?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 60 Verse 8. Therefore, tell me the truth, O all-pervading one, about the death of your nephew. How was one like him, your equal, slain by enemies in battle?

sanskrit: दुर्मरं बत वार्ष्णेय कालेऽप्राप्ते नृभिः सदा ।यत्र मे हृदयं दुःखाच्छतधा न विदीर्यते ॥९॥
transliteration: durmaraṁ bata vārṣṇeya kāle'prāpte nṛbhiḥ sadā ,yatra me hṛdayaṁ duḥkhācchatadhā na vidīryate.
english: Alas, O Vārṣṇeya, it is truly difficult for men to die before their appointed time. How is it that my heart does not split into a hundred pieces from such grief?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 60 Verse 9. Alas, O Vārṣṇeya, it is truly difficult for men to die before their appointed time. How is it that my heart does not split into a hundred pieces from such grief?

sanskrit: किमब्रवीत्त्वा संग्रामे सुभद्रां मातरं प्रति ।मां चापि पुण्डरीकाक्ष चपलाक्षः प्रियो मम ॥१०॥
transliteration: kimabravīttvā saṁgrāme subhadrāṁ mātaraṁ prati ,māṁ cāpi puṇḍarīkākṣa capalākṣaḥ priyo mama.
english: O lotus-eyed one, what did my beloved, the swift-eyed one, say to you in battle concerning his mother Subhadra and also concerning me?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 60 Verse 10. O lotus-eyed one, what did my beloved, the swift-eyed one, say to you in battle concerning his mother Subhadra and also concerning me?

sanskrit: आहवं पृष्ठतः कृत्वा कच्चिन्न निहतः परैः ।कच्चिन्मुखं न गोविन्द तेनाजौ विकृतं कृतम् ॥११॥
transliteration: āhavaṁ pṛṣṭhataḥ kṛtvā kaccinna nihataḥ paraiḥ ,kaccinmukhaṁ na govinda tenājau vikṛtaṁ kṛtam.
english: Having turned his back on the battle, I hope he was not killed by enemies. O Govinda, I hope his face was not disfigured in battle on that account.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 60 Verse 11. Having turned his back on the battle, I hope he was not killed by enemies. O Govinda, I hope his face was not disfigured in battle on that account.

sanskrit: स हि कृष्ण महातेजाः श्लाघन्निव ममाग्रतः ।बालभावेन विजयमात्मनोऽकथयत्प्रभुः ॥१२॥
transliteration: sa hi kṛṣṇa mahātejāḥ ślāghanniva mamāgrataḥ ,bālabhāvena vijayamātmano'kathayatprabhuḥ.
english: Indeed, O Krishna, that mighty one, as if boasting before me, recounted his own victory due to his childishness.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 60 Verse 12. Indeed, O Krishna, that mighty one, as if boasting before me, recounted his own victory due to his childishness.

sanskrit: कच्चिन्न विकृतो बालो द्रोणकर्णकृपादिभिः ।धरण्यां निहतः शेते तन्ममाचक्ष्व केशव ॥१३॥
transliteration: kaccinna vikṛto bālo droṇakarṇakṛpādibhiḥ ,dharaṇyāṁ nihataḥ śete tanmamācakṣva keśava.
english: O Keśava, tell me, I hope the boy was not mutilated and killed by Droṇa, Karṇa, Kṛpa, and others, and now lies on the earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 60 Verse 13. O Keśava, tell me, I hope the boy was not mutilated and killed by Droṇa, Karṇa, Kṛpa, and others, and now lies on the earth.

sanskrit: स हि द्रोणं च भीष्मं च कर्णं च रथिनां वरम् ।स्पर्धते स्म रणे नित्यं दुहितुः पुत्रको मम ॥१४॥
transliteration: sa hi droṇaṁ ca bhīṣmaṁ ca karṇaṁ ca rathināṁ varam ,spardhate sma raṇe nityaṁ duhituḥ putrako mama.
english: Indeed, my daughter's dear son always used to compete with Droṇa, Bhīṣma, and Karṇa, the best among charioteers, in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 60 Verse 14. Indeed, my daughter's dear son always used to compete with Droṇa, Bhīṣma, and Karṇa, the best among charioteers, in battle.

sanskrit: एवंविधं बहु तदा विलपन्तं सुदुःखितम् ।पितरं दुःखिततरो गोविन्दो वाक्यमब्रवीत् ॥१५॥
transliteration: evaṁvidhaṁ bahu tadā vilapantaṁ suduḥkhitam ,pitaraṁ duḥkhitataro govindo vākyamabravīt.
english: Then, seeing his father lamenting profusely in such a deeply distressed state, Govinda, who was even more distressed, spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 60 Verse 15. Then, seeing his father lamenting profusely in such a deeply distressed state, Govinda, who was even more distressed, spoke these words.

sanskrit: न तेन विकृतं वक्त्रं कृतं संग्राममूर्धनि ।न पृष्ठतः कृतश्चापि संग्रामस्तेन दुस्तरः ॥१६॥
transliteration: na tena vikṛtaṁ vaktraṁ kṛtaṁ saṁgrāmamūrdhani ,na pṛṣṭhataḥ kṛtaścāpi saṁgrāmastena dustaraḥ.
english: He never made a distorted face on the battlefield, nor did he turn his back on that exceedingly difficult war.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 60 Verse 16. He never made a distorted face on the battlefield, nor did he turn his back on that exceedingly difficult war.
