sanskrit: निहत्य पृथिवीपालान्सहस्रशतसंघशः ।खेदितो द्रोणकर्णाभ्यां दौःशासनिवशं गतः ॥१७॥
transliteration: nihatya pṛthivīpālānsahasraśatasaṁghaśaḥ ,khedito droṇakarṇābhyāṁ dauḥśāsanivaśaṁ gataḥ.
english: After striking down thousands upon thousands of kings, he was exhausted by Droṇa and Karṇa and eventually fell under the control of Duḥśāsana's son.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 60 Verse 17. After striking down thousands upon thousands of kings, he was exhausted by Droṇa and Karṇa and eventually fell under the control of Duḥśāsana's son.

sanskrit: एको ह्येकेन सततं युध्यमानो यदि प्रभो ।न स शक्येत संग्रामे निहन्तुमपि वज्रिणा ॥१८॥
transliteration: eko hyekena satataṁ yudhyamāno yadi prabho ,na sa śakyeta saṁgrāme nihantumapi vajriṇā.
english: O Lord, if one person were to constantly fight alone against another, he could not be killed in battle, not even by Indra, the wielder of the thunderbolt.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 60 Verse 18. O Lord, if one person were to constantly fight alone against another, he could not be killed in battle, not even by Indra, the wielder of the thunderbolt.

sanskrit: समाहूते तु संग्रामे पार्थे संशप्तकैस्तदा ।पर्यवार्यत संक्रुद्धैः स द्रोणादिभिराहवे ॥१९॥
transliteration: samāhūte tu saṁgrāme pārthe saṁśaptakaistadā ,paryavāryata saṁkruddhaiḥ sa droṇādibhirāhave.
english: Indeed, when Pārtha (Arjuna) was summoned to battle by the Saṃśaptakas, he was then surrounded in that combat by Droṇa and other greatly enraged warriors.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 60 Verse 19. Indeed, when Pārtha (Arjuna) was summoned to battle by the Saṃśaptakas, he was then surrounded in that combat by Droṇa and other greatly enraged warriors.

sanskrit: ततः शत्रुक्षयं कृत्वा सुमहान्तं रणे पितुः ।दौहित्रस्तव वार्ष्णेय दौःशासनिवशं गतः ॥२०॥
transliteration: tataḥ śatrukṣayaṁ kṛtvā sumahāntaṁ raṇe pituḥ ,dauhitrastava vārṣṇeya dauḥśāsanivaśaṁ gataḥ.
english: Then, O Vārṣṇeya (Kṛṣṇa), your nephew, having wrought great destruction of enemies in battle for his father, fell into the hands of Duḥśāsani.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 60 Verse 20. Then, O Vārṣṇeya (Kṛṣṇa), your nephew, having wrought great destruction of enemies in battle for his father, fell into the hands of Duḥśāsani.

sanskrit: नूनं च स गतः स्वर्गं जहि शोकं महामते ।न हि व्यसनमासाद्य सीदन्ते सन्नराः क्वचित् ॥२१॥
transliteration: nūnaṁ ca sa gataḥ svargaṁ jahi śokaṁ mahāmate ,na hi vyasanamāsādya sīdante sannarāḥ kvacit.
english: Certainly, he (Abhimanyu) has gone to heaven. Therefore, O great-minded one, abandon your grief, for virtuous people never despond when they encounter misfortune.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 60 Verse 21. Certainly, he (Abhimanyu) has gone to heaven. Therefore, O great-minded one, abandon your grief, for virtuous people never despond when they encounter misfortune.

sanskrit: द्रोणकर्णप्रभृतयो येन प्रतिसमासिताः ।रणे महेन्द्रप्रतिमाः स कथं नाप्नुयाद्दिवम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: droṇakarṇaprabhṛtayo yena pratisamāsitāḥ ,raṇe mahendrapratimāḥ sa kathaṁ nāpnuyāddivam.
english: How could he, by whom Droṇa, Karṇa, and other warriors resembling mighty Indra were confronted in battle, not attain heaven?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 60 Verse 22. How could he, by whom Droṇa, Karṇa, and other warriors resembling mighty Indra were confronted in battle, not attain heaven?

sanskrit: स शोकं जहि दुर्धर्ष मा च मन्युवशं गमः ।शस्त्रपूतां हि स गतिं गतः परपुरंजयः ॥२३॥
transliteration: sa śokaṁ jahi durdharṣa mā ca manyuvaśaṁ gamaḥ ,śastrapūtāṁ hi sa gatiṁ gataḥ parapuraṁjayaḥ.
english: O formidable one, cast off that grief and do not fall prey to anger. For he, the conqueror of enemy cities, has indeed attained a destination purified by weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 60 Verse 23. O formidable one, cast off that grief and do not fall prey to anger. For he, the conqueror of enemy cities, has indeed attained a destination purified by weapons.

sanskrit: तस्मिंस्तु निहते वीरे सुभद्रेयं स्वसा मम ।दुःखार्ताथो पृथां प्राप्य कुररीव ननाद ह ॥२४॥
transliteration: tasmiṁstu nihate vīre subhadreyaṁ svasā mama ,duḥkhārtātho pṛthāṁ prāpya kurarīva nanāda ha.
english: But when that hero was slain, my sister Subhadrā, overcome with sorrow, then approached Pṛthā (Kunti) and wailed like a curlew.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 60 Verse 24. But when that hero was slain, my sister Subhadrā, overcome with sorrow, then approached Pṛthā (Kunti) and wailed like a curlew.

sanskrit: द्रौपदीं च समासाद्य पर्यपृच्छत दुःखिता ।आर्ये क्व दारकाः सर्वे द्रष्टुमिच्छामि तानहम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: draupadīṁ ca samāsādya paryapṛcchata duḥkhitā ,ārye kva dārakāḥ sarve draṣṭumicchāmi tānaham.
english: Distressed, she then approached Draupadī and asked: 'Noble lady, where are all the children? I wish to see them.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 60 Verse 25. Distressed, she then approached Draupadī and asked: 'Noble lady, where are all the children? I wish to see them.'

sanskrit: अस्यास्तु वचनं श्रुत्वा सर्वास्ताः कुरुयोषितः ।भुजाभ्यां परिगृह्यैनां चुक्रुशुः परमार्तवत् ॥२६॥
transliteration: asyāstu vacanaṁ śrutvā sarvāstāḥ kuruyoṣitaḥ ,bhujābhyāṁ parigṛhyaināṁ cukruśuḥ paramārtavat.
english: But hearing her words, all those Kuru women embraced her with their arms and wailed, as if in extreme anguish.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 60 Verse 26. But hearing her words, all those Kuru women embraced her with their arms and wailed, as if in extreme anguish.

sanskrit: उत्तरां चाब्रवीद्भद्रा भद्रे भर्ता क्व ते गतः ।क्षिप्रमागमनं मह्यं तस्मै त्वं वेदयस्व ह ॥२७॥
transliteration: uttarāṁ cābravīdbhadrā bhadre bhartā kva te gataḥ ,kṣipramāgamanaṁ mahyaṁ tasmai tvaṁ vedayasva ha.
english: Bhadrā then said to Uttarā, "O Bhadrā, where has your husband gone? You must quickly inform him of my arrival."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 60 Verse 27. Bhadrā then said to Uttarā, "O Bhadrā, where has your husband gone? You must quickly inform him of my arrival."

sanskrit: ननु नाम स वैराटि श्रुत्वा मम गिरं पुरा ।भवनान्निष्पतत्याशु कस्मान्नाभ्येति ते पतिः ॥२८॥
transliteration: nanu nāma sa vairāṭi śrutvā mama giraṁ purā ,bhavanānniṣpatatyāśu kasmānnābhyeti te patiḥ.
english: Surely, that son of Virāṭa, having heard my words before, would quickly rush out from the palace. Why does your husband not come to me now?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 60 Verse 28. Surely, that son of Virāṭa, having heard my words before, would quickly rush out from the palace. Why does your husband not come to me now?

sanskrit: अभिमन्यो कुशलिनो मातुलास्ते महारथाः ।कुशलं चाब्रुवन्सर्वे त्वां युयुत्सुमिहागतम् ॥२९॥
transliteration: abhimanyo kuśalino mātulāste mahārathāḥ ,kuśalaṁ cābruvansarve tvāṁ yuyutsumihāgatam.
english: O Abhimanyu, are your maternal uncles, the great charioteers, well? All of them also inquired about your well-being, as you have arrived here eager to fight.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 60 Verse 29. O Abhimanyu, are your maternal uncles, the great charioteers, well? All of them also inquired about your well-being, as you have arrived here eager to fight.

sanskrit: आचक्ष्व मेऽद्य संग्रामं यथापूर्वमरिंदम ।कस्मादेव विलपतीं नाद्येह प्रतिभाषसे ॥३०॥
transliteration: ācakṣva me'dya saṁgrāmaṁ yathāpūrvamariṁdama ,kasmādeva vilapatīṁ nādyeha pratibhāṣase.
english: O vanquisher of foes, tell me today about the battle as before. Why do you not speak to me today, who am lamenting here?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 60 Verse 30. O vanquisher of foes, tell me today about the battle as before. Why do you not speak to me today, who am lamenting here?

sanskrit: एवमादि तु वार्ष्णेय्यास्तदस्याः परिदेवितम् ।श्रुत्वा पृथा सुदुःखार्ता शनैर्वाक्यमथाब्रवीत् ॥३१॥
transliteration: evamādi tu vārṣṇeyyāstadasyāḥ paridevitam ,śrutvā pṛthā suduḥkhārtā śanairvākyamathābravīt.
english: Having heard this lamentation and similar words from Subhadra (the Vrishni woman), Kunti, deeply distressed, then slowly spoke.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 60 Verse 31. Having heard this lamentation and similar words from Subhadra (the Vrishni woman), Kunti, deeply distressed, then slowly spoke.

sanskrit: सुभद्रे वासुदेवेन तथा सात्यकिना रणे ।पित्रा च पालितो बालः स हतः कालधर्मणा ॥३२॥
transliteration: subhadre vāsudevena tathā sātyakinā raṇe ,pitrā ca pālito bālaḥ sa hataḥ kāladharmaṇā.
english: O Subhadra, that child, who was protected in battle by Vasudeva (Krishna), by Satyaki, and by his father (Arjuna), was nevertheless slain by the natural law (kāla dharma) of time.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 60 Verse 32. O Subhadra, that child, who was protected in battle by Vasudeva (Krishna), by Satyaki, and by his father (Arjuna), was nevertheless slain by the natural law (kāla dharma) of time.

sanskrit: ईदृशो मर्त्यधर्मोऽयं मा शुचो यदुनन्दिनि ।पुत्रो हि तव दुर्धर्षः संप्राप्तः परमां गतिम् ॥३३॥
transliteration: īdṛśo martyadharmo'yaṁ mā śuco yadunandini ,putro hi tava durdharṣaḥ saṁprāptaḥ paramāṁ gatim.
english: Such is the natural law (dharma) for mortals; do not grieve, O joy of the Yadus (Subhadra)! Your son, who was irresistible, has indeed attained the supreme state.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 60 Verse 33. Such is the natural law (dharma) for mortals; do not grieve, O joy of the Yadus (Subhadra)! Your son, who was irresistible, has indeed attained the supreme state.

sanskrit: कुले महति जातासि क्षत्रियाणां महात्मनाम् ।मा शुचश्चपलाक्षं त्वं पुण्डरीकनिभेक्षणे ॥३४॥
transliteration: kule mahati jātāsi kṣatriyāṇāṁ mahātmanām ,mā śucaścapalākṣaṁ tvaṁ puṇḍarīkanibhekṣaṇe.
english: You were born into a great lineage of magnanimous warriors (kṣatriyas). Do not grieve, O you with beautiful, restless eyes, whose gaze resembles lotus flowers!
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 60 Verse 34. You were born into a great lineage of magnanimous warriors (kṣatriyas). Do not grieve, O you with beautiful, restless eyes, whose gaze resembles lotus flowers!

sanskrit: उत्तरां त्वमवेक्षस्व गर्भिणीं मा शुचः शुभे ।पुत्रमेषा हि तस्याशु जनयिष्यति भामिनी ॥३५॥
transliteration: uttarāṁ tvamavekṣasva garbhiṇīṁ mā śucaḥ śubhe ,putrameṣā hi tasyāśu janayiṣyati bhāminī.
english: You should look after Uttara, who is pregnant. Do not grieve, O auspicious one! Indeed, this lady will soon give birth to her son.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 60 Verse 35. You should look after Uttara, who is pregnant. Do not grieve, O auspicious one! Indeed, this lady will soon give birth to her son.

sanskrit: एवमाश्वासयित्वैनां कुन्ती यदुकुलोद्वह ।विहाय शोकं दुर्धर्षं श्राद्धमस्य ह्यकल्पयत् ॥३६॥
transliteration: evamāśvāsayitvaināṁ kuntī yadukulodvaha ,vihāya śokaṁ durdharṣaṁ śrāddhamasya hyakalpayat.
english: Having thus consoled her (Uttara), Kunti, O best among the Yadu clan, abandoning her unbearable sorrow, then arranged the funeral ritual (śrāddha) for him.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 60 Verse 36. Having thus consoled her (Uttara), Kunti, O best among the Yadu clan, abandoning her unbearable sorrow, then arranged the funeral ritual (śrāddha) for him.

sanskrit: समनुज्ञाप्य धर्मज्ञा राजानं भीममेव च ।यमौ यमोपमौ चैव ददौ दानान्यनेकशः ॥३७॥
transliteration: samanujñāpya dharmajñā rājānaṁ bhīmameva ca ,yamau yamopamau caiva dadau dānānyanekaśaḥ.
english: Having obtained the assent of the king and Bhima, as well as the twins who were like Yama, Kunti, being conversant with natural law (dharma), gave various donations (dāna).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 60 Verse 37. Having obtained the assent of the king and Bhima, as well as the twins who were like Yama, Kunti, being conversant with natural law (dharma), gave various donations (dāna).

sanskrit: ततः प्रदाय बह्वीर्गा ब्राह्मणेभ्यो यदूद्वह ।समहृष्यत वार्ष्णेयी वैराटीं चाब्रवीदिदम् ॥३८॥
transliteration: tataḥ pradāya bahvīrgā brāhmaṇebhyo yadūdvaha ,samahṛṣyata vārṣṇeyī vairāṭīṁ cābravīdidam.
english: Then, having bestowed many cows upon the Brahmins, O foremost of the Yadus, Kunti, the Vrishni lady, rejoiced and spoke this to Uttara, the princess of Virata.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 60 Verse 38. Then, having bestowed many cows upon the Brahmins, O foremost of the Yadus, Kunti, the Vrishni lady, rejoiced and spoke this to Uttara, the princess of Virata.

sanskrit: वैराटि नेह संतापस्त्वया कार्यो यशस्विनि ।भर्तारं प्रति सुश्रोणि गर्भस्थं रक्ष मे शिशुम् ॥३९॥
transliteration: vairāṭi neha saṁtāpastvayā kāryo yaśasvini ,bhartāraṁ prati suśroṇi garbhasthaṁ rakṣa me śiśum.
english: O glorious one, daughter of Virata, you should not feel sorrow now. O beautiful-hipped one, for the sake of your husband, protect my grandchild, who is still in the womb.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 60 Verse 39. O glorious one, daughter of Virata, you should not feel sorrow now. O beautiful-hipped one, for the sake of your husband, protect my grandchild, who is still in the womb.

sanskrit: एवमुक्त्वा ततः कुन्ती विरराम महाद्युते ।तामनुज्ञाप्य चैवेमां सुभद्रां समुपानयम् ॥४०॥
transliteration: evamuktvā tataḥ kuntī virarāma mahādyute ,tāmanujñāpya caivemāṁ subhadrāṁ samupānayam.
english: Having spoken thus, O greatly radiant one, Kunti then ceased. And having taken leave of her (Uttaraa), I then brought this Subhadra.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 60 Verse 40. Having spoken thus, O greatly radiant one, Kunti then ceased. And having taken leave of her (Uttaraa), I then brought this Subhadra.

sanskrit: एवं स निधनं प्राप्तो दौहित्रस्तव माधव ।संतापं जहि दुर्धर्ष मा च शोके मनः कृथाः ॥४१॥
transliteration: evaṁ sa nidhanaṁ prāpto dauhitrastava mādhava ,saṁtāpaṁ jahi durdharṣa mā ca śoke manaḥ kṛthāḥ.
english: Thus, O Madhava, your grandson met his death. O unconquerable one, abandon your sorrow, and do not let your mind dwell in grief.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 60 Verse 41. Thus, O Madhava, your grandson met his death. O unconquerable one, abandon your sorrow, and do not let your mind dwell in grief.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एतच्छ्रुत्वा तु पुत्रस्य वचः शूरात्मजस्तदा ।विहाय शोकं धर्मात्मा ददौ श्राद्धमनुत्तमम् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,etacchrutvā tu putrasya vacaḥ śūrātmajastadā ,vihāya śokaṁ dharmātmā dadau śrāddhamanuttamam.
english: Vaishampayana said: Indeed, having heard these words of his son (Krishna), the righteous-souled (dharmātmā) son of Shura (Vasudeva) then abandoned his sorrow and performed an excellent śrāddha ritual.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 61 Verse 1. Vaishampayana said: Indeed, having heard these words of his son (Krishna), the righteous-souled (dharmātmā) son of Shura (Vasudeva) then abandoned his sorrow and performed an excellent śrāddha ritual.

sanskrit: तथैव वासुदेवोऽपि स्वस्रीयस्य महात्मनः ।दयितस्य पितुर्नित्यमकरोदौर्ध्वदेहिकम् ॥२॥
transliteration: tathaiva vāsudevo'pi svasrīyasya mahātmanaḥ ,dayitasya piturnityamakarodaurdhvadehikam.
english: Similarly, Vāsudeva (Krishna) also always performed the post-mortem rites for his great-souled and beloved father, and for his nephew.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 61 Verse 2. Similarly, Vāsudeva (Krishna) also always performed the post-mortem rites for his great-souled and beloved father, and for his nephew.

sanskrit: षष्टिं शतसहस्राणि ब्राह्मणानां महाभुजः ।विधिवद्भोजयामास भोज्यं सर्वगुणान्वितम् ॥३॥
transliteration: ṣaṣṭiṁ śatasahasrāṇi brāhmaṇānāṁ mahābhujaḥ ,vidhivadbhojayāmāsa bhojyaṁ sarvaguṇānvitam.
english: The mighty-armed one (Krishna) ritualistically fed six million Brahmins with food endowed with all excellent qualities.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 61 Verse 3. The mighty-armed one (Krishna) ritualistically fed six million Brahmins with food endowed with all excellent qualities.

sanskrit: आच्छाद्य च महाबाहुर्धनतृष्णामपानुदत् ।ब्राह्मणानां तदा कृष्णस्तदभूद्रोमहर्षणम् ॥४॥
transliteration: ācchādya ca mahābāhurdhanatṛṣṇāmapānudat ,brāhmaṇānāṁ tadā kṛṣṇastadabhūdromaharṣaṇam.
english: And the mighty-armed Krishna, having distributed coverings (or gifts), then satisfied the Brahmins' desire for wealth. That event was hair-raising.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 61 Verse 4. And the mighty-armed Krishna, having distributed coverings (or gifts), then satisfied the Brahmins' desire for wealth. That event was hair-raising.

sanskrit: सुवर्णं चैव गाश्चैव शयनाच्छादनं तथा ।दीयमानं तदा विप्राः प्रभूतमिति चाब्रुवन् ॥५॥
transliteration: suvarṇaṁ caiva gāścaiva śayanācchādanaṁ tathā ,dīyamānaṁ tadā viprāḥ prabhūtamiti cābruvan.
english: And gold, and cows, and also bedding and coverings; when these were being distributed, the Brahmins then exclaimed, "This is abundant!"
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 61 Verse 5. And gold, and cows, and also bedding and coverings; when these were being distributed, the Brahmins then exclaimed, "This is abundant!"

sanskrit: वासुदेवोऽथ दाशार्हो बलदेवः ससात्यकिः ।अभिमन्योस्तदा श्राद्धमकुर्वन्सत्यकस्तदा ।अतीव दुःखसंतप्ता न शमं चोपलेभिरे ॥६॥
transliteration: vāsudevo'tha dāśārho baladevaḥ sasātyakiḥ ,abhimanyostadā śrāddhamakurvansatyakastadā ,atīva duḥkhasaṁtaptā na śamaṁ copalebhire.
english: Then Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa), Dāśārha, Baladeva, and Sātyaki, along with Satyaka, performed the post-mortem ritual (śrāddha) for Abhimanyu. Deeply afflicted by sorrow, they found no peace.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 61 Verse 6. Then Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa), Dāśārha, Baladeva, and Sātyaki, along with Satyaka, performed the post-mortem ritual (śrāddha) for Abhimanyu. Deeply afflicted by sorrow, they found no peace.

sanskrit: तथैव पाण्डवा वीरा नगरे नागसाह्वये ।नोपगच्छन्ति वै शान्तिमभिमन्युविनाकृताः ॥७॥
transliteration: tathaiva pāṇḍavā vīrā nagare nāgasāhvaye ,nopagacchanti vai śāntimabhimanyuvinākṛtāḥ.
english: Similarly, the heroic Pāṇḍavas in the city of Nāgasāhvaya (Hastināpura), deprived of Abhimanyu, found no peace.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 61 Verse 7. Similarly, the heroic Pāṇḍavas in the city of Nāgasāhvaya (Hastināpura), deprived of Abhimanyu, found no peace.

sanskrit: सुबहूनि च राजेन्द्र दिवसानि विराटजा ।नाभुङ्क्त पतिशोकार्ता तदभूत्करुणं महत् ।कुक्षिस्थ एव तस्यास्तु स गर्भः संप्रलीयत ॥८॥
transliteration: subahūni ca rājendra divasāni virāṭajā ,nābhuṅkta patiśokārtā tadabhūtkaruṇaṁ mahat ,kukṣistha eva tasyāstu sa garbhaḥ saṁpralīyata.
english: O king (rājendra), for many days, Uttara, the daughter of Virāṭa, afflicted by sorrow for her husband, did not eat. This was a great pity. Indeed, the embryo (garbha) within her womb dissolved.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 61 Verse 8. O king (rājendra), for many days, Uttara, the daughter of Virāṭa, afflicted by sorrow for her husband, did not eat. This was a great pity. Indeed, the embryo (garbha) within her womb dissolved.

sanskrit: आजगाम ततो व्यासो ज्ञात्वा दिव्येन चक्षुषा ।आगम्य चाब्रवीद्धीमान्पृथां पृथुललोचनाम् ।उत्तरां च महातेजाः शोकः संत्यज्यतामयम् ॥९॥
transliteration: ājagāma tato vyāso jñātvā divyena cakṣuṣā ,āgamya cābravīddhīmānpṛthāṁ pṛthulalocanām ,uttarāṁ ca mahātejāḥ śokaḥ saṁtyajyatāmayam.
english: Then Vyāsa arrived, having perceived the situation with his divine eye. Having come, the wise and greatly energetic one spoke to wide-eyed Pṛthā (Kuntī) and to Uttarā: "Let this sorrow be given up!"
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 61 Verse 9. Then Vyāsa arrived, having perceived the situation with his divine eye. Having come, the wise and greatly energetic one spoke to wide-eyed Pṛthā (Kuntī) and to Uttarā: "Let this sorrow be given up!"

sanskrit: जनिष्यति महातेजाः पुत्रस्तव यशस्विनि ।प्रभावाद्वासुदेवस्य मम व्याहरणादपि ।पाण्डवानामयं चान्ते पालयिष्यति मेदिनीम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: janiṣyati mahātejāḥ putrastava yaśasvini ,prabhāvādvāsudevasya mama vyāharaṇādapi ,pāṇḍavānāmayaṁ cānte pālayiṣyati medinīm.
english: O glorious one, your greatly radiant son will be born through the power of Vāsudeva and also by my declaration. And this one, in the end, will rule the earth for the Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 61 Verse 10. O glorious one, your greatly radiant son will be born through the power of Vāsudeva and also by my declaration. And this one, in the end, will rule the earth for the Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: धनंजयं च संप्रेक्ष्य धर्मराजस्य पश्यतः ।व्यासो वाक्यमुवाचेदं हर्षयन्निव भारत ॥११॥
transliteration: dhanaṁjayaṁ ca saṁprekṣya dharmarājasya paśyataḥ ,vyāso vākyamuvācedaṁ harṣayanniva bhārata.
english: And, having thoroughly observed Dhanañjaya (Arjuna) in the presence of the king of natural law (dharma) (Yudhiṣṭhira), Vyāsa spoke these words, as if bringing joy, O Bhārata.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 61 Verse 11. And, having thoroughly observed Dhanañjaya (Arjuna) in the presence of the king of natural law (dharma) (Yudhiṣṭhira), Vyāsa spoke these words, as if bringing joy, O Bhārata.

sanskrit: पौत्रस्तव महाबाहो जनिष्यति महामनाः ।पृथ्वीं सागरपर्यन्तां पालयिष्यति चैव ह ॥१२॥
transliteration: pautrastava mahābāho janiṣyati mahāmanāḥ ,pṛthvīṁ sāgaraparyantāṁ pālayiṣyati caiva ha.
english: O mighty-armed one, your noble-minded grandson will be born. And he will certainly rule the earth, extending all the way to the oceans.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 61 Verse 12. O mighty-armed one, your noble-minded grandson will be born. And he will certainly rule the earth, extending all the way to the oceans.

sanskrit: तस्माच्छोकं कुरुश्रेष्ठ जहि त्वमरिकर्शन ।विचार्यमत्र न हि ते सत्यमेतद्भविष्यति ॥१३॥
transliteration: tasmācchokaṁ kuruśreṣṭha jahi tvamarikarśana ,vicāryamatra na hi te satyametadbhaviṣyati.
english: Therefore, O best among the Kurus, O tormentor of enemies, abandon your grief. Indeed, there is nothing for you to ponder here; this will certainly come true.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 61 Verse 13. Therefore, O best among the Kurus, O tormentor of enemies, abandon your grief. Indeed, there is nothing for you to ponder here; this will certainly come true.

sanskrit: यच्चापि वृष्णिवीरेण कृष्णेन कुरुनन्दन ।पुरोक्तं तत्तथा भावि मा तेऽत्रास्तु विचारणा ॥१४॥
transliteration: yaccāpi vṛṣṇivīreṇa kṛṣṇena kurunandana ,puroktaṁ tattathā bhāvi mā te'trāstu vicāraṇā.
english: And whatever was stated previously by Kṛṣṇa, the hero of the Vṛṣṇis, O joy of the Kurus, that will surely come to pass exactly as predicted. Therefore, do not have any doubt concerning this.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 61 Verse 14. And whatever was stated previously by Kṛṣṇa, the hero of the Vṛṣṇis, O joy of the Kurus, that will surely come to pass exactly as predicted. Therefore, do not have any doubt concerning this.

sanskrit: विबुधानां गतो लोकानक्षयानात्मनिर्जितान् ।न स शोच्यस्त्वया तात न चान्यैः कुरुभिस्तथा ॥१५॥
transliteration: vibudhānāṁ gato lokānakṣayānātmanirjitān ,na sa śocyastvayā tāta na cānyaiḥ kurubhistathā.
english: He has departed to the imperishable worlds of the gods, which were won by his own spiritual effort (ātman). He should not be grieved for by you, dear son, nor similarly by the other Kurus.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 61 Verse 15. He has departed to the imperishable worlds of the gods, which were won by his own spiritual effort (ātman). He should not be grieved for by you, dear son, nor similarly by the other Kurus.

sanskrit: एवं पितामहेनोक्तो धर्मात्मा स धनंजयः ।त्यक्त्वा शोकं महाराज हृष्टरूपोऽभवत्तदा ॥१६॥
transliteration: evaṁ pitāmahenokto dharmātmā sa dhanaṁjayaḥ ,tyaktvā śokaṁ mahārāja hṛṣṭarūpo'bhavattadā.
english: Thus addressed by his grandfather (Bhīṣma), that righteous-souled (dharmātman) Dhanañjaya, O great king, then abandoned his grief and became joyful.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 61 Verse 16. Thus addressed by his grandfather (Bhīṣma), that righteous-souled (dharmātman) Dhanañjaya, O great king, then abandoned his grief and became joyful.

sanskrit: पितापि तव धर्मज्ञ गर्भे तस्मिन्महामते ।अवर्धत यथाकालं शुक्लपक्षे यथा शशी ॥१७॥
transliteration: pitāpi tava dharmajña garbhe tasminmahāmate ,avardhata yathākālaṁ śuklapakṣe yathā śaśī.
english: Your father, O knower of constitutional law (dharma), O highly intelligent one, also grew in that womb in due course, just as the moon (śaśī) grows during the bright fortnight.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 61 Verse 17. Your father, O knower of constitutional law (dharma), O highly intelligent one, also grew in that womb in due course, just as the moon (śaśī) grows during the bright fortnight.

sanskrit: ततः संचोदयामास व्यासो धर्मात्मजं नृपम् ।अश्वमेधं प्रति तदा ततः सोऽन्तर्हितोऽभवत् ॥१८॥
transliteration: tataḥ saṁcodayāmāsa vyāso dharmātmajaṁ nṛpam ,aśvamedhaṁ prati tadā tataḥ so'ntarhito'bhavat.
english: Then, Vyasa encouraged the king, Yudhishthira, the son of dharma (dharma), concerning the aśvamedha (Vedic ritual). After that, he (Vyasa) vanished.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 61 Verse 18. Then, Vyasa encouraged the king, Yudhishthira, the son of dharma (dharma), concerning the aśvamedha (Vedic ritual). After that, he (Vyasa) vanished.

sanskrit: धर्मराजोऽपि मेधावी श्रुत्वा व्यासस्य तद्वचः ।वित्तोपनयने तात चकार गमने मतिम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: dharmarājo'pi medhāvī śrutvā vyāsasya tadvacaḥ ,vittopanayane tāta cakāra gamane matim.
english: O dear one (tāta), the intelligent king Yudhishthira (dharma-rāja), having heard that speech of Vyasa, made up his mind to go for the acquisition of wealth.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 61 Verse 19. O dear one (tāta), the intelligent king Yudhishthira (dharma-rāja), having heard that speech of Vyasa, made up his mind to go for the acquisition of wealth.

sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।श्रुत्वैतद्वचनं ब्रह्मन्व्यासेनोक्तं महात्मना ।अश्वमेधं प्रति तदा किं नृपः प्रचकार ह ॥१॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,śrutvaitadvacanaṁ brahmanvyāsenoktaṁ mahātmanā ,aśvamedhaṁ prati tadā kiṁ nṛpaḥ pracakāra ha.
english: Janamejaya said: "O Brahmin (brahman), having heard this instruction concerning the aśvamedha (Vedic ritual), which was spoken by the great sage Vyasa, what did the king then actually do?"
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 62 Verse 1. Janamejaya said: "O Brahmin (brahman), having heard this instruction concerning the aśvamedha (Vedic ritual), which was spoken by the great sage Vyasa, what did the king then actually do?"

sanskrit: रत्नं च यन्मरुत्तेन निहितं पृथिवीतले ।तदवाप कथं चेति तन्मे ब्रूहि द्विजोत्तम ॥२॥
transliteration: ratnaṁ ca yanmaruttena nihitaṁ pṛthivītale ,tadavāpa kathaṁ ceti tanme brūhi dvijottama.
english: And how did he acquire that treasure, which was placed on the surface of the earth by Marutta? Please tell me that, O best among the twice-born (dvija)!
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 62 Verse 2. And how did he acquire that treasure, which was placed on the surface of the earth by Marutta? Please tell me that, O best among the twice-born (dvija)!

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।श्रुत्वा द्वैपायनवचो धर्मराजो युधिष्ठिरः ।भ्रातॄन्सर्वान्समानाय्य काले वचनमब्रवीत् ।अर्जुनं भीमसेनं च माद्रीपुत्रौ यमावपि ॥३॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,śrutvā dvaipāyanavaco dharmarājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,bhrātṝnsarvānsamānāyya kāle vacanamabravīt ,arjunaṁ bhīmasenaṁ ca mādrīputrau yamāvapi.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Having heard the words of Dvaipāyana, Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of natural law (dharma-rāja), summoned all his brothers - Arjuna, Bhīmasena, and also the two sons of Mādrī (the twins, Nakula and Sahadeva) - and then spoke these words at the opportune moment.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 62 Verse 3. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Having heard the words of Dvaipāyana, Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of natural law (dharma-rāja), summoned all his brothers - Arjuna, Bhīmasena, and also the two sons of Mādrī (the twins, Nakula and Sahadeva) - and then spoke these words at the opportune moment.

sanskrit: श्रुतं वो वचनं वीराः सौहृदाद्यन्महात्मना ।कुरूणां हितकामेन प्रोक्तं कृष्णेन धीमता ॥४॥
transliteration: śrutaṁ vo vacanaṁ vīrāḥ sauhṛdādyanmahātmanā ,kurūṇāṁ hitakāmena proktaṁ kṛṣṇena dhīmatā.
english: O heroes, you have heard those words which were spoken out of friendship by the great-souled, intelligent Kṛṣṇa, who desired the welfare of the Kurus.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 62 Verse 4. O heroes, you have heard those words which were spoken out of friendship by the great-souled, intelligent Kṛṣṇa, who desired the welfare of the Kurus.

sanskrit: तपोवृद्धेन महता सुहृदां भूतिमिच्छता ।गुरुणा धर्मशीलेन व्यासेनाद्भुतकर्मणा ॥५॥
transliteration: tapovṛddhena mahatā suhṛdāṁ bhūtimicchatā ,guruṇā dharmaśīlena vyāsenādbhutakarmaṇā.
english: Also by the great Vyāsa, the teacher (guru), who has grown great through asceticism (tapas), who desires the welfare of his friends, who is of righteous conduct (dharma), and who performs wondrous deeds.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 62 Verse 5. Also by the great Vyāsa, the teacher (guru), who has grown great through asceticism (tapas), who desires the welfare of his friends, who is of righteous conduct (dharma), and who performs wondrous deeds.

sanskrit: भीष्मेण च महाप्राज्ञ गोविन्देन च धीमता ।संस्मृत्य तदहं सम्यक्कर्तुमिच्छामि पाण्डवाः ॥६॥
transliteration: bhīṣmeṇa ca mahāprājña govindena ca dhīmatā ,saṁsmṛtya tadahaṁ samyakkartumicchāmi pāṇḍavāḥ.
english: And by the greatly wise Bhīṣma, and by the intelligent Govinda (Kṛṣṇa). Remembering that (advice) thoroughly, O Pāṇḍavas, I desire to act.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 62 Verse 6. And by the greatly wise Bhīṣma, and by the intelligent Govinda (Kṛṣṇa). Remembering that (advice) thoroughly, O Pāṇḍavas, I desire to act.

sanskrit: आयत्यां च तदात्वे च सर्वेषां तद्धि नो हितम् ।अनुबन्धे च कल्याणं यद्वचो ब्रह्मवादिनः ॥७॥
transliteration: āyatyāṁ ca tadātve ca sarveṣāṁ taddhi no hitam ,anubandhe ca kalyāṇaṁ yadvaco brahmavādinaḥ.
english: Indeed, that counsel of the knowers of sacred texts (brahmavādinaḥ) is beneficial for us all, both in the present and in the future, and its long-term consequence is auspicious.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 62 Verse 7. Indeed, that counsel of the knowers of sacred texts (brahmavādinaḥ) is beneficial for us all, both in the present and in the future, and its long-term consequence is auspicious.

sanskrit: इयं हि वसुधा सर्वा क्षीणरत्ना कुरूद्वहाः ।तच्चाचष्ट बहु व्यासो मरुत्तस्य धनं नृपाः ॥८॥
transliteration: iyaṁ hi vasudhā sarvā kṣīṇaratnā kurūdvahāḥ ,taccācaṣṭa bahu vyāso maruttasya dhanaṁ nṛpāḥ.
english: O leaders of the Kurus, this entire earth is indeed diminished of its treasures. And Vyasa often recounted that great wealth of Marutta, O kings.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 62 Verse 8. O leaders of the Kurus, this entire earth is indeed diminished of its treasures. And Vyasa often recounted that great wealth of Marutta, O kings.

sanskrit: यद्येतद्वो बहुमतं मन्यध्वं वा क्षमं यदि ।तदानयामहे सर्वे कथं वा भीम मन्यसे ॥९॥
transliteration: yadyetadvo bahumataṁ manyadhvaṁ vā kṣamaṁ yadi ,tadānayāmahe sarve kathaṁ vā bhīma manyase.
english: If this is highly approved by you, or if you consider it proper, then all of us shall bring it. Or, what do you think, O Bhima?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 62 Verse 9. If this is highly approved by you, or if you consider it proper, then all of us shall bring it. Or, what do you think, O Bhima?

sanskrit: इत्युक्तवाक्ये नृपतौ तदा कुरुकुलोद्वह ।भीमसेनो नृपश्रेष्ठं प्राञ्जलिर्वाक्यमब्रवीत् ॥१०॥
transliteration: ityuktavākye nṛpatau tadā kurukulodvaha ,bhīmaseno nṛpaśreṣṭhaṁ prāñjalirvākyamabravīt.
english: When the king had thus spoken, then, O upholder of the Kuru lineage, Bhimasena, with folded hands, addressed these words to the foremost of kings.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 62 Verse 10. When the king had thus spoken, then, O upholder of the Kuru lineage, Bhimasena, with folded hands, addressed these words to the foremost of kings.

sanskrit: रोचते मे महाबाहो यदिदं भाषितं त्वया ।व्यासाख्यातस्य वित्तस्य समुपानयनं प्रति ॥११॥
transliteration: rocate me mahābāho yadidaṁ bhāṣitaṁ tvayā ,vyāsākhyātasya vittasya samupānayanaṁ prati.
english: O mighty-armed one, I am pleased by this speech of yours concerning the acquisition of the knowledge (vitta) narrated by Vyāsa.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 62 Verse 11. O mighty-armed one, I am pleased by this speech of yours concerning the acquisition of the knowledge (vitta) narrated by Vyāsa.

sanskrit: यदि तत्प्राप्नुयामेह धनमाविक्षितं प्रभो ।कृतमेव महाराज भवेदिति मतिर्मम ॥१२॥
transliteration: yadi tatprāpnuyāmeha dhanamāvikṣitaṁ prabho ,kṛtameva mahārāja bhavediti matirmama.
english: O lord, if I were to obtain that desired wealth (dhanam) here, then, O great king, it would indeed be accomplished – such is my conviction (mati).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 62 Verse 12. O lord, if I were to obtain that desired wealth (dhanam) here, then, O great king, it would indeed be accomplished – such is my conviction (mati).

sanskrit: ते वयं प्रणिपातेन गिरीशस्य महात्मनः ।तदानयाम भद्रं ते समभ्यर्च्य कपर्दिनम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: te vayaṁ praṇipātena girīśasya mahātmanaḥ ,tadānayāma bhadraṁ te samabhyarcya kapardinam.
english: Having prostrated before the great-souled Lord of mountains (Girīśa) and having duly worshipped Kapardin (Śiva), we shall then acquire that. May it be well with you.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 62 Verse 13. Having prostrated before the great-souled Lord of mountains (Girīśa) and having duly worshipped Kapardin (Śiva), we shall then acquire that. May it be well with you.

sanskrit: तं विभुं देवदेवेशं तस्यैवानुचरांश्च तान् ।प्रसाद्यार्थमवाप्स्यामो नूनं वाग्बुद्धिकर्मभिः ॥१४॥
transliteration: taṁ vibhuṁ devadeveśaṁ tasyaivānucarāṁśca tān ,prasādyārthamavāpsyāmo nūnaṁ vāgbuddhikarmabhiḥ.
english: Having propitiated that all-pervading one, the Lord of lords (Śiva), and indeed his followers, we shall certainly attain our objective (artha) through speech, intellect, and action (karma).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 62 Verse 14. Having propitiated that all-pervading one, the Lord of lords (Śiva), and indeed his followers, we shall certainly attain our objective (artha) through speech, intellect, and action (karma).

sanskrit: रक्षन्ते ये च तद्द्रव्यं किंकरा रौद्रदर्शनाः ।ते च वश्या भविष्यन्ति प्रसन्ने वृषभध्वजे ॥१५॥
transliteration: rakṣante ye ca taddravyaṁ kiṁkarā raudradarśanāḥ ,te ca vaśyā bhaviṣyanti prasanne vṛṣabhadhvaje.
english: And those terrifying-looking servants who guard that treasure will become submissive when Śiva, who has the bull banner, is pleased.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 62 Verse 15. And those terrifying-looking servants who guard that treasure will become submissive when Śiva, who has the bull banner, is pleased.

sanskrit: श्रुत्वैवं वदतस्तस्य वाक्यं भीमस्य भारत ।प्रीतो धर्मात्मजो राजा बभूवातीव भारत ।अर्जुनप्रमुखाश्चापि तथेत्येवाब्रुवन्मुदा ॥१६॥
transliteration: śrutvaivaṁ vadatastasya vākyaṁ bhīmasya bhārata ,prīto dharmātmajo rājā babhūvātīva bhārata ,arjunapramukhāścāpi tathetyevābruvanmudā.
english: O Bhārata, having heard Bhima speak thus, the righteous king (dharmātmajaḥ) became exceedingly pleased. And Arjuna and the others also joyfully said, 'So be it!'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 62 Verse 16. O Bhārata, having heard Bhima speak thus, the righteous king (dharmātmajaḥ) became exceedingly pleased. And Arjuna and the others also joyfully said, 'So be it!'

sanskrit: कृत्वा तु पाण्डवाः सर्वे रत्नाहरणनिश्चयम् ।सेनामाज्ञापयामासुर्नक्षत्रेऽहनि च ध्रुवे ॥१७॥
transliteration: kṛtvā tu pāṇḍavāḥ sarve ratnāharaṇaniścayam ,senāmājñāpayāmāsurnakṣatre'hani ca dhruve.
english: After making the decision to seize the jewels, all the Pāṇḍavas then commanded their army on an auspicious day and in a steady asterism.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 62 Verse 17. After making the decision to seize the jewels, all the Pāṇḍavas then commanded their army on an auspicious day and in a steady asterism.

sanskrit: ततो ययुः पाण्डुसुता ब्राह्मणान्स्वस्ति वाच्य च ।अर्चयित्वा सुरश्रेष्ठं पूर्वमेव महेश्वरम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: tato yayuḥ pāṇḍusutā brāhmaṇānsvasti vācya ca ,arcayitvā suraśreṣṭhaṁ pūrvameva maheśvaram.
english: Then, the sons of Pāṇḍu proceeded, after first having the brahmins utter blessings and having worshipped Maheśvara (Śiva), the best of the gods.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 62 Verse 18. Then, the sons of Pāṇḍu proceeded, after first having the brahmins utter blessings and having worshipped Maheśvara (Śiva), the best of the gods.

sanskrit: मोदकैः पायसेनाथ मांसापूपैस्तथैव च ।आशास्य च महात्मानं प्रययुर्मुदिता भृशम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: modakaiḥ pāyasenātha māṁsāpūpaistathaiva ca ,āśāsya ca mahātmānaṁ prayayurmuditā bhṛśam.
english: After invoking blessings for the revered one (meaning the supposedly deceased Pāṇḍavas) with sweetmeats, rice pudding, and also meat cakes, they (the citizens) departed greatly pleased.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 62 Verse 19. After invoking blessings for the revered one (meaning the supposedly deceased Pāṇḍavas) with sweetmeats, rice pudding, and also meat cakes, they (the citizens) departed greatly pleased.

sanskrit: तेषां प्रयास्यतां तत्र मङ्गलानि शुभान्यथ ।प्राहुः प्रहृष्टमनसो द्विजाग्र्या नागराश्च ते ॥२०॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ prayāsyatāṁ tatra maṅgalāni śubhānyatha ,prāhuḥ prahṛṣṭamanaso dvijāgryā nāgarāśca te.
english: Then, the chief Brahmins and those citizens, with joyful minds, uttered auspicious and propitious words for those (Pāṇḍavas) who were departing (from life).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 62 Verse 20. Then, the chief Brahmins and those citizens, with joyful minds, uttered auspicious and propitious words for those (Pāṇḍavas) who were departing (from life).

sanskrit: ततः प्रदक्षिणीकृत्य शिरोभिः प्रणिपत्य च ।ब्राह्मणानग्निसहितान्प्रययुः पाण्डुनन्दनाः ॥२१॥
transliteration: tataḥ pradakṣiṇīkṛtya śirobhiḥ praṇipatya ca ,brāhmaṇānagnisahitānprayayuḥ pāṇḍunandanāḥ.
english: Thereafter, having circumambulated and bowed down with their heads to the Brahmins who were accompanied by their sacred fires, the sons of Pāṇḍu (Pāṇḍavas) departed.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 62 Verse 21. Thereafter, having circumambulated and bowed down with their heads to the Brahmins who were accompanied by their sacred fires, the sons of Pāṇḍu (Pāṇḍavas) departed.

sanskrit: समनुज्ञाप्य राजानं पुत्रशोकसमाहतम् ।धृतराष्ट्रं सभार्यं वै पृथां पृथुललोचनाम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: samanujñāpya rājānaṁ putraśokasamāhatam ,dhṛtarāṣṭraṁ sabhāryaṁ vai pṛthāṁ pṛthulalocanām.
english: Having bid farewell to King Dhritarashtra, who was afflicted by grief for his sons, and who was accompanied by his wife (Gāndhārī), and also to Pritha (Kunti) of the wide eyes.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 62 Verse 22. Having bid farewell to King Dhritarashtra, who was afflicted by grief for his sons, and who was accompanied by his wife (Gāndhārī), and also to Pritha (Kunti) of the wide eyes.

sanskrit: मूले निक्षिप्य कौरव्यं युयुत्सुं धृतराष्ट्रजम् ।संपूज्यमानाः पौरैश्च ब्राह्मणैश्च मनीषिभिः ॥२३॥
transliteration: mūle nikṣipya kauravyaṁ yuyutsuṁ dhṛtarāṣṭrajam ,saṁpūjyamānāḥ pauraiśca brāhmaṇaiśca manīṣibhiḥ.
english: Having entrusted the Kuru prince Yuyutsu, son of Dhritarashtra, with the primary administrative position, they (the Pāṇḍavas) were being honored by the citizens, as well as by the wise brahmins.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 62 Verse 23. Having entrusted the Kuru prince Yuyutsu, son of Dhritarashtra, with the primary administrative position, they (the Pāṇḍavas) were being honored by the citizens, as well as by the wise brahmins.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततस्ते प्रययुर्हृष्टाः प्रहृष्टनरवाहनाः ।रथघोषेण महता पूरयन्तो वसुंधराम् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tataste prayayurhṛṣṭāḥ prahṛṣṭanaravāhanāḥ ,rathaghoṣeṇa mahatā pūrayanto vasuṁdharām.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Thereafter, they departed joyful, with their men and conveyances greatly delighted, filling the earth with a great roar of chariots.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 63 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Thereafter, they departed joyful, with their men and conveyances greatly delighted, filling the earth with a great roar of chariots.

sanskrit: संस्तूयमानाः स्तुतिभिः सूतमागधबन्दिभिः ।स्वेन सैन्येन संवीता यथादित्याः स्वरश्मिभिः ॥२॥
transliteration: saṁstūyamānāḥ stutibhiḥ sūtamāgadhabandibhiḥ ,svena sainyena saṁvītā yathādityāḥ svaraśmibhiḥ.
english: Being highly praised with eulogies by charioteers, bards, and panegyrists, they were surrounded by their own army, just as the solar deities (ādityas) are enveloped by their own rays.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 63 Verse 2. Being highly praised with eulogies by charioteers, bards, and panegyrists, they were surrounded by their own army, just as the solar deities (ādityas) are enveloped by their own rays.

sanskrit: पाण्डुरेणातपत्रेण ध्रियमाणेन मूर्धनि ।बभौ युधिष्ठिरस्तत्र पौर्णमास्यामिवोडुराट् ॥३॥
transliteration: pāṇḍureṇātapatreṇa dhriyamāṇena mūrdhani ,babhau yudhiṣṭhirastatra paurṇamāsyāmivoḍurāṭ.
english: With a white umbrella being held over his head, Yudhishthira shone there like the moon (uḍurāṭ) on a full moon night.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 63 Verse 3. With a white umbrella being held over his head, Yudhishthira shone there like the moon (uḍurāṭ) on a full moon night.

sanskrit: जयाशिषः प्रहृष्टानां नराणां पथि पाण्डवः ।प्रत्यगृह्णाद्यथान्यायं यथावत्पुरुषर्षभः ॥४॥
transliteration: jayāśiṣaḥ prahṛṣṭānāṁ narāṇāṁ pathi pāṇḍavaḥ ,pratyagṛhṇādyathānyāyaṁ yathāvatpuruṣarṣabhaḥ.
english: On the road, the Pandava, that bull among men, duly and properly received the blessings of victory from the joyful people.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 63 Verse 4. On the road, the Pandava, that bull among men, duly and properly received the blessings of victory from the joyful people.

sanskrit: तथैव सैनिका राजन्राजानमनुयान्ति ये ।तेषां हलहलाशब्दो दिवं स्तब्ध्वा व्यतिष्ठत ॥५॥
transliteration: tathaiva sainikā rājanrājānamanuyānti ye ,teṣāṁ halahalāśabdo divaṁ stabdhvā vyatiṣṭhata.
english: O King, similarly, the soldiers who followed the king raised a tumultuous shout (halahalāśabda) which, having filled the sky, remained there.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 63 Verse 5. O King, similarly, the soldiers who followed the king raised a tumultuous shout (halahalāśabda) which, having filled the sky, remained there.

sanskrit: स सरांसि नदीश्चैव वनान्युपवनानि च ।अत्यक्रामन्महाराजो गिरिं चैवान्वपद्यत ॥६॥
transliteration: sa sarāṁsi nadīścaiva vanānyupavanāni ca ,atyakrāmanmahārājo giriṁ caivānvapadyata.
english: That great king crossed over lakes, rivers, forests, and groves, and then indeed reached a mountain.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 63 Verse 6. That great king crossed over lakes, rivers, forests, and groves, and then indeed reached a mountain.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्देशे च राजेन्द्र यत्र तद्द्रव्यमुत्तमम् ।चक्रे निवेशनं राजा पाण्डवः सह सैनिकैः ।शिवे देशे समे चैव तदा भरतसत्तम ॥७॥
transliteration: tasmindeśe ca rājendra yatra taddravyamuttamam ,cakre niveśanaṁ rājā pāṇḍavaḥ saha sainikaiḥ ,śive deśe same caiva tadā bharatasattama.
english: And O best of kings (rājendra), in that region where there was excellent treasure, the Pandava king, with his soldiers, then made an encampment in an auspicious and level place, O best of Bharatas (bharatasattama).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 63 Verse 7. And O best of kings (rājendra), in that region where there was excellent treasure, the Pandava king, with his soldiers, then made an encampment in an auspicious and level place, O best of Bharatas (bharatasattama).

sanskrit: अग्रतो ब्राह्मणान्कृत्वा तपोविद्यादमान्वितान् ।पुरोहितं च कौरव्य वेदवेदाङ्गपारगम् ॥८॥
transliteration: agrato brāhmaṇānkṛtvā tapovidyādamānvitān ,purohitaṁ ca kauravya vedavedāṅgapāragam.
english: O descendant of Kuru, having placed in front the Brahmins who were endowed with spiritual discipline (tapas), knowledge, and self-control, as well as the chief priest who had mastered the Vedas and their auxiliary branches.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 63 Verse 8. O descendant of Kuru, having placed in front the Brahmins who were endowed with spiritual discipline (tapas), knowledge, and self-control, as well as the chief priest who had mastered the Vedas and their auxiliary branches.

sanskrit: प्राङ्निवेशात्तु राजानं ब्राह्मणाः सपुरोधसः ।कृत्वा शान्तिं यथान्यायं सर्वतः पर्यवारयन् ॥९॥
transliteration: prāṅniveśāttu rājānaṁ brāhmaṇāḥ sapurodhasaḥ ,kṛtvā śāntiṁ yathānyāyaṁ sarvataḥ paryavārayan.
english: Indeed, before the establishment of the settlement, the Brahmins, accompanied by their chief priest, performed the propitiatory rites (śānti) according to custom and then surrounded the king from all sides.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 63 Verse 9. Indeed, before the establishment of the settlement, the Brahmins, accompanied by their chief priest, performed the propitiatory rites (śānti) according to custom and then surrounded the king from all sides.

sanskrit: कृत्वा च मध्ये राजानममात्यांश्च यथाविधि ।षट्पथं नवसंस्थानं निवेशं चक्रिरे द्विजाः ॥१०॥
transliteration: kṛtvā ca madhye rājānamamātyāṁśca yathāvidhi ,ṣaṭpathaṁ navasaṁsthānaṁ niveśaṁ cakrire dvijāḥ.
english: And after duly placing the king and his ministers in the center, the Brahmins established a new settlement (niveśa) with six main roads, structured according to proper arrangements.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 63 Verse 10. And after duly placing the king and his ministers in the center, the Brahmins established a new settlement (niveśa) with six main roads, structured according to proper arrangements.

sanskrit: मत्तानां वारणेन्द्राणां निवेशं च यथाविधि ।कारयित्वा स राजेन्द्रो ब्राह्मणानिदमब्रवीत् ॥११॥
transliteration: mattānāṁ vāraṇendrāṇāṁ niveśaṁ ca yathāvidhi ,kārayitvā sa rājendro brāhmaṇānidamabravīt.
english: And after having properly established the enclosures for the rutting, lordly elephants, that excellent king then said this to the Brahmins.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 63 Verse 11. And after having properly established the enclosures for the rutting, lordly elephants, that excellent king then said this to the Brahmins.

sanskrit: अस्मिन्कार्ये द्विजश्रेष्ठा नक्षत्रे दिवसे शुभे ।यथा भवन्तो मन्यन्ते कर्तुमर्हथ तत्तथा ॥१२॥
transliteration: asminkārye dvijaśreṣṭhā nakṣatre divase śubhe ,yathā bhavanto manyante kartumarhatha tattathā.
english: O best among the twice-born (dvija), concerning this task, on an auspicious constellation and day, you should proceed exactly as you deem appropriate.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 63 Verse 12. O best among the twice-born (dvija), concerning this task, on an auspicious constellation and day, you should proceed exactly as you deem appropriate.

sanskrit: न नः कालात्ययो वै स्यादिहैव परिलम्बताम् ।इति निश्चित्य विप्रेन्द्राः क्रियतां यदनन्तरम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: na naḥ kālātyayo vai syādihaiva parilambatām ,iti niścitya viprendrāḥ kriyatāṁ yadanantaram.
english: Indeed, there should be no waste of our time by lingering here. Therefore, O chiefs of brahmins, having resolved this, let what comes next be done.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 63 Verse 13. Indeed, there should be no waste of our time by lingering here. Therefore, O chiefs of brahmins, having resolved this, let what comes next be done.

sanskrit: श्रुत्वैतद्वचनं राज्ञो ब्राह्मणाः सपुरोधसः ।इदमूचुर्वचो हृष्टा धर्मराजप्रियेप्सवः ॥१४॥
transliteration: śrutvaitadvacanaṁ rājño brāhmaṇāḥ sapurodhasaḥ ,idamūcurvaco hṛṣṭā dharmarājapriyepsavaḥ.
english: Having heard this speech from the king, the Brahmins, along with their chief priests, delighted and eager to please the King of Dharma (dharmarāja), spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 63 Verse 14. Having heard this speech from the king, the Brahmins, along with their chief priests, delighted and eager to please the King of Dharma (dharmarāja), spoke these words.

sanskrit: अद्यैव नक्षत्रमहश्च पुण्यं यतामहे श्रेष्ठतमं क्रियासु ।अम्भोभिरद्येह वसाम राजन्नुपोष्यतां चापि भवद्भिरद्य ॥१५॥
transliteration: adyaiva nakṣatramahaśca puṇyaṁ; yatāmahe śreṣṭhatamaṁ kriyāsu ,ambhobhiradyeha vasāma rāja;nnupoṣyatāṁ cāpi bhavadbhiradya.
english: Today itself the constellation and the day are auspicious, and we shall undertake the most excellent rituals. O King, we shall reside here today, sustained by water, and let fasting also be observed by you today.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 63 Verse 15. Today itself the constellation and the day are auspicious, and we shall undertake the most excellent rituals. O King, we shall reside here today, sustained by water, and let fasting also be observed by you today.

sanskrit: श्रुत्वा तु तेषां द्विजसत्तमानां कृतोपवासा रजनीं नरेन्द्राः ।ऊषुः प्रतीताः कुशसंस्तरेषु यथाध्वरेषु ज्वलिता हव्यवाहाः ॥१६॥
transliteration: śrutvā tu teṣāṁ dvijasattamānāṁ; kṛtopavāsā rajanīṁ narendrāḥ ,ūṣuḥ pratītāḥ kuśasaṁstareṣu; yathādhvareṣu jvalitā havyavāhāḥ.
english: Having heard those words from the best among the twice-born (brahmins), the kings, having observed a fast for the night, remained contentedly on beds of kuśa grass, just like the sacred fires kindled during (Vedic rituals).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 63 Verse 16. Having heard those words from the best among the twice-born (brahmins), the kings, having observed a fast for the night, remained contentedly on beds of kuśa grass, just like the sacred fires kindled during (Vedic rituals).

sanskrit: ततो निशा सा व्यगमन्महात्मनां संशृण्वतां विप्रसमीरिता गिरः ।ततः प्रभाते विमले द्विजर्षभा वचोऽब्रुवन्धर्मसुतं नराधिपम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: tato niśā sā vyagamanmahātmanāṁ; saṁśṛṇvatāṁ viprasamīritā giraḥ ,tataḥ prabhāte vimale dvijarṣabhā; vaco'bruvandharmasutaṁ narādhipam.
english: Then that night passed for the great-souled individuals as they listened to the words spoken by the brahmins. Subsequently, in the pure dawn, the foremost among the twice-born spoke words to the son of Dharma, the king.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 63 Verse 17. Then that night passed for the great-souled individuals as they listened to the words spoken by the brahmins. Subsequently, in the pure dawn, the foremost among the twice-born spoke words to the son of Dharma, the king.

sanskrit: ब्राह्मणा ऊचुः ।क्रियतामुपहारोऽद्य त्र्यम्बकस्य महात्मनः ।कृत्वोपहारं नृपते ततः स्वार्थे यतामहे ॥१॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇā ūcuḥ ,kriyatāmupahāro'dya tryambakasya mahātmanaḥ ,kṛtvopahāraṁ nṛpate tataḥ svārthe yatāmahe.
english: The brahmins said: "Let an offering be made today to the great-souled Tryambaka (Śiva). Having made this offering, O king, we shall then strive for our own objectives."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 64 Verse 1. The brahmins said: "Let an offering be made today to the great-souled Tryambaka (Śiva). Having made this offering, O king, we shall then strive for our own objectives."

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।श्रुत्वा तु वचनं तेषां ब्राह्मणानां युधिष्ठिरः ।गिरीशस्य यथान्यायमुपहारमुपाहरत् ॥२॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,śrutvā tu vacanaṁ teṣāṁ brāhmaṇānāṁ yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,girīśasya yathānyāyamupahāramupāharat.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: "Having heard those words from the brahmins, Yudhiṣṭhira then presented an offering to Giriśa (Śiva) in accordance with proper procedure."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 64 Verse 2. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: "Having heard those words from the brahmins, Yudhiṣṭhira then presented an offering to Giriśa (Śiva) in accordance with proper procedure."

sanskrit: आज्येन तर्पयित्वाग्निं विधिवत्संस्कृतेन ह ।मन्त्रसिद्धं चरुं कृत्वा पुरोधाः प्रययौ तदा ॥३॥
transliteration: ājyena tarpayitvāgniṁ vidhivatsaṁskṛtena ha ,mantrasiddhaṁ caruṁ kṛtvā purodhāḥ prayayau tadā.
english: Having gratified the fire with ghee, purified according to the rules, and having prepared the sacrificial oblation (caru) consecrated by sacred utterances (mantra), the chief priest then departed.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 64 Verse 3. Having gratified the fire with ghee, purified according to the rules, and having prepared the sacrificial oblation (caru) consecrated by sacred utterances (mantra), the chief priest then departed.

sanskrit: स गृहीत्वा सुमनसो मन्त्रपूता जनाधिप ।मोदकैः पायसेनाथ मांसैश्चोपाहरद्बलिम् ॥४॥
transliteration: sa gṛhītvā sumanaso mantrapūtā janādhipa ,modakaiḥ pāyasenātha māṁsaiścopāharadbalim.
english: O ruler of men, he, taking flowers purified by sacred utterances (mantra), then presented an offering with sweets (modaka), milk-rice, and meats.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 64 Verse 4. O ruler of men, he, taking flowers purified by sacred utterances (mantra), then presented an offering with sweets (modaka), milk-rice, and meats.

sanskrit: सुमनोभिश्च चित्राभिर्लाजैरुच्चावचैरपि ।सर्वं स्विष्टकृतं कृत्वा विधिवद्वेदपारगः ।किंकराणां ततः पश्चाच्चकार बलिमुत्तमम् ॥५॥
transliteration: sumanobhiśca citrābhirlājairuccāvacairapi ,sarvaṁ sviṣṭakṛtaṁ kṛtvā vidhivadvedapāragaḥ ,kiṁkarāṇāṁ tataḥ paścāccakāra balimuttamam.
english: The expert in the Vedas, having performed all the "sviṣṭakṛt" offerings according to the rules, using various colorful flowers and diverse parched grains, then afterwards made an excellent offering for the servants.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 64 Verse 5. The expert in the Vedas, having performed all the "sviṣṭakṛt" offerings according to the rules, using various colorful flowers and diverse parched grains, then afterwards made an excellent offering for the servants.

sanskrit: यक्षेन्द्राय कुबेराय मणिभद्राय चैव ह ।तथान्येषां च यक्षाणां भूताधिपतयश्च ये ॥६॥
transliteration: yakṣendrāya kuberāya maṇibhadrāya caiva ha ,tathānyeṣāṁ ca yakṣāṇāṁ bhūtādhipatayaśca ye.
english: And indeed, (the offering was made) to the chief of the yakṣas, to Kubera, and to Maṇibhadra; and similarly, to those other yakṣas who are lords of spirits.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 64 Verse 6. And indeed, (the offering was made) to the chief of the yakṣas, to Kubera, and to Maṇibhadra; and similarly, to those other yakṣas who are lords of spirits.

sanskrit: कृसरेण समांसेन निवापैस्तिलसंयुतैः ।शुशुभे स्थानमत्यर्थं देवदेवस्य पार्थिव ॥७॥
transliteration: kṛsareṇa samāṁsena nivāpaistilasaṁyutaiḥ ,śuśubhe sthānamatyarthaṁ devadevasya pārthiva.
english: O king, the place of the god of gods became exceedingly splendid, adorned with kṛsara (a dish of rice and sesame or lentils), with meat, and with oblations combined with sesame seeds.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 64 Verse 7. O king, the place of the god of gods became exceedingly splendid, adorned with kṛsara (a dish of rice and sesame or lentils), with meat, and with oblations combined with sesame seeds.

sanskrit: कृत्वा तु पूजां रुद्रस्य गणानां चैव सर्वशः ।ययौ व्यासं पुरस्कृत्य नृपो रत्ननिधिं प्रति ॥८॥
transliteration: kṛtvā tu pūjāṁ rudrasya gaṇānāṁ caiva sarvaśaḥ ,yayau vyāsaṁ puraskṛtya nṛpo ratnanidhiṁ prati.
english: Having performed the worship (pūjā) of Rudra and all his gaṇas, the king, with Vyāsa leading, went towards the jewel-hoard.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 64 Verse 8. Having performed the worship (pūjā) of Rudra and all his gaṇas, the king, with Vyāsa leading, went towards the jewel-hoard.

sanskrit: पूजयित्वा धनाध्यक्षं प्रणिपत्याभिवाद्य च ।सुमनोभिर्विचित्राभिरपूपैः कृसरेण च ॥९॥
transliteration: pūjayitvā dhanādhyakṣaṁ praṇipatyābhivādya ca ,sumanobhirvicitrābhirapūpaiḥ kṛsareṇa ca.
english: Having worshipped the lord of wealth (Kubera), and having bowed down and saluted, (he honored him) with various beautiful flowers, cakes, and kṛsara (a dish of rice and sesame).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 64 Verse 9. Having worshipped the lord of wealth (Kubera), and having bowed down and saluted, (he honored him) with various beautiful flowers, cakes, and kṛsara (a dish of rice and sesame).

sanskrit: शङ्खादींश्च निधीन्सर्वान्निधिपालांश्च सर्वशः ।अर्चयित्वा द्विजाग्र्यान्स स्वस्ति वाच्य च वीर्यवान् ॥१०॥
transliteration: śaṅkhādīṁśca nidhīnsarvānnidhipālāṁśca sarvaśaḥ ,arcayitvā dvijāgryānsa svasti vācya ca vīryavān.
english: And having worshipped Śaṅkha and all the other treasures (nidhis), and completely all the guardians of the treasures, and having caused the foremost twice-born (dvija) to pronounce blessings, that valorous (king)...
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 64 Verse 10. And having worshipped Śaṅkha and all the other treasures (nidhis), and completely all the guardians of the treasures, and having caused the foremost twice-born (dvija) to pronounce blessings, that valorous (king)...

sanskrit: तेषां पुण्याहघोषेण तेजसा समवस्थितः ।प्रीतिमान्स कुरुश्रेष्ठः खानयामास तं निधिम् ॥११॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ puṇyāhaghoṣeṇa tejasā samavasthitaḥ ,prītimānsa kuruśreṣṭhaḥ khānayāmāsa taṁ nidhim.
english: He, the best among the Kurus, pleased and empowered by the auspicious invocation and its radiant energy, then had that treasure unearthed.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 64 Verse 11. He, the best among the Kurus, pleased and empowered by the auspicious invocation and its radiant energy, then had that treasure unearthed.

sanskrit: ततः पात्र्यः सकरकाः साश्मन्तकमनोरमाः ।भृङ्गाराणि कटाहानि कलशान्वर्धमानकान् ॥१२॥
transliteration: tataḥ pātryaḥ sakarakāḥ sāśmantakamanoramāḥ ,bhṛṅgārāṇi kaṭāhāni kalaśānvardhamānakān.
english: Then, various items were unearthed: bowls with covers, charming with aśmantaka designs, golden pitchers, large cauldrons, water pots, and auspicious growth-vessels.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 64 Verse 12. Then, various items were unearthed: bowls with covers, charming with aśmantaka designs, golden pitchers, large cauldrons, water pots, and auspicious growth-vessels.

sanskrit: बहूनि च विचित्राणि भाजनानि सहस्रशः ।उद्धारयामास तदा धर्मराजो युधिष्ठिरः ॥१३॥
transliteration: bahūni ca vicitrāṇi bhājanāni sahasraśaḥ ,uddhārayāmāsa tadā dharmarājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: Then, Yudhishthira, the king who embodies righteousness (dharma), caused thousands of many diverse and wondrous vessels to be unearthed.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 64 Verse 13. Then, Yudhishthira, the king who embodies righteousness (dharma), caused thousands of many diverse and wondrous vessels to be unearthed.

sanskrit: तेषां लक्षणमप्यासीन्महान्करपुटस्तथा ।त्रिलक्षं भाजनं राजंस्तुलार्धमभवन्नृप ॥१४॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ lakṣaṇamapyāsīnmahānkarapuṭastathā ,trilakṣaṁ bhājanaṁ rājaṁstulārdhamabhavannṛpa.
english: O King, the distinguishing mark of those vessels was a large handful (of their contents). Furthermore, each vessel valued at three hundred thousand was found to weigh half a tulā.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 64 Verse 14. O King, the distinguishing mark of those vessels was a large handful (of their contents). Furthermore, each vessel valued at three hundred thousand was found to weigh half a tulā.

sanskrit: वाहनं पाण्डुपुत्रस्य तत्रासीत्तु विशां पते ।षष्टिरुष्ट्रसहस्राणि शतानि द्विगुणा हयाः ॥१५॥
transliteration: vāhanaṁ pāṇḍuputrasya tatrāsīttu viśāṁ pate ,ṣaṣṭiruṣṭrasahasrāṇi śatāni dviguṇā hayāḥ.
english: O lord of men, the conveyances of Pāṇḍu's son there consisted of sixty thousand camels and twenty thousand horses.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 64 Verse 15. O lord of men, the conveyances of Pāṇḍu's son there consisted of sixty thousand camels and twenty thousand horses.

sanskrit: वारणाश्च महाराज सहस्रशतसंमिताः ।शकटानि रथाश्चैव तावदेव करेणवः ।खराणां पुरुषाणां च परिसंख्या न विद्यते ॥१६॥
transliteration: vāraṇāśca mahārāja sahasraśatasaṁmitāḥ ,śakaṭāni rathāścaiva tāvadeva kareṇavaḥ ,kharāṇāṁ puruṣāṇāṁ ca parisaṁkhyā na vidyate.
english: And, O great king, there were elephants numbering a hundred thousand. Carts, chariots, and likewise female elephants were also that many. The count of donkeys and men was beyond measure.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 64 Verse 16. And, O great king, there were elephants numbering a hundred thousand. Carts, chariots, and likewise female elephants were also that many. The count of donkeys and men was beyond measure.

sanskrit: एतद्वित्तं तदभवद्यदुद्दध्रे युधिष्ठिरः ।षोडशाष्टौ चतुर्विंशत्सहस्रं भारलक्षणम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: etadvittaṁ tadabhavadyaduddadhre yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,ṣoḍaśāṣṭau caturviṁśatsahasraṁ bhāralakṣaṇam.
english: This was the wealth collected by Yudhiṣṭhira, consisting of sixteen and eight (thousand units), totaling twenty-four thousand *bhāras*.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 64 Verse 17. This was the wealth collected by Yudhiṣṭhira, consisting of sixteen and eight (thousand units), totaling twenty-four thousand *bhāras*.

sanskrit: एतेष्वाधाय तद्द्रव्यं पुनरभ्यर्च्य पाण्डवः ।महादेवं प्रति ययौ पुरं नागाह्वयं प्रति ॥१८॥
transliteration: eteṣvādhāya taddravyaṁ punarabhyarcya pāṇḍavaḥ ,mahādevaṁ prati yayau puraṁ nāgāhvayaṁ prati.
english: Having loaded that wealth onto these (conveyances) and having worshipped again, the Pāṇḍava (Yudhiṣṭhira) went towards Mahādeva and towards the city named Nāga.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 64 Verse 18. Having loaded that wealth onto these (conveyances) and having worshipped again, the Pāṇḍava (Yudhiṣṭhira) went towards Mahādeva and towards the city named Nāga.

sanskrit: द्वैपायनाभ्यनुज्ञातः पुरस्कृत्य पुरोहितम् ।गोयुते गोयुते चैव न्यवसत्पुरुषर्षभः ॥१९॥
transliteration: dvaipāyanābhyanujñātaḥ puraskṛtya purohitam ,goyute goyute caiva nyavasatpuruṣarṣabhaḥ.
english: The chief of men, having received permission from Dvaipāyana and placing his priest (purohita) at the forefront, resided in various places, each rich with cattle.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 64 Verse 19. The chief of men, having received permission from Dvaipāyana and placing his priest (purohita) at the forefront, resided in various places, each rich with cattle.

sanskrit: सा पुराभिमुखी राजञ्जगाम महती चमूः ।कृच्छ्राद्द्रविणभारार्ता हर्षयन्ती कुरूद्वहान् ॥२०॥
transliteration: sā purābhimukhī rājañjagāma mahatī camūḥ ,kṛcchrāddraviṇabhārārtā harṣayantī kurūdvahān.
english: O King, that large army, facing the city and troubled by the heavy load of treasure, proceeded with difficulty, yet it brought joy to the leaders of the Kurus.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 64 Verse 20. O King, that large army, facing the city and troubled by the heavy load of treasure, proceeded with difficulty, yet it brought joy to the leaders of the Kurus.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एतस्मिन्नेव काले तु वासुदेवोऽपि वीर्यवान् ।उपायाद्वृष्णिभिः सार्धं पुरं वारणसाह्वयम् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,etasminneva kāle tu vāsudevo'pi vīryavān ,upāyādvṛṣṇibhiḥ sārdhaṁ puraṁ vāraṇasāhvayam.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: 'At this very time, the powerful Vāsudeva also arrived with the Vṛṣṇis at the city known as Vāraṇasā (Vārāṇasī).'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 65 Verse 1. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: 'At this very time, the powerful Vāsudeva also arrived with the Vṛṣṇis at the city known as Vāraṇasā (Vārāṇasī).'

sanskrit: समयं वाजिमेधस्य विदित्वा पुरुषर्षभः ।यथोक्तो धर्मपुत्रेण व्रजन्स स्वपुरीं प्रति ॥२॥
transliteration: samayaṁ vājimedhasya viditvā puruṣarṣabhaḥ ,yathokto dharmaputreṇa vrajansa svapurīṁ prati.
english: The chief of men (puruṣarṣabha), having learned of the time for the Vedic horse ritual (vājimedha), and as instructed by Yudhiṣṭhira (dharmaputra), proceeded towards his own city.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 65 Verse 2. The chief of men (puruṣarṣabha), having learned of the time for the Vedic horse ritual (vājimedha), and as instructed by Yudhiṣṭhira (dharmaputra), proceeded towards his own city.

sanskrit: रौक्मिणेयेन सहितो युयुधानेन चैव ह ।चारुदेष्णेन साम्बेन गदेन कृतवर्मणा ॥३॥
transliteration: raukmiṇeyena sahito yuyudhānena caiva ha ,cārudeṣṇena sāmbena gadena kṛtavarmaṇā.
english: Accompanied by Pradyumna (Raukmiṇeya), Yuyudhāna, Cārudeṣṇa, Sāmba, Gada, and Kṛtavarmā.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 65 Verse 3. Accompanied by Pradyumna (Raukmiṇeya), Yuyudhāna, Cārudeṣṇa, Sāmba, Gada, and Kṛtavarmā.

sanskrit: सारणेन च वीरेण निशठेनोल्मुकेन च ।बलदेवं पुरस्कृत्य सुभद्रासहितस्तदा ॥४॥
transliteration: sāraṇena ca vīreṇa niśaṭhenolmukena ca ,baladevaṁ puraskṛtya subhadrāsahitastadā.
english: And by the heroic Sāraṇa, Niśaṭha, and Ulmuka; then, placing Baladeva (Balarāma) at the forefront, (he was) accompanied by Subhadrā.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 65 Verse 4. And by the heroic Sāraṇa, Niśaṭha, and Ulmuka; then, placing Baladeva (Balarāma) at the forefront, (he was) accompanied by Subhadrā.

sanskrit: द्रौपदीमुत्तरां चैव पृथां चाप्यवलोककः ।समाश्वासयितुं चापि क्षत्रिया निहतेश्वराः ॥५॥
transliteration: draupadīmuttarāṁ caiva pṛthāṁ cāpyavalokakaḥ ,samāśvāsayituṁ cāpi kṣatriyā nihateśvarāḥ.
english: And indeed, observing Draupadī, Uttarā, and Pṛthā, these Kṣatriya women whose lords (husbands) had been slain, (he came) also to console them.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 65 Verse 5. And indeed, observing Draupadī, Uttarā, and Pṛthā, these Kṣatriya women whose lords (husbands) had been slain, (he came) also to console them.

sanskrit: तानागतान्समीक्ष्यैव धृतराष्ट्रो महीपतिः ।प्रत्यगृह्णाद्यथान्यायं विदुरश्च महामनाः ॥६॥
transliteration: tānāgatānsamīkṣyaiva dhṛtarāṣṭro mahīpatiḥ ,pratyagṛhṇādyathānyāyaṁ viduraśca mahāmanāḥ.
english: Indeed, seeing those who had arrived, King Dhṛtarāṣṭra and the noble-minded Vidura welcomed them appropriately.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 65 Verse 6. Indeed, seeing those who had arrived, King Dhṛtarāṣṭra and the noble-minded Vidura welcomed them appropriately.

sanskrit: तत्रैव न्यवसत्कृष्णः स्वर्चितः पुरुषर्षभः ।विदुरेण महातेजास्तथैव च युयुत्सुना ॥७॥
transliteration: tatraiva nyavasatkṛṣṇaḥ svarcitaḥ puruṣarṣabhaḥ ,vidureṇa mahātejāstathaiva ca yuyutsunā.
english: The pre-eminent man (puruṣa) Kṛṣṇa, of great splendor, remained there, well-honored by Vidura and likewise by Yuyutsu.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 65 Verse 7. The pre-eminent man (puruṣa) Kṛṣṇa, of great splendor, remained there, well-honored by Vidura and likewise by Yuyutsu.

sanskrit: वसत्सु वृष्णिवीरेषु तत्राथ जनमेजय ।जज्ञे तव पिता राजन्परिक्षित्परवीरहा ॥८॥
transliteration: vasatsu vṛṣṇivīreṣu tatrātha janamejaya ,jajñe tava pitā rājanparikṣitparavīrahā.
english: Then, O Janamejaya, while the Vṛṣṇi heroes were residing there, your father Parikṣit, the slayer of enemy heroes, was born.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 65 Verse 8. Then, O Janamejaya, while the Vṛṣṇi heroes were residing there, your father Parikṣit, the slayer of enemy heroes, was born.

sanskrit: स तु राजा महाराज ब्रह्मास्त्रेणाभिपीडितः ।शवो बभूव निश्चेष्टो हर्षशोकविवर्धनः ॥९॥
transliteration: sa tu rājā mahārāja brahmāstreṇābhipīḍitaḥ ,śavo babhūva niśceṣṭo harṣaśokavivardhanaḥ.
english: But that king, O great king, afflicted by the Brahmāstra, became an inert corpse, causing an increase of joy and sorrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 65 Verse 9. But that king, O great king, afflicted by the Brahmāstra, became an inert corpse, causing an increase of joy and sorrow.

sanskrit: हृष्टानां सिंहनादेन जनानां तत्र निस्वनः ।आविश्य प्रदिशः सर्वाः पुनरेव व्युपारमत् ॥१०॥
transliteration: hṛṣṭānāṁ siṁhanādena janānāṁ tatra nisvanaḥ ,āviśya pradiśaḥ sarvāḥ punareva vyupāramat.
english: Then, the roar-like sound made by the joyous people, having filled all directions, again ceased.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 65 Verse 10. Then, the roar-like sound made by the joyous people, having filled all directions, again ceased.

sanskrit: ततः सोऽतित्वरः कृष्णो विवेशान्तःपुरं तदा ।युयुधानद्वितीयो वै व्यथितेन्द्रियमानसः ॥११॥
transliteration: tataḥ so'titvaraḥ kṛṣṇo viveśāntaḥpuraṁ tadā ,yuyudhānadvitīyo vai vyathitendriyamānasaḥ.
english: Then Krishna, in great haste, entered the inner apartments at that time, accompanied by Yuyudhana, his senses and mind greatly distressed.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 65 Verse 11. Then Krishna, in great haste, entered the inner apartments at that time, accompanied by Yuyudhana, his senses and mind greatly distressed.

sanskrit: ततस्त्वरितमायान्तीं ददर्श स्वां पितृष्वसाम् ।क्रोशन्तीमभिधावेति वासुदेवं पुनः पुनः ॥१२॥
transliteration: tatastvaritamāyāntīṁ dadarśa svāṁ pitṛṣvasām ,krośantīmabhidhāveti vāsudevaṁ punaḥ punaḥ.
english: Then he saw his own paternal aunt swiftly approaching, repeatedly crying out to Vāsudeva (Krishna), 'Run!'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 65 Verse 12. Then he saw his own paternal aunt swiftly approaching, repeatedly crying out to Vāsudeva (Krishna), 'Run!'

sanskrit: पृष्ठतो द्रौपदीं चैव सुभद्रां च यशस्विनीम् ।सविक्रोशं सकरुणं बान्धवानां स्त्रियो नृप ॥१३॥
transliteration: pṛṣṭhato draupadīṁ caiva subhadrāṁ ca yaśasvinīm ,savikrośaṁ sakaruṇaṁ bāndhavānāṁ striyo nṛpa.
english: O king, behind them were Draupadi and the glorious Subhadra, and the women of the kinsmen, all lamenting piteously.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 65 Verse 13. O king, behind them were Draupadi and the glorious Subhadra, and the women of the kinsmen, all lamenting piteously.

sanskrit: ततः कृष्णं समासाद्य कुन्ती राजसुता तदा ।प्रोवाच राजशार्दूल बाष्पगद्गदया गिरा ॥१४॥
transliteration: tataḥ kṛṣṇaṁ samāsādya kuntī rājasutā tadā ,provāca rājaśārdūla bāṣpagadgadayā girā.
english: Then Kunti, the royal princess, having approached Krishna at that time, spoke with a voice choked with tears. O best of kings!
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 65 Verse 14. Then Kunti, the royal princess, having approached Krishna at that time, spoke with a voice choked with tears. O best of kings!

sanskrit: वासुदेव महाबाहो सुप्रजा देवकी त्वया ।त्वं नो गतिः प्रतिष्ठा च त्वदायत्तमिदं कुलम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: vāsudeva mahābāho suprajā devakī tvayā ,tvaṁ no gatiḥ pratiṣṭhā ca tvadāyattamidaṁ kulam.
english: O mighty-armed Vāsudeva, Devaki is truly blessed with noble children through you. You are our ultimate refuge (gati) and our firm foundation; this entire family is dependent on you.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 65 Verse 15. O mighty-armed Vāsudeva, Devaki is truly blessed with noble children through you. You are our ultimate refuge (gati) and our firm foundation; this entire family is dependent on you.

sanskrit: यदुप्रवीर योऽयं ते स्वस्रीयस्यात्मजः प्रभो ।अश्वत्थाम्ना हतो जातस्तमुज्जीवय केशव ॥१६॥
transliteration: yadupravīra yo'yaṁ te svasrīyasyātmajaḥ prabho ,aśvatthāmnā hato jātastamujjīvaya keśava.
english: O foremost hero of the Yadus, O Lord Keśava, please revive this son of your nephew, who was born and then slain by Aśvatthāman.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 65 Verse 16. O foremost hero of the Yadus, O Lord Keśava, please revive this son of your nephew, who was born and then slain by Aśvatthāman.

sanskrit: त्वया ह्येतत्प्रतिज्ञातमैषीके यदुनन्दन ।अहं संजीवयिष्यामि मृतं जातमिति प्रभो ॥१७॥
transliteration: tvayā hyetatpratijñātamaiṣīke yadunandana ,ahaṁ saṁjīvayiṣyāmi mṛtaṁ jātamiti prabho.
english: Indeed, O delight of the Yadus, O Lord, you yourself made this promise during the Aiṣīka incident: "I will bring back to life anyone born dead."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 65 Verse 17. Indeed, O delight of the Yadus, O Lord, you yourself made this promise during the Aiṣīka incident: "I will bring back to life anyone born dead."

sanskrit: सोऽयं जातो मृतस्तात पश्यैनं पुरुषर्षभ ।उत्तरां च सुभद्रां च द्रौपदीं मां च माधव ॥१८॥
transliteration: so'yaṁ jāto mṛtastāta paśyainaṁ puruṣarṣabha ,uttarāṁ ca subhadrāṁ ca draupadīṁ māṁ ca mādhava.
english: O dear one, O best of men, O Mādhava, look at this child who was born dead. Also behold Uttarā, Subhadrā, Draupadī, and me.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 65 Verse 18. O dear one, O best of men, O Mādhava, look at this child who was born dead. Also behold Uttarā, Subhadrā, Draupadī, and me.

sanskrit: धर्मपुत्रं च भीमं च फल्गुनं नकुलं तथा ।सहदेवं च दुर्धर्ष सर्वान्नस्त्रातुमर्हसि ॥१९॥
transliteration: dharmaputraṁ ca bhīmaṁ ca phalgunaṁ nakulaṁ tathā ,sahadevaṁ ca durdharṣa sarvānnastrātumarhasi.
english: O unconquerable one, you ought to protect all of us: Yudhishthira (the son of dharma), Bhima, Arjuna, Nakula, and Sahadeva.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 65 Verse 19. O unconquerable one, you ought to protect all of us: Yudhishthira (the son of dharma), Bhima, Arjuna, Nakula, and Sahadeva.

sanskrit: अस्मिन्प्राणाः समायत्ताः पाण्डवानां ममैव च ।पाण्डोश्च पिण्डो दाशार्ह तथैव श्वशुरस्य मे ॥२०॥
transliteration: asminprāṇāḥ samāyattāḥ pāṇḍavānāṁ mamaiva ca ,pāṇḍośca piṇḍo dāśārha tathaiva śvaśurasya me.
english: In him (Abhimanyu), the lives of the Pandavas and my own are completely dependent. And, O descendant of Dasharha (Krishna), the ancestral offering (piṇḍa) for Pandu, and similarly for my father-in-law, depends on him.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 65 Verse 20. In him (Abhimanyu), the lives of the Pandavas and my own are completely dependent. And, O descendant of Dasharha (Krishna), the ancestral offering (piṇḍa) for Pandu, and similarly for my father-in-law, depends on him.

sanskrit: अभिमन्योश्च भद्रं ते प्रियस्य सदृशस्य च ।प्रियमुत्पादयाद्य त्वं प्रेतस्यापि जनार्दन ॥२१॥
transliteration: abhimanyośca bhadraṁ te priyasya sadṛśasya ca ,priyamutpādayādya tvaṁ pretasyāpi janārdana.
english: May good fortune be with you regarding Abhimanyu, who was dear and worthy. O Janardana (Krishna), today you must bring about a favorable outcome even for the departed one.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 65 Verse 21. May good fortune be with you regarding Abhimanyu, who was dear and worthy. O Janardana (Krishna), today you must bring about a favorable outcome even for the departed one.

sanskrit: उत्तरा हि प्रियोक्तं वै कथयत्यरिसूदन ।अभिमन्योर्वचः कृष्ण प्रियत्वात्ते न संशयः ॥२२॥
transliteration: uttarā hi priyoktaṁ vai kathayatyarisūdana ,abhimanyorvacaḥ kṛṣṇa priyatvātte na saṁśayaḥ.
english: O slayer of enemies, Uttara indeed recounts a cherished saying. O Krishna, there is no doubt that it is due to her affection for you that she speaks Abhimanyu's words.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 65 Verse 22. O slayer of enemies, Uttara indeed recounts a cherished saying. O Krishna, there is no doubt that it is due to her affection for you that she speaks Abhimanyu's words.

sanskrit: अब्रवीत्किल दाशार्ह वैराटीमार्जुनिः पुरा ।मातुलस्य कुलं भद्रे तव पुत्रो गमिष्यति ॥२३॥
transliteration: abravītkila dāśārha vairāṭīmārjuniḥ purā ,mātulasya kulaṁ bhadre tava putro gamiṣyati.
english: O Kṛṣṇa (dāśārha), Arjuna's son (Abhimanyu) indeed told Uttara previously: 'O auspicious one (bhadre), your son will go to his maternal uncle's family.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 65 Verse 23. O Kṛṣṇa (dāśārha), Arjuna's son (Abhimanyu) indeed told Uttara previously: 'O auspicious one (bhadre), your son will go to his maternal uncle's family.'

sanskrit: गत्वा वृष्ण्यन्धककुलं धनुर्वेदं ग्रहीष्यति ।अस्त्राणि च विचित्राणि नीतिशास्त्रं च केवलम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: gatvā vṛṣṇyandhakakulaṁ dhanurvedaṁ grahīṣyati ,astrāṇi ca vicitrāṇi nītiśāstraṁ ca kevalam.
english: Having gone to the family of the Vṛṣṇis and Andhakas, he will learn the science of archery and various wonderful weapons, as well as the complete science of statecraft.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 65 Verse 24. Having gone to the family of the Vṛṣṇis and Andhakas, he will learn the science of archery and various wonderful weapons, as well as the complete science of statecraft.

sanskrit: इत्येतत्प्रणयात्तात सौभद्रः परवीरहा ।कथयामास दुर्धर्षस्तथा चैतन्न संशयः ॥२५॥
transliteration: ityetatpraṇayāttāta saubhadraḥ paravīrahā ,kathayāmāsa durdharṣastathā caitanna saṁśayaḥ.
english: Thus, O dear one (tāta), the invincible (durdharṣaḥ) son of Subhadrā (saubhadraḥ), the slayer of mighty heroes (paravīrahā), spoke this out of affection, and there is no doubt that it is so.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 65 Verse 25. Thus, O dear one (tāta), the invincible (durdharṣaḥ) son of Subhadrā (saubhadraḥ), the slayer of mighty heroes (paravīrahā), spoke this out of affection, and there is no doubt that it is so.

sanskrit: तास्त्वां वयं प्रणम्येह याचामो मधुसूदन ।कुलस्यास्य हितार्थं त्वं कुरु कल्याणमुत्तमम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: tāstvāṁ vayaṁ praṇamyeha yācāmo madhusūdana ,kulasyāsya hitārthaṁ tvaṁ kuru kalyāṇamuttamam.
english: Therefore, O Madhusūdana (madhusūdana), we bow to you here and entreat you: 'For the welfare of this family, may you bring about the highest good.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 65 Verse 26. Therefore, O Madhusūdana (madhusūdana), we bow to you here and entreat you: 'For the welfare of this family, may you bring about the highest good.'

sanskrit: एवमुक्त्वा तु वार्ष्णेयं पृथा पृथुललोचना ।उच्छ्रित्य बाहू दुःखार्ता ताश्चान्याः प्रापतन्भुवि ॥२७॥
transliteration: evamuktvā tu vārṣṇeyaṁ pṛthā pṛthulalocanā ,ucchritya bāhū duḥkhārtā tāścānyāḥ prāpatanbhuvi.
english: Having thus spoken to Vārṣṇeya (Krishna), Pṛthā, wide-eyed and afflicted with sorrow, raised her arms. And those other women also fell to the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 65 Verse 27. Having thus spoken to Vārṣṇeya (Krishna), Pṛthā, wide-eyed and afflicted with sorrow, raised her arms. And those other women also fell to the ground.

sanskrit: अब्रुवंश्च महाराज सर्वाः सास्राविलेक्षणाः ।स्वस्रीयो वासुदेवस्य मृतो जात इति प्रभो ॥२८॥
transliteration: abruvaṁśca mahārāja sarvāḥ sāsrāvilekṣaṇāḥ ,svasrīyo vāsudevasya mṛto jāta iti prabho.
english: And all of them, O great king, with tear-filled eyes, cried out, "Vāsudeva's nephew is dead, O lord!"
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 65 Verse 28. And all of them, O great king, with tear-filled eyes, cried out, "Vāsudeva's nephew is dead, O lord!"

sanskrit: एवमुक्ते ततः कुन्तीं प्रत्यगृह्णाज्जनार्दनः ।भूमौ निपतितां चैनां सान्त्वयामास भारत ॥२९॥
transliteration: evamukte tataḥ kuntīṁ pratyagṛhṇājjanārdanaḥ ,bhūmau nipatitāṁ caināṁ sāntvayāmāsa bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, when this had been said, Janārdana (Krishna) then took Kunti, who had fallen to the ground, and consoled her.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 65 Verse 29. O Bhārata, when this had been said, Janārdana (Krishna) then took Kunti, who had fallen to the ground, and consoled her.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।उत्थितायां पृथायां तु सुभद्रा भ्रातरं तदा ।दृष्ट्वा चुक्रोश दुःखार्ता वचनं चेदमब्रवीत् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,utthitāyāṁ pṛthāyāṁ tu subhadrā bhrātaraṁ tadā ,dṛṣṭvā cukrośa duḥkhārtā vacanaṁ cedamabravīt.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: "When Pṛthā stood up, Subhadrā, distressed with sorrow, then saw her brother (Krishna), cried out, and spoke these words."
