search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 66 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: "When Pṛthā stood up, Subhadrā, distressed with sorrow, then saw her brother (Krishna), cried out, and spoke these words."

sanskrit: पुण्डरीकाक्ष पश्यस्व पौत्रं पार्थस्य धीमतः ।परिक्षीणेषु कुरुषु परिक्षीणं गतायुषम् ॥२॥
transliteration: puṇḍarīkākṣa paśyasva pautraṁ pārthasya dhīmataḥ ,parikṣīṇeṣu kuruṣu parikṣīṇaṁ gatāyuṣam.
english: O lotus-eyed one (puṇḍarīkākṣa), behold the grandson of the wise Partha, now utterly exhausted and whose life has come to an end, among the perished Kurus.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 66 Verse 2. O lotus-eyed one (puṇḍarīkākṣa), behold the grandson of the wise Partha, now utterly exhausted and whose life has come to an end, among the perished Kurus.

sanskrit: इषीका द्रोणपुत्रेण भीमसेनार्थमुद्यता ।सोत्तरायां निपतिता विजये मयि चैव ह ॥३॥
transliteration: iṣīkā droṇaputreṇa bhīmasenārthamudyatā ,sottarāyāṁ nipatitā vijaye mayi caiva ha.
english: The divine arrow (iṣīkā), wielded by Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāmā) due to Bhīmasena's actions, fell upon Uttara, upon Vijaya (Arjuna), and indeed upon me.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 66 Verse 3. The divine arrow (iṣīkā), wielded by Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāmā) due to Bhīmasena's actions, fell upon Uttara, upon Vijaya (Arjuna), and indeed upon me.

sanskrit: सेयं ज्वलन्ती हृदये मयि तिष्ठति केशव ।यन्न पश्यामि दुर्धर्ष मम पुत्रसुतं विभो ॥४॥
transliteration: seyaṁ jvalantī hṛdaye mayi tiṣṭhati keśava ,yanna paśyāmi durdharṣa mama putrasutaṁ vibho.
english: O Keśava, this burning grief remains in my heart, for I do not see my formidable grandson (putrasuta), O mighty one.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 66 Verse 4. O Keśava, this burning grief remains in my heart, for I do not see my formidable grandson (putrasuta), O mighty one.

sanskrit: किं नु वक्ष्यति धर्मात्मा धर्मराजो युधिष्ठिरः ।भीमसेनार्जुनौ चापि माद्रवत्याः सुतौ च तौ ॥५॥
transliteration: kiṁ nu vakṣyati dharmātmā dharmarājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,bhīmasenārjunau cāpi mādravatyāḥ sutau ca tau.
english: What indeed will the righteous-souled (dharmātmā) king of natural law (dharmarāja), Yudhiṣṭhira, say? And what of Bhīmasena and Arjuna, and those two sons of Mādrī (Nakula and Sahadeva)?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 66 Verse 5. What indeed will the righteous-souled (dharmātmā) king of natural law (dharmarāja), Yudhiṣṭhira, say? And what of Bhīmasena and Arjuna, and those two sons of Mādrī (Nakula and Sahadeva)?

sanskrit: श्रुत्वाभिमन्योस्तनयं जातं च मृतमेव च ।मुषिता इव वार्ष्णेय द्रोणपुत्रेण पाण्डवाः ॥६॥
transliteration: śrutvābhimanyostanayaṁ jātaṁ ca mṛtameva ca ,muṣitā iva vārṣṇeya droṇaputreṇa pāṇḍavāḥ.
english: O Vārṣṇeya, having heard that Abhimanyu's son was both born and then died, the Pāṇḍavas feel as if they have been robbed by Droṇa's son.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 66 Verse 6. O Vārṣṇeya, having heard that Abhimanyu's son was both born and then died, the Pāṇḍavas feel as if they have been robbed by Droṇa's son.

sanskrit: अभिमन्युः प्रियः कृष्ण पितॄणां नात्र संशयः ।ते श्रुत्वा किं नु वक्ष्यन्ति द्रोणपुत्रास्त्रनिर्जिताः ॥७॥
transliteration: abhimanyuḥ priyaḥ kṛṣṇa pitṝṇāṁ nātra saṁśayaḥ ,te śrutvā kiṁ nu vakṣyanti droṇaputrāstranirjitāḥ.
english: O Kṛṣṇa, Abhimanyu was dear to the elders, there is no doubt about that. What will they say when they hear this news, those who have been defeated by Droṇa's son's weapon?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 66 Verse 7. O Kṛṣṇa, Abhimanyu was dear to the elders, there is no doubt about that. What will they say when they hear this news, those who have been defeated by Droṇa's son's weapon?

sanskrit: भवितातः परं दुःखं किं नु मन्ये जनार्दन ।अभिमन्योः सुतात्कृष्ण मृताज्जातादरिंदम ॥८॥
transliteration: bhavitātaḥ paraṁ duḥkhaṁ kiṁ nu manye janārdana ,abhimanyoḥ sutātkṛṣṇa mṛtājjātādariṁdama.
english: O Janārdana, what greater sorrow (duḥkha) can there be after this? What do I expect, O Kṛṣṇa, O destroyer of foes, from Abhimanyu's son, who was born and then died?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 66 Verse 8. O Janārdana, what greater sorrow (duḥkha) can there be after this? What do I expect, O Kṛṣṇa, O destroyer of foes, from Abhimanyu's son, who was born and then died?

sanskrit: साहं प्रसादये कृष्ण त्वामद्य शिरसा नता ।पृथेयं द्रौपदी चैव ताः पश्य पुरुषोत्तम ॥९॥
transliteration: sāhaṁ prasādaye kṛṣṇa tvāmadya śirasā natā ,pṛtheyaṁ draupadī caiva tāḥ paśya puruṣottama.
english: O Kṛṣṇa, I, with bowed head, supplicate you today. O supreme cosmic person (puruṣottama), please also consider Pṛthā (Kuntī) and Draupadī.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 66 Verse 9. O Kṛṣṇa, I, with bowed head, supplicate you today. O supreme cosmic person (puruṣottama), please also consider Pṛthā (Kuntī) and Draupadī.

sanskrit: यदा द्रोणसुतो गर्भान्पाण्डूनां हन्ति माधव ।तदा किल त्वया द्रौणिः क्रुद्धेनोक्तोऽरिमर्दन ॥१०॥
transliteration: yadā droṇasuto garbhānpāṇḍūnāṁ hanti mādhava ,tadā kila tvayā drauṇiḥ kruddhenokto'rimardana.
english: O Mādhava, O Destroyer of Enemies, when Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāmā) kills the embryos of the Pāṇḍavas, then indeed you angrily addressed Droṇi (Aśvatthāmā).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 66 Verse 10. O Mādhava, O Destroyer of Enemies, when Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāmā) kills the embryos of the Pāṇḍavas, then indeed you angrily addressed Droṇi (Aśvatthāmā).

sanskrit: अकामं त्वा करिष्यामि ब्रह्मबन्धो नराधम ।अहं संजीवयिष्यामि किरीटितनयात्मजम् ॥११॥
transliteration: akāmaṁ tvā kariṣyāmi brahmabandho narādhama ,ahaṁ saṁjīvayiṣyāmi kirīṭitanayātmajam.
english: O false brahmin (brahmabandhu), O vilest of men, I will render you utterly helpless. I will revive the son of Arjuna's son.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 66 Verse 11. O false brahmin (brahmabandhu), O vilest of men, I will render you utterly helpless. I will revive the son of Arjuna's son.

sanskrit: इत्येतद्वचनं श्रुत्वा जानमाना बलं तव ।प्रसादये त्वा दुर्धर्ष जीवतामभिमन्युजः ॥१२॥
transliteration: ityetadvacanaṁ śrutvā jānamānā balaṁ tava ,prasādaye tvā durdharṣa jīvatāmabhimanyujaḥ.
english: Having heard this statement and realizing your power, O irresistible one, I implore you, so that Abhimanyu's son may live.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 66 Verse 12. Having heard this statement and realizing your power, O irresistible one, I implore you, so that Abhimanyu's son may live.

sanskrit: यद्येवं त्वं प्रतिश्रुत्य न करोषि वचः शुभम् ।सफलं वृष्णिशार्दूल मृतां मामुपधारय ॥१३॥
transliteration: yadyevaṁ tvaṁ pratiśrutya na karoṣi vacaḥ śubham ,saphalaṁ vṛṣṇiśārdūla mṛtāṁ māmupadhāraya.
english: O best of the Vṛṣṇis, if, having given this promise, you do not fulfill your auspicious word, then consider me dead and (my death) purposeful.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 66 Verse 13. O best of the Vṛṣṇis, if, having given this promise, you do not fulfill your auspicious word, then consider me dead and (my death) purposeful.

sanskrit: अभिमन्योः सुतो वीर न संजीवति यद्ययम् ।जीवति त्वयि दुर्धर्ष किं करिष्याम्यहं त्वया ॥१४॥
transliteration: abhimanyoḥ suto vīra na saṁjīvati yadyayam ,jīvati tvayi durdharṣa kiṁ kariṣyāmyahaṁ tvayā.
english: O hero, if this son of Abhimanyu does not revive, what use will you be to me, O unconquerable one, even if you are alive?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 66 Verse 14. O hero, if this son of Abhimanyu does not revive, what use will you be to me, O unconquerable one, even if you are alive?

sanskrit: संजीवयैनं दुर्धर्ष मृतं त्वमभिमन्युजम् ।सदृशाक्षसुतं वीर सस्यं वर्षन्निवाम्बुदः ॥१५॥
transliteration: saṁjīvayainaṁ durdharṣa mṛtaṁ tvamabhimanyujam ,sadṛśākṣasutaṁ vīra sasyaṁ varṣannivāmbudaḥ.
english: O unconquerable one, O hero, you must revive this dead son of Abhimanyu, just as a cloud showers rain upon crops.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 66 Verse 15. O unconquerable one, O hero, you must revive this dead son of Abhimanyu, just as a cloud showers rain upon crops.

sanskrit: त्वं हि केशव धर्मात्मा सत्यवान्सत्यविक्रमः ।स तां वाचमृतां कर्तुमर्हसि त्वमरिंदम ॥१६॥
transliteration: tvaṁ hi keśava dharmātmā satyavānsatyavikramaḥ ,sa tāṁ vācamṛtāṁ kartumarhasi tvamariṁdama.
english: Indeed, O Keśava, you are one whose inner nature (dharma) is righteousness, who is truthful, and whose prowess is unwavering. Therefore, O vanquisher of foes, you ought to make that word of yours unfailing.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 66 Verse 16. Indeed, O Keśava, you are one whose inner nature (dharma) is righteousness, who is truthful, and whose prowess is unwavering. Therefore, O vanquisher of foes, you ought to make that word of yours unfailing.

sanskrit: इच्छन्नपि हि लोकांस्त्रीञ्जीवयेथा मृतानिमान् ।किं पुनर्दयितं जातं स्वस्रीयस्यात्मजं मृतम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: icchannapi hi lokāṁstrīñjīvayethā mṛtānimān ,kiṁ punardayitaṁ jātaṁ svasrīyasyātmajaṁ mṛtam.
english: Indeed, even if you simply wished, you could revive these three dead worlds. How much more, then, should you revive this beloved, born son of your sister's son, who is dead?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 66 Verse 17. Indeed, even if you simply wished, you could revive these three dead worlds. How much more, then, should you revive this beloved, born son of your sister's son, who is dead?

sanskrit: प्रभावज्ञास्मि ते कृष्ण तस्मादेतद्ब्रवीमि ते ।कुरुष्व पाण्डुपुत्राणामिमं परमनुग्रहम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: prabhāvajñāsmi te kṛṣṇa tasmādetadbravīmi te ,kuruṣva pāṇḍuputrāṇāmimaṁ paramanugraham.
english: O Kṛṣṇa, I know your influence, therefore I tell you this: please bestow this supreme favor upon the sons of Pāṇḍu.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 66 Verse 18. O Kṛṣṇa, I know your influence, therefore I tell you this: please bestow this supreme favor upon the sons of Pāṇḍu.

sanskrit: स्वसेति वा महाबाहो हतपुत्रेति वा पुनः ।प्रपन्ना मामियं वेति दयां कर्तुमिहार्हसि ॥१९॥
transliteration: svaseti vā mahābāho hataputreti vā punaḥ ,prapannā māmiyaṁ veti dayāṁ kartumihārhasi.
english: O mighty-armed one, whether considering her as your sister, or again, as a mother whose sons have been slain, or (you know that) this one has sought refuge in me -  you should show compassion here.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 66 Verse 19. O mighty-armed one, whether considering her as your sister, or again, as a mother whose sons have been slain, or (you know that) this one has sought refuge in me -  you should show compassion here.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवमुक्तस्तु राजेन्द्र केशिहा दुःखमूर्छितः ।तथेति व्याजहारोच्चैर्ह्लादयन्निव तं जनम् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evamuktastu rājendra keśihā duḥkhamūrchitaḥ ,tatheti vyājahāroccairhlādayanniva taṁ janam.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: O King, Kṛṣṇa (keśihā), thus addressed and overcome with sorrow, then loudly replied, "So be it," as if delighting those people.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 67 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: O King, Kṛṣṇa (keśihā), thus addressed and overcome with sorrow, then loudly replied, "So be it," as if delighting those people.

sanskrit: वाक्येन तेन हि तदा तं जनं पुरुषर्षभः ।ह्लादयामास स विभुर्घर्मार्तं सलिलैरिव ॥२॥
transliteration: vākyena tena hi tadā taṁ janaṁ puruṣarṣabhaḥ ,hlādayāmāsa sa vibhurgharmārtaṁ salilairiva.
english: Indeed, at that moment, by that very utterance, he, the supreme person (puruṣarṣabha) and all-pervading (vibhu) lord, gladdened those people, just as one refreshes those tormented by heat with water.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 67 Verse 2. Indeed, at that moment, by that very utterance, he, the supreme person (puruṣarṣabha) and all-pervading (vibhu) lord, gladdened those people, just as one refreshes those tormented by heat with water.

sanskrit: ततः स प्राविशत्तूर्णं जन्मवेश्म पितुस्तव ।अर्चितं पुरुषव्याघ्र सितैर्माल्यैर्यथाविधि ॥३॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa prāviśattūrṇaṁ janmaveśma pitustava ,arcitaṁ puruṣavyāghra sitairmālyairyathāvidhi.
english: Then, O tiger among men, he quickly entered your father's birth-chamber, which was properly adorned with white garlands.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 67 Verse 3. Then, O tiger among men, he quickly entered your father's birth-chamber, which was properly adorned with white garlands.

sanskrit: अपां कुम्भैः सुपूर्णैश्च विन्यस्तैः सर्वतोदिशम् ।घृतेन तिन्दुकालातैः सर्षपैश्च महाभुज ॥४॥
transliteration: apāṁ kumbhaiḥ supūrṇaiśca vinyastaiḥ sarvatodiśam ,ghṛtena tindukālātaiḥ sarṣapaiśca mahābhuja.
english: O mighty-armed, (he saw) water pots filled to the brim and placed in all directions, along with ghee, tinduka wood firebrands, and mustard seeds.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 67 Verse 4. O mighty-armed, (he saw) water pots filled to the brim and placed in all directions, along with ghee, tinduka wood firebrands, and mustard seeds.

sanskrit: शस्त्रैश्च विमलैर्न्यस्तैः पावकैश्च समन्ततः ।वृद्धाभिश्चाभिरामाभिः परिचारार्थमच्युतः ॥५॥
transliteration: śastraiśca vimalairnyastaiḥ pāvakaiśca samantataḥ ,vṛddhābhiścābhirāmābhiḥ paricārārthamacyutaḥ.
english: O Acyuta, (he saw) clean weapons placed everywhere, purifying fires, and charming old women for attendance.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 67 Verse 5. O Acyuta, (he saw) clean weapons placed everywhere, purifying fires, and charming old women for attendance.

sanskrit: दक्षैश्च परितो वीर भिषग्भिः कुशलैस्तथा ।ददर्श च स तेजस्वी रक्षोघ्नान्यपि सर्वशः ।द्रव्याणि स्थापितानि स्म विधिवत्कुशलैर्जनैः ॥६॥
transliteration: dakṣaiśca parito vīra bhiṣagbhiḥ kuśalaistathā ,dadarśa ca sa tejasvī rakṣoghnānyapi sarvaśaḥ ,dravyāṇi sthāpitāni sma vidhivatkuśalairjanaiḥ.
english: O hero, that radiant one further saw skilled physicians all around, and all sorts of demon-averting articles that had been properly placed by expert people.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 67 Verse 6. O hero, that radiant one further saw skilled physicians all around, and all sorts of demon-averting articles that had been properly placed by expert people.

sanskrit: तथायुक्तं च तद्दृष्ट्वा जन्मवेश्म पितुस्तव ।हृष्टोऽभवद्धृषीकेशः साधु साध्विति चाब्रवीत् ॥७॥
transliteration: tathāyuktaṁ ca taddṛṣṭvā janmaveśma pitustava ,hṛṣṭo'bhavaddhṛṣīkeśaḥ sādhu sādhviti cābravīt.
english: And seeing your father's birthplace thus furnished, Hṛṣīkeśa (Kṛṣṇa) became pleased and said, "Excellent! Excellent!"
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 67 Verse 7. And seeing your father's birthplace thus furnished, Hṛṣīkeśa (Kṛṣṇa) became pleased and said, "Excellent! Excellent!"

sanskrit: तथा ब्रुवति वार्ष्णेये प्रहृष्टवदने तदा ।द्रौपदी त्वरिता गत्वा वैराटीं वाक्यमब्रवीत् ॥८॥
transliteration: tathā bruvati vārṣṇeye prahṛṣṭavadane tadā ,draupadī tvaritā gatvā vairāṭīṁ vākyamabravīt.
english: While Vārṣṇeya (Kṛṣṇa) was speaking thus, with a joyful face, at that moment, Draupadī quickly went and spoke to the princess of Virāṭa.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 67 Verse 8. While Vārṣṇeya (Kṛṣṇa) was speaking thus, with a joyful face, at that moment, Draupadī quickly went and spoke to the princess of Virāṭa.

sanskrit: अयमायाति ते भद्रे श्वशुरो मधुसूदनः ।पुराणर्षिरचिन्त्यात्मा समीपमपराजितः ॥९॥
transliteration: ayamāyāti te bhadre śvaśuro madhusūdanaḥ ,purāṇarṣiracintyātmā samīpamaparājitaḥ.
english: “O noble lady, your unconquered father-in-law, Madhusūdana (Kṛṣṇa), the ancient sage whose intrinsic nature (ātman) is inconceivable, is coming near.”
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 67 Verse 9. “O noble lady, your unconquered father-in-law, Madhusūdana (Kṛṣṇa), the ancient sage whose intrinsic nature (ātman) is inconceivable, is coming near.”

sanskrit: सापि बाष्पकलां वाचं निगृह्याश्रूणि चैव ह ।सुसंवीताभवद्देवी देववत्कृष्णमीक्षती ॥१०॥
transliteration: sāpi bāṣpakalāṁ vācaṁ nigṛhyāśrūṇi caiva ha ,susaṁvītābhavaddevī devavatkṛṣṇamīkṣatī.
english: She too, restraining her tear-choked voice and indeed her tears, became well-composed, gazing at Kṛṣṇa as if he were a deity.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 67 Verse 10. She too, restraining her tear-choked voice and indeed her tears, became well-composed, gazing at Kṛṣṇa as if he were a deity.

sanskrit: सा तथा दूयमानेन हृदयेन तपस्विनी ।दृष्ट्वा गोविन्दमायान्तं कृपणं पर्यदेवयत् ॥११॥
transliteration: sā tathā dūyamānena hṛdayena tapasvinī ,dṛṣṭvā govindamāyāntaṁ kṛpaṇaṁ paryadevayat.
english: The distressed woman, her heart thus grieving, lamented pitifully upon seeing Govinda (Kṛṣṇa) approach.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 67 Verse 11. The distressed woman, her heart thus grieving, lamented pitifully upon seeing Govinda (Kṛṣṇa) approach.

sanskrit: पुण्डरीकाक्ष पश्यस्व बालाविह विनाकृतौ ।अभिमन्युं च मां चैव हतौ तुल्यं जनार्दन ॥१२॥
transliteration: puṇḍarīkākṣa paśyasva bālāviha vinākṛtau ,abhimanyuṁ ca māṁ caiva hatau tulyaṁ janārdana.
english: O Lotus-Eyed one (Puṇḍarīkākṣa), behold these two children (Abhimanyu and my unborn son) here, left utterly helpless. O Janārdana, Abhimanyu and I are equally as good as slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 67 Verse 12. O Lotus-Eyed one (Puṇḍarīkākṣa), behold these two children (Abhimanyu and my unborn son) here, left utterly helpless. O Janārdana, Abhimanyu and I are equally as good as slain.

sanskrit: वार्ष्णेय मधुहन्वीर शिरसा त्वां प्रसादये ।द्रोणपुत्रास्त्रनिर्दग्धं जीवयैनं ममात्मजम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: vārṣṇeya madhuhanvīra śirasā tvāṁ prasādaye ,droṇaputrāstranirdagdhaṁ jīvayainaṁ mamātmajam.
english: O Vārṣṇeya, O destroyer of Madhu, O hero, I supplicate you with my head. Revive this son of mine, who has been incinerated by the weapon of Droṇa's son.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 67 Verse 13. O Vārṣṇeya, O destroyer of Madhu, O hero, I supplicate you with my head. Revive this son of mine, who has been incinerated by the weapon of Droṇa's son.

sanskrit: यदि स्म धर्मराज्ञा वा भीमसेनेन वा पुनः ।त्वया वा पुण्डरीकाक्ष वाक्यमुक्तमिदं भवेत् ॥१४॥
transliteration: yadi sma dharmarājñā vā bhīmasenena vā punaḥ ,tvayā vā puṇḍarīkākṣa vākyamuktamidaṁ bhavet.
english: O Lotus-Eyed one (Puṇḍarīkākṣa), if this statement (vākyam) was indeed spoken either by Dharmarāja (Yudhiṣṭhira), or by Bhīmasena, or again by you...
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 67 Verse 14. O Lotus-Eyed one (Puṇḍarīkākṣa), if this statement (vākyam) was indeed spoken either by Dharmarāja (Yudhiṣṭhira), or by Bhīmasena, or again by you...

sanskrit: अजानतीमिषीकेयं जनित्रीं हन्त्विति प्रभो ।अहमेव विनष्टा स्यां नेदमेवंगतं भवेत् ॥१५॥
transliteration: ajānatīmiṣīkeyaṁ janitrīṁ hantviti prabho ,ahameva vinaṣṭā syāṁ nedamevaṁgataṁ bhavet.
english: O Lord, if this arrow (made of a reed) were to kill the unsuspecting mother, I myself would be ruined. Such a thing must not happen.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 67 Verse 15. O Lord, if this arrow (made of a reed) were to kill the unsuspecting mother, I myself would be ruined. Such a thing must not happen.

sanskrit: गर्भस्थस्यास्य बालस्य ब्रह्मास्त्रेण निपातनम् ।कृत्वा नृशंसं दुर्बुद्धिर्द्रौणिः किं फलमश्नुते ॥१६॥
transliteration: garbhasthasyāsya bālasya brahmāstreṇa nipātanam ,kṛtvā nṛśaṁsaṁ durbuddhirdrauṇiḥ kiṁ phalamaśnute.
english: What fruit does the evil-minded Droṇi achieve by cruelly slaying this child, who is still in the womb, with the divine weapon (brahmāstra)?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 67 Verse 16. What fruit does the evil-minded Droṇi achieve by cruelly slaying this child, who is still in the womb, with the divine weapon (brahmāstra)?

sanskrit: सा त्वा प्रसाद्य शिरसा याचे शत्रुनिबर्हण ।प्राणांस्त्यक्ष्यामि गोविन्द नायं संजीवते यदि ॥१७॥
transliteration: sā tvā prasādya śirasā yāce śatrunibarhaṇa ,prāṇāṁstyakṣyāmi govinda nāyaṁ saṁjīvate yadi.
english: O Destroyer of enemies, bowing my head, I implore you, O Govinda! I shall give up my life if this (child) does not revive.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 67 Verse 17. O Destroyer of enemies, bowing my head, I implore you, O Govinda! I shall give up my life if this (child) does not revive.

sanskrit: अस्मिन्हि बहवः साधो ये ममासन्मनोरथाः ।ते द्रोणपुत्रेण हताः किं नु जीवामि केशव ॥१८॥
transliteration: asminhi bahavaḥ sādho ye mamāsanmanorathāḥ ,te droṇaputreṇa hatāḥ kiṁ nu jīvāmi keśava.
english: O virtuous one, many hopes of mine were indeed centered on this (child). Those have been destroyed by the son of Droṇa. O Keśava, why should I continue to live?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 67 Verse 18. O virtuous one, many hopes of mine were indeed centered on this (child). Those have been destroyed by the son of Droṇa. O Keśava, why should I continue to live?

sanskrit: आसीन्मम मतिः कृष्ण पूर्णोत्सङ्गा जनार्दन ।अभिवादयिष्ये दिष्ट्येति तदिदं वितथीकृतम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: āsīnmama matiḥ kṛṣṇa pūrṇotsaṅgā janārdana ,abhivādayiṣye diṣṭyeti tadidaṁ vitathīkṛtam.
english: O Kṛṣṇa, O Janārdana, my hope was, 'Fortunately, I will joyfully welcome him.' But now that very hope has been rendered futile.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 67 Verse 19. O Kṛṣṇa, O Janārdana, my hope was, 'Fortunately, I will joyfully welcome him.' But now that very hope has been rendered futile.

sanskrit: चपलाक्षस्य दायादे मृतेऽस्मिन्पुरुषर्षभ ।विफला मे कृताः कृष्ण हृदि सर्वे मनोरथाः ॥२०॥
transliteration: capalākṣasya dāyāde mṛte'sminpuruṣarṣabha ,viphalā me kṛtāḥ kṛṣṇa hṛdi sarve manorathāḥ.
english: O Kṛṣṇa, O best among men, with the death of this heir, the son of the swift-eyed one, all my heart's desires have been rendered fruitless.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 67 Verse 20. O Kṛṣṇa, O best among men, with the death of this heir, the son of the swift-eyed one, all my heart's desires have been rendered fruitless.

sanskrit: चपलाक्षः किलातीव प्रियस्ते मधुसूदन ।सुतं पश्यस्व तस्येमं ब्रह्मास्त्रेण निपातितम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: capalākṣaḥ kilātīva priyaste madhusūdana ,sutaṁ paśyasva tasyemaṁ brahmāstreṇa nipātitam.
english: O Madhusūdana, they say that the swift-eyed one was exceedingly dear to you. Behold this son of his, struck down by the Brahmāstra.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 67 Verse 21. O Madhusūdana, they say that the swift-eyed one was exceedingly dear to you. Behold this son of his, struck down by the Brahmāstra.

sanskrit: कृतघ्नोऽयं नृशंसोऽयं यथास्य जनकस्तथा ।यः पाण्डवीं श्रियं त्यक्त्वा गतोऽद्य यमसादनम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: kṛtaghno'yaṁ nṛśaṁso'yaṁ yathāsya janakastathā ,yaḥ pāṇḍavīṁ śriyaṁ tyaktvā gato'dya yamasādanam.
english: This one is ungrateful; this one is cruel, just as his father was. He, who, having abandoned the prosperity of the Pāṇḍavas, has gone to the abode of Yama today.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 67 Verse 22. This one is ungrateful; this one is cruel, just as his father was. He, who, having abandoned the prosperity of the Pāṇḍavas, has gone to the abode of Yama today.

sanskrit: मया चैतत्प्रतिज्ञातं रणमूर्धनि केशव ।अभिमन्यौ हते वीर त्वामेष्याम्यचिरादिति ॥२३॥
transliteration: mayā caitatpratijñātaṁ raṇamūrdhani keśava ,abhimanyau hate vīra tvāmeṣyāmyacirāditi.
english: O Keśava, O hero, I made this vow on the battlefield: 'When Abhimanyu is killed, I will soon follow you (in death).'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 67 Verse 23. O Keśava, O hero, I made this vow on the battlefield: 'When Abhimanyu is killed, I will soon follow you (in death).'

sanskrit: तच्च नाकरवं कृष्ण नृशंसा जीवितप्रिया ।इदानीमागतां तत्र किं नु वक्ष्यति फाल्गुनिः ॥२४॥
transliteration: tacca nākaravaṁ kṛṣṇa nṛśaṁsā jīvitapriyā ,idānīmāgatāṁ tatra kiṁ nu vakṣyati phālguniḥ.
english: O Krishna, I am a cruel woman, fond of life, and I did not fulfill that vow. Now that I have come (remained alive), what will Phālguni (Arjuna) say to me there?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 67 Verse 24. O Krishna, I am a cruel woman, fond of life, and I did not fulfill that vow. Now that I have come (remained alive), what will Phālguni (Arjuna) say to me there?

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।सैवं विलप्य करुणं सोन्मादेव तपस्विनी ।उत्तरा न्यपतद्भूमौ कृपणा पुत्रगृद्धिनी ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,saivaṁ vilapya karuṇaṁ sonmādeva tapasvinī ,uttarā nyapatadbhūmau kṛpaṇā putragṛddhinī.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having thus lamented piteously, that wretched Uttarā, miserable and longing for her son, fell to the ground as if driven mad.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 68 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Having thus lamented piteously, that wretched Uttarā, miserable and longing for her son, fell to the ground as if driven mad.

sanskrit: तां तु दृष्ट्वा निपतितां हतबन्धुपरिच्छदाम् ।चुक्रोश कुन्ती दुःखार्ता सर्वाश्च भरतस्त्रियः ॥२॥
transliteration: tāṁ tu dṛṣṭvā nipatitāṁ hatabandhuparicchadām ,cukrośa kuntī duḥkhārtā sarvāśca bharatastriyaḥ.
english: But seeing her fallen, with her kinsmen and family destroyed, Kuntī, afflicted by sorrow, and all the Bhārata women cried out.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 68 Verse 2. But seeing her fallen, with her kinsmen and family destroyed, Kuntī, afflicted by sorrow, and all the Bhārata women cried out.

sanskrit: मुहूर्तमिव तद्राजन्पाण्डवानां निवेशनम् ।अप्रेक्षणीयमभवदार्तस्वरनिनादितम् ॥३॥
transliteration: muhūrtamiva tadrājanpāṇḍavānāṁ niveśanam ,aprekṣaṇīyamabhavadārtasvaranināditam.
english: O King, for a moment, that abode of the Pāṇḍavas became dreadful to behold, filled with sounds of lamentation.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 68 Verse 3. O King, for a moment, that abode of the Pāṇḍavas became dreadful to behold, filled with sounds of lamentation.

sanskrit: सा मुहूर्तं च राजेन्द्र पुत्रशोकाभिपीडिता ।कश्मलाभिहता वीर वैराटी त्वभवत्तदा ॥४॥
transliteration: sā muhūrtaṁ ca rājendra putraśokābhipīḍitā ,kaśmalābhihatā vīra vairāṭī tvabhavattadā.
english: And then, O King of kings, O hero, Uttarā, tormented by sorrow for her son, became overwhelmed by faintness for a moment.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 68 Verse 4. And then, O King of kings, O hero, Uttarā, tormented by sorrow for her son, became overwhelmed by faintness for a moment.

sanskrit: प्रतिलभ्य तु सा संज्ञामुत्तरा भरतर्षभ ।अङ्कमारोप्य तं पुत्रमिदं वचनमब्रवीत् ॥५॥
transliteration: pratilabhya tu sā saṁjñāmuttarā bharatarṣabha ,aṅkamāropya taṁ putramidaṁ vacanamabravīt.
english: However, O best of the Bhāratas, Uttarā, having regained her consciousness, took that son onto her lap and spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 68 Verse 5. However, O best of the Bhāratas, Uttarā, having regained her consciousness, took that son onto her lap and spoke these words.

sanskrit: धर्मज्ञस्य सुतः संस्त्वमधर्ममवबुध्यसे ।यस्त्वं वृष्णिप्रवीरस्य कुरुषे नाभिवादनम् ॥६॥
transliteration: dharmajñasya sutaḥ saṁstvamadharmamavabudhyase ,yastvaṁ vṛṣṇipravīrasya kuruṣe nābhivādanam.
english: You, being the son of one who understands natural law (dharma), how is it that you now perceive unrighteousness (adharma) – you, who do not offer salutation to the foremost hero of the Vṛṣṇis (Kṛṣṇa)?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 68 Verse 6. You, being the son of one who understands natural law (dharma), how is it that you now perceive unrighteousness (adharma) – you, who do not offer salutation to the foremost hero of the Vṛṣṇis (Kṛṣṇa)?

sanskrit: पुत्र गत्वा मम वचो ब्रूयास्त्वं पितरं तव ।दुर्मरं प्राणिनां वीर काले प्राप्ते कथंचन ॥७॥
transliteration: putra gatvā mama vaco brūyāstvaṁ pitaraṁ tava ,durmaraṁ prāṇināṁ vīra kāle prāpte kathaṁcana.
english: Son, go and convey my words to your father. O hero, for living beings, it is somehow difficult to avoid death once the destined time has arrived.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 68 Verse 7. Son, go and convey my words to your father. O hero, for living beings, it is somehow difficult to avoid death once the destined time has arrived.

sanskrit: याहं त्वया विहीनाद्य पत्या पुत्रेण चैव ह ।मर्तव्ये सति जीवामि हतस्वस्तिरकिंचना ॥८॥
transliteration: yāhaṁ tvayā vihīnādya patyā putreṇa caiva ha ,martavye sati jīvāmi hatasvastirakiṁcanā.
english: I, who today am bereft of you, my son, and my husband, indeed continue to live when I ought to die, having lost all well-being, utterly destitute.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 68 Verse 8. I, who today am bereft of you, my son, and my husband, indeed continue to live when I ought to die, having lost all well-being, utterly destitute.

sanskrit: अथ वा धर्मराज्ञाहमनुज्ञाता महाभुज ।भक्षयिष्ये विषं तीक्ष्णं प्रवेक्ष्ये वा हुताशनम् ॥९॥
transliteration: atha vā dharmarājñāhamanujñātā mahābhuja ,bhakṣayiṣye viṣaṁ tīkṣṇaṁ pravekṣye vā hutāśanam.
english: Or else, O mighty-armed one, if I am permitted by Yama, the king of (dharma), I will consume potent poison or enter the fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 68 Verse 9. Or else, O mighty-armed one, if I am permitted by Yama, the king of (dharma), I will consume potent poison or enter the fire.

sanskrit: अथ वा दुर्मरं तात यदिदं मे सहस्रधा ।पतिपुत्रविहीनाया हृदयं न विदीर्यते ॥१०॥
transliteration: atha vā durmaraṁ tāta yadidaṁ me sahasradhā ,patiputravihīnāyā hṛdayaṁ na vidīryate.
english: Or else, O dear one, that my heart, of one bereft of husband and son, does not tear into a thousand pieces – this, indeed, is a difficult death (durmara).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 68 Verse 10. Or else, O dear one, that my heart, of one bereft of husband and son, does not tear into a thousand pieces – this, indeed, is a difficult death (durmara).

sanskrit: उत्तिष्ठ पुत्र पश्येमां दुःखितां प्रपितामहीम् ।आर्तामुपप्लुतां दीनां निमग्नां शोकसागरे ॥११॥
transliteration: uttiṣṭha putra paśyemāṁ duḥkhitāṁ prapitāmahīm ,ārtāmupaplutāṁ dīnāṁ nimagnāṁ śokasāgare.
english: Rise up, my son, and behold your great-grandmother, who is distressed, afflicted, overwhelmed, pitiable, and submerged in an ocean of grief.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 68 Verse 11. Rise up, my son, and behold your great-grandmother, who is distressed, afflicted, overwhelmed, pitiable, and submerged in an ocean of grief.

sanskrit: आर्यां च पश्य पाञ्चालीं सात्वतीं च तपस्विनीम् ।मां च पश्य सुदुःखार्तां व्याधविद्धां मृगीमिव ॥१२॥
transliteration: āryāṁ ca paśya pāñcālīṁ sātvatīṁ ca tapasvinīm ,māṁ ca paśya suduḥkhārtāṁ vyādhaviddhāṁ mṛgīmiva.
english: And behold the noble Draupadī, the pious one, and behold me, greatly afflicted, like a female deer pierced by a hunter.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 68 Verse 12. And behold the noble Draupadī, the pious one, and behold me, greatly afflicted, like a female deer pierced by a hunter.

sanskrit: उत्तिष्ठ पश्य वदनं लोकनाथस्य धीमतः ।पुण्डरीकपलाशाक्षं पुरेव चपलेक्षणम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: uttiṣṭha paśya vadanaṁ lokanāthasya dhīmataḥ ,puṇḍarīkapalāśākṣaṁ pureva capalekṣaṇam.
english: Rise up, and behold the face of the wise lord of the world, whose eyes were like lotus petals and as vibrant as before.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 68 Verse 13. Rise up, and behold the face of the wise lord of the world, whose eyes were like lotus petals and as vibrant as before.

sanskrit: एवं विप्रलपन्तीं तु दृष्ट्वा निपतितां पुनः ।उत्तरां ताः स्त्रियः सर्वाः पुनरुत्थापयन्त्युत ॥१४॥
transliteration: evaṁ vipralapantīṁ tu dṛṣṭvā nipatitāṁ punaḥ ,uttarāṁ tāḥ striyaḥ sarvāḥ punarutthāpayantyuta.
english: Having seen Uttara thus lamenting and fallen down again, all those women then raised her up once more.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 68 Verse 14. Having seen Uttara thus lamenting and fallen down again, all those women then raised her up once more.

sanskrit: उत्थाय तु पुनर्धैर्यात्तदा मत्स्यपतेः सुता ।प्राञ्जलिः पुण्डरीकाक्षं भूमावेवाभ्यवादयत् ॥१५॥
transliteration: utthāya tu punardhairyāttadā matsyapateḥ sutā ,prāñjaliḥ puṇḍarīkākṣaṁ bhūmāvevābhyavādayat.
english: Then, the daughter of the Matsya king, regaining her courage, rose and, with folded hands, saluted the lotus-eyed one (Krishna) right there on the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 68 Verse 15. Then, the daughter of the Matsya king, regaining her courage, rose and, with folded hands, saluted the lotus-eyed one (Krishna) right there on the ground.

sanskrit: श्रुत्वा स तस्या विपुलं विलापं पुरुषर्षभः ।उपस्पृश्य ततः कृष्णो ब्रह्मास्त्रं संजहार तत् ॥१६॥
transliteration: śrutvā sa tasyā vipulaṁ vilāpaṁ puruṣarṣabhaḥ ,upaspṛśya tataḥ kṛṣṇo brahmāstraṁ saṁjahāra tat.
english: Having heard her extensive lamentation, Krishna, the best among men (puruṣarṣabha), then performed the ritual ablution (upaspṛśya) and withdrew that Brahmāstra.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 68 Verse 16. Having heard her extensive lamentation, Krishna, the best among men (puruṣarṣabha), then performed the ritual ablution (upaspṛśya) and withdrew that Brahmāstra.

sanskrit: प्रतिजज्ञे च दाशार्हस्तस्य जीवितमच्युतः ।अब्रवीच्च विशुद्धात्मा सर्वं विश्रावयञ्जगत् ॥१७॥
transliteration: pratijajñe ca dāśārhastasya jīvitamacyutaḥ ,abravīcca viśuddhātmā sarvaṁ viśrāvayañjagat.
english: And the infallible (acyuta) descendant of Daśārha (Krishna) promised him life. And the pure-souled one said, proclaiming this to the entire world:
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 68 Verse 17. And the infallible (acyuta) descendant of Daśārha (Krishna) promised him life. And the pure-souled one said, proclaiming this to the entire world:

sanskrit: न ब्रवीम्युत्तरे मिथ्या सत्यमेतद्भविष्यति ।एष संजीवयाम्येनं पश्यतां सर्वदेहिनाम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: na bravīmyuttare mithyā satyametadbhaviṣyati ,eṣa saṁjīvayāmyenaṁ paśyatāṁ sarvadehinām.
english: “O Uttara, I do not speak falsely; this will be the truth. I will revive him in the presence of all embodied beings.”
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 68 Verse 18. “O Uttara, I do not speak falsely; this will be the truth. I will revive him in the presence of all embodied beings.”

sanskrit: नोक्तपूर्वं मया मिथ्या स्वैरेष्वपि कदाचन ।न च युद्धे परावृत्तस्तथा संजीवतामयम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: noktapūrvaṁ mayā mithyā svaireṣvapi kadācana ,na ca yuddhe parāvṛttastathā saṁjīvatāmayam.
english: I have never spoken a falsehood, not even in jest, nor have I ever retreated in battle. By the power of this truth, may this one be revived.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 68 Verse 19. I have never spoken a falsehood, not even in jest, nor have I ever retreated in battle. By the power of this truth, may this one be revived.

sanskrit: यथा मे दयितो धर्मो ब्राह्मणाश्च विशेषतः ।अभिमन्योः सुतो जातो मृतो जीवत्वयं तथा ॥२०॥
transliteration: yathā me dayito dharmo brāhmaṇāśca viśeṣataḥ ,abhimanyoḥ suto jāto mṛto jīvatvayaṁ tathā.
english: Just as the intrinsic nature (dharma) of righteousness is dear to me, and Brahmins are especially so, may this son of Abhimanyu, who was born dead, be revived.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 68 Verse 20. Just as the intrinsic nature (dharma) of righteousness is dear to me, and Brahmins are especially so, may this son of Abhimanyu, who was born dead, be revived.

sanskrit: यथाहं नाभिजानामि विजयेन कदाचन ।विरोधं तेन सत्येन मृतो जीवत्वयं शिशुः ॥२१॥
transliteration: yathāhaṁ nābhijānāmi vijayena kadācana ,virodhaṁ tena satyena mṛto jīvatvayaṁ śiśuḥ.
english: Just as I have never experienced any animosity with Vijaya (Arjuna), by that truth, may this dead child be brought back to life.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 68 Verse 21. Just as I have never experienced any animosity with Vijaya (Arjuna), by that truth, may this dead child be brought back to life.

sanskrit: यथा सत्यं च धर्मश्च मयि नित्यं प्रतिष्ठितौ ।तथा मृतः शिशुरयं जीवतामभिमन्युजः ॥२२॥
transliteration: yathā satyaṁ ca dharmaśca mayi nityaṁ pratiṣṭhitau ,tathā mṛtaḥ śiśurayaṁ jīvatāmabhimanyujaḥ.
english: Just as truth and righteousness (dharma) are eternally established within me, so may this dead child, the son of Abhimanyu, be brought back to life.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 68 Verse 22. Just as truth and righteousness (dharma) are eternally established within me, so may this dead child, the son of Abhimanyu, be brought back to life.

sanskrit: यथा कंसश्च केशी च धर्मेण निहतौ मया ।तेन सत्येन बालोऽयं पुनरुज्जीवतामिह ॥२३॥
transliteration: yathā kaṁsaśca keśī ca dharmeṇa nihatau mayā ,tena satyena bālo'yaṁ punarujjīvatāmiha.
english: Just as Kamsa and Keśī were killed by me in accordance with natural law (dharma), by the power of that truth, may this child revive here again.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 68 Verse 23. Just as Kamsa and Keśī were killed by me in accordance with natural law (dharma), by the power of that truth, may this child revive here again.

sanskrit: इत्युक्तो वासुदेवेन स बालो भरतर्षभ ।शनैः शनैर्महाराज प्रास्पन्दत सचेतनः ॥२४॥
transliteration: ityukto vāsudevena sa bālo bharatarṣabha ,śanaiḥ śanairmahārāja prāspandata sacetanaḥ.
english: O best among the Bharatas, O great king, when Vāsudeva had thus spoken, that child slowly began to stir, regaining consciousness.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 68 Verse 24. O best among the Bharatas, O great king, when Vāsudeva had thus spoken, that child slowly began to stir, regaining consciousness.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ब्रह्मास्त्रं तु यदा राजन्कृष्णेन प्रतिसंहृतम् ।तदा तद्वेश्म ते पित्रा तेजसाभिविदीपितम् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,brahmāstraṁ tu yadā rājankṛṣṇena pratisaṁhṛtam ,tadā tadveśma te pitrā tejasābhividīpitam.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: 'O king, when the brahmāstra was indeed withdrawn by Krishna, then that house of your father was brilliantly illuminated by a divine radiance.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 69 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: 'O king, when the brahmāstra was indeed withdrawn by Krishna, then that house of your father was brilliantly illuminated by a divine radiance.'

sanskrit: ततो रक्षांसि सर्वाणि नेशुस्त्यक्त्वा गृहं तु तत् ।अन्तरिक्षे च वागासीत्साधु केशव साध्विति ॥२॥
transliteration: tato rakṣāṁsi sarvāṇi neśustyaktvā gṛhaṁ tu tat ,antarikṣe ca vāgāsītsādhu keśava sādhviti.
english: Then all the demons (rakṣas) abandoned that house and perished. And a voice in the sky proclaimed, 'Excellent, Keśava, excellent!'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 69 Verse 2. Then all the demons (rakṣas) abandoned that house and perished. And a voice in the sky proclaimed, 'Excellent, Keśava, excellent!'

sanskrit: तदस्त्रं ज्वलितं चापि पितामहमगात्तदा ।ततः प्राणान्पुनर्लेभे पिता तव जनेश्वर ।व्यचेष्टत च बालोऽसौ यथोत्साहं यथाबलम् ॥३॥
transliteration: tadastraṁ jvalitaṁ cāpi pitāmahamagāttadā ,tataḥ prāṇānpunarlebhe pitā tava janeśvara ,vyaceṣṭata ca bālo'sau yathotsāhaṁ yathābalam.
english: That blazing weapon also approached the venerable protector (Kṛṣṇa). Then, O lord of men (Yudhiṣṭhira), the lineage of your father (Abhimanyu) regained life. And that child (Parikṣit) struggled with full enthusiasm and strength.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 69 Verse 3. That blazing weapon also approached the venerable protector (Kṛṣṇa). Then, O lord of men (Yudhiṣṭhira), the lineage of your father (Abhimanyu) regained life. And that child (Parikṣit) struggled with full enthusiasm and strength.

sanskrit: बभूवुर्मुदिता राजंस्ततस्ता भरतस्त्रियः ।ब्राह्मणान्वाचयामासुर्गोविन्दस्य च शासनात् ॥४॥
transliteration: babhūvurmuditā rājaṁstatastā bharatastriyaḥ ,brāhmaṇānvācayāmāsurgovindasya ca śāsanāt.
english: Then, O king (Yudhiṣṭhira), those women of the Bharata lineage became delighted. And they had the Brahmins recite blessings by the command of Govinda (Kṛṣṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 69 Verse 4. Then, O king (Yudhiṣṭhira), those women of the Bharata lineage became delighted. And they had the Brahmins recite blessings by the command of Govinda (Kṛṣṇa).

sanskrit: ततस्ता मुदिताः सर्वाः प्रशशंसुर्जनार्दनम् ।स्त्रियो भरतसिंहानां नावं लब्ध्वेव पारगाः ॥५॥
transliteration: tatastā muditāḥ sarvāḥ praśaśaṁsurjanārdanam ,striyo bharatasiṁhānāṁ nāvaṁ labdhveva pāragāḥ.
english: Then, all those delighted women, the wives of the lion-like Bharatas (the Pāṇḍavas), praised Janārdana (Kṛṣṇa) as if they had found a boat to cross to the other shore.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 69 Verse 5. Then, all those delighted women, the wives of the lion-like Bharatas (the Pāṇḍavas), praised Janārdana (Kṛṣṇa) as if they had found a boat to cross to the other shore.

sanskrit: कुन्ती द्रुपदपुत्री च सुभद्रा चोत्तरा तथा ।स्त्रियश्चान्या नृसिंहानां बभूवुर्हृष्टमानसाः ॥६॥
transliteration: kuntī drupadaputrī ca subhadrā cottarā tathā ,striyaścānyā nṛsiṁhānāṁ babhūvurhṛṣṭamānasāḥ.
english: Kuntī, and Draupadī (Drupada's daughter), and Subhadrā, and Uttarā, as well as other wives of the lion-like men (the Pāṇḍavas), became gladdened in heart.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 69 Verse 6. Kuntī, and Draupadī (Drupada's daughter), and Subhadrā, and Uttarā, as well as other wives of the lion-like men (the Pāṇḍavas), became gladdened in heart.

sanskrit: तत्र मल्ला नटा झल्ला ग्रन्थिकाः सौखशायिकाः ।सूतमागधसंघाश्चाप्यस्तुवन्वै जनार्दनम् ।कुरुवंशस्तवाख्याभिराशीर्भिर्भरतर्षभ ॥७॥
transliteration: tatra mallā naṭā jhallā granthikāḥ saukhaśāyikāḥ ,sūtamāgadhasaṁghāścāpyastuvanvai janārdanam ,kuruvaṁśastavākhyābhirāśīrbhirbharatarṣabha.
english: There, wrestlers, actors, acrobats, storytellers, and royal bards, along with groups of bards and eulogists, all indeed praised Janardana (Kṛṣṇa) with descriptions of the Kuru dynasty and with blessings, O best of Bharatas.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 69 Verse 7. There, wrestlers, actors, acrobats, storytellers, and royal bards, along with groups of bards and eulogists, all indeed praised Janardana (Kṛṣṇa) with descriptions of the Kuru dynasty and with blessings, O best of Bharatas.

sanskrit: उत्थाय तु यथाकालमुत्तरा यदुनन्दनम् ।अभ्यवादयत प्रीता सह पुत्रेण भारत ।ततस्तस्यै ददौ प्रीतो बहुरत्नं विशेषतः ॥८॥
transliteration: utthāya tu yathākālamuttarā yadunandanam ,abhyavādayata prītā saha putreṇa bhārata ,tatastasyai dadau prīto bahuratnaṁ viśeṣataḥ.
english: Having risen at the proper time, Uttara, delighted, respectfully greeted Kṛṣṇa, the joy of the Yadus, along with her son, O Bharata. Then, Kṛṣṇa, being pleased, gave her many jewels in particular.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 69 Verse 8. Having risen at the proper time, Uttara, delighted, respectfully greeted Kṛṣṇa, the joy of the Yadus, along with her son, O Bharata. Then, Kṛṣṇa, being pleased, gave her many jewels in particular.

sanskrit: तथान्ये वृष्णिशार्दूला नाम चास्याकरोत्प्रभुः ।पितुस्तव महाराज सत्यसंधो जनार्दनः ॥९॥
transliteration: tathānye vṛṣṇiśārdūlā nāma cāsyākarotprabhuḥ ,pitustava mahārāja satyasaṁdho janārdanaḥ.
english: Similarly, O great king, other chief Vṛṣṇis, and Lord Janardana (Kṛṣṇa), who is true to his word, gave him (Parikṣit) a name, after your father.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 69 Verse 9. Similarly, O great king, other chief Vṛṣṇis, and Lord Janardana (Kṛṣṇa), who is true to his word, gave him (Parikṣit) a name, after your father.

sanskrit: परिक्षीणे कुले यस्माज्जातोऽयमभिमन्युजः ।परिक्षिदिति नामास्य भवत्वित्यब्रवीत्तदा ॥१०॥
transliteration: parikṣīṇe kule yasmājjāto'yamabhimanyujaḥ ,parikṣiditi nāmāsya bhavatvityabravīttadā.
english: Because this son of Abhimanyu was born when the lineage was nearly destroyed, he then declared, 'Let his name be Parikṣit!'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 69 Verse 10. Because this son of Abhimanyu was born when the lineage was nearly destroyed, he then declared, 'Let his name be Parikṣit!'

sanskrit: सोऽवर्धत यथाकालं पिता तव नराधिप ।मनःप्रह्लादनश्चासीत्सर्वलोकस्य भारत ॥११॥
transliteration: so'vardhata yathākālaṁ pitā tava narādhipa ,manaḥprahlādanaścāsītsarvalokasya bhārata.
english: O ruler of men (narādhipa), your father grew up in due course. And, O Bhārata, he was a delight to the hearts of all people.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 69 Verse 11. O ruler of men (narādhipa), your father grew up in due course. And, O Bhārata, he was a delight to the hearts of all people.

sanskrit: मासजातस्तु ते वीर पिता भवति भारत ।अथाजग्मुः सुबहुलं रत्नमादाय पाण्डवाः ॥१२॥
transliteration: māsajātastu te vīra pitā bhavati bhārata ,athājagmuḥ subahulaṁ ratnamādāya pāṇḍavāḥ.
english: O hero (vīra), O Bhārata, your father is just one month old. Then the Pāṇḍavas arrived, bringing a great many jewels.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 69 Verse 12. O hero (vīra), O Bhārata, your father is just one month old. Then the Pāṇḍavas arrived, bringing a great many jewels.

sanskrit: तान्समीपगताञ्श्रुत्वा निर्ययुर्वृष्णिपुंगवाः ।अलंचक्रुश्च माल्यौघैः पुरुषा नागसाह्वयम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: tānsamīpagatāñśrutvā niryayurvṛṣṇipuṁgavāḥ ,alaṁcakruśca mālyaughaiḥ puruṣā nāgasāhvayam.
english: Having heard that they had arrived nearby, the chief Vṛṣṇis went forth. And the people decorated Hastināpura with abundant garlands.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 69 Verse 13. Having heard that they had arrived nearby, the chief Vṛṣṇis went forth. And the people decorated Hastināpura with abundant garlands.

sanskrit: पताकाभिर्विचित्राभिर्ध्वजैश्च विविधैरपि ।वेश्मानि समलंचक्रुः पौराश्चापि जनाधिप ॥१४॥
transliteration: patākābhirvicitrābhirdhvajaiśca vividhairapi ,veśmāni samalaṁcakruḥ paurāścāpi janādhipa.
english: O ruler of men (janādhipa), the citizens also beautifully decorated the houses with diverse colorful banners and various flags.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 69 Verse 14. O ruler of men (janādhipa), the citizens also beautifully decorated the houses with diverse colorful banners and various flags.

sanskrit: देवतायतनानां च पूजा बहुविधास्तथा ।संदिदेशाथ विदुरः पाण्डुपुत्रप्रियेप्सया ॥१५॥
transliteration: devatāyatanānāṁ ca pūjā bahuvidhāstathā ,saṁdideśātha viduraḥ pāṇḍuputrapriyepsayā.
english: Then, desiring to please the sons of Pāṇḍu, Vidura gave instructions for various kinds of worship and offerings to be performed in the temples of the deities.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 69 Verse 15. Then, desiring to please the sons of Pāṇḍu, Vidura gave instructions for various kinds of worship and offerings to be performed in the temples of the deities.

sanskrit: राजमार्गाश्च तत्रासन्सुमनोभिरलंकृताः ।शुशुभे तत्पुरं चापि समुद्रौघनिभस्वनम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: rājamārgāśca tatrāsansumanobhiralaṁkṛtāḥ ,śuśubhe tatpuraṁ cāpi samudraughanibhasvanam.
english: The main thoroughfares there were adorned with flowers. That city also shone splendidly, its sound resembling the roar of the ocean waves.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 69 Verse 16. The main thoroughfares there were adorned with flowers. That city also shone splendidly, its sound resembling the roar of the ocean waves.

sanskrit: नर्तकैश्चापि नृत्यद्भिर्गायनानां च निस्वनैः ।आसीद्वैश्रवणस्येव निवासस्तत्पुरं तदा ॥१७॥
transliteration: nartakaiścāpi nṛtyadbhirgāyanānāṁ ca nisvanaiḥ ,āsīdvaiśravaṇasyeva nivāsastatpuraṁ tadā.
english: With dancing performers and the melodies of singers, that city then appeared like the dwelling of Vaiśravaṇa (Kubera).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 69 Verse 17. With dancing performers and the melodies of singers, that city then appeared like the dwelling of Vaiśravaṇa (Kubera).

sanskrit: बन्दिभिश्च नरै राजन्स्त्रीसहायैः सहस्रशः ।तत्र तत्र विविक्तेषु समन्तादुपशोभितम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: bandibhiśca narai rājanstrīsahāyaiḥ sahasraśaḥ ,tatra tatra vivikteṣu samantādupaśobhitam.
english: O king, that place was splendidly adorned everywhere, in various clean and secluded spots, by thousands of bards and men accompanied by women.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 69 Verse 18. O king, that place was splendidly adorned everywhere, in various clean and secluded spots, by thousands of bards and men accompanied by women.

sanskrit: पताका धूयमानाश्च श्वसता मातरिश्वना ।अदर्शयन्निव तदा कुरून्वै दक्षिणोत्तरान् ॥१९॥
transliteration: patākā dhūyamānāśca śvasatā mātariśvanā ,adarśayanniva tadā kurūnvai dakṣiṇottarān.
english: At that time, the banners, being swayed by the blowing wind, seemed to indicate the Kurus' positions to the south and north.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 69 Verse 19. At that time, the banners, being swayed by the blowing wind, seemed to indicate the Kurus' positions to the south and north.

sanskrit: अघोषयत्तदा चापि पुरुषो राजधूर्गतः ।सर्वरात्रिविहारोऽद्य रत्नाभरणलक्षणः ॥२०॥
transliteration: aghoṣayattadā cāpi puruṣo rājadhūrgataḥ ,sarvarātrivihāro'dya ratnābharaṇalakṣaṇaḥ.
english: A royal official then also proclaimed, "Tonight's entertainment will feature jewels and ornaments."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 69 Verse 20. A royal official then also proclaimed, "Tonight's entertainment will feature jewels and ornaments."

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तान्समीपगताञ्श्रुत्वा पाण्डवाञ्शत्रुकर्शनः ।वासुदेवः सहामात्यः प्रत्युद्यातो दिदृक्षया ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tānsamīpagatāñśrutvā pāṇḍavāñśatrukarśanaḥ ,vāsudevaḥ sahāmātyaḥ pratyudyāto didṛkṣayā.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Hearing that the Pāṇḍavas had approached, Vāsudeva, the subduer of enemies, accompanied by his ministers, went forth, eager to see them.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 70 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Hearing that the Pāṇḍavas had approached, Vāsudeva, the subduer of enemies, accompanied by his ministers, went forth, eager to see them.

sanskrit: ते समेत्य यथान्यायं पाण्डवा वृष्णिभिः सह ।विविशुः सहिता राजन्पुरं वारणसाह्वयम् ॥२॥
transliteration: te sametya yathānyāyaṁ pāṇḍavā vṛṣṇibhiḥ saha ,viviśuḥ sahitā rājanpuraṁ vāraṇasāhvayam.
english: O King, the Pāṇḍavas, having met appropriately with the Vṛṣṇis, then together entered the city named Vāraṇāsa.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 70 Verse 2. O King, the Pāṇḍavas, having met appropriately with the Vṛṣṇis, then together entered the city named Vāraṇāsa.

sanskrit: महतस्तस्य सैन्यस्य खुरनेमिस्वनेन च ।द्यावापृथिव्यौ खं चैव शब्देनासीत्समावृतम् ॥३॥
transliteration: mahatastasya sainyasya khuranemisvanena ca ,dyāvāpṛthivyau khaṁ caiva śabdenāsītsamāvṛtam.
english: Heaven and earth, and even the sky, were completely enveloped by the sound of the hooves and wheel-rims of that great army.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 70 Verse 3. Heaven and earth, and even the sky, were completely enveloped by the sound of the hooves and wheel-rims of that great army.

sanskrit: ते कोशमग्रतः कृत्वा विविशुः स्वपुरं तदा ।पाण्डवाः प्रीतमनसः सामात्याः ससुहृद्गणाः ॥४॥
transliteration: te kośamagrataḥ kṛtvā viviśuḥ svapuraṁ tadā ,pāṇḍavāḥ prītamanasaḥ sāmātyāḥ sasuhṛdgaṇāḥ.
english: Then, the Pāṇḍavas, with cheerful minds, accompanied by their ministers and groups of friends, placed the wealth in front and entered their own city.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 70 Verse 4. Then, the Pāṇḍavas, with cheerful minds, accompanied by their ministers and groups of friends, placed the wealth in front and entered their own city.

sanskrit: ते समेत्य यथान्यायं धृतराष्ट्रं जनाधिपम् ।कीर्तयन्तः स्वनामानि तस्य पादौ ववन्दिरे ॥५॥
transliteration: te sametya yathānyāyaṁ dhṛtarāṣṭraṁ janādhipam ,kīrtayantaḥ svanāmāni tasya pādau vavandire.
english: They, having properly approached King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, and proclaiming their own names, bowed down to his feet.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 70 Verse 5. They, having properly approached King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, and proclaiming their own names, bowed down to his feet.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्रादनु च ते गान्धारीं सुबलात्मजाम् ।कुन्तीं च राजशार्दूल तदा भरतसत्तमाः ॥६॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭrādanu ca te gāndhārīṁ subalātmajām ,kuntīṁ ca rājaśārdūla tadā bharatasattamāḥ.
english: And then, O tiger among kings, O best of the Bharatas, after (bowing to) Dhṛtarāṣṭra, they (bowed to) Gāndhārī, the daughter of Subala, and Kuntī.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 70 Verse 6. And then, O tiger among kings, O best of the Bharatas, after (bowing to) Dhṛtarāṣṭra, they (bowed to) Gāndhārī, the daughter of Subala, and Kuntī.

sanskrit: विदुरं पूजयित्वा च वैश्यापुत्रं समेत्य च ।पूज्यमानाः स्म ते वीरा व्यराजन्त विशां पते ॥७॥
transliteration: viduraṁ pūjayitvā ca vaiśyāputraṁ sametya ca ,pūjyamānāḥ sma te vīrā vyarājanta viśāṁ pate.
english: Having honored Vidura and having met the son of the vaiśya woman, those heroes, being honored in return, shone brightly, O lord of people.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 70 Verse 7. Having honored Vidura and having met the son of the vaiśya woman, those heroes, being honored in return, shone brightly, O lord of people.

sanskrit: ततस्तत्परमाश्चर्यं विचित्रं महदद्भुतम् ।शुश्रुवुस्ते तदा वीराः पितुस्ते जन्म भारत ॥८॥
transliteration: tatastatparamāścaryaṁ vicitraṁ mahadadbhutam ,śuśruvuste tadā vīrāḥ pituste janma bhārata.
english: Then, O Bhārata, those heroes heard that supreme, amazing, great marvel: the birth of your father.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 70 Verse 8. Then, O Bhārata, those heroes heard that supreme, amazing, great marvel: the birth of your father.

sanskrit: तदुपश्रुत्य ते कर्म वासुदेवस्य धीमतः ।पूजार्हं पूजयामासुः कृष्णं देवकिनन्दनम् ॥९॥
transliteration: tadupaśrutya te karma vāsudevasya dhīmataḥ ,pūjārhaṁ pūjayāmāsuḥ kṛṣṇaṁ devakinandanam.
english: Having heard of that deed (karma) of the wise Vāsudeva, they honored Kṛṣṇa, the son of Devakī, who was truly worthy of honor.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 70 Verse 9. Having heard of that deed (karma) of the wise Vāsudeva, they honored Kṛṣṇa, the son of Devakī, who was truly worthy of honor.

sanskrit: ततः कतिपयाहस्य व्यासः सत्यवतीसुतः ।आजगाम महातेजा नगरं नागसाह्वयम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: tataḥ katipayāhasya vyāsaḥ satyavatīsutaḥ ,ājagāma mahātejā nagaraṁ nāgasāhvayam.
english: Then, after a few days, the greatly effulgent Vyāsa, son of Satyavatī, arrived at the city named Nāga (Hastināpura).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 70 Verse 10. Then, after a few days, the greatly effulgent Vyāsa, son of Satyavatī, arrived at the city named Nāga (Hastināpura).

sanskrit: तस्य सर्वे यथान्यायं पूजां चक्रुः कुरूद्वहाः ।सह वृष्ण्यन्धकव्याघ्रैरुपासां चक्रिरे तदा ॥११॥
transliteration: tasya sarve yathānyāyaṁ pūjāṁ cakruḥ kurūdvahāḥ ,saha vṛṣṇyandhakavyāghrairupāsāṁ cakrire tadā.
english: Then all the leaders of the Kurus, along with the eminent warriors of the Vṛṣṇi and Andhaka clans, duly offered him honor and attended upon him.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 70 Verse 11. Then all the leaders of the Kurus, along with the eminent warriors of the Vṛṣṇi and Andhaka clans, duly offered him honor and attended upon him.

sanskrit: तत्र नानाविधाकाराः कथाः समनुकीर्त्य वै ।युधिष्ठिरो धर्मसुतो व्यासं वचनमब्रवीत् ॥१२॥
transliteration: tatra nānāvidhākārāḥ kathāḥ samanukīrtya vai ,yudhiṣṭhiro dharmasuto vyāsaṁ vacanamabravīt.
english: Then, after indeed narrating various kinds of stories, Yudhishthira, the son of natural law (dharma), spoke to Vyasa.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 70 Verse 12. Then, after indeed narrating various kinds of stories, Yudhishthira, the son of natural law (dharma), spoke to Vyasa.

sanskrit: भवत्प्रसादाद्भगवन्यदिदं रत्नमाहृतम् ।उपयोक्तुं तदिच्छामि वाजिमेधे महाक्रतौ ॥१३॥
transliteration: bhavatprasādādbhagavanyadidaṁ ratnamāhṛtam ,upayoktuṁ tadicchāmi vājimedhe mahākratau.
english: By your grace, O venerable one, I wish to employ this jewel, which has been acquired, in the great horse (Vedic ritual) (vājimegha).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 70 Verse 13. By your grace, O venerable one, I wish to employ this jewel, which has been acquired, in the great horse (Vedic ritual) (vājimegha).

sanskrit: तदनुज्ञातुमिच्छामि भवता मुनिसत्तम ।त्वदधीना वयं सर्वे कृष्णस्य च महात्मनः ॥१४॥
transliteration: tadanujñātumicchāmi bhavatā munisattama ,tvadadhīnā vayaṁ sarve kṛṣṇasya ca mahātmanaḥ.
english: O best among sages, I desire that you permit this. We are all dependent on you and on the great-souled Krishna.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 70 Verse 14. O best among sages, I desire that you permit this. We are all dependent on you and on the great-souled Krishna.

sanskrit: व्यास उवाच ।अनुजानामि राजंस्त्वां क्रियतां यदनन्तरम् ।यजस्व वाजिमेधेन विधिवद्दक्षिणावता ॥१५॥
transliteration: vyāsa uvāca ,anujānāmi rājaṁstvāṁ kriyatāṁ yadanantaram ,yajasva vājimedhena vidhivaddakṣiṇāvatā.
english: Vyasa said: "O King, I permit you. Let what is next be done. Perform the aśvamedha (Vedic ritual) according to the rules, accompanied by generous offerings."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 70 Verse 15. Vyasa said: "O King, I permit you. Let what is next be done. Perform the aśvamedha (Vedic ritual) according to the rules, accompanied by generous offerings."

sanskrit: अश्वमेधो हि राजेन्द्र पावनः सर्वपाप्मनाम् ।तेनेष्ट्वा त्वं विपाप्मा वै भविता नात्र संशयः ॥१६॥
transliteration: aśvamedho hi rājendra pāvanaḥ sarvapāpmanām ,teneṣṭvā tvaṁ vipāpmā vai bhavitā nātra saṁśayaḥ.
english: Indeed, O King of Kings, the aśvamedha (Vedic ritual) is a purifier of all transgressions. Having performed that, you will certainly become free from misdeeds; there is no doubt about this.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 70 Verse 16. Indeed, O King of Kings, the aśvamedha (Vedic ritual) is a purifier of all transgressions. Having performed that, you will certainly become free from misdeeds; there is no doubt about this.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।इत्युक्तः स तु धर्मात्मा कुरुराजो युधिष्ठिरः ।अश्वमेधस्य कौरव्य चकाराहरणे मतिम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,ityuktaḥ sa tu dharmātmā kururājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,aśvamedhasya kauravya cakārāharaṇe matim.
english: Vaishampayana said: "Thus addressed, that virtuous King Yudhiṣṭhira, ruler of the Kurus, O descendant of Kuru, resolved to arrange for the aśvamedha (Vedic ritual)."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 70 Verse 17. Vaishampayana said: "Thus addressed, that virtuous King Yudhiṣṭhira, ruler of the Kurus, O descendant of Kuru, resolved to arrange for the aśvamedha (Vedic ritual)."

sanskrit: समनुज्ञाप्य तु स तं कृष्णद्वैपायनं नृपः ।वासुदेवमथामन्त्र्य वाग्मी वचनमब्रवीत् ॥१८॥
transliteration: samanujñāpya tu sa taṁ kṛṣṇadvaipāyanaṁ nṛpaḥ ,vāsudevamathāmantrya vāgmī vacanamabravīt.
english: But having taken leave of Vyasa (Kṛṣṇa Dvaipāyana), the king (Yudhiṣṭhira) then consulted Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa), and the eloquent one spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 70 Verse 18. But having taken leave of Vyasa (Kṛṣṇa Dvaipāyana), the king (Yudhiṣṭhira) then consulted Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa), and the eloquent one spoke these words.

sanskrit: देवकी सुप्रजा देवी त्वया पुरुषसत्तम ।यद्ब्रूयां त्वां महाबाहो तत्कृथास्त्वमिहाच्युत ॥१९॥
transliteration: devakī suprajā devī tvayā puruṣasattama ,yadbrūyāṁ tvāṁ mahābāho tatkṛthāstvamihācyuta.
english: O best among men (puruṣasattama), Devaki is blessed with good children through you. Whatever I may tell you here, O mighty-armed one (mahābāho), O infallible one (acyuta), you should accomplish that.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 70 Verse 19. O best among men (puruṣasattama), Devaki is blessed with good children through you. Whatever I may tell you here, O mighty-armed one (mahābāho), O infallible one (acyuta), you should accomplish that.

sanskrit: त्वत्प्रभावार्जितान्भोगानश्नीम यदुनन्दन ।पराक्रमेण बुद्ध्या च त्वयेयं निर्जिता मही ॥२०॥
transliteration: tvatprabhāvārjitānbhogānaśnīma yadunandana ,parākrameṇa buddhyā ca tvayeyaṁ nirjitā mahī.
english: O delight of the Yadus (yadunandana), we enjoy the pleasures (or possessions) acquired through your influence. This earth has been conquered by you by means of your valor and intelligence.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 70 Verse 20. O delight of the Yadus (yadunandana), we enjoy the pleasures (or possessions) acquired through your influence. This earth has been conquered by you by means of your valor and intelligence.

sanskrit: दीक्षयस्व त्वमात्मानं त्वं नः परमको गुरुः ।त्वयीष्टवति धर्मज्ञ विपाप्मा स्यामहं विभो ।त्वं हि यज्ञोऽक्षरः सर्वस्त्वं धर्मस्त्वं प्रजापतिः ॥२१॥
transliteration: dīkṣayasva tvamātmānaṁ tvaṁ naḥ paramako guruḥ ,tvayīṣṭavati dharmajña vipāpmā syāmahaṁ vibho ,tvaṁ hi yajño'kṣaraḥ sarvastvaṁ dharmastvaṁ prajāpatiḥ.
english: Consecrate yourself; you are our supreme preceptor (guru). O knower of natural law (dharma) and mighty lord (vibho), when you perform the Vedic ritual (yajña), I will become free from sin. Indeed, you are the imperishable, entire Vedic ritual; you are natural law; you are Prajāpati.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 70 Verse 21. Consecrate yourself; you are our supreme preceptor (guru). O knower of natural law (dharma) and mighty lord (vibho), when you perform the Vedic ritual (yajña), I will become free from sin. Indeed, you are the imperishable, entire Vedic ritual; you are natural law; you are Prajāpati.

sanskrit: वासुदेव उवाच ।त्वमेवैतन्महाबाहो वक्तुमर्हस्यरिंदम ।त्वं गतिः सर्वभूतानामिति मे निश्चिता मतिः ॥२२॥
transliteration: vāsudeva uvāca ,tvamevaitanmahābāho vaktumarhasyariṁdama ,tvaṁ gatiḥ sarvabhūtānāmiti me niścitā matiḥ.
english: Vāsudeva said: O mighty-armed one (mahābāho), O conqueror of enemies (arindama), only you are worthy to speak of this. My mind is firmly convinced that you are the ultimate refuge of all beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 70 Verse 22. Vāsudeva said: O mighty-armed one (mahābāho), O conqueror of enemies (arindama), only you are worthy to speak of this. My mind is firmly convinced that you are the ultimate refuge of all beings.

sanskrit: त्वं चाद्य कुरुवीराणां धर्मेणाभिविराजसे ।गुणभूताः स्म ते राजंस्त्वं नो राजन्मतो गुरुः ॥२३॥
transliteration: tvaṁ cādya kuruvīrāṇāṁ dharmeṇābhivirājase ,guṇabhūtāḥ sma te rājaṁstvaṁ no rājanmato guruḥ.
english: And today, among the Kuru heroes, you shine forth through your intrinsic nature (dharma). We are your subordinates, O King; you are regarded as our teacher (guru), O King.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 70 Verse 23. And today, among the Kuru heroes, you shine forth through your intrinsic nature (dharma). We are your subordinates, O King; you are regarded as our teacher (guru), O King.

sanskrit: यजस्व मदनुज्ञातः प्राप्त एव क्रतुर्मया ।युनक्तु नो भवान्कार्ये यत्र वाञ्छसि भारत ।सत्यं ते प्रतिजानामि सर्वं कर्तास्मि तेऽनघ ॥२४॥
transliteration: yajasva madanujñātaḥ prāpta eva kraturmayā ,yunaktu no bhavānkārye yatra vāñchasi bhārata ,satyaṁ te pratijānāmi sarvaṁ kartāsmi te'nagha.
english: Perform the Vedic ritual (yajña) as sanctioned by me; indeed, the performance has been secured by me. May you engage us in any task you desire, O Bhārata. I truly promise you, O sinless one, I will accomplish everything for you.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 70 Verse 24. Perform the Vedic ritual (yajña) as sanctioned by me; indeed, the performance has been secured by me. May you engage us in any task you desire, O Bhārata. I truly promise you, O sinless one, I will accomplish everything for you.

sanskrit: भीमसेनार्जुनौ चैव तथा माद्रवतीसुतौ ।इष्टवन्तो भविष्यन्ति त्वयीष्टवति भारत ॥२५॥
transliteration: bhīmasenārjunau caiva tathā mādravatīsutau ,iṣṭavanto bhaviṣyanti tvayīṣṭavati bhārata.
english: Bhīmasena and Arjuna, as well as the sons of Mādrī (Nakula and Sahadeva), will indeed perform rituals when you yourself have performed them, O Bhārata.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 70 Verse 25. Bhīmasena and Arjuna, as well as the sons of Mādrī (Nakula and Sahadeva), will indeed perform rituals when you yourself have performed them, O Bhārata.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवमुक्तस्तु कृष्णेन धर्मपुत्रो युधिष्ठिरः ।व्यासमामन्त्र्य मेधावी ततो वचनमब्रवीत् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evamuktastu kṛṣṇena dharmaputro yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,vyāsamāmantrya medhāvī tato vacanamabravīt.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Addressed thus by Kṛṣṇa, Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of dharma (dharma), the intelligent one, then having consulted Vyāsa, spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 71 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Addressed thus by Kṛṣṇa, Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of dharma (dharma), the intelligent one, then having consulted Vyāsa, spoke these words.

sanskrit: यथा कालं भवान्वेत्ति हयमेधस्य तत्त्वतः ।दीक्षयस्व तदा मा त्वं त्वय्यायत्तो हि मे क्रतुः ॥२॥
transliteration: yathā kālaṁ bhavānvetti hayamedhasya tattvataḥ ,dīkṣayasva tadā mā tvaṁ tvayyāyatto hi me kratuḥ.
english: Since you truly know the essence of the horse-Vedic ritual (yajña) and its proper timing, please initiate me then, for my ritual (kratu) indeed depends on you.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 71 Verse 2. Since you truly know the essence of the horse-Vedic ritual (yajña) and its proper timing, please initiate me then, for my ritual (kratu) indeed depends on you.

sanskrit: व्यास उवाच ।अहं पैलोऽथ कौन्तेय याज्ञवल्क्यस्तथैव च ।विधानं यद्यथाकालं तत्कर्तारो न संशयः ॥३॥
transliteration: vyāsa uvāca ,ahaṁ pailo'tha kaunteya yājñavalkyastathaiva ca ,vidhānaṁ yadyathākālaṁ tatkartāro na saṁśayaḥ.
english: Vyāsa said: 'O Kaunteya, I am Paila, and Yājñavalkya is also here. We are certainly the performers of whatever procedures are required at the proper time; there is no doubt about this.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 71 Verse 3. Vyāsa said: 'O Kaunteya, I am Paila, and Yājñavalkya is also here. We are certainly the performers of whatever procedures are required at the proper time; there is no doubt about this.'

sanskrit: चैत्र्यां हि पौर्णमास्यां च तव दीक्षा भविष्यति ।संभाराः संभ्रियन्तां ते यज्ञार्थं पुरुषर्षभ ॥४॥
transliteration: caitryāṁ hi paurṇamāsyāṁ ca tava dīkṣā bhaviṣyati ,saṁbhārāḥ saṁbhriyantāṁ te yajñārthaṁ puruṣarṣabha.
english: Indeed, your initiation will take place on the full moon day of the month of Caitra. O best among men, let your provisions for the Vedic ritual (yajña) be gathered.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 71 Verse 4. Indeed, your initiation will take place on the full moon day of the month of Caitra. O best among men, let your provisions for the Vedic ritual (yajña) be gathered.

sanskrit: अश्वविद्याविदश्चैव सूता विप्राश्च तद्विदः ।मेध्यमश्वं परीक्षन्तां तव यज्ञार्थसिद्धये ॥५॥
transliteration: aśvavidyāvidaścaiva sūtā viprāśca tadvidaḥ ,medhyamaśvaṁ parīkṣantāṁ tava yajñārthasiddhaye.
english: Let the experts in horse-knowledge, the charioteers, and the Brahmins who understand these matters examine the sacred horse for the successful completion of your Vedic ritual (yajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 71 Verse 5. Let the experts in horse-knowledge, the charioteers, and the Brahmins who understand these matters examine the sacred horse for the successful completion of your Vedic ritual (yajña).

sanskrit: तमुत्सृज्य यथाशास्त्रं पृथिवीं सागराम्बराम् ।स पर्येतु यशो नाम्ना तव पार्थिव वर्धयन् ॥६॥
transliteration: tamutsṛjya yathāśāstraṁ pṛthivīṁ sāgarāmbarām ,sa paryetu yaśo nāmnā tava pārthiva vardhayan.
english: O King, having given up the ocean-girdled earth according to the scriptures, may he travel around, increasing your fame by name.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 71 Verse 6. O King, having given up the ocean-girdled earth according to the scriptures, may he travel around, increasing your fame by name.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।इत्युक्तः स तथेत्युक्त्वा पाण्डवः पृथिवीपतिः ।चकार सर्वं राजेन्द्र यथोक्तं ब्रह्मवादिना ।संभाराश्चैव राजेन्द्र सर्वे संकल्पिताभवन् ॥७॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,ityuktaḥ sa tathetyuktvā pāṇḍavaḥ pṛthivīpatiḥ ,cakāra sarvaṁ rājendra yathoktaṁ brahmavādinā ,saṁbhārāścaiva rājendra sarve saṁkalpitābhavan.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Thus addressed, that Pāṇḍava, the lord of the earth, saying, "So be it," did everything as instructed by the speaker of (brahman). And indeed, O King, all the preparations were arranged.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 71 Verse 7. Vaiśampāyana said: Thus addressed, that Pāṇḍava, the lord of the earth, saying, "So be it," did everything as instructed by the speaker of (brahman). And indeed, O King, all the preparations were arranged.

sanskrit: स संभारान्समाहृत्य नृपो धर्मात्मजस्तदा ।न्यवेदयदमेयात्मा कृष्णद्वैपायनाय वै ॥८॥
transliteration: sa saṁbhārānsamāhṛtya nṛpo dharmātmajastadā ,nyavedayadameyātmā kṛṣṇadvaipāyanāya vai.
english: Then, that king, the son of (dharma), of immeasurable spirit (ātman), having collected all the preparations, indeed presented them to Kṛṣṇadvaipāyana (Vyāsa).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 71 Verse 8. Then, that king, the son of (dharma), of immeasurable spirit (ātman), having collected all the preparations, indeed presented them to Kṛṣṇadvaipāyana (Vyāsa).

sanskrit: ततोऽब्रवीन्महातेजा व्यासो धर्मात्मजं नृपम् ।यथाकालं यथायोगं सज्जाः स्म तव दीक्षणे ॥९॥
transliteration: tato'bravīnmahātejā vyāso dharmātmajaṁ nṛpam ,yathākālaṁ yathāyogaṁ sajjāḥ sma tava dīkṣaṇe.
english: Then, the greatly effulgent Vyāsa spoke to the king, the son of (dharma): "We are ready, at the appropriate time and in the proper manner, for your initiation (dīkṣā)."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 71 Verse 9. Then, the greatly effulgent Vyāsa spoke to the king, the son of (dharma): "We are ready, at the appropriate time and in the proper manner, for your initiation (dīkṣā)."

sanskrit: स्फ्यश्च कूर्चश्च सौवर्णो यच्चान्यदपि कौरव ।तत्र योग्यं भवेत्किंचित्तद्रौक्मं क्रियतामिति ॥१०॥
transliteration: sphyaśca kūrcaśca sauvarṇo yaccānyadapi kaurava ,tatra yogyaṁ bhavetkiṁcittadraukmaṁ kriyatāmiti.
english: O Kaurava, the ritual wooden sword and the darbha grass brush should be golden. Whatever else is suitable there, let that too be made of gold.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 71 Verse 10. O Kaurava, the ritual wooden sword and the darbha grass brush should be golden. Whatever else is suitable there, let that too be made of gold.

sanskrit: अश्वश्चोत्सृज्यतामद्य पृथ्व्यामथ यथाक्रमम् ।सुगुप्तश्च चरत्वेष यथाशास्त्रं युधिष्ठिर ॥११॥
transliteration: aśvaścotsṛjyatāmadya pṛthvyāmatha yathākramam ,suguptaśca caratveṣa yathāśāstraṁ yudhiṣṭhira.
english: O Yudhiṣṭhira, let the horse be released onto the earth today, in due order. And let this horse wander well-guarded, in accordance with the scriptures.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 71 Verse 11. O Yudhiṣṭhira, let the horse be released onto the earth today, in due order. And let this horse wander well-guarded, in accordance with the scriptures.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।अयमश्वो मया ब्रह्मन्नुत्सृष्टः पृथिवीमिमाम् ।चरिष्यति यथाकामं तत्र वै संविधीयताम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,ayamaśvo mayā brahmannutsṛṣṭaḥ pṛthivīmimām ,cariṣyati yathākāmaṁ tatra vai saṁvidhīyatām.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: 'O brahmin (brahman), this horse has been released by me onto this earth. It will wander as it pleases. Therefore, let arrangements be made accordingly.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 71 Verse 12. Yudhiṣṭhira said: 'O brahmin (brahman), this horse has been released by me onto this earth. It will wander as it pleases. Therefore, let arrangements be made accordingly.'

sanskrit: पृथिवीं पर्यटन्तं हि तुरगं कामचारिणम् ।कः पालयेदिति मुने तद्भवान्वक्तुमर्हति ॥१३॥
transliteration: pṛthivīṁ paryaṭantaṁ hi turagaṁ kāmacāriṇam ,kaḥ pālayediti mune tadbhavānvaktumarhati.
english: O sage (muni), who indeed should protect this horse that wanders the earth at will? You ought to tell me that.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 71 Verse 13. O sage (muni), who indeed should protect this horse that wanders the earth at will? You ought to tell me that.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।इत्युक्तः स तु राजेन्द्र कृष्णद्वैपायनोऽब्रवीत् ।भीमसेनादवरजः श्रेष्ठः सर्वधनुष्मताम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,ityuktaḥ sa tu rājendra kṛṣṇadvaipāyano'bravīt ,bhīmasenādavarajaḥ śreṣṭhaḥ sarvadhanuṣmatām.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: "Thus addressed, O sovereign king, Kṛṣṇa Dvaipāyana (Vyāsa) replied: 'He is the younger brother of Bhīmasena, and the foremost among all wielders of bows.'"
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 71 Verse 14. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: "Thus addressed, O sovereign king, Kṛṣṇa Dvaipāyana (Vyāsa) replied: 'He is the younger brother of Bhīmasena, and the foremost among all wielders of bows.'"

sanskrit: जिष्णुः सहिष्णुर्धृष्णुश्च स एनं पालयिष्यति ।शक्तः स हि महीं जेतुं निवातकवचान्तकः ॥१५॥
transliteration: jiṣṇuḥ sahiṣṇurdhṛṣṇuśca sa enaṁ pālayiṣyati ,śaktaḥ sa hi mahīṁ jetuṁ nivātakavacāntakaḥ.
english: He, who is victorious, enduring, and bold, will protect him. Indeed, he is capable of conquering the earth, being the annihilator of the Nivātakavacas.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 71 Verse 15. He, who is victorious, enduring, and bold, will protect him. Indeed, he is capable of conquering the earth, being the annihilator of the Nivātakavacas.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्ह्यस्त्राणि दिव्यानि दिव्यं संहननं तथा ।दिव्यं धनुश्चेषुधी च स एनमनुयास्यति ॥१६॥
transliteration: tasminhyastrāṇi divyāni divyaṁ saṁhananaṁ tathā ,divyaṁ dhanuśceṣudhī ca sa enamanuyāsyati.
english: For in him are divine weapons, and also a divine physique, as well as a divine bow and quivers. He will follow him (the horse).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 71 Verse 16. For in him are divine weapons, and also a divine physique, as well as a divine bow and quivers. He will follow him (the horse).

sanskrit: स हि धर्मार्थकुशलः सर्वविद्याविशारदः ।यथाशास्त्रं नृपश्रेष्ठ चारयिष्यति ते हयम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: sa hi dharmārthakuśalaḥ sarvavidyāviśāradaḥ ,yathāśāstraṁ nṛpaśreṣṭha cārayiṣyati te hayam.
english: For he is adept in both natural law (dharma) and material prosperity (artha), and an expert in all branches of knowledge. O best of kings, he will guide your horse according to the scriptures.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 71 Verse 17. For he is adept in both natural law (dharma) and material prosperity (artha), and an expert in all branches of knowledge. O best of kings, he will guide your horse according to the scriptures.

sanskrit: राजपुत्रो महाबाहुः श्यामो राजीवलोचनः ।अभिमन्योः पिता वीरः स एनमनुयास्यति ॥१८॥
transliteration: rājaputro mahābāhuḥ śyāmo rājīvalocanaḥ ,abhimanyoḥ pitā vīraḥ sa enamanuyāsyati.
english: The prince, mighty-armed, dark-complexioned, and lotus-eyed, the hero who is Abhimanyu's father, will follow him.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 71 Verse 18. The prince, mighty-armed, dark-complexioned, and lotus-eyed, the hero who is Abhimanyu's father, will follow him.

sanskrit: भीमसेनोऽपि तेजस्वी कौन्तेयोऽमितविक्रमः ।समर्थो रक्षितुं राष्ट्रं नकुलश्च विशां पते ॥१९॥
transliteration: bhīmaseno'pi tejasvī kaunteyo'mitavikramaḥ ,samartho rakṣituṁ rāṣṭraṁ nakulaśca viśāṁ pate.
english: O lord of the people, the brilliant Bhimasena, Kaunteya of immeasurable valor, is capable of protecting the kingdom, and Nakula also.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 71 Verse 19. O lord of the people, the brilliant Bhimasena, Kaunteya of immeasurable valor, is capable of protecting the kingdom, and Nakula also.

sanskrit: सहदेवस्तु कौरव्य समाधास्यति बुद्धिमान् ।कुटुम्बतन्त्रं विधिवत्सर्वमेव महायशाः ॥२०॥
transliteration: sahadevastu kauravya samādhāsyati buddhimān ,kuṭumbatantraṁ vidhivatsarvameva mahāyaśāḥ.
english: O descendant of Kuru, the intelligent and very famous Sahadeva, however, will properly manage all the family affairs.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 71 Verse 20. O descendant of Kuru, the intelligent and very famous Sahadeva, however, will properly manage all the family affairs.

sanskrit: तत्तु सर्वं यथान्यायमुक्तं कुरुकुलोद्वहः ।चकार फल्गुनं चापि संदिदेश हयं प्रति ॥२१॥
transliteration: tattu sarvaṁ yathānyāyamuktaṁ kurukulodvahaḥ ,cakāra phalgunaṁ cāpi saṁdideśa hayaṁ prati.
english: O upholder of the Kuru lineage, he carried out all that was properly stated; and he also gave instructions to Phalguna concerning the horse.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 71 Verse 21. O upholder of the Kuru lineage, he carried out all that was properly stated; and he also gave instructions to Phalguna concerning the horse.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।एह्यर्जुन त्वया वीर हयोऽयं परिपाल्यताम् ।त्वमर्हो रक्षितुं ह्येनं नान्यः कश्चन मानवः ॥२२॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,ehyarjuna tvayā vīra hayo'yaṁ paripālyatām ,tvamarho rakṣituṁ hyenaṁ nānyaḥ kaścana mānavaḥ.
english: Yudhishthira said: "Come, Arjuna, O hero! This horse must be guarded by you. Indeed, you alone are capable of protecting it; no other human being is."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 71 Verse 22. Yudhishthira said: "Come, Arjuna, O hero! This horse must be guarded by you. Indeed, you alone are capable of protecting it; no other human being is."

sanskrit: ये चापि त्वां महाबाहो प्रत्युदीयुर्नराधिपाः ।तैर्विग्रहो यथा न स्यात्तथा कार्यं त्वयानघ ॥२३॥
transliteration: ye cāpi tvāṁ mahābāho pratyudīyurnarādhipāḥ ,tairvigraho yathā na syāttathā kāryaṁ tvayānagha.
english: O mighty-armed (mahābāho) one, O sinless one, should any kings confront you, then you must act in such a way that there is no conflict with them.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 71 Verse 23. O mighty-armed (mahābāho) one, O sinless one, should any kings confront you, then you must act in such a way that there is no conflict with them.

sanskrit: आख्यातव्यश्च भवता यज्ञोऽयं मम सर्वशः ।पार्थिवेभ्यो महाबाहो समये गम्यतामिति ॥२४॥
transliteration: ākhyātavyaśca bhavatā yajño'yaṁ mama sarvaśaḥ ,pārthivebhyo mahābāho samaye gamyatāmiti.
english: O mighty-armed (mahābāho) one, you must announce this Vedic ritual (yajña) of mine comprehensively to the kings, informing them, 'Let them come at the appointed time.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 71 Verse 24. O mighty-armed (mahābāho) one, you must announce this Vedic ritual (yajña) of mine comprehensively to the kings, informing them, 'Let them come at the appointed time.'

sanskrit: एवमुक्त्वा स धर्मात्मा भ्रातरं सव्यसाचिनम् ।भीमं च नकुलं चैव पुरगुप्तौ समादधत् ॥२५॥
transliteration: evamuktvā sa dharmātmā bhrātaraṁ savyasācinam ,bhīmaṁ ca nakulaṁ caiva puraguptau samādadhat.
english: Having spoken thus to his brother Savyasachin (Arjuna), that righteous one (dharmātmā) then appointed Bhima and Nakula as protectors of the city.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 71 Verse 25. Having spoken thus to his brother Savyasachin (Arjuna), that righteous one (dharmātmā) then appointed Bhima and Nakula as protectors of the city.

sanskrit: कुटुम्बतन्त्रे च तथा सहदेवं युधां पतिम् ।अनुमान्य महीपालं धृतराष्ट्रं युधिष्ठिरः ॥२६॥
transliteration: kuṭumbatantre ca tathā sahadevaṁ yudhāṁ patim ,anumānya mahīpālaṁ dhṛtarāṣṭraṁ yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira, having secured the approval of King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, likewise attended to the family administration and (the matters concerning) Sahadeva, the commander of forces.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 71 Verse 26. Yudhiṣṭhira, having secured the approval of King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, likewise attended to the family administration and (the matters concerning) Sahadeva, the commander of forces.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।दीक्षाकाले तु संप्राप्ते ततस्ते सुमहर्त्विजः ।विधिवद्दीक्षयामासुरश्वमेधाय पार्थिवम् ॥१॥
