transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,dīkṣākāle tu saṁprāpte tataste sumahartvijaḥ ,vidhivaddīkṣayāmāsuraśvamedhāya pārthivam.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: When the time for the initiation ceremony arrived, those eminent priests duly initiated the king for the Aśvamedha (Vedic ritual).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 72 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: When the time for the initiation ceremony arrived, those eminent priests duly initiated the king for the Aśvamedha (Vedic ritual).

sanskrit: कृत्वा स पशुबन्धांश्च दीक्षितः पाण्डुनन्दनः ।धर्मराजो महातेजाः सहर्त्विग्भिर्व्यरोचत ॥२॥
transliteration: kṛtvā sa paśubandhāṁśca dīkṣitaḥ pāṇḍunandanaḥ ,dharmarājo mahātejāḥ sahartvigbhirvyarocata.
english: Having performed the animal offerings (paśubandha), that initiated son of Pāṇḍu, Yudhiṣṭhira, the greatly radiant king of (dharma), shone brightly along with the priests.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 72 Verse 2. Having performed the animal offerings (paśubandha), that initiated son of Pāṇḍu, Yudhiṣṭhira, the greatly radiant king of (dharma), shone brightly along with the priests.

sanskrit: हयश्च हयमेधार्थं स्वयं स ब्रह्मवादिना ।उत्सृष्टः शास्त्रविधिना व्यासेनामिततेजसा ॥३॥
transliteration: hayaśca hayamedhārthaṁ svayaṁ sa brahmavādinā ,utsṛṣṭaḥ śāstravidhinā vyāsenāmitatejasā.
english: And the horse for the Aśvamedha (Vedic ritual) was himself released according to scriptural injunctions by Vyāsa, that speaker of (brahman) and one of immeasurable splendor.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 72 Verse 3. And the horse for the Aśvamedha (Vedic ritual) was himself released according to scriptural injunctions by Vyāsa, that speaker of (brahman) and one of immeasurable splendor.

sanskrit: स राजा धर्मजो राजन्दीक्षितो विबभौ तदा ।हेममाली रुक्मकण्ठः प्रदीप्त इव पावकः ॥४॥
transliteration: sa rājā dharmajo rājandīkṣito vibabhau tadā ,hemamālī rukmakaṇṭhaḥ pradīpta iva pāvakaḥ.
english: O King, that king, the son of dharma (Yudhiṣṭhira), having been consecrated for the ritual, shone brightly then. Adorned with a golden garland and a golden necklace, he resembled a blazing fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 72 Verse 4. O King, that king, the son of dharma (Yudhiṣṭhira), having been consecrated for the ritual, shone brightly then. Adorned with a golden garland and a golden necklace, he resembled a blazing fire.

sanskrit: कृष्णाजिनी दण्डपाणिः क्षौमवासाः स धर्मजः ।विबभौ द्युतिमान्भूयः प्रजापतिरिवाध्वरे ॥५॥
transliteration: kṛṣṇājinī daṇḍapāṇiḥ kṣaumavāsāḥ sa dharmajaḥ ,vibabhau dyutimānbhūyaḥ prajāpatirivādhvare.
english: That son of dharma (Yudhiṣṭhira), wearing a black antelope skin, holding a staff, and dressed in silk, shone even more radiantly, like Prajāpati in a Vedic ritual (adhvara).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 72 Verse 5. That son of dharma (Yudhiṣṭhira), wearing a black antelope skin, holding a staff, and dressed in silk, shone even more radiantly, like Prajāpati in a Vedic ritual (adhvara).

sanskrit: तथैवास्यर्त्विजः सर्वे तुल्यवेषा विशां पते ।बभूवुरर्जुनश्चैव प्रदीप्त इव पावकः ॥६॥
transliteration: tathaivāsyartvijaḥ sarve tulyaveṣā viśāṁ pate ,babhūvurarjunaścaiva pradīpta iva pāvakaḥ.
english: O Lord of the people, similarly, all his priests were dressed alike. And Arjuna, too, resembled a blazing fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 72 Verse 6. O Lord of the people, similarly, all his priests were dressed alike. And Arjuna, too, resembled a blazing fire.

sanskrit: श्वेताश्वः कृष्णसारं तं ससाराश्वं धनंजयः ।विधिवत्पृथिवीपाल धर्मराजस्य शासनात् ॥७॥
transliteration: śvetāśvaḥ kṛṣṇasāraṁ taṁ sasārāśvaṁ dhanaṁjayaḥ ,vidhivatpṛthivīpāla dharmarājasya śāsanāt.
english: O protector of the earth, Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), with his white horses, chased that black antelope-like horse according to the rules, by the command of the king of dharma (Yudhiṣṭhira).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 72 Verse 7. O protector of the earth, Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), with his white horses, chased that black antelope-like horse according to the rules, by the command of the king of dharma (Yudhiṣṭhira).

sanskrit: विक्षिपन्गाण्डिवं राजन्बद्धगोधाङ्गुलित्रवान् ।तमश्वं पृथिवीपाल मुदा युक्तः ससार ह ॥८॥
transliteration: vikṣipangāṇḍivaṁ rājanbaddhagodhāṅgulitravān ,tamaśvaṁ pṛthivīpāla mudā yuktaḥ sasāra ha.
english: O King, O protector of the earth, he, having fastened his leather finger-guards and brandishing his Gāṇḍīva bow, indeed followed that horse with great joy.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 72 Verse 8. O King, O protector of the earth, he, having fastened his leather finger-guards and brandishing his Gāṇḍīva bow, indeed followed that horse with great joy.

sanskrit: आकुमारं तदा राजन्नागमत्तत्पुरं विभो ।द्रष्टुकामं कुरुश्रेष्ठं प्रयास्यन्तं धनंजयम् ॥९॥
transliteration: ākumāraṁ tadā rājannāgamattatpuraṁ vibho ,draṣṭukāmaṁ kuruśreṣṭhaṁ prayāsyantaṁ dhanaṁjayam.
english: Then, O King, O mighty one, the entire city, down to its children, came forth, desirous of seeing Dhanañjaya, the best of the Kurus, who was about to set out.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 72 Verse 9. Then, O King, O mighty one, the entire city, down to its children, came forth, desirous of seeing Dhanañjaya, the best of the Kurus, who was about to set out.

sanskrit: तेषामन्योन्यसंमर्दादूष्मेव समजायत ।दिदृक्षूणां हयं तं च तं चैव हयसारिणम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: teṣāmanyonyasaṁmardādūṣmeva samajāyata ,didṛkṣūṇāṁ hayaṁ taṁ ca taṁ caiva hayasāriṇam.
english: From the mutual jostling of those who wished to see that horse and the one guiding it, a heat, as it were, arose.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 72 Verse 10. From the mutual jostling of those who wished to see that horse and the one guiding it, a heat, as it were, arose.

sanskrit: ततः शब्दो महाराज दशाशाः प्रतिपूरयन् ।बभूव प्रेक्षतां नॄणां कुन्तीपुत्रं धनंजयम् ॥११॥
transliteration: tataḥ śabdo mahārāja daśāśāḥ pratipūrayan ,babhūva prekṣatāṁ nṝṇāṁ kuntīputraṁ dhanaṁjayam.
english: Then, O great king, a great sound arose, filling all ten directions, from the people who were gazing upon Kuntī's son, Dhanañjaya.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 72 Verse 11. Then, O great king, a great sound arose, filling all ten directions, from the people who were gazing upon Kuntī's son, Dhanañjaya.

sanskrit: एष गच्छति कौन्तेयस्तुरगश्चैव दीप्तिमान् ।यमन्वेति महाबाहुः संस्पृशन्धनुरुत्तमम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: eṣa gacchati kaunteyasturagaścaiva dīptimān ,yamanveti mahābāhuḥ saṁspṛśandhanuruttamam.
english: Here goes Arjuna, the son of Kunti, along with the radiant horse. The mighty-armed one (Arjuna) moves with it, touching his excellent bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 72 Verse 12. Here goes Arjuna, the son of Kunti, along with the radiant horse. The mighty-armed one (Arjuna) moves with it, touching his excellent bow.

sanskrit: एवं शुश्राव वदतां गिरो जिष्णुरुदारधीः ।स्वस्ति तेऽस्तु व्रजारिष्टं पुनश्चैहीति भारत ॥१३॥
transliteration: evaṁ śuśrāva vadatāṁ giro jiṣṇurudāradhīḥ ,svasti te'stu vrajāriṣṭaṁ punaścaihīti bhārata.
english: Thus, Arjuna (Jiṣṇu), the noble-minded and victorious one, heard the words of those speaking: 'May well-being be yours. Go safely, and come back again, O Bhārata (Arjuna).'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 72 Verse 13. Thus, Arjuna (Jiṣṇu), the noble-minded and victorious one, heard the words of those speaking: 'May well-being be yours. Go safely, and come back again, O Bhārata (Arjuna).'

sanskrit: अथापरे मनुष्येन्द्र पुरुषा वाक्यमब्रुवन् ।नैनं पश्याम संमर्दे धनुरेतत्प्रदृश्यते ॥१४॥
transliteration: athāpare manuṣyendra puruṣā vākyamabruvan ,nainaṁ paśyāma saṁmarde dhanuretatpradṛśyate.
english: Then, O king among men, other people said: 'We do not see him in the throng, but this bow is visible.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 72 Verse 14. Then, O king among men, other people said: 'We do not see him in the throng, but this bow is visible.'

sanskrit: एतद्धि भीमनिर्ह्रादं विश्रुतं गाण्डिवं धनुः ।स्वस्ति गच्छत्वरिष्टं वै पन्थानमकुतोभयम् ।निवृत्तमेनं द्रक्ष्यामः पुनरेवं च तेऽब्रुवन् ॥१५॥
transliteration: etaddhi bhīmanirhrādaṁ viśrutaṁ gāṇḍivaṁ dhanuḥ ,svasti gacchatvariṣṭaṁ vai panthānamakutobhayam ,nivṛttamenaṁ drakṣyāmaḥ punarevaṁ ca te'bruvan.
english: Indeed, this is the renowned Gāṇḍīva bow with its formidable roar. (They continued:) 'May he travel safely along a path free from all danger. We shall see him again upon his return.' Thus, they spoke.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 72 Verse 15. Indeed, this is the renowned Gāṇḍīva bow with its formidable roar. (They continued:) 'May he travel safely along a path free from all danger. We shall see him again upon his return.' Thus, they spoke.

sanskrit: एवमाद्या मनुष्याणां स्त्रीणां च भरतर्षभ ।शुश्राव मधुरा वाचः पुनः पुनरुदीरिताः ॥१६॥
transliteration: evamādyā manuṣyāṇāṁ strīṇāṁ ca bharatarṣabha ,śuśrāva madhurā vācaḥ punaḥ punarudīritāḥ.
english: O best among Bharatas, he heard such sweet words, spoken repeatedly by both men and women.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 72 Verse 16. O best among Bharatas, he heard such sweet words, spoken repeatedly by both men and women.

sanskrit: याज्ञवल्क्यस्य शिष्यश्च कुशलो यज्ञकर्मणि ।प्रायात्पार्थेन सहितः शान्त्यर्थं वेदपारगः ॥१७॥
transliteration: yājñavalkyasya śiṣyaśca kuśalo yajñakarmaṇi ,prāyātpārthena sahitaḥ śāntyarthaṁ vedapāragaḥ.
english: And the disciple of Yājñavalkya, who was skilled in Vedic rituals (yajña) and thoroughly versed in the Vedas, went with Pārtha (Arjuna) for the purpose of propitiation.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 72 Verse 17. And the disciple of Yājñavalkya, who was skilled in Vedic rituals (yajña) and thoroughly versed in the Vedas, went with Pārtha (Arjuna) for the purpose of propitiation.

sanskrit: ब्राह्मणाश्च महीपाल बहवो वेदपारगाः ।अनुजग्मुर्महात्मानं क्षत्रियाश्च विशोऽपि च ॥१८॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇāśca mahīpāla bahavo vedapāragāḥ ,anujagmurmahātmānaṁ kṣatriyāśca viśo'pi ca.
english: O king (mahīpāla), many Brahmins, who were proficient in the Vedas, followed the great-souled one, and so did Kshatriyas and Vaishyas.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 72 Verse 18. O king (mahīpāla), many Brahmins, who were proficient in the Vedas, followed the great-souled one, and so did Kshatriyas and Vaishyas.

sanskrit: पाण्डवैः पृथिवीमश्वो निर्जितामस्त्रतेजसा ।चचार स महाराज यथादेशं स सत्तम ॥१९॥
transliteration: pāṇḍavaiḥ pṛthivīmaśvo nirjitāmastratejasā ,cacāra sa mahārāja yathādeśaṁ sa sattama.
english: O great king (mahārāja), O best among men (sattama), that horse wandered the earth, which had been conquered by the Pāṇḍavas through the might of their weapons (astra), moving according to each region.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 72 Verse 19. O great king (mahārāja), O best among men (sattama), that horse wandered the earth, which had been conquered by the Pāṇḍavas through the might of their weapons (astra), moving according to each region.

sanskrit: तत्र युद्धानि वृत्तानि यान्यासन्पाण्डवस्य ह ।तानि वक्ष्यामि ते वीर विचित्राणि महान्ति च ॥२०॥
transliteration: tatra yuddhāni vṛttāni yānyāsanpāṇḍavasya ha ,tāni vakṣyāmi te vīra vicitrāṇi mahānti ca.
english: O hero, I will recount to you those diverse and great battles which indeed occurred there for the Pāṇḍava.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 72 Verse 20. O hero, I will recount to you those diverse and great battles which indeed occurred there for the Pāṇḍava.

sanskrit: स हयः पृथिवीं राजन्प्रदक्षिणमरिंदम ।ससारोत्तरतः पूर्वं तन्निबोध महीपते ॥२१॥
transliteration: sa hayaḥ pṛthivīṁ rājanpradakṣiṇamariṁdama ,sasārottarataḥ pūrvaṁ tannibodha mahīpate.
english: O King, subduer of enemies, that horse travelled around the earth clockwise, going first to the north, then to the east. O lord of the earth, understand this.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 72 Verse 21. O King, subduer of enemies, that horse travelled around the earth clockwise, going first to the north, then to the east. O lord of the earth, understand this.

sanskrit: अवमृद्नन्स राष्ट्राणि पार्थिवानां हयोत्तमः ।शनैस्तदा परिययौ श्वेताश्वश्च महारथः ॥२२॥
transliteration: avamṛdnansa rāṣṭrāṇi pārthivānāṁ hayottamaḥ ,śanaistadā pariyayau śvetāśvaśca mahārathaḥ.
english: Trampling down the kingdoms of kings, that excellent horse, and the great charioteer, Arjuna, the one with the white horse (śvetāśva), then slowly travelled around.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 72 Verse 22. Trampling down the kingdoms of kings, that excellent horse, and the great charioteer, Arjuna, the one with the white horse (śvetāśva), then slowly travelled around.

sanskrit: तत्र संकलना नास्ति राज्ञामयुतशस्तदा ।येऽयुध्यन्त महाराज क्षत्रिया हतबान्धवाः ॥२३॥
transliteration: tatra saṁkalanā nāsti rājñāmayutaśastadā ,ye'yudhyanta mahārāja kṣatriyā hatabāndhavāḥ.
english: O great king, at that time, there was no counting (saṃkalanā) of the kings and warriors (kṣatriyāḥ) who fought there in tens of thousands, those whose kinsmen had been slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 72 Verse 23. O great king, at that time, there was no counting (saṃkalanā) of the kings and warriors (kṣatriyāḥ) who fought there in tens of thousands, those whose kinsmen had been slain.

sanskrit: किराता विकृता राजन्बहवोऽसिधनुर्धराः ।म्लेच्छाश्चान्ये बहुविधाः पूर्वं विनिकृता रणे ॥२४॥
transliteration: kirātā vikṛtā rājanbahavo'sidhanurdharāḥ ,mlecchāścānye bahuvidhāḥ pūrvaṁ vinikṛtā raṇe.
english: O king, many fierce Kiratas who bore swords and bows, and various other Mlecchas, had previously been vanquished in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 72 Verse 24. O king, many fierce Kiratas who bore swords and bows, and various other Mlecchas, had previously been vanquished in battle.

sanskrit: आर्याश्च पृथिवीपालाः प्रहृष्टनरवाहनाः ।समीयुः पाण्डुपुत्रेण बहवो युद्धदुर्मदाः ॥२५॥
transliteration: āryāśca pṛthivīpālāḥ prahṛṣṭanaravāhanāḥ ,samīyuḥ pāṇḍuputreṇa bahavo yuddhadurmadāḥ.
english: And many Aryan kings, with their men and vehicles exhilarated, fiercely eager for war, came together with the son of Pandu (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 72 Verse 25. And many Aryan kings, with their men and vehicles exhilarated, fiercely eager for war, came together with the son of Pandu (Arjuna).

sanskrit: एवं युद्धानि वृत्तानि तत्र तत्र महीपते ।अर्जुनस्य महीपालैर्नानादेशनिवासिभिः ॥२६॥
transliteration: evaṁ yuddhāni vṛttāni tatra tatra mahīpate ,arjunasya mahīpālairnānādeśanivāsibhiḥ.
english: O king, in this manner, battles took place here and there (in various regions) between Arjuna and kings residing in different countries.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 72 Verse 26. O king, in this manner, battles took place here and there (in various regions) between Arjuna and kings residing in different countries.

sanskrit: यानि तूभयतो राजन्प्रतप्तानि महान्ति च ।तानि युद्धानि वक्ष्यामि कौन्तेयस्य तवानघ ॥२७॥
transliteration: yāni tūbhayato rājanprataptāni mahānti ca ,tāni yuddhāni vakṣyāmi kaunteyasya tavānagha.
english: O blameless king, I will indeed describe to you those great and fierce battles of the son of Kunti (Arjuna) which took place on both sides.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 72 Verse 27. O blameless king, I will indeed describe to you those great and fierce battles of the son of Kunti (Arjuna) which took place on both sides.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।त्रिगर्तैरभवद्युद्धं कृतवैरैः किरीटिनः ।महारथसमाज्ञातैर्हतानां पुत्रनप्तृभिः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,trigartairabhavadyuddhaṁ kṛtavairaiḥ kirīṭinaḥ ,mahārathasamājñātairhatānāṁ putranaptṛbhiḥ.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: A battle ensued with the Trigartas, who harbored a deep-seated enmity towards Kirīṭin (Arjuna). This engagement was fought by the sons and grandsons of those Trigartas who had previously been slain and were renowned as great charioteers.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 73 Verse 1. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: A battle ensued with the Trigartas, who harbored a deep-seated enmity towards Kirīṭin (Arjuna). This engagement was fought by the sons and grandsons of those Trigartas who had previously been slain and were renowned as great charioteers.

sanskrit: ते समाज्ञाय संप्राप्तं यज्ञियं तुरगोत्तमम् ।विषयान्ते ततो वीरा दंशिताः पर्यवारयन् ॥२॥
transliteration: te samājñāya saṁprāptaṁ yajñiyaṁ turagottamam ,viṣayānte tato vīrā daṁśitāḥ paryavārayan.
english: Having come to know that the magnificent horse, meant for the Vedic ritual (yajña), had arrived at the border of their territory, those warriors then armed themselves and surrounded it.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 73 Verse 2. Having come to know that the magnificent horse, meant for the Vedic ritual (yajña), had arrived at the border of their territory, those warriors then armed themselves and surrounded it.

sanskrit: रथिनो बद्धतूणीराः सदश्वैः समलंकृतैः ।परिवार्य हयं राजन्ग्रहीतुं संप्रचक्रमुः ॥३॥
transliteration: rathino baddhatūṇīrāḥ sadaśvaiḥ samalaṁkṛtaiḥ ,parivārya hayaṁ rājangrahītuṁ saṁpracakramuḥ.
english: O King, the charioteers, having tied on their quivers and mounted on well-decorated excellent horses, surrounded the horse and began to seize it.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 73 Verse 3. O King, the charioteers, having tied on their quivers and mounted on well-decorated excellent horses, surrounded the horse and began to seize it.

sanskrit: ततः किरीटी संचिन्त्य तेषां राज्ञां चिकीर्षितम् ।वारयामास तान्वीरान्सान्त्वपूर्वमरिंदमः ॥४॥
transliteration: tataḥ kirīṭī saṁcintya teṣāṁ rājñāṁ cikīrṣitam ,vārayāmāsa tānvīrānsāntvapūrvamariṁdamaḥ.
english: Then Kirīṭin (Arjuna), the subduer of enemies, having understood the intention of those kings, first tried to restrain those warriors with gentle, conciliatory words.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 73 Verse 4. Then Kirīṭin (Arjuna), the subduer of enemies, having understood the intention of those kings, first tried to restrain those warriors with gentle, conciliatory words.

sanskrit: तमनादृत्य ते सर्वे शरैरभ्यहनंस्तदा ।तमोरजोभ्यां संछन्नांस्तान्किरीटी न्यवारयत् ॥५॥
transliteration: tamanādṛtya te sarve śarairabhyahanaṁstadā ,tamorajobhyāṁ saṁchannāṁstānkirīṭī nyavārayat.
english: Disregarding him, they all then struck him with arrows. Arjuna (kirīṭī) stopped those who were shrouded by darkness and passion.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 73 Verse 5. Disregarding him, they all then struck him with arrows. Arjuna (kirīṭī) stopped those who were shrouded by darkness and passion.

sanskrit: अब्रवीच्च ततो जिष्णुः प्रहसन्निव भारत ।निवर्तध्वमधर्मज्ञाः श्रेयो जीवितमेव वः ॥६॥
transliteration: abravīcca tato jiṣṇuḥ prahasanniva bhārata ,nivartadhvamadharmajñāḥ śreyo jīvitameva vaḥ.
english: And then Arjuna (Jiṣṇu), as if laughing, said, 'O Bhārata, turn back, you who are ignorant of natural law (dharma)! It is better for you to preserve your life.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 73 Verse 6. And then Arjuna (Jiṣṇu), as if laughing, said, 'O Bhārata, turn back, you who are ignorant of natural law (dharma)! It is better for you to preserve your life.'

sanskrit: स हि वीरः प्रयास्यन्वै धर्मराजेन वारितः ।हतबान्धवा न ते पार्थ हन्तव्याः पार्थिवा इति ॥७॥
transliteration: sa hi vīraḥ prayāsyanvai dharmarājena vāritaḥ ,hatabāndhavā na te pārtha hantavyāḥ pārthivā iti.
english: Indeed, that hero (Arjuna) was restrained by King Yudhiṣṭhira (dharma-rāja) when he was about to proceed, [as Yudhiṣṭhira said], 'O Arjuna (Pārtha), those kings whose kinsmen have been killed should not be slain.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 73 Verse 7. Indeed, that hero (Arjuna) was restrained by King Yudhiṣṭhira (dharma-rāja) when he was about to proceed, [as Yudhiṣṭhira said], 'O Arjuna (Pārtha), those kings whose kinsmen have been killed should not be slain.'

sanskrit: स तदा तद्वचः श्रुत्वा धर्मराजस्य धीमतः ।तान्निवर्तध्वमित्याह न न्यवर्तन्त चापि ते ॥८॥
transliteration: sa tadā tadvacaḥ śrutvā dharmarājasya dhīmataḥ ,tānnivartadhvamityāha na nyavartanta cāpi te.
english: He (Arjuna) then, having heard those words of the wise King Yudhiṣṭhira (dharma-rāja), said to them, 'Turn back!' Yet, they did not turn back.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 73 Verse 8. He (Arjuna) then, having heard those words of the wise King Yudhiṣṭhira (dharma-rāja), said to them, 'Turn back!' Yet, they did not turn back.

sanskrit: ततस्त्रिगर्तराजानं सूर्यवर्माणमाहवे ।वितत्य शरजालेन प्रजहास धनंजयः ॥९॥
transliteration: tatastrigartarājānaṁ sūryavarmāṇamāhave ,vitatya śarajālena prajahāsa dhanaṁjayaḥ.
english: Then, Arjuna, having enveloped King Sūryavarman of Trigarta in battle with a net of arrows, laughed.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 73 Verse 9. Then, Arjuna, having enveloped King Sūryavarman of Trigarta in battle with a net of arrows, laughed.

sanskrit: ततस्ते रथघोषेण खुरनेमिस्वनेन च ।पूरयन्तो दिशः सर्वा धनंजयमुपाद्रवन् ॥१०॥
transliteration: tataste rathaghoṣeṇa khuranemisvanena ca ,pūrayanto diśaḥ sarvā dhanaṁjayamupādravan.
english: Then, filling all directions with the roar of their chariots and the sound of hooves and wheel rims, they rushed towards Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 73 Verse 10. Then, filling all directions with the roar of their chariots and the sound of hooves and wheel rims, they rushed towards Arjuna.

sanskrit: सूर्यवर्मा ततः पार्थे शराणां नतपर्वणाम् ।शतान्यमुञ्चद्राजेन्द्र लघ्वस्त्रमभिदर्शयन् ॥११॥
transliteration: sūryavarmā tataḥ pārthe śarāṇāṁ nataparvaṇām ,śatānyamuñcadrājendra laghvastramabhidarśayan.
english: Then, O best of kings, Sūryavarman, displaying his swift weaponry, discharged hundreds of arrows with bent shafts at Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 73 Verse 11. Then, O best of kings, Sūryavarman, displaying his swift weaponry, discharged hundreds of arrows with bent shafts at Arjuna.

sanskrit: तथैवान्ये महेष्वासा ये तस्यैवानुयायिनः ।मुमुचुः शरवर्षाणि धनंजयवधैषिणः ॥१२॥
transliteration: tathaivānye maheṣvāsā ye tasyaivānuyāyinaḥ ,mumucuḥ śaravarṣāṇi dhanaṁjayavadhaiṣiṇaḥ.
english: Similarly, other great archers, who were his direct followers and desired Arjuna's death, also released showers of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 73 Verse 12. Similarly, other great archers, who were his direct followers and desired Arjuna's death, also released showers of arrows.

sanskrit: स ताञ्ज्यापुङ्खनिर्मुक्तैर्बहुभिः सुबहूञ्शरान् ।चिच्छेद पाण्डवो राजंस्ते भूमौ न्यपतंस्तदा ॥१३॥
transliteration: sa tāñjyāpuṅkhanirmuktairbahubhiḥ subahūñśarān ,ciccheda pāṇḍavo rājaṁste bhūmau nyapataṁstadā.
english: O King, that Pāṇḍava then severed those (incoming arrows) with many, very many arrows released from his bowstring's nock, and they (the severed arrows) fell to the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 73 Verse 13. O King, that Pāṇḍava then severed those (incoming arrows) with many, very many arrows released from his bowstring's nock, and they (the severed arrows) fell to the ground.

sanskrit: केतुवर्मा तु तेजस्वी तस्यैवावरजो युवा ।युयुधे भ्रातुरर्थाय पाण्डवेन महात्मना ॥१४॥
transliteration: ketuvarmā tu tejasvī tasyaivāvarajo yuvā ,yuyudhe bhrāturarthāya pāṇḍavena mahātmanā.
english: But the spirited Ketuvarman, his own younger brother, a young man, fought for his (elder) brother's sake against the great-souled Pāṇḍava.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 73 Verse 14. But the spirited Ketuvarman, his own younger brother, a young man, fought for his (elder) brother's sake against the great-souled Pāṇḍava.

sanskrit: तमापतन्तं संप्रेक्ष्य केतुवर्माणमाहवे ।अभ्यघ्नन्निशितैर्बाणैर्बीभत्सुः परवीरहा ॥१५॥
transliteration: tamāpatantaṁ saṁprekṣya ketuvarmāṇamāhave ,abhyaghnanniśitairbāṇairbībhatsuḥ paravīrahā.
english: Having seen Ketuvarman approaching in battle, Bibhatsu, the destroyer of enemy heroes, struck him with sharpened arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 73 Verse 15. Having seen Ketuvarman approaching in battle, Bibhatsu, the destroyer of enemy heroes, struck him with sharpened arrows.

sanskrit: केतुवर्मण्यभिहते धृतवर्मा महारथः ।रथेनाशु समावृत्य शरैर्जिष्णुमवाकिरत् ॥१६॥
transliteration: ketuvarmaṇyabhihate dhṛtavarmā mahārathaḥ ,rathenāśu samāvṛtya śarairjiṣṇumavākirat.
english: When Ketuvarman was struck, Dhritavarman, a great charioteer, quickly turning with his chariot, showered Jishnu (Arjuna) with arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 73 Verse 16. When Ketuvarman was struck, Dhritavarman, a great charioteer, quickly turning with his chariot, showered Jishnu (Arjuna) with arrows.

sanskrit: तस्य तां शीघ्रतामीक्ष्य तुतोषातीव वीर्यवान् ।गुडाकेशो महातेजा बालस्य धृतवर्मणः ॥१७॥
transliteration: tasya tāṁ śīghratāmīkṣya tutoṣātīva vīryavān ,guḍākeśo mahātejā bālasya dhṛtavarmaṇaḥ.
english: Seeing that armored boy's swiftness, the exceedingly valorous and greatly effulgent Guḍākeśa (Arjuna) was very pleased.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 73 Verse 17. Seeing that armored boy's swiftness, the exceedingly valorous and greatly effulgent Guḍākeśa (Arjuna) was very pleased.

sanskrit: न संदधानं ददृशे नाददानं च तं तदा ।किरन्तमेव स शरान्ददृशे पाकशासनिः ॥१८॥
transliteration: na saṁdadhānaṁ dadṛśe nādadānaṁ ca taṁ tadā ,kirantameva sa śarāndadṛśe pākaśāsaniḥ.
english: Then, the son of Pākaśāsana (Arjuna) did not see him (the boy) either stringing his bow or taking up arrows; he only saw him showering arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 73 Verse 18. Then, the son of Pākaśāsana (Arjuna) did not see him (the boy) either stringing his bow or taking up arrows; he only saw him showering arrows.

sanskrit: स तु तं पूजयामास धृतवर्माणमाहवे ।मनसा स मुहूर्तं वै रणे समभिहर्षयन् ॥१९॥
transliteration: sa tu taṁ pūjayāmāsa dhṛtavarmāṇamāhave ,manasā sa muhūrtaṁ vai raṇe samabhiharṣayan.
english: But he (Arjuna) truly honored that armored one in battle, mentally inspiring him for a moment during the conflict.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 73 Verse 19. But he (Arjuna) truly honored that armored one in battle, mentally inspiring him for a moment during the conflict.

sanskrit: तं पन्नगमिव क्रुद्धं कुरुवीरः स्मयन्निव ।प्रीतिपूर्वं महाराज प्राणैर्न व्यपरोपयत् ॥२०॥
transliteration: taṁ pannagamiva kruddhaṁ kuruvīraḥ smayanniva ,prītipūrvaṁ mahārāja prāṇairna vyaparopayat.
english: O great king, the Kuru hero (Arjuna), as if smiling, out of affection did not deprive that enraged boy, who was like a furious serpent, of his life.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 73 Verse 20. O great king, the Kuru hero (Arjuna), as if smiling, out of affection did not deprive that enraged boy, who was like a furious serpent, of his life.

sanskrit: स तथा रक्ष्यमाणो वै पार्थेनामिततेजसा ।धृतवर्मा शरं तीक्ष्णं मुमोच विजये तदा ॥२१॥
transliteration: sa tathā rakṣyamāṇo vai pārthenāmitatejasā ,dhṛtavarmā śaraṁ tīkṣṇaṁ mumoca vijaye tadā.
english: As he was being protected in this manner by Pārtha (Arjuna), of immeasurable energy, Dhṛtavarmā then shot a sharp arrow at Vijaya (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 73 Verse 21. As he was being protected in this manner by Pārtha (Arjuna), of immeasurable energy, Dhṛtavarmā then shot a sharp arrow at Vijaya (Arjuna).

sanskrit: स तेन विजयस्तूर्णमस्यन्विद्धः करे भृशम् ।मुमोच गाण्डीवं दुःखात्तत्पपाताथ भूतले ॥२२॥
transliteration: sa tena vijayastūrṇamasyanviddhaḥ kare bhṛśam ,mumoca gāṇḍīvaṁ duḥkhāttatpapātātha bhūtale.
english: Struck severely in the hand by that (arrow) while he was swiftly discharging (arrows), Vijaya (Arjuna), in pain, released the Gāṇḍīva bow, and it then fell to the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 73 Verse 22. Struck severely in the hand by that (arrow) while he was swiftly discharging (arrows), Vijaya (Arjuna), in pain, released the Gāṇḍīva bow, and it then fell to the ground.

sanskrit: धनुषः पततस्तस्य सव्यसाचिकराद्विभो ।इन्द्रस्येवायुधस्यासीद्रूपं भरतसत्तम ॥२३॥
transliteration: dhanuṣaḥ patatastasya savyasācikarādvibho ,indrasyevāyudhasyāsīdrūpaṁ bharatasattama.
english: O best among the Bhāratas, O lord, as that bow fell from the hand of Savyasācin (Arjuna), its appearance was like that of the weapon of Indra.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 73 Verse 23. O best among the Bhāratas, O lord, as that bow fell from the hand of Savyasācin (Arjuna), its appearance was like that of the weapon of Indra.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्निपतिते दिव्ये महाधनुषि पार्थिव ।जहास सस्वनं हासं धृतवर्मा महाहवे ॥२४॥
transliteration: tasminnipatite divye mahādhanuṣi pārthiva ,jahāsa sasvanaṁ hāsaṁ dhṛtavarmā mahāhave.
english: O king (pārthiva), when that divine, great bow had fallen, Dhṛtavarmā laughed a loud laugh in the great battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 73 Verse 24. O king (pārthiva), when that divine, great bow had fallen, Dhṛtavarmā laughed a loud laugh in the great battle.

sanskrit: ततो रोषान्वितो जिष्णुः प्रमृज्य रुधिरं करात् ।धनुरादत्त तद्दिव्यं शरवर्षं ववर्ष च ॥२५॥
transliteration: tato roṣānvito jiṣṇuḥ pramṛjya rudhiraṁ karāt ,dhanurādatta taddivyaṁ śaravarṣaṁ vavarṣa ca.
english: Then, filled with rage, Jishnu, wiping the blood from his hand, took up that divine bow and rained down arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 73 Verse 25. Then, filled with rage, Jishnu, wiping the blood from his hand, took up that divine bow and rained down arrows.

sanskrit: ततो हलहलाशब्दो दिवस्पृगभवत्तदा ।नानाविधानां भूतानां तत्कर्मातीव शंसताम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: tato halahalāśabdo divaspṛgabhavattadā ,nānāvidhānāṁ bhūtānāṁ tatkarmātīva śaṁsatām.
english: Then a great clamor, reaching the heavens, arose at that time, from various beings excessively praising that deed.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 73 Verse 26. Then a great clamor, reaching the heavens, arose at that time, from various beings excessively praising that deed.

sanskrit: ततः संप्रेक्ष्य तं क्रुद्धं कालान्तकयमोपमम् ।जिष्णुं त्रैगर्तका योधास्त्वरिताः पर्यवारयन् ॥२७॥
transliteration: tataḥ saṁprekṣya taṁ kruddhaṁ kālāntakayamopamam ,jiṣṇuṁ traigartakā yodhāstvaritāḥ paryavārayan.
english: Then, seeing him enraged, resembling Yama, the destroyer at the end of time, the Trigarta warriors swiftly surrounded Jishnu.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 73 Verse 27. Then, seeing him enraged, resembling Yama, the destroyer at the end of time, the Trigarta warriors swiftly surrounded Jishnu.

sanskrit: अभिसृत्य परीप्सार्थं ततस्ते धृतवर्मणः ।परिवव्रुर्गुडाकेशं तत्राक्रुध्यद्धनंजयः ॥२८॥
transliteration: abhisṛtya parīpsārthaṁ tataste dhṛtavarmaṇaḥ ,parivavrurguḍākeśaṁ tatrākrudhyaddhanaṁjayaḥ.
english: Then, having rushed forward for the purpose of overcoming, those armored warriors surrounded Gudakesha (Arjuna), and thereupon Dhananjaya (Arjuna) became enraged.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 73 Verse 28. Then, having rushed forward for the purpose of overcoming, those armored warriors surrounded Gudakesha (Arjuna), and thereupon Dhananjaya (Arjuna) became enraged.

sanskrit: ततो योधाञ्जघानाशु तेषां स दश चाष्ट च ।महेन्द्रवज्रप्रतिमैरायसैर्निशितैः शरैः ॥२९॥
transliteration: tato yodhāñjaghānāśu teṣāṁ sa daśa cāṣṭa ca ,mahendravajrapratimairāyasairniśitaiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: Then he quickly struck down eighteen of those warriors with sharpened iron arrows resembling Indra's thunderbolt.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 73 Verse 29. Then he quickly struck down eighteen of those warriors with sharpened iron arrows resembling Indra's thunderbolt.

sanskrit: तांस्तु प्रभग्नान्संप्रेक्ष्य त्वरमाणो धनंजयः ।शरैराशीविषाकारैर्जघान स्वनवद्धसन् ॥३०॥
transliteration: tāṁstu prabhagnānsaṁprekṣya tvaramāṇo dhanaṁjayaḥ ,śarairāśīviṣākārairjaghāna svanavaddhasan.
english: But seeing those (warriors) routed, Arjuna (Dhanañjaya), hastening, struck them down with arrows resembling venomous serpents, roaring and laughing.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 73 Verse 30. But seeing those (warriors) routed, Arjuna (Dhanañjaya), hastening, struck them down with arrows resembling venomous serpents, roaring and laughing.

sanskrit: ते भग्नमनसः सर्वे त्रैगर्तकमहारथाः ।दिशो विदुद्रुवुः सर्वा धनंजयशरार्दिताः ॥३१॥
transliteration: te bhagnamanasaḥ sarve traigartakamahārathāḥ ,diśo vidudruvuḥ sarvā dhanaṁjayaśarārditāḥ.
english: All those great charioteers of Trigarta, their spirits broken and tormented by Arjuna's (Dhanañjaya's) arrows, fled in all directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 73 Verse 31. All those great charioteers of Trigarta, their spirits broken and tormented by Arjuna's (Dhanañjaya's) arrows, fled in all directions.

sanskrit: त ऊचुः पुरुषव्याघ्रं संशप्तकनिषूदनम् ।तव स्म किंकराः सर्वे सर्वे च वशगास्तव ॥३२॥
transliteration: ta ūcuḥ puruṣavyāghraṁ saṁśaptakaniṣūdanam ,tava sma kiṁkarāḥ sarve sarve ca vaśagāstava.
english: They (the warriors) said to that tiger among men (Arjuna), the destroyer of the Saṃśaptakas: 'We are all your servants, and all are subject to you.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 73 Verse 32. They (the warriors) said to that tiger among men (Arjuna), the destroyer of the Saṃśaptakas: 'We are all your servants, and all are subject to you.'

sanskrit: आज्ञापयस्व नः पार्थ प्रह्वान्प्रेष्यानवस्थितान् ।करिष्यामः प्रियं सर्वं तव कौरवनन्दन ॥३३॥
transliteration: ājñāpayasva naḥ pārtha prahvānpreṣyānavasthitān ,kariṣyāmaḥ priyaṁ sarvaṁ tava kauravanandana.
english: O Pārtha, O delight of the Kurus, command us, your humble servants who stand ready. We shall do everything that is pleasing to you.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 73 Verse 33. O Pārtha, O delight of the Kurus, command us, your humble servants who stand ready. We shall do everything that is pleasing to you.

sanskrit: एतदाज्ञाय वचनं सर्वांस्तानब्रवीत्तदा ।जीवितं रक्षत नृपाः शासनं गृह्यतामिति ॥३४॥
transliteration: etadājñāya vacanaṁ sarvāṁstānabravīttadā ,jīvitaṁ rakṣata nṛpāḥ śāsanaṁ gṛhyatāmiti.
english: Having understood this statement, he then said to all those kings, 'O kings, protect your lives! Let my command be accepted.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 73 Verse 34. Having understood this statement, he then said to all those kings, 'O kings, protect your lives! Let my command be accepted.'

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।प्राग्ज्योतिषमथाभ्येत्य व्यचरत्स हयोत्तमः ।भगदत्तात्मजस्तत्र निर्ययौ रणकर्कशः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,prāgjyotiṣamathābhyetya vyacaratsa hayottamaḥ ,bhagadattātmajastatra niryayau raṇakarkaśaḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then, having arrived at Prāgjyotiṣa, that excellent horse wandered. There, Bhagadatta's son, who was formidable in battle, came forth.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 74 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Then, having arrived at Prāgjyotiṣa, that excellent horse wandered. There, Bhagadatta's son, who was formidable in battle, came forth.

sanskrit: स हयं पाण्डुपुत्रस्य विषयान्तमुपागतम् ।युयुधे भरतश्रेष्ठ वज्रदत्तो महीपतिः ॥२॥
transliteration: sa hayaṁ pāṇḍuputrasya viṣayāntamupāgatam ,yuyudhe bharataśreṣṭha vajradatto mahīpatiḥ.
english: O best of the Bharatas, King Vajradatta fought against the horse of Pāṇḍu's son, which had entered his dominion.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 74 Verse 2. O best of the Bharatas, King Vajradatta fought against the horse of Pāṇḍu's son, which had entered his dominion.

sanskrit: सोऽभिनिर्याय नगराद्भगदत्तसुतो नृपः ।अश्वमायान्तमुन्मथ्य नगराभिमुखो ययौ ॥३॥
transliteration: so'bhiniryāya nagarādbhagadattasuto nṛpaḥ ,aśvamāyāntamunmathya nagarābhimukho yayau.
english: Having emerged from the city, that king, Bhagadatta's son, having struck down the approaching horse, then headed back towards the city.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 74 Verse 3. Having emerged from the city, that king, Bhagadatta's son, having struck down the approaching horse, then headed back towards the city.

sanskrit: तमालक्ष्य महाबाहुः कुरूणामृषभस्तदा ।गाण्डीवं विक्षिपंस्तूर्णं सहसा समुपाद्रवत् ॥४॥
transliteration: tamālakṣya mahābāhuḥ kurūṇāmṛṣabhastadā ,gāṇḍīvaṁ vikṣipaṁstūrṇaṁ sahasā samupādravat.
english: Having caught sight of him, the mighty-armed one, that best of the Kurus (Arjuna), then quickly rushed forward with his Gāṇḍīva bow, charging suddenly.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 74 Verse 4. Having caught sight of him, the mighty-armed one, that best of the Kurus (Arjuna), then quickly rushed forward with his Gāṇḍīva bow, charging suddenly.

sanskrit: ततो गाण्डीवनिर्मुक्तैरिषुभिर्मोहितो नृपः ।हयमुत्सृज्य तं वीरस्ततः पार्थमुपाद्रवत् ॥५॥
transliteration: tato gāṇḍīvanirmuktairiṣubhirmohito nṛpaḥ ,hayamutsṛjya taṁ vīrastataḥ pārthamupādravat.
english: Then the king, bewildered by the arrows released from the Gāṇḍīva bow, that hero, abandoned his horse and rushed towards Pārtha (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 74 Verse 5. Then the king, bewildered by the arrows released from the Gāṇḍīva bow, that hero, abandoned his horse and rushed towards Pārtha (Arjuna).

sanskrit: पुनः प्रविश्य नगरं दंशितः स नृपोत्तमः ।आरुह्य नागप्रवरं निर्ययौ युद्धकाङ्क्षया ॥६॥
transliteration: punaḥ praviśya nagaraṁ daṁśitaḥ sa nṛpottamaḥ ,āruhya nāgapravaraṁ niryayau yuddhakāṅkṣayā.
english: Having re-entered the city, that armored king, the best of rulers, then mounted an excellent elephant and set out, eager for battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 74 Verse 6. Having re-entered the city, that armored king, the best of rulers, then mounted an excellent elephant and set out, eager for battle.

sanskrit: पाण्डुरेणातपत्रेण ध्रियमाणेन मूर्धनि ।दोधूयता चामरेण श्वेतेन च महारथः ॥७॥
transliteration: pāṇḍureṇātapatreṇa dhriyamāṇena mūrdhani ,dodhūyatā cāmareṇa śvetena ca mahārathaḥ.
english: The great chariot warrior, with a white umbrella being held over his head and a white whisk being continuously waved...
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 74 Verse 7. The great chariot warrior, with a white umbrella being held over his head and a white whisk being continuously waved...

sanskrit: ततः पार्थं समासाद्य पाण्डवानां महारथम् ।आह्वयामास कौरव्यं बाल्यान्मोहाच्च संयुगे ॥८॥
transliteration: tataḥ pārthaṁ samāsādya pāṇḍavānāṁ mahāratham ,āhvayāmāsa kauravyaṁ bālyānmohācca saṁyuge.
english: Then, having approached Pārtha (Arjuna), the great chariot warrior among the Pāṇḍavas, he challenged that Kuru prince (Arjuna) in battle, due to childishness and delusion (moha).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 74 Verse 8. Then, having approached Pārtha (Arjuna), the great chariot warrior among the Pāṇḍavas, he challenged that Kuru prince (Arjuna) in battle, due to childishness and delusion (moha).

sanskrit: स वारणं नगप्रख्यं प्रभिन्नकरटामुखम् ।प्रेषयामास संक्रुद्धस्ततः श्वेतहयं प्रति ॥९॥
transliteration: sa vāraṇaṁ nagaprakhyaṁ prabhinnakaraṭāmukham ,preṣayāmāsa saṁkruddhastataḥ śvetahayaṁ prati.
english: Then, enraged, he dispatched the elephant, which was mountain-like and with ichor flowing from its temples, towards the white-horsed warrior (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 74 Verse 9. Then, enraged, he dispatched the elephant, which was mountain-like and with ichor flowing from its temples, towards the white-horsed warrior (Arjuna).

sanskrit: विक्षरन्तं यथा मेघं परवारणवारणम् ।शास्त्रवत्कल्पितं संख्ये त्रिसाहं युद्धदुर्मदम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: vikṣarantaṁ yathā meghaṁ paravāraṇavāraṇam ,śāstravatkalpitaṁ saṁkhye trisāhaṁ yuddhadurmadam.
english: The elephant, discharging (ichor) like a cloud, was capable of repelling enemy elephants, arrayed in battle according to military science, able to face three thousand (foes), and formidable in combat.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 74 Verse 10. The elephant, discharging (ichor) like a cloud, was capable of repelling enemy elephants, arrayed in battle according to military science, able to face three thousand (foes), and formidable in combat.

sanskrit: प्रचोद्यमानः स गजस्तेन राज्ञा महाबलः ।तदाङ्कुशेन विबभावुत्पतिष्यन्निवाम्बरम् ॥११॥
transliteration: pracodyamānaḥ sa gajastena rājñā mahābalaḥ ,tadāṅkuśena vibabhāvutpatiṣyannivāmbaram.
english: That mighty elephant, being driven by the king with his goad, then appeared as if it were about to fly up into the sky.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 74 Verse 11. That mighty elephant, being driven by the king with his goad, then appeared as if it were about to fly up into the sky.

sanskrit: तमापतन्तं संप्रेक्ष्य क्रुद्धो राजन्धनंजयः ।भूमिष्ठो वारणगतं योधयामास भारत ॥१२॥
transliteration: tamāpatantaṁ saṁprekṣya kruddho rājandhanaṁjayaḥ ,bhūmiṣṭho vāraṇagataṁ yodhayāmāsa bhārata.
english: O King, upon seeing that (elephant) charging, the enraged Arjuna, who was standing on the ground, fought against the one mounted on the elephant, O Bhārata.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 74 Verse 12. O King, upon seeing that (elephant) charging, the enraged Arjuna, who was standing on the ground, fought against the one mounted on the elephant, O Bhārata.

sanskrit: वज्रदत्तस्तु संक्रुद्धो मुमोचाशु धनंजये ।तोमरानग्निसंकाशाञ्शलभानिव वेगितान् ॥१३॥
transliteration: vajradattastu saṁkruddho mumocāśu dhanaṁjaye ,tomarānagnisaṁkāśāñśalabhāniva vegitān.
english: But Vajradatta, greatly enraged, quickly discharged at Arjuna fire-like javelins, swift as locusts.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 74 Verse 13. But Vajradatta, greatly enraged, quickly discharged at Arjuna fire-like javelins, swift as locusts.

sanskrit: अर्जुनस्तानसंप्राप्तान्गाण्डीवप्रेषितैः शरैः ।द्विधा त्रिधा च चिच्छेद ख एव खगमैस्तदा ॥१४॥
transliteration: arjunastānasaṁprāptāngāṇḍīvapreṣitaiḥ śaraiḥ ,dvidhā tridhā ca ciccheda kha eva khagamaistadā.
english: Arjuna then, with his sky-flying arrows discharged from the Gaṇḍīva bow, cut those javelins into two and three pieces in the sky itself, before they could even reach him.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 74 Verse 14. Arjuna then, with his sky-flying arrows discharged from the Gaṇḍīva bow, cut those javelins into two and three pieces in the sky itself, before they could even reach him.

sanskrit: स तान्दृष्ट्वा तथा छिन्नांस्तोमरान्भगदत्तजः ।इषूनसक्तांस्त्वरितः प्राहिणोत्पाण्डवं प्रति ॥१५॥
transliteration: sa tāndṛṣṭvā tathā chinnāṁstomarānbhagadattajaḥ ,iṣūnasaktāṁstvaritaḥ prāhiṇotpāṇḍavaṁ prati.
english: The son of Bhagadatta, seeing those javelins thus cut down, quickly dispatched unhindered arrows towards the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 74 Verse 15. The son of Bhagadatta, seeing those javelins thus cut down, quickly dispatched unhindered arrows towards the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna).

sanskrit: ततोऽर्जुनस्तूर्णतरं रुक्मपुङ्खानजिह्मगान् ।प्रेषयामास संक्रुद्धो भगदत्तात्मजं प्रति ॥१६॥
transliteration: tato'rjunastūrṇataraṁ rukmapuṅkhānajihmagān ,preṣayāmāsa saṁkruddho bhagadattātmajaṁ prati.
english: Then, Arjuna, very swiftly and enraged, sent gold-shafted, unfaltering arrows towards Bhagadatta's son.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 74 Verse 16. Then, Arjuna, very swiftly and enraged, sent gold-shafted, unfaltering arrows towards Bhagadatta's son.

sanskrit: स तैर्विद्धो महातेजा वज्रदत्तो महाहवे ।भृशाहतः पपातोर्व्यां न त्वेनमजहात्स्मृतिः ॥१७॥
transliteration: sa tairviddho mahātejā vajradatto mahāhave ,bhṛśāhataḥ papātorvyāṁ na tvenamajahātsmṛtiḥ.
english: That greatly valorous Vajradatta, struck by those (arrows) in the great battle and severely wounded, fell to the ground, but his consciousness (smṛti) did not leave him.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 74 Verse 17. That greatly valorous Vajradatta, struck by those (arrows) in the great battle and severely wounded, fell to the ground, but his consciousness (smṛti) did not leave him.

sanskrit: ततः स पुनरारुह्य वारणप्रवरं रणे ।अव्यग्रः प्रेषयामास जयार्थी विजयं प्रति ॥१८॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa punarāruhya vāraṇapravaraṁ raṇe ,avyagraḥ preṣayāmāsa jayārthī vijayaṁ prati.
english: Then, he, desiring victory (vijaya), again mounted his excellent war-elephant on the battlefield and, undisturbed, dispatched (arrows) towards Arjuna (vijaya).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 74 Verse 18. Then, he, desiring victory (vijaya), again mounted his excellent war-elephant on the battlefield and, undisturbed, dispatched (arrows) towards Arjuna (vijaya).

sanskrit: तस्मै बाणांस्ततो जिष्णुर्निर्मुक्ताशीविषोपमान् ।प्रेषयामास संक्रुद्धो ज्वलितानिव पावकान् ॥१९॥
transliteration: tasmai bāṇāṁstato jiṣṇurnirmuktāśīviṣopamān ,preṣayāmāsa saṁkruddho jvalitāniva pāvakān.
english: Then, Arjuna, greatly enraged, dispatched arrows towards him that resembled unleashed venomous snakes and blazing fires.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 74 Verse 19. Then, Arjuna, greatly enraged, dispatched arrows towards him that resembled unleashed venomous snakes and blazing fires.

sanskrit: स तैर्विद्धो महानागो विस्रवन्रुधिरं बभौ ।हिमवानिव शैलेन्द्रो बहुप्रस्रवणस्तदा ॥२०॥
transliteration: sa tairviddho mahānāgo visravanrudhiraṁ babhau ,himavāniva śailendro bahuprasravaṇastadā.
english: Struck by those arrows, that great warrior (like a mighty elephant), gushing blood, then appeared like the Himalaya, the king of mountains, with its numerous flowing springs.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 74 Verse 20. Struck by those arrows, that great warrior (like a mighty elephant), gushing blood, then appeared like the Himalaya, the king of mountains, with its numerous flowing springs.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवं त्रिरात्रमभवत्तद्युद्धं भरतर्षभ ।अर्जुनस्य नरेन्द्रेण वृत्रेणेव शतक्रतोः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evaṁ trirātramabhavattadyuddhaṁ bharatarṣabha ,arjunasya narendreṇa vṛtreṇeva śatakratoḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: "O best of Bharatas, that battle thus continued for three nights; it was a battle of Arjuna with the king (Vajradatta), similar to the battle of Indra (Śatakratu) with Vṛtra."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 75 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: "O best of Bharatas, that battle thus continued for three nights; it was a battle of Arjuna with the king (Vajradatta), similar to the battle of Indra (Śatakratu) with Vṛtra."

sanskrit: ततश्चतुर्थे दिवसे वज्रदत्तो महाबलः ।जहास सस्वनं हासं वाक्यं चेदमथाब्रवीत् ॥२॥
transliteration: tataścaturthe divase vajradatto mahābalaḥ ,jahāsa sasvanaṁ hāsaṁ vākyaṁ cedamathābravīt.
english: Then, on the fourth day, Vajradatta, who was greatly powerful, laughed loudly and then spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 75 Verse 2. Then, on the fourth day, Vajradatta, who was greatly powerful, laughed loudly and then spoke these words.

sanskrit: अर्जुनार्जुन तिष्ठस्व न मे जीवन्विमोक्ष्यसे ।त्वां निहत्य करिष्यामि पितुस्तोयं यथाविधि ॥३॥
transliteration: arjunārjuna tiṣṭhasva na me jīvanvimokṣyase ,tvāṁ nihatya kariṣyāmi pitustoyaṁ yathāvidhi.
english: Arjuna, Arjuna, stop! You will not escape me alive. Having killed you, I will perform the water libations for my father according to the proper rites.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 75 Verse 3. Arjuna, Arjuna, stop! You will not escape me alive. Having killed you, I will perform the water libations for my father according to the proper rites.

sanskrit: त्वया वृद्धो मम पिता भगदत्तः पितुः सखा ।हतो वृद्धोऽपचायित्वाच्छिशुं मामद्य योधय ॥४॥
transliteration: tvayā vṛddho mama pitā bhagadattaḥ pituḥ sakhā ,hato vṛddho'pacāyitvācchiśuṁ māmadya yodhaya.
english: My aged father Bhagadatta, who was a friend of your father (Pāṇḍu), was killed by you. Having thus shown disrespect to an elder, now fight me, a mere child, today.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 75 Verse 4. My aged father Bhagadatta, who was a friend of your father (Pāṇḍu), was killed by you. Having thus shown disrespect to an elder, now fight me, a mere child, today.

sanskrit: इत्येवमुक्त्वा संक्रुद्धो वज्रदत्तो नराधिपः ।प्रेषयामास कौरव्य वारणं पाण्डवं प्रति ॥५॥
transliteration: ityevamuktvā saṁkruddho vajradatto narādhipaḥ ,preṣayāmāsa kauravya vāraṇaṁ pāṇḍavaṁ prati.
english: Having thus spoken, the greatly enraged King Vajradatta, O descendant of Kuru, sent forth his elephant towards Pāṇḍava (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 75 Verse 5. Having thus spoken, the greatly enraged King Vajradatta, O descendant of Kuru, sent forth his elephant towards Pāṇḍava (Arjuna).

sanskrit: संप्रेष्यमाणो नागेन्द्रो वज्रदत्तेन धीमता ।उत्पतिष्यन्निवाकाशमभिदुद्राव पाण्डवम् ॥६॥
transliteration: saṁpreṣyamāṇo nāgendro vajradattena dhīmatā ,utpatiṣyannivākāśamabhidudrāva pāṇḍavam.
english: The lordly elephant, being dispatched by the intelligent Vajradatta, rushed towards Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) as if it were about to fly up into the sky.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 75 Verse 6. The lordly elephant, being dispatched by the intelligent Vajradatta, rushed towards Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) as if it were about to fly up into the sky.

sanskrit: अग्रहस्तप्रमुक्तेन शीकरेण स फल्गुनम् ।समुक्षत महाराज शैलं नील इवाम्बुदः ॥७॥
transliteration: agrahastapramuktena śīkareṇa sa phalgunam ,samukṣata mahārāja śailaṁ nīla ivāmbudaḥ.
english: O great king, he sprinkled Arjuna with the spray released from the tip of his trunk, just as a dark cloud showers a mountain.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 75 Verse 7. O great king, he sprinkled Arjuna with the spray released from the tip of his trunk, just as a dark cloud showers a mountain.

sanskrit: स तेन प्रेषितो राज्ञा मेघवन्निनदन्मुहुः ।मुखाडम्बरघोषेण समाद्रवत फल्गुनम् ॥८॥
transliteration: sa tena preṣito rājñā meghavanninadanmuhuḥ ,mukhāḍambaraghoṣeṇa samādravata phalgunam.
english: Sent by that king, the elephant repeatedly trumpeted like a cloud and, with the thunderous roar from its mouth, charged towards Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 75 Verse 8. Sent by that king, the elephant repeatedly trumpeted like a cloud and, with the thunderous roar from its mouth, charged towards Arjuna.

sanskrit: स नृत्यन्निव नागेन्द्रो वज्रदत्तप्रचोदितः ।आससाद द्रुतं राजन्कौरवाणां महारथम् ॥९॥
transliteration: sa nṛtyanniva nāgendro vajradattapracoditaḥ ,āsasāda drutaṁ rājankauravāṇāṁ mahāratham.
english: O king, that great elephant, urged by Vajradatta, swiftly approached the Kaurava's mighty warrior (Arjuna), as if it were dancing.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 75 Verse 9. O king, that great elephant, urged by Vajradatta, swiftly approached the Kaurava's mighty warrior (Arjuna), as if it were dancing.

sanskrit: तमापतन्तं संप्रेक्ष्य वज्रदत्तस्य वारणम् ।गाण्डीवमाश्रित्य बली न व्यकम्पत शत्रुहा ॥१०॥
transliteration: tamāpatantaṁ saṁprekṣya vajradattasya vāraṇam ,gāṇḍīvamāśritya balī na vyakampata śatruhā.
english: Having observed Vajradatta's elephant charging towards him, the mighty slayer of foes (Arjuna), relying on his Gaṇḍīva bow, did not tremble.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 75 Verse 10. Having observed Vajradatta's elephant charging towards him, the mighty slayer of foes (Arjuna), relying on his Gaṇḍīva bow, did not tremble.

sanskrit: चुक्रोध बलवच्चापि पाण्डवस्तस्य भूपतेः ।कार्यविघ्नमनुस्मृत्य पूर्ववैरं च भारत ॥११॥
transliteration: cukrodha balavaccāpi pāṇḍavastasya bhūpateḥ ,kāryavighnamanusmṛtya pūrvavairaṁ ca bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, recalling the obstruction of his purpose and the previous animosity, the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) also became exceedingly enraged at that king.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 75 Verse 11. O Bhārata, recalling the obstruction of his purpose and the previous animosity, the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) also became exceedingly enraged at that king.

sanskrit: ततस्तं वारणं क्रुद्धः शरजालेन पाण्डवः ।निवारयामास तदा वेलेव मकरालयम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: tatastaṁ vāraṇaṁ kruddhaḥ śarajālena pāṇḍavaḥ ,nivārayāmāsa tadā veleva makarālayam.
english: Then the enraged Pāṇḍava (Arjuna), with a shower of arrows, stopped that elephant, just as a shore restrains the ocean.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 75 Verse 12. Then the enraged Pāṇḍava (Arjuna), with a shower of arrows, stopped that elephant, just as a shore restrains the ocean.

sanskrit: स नागप्रवरो वीर्यादर्जुनेन निवारितः ।तस्थौ शरैर्वितुन्नाङ्गः श्वाविच्छललितो यथा ॥१३॥
transliteration: sa nāgapravaro vīryādarjunena nivāritaḥ ,tasthau śarairvitunnāṅgaḥ śvāvicchalalito yathā.
english: That excellent elephant, stopped by Arjuna's valor, stood with its body pierced by arrows, just like a porcupine covered with quills.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 75 Verse 13. That excellent elephant, stopped by Arjuna's valor, stood with its body pierced by arrows, just like a porcupine covered with quills.

sanskrit: निवारितं गजं दृष्ट्वा भगदत्तात्मजो नृपः ।उत्ससर्ज शितान्बाणानर्जुने क्रोधमूर्छितः ॥१४॥
transliteration: nivāritaṁ gajaṁ dṛṣṭvā bhagadattātmajo nṛpaḥ ,utsasarja śitānbāṇānarjune krodhamūrchitaḥ.
english: Having seen his elephant stopped, the king, Bhagadatta's son, overcome with anger, discharged sharp arrows at Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 75 Verse 14. Having seen his elephant stopped, the king, Bhagadatta's son, overcome with anger, discharged sharp arrows at Arjuna.

sanskrit: अर्जुनस्तु महाराज शरैः शरविघातिभिः ।वारयामास तानस्तांस्तदद्भुतमिवाभवत् ॥१५॥
transliteration: arjunastu mahārāja śaraiḥ śaravighātibhiḥ ,vārayāmāsa tānastāṁstadadbhutamivābhavat.
english: However, O great king, Arjuna repelled those arrows shot by the enemy using his own arrows that countered them. This feat seemed truly astonishing.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 75 Verse 15. However, O great king, Arjuna repelled those arrows shot by the enemy using his own arrows that countered them. This feat seemed truly astonishing.

sanskrit: ततः पुनरतिक्रुद्धो राजा प्राग्ज्योतिषाधिपः ।प्रेषयामास नागेन्द्रं बलवच्छ्वसनोपमम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: tataḥ punaratikruddho rājā prāgjyotiṣādhipaḥ ,preṣayāmāsa nāgendraṁ balavacchvasanopamam.
english: Then, the king of Pragjyotisha, greatly enraged once more, dispatched a mighty elephant, resembling a powerful gale.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 75 Verse 16. Then, the king of Pragjyotisha, greatly enraged once more, dispatched a mighty elephant, resembling a powerful gale.

sanskrit: तमापतन्तं संप्रेक्ष्य बलवान्पाकशासनिः ।नाराचमग्निसंकाशं प्राहिणोद्वारणं प्रति ॥१७॥
transliteration: tamāpatantaṁ saṁprekṣya balavānpākaśāsaniḥ ,nārācamagnisaṁkāśaṁ prāhiṇodvāraṇaṁ prati.
english: Observing that elephant advancing, the mighty son of Indra (Arjuna) shot forth a flaming iron arrow, brilliant as fire, directly at the elephant.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 75 Verse 17. Observing that elephant advancing, the mighty son of Indra (Arjuna) shot forth a flaming iron arrow, brilliant as fire, directly at the elephant.

sanskrit: स तेन वारणो राजन्मर्मण्यभिहतो भृशम् ।पपात सहसा भूमौ वज्ररुग्ण इवाचलः ॥१८॥
transliteration: sa tena vāraṇo rājanmarmaṇyabhihato bhṛśam ,papāta sahasā bhūmau vajrarugṇa ivācalaḥ.
english: That elephant, O king, struck severely in its vital spot by that (arrow), instantly collapsed onto the earth, just like a mountain rent asunder by a thunderbolt.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 75 Verse 18. That elephant, O king, struck severely in its vital spot by that (arrow), instantly collapsed onto the earth, just like a mountain rent asunder by a thunderbolt.

sanskrit: स पतञ्शुशुभे नागो धनंजयशराहतः ।विशन्निव महाशैलो महीं वज्रप्रपीडितः ॥१९॥
transliteration: sa patañśuśubhe nāgo dhanaṁjayaśarāhataḥ ,viśanniva mahāśailo mahīṁ vajraprapīḍitaḥ.
english: That serpent (elephant), struck by Arjuna's (Dhananjaya's) arrows, fell and appeared like a great mountain, afflicted by a thunderbolt, sinking into the earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 75 Verse 19. That serpent (elephant), struck by Arjuna's (Dhananjaya's) arrows, fell and appeared like a great mountain, afflicted by a thunderbolt, sinking into the earth.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्निपतिते नागे वज्रदत्तस्य पाण्डवः ।तं न भेतव्यमित्याह ततो भूमिगतं नृपम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: tasminnipatite nāge vajradattasya pāṇḍavaḥ ,taṁ na bhetavyamityāha tato bhūmigataṁ nṛpam.
english: When that elephant (nāga) belonging to Vajradatta had fallen, the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) then said to that king, who had fallen to the ground, 'You should not be afraid.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 75 Verse 20. When that elephant (nāga) belonging to Vajradatta had fallen, the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) then said to that king, who had fallen to the ground, 'You should not be afraid.'

sanskrit: अब्रवीद्धि महातेजाः प्रस्थितं मां युधिष्ठिरः ।राजानस्ते न हन्तव्या धनंजय कथंचन ॥२१॥
transliteration: abravīddhi mahātejāḥ prasthitaṁ māṁ yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,rājānaste na hantavyā dhanaṁjaya kathaṁcana.
english: Indeed, the greatly glorious Yudhishthira said to me as I was setting out: 'Those kings are not to be killed by you, Dhananjaya (Arjuna), under any circumstances.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 75 Verse 21. Indeed, the greatly glorious Yudhishthira said to me as I was setting out: 'Those kings are not to be killed by you, Dhananjaya (Arjuna), under any circumstances.'

sanskrit: सर्वमेतन्नरव्याघ्र भवत्वेतावता कृतम् ।योधाश्चापि न हन्तव्या धनंजय रणे त्वया ॥२२॥
transliteration: sarvametannaravyāghra bhavatvetāvatā kṛtam ,yodhāścāpi na hantavyā dhanaṁjaya raṇe tvayā.
english: O tiger among men (Arjuna), let all this (action/restraint) be accomplished to this extent. And Dhananjaya, the warriors too should not be killed by you in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 75 Verse 22. O tiger among men (Arjuna), let all this (action/restraint) be accomplished to this extent. And Dhananjaya, the warriors too should not be killed by you in battle.

sanskrit: वक्तव्याश्चापि राजानः सर्वैः सह सुहृज्जनैः ।युधिष्ठिरस्याश्वमेधो भवद्भिरनुभूयताम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: vaktavyāścāpi rājānaḥ sarvaiḥ saha suhṛjjanaiḥ ,yudhiṣṭhirasyāśvamedho bhavadbhiranubhūyatām.
english: And the kings, along with all their well-wishers, should be informed: 'You all should experience Yudhishthira's Aśvamedha (Vedic ritual).'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 75 Verse 23. And the kings, along with all their well-wishers, should be informed: 'You all should experience Yudhishthira's Aśvamedha (Vedic ritual).'

sanskrit: इति भ्रातृवचः श्रुत्वा न हन्मि त्वां जनाधिप ।उत्तिष्ठ न भयं तेऽस्ति स्वस्तिमान्गच्छ पार्थिव ॥२४॥
transliteration: iti bhrātṛvacaḥ śrutvā na hanmi tvāṁ janādhipa ,uttiṣṭha na bhayaṁ te'sti svastimāngaccha pārthiva.
english: Having heard his brother's words, (he said,) 'I will not kill you, O ruler of people. Arise! There is no fear for you. Go forth safely, O king!'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 75 Verse 24. Having heard his brother's words, (he said,) 'I will not kill you, O ruler of people. Arise! There is no fear for you. Go forth safely, O king!'

sanskrit: आगच्छेथा महाराज परां चैत्रीमुपस्थिताम् ।तदाश्वमेधो भविता धर्मराजस्य धीमतः ॥२५॥
transliteration: āgacchethā mahārāja parāṁ caitrīmupasthitām ,tadāśvamedho bhavitā dharmarājasya dhīmataḥ.
english: O great king, you should come when the upcoming month of Caitra arrives. Then, the Aśvamedha (Vedic ritual) of the wise king of natural law (dharma) (Yudhishthira) will take place.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 75 Verse 25. O great king, you should come when the upcoming month of Caitra arrives. Then, the Aśvamedha (Vedic ritual) of the wise king of natural law (dharma) (Yudhishthira) will take place.

sanskrit: एवमुक्तः स राजा तु भगदत्तात्मजस्तदा ।तथेत्येवाब्रवीद्वाक्यं पाण्डवेनाभिनिर्जितः ॥२६॥
transliteration: evamuktaḥ sa rājā tu bhagadattātmajastadā ,tathetyevābravīdvākyaṁ pāṇḍavenābhinirjitaḥ.
english: Thus addressed, that king, the son of Bhagadatta, who had been completely defeated by the Pandava, then spoke the word, 'So be it!'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 75 Verse 26. Thus addressed, that king, the son of Bhagadatta, who had been completely defeated by the Pandava, then spoke the word, 'So be it!'

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।सैन्धवैरभवद्युद्धं ततस्तस्य किरीटिनः ।हतशेषैर्महाराज हतानां च सुतैरपि ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,saindhavairabhavadyuddhaṁ tatastasya kirīṭinaḥ ,hataśeṣairmahārāja hatānāṁ ca sutairapi.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: O great king, then a battle took place for the crowned one (Arjuna) with the surviving Saindhavas and also with the sons of those who had been slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 76 Verse 1. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: O great king, then a battle took place for the crowned one (Arjuna) with the surviving Saindhavas and also with the sons of those who had been slain.

sanskrit: तेऽवतीर्णमुपश्रुत्य विषयं श्वेतवाहनम् ।प्रत्युद्ययुरमृष्यन्तो राजानः पाण्डवर्षभम् ॥२॥
transliteration: te'vatīrṇamupaśrutya viṣayaṁ śvetavāhanam ,pratyudyayuramṛṣyanto rājānaḥ pāṇḍavarṣabham.
english: Having heard that the white-horsed one (Arjuna) had entered their territory, the kings, unable to tolerate this, went forth to meet that best of the Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 76 Verse 2. Having heard that the white-horsed one (Arjuna) had entered their territory, the kings, unable to tolerate this, went forth to meet that best of the Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: अश्वं च तं परामृश्य विषयान्ते विषोपमाः ।न भयं चक्रिरे पार्थाद्भीमसेनादनन्तरात् ॥३॥
transliteration: aśvaṁ ca taṁ parāmṛśya viṣayānte viṣopamāḥ ,na bhayaṁ cakrire pārthādbhīmasenādanantarāt.
english: And having recognized that horse at the border of their territory, these kings, venomous like poison, felt no fear from Pārtha (Arjuna), nor from Bhīmasena who followed immediately.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 76 Verse 3. And having recognized that horse at the border of their territory, these kings, venomous like poison, felt no fear from Pārtha (Arjuna), nor from Bhīmasena who followed immediately.

sanskrit: तेऽविदूराद्धनुष्पाणिं यज्ञियस्य हयस्य च ।बीभत्सुं प्रत्यपद्यन्त पदातिनमवस्थितम् ॥४॥
transliteration: te'vidūrāddhanuṣpāṇiṁ yajñiyasya hayasya ca ,bībhatsuṁ pratyapadyanta padātinamavasthitam.
english: At no great distance, they confronted Bībhatsu (Arjuna), who was standing on foot with bow in hand, near the sacrificial horse.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 76 Verse 4. At no great distance, they confronted Bībhatsu (Arjuna), who was standing on foot with bow in hand, near the sacrificial horse.

sanskrit: ततस्ते तु महावीर्या राजानः पर्यवारयन् ।जिगीषन्तो नरव्याघ्राः पूर्वं विनिकृता युधि ॥५॥
transliteration: tataste tu mahāvīryā rājānaḥ paryavārayan ,jigīṣanto naravyāghrāḥ pūrvaṁ vinikṛtā yudhi.
english: Then, those greatly valorous kings, veritable tigers among men, who had previously been humiliated in battle, surrounded him, eager for victory.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 76 Verse 5. Then, those greatly valorous kings, veritable tigers among men, who had previously been humiliated in battle, surrounded him, eager for victory.

sanskrit: ते नामान्यथ गोत्राणि कर्माणि विविधानि च ।कीर्तयन्तस्तदा पार्थं शरवर्षैरवाकिरन् ॥६॥
transliteration: te nāmānyatha gotrāṇi karmāṇi vividhāni ca ,kīrtayantastadā pārthaṁ śaravarṣairavākiran.
english: Then, proclaiming their names, lineages, and various deeds, they showered Pārtha (Arjuna) with showers of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 76 Verse 6. Then, proclaiming their names, lineages, and various deeds, they showered Pārtha (Arjuna) with showers of arrows.

sanskrit: ते किरन्तः शरांस्तीक्ष्णान्वारणेन्द्रनिवारणान् ।रणे जयमभीप्सन्तः कौन्तेयं पर्यवारयन् ॥७॥
transliteration: te kirantaḥ śarāṁstīkṣṇānvāraṇendranivāraṇān ,raṇe jayamabhīpsantaḥ kaunteyaṁ paryavārayan.
english: Showering sharp arrows, capable of stopping even lordly elephants, they surrounded Kaunteya (Arjuna) in battle, eager for victory.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 76 Verse 7. Showering sharp arrows, capable of stopping even lordly elephants, they surrounded Kaunteya (Arjuna) in battle, eager for victory.

sanskrit: तेऽसमीक्ष्यैव तं वीरमुग्रकर्माणमाहवे ।सर्वे युयुधिरे वीरा रथस्थास्तं पदातिनम् ॥८॥
transliteration: te'samīkṣyaiva taṁ vīramugrakarmāṇamāhave ,sarve yuyudhire vīrā rathasthāstaṁ padātinam.
english: Without even properly assessing that hero of fierce deeds in battle, all those brave warriors, who were on their chariots, fought against him, who was fighting on foot.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 76 Verse 8. Without even properly assessing that hero of fierce deeds in battle, all those brave warriors, who were on their chariots, fought against him, who was fighting on foot.

sanskrit: ते तमाजघ्निरे वीरं निवातकवचान्तकम् ।संशप्तकनिहन्तारं हन्तारं सैन्धवस्य च ॥९॥
transliteration: te tamājaghnire vīraṁ nivātakavacāntakam ,saṁśaptakanihantāraṁ hantāraṁ saindhavasya ca.
english: They attacked that hero who vanquished the Nivātakavacas, the slayer of the Saṃśaptakas, and the killer of the King of Sindhu.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 76 Verse 9. They attacked that hero who vanquished the Nivātakavacas, the slayer of the Saṃśaptakas, and the killer of the King of Sindhu.

sanskrit: ततो रथसहस्रेण हयानामयुतेन च ।कोष्ठकीकृत्य कौन्तेयं संप्रहृष्टमयोधयन् ॥१०॥
transliteration: tato rathasahasreṇa hayānāmayutena ca ,koṣṭhakīkṛtya kaunteyaṁ saṁprahṛṣṭamayodhayan.
english: Then, with a thousand chariots and ten thousand horses, they encircled the son of Kunti (Arjuna) and, greatly delighted, fought against him.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 76 Verse 10. Then, with a thousand chariots and ten thousand horses, they encircled the son of Kunti (Arjuna) and, greatly delighted, fought against him.

sanskrit: संस्मरन्तो वधं वीराः सिन्धुराजस्य धीमतः ।जयद्रथस्य कौरव्य समरे सव्यसाचिना ॥११॥
transliteration: saṁsmaranto vadhaṁ vīrāḥ sindhurājasya dhīmataḥ ,jayadrathasya kauravya samare savyasācinā.
english: O scion of Kuru, the warriors, remembering the killing of the wise King of Sindhu, Jayadratha, by Arjuna (Savyasācin) in that battle, (fought).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 76 Verse 11. O scion of Kuru, the warriors, remembering the killing of the wise King of Sindhu, Jayadratha, by Arjuna (Savyasācin) in that battle, (fought).

sanskrit: ततः पर्जन्यवत्सर्वे शरवृष्टिमवासृजन् ।तैः कीर्णः शुशुभे पार्थो रविर्मेघान्तरे यथा ॥१२॥
transliteration: tataḥ parjanyavatsarve śaravṛṣṭimavāsṛjan ,taiḥ kīrṇaḥ śuśubhe pārtho ravirmeghāntare yathā.
english: Then, all of them, like a rain cloud, unleashed a shower of arrows. Covered by these (arrows), Arjuna shone radiantly, just as the sun appears amidst the clouds.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 76 Verse 12. Then, all of them, like a rain cloud, unleashed a shower of arrows. Covered by these (arrows), Arjuna shone radiantly, just as the sun appears amidst the clouds.

sanskrit: स शरैः समवच्छन्नो ददृशे पाण्डवर्षभः ।पञ्जरान्तरसंचारी शकुन्त इव भारत ॥१३॥
transliteration: sa śaraiḥ samavacchanno dadṛśe pāṇḍavarṣabhaḥ ,pañjarāntarasaṁcārī śakunta iva bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, that best of the Pāṇḍavas (Arjuna), completely enveloped by arrows, was seen like a bird moving within a cage.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 76 Verse 13. O Bhārata, that best of the Pāṇḍavas (Arjuna), completely enveloped by arrows, was seen like a bird moving within a cage.

sanskrit: ततो हाहाकृतं सर्वं कौन्तेये शरपीडिते ।त्रैलोक्यमभवद्राजन्रविश्चासीद्रजोरुणः ॥१४॥
transliteration: tato hāhākṛtaṁ sarvaṁ kaunteye śarapīḍite ,trailokyamabhavadrājanraviścāsīdrajoruṇaḥ.
english: Then, O King, when the son of Kuntī (Arjuna) was tormented by arrows, a great cry of lamentation arose everywhere in the three worlds, and the sun became reddish, as if covered by dust.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 76 Verse 14. Then, O King, when the son of Kuntī (Arjuna) was tormented by arrows, a great cry of lamentation arose everywhere in the three worlds, and the sun became reddish, as if covered by dust.

sanskrit: ततो ववौ महाराज मारुतो रोमहर्षणः ।राहुरग्रसदादित्यं युगपत्सोममेव च ॥१५॥
transliteration: tato vavau mahārāja māruto romaharṣaṇaḥ ,rāhuragrasadādityaṁ yugapatsomameva ca.
english: Then, O great king, a terrifying, hair-raising wind blew. Rāhu simultaneously swallowed both the sun and the moon.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 76 Verse 15. Then, O great king, a terrifying, hair-raising wind blew. Rāhu simultaneously swallowed both the sun and the moon.

sanskrit: उल्काश्च जघ्निरे सूर्यं विकीर्यन्त्यः समन्ततः ।वेपथुश्चाभवद्राजन्कैलासस्य महागिरेः ॥१६॥
transliteration: ulkāśca jaghnire sūryaṁ vikīryantyaḥ samantataḥ ,vepathuścābhavadrājankailāsasya mahāgireḥ.
english: And meteors, scattering all around, seemed to strike the sun. And, O King, a trembling occurred in the great mountain Kailāsa.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 76 Verse 16. And meteors, scattering all around, seemed to strike the sun. And, O King, a trembling occurred in the great mountain Kailāsa.

sanskrit: मुमुचुश्चास्रमत्युष्णं दुःखशोकसमन्विताः ।सप्तर्षयो जातभयास्तथा देवर्षयोऽपि च ॥१७॥
transliteration: mumucuścāsramatyuṣṇaṁ duḥkhaśokasamanvitāḥ ,saptarṣayo jātabhayāstathā devarṣayo'pi ca.
english: The seven sages and also the divine sages, overcome by sorrow and grief, and filled with fear, shed extremely hot tears.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 76 Verse 17. The seven sages and also the divine sages, overcome by sorrow and grief, and filled with fear, shed extremely hot tears.

sanskrit: शशश्चाशु विनिर्भिद्य मण्डलं शशिनोऽपतत् ।विपरीतस्तदा राजंस्तस्मिन्नुत्पातलक्षणे ॥१८॥
transliteration: śaśaścāśu vinirbhidya maṇḍalaṁ śaśino'patat ,viparītastadā rājaṁstasminnutpātalakṣaṇe.
english: O king, then a hare quickly pierced the moon's orb and fell. At that time, in that ominous sign, everything appeared contrary or inverted.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 76 Verse 18. O king, then a hare quickly pierced the moon's orb and fell. At that time, in that ominous sign, everything appeared contrary or inverted.

sanskrit: रासभारुणसंकाशा धनुष्मन्तः सविद्युतः ।आवृत्य गगनं मेघा मुमुचुर्मांसशोणितम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: rāsabhāruṇasaṁkāśā dhanuṣmantaḥ savidyutaḥ ,āvṛtya gaganaṁ meghā mumucurmāṁsaśoṇitam.
english: Clouds, appearing like red donkeys, bearing rainbows and accompanied by lightning, covered the sky and rained down flesh and blood.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 76 Verse 19. Clouds, appearing like red donkeys, bearing rainbows and accompanied by lightning, covered the sky and rained down flesh and blood.

sanskrit: एवमासीत्तदा वीरे शरवर्षाभिसंवृते ।लोकेऽस्मिन्भरतश्रेष्ठ तदद्भुतमिवाभवत् ॥२०॥
transliteration: evamāsīttadā vīre śaravarṣābhisaṁvṛte ,loke'sminbharataśreṣṭha tadadbhutamivābhavat.
english: O best of Bharatas, O hero! At that time, in this world, which was enveloped by a shower of arrows, things were thus. It truly seemed like a wonder.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 76 Verse 20. O best of Bharatas, O hero! At that time, in this world, which was enveloped by a shower of arrows, things were thus. It truly seemed like a wonder.

sanskrit: तस्य तेनावकीर्णस्य शरजालेन सर्वशः ।मोहात्पपात गाण्डीवमावापश्च करादपि ॥२१॥
transliteration: tasya tenāvakīrṇasya śarajālena sarvaśaḥ ,mohātpapāta gāṇḍīvamāvāpaśca karādapi.
english: As he was completely overwhelmed on all sides by a shower of arrows, his Gāṇḍīva bow fell from his hand due to unconsciousness (moha), and his quiver also dropped.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 76 Verse 21. As he was completely overwhelmed on all sides by a shower of arrows, his Gāṇḍīva bow fell from his hand due to unconsciousness (moha), and his quiver also dropped.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्मोहमनुप्राप्ते शरजालं महत्तरम् ।सैन्धवा मुमुचुस्तूर्णं गतसत्त्वे महारथे ॥२२॥
transliteration: tasminmohamanuprāpte śarajālaṁ mahattaram ,saindhavā mumucustūrṇaṁ gatasattve mahārathe.
english: As that great warrior, whose vigor (sattva) was gone, fell into unconsciousness (moha), the Saindhavas quickly discharged an even greater shower of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 76 Verse 22. As that great warrior, whose vigor (sattva) was gone, fell into unconsciousness (moha), the Saindhavas quickly discharged an even greater shower of arrows.

sanskrit: ततो मोहसमापन्नं ज्ञात्वा पार्थं दिवौकसः ।सर्वे वित्रस्तमनसस्तस्य शान्तिपराभवन् ॥२३॥
transliteration: tato mohasamāpannaṁ jñātvā pārthaṁ divaukasaḥ ,sarve vitrastamanasastasya śāntiparābhavan.
english: Then, realizing that Pārtha (Arjuna) had fallen into unconsciousness (moha), all the gods, with terrified minds, became intent on restoring his peace.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 76 Verse 23. Then, realizing that Pārtha (Arjuna) had fallen into unconsciousness (moha), all the gods, with terrified minds, became intent on restoring his peace.

sanskrit: ततो देवर्षयः सर्वे तथा सप्तर्षयोऽपि च ।ब्रह्मर्षयश्च विजयं जेपुः पार्थस्य धीमतः ॥२४॥
transliteration: tato devarṣayaḥ sarve tathā saptarṣayo'pi ca ,brahmarṣayaśca vijayaṁ jepuḥ pārthasya dhīmataḥ.
english: Then, all the divine sages (devarṣis), as well as the seven sages (saptarṣis) and the Brahmin sages (brahmarṣis), prayed for the victory of the intelligent Pārtha (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 76 Verse 24. Then, all the divine sages (devarṣis), as well as the seven sages (saptarṣis) and the Brahmin sages (brahmarṣis), prayed for the victory of the intelligent Pārtha (Arjuna).

sanskrit: ततः प्रदीपिते देवैः पार्थतेजसि पार्थिव ।तस्थावचलवद्धीमान्संग्रामे परमास्त्रवित् ॥२५॥
transliteration: tataḥ pradīpite devaiḥ pārthatejasi pārthiva ,tasthāvacalavaddhīmānsaṁgrāme paramāstravit.
english: O King, then, when Pārtha's valor was kindled by the gods, the wise one, skilled in supreme weapons, stood firm in battle like an unmoving object.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 76 Verse 25. O King, then, when Pārtha's valor was kindled by the gods, the wise one, skilled in supreme weapons, stood firm in battle like an unmoving object.

sanskrit: विचकर्ष धनुर्दिव्यं ततः कौरवनन्दनः ।यन्त्रस्येवेह शब्दोऽभून्महांस्तस्य पुनः पुनः ॥२६॥
transliteration: vicakarṣa dhanurdivyaṁ tataḥ kauravanandanaḥ ,yantrasyeveha śabdo'bhūnmahāṁstasya punaḥ punaḥ.
english: Then, that joy of the Kurus (Arjuna) drew his divine bow. From it, a great sound, like that of a machine, repeatedly arose.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 76 Verse 26. Then, that joy of the Kurus (Arjuna) drew his divine bow. From it, a great sound, like that of a machine, repeatedly arose.

sanskrit: ततः स शरवर्षाणि प्रत्यमित्रान्प्रति प्रभुः ।ववर्ष धनुषा पार्थो वर्षाणीव सुरेश्वरः ॥२७॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa śaravarṣāṇi pratyamitrānprati prabhuḥ ,vavarṣa dhanuṣā pārtho varṣāṇīva sureśvaraḥ.
english: Then, the powerful Pārtha (Arjuna), with his bow, rained down showers of arrows upon his adversaries, just as Indra, the lord of gods, sends down rain.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 76 Verse 27. Then, the powerful Pārtha (Arjuna), with his bow, rained down showers of arrows upon his adversaries, just as Indra, the lord of gods, sends down rain.

sanskrit: ततस्ते सैन्धवा योधाः सर्व एव सराजकाः ।नादृश्यन्त शरैः कीर्णाः शलभैरिव पावकाः ॥२८॥
transliteration: tataste saindhavā yodhāḥ sarva eva sarājakāḥ ,nādṛśyanta śaraiḥ kīrṇāḥ śalabhairiva pāvakāḥ.
english: Then, all those Saindhava warriors, including their king, became invisible, covered by arrows, just as fires become obscured by swarms of moths.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 76 Verse 28. Then, all those Saindhava warriors, including their king, became invisible, covered by arrows, just as fires become obscured by swarms of moths.

sanskrit: तस्य शब्देन वित्रेसुर्भयार्ताश्च विदुद्रुवुः ।मुमुचुश्चाश्रु शोकार्ताः सुषुपुश्चापि सैन्धवाः ॥२९॥
transliteration: tasya śabdena vitresurbhayārtāśca vidudruvuḥ ,mumucuścāśru śokārtāḥ suṣupuścāpi saindhavāḥ.
english: At his roar, those tormented by fear trembled and fled. Afflicted by sorrow, they shed tears, and even the Sindhu warriors became inert.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 76 Verse 29. At his roar, those tormented by fear trembled and fled. Afflicted by sorrow, they shed tears, and even the Sindhu warriors became inert.

sanskrit: तांस्तु सर्वान्नरश्रेष्ठः सर्वतो विचरन्बली ।अलातचक्रवद्राजञ्शरजालैः समर्पयत् ॥३०॥
transliteration: tāṁstu sarvānnaraśreṣṭhaḥ sarvato vicaranbalī ,alātacakravadrājañśarajālaiḥ samarpayat.
english: But that powerful best of men, O King, moving everywhere like a whirling firebrand, overwhelmed all of them with barrages of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 76 Verse 30. But that powerful best of men, O King, moving everywhere like a whirling firebrand, overwhelmed all of them with barrages of arrows.

sanskrit: तदिन्द्रजालप्रतिमं बाणजालममित्रहा ।व्यसृजद्दिक्षु सर्वासु महेन्द्र इव वज्रभृत् ॥३१॥
transliteration: tadindrajālapratimaṁ bāṇajālamamitrahā ,vyasṛjaddikṣu sarvāsu mahendra iva vajrabhṛt.
english: The slayer of enemies unleashed that network of arrows, which appeared like a magical illusion, in all directions, just as the great Indra, the wielder of the thunderbolt, does.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 76 Verse 31. The slayer of enemies unleashed that network of arrows, which appeared like a magical illusion, in all directions, just as the great Indra, the wielder of the thunderbolt, does.

sanskrit: मेघजालनिभं सैन्यं विदार्य स रविप्रभः ।विबभौ कौरवश्रेष्ठः शरदीव दिवाकरः ॥३२॥
transliteration: meghajālanibhaṁ sainyaṁ vidārya sa raviprabhaḥ ,vibabhau kauravaśreṣṭhaḥ śaradīva divākaraḥ.
english: Having torn apart the army which resembled a mass of clouds, that best of the Kurus, radiant like the sun, shone brilliantly, just like the sun in autumn.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 76 Verse 32. Having torn apart the army which resembled a mass of clouds, that best of the Kurus, radiant like the sun, shone brilliantly, just like the sun in autumn.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततो गाण्डीवभृच्छूरो युद्धाय समवस्थितः ।विबभौ युधि दुर्धर्षो हिमवानचलो यथा ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tato gāṇḍīvabhṛcchūro yuddhāya samavasthitaḥ ,vibabhau yudhi durdharṣo himavānacalo yathā.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then, the heroic wielder of the Gaṇḍīva bow, standing ready for battle, shone splendidly in combat, unconquerable like the Himālaya.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Then, the heroic wielder of the Gaṇḍīva bow, standing ready for battle, shone splendidly in combat, unconquerable like the Himālaya.

sanskrit: ततः सैन्धवयोधास्ते पुनरेव व्यवस्थिताः ।विमुञ्चन्तः सुसंरब्धाः शरवर्षाणि भारत ॥२॥
transliteration: tataḥ saindhavayodhāste punareva vyavasthitāḥ ,vimuñcantaḥ susaṁrabdhāḥ śaravarṣāṇi bhārata.
english: Then, those Saindhava warriors, greatly enraged, positioned themselves once again, showering arrows, O Bhārata.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 2. Then, those Saindhava warriors, greatly enraged, positioned themselves once again, showering arrows, O Bhārata.

sanskrit: तान्प्रसह्य महावीर्यः पुनरेव व्यवस्थितान् ।ततः प्रोवाच कौन्तेयो मुमूर्षूञ्श्लक्ष्णया गिरा ॥३॥
transliteration: tānprasahya mahāvīryaḥ punareva vyavasthitān ,tataḥ provāca kaunteyo mumūrṣūñślakṣṇayā girā.
