english: Having overcome those who had again positioned themselves, the greatly valorous son of Kuntī (Arjuna) then spoke in a gentle voice to those desiring to die.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 3. Having overcome those who had again positioned themselves, the greatly valorous son of Kuntī (Arjuna) then spoke in a gentle voice to those desiring to die.

sanskrit: युध्यध्वं परया शक्त्या यतध्वं च वधे मम ।कुरुध्वं सर्वकार्याणि महद्वो भयमागतम् ॥४॥
transliteration: yudhyadhvaṁ parayā śaktyā yatadhvaṁ ca vadhe mama ,kurudhvaṁ sarvakāryāṇi mahadvo bhayamāgatam.
english: Fight with supreme power (śakti), and strive for my death. Accomplish all your tasks, for a great fear has come upon you.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 4. Fight with supreme power (śakti), and strive for my death. Accomplish all your tasks, for a great fear has come upon you.

sanskrit: एष योत्स्यामि वः सर्वान्निवार्य शरवागुराम् ।तिष्ठध्वं युद्धमनसो दर्पं विनयितास्मि वः ॥५॥
transliteration: eṣa yotsyāmi vaḥ sarvānnivārya śaravāgurām ,tiṣṭhadhvaṁ yuddhamanaso darpaṁ vinayitāsmi vaḥ.
english: I will fight all of you, having first warded off your net of arrows. Stand firm, you whose minds are set on battle! I will humble your arrogance.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 5. I will fight all of you, having first warded off your net of arrows. Stand firm, you whose minds are set on battle! I will humble your arrogance.

sanskrit: एतावदुक्त्वा कौरव्यो रुषा गाण्डीवभृत्तदा ।ततोऽथ वचनं स्मृत्वा भ्रातुर्ज्येष्ठस्य भारत ॥६॥
transliteration: etāvaduktvā kauravyo ruṣā gāṇḍīvabhṛttadā ,tato'tha vacanaṁ smṛtvā bhrāturjyeṣṭhasya bhārata.
english: Having said this much, the Kuru prince, Arjuna, the wielder of the Gāṇḍīva bow, then, filled with anger, O Bhārata, remembered the words of his eldest brother.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 6. Having said this much, the Kuru prince, Arjuna, the wielder of the Gāṇḍīva bow, then, filled with anger, O Bhārata, remembered the words of his eldest brother.

sanskrit: न हन्तव्या रणे तात क्षत्रिया विजिगीषवः ।जेतव्याश्चेति यत्प्रोक्तं धर्मराज्ञा महात्मना ।चिन्तयामास च तदा फल्गुनः पुरुषर्षभः ॥७॥
transliteration: na hantavyā raṇe tāta kṣatriyā vijigīṣavaḥ ,jetavyāśceti yatproktaṁ dharmarājñā mahātmanā ,cintayāmāsa ca tadā phalgunaḥ puruṣarṣabhaḥ.
english: Then Arjuna, that best of men, reflected on what the great-souled Dharmarāja (Yudhiṣṭhira) had said: 'My dear, Kṣatriyas who desire victory should not be killed in battle; rather, they should be subdued.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 7. Then Arjuna, that best of men, reflected on what the great-souled Dharmarāja (Yudhiṣṭhira) had said: 'My dear, Kṣatriyas who desire victory should not be killed in battle; rather, they should be subdued.'

sanskrit: इत्युक्तोऽहं नरेन्द्रेण न हन्तव्या नृपा इति ।कथं तन्न मृषेह स्याद्धर्मराजवचः शुभम् ॥८॥
transliteration: ityukto'haṁ narendreṇa na hantavyā nṛpā iti ,kathaṁ tanna mṛṣeha syāddharmarājavacaḥ śubham.
english: I was thus told by the king, 'Kings should not be killed.' How then could that auspicious statement of Dharmarāja (Yudhiṣṭhira) prove false in this situation?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 8. I was thus told by the king, 'Kings should not be killed.' How then could that auspicious statement of Dharmarāja (Yudhiṣṭhira) prove false in this situation?

sanskrit: न हन्येरंश्च राजानो राज्ञश्चाज्ञा कृता भवेत् ।इति संचिन्त्य स तदा भ्रातुः प्रियहिते रतः ।प्रोवाच वाक्यं धर्मज्ञः सैन्धवान्युद्धदुर्मदान् ॥९॥
transliteration: na hanyeraṁśca rājāno rājñaścājñā kṛtā bhavet ,iti saṁcintya sa tadā bhrātuḥ priyahite rataḥ ,provāca vākyaṁ dharmajñaḥ saindhavānyuddhadurmadān.
english: Kings should not be killed, and the king's command should be obeyed. Reflecting thus, he, intent on his brother's welfare, then, being one who understood natural law (dharma), spoke these words to the Saindhavas, who were intoxicated with war.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 9. Kings should not be killed, and the king's command should be obeyed. Reflecting thus, he, intent on his brother's welfare, then, being one who understood natural law (dharma), spoke these words to the Saindhavas, who were intoxicated with war.

sanskrit: बालान्स्त्रियो वा युष्माकं न हनिष्ये व्यवस्थितान् ।यश्च वक्ष्यति संग्रामे तवास्मीति पराजितः ॥१०॥
transliteration: bālānstriyo vā yuṣmākaṁ na haniṣye vyavasthitān ,yaśca vakṣyati saṁgrāme tavāsmīti parājitaḥ.
english: I will not kill your children or women who remain (as non-combatants). And whoever, defeated in battle, will say, 'I am yours,' (him too I will spare).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 10. I will not kill your children or women who remain (as non-combatants). And whoever, defeated in battle, will say, 'I am yours,' (him too I will spare).

sanskrit: एतच्छ्रुत्वा वचो मह्यं कुरुध्वं हितमात्मनः ।अतोऽन्यथा कृच्छ्रगता भविष्यथ मयार्दिताः ॥११॥
transliteration: etacchrutvā vaco mahyaṁ kurudhvaṁ hitamātmanaḥ ,ato'nyathā kṛcchragatā bhaviṣyatha mayārditāḥ.
english: Having heard these words of mine, do what is beneficial for your own self (ātman). Otherwise, you will fall into distress, afflicted by me.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 11. Having heard these words of mine, do what is beneficial for your own self (ātman). Otherwise, you will fall into distress, afflicted by me.

sanskrit: एवमुक्त्वा तु तान्वीरान्युयुधे कुरुपुंगवः ।अत्वरावानसंरब्धः संरब्धैर्विजिगीषुभिः ॥१२॥
transliteration: evamuktvā tu tānvīrānyuyudhe kurupuṁgavaḥ ,atvarāvānasaṁrabdhaḥ saṁrabdhairvijigīṣubhiḥ.
english: Having spoken thus, the foremost of the Kurus then fought those heroes. He was unhurried and unagitated, (while fighting) those who were agitated and desirous of victory.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 12. Having spoken thus, the foremost of the Kurus then fought those heroes. He was unhurried and unagitated, (while fighting) those who were agitated and desirous of victory.

sanskrit: ततः शतसहस्राणि शराणां नतपर्वणाम् ।मुमुचुः सैन्धवा राजंस्तदा गाण्डीवधन्वनि ॥१३॥
transliteration: tataḥ śatasahasrāṇi śarāṇāṁ nataparvaṇām ,mumucuḥ saindhavā rājaṁstadā gāṇḍīvadhanvani.
english: Then, O King, the Saindhavas (warriors from Sindh) at that moment released hundreds of thousands of straight-flying arrows upon the wielder of the Gāṇḍīva bow (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 13. Then, O King, the Saindhavas (warriors from Sindh) at that moment released hundreds of thousands of straight-flying arrows upon the wielder of the Gāṇḍīva bow (Arjuna).

sanskrit: स तानापततः क्रूरानाशीविषविषोपमान् ।चिच्छेद निशितैर्बाणैरन्तरैव धनंजयः ॥१४॥
transliteration: sa tānāpatataḥ krūrānāśīviṣaviṣopamān ,ciccheda niśitairbāṇairantaraiva dhanaṁjayaḥ.
english: Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna) cut down those fierce, approaching arrows, which resembled the venom of poisonous snakes, with his sharp arrows while they were still in the air.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 14. Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna) cut down those fierce, approaching arrows, which resembled the venom of poisonous snakes, with his sharp arrows while they were still in the air.

sanskrit: छित्त्वा तु तानाशुगमान्कङ्कपत्राञ्शिलाशितान् ।एकैकमेष दशभिर्बिभेद समरे शरैः ॥१५॥
transliteration: chittvā tu tānāśugamānkaṅkapatrāñśilāśitān ,ekaikameṣa daśabhirbibheda samare śaraiḥ.
english: Having indeed cut down those swift-flying arrows, which were feathered with heron plumes and sharpened on stone, he (Arjuna) then pierced each one (of the Saindhava warriors) with ten arrows in that battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 15. Having indeed cut down those swift-flying arrows, which were feathered with heron plumes and sharpened on stone, he (Arjuna) then pierced each one (of the Saindhava warriors) with ten arrows in that battle.

sanskrit: ततः प्रासांश्च शक्तीश्च पुनरेव धनंजये ।जयद्रथं हतं स्मृत्वा चिक्षिपुः सैन्धवा नृपाः ॥१६॥
transliteration: tataḥ prāsāṁśca śaktīśca punareva dhanaṁjaye ,jayadrathaṁ hataṁ smṛtvā cikṣipuḥ saindhavā nṛpāḥ.
english: Then, remembering that Jayadratha had been slain, the Saindhava kings again hurled spears and lances upon Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 16. Then, remembering that Jayadratha had been slain, the Saindhava kings again hurled spears and lances upon Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna).

sanskrit: तेषां किरीटी संकल्पं मोघं चक्रे महामनाः ।सर्वांस्तानन्तरा छित्त्वा मुदा चुक्रोश पाण्डवः ॥१७॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ kirīṭī saṁkalpaṁ moghaṁ cakre mahāmanāḥ ,sarvāṁstānantarā chittvā mudā cukrośa pāṇḍavaḥ.
english: The great-souled (mahāmanāḥ) Arjuna, the crowned one, made their resolve futile. Having pierced through all of them, the Pāṇḍava warrior shouted with delight.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 17. The great-souled (mahāmanāḥ) Arjuna, the crowned one, made their resolve futile. Having pierced through all of them, the Pāṇḍava warrior shouted with delight.

sanskrit: तथैवापततां तेषां योधानां जयगृद्धिनाम् ।शिरांसि पातयामास भल्लैः संनतपर्वभिः ॥१८॥
transliteration: tathaivāpatatāṁ teṣāṁ yodhānāṁ jayagṛddhinām ,śirāṁsi pātayāmāsa bhallaiḥ saṁnataparvabhiḥ.
english: Similarly, he caused the heads of those attacking warriors, who were eager for victory, to fall with his sharp, broad-headed arrows (bhallaiḥ).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 18. Similarly, he caused the heads of those attacking warriors, who were eager for victory, to fall with his sharp, broad-headed arrows (bhallaiḥ).

sanskrit: तेषां प्रद्रवतां चैव पुनरेव च धावताम् ।निवर्ततां च शब्दोऽभूत्पूर्णस्येव महोदधेः ॥१९॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ pradravatāṁ caiva punareva ca dhāvatām ,nivartatāṁ ca śabdo'bhūtpūrṇasyeva mahodadheḥ.
english: A roar arose from them – from those fleeing, and from those rushing forward again, and from those turning back – like the sound of a full, great ocean.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 19. A roar arose from them – from those fleeing, and from those rushing forward again, and from those turning back – like the sound of a full, great ocean.

sanskrit: ते वध्यमानास्तु तदा पार्थेनामिततेजसा ।यथाप्राणं यथोत्साहं योधयामासुरर्जुनम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: te vadhyamānāstu tadā pārthenāmitatejasā ,yathāprāṇaṁ yathotsāhaṁ yodhayāmāsurarjunam.
english: But they, being slain at that time by Arjuna (Pārtha) of immeasurable prowess, fought him with all their strength and zeal.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 20. But they, being slain at that time by Arjuna (Pārtha) of immeasurable prowess, fought him with all their strength and zeal.

sanskrit: ततस्ते फल्गुनेनाजौ शरैः संनतपर्वभिः ।कृता विसंज्ञा भूयिष्ठाः क्लान्तवाहनसैनिकाः ॥२१॥
transliteration: tataste phalgunenājau śaraiḥ saṁnataparvabhiḥ ,kṛtā visaṁjñā bhūyiṣṭhāḥ klāntavāhanasainikāḥ.
english: Then, in battle, a great many of those warriors, whose mounts and soldiers were exhausted, were rendered unconscious by Arjuna with his straight-shafted arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 21. Then, in battle, a great many of those warriors, whose mounts and soldiers were exhausted, were rendered unconscious by Arjuna with his straight-shafted arrows.

sanskrit: तांस्तु सर्वान्परिग्लानान्विदित्वा धृतराष्ट्रजा ।दुःशला बालमादाय नप्तारं प्रययौ तदा ।सुरथस्य सुतं वीरं रथेनानागसं तदा ॥२२॥
transliteration: tāṁstu sarvānpariglānānviditvā dhṛtarāṣṭrajā ,duḥśalā bālamādāya naptāraṁ prayayau tadā ,surathasya sutaṁ vīraṁ rathenānāgasaṁ tadā.
english: Then, realizing that all those warriors were utterly exhausted, Duḥśalā, the daughter of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, took her grandson, the brave and innocent son of Suratha, and set out in her chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 22. Then, realizing that all those warriors were utterly exhausted, Duḥśalā, the daughter of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, took her grandson, the brave and innocent son of Suratha, and set out in her chariot.

sanskrit: शान्त्यर्थं सर्वयोधानामभ्यगच्छत पाण्डवम् ।सा धनंजयमासाद्य मुमोचार्तस्वरं तदा ।धनंजयोऽपि तां दृष्ट्वा धनुर्विससृजे प्रभुः ॥२३॥
transliteration: śāntyarthaṁ sarvayodhānāmabhyagacchata pāṇḍavam ,sā dhanaṁjayamāsādya mumocārtasvaraṁ tadā ,dhanaṁjayo'pi tāṁ dṛṣṭvā dhanurvisasṛje prabhuḥ.
english: For the sake of peace for all the warriors, she (Duḥśalā) approached the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna). Having reached Dhananjaya, she then let out a distressed cry. The lord Dhananjaya, seeing her, also laid down his bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 23. For the sake of peace for all the warriors, she (Duḥśalā) approached the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna). Having reached Dhananjaya, she then let out a distressed cry. The lord Dhananjaya, seeing her, also laid down his bow.

sanskrit: समुत्सृष्टधनुः पार्थो विधिवद्भगिनीं तदा ।प्राह किं करवाणीति सा च तं वाक्यमब्रवीत् ॥२४॥
transliteration: samutsṛṣṭadhanuḥ pārtho vidhivadbhaginīṁ tadā ,prāha kiṁ karavāṇīti sā ca taṁ vākyamabravīt.
english: Then Pārtha (Arjuna), having laid down his bow, respectfully asked his sister (Duḥśalā), "What should I do?" And she spoke these words to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 24. Then Pārtha (Arjuna), having laid down his bow, respectfully asked his sister (Duḥśalā), "What should I do?" And she spoke these words to him.

sanskrit: एष ते भरतश्रेष्ठ स्वस्रीयस्यात्मजः शिशुः ।अभिवादयते वीर तं पश्य पुरुषर्षभ ॥२५॥
transliteration: eṣa te bharataśreṣṭha svasrīyasyātmajaḥ śiśuḥ ,abhivādayate vīra taṁ paśya puruṣarṣabha.
english: O best among Bharatas, O hero, O bull among men! This child, your sister's grandson, salutes you. Please look at him.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 25. O best among Bharatas, O hero, O bull among men! This child, your sister's grandson, salutes you. Please look at him.

sanskrit: इत्युक्तस्तस्य पितरं स पप्रच्छार्जुनस्तदा ।क्वासाविति ततो राजन्दुःशला वाक्यमब्रवीत् ॥२६॥
transliteration: ityuktastasya pitaraṁ sa papracchārjunastadā ,kvāsāviti tato rājanduḥśalā vākyamabravīt.
english: Having been thus addressed, Arjuna then inquired about the child's father, asking, 'Where is he?' Thereupon, O king, Duḥśalā spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 26. Having been thus addressed, Arjuna then inquired about the child's father, asking, 'Where is he?' Thereupon, O king, Duḥśalā spoke these words.

sanskrit: पितृशोकाभिसंतप्तो विषादार्तोऽस्य वै पिता ।पञ्चत्वमगमद्वीर यथा तन्मे निबोध ह ॥२७॥
transliteration: pitṛśokābhisaṁtapto viṣādārto'sya vai pitā ,pañcatvamagamadvīra yathā tanme nibodha ha.
english: O hero, this child's father (Abhimanyu), afflicted by sorrow for his own father (whom he believed to be deceased) and greatly distressed, indeed met his demise. Listen to me now as to how that occurred.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 27. O hero, this child's father (Abhimanyu), afflicted by sorrow for his own father (whom he believed to be deceased) and greatly distressed, indeed met his demise. Listen to me now as to how that occurred.

sanskrit: स पूर्वं पितरं श्रुत्वा हतं युद्धे त्वयानघ ।त्वामागतं च संश्रुत्य युद्धाय हयसारिणम् ।पितुश्च मृत्युदुःखार्तोऽजहात्प्राणान्धनंजय ॥२८॥
transliteration: sa pūrvaṁ pitaraṁ śrutvā hataṁ yuddhe tvayānagha ,tvāmāgataṁ ca saṁśrutya yuddhāya hayasāriṇam ,pituśca mṛtyuduḥkhārto'jahātprāṇāndhanaṁjaya.
english: O sinless one, he (Abhimanyu), having previously heard that a revered elder (lit. 'father') was killed in battle by you, and having also heard that you, a skilled horse-warrior, had arrived for battle, then, distressed by the grief of that father's death, gave up his life, O Dhananjaya (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 28. O sinless one, he (Abhimanyu), having previously heard that a revered elder (lit. 'father') was killed in battle by you, and having also heard that you, a skilled horse-warrior, had arrived for battle, then, distressed by the grief of that father's death, gave up his life, O Dhananjaya (Arjuna).

sanskrit: प्राप्तो बीभत्सुरित्येव नाम श्रुत्वैव तेऽनघ ।विषादार्तः पपातोर्व्यां ममार च ममात्मजः ॥२९॥
transliteration: prāpto bībhatsurityeva nāma śrutvaiva te'nagha ,viṣādārtaḥ papātorvyāṁ mamāra ca mamātmajaḥ.
english: O sinless one, upon merely hearing your name 'Bībhatsu', my son, grief-stricken, fell to the ground and died.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 29. O sinless one, upon merely hearing your name 'Bībhatsu', my son, grief-stricken, fell to the ground and died.

sanskrit: तं तु दृष्ट्वा निपतितं ततस्तस्यात्मजं विभो ।गृहीत्वा समनुप्राप्ता त्वामद्य शरणैषिणी ॥३०॥
transliteration: taṁ tu dṛṣṭvā nipatitaṁ tatastasyātmajaṁ vibho ,gṛhītvā samanuprāptā tvāmadya śaraṇaiṣiṇī.
english: But, O lord (vibho), upon seeing him fallen, and then taking his son, I, seeking refuge, have now come to you.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 30. But, O lord (vibho), upon seeing him fallen, and then taking his son, I, seeking refuge, have now come to you.

sanskrit: इत्युक्त्वार्तस्वरं सा तु मुमोच धृतराष्ट्रजा ।दीना दीनं स्थितं पार्थमब्रवीच्चाप्यधोमुखम् ॥३१॥
transliteration: ityuktvārtasvaraṁ sā tu mumoca dhṛtarāṣṭrajā ,dīnā dīnaṁ sthitaṁ pārthamabravīccāpyadhomukham.
english: Having spoken thus with a sorrowful voice, she, the daughter of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, then uttered (a cry). Distressed herself, she also spoke to Pārtha, who was standing there, pitiable and downcast.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 31. Having spoken thus with a sorrowful voice, she, the daughter of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, then uttered (a cry). Distressed herself, she also spoke to Pārtha, who was standing there, pitiable and downcast.

sanskrit: स्वसारं मामवेक्षस्व स्वस्रीयात्मजमेव च ।कर्तुमर्हसि धर्मज्ञ दयां मयि कुरूद्वह ।विस्मृत्य कुरुराजानं तं च मन्दं जयद्रथम् ॥३२॥
transliteration: svasāraṁ māmavekṣasva svasrīyātmajameva ca ,kartumarhasi dharmajña dayāṁ mayi kurūdvaha ,vismṛtya kururājānaṁ taṁ ca mandaṁ jayadratham.
english: O knower of (dharma), O leader of the Kurus, you ought to show compassion to me, your sister, and also to my son (your nephew), forgetting that foolish Jayadratha and King Duryodhana.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 32. O knower of (dharma), O leader of the Kurus, you ought to show compassion to me, your sister, and also to my son (your nephew), forgetting that foolish Jayadratha and King Duryodhana.

sanskrit: अभिमन्योर्यथा जातः परिक्षित्परवीरहा ।तथायं सुरथाज्जातो मम पौत्रो महाभुज ॥३३॥
transliteration: abhimanyoryathā jātaḥ parikṣitparavīrahā ,tathāyaṁ surathājjāto mama pautro mahābhuja.
english: Just as Parikshit, the slayer of enemy warriors, was born from Abhimanyu, so too is this mighty-armed grandson of mine born from Suratha.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 33. Just as Parikshit, the slayer of enemy warriors, was born from Abhimanyu, so too is this mighty-armed grandson of mine born from Suratha.

sanskrit: तमादाय नरव्याघ्र संप्राप्तास्मि तवान्तिकम् ।शमार्थं सर्वयोधानां शृणु चेदं वचो मम ॥३४॥
transliteration: tamādāya naravyāghra saṁprāptāsmi tavāntikam ,śamārthaṁ sarvayodhānāṁ śṛṇu cedaṁ vaco mama.
english: Having brought him, O tiger among men, I have come near you for the sake of peace for all warriors. Now, listen to these words of mine.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 34. Having brought him, O tiger among men, I have come near you for the sake of peace for all warriors. Now, listen to these words of mine.

sanskrit: आगतोऽयं महाबाहो तस्य मन्दस्य पौत्रकः ।प्रसादमस्य बालस्य तस्मात्त्वं कर्तुमर्हसि ॥३५॥
transliteration: āgato'yaṁ mahābāho tasya mandasya pautrakaḥ ,prasādamasya bālasya tasmāttvaṁ kartumarhasi.
english: This grandson of that foolish one has arrived, O mighty-armed one. Therefore, you should show favor to this child.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 35. This grandson of that foolish one has arrived, O mighty-armed one. Therefore, you should show favor to this child.

sanskrit: एष प्रसाद्य शिरसा मया सार्धमरिंदम ।याचते त्वां महाबाहो शमं गच्छ धनंजय ॥३६॥
transliteration: eṣa prasādya śirasā mayā sārdhamariṁdama ,yācate tvāṁ mahābāho śamaṁ gaccha dhanaṁjaya.
english: O subduer of enemies, O mighty-armed one, this child, along with me, bowing his head in supplication, entreats you: 'Attain peace, O Dhananjaya!'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 36. O subduer of enemies, O mighty-armed one, this child, along with me, bowing his head in supplication, entreats you: 'Attain peace, O Dhananjaya!'

sanskrit: बालस्य हतबन्धोश्च पार्थ किंचिदजानतः ।प्रसादं कुरु धर्मज्ञ मा मन्युवशमन्वगाः ॥३७॥
transliteration: bālasya hatabandhośca pārtha kiṁcidajānataḥ ,prasādaṁ kuru dharmajña mā manyuvaśamanvagāḥ.
english: O Pārtha, O knower of intrinsic nature (dharma), show kindness to this boy whose relatives have been killed and who is ignorant. Do not succumb to the power of anger.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 37. O Pārtha, O knower of intrinsic nature (dharma), show kindness to this boy whose relatives have been killed and who is ignorant. Do not succumb to the power of anger.

sanskrit: तमनार्यं नृशंसं च विस्मृत्यास्य पितामहम् ।आगस्कारिणमत्यर्थं प्रसादं कर्तुमर्हसि ॥३८॥
transliteration: tamanāryaṁ nṛśaṁsaṁ ca vismṛtyāsya pitāmaham ,āgaskāriṇamatyarthaṁ prasādaṁ kartumarhasi.
english: You ought to show kindness (prasāda) to him, forgetting his grandfather who was ignoble, cruel, and exceedingly offensive.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 38. You ought to show kindness (prasāda) to him, forgetting his grandfather who was ignoble, cruel, and exceedingly offensive.

sanskrit: एवं ब्रुवत्यां करुणं दुःशलायां धनंजयः ।संस्मृत्य देवीं गान्धारीं धृतराष्ट्रं च पार्थिवम् ।प्रोवाच दुःखशोकार्तः क्षत्रधर्मं विगर्हयन् ॥३९॥
transliteration: evaṁ bruvatyāṁ karuṇaṁ duḥśalāyāṁ dhanaṁjayaḥ ,saṁsmṛtya devīṁ gāndhārīṁ dhṛtarāṣṭraṁ ca pārthivam ,provāca duḥkhaśokārtaḥ kṣatradharmaṁ vigarhayan.
english: While Duḥśalā was speaking so piteously, Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), recalling Queen Gāndhārī and King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, spoke, afflicted by sorrow and grief, condemning the warrior's intrinsic nature (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 39. While Duḥśalā was speaking so piteously, Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), recalling Queen Gāndhārī and King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, spoke, afflicted by sorrow and grief, condemning the warrior's intrinsic nature (dharma).

sanskrit: धिक्तं दुर्योधनं क्षुद्रं राज्यलुब्धं च मानिनम् ।यत्कृते बान्धवाः सर्वे मया नीता यमक्षयम् ॥४०॥
transliteration: dhiktaṁ duryodhanaṁ kṣudraṁ rājyalubdhaṁ ca māninam ,yatkṛte bāndhavāḥ sarve mayā nītā yamakṣayam.
english: Shame on that wretched, kingdom-greedy, and arrogant Duryodhana! Because of him, all my relatives were led by me to the abode of Yama (the god of death).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 40. Shame on that wretched, kingdom-greedy, and arrogant Duryodhana! Because of him, all my relatives were led by me to the abode of Yama (the god of death).

sanskrit: इत्युक्त्वा बहु सान्त्वादि प्रसादमकरोज्जयः ।परिष्वज्य च तां प्रीतो विससर्ज गृहान्प्रति ॥४१॥
transliteration: ityuktvā bahu sāntvādi prasādamakarojjayaḥ ,pariṣvajya ca tāṁ prīto visasarja gṛhānprati.
english: Having said this, Jaya, much pleased, offered her many consolations and showed his favor. Then, embracing her, he sent her back to her home.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 41. Having said this, Jaya, much pleased, offered her many consolations and showed his favor. Then, embracing her, he sent her back to her home.

sanskrit: दुःशला चापि तान्योधान्निवार्य महतो रणात् ।संपूज्य पार्थं प्रययौ गृहान्प्रति शुभानना ॥४२॥
transliteration: duḥśalā cāpi tānyodhānnivārya mahato raṇāt ,saṁpūjya pārthaṁ prayayau gṛhānprati śubhānanā.
english: And Duḥśalā, the fair-faced one, having prevented those warriors from the great battle and having honored Pārtha (Arjuna), returned to her home.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 42. And Duḥśalā, the fair-faced one, having prevented those warriors from the great battle and having honored Pārtha (Arjuna), returned to her home.

sanskrit: ततः सैन्धवकान्योधान्विनिर्जित्य नरर्षभः ।पुनरेवान्वधावत्स तं हयं कामचारिणम् ॥४३॥
transliteration: tataḥ saindhavakānyodhānvinirjitya nararṣabhaḥ ,punarevānvadhāvatsa taṁ hayaṁ kāmacāriṇam.
english: Then, the best of men (Arjuna), having completely vanquished the Sindhu warriors, again pursued that horse, which was free to roam at will.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 43. Then, the best of men (Arjuna), having completely vanquished the Sindhu warriors, again pursued that horse, which was free to roam at will.

sanskrit: ससार यज्ञियं वीरो विधिवत्स विशां पते ।तारामृगमिवाकाशे देवदेवः पिनाकधृक् ॥४४॥
transliteration: sasāra yajñiyaṁ vīro vidhivatsa viśāṁ pate ,tārāmṛgamivākāśe devadevaḥ pinākadhṛk.
english: O Lord of the people, that hero (Arjuna) moved along with the horse pertaining to the (Vedic ritual) yajña, according to the sacred injunctions, just as the god of gods, Pinākadhṛk (Śiva), pursues the constellation of the deer (Orion) in the sky.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 44. O Lord of the people, that hero (Arjuna) moved along with the horse pertaining to the (Vedic ritual) yajña, according to the sacred injunctions, just as the god of gods, Pinākadhṛk (Śiva), pursues the constellation of the deer (Orion) in the sky.

sanskrit: स च वाजी यथेष्टेन तांस्तान्देशान्यथासुखम् ।विचचार यथाकामं कर्म पार्थस्य वर्धयन् ॥४५॥
transliteration: sa ca vājī yatheṣṭena tāṁstāndeśānyathāsukham ,vicacāra yathākāmaṁ karma pārthasya vardhayan.
english: And that horse wandered freely, at its will and comfort, through those various regions, thereby increasing Pārtha's (Arjuna's) deed (karma).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 45. And that horse wandered freely, at its will and comfort, through those various regions, thereby increasing Pārtha's (Arjuna's) deed (karma).

sanskrit: क्रमेण स हयस्त्वेवं विचरन्भरतर्षभ ।मणिपूरपतेर्देशमुपायात्सहपाण्डवः ॥४६॥
transliteration: krameṇa sa hayastvevaṁ vicaranbharatarṣabha ,maṇipūrapaterdeśamupāyātsahapāṇḍavaḥ.
english: O best of Bharatas, in due course, that horse, thus wandering, Arjuna, accompanied by (his retinue of) Pāṇḍavas, reached the country of the lord of Maṇipūra.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 77 Verse 46. O best of Bharatas, in due course, that horse, thus wandering, Arjuna, accompanied by (his retinue of) Pāṇḍavas, reached the country of the lord of Maṇipūra.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।श्रुत्वा तु नृपतिर्वीरं पितरं बभ्रुवाहनः ।निर्ययौ विनयेनार्यो ब्राह्मणार्घ्यपुरःसरः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,śrutvā tu nṛpatirvīraṁ pitaraṁ babhruvāhanaḥ ,niryayau vinayenāryo brāhmaṇārghyapuraḥsaraḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Babhruvāhana, the noble king, having heard of his heroic father, went out respectfully, preceded by Brahmins carrying offerings.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 78 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Babhruvāhana, the noble king, having heard of his heroic father, went out respectfully, preceded by Brahmins carrying offerings.

sanskrit: मणिपूरेश्वरं त्वेवमुपयातं धनंजयः ।नाभ्यनन्दत मेधावी क्षत्रधर्ममनुस्मरन् ॥२॥
transliteration: maṇipūreśvaraṁ tvevamupayātaṁ dhanaṁjayaḥ ,nābhyanandata medhāvī kṣatradharmamanusmaran.
english: But Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), sagacious and remembering the duty (dharma) of a warrior, did not welcome the lord of Maṇipūra (Babhruvāhana) who had thus approached.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 78 Verse 2. But Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), sagacious and remembering the duty (dharma) of a warrior, did not welcome the lord of Maṇipūra (Babhruvāhana) who had thus approached.

sanskrit: उवाच चैनं धर्मात्मा समन्युः फल्गुनस्तदा ।प्रक्रियेयं न ते युक्ता बहिस्त्वं क्षत्रधर्मतः ॥३॥
transliteration: uvāca cainaṁ dharmātmā samanyuḥ phalgunastadā ,prakriyeyaṁ na te yuktā bahistvaṁ kṣatradharmataḥ.
english: Then, the righteous (dharmātmā) Arjuna (phalguna), filled with anger, said to him: 'This action of yours is not proper; you are acting outside the natural law (dharma) of a warrior.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 78 Verse 3. Then, the righteous (dharmātmā) Arjuna (phalguna), filled with anger, said to him: 'This action of yours is not proper; you are acting outside the natural law (dharma) of a warrior.'

sanskrit: संरक्ष्यमाणं तुरगं यौधिष्ठिरमुपागतम् ।यज्ञियं विषयान्ते मां नायोत्सीः किं नु पुत्रक ॥४॥
transliteration: saṁrakṣyamāṇaṁ turagaṁ yaudhiṣṭhiramupāgatam ,yajñiyaṁ viṣayānte māṁ nāyotsīḥ kiṁ nu putraka.
english: O son, why then did you not fight me, when Yudhiṣṭhira's horse - which was being protected, designated for the Vedic ritual (yajña), and had arrived at the boundary of your domain - was present?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 78 Verse 4. O son, why then did you not fight me, when Yudhiṣṭhira's horse - which was being protected, designated for the Vedic ritual (yajña), and had arrived at the boundary of your domain - was present?

sanskrit: धिक्त्वामस्तु सुदुर्बुद्धिं क्षत्रधर्माविशारदम् ।यो मां युद्धाय संप्राप्तं साम्नैवाथो त्वमग्रहीः ॥५॥
transliteration: dhiktvāmastu sudurbuddhiṁ kṣatradharmāviśāradam ,yo māṁ yuddhāya saṁprāptaṁ sāmnaivātho tvamagrahīḥ.
english: Shame on you, O exceedingly foolish one, ignorant of the warrior's natural law (dharma)! You, who apprehended me with mere conciliation when I had arrived for battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 78 Verse 5. Shame on you, O exceedingly foolish one, ignorant of the warrior's natural law (dharma)! You, who apprehended me with mere conciliation when I had arrived for battle.

sanskrit: न त्वया पुरुषार्थश्च कश्चिदस्तीह जीवता ।यस्त्वं स्त्रीवद्युधा प्राप्तं साम्ना मां प्रत्यगृह्णथाः ॥६॥
transliteration: na tvayā puruṣārthaśca kaścidastīha jīvatā ,yastvaṁ strīvadyudhā prāptaṁ sāmnā māṁ pratyagṛhṇathāḥ.
english: While you are alive, there is no manly purpose (puruṣārtha) for you here, since you received me - who had come for battle - with conciliation, like a woman.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 78 Verse 6. While you are alive, there is no manly purpose (puruṣārtha) for you here, since you received me - who had come for battle - with conciliation, like a woman.

sanskrit: यद्यहं न्यस्तशस्त्रस्त्वामागच्छेयं सुदुर्मते ।प्रक्रियेयं ततो युक्ता भवेत्तव नराधम ॥७॥
transliteration: yadyahaṁ nyastaśastrastvāmāgaccheyaṁ sudurmate ,prakriyeyaṁ tato yuktā bhavettava narādhama.
english: O extremely wicked one! If I were to approach you having laid down my weapons, then this action of yours, O lowest of men, would be appropriate.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 78 Verse 7. O extremely wicked one! If I were to approach you having laid down my weapons, then this action of yours, O lowest of men, would be appropriate.

sanskrit: तमेवमुक्तं भर्त्रा तु विदित्वा पन्नगात्मजा ।अमृष्यमाणा भित्त्वोर्वीमुलूपी तमुपागमत् ॥८॥
transliteration: tamevamuktaṁ bhartrā tu viditvā pannagātmajā ,amṛṣyamāṇā bhittvorvīmulūpī tamupāgamat.
english: Ulūpī, the daughter of the nāga (pannagātmajā), understanding that he had been spoken to in this manner by her husband, and unable to tolerate it, split the earth and approached him.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 78 Verse 8. Ulūpī, the daughter of the nāga (pannagātmajā), understanding that he had been spoken to in this manner by her husband, and unable to tolerate it, split the earth and approached him.

sanskrit: सा ददर्श ततः पुत्रं विमृशन्तमधोमुखम् ।संतर्ज्यमानमसकृद्भर्त्रा युद्धार्थिना विभो ॥९॥
transliteration: sā dadarśa tataḥ putraṁ vimṛśantamadhomukham ,saṁtarjyamānamasakṛdbhartrā yuddhārthinā vibho.
english: O Lord (vibho)! She then saw her son, who was contemplating with his face cast downwards, being repeatedly reproached by her husband, who was eager for battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 78 Verse 9. O Lord (vibho)! She then saw her son, who was contemplating with his face cast downwards, being repeatedly reproached by her husband, who was eager for battle.

sanskrit: ततः सा चारुसर्वाङ्गी तमुपेत्योरगात्मजा ।उलूपी प्राह वचनं क्षत्रधर्मविशारदा ॥१०॥
transliteration: tataḥ sā cārusarvāṅgī tamupetyoragātmajā ,ulūpī prāha vacanaṁ kṣatradharmaviśāradā.
english: Then, she, Ulūpī, the beautiful-limbed daughter of the serpent (uragātmajā), who was an expert in the natural law of warriors (kṣatradharma), approached him and spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 78 Verse 10. Then, she, Ulūpī, the beautiful-limbed daughter of the serpent (uragātmajā), who was an expert in the natural law of warriors (kṣatradharma), approached him and spoke these words.

sanskrit: उलूपीं मां निबोध त्वं मातरं पन्नगात्मजाम् ।कुरुष्व वचनं पुत्र धर्मस्ते भविता परः ॥११॥
transliteration: ulūpīṁ māṁ nibodha tvaṁ mātaraṁ pannagātmajām ,kuruṣva vacanaṁ putra dharmaste bhavitā paraḥ.
english: Son, know me, Ulupi, your mother, the daughter of the serpents. Obey my instruction; your righteousness (dharma) will be paramount.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 78 Verse 11. Son, know me, Ulupi, your mother, the daughter of the serpents. Obey my instruction; your righteousness (dharma) will be paramount.

sanskrit: युध्यस्वैनं कुरुश्रेष्ठं धनंजयमरिंदम ।एवमेष हि ते प्रीतो भविष्यति न संशयः ॥१२॥
transliteration: yudhyasvainaṁ kuruśreṣṭhaṁ dhanaṁjayamariṁdama ,evameṣa hi te prīto bhaviṣyati na saṁśayaḥ.
english: Fight him, this best of Kurus, Dhananjaya, the subduer of enemies! In this way, he will indeed be pleased with you, without a doubt.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 78 Verse 12. Fight him, this best of Kurus, Dhananjaya, the subduer of enemies! In this way, he will indeed be pleased with you, without a doubt.

sanskrit: एवमुद्धर्षितो मात्रा स राजा बभ्रुवाहनः ।मनश्चक्रे महातेजा युद्धाय भरतर्षभ ॥१३॥
transliteration: evamuddharṣito mātrā sa rājā babhruvāhanaḥ ,manaścakre mahātejā yuddhāya bharatarṣabha.
english: Thus encouraged by his mother, that greatly powerful king Babhruvahana made up his mind for battle, O best of Bharatas.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 78 Verse 13. Thus encouraged by his mother, that greatly powerful king Babhruvahana made up his mind for battle, O best of Bharatas.

sanskrit: संनह्य काञ्चनं वर्म शिरस्त्राणं च भानुमत् ।तूणीरशतसंबाधमारुरोह महारथम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: saṁnahya kāñcanaṁ varma śirastrāṇaṁ ca bhānumat ,tūṇīraśatasaṁbādhamāruroha mahāratham.
english: Having put on his golden armor and a shining helmet, he ascended his great chariot, which was crowded with hundreds of quivers.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 78 Verse 14. Having put on his golden armor and a shining helmet, he ascended his great chariot, which was crowded with hundreds of quivers.

sanskrit: सर्वोपकरणैर्युक्तं युक्तमश्वैर्मनोजवैः ।सुचक्रोपस्करं धीमान्हेमभाण्डपरिष्कृतम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: sarvopakaraṇairyuktaṁ yuktamaśvairmanojavaiḥ ,sucakropaskaraṁ dhīmānhemabhāṇḍapariṣkṛtam.
english: The wise one had a vehicle equipped with all accessories, harnessed with mind-swift horses, having excellent wheels and apparatus, and adorned with golden vessels.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 78 Verse 15. The wise one had a vehicle equipped with all accessories, harnessed with mind-swift horses, having excellent wheels and apparatus, and adorned with golden vessels.

sanskrit: परमार्चितमुच्छ्रित्य ध्वजं सिंहं हिरण्मयम् ।प्रययौ पार्थमुद्दिश्य स राजा बभ्रुवाहनः ॥१६॥
transliteration: paramārcitamucchritya dhvajaṁ siṁhaṁ hiraṇmayam ,prayayau pārthamuddiśya sa rājā babhruvāhanaḥ.
english: That King Babhruvahana, having hoisted a highly revered, golden lion-emblemed banner, proceeded, aiming for Pārtha (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 78 Verse 16. That King Babhruvahana, having hoisted a highly revered, golden lion-emblemed banner, proceeded, aiming for Pārtha (Arjuna).

sanskrit: ततोऽभ्येत्य हयं वीरो यज्ञियं पार्थरक्षितम् ।ग्राहयामास पुरुषैर्हयशिक्षाविशारदैः ॥१७॥
transliteration: tato'bhyetya hayaṁ vīro yajñiyaṁ pārtharakṣitam ,grāhayāmāsa puruṣairhayaśikṣāviśāradaiḥ.
english: Then the hero (Babhruvahana), having approached the horse that was intended for the (Vedic ritual) yajña and protected by Pārtha (Arjuna), had it seized by men skilled in horse training.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 78 Verse 17. Then the hero (Babhruvahana), having approached the horse that was intended for the (Vedic ritual) yajña and protected by Pārtha (Arjuna), had it seized by men skilled in horse training.

sanskrit: गृहीतं वाजिनं दृष्ट्वा प्रीतात्मा स धनंजयः ।पुत्रं रथस्थं भूमिष्ठः संन्यवारयदाहवे ॥१८॥
transliteration: gṛhītaṁ vājinaṁ dṛṣṭvā prītātmā sa dhanaṁjayaḥ ,putraṁ rathasthaṁ bhūmiṣṭhaḥ saṁnyavārayadāhave.
english: Having seen the horse seized, that joyful Dhananjaya (Arjuna), standing on the ground, restrained his son, who was in the chariot, from battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 78 Verse 18. Having seen the horse seized, that joyful Dhananjaya (Arjuna), standing on the ground, restrained his son, who was in the chariot, from battle.

sanskrit: ततः स राजा तं वीरं शरव्रातैः सहस्रशः ।अर्दयामास निशितैराशीविषविषोपमैः ॥१९॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa rājā taṁ vīraṁ śaravrātaiḥ sahasraśaḥ ,ardayāmāsa niśitairāśīviṣaviṣopamaiḥ.
english: Then that king tormented that hero with thousands of sharp arrows, which were comparable to the venom of venomous snakes.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 78 Verse 19. Then that king tormented that hero with thousands of sharp arrows, which were comparable to the venom of venomous snakes.

sanskrit: तयोः समभवद्युद्धं पितुः पुत्रस्य चातुलम् ।देवासुररणप्रख्यमुभयोः प्रीयमाणयोः ॥२०॥
transliteration: tayoḥ samabhavadyuddhaṁ pituḥ putrasya cātulam ,devāsuraraṇaprakhyamubhayoḥ prīyamāṇayoḥ.
english: An unparalleled battle (yuddha) took place between the father and the son. It resembled the conflict between the gods and asuras, with both of them delighting in the fight.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 78 Verse 20. An unparalleled battle (yuddha) took place between the father and the son. It resembled the conflict between the gods and asuras, with both of them delighting in the fight.

sanskrit: किरीटिनं तु विव्याध शरेण नतपर्वणा ।जत्रुदेशे नरव्याघ्रः प्रहसन्बभ्रुवाहनः ॥२१॥
transliteration: kirīṭinaṁ tu vivyādha śareṇa nataparvaṇā ,jatrudeśe naravyāghraḥ prahasanbabhruvāhanaḥ.
english: Babhruvāhana, a tiger among men, smiling, pierced the crowned one (Arjuna) in the collarbone region with a bent-jointed arrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 78 Verse 21. Babhruvāhana, a tiger among men, smiling, pierced the crowned one (Arjuna) in the collarbone region with a bent-jointed arrow.

sanskrit: सोऽभ्यगात्सह पुङ्खेन वल्मीकमिव पन्नगः ।विनिर्भिद्य च कौन्तेयं महीतलमथाविशत् ॥२२॥
transliteration: so'bhyagātsaha puṅkhena valmīkamiva pannagaḥ ,vinirbhidya ca kaunteyaṁ mahītalamathāviśat.
english: That arrow, having completely pierced Kaunteya (Arjuna), then entered the surface of the earth (mahītala) with its fletching, just as a snake enters an anthill.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 78 Verse 22. That arrow, having completely pierced Kaunteya (Arjuna), then entered the surface of the earth (mahītala) with its fletching, just as a snake enters an anthill.

sanskrit: स गाढवेदनो धीमानालम्ब्य धनुरुत्तमम् ।दिव्यं तेजः समाविश्य प्रमीत इव संबभौ ॥२३॥
transliteration: sa gāḍhavedano dhīmānālambya dhanuruttamam ,divyaṁ tejaḥ samāviśya pramīta iva saṁbabhau.
english: Deeply pained, the wise hero, leaning upon his excellent bow and imbued with a divine radiance, appeared as if dead.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 78 Verse 23. Deeply pained, the wise hero, leaning upon his excellent bow and imbued with a divine radiance, appeared as if dead.

sanskrit: स संज्ञामुपलभ्याथ प्रशस्य पुरुषर्षभः ।पुत्रं शक्रात्मजो वाक्यमिदमाह महीपते ॥२४॥
transliteration: sa saṁjñāmupalabhyātha praśasya puruṣarṣabhaḥ ,putraṁ śakrātmajo vākyamidamāha mahīpate.
english: O king, having then regained consciousness, that foremost among men, Arjuna (śakrāātmaja), praised his son and spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 78 Verse 24. O king, having then regained consciousness, that foremost among men, Arjuna (śakrāātmaja), praised his son and spoke these words.

sanskrit: साधु साधु महाबाहो वत्स चित्राङ्गदात्मज ।सदृशं कर्म ते दृष्ट्वा प्रीतिमानस्मि पुत्रक ॥२५॥
transliteration: sādhu sādhu mahābāho vatsa citrāṅgadātmaja ,sadṛśaṁ karma te dṛṣṭvā prītimānasmi putraka.
english: Excellent, excellent, O mighty-armed one, my child, son of Citrāṅgadā! Having witnessed your action (karma), I am greatly pleased, dear son.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 78 Verse 25. Excellent, excellent, O mighty-armed one, my child, son of Citrāṅgadā! Having witnessed your action (karma), I am greatly pleased, dear son.

sanskrit: विमुञ्चाम्येष बाणांस्ते पुत्र युद्धे स्थिरो भव ।इत्येवमुक्त्वा नाराचैरभ्यवर्षदमित्रहा ॥२६॥
transliteration: vimuñcāmyeṣa bāṇāṁste putra yuddhe sthiro bhava ,ityevamuktvā nārācairabhyavarṣadamitrahā.
english: O son, I unleash these arrows upon you; remain steadfast in battle! Having spoken thus, the destroyer of enemies showered [him] with iron arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 78 Verse 26. O son, I unleash these arrows upon you; remain steadfast in battle! Having spoken thus, the destroyer of enemies showered [him] with iron arrows.

sanskrit: तान्स गाण्डीवनिर्मुक्तान्वज्राशनिसमप्रभान् ।नाराचैरच्छिनद्राजा सर्वानेव त्रिधा त्रिधा ॥२७॥
transliteration: tānsa gāṇḍīvanirmuktānvajrāśanisamaprabhān ,nārācairacchinadrājā sarvāneva tridhā tridhā.
english: The king, with his arrows, cut all those arrows  -  released from the Gāṇḍīva bow and shining like thunderbolts  -  each into three pieces.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 78 Verse 27. The king, with his arrows, cut all those arrows  -  released from the Gāṇḍīva bow and shining like thunderbolts  -  each into three pieces.

sanskrit: तस्य पार्थः शरैर्दिव्यैर्ध्वजं हेमपरिष्कृतम् ।सुवर्णतालप्रतिमं क्षुरेणापाहरद्रथात् ॥२८॥
transliteration: tasya pārthaḥ śarairdivyairdhvajaṁ hemapariṣkṛtam ,suvarṇatālapratimaṁ kṣureṇāpāharadrathāt.
english: Arjuna (Pārtha), using divine arrows, severed from the chariot the king's gold-adorned banner, which resembled a golden palm tree, by means of a razor-sharp arrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 78 Verse 28. Arjuna (Pārtha), using divine arrows, severed from the chariot the king's gold-adorned banner, which resembled a golden palm tree, by means of a razor-sharp arrow.

sanskrit: हयांश्चास्य महाकायान्महावेगपराक्रमान् ।चकार राज्ञो निर्जीवान्प्रहसन्पाण्डवर्षभः ॥२९॥
transliteration: hayāṁścāsya mahākāyānmahāvegaparākramān ,cakāra rājño nirjīvānprahasanpāṇḍavarṣabhaḥ.
english: The chief of the Pāṇḍavas, laughing, rendered lifeless the king's massive horses, which possessed great speed and might.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 78 Verse 29. The chief of the Pāṇḍavas, laughing, rendered lifeless the king's massive horses, which possessed great speed and might.

sanskrit: स रथादवतीर्याशु राजा परमकोपनः ।पदातिः पितरं कोपाद्योधयामास पाण्डवम् ॥३०॥
transliteration: sa rathādavatīryāśu rājā paramakopanaḥ ,padātiḥ pitaraṁ kopādyodhayāmāsa pāṇḍavam.
english: That exceedingly wrathful king quickly dismounted from his chariot and, fighting on foot, angrily engaged the Pāṇḍava, his father.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 78 Verse 30. That exceedingly wrathful king quickly dismounted from his chariot and, fighting on foot, angrily engaged the Pāṇḍava, his father.

sanskrit: संप्रीयमाणः पाण्डूनामृषभः पुत्रविक्रमात् ।नात्यर्थं पीडयामास पुत्रं वज्रधरात्मजः ॥३१॥
transliteration: saṁprīyamāṇaḥ pāṇḍūnāmṛṣabhaḥ putravikramāt ,nātyarthaṁ pīḍayāmāsa putraṁ vajradharātmajaḥ.
english: Arjuna, the foremost of the Pāṇḍavas and son of Indra, was delighted by his son's valor, and therefore did not excessively torment his son.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 78 Verse 31. Arjuna, the foremost of the Pāṇḍavas and son of Indra, was delighted by his son's valor, and therefore did not excessively torment his son.

sanskrit: स हन्यमानो विमुखं पितरं बभ्रुवाहनः ।शरैराशीविषाकारैः पुनरेवार्दयद्बली ॥३२॥
transliteration: sa hanyamāno vimukhaṁ pitaraṁ babhruvāhanaḥ ,śarairāśīviṣākāraiḥ punarevārdayadbalī.
english: Though being struck, the mighty Babhruvahana, with arrows resembling venomous snakes, again tormented his father who had turned away.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 78 Verse 32. Though being struck, the mighty Babhruvahana, with arrows resembling venomous snakes, again tormented his father who had turned away.

sanskrit: ततः स बाल्यात्पितरं विव्याध हृदि पत्रिणा ।निशितेन सुपुङ्खेन बलवद्बभ्रुवाहनः ॥३३॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa bālyātpitaraṁ vivyādha hṛdi patriṇā ,niśitena supuṅkhena balavadbabhruvāhanaḥ.
english: Then, the powerful Babhruvahana, out of youthful impulse, mightily pierced his father in the heart with a sharp, well-feathered arrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 78 Verse 33. Then, the powerful Babhruvahana, out of youthful impulse, mightily pierced his father in the heart with a sharp, well-feathered arrow.

sanskrit: स बाणस्तेजसा दीप्तो ज्वलन्निव हुताशनः ।विवेश पाण्डवं राजन्मर्म भित्त्वातिदुःखकृत् ॥३४॥
transliteration: sa bāṇastejasā dīpto jvalanniva hutāśanaḥ ,viveśa pāṇḍavaṁ rājanmarma bhittvātiduḥkhakṛt.
english: O King, that arrow, blazing with its power and like a burning fire, having pierced a vital spot, entered the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna), causing him immense pain.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 78 Verse 34. O King, that arrow, blazing with its power and like a burning fire, having pierced a vital spot, entered the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna), causing him immense pain.

sanskrit: स तेनातिभृशं विद्धः पुत्रेण कुरुनन्दनः ।महीं जगाम मोहार्तस्ततो राजन्धनंजयः ॥३५॥
transliteration: sa tenātibhṛśaṁ viddhaḥ putreṇa kurunandanaḥ ,mahīṁ jagāma mohārtastato rājandhanaṁjayaḥ.
english: That Kuru warrior (kurunandana), deeply wounded by his son, fell to the ground, afflicted by bewilderment. Then, O King, (it was the turn of) Arjuna (dhanañjaya).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 78 Verse 35. That Kuru warrior (kurunandana), deeply wounded by his son, fell to the ground, afflicted by bewilderment. Then, O King, (it was the turn of) Arjuna (dhanañjaya).

sanskrit: तस्मिन्निपतिते वीरे कौरवाणां धुरंधरे ।सोऽपि मोहं जगामाशु ततश्चित्राङ्गदासुतः ॥३६॥
transliteration: tasminnipatite vīre kauravāṇāṁ dhuraṁdhare ,so'pi mohaṁ jagāmāśu tataścitrāṅgadāsutaḥ.
english: When that hero, the leader of the Kauravas, had fallen, then Citrāṅgadā's son also swiftly fell into unconsciousness (moha).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 78 Verse 36. When that hero, the leader of the Kauravas, had fallen, then Citrāṅgadā's son also swiftly fell into unconsciousness (moha).

sanskrit: व्यायम्य संयुगे राजा दृष्ट्वा च पितरं हतम् ।पूर्वमेव च बाणौघैर्गाढविद्धोऽर्जुनेन सः ॥३७॥
transliteration: vyāyamya saṁyuge rājā dṛṣṭvā ca pitaraṁ hatam ,pūrvameva ca bāṇaughairgāḍhaviddho'rjunena saḥ.
english: That king, having exerted himself in battle and having seen his father slain, was also already deeply wounded by Arjuna with floods of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 78 Verse 37. That king, having exerted himself in battle and having seen his father slain, was also already deeply wounded by Arjuna with floods of arrows.

sanskrit: भर्तारं निहतं दृष्ट्वा पुत्रं च पतितं भुवि ।चित्राङ्गदा परित्रस्ता प्रविवेश रणाजिरम् ॥३८॥
transliteration: bhartāraṁ nihataṁ dṛṣṭvā putraṁ ca patitaṁ bhuvi ,citrāṅgadā paritrastā praviveśa raṇājiram.
english: Seeing her husband slain and her son fallen on the ground, Citrāṅgadā, utterly terrified, entered the battlefield.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 78 Verse 38. Seeing her husband slain and her son fallen on the ground, Citrāṅgadā, utterly terrified, entered the battlefield.

sanskrit: शोकसंतप्तहृदया रुदती सा ततः शुभा ।मणिपूरपतेर्माता ददर्श निहतं पतिम् ॥३९॥
transliteration: śokasaṁtaptahṛdayā rudatī sā tataḥ śubhā ,maṇipūrapatermātā dadarśa nihataṁ patim.
english: Then, the beautiful mother of the lord of Maṇipūra, her heart tormented by grief, weeping, saw her slain husband.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 78 Verse 39. Then, the beautiful mother of the lord of Maṇipūra, her heart tormented by grief, weeping, saw her slain husband.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततो बहुविधं भीरुर्विलप्य कमलेक्षणा ।मुमोह दुःखाद्दुर्धर्षा निपपात च भूतले ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tato bahuvidhaṁ bhīrurvilapya kamalekṣaṇā ,mumoha duḥkhāddurdharṣā nipapāta ca bhūtale.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Then, the lotus-eyed, fearful woman, who was normally formidable, having lamented extensively, fainted from sorrow and fell to the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 79 Verse 1. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Then, the lotus-eyed, fearful woman, who was normally formidable, having lamented extensively, fainted from sorrow and fell to the ground.

sanskrit: प्रतिलभ्य च सा संज्ञां देवी दिव्यवपुर्धरा ।उलूपीं पन्नगसुतां दृष्ट्वेदं वाक्यमब्रवीत् ॥२॥
transliteration: pratilabhya ca sā saṁjñāṁ devī divyavapurdharā ,ulūpīṁ pannagasutāṁ dṛṣṭvedaṁ vākyamabravīt.
english: And she, the divine lady with a beautiful form, having regained her consciousness, saw Ulūpī, the daughter of the nāgas, and spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 79 Verse 2. And she, the divine lady with a beautiful form, having regained her consciousness, saw Ulūpī, the daughter of the nāgas, and spoke these words.

sanskrit: उलूपि पश्य भर्तारं शयानं निहतं रणे ।त्वत्कृते मम पुत्रेण बालेन समितिंजयम् ॥३॥
transliteration: ulūpi paśya bhartāraṁ śayānaṁ nihataṁ raṇe ,tvatkṛte mama putreṇa bālena samitiṁjayam.
english: O Ulūpī, behold my husband, the conqueror of battles, lying slain on the battlefield by my young son, all for your sake.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 79 Verse 3. O Ulūpī, behold my husband, the conqueror of battles, lying slain on the battlefield by my young son, all for your sake.

sanskrit: ननु त्वमार्ये धर्मज्ञा ननु चासि पतिव्रता ।यत्त्वत्कृतेऽयं पतितः पतिस्ते निहतो रणे ॥४॥
transliteration: nanu tvamārye dharmajñā nanu cāsi pativratā ,yattvatkṛte'yaṁ patitaḥ patiste nihato raṇe.
english: Indeed, you, noble lady (ārye), are knowledgeable in moral law (dharma), and surely you are a devoted wife. Yet, because of you, your husband has fallen and been slain in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 79 Verse 4. Indeed, you, noble lady (ārye), are knowledgeable in moral law (dharma), and surely you are a devoted wife. Yet, because of you, your husband has fallen and been slain in battle.

sanskrit: किं तु सर्वापराधोऽयं यदि तेऽद्य धनंजयः ।क्षमस्व याच्यमाना मे संजीवय धनंजयम् ॥५॥
transliteration: kiṁ tu sarvāparādho'yaṁ yadi te'dya dhanaṁjayaḥ ,kṣamasva yācyamānā me saṁjīvaya dhanaṁjayam.
english: But if this Dhananjaya is guilty of all offenses towards you today, please forgive me, who am begging, and revive Dhananjaya.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 79 Verse 5. But if this Dhananjaya is guilty of all offenses towards you today, please forgive me, who am begging, and revive Dhananjaya.

sanskrit: ननु त्वमार्ये धर्मज्ञा त्रैलोक्यविदिता शुभे ।यद्घातयित्वा भर्तारं पुत्रेणेह न शोचसि ॥६॥
transliteration: nanu tvamārye dharmajñā trailokyaviditā śubhe ,yadghātayitvā bhartāraṁ putreṇeha na śocasi.
english: Indeed, you, noble lady (ārye), are knowledgeable in moral law (dharma) and renowned throughout the three worlds, O auspicious one. Yet, having caused your husband to be slain by your son here, you do not grieve.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 79 Verse 6. Indeed, you, noble lady (ārye), are knowledgeable in moral law (dharma) and renowned throughout the three worlds, O auspicious one. Yet, having caused your husband to be slain by your son here, you do not grieve.

sanskrit: नाहं शोचामि तनयं निहतं पन्नगात्मजे ।पतिमेव तु शोचामि यस्यातिथ्यमिदं कृतम् ॥७॥
transliteration: nāhaṁ śocāmi tanayaṁ nihataṁ pannagātmaje ,patimeva tu śocāmi yasyātithyamidaṁ kṛtam.
english: I do not grieve for my slain son, O daughter of a serpent. However, I grieve for my husband, to whom this hospitality (his death) was rendered.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 79 Verse 7. I do not grieve for my slain son, O daughter of a serpent. However, I grieve for my husband, to whom this hospitality (his death) was rendered.

sanskrit: इत्युक्त्वा सा तदा देवीमुलूपीं पन्नगात्मजाम् ।भर्तारमभिगम्येदमित्युवाच यशस्विनी ॥८॥
transliteration: ityuktvā sā tadā devīmulūpīṁ pannagātmajām ,bhartāramabhigamyedamityuvāca yaśasvinī.
english: Having thus spoken to the goddess Ulūpī, the daughter of the serpents, the glorious (Citrāṅgadā) then approached her husband and spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 79 Verse 8. Having thus spoken to the goddess Ulūpī, the daughter of the serpents, the glorious (Citrāṅgadā) then approached her husband and spoke these words.

sanskrit: उत्तिष्ठ कुरुमुख्यस्य प्रियकाम मम प्रिय ।अयमश्वो महाबाहो मया ते परिमोक्षितः ॥९॥
transliteration: uttiṣṭha kurumukhyasya priyakāma mama priya ,ayamaśvo mahābāho mayā te parimokṣitaḥ.
english: Arise, my beloved, you who seek the welfare of the chief of the Kurus! O mighty-armed one, this horse has been set free by me for you.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 79 Verse 9. Arise, my beloved, you who seek the welfare of the chief of the Kurus! O mighty-armed one, this horse has been set free by me for you.

sanskrit: ननु नाम त्वया वीर धर्मराजस्य यज्ञियः ।अयमश्वोऽनुसर्तव्यः स शेषे किं महीतले ॥१०॥
transliteration: nanu nāma tvayā vīra dharmarājasya yajñiyaḥ ,ayamaśvo'nusartavyaḥ sa śeṣe kiṁ mahītale.
english: O hero, should not this horse, designated for the (Vedic ritual) of the king of (dharma) (Yudhiṣṭhira), surely be followed by you? Why are you resting on the ground?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 79 Verse 10. O hero, should not this horse, designated for the (Vedic ritual) of the king of (dharma) (Yudhiṣṭhira), surely be followed by you? Why are you resting on the ground?

sanskrit: त्वयि प्राणाः समायत्ताः कुरूणां कुरुनन्दन ।स कस्मात्प्राणदोऽन्येषां प्राणान्संत्यक्तवानसि ॥११॥
transliteration: tvayi prāṇāḥ samāyattāḥ kurūṇāṁ kurunandana ,sa kasmātprāṇado'nyeṣāṁ prāṇānsaṁtyaktavānasi.
english: O delighter of the Kurus, the lives of the Kurus depend on you! Why then have you, the giver of life to others, abandoned your life?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 79 Verse 11. O delighter of the Kurus, the lives of the Kurus depend on you! Why then have you, the giver of life to others, abandoned your life?

sanskrit: उलूपि साधु संपश्य भर्तारं निहतं रणे ।पुत्रं चैनं समुत्साह्य घातयित्वा न शोचसि ॥१२॥
transliteration: ulūpi sādhu saṁpaśya bhartāraṁ nihataṁ raṇe ,putraṁ cainaṁ samutsāhya ghātayitvā na śocasi.
english: O Ulupi, you clearly observe your husband slain in battle. And yet, after having encouraged this son and caused him to be killed, you do not grieve?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 79 Verse 12. O Ulupi, you clearly observe your husband slain in battle. And yet, after having encouraged this son and caused him to be killed, you do not grieve?

sanskrit: कामं स्वपितु बालोऽयं भूमौ प्रेतगतिं गतः ।लोहिताक्षो गुडाकेशो विजयः साधु जीवतु ॥१३॥
transliteration: kāmaṁ svapitu bālo'yaṁ bhūmau pretagatiṁ gataḥ ,lohitākṣo guḍākeśo vijayaḥ sādhu jīvatu.
english: Indeed, let this boy, who has attained the state of a dead person, sleep on the ground. But let the red-eyed one, Gūḍākeśa, Vijaya, live well.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 79 Verse 13. Indeed, let this boy, who has attained the state of a dead person, sleep on the ground. But let the red-eyed one, Gūḍākeśa, Vijaya, live well.

sanskrit: नापराधोऽस्ति सुभगे नराणां बहुभार्यता ।नारीणां तु भवत्येतन्मा ते भूद्बुद्धिरीदृशी ॥१४॥
transliteration: nāparādho'sti subhage narāṇāṁ bahubhāryatā ,nārīṇāṁ tu bhavatyetanmā te bhūdbuddhirīdṛśī.
english: O fortunate lady, there is no offense in men having multiple wives. However, for women, this is the case. May such a thought not arise in your mind.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 79 Verse 14. O fortunate lady, there is no offense in men having multiple wives. However, for women, this is the case. May such a thought not arise in your mind.

sanskrit: सख्यं ह्येतत्कृतं धात्रा शाश्वतं चाव्ययं च ह ।सख्यं समभिजानीहि सत्यं संगतमस्तु ते ॥१५॥
transliteration: sakhyaṁ hyetatkṛtaṁ dhātrā śāśvataṁ cāvyayaṁ ca ha ,sakhyaṁ samabhijānīhi satyaṁ saṁgatamastu te.
english: Indeed, this friendship was established by the Creator, eternal and imperishable. Therefore, understand this friendship well. May truth be united with you.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 79 Verse 15. Indeed, this friendship was established by the Creator, eternal and imperishable. Therefore, understand this friendship well. May truth be united with you.

sanskrit: पुत्रेण घातयित्वेमं पतिं यदि न मेऽद्य वै ।जीवन्तं दर्शयस्यद्य परित्यक्ष्यामि जीवितम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: putreṇa ghātayitvemaṁ patiṁ yadi na me'dya vai ,jīvantaṁ darśayasyadya parityakṣyāmi jīvitam.
english: If today you do not show me my husband, whom my son caused to be killed, alive, then I will abandon my life this very day.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 79 Verse 16. If today you do not show me my husband, whom my son caused to be killed, alive, then I will abandon my life this very day.

sanskrit: साहं दुःखान्विता भीरु पतिपुत्रविनाकृता ।इहैव प्रायमाशिष्ये प्रेक्षन्त्यास्ते न संशयः ॥१७॥
transliteration: sāhaṁ duḥkhānvitā bhīru patiputravinākṛtā ,ihaiva prāyamāśiṣye prekṣantyāste na saṁśayaḥ.
english: O timid one, I, afflicted by sorrow and bereft of my husband and son, will undertake a fast unto death (prāya) right here, while you are watching. There is no doubt about this.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 79 Verse 17. O timid one, I, afflicted by sorrow and bereft of my husband and son, will undertake a fast unto death (prāya) right here, while you are watching. There is no doubt about this.

sanskrit: इत्युक्त्वा पन्नगसुतां सपत्नीं चैत्रवाहिनी ।ततः प्रायमुपासीना तूष्णीमासीज्जनाधिप ॥१८॥
transliteration: ityuktvā pannagasutāṁ sapatnīṁ caitravāhinī ,tataḥ prāyamupāsīnā tūṣṇīmāsījjanādhipa.
english: Having spoken thus to the daughter of the serpents, her co-wife Cāitravāhinī then sat down silently to undertake the fast unto death (prāya), O lord of men.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 79 Verse 18. Having spoken thus to the daughter of the serpents, her co-wife Cāitravāhinī then sat down silently to undertake the fast unto death (prāya), O lord of men.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तथा विलप्योपरता भर्तुः पादौ प्रगृह्य सा ।उपविष्टाभवद्देवी सोच्छ्वासं पुत्रमीक्षती ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tathā vilapyoparatā bhartuḥ pādau pragṛhya sā ,upaviṣṭābhavaddevī socchvāsaṁ putramīkṣatī.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Having lamented thus and then ceased, that goddess, grasping her husband's feet, sat down, sighing as she looked at her son.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 80 Verse 1. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Having lamented thus and then ceased, that goddess, grasping her husband's feet, sat down, sighing as she looked at her son.

sanskrit: ततः संज्ञां पुनर्लब्ध्वा स राजा बभ्रुवाहनः ।मातरं तामथालोक्य रणभूमावथाब्रवीत् ॥२॥
transliteration: tataḥ saṁjñāṁ punarlabdhvā sa rājā babhruvāhanaḥ ,mātaraṁ tāmathālokya raṇabhūmāvathābravīt.
english: Then, having regained consciousness, that king Babhruvāhana, after seeing his mother on the battlefield, then spoke.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 80 Verse 2. Then, having regained consciousness, that king Babhruvāhana, after seeing his mother on the battlefield, then spoke.

sanskrit: इतो दुःखतरं किं नु यन्मे माता सुखैधिता ।भूमौ निपतितं वीरमनुशेते मृतं पतिम् ॥३॥
transliteration: ito duḥkhataraṁ kiṁ nu yanme mātā sukhaidhitā ,bhūmau nipatitaṁ vīramanuśete mṛtaṁ patim.
english: What, indeed, is more sorrowful than this, that my mother, who was nurtured in comfort, lies on the ground, grieving for (anuśete) her dead hero husband who has fallen?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 80 Verse 3. What, indeed, is more sorrowful than this, that my mother, who was nurtured in comfort, lies on the ground, grieving for (anuśete) her dead hero husband who has fallen?

sanskrit: निहन्तारं रणेऽरीणां सर्वशस्त्रभृतां वरम् ।मया विनिहतं संख्ये प्रेक्षते दुर्मरं बत ॥४॥
transliteration: nihantāraṁ raṇe'rīṇāṁ sarvaśastrabhṛtāṁ varam ,mayā vinihataṁ saṁkhye prekṣate durmaraṁ bata.
english: She (my mother) gazes upon her husband, the destroyer of foes in battle, the foremost of all weapon-bearers, who was, alas, slain by me in combat - a truly difficult death to witness.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 80 Verse 4. She (my mother) gazes upon her husband, the destroyer of foes in battle, the foremost of all weapon-bearers, who was, alas, slain by me in combat - a truly difficult death to witness.

sanskrit: अहोऽस्या हृदयं देव्या दृढं यन्न विदीर्यते ।व्यूढोरस्कं महाबाहुं प्रेक्षन्त्या निहतं पतिम् ॥५॥
transliteration: aho'syā hṛdayaṁ devyā dṛḍhaṁ yanna vidīryate ,vyūḍhoraskaṁ mahābāhuṁ prekṣantyā nihataṁ patim.
english: Alas, how firm is the heart of this goddess-like lady (devī), that it does not burst, even as she beholds her husband, broad-chested and mighty-armed, lying slain!
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 80 Verse 5. Alas, how firm is the heart of this goddess-like lady (devī), that it does not burst, even as she beholds her husband, broad-chested and mighty-armed, lying slain!

sanskrit: दुर्मरं पुरुषेणेह मन्ये ह्यध्वन्यनागते ।यत्र नाहं न मे माता विप्रयुज्येत जीवितात् ॥६॥
transliteration: durmaraṁ puruṣeṇeha manye hyadhvanyanāgate ,yatra nāhaṁ na me mātā viprayujyeta jīvitāt.
english: I consider it truly a difficult death for a man here, on an untimely path, where neither I nor my mother would be separated from life (i.e., we are left alive to face this sorrow).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 80 Verse 6. I consider it truly a difficult death for a man here, on an untimely path, where neither I nor my mother would be separated from life (i.e., we are left alive to face this sorrow).

sanskrit: अहो धिक्कुरुवीरस्य ह्युरःस्थं काञ्चनं भुवि ।व्यपविद्धं हतस्येह मया पुत्रेण पश्यत ॥७॥
transliteration: aho dhikkuruvīrasya hyuraḥsthaṁ kāñcanaṁ bhuvi ,vyapaviddhaṁ hatasyeha mayā putreṇa paśyata.
english: Oh, alas! Behold, on the ground here, the golden ornament from the chest of the slain Kuru hero, cast aside by me, his son!
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 80 Verse 7. Oh, alas! Behold, on the ground here, the golden ornament from the chest of the slain Kuru hero, cast aside by me, his son!

sanskrit: भो भो पश्यत मे वीरं पितरं ब्राह्मणा भुवि ।शयानं वीरशयने मया पुत्रेण पातितम् ॥८॥
transliteration: bho bho paśyata me vīraṁ pitaraṁ brāhmaṇā bhuvi ,śayānaṁ vīraśayane mayā putreṇa pātitam.
english: O Brahmins (brāhmaṇāḥ), behold my heroic father lying on the ground, on a hero's bed (the battlefield), struck down by me, his own son!
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 80 Verse 8. O Brahmins (brāhmaṇāḥ), behold my heroic father lying on the ground, on a hero's bed (the battlefield), struck down by me, his own son!

sanskrit: ब्राह्मणाः कुरुमुख्यस्य प्रयुक्ता हयसारिणः ।कुर्वन्तु शान्तिकां त्वद्य रणे योऽयं मया हतः ॥९॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇāḥ kurumukhyasya prayuktā hayasāriṇaḥ ,kurvantu śāntikāṁ tvadya raṇe yo'yaṁ mayā hataḥ.
english: O Brahmins (brāhmaṇāḥ), appointed and swift like horses, let them perform a pacification rite (śāntikā) today for this chief of the Kurus who was slain by me in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 80 Verse 9. O Brahmins (brāhmaṇāḥ), appointed and swift like horses, let them perform a pacification rite (śāntikā) today for this chief of the Kurus who was slain by me in battle.

sanskrit: व्यादिशन्तु च किं विप्राः प्रायश्चित्तमिहाद्य मे ।सुनृशंसस्य पापस्य पितृहन्तू रणाजिरे ॥१०॥
transliteration: vyādiśantu ca kiṁ viprāḥ prāyaścittamihādya me ,sunṛśaṁsasya pāpasya pitṛhantū raṇājire.
english: O Brahmins, please prescribe for me today what expiation there is for me, the exceedingly cruel and sinful killer of my father on the battlefield.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 80 Verse 10. O Brahmins, please prescribe for me today what expiation there is for me, the exceedingly cruel and sinful killer of my father on the battlefield.

sanskrit: दुश्चरा द्वादश समा हत्वा पितरमद्य वै ।ममेह सुनृशंसस्य संवीतस्यास्य चर्मणा ॥११॥
transliteration: duścarā dvādaśa samā hatvā pitaramadya vai ,mameha sunṛśaṁsasya saṁvītasyāsya carmaṇā.
english: Indeed, for me, the exceedingly cruel one, who killed my father today and is now clad in this skin, even twelve years (of penance) here are difficult to perform.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 80 Verse 11. Indeed, for me, the exceedingly cruel one, who killed my father today and is now clad in this skin, even twelve years (of penance) here are difficult to perform.

sanskrit: शिरःकपाले चास्यैव भुञ्जतः पितुरद्य मे ।प्रायश्चित्तं हि नास्त्यन्यद्धत्वाद्य पितरं मम ॥१२॥
transliteration: śiraḥkapāle cāsyaiva bhuñjataḥ pituradya me ,prāyaścittaṁ hi nāstyanyaddhatvādya pitaraṁ mama.
english: And indeed, for me, who today eats from this very father's skull, there is no other expiation possible after having killed my father.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 80 Verse 12. And indeed, for me, who today eats from this very father's skull, there is no other expiation possible after having killed my father.

sanskrit: पश्य नागोत्तमसुते भर्तारं निहतं मया ।कृतं प्रियं मया तेऽद्य निहत्य समरेऽर्जुनम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: paśya nāgottamasute bhartāraṁ nihataṁ mayā ,kṛtaṁ priyaṁ mayā te'dya nihatya samare'rjunam.
english: O daughter of the best of Nāgas, behold your husband, slain by me. Today, I have done a favor for you by killing Arjuna in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 80 Verse 13. O daughter of the best of Nāgas, behold your husband, slain by me. Today, I have done a favor for you by killing Arjuna in battle.

sanskrit: सोऽहमप्यद्य यास्यामि गतिं पितृनिषेविताम् ।न शक्नोम्यात्मनात्मानमहं धारयितुं शुभे ॥१४॥
transliteration: so'hamapyadya yāsyāmi gatiṁ pitṛniṣevitām ,na śaknomyātmanātmānamahaṁ dhārayituṁ śubhe.
english: Therefore, I too will now attain the path traversed by my ancestors. O beautiful one, I am unable to sustain my very self (ātman) by my own will.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 80 Verse 14. Therefore, I too will now attain the path traversed by my ancestors. O beautiful one, I am unable to sustain my very self (ātman) by my own will.

sanskrit: सा त्वं मयि मृते मातस्तथा गाण्डीवधन्वनि ।भव प्रीतिमती देवि सत्येनात्मानमालभे ॥१५॥
transliteration: sā tvaṁ mayi mṛte mātastathā gāṇḍīvadhanvani ,bhava prītimatī devi satyenātmānamālabhe.
english: Therefore, O mother, even after I die, you should be affectionate towards the wielder of the Gāṇḍīva bow. O goddess, I swear by truth (satya) upon my very being (ātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 80 Verse 15. Therefore, O mother, even after I die, you should be affectionate towards the wielder of the Gāṇḍīva bow. O goddess, I swear by truth (satya) upon my very being (ātman).

sanskrit: इत्युक्त्वा स तदा राजा दुःखशोकसमाहतः ।उपस्पृश्य महाराज दुःखाद्वचनमब्रवीत् ॥१६॥
transliteration: ityuktvā sa tadā rājā duḥkhaśokasamāhataḥ ,upaspṛśya mahārāja duḥkhādvacanamabravīt.
english: Having said this, that king, then overcome with sorrow and grief, O great king, spoke these words out of anguish after touching water for purification.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 80 Verse 16. Having said this, that king, then overcome with sorrow and grief, O great king, spoke these words out of anguish after touching water for purification.

sanskrit: शृण्वन्तु सर्वभूतानि स्थावराणि चराणि च ।त्वं च मातर्यथा सत्यं ब्रवीमि भुजगोत्तमे ॥१७॥
transliteration: śṛṇvantu sarvabhūtāni sthāvarāṇi carāṇi ca ,tvaṁ ca mātaryathā satyaṁ bravīmi bhujagottame.
english: Let all beings, both stationary and moving, listen! And you, O mother, O best of serpents, I speak the truth as it is.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 80 Verse 17. Let all beings, both stationary and moving, listen! And you, O mother, O best of serpents, I speak the truth as it is.

sanskrit: यदि नोत्तिष्ठति जयः पिता मे भरतर्षभः ।अस्मिन्नेव रणोद्देशे शोषयिष्ये कलेवरम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: yadi nottiṣṭhati jayaḥ pitā me bharatarṣabhaḥ ,asminneva raṇoddeśe śoṣayiṣye kalevaram.
english: If victory does not arise for my father, O best of the Bharatas, then I shall give up my life on this very battlefield.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 80 Verse 18. If victory does not arise for my father, O best of the Bharatas, then I shall give up my life on this very battlefield.

sanskrit: न हि मे पितरं हत्वा निष्कृतिर्विद्यते क्वचित् ।नरकं प्रतिपत्स्यामि ध्रुवं गुरुवधार्दितः ॥१९॥
transliteration: na hi me pitaraṁ hatvā niṣkṛtirvidyate kvacit ,narakaṁ pratipatsyāmi dhruvaṁ guruvadhārditaḥ.
english: Indeed, there is no expiation (niṣkṛti) for me anywhere after killing my father. Afflicted by the murder of my venerable father (guruvadhārdita), I shall certainly attain hell (naraka).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 80 Verse 19. Indeed, there is no expiation (niṣkṛti) for me anywhere after killing my father. Afflicted by the murder of my venerable father (guruvadhārdita), I shall certainly attain hell (naraka).

sanskrit: वीरं हि क्षत्रियं हत्वा गोशतेन प्रमुच्यते ।पितरं तु निहत्यैवं दुस्तरा निष्कृतिर्मया ॥२०॥
transliteration: vīraṁ hi kṣatriyaṁ hatvā gośatena pramucyate ,pitaraṁ tu nihatyaivaṁ dustarā niṣkṛtirmayā.
english: Indeed, by killing a heroic warrior (kṣatriya), one is absolved by offering a hundred cows. But for me, having thus killed my father, expiation (niṣkṛti) is insurmountable.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 80 Verse 20. Indeed, by killing a heroic warrior (kṣatriya), one is absolved by offering a hundred cows. But for me, having thus killed my father, expiation (niṣkṛti) is insurmountable.

sanskrit: एष ह्येको महातेजाः पाण्डुपुत्रो धनंजयः ।पिता च मम धर्मात्मा तस्य मे निष्कृतिः कुतः ॥२१॥
transliteration: eṣa hyeko mahātejāḥ pāṇḍuputro dhanaṁjayaḥ ,pitā ca mama dharmātmā tasya me niṣkṛtiḥ kutaḥ.
english: Indeed, this greatly glorious son of Pāṇḍu, Dhanañjaya, is my righteous-souled (dharmātmā) father. From where, then, can there be any expiation (niṣkṛti) for me concerning him?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 80 Verse 21. Indeed, this greatly glorious son of Pāṇḍu, Dhanañjaya, is my righteous-souled (dharmātmā) father. From where, then, can there be any expiation (niṣkṛti) for me concerning him?

sanskrit: इत्येवमुक्त्वा नृपते धनंजयसुतो नृपः ।उपस्पृश्याभवत्तूष्णीं प्रायोपेतो महामतिः ॥२२॥
transliteration: ityevamuktvā nṛpate dhanaṁjayasuto nṛpaḥ ,upaspṛśyābhavattūṣṇīṁ prāyopeto mahāmatiḥ.
english: O King, having spoken thus, the great-minded king, son of Arjuna, having undertaken the vow of fasting unto death (prāyopaveśa), purified himself and became silent.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 80 Verse 22. O King, having spoken thus, the great-minded king, son of Arjuna, having undertaken the vow of fasting unto death (prāyopaveśa), purified himself and became silent.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।प्रायोपविष्टे नृपतौ मणिपूरेश्वरे तदा ।पितृशोकसमाविष्टे सह मात्रा परंतप ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,prāyopaviṣṭe nṛpatau maṇipūreśvare tadā ,pitṛśokasamāviṣṭe saha mātrā paraṁtapa.
english: Vaishampayana said: 'O tormentor of foes, then, when the king, the ruler of Manipura, sat down for his fast unto death (prāyopaveśa), deeply afflicted by grief for his father, accompanied by his mother...'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 81 Verse 1. Vaishampayana said: 'O tormentor of foes, then, when the king, the ruler of Manipura, sat down for his fast unto death (prāyopaveśa), deeply afflicted by grief for his father, accompanied by his mother...'

sanskrit: उलूपी चिन्तयामास तदा संजीवनं मणिम् ।स चोपातिष्ठत तदा पन्नगानां परायणम् ॥२॥
transliteration: ulūpī cintayāmāsa tadā saṁjīvanaṁ maṇim ,sa copātiṣṭhata tadā pannagānāṁ parāyaṇam.
english: Then Ulupi contemplated the life-restoring gem. And at that moment, that gem, which was the supreme resource of the Nagas, manifested.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 81 Verse 2. Then Ulupi contemplated the life-restoring gem. And at that moment, that gem, which was the supreme resource of the Nagas, manifested.

sanskrit: तं गृहीत्वा तु कौरव्य नागराजपतेः सुता ।मनःप्रह्लादनीं वाचं सैनिकानामथाब्रवीत् ॥३॥
transliteration: taṁ gṛhītvā tu kauravya nāgarājapateḥ sutā ,manaḥprahlādanīṁ vācaṁ sainikānāmathābravīt.
english: O descendant of Kuru, the daughter of the Naga king, having indeed taken that gem, then spoke words that delighted the minds of the soldiers.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 81 Verse 3. O descendant of Kuru, the daughter of the Naga king, having indeed taken that gem, then spoke words that delighted the minds of the soldiers.

sanskrit: उत्तिष्ठ मा शुचः पुत्र नैष जिष्णुस्त्वया हतः ।अजेयः पुरुषैरेष देवैर्वापि सवासवैः ॥४॥
transliteration: uttiṣṭha mā śucaḥ putra naiṣa jiṣṇustvayā hataḥ ,ajeyaḥ puruṣaireṣa devairvāpi savāsavaiḥ.
english: Son, rise up, do not grieve. This Jiṣṇu was not killed by you. He is unconquerable by men, or even by gods along with the Vasavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 81 Verse 4. Son, rise up, do not grieve. This Jiṣṇu was not killed by you. He is unconquerable by men, or even by gods along with the Vasavas.

sanskrit: मया तु मोहिनी नाम मायैषा संप्रयोजिता ।प्रियार्थं पुरुषेन्द्रस्य पितुस्तेऽद्य यशस्विनः ॥५॥
transliteration: mayā tu mohinī nāma māyaiṣā saṁprayojitā ,priyārthaṁ puruṣendrasya pituste'dya yaśasvinaḥ.
english: But by me, this illusion (māyā) named Mohinī was employed today for the pleasure of your glorious father, the lord of men.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 81 Verse 5. But by me, this illusion (māyā) named Mohinī was employed today for the pleasure of your glorious father, the lord of men.

sanskrit: जिज्ञासुर्ह्येष वै पुत्र बलस्य तव कौरवः ।संग्रामे युध्यतो राजन्नागतः परवीरहा ॥६॥
transliteration: jijñāsurhyeṣa vai putra balasya tava kauravaḥ ,saṁgrāme yudhyato rājannāgataḥ paravīrahā.
english: Indeed, O son, this Kaurava (Arjuna), the slayer of hostile heroes, has come desiring to know your strength, O king, while you are fighting in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 81 Verse 6. Indeed, O son, this Kaurava (Arjuna), the slayer of hostile heroes, has come desiring to know your strength, O king, while you are fighting in battle.

sanskrit: तस्मादसि मया पुत्र युद्धार्थं परिचोदितः ।मा पापमात्मनः पुत्र शङ्केथास्त्वण्वपि प्रभो ॥७॥
transliteration: tasmādasi mayā putra yuddhārthaṁ paricoditaḥ ,mā pāpamātmanaḥ putra śaṅkethāstvaṇvapi prabho.
english: Therefore, O son, you were instigated by me for battle. O son, O lord, do not think even the slightest sin in your mind (ātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 81 Verse 7. Therefore, O son, you were instigated by me for battle. O son, O lord, do not think even the slightest sin in your mind (ātman).

sanskrit: ऋषिरेष महातेजाः पुरुषः शाश्वतोऽव्ययः ।नैनं शक्तो हि संग्रामे जेतुं शक्रोऽपि पुत्रक ॥८॥
transliteration: ṛṣireṣa mahātejāḥ puruṣaḥ śāśvato'vyayaḥ ,nainaṁ śakto hi saṁgrāme jetuṁ śakro'pi putraka.
english: My son, this sage is supremely radiant, the eternal, imperishable person (puruṣa). Indeed, even Indra cannot defeat him in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 81 Verse 8. My son, this sage is supremely radiant, the eternal, imperishable person (puruṣa). Indeed, even Indra cannot defeat him in battle.

sanskrit: अयं तु मे मणिर्दिव्यः समानीतो विशां पते ।मृतान्मृतान्पन्नगेन्द्रान्यो जीवयति नित्यदा ॥९॥
transliteration: ayaṁ tu me maṇirdivyaḥ samānīto viśāṁ pate ,mṛtānmṛtānpannagendrānyo jīvayati nityadā.
english: O lord of the people, this divine gem has indeed been brought by me, which constantly revives even the dead serpent kings.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 81 Verse 9. O lord of the people, this divine gem has indeed been brought by me, which constantly revives even the dead serpent kings.

sanskrit: एतमस्योरसि त्वं तु स्थापयस्व पितुः प्रभो ।संजीवितं पुनः पुत्र ततो द्रष्टासि पाण्डवम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: etamasyorasi tvaṁ tu sthāpayasva pituḥ prabho ,saṁjīvitaṁ punaḥ putra tato draṣṭāsi pāṇḍavam.
english: O lord, my son, you must place this (gem) on the father's chest. Then you will see the Pāṇḍava revived.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 81 Verse 10. O lord, my son, you must place this (gem) on the father's chest. Then you will see the Pāṇḍava revived.

sanskrit: इत्युक्तः स्थापयामास तस्योरसि मणिं तदा ।पार्थस्यामिततेजाः स पितुः स्नेहादपापकृत् ॥११॥
transliteration: ityuktaḥ sthāpayāmāsa tasyorasi maṇiṁ tadā ,pārthasyāmitatejāḥ sa pituḥ snehādapāpakṛt.
english: Thus addressed, he, Pārtha, of immeasurable splendor and free from sin, then placed the gem on his father's chest out of affection for Pārtha's father (Pāṇḍu).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 81 Verse 11. Thus addressed, he, Pārtha, of immeasurable splendor and free from sin, then placed the gem on his father's chest out of affection for Pārtha's father (Pāṇḍu).

sanskrit: तस्मिन्न्यस्ते मणौ वीर जिष्णुरुज्जीवितः प्रभुः ।सुप्तोत्थित इवोत्तस्थौ मृष्टलोहितलोचनः ॥१२॥
transliteration: tasminnyaste maṇau vīra jiṣṇurujjīvitaḥ prabhuḥ ,suptotthita ivottasthau mṛṣṭalohitalocanaḥ.
english: O hero, when that gem was placed upon him, the lord Jishnu was revived. He rose as if waking from sleep, with eyes reddened.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 81 Verse 12. O hero, when that gem was placed upon him, the lord Jishnu was revived. He rose as if waking from sleep, with eyes reddened.

sanskrit: तमुत्थितं महात्मानं लब्धसंज्ञं मनस्विनम् ।समीक्ष्य पितरं स्वस्थं ववन्दे बभ्रुवाहनः ॥१३॥
transliteration: tamutthitaṁ mahātmānaṁ labdhasaṁjñaṁ manasvinam ,samīkṣya pitaraṁ svasthaṁ vavande babhruvāhanaḥ.
english: Having seen his great-souled (mahātman), thoughtful father, who had risen and regained consciousness and was well, Babhruvahana bowed to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 81 Verse 13. Having seen his great-souled (mahātman), thoughtful father, who had risen and regained consciousness and was well, Babhruvahana bowed to him.

sanskrit: उत्थिते पुरुषव्याघ्रे पुनर्लक्ष्मीवति प्रभो ।दिव्याः सुमनसः पुण्या ववृषे पाकशासनः ॥१४॥
transliteration: utthite puruṣavyāghre punarlakṣmīvati prabho ,divyāḥ sumanasaḥ puṇyā vavṛṣe pākaśāsanaḥ.
english: O lord, when that tiger among men, endowed with glory, rose again, Pakaśāsana (Indra) rained down divine, sacred flowers.
