search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 81 Verse 14. O lord, when that tiger among men, endowed with glory, rose again, Pakaśāsana (Indra) rained down divine, sacred flowers.

sanskrit: अनाहता दुन्दुभयः प्रणेदुर्मेघनिस्वनाः ।साधु साध्विति चाकाशे बभूव सुमहान्स्वनः ॥१५॥
transliteration: anāhatā dundubhayaḥ praṇedurmeghanisvanāḥ ,sādhu sādhviti cākāśe babhūva sumahānsvanaḥ.
english: Unstruck drums with the sound of thunder roared forth, and a very great sound of 'Well done! Well done!' arose in the sky.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 81 Verse 15. Unstruck drums with the sound of thunder roared forth, and a very great sound of 'Well done! Well done!' arose in the sky.

sanskrit: उत्थाय तु महाबाहुः पर्याश्वस्तो धनंजयः ।बभ्रुवाहनमालिङ्ग्य समाजिघ्रत मूर्धनि ॥१६॥
transliteration: utthāya tu mahābāhuḥ paryāśvasto dhanaṁjayaḥ ,babhruvāhanamāliṅgya samājighrata mūrdhani.
english: Then, the mighty-armed Arjuna, having risen and being completely comforted, embraced Babhruvahana and kissed him on the head.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 81 Verse 16. Then, the mighty-armed Arjuna, having risen and being completely comforted, embraced Babhruvahana and kissed him on the head.

sanskrit: ददर्श चाविदूरेऽस्य मातरं शोककर्शिताम् ।उलूप्या सह तिष्ठन्तीं ततोऽपृच्छद्धनंजयः ॥१७॥
transliteration: dadarśa cāvidūre'sya mātaraṁ śokakarśitām ,ulūpyā saha tiṣṭhantīṁ tato'pṛcchaddhanaṁjayaḥ.
english: And he saw his mother, emaciated by grief, standing with Ulupi not far from him. Then Arjuna asked.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 81 Verse 17. And he saw his mother, emaciated by grief, standing with Ulupi not far from him. Then Arjuna asked.

sanskrit: किमिदं लक्ष्यते सर्वं शोकविस्मयहर्षवत् ।रणाजिरममित्रघ्न यदि जानासि शंस मे ॥१८॥
transliteration: kimidaṁ lakṣyate sarvaṁ śokavismayaharṣavat ,raṇājiramamitraghna yadi jānāsi śaṁsa me.
english: What is all this that is observed on the battlefield, full of grief, astonishment, and joy? O slayer of enemies (amitraghna), if you know, tell me.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 81 Verse 18. What is all this that is observed on the battlefield, full of grief, astonishment, and joy? O slayer of enemies (amitraghna), if you know, tell me.

sanskrit: जननी च किमर्थं ते रणभूमिमुपागता ।नागेन्द्रदुहिता चेयमुलूपी किमिहागता ॥१९॥
transliteration: jananī ca kimarthaṁ te raṇabhūmimupāgatā ,nāgendraduhitā ceyamulūpī kimihāgatā.
english: And why has your mother come to this battlefield? And why has this Ulupi, daughter of the king of serpents, come here?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 81 Verse 19. And why has your mother come to this battlefield? And why has this Ulupi, daughter of the king of serpents, come here?

sanskrit: जानाम्यहमिदं युद्धं त्वया मद्वचनात्कृतम् ।स्त्रीणामागमने हेतुमहमिच्छामि वेदितुम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: jānāmyahamidaṁ yuddhaṁ tvayā madvacanātkṛtam ,strīṇāmāgamane hetumahamicchāmi veditum.
english: I know that this battle was fought by you at my command. I wish to know the reason for the women's arrival.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 81 Verse 20. I know that this battle was fought by you at my command. I wish to know the reason for the women's arrival.

sanskrit: तमुवाच ततः पृष्टो मणिपूरपतिस्तदा ।प्रसाद्य शिरसा विद्वानुलूपी पृच्छ्यतामिति ॥२१॥
transliteration: tamuvāca tataḥ pṛṣṭo maṇipūrapatistadā ,prasādya śirasā vidvānulūpī pṛcchyatāmiti.
english: Then, being questioned, the wise lord of Maṇipūra spoke to him. Having propitiated him by bowing his head, he said, 'Let Ulūpī be asked about this.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 81 Verse 21. Then, being questioned, the wise lord of Maṇipūra spoke to him. Having propitiated him by bowing his head, he said, 'Let Ulūpī be asked about this.'

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच ।किमागमनकृत्यं ते कौरव्यकुलनन्दिनि ।मणिपूरपतेर्मातुस्तथैव च रणाजिरे ॥१॥
transliteration: arjuna uvāca ,kimāgamanakṛtyaṁ te kauravyakulanandini ,maṇipūrapatermātustathaiva ca raṇājire.
english: Arjuna said, 'What is the purpose of your arrival, O delight of the Kuru family? And likewise, what is the purpose of the mother of the lord of Maṇipūra on the battlefield?'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 82 Verse 1. Arjuna said, 'What is the purpose of your arrival, O delight of the Kuru family? And likewise, what is the purpose of the mother of the lord of Maṇipūra on the battlefield?'

sanskrit: कच्चित्कुशलकामासि राज्ञोऽस्य भुजगात्मजे ।मम वा चञ्चलापाङ्गे कच्चित्त्वं शुभमिच्छसि ॥२॥
transliteration: kaccitkuśalakāmāsi rājño'sya bhujagātmaje ,mama vā cañcalāpāṅge kaccittvaṁ śubhamicchasi.
english: O daughter of the serpent (Bhujagātmajā), do you wish for the welfare of this king? Or, O you with restless glances, do you wish for my good?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 82 Verse 2. O daughter of the serpent (Bhujagātmajā), do you wish for the welfare of this king? Or, O you with restless glances, do you wish for my good?

sanskrit: कच्चित्ते पृथुलश्रोणि नाप्रियं शुभदर्शने ।अकार्षमहमज्ञानादयं वा बभ्रुवाहनः ॥३॥
transliteration: kaccitte pṛthulaśroṇi nāpriyaṁ śubhadarśane ,akārṣamahamajñānādayaṁ vā babhruvāhanaḥ.
english: O lady with broad hips, O beautiful one, I hope that I have not, out of ignorance, done anything displeasing to you. Or perhaps it is this Babhruvahana who has?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 82 Verse 3. O lady with broad hips, O beautiful one, I hope that I have not, out of ignorance, done anything displeasing to you. Or perhaps it is this Babhruvahana who has?

sanskrit: कच्चिच्च राजपुत्री ते सपत्नी चैत्रवाहिनी ।चित्राङ्गदा वरारोहा नापराध्यति किंचन ॥४॥
transliteration: kaccicca rājaputrī te sapatnī caitravāhinī ,citrāṅgadā varārohā nāparādhyati kiṁcana.
english: And I hope that your princess co-wife, Caitravahini, the fair-hipped Chitrangada, has not offended you in any way?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 82 Verse 4. And I hope that your princess co-wife, Caitravahini, the fair-hipped Chitrangada, has not offended you in any way?

sanskrit: तमुवाचोरगपतेर्दुहिता प्रहसन्त्यथ ।न मे त्वमपराद्धोऽसि न नृपो बभ्रुवाहनः ।न जनित्री तथास्येयं मम या प्रेष्यवत्स्थिता ॥५॥
transliteration: tamuvācoragapaterduhitā prahasantyatha ,na me tvamaparāddho'si na nṛpo babhruvāhanaḥ ,na janitrī tathāsyeyaṁ mama yā preṣyavatsthitā.
english: Then the daughter of the lord of serpents (Uloopi), smiling, said to him: 'You have not offended me, nor has King Babhruvahana, nor has his mother, who acts like a servant to me.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 82 Verse 5. Then the daughter of the lord of serpents (Uloopi), smiling, said to him: 'You have not offended me, nor has King Babhruvahana, nor has his mother, who acts like a servant to me.'

sanskrit: श्रूयतां यद्यथा चेदं मया सर्वं विचेष्टितम् ।न मे कोपस्त्वया कार्यः शिरसा त्वां प्रसादये ॥६॥
transliteration: śrūyatāṁ yadyathā cedaṁ mayā sarvaṁ viceṣṭitam ,na me kopastvayā kāryaḥ śirasā tvāṁ prasādaye.
english: Listen to all this that has been done by me and how. You should not be angry with me; I appease you with my head.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 82 Verse 6. Listen to all this that has been done by me and how. You should not be angry with me; I appease you with my head.

sanskrit: त्वत्प्रीत्यर्थं हि कौरव्य कृतमेतन्मयानघ ।यत्तच्छृणु महाबाहो निखिलेन धनंजय ॥७॥
transliteration: tvatprītyarthaṁ hi kauravya kṛtametanmayānagha ,yattacchṛṇu mahābāho nikhilena dhanaṁjaya.
english: Indeed, O descendant of Kuru, this has been done by me for your satisfaction, O faultless one. Listen to that fully, O mighty-armed Dhanañjaya.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 82 Verse 7. Indeed, O descendant of Kuru, this has been done by me for your satisfaction, O faultless one. Listen to that fully, O mighty-armed Dhanañjaya.

sanskrit: महाभारतयुद्धे यत्त्वया शांतनवो नृपः ।अधर्मेण हतः पार्थ तस्यैषा निष्कृतिः कृता ॥८॥
transliteration: mahābhāratayuddhe yattvayā śāṁtanavo nṛpaḥ ,adharmeṇa hataḥ pārtha tasyaiṣā niṣkṛtiḥ kṛtā.
english: Because in the Mahābhārata war, O Pārtha, the king, son of Śāntanu (Bhīṣma), was killed by you through unrighteousness (adharma), this expiation has been made for him.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 82 Verse 8. Because in the Mahābhārata war, O Pārtha, the king, son of Śāntanu (Bhīṣma), was killed by you through unrighteousness (adharma), this expiation has been made for him.

sanskrit: न हि भीष्मस्त्वया वीर युध्यमानो निपातितः ।शिखण्डिना तु संसक्तस्तमाश्रित्य हतस्त्वया ॥९॥
transliteration: na hi bhīṣmastvayā vīra yudhyamāno nipātitaḥ ,śikhaṇḍinā tu saṁsaktastamāśritya hatastvayā.
english: Indeed, O hero, Bhīṣma was not felled by you while he was directly fighting. Rather, you killed him by relying on Shikhaṇḍī, who was engaged with him (Bhīṣma).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 82 Verse 9. Indeed, O hero, Bhīṣma was not felled by you while he was directly fighting. Rather, you killed him by relying on Shikhaṇḍī, who was engaged with him (Bhīṣma).

sanskrit: तस्य शान्तिमकृत्वा तु त्यजेस्त्वं यदि जीवितम् ।कर्मणा तेन पापेन पतेथा निरये ध्रुवम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: tasya śāntimakṛtvā tu tyajestvaṁ yadi jīvitam ,karmaṇā tena pāpena patethā niraye dhruvam.
english: If you abandon your life without having performed expiation for him (Bhīṣma), then by that sinful deed (karma) you would surely fall into hell.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 82 Verse 10. If you abandon your life without having performed expiation for him (Bhīṣma), then by that sinful deed (karma) you would surely fall into hell.

sanskrit: एषा तु विहिता शान्तिः पुत्राद्यां प्राप्तवानसि ।वसुभिर्वसुधापाल गङ्गया च महामते ॥११॥
transliteration: eṣā tu vihitā śāntiḥ putrādyāṁ prāptavānasi ,vasubhirvasudhāpāla gaṅgayā ca mahāmate.
english: O protector of the earth (vasudhāpāla), O great-minded one, you have indeed obtained this prescribed peace (śāntiḥ) concerning your sons and others, ordained by the Vasus and by Gaṅgā.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 82 Verse 11. O protector of the earth (vasudhāpāla), O great-minded one, you have indeed obtained this prescribed peace (śāntiḥ) concerning your sons and others, ordained by the Vasus and by Gaṅgā.

sanskrit: पुरा हि श्रुतमेतद्वै वसुभिः कथितं मया ।गङ्गायास्तीरमागम्य हते शांतनवे नृपे ॥१२॥
transliteration: purā hi śrutametadvai vasubhiḥ kathitaṁ mayā ,gaṅgāyāstīramāgamya hate śāṁtanave nṛpe.
english: Indeed, this was heard by me long ago, spoken by the Vasus when they came to the bank of the Gaṅgā, after the king, the son of Śāntanu (Bhīṣma), had been slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 82 Verse 12. Indeed, this was heard by me long ago, spoken by the Vasus when they came to the bank of the Gaṅgā, after the king, the son of Śāntanu (Bhīṣma), had been slain.

sanskrit: आप्लुत्य देवा वसवः समेत्य च महानदीम् ।इदमूचुर्वचो घोरं भागीरथ्या मते तदा ॥१३॥
transliteration: āplutya devā vasavaḥ sametya ca mahānadīm ,idamūcurvaco ghoraṁ bhāgīrathyā mate tadā.
english: Then, the gods, the Vasus, having bathed and gathered at the great river (Gaṅgā), spoke these dreadful words in the presence of Bhāgīrathī (Gaṅgā).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 82 Verse 13. Then, the gods, the Vasus, having bathed and gathered at the great river (Gaṅgā), spoke these dreadful words in the presence of Bhāgīrathī (Gaṅgā).

sanskrit: एष शांतनवो भीष्मो निहतः सव्यसाचिना ।अयुध्यमानः संग्रामे संसक्तोऽन्येन भामिनि ॥१४॥
transliteration: eṣa śāṁtanavo bhīṣmo nihataḥ savyasācinā ,ayudhyamānaḥ saṁgrāme saṁsakto'nyena bhāmini.
english: O passionate one (bhāmini), this Bhīṣma, the son of Śāntanu, has been slain by Savyasācin (Arjuna) in battle, while he was not fighting, being engaged with another (Śikhaṇḍī).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 82 Verse 14. O passionate one (bhāmini), this Bhīṣma, the son of Śāntanu, has been slain by Savyasācin (Arjuna) in battle, while he was not fighting, being engaged with another (Śikhaṇḍī).

sanskrit: तदनेनाभिषङ्गेण वयमप्यर्जुनं शुभे ।शापेन योजयामेति तथास्त्विति च साब्रवीत् ॥१५॥
transliteration: tadanenābhiṣaṅgeṇa vayamapyarjunaṁ śubhe ,śāpena yojayāmeti tathāstviti ca sābravīt.
english: Therefore, because of this transgression, O auspicious one, we shall afflict Arjuna with a curse." She then said, "So be it."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 82 Verse 15. Therefore, because of this transgression, O auspicious one, we shall afflict Arjuna with a curse." She then said, "So be it."

sanskrit: तदहं पितुरावेद्य भृशं प्रव्यथितेन्द्रिया ।अभवं स च तच्छ्रुत्वा विषादमगमत्परम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: tadahaṁ piturāvedya bhṛśaṁ pravyathitendriyā ,abhavaṁ sa ca tacchrutvā viṣādamagamatparam.
english: Therefore, after informing my father, my senses became greatly agitated. And upon hearing that, he (my father) fell into deep sorrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 82 Verse 16. Therefore, after informing my father, my senses became greatly agitated. And upon hearing that, he (my father) fell into deep sorrow.

sanskrit: पिता तु मे वसून्गत्वा त्वदर्थं समयाचत ।पुनः पुनः प्रसाद्यैनांस्त एनमिदमब्रुवन् ॥१७॥
transliteration: pitā tu me vasūngatvā tvadarthaṁ samayācata ,punaḥ punaḥ prasādyaināṁsta enamidamabruvan.
english: However, my father went to the Vasus and pleaded for your sake. After repeatedly propitiating them, they said this to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 82 Verse 17. However, my father went to the Vasus and pleaded for your sake. After repeatedly propitiating them, they said this to him.

sanskrit: पुनस्तस्य महाभाग मणिपूरेश्वरो युवा ।स एनं रणमध्यस्थं शरैः पातयिता भुवि ॥१८॥
transliteration: punastasya mahābhāga maṇipūreśvaro yuvā ,sa enaṁ raṇamadhyasthaṁ śaraiḥ pātayitā bhuvi.
english: Furthermore, regarding him, O greatly blessed one, the young lord of Maṇipūra will cause him, who stands in the thick of battle, to fall to the ground with arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 82 Verse 18. Furthermore, regarding him, O greatly blessed one, the young lord of Maṇipūra will cause him, who stands in the thick of battle, to fall to the ground with arrows.

sanskrit: एवं कृते स नागेन्द्र मुक्तशापो भविष्यति ।गच्छेति वसुभिश्चोक्तो मम चेदं शशंस सः ॥१९॥
transliteration: evaṁ kṛte sa nāgendra muktaśāpo bhaviṣyati ,gaccheti vasubhiścokto mama cedaṁ śaśaṁsa saḥ.
english: When this is accomplished, that lord of serpents will be freed from the curse. Having been instructed 'Go!' by the Vasus, he then reported this to me.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 82 Verse 19. When this is accomplished, that lord of serpents will be freed from the curse. Having been instructed 'Go!' by the Vasus, he then reported this to me.

sanskrit: तच्छ्रुत्वा त्वं मया तस्माच्छापादसि विमोक्षितः ।न हि त्वां देवराजोऽपि समरेषु पराजयेत् ॥२०॥
transliteration: tacchrutvā tvaṁ mayā tasmācchāpādasi vimokṣitaḥ ,na hi tvāṁ devarājo'pi samareṣu parājayet.
english: Having heard that, you have been liberated by me from that curse. Indeed, not even the king of gods can defeat you in battles.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 82 Verse 20. Having heard that, you have been liberated by me from that curse. Indeed, not even the king of gods can defeat you in battles.

sanskrit: आत्मा पुत्रः स्मृतस्तस्मात्तेनेहासि पराजितः ।नात्र दोषो मम मतः कथं वा मन्यसे विभो ॥२१॥
transliteration: ātmā putraḥ smṛtastasmāttenehāsi parājitaḥ ,nātra doṣo mama mataḥ kathaṁ vā manyase vibho.
english: The self (ātman) is regarded as a son; therefore, you have been conquered by this [son]. I do not consider this a fault here. How, O lord, do you perceive it?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 82 Verse 21. The self (ātman) is regarded as a son; therefore, you have been conquered by this [son]. I do not consider this a fault here. How, O lord, do you perceive it?

sanskrit: इत्येवमुक्तो विजयः प्रसन्नात्माब्रवीदिदम् ।सर्वं मे सुप्रियं देवि यदेतत्कृतवत्यसि ॥२२॥
transliteration: ityevamukto vijayaḥ prasannātmābravīdidam ,sarvaṁ me supriyaṁ devi yadetatkṛtavatyasi.
english: Thus addressed, Vijaya, with a joyful spirit, spoke this: 'O Goddess, everything that you have done is very dear to me.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 82 Verse 22. Thus addressed, Vijaya, with a joyful spirit, spoke this: 'O Goddess, everything that you have done is very dear to me.'

sanskrit: इत्युक्त्वाथाब्रवीत्पुत्रं मणिपूरेश्वरं जयः ।चित्राङ्गदायाः शृण्वन्त्याः कौरव्यदुहितुस्तथा ॥२३॥
transliteration: ityuktvāthābravītputraṁ maṇipūreśvaraṁ jayaḥ ,citrāṅgadāyāḥ śṛṇvantyāḥ kauravyaduhitustathā.
english: Thus having spoken, Jayā (Arjuna) then addressed his son, the lord of Maṇipūra, while Citrāṅgadā was listening, and also a princess of the Kaurava lineage.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 82 Verse 23. Thus having spoken, Jayā (Arjuna) then addressed his son, the lord of Maṇipūra, while Citrāṅgadā was listening, and also a princess of the Kaurava lineage.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरस्याश्वमेधः परां चैत्रीं भविष्यति ।तत्रागच्छेः सहामात्यो मातृभ्यां सहितो नृप ॥२४॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhirasyāśvamedhaḥ parāṁ caitrīṁ bhaviṣyati ,tatrāgaccheḥ sahāmātyo mātṛbhyāṁ sahito nṛpa.
english: O king, Yudhiṣṭhira's horse-ritual (yajña) will take place in the coming Caitrī (full moon of the Caitra month). You should come there with your ministers, accompanied by your two mothers.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 82 Verse 24. O king, Yudhiṣṭhira's horse-ritual (yajña) will take place in the coming Caitrī (full moon of the Caitra month). You should come there with your ministers, accompanied by your two mothers.

sanskrit: इत्येवमुक्तः पार्थेन स राजा बभ्रुवाहनः ।उवाच पितरं धीमानिदमस्राविलेक्षणः ॥२५॥
transliteration: ityevamuktaḥ pārthena sa rājā babhruvāhanaḥ ,uvāca pitaraṁ dhīmānidamasrāvilekṣaṇaḥ.
english: The intelligent King Babhruvāhana, thus addressed by Pārtha (Arjuna), spoke this to his father with tearful eyes.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 82 Verse 25. The intelligent King Babhruvāhana, thus addressed by Pārtha (Arjuna), spoke this to his father with tearful eyes.

sanskrit: उपयास्यामि धर्मज्ञ भवतः शासनादहम् ।अश्वमेधे महायज्ञे द्विजातिपरिवेषकः ॥२६॥
transliteration: upayāsyāmi dharmajña bhavataḥ śāsanādaham ,aśvamedhe mahāyajñe dvijātipariveṣakaḥ.
english: O knower of natural law (dharma), I shall attend the great horse-ritual (yajña) by your command, serving the twice-born.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 82 Verse 26. O knower of natural law (dharma), I shall attend the great horse-ritual (yajña) by your command, serving the twice-born.

sanskrit: मम त्वनुग्रहार्थाय प्रविशस्व पुरं स्वकम् ।भार्याभ्यां सह शत्रुघ्न मा भूत्तेऽत्र विचारणा ॥२७॥
transliteration: mama tvanugrahārthāya praviśasva puraṁ svakam ,bhāryābhyāṁ saha śatrughna mā bhūtte'tra vicāraṇā.
english: O Shatrughna, for the sake of showing me favor, please enter your own city with your two wives. Let there be no hesitation on your part in this matter.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 82 Verse 27. O Shatrughna, for the sake of showing me favor, please enter your own city with your two wives. Let there be no hesitation on your part in this matter.

sanskrit: उषित्वेह विशल्यस्त्वं सुखं स्वे वेश्मनि प्रभो ।पुनरश्वानुगमनं कर्तासि जयतां वर ॥२८॥
transliteration: uṣitveha viśalyastvaṁ sukhaṁ sve veśmani prabho ,punaraśvānugamanaṁ kartāsi jayatāṁ vara.
english: O Lord, O best among the victorious, having stayed here comfortably in your own home, relieved of your burdens, you will again follow the horse.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 82 Verse 28. O Lord, O best among the victorious, having stayed here comfortably in your own home, relieved of your burdens, you will again follow the horse.

sanskrit: इत्युक्तः स तु पुत्रेण तदा वानरकेतनः ।स्मयन्प्रोवाच कौन्तेयस्तदा चित्राङ्गदासुतम् ॥२९॥
transliteration: ityuktaḥ sa tu putreṇa tadā vānaraketanaḥ ,smayanprovāca kaunteyastadā citrāṅgadāsutam.
english: Thus addressed by his son, Arjuna (whose banner bears a monkey, the son of Kunti) then, smiling, spoke to the son of Citrāṅgadā.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 82 Verse 29. Thus addressed by his son, Arjuna (whose banner bears a monkey, the son of Kunti) then, smiling, spoke to the son of Citrāṅgadā.

sanskrit: विदितं ते महाबाहो यथा दीक्षां चराम्यहम् ।न स तावत्प्रवेक्ष्यामि पुरं ते पृथुलोचन ॥३०॥
transliteration: viditaṁ te mahābāho yathā dīkṣāṁ carāmyaham ,na sa tāvatpravekṣyāmi puraṁ te pṛthulocana.
english: O mighty-armed one, you know how I observe my solemn vow (dīkṣā). Therefore, I will not immediately enter your city, O broad-eyed one.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 82 Verse 30. O mighty-armed one, you know how I observe my solemn vow (dīkṣā). Therefore, I will not immediately enter your city, O broad-eyed one.

sanskrit: यथाकामं प्रयात्येष यज्ञियश्च तुरंगमः ।स्वस्ति तेऽस्तु गमिष्यामि न स्थानं विद्यते मम ॥३१॥
transliteration: yathākāmaṁ prayātyeṣa yajñiyaśca turaṁgamaḥ ,svasti te'stu gamiṣyāmi na sthānaṁ vidyate mama.
english: This horse, suitable for the Vedic ritual (yajña), goes as it pleases. May well-being be upon you; I will go, as there is no place for me here.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 82 Verse 31. This horse, suitable for the Vedic ritual (yajña), goes as it pleases. May well-being be upon you; I will go, as there is no place for me here.

sanskrit: स तत्र विधिवत्तेन पूजितः पाकशासनिः ।भार्याभ्यामभ्यनुज्ञातः प्रायाद्भरतसत्तमः ॥३२॥
transliteration: sa tatra vidhivattena pūjitaḥ pākaśāsaniḥ ,bhāryābhyāmabhyanujñātaḥ prāyādbharatasattamaḥ.
english: Then, that best of the Bhāratas (Arjuna), son of Indra, having been properly honored there by him and given leave by his two wives, departed.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 82 Verse 32. Then, that best of the Bhāratas (Arjuna), son of Indra, having been properly honored there by him and given leave by his two wives, departed.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।स तु वाजी समुद्रान्तां पर्येत्य पृथिवीमिमाम् ।निवृत्तोऽभिमुखो राजन्येन नागाह्वयं पुरम् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,sa tu vājī samudrāntāṁ paryetya pṛthivīmimām ,nivṛtto'bhimukho rājanyena nāgāhvayaṁ puram.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: 'O king, that very horse, having traversed this ocean-bound earth, returned, facing towards the city called Nāga (Hastinapura).'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 83 Verse 1. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: 'O king, that very horse, having traversed this ocean-bound earth, returned, facing towards the city called Nāga (Hastinapura).'

sanskrit: अनुगच्छंश्च तेजस्वी निवृत्तोऽथ किरीटभृत् ।यदृच्छया समापेदे पुरं राजगृहं तदा ॥२॥
transliteration: anugacchaṁśca tejasvī nivṛtto'tha kirīṭabhṛt ,yadṛcchayā samāpede puraṁ rājagṛhaṁ tadā.
english: And then, the glorious diadem-wearer (Arjuna), who was following (the horse), returned; and at that time, by chance, he reached the city of Rājagṛha.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 83 Verse 2. And then, the glorious diadem-wearer (Arjuna), who was following (the horse), returned; and at that time, by chance, he reached the city of Rājagṛha.

sanskrit: तमभ्याशगतं राजा जरासंधात्मजात्मजः ।क्षत्रधर्मे स्थितो वीरः समरायाजुहाव ह ॥३॥
transliteration: tamabhyāśagataṁ rājā jarāsaṁdhātmajātmajaḥ ,kṣatradharme sthito vīraḥ samarāyājuhāva ha.
english: The king, the valiant grandson of Jarāsandha, upholding the constitution (dharma) of a warrior, challenged him who had approached, to battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 83 Verse 3. The king, the valiant grandson of Jarāsandha, upholding the constitution (dharma) of a warrior, challenged him who had approached, to battle.

sanskrit: ततः पुरात्स निष्क्रम्य रथी धन्वी शरी तली ।मेघसंधिः पदातिं तं धनंजयमुपाद्रवत् ॥४॥
transliteration: tataḥ purātsa niṣkramya rathī dhanvī śarī talī ,meghasaṁdhiḥ padātiṁ taṁ dhanaṁjayamupādravat.
english: Then, Meghasandhi, equipped with a chariot, bow, arrows, and quiver, came out from the city and rushed towards Dhananjaya (Arjuna), who was on foot.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 83 Verse 4. Then, Meghasandhi, equipped with a chariot, bow, arrows, and quiver, came out from the city and rushed towards Dhananjaya (Arjuna), who was on foot.

sanskrit: आसाद्य च महातेजा मेघसंधिर्धनंजयम् ।बालभावान्महाराज प्रोवाचेदं न कौशलात् ॥५॥
transliteration: āsādya ca mahātejā meghasaṁdhirdhanaṁjayam ,bālabhāvānmahārāja provācedaṁ na kauśalāt.
english: And having approached Dhananjaya (Arjuna), the mighty Meghasandhi, O great king, spoke these words out of youthful impulsiveness, not from any cunning (kauśala).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 83 Verse 5. And having approached Dhananjaya (Arjuna), the mighty Meghasandhi, O great king, spoke these words out of youthful impulsiveness, not from any cunning (kauśala).

sanskrit: किमयं चार्यते वाजी स्त्रीमध्य इव भारत ।हयमेनं हरिष्यामि प्रयतस्व विमोक्षणे ॥६॥
transliteration: kimayaṁ cāryate vājī strīmadhya iva bhārata ,hayamenaṁ hariṣyāmi prayatasva vimokṣaṇe.
english: O Bhārata, why is this horse being protected as if it were among women? I shall seize this horse, so strive to liberate it.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 83 Verse 6. O Bhārata, why is this horse being protected as if it were among women? I shall seize this horse, so strive to liberate it.

sanskrit: अदत्तानुनयो युद्धे यदि त्वं पितृभिर्मम ।करिष्यामि तवातिथ्यं प्रहर प्रहरामि वा ॥७॥
transliteration: adattānunayo yuddhe yadi tvaṁ pitṛbhirmama ,kariṣyāmi tavātithyaṁ prahara praharāmi vā.
english: If you engage in battle without the consent of my elders, I will offer you hospitality (by fighting you). You strike first, or I will.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 83 Verse 7. If you engage in battle without the consent of my elders, I will offer you hospitality (by fighting you). You strike first, or I will.

sanskrit: इत्युक्तः प्रत्युवाचैनं पाण्डवः प्रहसन्निव ।विघ्नकर्ता मया वार्य इति मे व्रतमाहितम् ॥८॥
transliteration: ityuktaḥ pratyuvācainaṁ pāṇḍavaḥ prahasanniva ,vighnakartā mayā vārya iti me vratamāhitam.
english: Thus addressed, the Pāṇḍava (Bhima) replied to him, as if smiling, "One who creates obstacles must be restrained by me; this is my established vow."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 83 Verse 8. Thus addressed, the Pāṇḍava (Bhima) replied to him, as if smiling, "One who creates obstacles must be restrained by me; this is my established vow."

sanskrit: भ्रात्रा ज्येष्ठेन नृपते तवापि विदितं ध्रुवम् ।प्रहरस्व यथाशक्ति न मन्युर्विद्यते मम ॥९॥
transliteration: bhrātrā jyeṣṭhena nṛpate tavāpi viditaṁ dhruvam ,praharasva yathāśakti na manyurvidyate mama.
english: O King, this (vow) is certainly known to you also through my elder brother. Strike with all your might; I bear no resentment.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 83 Verse 9. O King, this (vow) is certainly known to you also through my elder brother. Strike with all your might; I bear no resentment.

sanskrit: इत्युक्तः प्राहरत्पूर्वं पाण्डवं मगधेश्वरः ।किरञ्शरसहस्राणि वर्षाणीव सहस्रदृक् ॥१०॥
transliteration: ityuktaḥ prāharatpūrvaṁ pāṇḍavaṁ magadheśvaraḥ ,kirañśarasahasrāṇi varṣāṇīva sahasradṛk.
english: Thus addressed, the king of Magadha (Jarāsandha) struck the Pāṇḍava (Bhima) first, showering thousands of arrows, just as the thousand-eyed (Indra) sends down torrents of rain.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 83 Verse 10. Thus addressed, the king of Magadha (Jarāsandha) struck the Pāṇḍava (Bhima) first, showering thousands of arrows, just as the thousand-eyed (Indra) sends down torrents of rain.

sanskrit: ततो गाण्डीवभृच्छूरो गाण्डीवप्रेषितैः शरैः ।चकार मोघांस्तान्बाणानयत्नाद्भरतर्षभ ॥११॥
transliteration: tato gāṇḍīvabhṛcchūro gāṇḍīvapreṣitaiḥ śaraiḥ ,cakāra moghāṁstānbāṇānayatnādbharatarṣabha.
english: Then, O best of the Bhāratas, the heroic wielder of the Gaṇḍīva (Arjuna) effortlessly nullified those arrows with his own arrows discharged from the Gaṇḍīva bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 83 Verse 11. Then, O best of the Bhāratas, the heroic wielder of the Gaṇḍīva (Arjuna) effortlessly nullified those arrows with his own arrows discharged from the Gaṇḍīva bow.

sanskrit: स मोघं तस्य बाणौघं कृत्वा वानरकेतनः ।शरान्मुमोच ज्वलितान्दीप्तास्यानिव पन्नगान् ॥१२॥
transliteration: sa moghaṁ tasya bāṇaughaṁ kṛtvā vānaraketanaḥ ,śarānmumoca jvalitāndīptāsyāniva pannagān.
english: Having rendered his opponent's volley of arrows futile, he, whose banner bore the monkey emblem (Arjuna), then released flaming arrows, like serpents with blazing mouths.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 83 Verse 12. Having rendered his opponent's volley of arrows futile, he, whose banner bore the monkey emblem (Arjuna), then released flaming arrows, like serpents with blazing mouths.

sanskrit: ध्वजे पताकादण्डेषु रथयन्त्रे हयेषु च ।अन्येषु च रथाङ्गेषु न शरीरे न सारथौ ॥१३॥
transliteration: dhvaje patākādaṇḍeṣu rathayantre hayeṣu ca ,anyeṣu ca rathāṅgeṣu na śarīre na sārathau.
english: He directed his arrows to the banner, the flagstaffs, the chariot's mechanisms, the horses, and other components of the chariot, but not to the body (of the opponent) nor to the charioteer.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 83 Verse 13. He directed his arrows to the banner, the flagstaffs, the chariot's mechanisms, the horses, and other components of the chariot, but not to the body (of the opponent) nor to the charioteer.

sanskrit: संरक्ष्यमाणः पार्थेन शरीरे फल्गुनस्य ह ।मन्यमानः स्ववीर्यं तन्मागधः प्राहिणोच्छरान् ॥१४॥
transliteration: saṁrakṣyamāṇaḥ pārthena śarīre phalgunasya ha ,manyamānaḥ svavīryaṁ tanmāgadhaḥ prāhiṇoccharān.
english: Although his (Magadha's) body was being protected by Pārtha (Arjuna) – by Phālguna himself, indeed – the Magadha king, considering his own prowess, (still) dispatched arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 83 Verse 14. Although his (Magadha's) body was being protected by Pārtha (Arjuna) – by Phālguna himself, indeed – the Magadha king, considering his own prowess, (still) dispatched arrows.

sanskrit: ततो गाण्डीवभृच्छूरो मागधेन समाहतः ।बभौ वासन्तिक इव पलाशः पुष्पितो महान् ॥१५॥
transliteration: tato gāṇḍīvabhṛcchūro māgadhena samāhataḥ ,babhau vāsantika iva palāśaḥ puṣpito mahān.
english: Then the brave bearer of the Gaṇḍīva (Arjuna), struck by Jarasandha, shone like a magnificent palāśa tree in full bloom in spring.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 83 Verse 15. Then the brave bearer of the Gaṇḍīva (Arjuna), struck by Jarasandha, shone like a magnificent palāśa tree in full bloom in spring.

sanskrit: अवध्यमानः सोऽभ्यघ्नन्मागधः पाण्डवर्षभम् ।तेन तस्थौ स कौरव्य लोकवीरस्य दर्शने ॥१६॥
transliteration: avadhyamānaḥ so'bhyaghnanmāgadhaḥ pāṇḍavarṣabham ,tena tasthau sa kauravya lokavīrasya darśane.
english: Though incapable of being slain, that Magadha (Jarasandha) struck the foremost of the Pāṇḍavas (Arjuna). O scion of Kuru, he (Jarasandha) stood firm in the presence of the world-hero (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 83 Verse 16. Though incapable of being slain, that Magadha (Jarasandha) struck the foremost of the Pāṇḍavas (Arjuna). O scion of Kuru, he (Jarasandha) stood firm in the presence of the world-hero (Arjuna).

sanskrit: सव्यसाची तु संक्रुद्धो विकृष्य बलवद्धनुः ।हयांश्चकार निर्देहान्सारथेश्च शिरोऽहरत् ॥१७॥
transliteration: savyasācī tu saṁkruddho vikṛṣya balavaddhanuḥ ,hayāṁścakāra nirdehānsāratheśca śiro'harat.
english: But Arjuna (Savyasācin), greatly enraged, having drawn his powerful bow, incinerated the horses and cut off the charioteer's head.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 83 Verse 17. But Arjuna (Savyasācin), greatly enraged, having drawn his powerful bow, incinerated the horses and cut off the charioteer's head.

sanskrit: धनुश्चास्य महच्चित्रं क्षुरेण प्रचकर्त ह ।हस्तावापं पताकां च ध्वजं चास्य न्यपातयत् ॥१८॥
transliteration: dhanuścāsya mahaccitraṁ kṣureṇa pracakarta ha ,hastāvāpaṁ patākāṁ ca dhvajaṁ cāsya nyapātayat.
english: And he severed his great, splendid bow with a razor-arrow. He also threw down his hand-guard, banner, and standard.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 83 Verse 18. And he severed his great, splendid bow with a razor-arrow. He also threw down his hand-guard, banner, and standard.

sanskrit: स राजा व्यथितो व्यश्वो विधनुर्हतसारथिः ।गदामादाय कौन्तेयमभिदुद्राव वेगवान् ॥१९॥
transliteration: sa rājā vyathito vyaśvo vidhanurhatasārathiḥ ,gadāmādāya kaunteyamabhidudrāva vegavān.
english: That king, distressed, horseless, without a bow, and with his charioteer killed, swiftly took up a mace and rushed towards the son of Kunti (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 83 Verse 19. That king, distressed, horseless, without a bow, and with his charioteer killed, swiftly took up a mace and rushed towards the son of Kunti (Arjuna).

sanskrit: तस्यापतत एवाशु गदां हेमपरिष्कृताम् ।शरैश्चकर्त बहुधा बहुभिर्गृध्रवाजितैः ॥२०॥
transliteration: tasyāpatata evāśu gadāṁ hemapariṣkṛtām ,śaraiścakarta bahudhā bahubhirgṛdhravājitaiḥ.
english: As he was rushing forward, (Arjuna) immediately cut that mace, which was adorned with gold, into many pieces with numerous arrows furnished with vulture feathers.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 83 Verse 20. As he was rushing forward, (Arjuna) immediately cut that mace, which was adorned with gold, into many pieces with numerous arrows furnished with vulture feathers.

sanskrit: सा गदा शकलीभूता विशीर्णमणिबन्धना ।व्याली निर्मुच्यमानेव पपातास्य सहस्रधा ॥२१॥
transliteration: sā gadā śakalībhūtā viśīrṇamaṇibandhanā ,vyālī nirmucyamāneva papātāsya sahasradhā.
english: That mace, broken into pieces with its jeweled bindings shattered, fell from his grasp into a thousand fragments, like a serpent shedding its skin.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 83 Verse 21. That mace, broken into pieces with its jeweled bindings shattered, fell from his grasp into a thousand fragments, like a serpent shedding its skin.

sanskrit: विरथं तं विधन्वानं गदया परिवर्जितम् ।नैच्छत्ताडयितुं धीमानर्जुनः समराग्रणीः ॥२२॥
transliteration: virathaṁ taṁ vidhanvānaṁ gadayā parivarjitam ,naicchattāḍayituṁ dhīmānarjunaḥ samarāgraṇīḥ.
english: Arjuna, the wise and foremost leader in battle, did not wish to strike that king who was chariotiess, bowless, and deprived of his mace.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 83 Verse 22. Arjuna, the wise and foremost leader in battle, did not wish to strike that king who was chariotiess, bowless, and deprived of his mace.

sanskrit: तत एनं विमनसं क्षत्रधर्मे समास्थितम् ।सान्त्वपूर्वमिदं वाक्यमब्रवीत्कपिकेतनः ॥२३॥
transliteration: tata enaṁ vimanasaṁ kṣatradharme samāsthitam ,sāntvapūrvamidaṁ vākyamabravītkapiketanaḥ.
english: Then, Arjuna, whose banner bore a monkey (kapiketana), spoke these conciliatory words to him, who was disheartened yet firmly engaged in the warrior's code (kṣatradharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 83 Verse 23. Then, Arjuna, whose banner bore a monkey (kapiketana), spoke these conciliatory words to him, who was disheartened yet firmly engaged in the warrior's code (kṣatradharma).

sanskrit: पर्याप्तः क्षत्रधर्मोऽयं दर्शितः पुत्र गम्यताम् ।बह्वेतत्समरे कर्म तव बालस्य पार्थिव ॥२४॥
transliteration: paryāptaḥ kṣatradharmo'yaṁ darśitaḥ putra gamyatām ,bahvetatsamare karma tava bālasya pārthiva.
english: O son, this warrior's code (kṣatradharma) you have displayed is sufficient; you may now depart. O king, this is a great feat (karma) in battle for you, a mere boy.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 83 Verse 24. O son, this warrior's code (kṣatradharma) you have displayed is sufficient; you may now depart. O king, this is a great feat (karma) in battle for you, a mere boy.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरस्य संदेशो न हन्तव्या नृपा इति ।तेन जीवसि राजंस्त्वमपराद्धोऽपि मे रणे ॥२५॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhirasya saṁdeśo na hantavyā nṛpā iti ,tena jīvasi rājaṁstvamaparāddho'pi me raṇe.
english: This is Yudhiṣṭhira's message (sandeśa): 'Kings are not to be slain.' Therefore, O king, you live, even though you have committed an offense against me in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 83 Verse 25. This is Yudhiṣṭhira's message (sandeśa): 'Kings are not to be slain.' Therefore, O king, you live, even though you have committed an offense against me in battle.

sanskrit: इति मत्वा स चात्मानं प्रत्यादिष्टं स्म मागधः ।तथ्यमित्यवगम्यैनं प्राञ्जलिः प्रत्यपूजयत् ॥२६॥
transliteration: iti matvā sa cātmānaṁ pratyādiṣṭaṁ sma māgadhaḥ ,tathyamityavagamyainaṁ prāñjaliḥ pratyapūjayat.
english: And having considered himself (ātman) as rejected, the Magadha king, understanding it to be true, honored him with joined hands.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 83 Verse 26. And having considered himself (ātman) as rejected, the Magadha king, understanding it to be true, honored him with joined hands.

sanskrit: तमर्जुनः समाश्वास्य पुनरेवेदमब्रवीत् ।आगन्तव्यं परां चैत्रीमश्वमेधे नृपस्य नः ॥२७॥
transliteration: tamarjunaḥ samāśvāsya punarevedamabravīt ,āgantavyaṁ parāṁ caitrīmaśvamedhe nṛpasya naḥ.
english: Arjuna, having comforted him, then spoke this again: 'You must come to our king's aśvamedha (Vedic ritual) in the coming month of Caitra.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 83 Verse 27. Arjuna, having comforted him, then spoke this again: 'You must come to our king's aśvamedha (Vedic ritual) in the coming month of Caitra.'

sanskrit: इत्युक्तः स तथेत्युक्त्वा पूजयामास तं हयम् ।फल्गुनं च युधां श्रेष्ठं विधिवत्सहदेवजः ॥२८॥
transliteration: ityuktaḥ sa tathetyuktvā pūjayāmāsa taṁ hayam ,phalgunaṁ ca yudhāṁ śreṣṭhaṁ vidhivatsahadevajaḥ.
english: Having been told this, he (Sahadevaja) said, 'So be it,' and then duly worshipped that horse and Phalguna (Arjuna), the foremost of warriors.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 83 Verse 28. Having been told this, he (Sahadevaja) said, 'So be it,' and then duly worshipped that horse and Phalguna (Arjuna), the foremost of warriors.

sanskrit: ततो यथेष्टमगमत्पुनरेव स केसरी ।ततः समुद्रतीरेण वङ्गान्पुण्ड्रान्सकेरलान् ॥२९॥
transliteration: tato yatheṣṭamagamatpunareva sa kesarī ,tataḥ samudratīreṇa vaṅgānpuṇḍrānsakeralān.
english: Then, that horse (likened to a lion) again went wherever it pleased. After that, it proceeded along the seashore through the Vangas, Pundras, and including the Keralas.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 83 Verse 29. Then, that horse (likened to a lion) again went wherever it pleased. After that, it proceeded along the seashore through the Vangas, Pundras, and including the Keralas.

sanskrit: तत्र तत्र च भूरीणि म्लेच्छसैन्यान्यनेकशः ।विजिग्ये धनुषा राजन्गाण्डीवेन धनंजयः ॥३०॥
transliteration: tatra tatra ca bhūrīṇi mlecchasainyānyanekaśaḥ ,vijigye dhanuṣā rājangāṇḍīvena dhanaṁjayaḥ.
english: And there, in many places, Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), O king, repeatedly conquered numerous barbarian armies with his Gāṇḍīva bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 83 Verse 30. And there, in many places, Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), O king, repeatedly conquered numerous barbarian armies with his Gāṇḍīva bow.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।मागधेनार्चितो राजन्पाण्डवः श्वेतवाहनः ।दक्षिणां दिशमास्थाय चारयामास तं हयम् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,māgadhenārcito rājanpāṇḍavaḥ śvetavāhanaḥ ,dakṣiṇāṁ diśamāsthāya cārayāmāsa taṁ hayam.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: O King, the Pāṇḍava Arjuna, known for his white steeds, after being honored by the king of Magadha, took his position in the southern direction and caused that horse to wander.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 84 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: O King, the Pāṇḍava Arjuna, known for his white steeds, after being honored by the king of Magadha, took his position in the southern direction and caused that horse to wander.

sanskrit: ततः स पुनरावृत्य हयः कामचरो बली ।आससाद पुरीं रम्यां चेदीनां शुक्तिसाह्वयाम् ॥२॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa punarāvṛtya hayaḥ kāmacaro balī ,āsasāda purīṁ ramyāṁ cedīnāṁ śuktisāhvayām.
english: Then, that strong horse, moving at will, returned again and reached the beautiful city of the Cedis named Śuktisa.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 84 Verse 2. Then, that strong horse, moving at will, returned again and reached the beautiful city of the Cedis named Śuktisa.

sanskrit: शरभेणार्चितस्तत्र शिशुपालात्मजेन सः ।युद्धपूर्वेण मानेन पूजया च महाबलः ॥३॥
transliteration: śarabheṇārcitastatra śiśupālātmajena saḥ ,yuddhapūrveṇa mānena pūjayā ca mahābalaḥ.
english: There, that mighty Arjuna was honored by Śarabha, the son of Śiśupāla, with respect and offerings (pūjā) preceded by a battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 84 Verse 3. There, that mighty Arjuna was honored by Śarabha, the son of Śiśupāla, with respect and offerings (pūjā) preceded by a battle.

sanskrit: तत्रार्चितो ययौ राजंस्तदा स तुरगोत्तमः ।काशीनन्ध्रान्कोसलांश्च किरातानथ तङ्गणान् ॥४॥
transliteration: tatrārcito yayau rājaṁstadā sa turagottamaḥ ,kāśīnandhrānkosalāṁśca kirātānatha taṅgaṇān.
english: O King, after being honored there, that excellent horse then went to the Kāśis, the Āndhras, the Kosalas, the Kirātas, and then the Taṅgaṇas.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 84 Verse 4. O King, after being honored there, that excellent horse then went to the Kāśis, the Āndhras, the Kosalas, the Kirātas, and then the Taṅgaṇas.

sanskrit: तत्र पूजां यथान्यायं प्रतिगृह्य स पाण्डवः ।पुनरावृत्य कौन्तेयो दशार्णानगमत्तदा ॥५॥
transliteration: tatra pūjāṁ yathānyāyaṁ pratigṛhya sa pāṇḍavaḥ ,punarāvṛtya kaunteyo daśārṇānagamattadā.
english: Having accepted the honors there, as was proper, that son of Pāṇḍu (Pāṇḍava), the son of Kuntī, then returned and went to the Daśārṇa country.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 84 Verse 5. Having accepted the honors there, as was proper, that son of Pāṇḍu (Pāṇḍava), the son of Kuntī, then returned and went to the Daśārṇa country.

sanskrit: तत्र चित्राङ्गदो नाम बलवान्वसुधाधिपः ।तेन युद्धमभूत्तस्य विजयस्यातिभैरवम् ॥६॥
transliteration: tatra citrāṅgado nāma balavānvasudhādhipaḥ ,tena yuddhamabhūttasya vijayasyātibhairavam.
english: There, a powerful king named Citrāṅgada, a ruler of the earth, resided. A very dreadful battle took place between him and Arjuna (the victorious one).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 84 Verse 6. There, a powerful king named Citrāṅgada, a ruler of the earth, resided. A very dreadful battle took place between him and Arjuna (the victorious one).

sanskrit: तं चापि वशमानीय किरीटी पुरुषर्षभः ।निषादराज्ञो विषयमेकलव्यस्य जग्मिवान् ॥७॥
transliteration: taṁ cāpi vaśamānīya kirīṭī puruṣarṣabhaḥ ,niṣādarājño viṣayamekalavyasya jagmivān.
english: And having brought him (Citrāṅgada) under his control, Arjuna (the crowned one), the best of men, then went to the territory of Ekalavya, the king of the Niṣādas.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 84 Verse 7. And having brought him (Citrāṅgada) under his control, Arjuna (the crowned one), the best of men, then went to the territory of Ekalavya, the king of the Niṣādas.

sanskrit: एकलव्यसुतश्चैनं युद्धेन जगृहे तदा ।ततश्चक्रे निषादैः स संग्रामं रोमहर्षणम् ॥८॥
transliteration: ekalavyasutaścainaṁ yuddhena jagṛhe tadā ,tataścakre niṣādaiḥ sa saṁgrāmaṁ romaharṣaṇam.
english: And then, Ekalavya's son challenged him (Arjuna) to battle. Consequently, Arjuna fought a dreadful, hair-raising battle with the Niṣādas.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 84 Verse 8. And then, Ekalavya's son challenged him (Arjuna) to battle. Consequently, Arjuna fought a dreadful, hair-raising battle with the Niṣādas.

sanskrit: ततस्तमपि कौन्तेयः समरेष्वपराजितः ।जिगाय समरे वीरो यज्ञविघ्नार्थमुद्यतम् ॥९॥
transliteration: tatastamapi kaunteyaḥ samareṣvaparājitaḥ ,jigāya samare vīro yajñavighnārthamudyatam.
english: Then, the undefeated son of Kunti, the hero, conquered him in battle who was prepared to obstruct the Vedic ritual (yajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 84 Verse 9. Then, the undefeated son of Kunti, the hero, conquered him in battle who was prepared to obstruct the Vedic ritual (yajña).

sanskrit: स तं जित्वा महाराज नैषादिं पाकशासनिः ।अर्चितः प्रययौ भूयो दक्षिणं सलिलार्णवम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: sa taṁ jitvā mahārāja naiṣādiṁ pākaśāsaniḥ ,arcitaḥ prayayau bhūyo dakṣiṇaṁ salilārṇavam.
english: O great king, having conquered that Niṣāda, the son of Indra (Arjuna), being honored, proceeded again to the southern ocean.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 84 Verse 10. O great king, having conquered that Niṣāda, the son of Indra (Arjuna), being honored, proceeded again to the southern ocean.

sanskrit: तत्रापि द्रविडैरन्ध्रै रौद्रैर्माहिषकैरपि ।तथा कोल्लगिरेयैश्च युद्धमासीत्किरीटिनः ॥११॥
transliteration: tatrāpi draviḍairandhrai raudrairmāhiṣakairapi ,tathā kollagireyaiśca yuddhamāsītkirīṭinaḥ.
english: There also, a battle ensued for the crowned one (Arjuna) with the Draviḍas, the Āndhras, the fierce Māhiṣakas, and similarly with the Kollagirīyas.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 84 Verse 11. There also, a battle ensued for the crowned one (Arjuna) with the Draviḍas, the Āndhras, the fierce Māhiṣakas, and similarly with the Kollagirīyas.

sanskrit: तुरगस्य वशेनाथ सुराष्ट्रानभितो ययौ ।गोकर्णमपि चासाद्य प्रभासमपि जग्मिवान् ॥१२॥
transliteration: turagasya vaśenātha surāṣṭrānabhito yayau ,gokarṇamapi cāsādya prabhāsamapi jagmivān.
english: Then, following the horse, he went towards Surashtra. And having reached Gokarna, he also proceeded to Prabhasa.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 84 Verse 12. Then, following the horse, he went towards Surashtra. And having reached Gokarna, he also proceeded to Prabhasa.

sanskrit: ततो द्वारवतीं रम्यां वृष्णिवीराभिरक्षिताम् ।आससाद हयः श्रीमान्कुरुराजस्य यज्ञियः ॥१३॥
transliteration: tato dvāravatīṁ ramyāṁ vṛṣṇivīrābhirakṣitām ,āsasāda hayaḥ śrīmānkururājasya yajñiyaḥ.
english: Then the magnificent horse of the Kuru king, intended for the great Vedic ritual (yajña), arrived at the beautiful city of Dvāravatī, which was well-protected by the valiant Vṛṣṇi heroes.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 84 Verse 13. Then the magnificent horse of the Kuru king, intended for the great Vedic ritual (yajña), arrived at the beautiful city of Dvāravatī, which was well-protected by the valiant Vṛṣṇi heroes.

sanskrit: तमुन्मथ्य हयश्रेष्ठं यादवानां कुमारकाः ।प्रययुस्तांस्तदा राजन्नुग्रसेनो न्यवारयत् ॥१४॥
transliteration: tamunmathya hayaśreṣṭhaṁ yādavānāṁ kumārakāḥ ,prayayustāṁstadā rājannugraseno nyavārayat.
english: O king, having seized that excellent horse, the young Yadava princes were about to rush forth (to fight), but then Ugrasena restrained them.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 84 Verse 14. O king, having seized that excellent horse, the young Yadava princes were about to rush forth (to fight), but then Ugrasena restrained them.

sanskrit: ततः पुर्या विनिष्क्रम्य वृष्ण्यन्धकपतिस्तदा ।सहितो वसुदेवेन मातुलेन किरीटिनः ॥१५॥
transliteration: tataḥ puryā viniṣkramya vṛṣṇyandhakapatistadā ,sahito vasudevena mātulena kirīṭinaḥ.
english: Then, having emerged from the city, the lord of the Vṛṣṇis and Andhakas (Ugrasena) at that time, accompanied by Vasudeva, who was the maternal uncle of Arjuna (Kirīṭin).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 84 Verse 15. Then, having emerged from the city, the lord of the Vṛṣṇis and Andhakas (Ugrasena) at that time, accompanied by Vasudeva, who was the maternal uncle of Arjuna (Kirīṭin).

sanskrit: तौ समेत्य कुरुश्रेष्ठं विधिवत्प्रीतिपूर्वकम् ।परया भरतश्रेष्ठं पूजया समवस्थितौ ।ततस्ताभ्यामनुज्ञातो ययौ येन हयो गतः ॥१६॥
transliteration: tau sametya kuruśreṣṭhaṁ vidhivatprītipūrvakam ,parayā bharataśreṣṭhaṁ pūjayā samavasthitau ,tatastābhyāmanujñāto yayau yena hayo gataḥ.
english: Having formally and affectionately met that best among the Kurus (Arjuna) and honored him, O best among the Bharatas, with supreme reverence, the two (Ugrasena and Vasudeva) then remained there. Subsequently, having been permitted by them, he (Arjuna) departed to follow the horse.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 84 Verse 16. Having formally and affectionately met that best among the Kurus (Arjuna) and honored him, O best among the Bharatas, with supreme reverence, the two (Ugrasena and Vasudeva) then remained there. Subsequently, having been permitted by them, he (Arjuna) departed to follow the horse.

sanskrit: ततः स पश्चिमं देशं समुद्रस्य तदा हयः ।क्रमेण व्यचरत्स्फीतं ततः पञ्चनदं ययौ ॥१७॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa paścimaṁ deśaṁ samudrasya tadā hayaḥ ,krameṇa vyacaratsphītaṁ tataḥ pañcanadaṁ yayau.
english: Then, the horse gradually traversed the prosperous western region by the ocean. From there, it proceeded to the land of Pañcanada.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 84 Verse 17. Then, the horse gradually traversed the prosperous western region by the ocean. From there, it proceeded to the land of Pañcanada.

sanskrit: तस्मादपि स कौरव्य गान्धारविषयं हयः ।विचचार यथाकामं कौन्तेयानुगतस्तदा ॥१८॥
transliteration: tasmādapi sa kauravya gāndhāraviṣayaṁ hayaḥ ,vicacāra yathākāmaṁ kaunteyānugatastadā.
english: O descendant of Kuru (kauravya), from there the horse roamed the region of Gandhāra (Gāndhāra) as it pleased, followed by the son of Kunti (Kaunteya) at that time.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 84 Verse 18. O descendant of Kuru (kauravya), from there the horse roamed the region of Gandhāra (Gāndhāra) as it pleased, followed by the son of Kunti (Kaunteya) at that time.

sanskrit: तत्र गान्धारराजेन युद्धमासीन्महात्मनः ।घोरं शकुनिपुत्रेण पूर्ववैरानुसारिणा ॥१९॥
transliteration: tatra gāndhārarājena yuddhamāsīnmahātmanaḥ ,ghoraṁ śakuniputreṇa pūrvavairānusāriṇā.
english: There, a fierce battle ensued for the great-souled (mahātman) Arjuna with the king of Gandhāra, Śakuni's son, who was driven by past enmity.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 84 Verse 19. There, a fierce battle ensued for the great-souled (mahātman) Arjuna with the king of Gandhāra, Śakuni's son, who was driven by past enmity.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।शकुनेस्तु सुतो वीरो गान्धाराणां महारथः ।प्रत्युद्ययौ गुडाकेशं सैन्येन महता वृतः ।हस्त्यश्वरथपूर्णेन पताकाध्वजमालिना ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,śakunestu suto vīro gāndhārāṇāṁ mahārathaḥ ,pratyudyayau guḍākeśaṁ sainyena mahatā vṛtaḥ ,hastyaśvarathapūrṇena patākādhvajamālinā.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then, Śakuni's heroic son, a great warrior (mahāratha) among the Gandhāras, advanced to meet Guḍākeśa [Arjuna]. He was accompanied by a vast army, complete with elephants, horses, and chariots, and adorned with flags and banners.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 85 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Then, Śakuni's heroic son, a great warrior (mahāratha) among the Gandhāras, advanced to meet Guḍākeśa [Arjuna]. He was accompanied by a vast army, complete with elephants, horses, and chariots, and adorned with flags and banners.

sanskrit: अमृष्यमाणास्ते योधा नृपतेः शकुनेर्वधम् ।अभ्ययुः सहिताः पार्थं प्रगृहीतशरासनाः ॥२॥
transliteration: amṛṣyamāṇāste yodhā nṛpateḥ śakunervadham ,abhyayuḥ sahitāḥ pārthaṁ pragṛhītaśarāsanāḥ.
english: Unable to tolerate the slaying of King Shakuni, those warriors, united and with bows grasped, advanced upon Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 85 Verse 2. Unable to tolerate the slaying of King Shakuni, those warriors, united and with bows grasped, advanced upon Arjuna.

sanskrit: तानुवाच स धर्मात्मा बीभत्सुरपराजितः ।युधिष्ठिरस्य वचनं न च ते जगृहुर्हितम् ॥३॥
transliteration: tānuvāca sa dharmātmā bībhatsuraparājitaḥ ,yudhiṣṭhirasya vacanaṁ na ca te jagṛhurhitam.
english: He, the virtuous (dharma-ātmā) Arjuna, the unconquered Bibhatsu, spoke Yudhishthira's words to them, but they did not accept his beneficial advice.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 85 Verse 3. He, the virtuous (dharma-ātmā) Arjuna, the unconquered Bibhatsu, spoke Yudhishthira's words to them, but they did not accept his beneficial advice.

sanskrit: वार्यमाणास्तु पार्थेन सान्त्वपूर्वममर्षिताः ।परिवार्य हयं जग्मुस्ततश्चुक्रोध पाण्डवः ॥४॥
transliteration: vāryamāṇāstu pārthena sāntvapūrvamamarṣitāḥ ,parivārya hayaṁ jagmustataścukrodha pāṇḍavaḥ.
english: But those infuriated warriors, even though being restrained by Arjuna with conciliatory words, surrounded the horse and advanced. Thereupon, the Pandava (Arjuna) became enraged.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 85 Verse 4. But those infuriated warriors, even though being restrained by Arjuna with conciliatory words, surrounded the horse and advanced. Thereupon, the Pandava (Arjuna) became enraged.

sanskrit: ततः शिरांसि दीप्ताग्रैस्तेषां चिच्छेद पाण्डवः ।क्षुरैर्गाण्डीवनिर्मुक्तैर्नातियत्नादिवार्जुनः ॥५॥
transliteration: tataḥ śirāṁsi dīptāgraisteṣāṁ ciccheda pāṇḍavaḥ ,kṣurairgāṇḍīvanirmuktairnātiyatnādivārjunaḥ.
english: Then the Pandava (Arjuna) cut off their heads with razor-sharp arrows released from his Gāṇḍīva bow, as if without much effort.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 85 Verse 5. Then the Pandava (Arjuna) cut off their heads with razor-sharp arrows released from his Gāṇḍīva bow, as if without much effort.

sanskrit: ते वध्यमानाः पार्थेन हयमुत्सृज्य संभ्रमात् ।न्यवर्तन्त महाराज शरवर्षार्दिता भृशम् ॥६॥
transliteration: te vadhyamānāḥ pārthena hayamutsṛjya saṁbhramāt ,nyavartanta mahārāja śaravarṣārditā bhṛśam.
english: O great king, those warriors, being attacked by Pārtha and greatly afflicted by a shower of arrows, abandoned their horses in bewilderment and retreated.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 85 Verse 6. O great king, those warriors, being attacked by Pārtha and greatly afflicted by a shower of arrows, abandoned their horses in bewilderment and retreated.

sanskrit: वितुद्यमानस्तैश्चापि गान्धारैः पाण्डवर्षभः ।आदिश्यादिश्य तेजस्वी शिरांस्येषां न्यपातयत् ॥७॥
transliteration: vitudyamānastaiścāpi gāndhāraiḥ pāṇḍavarṣabhaḥ ,ādiśyādiśya tejasvī śirāṁsyeṣāṁ nyapātayat.
english: Even while being harassed by those Gandharas, the brilliant foremost of the Pāṇḍavas (Arjuna), repeatedly aiming, struck off their heads.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 85 Verse 7. Even while being harassed by those Gandharas, the brilliant foremost of the Pāṇḍavas (Arjuna), repeatedly aiming, struck off their heads.

sanskrit: वध्यमानेषु तेष्वाजौ गान्धारेषु समन्ततः ।स राजा शकुनेः पुत्रः पाण्डवं प्रत्यवारयत् ॥८॥
transliteration: vadhyamāneṣu teṣvājau gāndhāreṣu samantataḥ ,sa rājā śakuneḥ putraḥ pāṇḍavaṁ pratyavārayat.
english: As those Gandharas were being slain on all sides in battle, that king, the son of Śakuni, resisted the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 85 Verse 8. As those Gandharas were being slain on all sides in battle, that king, the son of Śakuni, resisted the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna).

sanskrit: तं युध्यमानं राजानं क्षत्रधर्मे व्यवस्थितम् ।पार्थोऽब्रवीन्न मे वध्या राजानो राजशासनात् ।अलं युद्धेन ते वीर न तेऽस्त्यद्य पराजयः ॥९॥
transliteration: taṁ yudhyamānaṁ rājānaṁ kṣatradharme vyavasthitam ,pārtho'bravīnna me vadhyā rājāno rājaśāsanāt ,alaṁ yuddhena te vīra na te'styadya parājayaḥ.
english: To that king, who was fighting and upholding the intrinsic nature (dharma) of a warrior, Pārtha (Arjuna) said: "Kings are not to be slain by me according to royal command. Enough of fighting for you, O hero; you have no defeat today."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 85 Verse 9. To that king, who was fighting and upholding the intrinsic nature (dharma) of a warrior, Pārtha (Arjuna) said: "Kings are not to be slain by me according to royal command. Enough of fighting for you, O hero; you have no defeat today."

sanskrit: इत्युक्तस्तदनादृत्य वाक्यमज्ञानमोहितः ।स शक्रसमकर्माणमवाकिरत सायकैः ॥१०॥
transliteration: ityuktastadanādṛtya vākyamajñānamohitaḥ ,sa śakrasamakarmāṇamavākirata sāyakaiḥ.
english: Thus spoken to, he, deluded by ignorance, disregarded that statement and showered with arrows the one whose deeds were equal to Indra's.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 85 Verse 10. Thus spoken to, he, deluded by ignorance, disregarded that statement and showered with arrows the one whose deeds were equal to Indra's.

sanskrit: तस्य पार्थः शिरस्त्राणमर्धचन्द्रेण पत्रिणा ।अपाहरदसंभ्रान्तो जयद्रथशिरो यथा ॥११॥
transliteration: tasya pārthaḥ śirastrāṇamardhacandreṇa patriṇā ,apāharadasaṁbhrānto jayadrathaśiro yathā.
english: Undisturbed, Pārtha removed his helmet with a crescent-shaped arrow, just as he had removed Jayadratha's head.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 85 Verse 11. Undisturbed, Pārtha removed his helmet with a crescent-shaped arrow, just as he had removed Jayadratha's head.

sanskrit: तद्दृष्ट्वा विस्मयं जग्मुर्गान्धाराः सर्व एव ते ।इच्छता तेन न हतो राजेत्यपि च ते विदुः ॥१२॥
transliteration: taddṛṣṭvā vismayaṁ jagmurgāndhārāḥ sarva eva te ,icchatā tena na hato rājetyapi ca te viduḥ.
english: Having witnessed that, all those Gāndhāras were indeed astonished. And they also understood that the king was not killed by him out of his own desire.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 85 Verse 12. Having witnessed that, all those Gāndhāras were indeed astonished. And they also understood that the king was not killed by him out of his own desire.

sanskrit: गान्धारराजपुत्रस्तु पलायनकृतक्षणः ।बभौ तैरेव सहितस्त्रस्तैः क्षुद्रमृगैरिव ॥१३॥
transliteration: gāndhārarājaputrastu palāyanakṛtakṣaṇaḥ ,babhau taireva sahitastrastaiḥ kṣudramṛgairiva.
english: But the Gāndhāra king's son (Jayadratha), who was poised for flight, appeared with those very frightened ones, just like timid deer.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 85 Verse 13. But the Gāndhāra king's son (Jayadratha), who was poised for flight, appeared with those very frightened ones, just like timid deer.

sanskrit: तेषां तु तरसा पार्थस्तत्रैव परिधावताम् ।विजहारोत्तमाङ्गानि भल्लैः संनतपर्वभिः ॥१४॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ tu tarasā pārthastatraiva paridhāvatām ,vijahārottamāṅgāni bhallaiḥ saṁnataparvabhiḥ.
english: Arjuna, with great speed, severed the heads of those who were running about right there, using arrows with bent shafts.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 85 Verse 14. Arjuna, with great speed, severed the heads of those who were running about right there, using arrows with bent shafts.

sanskrit: उच्छ्रितांस्तु भुजान्केचिन्नाबुध्यन्त शरैर्हृतान् ।शरैर्गाण्डीवनिर्मुक्तैः पृथुभिः पार्थचोदितैः ॥१५॥
transliteration: ucchritāṁstu bhujānkecinnābudhyanta śarairhṛtān ,śarairgāṇḍīvanirmuktaiḥ pṛthubhiḥ pārthacoditaiḥ.
english: Some of them did not even realize that their raised arms had been severed by the broad arrows, which were released from the Gāṇḍīva bow and impelled by (Arjuna) Pārtha.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 85 Verse 15. Some of them did not even realize that their raised arms had been severed by the broad arrows, which were released from the Gāṇḍīva bow and impelled by (Arjuna) Pārtha.

sanskrit: संभ्रान्तनरनागाश्वमथ तद्विद्रुतं बलम् ।हतविध्वस्तभूयिष्ठमावर्तत मुहुर्मुहुः ॥१६॥
transliteration: saṁbhrāntanaranāgāśvamatha tadvidrutaṁ balam ,hatavidhvastabhūyiṣṭhamāvartata muhurmuhuḥ.
english: Then that army, whose men, elephants, and horses were bewildered and scattered, and which was mostly slain and shattered, repeatedly wheeled around.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 85 Verse 16. Then that army, whose men, elephants, and horses were bewildered and scattered, and which was mostly slain and shattered, repeatedly wheeled around.

sanskrit: न ह्यदृश्यन्त वीरस्य केचिदग्रेऽग्र्यकर्मणः ।रिपवः पात्यमाना वै ये सहेयुर्महाशरान् ॥१७॥
transliteration: na hyadṛśyanta vīrasya kecidagre'gryakarmaṇaḥ ,ripavaḥ pātyamānā vai ye saheyurmahāśarān.
english: Indeed, no enemies who could endure his great arrows were seen being felled before that hero of excellent deeds.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 85 Verse 17. Indeed, no enemies who could endure his great arrows were seen being felled before that hero of excellent deeds.

sanskrit: ततो गान्धारराजस्य मन्त्रिवृद्धपुरःसरा ।जननी निर्ययौ भीता पुरस्कृत्यार्घ्यमुत्तमम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: tato gāndhārarājasya mantrivṛddhapuraḥsarā ,jananī niryayau bhītā puraskṛtyārghyamuttamam.
english: Then, the mother of the king of Gāndhāra, preceded by the elderly ministers, emerged fearfully, holding forth an excellent respectful offering.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 85 Verse 18. Then, the mother of the king of Gāndhāra, preceded by the elderly ministers, emerged fearfully, holding forth an excellent respectful offering.

sanskrit: सा न्यवारयदव्यग्रा तं पुत्रं युद्धदुर्मदम् ।प्रसादयामास च तं जिष्णुमक्लिष्टकारिणम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: sā nyavārayadavyagrā taṁ putraṁ yuddhadurmadam ,prasādayāmāsa ca taṁ jiṣṇumakliṣṭakāriṇam.
english: She, undisturbed, restrained her son, who was maddened by battle. And she also appeased Jishnu (Arjuna), the one who performs deeds effortlessly.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 85 Verse 19. She, undisturbed, restrained her son, who was maddened by battle. And she also appeased Jishnu (Arjuna), the one who performs deeds effortlessly.

sanskrit: तां पूजयित्वा कौन्तेयः प्रसादमकरोत्तदा ।शकुनेश्चापि तनयं सान्त्वयन्निदमब्रवीत् ॥२०॥
transliteration: tāṁ pūjayitvā kaunteyaḥ prasādamakarottadā ,śakuneścāpi tanayaṁ sāntvayannidamabravīt.
english: Having honored her, Kaunteya (Arjuna) then showed favor. And conciliating Shakuni's son (Ulūka), he also said this:
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 85 Verse 20. Having honored her, Kaunteya (Arjuna) then showed favor. And conciliating Shakuni's son (Ulūka), he also said this:

sanskrit: न मे प्रियं महाबाहो यत्ते बुद्धिरियं कृता ।प्रतियोद्धुममित्रघ्न भ्रातैव त्वं ममानघ ॥२१॥
transliteration: na me priyaṁ mahābāho yatte buddhiriyaṁ kṛtā ,pratiyoddhumamitraghna bhrātaiva tvaṁ mamānagha.
english: "O mighty-armed one, it is not pleasing to me that this intention to fight back has been made by you. O slayer of enemies, you are indeed my brother, O sinless one."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 85 Verse 21. "O mighty-armed one, it is not pleasing to me that this intention to fight back has been made by you. O slayer of enemies, you are indeed my brother, O sinless one."

sanskrit: गान्धारीं मातरं स्मृत्वा धृतराष्ट्रकृतेन च ।तेन जीवसि राजंस्त्वं निहतास्त्वनुगास्तव ॥२२॥
transliteration: gāndhārīṁ mātaraṁ smṛtvā dhṛtarāṣṭrakṛtena ca ,tena jīvasi rājaṁstvaṁ nihatāstvanugāstava.
english: Remembering your mother Gāndhārī, and for Dhṛtarāṣṭra's sake, you, O King, are alive, but your followers have been killed.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 85 Verse 22. Remembering your mother Gāndhārī, and for Dhṛtarāṣṭra's sake, you, O King, are alive, but your followers have been killed.

sanskrit: मैवं भूः शाम्यतां वैरं मा ते भूद्बुद्धिरीदृशी ।आगन्तव्यं परां चैत्रीमश्वमेधे नृपस्य नः ॥२३॥
transliteration: maivaṁ bhūḥ śāmyatāṁ vairaṁ mā te bhūdbuddhirīdṛśī ,āgantavyaṁ parāṁ caitrīmaśvamedhe nṛpasya naḥ.
english: Do not act in this manner; let the animosity cease. Do not let such a thought come to your mind. You must come for our king's (aśvamedha) Vedic ritual in the coming month of Caitra.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 85 Verse 23. Do not act in this manner; let the animosity cease. Do not let such a thought come to your mind. You must come for our king's (aśvamedha) Vedic ritual in the coming month of Caitra.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।इत्युक्त्वानुययौ पार्थो हयं तं कामचारिणम् ।न्यवर्तत ततो वाजी येन नागाह्वयं पुरम् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,ityuktvānuyayau pārtho hayaṁ taṁ kāmacāriṇam ,nyavartata tato vājī yena nāgāhvayaṁ puram.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having spoken thus, Pārtha (Arjuna) followed that freely wandering horse. From there, the horse turned back towards the city named Nāgāhvaya (Hastināpura).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 86 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Having spoken thus, Pārtha (Arjuna) followed that freely wandering horse. From there, the horse turned back towards the city named Nāgāhvaya (Hastināpura).

sanskrit: तं निवृत्तं तु शुश्राव चारेणैव युधिष्ठिरः ।श्रुत्वार्जुनं कुशलिनं स च हृष्टमनाभवत् ॥२॥
transliteration: taṁ nivṛttaṁ tu śuśrāva cāreṇaiva yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,śrutvārjunaṁ kuśalinaṁ sa ca hṛṣṭamanābhavat.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira heard of its (the horse's) return through a spy. And having heard that Arjuna was well, he (Yudhiṣṭhira) became joyful in mind.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 86 Verse 2. Yudhiṣṭhira heard of its (the horse's) return through a spy. And having heard that Arjuna was well, he (Yudhiṣṭhira) became joyful in mind.

sanskrit: विजयस्य च तत्कर्म गान्धारविषये तदा ।श्रुत्वान्येषु च देशेषु स सुप्रीतोऽभवन्नृपः ॥३॥
transliteration: vijayasya ca tatkarma gāndhāraviṣaye tadā ,śrutvānyeṣu ca deśeṣu sa suprīto'bhavannṛpaḥ.
english: At that time, having heard of Vijaya's action in the Gandhara region and in other lands as well, the king became very pleased.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 86 Verse 3. At that time, having heard of Vijaya's action in the Gandhara region and in other lands as well, the king became very pleased.

sanskrit: एतस्मिन्नेव काले तु द्वादशीं माघपाक्षिकीम् ।इष्टं गृहीत्वा नक्षत्रं धर्मराजो युधिष्ठिरः ॥४॥
transliteration: etasminneva kāle tu dvādaśīṁ māghapākṣikīm ,iṣṭaṁ gṛhītvā nakṣatraṁ dharmarājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: And at this very time, Yudhishthira, the king of (dharma), having chosen the auspicious twelfth day of the Māgha fortnight and a favorable constellation...
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 86 Verse 4. And at this very time, Yudhishthira, the king of (dharma), having chosen the auspicious twelfth day of the Māgha fortnight and a favorable constellation...

sanskrit: समानाय्य महातेजाः सर्वान्भ्रातॄन्महामनाः ।भीमं च नकुलं चैव सहदेवं च कौरवः ॥५॥
transliteration: samānāyya mahātejāḥ sarvānbhrātṝnmahāmanāḥ ,bhīmaṁ ca nakulaṁ caiva sahadevaṁ ca kauravaḥ.
english: The greatly effulgent and great-minded Kuru, having summoned all his brothers - Bhīma, Nakula, and Sahadeva as well -
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 86 Verse 5. The greatly effulgent and great-minded Kuru, having summoned all his brothers - Bhīma, Nakula, and Sahadeva as well -

sanskrit: प्रोवाचेदं वचः काले तदा धर्मभृतां वरः ।आमन्त्र्य वदतां श्रेष्ठो भीमं भीमपराक्रमम् ॥६॥
transliteration: provācedaṁ vacaḥ kāle tadā dharmabhṛtāṁ varaḥ ,āmantrya vadatāṁ śreṣṭho bhīmaṁ bhīmaparākramam.
english: Then, at that opportune moment, the best among those who uphold (dharma) and the foremost among speakers, having addressed Bhīma, who possessed formidable prowess, uttered these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 86 Verse 6. Then, at that opportune moment, the best among those who uphold (dharma) and the foremost among speakers, having addressed Bhīma, who possessed formidable prowess, uttered these words.

sanskrit: आयाति भीमसेनासौ सहाश्वेन तवानुजः ।यथा मे पुरुषाः प्राहुर्ये धनंजयसारिणः ॥७॥
transliteration: āyāti bhīmasenāsau sahāśvena tavānujaḥ ,yathā me puruṣāḥ prāhurye dhanaṁjayasāriṇaḥ.
english: That Bhīmasena, your younger brother, is approaching with his horse, as my men who accompany Dhananjaya (Arjuna) report.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 86 Verse 7. That Bhīmasena, your younger brother, is approaching with his horse, as my men who accompany Dhananjaya (Arjuna) report.

sanskrit: उपस्थितश्च कालोऽयमभितो वर्तते हयः ।माघी च पौर्णमासीयं मासः शेषो वृकोदर ॥८॥
transliteration: upasthitaśca kālo'yamabhito vartate hayaḥ ,māghī ca paurṇamāsīyaṁ māsaḥ śeṣo vṛkodara.
english: This appointed time has arrived, and the horse is now nearby. This is the full moon of Māgha, O Vṛkodara; only one month remains.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 86 Verse 8. This appointed time has arrived, and the horse is now nearby. This is the full moon of Māgha, O Vṛkodara; only one month remains.

sanskrit: तत्प्रस्थाप्यन्तु विद्वांसो ब्राह्मणा वेदपारगाः ।वाजिमेधार्थसिद्ध्यर्थं देशं पश्यन्तु यज्ञियम् ॥९॥
transliteration: tatprasthāpyantu vidvāṁso brāhmaṇā vedapāragāḥ ,vājimedhārthasiddhyarthaṁ deśaṁ paśyantu yajñiyam.
english: Therefore, let learned Brahmins, who are experts in the Vedas, be dispatched to survey a suitable place for the purpose of achieving the goal of the horse- (aśvamedha) Vedic ritual (yajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 86 Verse 9. Therefore, let learned Brahmins, who are experts in the Vedas, be dispatched to survey a suitable place for the purpose of achieving the goal of the horse- (aśvamedha) Vedic ritual (yajña).

sanskrit: इत्युक्तः स तु तच्चक्रे भीमो नृपतिशासनम् ।हृष्टः श्रुत्वा नरपतेरायान्तं सव्यसाचिनम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: ityuktaḥ sa tu taccakre bhīmo nṛpatiśāsanam ,hṛṣṭaḥ śrutvā narapaterāyāntaṁ savyasācinam.
english: Thus spoken to, Bhīma indeed carried out that command of the king, joyful upon hearing of Savyasācin (Arjuna) approaching.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 86 Verse 10. Thus spoken to, Bhīma indeed carried out that command of the king, joyful upon hearing of Savyasācin (Arjuna) approaching.

sanskrit: ततो ययौ भीमसेनः प्राज्ञैः स्थपतिभिः सह ।ब्राह्मणानग्रतः कृत्वा कुशलान्यज्ञकर्मसु ॥११॥
transliteration: tato yayau bhīmasenaḥ prājñaiḥ sthapatibhiḥ saha ,brāhmaṇānagrataḥ kṛtvā kuśalānyajñakarmasu.
english: Then Bhimasena went, accompanied by skilled architects, having placed at the forefront the Brahmins who were expert in the performance of (Vedic ritual)s.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 86 Verse 11. Then Bhimasena went, accompanied by skilled architects, having placed at the forefront the Brahmins who were expert in the performance of (Vedic ritual)s.

sanskrit: तं सशालचयग्रामं संप्रतोलीविटङ्किनम् ।मापयामास कौरव्यो यज्ञवाटं यथाविधि ॥१२॥
transliteration: taṁ saśālacayagrāmaṁ saṁpratolīviṭaṅkinam ,māpayāmāsa kauravyo yajñavāṭaṁ yathāvidhi.
english: The Kuru prince had that (Vedic ritual) ground measured out and constructed according to the prescribed rules, complete with rows of halls and dwelling places, and adorned with broad streets and balconies.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 86 Verse 12. The Kuru prince had that (Vedic ritual) ground measured out and constructed according to the prescribed rules, complete with rows of halls and dwelling places, and adorned with broad streets and balconies.

sanskrit: सदः सपत्नीसदनं साग्नीध्रमपि चोत्तरम् ।कारयामास विधिवन्मणिहेमविभूषितम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: sadaḥ sapatnīsadanaṁ sāgnīdhramapi cottaram ,kārayāmāsa vidhivanmaṇihemavibhūṣitam.
english: He also had the assembly hall, the wives' quarters, the Agnimdhra fire-chamber, and the northern hall constructed properly, adorned with jewels and gold.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 86 Verse 13. He also had the assembly hall, the wives' quarters, the Agnimdhra fire-chamber, and the northern hall constructed properly, adorned with jewels and gold.

sanskrit: स्तम्भान्कनकचित्रांश्च तोरणानि बृहन्ति च ।यज्ञायतनदेशेषु दत्त्वा शुद्धं च काञ्चनम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: stambhānkanakacitrāṁśca toraṇāni bṛhanti ca ,yajñāyatanadeśeṣu dattvā śuddhaṁ ca kāñcanam.
english: And having established pillars decorated with gold and large gateways in the areas of the (Vedic ritual) altars, he also provided pure gold.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 86 Verse 14. And having established pillars decorated with gold and large gateways in the areas of the (Vedic ritual) altars, he also provided pure gold.

sanskrit: अन्तःपुराणि राज्ञां च नानादेशनिवासिनाम् ।कारयामास धर्मात्मा तत्र तत्र यथाविधि ॥१५॥
transliteration: antaḥpurāṇi rājñāṁ ca nānādeśanivāsinām ,kārayāmāsa dharmātmā tatra tatra yathāvidhi.
english: The righteous one (dharmātman) also had inner palaces built for kings and residents from various countries, here and there, according to the prescribed rites.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 86 Verse 15. The righteous one (dharmātman) also had inner palaces built for kings and residents from various countries, here and there, according to the prescribed rites.

sanskrit: ब्राह्मणानां च वेश्मानि नानादेशसमेयुषाम् ।कारयामास भीमः स विविधानि ह्यनेकशः ॥१६॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇānāṁ ca veśmāni nānādeśasameyuṣām ,kārayāmāsa bhīmaḥ sa vividhāni hyanekaśaḥ.
english: And that Bhīma also had many diverse dwellings built for brahmins who had assembled from various countries.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 86 Verse 16. And that Bhīma also had many diverse dwellings built for brahmins who had assembled from various countries.

sanskrit: तथा संप्रेषयामास दूतान्नृपतिशासनात् ।भीमसेनो महाराज राज्ञामक्लिष्टकर्मणाम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: tathā saṁpreṣayāmāsa dūtānnṛpatiśāsanāt ,bhīmaseno mahārāja rājñāmakliṣṭakarmaṇām.
english: Similarly, O great king, Bhīmasena sent envoys in accordance with the command of the kings, specifically to those kings whose actions were effortless (akliṣṭakarman).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 86 Verse 17. Similarly, O great king, Bhīmasena sent envoys in accordance with the command of the kings, specifically to those kings whose actions were effortless (akliṣṭakarman).

sanskrit: ते प्रियार्थं कुरुपतेराययुर्नृपसत्तमाः ।रत्नान्यनेकान्यादाय स्त्रियोऽश्वानायुधानि च ॥१८॥
transliteration: te priyārthaṁ kurupaterāyayurnṛpasattamāḥ ,ratnānyanekānyādāya striyo'śvānāyudhāni ca.
english: Those foremost among kings came for the pleasure of the lord of Kurus, bringing many jewels, women, horses, and weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 86 Verse 18. Those foremost among kings came for the pleasure of the lord of Kurus, bringing many jewels, women, horses, and weapons.

sanskrit: तेषां निविशतां तेषु शिबिरेषु सहस्रशः ।नर्दतः सागरस्येव शब्दो दिवमिवास्पृशत् ॥१९॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ niviśatāṁ teṣu śibireṣu sahasraśaḥ ,nardataḥ sāgarasyeva śabdo divamivāspṛśat.
english: The sound of those thousands entering their camps, like the roar of the ocean, touched the sky.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 86 Verse 19. The sound of those thousands entering their camps, like the roar of the ocean, touched the sky.

sanskrit: तेषामभ्यागतानां स राजा राजीवलोचनः ।व्यादिदेशान्नपानानि शय्याश्चाप्यतिमानुषाः ॥२०॥
transliteration: teṣāmabhyāgatānāṁ sa rājā rājīvalocanaḥ ,vyādideśānnapānāni śayyāścāpyatimānuṣāḥ.
english: That lotus-eyed king provided food and drink, and also extraordinary beds, for those who had arrived.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 86 Verse 20. That lotus-eyed king provided food and drink, and also extraordinary beds, for those who had arrived.

sanskrit: वाहनानां च विविधाः शालाः शालीक्षुगोरसैः ।उपेताः पुरुषव्याघ्र व्यादिदेश स धर्मराट् ॥२१॥
transliteration: vāhanānāṁ ca vividhāḥ śālāḥ śālīkṣugorasaiḥ ,upetāḥ puruṣavyāghra vyādideśa sa dharmarāṭ.
english: And that king, the upholder of (dharma), O tiger among men, provided various stables for the conveyances, stocked with rice, sugarcane, and dairy products.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 86 Verse 21. And that king, the upholder of (dharma), O tiger among men, provided various stables for the conveyances, stocked with rice, sugarcane, and dairy products.

sanskrit: तथा तस्मिन्महायज्ञे धर्मराजस्य धीमतः ।समाजग्मुर्मुनिगणा बहवो ब्रह्मवादिनः ॥२२॥
transliteration: tathā tasminmahāyajñe dharmarājasya dhīmataḥ ,samājagmurmunigaṇā bahavo brahmavādinaḥ.
english: Thus, many groups of sages (muni), expounders of (brahman), gathered at that great Vedic ritual (yajña) of the wise king of (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 86 Verse 22. Thus, many groups of sages (muni), expounders of (brahman), gathered at that great Vedic ritual (yajña) of the wise king of (dharma).

sanskrit: ये च द्विजातिप्रवरास्तत्रासन्पृथिवीपते ।समाजग्मुः सशिष्यांस्तान्प्रतिजग्राह कौरवः ॥२३॥
transliteration: ye ca dvijātipravarāstatrāsanpṛthivīpate ,samājagmuḥ saśiṣyāṁstānpratijagrāha kauravaḥ.
english: O lord of the earth, the foremost among the twice-born (dvija), who were present there, arrived with their disciples, and the Kuru (Yudhishthira) welcomed them.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 86 Verse 23. O lord of the earth, the foremost among the twice-born (dvija), who were present there, arrived with their disciples, and the Kuru (Yudhishthira) welcomed them.

sanskrit: सर्वांश्च ताननुययौ यावदावसथादिति ।स्वयमेव महातेजा दम्भं त्यक्त्वा युधिष्ठिरः ॥२४॥
transliteration: sarvāṁśca tānanuyayau yāvadāvasathāditi ,svayameva mahātejā dambhaṁ tyaktvā yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: And the greatly energetic Yudhishthira himself, having abandoned hypocrisy, followed all of them even to their residences.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 86 Verse 24. And the greatly energetic Yudhishthira himself, having abandoned hypocrisy, followed all of them even to their residences.

sanskrit: ततः कृत्वा स्थपतयः शिल्पिनोऽन्ये च ये तदा ।कृत्स्नं यज्ञविधिं राजन्धर्मराज्ञे न्यवेदयन् ॥२५॥
transliteration: tataḥ kṛtvā sthapatayaḥ śilpino'nye ca ye tadā ,kṛtsnaṁ yajñavidhiṁ rājandharmarājñe nyavedayan.
english: O king, then the master builders and other artisans, and all those who were present at that time, after completing the entire procedure of the Vedic ritual (yajña), reported it to Dharmaraja (Yudhishthira).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 86 Verse 25. O king, then the master builders and other artisans, and all those who were present at that time, after completing the entire procedure of the Vedic ritual (yajña), reported it to Dharmaraja (Yudhishthira).

sanskrit: तच्छ्रुत्वा धर्मराजः स कृतं सर्वमनिन्दितम् ।हृष्टरूपोऽभवद्राजा सह भ्रातृभिरच्युतः ॥२६॥
transliteration: tacchrutvā dharmarājaḥ sa kṛtaṁ sarvamaninditam ,hṛṣṭarūpo'bhavadrājā saha bhrātṛbhiracyutaḥ.
english: When he heard that all the work had been flawlessly accomplished, Dharmaraja (Yudhishthira), that unswerving (acyuta) king, became joyful in appearance along with his brothers.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 86 Verse 26. When he heard that all the work had been flawlessly accomplished, Dharmaraja (Yudhishthira), that unswerving (acyuta) king, became joyful in appearance along with his brothers.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तस्मिन्यज्ञे प्रवृत्ते तु वाग्मिनो हेतुवादिनः ।हेतुवादान्बहून्प्राहुः परस्परजिगीषवः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tasminyajñe pravṛtte tu vāgmino hetuvādinaḥ ,hetuvādānbahūnprāhuḥ parasparajigīṣavaḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: When that great Vedic ritual (yajña) began, eloquent debaters, eager to overpower each other, put forth many arguments.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 87 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: When that great Vedic ritual (yajña) began, eloquent debaters, eager to overpower each other, put forth many arguments.

sanskrit: ददृशुस्तं नृपतयो यज्ञस्य विधिमुत्तमम् ।देवेन्द्रस्येव विहितं भीमेन कुरुनन्दन ॥२॥
transliteration: dadṛśustaṁ nṛpatayo yajñasya vidhimuttamam ,devendrasyeva vihitaṁ bhīmena kurunandana.
english: O joy of the Kurus, the kings saw that excellent procedure of the Vedic ritual (yajña), which had been arranged by Bhīma as if for the lord of gods (Devendra) himself.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 87 Verse 2. O joy of the Kurus, the kings saw that excellent procedure of the Vedic ritual (yajña), which had been arranged by Bhīma as if for the lord of gods (Devendra) himself.
