sanskrit: ददृशुस्तोरणान्यत्र शातकुम्भमयानि ते ।शय्यासनविहारांश्च सुबहून्रत्नभूषितान् ॥३॥
transliteration: dadṛśustoraṇānyatra śātakumbhamayāni te ,śayyāsanavihārāṁśca subahūnratnabhūṣitān.
english: There they (the kings) saw arches made of pure gold, and many beds, seats, and pleasure-gardens, all adorned with jewels.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 87 Verse 3. There they (the kings) saw arches made of pure gold, and many beds, seats, and pleasure-gardens, all adorned with jewels.

sanskrit: घटान्पात्रीः कटाहानि कलशान्वर्धमानकान् ।न हि किंचिदसौवर्णमपश्यंस्तत्र पार्थिवाः ॥४॥
transliteration: ghaṭānpātrīḥ kaṭāhāni kalaśānvardhamānakān ,na hi kiṁcidasauvarṇamapaśyaṁstatra pārthivāḥ.
english: The kings there saw pots, plates, cauldrons, water-jars, and auspicious vessels; indeed, they saw nothing there that was not made of gold.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 87 Verse 4. The kings there saw pots, plates, cauldrons, water-jars, and auspicious vessels; indeed, they saw nothing there that was not made of gold.

sanskrit: यूपांश्च शास्त्रपठितान्दारवान्हेमभूषितान् ।उपकॢप्तान्यथाकालं विधिवद्भूरिवर्चसः ॥५॥
transliteration: yūpāṁśca śāstrapaṭhitāndāravānhemabhūṣitān ,upakḷptānyathākālaṁ vidhivadbhūrivarcasaḥ.
english: They also saw wooden sacrificial posts (yūpa) that were prescribed by the scriptures, adorned with gold, prepared in due time, according to ritual rules, and shining brightly with great splendor.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 87 Verse 5. They also saw wooden sacrificial posts (yūpa) that were prescribed by the scriptures, adorned with gold, prepared in due time, according to ritual rules, and shining brightly with great splendor.

sanskrit: स्थलजा जलजा ये च पशवः केचन प्रभो ।सर्वानेव समानीतांस्तानपश्यन्त ते नृपाः ॥६॥
transliteration: sthalajā jalajā ye ca paśavaḥ kecana prabho ,sarvāneva samānītāṁstānapaśyanta te nṛpāḥ.
english: And those kings (nṛpa) saw all those animals - whether terrestrial or aquatic, whatever they might be, O lord - which had been gathered together.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 87 Verse 6. And those kings (nṛpa) saw all those animals - whether terrestrial or aquatic, whatever they might be, O lord - which had been gathered together.

sanskrit: गाश्चैव महिषीश्चैव तथा वृद्धाः स्त्रियोऽपि च ।औदकानि च सत्त्वानि श्वापदानि वयांसि च ॥७॥
transliteration: gāścaiva mahiṣīścaiva tathā vṛddhāḥ striyo'pi ca ,audakāni ca sattvāni śvāpadāni vayāṁsi ca.
english: And they saw cows, and buffaloes, and also old women, as well as aquatic creatures, predatory animals, and birds.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 87 Verse 7. And they saw cows, and buffaloes, and also old women, as well as aquatic creatures, predatory animals, and birds.

sanskrit: जरायुजान्यण्डजानि स्वेदजान्युद्भिदानि च ।पर्वतानूपवन्यानि भूतानि ददृशुश्च ते ॥८॥
transliteration: jarāyujānyaṇḍajāni svedajānyudbhidāni ca ,parvatānūpavanyāni bhūtāni dadṛśuśca te.
english: And they also saw creatures born from a womb (viviparous), from an egg (oviparous), from sweat (svedaja), and from sprouts (udbhida, plants), as well as those belonging to mountains, marshes, and forests.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 87 Verse 8. And they also saw creatures born from a womb (viviparous), from an egg (oviparous), from sweat (svedaja), and from sprouts (udbhida, plants), as well as those belonging to mountains, marshes, and forests.

sanskrit: एवं प्रमुदितं सर्वं पशुगोधनधान्यतः ।यज्ञवाटं नृपा दृष्ट्वा परं विस्मयमागमन् ।ब्राह्मणानां विशां चैव बहुमृष्टान्नमृद्धिमत् ॥९॥
transliteration: evaṁ pramuditaṁ sarvaṁ paśugodhanadhānyataḥ ,yajñavāṭaṁ nṛpā dṛṣṭvā paraṁ vismayamāgaman ,brāhmaṇānāṁ viśāṁ caiva bahumṛṣṭānnamṛddhimat.
english: Thus, with all aspects flourishing due to animals, cows, and grain, the kings, upon seeing the Vedic ritual enclosure (yajñavāṭa), were greatly astonished, particularly by the abundant and rich food for the Brahmins and Vaiśyas.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 87 Verse 9. Thus, with all aspects flourishing due to animals, cows, and grain, the kings, upon seeing the Vedic ritual enclosure (yajñavāṭa), were greatly astonished, particularly by the abundant and rich food for the Brahmins and Vaiśyas.

sanskrit: पूर्णे शतसहस्रे तु विप्राणां तत्र भुञ्जताम् ।दुन्दुभिर्मेघनिर्घोषो मुहुर्मुहुरताड्यत ॥१०॥
transliteration: pūrṇe śatasahasre tu viprāṇāṁ tatra bhuñjatām ,dundubhirmeghanirghoṣo muhurmuhuratāḍyata.
english: Indeed, while a full hundred thousand Brahmins were eating there, a drum, whose sound was like the rumble of a cloud, was repeatedly beaten.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 87 Verse 10. Indeed, while a full hundred thousand Brahmins were eating there, a drum, whose sound was like the rumble of a cloud, was repeatedly beaten.

sanskrit: विननादासकृत्सोऽथ दिवसे दिवसे तदा ।एवं स ववृते यज्ञो धर्मराजस्य धीमतः ॥११॥
transliteration: vinanādāsakṛtso'tha divase divase tadā ,evaṁ sa vavṛte yajño dharmarājasya dhīmataḥ.
english: Then, that (drum) repeatedly resounded day after day. Thus, that Vedic ritual (yajña) of the intelligent king of natural law (dharma) proceeded.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 87 Verse 11. Then, that (drum) repeatedly resounded day after day. Thus, that Vedic ritual (yajña) of the intelligent king of natural law (dharma) proceeded.

sanskrit: अन्नस्य बहवो राजन्नुत्सर्गाः पर्वतोपमाः ।दधिकुल्याश्च ददृशुः सर्पिषश्च ह्रदाञ्जनाः ॥१२॥
transliteration: annasya bahavo rājannutsargāḥ parvatopamāḥ ,dadhikulyāśca dadṛśuḥ sarpiṣaśca hradāñjanāḥ.
english: O King, many mountain-like heaps of food were observed. Also seen were streams of curds and deep, dark-colored lakes of ghee.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 87 Verse 12. O King, many mountain-like heaps of food were observed. Also seen were streams of curds and deep, dark-colored lakes of ghee.

sanskrit: जम्बूद्वीपो हि सकलो नानाजनपदायुतः ।राजन्नदृश्यतैकस्थो राज्ञस्तस्मिन्महाक्रतौ ॥१३॥
transliteration: jambūdvīpo hi sakalo nānājanapadāyutaḥ ,rājannadṛśyataikastho rājñastasminmahākratau.
english: O king, the entire Jambudvīpa, endowed with various provinces, was seen by the king as if present in one place during that great Vedic ritual (kratu).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 87 Verse 13. O king, the entire Jambudvīpa, endowed with various provinces, was seen by the king as if present in one place during that great Vedic ritual (kratu).

sanskrit: तत्र जातिसहस्राणि पुरुषाणां ततस्ततः ।गृहीत्वा धनमाजग्मुर्बहूनि भरतर्षभ ॥१४॥
transliteration: tatra jātisahasrāṇi puruṣāṇāṁ tatastataḥ ,gṛhītvā dhanamājagmurbahūni bharatarṣabha.
english: O best of Bharatas, many thousands of communities (jāti) of people came there from various places, having brought wealth.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 87 Verse 14. O best of Bharatas, many thousands of communities (jāti) of people came there from various places, having brought wealth.

sanskrit: राजानः स्रग्विणश्चापि सुमृष्टमणिकुण्डलाः ।पर्यवेषन्द्विजाग्र्यांस्ताञ्शतशोऽथ सहस्रशः ॥१५॥
transliteration: rājānaḥ sragviṇaścāpi sumṛṣṭamaṇikuṇḍalāḥ ,paryaveṣandvijāgryāṁstāñśataśo'tha sahasraśaḥ.
english: Kings, adorned with garlands and brightly polished jewel earrings, then served those eminent Brahmins by hundreds and thousands.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 87 Verse 15. Kings, adorned with garlands and brightly polished jewel earrings, then served those eminent Brahmins by hundreds and thousands.

sanskrit: विविधान्यन्नपानानि पुरुषा येऽनुयायिनः ।तेषां नृपोपभोज्यानि ब्राह्मणेभ्यो ददुः स्म ते ॥१६॥
transliteration: vividhānyannapānāni puruṣā ye'nuyāyinaḥ ,teṣāṁ nṛpopabhojyāni brāhmaṇebhyo daduḥ sma te.
english: Those men who were attendants regularly gave various foods and drinks, suitable for kings to enjoy, to those Brahmins.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 87 Verse 16. Those men who were attendants regularly gave various foods and drinks, suitable for kings to enjoy, to those Brahmins.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।समागतान्वेदविदो राज्ञश्च पृथिवीश्वरान् ।दृष्ट्वा युधिष्ठिरो राजा भीमसेनमथाब्रवीत् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,samāgatānvedavido rājñaśca pṛthivīśvarān ,dṛṣṭvā yudhiṣṭhiro rājā bhīmasenamathābravīt.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having seen the assembled knowers of the Vedas and the kings, the rulers of the earth, King Yudhiṣṭhira then spoke to Bhīmasena.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 88 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Having seen the assembled knowers of the Vedas and the kings, the rulers of the earth, King Yudhiṣṭhira then spoke to Bhīmasena.

sanskrit: उपयाता नरव्याघ्रा य इमे जगदीश्वराः ।एतेषां क्रियतां पूजा पूजार्हा हि नरेश्वराः ॥२॥
transliteration: upayātā naravyāghrā ya ime jagadīśvarāḥ ,eteṣāṁ kriyatāṁ pūjā pūjārhā hi nareśvarāḥ.
english: These tiger-like men (heroes), who are the lords of the world, have arrived. Let worship be offered to them, for these lords of men are indeed worthy of veneration.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 88 Verse 2. These tiger-like men (heroes), who are the lords of the world, have arrived. Let worship be offered to them, for these lords of men are indeed worthy of veneration.

sanskrit: इत्युक्तः स तथा चक्रे नरेन्द्रेण यशस्विना ।भीमसेनो महातेजा यमाभ्यां सह भारत ॥३॥
transliteration: ityuktaḥ sa tathā cakre narendreṇa yaśasvinā ,bhīmaseno mahātejā yamābhyāṁ saha bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, thus instructed by the glorious king (Yudhiṣṭhira), that greatly powerful Bhīmasena, along with the two Yamas (Nakula and Sahadeva), acted accordingly.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 88 Verse 3. O Bhārata, thus instructed by the glorious king (Yudhiṣṭhira), that greatly powerful Bhīmasena, along with the two Yamas (Nakula and Sahadeva), acted accordingly.

sanskrit: अथाभ्यगच्छद्गोविन्दो वृष्णिभिः सह धर्मजम् ।बलदेवं पुरस्कृत्य सर्वप्राणभृतां वरः ॥४॥
transliteration: athābhyagacchadgovindo vṛṣṇibhiḥ saha dharmajam ,baladevaṁ puraskṛtya sarvaprāṇabhṛtāṁ varaḥ.
english: Then Govinda (Kṛṣṇa), the best of all living beings, approached Yudhiṣṭhira (dharmaja) along with the Vṛṣṇis, placing Baladeva at the forefront.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 88 Verse 4. Then Govinda (Kṛṣṇa), the best of all living beings, approached Yudhiṣṭhira (dharmaja) along with the Vṛṣṇis, placing Baladeva at the forefront.

sanskrit: युयुधानेन सहितः प्रद्युम्नेन गदेन च ।निशठेनाथ साम्बेन तथैव कृतवर्मणा ॥५॥
transliteration: yuyudhānena sahitaḥ pradyumnena gadena ca ,niśaṭhenātha sāmbena tathaiva kṛtavarmaṇā.
english: Accompanied by Yuyudhana, Pradyumna, and Gada, and similarly by Niśāṭha, Sāmba, and Kṛtavarman.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 88 Verse 5. Accompanied by Yuyudhana, Pradyumna, and Gada, and similarly by Niśāṭha, Sāmba, and Kṛtavarman.

sanskrit: तेषामपि परां पूजां चक्रे भीमो महाभुजः ।विविशुस्ते च वेश्मानि रत्नवन्ति नरर्षभाः ॥६॥
transliteration: teṣāmapi parāṁ pūjāṁ cakre bhīmo mahābhujaḥ ,viviśuste ca veśmāni ratnavanti nararṣabhāḥ.
english: The mighty-armed Bhima offered great honor to them. And those excellent among men entered the jewel-filled dwellings.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 88 Verse 6. The mighty-armed Bhima offered great honor to them. And those excellent among men entered the jewel-filled dwellings.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरसमीपे तु कथान्ते मधुसूदनः ।अर्जुनं कथयामास बहुसंग्रामकर्शितम् ॥७॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhirasamīpe tu kathānte madhusūdanaḥ ,arjunaṁ kathayāmāsa bahusaṁgrāmakarśitam.
english: At the conclusion of the discourse, Madhusūdana (Kṛṣṇa), in the presence of Yudhiṣṭhira, spoke about Arjuna, who was exhausted by many battles.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 88 Verse 7. At the conclusion of the discourse, Madhusūdana (Kṛṣṇa), in the presence of Yudhiṣṭhira, spoke about Arjuna, who was exhausted by many battles.

sanskrit: स तं पप्रच्छ कौन्तेयः पुनः पुनररिंदमम् ।धर्मराड्भ्रातरं जिष्णुं समाचष्ट जगत्पतिः ॥८॥
transliteration: sa taṁ papraccha kaunteyaḥ punaḥ punarariṁdamam ,dharmarāḍbhrātaraṁ jiṣṇuṁ samācaṣṭa jagatpatiḥ.
english: That son of Kunti (Yudhiṣṭhira) repeatedly questioned him, the subduer of foes (Arjuna). Then, the Lord of the world (Kṛṣṇa) recounted (the events) to the victorious brother of the king of (dharma) (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 88 Verse 8. That son of Kunti (Yudhiṣṭhira) repeatedly questioned him, the subduer of foes (Arjuna). Then, the Lord of the world (Kṛṣṇa) recounted (the events) to the victorious brother of the king of (dharma) (Arjuna).

sanskrit: आगमद्द्वारकावासी ममाप्तः पुरुषो नृप ।योऽद्राक्षीत्पाण्डवश्रेष्ठं बहुसंग्रामकर्शितम् ॥९॥
transliteration: āgamaddvārakāvāsī mamāptaḥ puruṣo nṛpa ,yo'drākṣītpāṇḍavaśreṣṭhaṁ bahusaṁgrāmakarśitam.
english: Oh King, my trusted man, a resident of Dvārakā, arrived. He had seen the foremost of the Pāṇḍavas, weakened by many battles.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 88 Verse 9. Oh King, my trusted man, a resident of Dvārakā, arrived. He had seen the foremost of the Pāṇḍavas, weakened by many battles.

sanskrit: समीपे च महाबाहुमाचष्ट च मम प्रभो ।कुरु कार्याणि कौन्तेय हयमेधार्थसिद्धये ॥१०॥
transliteration: samīpe ca mahābāhumācaṣṭa ca mama prabho ,kuru kāryāṇi kaunteya hayamedhārthasiddhaye.
english: And, O Lord (prabho), he told that mighty-armed one in my presence: 'O son of Kuntī (Kaunteya), undertake the tasks for the successful completion of the horse (Vedic ritual) (hayamedha yajña).'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 88 Verse 10. And, O Lord (prabho), he told that mighty-armed one in my presence: 'O son of Kuntī (Kaunteya), undertake the tasks for the successful completion of the horse (Vedic ritual) (hayamedha yajña).'

sanskrit: इत्युक्तः प्रत्युवाचैनं धर्मराजो युधिष्ठिरः ।दिष्ट्या स कुशली जिष्णुरुपयाति च माधव ॥११॥
transliteration: ityuktaḥ pratyuvācainaṁ dharmarājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,diṣṭyā sa kuśalī jiṣṇurupayāti ca mādhava.
english: Thus addressed, Yudhishthira, the king of righteousness (dharma), replied to him: 'Fortunately, he, Jiṣṇu (Arjuna), is well and is approaching, O Mādhava!'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 88 Verse 11. Thus addressed, Yudhishthira, the king of righteousness (dharma), replied to him: 'Fortunately, he, Jiṣṇu (Arjuna), is well and is approaching, O Mādhava!'

sanskrit: तव यत्संदिदेशासौ पाण्डवानां बलाग्रणीः ।तदाख्यातुमिहेच्छामि भवता यदुनन्दन ॥१२॥
transliteration: tava yatsaṁdideśāsau pāṇḍavānāṁ balāgraṇīḥ ,tadākhyātumihecchāmi bhavatā yadunandana.
english: O descendant of Yadu (Yadunandana), I wish to relate here to your esteemed self (bhavatā) what that foremost of the Pāṇḍavas (Arjuna) instructed for you (tava).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 88 Verse 12. O descendant of Yadu (Yadunandana), I wish to relate here to your esteemed self (bhavatā) what that foremost of the Pāṇḍavas (Arjuna) instructed for you (tava).

sanskrit: इत्युक्ते राजशार्दूल वृष्ण्यन्धकपतिस्तदा ।प्रोवाचेदं वचो वाग्मी धर्मात्मानं युधिष्ठिरम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: ityukte rājaśārdūla vṛṣṇyandhakapatistadā ,provācedaṁ vaco vāgmī dharmātmānaṁ yudhiṣṭhiram.
english: When this was said, O tiger among kings, then the eloquent lord of the Vṛṣṇis and Andhakas spoke these words to the righteous-souled (dharmātman) Yudhiṣṭhira.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 88 Verse 13. When this was said, O tiger among kings, then the eloquent lord of the Vṛṣṇis and Andhakas spoke these words to the righteous-souled (dharmātman) Yudhiṣṭhira.

sanskrit: इदमाह महाराज पार्थवाक्यं नरः स माम् ।वाच्यो युधिष्ठिरः कृष्ण काले वाक्यमिदं मम ॥१४॥
transliteration: idamāha mahārāja pārthavākyaṁ naraḥ sa mām ,vācyo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ kṛṣṇa kāle vākyamidaṁ mama.
english: O great king, that man (Arjuna) spoke these words of Arjuna to me. O Kṛṣṇa, this message of mine should be conveyed to Yudhiṣṭhira at the appropriate time.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 88 Verse 14. O great king, that man (Arjuna) spoke these words of Arjuna to me. O Kṛṣṇa, this message of mine should be conveyed to Yudhiṣṭhira at the appropriate time.

sanskrit: आगमिष्यन्ति राजानः सर्वतः कौरवान्प्रति ।तेषामेकैकशः पूजा कार्येत्येतत्क्षमं हि नः ॥१५॥
transliteration: āgamiṣyanti rājānaḥ sarvataḥ kauravānprati ,teṣāmekaikaśaḥ pūjā kāryetyetatkṣamaṁ hi naḥ.
english: Kings will come from all directions to the Kauravas. Indeed, it is proper for us that honor (pūjā) should be shown to each of them individually.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 88 Verse 15. Kings will come from all directions to the Kauravas. Indeed, it is proper for us that honor (pūjā) should be shown to each of them individually.

sanskrit: इत्येतद्वचनाद्राजा विज्ञाप्यो मम मानद ।न तदात्ययिकं हि स्याद्यदर्घ्यानयने भवेत् ॥१६॥
transliteration: ityetadvacanādrājā vijñāpyo mama mānada ,na tadātyayikaṁ hi syādyadarghyānayane bhavet.
english: O giver of honor (mānada), the king (Yudhiṣṭhira) should be informed by me based on these words. For then, there would be no urgency regarding what might happen in the presentation of the respectful offering (arghya).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 88 Verse 16. O giver of honor (mānada), the king (Yudhiṣṭhira) should be informed by me based on these words. For then, there would be no urgency regarding what might happen in the presentation of the respectful offering (arghya).

sanskrit: कर्तुमर्हति तद्राजा भवांश्चाप्यनुमन्यताम् ।राजद्वेषाद्विनश्येयुर्नेमा राजन्प्रजाः पुनः ॥१७॥
transliteration: kartumarhati tadrājā bhavāṁścāpyanumanyatām ,rājadveṣādvinaśyeyurnemā rājanprajāḥ punaḥ.
english: That king is capable of doing this, and you too should approve. O king, these subjects should not perish again because of hostility towards the king.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 88 Verse 17. That king is capable of doing this, and you too should approve. O king, these subjects should not perish again because of hostility towards the king.

sanskrit: इदमन्यच्च कौन्तेय वचः स पुरुषोऽब्रवीत् ।धनंजयस्य नृपते तन्मे निगदतः शृणु ॥१८॥
transliteration: idamanyacca kaunteya vacaḥ sa puruṣo'bravīt ,dhanaṁjayasya nṛpate tanme nigadataḥ śṛṇu.
english: O son of Kunti (Arjuna), that man spoke these other words. Listen to them from me, as I narrate about King Dhanañjaya.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 88 Verse 18. O son of Kunti (Arjuna), that man spoke these other words. Listen to them from me, as I narrate about King Dhanañjaya.

sanskrit: उपयास्यति यज्ञं नो मणिपूरपतिर्नृपः ।पुत्रो मम महातेजा दयितो बभ्रुवाहनः ॥१९॥
transliteration: upayāsyati yajñaṁ no maṇipūrapatirnṛpaḥ ,putro mama mahātejā dayito babhruvāhanaḥ.
english: The king, lord of Maṇipūra, my illustrious and beloved son, Babhruvāhana, will come to our Vedic ritual (yajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 88 Verse 19. The king, lord of Maṇipūra, my illustrious and beloved son, Babhruvāhana, will come to our Vedic ritual (yajña).

sanskrit: तं भवान्मदपेक्षार्थं विधिवत्प्रतिपूजयेत् ।स हि भक्तोऽनुरक्तश्च मम नित्यमिति प्रभो ॥२०॥
transliteration: taṁ bhavānmadapekṣārthaṁ vidhivatpratipūjayet ,sa hi bhakto'nuraktaśca mama nityamiti prabho.
english: You should duly honor him for my sake. For he is always devoted and attached to me, O Lord.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 88 Verse 20. You should duly honor him for my sake. For he is always devoted and attached to me, O Lord.

sanskrit: इत्येतद्वचनं श्रुत्वा धर्मराजो युधिष्ठिरः ।अभिनन्द्यास्य तद्वाक्यमिदं वचनमब्रवीत् ॥२१॥
transliteration: ityetadvacanaṁ śrutvā dharmarājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,abhinandyāsya tadvākyamidaṁ vacanamabravīt.
english: Having heard this speech, Yudhishthira, the king of righteousness, welcomed his words and then spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 88 Verse 21. Having heard this speech, Yudhishthira, the king of righteousness, welcomed his words and then spoke these words.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।श्रुतं प्रियमिदं कृष्ण यत्त्वमर्हसि भाषितुम् ।तन्मेऽमृतरसप्रख्यं मनो ह्लादयते विभो ॥१॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,śrutaṁ priyamidaṁ kṛṣṇa yattvamarhasi bhāṣitum ,tanme'mṛtarasaprakhyaṁ mano hlādayate vibho.
english: Yudhishthira said: "Krishna, I have heard these dear words which you are worthy to speak. That, which resembles the taste of nectar, delights my mind, O Lord."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 89 Verse 1. Yudhishthira said: "Krishna, I have heard these dear words which you are worthy to speak. That, which resembles the taste of nectar, delights my mind, O Lord."

sanskrit: बहूनि किल युद्धानि विजयस्य नराधिपैः ।पुनरासन्हृषीकेश तत्र तत्रेति मे श्रुतम् ॥२॥
transliteration: bahūni kila yuddhāni vijayasya narādhipaiḥ ,punarāsanhṛṣīkeśa tatra tatreti me śrutam.
english: Indeed, many battles of Vijaya (Arjuna) with kings occurred again and again in various places, O Hrishikesha; this I have heard.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 89 Verse 2. Indeed, many battles of Vijaya (Arjuna) with kings occurred again and again in various places, O Hrishikesha; this I have heard.

sanskrit: मन्निमित्तं हि स सदा पार्थः सुखविवर्जितः ।अतीव विजयो धीमानिति मे दूयते मनः ॥३॥
transliteration: mannimittaṁ hi sa sadā pārthaḥ sukhavivarjitaḥ ,atīva vijayo dhīmāniti me dūyate manaḥ.
english: Indeed, because of me, that intelligent Vijaya (Arjuna) was always exceedingly devoid of happiness; thus my mind grieves.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 89 Verse 3. Indeed, because of me, that intelligent Vijaya (Arjuna) was always exceedingly devoid of happiness; thus my mind grieves.

sanskrit: संचिन्तयामि वार्ष्णेय सदा कुन्तीसुतं रहः ।किं नु तस्य शरीरेऽस्ति सर्वलक्षणपूजिते ।अनिष्टं लक्षणं कृष्ण येन दुःखान्युपाश्नुते ॥४॥
transliteration: saṁcintayāmi vārṣṇeya sadā kuntīsutaṁ rahaḥ ,kiṁ nu tasya śarīre'sti sarvalakṣaṇapūjite ,aniṣṭaṁ lakṣaṇaṁ kṛṣṇa yena duḥkhānyupāśnute.
english: O Vārṣṇeya (Krishna), I constantly ponder in private about Kunti's son (Arjuna). What inauspicious mark could possibly be in his body, which is revered for all its auspicious signs, O Krishna, that causes him to experience so many sufferings?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 89 Verse 4. O Vārṣṇeya (Krishna), I constantly ponder in private about Kunti's son (Arjuna). What inauspicious mark could possibly be in his body, which is revered for all its auspicious signs, O Krishna, that causes him to experience so many sufferings?

sanskrit: अतीव दुःखभागी स सततं कुन्तिनन्दनः ।न च पश्यामि बीभत्सोर्निन्द्यं गात्रेषु किंचन ।श्रोतव्यं चेन्मयैतद्वै तन्मे व्याख्यातुमर्हसि ॥५॥
transliteration: atīva duḥkhabhāgī sa satataṁ kuntinandanaḥ ,na ca paśyāmi bībhatsornindyaṁ gātreṣu kiṁcana ,śrotavyaṁ cenmayaitadvai tanme vyākhyātumarhasi.
english: That son of Kunti (Arjuna) constantly experiences great suffering. Yet I see nothing blameworthy in the body of Bibhatsu (Arjuna). If this indeed ought to be revealed to me, then you should explain it.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 89 Verse 5. That son of Kunti (Arjuna) constantly experiences great suffering. Yet I see nothing blameworthy in the body of Bibhatsu (Arjuna). If this indeed ought to be revealed to me, then you should explain it.

sanskrit: इत्युक्तः स हृषीकेशो ध्यात्वा सुमहदन्तरम् ।राजानं भोजराजन्यवर्धनो विष्णुरब्रवीत् ॥६॥
transliteration: ityuktaḥ sa hṛṣīkeśo dhyātvā sumahadantaram ,rājānaṁ bhojarājanyavardhano viṣṇurabravīt.
english: Thus addressed, Hrishikesha (Krishna), Vishnu, the augmenter of the kings of Bhoja, having deeply considered the profound matter, spoke to the king.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 89 Verse 6. Thus addressed, Hrishikesha (Krishna), Vishnu, the augmenter of the kings of Bhoja, having deeply considered the profound matter, spoke to the king.

sanskrit: न ह्यस्य नृपते किंचिदनिष्टमुपलक्षये ।ऋते पुरुषसिंहस्य पिण्डिकेऽस्यातिकायतः ॥७॥
transliteration: na hyasya nṛpate kiṁcidaniṣṭamupalakṣaye ,ṛte puruṣasiṁhasya piṇḍike'syātikāyataḥ.
english: O King, I certainly do not perceive anything inauspicious in him, except for the calves of this lion among men (Arjuna), which are exceedingly long.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 89 Verse 7. O King, I certainly do not perceive anything inauspicious in him, except for the calves of this lion among men (Arjuna), which are exceedingly long.

sanskrit: ताभ्यां स पुरुषव्याघ्रो नित्यमध्वसु युज्यते ।न ह्यन्यदनुपश्यामि येनासौ दुःखभाग्जयः ॥८॥
transliteration: tābhyāṁ sa puruṣavyāghro nityamadhvasu yujyate ,na hyanyadanupaśyāmi yenāsau duḥkhabhāgjayaḥ.
english: That best of persons (puruṣa) is always engaged in his endeavors by those two (qualities/means). Indeed, I do not see anything else by which he, who experiences sorrow, could achieve victory.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 89 Verse 8. That best of persons (puruṣa) is always engaged in his endeavors by those two (qualities/means). Indeed, I do not see anything else by which he, who experiences sorrow, could achieve victory.

sanskrit: इत्युक्तः स कुरुश्रेष्ठस्तथ्यं कृष्णेन धीमता ।प्रोवाच वृष्णिशार्दूलमेवमेतदिति प्रभो ॥९॥
transliteration: ityuktaḥ sa kuruśreṣṭhastathyaṁ kṛṣṇena dhīmatā ,provāca vṛṣṇiśārdūlamevametaditi prabho.
english: O lord, thus addressed truthfully by the intelligent Kṛṣṇa, that best of Kurus said to the tiger among Vṛṣṇis, 'This is indeed so!'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 89 Verse 9. O lord, thus addressed truthfully by the intelligent Kṛṣṇa, that best of Kurus said to the tiger among Vṛṣṇis, 'This is indeed so!'

sanskrit: कृष्णा तु द्रौपदी कृष्णं तिर्यक्सासूयमैक्षत ।प्रतिजग्राह तस्यास्तं प्रणयं चापि केशिहा ।सख्युः सखा हृषीकेशः साक्षादिव धनंजयः ॥१०॥
transliteration: kṛṣṇā tu draupadī kṛṣṇaṁ tiryaksāsūyamaikṣata ,pratijagrāha tasyāstaṁ praṇayaṁ cāpi keśihā ,sakhyuḥ sakhā hṛṣīkeśaḥ sākṣādiva dhanaṁjayaḥ.
english: But Draupadī (Kṛṣṇā) looked askance at Kṛṣṇa, expressing subtle resentment. And Keśihā (Kṛṣṇa) accepted her affection (praṇaya). Hṛṣīkeśa (Kṛṣṇa) was a true friend to his companion Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), as if directly present.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 89 Verse 10. But Draupadī (Kṛṣṇā) looked askance at Kṛṣṇa, expressing subtle resentment. And Keśihā (Kṛṣṇa) accepted her affection (praṇaya). Hṛṣīkeśa (Kṛṣṇa) was a true friend to his companion Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), as if directly present.

sanskrit: तत्र भीमादयस्ते तु कुरवो यादवास्तथा ।रेमुः श्रुत्वा विचित्रार्था धनंजयकथा विभो ॥११॥
transliteration: tatra bhīmādayaste tu kuravo yādavāstathā ,remuḥ śrutvā vicitrārthā dhanaṁjayakathā vibho.
english: O lord, there, Bhīma and others, those Kurus and also the Yadavas, rejoiced after hearing the wonderful stories of Dhanañjaya (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 89 Verse 11. O lord, there, Bhīma and others, those Kurus and also the Yadavas, rejoiced after hearing the wonderful stories of Dhanañjaya (Arjuna).

sanskrit: तथा कथयतामेव तेषामर्जुनसंकथाः ।उपायाद्वचनान्मर्त्यो विजयस्य महात्मनः ॥१२॥
transliteration: tathā kathayatāmeva teṣāmarjunasaṁkathāḥ ,upāyādvacanānmartyo vijayasya mahātmanaḥ.
english: Thus, while they were narrating the stories of Arjuna, a man approached, prompted by the words of the great-souled Arjuna (Vijaya).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 89 Verse 12. Thus, while they were narrating the stories of Arjuna, a man approached, prompted by the words of the great-souled Arjuna (Vijaya).

sanskrit: सोऽभिगम्य कुरुश्रेष्ठं नमस्कृत्य च बुद्धिमान् ।उपायातं नरव्याघ्रमर्जुनं प्रत्यवेदयत् ॥१३॥
transliteration: so'bhigamya kuruśreṣṭhaṁ namaskṛtya ca buddhimān ,upāyātaṁ naravyāghramarjunaṁ pratyavedayat.
english: And the intelligent man, having approached the best of the Kurus and paid his respects, reported that Arjuna, the tiger among men, had arrived.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 89 Verse 13. And the intelligent man, having approached the best of the Kurus and paid his respects, reported that Arjuna, the tiger among men, had arrived.

sanskrit: तच्छ्रुत्वा नृपतिस्तस्य हर्षबाष्पाकुलेक्षणः ।प्रियाख्याननिमित्तं वै ददौ बहु धनं तदा ॥१४॥
transliteration: tacchrutvā nṛpatistasya harṣabāṣpākulekṣaṇaḥ ,priyākhyānanimittaṁ vai dadau bahu dhanaṁ tadā.
english: Having heard that, the king, whose eyes were agitated by tears of joy, then indeed gave much wealth to him (the messenger) for delivering the good news.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 89 Verse 14. Having heard that, the king, whose eyes were agitated by tears of joy, then indeed gave much wealth to him (the messenger) for delivering the good news.

sanskrit: ततो द्वितीये दिवसे महाञ्शब्दो व्यवर्धत ।आयाति पुरुषव्याघ्रे पाण्डवानां धुरंधरे ॥१५॥
transliteration: tato dvitīye divase mahāñśabdo vyavardhata ,āyāti puruṣavyāghre pāṇḍavānāṁ dhuraṁdhare.
english: Then, on the second day, a great commotion arose as the man-tiger, the chief (dhurandhara) of the Pāṇḍavas, was arriving.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 89 Verse 15. Then, on the second day, a great commotion arose as the man-tiger, the chief (dhurandhara) of the Pāṇḍavas, was arriving.

sanskrit: ततो रेणुः समुद्भूतो विबभौ तस्य वाजिनः ।अभितो वर्तमानस्य यथोच्चैःश्रवसस्तथा ॥१६॥
transliteration: tato reṇuḥ samudbhūto vibabhau tasya vājinaḥ ,abhito vartamānasya yathoccaiḥśravasastathā.
english: Then, the dust that rose from that horse, as it moved all around, gleamed like (the divine horse) Uccaiḥśravas.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 89 Verse 16. Then, the dust that rose from that horse, as it moved all around, gleamed like (the divine horse) Uccaiḥśravas.

sanskrit: तत्र हर्षकला वाचो नराणां शुश्रुवेऽर्जुनः ।दिष्ट्यासि पार्थ कुशली धन्यो राजा युधिष्ठिरः ॥१७॥
transliteration: tatra harṣakalā vāco narāṇāṁ śuśruve'rjunaḥ ,diṣṭyāsi pārtha kuśalī dhanyo rājā yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: There, Arjuna heard joyful exclamations from the men: 'By good fortune, you, Pārtha, are well! Blessed is King Yudhiṣṭhira!'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 89 Verse 17. There, Arjuna heard joyful exclamations from the men: 'By good fortune, you, Pārtha, are well! Blessed is King Yudhiṣṭhira!'

sanskrit: कोऽन्यो हि पृथिवीं कृत्स्नामवजित्य सपार्थिवाम् ।चारयित्वा हयश्रेष्ठमुपायायादृतेऽर्जुनम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: ko'nyo hi pṛthivīṁ kṛtsnāmavajitya sapārthivām ,cārayitvā hayaśreṣṭhamupāyāyādṛte'rjunam.
english: Who else, indeed, apart from Arjuna, after conquering the entire earth with its rulers and making the finest horse roam freely, would be able to return?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 89 Verse 18. Who else, indeed, apart from Arjuna, after conquering the entire earth with its rulers and making the finest horse roam freely, would be able to return?

sanskrit: ये व्यतीता महात्मानो राजानः सगरादयः ।तेषामपीदृशं कर्म न किंचिदनुशुश्रुम ॥१९॥
transliteration: ye vyatītā mahātmāno rājānaḥ sagarādayaḥ ,teṣāmapīdṛśaṁ karma na kiṁcidanuśuśruma.
english: We have never heard of any such deed (karma), even a small one, from those great-souled kings of the past, like Sagara and others.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 89 Verse 19. We have never heard of any such deed (karma), even a small one, from those great-souled kings of the past, like Sagara and others.

sanskrit: नैतदन्ये करिष्यन्ति भविष्याः पृथिवीक्षितः ।यत्त्वं कुरुकुलश्रेष्ठ दुष्करं कृतवानिह ॥२०॥
transliteration: naitadanye kariṣyanti bhaviṣyāḥ pṛthivīkṣitaḥ ,yattvaṁ kurukulaśreṣṭha duṣkaraṁ kṛtavāniha.
english: No other future kings of the earth will achieve this, which you, O best of the Kuru dynasty, have accomplished here, a deed so difficult to perform.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 89 Verse 20. No other future kings of the earth will achieve this, which you, O best of the Kuru dynasty, have accomplished here, a deed so difficult to perform.

sanskrit: इत्येवं वदतां तेषां नॄणां श्रुतिसुखा गिरः ।शृण्वन्विवेश धर्मात्मा फल्गुनो यज्ञसंस्तरम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: ityevaṁ vadatāṁ teṣāṁ nṝṇāṁ śrutisukhā giraḥ ,śṛṇvanviveśa dharmātmā phalguno yajñasaṁstaram.
english: Thus, hearing the delightful words of those men speaking, Arjuna, whose nature was righteousness (dharma), entered the arena of the Vedic ritual (yajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 89 Verse 21. Thus, hearing the delightful words of those men speaking, Arjuna, whose nature was righteousness (dharma), entered the arena of the Vedic ritual (yajña).

sanskrit: ततो राजा सहामात्यः कृष्णश्च यदुनन्दनः ।धृतराष्ट्रं पुरस्कृत्य ते तं प्रत्युद्ययुस्तदा ॥२२॥
transliteration: tato rājā sahāmātyaḥ kṛṣṇaśca yadunandanaḥ ,dhṛtarāṣṭraṁ puraskṛtya te taṁ pratyudyayustadā.
english: Then, the king, accompanied by his ministers, and Krishna, the joy of the Yadus, putting Dhritarashtra at the forefront, went forth to meet him (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 89 Verse 22. Then, the king, accompanied by his ministers, and Krishna, the joy of the Yadus, putting Dhritarashtra at the forefront, went forth to meet him (Arjuna).

sanskrit: सोऽभिवाद्य पितुः पादौ धर्मराजस्य धीमतः ।भीमादींश्चापि संपूज्य पर्यष्वजत केशवम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: so'bhivādya pituḥ pādau dharmarājasya dhīmataḥ ,bhīmādīṁścāpi saṁpūjya paryaṣvajata keśavam.
english: He, having saluted the feet of his father and of the wise king of righteousness (dharma), and also having honored Bhima and others, then embraced Keśava (Krishna).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 89 Verse 23. He, having saluted the feet of his father and of the wise king of righteousness (dharma), and also having honored Bhima and others, then embraced Keśava (Krishna).

sanskrit: तैः समेत्यार्चितस्तान्स प्रत्यर्च्य च यथाविधि ।विशश्रामाथ धर्मात्मा तीरं लब्ध्वेव पारगः ॥२४॥
transliteration: taiḥ sametyārcitastānsa pratyarcya ca yathāvidhi ,viśaśrāmātha dharmātmā tīraṁ labdhveva pāragaḥ.
english: Having met them and been honored by them, and having reciprocated their honors according to custom, the righteous soul (dharmātmā) then rested, just as a voyager having reached the shore.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 89 Verse 24. Having met them and been honored by them, and having reciprocated their honors according to custom, the righteous soul (dharmātmā) then rested, just as a voyager having reached the shore.

sanskrit: एतस्मिन्नेव काले तु स राजा बभ्रुवाहनः ।मातृभ्यां सहितो धीमान्कुरूनभ्याजगाम ह ॥२५॥
transliteration: etasminneva kāle tu sa rājā babhruvāhanaḥ ,mātṛbhyāṁ sahito dhīmānkurūnabhyājagāma ha.
english: Indeed, at this very time, that intelligent King Babhruvāhana, accompanied by his two mothers, came to the Kurus.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 89 Verse 25. Indeed, at this very time, that intelligent King Babhruvāhana, accompanied by his two mothers, came to the Kurus.

sanskrit: स समेत्य कुरून्सर्वान्सर्वैस्तैरभिनन्दितः ।प्रविवेश पितामह्याः कुन्त्या भवनमुत्तमम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: sa sametya kurūnsarvānsarvaistairabhinanditaḥ ,praviveśa pitāmahyāḥ kuntyā bhavanamuttamam.
english: He, having met all the Kurus and been welcomed by all of them, then entered the excellent dwelling of his grandmother, Kunti.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 89 Verse 26. He, having met all the Kurus and been welcomed by all of them, then entered the excellent dwelling of his grandmother, Kunti.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।स प्रविश्य यथान्यायं पाण्डवानां निवेशनम् ।पितामहीमभ्यवदत्साम्ना परमवल्गुना ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,sa praviśya yathānyāyaṁ pāṇḍavānāṁ niveśanam ,pitāmahīmabhyavadatsāmnā paramavalgunā.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having entered the dwelling of the Pandavas in the proper manner, he addressed his grandmother with exceedingly pleasing words.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 90 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Having entered the dwelling of the Pandavas in the proper manner, he addressed his grandmother with exceedingly pleasing words.

sanskrit: तथा चित्राङ्गदा देवी कौरव्यस्यात्मजापि च ।पृथां कृष्णां च सहिते विनयेनाभिजग्मतुः ।सुभद्रां च यथान्यायं याश्चान्याः कुरुयोषितः ॥२॥
transliteration: tathā citrāṅgadā devī kauravyasyātmajāpi ca ,pṛthāṁ kṛṣṇāṁ ca sahite vinayenābhijagmatuḥ ,subhadrāṁ ca yathānyāyaṁ yāścānyāḥ kuruyoṣitaḥ.
english: Thus, Princess Citrāṅgadā and also the daughter of Kauravya (Ulupī) both together approached Pṛthā (Kuntī) and Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī) with humility. They also approached Subhadrā appropriately, and all the other women of the Kuru household.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 90 Verse 2. Thus, Princess Citrāṅgadā and also the daughter of Kauravya (Ulupī) both together approached Pṛthā (Kuntī) and Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī) with humility. They also approached Subhadrā appropriately, and all the other women of the Kuru household.

sanskrit: ददौ कुन्ती ततस्ताभ्यां रत्नानि विविधानि च ।द्रौपदी च सुभद्रा च याश्चाप्यन्या ददुः स्त्रियः ॥३॥
transliteration: dadau kuntī tatastābhyāṁ ratnāni vividhāni ca ,draupadī ca subhadrā ca yāścāpyanyā daduḥ striyaḥ.
english: Then Kuntī gave various jewels to those two (Citrāṅgadā and Ulupī). Draupadī, Subhadrā, and any other women present also gave gifts.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 90 Verse 3. Then Kuntī gave various jewels to those two (Citrāṅgadā and Ulupī). Draupadī, Subhadrā, and any other women present also gave gifts.

sanskrit: ऊषतुस्तत्र ते देव्यौ महार्हशयनासने ।सुपूजिते स्वयं कुन्त्या पार्थस्य प्रियकाम्यया ॥४॥
transliteration: ūṣatustatra te devyau mahārhaśayanāsane ,supūjite svayaṁ kuntyā pārthasya priyakāmyayā.
english: Those two royal ladies (Citrāṅgadā and Ulupī) stayed there, well-honored personally by Kuntī on highly valuable beds and seats, out of Kuntī's desire for the welfare of Pārtha (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 90 Verse 4. Those two royal ladies (Citrāṅgadā and Ulupī) stayed there, well-honored personally by Kuntī on highly valuable beds and seats, out of Kuntī's desire for the welfare of Pārtha (Arjuna).

sanskrit: स च राजा महावीर्यः पूजितो बभ्रुवाहनः ।धृतराष्ट्रं महीपालमुपतस्थे यथाविधि ॥५॥
transliteration: sa ca rājā mahāvīryaḥ pūjito babhruvāhanaḥ ,dhṛtarāṣṭraṁ mahīpālamupatasthe yathāvidhi.
english: And that exceedingly mighty King Babhrūvāhana, having been honored, then duly approached King Dhṛtarāṣṭra.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 90 Verse 5. And that exceedingly mighty King Babhrūvāhana, having been honored, then duly approached King Dhṛtarāṣṭra.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरं च राजानं भीमादींश्चापि पाण्डवान् ।उपगम्य महातेजा विनयेनाभ्यवादयत् ॥६॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhiraṁ ca rājānaṁ bhīmādīṁścāpi pāṇḍavān ,upagamya mahātejā vinayenābhyavādayat.
english: The greatly powerful one, approaching King Yudhiṣṭhira and also Bhīma and the other Pāṇḍavas, respectfully greeted them.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 90 Verse 6. The greatly powerful one, approaching King Yudhiṣṭhira and also Bhīma and the other Pāṇḍavas, respectfully greeted them.

sanskrit: स तैः प्रेम्णा परिष्वक्तः पूजितश्च यथाविधि ।धनं चास्मै ददुर्भूरि प्रीयमाणा महारथाः ॥७॥
transliteration: sa taiḥ premṇā pariṣvaktaḥ pūjitaśca yathāvidhi ,dhanaṁ cāsmai dadurbhūri prīyamāṇā mahārathāḥ.
english: He was embraced by them with affection and properly honored. The delighted great warriors (mahāratha) then gave him abundant wealth.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 90 Verse 7. He was embraced by them with affection and properly honored. The delighted great warriors (mahāratha) then gave him abundant wealth.

sanskrit: तथैव स महीपालः कृष्णं चक्रगदाधरम् ।प्रद्युम्न इव गोविन्दं विनयेनोपतस्थिवान् ॥८॥
transliteration: tathaiva sa mahīpālaḥ kṛṣṇaṁ cakragadādharam ,pradyumna iva govindaṁ vinayenopatasthivān.
english: Similarly, that king (Yudhiṣṭhira) reverently approached Kṛṣṇa, who holds the discus and mace, just as Pradyumna approaches Govinda.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 90 Verse 8. Similarly, that king (Yudhiṣṭhira) reverently approached Kṛṣṇa, who holds the discus and mace, just as Pradyumna approaches Govinda.

sanskrit: तस्मै कृष्णो ददौ राज्ञे महार्हमभिपूजितम् ।रथं हेमपरिष्कारं दिव्याश्वयुजमुत्तमम् ॥९॥
transliteration: tasmai kṛṣṇo dadau rājñe mahārhamabhipūjitam ,rathaṁ hemapariṣkāraṁ divyāśvayujamuttamam.
english: To that king, Kṛṣṇa gave an excellent and highly esteemed chariot, adorned with gold and yoked with divine horses.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 90 Verse 9. To that king, Kṛṣṇa gave an excellent and highly esteemed chariot, adorned with gold and yoked with divine horses.

sanskrit: धर्मराजश्च भीमश्च यमजौ फल्गुनस्तथा ।पृथक्पृथगतीवैनं मानार्हं समपूजयन् ॥१०॥
transliteration: dharmarājaśca bhīmaśca yamajau phalgunastathā ,pṛthakpṛthagatīvainaṁ mānārhaṁ samapūjayan.
english: Dharmarāja (Yudhiṣṭhira), Bhīma, the twins (Nakula and Sahadeva), and Arjuna, each separately and intensely, honored him who was worthy of honor.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 90 Verse 10. Dharmarāja (Yudhiṣṭhira), Bhīma, the twins (Nakula and Sahadeva), and Arjuna, each separately and intensely, honored him who was worthy of honor.

sanskrit: ततस्तृतीये दिवसे सत्यवत्याः सुतो मुनिः ।युधिष्ठिरं समभ्येत्य वाग्मी वचनमब्रवीत् ॥११॥
transliteration: tatastṛtīye divase satyavatyāḥ suto muniḥ ,yudhiṣṭhiraṁ samabhyetya vāgmī vacanamabravīt.
english: Then, on the third day, the eloquent sage, son of Satyavatī (Vyāsa), having approached Yudhiṣṭhira, spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 90 Verse 11. Then, on the third day, the eloquent sage, son of Satyavatī (Vyāsa), having approached Yudhiṣṭhira, spoke these words.

sanskrit: अद्य प्रभृति कौन्तेय यजस्व समयो हि ते ।मुहूर्तो यज्ञियः प्राप्तश्चोदयन्ति च याजकाः ॥१२॥
transliteration: adya prabhṛti kaunteya yajasva samayo hi te ,muhūrto yajñiyaḥ prāptaścodayanti ca yājakāḥ.
english: O son of Kuntī (Yudhiṣṭhira), perform the Vedic ritual (yajña) from today onwards, for indeed, it is your opportune time. The auspicious moment for the Vedic ritual (yajña) has arrived, and the priests (yajakas) are urging you.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 90 Verse 12. O son of Kuntī (Yudhiṣṭhira), perform the Vedic ritual (yajña) from today onwards, for indeed, it is your opportune time. The auspicious moment for the Vedic ritual (yajña) has arrived, and the priests (yajakas) are urging you.

sanskrit: अहीनो नाम राजेन्द्र क्रतुस्तेऽयं विकल्पवान् ।बहुत्वात्काञ्चनस्यास्य ख्यातो बहुसुवर्णकः ॥१३॥
transliteration: ahīno nāma rājendra kratuste'yaṁ vikalpavān ,bahutvātkāñcanasyāsya khyāto bahusuvarṇakaḥ.
english: O king of kings, this Vedic ritual (kratu) of yours, named Ahīna, is characterized by its variations. It is also renowned as "Bahusuvarṇaka" (having much gold) due to the abundance of gold involved.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 90 Verse 13. O king of kings, this Vedic ritual (kratu) of yours, named Ahīna, is characterized by its variations. It is also renowned as "Bahusuvarṇaka" (having much gold) due to the abundance of gold involved.

sanskrit: एवमेव महाराज दक्षिणां त्रिगुणां कुरु ।त्रित्वं व्रजतु ते राजन्ब्राह्मणा ह्यत्र कारणम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: evameva mahārāja dakṣiṇāṁ triguṇāṁ kuru ,tritvaṁ vrajatu te rājanbrāhmaṇā hyatra kāraṇam.
english: Therefore, O great king (mahārāja), make the ritual fee (dakṣiṇā) threefold. Let this threefold nature come to you, O king, for the brahmins are indeed the reason here.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 90 Verse 14. Therefore, O great king (mahārāja), make the ritual fee (dakṣiṇā) threefold. Let this threefold nature come to you, O king, for the brahmins are indeed the reason here.

sanskrit: त्रीनश्वमेधानत्र त्वं संप्राप्य बहुदक्षिणान् ।ज्ञातिवध्याकृतं पापं प्रहास्यसि नराधिप ॥१५॥
transliteration: trīnaśvamedhānatra tvaṁ saṁprāpya bahudakṣiṇān ,jñātivadhyākṛtaṁ pāpaṁ prahāsyasi narādhipa.
english: Here, having successfully performed three horse-Vedic rituals (aśvamedha) with ample ritual fees (dakṣiṇā), you, O ruler of men (narādhipa), will abandon the sin incurred by the slaughter of kinsmen.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 90 Verse 15. Here, having successfully performed three horse-Vedic rituals (aśvamedha) with ample ritual fees (dakṣiṇā), you, O ruler of men (narādhipa), will abandon the sin incurred by the slaughter of kinsmen.

sanskrit: पवित्रं परमं ह्येतत्पावनानां च पावनम् ।यदश्वमेधावभृथं प्राप्स्यसे कुरुनन्दन ॥१६॥
transliteration: pavitraṁ paramaṁ hyetatpāvanānāṁ ca pāvanam ,yadaśvamedhāvabhṛthaṁ prāpsyase kurunandana.
english: For this is the supreme purifier, indeed the holiest of purifiers, that you, O delight of the Kurus (kurunandana), will attain the ritual bath (avabhṛtha) of the horse-Vedic ritual (aśvamedha).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 90 Verse 16. For this is the supreme purifier, indeed the holiest of purifiers, that you, O delight of the Kurus (kurunandana), will attain the ritual bath (avabhṛtha) of the horse-Vedic ritual (aśvamedha).

sanskrit: इत्युक्तः स तु तेजस्वी व्यासेनामिततेजसा ।दीक्षां विवेश धर्मात्मा वाजिमेधाप्तये तदा ।नराधिपः प्रायजत वाजिमेधं महाक्रतुम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: ityuktaḥ sa tu tejasvī vyāsenāmitatejasā ,dīkṣāṁ viveśa dharmātmā vājimedhāptaye tadā ,narādhipaḥ prāyajata vājimedhaṁ mahākratum.
english: Thus spoken to by Vyāsa, who possessed immeasurable radiance, that illustrious king, whose soul was intent on natural law (dharmātman), then entered the initiatory rite (dīkṣā) for the accomplishment of the horse-Vedic ritual (vājimedha). The ruler of men (narādhipa) then performed that great Vedic ritual (vājimedha mahākrātu).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 90 Verse 17. Thus spoken to by Vyāsa, who possessed immeasurable radiance, that illustrious king, whose soul was intent on natural law (dharmātman), then entered the initiatory rite (dīkṣā) for the accomplishment of the horse-Vedic ritual (vājimedha). The ruler of men (narādhipa) then performed that great Vedic ritual (vājimedha mahākrātu).

sanskrit: तत्र वेदविदो राजंश्चक्रुः कर्माणि याजकाः ।परिक्रमन्तः शास्त्रज्ञा विधिवत्साधुशिक्षिताः ॥१८॥
transliteration: tatra vedavido rājaṁścakruḥ karmāṇi yājakāḥ ,parikramantaḥ śāstrajñā vidhivatsādhuśikṣitāḥ.
english: O king, the officiating priests, who were well-versed in the Vedas, knowledgeable in the scriptures, and thoroughly trained, performed the rituals there in accordance with the prescribed rules, moving about.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 90 Verse 18. O king, the officiating priests, who were well-versed in the Vedas, knowledgeable in the scriptures, and thoroughly trained, performed the rituals there in accordance with the prescribed rules, moving about.

sanskrit: न तेषां स्खलितं तत्र नासीदपहुतं तथा ।क्रमयुक्तं च युक्तं च चक्रुस्तत्र द्विजर्षभाः ॥१९॥
transliteration: na teṣāṁ skhalitaṁ tatra nāsīdapahutaṁ tathā ,kramayuktaṁ ca yuktaṁ ca cakrustatra dvijarṣabhāḥ.
english: There was no error (skhalita) or omission (apahuta) on their part during the ritual, nor was anything neglected. The foremost among the twice-born (dvija) performed everything there in proper sequence and appropriately.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 90 Verse 19. There was no error (skhalita) or omission (apahuta) on their part during the ritual, nor was anything neglected. The foremost among the twice-born (dvija) performed everything there in proper sequence and appropriately.

sanskrit: कृत्वा प्रवर्ग्यं धर्मज्ञा यथावद्द्विजसत्तमाः ।चक्रुस्ते विधिवद्राजंस्तथैवाभिषवं द्विजाः ॥२०॥
transliteration: kṛtvā pravargyaṁ dharmajñā yathāvaddvijasattamāḥ ,cakruste vidhivadrājaṁstathaivābhiṣavaṁ dvijāḥ.
english: O king, those excellent twice-born (dvija) priests, who were knowers of the proper constitution (dharma) for rituals, having duly performed the Pravargya ritual, then similarly carried out the pressing of the Soma (abhiṣava) in accordance with the prescribed rules.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 90 Verse 20. O king, those excellent twice-born (dvija) priests, who were knowers of the proper constitution (dharma) for rituals, having duly performed the Pravargya ritual, then similarly carried out the pressing of the Soma (abhiṣava) in accordance with the prescribed rules.

sanskrit: अभिषूय ततो राजन्सोमं सोमपसत्तमाः ।सवनान्यानुपूर्व्येण चक्रुः शास्त्रानुसारिणः ॥२१॥
transliteration: abhiṣūya tato rājansomaṁ somapasattamāḥ ,savanānyānupūrvyeṇa cakruḥ śāstrānusāriṇaḥ.
english: O king, having pressed the Soma, those most excellent Soma-drinkers, who adhered to the scriptures, then performed the Soma rituals in their proper sequence.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 90 Verse 21. O king, having pressed the Soma, those most excellent Soma-drinkers, who adhered to the scriptures, then performed the Soma rituals in their proper sequence.

sanskrit: न तत्र कृपणः कश्चिन्न दरिद्रो बभूव ह ।क्षुधितो दुःखितो वापि प्राकृतो वापि मानवः ॥२२॥
transliteration: na tatra kṛpaṇaḥ kaścinna daridro babhūva ha ,kṣudhito duḥkhito vāpi prākṛto vāpi mānavaḥ.
english: No one there was wretched or destitute; nor was any human being hungry, suffering, or even common.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 90 Verse 22. No one there was wretched or destitute; nor was any human being hungry, suffering, or even common.

sanskrit: भोजनं भोजनार्थिभ्यो दापयामास नित्यदा ।भीमसेनो महातेजाः सततं राजशासनात् ॥२३॥
transliteration: bhojanaṁ bhojanārthibhyo dāpayāmāsa nityadā ,bhīmaseno mahātejāḥ satataṁ rājaśāsanāt.
english: Bhimasena, the greatly energetic one, continually distributed food to those who sought it, always acting under the king's command.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 90 Verse 23. Bhimasena, the greatly energetic one, continually distributed food to those who sought it, always acting under the king's command.

sanskrit: संस्तरे कुशलाश्चापि सर्वकर्माणि याजकाः ।दिवसे दिवसे चक्रुर्यथाशास्त्रार्थचक्षुषः ॥२४॥
transliteration: saṁstare kuśalāścāpi sarvakarmāṇi yājakāḥ ,divase divase cakruryathāśāstrārthacakṣuṣaḥ.
english: And the priests, whose insight was guided by the true meaning of the scriptures, being skilled in all arrangements and rituals, performed all tasks daily.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 90 Verse 24. And the priests, whose insight was guided by the true meaning of the scriptures, being skilled in all arrangements and rituals, performed all tasks daily.

sanskrit: नाषडङ्गविदत्रासीत्सदस्यस्तस्य धीमतः ।नाव्रतो नानुपाध्यायो न च वादाक्षमो द्विजः ॥२५॥
transliteration: nāṣaḍaṅgavidatrāsītsadasyastasya dhīmataḥ ,nāvrato nānupādhyāyo na ca vādākṣamo dvijaḥ.
english: Among the assembly members of that wise man, there was no one who was not conversant with the six auxiliaries of the Vedas; nor was there any brahmin (dvija) who lacked vows, was without a preceptor, or was unskilled in debate.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 90 Verse 25. Among the assembly members of that wise man, there was no one who was not conversant with the six auxiliaries of the Vedas; nor was there any brahmin (dvija) who lacked vows, was without a preceptor, or was unskilled in debate.

sanskrit: ततो यूपोच्छ्रये प्राप्ते षड्बैल्वान्भरतर्षभ ।खादिरान्बिल्वसमितांस्तावतः सर्ववर्णिनः ॥२६॥
transliteration: tato yūpocchraye prāpte ṣaḍbailvānbharatarṣabha ,khādirānbilvasamitāṁstāvataḥ sarvavarṇinaḥ.
english: Then, O best of Bharatas, when the time arrived for the erection of the sacrificial posts for the great Vedic ritual (yajña), six posts made of Bilva wood and an equal number made of Khadira wood, suitable for all classes, were prepared.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 90 Verse 26. Then, O best of Bharatas, when the time arrived for the erection of the sacrificial posts for the great Vedic ritual (yajña), six posts made of Bilva wood and an equal number made of Khadira wood, suitable for all classes, were prepared.

sanskrit: देवदारुमयौ द्वौ तु यूपौ कुरुपतेः क्रतौ ।श्लेष्मातकमयं चैकं याजकाः समकारयन् ॥२७॥
transliteration: devadārumayau dvau tu yūpau kurupateḥ kratau ,śleṣmātakamayaṁ caikaṁ yājakāḥ samakārayan.
english: And in the Vedic ritual (yajña) of the lord of the Kurus, the officiating priests had two sacrificial posts made of Deodar wood and one made of Śleṣmātaka wood constructed.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 90 Verse 27. And in the Vedic ritual (yajña) of the lord of the Kurus, the officiating priests had two sacrificial posts made of Deodar wood and one made of Śleṣmātaka wood constructed.

sanskrit: शोभार्थं चापरान्यूपान्काञ्चनान्पुरुषर्षभ ।स भीमः कारयामास धर्मराजस्य शासनात् ॥२८॥
transliteration: śobhārthaṁ cāparānyūpānkāñcanānpuruṣarṣabha ,sa bhīmaḥ kārayāmāsa dharmarājasya śāsanāt.
english: And for adornment, O best of men, that Bhīma, by the command of the king of natural law (dharma-rāja), had other golden sacrificial posts constructed.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 90 Verse 28. And for adornment, O best of men, that Bhīma, by the command of the king of natural law (dharma-rāja), had other golden sacrificial posts constructed.

sanskrit: ते व्यराजन्त राजर्षे वासोभिरुपशोभिताः ।नरेन्द्राभिगता देवान्यथा सप्तर्षयो दिवि ॥२९॥
transliteration: te vyarājanta rājarṣe vāsobhirupaśobhitāḥ ,narendrābhigatā devānyathā saptarṣayo divi.
english: O royal sage, those posts, beautifully adorned with garments and visited by kings, shone brightly, just as the seven sages shine among the gods in the sky.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 90 Verse 29. O royal sage, those posts, beautifully adorned with garments and visited by kings, shone brightly, just as the seven sages shine among the gods in the sky.

sanskrit: इष्टकाः काञ्चनीश्चात्र चयनार्थं कृताभवन् ।शुशुभे चयनं तत्र दक्षस्येव प्रजापतेः ॥३०॥
transliteration: iṣṭakāḥ kāñcanīścātra cayanārthaṁ kṛtābhavan ,śuśubhe cayanaṁ tatra dakṣasyeva prajāpateḥ.
english: Golden bricks were made there for the construction of the fire altar. That altar shone beautifully, just like the altar of Prajāpati Dakṣa.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 90 Verse 30. Golden bricks were made there for the construction of the fire altar. That altar shone beautifully, just like the altar of Prajāpati Dakṣa.

sanskrit: चतुश्चित्यः स तस्यासीदष्टादशकरात्मकः ।स रुक्मपक्षो निचितस्त्रिगुणो गरुडाकृतिः ॥३१॥
transliteration: catuścityaḥ sa tasyāsīdaṣṭādaśakarātmakaḥ ,sa rukmapakṣo nicitastriguṇo garuḍākṛtiḥ.
english: That altar was constructed in four layers, measuring eighteen "kara" (units of a hand's length). It was built with golden wings, threefold in its dimensions, and had the form of Garuḍa.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 90 Verse 31. That altar was constructed in four layers, measuring eighteen "kara" (units of a hand's length). It was built with golden wings, threefold in its dimensions, and had the form of Garuḍa.

sanskrit: ततो नियुक्ताः पशवो यथाशास्त्रं मनीषिभिः ।तं तं देवं समुद्दिश्य पक्षिणः पशवश्च ये ॥३२॥
transliteration: tato niyuktāḥ paśavo yathāśāstraṁ manīṣibhiḥ ,taṁ taṁ devaṁ samuddiśya pakṣiṇaḥ paśavaśca ye.
english: Then, the learned (manīṣibhiḥ), dedicating them to their respective deities, offered the animals and birds according to scriptural injunctions.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 90 Verse 32. Then, the learned (manīṣibhiḥ), dedicating them to their respective deities, offered the animals and birds according to scriptural injunctions.

sanskrit: ऋषभाः शास्त्रपठितास्तथा जलचराश्च ये ।सर्वांस्तानभ्ययुञ्जंस्ते तत्राग्निचयकर्मणि ॥३३॥
transliteration: ṛṣabhāḥ śāstrapaṭhitāstathā jalacarāśca ye ,sarvāṁstānabhyayuñjaṁste tatrāgnicayakarmaṇi.
english: And those bulls specified in the scriptures, as well as the aquatic creatures, all of them they offered there in the "agnicayana" (Vedic fire ritual).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 90 Verse 33. And those bulls specified in the scriptures, as well as the aquatic creatures, all of them they offered there in the "agnicayana" (Vedic fire ritual).

sanskrit: यूपेषु नियतं चासीत्पशूनां त्रिशतं तथा ।अश्वरत्नोत्तरं राज्ञः कौन्तेयस्य महात्मनः ॥३४॥
transliteration: yūpeṣu niyataṁ cāsītpaśūnāṁ triśataṁ tathā ,aśvaratnottaraṁ rājñaḥ kaunteyasya mahātmanaḥ.
english: Certainly, three hundred animals were tied to the sacrificial posts. Additionally, there was the paramount horse belonging to the great-souled King, the son of Kunti (Yudhishthira).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 90 Verse 34. Certainly, three hundred animals were tied to the sacrificial posts. Additionally, there was the paramount horse belonging to the great-souled King, the son of Kunti (Yudhishthira).

sanskrit: स यज्ञः शुशुभे तस्य साक्षाद्देवर्षिसंकुलः ।गन्धर्वगणसंकीर्णः शोभितोऽप्सरसां गणैः ॥३५॥
transliteration: sa yajñaḥ śuśubhe tasya sākṣāddevarṣisaṁkulaḥ ,gandharvagaṇasaṁkīrṇaḥ śobhito'psarasāṁ gaṇaiḥ.
english: That Vedic ritual (yajña) of his shone splendidly, visibly crowded with divine sages (devarṣis), thronged by groups of Gandharvas, and graced by hosts of Apsaras.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 90 Verse 35. That Vedic ritual (yajña) of his shone splendidly, visibly crowded with divine sages (devarṣis), thronged by groups of Gandharvas, and graced by hosts of Apsaras.

sanskrit: स किंपुरुषगीतैश्च किंनरैरुपशोभितः ।सिद्धविप्रनिवासैश्च समन्तादभिसंवृतः ॥३६॥
transliteration: sa kiṁpuruṣagītaiśca kiṁnarairupaśobhitaḥ ,siddhavipranivāsaiśca samantādabhisaṁvṛtaḥ.
english: That (Vedic ritual) was adorned by the songs of Kimpurushas and by Kinnaras. Moreover, it was entirely surrounded by the residences of perfected beings (Siddhas) and Brahmins.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 90 Verse 36. That (Vedic ritual) was adorned by the songs of Kimpurushas and by Kinnaras. Moreover, it was entirely surrounded by the residences of perfected beings (Siddhas) and Brahmins.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्सदसि नित्यास्तु व्यासशिष्या द्विजोत्तमाः ।सर्वशास्त्रप्रणेतारः कुशला यज्ञकर्मसु ॥३७॥
transliteration: tasminsadasi nityāstu vyāsaśiṣyā dvijottamāḥ ,sarvaśāstrapraṇetāraḥ kuśalā yajñakarmasu.
english: Indeed, in that assembly, the disciples of Vyasa were constantly present – excellent twice-born (dvijas) who were promulgators of all scriptures and skilled in the acts of the Vedic ritual (yajña) (karma).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 90 Verse 37. Indeed, in that assembly, the disciples of Vyasa were constantly present – excellent twice-born (dvijas) who were promulgators of all scriptures and skilled in the acts of the Vedic ritual (yajña) (karma).

sanskrit: नारदश्च बभूवात्र तुम्बुरुश्च महाद्युतिः ।विश्वावसुश्चित्रसेनस्तथान्ये गीतकोविदाः ॥३८॥
transliteration: nāradaśca babhūvātra tumburuśca mahādyutiḥ ,viśvāvasuścitrasenastathānye gītakovidāḥ.
english: Nārada was present there, along with Tumburu, who was greatly effulgent, and Viśvāvasu, Citrasena, and other experts in music.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 90 Verse 38. Nārada was present there, along with Tumburu, who was greatly effulgent, and Viśvāvasu, Citrasena, and other experts in music.

sanskrit: गन्धर्वा गीतकुशला नृत्तेषु च विशारदाः ।रमयन्ति स्म तान्विप्रान्यज्ञकर्मान्तरेष्वथ ॥३९॥
transliteration: gandharvā gītakuśalā nṛtteṣu ca viśāradāḥ ,ramayanti sma tānviprānyajñakarmāntareṣvatha.
english: The Gandharvas, skilled in music and expert in dances, then entertained those Brahmins during the intervals of the Vedic rituals (yajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 90 Verse 39. The Gandharvas, skilled in music and expert in dances, then entertained those Brahmins during the intervals of the Vedic rituals (yajña).

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।शमयित्वा पशूनन्यान्विधिवद्द्विजसत्तमाः ।तुरगं तं यथाशास्त्रमालभन्त द्विजातयः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,śamayitvā paśūnanyānvidhivaddvijasattamāḥ ,turagaṁ taṁ yathāśāstramālabhanta dvijātayaḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: 'O best among the twice-born (dvija) Brahmins, having properly consecrated the other animals, the twice-born (dvija) priests then consecrated that horse according to the scriptures.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 91 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: 'O best among the twice-born (dvija) Brahmins, having properly consecrated the other animals, the twice-born (dvija) priests then consecrated that horse according to the scriptures.'

sanskrit: ततः संज्ञाप्य तुरगं विधिवद्याजकर्षभाः ।उपसंवेशयन्राजंस्ततस्तां द्रुपदात्मजाम् ।कलाभिस्तिसृभी राजन्यथाविधि मनस्विनीम् ॥२॥
transliteration: tataḥ saṁjñāpya turagaṁ vidhivadyājakarṣabhāḥ ,upasaṁveśayanrājaṁstatastāṁ drupadātmajām ,kalābhistisṛbhī rājanyathāvidhi manasvinīm.
english: Then, O King, the chief priests, having properly consecrated the horse, subsequently caused that high-minded daughter of Drupada (Draupadī) to sit (for the ritual), in accordance with three ritual aspects (kalā) and the prescribed procedure.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 91 Verse 2. Then, O King, the chief priests, having properly consecrated the horse, subsequently caused that high-minded daughter of Drupada (Draupadī) to sit (for the ritual), in accordance with three ritual aspects (kalā) and the prescribed procedure.

sanskrit: उद्धृत्य तु वपां तस्य यथाशास्त्रं द्विजर्षभाः ।श्रपयामासुरव्यग्राः शास्त्रवद्भरतर्षभ ॥३॥
transliteration: uddhṛtya tu vapāṁ tasya yathāśāstraṁ dvijarṣabhāḥ ,śrapayāmāsuravyagrāḥ śāstravadbharatarṣabha.
english: O best of the Bharatas, the foremost among the twice-born priests, unhurriedly and precisely according to the scriptures, extracted the omentum from it and cooked it ritually.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 91 Verse 3. O best of the Bharatas, the foremost among the twice-born priests, unhurriedly and precisely according to the scriptures, extracted the omentum from it and cooked it ritually.

sanskrit: तं वपाधूमगन्धं तु धर्मराजः सहानुजः ।उपाजिघ्रद्यथान्यायं सर्वपाप्मापहं तदा ॥४॥
transliteration: taṁ vapādhūmagandhaṁ tu dharmarājaḥ sahānujaḥ ,upājighradyathānyāyaṁ sarvapāpmāpahaṁ tadā.
english: Then, King Dharmarāja (Yudhishthira), accompanied by his younger brothers, properly inhaled that smoke-scent of the omentum, which purifies and removes all sins.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 91 Verse 4. Then, King Dharmarāja (Yudhishthira), accompanied by his younger brothers, properly inhaled that smoke-scent of the omentum, which purifies and removes all sins.

sanskrit: शिष्टान्यङ्गानि यान्यासंस्तस्याश्वस्य नराधिप ।तान्यग्नौ जुहुवुर्धीराः समस्ताः षोडशर्त्विजः ॥५॥
transliteration: śiṣṭānyaṅgāni yānyāsaṁstasyāśvasya narādhipa ,tānyagnau juhuvurdhīrāḥ samastāḥ ṣoḍaśartvijaḥ.
english: O King, all sixteen resolute priests collectively offered into the fire those remaining parts which belonged to that horse.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 91 Verse 5. O King, all sixteen resolute priests collectively offered into the fire those remaining parts which belonged to that horse.

sanskrit: संस्थाप्यैवं तस्य राज्ञस्तं क्रतुं शक्रतेजसः ।व्यासः सशिष्यो भगवान्वर्धयामास तं नृपम् ॥६॥
transliteration: saṁsthāpyaivaṁ tasya rājñastaṁ kratuṁ śakratejasaḥ ,vyāsaḥ saśiṣyo bhagavānvardhayāmāsa taṁ nṛpam.
english: Thus, having successfully concluded that Vedic ritual (kratu) of the king, whose prowess was like Indra's, the venerable Vyasa, accompanied by his disciples, blessed that king.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 91 Verse 6. Thus, having successfully concluded that Vedic ritual (kratu) of the king, whose prowess was like Indra's, the venerable Vyasa, accompanied by his disciples, blessed that king.

sanskrit: ततो युधिष्ठिरः प्रादात्सदस्येभ्यो यथाविधि ।कोटीसहस्रं निष्काणां व्यासाय तु वसुंधराम् ॥७॥
transliteration: tato yudhiṣṭhiraḥ prādātsadasyebhyo yathāvidhi ,koṭīsahasraṁ niṣkāṇāṁ vyāsāya tu vasuṁdharām.
english: Then, Yudhishthira, as per proper procedure, presented a thousand crores of gold coins (nishkas) to the assembly members, and the entire earth to Vyasa.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 91 Verse 7. Then, Yudhishthira, as per proper procedure, presented a thousand crores of gold coins (nishkas) to the assembly members, and the entire earth to Vyasa.

sanskrit: प्रतिगृह्य धरां राजन्व्यासः सत्यवतीसुतः ।अब्रवीद्भरतश्रेष्ठं धर्मात्मानं युधिष्ठिरम् ॥८॥
transliteration: pratigṛhya dharāṁ rājanvyāsaḥ satyavatīsutaḥ ,abravīdbharataśreṣṭhaṁ dharmātmānaṁ yudhiṣṭhiram.
english: O King, after accepting the earth, Vyasa, the son of Satyavati, addressed Yudhishthira, who was the foremost among the Bharatas and embodied (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 91 Verse 8. O King, after accepting the earth, Vyasa, the son of Satyavati, addressed Yudhishthira, who was the foremost among the Bharatas and embodied (dharma).

sanskrit: पृथिवी भवतस्त्वेषा संन्यस्ता राजसत्तम ।निष्क्रयो दीयतां मह्यं ब्राह्मणा हि धनार्थिनः ॥९॥
transliteration: pṛthivī bhavatastveṣā saṁnyastā rājasattama ,niṣkrayo dīyatāṁ mahyaṁ brāhmaṇā hi dhanārthinaḥ.
english: “O best of kings, this earth has been truly given by you. Therefore, let compensation be given to me, for brahmins (brāhmaṇa) are indeed seekers of wealth.”
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 91 Verse 9. “O best of kings, this earth has been truly given by you. Therefore, let compensation be given to me, for brahmins (brāhmaṇa) are indeed seekers of wealth.”

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरस्तु तान्विप्रान्प्रत्युवाच महामनाः ।भ्रातृभिः सहितो धीमान्मध्ये राज्ञां महात्मनाम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhirastu tānviprānpratyuvāca mahāmanāḥ ,bhrātṛbhiḥ sahito dhīmānmadhye rājñāṁ mahātmanām.
english: However, the magnanimous and wise Yudhishthira, accompanied by his brothers, responded to those brahmins (brāhmaṇa) while amidst the assembly of great-souled kings.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 91 Verse 10. However, the magnanimous and wise Yudhishthira, accompanied by his brothers, responded to those brahmins (brāhmaṇa) while amidst the assembly of great-souled kings.

sanskrit: अश्वमेधे महायज्ञे पृथिवी दक्षिणा स्मृता ।अर्जुनेन जिता सेयमृत्विग्भ्यः प्रापिता मया ॥११॥
transliteration: aśvamedhe mahāyajñe pṛthivī dakṣiṇā smṛtā ,arjunena jitā seyamṛtvigbhyaḥ prāpitā mayā.
english: In the great aśvamedha (Vedic ritual), the earth is declared as the sacrificial fee (dakṣiṇā). This very earth, conquered by Arjuna, was obtained by me for the priests (ṛtvij).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 91 Verse 11. In the great aśvamedha (Vedic ritual), the earth is declared as the sacrificial fee (dakṣiṇā). This very earth, conquered by Arjuna, was obtained by me for the priests (ṛtvij).

sanskrit: वनं प्रवेक्ष्ये विप्रेन्द्रा विभजध्वं महीमिमाम् ।चतुर्धा पृथिवीं कृत्वा चातुर्होत्रप्रमाणतः ॥१२॥
transliteration: vanaṁ pravekṣye viprendrā vibhajadhvaṁ mahīmimām ,caturdhā pṛthivīṁ kṛtvā cāturhotrapramāṇataḥ.
english: O chief among brahmins (viprendra)! I shall enter the forest. Divide this earth, having apportioned it into four parts according to the standard of the four hotṛ priests.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 91 Verse 12. O chief among brahmins (viprendra)! I shall enter the forest. Divide this earth, having apportioned it into four parts according to the standard of the four hotṛ priests.

sanskrit: नाहमादातुमिच्छामि ब्रह्मस्वं मुनिसत्तमाः ।इदं हि मे मतं नित्यं भ्रातॄणां च ममानघाः ॥१३॥
transliteration: nāhamādātumicchāmi brahmasvaṁ munisattamāḥ ,idaṁ hi me mataṁ nityaṁ bhrātṝṇāṁ ca mamānaghāḥ.
english: O best among sages (muni), O sinless ones! I do not wish to take the property of brahmins (brahmasva). This, indeed, is my constant opinion, and that of my brothers as well.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 91 Verse 13. O best among sages (muni), O sinless ones! I do not wish to take the property of brahmins (brahmasva). This, indeed, is my constant opinion, and that of my brothers as well.

sanskrit: इत्युक्तवति तस्मिंस्ते भ्रातरो द्रौपदी च सा ।एवमेतदिति प्राहुस्तदभूद्रोमहर्षणम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: ityuktavati tasmiṁste bhrātaro draupadī ca sā ,evametaditi prāhustadabhūdromaharṣaṇam.
english: When he had thus spoken, his brothers and Draupadī said, "So be it!" That was indeed a hair-raising moment.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 91 Verse 14. When he had thus spoken, his brothers and Draupadī said, "So be it!" That was indeed a hair-raising moment.

sanskrit: ततोऽन्तरिक्षे वागासीत्साधु साध्विति भारत ।तथैव द्विजसंघानां शंसतां विबभौ स्वनः ॥१५॥
transliteration: tato'ntarikṣe vāgāsītsādhu sādhviti bhārata ,tathaiva dvijasaṁghānāṁ śaṁsatāṁ vibabhau svanaḥ.
english: Then, O Bhārata, a voice in the sky declared, 'Excellent! Excellent!' Similarly, the sound of the assembled twice-born (dvija) praising resounded.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 91 Verse 15. Then, O Bhārata, a voice in the sky declared, 'Excellent! Excellent!' Similarly, the sound of the assembled twice-born (dvija) praising resounded.

sanskrit: द्वैपायनस्तथोक्तस्तु पुनरेव युधिष्ठिरम् ।उवाच मध्ये विप्राणामिदं संपूजयन्मुनिः ॥१६॥
transliteration: dvaipāyanastathoktastu punareva yudhiṣṭhiram ,uvāca madhye viprāṇāmidaṁ saṁpūjayanmuniḥ.
english: The sage (muni) Dvaipāyana, having been thus addressed, again spoke these words to Yudhiṣṭhira, in the midst of the brāhmins, showing reverence.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 91 Verse 16. The sage (muni) Dvaipāyana, having been thus addressed, again spoke these words to Yudhiṣṭhira, in the midst of the brāhmins, showing reverence.

sanskrit: दत्तैषा भवता मह्यं तां ते प्रतिददाम्यहम् ।हिरण्यं दीयतामेभ्यो द्विजातिभ्यो धरास्तु ते ॥१७॥
transliteration: dattaiṣā bhavatā mahyaṁ tāṁ te pratidadāmyaham ,hiraṇyaṁ dīyatāmebhyo dvijātibhyo dharāstu te.
english: This (earth) was given by you to me; I return it to you. Let gold be given to these twice-born (dvijāti), and let the lands (dharā) remain yours.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 91 Verse 17. This (earth) was given by you to me; I return it to you. Let gold be given to these twice-born (dvijāti), and let the lands (dharā) remain yours.

sanskrit: ततोऽब्रवीद्वासुदेवो धर्मराजं युधिष्ठिरम् ।यथाह भगवान्व्यासस्तथा तत्कर्तुमर्हसि ॥१८॥
transliteration: tato'bravīdvāsudevo dharmarājaṁ yudhiṣṭhiram ,yathāha bhagavānvyāsastathā tatkartumarhasi.
english: Then Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa) spoke to Dharmarāja Yudhiṣṭhira: 'Just as the revered Bhagavān Vyāsa has said, so you should do that.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 91 Verse 18. Then Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa) spoke to Dharmarāja Yudhiṣṭhira: 'Just as the revered Bhagavān Vyāsa has said, so you should do that.'

sanskrit: इत्युक्तः स कुरुश्रेष्ठः प्रीतात्मा भ्रातृभिः सह ।कोटिकोटिकृतां प्रादाद्दक्षिणां त्रिगुणां क्रतोः ॥१९॥
transliteration: ityuktaḥ sa kuruśreṣṭhaḥ prītātmā bhrātṛbhiḥ saha ,koṭikoṭikṛtāṁ prādāddakṣiṇāṁ triguṇāṁ kratoḥ.
english: Thus addressed, that best of Kurus, Yudhishthira, with a satisfied mind, accompanied by his brothers, presented an immense fee (dakṣiṇā), three times the customary amount for the (Vedic ritual).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 91 Verse 19. Thus addressed, that best of Kurus, Yudhishthira, with a satisfied mind, accompanied by his brothers, presented an immense fee (dakṣiṇā), three times the customary amount for the (Vedic ritual).

sanskrit: न करिष्यति तल्लोके कश्चिदन्यो नराधिपः ।यत्कृतं कुरुसिंहेन मरुत्तस्यानुकुर्वता ॥२०॥
transliteration: na kariṣyati talloke kaścidanyo narādhipaḥ ,yatkṛtaṁ kurusiṁhena maruttasyānukurvatā.
english: No other king (narādhipa) in the world will ever achieve what the lion of the Kurus (Yudhishthira) accomplished, as he emulated (king) Marutta.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 91 Verse 20. No other king (narādhipa) in the world will ever achieve what the lion of the Kurus (Yudhishthira) accomplished, as he emulated (king) Marutta.

sanskrit: प्रतिगृह्य तु तद्द्रव्यं कृष्णद्वैपायनः प्रभुः ।ऋत्विग्भ्यः प्रददौ विद्वांश्चतुर्धा व्यभजंश्च ते ॥२१॥
transliteration: pratigṛhya tu taddravyaṁ kṛṣṇadvaipāyanaḥ prabhuḥ ,ṛtvigbhyaḥ pradadau vidvāṁścaturdhā vyabhajaṁśca te.
english: Indeed, the learned and authoritative Krishna Dvaipayana (Vyasa), having received that wealth, distributed it to the officiating priests (ṛtvij); and they, in turn, divided it into four parts.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 91 Verse 21. Indeed, the learned and authoritative Krishna Dvaipayana (Vyasa), having received that wealth, distributed it to the officiating priests (ṛtvij); and they, in turn, divided it into four parts.

sanskrit: पृथिव्या निष्क्रयं दत्त्वा तद्धिरण्यं युधिष्ठिरः ।धूतपाप्मा जितस्वर्गो मुमुदे भ्रातृभिः सह ॥२२॥
transliteration: pṛthivyā niṣkrayaṁ dattvā taddhiraṇyaṁ yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,dhūtapāpmā jitasvargo mumude bhrātṛbhiḥ saha.
english: Having given that gold as compensation for the earth, Yudhishthira, purified from sin and having secured heaven, rejoiced with his brothers.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 91 Verse 22. Having given that gold as compensation for the earth, Yudhishthira, purified from sin and having secured heaven, rejoiced with his brothers.

sanskrit: ऋत्विजस्तमपर्यन्तं सुवर्णनिचयं तदा ।व्यभजन्त द्विजातिभ्यो यथोत्साहं यथाबलम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: ṛtvijastamaparyantaṁ suvarṇanicayaṁ tadā ,vyabhajanta dvijātibhyo yathotsāhaṁ yathābalam.
english: At that time, the officiating priests distributed that boundless heap of gold among the twice-born (dvija) according to their enthusiasm and capacity.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 91 Verse 23. At that time, the officiating priests distributed that boundless heap of gold among the twice-born (dvija) according to their enthusiasm and capacity.

sanskrit: यज्ञवाटे तु यत्किंचिद्धिरण्यमपि भूषणम् ।तोरणानि च यूपांश्च घटाः पात्रीस्तथेष्टकाः ।युधिष्ठिराभ्यनुज्ञाताः सर्वं तद्व्यभजन्द्विजाः ॥२४॥
transliteration: yajñavāṭe tu yatkiṁciddhiraṇyamapi bhūṣaṇam ,toraṇāni ca yūpāṁśca ghaṭāḥ pātrīstatheṣṭakāḥ ,yudhiṣṭhirābhyanujñātāḥ sarvaṁ tadvyabhajandvijāḥ.
english: Moreover, whatever gold and ornaments, as well as the arches, sacrificial posts, pots, vessels, and bricks, were present in the enclosure of the Vedic ritual (yajña), the twice-born (dvija), authorized by Yudhiṣṭhira, distributed all of that.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 91 Verse 24. Moreover, whatever gold and ornaments, as well as the arches, sacrificial posts, pots, vessels, and bricks, were present in the enclosure of the Vedic ritual (yajña), the twice-born (dvija), authorized by Yudhiṣṭhira, distributed all of that.

sanskrit: अनन्तरं ब्राह्मणेभ्यः क्षत्रिया जह्रिरे वसु ।तथा विट्शूद्रसंघाश्च तथान्ये म्लेच्छजातयः ।कालेन महता जह्रुस्तत्सुवर्णं ततस्ततः ॥२५॥
transliteration: anantaraṁ brāhmaṇebhyaḥ kṣatriyā jahrire vasu ,tathā viṭśūdrasaṁghāśca tathānye mlecchajātayaḥ ,kālena mahatā jahrustatsuvarṇaṁ tatastataḥ.
english: Subsequently, the Kṣatriyas took wealth from the Brahmins. Similarly, the communities of Vaiśyas and Śūdras, and other Mleccha communities, gradually took that gold from various places over a long period of time.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 91 Verse 25. Subsequently, the Kṣatriyas took wealth from the Brahmins. Similarly, the communities of Vaiśyas and Śūdras, and other Mleccha communities, gradually took that gold from various places over a long period of time.

sanskrit: ततस्ते ब्राह्मणाः सर्वे मुदिता जग्मुरालयान् ।तर्पिता वसुना तेन धर्मराज्ञा महात्मना ॥२६॥
transliteration: tataste brāhmaṇāḥ sarve muditā jagmurālayān ,tarpitā vasunā tena dharmarājñā mahātmanā.
english: Thereupon, all those Brahmins, delighted and satisfied by that wealth from the great-souled King of natural law (dharma), returned to their homes.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 91 Verse 26. Thereupon, all those Brahmins, delighted and satisfied by that wealth from the great-souled King of natural law (dharma), returned to their homes.

sanskrit: स्वमंशं भगवान्व्यासः कुन्त्यै पादाभिवादनात् ।प्रददौ तस्य महतो हिरण्यस्य महाद्युतिः ॥२७॥
transliteration: svamaṁśaṁ bhagavānvyāsaḥ kuntyai pādābhivādanāt ,pradadau tasya mahato hiraṇyasya mahādyutiḥ.
english: The revered Vyāsa (bhagavān), of great splendor, gave Kuntī her share of that abundant gold after she bowed at his feet.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 91 Verse 27. The revered Vyāsa (bhagavān), of great splendor, gave Kuntī her share of that abundant gold after she bowed at his feet.

sanskrit: श्वशुरात्प्रीतिदायं तं प्राप्य सा प्रीतमानसा ।चकार पुण्यं लोके तु सुमहान्तं पृथा तदा ॥२८॥
transliteration: śvaśurātprītidāyaṁ taṁ prāpya sā prītamānasā ,cakāra puṇyaṁ loke tu sumahāntaṁ pṛthā tadā.
english: Having received that loving gift from her father-in-law, she, Pṛthā, with a joyful mind, then performed a very great meritorious act (puṇyam) in the world.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 91 Verse 28. Having received that loving gift from her father-in-law, she, Pṛthā, with a joyful mind, then performed a very great meritorious act (puṇyam) in the world.

sanskrit: गत्वा त्ववभृथं राजा विपाप्मा भ्रातृभिः सह ।सभाज्यमानः शुशुभे महेन्द्रो दैवतैरिव ॥२९॥
transliteration: gatvā tvavabhṛthaṁ rājā vipāpmā bhrātṛbhiḥ saha ,sabhājyamānaḥ śuśubhe mahendro daivatairiva.
english: Having gone to the purificatory bath (avabhṛtham), the king, now free from sin, shone splendidly alongside his brothers, being honored like Mahendra (Indra) among the deities.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 91 Verse 29. Having gone to the purificatory bath (avabhṛtham), the king, now free from sin, shone splendidly alongside his brothers, being honored like Mahendra (Indra) among the deities.

sanskrit: पाण्डवाश्च महीपालैः समेतैः संवृतास्तदा ।अशोभन्त महाराज ग्रहास्तारागणैरिव ॥३०॥
transliteration: pāṇḍavāśca mahīpālaiḥ sametaiḥ saṁvṛtāstadā ,aśobhanta mahārāja grahāstārāgaṇairiva.
english: O great king (mahārāja), the Pāṇḍavas, surrounded by the assembled kings, then shone splendidly, just like planets amidst clusters of stars.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 91 Verse 30. O great king (mahārāja), the Pāṇḍavas, surrounded by the assembled kings, then shone splendidly, just like planets amidst clusters of stars.

sanskrit: राजभ्योऽपि ततः प्रादाद्रत्नानि विविधानि च ।गजानश्वानलंकारान्स्त्रियो वस्त्राणि काञ्चनम् ॥३१॥
transliteration: rājabhyo'pi tataḥ prādādratnāni vividhāni ca ,gajānaśvānalaṁkārānstriyo vastrāṇi kāñcanam.
english: Thereafter, he presented various jewels, and elephants, horses, ornaments, women, clothing, and gold even to the kings.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 91 Verse 31. Thereafter, he presented various jewels, and elephants, horses, ornaments, women, clothing, and gold even to the kings.

sanskrit: तद्धनौघमपर्यन्तं पार्थः पार्थिवमण्डले ।विसृजञ्शुशुभे राजा यथा वैश्रवणस्तथा ॥३२॥
transliteration: taddhanaughamaparyantaṁ pārthaḥ pārthivamaṇḍale ,visṛjañśuśubhe rājā yathā vaiśravaṇastathā.
english: Distributing that boundless flood of riches among the assembly of rulers, King Pārtha (Arjuna) shone splendidly, just like Vaiśravaṇa.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 91 Verse 32. Distributing that boundless flood of riches among the assembly of rulers, King Pārtha (Arjuna) shone splendidly, just like Vaiśravaṇa.

sanskrit: आनाय्य च तथा वीरं राजानं बभ्रुवाहनम् ।प्रदाय विपुलं वित्तं गृहान्प्रास्थापयत्तदा ॥३३॥
transliteration: ānāyya ca tathā vīraṁ rājānaṁ babhruvāhanam ,pradāya vipulaṁ vittaṁ gṛhānprāsthāpayattadā.
english: And then, having summoned the heroic King Babhruvahana, and having bestowed upon him ample wealth, he dispatched him to his home.
