search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 91 Verse 33. And then, having summoned the heroic King Babhruvahana, and having bestowed upon him ample wealth, he dispatched him to his home.

sanskrit: दुःशलायाश्च तं पौत्रं बालकं पार्थिवर्षभ ।स्वराज्ये पितृभिर्गुप्ते प्रीत्या समभिषेचयत् ॥३४॥
transliteration: duḥśalāyāśca taṁ pautraṁ bālakaṁ pārthivarṣabha ,svarājye pitṛbhirgupte prītyā samabhiṣecayat.
english: And O best among kings, he lovingly consecrated that child, the grandson of Duḥśalā, in his own kingdom, which was protected by his ancestors.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 91 Verse 34. And O best among kings, he lovingly consecrated that child, the grandson of Duḥśalā, in his own kingdom, which was protected by his ancestors.

sanskrit: राज्ञश्चैवापि तान्सर्वान्सुविभक्तान्सुपूजितान् ।प्रस्थापयामास वशी कुरुराजो युधिष्ठिरः ॥३५॥
transliteration: rājñaścaivāpi tānsarvānsuvibhaktānsupūjitān ,prasthāpayāmāsa vaśī kururājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: And indeed, the self-controlled King of the Kurus, Yudhishthira, sent forth all those kings who had been well-assigned (their duties) and duly honored.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 91 Verse 35. And indeed, the self-controlled King of the Kurus, Yudhishthira, sent forth all those kings who had been well-assigned (their duties) and duly honored.

sanskrit: एवं बभूव यज्ञः स धर्मराजस्य धीमतः ।बह्वन्नधनरत्नौघः सुरामैरेयसागरः ॥३६॥
transliteration: evaṁ babhūva yajñaḥ sa dharmarājasya dhīmataḥ ,bahvannadhanaratnaughaḥ surāmaireyasāgaraḥ.
english: Thus, that (Vedic ritual) (yajña) of the wise King of Righteousness (dharma), Yudhishthira, became an overwhelming abundance of food, wealth, and jewels, like an ocean of liquor and Maireya.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 91 Verse 36. Thus, that (Vedic ritual) (yajña) of the wise King of Righteousness (dharma), Yudhishthira, became an overwhelming abundance of food, wealth, and jewels, like an ocean of liquor and Maireya.

sanskrit: सर्पिःपङ्का ह्रदा यत्र बहवश्चान्नपर्वताः ।रसालाकर्दमाः कुल्या बभूवुर्भरतर्षभ ॥३७॥
transliteration: sarpiḥpaṅkā hradā yatra bahavaścānnaparvatāḥ ,rasālākardamāḥ kulyā babhūvurbharatarṣabha.
english: O best of Bharatas, where there were lakes muddy with ghee, and many mountains of food; and canals became muddy with rasālā (a sweet dish).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 91 Verse 37. O best of Bharatas, where there were lakes muddy with ghee, and many mountains of food; and canals became muddy with rasālā (a sweet dish).

sanskrit: भक्ष्यषाण्डवरागाणां क्रियतां भुज्यतामिति ।पशूनां वध्यतां चापि नान्तस्तत्र स्म दृश्यते ॥३८॥
transliteration: bhakṣyaṣāṇḍavarāgāṇāṁ kriyatāṁ bhujyatāmiti ,paśūnāṁ vadhyatāṁ cāpi nāntastatra sma dṛśyate.
english: There, regarding the various edibles, sauces, and syrups, (one would hear calls like) "Let them be prepared!" and "Let them be eaten!" Also, regarding animals, "Let them be slaughtered!" Indeed, no end to this was seen there.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 91 Verse 38. There, regarding the various edibles, sauces, and syrups, (one would hear calls like) "Let them be prepared!" and "Let them be eaten!" Also, regarding animals, "Let them be slaughtered!" Indeed, no end to this was seen there.

sanskrit: मत्तोन्मत्तप्रमुदितं प्रगीतयुवतीजनम् ।मृदङ्गशङ्खशब्दैश्च मनोरममभूत्तदा ॥३९॥
transliteration: mattonmattapramuditaṁ pragītayuvatījanam ,mṛdaṅgaśaṅkhaśabdaiśca manoramamabhūttadā.
english: At that time, it was delightful, filled with joyfully intoxicated and frenzied people, and with young women singing, accompanied by the sounds of mridangas and conches.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 91 Verse 39. At that time, it was delightful, filled with joyfully intoxicated and frenzied people, and with young women singing, accompanied by the sounds of mridangas and conches.

sanskrit: दीयतां भुज्यतां चेति दिवारात्रमवारितम् ।तं महोत्सवसंकाशमतिहृष्टजनाकुलम् ।कथयन्ति स्म पुरुषा नानादेशनिवासिनः ॥४०॥
transliteration: dīyatāṁ bhujyatāṁ ceti divārātramavāritam ,taṁ mahotsavasaṁkāśamatihṛṣṭajanākulam ,kathayanti sma puruṣā nānādeśanivāsinaḥ.
english: Day and night, without interruption, men from various lands would say, 'Let it be given! Let it be enjoyed!' They described that event as resembling a grand festival, teeming with exceedingly joyful individuals.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 91 Verse 40. Day and night, without interruption, men from various lands would say, 'Let it be given! Let it be enjoyed!' They described that event as resembling a grand festival, teeming with exceedingly joyful individuals.

sanskrit: वर्षित्वा धनधाराभिः कामै रत्नैर्धनैस्तथा ।विपाप्मा भरतश्रेष्ठः कृतार्थः प्राविशत्पुरम् ॥४१॥
transliteration: varṣitvā dhanadhārābhiḥ kāmai ratnairdhanaistathā ,vipāpmā bharataśreṣṭhaḥ kṛtārthaḥ prāviśatpuram.
english: Having showered forth streams of wealth, fulfilling desires with jewels and riches, the best of Bharatas (Bharatśreṣṭhaḥ), now free from impurity (vipāpmā) and having achieved his objectives, entered the city.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 91 Verse 41. Having showered forth streams of wealth, fulfilling desires with jewels and riches, the best of Bharatas (Bharatśreṣṭhaḥ), now free from impurity (vipāpmā) and having achieved his objectives, entered the city.

sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।पितामहस्य मे यज्ञे धर्मपुत्रस्य धीमतः ।यदाश्चर्यमभूत्किंचित्तद्भवान्वक्तुमर्हति ॥१॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,pitāmahasya me yajñe dharmaputrasya dhīmataḥ ,yadāścaryamabhūtkiṁcittadbhavānvaktumarhati.
english: Janamejaya said: 'You (your honor) should recount whatever marvel occurred during the [yajña] (Vedic ritual) of my wise grandfather, the son of [dharma].'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 92 Verse 1. Janamejaya said: 'You (your honor) should recount whatever marvel occurred during the [yajña] (Vedic ritual) of my wise grandfather, the son of [dharma].'

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।श्रूयतां राजशार्दूल महदाश्चर्यमुत्तमम् ।अश्वमेधे महायज्ञे निवृत्ते यदभूद्विभो ॥२॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,śrūyatāṁ rājaśārdūla mahadāścaryamuttamam ,aśvamedhe mahāyajñe nivṛtte yadabhūdvibho.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: "O tiger among kings, O lord, listen to the great and most excellent wonder that occurred upon the completion of the great horse Vedic ritual (aśvamedha yajña)."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 92 Verse 2. Vaiśampāyana said: "O tiger among kings, O lord, listen to the great and most excellent wonder that occurred upon the completion of the great horse Vedic ritual (aśvamedha yajña)."

sanskrit: तर्पितेषु द्विजाग्र्येषु ज्ञातिसंबन्धिबन्धुषु ।दीनान्धकृपणे चापि तदा भरतसत्तम ॥३॥
transliteration: tarpiteṣu dvijāgryeṣu jñātisaṁbandhibandhuṣu ,dīnāndhakṛpaṇe cāpi tadā bharatasattama.
english: O best among the Bhāratas (Bharatasattama), at that time, when the foremost twice-born priests, relatives, kinsmen, and friends had been satisfied, and even the poor, blind, and helpless had been attended to.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 92 Verse 3. O best among the Bhāratas (Bharatasattama), at that time, when the foremost twice-born priests, relatives, kinsmen, and friends had been satisfied, and even the poor, blind, and helpless had been attended to.

sanskrit: घुष्यमाणे महादाने दिक्षु सर्वासु भारत ।पतत्सु पुष्पवर्षेषु धर्मराजस्य मूर्धनि ॥४॥
transliteration: ghuṣyamāṇe mahādāne dikṣu sarvāsu bhārata ,patatsu puṣpavarṣeṣu dharmarājasya mūrdhani.
english: O Bhārata, at that time, as a great gift (dāna) was being proclaimed in all directions, and showers of flowers were falling upon the head of the King of natural law (dharma-rāja), Yudhiṣṭhira.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 92 Verse 4. O Bhārata, at that time, as a great gift (dāna) was being proclaimed in all directions, and showers of flowers were falling upon the head of the King of natural law (dharma-rāja), Yudhiṣṭhira.

sanskrit: बिलान्निष्क्रम्य नकुलो रुक्मपार्श्वस्तदानघ ।वज्राशनिसमं नादममुञ्चत विशां पते ॥५॥
transliteration: bilānniṣkramya nakulo rukmapārśvastadānagha ,vajrāśanisamaṁ nādamamuñcata viśāṁ pate.
english: O sinless one (anagha), O lord of the people (viśāṃ pate), then a mongoose with golden sides emerged from its hole and uttered a roar like a thunderbolt.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 92 Verse 5. O sinless one (anagha), O lord of the people (viśāṃ pate), then a mongoose with golden sides emerged from its hole and uttered a roar like a thunderbolt.

sanskrit: सकृदुत्सृज्य तं नादं त्रासयानो मृगद्विजान् ।मानुषं वचनं प्राह धृष्टो बिलशयो महान् ॥६॥
transliteration: sakṛdutsṛjya taṁ nādaṁ trāsayāno mṛgadvijān ,mānuṣaṁ vacanaṁ prāha dhṛṣṭo bilaśayo mahān.
english: Once, having let out that roar and frightened the deer and birds, the great and audacious cave-dweller spoke human words.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 92 Verse 6. Once, having let out that roar and frightened the deer and birds, the great and audacious cave-dweller spoke human words.

sanskrit: सक्तुप्रस्थेन वो नायं यज्ञस्तुल्यो नराधिपाः ।उञ्छवृत्तेर्वदान्यस्य कुरुक्षेत्रनिवासिनः ॥७॥
transliteration: saktuprasthena vo nāyaṁ yajñastulyo narādhipāḥ ,uñchavṛttervadānyasya kurukṣetranivāsinaḥ.
english: O kings, this "Vedic ritual" (yajña) of yours is not comparable to that of the generous gleaner who resides in Kurukṣetra, not even by a "prastha" of barley meal.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 92 Verse 7. O kings, this "Vedic ritual" (yajña) of yours is not comparable to that of the generous gleaner who resides in Kurukṣetra, not even by a "prastha" of barley meal.

sanskrit: तस्य तद्वचनं श्रुत्वा नकुलस्य विशां पते ।विस्मयं परमं जग्मुः सर्वे ते ब्राह्मणर्षभाः ॥८॥
transliteration: tasya tadvacanaṁ śrutvā nakulasya viśāṁ pate ,vismayaṁ paramaṁ jagmuḥ sarve te brāhmaṇarṣabhāḥ.
english: O lord of the people, upon hearing those words of the mongoose, all those foremost among the Brahmins were greatly astonished.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 92 Verse 8. O lord of the people, upon hearing those words of the mongoose, all those foremost among the Brahmins were greatly astonished.

sanskrit: ततः समेत्य नकुलं पर्यपृच्छन्त ते द्विजाः ।कुतस्त्वं समनुप्राप्तो यज्ञं साधुसमागमम् ॥९॥
transliteration: tataḥ sametya nakulaṁ paryapṛcchanta te dvijāḥ ,kutastvaṁ samanuprāpto yajñaṁ sādhusamāgamam.
english: Thereupon, those Brahmins approached the mongoose and asked, 'From where have you arrived at this "Vedic ritual" (yajña), which is an assembly of virtuous people?'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 92 Verse 9. Thereupon, those Brahmins approached the mongoose and asked, 'From where have you arrived at this "Vedic ritual" (yajña), which is an assembly of virtuous people?'

sanskrit: किं बलं परमं तुभ्यं किं श्रुतं किं परायणम् ।कथं भवन्तं विद्याम यो नो यज्ञं विगर्हसे ॥१०॥
transliteration: kiṁ balaṁ paramaṁ tubhyaṁ kiṁ śrutaṁ kiṁ parāyaṇam ,kathaṁ bhavantaṁ vidyāma yo no yajñaṁ vigarhase.
english: What is your supreme strength, what is your scriptural knowledge, and what is your ultimate refuge? How can we know you, who criticize our Vedic ritual (yajña)?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 92 Verse 10. What is your supreme strength, what is your scriptural knowledge, and what is your ultimate refuge? How can we know you, who criticize our Vedic ritual (yajña)?

sanskrit: अविलुप्यागमं कृत्स्नं विधिज्ञैर्याजकैः कृतम् ।यथागमं यथान्यायं कर्तव्यं च यथाकृतम् ॥११॥
transliteration: avilupyāgamaṁ kṛtsnaṁ vidhijñairyājakaiḥ kṛtam ,yathāgamaṁ yathānyāyaṁ kartavyaṁ ca yathākṛtam.
english: Without violating the entire sacred tradition (āgama), (it should be) performed by priests who know the rituals, according to the sacred texts and according to propriety, and it should be done as it has been done.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 92 Verse 11. Without violating the entire sacred tradition (āgama), (it should be) performed by priests who know the rituals, according to the sacred texts and according to propriety, and it should be done as it has been done.

sanskrit: पूजार्हाः पूजिताश्चात्र विधिवच्छास्त्रचक्षुषा ।मन्त्रपूतं हुतश्चाग्निर्दत्तं देयममत्सरम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: pūjārhāḥ pūjitāścātra vidhivacchāstracakṣuṣā ,mantrapūtaṁ hutaścāgnirdattaṁ deyamamatsaram.
english: Here, those worthy of reverence have been duly honored by persons guided by the sacred texts (śāstra). The fire (agni) has been offered purified by mantras, and the proper offering (dāna) was given without jealousy.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 92 Verse 12. Here, those worthy of reverence have been duly honored by persons guided by the sacred texts (śāstra). The fire (agni) has been offered purified by mantras, and the proper offering (dāna) was given without jealousy.

sanskrit: तुष्टा द्विजर्षभाश्चात्र दानैर्बहुविधैरपि ।क्षत्रियाश्च सुयुद्धेन श्राद्धैरपि पितामहाः ॥१३॥
transliteration: tuṣṭā dvijarṣabhāścātra dānairbahuvidhairapi ,kṣatriyāśca suyuddhena śrāddhairapi pitāmahāḥ.
english: Here, the most excellent of the twice-born were satisfied by various kinds of gifts (dāna); the kṣatriyas by honorable combat; and the ancestors by ancestral offerings (śrāddha).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 92 Verse 13. Here, the most excellent of the twice-born were satisfied by various kinds of gifts (dāna); the kṣatriyas by honorable combat; and the ancestors by ancestral offerings (śrāddha).

sanskrit: पालनेन विशस्तुष्टाः कामैस्तुष्टा वरस्त्रियः ।अनुक्रोशैस्तथा शूद्रा दानशेषैः पृथग्जनाः ॥१४॥
transliteration: pālanena viśastuṣṭāḥ kāmaistuṣṭā varastriyaḥ ,anukrośaistathā śūdrā dānaśeṣaiḥ pṛthagjanāḥ.
english: The Vaiśyas are content with proper governance, and noble women are pleased by their desires. Similarly, Śūdras are satisfied by compassion, and ordinary people by the remains of charitable offerings.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 92 Verse 14. The Vaiśyas are content with proper governance, and noble women are pleased by their desires. Similarly, Śūdras are satisfied by compassion, and ordinary people by the remains of charitable offerings.

sanskrit: ज्ञातिसंबन्धिनस्तुष्टाः शौचेन च नृपस्य नः ।देवा हविर्भिः पुण्यैश्च रक्षणैः शरणागताः ॥१५॥
transliteration: jñātisaṁbandhinastuṣṭāḥ śaucena ca nṛpasya naḥ ,devā havirbhiḥ puṇyaiśca rakṣaṇaiḥ śaraṇāgatāḥ.
english: Our kinsmen and relatives are pleased by the king's purity. The gods are satisfied by sacred offerings and pious deeds, and those seeking refuge (śaraṇāgatāḥ) are pleased by protection.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 92 Verse 15. Our kinsmen and relatives are pleased by the king's purity. The gods are satisfied by sacred offerings and pious deeds, and those seeking refuge (śaraṇāgatāḥ) are pleased by protection.

sanskrit: यदत्र तथ्यं तद्ब्रूहि सत्यसंध द्विजातिषु ।यथाश्रुतं यथादृष्टं पृष्टो ब्राह्मणकाम्यया ॥१६॥
transliteration: yadatra tathyaṁ tadbrūhi satyasaṁdha dvijātiṣu ,yathāśrutaṁ yathādṛṣṭaṁ pṛṣṭo brāhmaṇakāmyayā.
english: O truthful one, explain what is truly the case in this matter concerning the twice-born (dvijāti), just as you have heard and as you have seen, since you have been questioned out of the Brahmins' desire.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 92 Verse 16. O truthful one, explain what is truly the case in this matter concerning the twice-born (dvijāti), just as you have heard and as you have seen, since you have been questioned out of the Brahmins' desire.

sanskrit: श्रद्धेयवाक्यः प्राज्ञस्त्वं दिव्यं रूपं बिभर्षि च ।समागतश्च विप्रैस्त्वं तत्त्वतो वक्तुमर्हसि ॥१७॥
transliteration: śraddheyavākyaḥ prājñastvaṁ divyaṁ rūpaṁ bibharṣi ca ,samāgataśca vipraistvaṁ tattvato vaktumarhasi.
english: You are trustworthy and wise, and you possess a divine form. Furthermore, since you have gathered with the Brahmins, you should speak the truth.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 92 Verse 17. You are trustworthy and wise, and you possess a divine form. Furthermore, since you have gathered with the Brahmins, you should speak the truth.

sanskrit: इति पृष्टो द्विजैस्तैः स प्रहस्य नकुलोऽब्रवीत् ।नैषानृता मया वाणी प्रोक्ता दर्पेण वा द्विजाः ॥१८॥
transliteration: iti pṛṣṭo dvijaistaiḥ sa prahasya nakulo'bravīt ,naiṣānṛtā mayā vāṇī proktā darpeṇa vā dvijāḥ.
english: When asked thus by those Brahmins, the mongoose laughed and said, 'O Brahmins, this statement I made is not false, nor was it spoken out of arrogance.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 92 Verse 18. When asked thus by those Brahmins, the mongoose laughed and said, 'O Brahmins, this statement I made is not false, nor was it spoken out of arrogance.'

sanskrit: यन्मयोक्तमिदं किंचिद्युष्माभिश्चाप्युपश्रुतम् ।सक्तुप्रस्थेन वो नायं यज्ञस्तुल्यो नराधिपाः ।उञ्छवृत्तेर्वदान्यस्य कुरुक्षेत्रनिवासिनः ॥१९॥
transliteration: yanmayoktamidaṁ kiṁcidyuṣmābhiścāpyupaśrutam ,saktuprasthena vo nāyaṁ yajñastulyo narādhipāḥ ,uñchavṛttervadānyasya kurukṣetranivāsinaḥ.
english: O kings, whatever little I said, and what you have also heard, this (Vedic ritual) of yours is not comparable to the (Vedic ritual) involving a prastha of barley-meal, performed by the generous gleaner who resided in Kurukṣetra.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 92 Verse 19. O kings, whatever little I said, and what you have also heard, this (Vedic ritual) of yours is not comparable to the (Vedic ritual) involving a prastha of barley-meal, performed by the generous gleaner who resided in Kurukṣetra.

sanskrit: इत्यवश्यं मयैतद्वो वक्तव्यं द्विजपुंगवाः ।शृणुताव्यग्रमनसः शंसतो मे द्विजर्षभाः ॥२०॥
transliteration: ityavaśyaṁ mayaitadvo vaktavyaṁ dvijapuṁgavāḥ ,śṛṇutāvyagramanasaḥ śaṁsato me dvijarṣabhāḥ.
english: Therefore, O excellent Brahmins, this must certainly be told by me to you. O best of Brahmins, listen with calm minds as I speak.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 92 Verse 20. Therefore, O excellent Brahmins, this must certainly be told by me to you. O best of Brahmins, listen with calm minds as I speak.

sanskrit: अनुभूतं च दृष्टं च यन्मयाद्भुतमुत्तमम् ।उञ्छवृत्तेर्यथावृत्तं कुरुक्षेत्रनिवासिनः ॥२१॥
transliteration: anubhūtaṁ ca dṛṣṭaṁ ca yanmayādbhutamuttamam ,uñchavṛtteryathāvṛttaṁ kurukṣetranivāsinaḥ.
english: I will tell of what I have personally experienced and witnessed, a most wonderful and excellent event, specifically the full account of the gleaner who lived in Kurukṣetra.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 92 Verse 21. I will tell of what I have personally experienced and witnessed, a most wonderful and excellent event, specifically the full account of the gleaner who lived in Kurukṣetra.

sanskrit: स्वर्गं येन द्विजः प्राप्तः सभार्यः ससुतस्नुषः ।यथा चार्धं शरीरस्य ममेदं काञ्चनीकृतम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: svargaṁ yena dvijaḥ prāptaḥ sabhāryaḥ sasutasnuṣaḥ ,yathā cārdhaṁ śarīrasya mamedaṁ kāñcanīkṛtam.
english: Through which the brahmin (dvija) attained heaven (svarga) with his wife, sons, and daughters-in-law, just as this half of my body was turned golden.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 92 Verse 22. Through which the brahmin (dvija) attained heaven (svarga) with his wife, sons, and daughters-in-law, just as this half of my body was turned golden.

sanskrit: नकुल उवाच ।हन्त वो वर्तयिष्यामि दानस्य परमं फलम् ।न्यायलब्धस्य सूक्ष्मस्य विप्रदत्तस्य यद्द्विजाः ॥१॥
transliteration: nakula uvāca ,hanta vo vartayiṣyāmi dānasya paramaṁ phalam ,nyāyalabdhasya sūkṣmasya vipradattasya yaddvijāḥ.
english: Nakula said: Indeed, O brahmins (dvija), I shall relate to you the supreme fruit of charity (dāna) that comes from what is rightfully acquired, even if subtle, and given to a brahmin.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 1. Nakula said: Indeed, O brahmins (dvija), I shall relate to you the supreme fruit of charity (dāna) that comes from what is rightfully acquired, even if subtle, and given to a brahmin.

sanskrit: धर्मक्षेत्रे कुरुक्षेत्रे धर्मज्ञैर्बहुभिर्वृते ।उञ्छवृत्तिर्द्विजः कश्चित्कापोतिरभवत्पुरा ॥२॥
transliteration: dharmakṣetre kurukṣetre dharmajñairbahubhirvṛte ,uñchavṛttirdvijaḥ kaścitkāpotirabhavatpurā.
english: In Kurukshetra, the field of (dharma), which was frequented by many knowers of (dharma), there once lived a certain brahmin (dvija) who sustained himself by gleaning, like a pigeon.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 2. In Kurukshetra, the field of (dharma), which was frequented by many knowers of (dharma), there once lived a certain brahmin (dvija) who sustained himself by gleaning, like a pigeon.

sanskrit: सभार्यः सह पुत्रेण सस्नुषस्तपसि स्थितः ।वधूचतुर्थो वृद्धः स धर्मात्मा नियतेन्द्रियः ॥३॥
transliteration: sabhāryaḥ saha putreṇa sasnuṣastapasi sthitaḥ ,vadhūcaturtho vṛddhaḥ sa dharmātmā niyatendriyaḥ.
english: He, an old man, was engaged in austerity (tapas) with his wife, his son, and his daughter-in-law, making a group of four with the daughter-in-law. He was righteous (dharmātmā) and had controlled senses.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 3. He, an old man, was engaged in austerity (tapas) with his wife, his son, and his daughter-in-law, making a group of four with the daughter-in-law. He was righteous (dharmātmā) and had controlled senses.

sanskrit: षष्ठे काले तदा विप्रो भुङ्क्ते तैः सह सुव्रतः ।षष्ठे काले कदाचिच्च तस्याहारो न विद्यते ।भुङ्क्तेऽन्यस्मिन्कदाचित्स षष्ठे काले द्विजोत्तमः ॥४॥
transliteration: ṣaṣṭhe kāle tadā vipro bhuṅkte taiḥ saha suvrataḥ ,ṣaṣṭhe kāle kadācicca tasyāhāro na vidyate ,bhuṅkte'nyasminkadācitsa ṣaṣṭhe kāle dvijottamaḥ.
english: Then, at the sixth period, the pious brahmin would eat with them. And sometimes, at the sixth period, his food was not available. Sometimes, that best of twice-born would eat on another occasion at the sixth period.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 4. Then, at the sixth period, the pious brahmin would eat with them. And sometimes, at the sixth period, his food was not available. Sometimes, that best of twice-born would eat on another occasion at the sixth period.

sanskrit: कपोतधर्मिणस्तस्य दुर्भिक्षे सति दारुणे ।नाविद्यत तदा विप्राः संचयस्तान्निबोधत ।क्षीणौषधिसमावायो द्रव्यहीनोऽभवत्तदा ॥५॥
transliteration: kapotadharmiṇastasya durbhikṣe sati dāruṇe ,nāvidyata tadā viprāḥ saṁcayastānnibodhata ,kṣīṇauṣadhisamāvāyo dravyahīno'bhavattadā.
english: O brahmins, understand this: when a terrible famine occurred, that brahmin, who followed the principle of a dove (gleaning grains), had no store then. At that time, he became devoid of wealth and his collection of medicinal herbs was depleted.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 5. O brahmins, understand this: when a terrible famine occurred, that brahmin, who followed the principle of a dove (gleaning grains), had no store then. At that time, he became devoid of wealth and his collection of medicinal herbs was depleted.

sanskrit: काले कालेऽस्य संप्राप्ते नैव विद्येत भोजनम् ।क्षुधापरिगताः सर्वे प्रातिष्ठन्त तदा तु ते ॥६॥
transliteration: kāle kāle'sya saṁprāpte naiva vidyeta bhojanam ,kṣudhāparigatāḥ sarve prātiṣṭhanta tadā tu te.
english: As his appointed times came, food was simply not available. Then, all of them, afflicted by hunger, remained.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 6. As his appointed times came, food was simply not available. Then, all of them, afflicted by hunger, remained.

sanskrit: उञ्छंस्तदा शुक्लपक्षे मध्यं तपति भास्करे ।उष्णार्तश्च क्षुधार्तश्च स विप्रस्तपसि स्थितः ।उञ्छमप्राप्तवानेव सार्धं परिजनेन ह ॥७॥
transliteration: uñchaṁstadā śuklapakṣe madhyaṁ tapati bhāskare ,uṣṇārtaśca kṣudhārtaśca sa viprastapasi sthitaḥ ,uñchamaprāptavāneva sārdhaṁ parijanena ha.
english: Then, during the bright fortnight, as the sun shone intensely at midday, that brahmin, afflicted by heat and hunger, persisted in his austerity (tapas). He did not obtain even gleanings, along with his family.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 7. Then, during the bright fortnight, as the sun shone intensely at midday, that brahmin, afflicted by heat and hunger, persisted in his austerity (tapas). He did not obtain even gleanings, along with his family.

sanskrit: स तथैव क्षुधाविष्टः स्पृष्ट्वा तोयं यथाविधि ।क्षपयामास तं कालं कृच्छ्रप्राणो द्विजोत्तमः ॥८॥
transliteration: sa tathaiva kṣudhāviṣṭaḥ spṛṣṭvā toyaṁ yathāvidhi ,kṣapayāmāsa taṁ kālaṁ kṛcchraprāṇo dvijottamaḥ.
english: That excellent brahmin (dvija), whose life (prāṇa) was in great difficulty, likewise afflicted by hunger, having ritually touched water according to the prescribed method, passed that time.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 8. That excellent brahmin (dvija), whose life (prāṇa) was in great difficulty, likewise afflicted by hunger, having ritually touched water according to the prescribed method, passed that time.

sanskrit: अथ षष्ठे गते काले यवप्रस्थमुपार्जयत् ।यवप्रस्थं च ते सक्तूनकुर्वन्त तपस्विनः ॥९॥
transliteration: atha ṣaṣṭhe gate kāle yavaprasthamupārjayat ,yavaprasthaṁ ca te saktūnakurvanta tapasvinaḥ.
english: Then, after the sixth period had passed, he acquired a prastha of barley. And those ascetics, using that prastha of barley, prepared barley meal.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 9. Then, after the sixth period had passed, he acquired a prastha of barley. And those ascetics, using that prastha of barley, prepared barley meal.

sanskrit: कृतजप्याह्विकास्ते तु हुत्वा वह्निं यथाविधि ।कुडवं कुडवं सर्वे व्यभजन्त तपस्विनः ॥१०॥
transliteration: kṛtajapyāhvikāste tu hutvā vahniṁ yathāvidhi ,kuḍavaṁ kuḍavaṁ sarve vyabhajanta tapasvinaḥ.
english: But those ascetics, having completed their daily prayers and invocations, and having offered oblations into the fire according to the prescribed method, all divided it, a kuḍava each.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 10. But those ascetics, having completed their daily prayers and invocations, and having offered oblations into the fire according to the prescribed method, all divided it, a kuḍava each.

sanskrit: अथागच्छद्द्विजः कश्चिदतिथिर्भुञ्जतां तदा ।ते तं दृष्ट्वातिथिं तत्र प्रहृष्टमनसोऽभवन् ॥११॥
transliteration: athāgacchaddvijaḥ kaścidatithirbhuñjatāṁ tadā ,te taṁ dṛṣṭvātithiṁ tatra prahṛṣṭamanaso'bhavan.
english: Then, as they were eating, a certain brahmin (dvija) arrived as a guest. Seeing that guest there, they all became greatly joyful.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 11. Then, as they were eating, a certain brahmin (dvija) arrived as a guest. Seeing that guest there, they all became greatly joyful.

sanskrit: तेऽभिवाद्य सुखप्रश्नं पृष्ट्वा तमतिथिं तदा ।विशुद्धमनसो दान्ताः श्रद्धादमसमन्विताः ॥१२॥
transliteration: te'bhivādya sukhapraśnaṁ pṛṣṭvā tamatithiṁ tadā ,viśuddhamanaso dāntāḥ śraddhādamasamanvitāḥ.
english: Then, having greeted that guest and inquired about his well-being, they were pure-minded, self-controlled, and endowed with faith (śraddhā) and self-restraint.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 12. Then, having greeted that guest and inquired about his well-being, they were pure-minded, self-controlled, and endowed with faith (śraddhā) and self-restraint.

sanskrit: अनसूयवो गतक्रोधाः साधवो गतमत्सराः ।त्यक्तमाना जितक्रोधा धर्मज्ञा द्विजसत्तमाः ॥१३॥
transliteration: anasūyavo gatakrodhāḥ sādhavo gatamatsarāḥ ,tyaktamānā jitakrodhā dharmajñā dvijasattamāḥ.
english: They were free from envy, devoid of anger, righteous, free from jealousy, having renounced pride, having conquered wrath, discerning of natural law (dharma), and were indeed the best among the twice-born (dvija).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 13. They were free from envy, devoid of anger, righteous, free from jealousy, having renounced pride, having conquered wrath, discerning of natural law (dharma), and were indeed the best among the twice-born (dvija).

sanskrit: सब्रह्मचर्यं स्वं गोत्रं समाख्याय परस्परम् ।कुटीं प्रवेशयामासुः क्षुधार्तमतिथिं तदा ॥१४॥
transliteration: sabrahmacaryaṁ svaṁ gotraṁ samākhyāya parasparam ,kuṭīṁ praveśayāmāsuḥ kṣudhārtamatithiṁ tadā.
english: Then, having mutually declared their common studentship and their lineage (gotra), they led that guest, who was afflicted by hunger, into their hut.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 14. Then, having mutually declared their common studentship and their lineage (gotra), they led that guest, who was afflicted by hunger, into their hut.

sanskrit: इदमर्घ्यं च पाद्यं च बृसी चेयं तवानघ ।शुचयः सक्तवश्चेमे नियमोपार्जिताः प्रभो ।प्रतिगृह्णीष्व भद्रं ते मया दत्ता द्विजोत्तम ॥१५॥
transliteration: idamarghyaṁ ca pādyaṁ ca bṛsī ceyaṁ tavānagha ,śucayaḥ saktavaśceme niyamopārjitāḥ prabho ,pratigṛhṇīṣva bhadraṁ te mayā dattā dvijottama.
english: This offering, this water for the feet, and this grass mat are for you, O sinless one. And these pure ground grains, O lord, obtained through regulated practices, are offered by me. Please accept them; may good fortune be yours, O best of the twice-born (dvija)!
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 15. This offering, this water for the feet, and this grass mat are for you, O sinless one. And these pure ground grains, O lord, obtained through regulated practices, are offered by me. Please accept them; may good fortune be yours, O best of the twice-born (dvija)!

sanskrit: इत्युक्तः प्रतिगृह्याथ सक्तूनां कुडवं द्विजः ।भक्षयामास राजेन्द्र न च तुष्टिं जगाम सः ॥१६॥
transliteration: ityuktaḥ pratigṛhyātha saktūnāṁ kuḍavaṁ dvijaḥ ,bhakṣayāmāsa rājendra na ca tuṣṭiṁ jagāma saḥ.
english: O King, thus addressed, the brahmin (dvija) then accepted a measure of roasted grain flour and consumed it. However, he did not attain satisfaction.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 16. O King, thus addressed, the brahmin (dvija) then accepted a measure of roasted grain flour and consumed it. However, he did not attain satisfaction.

sanskrit: स उञ्छवृत्तिः तं प्रेक्ष्य क्षुधापरिगतं द्विजम् ।आहारं चिन्तयामास कथं तुष्टो भवेदिति ॥१७॥
transliteration: sa uñchavṛttiḥ taṁ prekṣya kṣudhāparigataṁ dvijam ,āhāraṁ cintayāmāsa kathaṁ tuṣṭo bhavediti.
english: That gleaner, seeing the brahmin (dvija) overcome by hunger, then pondered about food, thinking, 'How might he become satisfied?'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 17. That gleaner, seeing the brahmin (dvija) overcome by hunger, then pondered about food, thinking, 'How might he become satisfied?'

sanskrit: तस्य भार्याब्रवीद्राजन्मद्भागो दीयतामिति ।गच्छत्वेष यथाकामं संतुष्टो द्विजसत्तमः ॥१८॥
transliteration: tasya bhāryābravīdrājanmadbhāgo dīyatāmiti ,gacchatveṣa yathākāmaṁ saṁtuṣṭo dvijasattamaḥ.
english: His wife said, 'O King, let my portion be given. Let this excellent brahmin (dvija) depart, fully satisfied, as he wishes.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 18. His wife said, 'O King, let my portion be given. Let this excellent brahmin (dvija) depart, fully satisfied, as he wishes.'

sanskrit: इति ब्रुवन्तीं तां साध्वीं धर्मात्मा स द्विजर्षभः ।क्षुधापरिगतां ज्ञात्वा सक्तूंस्तान्नाभ्यनन्दत ॥१९॥
transliteration: iti bruvantīṁ tāṁ sādhvīṁ dharmātmā sa dvijarṣabhaḥ ,kṣudhāparigatāṁ jñātvā saktūṁstānnābhyanandata.
english: Knowing that virtuous woman was speaking thus, and was herself overcome by hunger, that righteous-souled, excellent brahmin (dvija) did not accept those roasted grain flours.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 19. Knowing that virtuous woman was speaking thus, and was herself overcome by hunger, that righteous-souled, excellent brahmin (dvija) did not accept those roasted grain flours.

sanskrit: जानन्वृद्धां क्षुधार्तां च श्रान्तां ग्लानां तपस्विनीम् ।त्वगस्थिभूतां वेपन्तीं ततो भार्यामुवाच ताम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: jānanvṛddhāṁ kṣudhārtāṁ ca śrāntāṁ glānāṁ tapasvinīm ,tvagasthibhūtāṁ vepantīṁ tato bhāryāmuvāca tām.
english: Knowing her to be old, afflicted by hunger, exhausted, weak, and miserable (tapasvinī), her body reduced to skin and bones (tvag-asthi-bhūtām) and trembling, he then spoke to that wife.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 20. Knowing her to be old, afflicted by hunger, exhausted, weak, and miserable (tapasvinī), her body reduced to skin and bones (tvag-asthi-bhūtām) and trembling, he then spoke to that wife.

sanskrit: अपि कीटपतंगानां मृगाणां चैव शोभने ।स्त्रियो रक्ष्याश्च पोष्याश्च नैवं त्वं वक्तुमर्हसि ॥२१॥
transliteration: api kīṭapataṁgānāṁ mṛgāṇāṁ caiva śobhane ,striyo rakṣyāśca poṣyāśca naivaṁ tvaṁ vaktumarhasi.
english: O beautiful one (śobhane), even the females of insects, birds, and animals (mṛgāṇām) are to be protected and nurtured. You should not speak in this manner.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 21. O beautiful one (śobhane), even the females of insects, birds, and animals (mṛgāṇām) are to be protected and nurtured. You should not speak in this manner.

sanskrit: अनुकम्पितो नरो नार्या पुष्टो रक्षित एव च ।प्रपतेद्यशसो दीप्तान्न च लोकानवाप्नुयात् ॥२२॥
transliteration: anukampito naro nāryā puṣṭo rakṣita eva ca ,prapatedyaśaso dīptānna ca lokānavāpnuyāt.
english: A man who is pitied, nourished, and protected by a woman (nāryā) would fall from his brilliant fame and would not attain higher realms.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 22. A man who is pitied, nourished, and protected by a woman (nāryā) would fall from his brilliant fame and would not attain higher realms.

sanskrit: इत्युक्ता सा ततः प्राह धर्मार्थौ नौ समौ द्विज ।सक्तुप्रस्थचतुर्भागं गृहाणेमं प्रसीद मे ॥२३॥
transliteration: ityuktā sā tataḥ prāha dharmārthau nau samau dvija ,saktuprasthacaturbhāgaṁ gṛhāṇemaṁ prasīda me.
english: Thus addressed, she then said, "O twice-born one (dvija), for us, our inherent constitution (dharma) and material prosperity (artha) are equally important. Please accept this fourth part of a prastha of flour and be pleased with me."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 23. Thus addressed, she then said, "O twice-born one (dvija), for us, our inherent constitution (dharma) and material prosperity (artha) are equally important. Please accept this fourth part of a prastha of flour and be pleased with me."

sanskrit: सत्यं रतिश्च धर्मश्च स्वर्गश्च गुणनिर्जितः ।स्त्रीणां पतिसमाधीनं काङ्क्षितं च द्विजोत्तम ॥२४॥
transliteration: satyaṁ ratiśca dharmaśca svargaśca guṇanirjitaḥ ,strīṇāṁ patisamādhīnaṁ kāṅkṣitaṁ ca dvijottama.
english: O best among the twice-born, for women, truth, happiness, the natural law (dharma), and heaven acquired through virtues - all these are desired and dependent on their husband.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 24. O best among the twice-born, for women, truth, happiness, the natural law (dharma), and heaven acquired through virtues - all these are desired and dependent on their husband.

sanskrit: ऋतुर्मातुः पितुर्बीजं दैवतं परमं पतिः ।भर्तुः प्रसादात्स्त्रीणां वै रतिः पुत्रफलं तथा ॥२५॥
transliteration: ṛturmātuḥ piturbījaṁ daivataṁ paramaṁ patiḥ ,bhartuḥ prasādātstrīṇāṁ vai ratiḥ putraphalaṁ tathā.
english: The fertile period is from the mother, the seed from the father; the husband is the supreme deity. Indeed, for women, happiness and the fruit of sons are attained through the grace of the husband.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 25. The fertile period is from the mother, the seed from the father; the husband is the supreme deity. Indeed, for women, happiness and the fruit of sons are attained through the grace of the husband.

sanskrit: पालनाद्धि पतिस्त्वं मे भर्तासि भरणान्मम ।पुत्रप्रदानाद्वरदस्तस्मात्सक्तून्गृहाण मे ॥२६॥
transliteration: pālanāddhi patistvaṁ me bhartāsi bharaṇānmama ,putrapradānādvaradastasmātsaktūngṛhāṇa me.
english: Indeed, you are my husband (pati) by your protection, and my maintainer (bhartā) by your support. You are the bestower of boons by granting sons. Therefore, please accept these groats from me.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 26. Indeed, you are my husband (pati) by your protection, and my maintainer (bhartā) by your support. You are the bestower of boons by granting sons. Therefore, please accept these groats from me.

sanskrit: जरापरिगतो वृद्धः क्षुधार्तो दुर्बलो भृशम् ।उपवासपरिश्रान्तो यदा त्वमपि कर्शितः ॥२७॥
transliteration: jarāparigato vṛddhaḥ kṣudhārto durbalo bhṛśam ,upavāsapariśrānto yadā tvamapi karśitaḥ.
english: When you are overcome by old age, old, afflicted by hunger, exceedingly weak, exhausted by fasting, and also emaciated.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 27. When you are overcome by old age, old, afflicted by hunger, exceedingly weak, exhausted by fasting, and also emaciated.

sanskrit: इत्युक्तः स तया सक्तून्प्रगृह्येदं वचोऽब्रवीत् ।द्विज सक्तूनिमान्भूयः प्रतिगृह्णीष्व सत्तम ॥२८॥
transliteration: ityuktaḥ sa tayā saktūnpragṛhyedaṁ vaco'bravīt ,dvija saktūnimānbhūyaḥ pratigṛhṇīṣva sattama.
english: Addressed thus by her, he took the barley meal and spoke these words: "O best among the good (sattama) brahmin, please accept this barley meal again."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 28. Addressed thus by her, he took the barley meal and spoke these words: "O best among the good (sattama) brahmin, please accept this barley meal again."

sanskrit: स तान्प्रगृह्य भुक्त्वा च न तुष्टिमगमद्द्विजः ।तमुञ्छवृत्तिरालक्ष्य ततश्चिन्तापरोऽभवत् ॥२९॥
transliteration: sa tānpragṛhya bhuktvā ca na tuṣṭimagamaddvijaḥ ,tamuñchavṛttirālakṣya tataścintāparo'bhavat.
english: Having taken and eaten that (barley meal), the brahmin did not achieve satisfaction. Observing him, the gleaner (uñchavṛtti) then became very anxious.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 29. Having taken and eaten that (barley meal), the brahmin did not achieve satisfaction. Observing him, the gleaner (uñchavṛtti) then became very anxious.

sanskrit: पुत्र उवाच ।सक्तूनिमान्प्रगृह्य त्वं देहि विप्राय सत्तम ।इत्येवं सुकृतं मन्ये तस्मादेतत्करोम्यहम् ॥३०॥
transliteration: putra uvāca ,saktūnimānpragṛhya tvaṁ dehi viprāya sattama ,ityevaṁ sukṛtaṁ manye tasmādetatkaromyaham.
english: The son said: "O best among the good (sattama) father, you should take this barley meal and give it to the brahmin. I consider this to be a good deed, and therefore I will do it."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 30. The son said: "O best among the good (sattama) father, you should take this barley meal and give it to the brahmin. I consider this to be a good deed, and therefore I will do it."

sanskrit: भवान्हि परिपाल्यो मे सर्वयत्नैर्द्विजोत्तम ।साधूनां काङ्क्षितं ह्येतत्पितुर्वृद्धस्य पोषणम् ॥३१॥
transliteration: bhavānhi paripālyo me sarvayatnairdvijottama ,sādhūnāṁ kāṅkṣitaṁ hyetatpiturvṛddhasya poṣaṇam.
english: Indeed, you, my father, are to be nourished by me with all efforts, O best among brahmins (dvijottama). For this support of an aged father is indeed what virtuous people desire.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 31. Indeed, you, my father, are to be nourished by me with all efforts, O best among brahmins (dvijottama). For this support of an aged father is indeed what virtuous people desire.

sanskrit: पुत्रार्थो विहितो ह्येष स्थाविर्ये परिपालनम् ।श्रुतिरेषा हि विप्रर्षे त्रिषु लोकेषु विश्रुता ॥३२॥
transliteration: putrārtho vihito hyeṣa sthāvirye paripālanam ,śrutireṣā hi viprarṣe triṣu lokeṣu viśrutā.
english: Indeed, having a son is ordained for support in old age. This sacred tradition (śruti), O sage, is indeed renowned throughout the three worlds.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 32. Indeed, having a son is ordained for support in old age. This sacred tradition (śruti), O sage, is indeed renowned throughout the three worlds.

sanskrit: प्राणधारणमात्रेण शक्यं कर्तुं तपस्त्वया ।प्राणो हि परमो धर्मः स्थितो देहेषु देहिनाम् ॥३३॥
transliteration: prāṇadhāraṇamātreṇa śakyaṁ kartuṁ tapastvayā ,prāṇo hi paramo dharmaḥ sthito deheṣu dehinām.
english: You can perform penance (tapas) merely by sustaining life. Indeed, life-breath (prāṇa) is the supreme natural law (dharma) inherent in the bodies of all living beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 33. You can perform penance (tapas) merely by sustaining life. Indeed, life-breath (prāṇa) is the supreme natural law (dharma) inherent in the bodies of all living beings.

sanskrit: पितोवाच ।अपि वर्षसहस्री त्वं बाल एव मतो मम ।उत्पाद्य पुत्रं हि पिता कृतकृत्यो भवत्युत ॥३४॥
transliteration: pitovāca ,api varṣasahasrī tvaṁ bāla eva mato mama ,utpādya putraṁ hi pitā kṛtakṛtyo bhavatyuta.
english: The father said: 'Even if you are a thousand years old, you are considered a child by me. Indeed, a father becomes one who has fulfilled his purpose only after begetting a son.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 34. The father said: 'Even if you are a thousand years old, you are considered a child by me. Indeed, a father becomes one who has fulfilled his purpose only after begetting a son.'

sanskrit: बालानां क्षुद्बलवती जानाम्येतदहं विभो ।वृद्धोऽहं धारयिष्यामि त्वं बली भव पुत्रक ॥३५॥
transliteration: bālānāṁ kṣudbalavatī jānāmyetadahaṁ vibho ,vṛddho'haṁ dhārayiṣyāmi tvaṁ balī bhava putraka.
english: O powerful one, I know that the hunger of children is intense. I am old; I will endure. You, my dear son, become strong.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 35. O powerful one, I know that the hunger of children is intense. I am old; I will endure. You, my dear son, become strong.

sanskrit: जीर्णेन वयसा पुत्र न मा क्षुद्बाधतेऽपि च ।दीर्घकालं तपस्तप्तं न मे मरणतो भयम् ॥३६॥
transliteration: jīrṇena vayasā putra na mā kṣudbādhate'pi ca ,dīrghakālaṁ tapastaptaṁ na me maraṇato bhayam.
english: Son, even with this old age, hunger does not trouble me. I have performed austerities (tapas) for a long time, therefore I have no fear of death.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 36. Son, even with this old age, hunger does not trouble me. I have performed austerities (tapas) for a long time, therefore I have no fear of death.

sanskrit: पुत्र उवाच ।अपत्यमस्मि ते पुत्रस्त्राणात्पुत्रो हि विश्रुतः ।आत्मा पुत्रः स्मृतस्तस्मात्त्राह्यात्मानमिहात्मना ॥३७॥
transliteration: putra uvāca ,apatyamasmi te putrastrāṇātputro hi viśrutaḥ ,ātmā putraḥ smṛtastasmāttrāhyātmānamihātmanā.
english: The son said: I am your offspring. Indeed, a son (putra) is renowned for saving [his father from the hell called Put]. The self (ātman) is considered the son. Therefore, protect your own self here by yourself.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 37. The son said: I am your offspring. Indeed, a son (putra) is renowned for saving [his father from the hell called Put]. The self (ātman) is considered the son. Therefore, protect your own self here by yourself.

sanskrit: पितोवाच ।रूपेण सदृशस्त्वं मे शीलेन च दमेन च ।परीक्षितश्च बहुधा सक्तूनादद्मि ते ततः ॥३८॥
transliteration: pitovāca ,rūpeṇa sadṛśastvaṁ me śīlena ca damena ca ,parīkṣitaśca bahudhā saktūnādadmi te tataḥ.
english: The father said: You are similar to me in appearance, character, and self-control. Furthermore, you have been tested in various ways. Therefore, I will accept your groats.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 38. The father said: You are similar to me in appearance, character, and self-control. Furthermore, you have been tested in various ways. Therefore, I will accept your groats.

sanskrit: इत्युक्त्वादाय तान्सक्तून्प्रीतात्मा द्विजसत्तमः ।प्रहसन्निव विप्राय स तस्मै प्रददौ तदा ॥३९॥
transliteration: ityuktvādāya tānsaktūnprītātmā dvijasattamaḥ ,prahasanniva viprāya sa tasmai pradadau tadā.
english: Having said this and taken those groats, the best among the twice-born (the father), with a pleased mind, as if smiling, then gave them to that Brahmin (his son).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 39. Having said this and taken those groats, the best among the twice-born (the father), with a pleased mind, as if smiling, then gave them to that Brahmin (his son).

sanskrit: भुक्त्वा तानपि सक्तून्स नैव तुष्टो बभूव ह ।उञ्छवृत्तिस्तु सव्रीडो बभूव द्विजसत्तमः ॥४०॥
transliteration: bhuktvā tānapi saktūnsa naiva tuṣṭo babhūva ha ,uñchavṛttistu savrīḍo babhūva dvijasattamaḥ.
english: Even after eating those meals, he was not satisfied. But the excellent brahmin, who lived by gleaning, became quite ashamed.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 40. Even after eating those meals, he was not satisfied. But the excellent brahmin, who lived by gleaning, became quite ashamed.

sanskrit: तं वै वधूः स्थिता साध्वी ब्राह्मणप्रियकाम्यया ।सक्तूनादाय संहृष्टा गुरुं तं वाक्यमब्रवीत् ॥४१॥
transliteration: taṁ vai vadhūḥ sthitā sādhvī brāhmaṇapriyakāmyayā ,saktūnādāya saṁhṛṣṭā guruṁ taṁ vākyamabravīt.
english: Indeed, his virtuous wife (sādhvī), who was standing there, delighted and desiring to please the brahmin, took the barley meal and spoke these words to him, her revered husband.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 41. Indeed, his virtuous wife (sādhvī), who was standing there, delighted and desiring to please the brahmin, took the barley meal and spoke these words to him, her revered husband.

sanskrit: संतानात्तव संतानं मम विप्र भविष्यति ।सक्तूनिमानतिथये गृहीत्वा त्वं प्रयच्छ मे ॥४२॥
transliteration: saṁtānāttava saṁtānaṁ mama vipra bhaviṣyati ,saktūnimānatithaye gṛhītvā tvaṁ prayaccha me.
english: O brahmin (vipra)! My lineage will continue through your offspring. Please take these barley meals for the guest and give them to me.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 42. O brahmin (vipra)! My lineage will continue through your offspring. Please take these barley meals for the guest and give them to me.

sanskrit: तव प्रसवनिर्वृत्या मम लोकाः किलाक्षयाः ।पौत्रेण तानवाप्नोति यत्र गत्वा न शोचति ॥४३॥
transliteration: tava prasavanirvṛtyā mama lokāḥ kilākṣayāḥ ,pautreṇa tānavāpnoti yatra gatvā na śocati.
english: Through the fulfillment of your progeny, my realms will surely become imperishable. One attains those realms through a grandson, and having reached them, one never grieves.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 43. Through the fulfillment of your progeny, my realms will surely become imperishable. One attains those realms through a grandson, and having reached them, one never grieves.

sanskrit: धर्माद्या हि यथा त्रेता वह्नित्रेता तथैव च ।तथैव पुत्रपौत्राणां स्वर्गे त्रेता किलाक्षया ॥४४॥
transliteration: dharmādyā hi yathā tretā vahnitretā tathaiva ca ,tathaiva putrapautrāṇāṁ svarge tretā kilākṣayā.
english: Indeed, just as the primary principles (dharma) and others are considered a triad, and similarly the triad of sacred fires is established, so too, for sons and grandsons, there is certainly an imperishable triad in heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 44. Indeed, just as the primary principles (dharma) and others are considered a triad, and similarly the triad of sacred fires is established, so too, for sons and grandsons, there is certainly an imperishable triad in heaven.

sanskrit: पितॄंस्त्राणात्तारयति पुत्र इत्यनुशुश्रुम ।पुत्रपौत्रैश्च नियतं साधुलोकानुपाश्नुते ॥४५॥
transliteration: pitṝṁstrāṇāttārayati putra ityanuśuśruma ,putrapautraiśca niyataṁ sādhulokānupāśnute.
english: A son saves his ancestors (pitṛs) by delivering them; thus we have heard. And by means of sons and grandsons, one certainly attains the realms of the virtuous.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 45. A son saves his ancestors (pitṛs) by delivering them; thus we have heard. And by means of sons and grandsons, one certainly attains the realms of the virtuous.

sanskrit: श्वशुर उवाच ।वातातपविशीर्णाङ्गीं त्वां विवर्णां निरीक्ष्य वै ।कर्शितां सुव्रताचारे क्षुधाविह्वलचेतसम् ॥४६॥
transliteration: śvaśura uvāca ,vātātapaviśīrṇāṅgīṁ tvāṁ vivarṇāṁ nirīkṣya vai ,karśitāṁ suvratācāre kṣudhāvihvalacetasam.
english: The father-in-law said: "Indeed, beholding you - whose limbs are withered by wind and sun, who are discolored, emaciated, and whose mind is agitated by hunger - O you who practice excellent vows...
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 46. The father-in-law said: "Indeed, beholding you - whose limbs are withered by wind and sun, who are discolored, emaciated, and whose mind is agitated by hunger - O you who practice excellent vows...

sanskrit: कथं सक्तून्ग्रहीष्यामि भूत्वा धर्मोपघातकः ।कल्याणवृत्ते कल्याणि नैवं त्वं वक्तुमर्हसि ॥४७॥
transliteration: kathaṁ saktūngrahīṣyāmi bhūtvā dharmopaghātakaḥ ,kalyāṇavṛtte kalyāṇi naivaṁ tvaṁ vaktumarhasi.
english: How can I accept (these) groats, having become a violator of (dharma)? O you of auspicious conduct, O auspicious one, you ought not to speak like this.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 47. How can I accept (these) groats, having become a violator of (dharma)? O you of auspicious conduct, O auspicious one, you ought not to speak like this.

sanskrit: षष्ठे काले व्रतवतीं शीलशौचसमन्विताम् ।कृच्छ्रवृत्तिं निराहारां द्रक्ष्यामि त्वां कथं न्वहम् ॥४८॥
transliteration: ṣaṣṭhe kāle vratavatīṁ śīlaśaucasamanvitām ,kṛcchravṛttiṁ nirāhārāṁ drakṣyāmi tvāṁ kathaṁ nvaham.
english: How can I possibly see you, observing a vow (vrata), endowed with good conduct and purity, leading an austere life, and fasting during the sixth period?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 48. How can I possibly see you, observing a vow (vrata), endowed with good conduct and purity, leading an austere life, and fasting during the sixth period?

sanskrit: बाला क्षुधार्ता नारी च रक्ष्या त्वं सततं मया ।उपवासपरिश्रान्ता त्वं हि बान्धवनन्दिनी ॥४९॥
transliteration: bālā kṣudhārtā nārī ca rakṣyā tvaṁ satataṁ mayā ,upavāsapariśrāntā tvaṁ hi bāndhavanandinī.
english: You, a young woman, distressed by hunger, must always be protected by me. Indeed, you are exhausted by fasting and are the daughter of a kinsman.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 49. You, a young woman, distressed by hunger, must always be protected by me. Indeed, you are exhausted by fasting and are the daughter of a kinsman.

sanskrit: स्नुषोवाच ।गुरोर्मम गुरुस्त्वं वै यतो दैवतदैवतम् ।देवातिदेवस्तस्मात्त्वं सक्तूनादत्स्व मे विभो ॥५०॥
transliteration: snuṣovāca ,gurormama gurustvaṁ vai yato daivatadaivatam ,devātidevastasmāttvaṁ saktūnādatsva me vibho.
english: The daughter-in-law said: 'You are indeed the preceptor (guru) of my preceptor (guru), for you are the god of gods, the supreme deity (devātideva). Therefore, O Lord, please accept the roasted grain flour from me.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 50. The daughter-in-law said: 'You are indeed the preceptor (guru) of my preceptor (guru), for you are the god of gods, the supreme deity (devātideva). Therefore, O Lord, please accept the roasted grain flour from me.'

sanskrit: देहः प्राणश्च धर्मश्च शुश्रूषार्थमिदं गुरोः ।तव विप्र प्रसादेन लोकान्प्राप्स्याम्यभीप्सितान् ॥५१॥
transliteration: dehaḥ prāṇaśca dharmaśca śuśrūṣārthamidaṁ guroḥ ,tava vipra prasādena lokānprāpsyāmyabhīpsitān.
english: This body, my life (prāṇa), and my natural law (dharma) are for the purpose of serving the preceptor (guru). O Brāhmaṇa, by your grace, I shall attain the desired realms.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 51. This body, my life (prāṇa), and my natural law (dharma) are for the purpose of serving the preceptor (guru). O Brāhmaṇa, by your grace, I shall attain the desired realms.

sanskrit: अवेक्ष्या इति कृत्वा त्वं दृढभक्त्येति वा द्विज ।चिन्त्या ममेयमिति वा सक्तूनादातुमर्हसि ॥५२॥
transliteration: avekṣyā iti kṛtvā tvaṁ dṛḍhabhaktyeti vā dvija ,cintyā mameyamiti vā saktūnādātumarhasi.
english: O brahmin, you should accept the barley flour, either by thinking 'this (offering) is to be regarded as mine (for me to accept)', or by acknowledging your (the giver's) firm devotion (bhakti).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 52. O brahmin, you should accept the barley flour, either by thinking 'this (offering) is to be regarded as mine (for me to accept)', or by acknowledging your (the giver's) firm devotion (bhakti).

sanskrit: श्वशुर उवाच ।अनेन नित्यं साध्वी त्वं शीलवृत्तेन शोभसे ।या त्वं धर्मव्रतोपेता गुरुवृत्तिमवेक्षसे ॥५३॥
transliteration: śvaśura uvāca ,anena nityaṁ sādhvī tvaṁ śīlavṛttena śobhase ,yā tvaṁ dharmavratopetā guruvṛttimavekṣase.
english: The father-in-law said: 'By this, you, virtuous woman, always shine through your excellent character, as you are devoted to the vow of natural law (dharma) and observe your duty towards elders (vṛtti).'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 53. The father-in-law said: 'By this, you, virtuous woman, always shine through your excellent character, as you are devoted to the vow of natural law (dharma) and observe your duty towards elders (vṛtti).'

sanskrit: तस्मात्सक्तून्ग्रहीष्यामि वधूर्नार्हसि वञ्चनाम् ।गणयित्वा महाभागे त्वं हि धर्मभृतां वरा ॥५४॥
transliteration: tasmātsaktūngrahīṣyāmi vadhūrnārhasi vañcanām ,gaṇayitvā mahābhāge tvaṁ hi dharmabhṛtāṁ varā.
english: Therefore, I will accept the barley flour. O daughter-in-law, you do not deserve disappointment, for, O greatly fortunate one, (when all is) considered, you are indeed the foremost among those who uphold natural law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 54. Therefore, I will accept the barley flour. O daughter-in-law, you do not deserve disappointment, for, O greatly fortunate one, (when all is) considered, you are indeed the foremost among those who uphold natural law (dharma).

sanskrit: इत्युक्त्वा तानुपादाय सक्तून्प्रादाद्द्विजातये ।ततस्तुष्टोऽभवद्विप्रस्तस्य साधोर्महात्मनः ॥५५॥
transliteration: ityuktvā tānupādāya saktūnprādāddvijātaye ,tatastuṣṭo'bhavadviprastasya sādhormahātmanaḥ.
english: Having spoken thus, and having accepted that barley flour, he (the father-in-law) offered it (to be consumed) as a brahmin. Thereupon, the brahmin (father-in-law) became pleased with that virtuous, great-souled person (the daughter-in-law).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 55. Having spoken thus, and having accepted that barley flour, he (the father-in-law) offered it (to be consumed) as a brahmin. Thereupon, the brahmin (father-in-law) became pleased with that virtuous, great-souled person (the daughter-in-law).

sanskrit: प्रीतात्मा स तु तं वाक्यमिदमाह द्विजर्षभम् ।वाग्मी तदा द्विजश्रेष्ठो धर्मः पुरुषविग्रहः ॥५६॥
transliteration: prītātmā sa tu taṁ vākyamidamāha dvijarṣabham ,vāgmī tadā dvijaśreṣṭho dharmaḥ puruṣavigrahaḥ.
english: But that happy-souled, eloquent best of the twice-born, (dharma) in human form, then spoke this word to that foremost brahmin.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 56. But that happy-souled, eloquent best of the twice-born, (dharma) in human form, then spoke this word to that foremost brahmin.

sanskrit: शुद्धेन तव दानेन न्यायोपात्तेन यत्नतः ।यथाशक्ति विमुक्तेन प्रीतोऽस्मि द्विजसत्तम ॥५७॥
transliteration: śuddhena tava dānena nyāyopāttena yatnataḥ ,yathāśakti vimuktena prīto'smi dvijasattama.
english: O best of brahmins, I am pleased by your pure gift (dāna), acquired righteously, and offered diligently according to your capacity.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 57. O best of brahmins, I am pleased by your pure gift (dāna), acquired righteously, and offered diligently according to your capacity.

sanskrit: अहो दानं घुष्यते ते स्वर्गे स्वर्गनिवासिभिः ।गगनात्पुष्पवर्षं च पश्यस्व पतितं भुवि ॥५८॥
transliteration: aho dānaṁ ghuṣyate te svarge svarganivāsibhiḥ ,gaganātpuṣpavarṣaṁ ca paśyasva patitaṁ bhuvi.
english: Oh, your gift (dāna) is proclaimed in heaven by the celestial dwellers! And behold the shower of flowers falling from the sky to the earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 58. Oh, your gift (dāna) is proclaimed in heaven by the celestial dwellers! And behold the shower of flowers falling from the sky to the earth.

sanskrit: सुरर्षिदेवगन्धर्वा ये च देवपुरःसराः ।स्तुवन्तो देवदूताश्च स्थिता दानेन विस्मिताः ॥५९॥
transliteration: surarṣidevagandharvā ye ca devapuraḥsarāḥ ,stuvanto devadūtāśca sthitā dānena vismitāḥ.
english: Divine sages, gods, Gandharvas, and those led by the gods, as well as divine messengers, stood there praising and astonished by the gift (dāna).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 59. Divine sages, gods, Gandharvas, and those led by the gods, as well as divine messengers, stood there praising and astonished by the gift (dāna).

sanskrit: ब्रह्मर्षयो विमानस्था ब्रह्मलोकगताश्च ये ।काङ्क्षन्ते दर्शनं तुभ्यं दिवं गच्छ द्विजर्षभ ॥६०॥
transliteration: brahmarṣayo vimānasthā brahmalokagatāśca ye ,kāṅkṣante darśanaṁ tubhyaṁ divaṁ gaccha dvijarṣabha.
english: Even the brahmin sages (brahmarṣi) residing in celestial vehicles and those who have reached the realm of Brahmā (brahmaloka) long for your sight. Therefore, O best among the twice-born (dvija), go to heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 60. Even the brahmin sages (brahmarṣi) residing in celestial vehicles and those who have reached the realm of Brahmā (brahmaloka) long for your sight. Therefore, O best among the twice-born (dvija), go to heaven.

sanskrit: पितृलोकगताः सर्वे तारिताः पितरस्त्वया ।अनागताश्च बहवः सुबहूनि युगानि च ॥६१॥
transliteration: pitṛlokagatāḥ sarve tāritāḥ pitarastvayā ,anāgatāśca bahavaḥ subahūni yugāni ca.
english: All your ancestors (pitṛ), even those who had already gone to the realm of ancestors (pitṛloka), have been liberated by you. And many others, for very many ages, are yet to arrive.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 61. All your ancestors (pitṛ), even those who had already gone to the realm of ancestors (pitṛloka), have been liberated by you. And many others, for very many ages, are yet to arrive.

sanskrit: ब्रह्मचर्येण यज्ञेन दानेन तपसा तथा ।अगह्वरेण धर्मेण तस्माद्गच्छ दिवं द्विज ॥६२॥
transliteration: brahmacaryeṇa yajñena dānena tapasā tathā ,agahvareṇa dharmeṇa tasmādgaccha divaṁ dvija.
english: Through celibacy, Vedic ritual (yajña), charity, and austerity (tapas), as well as by your unobstructed adherence to natural law (dharma), therefore, O twice-born, go to heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 62. Through celibacy, Vedic ritual (yajña), charity, and austerity (tapas), as well as by your unobstructed adherence to natural law (dharma), therefore, O twice-born, go to heaven.

sanskrit: श्रद्धया परया यस्त्वं तपश्चरसि सुव्रत ।तस्माद्देवास्तवानेन प्रीता द्विजवरोत्तम ॥६३॥
transliteration: śraddhayā parayā yastvaṁ tapaścarasi suvrata ,tasmāddevāstavānena prītā dvijavarottama.
english: O virtuous one, because you perform austerity (tapas) with supreme faith (śraddhā), therefore, the gods are pleased by this of yours, O best among the excellent twice-born (dvija).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 63. O virtuous one, because you perform austerity (tapas) with supreme faith (śraddhā), therefore, the gods are pleased by this of yours, O best among the excellent twice-born (dvija).

sanskrit: सर्वस्वमेतद्यस्मात्ते त्यक्तं शुद्धेन चेतसा ।कृच्छ्रकाले ततः स्वर्गो जितोऽयं तव कर्मणा ॥६४॥
transliteration: sarvasvametadyasmātte tyaktaṁ śuddhena cetasā ,kṛcchrakāle tataḥ svargo jito'yaṁ tava karmaṇā.
english: Because all of this was abandoned by you with a pure heart during a time of hardship, this heavenly realm has therefore been conquered by your action (karma).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 64. Because all of this was abandoned by you with a pure heart during a time of hardship, this heavenly realm has therefore been conquered by your action (karma).

sanskrit: क्षुधा निर्णुदति प्रज्ञां धर्म्यां बुद्धिं व्यपोहति ।क्षुधापरिगतज्ञानो धृतिं त्यजति चैव ह ॥६५॥
transliteration: kṣudhā nirṇudati prajñāṁ dharmyāṁ buddhiṁ vyapohati ,kṣudhāparigatajñāno dhṛtiṁ tyajati caiva ha.
english: Hunger dispels wisdom (prajñā) and drives away an intellect (buddhi) aligned with natural law (dharma). Indeed, one whose understanding is overwhelmed by hunger abandons fortitude.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 65. Hunger dispels wisdom (prajñā) and drives away an intellect (buddhi) aligned with natural law (dharma). Indeed, one whose understanding is overwhelmed by hunger abandons fortitude.

sanskrit: बुभुक्षां जयते यस्तु स स्वर्गं जयते ध्रुवम् ।यदा दानरुचिर्भवति तदा धर्मो न सीदति ॥६६॥
transliteration: bubhukṣāṁ jayate yastu sa svargaṁ jayate dhruvam ,yadā dānarucirbhavati tadā dharmo na sīdati.
english: Indeed, whoever conquers hunger certainly conquers the heavenly realm. When an inclination for giving (dāna) arises, then natural law (dharma) does not falter.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 66. Indeed, whoever conquers hunger certainly conquers the heavenly realm. When an inclination for giving (dāna) arises, then natural law (dharma) does not falter.

sanskrit: अनवेक्ष्य सुतस्नेहं कलत्रस्नेहमेव च ।धर्ममेव गुरुं ज्ञात्वा तृष्णा न गणिता त्वया ॥६७॥
transliteration: anavekṣya sutasnehaṁ kalatrasnehameva ca ,dharmameva guruṁ jñātvā tṛṣṇā na gaṇitā tvayā.
english: Disregarding attachment to your son and even attachment to your wife, and having recognized natural law (dharma) alone as your true guide (guru), you did not account for worldly craving.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 67. Disregarding attachment to your son and even attachment to your wife, and having recognized natural law (dharma) alone as your true guide (guru), you did not account for worldly craving.

sanskrit: द्रव्यागमो नृणां सूक्ष्मः पात्रे दानं ततः परम् ।कालः परतरो दानाच्छ्रद्धा चापि ततः परा ॥६८॥
transliteration: dravyāgamo nṛṇāṁ sūkṣmaḥ pātre dānaṁ tataḥ param ,kālaḥ parataro dānācchraddhā cāpi tataḥ parā.
english: The acquisition of wealth for human beings is a subtle matter. Giving to a worthy recipient is considered superior to that. The appropriate time for giving is even more important than the act of giving. And faith (śraddhā) is considered superior to all of these.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 68. The acquisition of wealth for human beings is a subtle matter. Giving to a worthy recipient is considered superior to that. The appropriate time for giving is even more important than the act of giving. And faith (śraddhā) is considered superior to all of these.

sanskrit: स्वर्गद्वारं सुसूक्ष्मं हि नरैर्मोहान्न दृश्यते ।स्वर्गार्गलं लोभबीजं रागगुप्तं दुरासदम् ॥६९॥
transliteration: svargadvāraṁ susūkṣmaṁ hi narairmohānna dṛśyate ,svargārgalaṁ lobhabījaṁ rāgaguptaṁ durāsadam.
english: Indeed, the gateway to heaven is very subtle and is not perceived by people due to delusion. The obstacle to heaven is the seed of avarice, concealed by attachment, and very difficult to overcome.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 69. Indeed, the gateway to heaven is very subtle and is not perceived by people due to delusion. The obstacle to heaven is the seed of avarice, concealed by attachment, and very difficult to overcome.

sanskrit: तत्तु पश्यन्ति पुरुषा जितक्रोधा जितेन्द्रियाः ।ब्राह्मणास्तपसा युक्ता यथाशक्तिप्रदायिनः ॥७०॥
transliteration: tattu paśyanti puruṣā jitakrodhā jitendriyāḥ ,brāhmaṇāstapasā yuktā yathāśaktipradāyinaḥ.
english: However, that (the subtle gate and its obstruction) is perceived by persons (puruṣa) who have conquered anger and subdued their senses, as well as by Brahmins who are endowed with spiritual discipline (tapas) and who give according to their ability.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 70. However, that (the subtle gate and its obstruction) is perceived by persons (puruṣa) who have conquered anger and subdued their senses, as well as by Brahmins who are endowed with spiritual discipline (tapas) and who give according to their ability.

sanskrit: सहस्रशक्तिश्च शतं शतशक्तिर्दशापि च ।दद्यादपश्च यः शक्त्या सर्वे तुल्यफलाः स्मृताः ॥७१॥
transliteration: sahasraśaktiśca śataṁ śataśaktirdaśāpi ca ,dadyādapaśca yaḥ śaktyā sarve tulyaphalāḥ smṛtāḥ.
english: One with a thousand units of capacity (śakti) gives a hundred, and one with a hundred units of capacity gives ten. But whoever gives even water according to their ability (śakti) – all such individuals are considered to achieve equal results.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 71. One with a thousand units of capacity (śakti) gives a hundred, and one with a hundred units of capacity gives ten. But whoever gives even water according to their ability (śakti) – all such individuals are considered to achieve equal results.

sanskrit: रन्तिदेवो हि नृपतिरपः प्रादादकिंचनः ।शुद्धेन मनसा विप्र नाकपृष्ठं ततो गतः ॥७२॥
transliteration: rantidevo hi nṛpatirapaḥ prādādakiṁcanaḥ ,śuddhena manasā vipra nākapṛṣṭhaṁ tato gataḥ.
english: King Rantideva, though possessing nothing, indeed gave water. With a pure mind, O brahmin, he thereupon went to the highest heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 72. King Rantideva, though possessing nothing, indeed gave water. With a pure mind, O brahmin, he thereupon went to the highest heaven.

sanskrit: न धर्मः प्रीयते तात दानैर्दत्तैर्महाफलैः ।न्यायलब्धैर्यथा सूक्ष्मैः श्रद्धापूतैः स तुष्यति ॥७३॥
transliteration: na dharmaḥ prīyate tāta dānairdattairmahāphalaiḥ ,nyāyalabdhairyathā sūkṣmaiḥ śraddhāpūtaiḥ sa tuṣyati.
english: O child, the natural law (dharma) is not pleased by opulent gifts. Rather, it is satisfied by small offerings, justly obtained and purified by faith (śraddhā).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 73. O child, the natural law (dharma) is not pleased by opulent gifts. Rather, it is satisfied by small offerings, justly obtained and purified by faith (śraddhā).

sanskrit: गोप्रदानसहस्राणि द्विजेभ्योऽदान्नृगो नृपः ।एकां दत्त्वा स पारक्यां नरकं समवाप्तवान् ॥७४॥
transliteration: gopradānasahasrāṇi dvijebhyo'dānnṛgo nṛpaḥ ,ekāṁ dattvā sa pārakyāṁ narakaṁ samavāptavān.
english: King Nṛga gave thousands of cows as gifts to brahmins. However, by giving away a single cow that belonged to another, he obtained hell.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 74. King Nṛga gave thousands of cows as gifts to brahmins. However, by giving away a single cow that belonged to another, he obtained hell.

sanskrit: आत्ममांसप्रदानेन शिबिरौशीनरो नृपः ।प्राप्य पुण्यकृताँल्लोकान्मोदते दिवि सुव्रतः ॥७५॥
transliteration: ātmamāṁsapradānena śibirauśīnaro nṛpaḥ ,prāpya puṇyakṛtāँllokānmodate divi suvrataḥ.
english: King Śibi, the descendant of Uśīnara, a man of excellent vows, by offering his own flesh, attained the worlds of the virtuous and rejoices in heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 75. King Śibi, the descendant of Uśīnara, a man of excellent vows, by offering his own flesh, attained the worlds of the virtuous and rejoices in heaven.

sanskrit: विभवे न नृणां पुण्यं स्वशक्त्या स्वर्जितं सताम् ।न यज्ञैर्विविधैर्विप्र यथान्यायेन संचितैः ॥७६॥
transliteration: vibhave na nṛṇāṁ puṇyaṁ svaśaktyā svarjitaṁ satām ,na yajñairvividhairvipra yathānyāyena saṁcitaiḥ.
english: O brahmin (vipra), the merit (puṇya) of righteous people, earned by their own effort, is not found in prosperity. Nor is it found through various Vedic rituals (yajñaiḥ), even if accumulated according to proper rule.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 76. O brahmin (vipra), the merit (puṇya) of righteous people, earned by their own effort, is not found in prosperity. Nor is it found through various Vedic rituals (yajñaiḥ), even if accumulated according to proper rule.

sanskrit: क्रोधो दानफलं हन्ति लोभात्स्वर्गं न गच्छति ।न्यायवृत्तिर्हि तपसा दानवित्स्वर्गमश्नुते ॥७७॥
transliteration: krodho dānaphalaṁ hanti lobhātsvargaṁ na gacchati ,nyāyavṛttirhi tapasā dānavitsvargamaśnute.
english: Anger destroys the fruit of giving (dāna). From greed, one does not go to heaven. Indeed, one who lives righteously, through spiritual discipline (tapas), and understands giving (dāna), attains heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 77. Anger destroys the fruit of giving (dāna). From greed, one does not go to heaven. Indeed, one who lives righteously, through spiritual discipline (tapas), and understands giving (dāna), attains heaven.

sanskrit: न राजसूयैर्बहुभिरिष्ट्वा विपुलदक्षिणैः ।न चाश्वमेधैर्बहुभिः फलं सममिदं तव ॥७८॥
transliteration: na rājasūyairbahubhiriṣṭvā vipuladakṣiṇaiḥ ,na cāśvamedhairbahubhiḥ phalaṁ samamidaṁ tava.
english: Even by performing many royal consecration Vedic rituals (rājasūyaiḥ) with ample offerings, and by many horse Vedic rituals (aśvamedhaiḥ), a fruit equal to this one (idam phalam) of yours (tava) is not obtained.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 78. Even by performing many royal consecration Vedic rituals (rājasūyaiḥ) with ample offerings, and by many horse Vedic rituals (aśvamedhaiḥ), a fruit equal to this one (idam phalam) of yours (tava) is not obtained.

sanskrit: सक्तुप्रस्थेन हि जितो ब्रह्मलोकस्त्वयानघ ।विरजो ब्रह्मभवनं गच्छ विप्र यथेच्छकम् ॥७९॥
transliteration: saktuprasthena hi jito brahmalokastvayānagha ,virajo brahmabhavanaṁ gaccha vipra yathecchakam.
english: Indeed (hi), by a measure of barley flour, the world of brahman (brahmaloka) has been conquered by you, O sinless one (anagha). Go, O brahmin (vipra), to the stainless abode of brahman (brahmabhavanam), as you desire.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 79. Indeed (hi), by a measure of barley flour, the world of brahman (brahmaloka) has been conquered by you, O sinless one (anagha). Go, O brahmin (vipra), to the stainless abode of brahman (brahmabhavanam), as you desire.

sanskrit: सर्वेषां वो द्विजश्रेष्ठ दिव्यं यानमुपस्थितम् ।आरोहत यथाकामं धर्मोऽस्मि द्विज पश्य माम् ॥८०॥
transliteration: sarveṣāṁ vo dvijaśreṣṭha divyaṁ yānamupasthitam ,ārohata yathākāmaṁ dharmo'smi dvija paśya mām.
english: O best among the twice-born (dvija), a divine vehicle has arrived for all of you. Ascend it as you desire. I am the natural law (dharma), O twice-born (dvija); see me.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 80. O best among the twice-born (dvija), a divine vehicle has arrived for all of you. Ascend it as you desire. I am the natural law (dharma), O twice-born (dvija); see me.

sanskrit: पावितो हि त्वया देहो लोके कीर्तिः स्थिरा च ते ।सभार्यः सहपुत्रश्च सस्नुषश्च दिवं व्रज ॥८१॥
transliteration: pāvito hi tvayā deho loke kīrtiḥ sthirā ca te ,sabhāryaḥ sahaputraśca sasnuṣaśca divaṁ vraja.
english: Indeed, your body has been purified by you, and your fame in the world is permanent. Go to heaven with your wife, your son, and your daughter-in-law.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 81. Indeed, your body has been purified by you, and your fame in the world is permanent. Go to heaven with your wife, your son, and your daughter-in-law.

sanskrit: इत्युक्तवाक्यो धर्मेण यानमारुह्य स द्विजः ।सभार्यः ससुतश्चापि सस्नुषश्च दिवं ययौ ॥८२॥
transliteration: ityuktavākyo dharmeṇa yānamāruhya sa dvijaḥ ,sabhāryaḥ sasutaścāpi sasnuṣaśca divaṁ yayau.
english: Having been addressed thus by the natural law (dharma), that twice-born (dvija), after ascending the vehicle, went to heaven with his wife, son, and also his daughter-in-law.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 82. Having been addressed thus by the natural law (dharma), that twice-born (dvija), after ascending the vehicle, went to heaven with his wife, son, and also his daughter-in-law.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्विप्रे गते स्वर्गं ससुते सस्नुषे तदा ।भार्याचतुर्थे धर्मज्ञे ततोऽहं निःसृतो बिलात् ॥८३॥
transliteration: tasminvipre gate svargaṁ sasute sasnuṣe tadā ,bhāryācaturthe dharmajñe tato'haṁ niḥsṛto bilāt.
english: When that brahmin, a knower of natural law (dharma), had gone to heaven, accompanied by his son, daughter-in-law, and wife (as the fourth member of the group), then I emerged from the cave.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 83. When that brahmin, a knower of natural law (dharma), had gone to heaven, accompanied by his son, daughter-in-law, and wife (as the fourth member of the group), then I emerged from the cave.

sanskrit: ततस्तु सक्तुगन्धेन क्लेदेन सलिलस्य च ।दिव्यपुष्पावमर्दाच्च साधोर्दानलवैश्च तैः ।विप्रस्य तपसा तस्य शिरो मे काञ्चनीकृतम् ॥८४॥
transliteration: tatastu saktugandhena kledena salilasya ca ,divyapuṣpāvamardācca sādhordānalavaiśca taiḥ ,viprasya tapasā tasya śiro me kāñcanīkṛtam.
english: Indeed, my head was made golden by the fragrance of barley flour, by the moisture of water, by the crushed divine flowers, and by those small charitable offerings (dāna) of a righteous man, as well as by the spiritual discipline (tapas) of that brahmin.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 84. Indeed, my head was made golden by the fragrance of barley flour, by the moisture of water, by the crushed divine flowers, and by those small charitable offerings (dāna) of a righteous man, as well as by the spiritual discipline (tapas) of that brahmin.

sanskrit: तस्य सत्याभिसंधस्य सूक्ष्मदानेन चैव ह ।शरीरार्धं च मे विप्राः शातकुम्भमयं कृतम् ।पश्यतेदं सुविपुलं तपसा तस्य धीमतः ॥८५॥
transliteration: tasya satyābhisaṁdhasya sūkṣmadānena caiva ha ,śarīrārdhaṁ ca me viprāḥ śātakumbhamayaṁ kṛtam ,paśyatedaṁ suvipulaṁ tapasā tasya dhīmataḥ.
english: O brahmins, half of my body was made of pure gold (śātakumbha) by the subtle charitable offering (dāna) of that truthful man, and by the spiritual discipline (tapas) of that wise one. Behold this immense (transformation).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 85. O brahmins, half of my body was made of pure gold (śātakumbha) by the subtle charitable offering (dāna) of that truthful man, and by the spiritual discipline (tapas) of that wise one. Behold this immense (transformation).

sanskrit: कथमेवंविधं मे स्यादन्यत्पार्श्वमिति द्विजाः ।तपोवनानि यज्ञांश्च हृष्टोऽभ्येमि पुनः पुनः ॥८६॥
transliteration: kathamevaṁvidhaṁ me syādanyatpārśvamiti dvijāḥ ,tapovanāni yajñāṁśca hṛṣṭo'bhyemi punaḥ punaḥ.
english: O brahmins (dvija), wondering 'how could my other side become like this?' I joyfully approach forest hermitages and perform Vedic rituals (yajña) again and again.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 86. O brahmins (dvija), wondering 'how could my other side become like this?' I joyfully approach forest hermitages and perform Vedic rituals (yajña) again and again.

sanskrit: यज्ञं त्वहमिमं श्रुत्वा कुरुराजस्य धीमतः ।आशया परया प्राप्तो न चाहं काञ्चनीकृतः ॥८७॥
transliteration: yajñaṁ tvahamimaṁ śrutvā kururājasya dhīmataḥ ,āśayā parayā prāpto na cāhaṁ kāñcanīkṛtaḥ.
english: But having heard of this Vedic ritual (yajña) by the wise king of the Kurus, I arrived with immense hope, yet I was not made golden.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 87. But having heard of this Vedic ritual (yajña) by the wise king of the Kurus, I arrived with immense hope, yet I was not made golden.

sanskrit: ततो मयोक्तं तद्वाक्यं प्रहस्य द्विजसत्तमाः ।सक्तुप्रस्थेन यज्ञोऽयं संमितो नेति सर्वथा ॥८८॥
transliteration: tato mayoktaṁ tadvākyaṁ prahasya dvijasattamāḥ ,saktuprasthena yajño'yaṁ saṁmito neti sarvathā.
english: Then, having laughed, I said to the best among the brahmins, "O best among brahmins, this great Vedic ritual (yajña) is by no means comparable to merely a prastha of barley flour."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 88. Then, having laughed, I said to the best among the brahmins, "O best among brahmins, this great Vedic ritual (yajña) is by no means comparable to merely a prastha of barley flour."

sanskrit: सक्तुप्रस्थलवैस्तैर्हि तदाहं काञ्चनीकृतः ।न हि यज्ञो महानेष सदृशस्तैर्मतो मम ॥८९॥
transliteration: saktuprasthalavaistairhi tadāhaṁ kāñcanīkṛtaḥ ,na hi yajño mahāneṣa sadṛśastairmato mama.
english: For indeed, I was made golden by those very particles of barley flour then. This great Vedic ritual (yajña) is certainly not considered by me to be comparable to them.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 89. For indeed, I was made golden by those very particles of barley flour then. This great Vedic ritual (yajña) is certainly not considered by me to be comparable to them.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।इत्युक्त्वा नकुलः सर्वान्यज्ञे द्विजवरांस्तदा ।जगामादर्शनं राजन्विप्रास्ते च ययुर्गृहान् ॥९०॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,ityuktvā nakulaḥ sarvānyajñe dvijavarāṁstadā ,jagāmādarśanaṁ rājanviprāste ca yayurgṛhān.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: "Having spoken thus to all the best of the brahmins at the Vedic ritual (yajña), Nakula then disappeared, O King, and those brahmins also returned to their homes."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 90. Vaiśampāyana said: "Having spoken thus to all the best of the brahmins at the Vedic ritual (yajña), Nakula then disappeared, O King, and those brahmins also returned to their homes."

sanskrit: एतत्ते सर्वमाख्यातं मया परपुरंजय ।यदाश्चर्यमभूत्तस्मिन्वाजिमेधे महाक्रतौ ॥९१॥
transliteration: etatte sarvamākhyātaṁ mayā parapuraṁjaya ,yadāścaryamabhūttasminvājimedhe mahākratau.
english: O conqueror of enemy cities, I have narrated to you all of this wonderful event that occurred during that great Horse Vedic ritual (vājimedha).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 91. O conqueror of enemy cities, I have narrated to you all of this wonderful event that occurred during that great Horse Vedic ritual (vājimedha).

sanskrit: न विस्मयस्ते नृपते यज्ञे कार्यः कथंचन ।ऋषिकोटिसहस्राणि तपोभिर्ये दिवं गताः ॥९२॥
transliteration: na vismayaste nṛpate yajñe kāryaḥ kathaṁcana ,ṛṣikoṭisahasrāṇi tapobhirye divaṁ gatāḥ.
english: O king, you should not feel any astonishment regarding the (Vedic ritual) yajña, because thousands of crores of sages have attained heaven through their ascetic practices (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 92. O king, you should not feel any astonishment regarding the (Vedic ritual) yajña, because thousands of crores of sages have attained heaven through their ascetic practices (tapas).

sanskrit: अद्रोहः सर्वभूतेषु संतोषः शीलमार्जवम् ।तपो दमश्च सत्यं च दानं चेति समं मतम् ॥९३॥
transliteration: adrohaḥ sarvabhūteṣu saṁtoṣaḥ śīlamārjavam ,tapo damaśca satyaṁ ca dānaṁ ceti samaṁ matam.
english: Non-hostility towards all beings, contentment, virtuous conduct, straightforwardness, ascetic practice (tapas), self-control, truthfulness, and charity – these are considered equally important.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 93 Verse 93. Non-hostility towards all beings, contentment, virtuous conduct, straightforwardness, ascetic practice (tapas), self-control, truthfulness, and charity – these are considered equally important.

sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।यज्ञे सक्ता नृपतयस्तपःसक्ता महर्षयः ।शान्तिव्यवसिता विप्राः शमो दम इति प्रभो ॥१॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,yajñe saktā nṛpatayastapaḥsaktā maharṣayaḥ ,śāntivyavasitā viprāḥ śamo dama iti prabho.
english: Janamejaya spoke: O lord, kings are attached to Vedic rituals (yajña), great sages are devoted to ascetic practices (tapas), and learned Brahmins are resolved on inner tranquility and external self-control.
