search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 94 Verse 1. Janamejaya spoke: O lord, kings are attached to Vedic rituals (yajña), great sages are devoted to ascetic practices (tapas), and learned Brahmins are resolved on inner tranquility and external self-control.

sanskrit: तस्माद्यज्ञफलैस्तुल्यं न किंचिदिह विद्यते ।इति मे वर्तते बुद्धिस्तथा चैतदसंशयम् ॥२॥
transliteration: tasmādyajñaphalaistulyaṁ na kiṁcidiha vidyate ,iti me vartate buddhistathā caitadasaṁśayam.
english: Therefore, nothing in this world is comparable to the results of a Vedic ritual (yajña). This is my firm conviction, and it is undoubtedly true.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 94 Verse 2. Therefore, nothing in this world is comparable to the results of a Vedic ritual (yajña). This is my firm conviction, and it is undoubtedly true.

sanskrit: यज्ञैरिष्ट्वा हि बहवो राजानो द्विजसत्तम ।इह कीर्तिं परां प्राप्य प्रेत्य स्वर्गमितो गताः ॥३॥
transliteration: yajñairiṣṭvā hi bahavo rājāno dvijasattama ,iha kīrtiṁ parāṁ prāpya pretya svargamito gatāḥ.
english: Indeed, O best among the twice-born (dvija), many kings, having performed Vedic rituals (yajñas), attained great fame in this world and, after death, went to heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 94 Verse 3. Indeed, O best among the twice-born (dvija), many kings, having performed Vedic rituals (yajñas), attained great fame in this world and, after death, went to heaven.

sanskrit: देवराजः सहस्राक्षः क्रतुभिर्भूरिदक्षिणैः ।देवराज्यं महातेजाः प्राप्तवानखिलं विभुः ॥४॥
transliteration: devarājaḥ sahasrākṣaḥ kratubhirbhūridakṣiṇaiḥ ,devarājyaṁ mahātejāḥ prāptavānakhilaṁ vibhuḥ.
english: The thousand-eyed Indra, the mighty king of the gods, who possessed great splendor, attained the entire sovereignty of the gods by means of Vedic rituals (kratu) that involved abundant gifts.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 94 Verse 4. The thousand-eyed Indra, the mighty king of the gods, who possessed great splendor, attained the entire sovereignty of the gods by means of Vedic rituals (kratu) that involved abundant gifts.

sanskrit: यथा युधिष्ठिरो राजा भीमार्जुनपुरःसरः ।सदृशो देवराजेन समृद्ध्या विक्रमेण च ॥५॥
transliteration: yathā yudhiṣṭhiro rājā bhīmārjunapuraḥsaraḥ ,sadṛśo devarājena samṛddhyā vikrameṇa ca.
english: Just as King Yudhiṣṭhira, with Bhīma and Arjuna at his forefront, is comparable to the king of the gods (Indra) in terms of prosperity and valor.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 94 Verse 5. Just as King Yudhiṣṭhira, with Bhīma and Arjuna at his forefront, is comparable to the king of the gods (Indra) in terms of prosperity and valor.

sanskrit: अथ कस्मात्स नकुलो गर्हयामास तं क्रतुम् ।अश्वमेधं महायज्ञं राज्ञस्तस्य महात्मनः ॥६॥
transliteration: atha kasmātsa nakulo garhayāmāsa taṁ kratum ,aśvamedhaṁ mahāyajñaṁ rājñastasya mahātmanaḥ.
english: Now, why did that Nakula (mongoose) criticize that particular Vedic ritual (kratu), the great aśvamedha (yajña) of that noble king?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 94 Verse 6. Now, why did that Nakula (mongoose) criticize that particular Vedic ritual (kratu), the great aśvamedha (yajña) of that noble king?

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।यज्ञस्य विधिमग्र्यं वै फलं चैव नरर्षभ ।गदतः शृणु मे राजन्यथावदिह भारत ॥७॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,yajñasya vidhimagryaṁ vai phalaṁ caiva nararṣabha ,gadataḥ śṛṇu me rājanyathāvadiha bhārata.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: O best among men, O King, O Bhārata, listen to me here as I properly explain the foremost procedure of the (Vedic ritual) (yajña) and its fruit.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 94 Verse 7. Vaiśampāyana said: O best among men, O King, O Bhārata, listen to me here as I properly explain the foremost procedure of the (Vedic ritual) (yajña) and its fruit.

sanskrit: पुरा शक्रस्य यजतः सर्व ऊचुर्महर्षयः ।ऋत्विक्षु कर्मव्यग्रेषु वितते यज्ञकर्मणि ॥८॥
transliteration: purā śakrasya yajataḥ sarva ūcurmaharṣayaḥ ,ṛtvikṣu karmavyagreṣu vitate yajñakarmaṇi.
english: Formerly, when Indra (Śakra) was performing a (Vedic ritual) (yajña) and the extensive ritual ceremony was underway, with the priests (ṛtvij) fully absorbed in their duties, all the great sages (maharṣi) spoke.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 94 Verse 8. Formerly, when Indra (Śakra) was performing a (Vedic ritual) (yajña) and the extensive ritual ceremony was underway, with the priests (ṛtvij) fully absorbed in their duties, all the great sages (maharṣi) spoke.

sanskrit: हूयमाने तथा वह्नौ होत्रे बहुगुणान्विते ।देवेष्वाहूयमानेषु स्थितेषु परमर्षिषु ॥९॥
transliteration: hūyamāne tathā vahnau hotre bahuguṇānvite ,deveṣvāhūyamāneṣu sthiteṣu paramarṣiṣu.
english: And when oblations were being offered into the fire (vahni), with the principal priest (hotṛ) possessing many excellent qualities, as the gods were being invoked, and the supreme sages (paramarṣi) were respectfully present.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 94 Verse 9. And when oblations were being offered into the fire (vahni), with the principal priest (hotṛ) possessing many excellent qualities, as the gods were being invoked, and the supreme sages (paramarṣi) were respectfully present.

sanskrit: सुप्रतीतैस्तदा विप्रैः स्वागमैः सुस्वनैर्नृप ।अश्रान्तैश्चापि लघुभिरध्वर्युवृषभैस्तथा ॥१०॥
transliteration: supratītaistadā vipraiḥ svāgamaiḥ susvanairnṛpa ,aśrāntaiścāpi laghubhiradhvaryuvṛṣabhaistathā.
english: O King, then, by the well-pleased Brahmins (vipra) with their appropriate and melodious chants, and likewise by the tireless and skilled chief Adhvaryu priests.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 94 Verse 10. O King, then, by the well-pleased Brahmins (vipra) with their appropriate and melodious chants, and likewise by the tireless and skilled chief Adhvaryu priests.

sanskrit: आलम्भसमये तस्मिन्गृहीतेषु पशुष्वथ ।महर्षयो महाराज संबभूवुः कृपान्विताः ॥११॥
transliteration: ālambhasamaye tasmingṛhīteṣu paśuṣvatha ,maharṣayo mahārāja saṁbabhūvuḥ kṛpānvitāḥ.
english: O great king, then, at that time of immolation, when the animals had been seized, the great sages became filled with compassion.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 94 Verse 11. O great king, then, at that time of immolation, when the animals had been seized, the great sages became filled with compassion.

sanskrit: ततो दीनान्पशून्दृष्ट्वा ऋषयस्ते तपोधनाः ।ऊचुः शक्रं समागम्य नायं यज्ञविधिः शुभः ॥१२॥
transliteration: tato dīnānpaśūndṛṣṭvā ṛṣayaste tapodhanāḥ ,ūcuḥ śakraṁ samāgamya nāyaṁ yajñavidhiḥ śubhaḥ.
english: Then, having seen those distressed animals, those sages, whose wealth was spiritual discipline (tapas), approached Indra and said, "This procedure for a Vedic ritual (yajña) is not proper."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 94 Verse 12. Then, having seen those distressed animals, those sages, whose wealth was spiritual discipline (tapas), approached Indra and said, "This procedure for a Vedic ritual (yajña) is not proper."

sanskrit: अपविज्ञानमेतत्ते महान्तं धर्ममिच्छतः ।न हि यज्ञे पशुगणा विधिदृष्टाः पुरंदर ॥१३॥
transliteration: apavijñānametatte mahāntaṁ dharmamicchataḥ ,na hi yajñe paśugaṇā vidhidṛṣṭāḥ puraṁdara.
english: This is your misunderstanding, you who desire great righteousness (dharma). For, O Purandara, multitudes of animals are not prescribed by the sacred rules for a Vedic ritual (yajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 94 Verse 13. This is your misunderstanding, you who desire great righteousness (dharma). For, O Purandara, multitudes of animals are not prescribed by the sacred rules for a Vedic ritual (yajña).

sanskrit: धर्मोपघातकस्त्वेष समारम्भस्तव प्रभो ।नायं धर्मकृतो धर्मो न हिंसा धर्म उच्यते ॥१४॥
transliteration: dharmopaghātakastveṣa samārambhastava prabho ,nāyaṁ dharmakṛto dharmo na hiṁsā dharma ucyate.
english: But this undertaking of yours, O Lord, is detrimental to the natural law (dharma). This is not an act for the sake of true righteousness (dharma), nor is violence ever called (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 94 Verse 14. But this undertaking of yours, O Lord, is detrimental to the natural law (dharma). This is not an act for the sake of true righteousness (dharma), nor is violence ever called (dharma).

sanskrit: आगमेनैव ते यज्ञं कुर्वन्तु यदि हेच्छसि ।विधिदृष्टेन यज्ञेन धर्मस्ते सुमहान्भवेत् ॥१५॥
transliteration: āgamenaiva te yajñaṁ kurvantu yadi hecchasi ,vidhidṛṣṭena yajñena dharmaste sumahānbhavet.
english: If you indeed wish, let them perform the Vedic ritual (yajña) solely according to the scriptures. Through a Vedic ritual performed as prescribed by rules, your natural law (dharma) would become exceedingly great.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 94 Verse 15. If you indeed wish, let them perform the Vedic ritual (yajña) solely according to the scriptures. Through a Vedic ritual performed as prescribed by rules, your natural law (dharma) would become exceedingly great.

sanskrit: यज बीजैः सहस्राक्ष त्रिवर्षपरमोषितैः ।एष धर्मो महाञ्शक्र चिन्त्यमानोऽधिगम्यते ॥१६॥
transliteration: yaja bījaiḥ sahasrākṣa trivarṣaparamoṣitaiḥ ,eṣa dharmo mahāñśakra cintyamāno'dhigamyate.
english: O thousand-eyed lord, perform the ritual with seeds preserved for a maximum of three years. This great natural law (dharma), O mighty one, is understood when contemplated.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 94 Verse 16. O thousand-eyed lord, perform the ritual with seeds preserved for a maximum of three years. This great natural law (dharma), O mighty one, is understood when contemplated.

sanskrit: शतक्रतुस्तु तद्वाक्यमृषिभिस्तत्त्वदर्शिभिः ।उक्तं न प्रतिजग्राह मानमोहवशानुगः ॥१७॥
transliteration: śatakratustu tadvākyamṛṣibhistattvadarśibhiḥ ,uktaṁ na pratijagrāha mānamohavaśānugaḥ.
english: But Indra (Śatakratu) did not accept that statement spoken by the truth-seeing sages, as he was under the sway of pride and delusion.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 94 Verse 17. But Indra (Śatakratu) did not accept that statement spoken by the truth-seeing sages, as he was under the sway of pride and delusion.

sanskrit: तेषां विवादः सुमहाञ्जज्ञे शक्रमहर्षिणाम् ।जङ्गमैः स्थावरैर्वापि यष्टव्यमिति भारत ॥१८॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ vivādaḥ sumahāñjajñe śakramaharṣiṇām ,jaṅgamaiḥ sthāvarairvāpi yaṣṭavyamiti bhārata.
english: A very great dispute arose between Indra and the great sages (maharṣis) regarding whether Vedic rituals should be performed by moving or stationary beings, O Bhārata.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 94 Verse 18. A very great dispute arose between Indra and the great sages (maharṣis) regarding whether Vedic rituals should be performed by moving or stationary beings, O Bhārata.

sanskrit: ते तु खिन्ना विवादेन ऋषयस्तत्त्वदर्शिनः ।ततः संधाय शक्रेण पप्रच्छुर्नृपतिं वसुम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: te tu khinnā vivādena ṛṣayastattvadarśinaḥ ,tataḥ saṁdhāya śakreṇa papracchurnṛpatiṁ vasum.
english: Those sages, who perceived the truth (tattva), were distressed by the dispute. Thereupon, having formed an alliance with Indra, they questioned King Vasu.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 94 Verse 19. Those sages, who perceived the truth (tattva), were distressed by the dispute. Thereupon, having formed an alliance with Indra, they questioned King Vasu.

sanskrit: महाभाग कथं यज्ञेष्वागमो नृपते स्मृतः ।यष्टव्यं पशुभिर्मेध्यैरथो बीजैरजैरपि ॥२०॥
transliteration: mahābhāga kathaṁ yajñeṣvāgamo nṛpate smṛtaḥ ,yaṣṭavyaṁ paśubhirmedhyairatho bījairajairapi.
english: O glorious one, King, how is the proper procedure (āgama) remembered for Vedic rituals (yajñas)? Should the Vedic ritual (yajña) be performed with pure animals, or alternatively, with seeds, or even with goats?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 94 Verse 20. O glorious one, King, how is the proper procedure (āgama) remembered for Vedic rituals (yajñas)? Should the Vedic ritual (yajña) be performed with pure animals, or alternatively, with seeds, or even with goats?

sanskrit: तच्छ्रुत्वा तु वचस्तेषामविचार्य बलाबलम् ।यथोपनीतैर्यष्टव्यमिति प्रोवाच पार्थिवः ॥२१॥
transliteration: tacchrutvā tu vacasteṣāmavicārya balābalam ,yathopanītairyaṣṭavyamiti provāca pārthivaḥ.
english: Having heard their words, and without considering the strengths and weaknesses (of the arguments), the king (Vasu) indeed declared, 'One should perform the Vedic ritual (yajña) with whatever is readily available!'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 94 Verse 21. Having heard their words, and without considering the strengths and weaknesses (of the arguments), the king (Vasu) indeed declared, 'One should perform the Vedic ritual (yajña) with whatever is readily available!'

sanskrit: एवमुक्त्वा स नृपतिः प्रविवेश रसातलम् ।उक्त्वेह वितथं राजंश्चेदीनामीश्वरः प्रभुः ॥२२॥
transliteration: evamuktvā sa nṛpatiḥ praviveśa rasātalam ,uktveha vitathaṁ rājaṁścedīnāmīśvaraḥ prabhuḥ.
english: Having spoken thus, and having uttered a falsehood in this world, that king, the lord of the Cedis and a powerful ruler, entered the netherworld (rasātala).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 94 Verse 22. Having spoken thus, and having uttered a falsehood in this world, that king, the lord of the Cedis and a powerful ruler, entered the netherworld (rasātala).

sanskrit: अन्यायोपगतं द्रव्यमतीतं यो ह्यपण्डितः ।धर्माभिकाङ्क्षी यजते न धर्मफलमश्नुते ॥२३॥
transliteration: anyāyopagataṁ dravyamatītaṁ yo hyapaṇḍitaḥ ,dharmābhikāṅkṣī yajate na dharmaphalamaśnute.
english: He who, indeed, being foolish and desiring (natural law) "dharma", performs a "Vedic ritual" (yajña) with wealth unjustly acquired (in the past), does not obtain the fruit of "dharma".
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 94 Verse 23. He who, indeed, being foolish and desiring (natural law) "dharma", performs a "Vedic ritual" (yajña) with wealth unjustly acquired (in the past), does not obtain the fruit of "dharma".

sanskrit: धर्मवैतंसिको यस्तु पापात्मा पुरुषस्तथा ।ददाति दानं विप्रेभ्यो लोकविश्वासकारकम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: dharmavaitaṁsiko yastu pāpātmā puruṣastathā ,dadāti dānaṁ viprebhyo lokaviśvāsakārakam.
english: But such a man, who is a hypocrite regarding (natural law) "dharma" and possesses a sinful nature (pāpātmā), gives (dāna) gifts to Brahmins to inspire public trust.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 94 Verse 24. But such a man, who is a hypocrite regarding (natural law) "dharma" and possesses a sinful nature (pāpātmā), gives (dāna) gifts to Brahmins to inspire public trust.

sanskrit: पापेन कर्मणा विप्रो धनं लब्ध्वा निरङ्कुशः ।रागमोहान्वितः सोऽन्ते कलुषां गतिमाप्नुते ॥२५॥
transliteration: pāpena karmaṇā vipro dhanaṁ labdhvā niraṅkuśaḥ ,rāgamohānvitaḥ so'nte kaluṣāṁ gatimāpnute.
english: A Brahmin, having obtained wealth through sinful actions, becomes unrestrained; filled with emotional attachment (rāga) and delusion (moha), he ultimately attains an impure destiny.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 94 Verse 25. A Brahmin, having obtained wealth through sinful actions, becomes unrestrained; filled with emotional attachment (rāga) and delusion (moha), he ultimately attains an impure destiny.

sanskrit: तेन दत्तानि दानानि पापेन हतबुद्धिना ।तानि सत्त्वमनासाद्य नश्यन्ति विपुलान्यपि ॥२६॥
transliteration: tena dattāni dānāni pāpena hatabuddhinā ,tāni sattvamanāsādya naśyanti vipulānyapi.
english: The gifts (dāna) given by that individual - whose intellect is corrupted by sin - even if numerous, perish without yielding true merit (sattva).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 94 Verse 26. The gifts (dāna) given by that individual - whose intellect is corrupted by sin - even if numerous, perish without yielding true merit (sattva).

sanskrit: तस्याधर्मप्रवृत्तस्य हिंसकस्य दुरात्मनः ।दाने न कीर्तिर्भवति प्रेत्य चेह च दुर्मतेः ॥२७॥
transliteration: tasyādharmapravṛttasya hiṁsakasya durātmanaḥ ,dāne na kīrtirbhavati pretya ceha ca durmateḥ.
english: For that evil-minded person who is inclined towards unrighteousness (adharma), is violent, and wicked, no fame arises from charity, neither in this world nor in the next.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 94 Verse 27. For that evil-minded person who is inclined towards unrighteousness (adharma), is violent, and wicked, no fame arises from charity, neither in this world nor in the next.

sanskrit: अपि संचयबुद्धिर्हि लोभमोहवशंगतः ।उद्वेजयति भूतानि हिंसया पापचेतनः ॥२८॥
transliteration: api saṁcayabuddhirhi lobhamohavaśaṁgataḥ ,udvejayati bhūtāni hiṁsayā pāpacetanaḥ.
english: Indeed, a wicked-minded person, whose intellect is focused on accumulation and who is overcome by greed and delusion, torments living beings through violence.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 94 Verse 28. Indeed, a wicked-minded person, whose intellect is focused on accumulation and who is overcome by greed and delusion, torments living beings through violence.

sanskrit: एवं लब्ध्वा धनं लोभाद्यजते यो ददाति च ।स कृत्वा कर्मणा तेन न सिध्यति दुरागमात् ॥२९॥
transliteration: evaṁ labdhvā dhanaṁ lobhādyajate yo dadāti ca ,sa kṛtvā karmaṇā tena na sidhyati durāgamāt.
english: He who thus obtains wealth through greed, and then performs Vedic rituals (yajña) and gives (dāna), does not achieve success by that action, because it comes from an improper source.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 94 Verse 29. He who thus obtains wealth through greed, and then performs Vedic rituals (yajña) and gives (dāna), does not achieve success by that action, because it comes from an improper source.

sanskrit: उञ्छं मूलं फलं शाकमुदपात्रं तपोधनाः ।दानं विभवतो दत्त्वा नराः स्वर्यान्ति धर्मिणः ॥३०॥
transliteration: uñchaṁ mūlaṁ phalaṁ śākamudapātraṁ tapodhanāḥ ,dānaṁ vibhavato dattvā narāḥ svaryānti dharmiṇaḥ.
english: Righteous men (dharmiṇaḥ), whose wealth is spiritual austerity (tapas), having given gifts like gleaned grains, roots, fruits, vegetables, or even a pot of water, according to their means, go to heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 94 Verse 30. Righteous men (dharmiṇaḥ), whose wealth is spiritual austerity (tapas), having given gifts like gleaned grains, roots, fruits, vegetables, or even a pot of water, according to their means, go to heaven.

sanskrit: एष धर्मो महांस्त्यागो दानं भूतदया तथा ।ब्रह्मचर्यं तथा सत्यमनुक्रोशो धृतिः क्षमा ।सनातनस्य धर्मस्य मूलमेतत्सनातनम् ॥३१॥
transliteration: eṣa dharmo mahāṁstyāgo dānaṁ bhūtadayā tathā ,brahmacaryaṁ tathā satyamanukrośo dhṛtiḥ kṣamā ,sanātanasya dharmasya mūlametatsanātanam.
english: This is the great natural law (dharma): renunciation, charity, and compassion for all beings; likewise, celibacy (brahmacarya), truthfulness, kindness, fortitude, and forgiveness. This is the eternal root of the eternal natural law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 94 Verse 31. This is the great natural law (dharma): renunciation, charity, and compassion for all beings; likewise, celibacy (brahmacarya), truthfulness, kindness, fortitude, and forgiveness. This is the eternal root of the eternal natural law (dharma).

sanskrit: श्रूयन्ते हि पुरा विप्रा विश्वामित्रादयो नृपाः ।विश्वामित्रोऽसितश्चैव जनकश्च महीपतिः ।कक्षसेनार्ष्टिषेणौ च सिन्धुद्वीपश्च पार्थिवः ॥३२॥
transliteration: śrūyante hi purā viprā viśvāmitrādayo nṛpāḥ ,viśvāmitro'sitaścaiva janakaśca mahīpatiḥ ,kakṣasenārṣṭiṣeṇau ca sindhudvīpaśca pārthivaḥ.
english: Indeed, it is heard that in ancient times, sages (vipra) who were also kings, like Viśvāmitra and others, existed. These included Viśvāmitra, Asita, King Janaka, Kakṣasena and Ārṣṭiṣeṇa, and King Sindhudvīpa.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 94 Verse 32. Indeed, it is heard that in ancient times, sages (vipra) who were also kings, like Viśvāmitra and others, existed. These included Viśvāmitra, Asita, King Janaka, Kakṣasena and Ārṣṭiṣeṇa, and King Sindhudvīpa.

sanskrit: एते चान्ये च बहवः सिद्धिं परमिकां गताः ।नृपाः सत्यैश्च दानैश्च न्यायलब्धैस्तपोधनाः ॥३३॥
transliteration: ete cānye ca bahavaḥ siddhiṁ paramikāṁ gatāḥ ,nṛpāḥ satyaiśca dānaiśca nyāyalabdhaistapodhanāḥ.
english: These, and many others, attained supreme perfection (siddhi). They were kings who possessed the wealth of austerity (tapas), gained through truthfulness and charities justly acquired.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 94 Verse 33. These, and many others, attained supreme perfection (siddhi). They were kings who possessed the wealth of austerity (tapas), gained through truthfulness and charities justly acquired.

sanskrit: ब्राह्मणाः क्षत्रिया वैश्याः शूद्रा ये चाश्रितास्तपः ।दानधर्माग्निना शुद्धास्ते स्वर्गं यान्ति भारत ॥३४॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇāḥ kṣatriyā vaiśyāḥ śūdrā ye cāśritāstapaḥ ,dānadharmāgninā śuddhāste svargaṁ yānti bhārata.
english: Brahmins, Kshatriyas, Vaishyas, and Shudras who resorted to austerity (tapas), purified by the fire of charity (dāna) and the performance of their natural law (dharma), attain heaven, O Bhārata.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 94 Verse 34. Brahmins, Kshatriyas, Vaishyas, and Shudras who resorted to austerity (tapas), purified by the fire of charity (dāna) and the performance of their natural law (dharma), attain heaven, O Bhārata.

sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।धर्मागतेन त्यागेन भगवन्सर्वमस्ति चेत् ।एतन्मे सर्वमाचक्ष्व कुशलो ह्यसि भाषितुम् ॥१॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,dharmāgatena tyāgena bhagavansarvamasti cet ,etanme sarvamācakṣva kuśalo hyasi bhāṣitum.
english: Janamejaya said, "O revered one, if everything is attained through proper renunciation (tyāga), then please explain all this to me, for you are indeed skilled in speaking."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 95 Verse 1. Janamejaya said, "O revered one, if everything is attained through proper renunciation (tyāga), then please explain all this to me, for you are indeed skilled in speaking."

sanskrit: ततोञ्छवृत्तेर्यद्वृत्तं सक्तुदाने फलं महत् ।कथितं मे महद्ब्रह्मंस्तथ्यमेतदसंशयम् ॥२॥
transliteration: tatoñchavṛtteryadvṛttaṁ saktudāne phalaṁ mahat ,kathitaṁ me mahadbrahmaṁstathyametadasaṁśayam.
english: O great brahmin, what was recounted to me concerning the life of the gleaner and the great reward (phala) from offering flour – this is truly so, beyond any doubt.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 95 Verse 2. O great brahmin, what was recounted to me concerning the life of the gleaner and the great reward (phala) from offering flour – this is truly so, beyond any doubt.

sanskrit: कथं हि सर्वयज्ञेषु निश्चयः परमो भवेत् ।एतदर्हसि मे वक्तुं निखिलेन द्विजर्षभ ॥३॥
transliteration: kathaṁ hi sarvayajñeṣu niścayaḥ paramo bhavet ,etadarhasi me vaktuṁ nikhilena dvijarṣabha.
english: Indeed, how can there be supreme certainty (niścaya) in all Vedic rituals (yajña)? You should explain all this to me completely, O best among the twice-born (dvijarṣabha).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 95 Verse 3. Indeed, how can there be supreme certainty (niścaya) in all Vedic rituals (yajña)? You should explain all this to me completely, O best among the twice-born (dvijarṣabha).

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।अत्राप्युदाहरन्तीममितिहासं पुरातनम् ।अगस्त्यस्य महायज्ञे पुरावृत्तमरिंदम ॥४॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,atrāpyudāharantīmamitihāsaṁ purātanam ,agastyasya mahāyajñe purāvṛttamariṁdama.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: "Regarding this, O vanquisher of foes (arindama), they also relate this ancient narrative (itihāsa) which transpired long ago during the great Vedic ritual (yajña) of Agastya."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 95 Verse 4. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: "Regarding this, O vanquisher of foes (arindama), they also relate this ancient narrative (itihāsa) which transpired long ago during the great Vedic ritual (yajña) of Agastya."

sanskrit: पुरागस्त्यो महातेजा दीक्षां द्वादशवार्षिकीम् ।प्रविवेश महाराज सर्वभूतहिते रतः ॥५॥
transliteration: purāgastyo mahātejā dīkṣāṁ dvādaśavārṣikīm ,praviveśa mahārāja sarvabhūtahite rataḥ.
english: O great king, formerly, the greatly effulgent Agastya, dedicated to the welfare of all beings, commenced a twelve-year initiation (dīkṣā).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 95 Verse 5. O great king, formerly, the greatly effulgent Agastya, dedicated to the welfare of all beings, commenced a twelve-year initiation (dīkṣā).

sanskrit: तत्राग्निकल्पा होतार आसन्सत्रे महात्मनः ।मूलाहारा निराहाराः साश्मकुट्टा मरीचिपाः ॥६॥
transliteration: tatrāgnikalpā hotāra āsansatre mahātmanaḥ ,mūlāhārā nirāhārāḥ sāśmakuṭṭā marīcipāḥ.
english: There, in that great Vedic ritual (sattra) of the high-souled (Agastya), there were priests (hotāraḥ) whose brilliance was like fire. Among them, some lived on roots, some observed fasts (nirāhārāḥ), some crushed their sustenance with stones, and some subsisted only on sunbeams.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 95 Verse 6. There, in that great Vedic ritual (sattra) of the high-souled (Agastya), there were priests (hotāraḥ) whose brilliance was like fire. Among them, some lived on roots, some observed fasts (nirāhārāḥ), some crushed their sustenance with stones, and some subsisted only on sunbeams.

sanskrit: परिघृष्टिका वैघसिकाः संप्रक्षालास्तथैव च ।यतयो भिक्षवश्चात्र बभूवुः पर्यवस्थिताः ॥७॥
transliteration: parighṛṣṭikā vaighasikāḥ saṁprakṣālāstathaiva ca ,yatayo bhikṣavaścātra babhūvuḥ paryavasthitāḥ.
english: And so, there were also present ascetics (yatayaḥ) and mendicants (bhikṣavaḥ) of various kinds: those who subsisted on scraped food remnants, those who lived on leftovers (vaighasikāḥ), and those who performed thorough purifications (samprākṣālāḥ).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 95 Verse 7. And so, there were also present ascetics (yatayaḥ) and mendicants (bhikṣavaḥ) of various kinds: those who subsisted on scraped food remnants, those who lived on leftovers (vaighasikāḥ), and those who performed thorough purifications (samprākṣālāḥ).

sanskrit: सर्वे प्रत्यक्षधर्माणो जितक्रोधा जितेन्द्रियाः ।दमे स्थिताश्च ते सर्वे दम्भमोहविवर्जिताः ॥८॥
transliteration: sarve pratyakṣadharmāṇo jitakrodhā jitendriyāḥ ,dame sthitāśca te sarve dambhamohavivarjitāḥ.
english: All of them had a direct realization of natural law (dharma), had conquered anger, and had controlled their senses. They were all established in self-control (dama) and completely free from hypocrisy and delusion.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 95 Verse 8. All of them had a direct realization of natural law (dharma), had conquered anger, and had controlled their senses. They were all established in self-control (dama) and completely free from hypocrisy and delusion.

sanskrit: वृत्ते शुद्धे स्थिता नित्यमिन्द्रियैश्चाप्यवाहिताः ।उपासते स्म तं यज्ञं भुञ्जानास्ते महर्षयः ॥९॥
transliteration: vṛtte śuddhe sthitā nityamindriyaiścāpyavāhitāḥ ,upāsate sma taṁ yajñaṁ bhuñjānāste maharṣayaḥ.
english: Those great sages, ever established in pure conduct, and not led astray by their senses, performed that Vedic ritual (yajña) while partaking of it.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 95 Verse 9. Those great sages, ever established in pure conduct, and not led astray by their senses, performed that Vedic ritual (yajña) while partaking of it.

sanskrit: यथाशक्त्या भगवता तदन्नं समुपार्जितम् ।तस्मिन्सत्रे तु यत्किंचिदयोग्यं तत्र नाभवत् ।तथा ह्यनेकैर्मुनिभिर्महान्तः क्रतवः कृताः ॥१०॥
transliteration: yathāśaktyā bhagavatā tadannaṁ samupārjitam ,tasminsatre tu yatkiṁcidayogyaṁ tatra nābhavat ,tathā hyanekairmunibhirmahāntaḥ kratavaḥ kṛtāḥ.
english: The venerable one procured that food according to his capacity. In that Vedic ritual (satra), absolutely nothing inappropriate occurred. Indeed, in this manner, many sages performed great Vedic rituals (kratu).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 95 Verse 10. The venerable one procured that food according to his capacity. In that Vedic ritual (satra), absolutely nothing inappropriate occurred. Indeed, in this manner, many sages performed great Vedic rituals (kratu).

sanskrit: एवंविधेस्त्वगस्त्यस्य वर्तमाने महाध्वरे ।न ववर्ष सहस्राक्षस्तदा भरतसत्तम ॥११॥
transliteration: evaṁvidhestvagastyasya vartamāne mahādhvare ,na vavarṣa sahasrākṣastadā bharatasattama.
english: But, O best of Bharatas, while Agastya's great Vedic ritual (adhvara) of this kind was ongoing, Indra, the thousand-eyed, did not send rain at that time.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 95 Verse 11. But, O best of Bharatas, while Agastya's great Vedic ritual (adhvara) of this kind was ongoing, Indra, the thousand-eyed, did not send rain at that time.

sanskrit: ततः कर्मान्तरे राजन्नगस्त्यस्य महात्मनः ।कथेयमभिनिर्वृत्ता मुनीनां भावितात्मनाम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: tataḥ karmāntare rājannagastyasya mahātmanaḥ ,katheyamabhinirvṛttā munīnāṁ bhāvitātmanām.
english: Then, O King, after the completion of the rites of the great-souled Agastya, this story took place among the sages whose souls were purified (bhāvitātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 95 Verse 12. Then, O King, after the completion of the rites of the great-souled Agastya, this story took place among the sages whose souls were purified (bhāvitātman).

sanskrit: अगस्त्यो यजमानोऽसौ ददात्यन्नं विमत्सरः ।न च वर्षति पर्जन्यः कथमन्नं भविष्यति ॥१३॥
transliteration: agastyo yajamāno'sau dadātyannaṁ vimatsaraḥ ,na ca varṣati parjanyaḥ kathamannaṁ bhaviṣyati.
english: That Agastya, the performer of the Vedic ritual, gives food without any jealousy. Yet, the rain-god does not shower rain. How will there be food?
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 95 Verse 13. That Agastya, the performer of the Vedic ritual, gives food without any jealousy. Yet, the rain-god does not shower rain. How will there be food?

sanskrit: सत्रं चेदं महद्विप्रा मुनेर्द्वादशवार्षिकम् ।न वर्षिष्यति देवश्च वर्षाण्येतानि द्वादश ॥१४॥
transliteration: satraṁ cedaṁ mahadviprā munerdvādaśavārṣikam ,na varṣiṣyati devaśca varṣāṇyetāni dvādaśa.
english: O sages, this great Vedic ritual of the sage is for twelve years. And the god will not shower rain for these twelve years.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 95 Verse 14. O sages, this great Vedic ritual of the sage is for twelve years. And the god will not shower rain for these twelve years.

sanskrit: एतद्भवन्तः संचिन्त्य महर्षेरस्य धीमतः ।अगस्त्यस्यातितपसः कर्तुमर्हन्त्यनुग्रहम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: etadbhavantaḥ saṁcintya maharṣerasya dhīmataḥ ,agastyasyātitapasaḥ kartumarhantyanugraham.
english: Considering this, you (plural) should show favor to this wise great sage (muni) Agastya, who has performed immense austerities (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 95 Verse 15. Considering this, you (plural) should show favor to this wise great sage (muni) Agastya, who has performed immense austerities (tapas).

sanskrit: इत्येवमुक्ते वचने ततोऽगस्त्यः प्रतापवान् ।प्रोवाचेदं वचो वाग्मी प्रसाद्य शिरसा मुनीन् ॥१६॥
transliteration: ityevamukte vacane tato'gastyaḥ pratāpavān ,provācedaṁ vaco vāgmī prasādya śirasā munīn.
english: When this statement was thus made, then the glorious Agastya, eloquent, having respectfully saluted the sages (muni) by bowing his head, spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 95 Verse 16. When this statement was thus made, then the glorious Agastya, eloquent, having respectfully saluted the sages (muni) by bowing his head, spoke these words.

sanskrit: यदि द्वादशवर्षाणि न वर्षिष्यति वासवः ।चिन्तायज्ञं करिष्यामि विधिरेष सनातनः ॥१७॥
transliteration: yadi dvādaśavarṣāṇi na varṣiṣyati vāsavaḥ ,cintāyajñaṁ kariṣyāmi vidhireṣa sanātanaḥ.
english: If Indra does not cause rain for twelve years, I will perform a Vedic ritual (yajña) of worry. This is an eternal law.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 95 Verse 17. If Indra does not cause rain for twelve years, I will perform a Vedic ritual (yajña) of worry. This is an eternal law.

sanskrit: यदि द्वादशवर्षाणि न वर्षिष्यति वासवः ।व्यायामेनाहरिष्यामि यज्ञानन्यानतिव्रतान् ॥१८॥
transliteration: yadi dvādaśavarṣāṇi na varṣiṣyati vāsavaḥ ,vyāyāmenāhariṣyāmi yajñānanyānativratān.
english: If Indra does not cause rain for twelve years, I will perform other Vedic rituals (yajñas) that involve extraordinary vows through strenuous effort.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 95 Verse 18. If Indra does not cause rain for twelve years, I will perform other Vedic rituals (yajñas) that involve extraordinary vows through strenuous effort.

sanskrit: बीजयज्ञो मयायं वै बहुवर्षसमाचितः ।बीजैः कृतैः करिष्ये च नात्र विघ्नो भविष्यति ॥१९॥
transliteration: bījayajño mayāyaṁ vai bahuvarṣasamācitaḥ ,bījaiḥ kṛtaiḥ kariṣye ca nātra vighno bhaviṣyati.
english: This seed-Vedic ritual (yajña) has indeed been prepared by me over many years. I will perform it with the seeds I have gathered, and there will be no obstacle (vighna) in this matter.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 95 Verse 19. This seed-Vedic ritual (yajña) has indeed been prepared by me over many years. I will perform it with the seeds I have gathered, and there will be no obstacle (vighna) in this matter.

sanskrit: नेदं शक्यं वृथा कर्तुं मम सत्रं कथंचन ।वर्षिष्यतीह वा देवो न वा देवो भविष्यति ॥२०॥
transliteration: nedaṁ śakyaṁ vṛthā kartuṁ mama satraṁ kathaṁcana ,varṣiṣyatīha vā devo na vā devo bhaviṣyati.
english: This grand Vedic ritual (satra) of mine cannot be performed in vain by any means. Either the deity (deva) will cause rain here, or there will be no deity.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 95 Verse 20. This grand Vedic ritual (satra) of mine cannot be performed in vain by any means. Either the deity (deva) will cause rain here, or there will be no deity.

sanskrit: अथ वाभ्यर्थनामिन्द्रः कुर्यान्न त्विह कामतः ।स्वयमिन्द्रो भविष्यामि जीवयिष्यामि च प्रजाः ॥२१॥
transliteration: atha vābhyarthanāmindraḥ kuryānna tviha kāmataḥ ,svayamindro bhaviṣyāmi jīvayiṣyāmi ca prajāḥ.
english: Or, Indra should make a request, but not out of his own desire in this matter. I myself will become Indra and will sustain the beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 95 Verse 21. Or, Indra should make a request, but not out of his own desire in this matter. I myself will become Indra and will sustain the beings.

sanskrit: यो यदाहारजातश्च स तथैव भविष्यति ।विशेषं चैव कर्तास्मि पुनः पुनरतीव हि ॥२२॥
transliteration: yo yadāhārajātaśca sa tathaiva bhaviṣyati ,viśeṣaṁ caiva kartāsmi punaḥ punaratīva hi.
english: Whatever one's origin or nature of sustenance may be, one will become exactly like that. And indeed, I will make distinctions again and again, most certainly.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 95 Verse 22. Whatever one's origin or nature of sustenance may be, one will become exactly like that. And indeed, I will make distinctions again and again, most certainly.

sanskrit: अद्येह स्वर्णमभ्येतु यच्चान्यद्वसु दुर्लभम् ।त्रिषु लोकेषु यच्चास्ति तदिहागच्छतां स्वयम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: adyeha svarṇamabhyetu yaccānyadvasu durlabham ,triṣu lokeṣu yaccāsti tadihāgacchatāṁ svayam.
english: Today, let gold and whatever other rare wealth come here. And whatever exists in the three worlds, let those come here by themselves.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 95 Verse 23. Today, let gold and whatever other rare wealth come here. And whatever exists in the three worlds, let those come here by themselves.

sanskrit: दिव्याश्चाप्सरसां संघाः सगन्धर्वाः सकिंनराः ।विश्वावसुश्च ये चान्ये तेऽप्युपासन्तु वः सदा ॥२४॥
transliteration: divyāścāpsarasāṁ saṁghāḥ sagandharvāḥ sakiṁnarāḥ ,viśvāvasuśca ye cānye te'pyupāsantu vaḥ sadā.
english: And let the divine hosts of Apsarases, along with Gandharvas and Kinnaras, as well as Viśvāvasu and all other (celestial beings), always attend upon you.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 95 Verse 24. And let the divine hosts of Apsarases, along with Gandharvas and Kinnaras, as well as Viśvāvasu and all other (celestial beings), always attend upon you.

sanskrit: उत्तरेभ्यः कुरुभ्यश्च यत्किंचिद्वसु विद्यते ।सर्वं तदिह यज्ञे मे स्वयमेवोपतिष्ठतु ।स्वर्गं स्वर्गसदश्चैव धर्मश्च स्वयमेव तु ॥२५॥
transliteration: uttarebhyaḥ kurubhyaśca yatkiṁcidvasu vidyate ,sarvaṁ tadiha yajñe me svayamevopatiṣṭhatu ,svargaṁ svargasadaścaiva dharmaśca svayameva tu.
english: And whatever wealth exists from the Northern Kurus, may all that come to me here in this Vedic ritual (yajña) by itself. May heaven, the dwellers of heaven, and natural law (dharma) also come by themselves.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 95 Verse 25. And whatever wealth exists from the Northern Kurus, may all that come to me here in this Vedic ritual (yajña) by itself. May heaven, the dwellers of heaven, and natural law (dharma) also come by themselves.

sanskrit: इत्युक्ते सर्वमेवैतदभवत्तस्य धीमतः ।ततस्ते मुनयो दृष्ट्वा मुनेस्तस्य तपोबलम् ।विस्मिता वचनं प्राहुरिदं सर्वे महार्थवत् ॥२६॥
transliteration: ityukte sarvamevaitadabhavattasya dhīmataḥ ,tataste munayo dṛṣṭvā munestasya tapobalam ,vismitā vacanaṁ prāhuridaṁ sarve mahārthavat.
english: When this was said, all that came to be for that wise one. Then, seeing the ascetic power (tapobalam) of that sage (muni), all those sages (muni) were astonished and spoke these words, full of great meaning.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 95 Verse 26. When this was said, all that came to be for that wise one. Then, seeing the ascetic power (tapobalam) of that sage (muni), all those sages (muni) were astonished and spoke these words, full of great meaning.

sanskrit: प्रीताः स्म तव वाक्येन न त्विच्छामस्तपोव्ययम् ।स्वैरेव यज्ञैस्तुष्टाः स्मो न्यायेनेच्छामहे वयम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: prītāḥ sma tava vākyena na tvicchāmastapovyayam ,svaireva yajñaistuṣṭāḥ smo nyāyenecchāmahe vayam.
english: We are pleased by your words, but we do not desire a waste of ascetic power (tapas). We are satisfied by our own Vedic rituals (yajña) and we desire things justly.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 95 Verse 27. We are pleased by your words, but we do not desire a waste of ascetic power (tapas). We are satisfied by our own Vedic rituals (yajña) and we desire things justly.

sanskrit: यज्ञान्दीक्षास्तथा होमान्यच्चान्यन्मृगयामहे ।तन्नोऽस्तु स्वकृतैर्यज्ञैर्नान्यतो मृगयामहे ॥२८॥
transliteration: yajñāndīkṣāstathā homānyaccānyanmṛgayāmahe ,tanno'stu svakṛtairyajñairnānyato mṛgayāmahe.
english: We seek Vedic rituals (yajña), initiations, and Vedic fire rituals (homa), and whatever else. May that be ours through our own performed Vedic rituals (yajña); we do not seek it from another source.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 95 Verse 28. We seek Vedic rituals (yajña), initiations, and Vedic fire rituals (homa), and whatever else. May that be ours through our own performed Vedic rituals (yajña); we do not seek it from another source.

sanskrit: न्यायेनोपार्जिताहाराः स्वकर्मनिरता वयम् ।वेदांश्च ब्रह्मचर्येण न्यायतः प्रार्थयामहे ॥२९॥
transliteration: nyāyenopārjitāhārāḥ svakarmaniratā vayam ,vedāṁśca brahmacaryeṇa nyāyataḥ prārthayāmahe.
english: We are devoted to our own duties, obtaining our sustenance through just means. We also justly seek the Vedas through sacred study.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 95 Verse 29. We are devoted to our own duties, obtaining our sustenance through just means. We also justly seek the Vedas through sacred study.

sanskrit: न्यायेनोत्तरकालं च गृहेभ्यो निःसृता वयम् ।धर्मदृष्टैर्विधिद्वारैस्तपस्तप्स्यामहे वयम् ॥३०॥
transliteration: nyāyenottarakālaṁ ca gṛhebhyo niḥsṛtā vayam ,dharmadṛṣṭairvidhidvāraistapastapsyāmahe vayam.
english: And afterwards, we have lawfully departed from our homes. We shall perform austerities (tapas) through rites sanctioned by natural law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 95 Verse 30. And afterwards, we have lawfully departed from our homes. We shall perform austerities (tapas) through rites sanctioned by natural law (dharma).

sanskrit: भवतः सम्यगेषा हि बुद्धिर्हिंसाविवर्जिता ।एतामहिंसां यज्ञेषु ब्रूयास्त्वं सततं प्रभो ॥३१॥
transliteration: bhavataḥ samyageṣā hi buddhirhiṁsāvivarjitā ,etāmahiṁsāṁ yajñeṣu brūyāstvaṁ satataṁ prabho.
english: Indeed, this understanding of yours, which is entirely free from violence, is perfect. O Lord, you should always declare this non-violence in Vedic rituals (yajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 95 Verse 31. Indeed, this understanding of yours, which is entirely free from violence, is perfect. O Lord, you should always declare this non-violence in Vedic rituals (yajña).

sanskrit: प्रीतास्ततो भविष्यामो वयं द्विजवरोत्तम ।विसर्जिताः समाप्तौ च सत्रादस्माद्व्रजामहे ॥३२॥
transliteration: prītāstato bhaviṣyāmo vayaṁ dvijavarottama ,visarjitāḥ samāptau ca satrādasmādvrajāmahe.
english: O best of excellent brahmins, then we shall be pleased. And being dismissed at the conclusion of this (Vedic ritual) session, we shall depart.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 95 Verse 32. O best of excellent brahmins, then we shall be pleased. And being dismissed at the conclusion of this (Vedic ritual) session, we shall depart.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तथा कथयतामेव देवराजः पुरंदरः ।ववर्ष सुमहातेजा दृष्ट्वा तस्य तपोबलम् ॥३३॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tathā kathayatāmeva devarājaḥ puraṁdaraḥ ,vavarṣa sumahātejā dṛṣṭvā tasya tapobalam.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Even as they were speaking, Indra, the king of the gods and one of great splendor, rained, having witnessed the power of his asceticism (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 95 Verse 33. Vaiśampāyana said: Even as they were speaking, Indra, the king of the gods and one of great splendor, rained, having witnessed the power of his asceticism (tapas).

sanskrit: असमाप्तौ च यज्ञस्य तस्यामितपराक्रमः ।निकामवर्षी देवेन्द्रो बभूव जनमेजय ॥३४॥
transliteration: asamāptau ca yajñasya tasyāmitaparākramaḥ ,nikāmavarṣī devendro babhūva janamejaya.
english: O Janamejaya, even before the completion of that Vedic ritual (yajña), Indra, the king of the gods and one of immeasurable might, rained profusely.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 95 Verse 34. O Janamejaya, even before the completion of that Vedic ritual (yajña), Indra, the king of the gods and one of immeasurable might, rained profusely.

sanskrit: प्रसादयामास च तमगस्त्यं त्रिदशेश्वरः ।स्वयमभ्येत्य राजर्षे पुरस्कृत्य बृहस्पतिम् ॥३५॥
transliteration: prasādayāmāsa ca tamagastyaṁ tridaśeśvaraḥ ,svayamabhyetya rājarṣe puraskṛtya bṛhaspatim.
english: O royal sage, the lord of the thirty gods (Indra) personally approached that Agastya, having Bṛhaspati as his escort, and propitiated him.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 95 Verse 35. O royal sage, the lord of the thirty gods (Indra) personally approached that Agastya, having Bṛhaspati as his escort, and propitiated him.

sanskrit: ततो यज्ञसमाप्तौ तान्विससर्ज महामुनीन् ।अगस्त्यः परमप्रीतः पूजयित्वा यथाविधि ॥३६॥
transliteration: tato yajñasamāptau tānvisasarja mahāmunīn ,agastyaḥ paramaprītaḥ pūjayitvā yathāvidhi.
english: Then, upon the completion of the Vedic ritual (yajña), Agastya, who was greatly pleased, honored those great sages (mahāmunīn) according to the prescribed rites and then dismissed them.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 95 Verse 36. Then, upon the completion of the Vedic ritual (yajña), Agastya, who was greatly pleased, honored those great sages (mahāmunīn) according to the prescribed rites and then dismissed them.

sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।कोऽसौ नकुलरूपेण शिरसा काञ्चनेन वै ।प्राह मानुषवद्वाचमेतत्पृष्टो वदस्व मे ॥१॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,ko'sau nakularūpeṇa śirasā kāñcanena vai ,prāha mānuṣavadvācametatpṛṣṭo vadasva me.
english: Janamejaya asked: "Who is this one who, in the form of a mongoose with a golden head, spoke words like a human? Please tell me this when I ask."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 96 Verse 1. Janamejaya asked: "Who is this one who, in the form of a mongoose with a golden head, spoke words like a human? Please tell me this when I ask."

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एतत्पूर्वं न पृष्टोऽहं न चास्माभिः प्रभाषितम् ।श्रूयतां नकुलो योऽसौ यथा वागस्य मानुषी ॥२॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,etatpūrvaṁ na pṛṣṭo'haṁ na cāsmābhiḥ prabhāṣitam ,śrūyatāṁ nakulo yo'sau yathā vāgasya mānuṣī.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: "I have not been asked this before, nor has this matter been discussed by us. Listen now to how the mongoose's speech is human-like."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 96 Verse 2. Vaiśampāyana said: "I have not been asked this before, nor has this matter been discussed by us. Listen now to how the mongoose's speech is human-like."

sanskrit: श्राद्धं संकल्पयामास जमदग्निः पुरा किल ।होमधेनुस्तमागाच्च स्वयं चापि दुदोह ताम् ॥३॥
transliteration: śrāddhaṁ saṁkalpayāmāsa jamadagniḥ purā kila ,homadhenustamāgācca svayaṁ cāpi dudoha tām.
english: Long ago, the sage Jamadagni indeed performed a śrāddha ritual. A ritual cow (homadhenu) came to him, and he himself milked her.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 96 Verse 3. Long ago, the sage Jamadagni indeed performed a śrāddha ritual. A ritual cow (homadhenu) came to him, and he himself milked her.

sanskrit: तत्क्षीरं स्थापयामास नवे भाण्डे दृढे शुचौ ।तच्च क्रोधः स्वरूपेण पिठरं पर्यवर्तयत् ॥४॥
transliteration: tatkṣīraṁ sthāpayāmāsa nave bhāṇḍe dṛḍhe śucau ,tacca krodhaḥ svarūpeṇa piṭharaṁ paryavartayat.
english: He placed that milk in a new, strong, and clean vessel. And anger, in its own form, overturned that pot.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 96 Verse 4. He placed that milk in a new, strong, and clean vessel. And anger, in its own form, overturned that pot.

sanskrit: जिज्ञासुस्तमृषिश्रेष्ठं किं कुर्याद्विप्रिये कृते ।इति संचिन्त्य दुर्मेधा धर्षयामास तत्पयः ॥५॥
transliteration: jijñāsustamṛṣiśreṣṭhaṁ kiṁ kuryādvipriye kṛte ,iti saṁcintya durmedhā dharṣayāmāsa tatpayaḥ.
english: The one desirous of knowing pondered, 'What would the best of sages do if an unpleasant act were committed?' Thus reflecting, the dull-witted one violated his sacred offering.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 96 Verse 5. The one desirous of knowing pondered, 'What would the best of sages do if an unpleasant act were committed?' Thus reflecting, the dull-witted one violated his sacred offering.

sanskrit: तमाज्ञाय मुनिः क्रोधं नैवास्य चुकुपे ततः ।स तु क्रोधस्तमाहेदं प्राञ्जलिर्मूर्तिमान्स्थितः ॥६॥
transliteration: tamājñāya muniḥ krodhaṁ naivāsya cukupe tataḥ ,sa tu krodhastamāhedaṁ prāñjalirmūrtimānsthitaḥ.
english: Having recognized the anger (krodha), the sage (muni) was indeed not enraged by it. Then, that anger (krodha) itself, standing embodied with folded hands, said this to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 96 Verse 6. Having recognized the anger (krodha), the sage (muni) was indeed not enraged by it. Then, that anger (krodha) itself, standing embodied with folded hands, said this to him.

sanskrit: जितोऽस्मीति भृगुश्रेष्ठ भृगवो ह्यतिरोषणाः ।लोके मिथ्याप्रवादोऽयं यत्त्वयास्मि पराजितः ॥७॥
transliteration: jito'smīti bhṛguśreṣṭha bhṛgavo hyatiroṣaṇāḥ ,loke mithyāpravādo'yaṁ yattvayāsmi parājitaḥ.
english: 'I am conquered!' (said personified anger). 'O best of Bhrigus! Indeed, the Bhrigus are exceedingly wrathful. This popular notion in the world - that I have been defeated by you - is untrue.'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 96 Verse 7. 'I am conquered!' (said personified anger). 'O best of Bhrigus! Indeed, the Bhrigus are exceedingly wrathful. This popular notion in the world - that I have been defeated by you - is untrue.'

sanskrit: सोऽहं त्वयि स्थितो ह्यद्य क्षमावति महात्मनि ।बिभेमि तपसः साधो प्रसादं कुरु मे विभो ॥८॥
transliteration: so'haṁ tvayi sthito hyadya kṣamāvati mahātmani ,bibhemi tapasaḥ sādho prasādaṁ kuru me vibho.
english: I (personified anger) am indeed present in you today, O forgiving and great-souled one (mahātman). I fear your spiritual power (tapas), O virtuous one. Therefore, O Lord, please grant me your grace.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 96 Verse 8. I (personified anger) am indeed present in you today, O forgiving and great-souled one (mahātman). I fear your spiritual power (tapas), O virtuous one. Therefore, O Lord, please grant me your grace.

sanskrit: जमदग्निरुवाच ।साक्षाद्दृष्टोऽसि मे क्रोध गच्छ त्वं विगतज्वरः ।न ममापकृतं तेऽद्य न मन्युर्विद्यते मम ॥९॥
transliteration: jamadagniruvāca ,sākṣāddṛṣṭo'si me krodha gaccha tvaṁ vigatajvaraḥ ,na mamāpakṛtaṁ te'dya na manyurvidyate mama.
english: Jamadagni said: "O Anger, I have directly perceived you. Depart now, free from agitation. No wrong has been committed by you against me today, and I hold no resentment."
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 96 Verse 9. Jamadagni said: "O Anger, I have directly perceived you. Depart now, free from agitation. No wrong has been committed by you against me today, and I hold no resentment."

sanskrit: यानुद्दिश्य तु संकल्पः पयसोऽस्य कृतो मया ।पितरस्ते महाभागास्तेभ्यो बुध्यस्व गम्यताम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: yānuddiśya tu saṁkalpaḥ payaso'sya kṛto mayā ,pitaraste mahābhāgāstebhyo budhyasva gamyatām.
english: Indeed, those exalted ancestors (pitaras) are the ones for whom I made this solemn resolve (saṃkalpa) with this water. Understand (this truth regarding) them, and depart!
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 96 Verse 10. Indeed, those exalted ancestors (pitaras) are the ones for whom I made this solemn resolve (saṃkalpa) with this water. Understand (this truth regarding) them, and depart!

sanskrit: इत्युक्तो जातसंत्रासः स तत्रान्तरधीयत ।पितॄणामभिषङ्गात्तु नकुलत्वमुपागतः ॥११॥
transliteration: ityukto jātasaṁtrāsaḥ sa tatrāntaradhīyata ,pitṝṇāmabhiṣaṅgāttu nakulatvamupāgataḥ.
english: Thus addressed, he (Anger) became greatly frightened and vanished from that place. However, due to the powerful influence related to the ancestors (pitaras), he assumed the form of a mongoose.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 96 Verse 11. Thus addressed, he (Anger) became greatly frightened and vanished from that place. However, due to the powerful influence related to the ancestors (pitaras), he assumed the form of a mongoose.

sanskrit: स तान्प्रसादयामास शापस्यान्तो भवेदिति ।तैश्चाप्युक्तो यदा धर्मं क्षेप्स्यसे मोक्ष्यसे तदा ॥१२॥
transliteration: sa tānprasādayāmāsa śāpasyānto bhavediti ,taiścāpyukto yadā dharmaṁ kṣepsyase mokṣyase tadā.
english: He (the mongoose) then propitiated them (the ancestors), hoping for an end to his curse. And they, in turn, told him: 'You will achieve liberation (mokṣa) when you cast away your natural law (dharma).'
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 96 Verse 12. He (the mongoose) then propitiated them (the ancestors), hoping for an end to his curse. And they, in turn, told him: 'You will achieve liberation (mokṣa) when you cast away your natural law (dharma).'

sanskrit: तैश्चोक्तो यज्ञियान्देशान्धर्मारण्यानि चैव ह ।जुगुप्सन्परिधावन्स यज्ञं तं समुपासदत् ॥१३॥
transliteration: taiścokto yajñiyāndeśāndharmāraṇyāni caiva ha ,jugupsanparidhāvansa yajñaṁ taṁ samupāsadat.
english: Advised by them about the regions suitable for a (Vedic ritual) and the sacred forests (dharma-āraṇya), he approached that (Vedic ritual) while harboring disgust and wandering aimlessly.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 96 Verse 13. Advised by them about the regions suitable for a (Vedic ritual) and the sacred forests (dharma-āraṇya), he approached that (Vedic ritual) while harboring disgust and wandering aimlessly.

sanskrit: धर्मपुत्रमथाक्षिप्य सक्तुप्रस्थेन तेन सः ।मुक्तः शापात्ततः क्रोधो धर्मो ह्यासीद्युधिष्ठिरः ॥१४॥
transliteration: dharmaputramathākṣipya saktuprasthena tena saḥ ,muktaḥ śāpāttataḥ krodho dharmo hyāsīdyudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: Then, after he had challenged Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of (Dharma), by referring to that measure of flour, he was released from a curse. For indeed, the angry (Dharma) was Yudhiṣṭhira.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 96 Verse 14. Then, after he had challenged Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of (Dharma), by referring to that measure of flour, he was released from a curse. For indeed, the angry (Dharma) was Yudhiṣṭhira.

sanskrit: एवमेतत्तदा वृत्तं तस्य यज्ञे महात्मनः ।पश्यतां चापि नस्तत्र नकुलोऽन्तर्हितस्तदा ॥१५॥
transliteration: evametattadā vṛttaṁ tasya yajñe mahātmanaḥ ,paśyatāṁ cāpi nastatra nakulo'ntarhitastadā.
english: Thus, this indeed occurred then at the (Vedic ritual) of that great-souled (ātman) one. And as we watched there, the mongoose then disappeared.
search_text: Mahabharata Aśvamedhika Parva Chapter 96 Verse 15. Thus, this indeed occurred then at the (Vedic ritual) of that great-souled (ātman) one. And as we watched there, the mongoose then disappeared.
